k.syn: unconstraint, uninhibitedness, unrestraint
k.en: carefree, freedom from constraint

ex.en: add spices to the stew with complete abandon
ex.cn: 肆无忌惮地向炖菜里面加调料

k.syn: indulge, surrender
k.en: to give（oneself）over unrestrainedly

ex.en: abandon oneself to emotion
ex.cn: 感情用事

ex.en: abandon herself to a life of complete idleness
ex.cn: 她放纵自己过着闲散的生活

k.ant: salvage 救援
k.en: to withdraw from often in the face of danger or encroachment

ex.en: abandon the ship/homes
ex.cn: 弃船；离家

k.syn: abort, drop, repeal, rescind, revoke, call off, give up
k.ant: keep, continue, maintain, carry on 继续
k.en: to put an end to（something planned or previously agreed to）

ex.en: The bad weather forced NASA to abandon the launch.
ex.cn: 坏天气迫使 NASA停止了发射。

m: contains BAND + ON — to abandon = leave the band on stage and walk off. — abandon, to leave behind / give up.

k.syn: debauch, degrade, profane, vitiate, discredit, foul, smirch, take down
k.ant: elevate, ennoble, uplift, aggrandize, canonize, deify, exalt 使高贵，使有声望
k.cn: 降低（地位、职位、威望或尊严）
k.en: to lower in rank, office, prestige, or esteem

ex.en: He was unwilling to abase himself by pleading guilty to a crime that he did not commit.
ex.cn: 他不愿意屈就自己去承认一个莫须有的罪名。

m: contains BASE — to abase = push someone down to the base level, lowering their status. — abase, to lower in rank / dignity.

k.syn: discomfit, disconcert, discountenance, faze, fluster, nonplus, mortify
k.ant: embolden 使大胆
k.en: to destroy the self-possession or self- confidence of, disconcert, embarrass

ex.en: Nothing could abash him.
ex.cn: 没有什么可以使他感到难堪。

m: contains BASH — bashed in front of everyone, you're left red-faced and embarrassed. — abash, to embarrass.

k.syn: moderate, recede, subside, remit, wane, die（away or down or out）, let up, phase down, taper off
k.ant: intensify 加强，加剧
k.en: to reduce in degree or intensity

ex.en: abate his rage/pain
ex.cn: 平息他的愤怒/减轻他的痛苦

k.syn: de-escalate, deplete, downscale, dwindle, ratchet（down）
k.ant: augment, promote 增加
k.en: to reduce in amount or value

ex.en: abate a tax
ex.cn: 降低税收

k.syn: abrogate, annul, invalidate, nullify, rescind, vacate
k.en: to put an end to

ex.en: abate a nuisance
ex.cn: 停止伤害

m: sounds like "a bait" — bait shrinks each time a fish nibbles. — abate, to lessen.

k.syn: abridge, curtail, cut back, syncopate, truncate
k.ant: extend, protract, elongate, lengthen, prolong 延长
k.en: to make briefer

ex.en: abbreviate the word“building”as“bldg.”
ex.cn: 将“building”缩写作“bldg.”

m: contains BRIEF — to abbreviate = make something brief. — abbreviate, to shorten.

k.syn: cede, relinquish, renounce, resign, step down（from）
k.ant: constitute, assume, usurp 任命；承担；篡位
k.en: to renounce a throne, to relinquish（power or responsibility）formally

ex.en: abdicate the throne/crown
ex.cn: 退位

m: contains DICTAT(or) — when a dictator finally agrees to ab-DICTATE (step away from dictating), he abdicates. — abdicate, to formally give up power.

k.syn: abnormal, anomalous, peculiar, singular, unwonted, uncustomary
k.ant: normal, natural, regular, standard, typical 正常的，标准的，典型的
k.en: deviating from the usual or natural type

ex.en: This behavior might be aberrant enough to draw attention.
ex.cn: 这样的行为已经异常得足以引起人们的注意。

m: "a-BARE-rant" — a bare rant is wildly off-norm behavior. — aberrant, deviating from the norm.

k.syn: ferment, foment, provoke, instigate, stir（up）, whip（up）
k.en: to assist or support in the achievement of a purpose

ex.en: aid and abet
ex.cn: 〈法律〉协助教唆

k.syn: aid, assist, back, support, prop（up）
k.ant: stymie, frustrate, obstruct, thwart, impede, forestall, hinder 阻挠
k.en: to actively encourage（as an activity or plan）

m: rhymes with BET — to abet = to bet on a crime, backing the criminal. — abet, to assist or encourage (often wrongdoing).

k.syn: doldrums, quiescence, moratorium, latency, dormancy, suspension, cold storage, deep freeze
k.ant: continuance, fulfillment 继续；履行
k.en: temporary inactivity

ex.en: hold the plan in abeyance
ex.cn: 使计划暂停

m: a + BAY + ance — a ship sits in the BAY, going nowhere yet — held in abeyance. — abeyance, temporary suspension.

k.syn: abominate, despise, detest, execrate, loathe
k.ant: greatly admire 非常崇拜
k.en: to regard with extreme repugnance

ex.en: He abhors the way people leave their trash at the picnic sites in the park.
ex.cn: 他很厌恶人们把垃圾留在公园的野餐点。

m: "ab-HOR" = "Ah, HORROR!" — that gut-level recoil. — abhor, to regard with extreme disgust.

k.syn: ageless, enduring, eternal, everlasting, perennial, perpetual, timeless
k.ant: evanescent, ephemeral 短暂的
k.en: lasting for a long time; enduring

ex.en: an abiding love of Espanol
ex.cn: 对西班牙语持久的热爱

m: a + BIDING — biding (waiting) for a long time = enduring. — abiding, lasting.

k.syn: spiritless
k.ant: spirited, excited, exultant 狂喜的，令人兴奋的
k.en: cast down in spirit, spiritless

ex.en: a man made abject by suffering
ex.cn: 被苦难折磨得无精打采的人

k.en: sunk to or existing in a low state or condition

ex.en: abject poverty
ex.cn: 让人绝望的贫穷

k.syn: base, humble, menial, servile, slavish
k.en: expressing or offered in a humble and often ingratiating spirit

ex.en: abject flattery
ex.cn: 卑躬屈膝般的恭维

ex.en: an abject apology
ex.cn: 讨好般的道歉

m: ab + JECT — being utterly REJECTed leaves you abject — miserable, hopeless. — abject, utterly miserable / hopeless.

k.syn: recant, renege, renounce, abnegate, forswear, repudiate
k.ant: affirm, espouse, embrace 接受，拥护
k.en: a firm and final rejecting or abandoning often made under oath

ex.en: abjure one's belief
ex.cn: 发誓放弃信仰

k.syn: keep（from）, refrain（from）, withhold（from）
k.ant: bow to, give in to, submit to, succumb to, surrender to, yield to 屈从于
k.en: to resist the temptation of

ex.en: abjure extravagance
ex.cn: 抵制铺张浪费

m: 啊不就(abjure)发个誓嘛 — 「啊不就」声音对，意思也合：表面不在乎地正式放弃某信念。 — abjure, to formally renounce.

k.syn: recant, renege, repudiate, unsay, forswear, take back
k.ant: reaffirm 再肯定，重申
k.en: deny, renounce

ex.en: abnegate the idea of freedom
ex.cn: 放弃自由的观念

k.syn: cede, resign, step aside（from）, step down（from）
k.ant: adhere to 依附；坚持
k.en: surrender, relinquish

ex.en: abnegate her powers
ex.cn: 交出她拥有的权力

m: ab + NEGATE — to negate utterly, denying yourself. — abnegate, to deny / renounce.

k.syn: despise, detest, execrate, loathe
k.ant: esteem, love, adore 尊敬，爱
k.en: to hate or loathe intensely, abhor

ex.en: We abominate jokes that make fun of people who have physical disabilities.
ex.cn: 我们鄙视拿有生理缺陷的人开玩笑。

m: contains ABOMINA(ble) — to find something abominable, like the abominable snowman. — abominate, to detest.

k.ant: surreptitious, underhanded 偷偷摸摸的
k.en: free from all traces of deceit or duplicity

ex.en: This transaction was totally aboveboard, so there was no reason to question it.
ex.cn: 这项交易完全公开公正，没有必要质疑。

m: literally ABOVE + BOARD — cards held above the board where everyone can see them, no cheating. — aboveboard, honest / open.

k.syn: chafe, excoriate, rasp, graze, scuff
k.ant: augment 增加
k.en: to rub or wear away especially by friction; wear down spiritually

ex.en: My skin was abraded.
ex.cn: 我的皮肤被磨损了。

m: contains BRAID — braid hair too tight and you ABRADE the scalp. — abrade, to wear down by friction.

k.syn: curtail, truncate, cut back
k.ant: extend in length, protract, amplify 延长，放大
k.en: to shorten in duration or extent

ex.en: The modern transportation abridges distance.
ex.cn: 现代运输方式缩短了路途。

k.syn: abbreviate, curtail, condense, syncopate
k.cn: 在不改变本意的条件下删减词语，缩写
k.en: to shorten by omission of words without sacrifice of sense

ex.en: an abridged edition
ex.cn: 精简版

m: a + BRIDGE — a bridge SHORTENS the distance across a river; an abridged book is shortened. — abridge, to shorten.

k.syn: disannul, invalidate, nullify, quash, rescind, roll back, strike down
k.ant: embrace, uphold, institute 采纳；支持；建立
k.en: to abolish by authoritative action, annul

ex.en: abrogate the law/treaty
ex.cn: 废除法律、条约

k.en: to treat as nonexistent

ex.en: abrogate their responsibilities
ex.cn: 抛开、不顾他们的责任

m: rhymes with OBLIGATE but reversed — to ab-rogate is to officially remove an obligation/law. — abrogate, to formally repeal.

k.en: the natural separation

m: "ab-SCISSORS-ion" — SNIP, leaf cut clean off. — abscission, the natural cutting-off.

k.syn: flee, lam, run off, break out（of）, clear out
k.en: to depart secretly and hide oneself

ex.en: abscond from the prison
ex.cn: 越狱

m: contains CON — the CON-man ABS-CON-ded with the cash. — abscond, to flee secretly.

k.syn: arbitrary, autocratic, despotic, dictatorial, monocratic, tyrannical
k.en: unconstrained by constitutional or other provisions

ex.en: absolute ruler
ex.cn: 专制的统治者

k.syn: complete, utter, deadly, downright, out-and-out
k.ant: qualified 有限的，有保留的
k.en: unqualified in extent or degree; total

ex.en: absolute silence
ex.cn: 鸦雀无声

k.syn: plain, refined, unadulterated, unalloyed, undiluted, unmixed
k.ant: adulterated, alloyed, diluted, impure, mixed 掺杂的，不纯的
k.en: free from imperfection; free or relatively free from mixture

ex.en: absolute alcohol
ex.cn: 无水酒精（纯酒精）

k.syn: clear, deciding, decisive, definitive
k.ant: inconclusive, indecisive, unclear 不明确的
k.en: positive, unquestionable

ex.en: absolute proof
ex.cn: 确凿的证据

m: a thing said to be "absolutely" so is total — ABSOLUTE truth = no exceptions. — absolute, total / unquestionable.

k.syn: acquit, exonerate, vindicate
k.ant: blame, criminate, incriminate 定罪
k.en: to set free from an obligation or the consequences of guilt, exculpate

ex.en: absolve somebody from blame
ex.cn: 使某人免受责备

m: contains SOLVE — to absolve = to "solve" the guilt away, freeing the person. — absolve, to clear from guilt.

k.syn: forgo, keep from, refrain from, withhold（from）
k.ant: bow to, give in to, submit to, succumb to, surrender to, yield to 屈服于
k.en: to refrain from something by one's own choice

ex.en: abstain from smoking/voting
ex.cn: 戒烟；弃权

m: contains STAIN — keep yourself away from the stain (alcohol, lying) by abstaining. — abstain, to refrain.

k.syn: continent, temperate, self-abnegating, self-denying
k.ant: indulgent 放纵的
k.en: marked by restraint especially in the consumption of food or alcohol, sparing

ex.en: an abstemious diet
ex.cn: 有节制的饮食

ex.en: abstemious meals
ex.cn: 节俭的饭餐

m: ab + STEM + ious — STEM the temptation to overeat or overdrink. — abstemious, restrained in eating/drinking.

k.syn: digest, recapitulate, synopsize, sum up, boil down
k.ant: elaborate 详细描述
k.en: to make an abstract of, summarize

ex.en: abstract the 135-page report in three short paragraphs
ex.cn: 将一份135页的报告概括为三段话

k.syn: detract, divert, call off, throw off
k.ant: attention 关注
k.en: to draw away the attention of

ex.en: Personal problems abstracted him.
ex.cn: 私人问题让他分心。

m: ab + STRACT (like "extract") — to abstract = to pull out the essence. — abstract, to summarize / pull out the core.

k.syn: arcane, esoteric, hermetic（also hermetical）, recondite
k.ant: accessible, patent, shallow, superficial 可了解的，肤浅的
k.en: difficult to comprehend: recondite

ex.en: the abstruse calculations
ex.cn: 复杂深奥的计算

m: rhymes with OBTUSE — to obtuse minds, everything is abstruse. — abstruse, hard to understand.

k.syn: wild, bizarre, ludicrous, insane, nonsensical, preposterous, half- baked
k.ant: rational, sensible, realistic, reasonable 理性的，合理的
k.en: ridiculously unreasonable, unsound, or incongruous

ex.en: an absurd argument
ex.cn: 无稽之谈

m: rhymes with OCCURRED — what just occurred is so weird, it's absurd. — absurd, ridiculous.

k.syn: ample, cornucopian, teeming, replete, abounding
k.ant: infrequent, rare, uncommon, inadequate, scanty, scarce 稀少的，罕见的
k.en: marked by great plenty（as of resources）

ex.en: an abundant land
ex.cn: 一片富饶的土地

m: contains BUN — so many BUNs at the bakery, an ABUNDANT supply. — abundant, plentiful.

k.syn: assail, bash, castigate, excoriate, lambaste
k.en: to condemn or vilify usually unjustly, intemperately, and angrily

ex.en: He alleged that he was verbally abused by his colleagues.
ex.cn: 他声称受到了同事的言语攻击。

k.syn: misemploy, misuse
k.en: to put to a wrong or improper use; 过分过量使用：to use excessively

ex.en: abuse a privilege
ex.cn: 滥用特权

ex.en: abuse alcohol
ex.cn: 酗酒

m: ab- (wrong) + USE — wrong-use, mistreat. — abuse, to mistreat.

k.syn: skirt, verge on, border on, butt on, march with
k.en: to touch at one end or side; lie adjacent

ex.en: Our land abuts a wildlife preserve.
ex.cn: 我们的土地靠近一片野生动物保护区。

m: contains BUTT — when two cars abut, their butts touch end-to-end. — abut, to touch at the edge.

k.syn: bottomless
k.en: immeasurably low or wretched

ex.en: abysmal living conditions
ex.cn: 极可怜的生活条件

k.syn: profound
k.ant: shallow, shoal, skin-deep, superficial 浅的；肤浅的
k.en: immeasurably great

ex.en: abysmal ignorance
ex.cn: 极端的无知

ex.en: an abysmal cliff
ex.cn: 深不见底的悬崖

m: contains ABYSS — falling into an abyss is abysmal. — abysmal, immeasurably bad.

k.syn: acquiesce, assent, consent, subscribe, come round
k.ant: demur, dissent 反对
k.en: to express approval or give consent

ex.en: accede to their pleas
ex.cn: 同意他们的请愿

k.en: to arrive at or come into an office or dignity

ex.en: accede to the throne
ex.cn: 就任国王

m: contains CEDE — to cede ground = to give in = to accede. — accede, to agree / yield.

k.syn: balloon, escalate, snowball, mushroom, proliferate, burgeon, build up
k.ant: retard 减速
k.en: to cause to move faster

ex.en: accelerate his steps
ex.cn: 他加快脚步

k.en: to bring about at an earlier time

ex.en: accelerate their departure
ex.cn: 提前离开

k.syn: accumulate, balloon, enlarge, escalate, mushroom, proliferate, snowball, roll up
k.ant: contract, decrease, diminish, dwindle, lessen, recede, wane 减小，减少
k.en: to become greater in size, extent, volume, amount, or number

ex.en: Toy purchases accelerate dramatically during the Christmas season.
ex.cn: 玩具销售量在圣诞期间暴涨。

m: shares CELER- with CELERITY (speed) — to accelerate = to add celerity. — accelerate, to speed up.

k.syn: handy, reachable
k.ant: inconvenient, unhandy, unreachable, untouchable 无法到达的，不方便的
k.en: situated within easy reach

ex.en: the town accessible by rail
ex.cn: 有铁路通达的城镇

k.syn: apprehensible, fathomable, legible, scrutable
k.ant: abstruse, incoherent, incomprehensible, inscrutable, insensible 深奥的；难以理解的
k.en: capable of being understood or appreciated

ex.en: The information ought to be made more accessible.
ex.cn: 信息应该更清晰易懂。

k.syn: available, attainable, obtainable, procurable
k.ant: unattainable, unavailable, unobtainable 无法获得的
k.en: possible to get

ex.en: The data is not currently accessible.
ex.cn: 该数据目前还不可用。

m: contains ACCESS — if you can ACCESS it, it's accessible. — accessible, reachable / understandable.

k.syn: peripheral, supplementary
k.ant: of primarily importance, chief, main, principal 主要的
k.en: having a secondary, supplementary, or subordinate function

ex.en: accessory features such as call-waiting
ex.cn: 类似呼叫等待的附属功能

k.syn: abettor, confederate
k.en: one associated with another in wrongdoing

ex.en: Two accessories, the driver of the getaway car and the dishonest bank teller, were charged in the robbery case.
ex.cn: 两个帮凶，逃逸车辆的司机和不诚信的银行出纳，在抢劫案中被控告。

m: contains ACCESS — a jewelry accessory; an accessory to a crime gives the criminal access. — accessory, supplementary item / aider in a crime.

k.syn: incidental, fortuitous, unintended, unintentional, unpremeditated, unwitting
k.ant: calculated, deliberate, intended, planned, premeditated 预先计划好的
k.en: occurring unexpectedly or by chance

ex.en: The discovery of gold was entirely accidental.
ex.cn: 黄金的发现纯属偶然。

k.syn: alien, extraneous, external, adventitious
k.ant: inherent, innate, intrinsic 内在的，固有的
k.en: not being a vital part of or belonging to something

ex.en: Its commercial value was accidental.
ex.cn: 它的商业价值微乎其微。

m: contains ACCIDENT — accidental = happening by accident. — accidental, unintended.

k.syn: adjust, accommodate, shape, suit, tailor
k.ant: make unfamiliar with 使不熟悉
k.en: to change（something）so as to make it suitable for a new use or situation

ex.en: acclimate oneself to a nine-to-five office job
ex.cn: 适应朝九晚五的坐班生活

m: contains CLIMATE — to acclimate = to adjust to a new climate. — acclimate, to adapt.

k.syn: applause, credit, distinction
k.ant: disapprobation 不赞成
k.en: an expression of approval

ex.en: The director won virtually every accolade that the film world offered.
ex.cn: 这位导演赢得了电影界几乎一致的赞赏。

k.ant: excoriate, criticise, reprobate, castigate, derogate, reproof, denigrate, denunciate 批评，谴责
k.en: to praise or honor

m: contains COLA — they spray COLA on the championship player at the awards ceremony — that's an accolade. — accolade, an award / honor.

k.syn: fit, hold, take
k.cn: 提供所需（例如服务、借贷、寄宿）
k.en: to provide with something desired, needed, or suited（as a helpful service, a loan, or lodgings）

ex.en: This cruise ship was big enough to accommodate over 600 people.
ex.cn: 该游轮可以为600多人提供食宿。

k.syn: acclimate, adjust, condition, conform, doctor, edit, shape, suit, tailor
k.cn: 改变以适应新情况、新场景
k.en: to change（something）so as to make it suitable for a new use or situation

ex.en: accommodate the lectern to the height of the guest speaker
ex.cn: 将讲台的高度调节得适合客座讲演者

k.syn: attune, conciliate, conform, coordinate, reconcile
k.ant: disharmonize 使不和谐
k.en: to bring to a state free of conflicts, inconsistencies, or differences

ex.en: The idea that the United States could harmoniously accommodate all was a fiction.
ex.cn: 认为美国能够和谐一切的想法只是幻想。

m: contains COMMOD(ious) — "commodious" means roomy; to accommodate = to make room for. — accommodate, to provide for / fit in.

k.en: to approach and speak to often in a challenging or aggressive way

ex.en: accosted by three gang members
ex.cn: 被三个混混搭讪

m: contains COST — being accosted COSTs you peace and time. — accost, to confront aggressively.

k.syn: accumulate, build up, pile（up）, stack（up）
k.ant: wear away 衰退
k.en: to grow or increase gradually, as by addition

ex.en: silt accreting at the mouth of the river
ex.cn: 泥沙在河口淤积

m: ac + CRETE (like "create") — to accrete = to create gradually by adding layers. — accrete, to grow by accumulation.

k.syn: pungent, sardonic, satiric, scalding, scathing
k.ant: sweet, saccharine 甜的
k.en: marked by the use of wit that is intended to cause hurt feelings

ex.en: acerbic commentary
ex.cn: 尖酸的评论

m: rhymes with ACID — acerbic remarks bite like acid. — acerbic, sharp / biting.

k.en: possessing no hue

m: a- (without) + CHROMA (color, as in "chromatic scale") — without color. — achromatic, colorless.

k.syn: culmination, pinnacle, tip-top, high-water mark
k.ant: bottom, nadir, rock bottom 最低点
k.en: the highest point or stage, as of achievement or development

ex.en: the acme of his career
ex.cn: 他事业的巅峰

k.syn: apotheosis, epitome, exemplar
k.en: the most perfect type or example

ex.en: The movie has come to be regarded as the acme of the Hollywood musical.
ex.cn: 这部影片被认为是好莱坞音乐剧的典范。

m: Wile E. Coyote's ACME products always claim to be "the peak of quality". — acme, the peak / highest point.

k.syn: assent, consent, subscribe, come round
k.ant: resist, defy, dissent 抵制，不同意
k.en: to accept, comply, or submit tacitly or passively

ex.en: acquiesce to my own fleecing
ex.cn: 默许我的敲竹杠行为

m: contains QUIET — to acquiesce is to QUIETly agree, without enthusiasm. — acquiesce, to silently consent.

k.en: the act of acquiring (acquire: to get as one’s own)

m: acquire(获得)+tion——公司收购别人，就是acquisition，获得。

k.en: a unit of area in the U.S. Customary System, used in land

m: acre——古时一头牛一天能犁完的地，acre，英亩（约半个足球场）。

k.en: sharp and harsh or unpleasantly pungent in taste or odor: irritating

ex.en: acrid smell of tobacco
ex.cn: 烟草刺鼻的味道

k.ant: gentle 温和的
k.en: marked by the use of wit that is intended to cause hurt feelings

ex.en: acrid temper
ex.cn: 刻薄的性情

m: rhymes with ACID — acrid smoke is acidic and harsh. — acrid, sharp / pungent.

k.syn: embittered, hard, rancorous, resentful, sore
k.en: having or showing deep-seated resentment

ex.en: the acrimonious debate between the two candidates
ex.cn: 两位候选人之间言辞激烈的辩论

m: shares ACRI- with acrid — sharp-tongued, bitter exchanges. — acrimonious, bitter / hostile.

k.syn: keenness, shrewdness, canniness, clear-sightedness, hardheadedness
k.ant: unable to discern 不能辨别的
k.cn: 不同寻常的洞察力和鉴别力
k.en: exceptional discernment and judgment especially in practical matters

ex.en: the business acumen
ex.cn: 商业方面的洞察力

m: contains ACU- (sharp, as in "acute") — sharp insight. — acumen, sharp practical insight.

k.syn: delicate, fine, keen, perceptive, sensitive
k.en: marked by keen discernment or intellectual perception especially of subtle distinctions, penetrating

ex.en: an acute thinker
ex.cn: 思维敏锐的思想者

k.syn: exigent, imperative, compelling, urgent
k.ant: mild, noncritical, nonurgent 轻微的，不严重的
k.en: needing immediate attention

ex.en: acute appendicitis
ex.cn: 急性阑尾炎

k.syn: dreadful, excruciating, profound
k.ant: light, moderate, soft 温和的
k.en: extreme in degree, or effect

ex.en: She was experiencing acute distress over the misunderstanding with her best friend.
ex.cn: 她因与死党之间发生误会而陷入了深深的痛苦中。

m: an ACUTE angle is sharp; an acute pain is sharp. — acute, sharp / severe.

k.syn: adj.st, conform, edit, accommodate, shape, suit, tailor
k.en: to modify according with the changing circumstances

ex.en: adapt to the change
ex.cn: 适应变化

ex.en: adapt the novel for the screen
ex.cn: 改编小说为影视作品

m: contains APT — adapting = becoming APT for a new situation. — adapt, to adjust.

k.en: an additional section at the end of a book or document

m: contains ADD — an ADDendum is something ADDed at the end. — addendum, an addition.

k.en: to devote or surrender（oneself）to something habitually or obsessively

ex.cn: 沉溺于毒品、酒

k.syn: devotee, enthusiast, fanatic, maniac
k.ant: nonfan 非粉丝
k.en: a person with a strong and habitual liking for something

ex.en: science-fiction addicts who eagerly await each new installment in the series
ex.cn: 科幻小说爱好者，等待着系列的每一次更新

m: an ADDICT is hooked, has to add another dose. — addict, person obsessed with a habit.

k.syn: cleave, cling, hew
k.ant: detach 分离
k.en: to cause to stick fast

ex.en: adhere to the surface
ex.cn: 附着在表面

k.syn: cling to, hew to, stand by, stick to, comply with
k.ant: defy, disobey, rebel against 不服从，反抗
k.en: to act according to the commands of

ex.en: adhere to the rules
ex.cn: 遵守规定

k.syn: keep to, stand by, stick to or with
k.ant: forerunner 先行者
k.der: adherent n. 追随者：a follower of a leader, party, or profession
k.en: to give steadfast support to

ex.en: Our coach adheres to the belief that we can win this game if we just have a positive attitude.
ex.cn: 我们的教练坚定地相信，只要我们有积极的态度，就能拿下比赛。

m: contains HERE — adhere = stick HERE. — adhere, to stick to.

k.syn: prorogate, prorogue, recess, suspend
k.ant: convoke 召集，召开会议

ex.en: The meeting adjourned for a week.
ex.cn: 会议延期一周。

m: contains JOURN(ey) — adjourn = end this session, time to journey home. — adjourn, to suspend a meeting.

k.syn: appendage, appliance, attachment, add-on
k.ant: essential element 重要部分
k.en: something joined or added to another thing but not essentially a part of it

ex.en: Massage therapy can be used as an adjunct along with the medication.
ex.cn: 按摩治疗可以作为药物治疗的补充疗法。

m: contains JUNCT (junction) — connected/joined onto something. — adjunct, an added/attached thing.

k.syn: counsel
k.en: to give advice to

ex.cn: 建议患者多吃健康食品

k.syn: chide, reprimand, reproach, reprove, tick off
k.en: to reprove gently but earnestly

ex.en: admonish her for littering
ex.cn: 责备她乱扔垃圾

m: shares MON- with MONITOR — both watch and warn; to admonish = to warn / scold. — admonish, to warn / mildly scold.

k.syn: fancy, relish, savor, delight in, rejoice in
k.en: to take pleasure in

ex.en: I adore those earrings.
ex.cn: 我相中那对耳环了。

k.syn: cherish
k.ant: abhor, abominate, despise, detest, execrate, loathe 讨厌，嫌弃
k.en: to feel passion, devotion, or tenderness for

ex.en: adore his wife
ex.cn: 宠爱他的妻子

m: to ADORE is the opposite of "abhor" — both two-syllable, same shape, opposite feeling. — adore, to love deeply.

k.syn: overpraise, belaud, soft-soap, butter up
k.ant: scorn, disdain, vituperate, disparage 鄙视
k.en: to praise too much

ex.en: incompetent assistants who spend all their time adulating her
ex.cn: 成天恭维她的不称职的下属

m: ad + ULATE (rhymes with "elate") — to elate someone with over-the-top praise. — adulate, to flatter excessively.

k.syn: alloy, contaminate, pollute, taint, water down
k.ant: enrich, fortify, strengthen 加入（养分）使增强
k.en: to corrupt, debase, or make impure by the addition of a foreign or inferior substance or element

ex.en: adulterate its products with cheap additives
ex.cn: 在产品中掺杂廉价的添加剂

m: contains ALTER — to adulterate = to alter (degrade) something by mixing in impurities. — adulterate, to corrupt / contaminate.

k.ant: revelation 完全显示
k.cn: 部分地或有保留地揭露
k.en: to disclose partially or guardedly

ex.en: adumbrate a plan
ex.cn: 透露计划

k.syn: forerun, harbinger, herald, prefigure
k.en: to give a slight indication of beforehand

ex.en: The strife in Bloody Kansas adumbrated the civil war that would follow.
ex.cn: 发生在堪萨斯州的流血冲突预示了之后的南北内战的爆发。

m: contains UMBR- (shadow, as in "umbrella") — to ADUMBRATE = to give a shadow-outline. — adumbrate, to outline / foreshadow.

k.syn: alien, extraneous, external, foreign, supervenient
k.ant: constitutional, essential, intrinsic, inborn, inbred, innate, inherent 天生的，内在的
k.cn: 外来的，后天的，非内在的
k.en: coming from another source and not inherent or innate

ex.en: Moral considerations are adventitious to the study of art.
ex.cn: 道德的考量对于艺术研究来说是不必要的。

m: ad+vent(来)——从外面飘来的，adventitious偶然外来的。

k.syn: antagonist, foe, opponent
k.ant: ally, amigo, friend 联盟；朋友
k.en: one that contends with, opposes, or resists: enemy

ex.en: political adversary
ex.cn: 政敌

m: adverse(不利的)+ary——对你不利的那个人，就是adversary，对手。

k.ant: inattentive, remiss, heedless, negligent 疏忽的
k.en: to call attention; refer

ex.en: He adverted to the problem in the opening paragraph.
ex.cn: 他开门见山地提出了问题。

m: 啊，我注意(advert)到了！——路上那块奇怪的牌子让你忍不住回头多看一眼。——advert，注意留意。

k.en: to announce publicly esp. by a printed notice or a broadcast

m: ADs in newspapers and TV ADVERTISE products. — advertise, to publicize.

k.syn: back, champion, endorse, patronize
k.ant: impugn 提出异议
k.en: to speak, plead, or argue in favor of; support

ex.en: advocate traditional teaching methods
ex.cn: 提倡传统教学方法

m: ad + VOCAT(e) (sounds like "vocal") — to advocate = to be vocal in support. — advocate, to support publicly.

k.en: a particular theory or conception of beauty or art; concerned with beauty

m: an aesthetician does skin care — both relate to beauty. — aesthetic, concerning beauty.

k.syn: cordial, genial, hospitable, sociable, good-natured, good-tempered, well-disposed
k.ant: irascible, testy, ill-tempered, unamiable, ungenial 易怒的，不随和的
k.en: characterized by ease and friendliness

ex.en: an affable manner
ex.cn: 随和的举止

m: sounds like "a FABLE" — an affable person tells friendly fables, easy to chat with. — affable, friendly / easy to talk to.

k.en: a tender feeling toward another; fondness.：love

m: contains AFFECT — affection is what AFFECTs your heart. — affection, fondness.

k.en: a sworn statement

m: contains FID (faith, as in "fidelity") — a sworn statement based on faith in the truth. — affidavit, a sworn written statement.

k.syn: bent, penchant, predilection, predisposition, proclivity, propensity
k.ant: aversion, repugnance, antipathy 厌恶
k.en: a habitual attraction to some activity or thing

ex.en: She always had an affinity for nurturing living things.
ex.cn: 她总是喜欢养一些活物。

k.syn: association, kinship
k.en: the fact or state of having something in common

ex.en: a study showing an affinity between obesity and socioeconomic status
ex.cn: 一项显示了肥胖程度和社会经济水 　平之间相似性的研究

m: affinity → af + fin + ity，fin像finish「结束」，但fin在「fine」里也表示「好」。两个人走到最后的good ending，正是因为彼此有天生的亲切感——affinity，喜爱、亲切感、天然吸引力。

k.syn: opulent, loaded, deep-pocketed, silk-stocking, well-endowed, well- off, well-to-do
k.ant: needy, impecunious, impoverished, indigent, penurious 贫困的
k.en: having a generously sufficient and typically increasing supply of material possessions

ex.en: affluent society
ex.cn: 富裕的社会

m: afFLUent — wealth FLU(id) flowing in. — affluent, wealthy.

k.en: a list or outline

m: AGEN ≈ "again-da" — the AGENDA lists what's to be done over and over (again and again). — agenda, list of items to do.

k.syn: augment, boost, expand, magnify, add（to）, pump up
k.ant: relegate, disparage, efface, abase, demean 降级；贬低
k.cn: 增加、提高（力量、财富、地位、声誉）等
k.en: to enhance the power, wealth, position, or reputation of

ex.en: exploit the situation to aggrandize himself
ex.cn: 借势而上使得自己地位提升、财富增长

m: contains GRAND — to aggrandize = to make GRANDer / inflate. — aggrandize, to make great or appear great.

k.syn: complicate, worsen
k.ant: alleviate, succor, console, assuage, mitigate, relieve 减轻，抚慰
k.en: to make worse, more serious, or more severe

ex.cn: 压力和睡眠不足使情况恶化。

k.syn: annoy, bother, nettle, vex
k.en: to disturb the peace of mind of（someone） especially by repeated disagreeable acts

ex.en: It really aggravates me; when I arrived 10 minutes before the stated closing time, the store had closed already.
ex.cn: 我在规定的打烊时间前10分钟到达，但发现商店已经关门了，这让我很恼火。

k.syn: sum, summation, totality
k.ant: isolated units 隔离的单位
k.en: a mass or body of units or parts somewhat loosely associated with one another

ex.en: An empire is the aggregate of many states under one common head.
ex.cn: 帝国是由一个共同领袖领导的若干国家的集合体。

k.syn: coalesce, join together
k.ant: disperse 分散
k.en: to collect or gather into a mass or whole

ex.cn: 搜罗集合许多其他网站的内容

k.syn: fierce, assaultive, combative, militant, confrontational, go-getting, self-assertive, truculent, pugnacious
k.ant: even-tempered, nonbelligerent, pacific, uncombative, uncontentious 心平气和的，不好斗的
k.en: having a quality of anger and determination that makes it ready to attack others

ex.en: aggressive behavior
ex.cn: 具有攻击性的行为

k.syn: dynamic, energetic, full-blooded, vigorous

ex.cn: 一次能够获得非裔美国人选票的强有力的竞选

m: AG- (toward) + GRESS (step) — stepping toward, on the attack. — aggressive, hostile / forceful.

k.ant: gratify 使高兴，满足
k.der: aggrieved adj. 苦恼的，怨念的
k.syn: discontent, disgruntled, displeased, dissatisfied, malcontent
k.en: to give pain or trouble to, distress

ex.en: a line of aggrieved ticket-holders, demanding a refund for the cancelled play
ex.cn: 一队心怀怨念的购票者，为取消的话剧要求退款

m: contains GRIEF — to aggrieve = to cause grief. — aggrieve, to wrong / give grief.

k.syn: debate, dispute, bat（around or back and forth）, hash（over or out）, talk over
k.en: to attempt to arouse public feeling

ex.en: agitate for better conditions
ex.cn: 为得到更好的条件而煽动

k.syn: bother, discomfort, discompose, perturb
k.ant: calm, compose, soothe, tranquilize 平息，抚慰
k.en: to excite and often trouble the mind or feelings of: disturb

ex.en: There's no need to agitate the patient about little things.
ex.cn: 没必要因为鸡毛蒜皮的事让患者烦躁。

m: rhymes with AGGREGATE in feel but means stir — AGI- (move, as in "agile") — keep moving things and people up. — agitate, to stir up.

k.syn: avid, ardent, enthusiastic, solicitous, voracious
k.ant: apathetic, indifferent, uneager, unenthusiastic 漠不关心的，冷淡的
k.en: showing urgent desire or interest

ex.en: Children were agog over new toys.
ex.cn: 孩子们见到了新玩具极其兴奋。

m: eyes go GOG-GOG (goggling) at the spectacle. — agog, eagerly excited.

k.syn: anguish, suffer
k.en: to（cause to）feel deep sadness or mental pain

ex.en: agonizes over every decision
ex.cn: 做每一个决定都非常痛苦

k.syn: bedevil, beset, besiege, curse, excruciate, harrow, persecute, plague, rack, torment, torture
k.ant: comfort, console, solace, soothe 安抚，安慰
k.der: agony n. 痛苦；agonizing adj. 痛苦的
k.en: to cause to suffer agony

ex.en: She got into more trouble, further agonizing her poor mother.
ex.cn: 她惹上了更多的麻烦，进一步加剧了她可怜的母亲所遭受的痛苦。

k.syn: anguish, bleed, hurt, mourn, sorrow, suffer
k.ant: cheer, crow, delight, exult, joy, rejoice 感到高兴
k.en: to suffer agony, torture, or anguish

ex.en: She agonized for days over the moral issues involved.
ex.cn: 一连几天她都为卷入的道德纠纷而感到痛苦。

m: contains AGONY — to feel agony. — agonize, to suffer torment.

k.en: relating to agricultural

m: contains AGR- (as in "agriculture") — relating to farming. — agrarian, relating to land/agriculture.

k.en: having no noticeable weakness, flaw, or loophole

ex.en: an airtight argument
ex.cn: 滴水不漏、无懈可击的论断

k.en: impermeable to air or nearly so

ex.en: an airtight seal
ex.cn: 不透气的封口

m: AIR + TIGHT — no air gets in or out; metaphorically a flawless argument with no gaps. — airtight, sealed / flawless.

k.syn: amenability, gameness, obligingness, willingness
k.ant: dilatoriness, hesitance and reluctance 拖延、犹豫和不情愿
k.en: promptness in response: cheerful readiness

ex.en: accept the invitation with alacrity
ex.cn: 欣然接受邀请

m: rhymes with CELERITY — both mean speed; alacrity is cheerful eagerness to act. — alacrity, cheerful eagerness.

k.en: an organism exhibiting

m: AL + BIN(o) — an ALBINO has "all" pigment dropped in the BIN, leaving pure white. — albino, a being without pigment.

k.cn: 炼金术， 是将一些基本金属转变为金子， 发现万灵药及制备长生不老药的中世纪化学学科
k.en: a medieval chemical science aimed at transforming base metals into gold; a mysterious transformation

m: AL + CHEM + Y — magical CHEMistry; medieval pseudoscience trying to turn lead into gold. — alchemy, mystical chemistry.

k.syn: defense, justification, plea, reason
k.cn: 不在场的证明；托辞，借口

ex.cn: 他对于不完成作业总是有各种新奇的借口。

m: your ALIBI places you elsewhere — "I was at the bar, here's my alibi." — alibi, proof of being elsewhere / excuse.

k.syn: disaffect, disgruntle, sour
k.ant: unite, reunite, reconcile 联合，重新联合，和好
k.en: to make unfriendly, or indifferent especially where attachment formerly existed

ex.en: He has alienated most of his colleagues with his bad temper.
ex.cn: 因为他的坏脾气，很多同事都和他疏远了。

k.syn: assign, cede, deed, make over
k.en: to convey or transfer（as property or a right）usually by a specific act rather than the due course of law

ex.en: A landowner has a right to alienate his right of ownership.
ex.cn: 地主有权利变卖自己的地产。

k.syn: alien, disaffect, disgruntle, sour
k.ant: reconcile 使…重归于好
k.en: to make unfriendly, hostile, or indifferent especially where attachment formerly existed

ex.en: His heartless treatment of their mother during the divorce proceeding has completely alienated the two children.
ex.cn: 他在离婚过程中对妻子表现出来的残酷，已经彻底地让两个孩子与他疏远起来。

k.syn: assign, cede, convey, deed, make over
k.ant: expropriate 接纳他人财产
k.der: unalienable adj. 不可予夺的，不可转让的
k.en: to convey or transfer（as property or a right）usually by a specific act rather than the due course of law

ex.en: A landowner has a right to alienate his right of ownership - in other words, he can sell the land if he wants to.
ex.cn: 地主拥有转让所有权的权力—— 也就是说如果愿意，他可以出售他的土地。

m: contains ALIEN — to make someone feel alien, separate from you. — alienate, to estrange.

k.ant: askew, awry, warped 歪斜的
k.en: to bring into line or alignment

ex.en: align the cars with the curb
ex.cn: 使车与路缘对齐

k.der: aligned adj. 对准的，均衡的
k.ant: improperly adjusted, irregular 调整不当的，不规则的
k.en: to adjust to produce a proper relationship or orientation

ex.en: align the wheels of the truck
ex.cn: 调整卡车的轮子

m: contains LINE — a + LINE = put in line. — align, to arrange in a line.

k.syn: assuage, ease, mitigate, mollify, palliate, relieve, soothe, alleviate
k.ant: excite, aggravate, foment, increase the intensity of, exacerbate 激起，使加重
k.en: to subdue or reduce in intensity or severity, alleviate

ex.en: allay one's fears or doubts
ex.cn: 减轻某人的恐惧或怀疑

m: sounds like "a-LAY" — to lay down (anxieties, fears). — allay, to calm / lessen.

k.syn: commitment, dedication, piety, faithfulness, steadfastness
k.ant: inconstancy, infidelity, perfidiousness, treachery 不忠实，背叛
k.en: devotion or loyalty to a person, group, or cause

ex.en: They swore their allegiance to the USA.
ex.cn: 他们宣誓效忠美国。

m: contains ALLY — allegiance = the loyalty owed to one's ally/lord. — allegiance, loyalty.

k.syn: ease, assuage, mitigate, mollify, palliate, soothe, allay, relieve
k.ant: excite, aggravate, foment, increase the intensity of, exacerbate 激起，使加重
k.en: relieve, lessen

ex.en: alleviate pain/suffering
ex.cn: 减轻痛苦

m: shares root with ELEVATE — both mean lift up; alleviate lifts the burden. — alleviate, to lessen / relieve.

k.en: Pride and prejudice is an example of alliteration. 傲慢与偏见是押头韵运用的一个例子

ex.en: Pride and prejudice is an example of alliteration.
ex.cn: 傲慢与偏见是押头韵运用的一个例子

m: ALL + LITERATE — when ALL the words start with the same letter (Peter Piper Picked...). — alliterate, to repeat starting letters.

k.syn: imply, indicate, infer, insinuate, intimate, suggest
k.en: to convey an idea indirectly

ex.en: He also alluded to his rival's past marital troubles.
ex.cn: 他还间接提到了对手过去的婚姻问题。

m: contains LUDE (rhymes with INCLUDE) — to include indirectly = allude. — allude, to refer to indirectly.

k.syn: entice, seduce, solicit, tempt, captivate, enchant, lead on
k.der: alluring adj. 诱惑的，有吸引力的
k.ant: unattractive 无吸引力的
k.en: to attract or delight as if by magic

ex.en: an alluring smile
ex.cn: 迷人的微笑

k.syn: bait, beguile, decoy, entice, seduce, lead on, tempt, solicit
k.en: to lead away from a usual or proper course by offering some pleasure or advantage

ex.en: Allured by the promise of big bucks, he decided to have a go at a job on the trading floor of the stock market.
ex.cn: 被赚大钱的许诺所引诱，他决定投身股票市场工作。

m: contains LURE — to allure = to lure with charm. — allure, to attract / charm.

k.syn: supporter, confederate, sympathizer
k.ant: adversary, rival 对手
k.en: one in helpful association with another

ex.en: enter the war as an ally of America
ex.cn: 以美国的同盟者身份加入战争

k.ant: disband 解散
k.en: to enter into an alliance

ex.en: several tribes allied to fend off the invaders
ex.cn: 几个部落联合起来抵抗侵略者

m: ALLY contains ALL + Y — ALL stand together as Y(es) friends. — ally, a partner.

k.syn: overhead
k.ant: grounded 着陆地
k.en: in the air especially: in flight（as in an airplane）

ex.en: The balloon stayed aloft for days.
ex.cn: 气球在空中停留了很多天。

m: contains LOFT — held up high in a loft. — aloft, in the air / up high.

k.syn: detached, offish, unsociable, withdrawn, standoffish
k.ant: sociable 好社交的
k.en: removed or distant either physically or emotionally

ex.en: an aloof church
ex.cn: 一座孤零零的教堂

ex.en: stood apart with aloof dignity
ex.cn: 带着超然的尊严站在一旁

m: ALONE on the ROOF — keeping apart from everyone. — aloof, distant / standoffish.

k.en: arranged in the order

m: contains ALPHABET — arranged in alphabet order. — alphabetical, ordered by letters.

k.ant: egotistic, self-centered, self-concerned, selfish 自私的
k.en: unselfish regard for or devotion to the welfare of others

ex.en: ambition that is masked as altruism
ex.cn: 利他主义掩盖下的野心

m: contains ALTER (other, as in "alter ego") — caring about others, not self. — altruism, selfless concern for others.

k.syn: mix, fuse, intermix, compound, meld, mingle, integrate, intermingle
k.ant: separate, isolate 分开，隔离
k.en: to combine into a unified or integrated whole; unite

ex.en: amalgamate with an American company
ex.cn: 与一家美国公司合并

m: an amalgam (in chemistry) is a mercury alloy — to amalgamate = to combine into one. — amalgamate, to combine into one.

k.syn: doubtful, equivocal, unclear, uncertain
k.ant: distinct, pellucid, patent, blatant, explicit, lucid, perspicuous 清楚的，明显的
k.cn: 有多种理解方式的；不确定的
k.en: open to more than one interpretation; doubtful or uncertain

ex.en: Students have ambiguous feelings about their role in the world.
ex.cn: 学生们为他们在世界中的角色感到迷茫。frustrated by ambiguous instructions因为不明确的指示而受挫

m: AMBI- (both, as in "ambidextrous") — open to BOTH interpretations. — ambiguous, having multiple meanings.

k.syn: ramble, saunter, stroll, wander, dally, dawdle
k.ant: step quickly 快走
k.en: to walk slowly or leisurely; stroll

ex.en: Every evening, they ambled along the bank.
ex.cn: 他们每晚沿河边散步。

m: rhymes with RAMBLE — both mean leisurely movement. — amble, to walk leisurely.

k.syn: savory, aromatic, perfumed, redolent, odorous
k.ant: fetid, noisome, stenchy, malodorous, rancid 恶臭的
k.en: something extremely pleasing to taste or smell

ex.en: The ambrosial aroma of the roast stimulated our appetites.
ex.cn: 烤肉的香气刺激了我们的食欲。

m: from AMBROSIA, the food of the Greek gods (also a coconut-fruit dessert in old cookbooks). — ambrosial, deliciously fragrant / divine.

k.syn: improve, recuperate
k.ant: damper 抑制因素
k.en: to make or become better; improve

ex.en: ameliorate the suffering of people who have lost their jobs
ex.cn: 缓解失业人员的痛苦

m: "a-MEL-iorate" sounds like "a-mellow-rate" — to mellow / improve a situation. — ameliorate, to make better.

k.syn: compliant, docile, submissive, tractable, obedient
k.ant: intransigent, contumacious 不妥协的；intractable, recalcitrant, refractory 倔强的；uncontrollable, ungovernable, unruly 难管束的
k.en: readily brought to yield, submit, or cooperate

ex.en: The high-spirited and rebellious girl is not at all amenable to persuasion.
ex.cn: 这个泼辣而反叛的女孩根本不听从劝告。

m: contains AMEN — saying AMEN to whatever you suggest, agreeable. — amenable, willing to comply.

k.syn: comfort, convenience, affability
k.en: something that conduces to comfort, convenience, or enjoyment

ex.en: the amenity of the new surroundings
ex.cn: 新环境的舒适宜人

k.syn: agreeability, harmony, accord, concord, consonance
k.ant: discordance, inharmony 不和谐
k.en: the quality of being pleasant or agreeable

ex.en: a discussion conducted in perfect amenity
ex.cn: 在和谐融洽的气氛中进行的讨论

m: shares AMEN with AMENABLE — both feel pleasant; an amenity is a pleasant feature. — amenity, a comfort/feature.

k.syn: friendly, affable, amicable, genial, easy to get along with
k.ant: disagreeable, ill-natured, ill-tempered, unamiable, ungenial, ungracious, unpleasant 坏脾气的，令人不愉快的
k.cn: 好脾气的，友好易相处的
k.en: being friendly, sociable, and congenial

ex.en: an amiable teacher not easily annoyed
ex.cn: 一个不易惹恼、易相处的老师

m: contains AMI (French for "friend", as in "mon ami") — friendly. — amiable, friendly.

k.syn: friendly, affable, amiable, genial, easy to get along with
k.ant: antagonistic, hostile, unfriendly 有敌意的
k.en: characterized by friendly goodwill; amiable

ex.en: maintain amicable relations
ex.cn: 保持友好关系

ex.en: an amicable divorce
ex.cn: 和平离婚

m: contains AMI (friend) — an AMICable separation is friendly. — amicable, friendly / peaceable.

k.syn: friendship
k.ant: enmity, hostility 敌意 Man errs so long as he strives. 人只要奋斗就会犯错误。 ——德国诗人、剧作家　歌德（Johann Wolfgang Goethe, German poet and dramatist） “壮丽的诗篇要以信念作为舞台，融着几多苦乐的拼搏历程是我想要延续的抚慰和寄托。” （金宇航，Verbal 720，Quantitative 800，录取院校：哈佛大学工程与应用科学）
k.en: friendship; especially: friendly relations between nations

ex.en: live in amity with his neighbors
ex.cn: 与邻居和睦相处

ex.en: They parted in amity.
ex.cn: 他们友好地分别了。

m: contains AMI (friend) — amity = friendship. — amity, friendship between groups.

k.syn: shapeless, unformed, unshaped
k.en: having no definite form: shapeless

ex.en: an amorphous cloud mass
ex.cn: 一团无定形的云

k.syn: unclassifiable
k.en: being without definite character or nature: unclassifiable

ex.en: an amorphous segment of society
ex.cn: 不可归类的社会阶段

m: A- (without) + MORPH (shape) — without shape. — amorphous, shapeless.

k.en: an ornament or charm worn to ward off evil or bring good luck

m: AMU + LET — "amuse let" — a small charm "let to amuse" away evil. — amulet, a protective charm.

k.syn: anesthetic, anodyne
k.en: a medication that reduces or eliminates pain

ex.en: Aspirin is a kind of analgesic.
ex.cn: 阿司匹林是一种止痛剂。

m: AN- (no) + ALGESIC (pain) — no pain. — analgesic, a painkiller.

k.der: anarchy n. 混乱：a state of lawlessness or political disorder due to the absence of governmental authority
k.syn: chaos, disarray, topsy-turviness, commotion, turmoil
k.ant: order 有序
k.en: a person who rebels against any authority, established order, or ruling power 同　rebel, insurgent

m: AN- (no) + ARCH (rule, as in "monarchy") — no rules. — anarchist, one who rejects authority.

k.syn: curse, execration, imprecation, malediction
k.ant: benediction, benison, blessing 祝福
k.en: a ban or curse solemnly pronounced by ecclesiastical authority and accompanied by excommunication

m: ANA- (against) + THEMA (the placed thing) — placed against, formally cursed. — anathema, a formal curse / something detested.

k.syn: subordinate, subsidiary
k.ant: main, paramount 主要的
k.en: of secondary importance

ex.en: The company hopes to boost its sales through ancillary products.
ex.cn: 公司想通过辅助产品来增加销量。

k.syn: auxiliary, supplementary
k.en: auxiliary, supplementary

ex.en: the need for ancillary evidence
ex.cn: 对补充证据的需要

ex.en: Some practice in the deft use of words may well be ancillary to the study of natural science.
ex.cn: 某些熟练使用文字的练习对于自然科学的研究也是有辅助作用的。

m: rhymes with AUXILIARY — both mean helping/supporting. — ancillary, supplementary.

k.syn: animosity, antagonism, antipathy, hostility, rancor
k.ant: friendliness, amity, amenity 友好
k.en: a usually prejudiced and often spiteful or malevolent ill will, enmity

ex.en: She felt no animus toward those who had wronged her.
ex.cn: 她对那些冤枉了她的人没有敌意。

m: contains ANIM (mind/soul) — animus is the hostile feeling held in the mind toward someone. — animus, hostility / ill will.

k.syn: aggravate, bother, chafe, gall, grate, irk, nettle, peeve, pique, rile, ruffle, spite, vex
k.ant: soothe, defuse, allay, conciliate, propitiate, mitigate, assuage, appease, pacify, placate, calm, settle, subdue, solace, mollify 平息，抚慰
k.en: to disturb or irritate especially by repeated acts

ex.en: Mosquitoes annoy us in the summer.
ex.cn: 夏天蚊子总是不断烦扰我们。

ex.en: The sound of footsteps on the bare floor annoyed the downstairs neighbors.
ex.cn: 楼上地板的脚步声吵得楼下住户心烦。

m: an + NOY — sounds like "a NOISE" — annoying noise that bothers you. — annoy, to bother.

k.syn: disannul, cancel, invalidate, abrogate, nullify, repeal, rescind
k.ant: make legal 使合法；enact 制定
k.en: to declare or make legally invalid or void

ex.en: The marriage was annulled last month.
ex.cn: 婚约上个月取消了。

k.syn: counterbalance, neutralize
k.en: to balance with an equal force so as to make ineffective

ex.en: Unfortunately, his arrogant attitude annuls the many generous favors he does for people.
ex.cn: 不幸的是，他傲慢的态度抵消了他之前对人们的慷慨恩惠。

m: contains NULL — to make null = annul. (Marriage annulment = NULL marriage.) — annul, to make void.

k.syn: aberrant, abnormal, atypical, phenomenal, singular, uncustomary, unwonted
k.ant: unexceptional, unextraordinary 普通的，平常的
k.der: anomaly n. 反常：deviation or departure from the normal or common order, form, or rule
k.en: being out of the ordinary

ex.en: He is in an anomalous position as the only part-time teacher in XDF.
ex.cn: 他和别人不一样的是，他是XDF唯一的兼职老师。

k.ant: natural 自然的；standard 标准的
k.en: departing from some accepted standard of what is normal

ex.en: an anomalous burst of anger from this usually easygoing person
ex.cn: 这个随和的人的一反常态的暴怒

m: contains ANOMALY — anomalous = forming an anomaly. — anomalous, abnormal.

k.syn: incognito, innominate, unnamed, unidentified, untitled
k.ant: dubbed, named, termed 有名字的
k.en: not named or identified

ex.en: He made an anonymous phone call to the police.
ex.cn: 他给警察打了一个匿名电话。

ex.en: The donor wishes to remain anonymous.
ex.cn: 捐赠者希望保持匿名身份。

k.en: lacking individuality, distinction, or recognizability

ex.en: the anonymous faces in the crowd
ex.cn: 大众脸

m: AN- (no) + NYM (name, as in "synonym") — without a name. — anonymous, without a name.

k.syn: counteract, disagree
k.ant: agree, concede, grant, win over（使）同意
k.en: to act in opposition to: counteract

ex.en: He did not mean to antagonize you.
ex.cn: 他并没有要反对你。

ex.en: antagonize a bill
ex.cn: 反对一项议案

k.syn: aggravate, exasperate, gall, inflame, nettle, provoke, peeve, pique, irritate, rile, roil, chafe, grate, ruffle, vex
k.ant: soothe, defuse, allay, conciliate, propitiate, mitigate, assuage, appease, pacify, placate, calm, settle, subdue, solace, mollify 平息，抚慰
k.en: to incur or provoke the hostility of

ex.en: His remark antagonized his friends.
ex.cn: 他的评述激怒了他的朋友。

m: contains AGON (struggle, as in "agony") — to stir up struggle/hostility. — antagonize, to stir up hostility.

k.syn: aged, age-old, prehistoric, antique, immemorial
k.ant: modern, new, recent 新的
k.en: extremely old and antiquated

ex.en: He has antediluvian notions about the role of women in the workplace.
ex.cn: 他对职场女性抱有老掉牙的看法。 an antediluvian automobile 古董级的汽车

k.syn: reactionary
k.ant: modern, trendy 潮人
k.en: a person with old-fashioned ideas

ex.en: an antediluvian who thought a woman without talent is virtuous
ex.cn: 一个认为“女子无才便是德”的保守的人

m: ANTE (before) + DILUV (flood, as in "deluge") — before Noah's flood = ancient. — antediluvian, ancient / outdated.

k.syn: antecedent, foregoing, former, forward, precedent, preceding, prior
k.ant: after, ensuing, following, posterior, subsequent, succeeding 后面的，后来的
k.en: coming before in time or development

ex.en: finish the work anterior to the schedule
ex.cn: 提早完成任务

ex.en: tests anterior to the college entrance examination
ex.cn: 高考前的考试

m: ANT(e)- (before) — the front (before-side) of something. — anterior, in front / earlier.

k.en: a collection

m: "Anthony's-logy" — Anthony's curated collection of his favorite poems in one book. — anthology, a collection of literary works.

k.syn: chucklesome, comedic, comic, droll, farcical, laughable, ludicrous, hilarious
k.ant: humorless, unamusing, uncomic, unfunny, unhumorous 不好笑的； grave, serious, solemn, somber 严肃的，庄重的
k.en: characterized by clownish extravagance or absurdity

ex.en: The clown came on with many antic gestures.
ex.cn: 那个小丑上场表演了许多滑稽动作。

m: ANT + IC — picture ANTs running in funny directions = silly antics. — antic, silly playful behavior.

k.en: a remedy to counteract the effects of poison or disease; something that relieves a harmful effect

m: ANTI- (against) + DOTE — counter-poison. — antidote, a counter-remedy.

k.syn: affectlessness, emotionlessness, impassiveness, impassivity, insensibility, numbness, phlegm
k.ant: emotion, feeling, sensibility 有感情
k.en: lack of feeling or emotion

ex.en: People have shown surprising apathy toward these important social problems.
ex.cn: 人们对于这些重要的社会问题显示出了令人惊讶的冷漠。

k.syn: disinterestedness, disregard, incuriosity, insouciance, nonchalance, unconcern
k.ant: concern, interest, regard 关心，有兴趣，在意
k.en: lack of interest or concern

ex.en: She heard the story with apathy.
ex.cn: 她毫无兴趣地听完了这个故事。

ex.en: Her poor grades are proof enough of her apathy concerning all matters academic.
ex.cn: 她可怜的分数足以证明她对所有的学术问题都没兴趣。

m: A- (no) + PATHY (feeling, as in "sympathy") — no feeling. — apathy, lack of interest / feeling.

k.syn: adage, epigram, maxim, proverb
k.en: a short witty sentence which expresses a general truth or comment

ex.en: When decorating, remember the familiar aphorism,“less is more.”
ex.cn: 装修时需要记住一句格言“少即是多”。

m: an APHORISM is a memorable witty saying ("Better late than never."). — aphorism, a concise pithy saying.

k.syn: prophetic, predictive, prognostic, farsighted
k.en: of a revelatory or prophetic nature

ex.en: No one listened to her apocalyptic predictions.
ex.cn: 没有人听她的预言。

k.ant: trivial, petty, minor, immaterial, inconsequential, insignificant 不重要的
k.en: of, relating to, or being a major turning point

ex.en: The apocalyptic Battle of Stalingrad led to the ultimate defeat of Nazi Germany.
ex.cn: 具有转折点意义的斯大林格勒战役导致了最后纳粹德国的战败。

k.en: wildly unrestrained: grandiose

ex.en: apocalyptic tone
ex.cn: 夸大的语气

m: contains APOCALYPSE — relating to end-times revelations. — apocalyptic, relating to the end of the world / dramatic.

k.syn: spurious, unauthentic, ungenuine
k.ant: factual, true, truthful, authentic 真的
k.en: of doubtful authenticity: spurious

ex.en: an apocryphal story about the president's childhood
ex.cn: 一个关于总统童年的假的故事

m: shares CRYPT- with CRYPTIC (hidden) — APOCRYPHAL stories were the "hidden" books of doubtful origin. — apocryphal, of doubtful authenticity.

k.cn: 表面否认要说或正说的话（通过声称“不再提某事”而将此事说出）

m: "I oppose saying it… but I just said it." apo- (oppose) + phasis (saying). — apophasis, mentioning by claiming not to mention.

k.syn: choleric, enraged, furious, incensed, indignant, infuriated, irate, ireful, outraged
k.ant: angerless, delighted, pleased 不生气的，高兴的
k.en: extremely angry; furious

ex.en: He became apoplectic about wasteful government spending.
ex.cn: 他对政府的浪费开销感到怒不可遏。 The coach was so apoplectic when the player missed the free throw that he threw his clipboard onto the court. 教练对球员罚篮不进非常恼怒，把战术板扔到了球场上。

m: contains POP — so furious you could POP a vein. — apoplectic, extremely furious.

k.en: renunciation of a religious faith

ex.en: Some people completely abandon the faith after apostasy.
ex.cn: 有些人在放弃自己的宗教信仰后就再也不信仰任何其他宗教了。

k.syn: defection, perfidy, treacherousness, recreancy
k.ant: fidelity, allegiance, loyalty, piety 忠诚，虔诚
k.en: abandonment of a previous loyalty: defection

ex.en: He was looked down upon for apostasy.
ex.cn: 他因为背叛而受到鄙视。

m: APO- (away) + STASY (standing, as in "stasis") — standing away from your former faith. — apostasy, abandonment of faith.

k.en: the superscript sign ( ' ) used to indicate

m: in literature, an APOSTROPHE is a direct address — "O Death, where is thy sting?" (Same word as the punctuation mark.) — apostrophe, direct address / the ' mark.

k.en: one who prepares and sells drugs or compounds for medicinal

m: an APOTHECARY is the old-fashioned pharmacist with rows of POTs and jars of remedies. — apothecary, an old-fashioned pharmacist.

k.syn: dismay, terrify, intimidate, frighten, horrify, daunt, deter
k.ant: embolden, encourage, nerve 使大胆，鼓起勇气
k.en: to overcome with consternation, shock, or dismay

ex.en: He felt appalled by the whole idea of marriage so we broke up.
ex.cn: 结婚使他恐惧，因此我们分手了。

m: A + PALL — to grow PALE in horror, like a pall (death-shroud) cast over you. — appall, to horrify.

k.en: something that clothes

m: contains APPEAR — what you APPEAR in is your APPAREL. — apparel, clothing.

k.syn: adjuration, conjuration, entreaty, petition, pleading, supplication
k.en: an application（as to a recognized authority）for corroboration, vindication, or decision

ex.en: a piteous appeal for help
ex.cn: 哀怜地请求帮助

ex.en: make an appeal to the public to donate needed blood
ex.cn: 向公众提出献血请求

k.syn: charge, accuse, incriminate, inculpate, indict
k.ant: absolve 赦免；exonerate, exculpate 开脱罪责；vindicate 辩护
k.en: to charge with a crime: accuse

ex.en: My lawyer said the court's decision wasn't correct and that we should file for an appeal.
ex.cn: 我的律师说，法庭的判决不对，我们应该起诉。

m: an APPEAL — either a court appeal or romantic appeal. PEAL the bell to call for it. — appeal, formal request / attractiveness.

k.en: an identifying name or title :

m: wine term: APPELLATION Bordeaux = the wine's official name/designation. — appellation, a name / title.

k.syn: acclamation, cheer, cheering, ovation, plaudit, rave
k.ant: hissing, booing 发出嘘声
k.en: approval publicly expressed（as by clapping the hands）

ex.en: Her appearance was greeted with applause.
ex.cn: 她的出现被报以热烈的掌声。

m: shares PLAU- with PLAUSIBLE / APPLAUD — APPLAUSE is the sound of clapping APPROVAL. — applause, clapping in approval.

k.syn: applicable, apropos, germane, pointed, relative, relevant
k.ant: extraneous, irrelevant, impertinent, irrelative, pointless 无关的
k.en: highly pertinent or appropriate: apt

ex.en: enrich his essay with some very apposite quotations from famous people
ex.cn: 用名人名言来丰富他的文章

m: contains POSIT (place) — placed near and fitting. (Don't confuse with "opposite".) — apposite, fitting / appropriate.

k.syn: apprehensible, perceptible, detectable, discernible, palpable, distinguishable, sensible
k.ant: impalpable, imperceptible, inappreciable, indistinguishable, insensible, undetectable 不能感知的
k.en: capable of being perceived or measured; perceptible

ex.en: the appreciable changes in temperature
ex.cn: 气温明显的变化

ex.en: There doesn't seem to be any appreciable difference between this piece and that one.
ex.cn: 这个和那个看上去没什么差别。

m: contains APPRECIATE — what you can appreciate (notice and value). — appreciable, noticeable / significant.

k.syn: dread, foreboding, misgiving, anxiousness, unease, uneasiness, worry
k.ant: composure, equanimity 镇定；unconcern 冷漠
k.en: suspicion or fear especially of future evil

ex.en: She had a strong apprehension about her sister's health.
ex.cn: 她非常担心她姐姐的健康。

k.syn: arrest
k.ant: discharge 释放
k.en: seizure by legal process: arrestment

ex.en: apprehension of the thief
ex.cn: 逮捕小偷

k.syn: understanding, comprehension
k.ant: incomprehension
k.en: the knowledge gained from the process of coming to know or understand something

ex.en: a good apprehension of how computer systems work
ex.cn: 对计算机是如何工作的有很好的理解

m: contains APPREHEND (to seize) — apprehension means seized in the mind: fear, understanding, or arrest. — apprehension, fear / understanding / arrest.

k.syn: inform, acquaint, make known to
k.ant: withhold information 隐瞒信息
k.en: to give notice to; inform

ex.en: apprise him of the danger that may be involved
ex.cn: 告知他可能涉及的危险

m: to APPRISE someone = give them the PRIZE of information. (Don't confuse with APPRAISE = evaluate.) — apprise, to inform.

k.syn: approval, favor
k.ant: disapproval, disapprobation, disfavor 不同意
k.en: an expression of warm approval

ex.en: The proposal met his approbation.
ex.cn: 这项建议得到了他的同意。

m: contains APPROVE — approbation = approval. — approbation, approval.

k.syn: purloin, pirate, embezzle, peculate, usurp
k.en: to take possession of or make use of exclusively for oneself, often without permission

ex.en: appropriate private property
ex.cn: 盗用私人财产

ex.en: The economy has been weakened by corrupt officials who have appropriated the country's resources for their own use.
ex.cn: 经济因为腐败的官员们私自挪用国家资源而被削弱了。

k.syn: apt, becoming, felicitous, fitting, proper, meet, suitable, apposite, apropos
k.ant: improper, inapposite, inappropriate, inapt, unmeet, unseemly, unsuitable 不合适的
k.en: especially suitable or compatible: fitting

ex.en: Red wine is a more appropriate choice with the meal.
ex.cn: 红酒更适合这顿饭。

ex.en: I don't think jeans and a T-shirt are appropriate attire for a wedding.
ex.cn: 我觉得穿牛仔裤和T恤衫去参加婚礼不太得体。

m: contains PROPER — "appropriate" = made proper for the situation; or to take for one's own use. — appropriate, suitable; or to take for oneself.

k.en: a garment usually of cloth, plastic, or leather usually tied around the waist and used

m: A + PRON ≈ "a PROtection" — an apron PROtects your clothes. — apron, kitchen garment.

k.syn: applicable, apposite, germane, pointed, relative, relevant
k.ant: extraneous, irrelevant, impertinent, irrelative, pointless 无关的
k.en: being both relevant and opportune

ex.en: The actor announced to reporters that he would only answer to apropos questions about the movie.
ex.cn: 演员向记者表示，他只回答和电影有关的问题。

k.syn: apropos, apropos of, as far as, as for, as regards（also as respects）, as to, concerning, regarding, respecting, touching, toward（or towards）
k.en: having to do with

ex.en: make a number of telling observations apropos the current political situation
ex.cn: 做了很多关于当前政治形势有力的观察

m: from French "à propos" (to the purpose) — relevant, on-topic. — apropos, fitting / on-topic.

k.syn: apropos, germane, relative, relevant, appropriate
k.ant: extraneous, irrelevant, impertinent, irrelative 无关的；inappropriate, improper, unseemly 不恰当的，不合时宜的
k.en: exactly suitable; appropriate

ex.en: apt remark/choice/description
ex.cn: 恰当的评述/选择/描述

k.syn: likely, inclined, tending, given
k.ant: unlikely 不可能的；disinclined 没有…倾向的
k.en: having a tendency: likely

ex.en: He is apt to fly out in rage.
ex.cn: 他容易发脾气。

k.syn: brilliant, clever, quick-witted, ready-witted, smart
k.ant: foolish, dull, dumb, stupid, unintelligent 傻的，愚蠢的
k.en: keenly intelligent and responsive

ex.en: an apt pupil
ex.cn: 一个聪明的小学生

ex.en: He is apt at mathematics.
ex.cn: 他擅长数学。

m: APT contains the same letters as PAT — a PAT answer is APT for the situation. — apt, suitable / quick to learn.

k.en: consisting of, relating to, or being in water

m: contains AQUA (water) — aqua-tic = water-related. — aquatic, water-related.

k.en: a shelter of vines or branches or of latticework covered with climbing shrubs or vines

m: rhymes with HARBOR — both are sheltered spots; an ARBOR is a leafy shelter. — arbor, a shaded garden shelter.

k.en: of, relating to, or resembling a tree; living in or among trees

m: contains ARBOR (tree) — arboreal = tree-related, living in trees. — arboreal, of / in trees.

k.syn: antiquated, outdated, outmoded, prehistoric, superannuated
k.ant: modern diction 新语
k.der: archaism n. 古语：the use of archaic diction or style
k.en: no longer current or applicable; antiquated

ex.en: archaic laws
ex.cn: 过时的法律

m: contains ARCH- (ancient, as in "archaeology") — very old. — archaic, ancient / outdated.

k.syn: ideal, a perfect example, quintessence
k.en: an ideal example of a type

ex.en: an archetype of the successful entrepreneur
ex.cn: 成功企业家的典范

k.syn: antecedent, foregoer, forerunner, precursor, predecessor
k.en: something belonging to an earlier time from which something else was later developed

ex.en: The abacus is sometimes cited as the archetype of the modern digital calculator.
ex.cn: 算盘是现代计算器的鼻祖。

k.syn: prototype
k.en: something from which copies are made

ex.en: “‘Frankenstein’...‘Dracula’...‘Dr. Jekyll and Mr. Hyde’... the archetypes that have influenced all subsequent horror stories”
ex.cn: （New York Times）“‘弗兰肯斯坦’…‘德拉库拉’…‘杰基尔博士和海德先生’是影响了所有继之而来的恐怖故事的原型”（《纽约时报》）

m: ARCH- (chief) + TYPE — the chief example, the original mold. — archetype, the original model.

k.en: a group of islands

m: the GREEK ARCHIPELAGO is the famous cluster of Greek islands in the Aegean. — archipelago, a cluster of islands.

k.en: a place in which public records or historical documents are preserved

ex.en: old land deeds in the municipal archive
ex.cn: 在市政档案室存放的旧土地契约

m: ARCH- (old) — an archive holds old records. — archive, a place storing old records.

k.syn: avidity, zeal, fervor, fervency, fervidness, passion, passionateness, vehemence
k.ant: apathy, torpor, impassivity, insensitivity 冷漠，麻木
k.en: strong enthusiasm or devotion; zeal

ex.en: His ardor was damped.
ex.cn: 他的热情被泼了冷水。

m: rhymes with HARDER — ARDOR works HARDER, with passionate intensity. — ardor, intense passion / zeal.

k.syn: grueling, laborious, taxing, onerous, burdensome
k.ant: easy, simple, unchallenging, undemanding, facile, effortless 不费劲的
k.en: hard to accomplish or achieve: difficult

ex.en: a long and arduous undertaking
ex.cn: 一项长期艰苦的任务

ex.en: an arduous journey across miles of desert
ex.cn: 艰苦的沙漠之旅

m: rhymes with ARDOR — both share intensity; arduous = requiring intense effort. — arduous, difficult / strenuous.

k.syn: cant, jargon, shoptalk
k.ant: standard language 标准的语言；common verbalism 大众的言语
k.en: a specialized vocabulary or set of idioms used by a particular group

ex.en: groups communicating in their own secret argots
ex.cn: 用秘密的行话交流的团体

m: ARGOT is the slang/jargon ("argot" itself sounds like "argo-jargon") used by a specific group — thieves' argot, hackers' argot. — argot, specialized slang.

k.syn: check, rein, curb, contain, hamper, thwart, bring to a halt
k.ant: vitalize, activate, animate, invigorate 使有生气；prod, goad, spur, galvanize, provoke, stimulate 刺激
k.en: to make inactive

ex.en: arrest the growth of the tumor
ex.cn: 抑制肿瘤生长

k.syn: cease, cessation, closure, conclusion, discontinuance, discontinuation, ending, halt, stop, termination; check, stop, cease, close, conclude, discontinue, end, halt, terminate
k.ant: continuance, continuation 继续
k.en: the stopping of a process or activity; to bring to a standstill

ex.en: Science cannot yet arrest the process of aging.
ex.cn: 科学不能阻止衰老。

k.syn: apprehend
k.ant: discharge 释放
k.en: to take or keep under one's control by authority of law

ex.en: She was charged with resisting arrest.
ex.cn: 她因为拒捕受到起诉。

k.syn: enchant, fascinate, bedazzle, grip, hypnotize, mesmerize
k.en: to hold the attention of as if by a spell

ex.en: The behavior of the daredevil arrested pedestrians.
ex.cn: 冒失鬼的行为吸引了行人们的注意力。

m: shares REST with REST — to ARREST = to bring to a forced REST (stop). — arrest, to stop / hold.

k.syn: absorbing, engaging, engrossing, enthralling, fascinating, gripping, immersing, intriguing, riveting
k.ant: boring, drab, tedious, monotonous, uninteresting 乏味的，单调的
k.en: attracting and holding the attention; striking

ex.en: an arresting spectacle
ex.cn: 吸引人的奇观

m: from ARREST — an arresting view stops you in your tracks. — arresting, attention-grabbing.

k.syn: irregular, disorderly
k.ant: regular, orderly 规律的
k.en: lacking rhythm or regularity

ex.en: arrhythmic pulse
ex.cn: 不规律的脉搏

m: A- (without) + RHYTHM — without rhythm. — arrhythmic, irregular.

k.syn: assumption, bumptiousness, haughtiness, hauteur, imperiousness, loftiness, peremptoriness, pomposity, pompousness, presumptuousness, superciliousness
k.ant: humility, modesty, humbleness, unassumingness 谦虚，谦逊
k.en: overbearing pride

ex.en: Her arrogance has earned her a lot of enemies.
ex.cn: 她的傲慢给她带来了很多敌人。

m: rhymes with ARROW + GANCE — an ARROW shot down at others = arrogant haughtiness. — arrogance, haughty pride.

k.syn: enunciate
k.ant: murmur, mumble, mutter, slur 含糊地说
k.en: to utter clearly and distinctly

ex.en: He cannot articulate his thoughts.
ex.cn: 他不能清楚地表达他的想法。

ex.en: a theory first articulated by ancient philosophers
ex.cn: 被古时哲学家阐明的理论

k.syn: eloquent, fluent, silver-tongued, well-spoken
k.ant: inarticulate, ineloquent, unvocal 表达不清楚的
k.en: able to express oneself clearly and well

ex.en: The television crew covering the science fair were looking for photogenic and articulate students to explain their projects on the air.
ex.cn: 负责报道科学展览的电视工作人员正在寻找上镜的、表达清晰的学生，请他们在节目中解释其（科学）项目。

m: contains ARTIC- (joint, as in "articulated bus") — articulate speech has well-jointed clear parts. — articulate, clearly expressed.

k.cn: 古人制作的有历史意义的物品
k.en: an object produced or shaped by human craft, especially a tool, weapon

ex.en: a museum with rare artifacts
ex.cn: 存有稀有手工制品的博物馆

m: ART + I + FACT — a man-made (art-made) object that is now a historical fact. — artifact, a historical man-made object.

k.syn: superior, dominant, sovereign
k.ant: having no influence/power 没有影响力的
k.en: dominant in position or influence; superior

ex.en: This idea was in the ascendant.
ex.cn: 这种思想处于主导地位。

k.ant: declining 下降的
k.en: moving upward: rising

ex.en: The teacher told the students to write even numbers in ascendant order.
ex.cn: 老师让学生们按升序写出偶数。

m: contains ASCEND — rising, on the rise = dominant. — ascendant, rising / dominant.

k.syn: abstemious, abstinent, self-denying
k.ant: sumptuous, luxurious 奢侈的；licentious, sybaritic, voluptuous 放荡的，沉溺于酒色的
k.en: practicing strict self-denial as a measure of personal and especially spiritual discipline

ex.en: This is an ascetic diet of rice and beans.
ex.cn: 这是一顿苦行餐，只有米饭和豌豆。

k.syn: stoic, spartan
k.ant: sybarite, hedonist, voluptuary 奢侈逸乐的人，享乐主义者，酒色之徒
k.en: a person who renounces material comforts and leads a life of austere self-discipline, especially as an act of religious devotion

ex.en: We normally see an ascetic meditating in a Yogic pose.
ex.cn: 我们通常会看见一个苦行者以瑜伽的姿势来冥想。

m: an ASCETIC monk lives a stripped-down life of discipline (don't confuse with "aesthetic"). — ascetic, severely self-disciplined.

k.syn: sterile, germfree
k.ant: germy, unsterile 有细菌的；contaminated, tainted 被污染的
k.en: preventing infection

ex.en: Surgery must be in aseptic environments.
ex.cn: 手术必须在无菌环境下进行。

k.syn: inactive, lackadaisical, languid, listless, spiritless
k.ant: active, animated, energetic, vivacious 有活力的
k.en: lacking vitality

k.syn: affectless, emotionless, impassive, insensible, numb, phlegm
k.ant: emotional, feeling, sensible 有感情的
k.en: lacking emotion, or warmth

ex.en: aseptic essays
ex.cn: 没有感情的文章

ex.en: an aseptic smile
ex.cn: 漠然的微笑

m: A- (without) + SEPTIC (infected) — without infection, sterile; or coldly clinical/lifeless. — aseptic, sterile / coldly clinical.

k.syn: crooked, cockeyed, oblique, lopsided, skewed, aslant, slanted, slanting, listing, tilted, awry
k.ant: aligned 列成一行的；straight, erect 直的
k.en: out of line, awry

ex.en: The picture hung askew.
ex.cn: 画挂歪了。

ex.en: His tie was askew.
ex.cn: 他的领带歪了。

m: A + SKEW — skewed, crooked, off-center. — askew, crooked.

k.syn: appearance, look, figure, presence, mien
k.en: appearance to the eye or mind

ex.en: His face had a frightening aspect.
ex.cn: 他的脸很吓人。

m: shares SPECT with SPECTACLES / INSPECT — what your SPECTACLES see, the visual aspect. — aspect, appearance / facet.

k.syn: roughness, crudity, rudeness, poignancy, harshness
k.ant: softness, mildness 脾气好
k.en: roughness of manner or of temper

ex.en: She responded with such asperity that we knew she was offended by the question.
ex.cn: 她如此粗鲁地回答问题，我们知道这个问题冒犯了她。

k.syn: severity, hardness, hardship, rigor
k.en: rigor, severity

ex.en: He has encountered more than his share of asperities on the road to success.
ex.cn: 在成功的道路上，他已经遇到过比他应该承受的更多的艰辛。

m: rhymes with PROSPERITY but the opposite mood — ASPERITY is a sharp/harsh manner. — asperity, harshness.

k.syn: defamation, besmirchment, calumny, calumniation, maligning, obloquy, vilification
k.ant: glowing tribute, eulogy, extolling, laudation, praise, commendation, compliment 热情赞赏，表扬；flattery, adulation 拍马屁
k.en: a false or misleading charge meant to harm someone's reputation

ex.en: cast aspersions on my loyalty
ex.cn: 诽谤我的忠诚

m: to cast ASPERSIONS = to splash dirt on someone's reputation. — aspersion, slander / damaging remark.

k.syn: seeker
k.ant: noncandidate 非候选人
k.en: one who aspires, as to advancement, honors, or a high position

ex.en: a bevy of ever-smiling aspirants for the Miss America title
ex.cn: 一群想当美国小姐的少女

ex.en: Envy can make oneself backward; self-confidence can tell oneself to be an aspirant.
ex.cn: 妒忌能使自己落后，自信能使自己上进。

k.en: seeking to attain a desired position or status

ex.en: The pilot was an aspirant astronaut.
ex.cn: 这个飞行员想成为航天员。

m: contains ASPIRE — an aspirant is one who aspires. — aspirant, an ambitious seeker.

k.syn: accede, agree, consent, subscribe, come round
k.ant: dissent, disagree 不同意
k.en: to agree to something especially after thoughtful consideration

ex.en: The general proposed a detailed plan and the president assented.
ex.cn: 将军提出了一个细节化的计划，总统同意了该计划。

m: contains SENT — you SENT your assent (your "yes") to the proposal. — assent, agreement.

k.syn: aver, allege, avow, avouch, insist
k.ant: deny, gainsay 否认
k.en: to state or declare positively and often forcefully or aggressively

ex.en: assert one's innocence
ex.cn: 坚称自己无罪

ex.en: He asserted that there were corrupt officials in the government.
ex.cn: 他断言政府里面一定存在腐败的官员。

m: rhymes with EXERT — to ASSERT yourself = to EXERT your voice firmly. — assert, to declare firmly.

k.syn: assertory, self-assured, self-assertive, peremptory
k.ant: diffident 不自信的
k.en: inclined to bold or confident assertion; aggressively self-assured

ex.en: You will have to make many personal judgments when being assertive.
ex.cn: 如果你要表现得坚定自信（坚持自己的立场和主张），就必须做出很多个人判断。

m: from ASSERT — an assertive person firmly states their position. — assertive, confidently forceful.

k.syn: evaluate, appraise, guesstimate
k.cn: 评估（重要性、尺寸、价值等）
k.en: to determine the importance, size, or value of

ex.en: We need to assess whether or not the system is working.
ex.cn: 我们需要评估这个系统是否有效。

ex.en: Damage to the boat was assessed at $5,000.
ex.cn: 对这艘船的损失的评估是5,000美元。

k.syn: assess, charge, exact, fine, lay, levy, put
k.ant: remit 免除（债务）
k.en: to establish or apply as a charge or penalty

ex.en: The utility company will assess a fee if your payment is late.
ex.cn: 如果你付款晚了，公共事业公司会罚款。

m: shares SES with SESSION — in a SESSION, an ASSESSor evaluates. Also "add SES (cents)" — you assess value in cents. — assess, to evaluate.

k.syn: diligent, industrious, sedulous
k.ant: idle, inactive, unbusy, unemployed, unoccupied 空闲的
k.der: assiduity n. 勤勉：persistent application or diligence; unflagging effort
k.en: marked by careful unremitting attention or persistent application; busy

ex.en: He always tended his garden with assiduous attention.
ex.cn: 他总是专心仔细地照看他的花园。

ex.en: The project required some assiduous planning.
ex.cn: 该项目需要细心的规划。

m: sounds like "a-SID-uous" → like "a STUDIOUS" person at work — diligent and careful. — assiduous, diligent.

k.ant: aggravate, exacerbate 加强，使恶化
k.en: to lessen the intensity of

ex.en: He couldn't assuage his guilt over the divorce.
ex.cn: 他无法减轻自己在离婚中的内疚。

k.syn: compose, soothe, calm, pacify, placate, appease, lighten, relieve, alleviate, assuage, allay, mitigate, moderate, conciliate, propitiate
k.ant: aggravate, annoy, enrage, exasperate, incense, infuriate, ire, irk, irritate, madden, nettle, peeve, provoke, rile, roil, vex 煽动，激怒
k.en: to pacify or calm

ex.en: Life contains sorrows that cannot be assuaged, and it is important to be honest in acknowledging this.
ex.cn: 生活中存在着无法平息的悲伤，诚实地承认这个事实是重要的。

k.syn: satisfy, sate, satiate, quench
k.en: to put an end to by satisfying

ex.en: assuage his thirst
ex.cn: 解渴

ex.en: That meal certainly assuaged my hunger.
ex.cn: 那顿大餐满足了我的口腹之欲。

m: rhymes with PERSUADE — to assuage = to gently persuade pain or hunger to lessen. — assuage, to soothe / lessen.

k.en: a substance that draws together or constricts

m: an ASTRINGENT toner makes your skin tighten/pucker — sharp and contracting. — astringent, sharp / contracting / harsh.

k.en: the divination of the supposed influences of the stars

m: ASTRO (stars) + LOGY (study) — study of stars (for fortunes). — astrology, divination by stars.

k.syn: canny, smart, shrewd, perspicacious, clear-eyed, clear-sighted, savvy, hardheaded
k.ant: unknowing 无知的
k.en: having or showing shrewdness and perspicacity

ex.en: Astute salesmen know how to invest emotionally.
ex.cn: 精明的推销员知道如何进行感情投资。

m: rhymes with ACUTE — an ASTUTE observer has acute (sharp) perception. — astute, shrewdly perceptive.

k.syn: piecemeal
k.ant: in a piece 完整一片；together 在一起
k.cn: 分离成多部分或多片地
k.en: into separate parts or pieces

ex.en: broken asunder
ex.cn: 打碎

ex.en: Buildings were burst asunder.
ex.cn: 建筑物已经被炸成碎片了。

k.syn: apart
k.ant: together 在一起
k.en: apart from each other in position

ex.en: A quiet conscience sleeps in thunder, but rest and guilt live far asunder.
ex.cn: 平静的良心能在雷声中入睡，而安宁和负罪则无法毗邻。

ex.en: Our opinions are wide as the poles asunder.
ex.cn: 我们的意见完全相反。

m: A + SUNDER — to SUNDER is to split apart; ASUNDER = into separate parts. — asunder, into separate parts.

k.syn: harbor, haven, refuge, sanctuary, sanctum
k.en: an inviolable place of refuge and protection giving shelter to criminals and debtors/something（as a building） that offers cover from the weather or protection from danger

ex.en: an insane asylum
ex.cn: 疯人院

ex.en: The embassy serves as an asylum for that country's nationals in need of help.
ex.cn: 大使馆作为那国需要帮助的公民的保护所。

m: A- (without) + SYLUM (rhymes with "asylum" — sanctuary). Cleaner: ASYLUM contains AS + Y(es) + LUM(p) — a refuge where you can rest as a YES-lump in safety. (Better:) shared root with old Greek "a-sylos" = un-seizable, untouchable refuge. — asylum, a refuge.

k.syn: asymmetric, nonsymmetrical, unsymmetrical, unbalanced
k.ant: symmetrical 对称的；balanced 平衡的
k.en: having no balance or symmetry

ex.en: the asymmetrical construction
ex.cn: 非对称的建筑

m: A- (not) + SYMMETRICAL — not symmetric. — asymmetrical, unbalanced.

k.syn: redeem
k.en: to make amends, as for a sin or fault

ex.en: Blood must atone for blood.
ex.cn: 以命抵命。

ex.en: Even death cannot atone for the offence.
ex.cn: 罪不容诛。

m: AT + ONE — to atone = to be "AT ONE" with the wronged party again, making amends. — atone, to make amends.

k.syn: heinous
k.ant: benign, kind, kindhearted, benignant, good-hearted, humane, sympathetic, tenderhearted 善良的，好的
k.en: extremely wicked, brutal, or cruel

ex.en: Murder is an atrocious crime.
ex.cn: 犯罪是一种极其恶劣的犯罪活动。

k.ant: satisfactory 令人满意的；wonderful 精彩的，极好的
k.en: extremely unsatisfactory

ex.en: The service on plane was atrocious.
ex.cn: 飞机上的服务让人极为不满意。

m: shares ATROC- with ATROCITY — ATROCIOUS = full of atrocity. — atrocious, horrifyingly bad.

k.syn: cheapen, devalue, downgrade, reduce, write down
k.ant: appreciate, enhance, upgrade, mark up 升值，提升
k.en: to lessen the amount, force, magnitude, or value of

ex.en: an investment attenuated by inflation
ex.cn: 一项因通货膨胀而贬值的投资

m: contains TENU- (thin, as in "tenuous") — to thin out, weaken. — attenuate, to lessen / weaken.

k.syn: bold, adventurous, brash, brassy, brazen, rash, reckless
k.ant: cautious, circumspect, guarded, wary 小心谨慎的
k.en: fearlessly, often recklessly daring; bold

ex.en: an audacious plan
ex.cn: 一个冒进的计划

k.syn: impertinent, impudent, insolent
k.ant: timid, meek 温顺的
k.cn: 蔑视（法律、礼节）的，无礼的
k.en: contemptuous of law, religion, or decorum

k.syn: daring, emboldened, enterprising, nervy, venturesome
k.en: inclined or willing to take risks

ex.en: audacious adventurers risking everything they had for a shot at glory
ex.cn: 为了荣誉而孤注一掷的冒险家们

k.en: marked by originality and verve

ex.en: an audacious interpretation of two dramas
ex.cn: 对两部戏剧的创造性阐述

m: contains AUDA- (bold) — also shares root with AUDIBLE (loud) — boldly loud. — audacious, daringly bold.

k.cn: 试镜，对演艺者资质的测验
k.en: a trial performance to appraise

m: contains AUDIT(ion) — performers AUDITION while the panel listens (audi- = hear). — audition, a trial performance.

k.syn: forecaster, foreseer, diviner, foreteller, prophesier, visionary
k.en: one who predicts future events or developments

ex.en: The ancient Roman augurs predicted the future by reading the flight of birds.
ex.cn: 古罗马的预言家们通过鸟类的航线来预测未来。

k.syn: promise
k.en: to show signs of a favorable or successful outcome

ex.en: This augurs well for us.
ex.cn: 这是一个好兆头。

k.syn: forecast, predict, presage, prognosticate, prophesy, forebode
k.en: to tell of or describe beforehand

ex.en: The fortune-teller augured nothing but a series of calamities for me.
ex.cn: 占卜人预言在我身上将会有一系列大灾难发生。

m: an AUGUR was an ancient Roman priest who read omens. "This AUGURs well" = predicts good. — augur, to predict / a soothsayer.

k.syn: bona fide, certified, genuine
k.ant: bogus, counterfeit, fake, mock, phony, spurious 伪造的
k.en: being exactly as appears or as claimed

ex.en: found an authentic Native American arrowhead
ex.cn: 发现了一支美洲土著的箭头

k.syn: accurate, exact, precise, right, veracious
k.ant: corrupt, false 错误的
k.en: following an original exactly

ex.en: an authentic reconstruction of the Parthenon
ex.cn: 帕台农神殿的准确复制

m: the AUTHENTIC version is the real one (authenticated, verified). — authentic, real / genuine.

k.syn: expert, connoisseur, maestro, master, virtuoso
k.ant: amateur 业余爱好者；inexpert 非专家
k.en: a person with a high level of knowledge or skill in a field

ex.en: a leading authority on neural anatomy
ex.cn: 一名神经解剖学领域的权威人士

k.syn: administration, governance, regime, regimen
k.en: lawful control over the affairs of a political unit（as a nation）

ex.en: The sheriff had authority over the whole county.
ex.cn: 郡守对于整个郡都有管辖权。

k.syn: clout, credit, leverage, weight
k.en: the power to direct the thinking or behavior of others usually indirectly

ex.en: He speaks with a persuasive authority on matters of public health.
ex.cn: 他就公共健康事务发表了具有影响力的、令人信服的评论。

k.syn: arm, command, control, dominion, reign
k.ant: impotence, powerlessness 无权力
k.en: the right or means to command or control others

ex.en: By the authority vested in me, I now pronounce you married.
ex.cn: 依我所拥有的权力，我宣布你们成为夫妻。

k.syn: grounds, justification
k.en: a warrant for action

ex.en: On what authority do you make such a claim?
ex.cn: 你的指控有何理由？

m: an AUTHORITY is the AUTHOR (creator/source) of rules or expertise. — authority, official power / expert.

k.syn: absolutism, dictatorship, totalitarianism, tyranny
k.ant: democracy 民主，民主制度
k.en: government in which a person possesses unlimited power

ex.en: It signified the British rejection of autocracy by constituting the first formal restraining of the power of the monarch.
ex.cn: 英国对于独裁体制的反抗表现在它对君王权力进行的正式约束。

m: AUTO (self) + CRACY (rule) — rule by oneself, dictatorship. — autocracy, dictatorship.

k.syn: self-governance, sovereignty
k.ant: dependence, subjection 附属，依赖
k.en: the quality or state of being self-governing

ex.en: The province has been granted autonomy.
ex.cn: 这个省份被授予了自治权。
