k.syn: independence, liberty, free will
k.ant: constraint, duress 限制
k.cn: 自我主导的自由，（尤其是）精神独立
k.en: self-directing freedom and especially moral independence

ex.en: a teacher who encourages individual autonomy
ex.cn: 一个鼓励精神独立的老师

m: AUTO (self) + NOMY (rule) — self-rule, independence. — autonomy, self-governance / independence.

k.syn: acquisitiveness, avidity, covetousness, cupidity, greediness, rapacity
k.ant: generosity, magnanimity 慷慨
k.en: excessive or insatiable desire for wealth or gain

ex.en: The bank official's embezzlement was motivated by pure avarice.
ex.cn: 银行官员盗用公款纯粹是出于贪婪。

m: shares AVAR with AVARICE / AVARICIOUS pronunciation "a-VAR-iss" — picture A VAR(ied) treasure pile being hoarded. Better: rhymes with HARRIS — Scrooge HARRIS hoards his coins. — avarice, greed for wealth.

k.syn: allege, assert, avouch, avow, declare, purport, warrant
k.ant: deny, gainsay 否认
k.en: to state as a fact usually forcefully

ex.en: was tearfully averring his innocence
ex.cn: 涕泪俱下地声明他的清白

k.syn: affirm, insist, maintain, profess
k.en: to state clearly and strongly

ex.en: She averred that she didn't need any help choosing her own clothes.
ex.cn: 她态度强硬地声明：在买衣服的问题上不需要别人指手画脚。

m: rhymes with EVER — to AVER is to declare it's so, EVER and always. — aver, to declare firmly.

k.syn: distaste, horror, loathing, nausea, repugnance, repulsion, revulsion
k.en: a dislike so strong as to cause stomach upset or queasiness

ex.en: a natural aversion toward insects
ex.cn: 天生对昆虫的反感

k.syn: abhorrence, abomination, antipathy, detestation, execration
k.ant: love 令人喜爱的事物
k.en: something or someone that is hated

ex.en: Clichés should be the aversion of every good writer.
ex.cn: 陈词滥调应该是让每个优秀的作家都感到厌恶的。

m: AVERS- (turn away) — to turn away from = strong dislike. — aversion, intense dislike.

k.syn: agog, ardent, eager, keen, impatient, thirsty
k.ant: apathetic, indifferent 不在乎的
k.en: marked by keen interest and enthusiasm

ex.en: avid movie fans
ex.cn: 急切的影迷

k.syn: acquisitive, avaricious, covetous, greedy, rapacious
k.en: having or marked by an eager and often selfish desire especially for material possessions

ex.en: She stared at the jewels with an avid glint in her eye.
ex.cn: 她带着贪婪的目光注视着那些珠宝。

m: rhymes with VIVID — an AVID fan has VIVID enthusiasm. — avid, eagerly enthusiastic.

k.en: to separate

m: rhymes with CONVULSE — both involve violent pulling. AVULSE = to tear/pull off. — avulse, to tear off.

k.syn: doused, drenched, saturated, sodden, soggy, soaked
k.ant: dry, arid, dehydrated, drained 干燥的
k.en: containing, covered with, or thoroughly penetrated by water

ex.en: The streets were awash from the heavy rains.
ex.cn: 街道因为大雨被水淹没了。

k.syn: abounding, abundant, flush, fraught, replete, swarming, teeming, thronging
k.ant: dearth, insufficient, scant 匮乏的
k.en: filled, covered, or completely overrun as if by a flood

ex.en: The program is currently awash in submissions and will not be accepting any more until next term.
ex.cn: 该项目的申请人数过多，因此下一轮之前不会再接受任何申请。

m: A + WASH — washed over, flooded. — awash, flooded / overflowing with.

k.syn: admiration, reverence, respect, veneration; admire, revere, respect, venerate
k.ant: irreverence, insolence, scorn, superciliousness 蔑视，不敬
k.en: an emotion variously combining dread, veneration, and wonder that is inspired by authority or by the sacred or sublime

ex.en: She gazed in awe at the great stone.
ex.cn: 她敬畏地凝视着那块巨石。

m: AWE — that breath-taken feeling at the Grand Canyon. — awe, reverent wonder.

k.syn: handless, maladroit
k.ant: adroit, deft, dexterous, proficient, skilled 灵巧的
k.en: lacking dexterity or skill

ex.en: awkward with a needle and thread
ex.cn: 缺乏灵巧的针线活技术

k.syn: botched, bungling, fumbled, inept, inexpert, maladroit
k.cn: （处理问题）缺乏手段和智谋的
k.en: showing or marked by a lack of skill and tact（as in dealing with a situation）

ex.en: Her awkward handling of the seating arrangements resulted in many hurt feelings.
ex.cn: 她那缺乏智谋的座次安排伤害了许多人的感情。

k.syn: clumsy, gauche, graceless, gawkish
k.ant: elegant, graceful, polished, lithe, coordinated 优雅的
k.en: lacking ease or grace（as of movement or expression）

ex.en: an awkward design
ex.cn: 一份粗劣的设计

k.syn: discomfiting, disturbing, disconcerting, embarrassing, flustering
k.en: causing embarrassment

ex.en: the awkward situation of having to listen as your host and hostess quarrel loudly in the next room
ex.cn: 不得不听着你的主人和他太太在隔壁大声争吵的尴尬境地

k.syn: ponderous, ungainly, unhandy, unwieldy, bunglesome, cranky
k.ant: handy 容易操纵的
k.cn: （因过大过重或设计问题）难以操作的
k.en: difficult to use or operate especially because of size, weight, or design

ex.en: The manual can opener is too awkward to hold.
ex.cn: 那个手动启瓶器太难握了。

m: A + WK + WARD — facing the WRONG WARD/way; clumsy or uncomfortable. — awkward, clumsy / uncomfortable.

k.en: a pointed tool for marking surfaces or piercing

m: an AWL is a small pointed tool for piercing leather — like a tiny ALL-purpose poker. — awl, a small piercing tool.

k.syn: ceiling, cover, roof, tent
k.en: a piece of material attached to a caravan or building which provides shelter from the rain or sun

ex.en: stay under the awning outside the Starbucks during the rainstorm
ex.cn: 在星巴克外面的遮阳棚下避雨

m: AWNING starts with AWN- like "DAWN" — an awning blocks the harsh DAWN sun. — awning, a fabric overhead shade.

k.syn: askew, aslant, lopsided, slanted
k.ant: aligned, direct, even, straight 成行的，笔直的
k.en: in a turned or twisted position or direction

ex.en: Her sunglass is awry.
ex.cn: 她的墨镜戴歪了。

k.syn: amiss, aside, astray, erroneous
k.ant: right, well 无差错的
k.en: off the correct or expected course

ex.en: Operation Redwing was initially launched by US special-operation troops in hopes of capturing or killing a Taliban leader but went awry.
ex.cn: 由美军特种部队发起、代号为“红翼”的军事行动的最初目的是捕获或击杀一名塔利班头目，然而行动在执行过程中出现了差错。

m: A + WRY — wry means twisted; awry = gone twisted, off-track. — awry, off-track / twisted.

k.syn: fundamental
k.en: based on or involving an axiom or system of axioms

ex.en: Euclidean geometry is based on five axiomatic principles.
ex.cn: 欧几里得几何学是在五条公理的基础上建立的。

k.syn: self-evident, prima facie
k.ant: controversial 有争议的
k.en: taken for granted, self-evident

ex.en: an axiomatic truth
ex.cn: 一个不言而明的真理

m: contains AXIOM — an axiom is a self-evident truth; axiomatic = self-evidently true. — axiomatic, self-evident.

k.syn: drivel, gabble, gibber, jabber, prattle
k.ant: articulate 清晰地说出
k.cn: 发出含糊无意义的嘟囔声，胡乱说
k.en: to utter a meaningless confusion of words or sounds

ex.en: Babies babble before they can talk.
ex.cn: 婴儿在会说话以前含糊不清地发音。

k.syn: chatter, prate
k.en: to engage in casual or rambling conversation

ex.en: The little girls babbled contentedly for the whole ride home.
ex.cn: 小姑娘们在回家的路上高兴地闲聊。

m: a baby BABBLES — meaningless burbling sounds. — babble, to talk meaninglessly.

k.syn: feigned, roundabout, sarcastic, hypocritical, two-faced, double-faced, left-handed
k.ant: forthright 直截了当的；artless, candid, genuine, honest, sincere 真挚的，真诚的
k.cn: 间接的，含沙射影的，虚情假意的
k.en: indirect, devious, especially sarcastic

ex.en: a backhanded compliment
ex.cn: 虚情假意的恭维话

m: BACK + HANDED — a BACKHAND tennis shot looks frontal but comes from behind; a backhanded compliment hits like that — looks like praise, sneaks in an insult. — backhanded, indirectly insulting.

k.en: microorganisms, especially those causing disease or fermentation

m: BACT ≈ "BAC(K)" — BACTERIA attack you from the BACK while you sleep. — bacteria, microscopic single-celled organisms.

k.en: a device or token

m: BADGE rhymes with BUDGE — wear a BADGE and the guard will BUDGE (let you pass). — badge, an emblem of authority.

k.syn: bait, plague
k.en: to harass or annoy persistently

ex.en: badger him into purchasing
ex.cn: 不断地骚扰劝说他购买

m: a BADGER is a tenacious burrowing animal — to badger someone = to keep at them, won't let go. — badger, to pester persistently.

k.syn: banter, persiflage, repartee
k.en: playful repartee, banter

ex.en: the sophisticated badinage of the characters in plays by Oscar Wilde
ex.cn: 奥斯卡·王尔德作品中意味深长的玩笑话

m: rhymes with ENTOURAGE — light, playful chitchat between friends, like a French witty exchange. — badinage, witty banter.

k.syn: badger, annoy, harass, heckle
k.ant: appease, pacify, mollify, disarm 平息，使缓和
k.en: to persecute or exasperate with unjust, malicious, or persistent attacks

ex.en: bait him with gibes about his humble origin
ex.cn: 取笑他的平凡出身以激怒他

k.syn: allure, decoy, entice, seduce
k.en: to lead away from a usual or proper course by offering some pleasure or advantage

ex.en: The investment scheme baits the greedy and the unscrupulous.
ex.cn: 这份投资计划吸引贪婪者和轻信者上钩。

k.syn: allurement, snare, trap, temptation
k.en: something（as food）used in luring especially to a hook or trap

ex.en: The squad leader has finally realized that his team was a bait.
ex.cn: 队长终于意识到他的队伍是一个诱饵。

m: BAIT on a hook — also to bait someone = to taunt them into reacting. — bait, lure / to provoke.

k.syn: misery, suffering, anguish, grief
k.ant: joy 快乐
k.en: woe, sorrow

ex.en: relieve spirit from the bale
ex.cn: 从悲痛中解脱

m: a BALE of woe — heavy bundle of sorrow. — bale, woe / great distress.

k.syn: harmful, pernicious, detrimental
k.ant: beneficent 有益的
k.en: harmful or malignant in intent or effect

ex.en: a policy with baleful effects
ex.cn: 一个带来不良后果的政策

k.syn: direful, doomy, ominous, ill-boding, minatory, portentous, sinister
k.ant: unthreatening 不构成威胁的
k.en: foreboding or threatening evil

ex.en: a baleful look
ex.cn: 面露凶色

k.syn: deadly, fatal, lethal
k.ant: healthy, wholesome 有益健康的
k.en: likely to cause or capable of causing death

ex.en: a medicine that is beneficial in small doses but baleful in large
ex.cn: 是药三分毒

m: from BALE — full of woe / threatening harm. A baleful glare promises trouble. — baleful, menacing / harmful.

k.syn: circumvent, frustrate, foil, thwart
k.ant: advance, forward, foster, nurture, promote 推进
k.en: to check or stop by or as if by an obstacle

k.syn: decline, refuse, spurn, repudiate, turn down
k.ant: accept, approve 同意
k.en: to show unwillingness to accept, do, engage in, or agree to

ex.en: She balked at the very idea of compromise.
ex.cn: 她固执地拒绝了妥协的想法。

m: a horse BALKs at the jump — stops short and refuses. — balk, to refuse / hesitate.

k.syn: contumacious, defiant, perverse, intractable, obstreperous, refractory, unruly, wayward, willful
k.ant: compliant, docile, subdued, submissive, tractable 顺从的
k.en: refusing or likely to refuse to proceed, act, or function as directed or expected

ex.en: a balky mule
ex.cn: 犟骡子

m: from BALK — a balky horse refuses to cooperate. — balky, stubbornly uncooperative.

k.syn: ditty, jingle, lyric, vocal
k.cn: （由简单诗节和叠句组成的）民歌
k.en: a narrative poem, often of folk origin and intended to be sung, consisting of simple stanzas and usually having a refrain

ex.en: a haunting ballad about loneliness
ex.cn: 一段有关寂寞的萦绕于心的民谣

m: BAL + LAD — a BAL(lad) tells the story of a LAD (young man's tale). — ballad, a narrative folk song.

k.cn: （提高稳定性和控制性的）压舱物
k.en: a heavy substance placed in such a way as to improve stability

m: BALLAST in a ship's hull keeps it stable — heavy material for stability. — ballast, stabilizing weight.

k.en: a theatrical art form using dancing, music, and scenery to convey a story

m: BAL + LET — BALLET dancers BAL-ance and LET themselves leap. — ballet, classical dance form.

k.syn: inflate, escalate, expand, burgeon, mushroom, snowball
k.ant: decrease, taper, dwindle, diminish, recede, wane 减少
k.en: to increase rapidly

ex.en: The use of computers has ballooned.
ex.cn: 电脑的使用量迅速增长。

m: BALL + OON — an inflated BALL — costs balloon when they swell up like one. — balloon, to expand rapidly.

k.ant: irritant 刺激物
k.en: a sweet-smelling oil that heals wounds or reduce pain; things that soothe the mind

ex.en: Friendship is the finest balm in need.
ex.cn: 危难关头，友情是最好的安慰。

k.syn: aroma, fragrance, incense, perfume, redolence
k.ant: fetor, malodor, stench, stink 恶臭
k.en: a sweet or pleasant smell

ex.en: The balm of the restaurant's backyard garden enhances the aura of romance.
ex.cn: 餐馆后院传来的阵阵芳香增加了浪漫的情调。

m: BALM is a soothing salve — "lip balm," "balm for the wound." — balm, soothing ointment / comfort.

k.syn: cliché, hackneyed, stereotyped, threadbare, trite, timeworn, shopworn, stale, moth-eaten
k.ant: novel, innovative, arresting　新颖的
k.en: lacking originality, freshness, or novelty; trite

ex.en: The slogan is too banal.
ex.cn: 这口号已是陈词滥调了。

m: rhymes with CANAL — BANAL is dull and trite, like the same canal scenery on every postcard. — banal, dull / unoriginal.

k.syn: affliction, curse, nemesis, scourge
k.ant: blessing, boon, felicity, windfall 福根
k.en: a source of harm or ruin

ex.en: Drinking was the bane of his life.
ex.cn: 饮酒是他一生的祸根。

k.syn: toxic, toxin, venom
k.en: a substance that by chemical action can kill or injure a living thing

ex.en: a plant that is believed to be the bane of the wolf
ex.cn: 一种被认为对狼有毒害作用的植物

m: the BANE of one's existence — the source of misery. — bane, source of harm / ruin.

k.syn: deport, exile, expatriate, expel
k.ant: repatriate 遣返
k.en: to require by authority to leave a country

ex.en: Since the diplomatic relation between the two nations has been broken, diplomats were all banished.
ex.cn: 随着两国外交关系的破裂，所有的外交人员都被驱逐出境。

k.en: to drive or force out

ex.en: The troublemakers were permanently banished from the youth recreational center.
ex.cn: 肇事者被永久地剥夺了进入青年娱乐中心的权力。

m: rhymes with VANISH — to BANISH someone is to make them VANISH from your sight. — banish, to drive away.

k.en: a handrail, especially on a staircase; the uprights supporting such a rail

m: rhymes with MINISTER, but BANISTER guides you — grip the BAN-ister so you don't fall. — banister, staircase handrail.

k.syn: badinage, persiflage, repartee Today is the first day of the rest of my life, I wake as a child to see the world begin. On monarch wings and birthday wonderings, want to put on faces, walk in the wet and cold. And look forward to my growing old, to grow is to change, to change is to be new, to be new is to be young again, I barely remember when. ——美国乡村歌手约翰·丹佛（John Denver） “没有风雨怎么见彩虹，无悔的拼搏为我带来加州的阳光和硅谷的清风。” （朱晨光，2007年6月，Verbal 720, Quantitative 800, AW 5. 0，录取院校：斯坦福大学计算机系）
k.en: good-humored, playful conversation

ex.en: banter with someone
ex.cn: 和某人调侃

m: rhymes with CANTER (a horse's gait) — light back-and-forth like a horse's casual canter. — banter, playful teasing talk.

k.syn: affront, criticism, offense, outrage, sarcasm
k.ant: praise, applause, compliment 称赞
k.en: a biting or pointedly critical remark or comment

ex.en: He delivered one last barb to his ex-girlfriend as he stalked away.
ex.cn: 在他转身离去之前，给他前女友留下了最后一句尖锐的批评。

m: a BARB on a hook hurts going in — a barbed comment is sharply hurtful. — barb, a sharp pointed remark.

k.syn: brutal, atrocious, fiendish, heartless, savage, truculent, vicious
k.ant: merciful, benevolent, humane, sympathetic 仁慈的
k.en: mercilessly harsh or cruel

ex.en: insulted by barbarous language
ex.cn: 被粗暴的言语侵犯

k.syn: wild, uncultivated
k.ant: civilized, decent, decorous 举止得体的
k.en: uncivilized

ex.en: some barbarous behaviors such as eating with your fingers
ex.cn: 一些没有教养的举止，比如直接用手拿东西吃

m: contains BARBAR — the BARBARIANS were uncivilized; barbarous = brutal, uncivilized. — barbarous, brutal / uncivilized.

k.syn: apparent, plain, bald, evident, manifest, obvious, perspicuous
k.ant: secret, furtive, clandestine, surreptitious 秘密的
k.en: undisguisedly bold; brazen

ex.en: a barefaced lie
ex.cn: 一个公然的谎言

m: BARE + FACED — your face bare and unashamed; a barefaced lie is told without any cover. — barefaced, openly shameless.

k.syn: accord, compact, contract, covenant, deal, pact, settlement
k.en: an agreement between parties settling what each gives or receives in a transaction

ex.en: They made a bargain that one would help the other next week.
ex.cn: 他们达成了一项协议：下周其中一人将帮助另外一人。

k.syn: haggle, negotiate
k.en: to negotiate over the terms of a purchase

ex.en: bargain over the price
ex.cn: 讨价还价

m: BAR + GAIN — at the BAR (counter), you GAIN a deal. — bargain, a deal / to haggle.

k.syn: byzantine, complicated, convoluted, elaborate, fancy, intricate, knotty, labyrinthine, lavish, tangled
k.ant: austere, plain, simple 朴实无华的
k.cn: 装饰华丽的，过分雕琢的，复杂的
k.en: characterized by extravagance, complexity, or flamboyance

ex.en: a baroque prose
ex.cn: 一篇辞藻华丽的散文

k.syn: exorbitant, extravagant, lavish, overdue
k.ant: moderate, modest, temperate 适度的
k.en: going beyond a normal or acceptable limit in degree or amount

ex.en: Eventually even the movie seems bored by its baroque violence.
ex.cn: 到最后，这电影也因为其中过度的暴力元素而显得让人厌烦。

m: BAROQUE music and architecture are heavily ornamented and complex (think Bach, Versailles). — baroque, elaborately ornate.

k.syn: bombardment, fusillade
k.en: a heavy curtain of artillery fire directed in front of friendly troops to protect them

ex.en: Troops are advancing under the shield of barrage.
ex.cn: 部队在弹幕的掩护下前进。

k.syn: salvo, hail, cannonade, shower, storm, volley
k.cn: 有压倒之势的、集中的倾泻（如言语）
k.en: an overwhelming, concentrated outpouring, as of words

ex.en: The announcement was met with a barrage of criticism and protests.
ex.cn: 此公告一出，批评和抗议的声音四起。

k.syn: bomb
k.en: to attack with a rapid or overwhelming outpouring of many things at once

ex.en: The athlete was barraged with requests for an autograph.
ex.cn: 请求这位运动员签名的呼声如排山倒海般袭来。

m: BAR + RAGE — a BARrier of RAGE-ful artillery, overwhelming. — barrage, an overwhelming volley.

k.syn: fruitless, impotent, infertile, effete
k.ant: prolific, fecund 多产的
k.en: incapable of producing offspring

ex.en: a poor, barren woman
ex.cn: 一名可怜的不孕女子

k.syn: bootless, ineffective, inefficacious, unavailing, vain
k.ant: effective, successful, productive, virtuous 有效的，成功的
k.en: producing no results; unproductive

ex.en: That line of investigation proved barren, so the police tried other avenues.
ex.cn: 那个方向的调查已经被证明是没有结果的了，所以警察开始尝试其他的途径。

k.syn: desolate, impoverished, waste
k.ant: arable, fruitful, luxuriant, verdant 富饶的，繁茂的
k.en: deficient in production of vegetation and especially crops

ex.en: barren deserts and wastelands
ex.cn: 贫瘠的沙漠和荒野

k.syn: bare, bereft, destitute, void
k.ant: filled, flush, fraught, full, replete, rife 充沛的
k.en: utterly lacking in something needed, wanted, or expected

ex.en: Their proposal for revitalizing the downtown business district is utterly barren of practical methods.
ex.cn: 他们意图重振中心商业区的计划缺乏能够起效的方法。

m: BAR + REN — a BAR on the womb / land = nothing comes out. — barren, infertile / fruitless.

k.syn: fence, hedge, wall, barrier, obstacle, blockade, obstruction
k.en: an obstruction or rampart thrown up across a way or passage

ex.en: The police put up barricades to block off the parade route.
ex.cn: 警察设立了路障来阻挡游行的队伍。

k.syn: bar, check, hinder, impede, obstruct, wall off
k.ant: permit 允许
k.en: to prevent access to by means of a barricade

ex.en: Streets have been barricaded by authorities.
ex.cn: 街道被当局封锁了。

m: shares BARR with BAR / BARRIER — a BARRICADE is a barrier built to block. — barricade, a blocking obstacle.

k.syn: swap, trade
k.en: to trade（goods or services）without the exchange of money

ex.en: barter wheat for cotton
ex.cn: 以小麦换棉花

m: rhymes with BARGAIN — both mean trade; BARTER is trade without money. — barter, to exchange goods.

k.en: a molding covering

m: BASE + BOARD — the board running along the BASE of the wall. — baseboard, the trim board at floor level.

k.syn: array, collection, package, parcel, group
k.en: a number of things considered as a unit

ex.en: a batch of cookies
ex.cn: 一批次烘烤的饼干

k.syn: band, body, cluster, party
k.en: a usually small number of persons considered as a unit

ex.en: Show the next batch of applicants in, please.
ex.cn: 请带下一批申请人进来。

m: a BATCH of cookies — one round baked together. — batch, a group produced together.

k.syn: trite, cliché, commonplace, hackneyed, stale, stereotyped
k.ant: offbeat 离奇的；exceptional 不平常的，非凡的
k.en: characterized by exceptional commonplaceness

ex.en: a bathetic funeral scene
ex.cn: 平凡的葬礼场面

m: from BATHOS (anticlimax) — bathetic writing collapses into the trite and dull. — bathetic, anticlimactic / overly sentimental.

k.syn: obscene, lewd, ribald, vulgar
k.ant: decent, decorous 得体的；chaste, noble 贞洁的，高贵的
k.en: boisterously or humorously indecent

ex.en: a bawdy joke
ex.cn: 黄色笑话

ex.en: a bawdy house
ex.cn: 妓院

m: rhymes with NAUGHTY — BAWDY humor is naughty, lewd. — bawdy, lewd / indecent.

k.syn: adorn, beautify, decorate, dress, embellish, emblaze
k.ant: strip 剥去；blemish, deface, mar, spoil 损害，破坏
k.en: to make more attractive by adding something that is beautiful or becoming

ex.en: flag bedecking the balcony
ex.cn: 用以装饰阳台的旗子

ex.en: bedeck with jewels
ex.cn: 用珠宝装饰

m: BE + DECK — to deck out, decorate fully. — bedeck, to adorn.

k.syn: beat, baste, batter, excoriate, lambaste, abuse, assail, vituperate, scathe
k.ant: applaud, extol, eulogize, endorse, acclaim 赞扬
k.en: to criticize harshly and usually publicly

ex.en: It's not wise to belabor other people's flaws when you're hardly perfect yourself.
ex.cn: 如果你自己还做不到完美，去指责他人是不明智的。

k.syn: dwell on, harp on
k.ant: disregard, omit, ignore, neglect, slight, slur over 忽略
k.cn: 就…作过度的说明，喋喋不休
k.en: to explain or insist on excessively

ex.en: belabor the obvious
ex.cn: 就显而易见的问题喋喋不休

m: BE + LABOR — to labor on excessively, over-explain. — belabor, to overdo / harp on.

k.syn: siege, besiege, invest, blockade
k.ant: retreat, withdraw 撤退
k.en: to surround（as a fortified place）with armed forces for the purpose of capturing or preventing commerce and communication

ex.en: beleaguered the castle for months
ex.cn: 围攻堡垒长达数月

k.syn: annoy, harass, pester, plague, tease
k.ant: delight 使愉悦
k.en: trouble, harass, beset

ex.en: We are still beleaguered by the very problem.
ex.cn: 我们仍然为这一个问题感到困扰。

m: BE + LEAGUER (besieger) — surrounded by leaguers (besiegers). — beleaguer, to besiege / harass.

k.syn: misrepresent, falsify
k.ant: betray, reveal 表明，暴露，揭露（伪装）
k.en: to picture falsely; misrepresent

ex.en: The report belied the real severity of the aftermath.
ex.cn: 这个报道错误地评价了后果的严重性。

k.syn: debunk, discredit, refute, shoot down
k.ant: attest, confirm, validate, verify 证实
k.en: to show（something）to be false or wrong

ex.en: Practical experience belies this theory.
ex.cn: 实践证明这个理论是错误的。

k.syn: conceal, curtain, disguise, mask
k.ant: disclose, expose 揭露
k.en: to keep secret or shut off from view

ex.en: Security Council issued false assurances that belied the true gravity of the situation.
ex.cn: 安理会发布了不真实的承诺，掩盖了局势的严重性。

k.syn: contradict, contravene
k.ant: agree 与…相一致
k.en: to be counter to; contradict

ex.en: At first glance, life at the boarding school seemed to belie all the bad things I had heard about it.
ex.cn: 乍看之下，寄宿学校的生活似乎不同于我所听说的有关它的糟糕情形。

m: BE + LIE — to LIE about, contradict, or disguise. Her smile BELIES her sadness. — belie, to contradict / disguise.

k.syn: leader, pacemaker, pilot, trendsetter
k.ant: follower, disciple, imitator 跟随者，弟子，模仿者
k.en: one that takes the lead or initiative

ex.en: Paris is a bellwether of the fashion industry.
ex.cn: 巴黎是一座引领时尚潮流的都市。

m: BELL + WETHER (a castrated ram with a bell) — the lead sheep with the bell that the flock follows. — bellwether, a leader / trend indicator.

k.syn: kind, altruistic, benevolent, philanthropic, benign, compassionate, sympathetic
k.ant: atrocious, barbarous, truculent, vicious 残忍的
k.en: characterized by or performing acts of kindness or charity

ex.en: a beneficent couple who are regular volunteers at an orphan
ex.cn: 一对常在孤儿院做义工的慈善的夫妇

k.syn: helpful, advantageous, favorable, kindly, profitable, salutary
k.ant: detrimental, harmful, noxious, toxic 有害的
k.en: promoting or contributing to personal or social well-being

ex.en: the beneficent effect of sunshine
ex.cn: 日光浴的益处

m: contains BENE (good) + FIC (do) — doing good. — beneficent, kind / charitable.

k.syn: beneficent, gentle, kind
k.ant: abrasive, caustic, coarse, hard, harsh, rough, scathing, stern 粗暴的，严厉的
k.en: showing kindness and gentleness

ex.en: a benign coach
ex.cn: 一名仁慈的教练

k.syn: harmless, innocent, innocuous, inoffensive
k.ant: baleful, deleterious, detrimental, harmful, injurious, pernicious 有害的
k.en: not causing or being capable of causing injury or hurt

ex.en: Don't worry; his eccentricities are entirely benign.
ex.cn: 别担心，他那些古怪行为不会给人带来伤害。

m: BEN- (good, as in "benefit") — good-natured / harmless. — benign, harmless / kindly.

k.syn: castigate, flay, lambaste, scold, rail, upbraid, reproach, reprimand
k.ant: commend, compliment, praise 表扬，赞颂
k.en: to scold or condemn vehemently and at length

ex.en: He berated them in public.
ex.cn: 他公开谴责他们。

m: BE + RATE — to RATE someone (low) sternly = to scold. — berate, to scold harshly.

k.syn: appeal, conjure, entreat, implore, petition, plead, supplicate
k.ant: demand（依据权力等）要求
k.en: to beg for urgently or anxiously

ex.en: They besought the military to act immediately.
ex.cn: 他们恳求军方立即采取行动。

m: BE + SEECH (seek) — to seek earnestly, beg. — beseech, to beg urgently.

k.syn: foul, smirch, smudge, taint
k.ant: clean, cleanse 清洁
k.en: to make dirty; soil

ex.en: They besmirched the white bed sheets with their dirty hands.
ex.cn: 他们的脏手把白床单弄脏了。

k.syn: defile, smear, soil, stain, smirch
k.ant: honor 授予荣誉
k.en: to detract from the honor or luster of

ex.en: besmirch your reputation by fabricating scandals
ex.cn: 通过捏造丑闻来玷污你的名声

m: BE + SMIRCH (smudge) — to smudge someone's reputation. — besmirch, to dirty / slander.

k.en: a list of works by a specific author or on a specific subject

m: BIBLI- (book) + GRAPHY (writing) — the list of books cited. — bibliography, a list of books / sources.

k.syn: divide, diverge, fork
k.ant: coalesce 联合；converge 汇合
k.en: （to cause）to divide into two branches or parts

ex.en: Their visions of the company's future slowly began to bifurcate.
ex.cn: 我们关于公司未来的构想慢慢出现分歧。

m: BI- (two) + FURC (fork) — to fork into two. — bifurcate, to split into two branches.

k.syn: dogmatist, partisan
k.ant: depreciator, disparager 贬低他人的人
k.cn: 固执己见者，有偏见的人
k.en: a person obstinately devoted to his own opinions and prejudices

ex.en: He is a bigot, or“a slave of dogma”.
ex.cn: 他是一个固执己见的人，换言之，就是教条主义的奴隶。

m: BIG + OT — a BIG-OT(headed) person clings to prejudice. (Or: "big shot" who refuses to budge.) — bigot, an intolerant prejudiced person.

k.syn: mild, light, soft, soothing, tender
k.ant: pungent, tangy, zesty 味道刺激的
k.en: not irritating or stimulating; soothing

ex.en: stick to bland diet to lose weight
ex.cn: 坚持清淡的饮食以减肥

ex.en: bland food that was good for babies and invalids
ex.cn: 对婴儿和病患有益的温和的食物

k.syn: banal, sapless, insipid
k.ant: riveting, enchanting 诱人的
k.en: dull, insipid

ex.en: a bland story with naïve plot
ex.cn: 一个情节幼稚的无聊故事

k.syn: balmy, benign, delicate, mellow, nonabrasive
k.ant: abrasive, caustic, coarse, hard, harsh, rough, scathing, stern 刺激性的；尖刻的，严厉的
k.en: not harsh or stern especially in nature or effect

ex.en: his face expanding in a bland smile
ex.cn: 他的脸上绽开了温和的笑容

m: BLAND rhymes with BLAND-ly — unseasoned. Contains BLA — like BLA-h, no flavor. — bland, dull / unflavored.

k.syn: flattery, adulation blasé［blɑ:'zeɪ］
k.cn: 甜言蜜语，讨好某人的话
k.en: something that tends to coax or cajole

ex.en: Our blandishment left her unmoved.
ex.cn: 纵使甜言蜜语她也无动于衷。

k.syn: indifferent, jaded, unconcerned, world-weary
k.ant: zealous, fanatic 狂热的；curious 好奇的
k.cn: （过度放纵之后）厌倦享乐的，腻烦的
k.en: apathetic to pleasure or excitement as a result of excessive indulgence or enjoyment

ex.en: Years of extravagance has made him totally blasé.
ex.cn: 长期的奢华生活已使他彻底麻木。

m: from BLAND — sweet bland flattery used to coax. — blandishment, flattering coaxing.

k.syn: burst, detonation, eruption, outburst
k.ant: implosion 向内爆裂
k.en: an explosion or violent detonation

ex.en: blast wave of a nuclear bomb
ex.cn: 原子弹的冲击波

k.syn: blow, flurry
k.en: a violent gust of wind

ex.en: blasts of bleak air
ex.cn: 阵阵阴风

k.syn: bang, boom, thunderclap
k.ant: murmur, whisper 轻言轻语
k.en: a loud explosive sound

ex.en: A sharp blast of the horn startled the other driver.
ex.cn: 一声尖锐刺耳的喇叭声惊吓到了另一名司机。

k.syn: demolish, explode, smash, blow up
k.en: to cause to break open or into pieces by or as if by an explosive

ex.en: The highway engineers will have to blast that hill in order to put a road through here.
ex.cn: 为了建设一条通过这里的公路，道路工程师们必须对那座山施行爆破。

k.syn: abuse, assail, belabor, castigate, lambaste, scathe, vituperate
k.en: to criticize harshly and usually publicly

ex.en: blast the new governor for every little misstep
ex.cn: 就每一个小的失误抨击新的执政官

m: BLAST onomatopoeia — the SOUND of an explosion. — blast, an explosion / sharp gust / harsh attack.

k.syn: boisterous, clamant, clamorous, vociferous
k.ant: quiet, reticent, taciturn 安静的，沉默少语的
k.en: noisy, especially in a vulgar or offensive manner

ex.en: blatant radios
ex.cn: 喧闹恼人的电台

k.syn: conspicuous, flagrant, glaring, patent, striking, pronounced
k.ant: subtle, unimpressive 细微的
k.en: very noticeable especially for being incorrect or bad

ex.en: a blatant lie
ex.cn: 明目张胆的谎言

ex.en: a blatant error in simple addition
ex.cn: 简单加法运算中的一个明显错误

m: rhymes with FLAGRANT — both mean glaringly obvious. — blatant, glaringly obvious.

k.syn: annunciate, broadcast, declare, publicize, proclaim
k.ant: withhold 保留，不透露
k.en: to make known openly or publicly

ex.en: Their very public canoodling has pretty much blazoned the fact that they are having an affair.
ex.cn: 他们彼此间公开的亲昵举动让他们之间的恋情大白于天下。

k.syn: adorn, bedeck, embellish, garnish
k.ant: blemish, deface, mar, spoil 损害，破坏
k.en: to make more attractive by adding something that is beautiful or becoming

ex.en: The university's dormitory has been blazoned with banners celebrating graduation.
ex.cn: 大学宿舍被那些庆祝毕业的横幅装饰一新。

m: contains BLAZE — to BLAZE forth on a banner; to display prominently. — blazon, to display prominently.

k.syn: blotch, defect, fault, flaw, mar, spot, scar
k.en: a noticeable imperfection

ex.en: The first LCD had several blemishes on its surface, so we took it back to the store.
ex.cn: 第一块液晶显示屏有许多坏点，所以我们拿回商场去退货了。

k.syn: break, disfigure, harm, hurt, impair, injure, spoil, vitiate
k.ant: fix, renovate, repair, revamp 翻新，维修
k.der: unblemished adj. 纯洁的，无瑕疵的
k.en: to reduce the soundness, effectiveness, or perfection of

ex.en: A scratch blemished the finish on the car.
ex.cn: 一道刮痕破坏了车子表面的涂层。

m: BLEM ≈ "BLEED" + ISH — a small BLEED-ish mark on the skin. Or shares BLEM- with EMBLEM — but reversed: an unwelcome "emblem" on your face. — blemish, a small flaw.

k.syn: wither, shrivel
k.ant: flourish 生长繁茂
k.en: to affect（as a plant）with blight

k.syn: damage, deteriorate, harm, impair, mar, ruin
k.der: blighted adj. 枯萎的；毁坏的
k.en: to impair the quality or effect of

ex.en: Illness blighted his career.
ex.cn: 伤病毁了他的职业生涯。

m: BLIGHT rhymes with BLIGHTNING (almost) and BIGHT — a BLIGHT strikes crops like LIGHTNING strikes a tree. — blight, to ruin / destroy.

k.syn: beatitude, joy, ecstasy, elation
k.ant: grief, misery 悲痛
k.en: extreme happiness; ecstasy

ex.en: Ignorance is bliss ——The Matrix.
ex.cn: 无知者，幸也。（电影《黑客帝国》）

k.syn: heaven, paradise
k.ant: hell 地狱
k.en: a dwelling place of perfect happiness for the soul after death

ex.en: the road to eternal bliss
ex.cn: 通往永恒乐土的道路

m: BLISS contains KISS — pure happiness, like a first KISS. — bliss, supreme happiness.

k.syn: bright, buoyant, gay, jocular, jocund, jovial
k.ant: dour, gloomy, morose, saturnine, sulky, sullen 忧郁的
k.en: of a happy lighthearted character or disposition

ex.en: Everyone loved her for her blithe spirit.
ex.cn: 所有人都喜欢她开朗的性格。

k.syn: debonair, insouciant, lighthearted
k.ant: careworn 焦虑的
k.cn: 无忧无虑的；漫不经心的
k.en: having or showing freedom from worries or troubles

ex.en: He has a blithe attitude about ever having to earn a living because he knows there's a trust fund in his future.
ex.cn: 他对生活无忧无虑，因为他知道他的未来有信托基金可以倚靠。

m: BLITHE rhymes with LITHE — light, carefree, breezy. — blithe, carefree / casual.

k.syn: arrangement, design, plan, scheme
k.en: a photographic print used especially for architects' plans

ex.en: a blueprint for the new library
ex.cn: 新图书馆的建造蓝图

k.syn: arrange, budget, calculate, organize, frame, lay out
k.en: to work out the details of（something）in advance

ex.en: blueprint the schedule of events for the festival right down to the last detail
ex.cn: 把节日活动的每一个细节都事先安排好

m: a BLUEPRINT is the architect's detailed plan, drawn in blue. — blueprint, a detailed plan.

k.syn: mistake, gaffe, lapse, error
k.en: a gross error or mistake resulting usually from stupidity, ignorance, or carelessness

ex.en: That's your second blunder today.
ex.cn: 这是你今天犯的第二个低级错误了。

k.syn: screw up, mess up
k.cn: 在…方面犯了很愚蠢且通常很严重的错误；糟蹋
k.en: to make a stupid, usually serious error in; botch

k.syn: stumble, falter, limp, plod
k.en: to move unsteadily or confusedly

ex.en: Without my glasses I blundered into the wrong room.
ex.cn: 因为没戴眼镜，所以我蹒跚地走入了错误的房间。

m: BLUNDER contains BLUN + UNDER — fall UNDER your own BLUN(t) feet. — blunder, a serious clumsy mistake.

k.syn: dull, deaden, hebetate, benumb, enfeeble
k.ant: whet, sharpen 磨尖
k.en: to make less sharp or definite

k.syn: dampen, deaden, attenuate
k.en: to reduce or weaken in strength or feeling

ex.en: The abrupt music blunted the effect of the movie's final tragic scene.
ex.cn: 突兀的音乐让电影结束时悲剧场景的氛围大打折扣。

k.syn: abrupt, bluff, brusque, curt, gruff
k.ant: circuitous, mealy-mouthed 拐弯抹角的
k.en: being or characterized by direct, brief, and potentially rude speech or manner

ex.en: He values honesty and is quite blunt about telling people what he doesn't like about them.
ex.cn: 他重视诚实，经常直率地告诉别人他们何处使他不满。

m: BLUNT rhymes with HUNT — a BLUNT knife can't HUNT well; a BLUNT person hunts with words straight on. — blunt, dull / direct.

k.syn: shroud, becloud, befog, obscure
k.ant: clear 使清晰
k.en: to（cause sth. to） become vague or indistinct

ex.en: Sorrowful tears blurred her eyes.
ex.cn: 悲伤的眼泪模糊了她的双眼。

k.syn: obfuscate
k.ant: clarify, illuminate 阐明，说清楚
k.en: to make（something）unclear to the understanding

ex.en: An article for the layman that blurs the distinction between the two kinds of cholesterol.
ex.cn: 一篇针对普通读者的文章对两种胆固醇的区别表述得含糊不清。

m: BLUR sounds like an out-of-focus BUZZ — letters BUZZing past your eyes. — blur, to make unclear.

k.en: a short publicity notice

m: BLURB ≈ BLAB + verb — a publisher's BLAB about the book on its back cover. — blurb, a short promotional notice.

k.syn: burst, bolt, ejaculate, cry out
k.ant: muffle, mute 使缄默
k.en: to utter abruptly and impulsively

ex.en: blurt out the secret
ex.cn: 脱口说出了秘密

m: BLURT rhymes with SPURT — words SPURT out before you can stop them. — blurt, to say impulsively.

k.syn: roar, clamor, rattle
k.ant: whisper 小声嘀咕
k.en: to speak in a loudly arrogant or bullying manner

ex.en: He was blustering alone in the meeting, which triggered wide dissatisfaction.
ex.cn: 他一人在会议上夸夸其谈，引得众人不满。

k.syn: grandiloquence, braggadocio
k.en: loudly boastful or threatening speech

k.syn: disturbance, pandemonium, tumult, turmoil
k.en: a state of noisy, confused activity

ex.en: a mayor who got things done without a lot of bluster
ex.cn: 一个能把事情低调解决的市长

m: a BLUSTERY wind is loud and showy — bluster = loud bragging or empty threat. — bluster, loud blustery talk / boasting.

k.en: a usually armed attendant or group of attendants whose duty is to protect a person

m: BODY + GUARD — guards a body. — bodyguard, a personal guard.

k.syn: hesitate, falter, waver
k.en: to hesitate because of doubt, fear, or scruples

ex.en: boggle at the dilemma
ex.cn: 身处困境而犹豫不决

k.syn: botch, bungle
k.en: to make or do（something）in a clumsy or unskillful way

ex.en: She boggled her first effort to make Christmas cookies.
ex.cn: 她第一次笨手笨脚尝试做圣诞蛋糕。

m: BOG + GLE — your mind sinks into a BOG(gy) marsh of confusion. — boggle, to overwhelm with confusion.

k.syn: rowdy, vociferous, blatant, clamorous, raucous, rambunctious
k.ant: quiet, sedate 安静的
k.en: noisily turbulent

ex.en: a boisterous queue in front of the pavilion
ex.cn: 场馆前喧闹的队伍

ex.en: boisterous mirth
ex.cn: 喧闹的欢笑

m: rhymes with ROISTEROUS — BOISTEROUS kids are loudly rambunctious. — boisterous, noisily rowdy.

k.syn: brace, buttress, bear, sustain, undergird, underpin, uphold, prop up
k.en: a structural part designed to eliminate friction or provide support or bearing

ex.en: pillars that bolster the building
ex.cn: 支撑房屋的柱子

k.syn: buoy, reinforce
k.ant: dampen 泼冷水
k.en: to give a boost to

ex.en: news that bolsters the morale of the troops
ex.cn: 鼓舞军队士气的消息

m: sounds like BOOSTER — both prop things up. — bolster, to support / prop up.

k.syn: braggadocio, grandiloquence, exaggeration
k.ant: understatement 保守的观点
k.en: grandiloquent, pompous speech or writing

ex.en: “Their eloquence is all bombast”, said Charles Kingsley.“
ex.cn: 他们的雄辩是虚张声势”，查尔斯·金斯利如此说道。

m: contains BOMB + BAST — bombastic speech is bombarded with inflated words. — bombast, pompous inflated speech.

k.syn: affability, amiability, geniality
k.en: a pleasant and affable disposition; geniality

m: from French BON (good) + HOMIE — "good homie" vibes — friendly cheer. — bonhomie, good-natured friendliness.

k.syn: jeer, scorn
k.ant: applaud 鼓掌
k.en: a sound uttered to show contempt, scorn, or disapproval

ex.en: boo the actor off the stage
ex.cn: 把演员嘘下舞台

m: BOO is the SOUND people make to jeer (also onomatopoeia for a scare). — boo, to jeer.

k.syn: gift, benevolence, present, windfall
k.ant: misfortune, scourge 灾祸
k.en: benefit, favor

ex.en: The new solar battery booster is a boon for photographers.
ex.cn: 这个光伏充电器是摄影师们的福音。

k.syn: convivial, extroverted, gregarious, social, outgoing
k.ant: reclusive 隐居
k.en: likely to seek or enjoy the company of others

m: BOON rhymes with NOON-day sun — a welcome warmth, a benefit. (Or: BOOST + ON = a boost.) — boon, a blessing / benefit.

k.syn: peasant, barbarian, buffoon
k.ant: sentimentalist 多愁善感的人
k.en: a rude or insensitive person

ex.en: acting like a boor
ex.cn: 表现得很粗鲁

m: BOOR rhymes with BOAR — a wild BOAR has no manners; a boor behaves like one. — boor, a rude person.

k.syn: barren, ineffective, futile, abortive, fruitless, vain
k.ant: worthy有价值的
k.en: useless, unprofitable, futile

ex.en: The meeting turns out to be a bootless attempt.
ex.cn: 这个会议被证明是一次徒劳的尝试。

m: BOOT + LESS — without boots, you can't walk; bootless = futile, useless. — bootless, useless.

k.syn: environs, limit, confines, perimeter
k.ant: unrestrainedness 无边无际
k.en: a real or imaginary point beyond which a person or thing cannot go

ex.en: The language in the novel really is beyond the bounds of decency.
ex.cn: 这本小说的语言实在太不得体了。

k.syn: limit, demarcate, delimit
k.ant: enfranchise, free, liberate 释放
k.en: to set limits or bounds to

ex.en: The country is bounded by river.
ex.cn: 这个国家的领土以河水为界。

k.syn: resolute, determined, single-minded, bent on
k.ant: faltering, hesitant, vacillating, wavering, weak-kneed 动摇的
k.der: boundless adj. 无边无际的，无约束的
k.en: fully committed to achieving a goal

ex.en: I am bound and determined to write a novel before I turn 30.
ex.cn: 我下定决心在30岁之前要写本小说。

m: BOUND has the same letters as IN-BOUND/OUT-BOUND — direction-bound, tied somewhere. — bound, tied / leaping / committed.

k.en: flowers picked and fastened together

m: BOUQUET ≈ "BOO-KAY" — they say "BOO!" then "KAY?" while presenting flowers. (Or: "buy a KAY" — buy a-bunch, K-bunch of flowers.) — bouquet, a bunch of flowers.

k.syn: refuse
k.ant: patronize 经常光顾
k.en: to engage in a concerted refusal to have dealings with（as a person, store, or organization）usually to express disapproval or to force acceptance of certain conditions

ex.en: This brand is being boycotted for damaging environment.
ex.cn: 因为破坏环境，这个品牌正在被抵制。

m: named after Captain Boycott, an Irish land agent everyone refused to deal with — to BOYCOTT = collectively shun. — boycott, to refuse to participate as protest.

k.syn: invigorating, rejuvenating, reviving, stimulating
k.ant: vapid 乏味的
k.der: brace v. 支持，使充满活力
k.cn: 令人振奋的，给人带来活力的
k.en: giving strength, vigor, or freshness

ex.en: a bracing news from the frontline
ex.cn: 来自前线的振奋人心的消息

m: rhymes with EMBRACING — but BRACING air EMBRACES you with a sharp wake-up. — bracing, refreshingly invigorating.

k.en: a simple rigid structure in the shape of an L, one arm of which is fixed to a vertical

m: BRACKET shape is like the letter L — L-shaped support. — bracket, an L-shaped support.

k.en: made by intertwining three or more strands

m: BRAID + ED — what you do to hair: BRAID three strands. — braided, woven / interlaced.

k.syn: decelerate, retard, slacken
k.ant: accelerate, hasten, rush, speed up 加速
k.en: to cause to move or proceed at a less rapid pace

ex.en: A seagull swooped down in front of her car, causing her to slam on the brakes.
ex.cn: 一只海鸥忽然俯冲到车窗前，她猛踩刹车。

m: BRAKE rhymes with BREAK — but "brake" stops the car so you don't BREAK things. — brake, a stopping mechanism.

k.syn: audacious, brassy, daredevil, madcap, reckless, temerarious
k.ant: circumspect, guarded, heedful, prudent, wary 谨慎的
k.en: foolishly adventurous or bold

ex.en: that brash motorcyclist likes to show off by riding on only one wheel
ex.cn: 那个鲁莽的摩托车手喜欢炫耀单轮骑车

k.syn: imprudent, injudicious, tactless, undiplomatic
k.ant: advisable, discreet, tactful 明智的
k.cn: 缺乏判断力的，不明智的
k.en: showing poor judgment especially in personal relationships or social situations

ex.en: He was reprimanded for his brash comments to the media about the team's coaching staff.
ex.cn: 他因对媒体说了一些有关教练组的不明智的评论而遭到指责。

m: BRASH rhymes with RASH (impulsive) — both mean reckless. — brash, rashly impudent.

k.syn: audacious, bold-faced, brazen, impertinent, impudent, insolent
k.ant: diffident, unassertive, retiring, timid 羞怯的，谦逊的
k.en: displaying or marked by rude boldness

ex.en: brassy reporters
ex.cn: 厚颜无耻的记者

k.ant: furtive 隐秘的
k.en: cheap and showy; flashy

m: BRASSY — like loud cheap brass — shamelessly bold. — brassy, shamelessly bold / showy.

k.cn: （小孩）被宠坏的，淘气的，无礼的
k.en: ill-mannered

m: from BRAT — a brattish kid acts spoiled and rude. — brattish, like a spoiled brat.

k.en: a pretense of bravery

ex.en: I remembered his youthful bravado.
ex.cn: 我还记得他的年少鲁莽。

k.en: blustering swaggering conduct

ex.en: strive to prevent our courage from turning into bravado
ex.cn: 努力阻止我们的勇气变成虚张声势

m: from BRAVE — exaggerated false bravery, all show. — bravado, showy false courage.

k.syn: adroit, artful, dexterous, masterful
k.ant: amateur, artless, unprofessional, unskillful 业余的，拙劣的
k.en: brilliant technique or style in performance

ex.en: a truly bravura performance of the ballet
ex.cn: 一场极其精彩的芭蕾舞演出

m: from BRAVO — a BRAVURA performance earns shouts of "bravo!" — bravura, brilliant technical display.

k.syn: bold-faced, impertinent, impudent, insolent
k.ant: modest, self-effacing, diffident, retiring, timid 谦虚的，胆小的
k.cn: 蛮横大胆的，厚颜无耻的
k.en: marked by contemptuous boldness

ex.en: a brazen disregard for the rules
ex.cn: 蛮横大胆地漠视规则

k.syn: confront, outface, defy
k.ant: dodge, duck, shirk, sidestep 躲开，避谈
k.cn: 大胆自信地去面对或从事
k.en: to face or undergo with bold self-assurance

ex.en: brazen out the crisis
ex.cn: 沉着面对危机

ex.en: Some people prefer to brazen a thing out rather than admit defeat.
ex.cn: 有的人不愿承认失败，而是宁肯厚着脸皮干下去。

m: BRAZEN means "made of brass" — bold and shameless like loud brass. — brazen, shamelessly bold.

k.syn: malefaction, transgression, trespass, violation, wrongdoing
k.en: a breaking of a moral or legal code

ex.en: cheating on the exam was a serious breach of the military academy's honor code
ex.cn: 考试作弊是对军校的荣誉准则的严重破坏

k.syn: contravene, fracture, infringe, transgress
k.ant: obey, observe, comply with, conform to 遵守
k.en: to fail to keep

ex.en: a builder being sued by a homeowner for breaching a contract
ex.cn: 建筑商因违约被房主起诉

m: BREACH rhymes with BEACH — waves BREACH the BEACH wall. — breach, to break through / a breaking.

k.cn: 有顶棚的通路（联接两个建筑的带屋顶的侧边敞开的过道）
k.en: a roofed often open passage connecting

m: BREEZE + WAY — a covered breezy passage between two buildings. — breezeway, a covered open-sided passage.

k.syn: briefness, conciseness, shortness
k.ant: lengthiness 冗长
k.en: shortness of duration

ex.en: The best quality a graduation speech can have is brevity.
ex.cn: 好的毕业演讲必须简洁。

k.syn: conciseness, pithiness, sententiousness, terseness
k.ant: diffuseness, long-windedness, prolixity, verbosity, wordiness 冗长，啰嗦
k.en: the quality or state of being marked by or using only few words to convey much meaning

ex.en: If brevity is the soul of wit, then that speech wasn't at all witty.
ex.cn: 如果说精炼是智慧的灵魂，那么那个演讲毫无智慧可言。

m: from BRIEF — the quality of being brief. — brevity, conciseness.

k.syn: corrupt, pay off, square
k.en: to give something, such as money or a favor, to a person in a position of trust to influence that person's views or conduct

ex.en: They bribed him to keep quiet about the incident.
ex.cn: 他们收买他，希望其保持缄默。

m: 「爸来了」(bribe) — 官员偷偷把钱塞进口袋。— bribe, money paid to corrupt.

k.syn: check, contain, curb, constrain, inhibit, regulate, restrain, tame, rein in
k.en: to keep from exceeding a desirable degree or level（as of expression）

ex.en: Try to bridle your criticism next time so that it is helpful and not hurtful.
ex.cn: 下回尽量控制好批评的语气，让其既顺耳又有益。

m: BRIDLE on a horse and BRIDE both come from "bind" — both restrain. — bridle, to restrain.

k.syn: animated, bouncing, bustling, frisky, kinetic, sprightly, vibrant
k.ant: lackadaisical, languid, leaden, dead, inactive, lifeless 没有生机的
k.en: marked by much life, movement, or activity

ex.en: brisk and concise response
ex.cn: 轻快而简洁的回答

k.cn: （言辞或方式）尖刻辛辣的，刻薄的
k.en: keen or sharp in speech or manner

ex.en: a brisk greeting
ex.cn: 尖刻的祝贺

m: BRISK rhymes with WHISK — both quick and lively. — brisk, lively / sharp.

k.syn: bluster, fulminate, rampage, fume, storm
k.ant: cower 畏缩
k.en: to express one's anger usually violently

ex.en: bristle at the suggestion of gay marriage
ex.cn: 对基情怒不可遏

k.syn: brim, overflow, swarm, teem
k.en: to be copiously supplied

ex.en: starting a new life in New York city bristling with possibilities
ex.cn: 在有着无限机会的纽约开始新生活

m: BRISTLE contains BRIST ≈ BURST — hairs BURST up in anger. (Also a stiff hair = a bristle on a brush.) — bristle, to react angrily / be densely set.

k.syn: crispy, crumbly, flaky, friable
k.en: easily broken, cracked, or snapped

ex.en: as brittle as glass
ex.cn: 像玻璃一样脆弱

k.syn: chilly, frigid, frosty, glacial, unfriendly, unsympathetic
k.ant: cordial, genial, warmhearted 热心的
k.en: lacking in friendliness or warmth of feeling

ex.en: a brittle apology that was anything but heartfelt
ex.cn: 一个毫不真诚的道歉

m: BRITTLE rhymes with LITTLE — a LITTLE pressure shatters BRITTLE bones. — brittle, fragile.

k.syn: break
k.ant: close off 关闭
k.en: to open for the first time

ex.en: broach a keg of beer
ex.cn: 开一小桶酒

k.syn: moot, place, raise, bring up
k.en: to present or bring forward for discussion

ex.en: broach the topic of plans for next year's parade
ex.cn: 将明年的游行活动计划提上讨论日程

m: to BROACH a topic = to open it for discussion (originally: to pierce a cask to start it pouring). — broach, to bring up for discussion.

k.syn: banality, cliché, platitude, homily, truism, chestnut, shibboleth
k.en: a commonplace or hackneyed statement or notion

ex.en: A newspaper editorial offered the timeworn bromide that people should settle their differences peacefully.
ex.cn: 报纸编辑又拿出那一套呼吁和平解决纷争的陈词滥调。

m: a BROMIDE was a sedative — figuratively a tired clichéd remark that puts you to sleep. — bromide, a trite remark.

k.syn: abide, countenance, endure, stomach
k.en: to stand for, tolerate

ex.en: brook no interference with his plans
ex.cn: 不能容忍别人对他的计划进行干涉

k.syn: creek, rivulet
k.en: a natural stream of water normally smaller than and often tributary to a river: creek

ex.en: There are tiny fish and frogs in that brook.
ex.cn: 那条小溪里有小鱼和青蛙。

m: to BROOK no insolence — same word as the small stream BROOK (originally: not let it flow past). — brook, to tolerate.

k.syn: blackjack, bulldoze, bully, cow, hector, intimidate
k.en: to intimidate by a stern manner or arrogant speech: bully

ex.en: They would often browbeat the younger child until he cried.
ex.cn: 他们总是把小朋友欺负到哭为止。

m: BROW + BEAT — to beat someone down with a furrowed brow / intimidate. — browbeat, to intimidate.

k.syn: circulate, whisper, noise about
k.ant: keep secret 保持秘密
k.en: to make（as a piece of information）the subject of common talk without any authority or confirmation of accuracy

ex.en: It's been bruited that...
ex.cn: 到处传播…

m: rhymes with FRUIT — to BRUIT it about = to spread (as gossip). — bruit, to spread (a rumor).

k.syn: defy, fight, oppose, repel, withstand
k.ant: assent to, bow to, submit to, succumb to, surrender to, yield to 赞成，服从
k.en: to refuse assent, to refuse to give in to

ex.en: buck the system
ex.cn: 不遵守制度

k.syn: transfer, hand over
k.en: to shift possession of（something）from one person to another

ex.en: buck each box to the next person in line
ex.cn: 将每一个盒子传给队伍中的后一人

m: BUCK rhymes with PLUCK — to BUCK a trend takes pluck (resistance). — buck, to resist / oppose.

k.en: to alter a position or attitude

ex.en: Nothing would budge him.
ex.cn: 没有什么可以让他改变主意。

k.syn: concede, relent, submit, succumb, surrender
k.ant: resist 拒绝，抵抗
k.en: to cease resistance（as to another's arguments, demands, or control）

ex.en: Despite hours of intense pressure, she refused to budge from her position.
ex.cn: 尽管被连续施压几小时，她仍旧不肯让步。

m: BUDGE rhymes with NUDGE — but BUDGE is what NUDGING tries (and fails) to do. — budge, to move slightly.

k.en: a ludicrous

m: BUF + FOON ≈ "buffer + goon" — a clownish goon. (Sounds like a comic puff of a face.) — buffoon, a foolish clown.

k.syn: convexity, projection, protrusion, protuberance, swell
k.ant: depressed region, cavity, dent, indent, recess, pit 凹陷，坑
k.en: a protuberant or swollen part or place

ex.en: a bulge in his jacket
ex.cn: 他衣服上鼓起来的一块

ex.en: bulging eyes
ex.cn: 暴鱼眼

k.syn: high ground, inside track, upper hand, whip hand
k.ant: disadvantage, drawback, handicap, liability 劣势
k.en: the more favorable condition or position in a competition

ex.en: Somehow she got the bulge on him in the race for the statehouse.
ex.cn: 她在州议员竞选中领先了他。

k.cn: 暴涨，突增，在数目或数量上突然而且是临时性的增加
k.en: a sudden, usually temporary increase in number or quantity

ex.en: The baby boom created a bulge in school enrollment.
ex.cn: 生育高峰造成学校入学人数的暴涨。

k.syn: brim, bristle, overflow, swarm, teem
k.en: to be copiously supplied

ex.en: This guidebook to San Francisco positively bulges with useful information.
ex.cn: 这本关于旧金山的指南包含了很多有用信息。

m: BULGE rhymes with INDULGE — INDULGing too much produces a BULGE. — bulge, a swelling.

k.syn: bullyboy, hector, intimidator
k.ant: underdog 被欺负的人
k.en: a person who habitually treats others in an overbearing or intimidating manner

ex.en: They had to deal with the local bullies.
ex.cn: 他们要对付当地恶霸。

k.syn: awesome, fabulous, fantastic, superb, marvelous, unsurpassed, excellent
k.en: of the very best kind

ex.en: That's a bully idea for reviving the town's retail center.
ex.cn: 真是个重整镇上零售中心的好主意。

m: BULLY contains BULL — a BULL pushes others around with sheer size. — bully, an aggressor.

k.syn: assumptive, supercilious, imperious, overweening, peremptory, pompous, presuming, presumptuous, self-assertive, haughty
k.ant: humble, modest, unarrogant, unpretentious 谦虚的，低调的
k.cn: 专横傲慢的，自以为是的
k.en: having a feeling of superiority that shows itself in an overbearing attitude

ex.en: a bumptious young man whose family wealth gave him a sense of entitlement
ex.cn: 一个傲慢的富二代

ex.en: be bumptious over one's inferiors
ex.cn: 对下级态度傲慢

m: from BUMP — pushing your way around with arrogance. — bumptious, obnoxiously self-assertive.

k.syn: boggle, bumble, fumble, mess up, screw up
k.ant: bring off 成功
k.en: to act or work clumsily and awkwardly

ex.en: bungle a job
ex.cn: 搞砸了一项工作

m: BUNGLE rhymes with JUNGLE — a tangled JUNGLE of mistakes. — bungle, to botch.

k.syn: embolden, hearten, inspire, bear up, buck up
k.ant: daunt, discourage, dishearten, dispirit 使沮丧，泼冷水
k.cn: 使充满勇气和力量，使振作
k.en: to fill with courage or strength of purpose

ex.en: The sudden improvement in his health buoyed him up.
ex.cn: 身体的突然好转让他很振奋。

m: a BUOY floats — to buoy spirits = lift them up. — buoy, to lift up / encourage.

k.ant: leaden 沉重的
k.en: capable of floating

ex.en: a buoyant balloon
ex.cn: 一个能浮起来的气球

k.syn: blithe, chipper, eupeptic, lightsome, upbeat, winsome, effervescent
k.ant: dour, gloomy, morose, saturnine, sullen 沮丧的
k.en: having or showing a good mood or disposition

ex.en: in a buoyant mood
ex.cn: 轻松快乐的心情

m: from BUOY — floating, light-hearted. — buoyant, floating / cheerful.

k.syn: accelerate, accumulate, balloon, boom, build up, escalate, mount, multiply, mushroom, proliferate, roll up, snowball, wax, flourish, prosper
k.ant: wane, wither, waste away, subside, subdue 衰退
k.cn: 迅速成长扩大，蓬勃发展
k.en: to grow and expand rapidly; flourish

ex.en: My confidence began to burgeon.
ex.cn: 我的信心开始迅速增强。

m: rhymes with SURGEON, but for growth — to BURGEON = to grow rapidly. — burgeon, to grow rapidly.

k.syn: caricature, imitate, mock, parody, spoof, travesty
k.cn: /v. 夸张滑稽地模仿以嘲弄他人的文学艺术作品，恶搞
k.en: to copy or exaggerate（someone or something）in order to make fun of

ex.en: Burlesquing the teacher's nervous tic isn't very nice.
ex.cn: 恶搞老师紧张的痉挛不是好的行为。

m: BURL + ESQUE — "BURly mockery" — exaggerated mocking style (also a comedic stage genre). — burlesque, a comic parody.

k.syn: buff, polish, furbish, grind, smoothen
k.en: to make smooth or glossy usually by repeatedly applying surface pressure

ex.en: burnish the knife
ex.cn: 磨光刀

m: BURNISH contains BURN — to BURN-ish = polish until it almost BURNs your eyes with shine. — burnish, to polish.

k.en: a solid emulsion of fat globules, air, and water used as food

m: BUTTER — also "to butter someone up" = flatter. — butter, dairy spread / to flatter.

k.syn: anchor, mainstay, pillar, reliance, standby
k.en: a projecting structure for supporting or giving stability to a wall or building

k.syn: back, bolster, corroborate, reinforce, substantiate, shore up
k.ant: contravene, challenge 反对，质疑
k.cn: 为…提供支撑的证据或者信息
k.en: to provide evidence or information for（as a claim or idea）

ex.en: A mass of circumstantial evidence buttresses the prosecutor's case.
ex.cn: 大量证据都支持起诉人的案件。

m: BUTTRESS contains BUTT — a stone butt-end pressed against the cathedral wall to hold it up. — buttress, to support.

k.en: a line at the beginning of a news story, magazine article, or book giving the author's name

m: BY + LINE — the line saying "BY [author]" under the article title. — byline, the author credit line.

k.syn: convoluted, intricate, involved, labyrinthine, sophisticated, tangled
k.ant: straightforward, plain, simple, uncomplicated 直截了当的，不复杂的
k.en: complicated or secretive, having many parts or aspects that are usually interrelated

ex.en: a bill to simplify the byzantine tax structure
ex.cn: 一项试图简化繁琐税收制度的提案

m: from the Byzantine Empire's intricate court politics — anything BYZANTINE is mazelike and complex. — byzantine, intricately complex.

k.syn: stash, stockpile, store, deposit, hoard, reserve
k.en: a supply stored up and often hidden away

ex.en: maintain a cache of food in case of emergencies
ex.cn: 保存食物，以防万一

k.syn: conceal, ensconce, secrete, squirrel away
k.ant: display, exhibit 展示
k.en: to put into a hiding place

ex.en: The fugitive slaves were cached in their cellar until they could make their way to Canada.
ex.cn: 奴隶们被藏匿在房间的地下室直到他们能够顺利逃去加拿大。

m: a CACHE is a stash — hidden supplies ("cache of weapons"). — cache, a hidden stash.

k.syn: blare, bluster, clamor, din, discordance, racket
k.ant: quiet, silence, still, stillness 安静
k.en: loud, confused, and usually inharmonious sound

ex.en: the cacophony of a pet store full of animals
ex.cn: 宠物商店里各种动物叽叽呱呱的声音

m: CACO- (bad) + PHONY (sound) — bad sound = harsh dissonance. — cacophony, harsh discordant noise.

k.ant: earn 赚钱
k.en: beg, sponge

ex.en: cadge a free cup of coffee
ex.cn: 讨到一杯免费的咖啡

m: rhymes with BADGE — a CADGER cadges drinks instead of paying. — cadge, to beg / mooch.

k.syn: blandish, blarney, palaver, wheedle, soft-soap, sweet-talk
k.en: to urge with gentle and repeated appeals, teasing, or flattery; wheedle

ex.en: He cajoled her into doing his laundry for him.
ex.cn: 他哄骗她帮他洗衣服。

m: contains JOLLY — be all jolly and sweet to coax someone. ca-JOLLY = cajole. — cajole, to coax with flattery.

k.syn: harden, ossify
k.ant: make malleable, make pliant, make more flexible 使灵活
k.en: to make inflexible or unchangeable

ex.en: a leg that calcified
ex.cn: 一条麻木僵直的腿

m: from CALCIUM — to harden into calcium-like rigidity, become inflexible. — calcify, to harden / become rigid.

k.ant: unstandardize 使不标准
k.en: to standardize（as a measuring instrument）by determining the deviation from a standard so as to ascertain the proper correction factors

ex.en: calibrate the polling procedures to ensure objectivity
ex.cn: 为保证客观性而使投票过程标准化

k.en: to measure precisely especially: to measure against a standard

k.syn: adjust, align, gauge, graduate, measure
k.en: to check, adjust, or determine by comparison with a standard（the graduations of a quantitative measuring instrument）

ex.en: We need to calibrate the sextants navigation.
ex.cn: 我们要为航行校准六分仪。

m: contains CALIBER — to set the caliber/precision of an instrument. — calibrate, to fine-tune to standard.

k.syn: longhand, manuscript, penmanship, script
k.en: artistic, stylized, or elegant handwriting or lettering

ex.en: She specializes in scrollwork with beautiful calligraphy.
ex.cn: 她很擅长花体字。

m: CALLI- (beautiful) + GRAPHY (writing) — beautiful writing. — calligraphy, decorative handwriting.

k.syn: affectless, uncharitable, unsparing, remorseless, indurate, ruthless
k.ant: sympathetic, compassionate, merciful, tender, warmhearted 有同情心的
k.cn: 无同情心的, 冷漠的
k.en: emotionally hardened; unfeeling

ex.en: a callous indifference to the suffering of others
ex.cn: 对他人痛苦的漠不关心

m: rhymes with CALLUS — a callus is a hardened patch of skin; a callous person has a hardened heart. — callous, unfeeling / hardened.

k.syn: green, immature, inexperienced, juvenile, unfledged, unripened, puerile
k.ant: adult, experienced, grown-up, mature, ripe 成熟的
k.en: lacking in adult experience or maturity

ex.en: callow young man
ex.cn: 未经世事的年轻人

m: a CALLOW youth is unfledged — like a baby bird with no feathers yet. — callow, immature.

k.en: a thickening of or a hard thickened area on skin

m: a CALLUS is a hardened patch of skin from friction. — callus, hardened skin.

k.en: the unit of heat or energy value in food; a measure of the energy in food

m: a CALORIE — a unit of food energy. — calorie, energy unit.

k.syn: asperse, blacken, defame, libel, malign, smear, traduce, vilify
k.ant: approbation 嘉许
k.der: calumny n. 诽谤，中伤：a false statement maliciously made to injure another's reputation
k.en: to utter maliciously false statements, charges, or imputations about

m: contains CALUMNY — to spread calumny (slander). — calumniate, to slander.

k.syn: brotherhood, comradeship, fellowship
k.ant: enmity 敌意
k.en: a spirit of friendly good-fellowship

ex.en: There is great camaraderie among the teammates.
ex.cn: 组员之间有着深厚的情谊。

m: from COMRADE — the warm fellowship of comrades. — camaraderie, warm friendship.

k.cn: 栩栩如生的描绘，简洁生动的描述或刻画
k.en: a brief, vivid portrayal or depiction

ex.en: a literary cameo
ex.cn: 文学描写

k.en: to make a brief but dramatic appearance, as in a film

ex.en: She cameoed as Anne Boleyn in A Man for All Seasons.
ex.cn: 在《四季之人》中她客串安妮·博林。

m: CAM + EO — a CAMera EO(brief)-shot, a brief vivid scene. — cameo, brief vivid portrayal.

k.syn: costume, guise, cloak, dress up
k.ant: unmask 揭露
k.en: behavior or artifice designed to deceive or hide

ex.en: The soldiers must wear protective jungle camouflage while on patrol.
ex.cn: 士兵们在巡逻的时候必须穿上迷彩服来保护自身安全。

m: CAMOU ≈ CAMO (military pattern) + FLAGE (hidden flag) — soldiers hide their FLAG with CAMO paint. — camouflage, disguise / concealment.

k.syn: story, whisper
k.en: an unfounded or false, deliberately misleading story.

ex.en: It's a popular canard that the actress died under scandalous circumstances.
ex.cn: 有一种广泛的说法声称，女演员死于绯闻的压力。

m: a CANARD is a fake rumor (French for "duck" — "selling a duck" was old slang for cheating). — canard, an unfounded rumor.

k.syn: bluntness, forthrightness, frankness, unreservedness, straightforwardness
k.ant: artifice, mendacity, dissembling, dissimulation, indirection 谎言，不直接
k.en: unreserved, honest, or sincere expression

ex.en: The members of the rock band speak with candor about their recent squabbling.
ex.cn: 摇滚乐队成员对于他们最近的内讧直言不讳。

m: from CANDID — frank, open honesty. — candor, frank honesty.

k.syn: dogma
k.en: a basis for judgment; a standard or criterion

ex.en: the canons of polite society
ex.cn: 文明社会的法规

k.ant: abase, degrade, demean, humiliate 贬低 “考G给予我们的，并不仅仅是一次拼搏和成功，而是向着更远更大目标的无畏与洒脱。” （蒋喆，2008年6月，Verbal 710，Quantitative 800，AW 5. 0，录取院校：哈佛大学工程与应用科学）
k.der: canonize v. 作为神圣的来对待，使神圣化：to treat as sacred; glorify
k.syn: adore, adulate, idolize, deify, worship
k.en: the authentic works of a writer

m: CANON rhymes with CANNON, both authoritative — but CANON is the authoritative book/list. — canon, an authoritative standard.

k.en: a cloth covering

m: CAN + OPY — a CAN opened upside-down OPens like a P(eaked) tent over you. (Or: "can-O-py" — a circular cover.) — canopy, overhead cover.

k.en: a piece of cloth framed as a surface for a painting; to solicit votes or opinions

m: CANVAS contains CAN — the artist CAN paint on heavy CANVAS fabric. — canvas, the painter's fabric.

k.en: to examine carefully or discuss thoroughly; scrutinize

ex.en: The evidence had been repeatedly canvassed in American courts.
ex.cn: 该项证据已在美国的法庭中反复被审查过。

k.syn: interview, poll, solicit, survey
k.cn: 游说，去（一个地区）的各处或找到（个人）来拉选票或订单
k.en: to go through（a region）or go to（persons）to solicit votes or orders

ex.en: canvass voters
ex.cn: 拉选票

m: CANVASS rhymes with CANVAS — but here you spread out (like a canvas) across a district to gather opinions. — canvass, to solicit / examine carefully.

k.en: a deep narrow valley

m: CANYON contains CAN — picture a giant tin CAN cut open into a deep gorge. — canyon, a deep gorge.

k.syn: budge, concede, relent, submit, succumb, surrender, knuckle under
k.ant: resist, oppose 抵抗
k.en: to give up all resistance; acquiesce; yield

ex.en: One side finally capitulated when it became clear that they couldn't win the argument.
ex.cn: 意识到他们不能拿下这场辩论后，该方投降了。

m: contains CAPIT (head) — to give up your head, surrender. — capitulate, to surrender.

k.syn: crank, fancy, vagary, vagrancy, whimsy
k.en: an impulsive change of mind

ex.en: An out-of-character caprice led him to take the day off from work and go to the beach.
ex.cn: 一时冲动让他决定休假一天，去海滩度假。

k.syn: freakishness, impulsiveness, whimsicalness
k.ant: steadfast, resolute, constant, pertinacious 坚定不变的
k.der: capricious adj. 反复无常的，善变的
k.en: an inclination to change one's mind impulsively

ex.en: His knack for picking racetrack winners appears to owe as much to caprice as it does to a canny assessment of horseflesh.
ex.cn: 他赌马屡赌屡赢，似乎要归功于自己的反复无常而不是对于赛马本身独到精明的评估。

m: CAP + RICE — your CAP is full of RICE one moment, then dumped the next — a sudden whim. (Or: shares CAPRI- with CAPRICCIO, a musical whim.) — caprice, a whim.

k.syn: allure, beguile, bewitch, enchant, fascinate, magnetize
k.ant: repulse 使反感
k.en: to attract and hold by charm, beauty, or excellence

ex.en: I was captivated by her brilliant mind.
ex.cn: 我被她的才华横溢所吸引。

m: contains CAPTIVE — to make someone a captive of your charm. — captivate, to charm utterly.

k.syn: dominant, overbearing, overriding, paramount, preeminent, primal, supreme
k.ant: minor 次要的
k.en: of foremost importance; paramount

ex.en: the cardinal rule of medicine: do no harm
ex.cn: 药物的首要标准就是无害

m: the CARDINAL rule = the chief rule (also: a cardinal in red robes; a cardinal direction). — cardinal, of primary importance.

k.en: a wedge-shaped mark made on written or printed matter to indicate

m: CARET ≈ CARROT (^-shaped tip) — proofreaders point with a carrot-tip ^. — caret, the insertion mark ^.

k.ant: lighthearted 心情愉快的
k.en: showing the effect of grief or anxiety

ex.en: a careworn face
ex.cn: 忧心忡忡的脸

m: CARE + WORN — worn down by cares. — careworn, exhausted by worry.

k.cn: 用讽刺歪曲等手法的夸张，漫画，讽刺画
k.en: exaggeration by means of often ludicrous distortion of parts or characteristics

k.syn: burlesque, imitate, mock, parody, spoof, travesty
k.en: to copy or exaggerate（someone or something）in order to make fun of

ex.en: caricature the supervisor's distinctive walk
ex.cn: 模仿督导员独特的走路方式

m: CARI + CATURE — a "CARry-CATure" sketch that exaggerates one feature comically. (Or: contains CARI ≈ CARTOON.) — caricature, an exaggerated portrayal.

k.syn: corporal, corporeal, fleshly, material, somatic
k.ant: spiritual 精神的
k.en: relating to the physical

ex.en: seen with carnal eyes
ex.cn: 用肉眼看

ex.en: carnal remains
ex.cn: 遗体

k.syn: earthborn, mundane, temporal, terrestrial, worldly
k.en: worldly

ex.en: a carnal mind
ex.cn: 凡心

m: contains CARN (flesh, as in "carnivore") — relating to bodily desires. — carnal, of the flesh / sensual.

k.syn: binge, revel, roister, wassail
k.en: to drink liquor freely or excessively

ex.en: stay at home instead of going out and carousing with friends
ex.cn: 呆在家里而不是出去和朋友们狂饮作乐

m: rhymes with AROUSE — drinkers AROUSE the night with carousing. — carouse, to drink and revel.

k.syn: cavil, fuss, niggle, nitpick, gripe, grizzle, grouch, grouse, grumble, wail
k.cn: 对小事吹毛求疵，挑剔，表现不满
k.en: to make often peevish criticisms or objections about matters that are minor, unimportant, or irrelevant; to express dissatisfaction, pain, or resentment usually tiresomely

ex.en: carp about the order of names on the wedding invitations
ex.cn: 对于婚礼请柬上的名单顺序吹毛求疵

m: to CARP — like the fish CARP, you nibble at small things constantly. — carp, to complain pettily.

k.en: one that makes maps; a mapmaker

m: CARTO (map) + GRAPHER (drawer) — a map-maker. — cartographer, a map-maker.

k.syn: sculpture
k.en: to create a three-dimensional representation of（something）using solid material

ex.en: carve a statue out of rare marble
ex.cn: 用纯的大理石雕刻一尊雕像

k.syn: forge, grind out, thrash out, work out, work up
k.cn: （通过长期不懈努力）开创，开拓
k.en: to produce or bring about especially by long or repeated effort

ex.en: He finally carved out a niche for the sport in the school's athletic program.
ex.cn: 他终于在学校运动计划中寻找到了自己的定位。

ex.en: carve out a way through the enemy
ex.cn: 从敌营中杀出一条血路

m: to CARVE a path through wilderness = to create through effort. — carve, to cut / create through effort.

k.en: a set of characters or persons

k.syn: He was cast in the leading role. 他被选为男一号。
k.en: to assign（as an actor）to a role or part

k.syn: discharge, emanate, irradiate, issue, shoot, throw out, give out
k.en: to put forth, give off, to place as if by throwing

ex.en: cast doubt on their reliability
ex.cn: 对它们的可靠性提出质疑

k.syn: ditch, dump, jettison, toss
k.en: to get rid of as useless or unwanted

ex.en: Once she became rich and didn't need them anymore, she cast off all her old friends like so much junk.
ex.cn: 当她有钱了不再需要朋友了，她把老友像糟粕一样抛弃了。

m: shares same letters as ACTS — a play's CAST does the ACTS. (Also a CAST iron throw.) — cast, to throw / assign roles.

k.syn: berate, chastise, lambaste, reprimand, reproach, rebuke, vituperate, excoriate, rail（at or against）
k.ant: approbate, accolade, extol 认可，赞美
k.en: to criticize harshly and usually publicly

ex.en: The author castigated the prime minister as an ineffective leader.
ex.cn: 该作者谴责首相行政无作为。

k.syn: chasten, chastise, correct, discipline, penalize
k.ant: excuse, pardon, spare 宽恕
k.en: to inflict a penalty on for a fault or crime

ex.en: a judge who believes in castigating criminals to the full extent of the law
ex.cn: 认为应该最大程度地惩罚罪犯的法官

m: contains CAST (throw) — to throw harsh criticism at. — castigate, to scold severely.

k.syn: breed, beget, effectuate, engender, generate, prompt, spawn, yield, result in, bring（about）
k.ant: inhibitor 抑制剂
k.der: catalyst n. 催化剂：an agent that provokes or speeds significant change or action
k.en: to be the cause of（a situation, action, or state of mind）

ex.en: A reinstitution of the draft would catalyze protests around the country.
ex.cn: 草案的提出会激发全国范围的抗议。

ex.en: a catalyst for change of lifestyle
ex.cn: 生活方式变化的催化剂

m: contains CATALYST — to act as a catalyst, sparking change. — catalyze, to set off / spark.

k.syn: apocalypse, calamity, cataclysm, debacle, tragedy
k.en: the final event of the dramatic action especially of a tragedy

k.syn: bummer, debacle, disaster, fiasco, fizzle, washout
k.ant: blockbuster, hit, smash, success, winner 大胜
k.en: utter failure: fiasco

ex.en: The party was a catastrophe.
ex.cn: 该派对就是一个杯具。

m: shares STROPHE (turn) with APOSTROPHE — CATA- (down) + STROPHE = a downward turn = disaster. — catastrophe, a disaster.

k.syn: definite, downright, fair, utter, thorough, unalloyed, unconditional, unqualified
k.ant: conditional, qualified 有条件的
k.cn: 没有例外的，无条件限制的；绝对的
k.en: being without exception or qualification; absolute

ex.en: a categorical denial
ex.cn: 完全的否认

m: CATEGORICAL ≈ "in every CATEGORY" — admits no exceptions in any category, absolute. — categorical, absolute / unqualified.

k.syn: unlimited, unqualified, unrestricted, unspecialized, all-around（also all-round）
k.ant: narrow, limited, restricted, specialized 狭隘的
k.en: not limited or specialized in application or purpose

ex.en: a catholic taste in music
ex.cn: 对于音乐的兼容并包的品味

m: lowercase "catholic" means broad/universal (the Catholic Church claimed to be universal). — catholic, broadly inclusive.

k.ant: palliating 减缓的
k.en: capable of destroying or eating away by chemical action: corrosive

ex.en: The chemical was so caustic that it ate through the pipes.
ex.cn: 化学性质具有如此强的腐蚀性，把管道都腐蚀了。

k.syn: acerbic, acrid, barbed, mordant, pungent, sardonic, satiric, scathing, sharp
k.ant: genial, smooth, kind, innocuous 和蔼的，（言论行为等）无害的
k.en: marked by incisive sarcasm

ex.en: caustic movie reviews
ex.cn: 尖酸刻薄的影评

m: CAUSTIC chemicals burn; caustic remarks burn the listener. — caustic, biting / corrosive.

k.syn: warning, admonish
k.en: a warning of a specific limitation of something such as information or an agreement

ex.en: a final caveat
ex.cn: 最终的告诫

m: Latin for "let him beware" — "caveat emptor" = buyer beware. A CAVEAT is a warning. — caveat, a warning.

k.en: a large deep

m: rhymes with TAVERN — but a CAVERN is a giant cave, not a cozy tavern. — cavern, a large cave.

k.syn: carp, fuss, niggle, nitpick, quibble
k.en: to find fault unnecessarily; raise trivial objections: quibble

ex.en: Let us not cavil too much.
ex.cn: 我们别太挑剔。

ex.en: cavil about the price of a cup of coffee
ex.cn: 对一杯咖啡的价钱挑剔

m: rhymes with EVIL — a CAVILler's EVIL nitpicking. — cavil, to raise petty objections.

k.syn: caper, disport, frisk, gambol, rollick, romp
k.ant: trudge 蹒跚地走
k.cn: 欢快地蹦跳或行走，雀跃
k.en: to bound or prance about in a sprightly manner; caper

ex.en: Children are cavorting in the sand.
ex.cn: 孩子们在沙子中嬉戏。

m: CAV + ORT — "CAV(orting) horse" + ORT(rhymes with sport) — frolic like a sporting horse. — cavort, to frolic.

k.syn: relinquish, render, yield, renounce, resign, turn in, turn over, step aside（from）, give up, hand over, lay down
k.ant: possess 拥有
k.en: to surrender possession of, especially by treaty

ex.en: cede the island to America
ex.cn: 把岛屿割让给了美国

m: CEDE rhymes with SEED — give up your SEED, hand it over. — cede, to yield / surrender.

k.syn: cord, knot, link, tie
k.en: a uniting or binding force or influence

ex.en: Justice is the cement that holds a political community together.
ex.cn: 正义是让政治个体团结的粘合剂。

m: CEMENT rhymes with COMMITMENT — both bond things together. — cement, to bond firmly.

k.syn: bowdlerize, expurgate, red-pencil, clean up
k.cn: 审查并删除不良的东西
k.en: to examine in order to suppress or delete anything considered objectionable

ex.en: censor the news
ex.cn: 审查新闻

m: CENSOR rhymes with SENSOR — a SENSOR detects bad content; the CENSOR removes it. — censor, to remove offensive material.

k.ant: eulogistic 赞美的
k.en: highly critical

ex.en: censorious comment
ex.cn: 尖刻的评论

m: from CENSURE — quick to find fault and condemn. — censorious, harshly critical.

k.syn: condemn, denounce, objurgate, rebuke, reprimand, reproach, reprehend, pan
k.ant: commend, extol, laud, endorse 支持，赞同
k.en: to express public or formal disapproval of

ex.en: He was censured by the committee for his failure to report the problem.
ex.cn: 他因为未上报事故受到了委员会的指责。

m: CENSURE rhymes with PRESSURE — formal PRESSURE = official condemnation. — censure, formal condemnation.

k.syn: tale, tally
k.en: an official survey of the population of a country that is carried out in order to find out how many people live there and to obtain details of such things as people's ages and jobs

m: CENSUS rhymes with SENSE-US — official COUNT of who's here ("us"). — census, a population count.

k.syn: check, cutoff, closure, discontinuance, expiration, halt, shutdown, termination
k.ant: commencement, start, continuation 开始，继续
k.en: the stopping of a process or activity

ex.en: The cessation of the storm was a relief.
ex.cn: 暴风雪终于停了，真是让人欣慰。

m: from CEASE — a ceasing, a stop. — cessation, a stopping.

k.syn: banter, jape, jest, quip
k.en: to make jokes

ex.en: A coworker likes to chaff at others' expense, and this often results in hurt feelings.
ex.cn: 同事喜欢开别人玩笑，往往会伤害他人感情。

m: CHAFF — leftover wheat husks, worthless; to chaff someone = playful teasing of "worthless" stuff. — chaff, to tease / wheat husks.

k.syn: anxiety, concern, disquiet, inquietude, restiveness, solicitude, unease, unrest, worry
k.ant: aplomb, composure, countenance, equanimity, sangfroid 镇定自若，淡定
k.cn: （因为失败、出糗而导致的）不安，焦虑
k.en: disquietude or distress of mind caused by humiliation, disappointment, or failure

ex.en: Edgar's chagrin at having bungled the simple assignment was clear from his trembling lips and averted eyes.
ex.cn: 从埃德加颤抖的嘴唇和躲闪的目光中可以看出他搞砸简单任务之后的不安。

m: rhymes with GRIN (but the opposite) — chagrin is the burning embarrassment after public failure. — chagrin, vexed embarrassment.

k.syn: chancer, opportunist, temporizer, timeserver, trimmer, weathercock
k.en: a person who dexterously and expediently changes or adopts opinions

ex.en: At the summer resort he acquired a reputation as a social chameleon —someone who could be whatever his hosts wanted him to be.
ex.cn: 在度假胜地，他被称为“社交多面手”——可以变成主人想要的任何角色。

m: shares CHAM- with "CHANGE" feel — the CHAMELEON CHANGes color. — chameleon, a fickle / adaptable person.

k.syn: advocate, back, endorse, patronize, plump for
k.ant: disparage, impugn, oppose 贬低，反对
k.en: to fight for, defend, or support as a champion

ex.en: champion the cause of civil rights
ex.cn: 支持民权事业

m: CHAMPION rhymes with CHAMP — the CHAMP CHAMPIONS a cause. — champion, to defend / advocate.

k.syn: disarrangement, dishevelment, disorder, disarray, havoc, mess, muddle, jumble, welter
k.ant: strictly structured 构造严谨的
k.der: chaotic adj. 混乱的，无序的：happening in a state of complete disorder and confusion
k.en: a condition or place of great disorder or confusion

ex.en: The boy's room is in such chaos that it looks as though a tornado had struck.
ex.cn: 小男孩的房间如此混乱，看起来像台风来袭。

m: CHAOS rhymes with KAOS (cartoon evil agency) — total disorder. (Or: from Greek chaos = the abyss before creation.) — chaos, total disorder.

k.syn: disguise, façade, playacting, pretense, put-on, semblance
k.en: a display of emotion or behavior that is insincere or intended to deceive

ex.en: His concern was a charade.
ex.cn: 他的关心只是装模作样。

k.en: a game in which words or phrases are represented in pantomime

m: CHARADE rhymes with PARADE — both are showy displays; a charade is a fake parade of pretense. — charade, a pretense.

k.syn: fake, fraud, hoaxer, mountebank, phony, pretender, quack, imposter
k.en: a person who makes elaborate, fraudulent, and often voluble claims to skill or knowledge; a quack or fraud

ex.en: The famed broker turned out to be a charlatan.
ex.cn: 一位有声望的经纪人结果是个骗子。

m: CHARLATAN rhymes with CHARLES (and his fake titles) — a fraud claiming false expertise. — charlatan, a fake expert.

k.syn: alert, cautious, circumspect, conservative, gingerly, guarded, heedful, wary
k.ant: rash, bold 鲁莽的
k.en: very cautious

ex.en: chary investors who weren't burned by the dot-com bust
ex.cn: 那些没有受到互联网萧条影响的谨慎投资者们

m: rhymes with WARY — both mean cautious. — chary, cautious / wary.

k.syn: contradiction, dissent, disjunction, discord, conflict, rift, rivalry, dichotomy
k.cn: 分歧，（意见、利益或忠诚上的）明显差异
k.en: a pronounced difference of opinion, interests, or loyalty

m: CHASM rhymes with SPASM — both involve a sudden gap/break. — chasm, a deep gap.

k.syn: jingoist, jingoistic, nationalistic, superpatriotic
k.en: having or showing excessive favoritism towards one's own country

ex.en: At times I have also been aggressive, chauvinistic and hot-tempered.
ex.cn: 我曾经非常好斗，热烈爱国，还脾气暴躁。

m: from Nicolas Chauvin, an over-the-top patriot — CHAUVINISTIC = excessively biased toward one's own group. — chauvinistic, blindly partisan.

k.syn: arrest, stall, bridle, contain, curb, tame, bring up, draw up, hold up, pull up
k.ant: propagate, goad, hasten 扩增，驱使，促进
k.en: to arrest the motion abruptly

ex.en: A tree finally checked the skidding car.
ex.cn: 一辆滑行的车终于被大树停住了。

k.syn: accord, cohere, conform, correspond, dovetail, fit, harmonize, jibe, tally
k.ant: differ, disagree with 反对
k.en: to be in agreement on every point

ex.en: Their story of what happened checks with the report of the eyewitness.
ex.cn: 他们的说法和目击者的供词一致。

m: CHECK rhymes with NECK — to CHECK something is to put a halt at its NECK. — check, to halt / verify.

k.en: a skilled cook who manages the kitchen of a restaurant or hotel

m: shares CHEF with the French "chef" (head/chief) — head of the kitchen. — chef, head cook.

k.ant: fiendish, devilish 恶魔般的
k.en: innocent-looking usually chubby and rosy

ex.en: A representation of Cupid as a naked, cherubic boy usually is used as a symbol of love.
ex.cn: 丘比特作为小天使般的少年裸体画像被用作爱的象征。

m: from CHERUB (plump baby angel) — sweetly innocent-looking. — cherubic, angelically sweet.

k.en: the interplay or contrast

m: Italian: CHIARO (bright) + SCURO (dark) — the painting technique of strong light/dark contrast. — chiaroscuro, light/dark contrast in art.

k.syn: deception, artifice, legerdemain, wile, subterfuge
k.ant: aboveboard action 光明正大的行为；honest dealing诚实的行为； forthrightness坦白
k.en: deception by artful subterfuge or sophistry

ex.en: Well-doer never does chicanery and person who is good at chicanery does not belong well-doer.
ex.cn: 善者不辩，辩者不善。

ex.en: He wasn't above using chicanery to win votes.
ex.cn: 他不是用欺骗来赢得选票的。

m: CHICANE + RY — a "chicane" is a tricky bend in racing; chicanery is a tricky maneuver. — chicanery, trickery.

k.syn: reprove, reprimand, reproach, tick off
k.ant: praise, commend 称赞
k.en: to scold mildly so as to correct or improve

ex.en: My wife chided me for forgetting to offer our guests some refreshments.
ex.cn: 我妻子责备我忘记给客人们上点心。

m: CHIDE rhymes with HIDE — kids HIDE when adults CHIDE them. — chide, to gently scold.

k.en: an organized company of singers

m: CHOIR contains CHOI ≈ "CHOICE" voices selected to sing together. — choir, a singing group.

k.syn: crabby, cranky, irascible, peevish, petulant, bad-tempered, hot- tempered, short-tempered, testy
k.ant: difficult to provoke, pacific, placid, calm, serene, tranquil, composed, nonchalant 难以被激怒的，平静的
k.der: choler n. 易怒：ready disposition to irritation: irascibility also:
k.en: easily angered; bad-tempered

ex.en: choleric disposition
ex.cn: 易怒的性情

ex.en: Men of the choleric type take to kicking and smashing.
ex.cn: 易怒的人喜欢踢和打碎东西。

m: from CHOLERA — but the temperament: hot-tempered, easily angered (one of the four humors). — choleric, hot-tempered.

k.syn: accord, agree, conform, consist, correspond, dovetail, fit, harmonize, jibe, rhyme, square, tally
k.ant: differ from, disagree with 与……不同，有分歧
k.en: to be in accord; agree

ex.en: The revised system chords perfectly with the original goals.
ex.cn: 新版的系统非常符合最初的目标。
