m: to STRIKE a CHORD = to harmonize. — chord, to harmonize / be in agreement.

k.en: the art of creating and arranging dances

m: CHORE (chorus) + GRAPHY (writing) — the writing/design of dance. — choreography, dance design.

k.en: an organized company of singers

m: CHORUS contains CHO + RUS — voices RUSH together CHO-rd-like. — chorus, a singing group.

k.syn: colored, colorful, motley, multicolored, multihued, varicolored, variegated, kaleidoscopic
k.ant: colorless, pallid, blanched 无色的；monochromatic, monochromic, monotone, self-colored 单色的
k.en: relating to colors or color

ex.en: the chromatic paintings of Matisse and the other Fauvists
ex.cn: 马蒂斯和其他野兽派画家的彩色画

m: CHROMA (color) — colored. — chromatic, colored / colorful.

k.syn: frequent, usual, routine
k.ant: sporadic 偶然发生的；infrequent 不经常的
k.en: marked by long duration or frequent recurrence

ex.en: chronic disease
ex.cn: 慢性病

k.syn: inveterate
k.cn: 习惯性的，不可能改变的
k.en: being such by habit and not likely to change

ex.en: a chronic smoker who has quit many times
ex.cn: 一个戒了很多次烟的老烟民

m: CHRONIC pain is constant; CHRON- = time. — chronic, persistent over time.

k.en: to laugh inwardly or with quiet amusement; a soft laugh

ex.en: He chucked to himself as he read the newspaper.
ex.cn: 他在读报时暗暗发笑

m: CHUCKLE rhymes with KNUCKLE — a soft laugh, not a knee-slapper. — chuckle, a soft laugh.

k.syn: crude, coarse, boorish, loutish, uncultured, unpolished
k.der: churlish adj. 粗野的，暴躁的：of, like, or befitting a churl; boorish or vulgar
k.ant: genteel, complaisant, courtly, polished 文雅的，彬彬有礼的； sophisticated 机智圆滑的
k.en: a rude, boorish person

ex.en: By the 19th century,“churl”had a new and pejorative meaning,“one inclined to uncivil or loutish behavior”. 19
ex.cn: 世纪的时候，churl有了一个新的带有贬义的意思，即“一个粗鲁没有教养的人”。

k.syn: miser, niggard, skinflint, penny-pincher
k.ant: generous/liberal/munificent person 慷慨的人；waster, wastrel, spendthrift, prodigal, profligate, dissipater 败家子
k.en: a mean grasping person who is usually stingy with money

ex.en: Don't bother asking a churl for donations.
ex.cn: 别自找麻烦向一个吝啬鬼要捐款。

m: CHURL rhymes with HURL — a CHURL HURLs rude remarks. — churl, a rude person.

k.en: a devotee of motion pictures

m: CINE (cinema) + AST — a movie buff. — cineast, a film enthusiast.

k.syn: dwarf, half-pint, insect, insignificancy, lightweight, morsel, nonentity, nothing, nullity, snippersnapper, whippersnapper, zero
k.ant: big shot, big wheel, bigwig, eminence, figure, personage 要人，名人
k.en: a person of no importance or influence

ex.en: The intern is a mere cipher in the company.
ex.cn: 这个实习生在公司里是个无足轻重的小人物。

k.syn: compute, work out
k.en: to determine（a value）by doing the necessary mathematical operations

ex.en: We were surprised by how much we had spent on the cruise after we had ciphered out the grand total.
ex.cn: 当我们算出我们沉船游览的总花费时，我们都惊呆了。

k.syn: code, secret message
k.en: a method of transforming a text in order to conceal its meaning

ex.en: convert their messages into cipher
ex.cn: 把他们的信息转换成密码

m: CIPHER rhymes with ZERO-pher — a ZERO (a nobody) or a coded ZERO-and-one message. — cipher, a code / a nobody.

k.syn: indirect, circular, roundabout
k.ant: straightforwardness, direction 直接
k.der: circuity n. 不直接：lack of straightforwardness
k.en: not being forthright or direct in language or action

ex.en: We took a circuitous route to the airport so as to avoid the massive traffic jam.
ex.cn: 我们走了一条迂回的路线去机场来躲避堵车。

k.syn: circumlocutory, diffuse, long-winded, prolix, rambling, verbose, windy
k.ant: compact, concise, pithy, succinct, terse 简洁的
k.en: using or containing more words than necessary to express an idea

ex.en: a circuitous explanation for what seems like a fairly basic concept
ex.cn: 对一个非常基本的概念的冗长解释

m: contains CIRCUIT — a circuitous route circles around instead of going straight. — circuitous, indirect / roundabout.

k.en: having the form of a circle；

m: from CIRCLE — round-shaped. — circular, ring-shaped.

k.en: the perimeter of a circle; the distance around a circular or curved object

m: CIRCUM- (around) + FERENCE (carrying) — the distance carried around a circle. — circumference, distance around a circle.

k.syn: equivocation, shuffle, tergiversation
k.ant: direct 直接的
k.en: the use of unnecessarily wordy and indirect language, evasion in speech

ex.en: The other son of your parents' is a circumlocution for your brother.
ex.cn: 你父母的“另一个儿子”是你“兄弟”的绕圈子的说法。

k.syn: diffuseness, diffusion, long-windedness, prolixity, redundancy, verbalism, verboseness, verbosity, windiness, wordage, wordiness
k.ant: conciseness, concision, pithiness, succinctness, terseness 简洁
k.en: the use of too many words to express an idea

ex.en: Your papers have to be five pages long, but that's five pages of substance, not circumlocution.
ex.cn: 你的论文要求是5页，但是那5页都是实质内容，不是废话。

m: CIRCUM- (around) + LOCUTION (speech) — talking around the topic. — circumlocution, roundabout speech.

k.syn: cap, limit, confine, delimit, restrict
k.ant: exceed 超过，超越
k.en: to limit narrowly; restrict

ex.en: Teammates circumscribed his enthusiasm so as not to make the losing side feel worse.
ex.cn: 队员们抑制了他们的热情，为了不让失败者更难过。

k.syn: surround, encompass
k.en: to surround by or as if by a boundary

ex.en: fields circumscribed by tall trees
ex.cn: 被高树包围的地方

ex.en: Circumscribe a circle around a square.
ex.cn: 画正方形的外接圆。

m: CIRCUM- (around) + SCRIBE (draw) — to draw a line around, restrict. — circumscribe, to restrict.

k.syn: alert, careful, gingerly, guarded, heedful, prudent, cautious, chary, wary
k.ant: careless, incautious, unmindful, unwary 不小心的，不谨慎的； audacious, reckless 大胆的，鲁莽的
k.en: careful to consider all circumstances and possible consequences: prudent

ex.en: The banks should have been more circumspect in their dealings.
ex.cn: 银行本应该在它们的交易当中更加谨慎。

m: CIRCUM- (around) + SPECT (look) — looking around carefully before acting. — circumspect, cautious.

k.syn: avoid, bypass, dodge, sidestep, skirt, get around
k.ant: comply with, follow, obey, observe 遵从；confront, directly encounter 直接面对
k.en: to avoid having to comply with（something）especially through cleverness

ex.en: circumvent all the red tape
ex.cn: 绕过所有官方程序繁文缛节

ex.en: He found a way to circumvent the law.
ex.cn: 他发现了一个逃避法律的方法。

m: CIRCUM- (around) + VENT (come) — to come around, bypass. — circumvent, to evade / get around.

k.en: an artificial reservoir for storing liquids

m: CISTERN rhymes with SISTER — but a CISTERN holds water (not your sister). Contains CIST ≈ "system" of water storage. — cistern, water-storage tank.

k.syn: politeness, courtesy, politeness, genteelness, gentility, graciousness
k.ant: discourteousness, discourtesy, impoliteness, incivility, rudeness, surliness, ungraciousness 无礼，粗鲁
k.en: courteous behavior; politeness

ex.en: They greeted us with civility.
ex.cn: 他们有礼貌地招呼我们。

m: from CIVIL — polite courtesy. — civility, politeness.

k.syn: call for, command, quest
k.ant: renounce 放弃
k.en: to ask for especially as a right

ex.en: a fragile claim to fame
ex.cn: 对名誉不切实际的要求

ex.en: After many years had passed, he suddenly appeared to claim his inheritance.
ex.cn: 很多年已经过去，他突然出现并要求继承遗产。

k.syn: allege, assert
k.ant: deny, gainsay 否认
k.en: to state as a fact usually forcefully

ex.en: People claim that they have been kidnapped by aliens.
ex.cn: 人们断言，他们被外星人绑架了。

k.ant: animate 使有生命
k.en: to deprive of life

ex.en: Cancer claims hundreds of thousands of Americans each year.
ex.cn: 癌症每年要剥夺成千上万美国人的生命。

k.en: a legal right to participation in the advantages, profits, and responsibility of something

ex.en: A shareholder has a claim in the business.
ex.cn: 股东在企业中有权利（收益权、选举权等）。

m: CLAIM rhymes with PROCLAIM — both involve declaring strongly. — claim, to demand / assert.

k.syn: secret, covert, furtive, surreptitious, sneaky, stealthy, undercover, underground, underhand, underhanded
k.ant: open, overt, public 公开的；aboveboard 光明正大的
k.en: kept or done in secret, often in order to conceal an illicit or improper purpose

ex.en: their clandestine love affair
ex.cn: 他们的秘密恋情

m: rhymes with PRISTINE — but secret: CLANDESTINE meetings happen in pristine secrecy. — clandestine, secret.

k.ant: soft and indistinct 柔和模糊的
k.en: loud and clear

ex.en: clarion call for democracy
ex.cn: 对民主的高声呼吁

ex.en: The internationale is a clarion call to the labouring people of the world.
ex.cn: 《国际歌》是唤起全世界劳动人民的响亮号角。

m: CLARION contains CLAR (clear) — a CLEARLY ringing trumpet call. — clarion, loudly clear.

k.syn: purify, clear, elucidate, explain, illuminate, illustrate
k.ant: obfuscate, obscure 使模糊
k.der: clarify v. 澄清，使清晰：to free of confusion
k.en: the quality or state of being clear: lucidity

ex.en: Clarity of diction is vital for a XDF teacher.
ex.cn: 发音清楚对新东方老师来说是至关重要的。

ex.en: clarify his mind
ex.cn: 理清思路；clarify a subject 澄清某一问题

k.syn: clearness, limpidity, limpidness, translucence, translucency, transparency
k.ant: cloudiness, opacity, opaqueness, turbidity, turbidness 浑浊，不透明
k.en: the state or quality of being easily seen through

ex.en: mountain streams with water of incredible clarity
ex.cn: 难以置信的清澈透明的山涧

m: from CLEAR — clearness. — clarity, clearness.

k.syn: clench, grapple, grasp, grip, handgrip, handhold
k.en: the act or manner of holding

ex.en: Be careful that your clasp on the cat isn't too tight, or she could get hurt.
ex.cn: 注意别抓那只猫太紧，否则她会受伤的。

m: CLASP rhymes with GRASP — both mean grip firmly. — clasp, to grip firmly.

k.en: a usually V-shaped

ex.en: a narrow cleft in the rocks
ex.cn: 岩石上的小裂缝

m: CLEFT — past tense of CLEAVE — what's been cleaved is a cleft. — cleft, a split.

k.syn: charitable, lenient, merciful
k.ant: harsh, severe, stern, strict 严厉的，严格的
k.en: tolerant and kind in the judgment of and expectations for others

ex.en: Clement judge reduced the sentence.
ex.cn: 仁慈的法官减轻了刑罚。

k.syn: mild, genial, gentle, balmy, equable
k.ant: harsh, inclement, severe 严酷的 cliché［kli:'∫eɪ］
k.en: marked by temperatures that are neither too high nor too low

ex.en: Hawaii is known for its delightfully clement climate.
ex.cn: 夏威夷以它宜人的温和气候著称。

k.syn: banality, bromide, platitude; trite, bathetic, hackneyed, stereotypical
k.ant: fresh, new, original, creative 新的，创造性的
k.en: a hackneyed theme, characterization, or situation

ex.en: Cliché is a feature of bad news.
ex.cn: 拙劣新闻的特征是使用陈词滥调。

m: from CLEMENCY — merciful, also (of weather) mild. — clement, merciful / mild.

k.en: a small exclusive group of friends

m: CLIQUE rhymes with CLICK — a CLICK is the sound of cliques excluding outsiders ("click of disapproval"). — clique, an exclusive small group.

k.syn: balk, bar, block, deterrent, drag, fetter, holdback, hurdle, impediment, inhibition, interference, obstacle, obstruction, shackles, stop, stumbling block,
k.en: something that makes movement or progress difficult

ex.en: impede with a clog
ex.cn: 用障碍物阻止

m: CLOG rhymes with FOG — both block flow (clog blocks pipes, fog blocks vision). — clog, an obstruction.

k.syn: array, assemblage, band, block, bunch, cluster, clutch, collection, constellation, grouping, huddle, knot, lot, muster, package
k.en: a number of things considered as a unit

ex.en: A clot of daisies occupied one corner of the flower bed.
ex.cn: 一簇雏菊占据了花床的一角。

k.syn: block, choke, clog, gum up, jam, obstruct, occlude, stop up, stuff
k.ant: clear, free, open up, unblock, unclog, unstop清除，排除障碍
k.en: to prevent passage through by filling with something

k.syn: congeal, jell, jelly
k.en: to turn from a liquid into a substance resembling jelly

ex.en: Scabs form over cuts when your blood starts to clot.
ex.cn: 当血液开始凝结的时候，伤口处会形成血痂。

m: CLOT rhymes with KNOT — a blood KNOT that blocks the vessel. — clot, to thicken / clump.

k.cn: 以投票的方式快速结束讨论
k.en: the closing of debate in a legislative body especially by calling for an immediate vote

m: CLOTURE contains CLOT + URE — vote to CLOT (block off) further debate. — cloture, ending of debate by vote.

k.syn: downfall, downpour
k.en: a sudden copious rainfall

ex.en: The weatherman warned of possible cloudbursts in the afternoon.
ex.cn: 天气预报员警告下午可能有暴雨。

m: CLOUD + BURST — a cloud bursting open with sudden heavy rain. — cloudburst, sudden heavy downpour.

k.syn: power, influence, capacity, heft, leverage
k.ant: impuissance, impotence 无力
k.en: influence; pull

ex.en: The queen may have privilege but she has no real political clout.
ex.cn: 女王有特权，但无真正的政治影响力。

m: CLOUT rhymes with SHOUT — having clout means your SHOUT moves things. — clout, influence / power.

k.syn: lovey-dovey, maudlin, mawkish, saccharine, sentimental
k.ant: unsentimental 不动感情的
k.cn: 甜得发腻的；感情用事的
k.en: excessively sweet; sentimental

ex.en: the cloying sentiments of so many Mother's Day cards
ex.cn: 这么多母亲节贺卡（所蕴含）的甜蜜感情

m: rhymes with ANNOYING — too sweet, like ten desserts at once. — cloying, sickly sweet.

k.syn: awkward, ham-handed, heavy-handed, maladroit, unhandy, bungling, inept
k.ant: adroit, deft, dexterous, handy 熟练的，灵巧的
k.en: lacking or showing a lack of nimbleness in using one's hands; a lack of skill and tact

ex.en: turn out to be a clumsy sleight of hand
ex.cn: 弄巧成拙

k.syn: awkward, graceless, ungainly, gauche, inelegant, rustic, ungraceful
k.ant: graceful, urbane, refined 优雅的
k.en: lacking social grace and assurance; showing an inability to move in a graceful manner

ex.en: be clumsy on the dance floor
ex.cn: 在舞池里表现不优雅的

k.syn: rough, unrefined
k.ant: refined 精致的
k.en: hastily or roughly constructed

ex.en: a clumsy mock-up of the real thing
ex.cn: 一个粗糙的实物模型

m: CLUMSY contains CLUM ≈ "klutz" sound — a klutzy person trips over their own feet. — clumsy, awkward.

k.cn: 凝结剂（使液体或溶液凝结）
k.en: an agent that causes a liquid to coagulate

ex.en: The blood coagulates to stop wounds bleeding.
ex.cn: 血液会凝结以防止伤口流血

m: from COAGULATE — a substance that makes liquids clot. — coagulant, a clotting agent.

k.syn: associate, combine, conjoin, connect, couple, fuse, interfuse, join, link（up）, unify, unite
k.ant: break up, dissever, section, separate, sever, split, sunder, unlink, disband 分开，解散
k.en: to unite into a whole: fuse

ex.en: Different units coalesced into one army.
ex.cn: 不同的党派融合成了一支部队。

m: CO- (together) + ALESCE — to come together, merge. — coalesce, to unite.

k.syn: blandish, cajole, wheedle, palaver
k.en: to persuade or try to persuade by pleading or flattery; cajole

ex.en: coax a child to take his medicine
ex.cn: 哄小孩吃药

m: COAX rhymes with HOAX — gentle hoax-like flattery to persuade. — coax, to persuade gently.

k.syn: finale, epilogue
k.ant: overture, prelude 前奏
k.en: the concluding passage of a movement or composition

ex.en: A song includes prelude, loud song and coda.
ex.cn: 一首歌包括前奏、高潮和尾声。

m: CODA contains CO + DA — "CO-da" sounds like the closing "do-da-day" of a song. — coda, a concluding section.

k.syn: force, threaten, compel
k.der: coercion n. 强力压迫：the act of persuading someone forcefully to do something that they do not want to do
k.ant: voluntary behavior 自愿的行为
k.en: to achieve by force or threat

ex.en: be coerced into agreeing
ex.cn: 被迫同意

ex.en: A confession was coerced from the suspect by police.
ex.cn: 嫌犯被警察逼供。

m: CO + ERCE ≈ "force" — coerce sounds like FORCE prefixed with CO-. — coerce, to force.

k.syn: coetaneous, coexisting, concurrent, contemporaneous, simultaneous, synchronic, synchronous
k.ant: asynchronous, noncontemporary, nonsimultaneous 不同时的
k.en: of the same or equal age, antiquity, or duration

ex.en: Two stars thought to be coeval because they have nearly the same mass and brightness.
ex.cn: 两个星星被认为是同龄，因为他们的质量和亮度几乎相同。

m: CO- (together) + EVAL (age, as in "medieval") — of the same age / time. — coeval, of the same era.

k.syn: convincing, compelling, conclusive, telling, persuasive, satisfying
k.ant: unconvincing, unpersuasive 不令人信服的
k.en: appealing forcibly to the mind or reason: convincing

ex.en: Six Sigma is one of the most cogent methods for modern enterprises to control quality and optimizing process.“
ex.cn: 六西格玛”方法是现代企业进行质量控制和工艺优化最令人信服的方法之一。

k.syn: apropos, germane, relative, relevant
k.ant: extraneous, irrelevant, impertinent, irrelative 无关的
k.en: pertinent, relevant

ex.en: a cogent analysis
ex.cn: 一项相关的研究

m: COGENT contains COG — well-COGGED arguments mesh together convincingly. — cogent, persuasive.

k.syn: aware, conscious, witting, apprehensive, sensible
k.ant: oblivious, unconscious, unaware, unmindful 没意识到的
k.der: incognizance n. 不认识；没知觉
k.en: fully informed; conscious; aware

ex.en: We are cognizant of the problem.
ex.cn: 我们已经意识到了这个问题。

m: from COGNITION — aware, knowing. — cognizant, aware.

k.en: a bowl-shaped kitchen utensil with perforations for draining

m: COLANDER rhymes with CALENDAR — a calendar of holes through which water drains. — colander, a strainer.

k.syn: compact, condense, constrict, constringe, contract, implode, squeeze
k.ant: decompress, expand, open, outspread, outstretch 展开，扩张
k.en: to fall or shrink together abruptly and completely

ex.en: President Bush is vowing to rebuild the bridge which collapsed last year.
ex.cn: 布什总统发誓要重建这座去年坍塌的桥梁。

ex.en: One ant-hole may cause the collapse of a thousand-li dyke.
ex.cn: 千里之堤，毁于蚁穴。

k.syn: exhaustion, tiredness, lassitude, weariness
k.ant: refreshment, rejuvenation, revitalization 重新充满活力
k.en: a complete depletion of energy or strength

ex.en: He suffered a mental collapse under the strain of studying for his bar exam.
ex.cn: 他在紧张的律师考试的复习下脑力消耗殆尽。

k.syn: defeat, nonachievement, nonsuccess
k.ant: accomplishment, achievement, success 成功，成就
k.en: to be unsuccessful; a falling short of one's goals

ex.en: The legal case collapsed in the face of the opposition's evidence.
ex.cn: 面对反方的证据，这个法律案件败诉了。

m: CO- (together) + LAPSE — fall together. — collapse, to fall down / fail.

k.en: a series of columns

m: from COLUMN — a long row of columns. — colonnade, a row of columns.

k.syn: huge, giant, titanic, gargantuan, mammoth, tremendous, elephantine, prodigious
k.ant: tiny, micro, minute, miniature, minuscule, wee, infinitesimal 微小的
k.en: of a size, extent, or degree that elicits awe or taxes belief; immense

ex.en: a colossal waste of public money
ex.cn: 对公共财产的巨大浪费

m: from COLOSSUS (the giant statue) — gigantic. — colossal, enormous.

k.ant: disciplined 遵守纪律的
k.en: not subjected to discipline

k.syn: antic, frisky, frolicsome, larky, sportful
k.ant: earnest, serious-minded, sober 严肃的
k.en: given to good-natured joking or teasing

ex.en: Off camera the actor is high-spiritedly coltish, but turns serious once the camera starts rolling.
ex.cn: 镜头下这个演员很喜欢开玩笑，但是当镜头开启，他就马上变得严肃起来。

m: from COLT (young horse) — like a young colt: frisky and undisciplined. — coltish, playfully unruly.

k.syn: insensibility, blackout, knockout
k.ant: consciousness, awareness 有知觉
k.en: a state of profound unconsciousness caused by disease, injury, or poison

ex.en: The girl lay in a coma for three days after the accident.
ex.cn: 那个女孩在事故之后昏迷了三天。

k.syn: sluggish, torpor
k.ant: activity, animation 有活力；alacrity 敏捷
k.en: a state of mental or physical sluggishness: torpor

m: COMA rhymes with COM-FOR-TER — but in COMA you don't feel comfort; deeply unconscious. (Or: A COMma is a small pause; a COMa is a long pause.) — coma, deep unconsciousness.

k.syn: burnable, combustive, flammable, ignitable, inflammable
k.ant: incombustible, nonburnable, noncombustible, nonflammable, noninflammable, unburnable 不可燃的；fireproof 防火的
k.en: capable of igniting and burning

ex.en: release a combustible gas
ex.cn: 释放出可燃性气体

k.syn: excitable, agitable, touchy
k.en: easily excited

ex.en: a high-strung combustible temper
ex.cn: 一碰就火的性格

m: from COMBUSTION — easily set on fire (literally or temperamentally). — combustible, flammable / easily provoked.

k.syn: attractive, cute, fair, good-looking, gorgeous, handsome, lovely, pretty, ravishing, well-favored, seemly, stunning
k.ant: homely, ill-favored, ugly, unattractive, unbeautiful, uncomely, uncute, unhandsome, unlovely, unpleasing, unpretty 不好看的
k.en: pleasing and wholesome in appearance; attractive

ex.en: a comely young woman
ex.cn: 年轻美丽的女人

m: COMELY rhymes with HOMELY but the opposite — pleasingly attractive. — comely, attractive.

k.syn: compatibility, concord, peace
k.ant: conflict, discord, dissension 冲突，不和
k.en: friendly social atmosphere: social harmony

ex.en: group activities promoting comity
ex.cn: 促进和谐氛围的团队活动

ex.en: comity of nations
ex.cn: 国际礼节

m: rhymes with AMITY — both mean friendly relations. — comity, social harmony.

k.syn: birth, onset, outset, start, genesis, inception, nascence, threshold
k.ant: close, conclusion, end, ending 结束
k.en: a beginning; a start

ex.en: There was a large turnout at the commencement of the conference, but the numbers dwindled as it progressed.
ex.cn: 在会议开始的时候，有很多人出席。但是随着会议的进行，人慢慢变少了。

k.ant: matriculation 录取入学
k.en: the ceremonies or the day for conferring degrees or diplomas

ex.en: The purpose of a commencement speaker is to dispense wisdom.
ex.cn: 毕业典礼上演讲者的目的是传播智慧。

m: from COMMENCE — the beginning (graduation = commencement of life). — commencement, the beginning / graduation.

k.syn: approbate, praise, acclaim, applaud, compliment, eulogize, extol
k.ant: blame, criticize, reprehend, reprobate, chide, rebuke, reprimand, reproach, reprove, censure, admonish berate, deplore, execrate 责备，批评，谴责，憎恶，痛骂
k.en: to mention with approbation: praise

ex.en: Jason commended his students' studious attitude. Jason
ex.cn: 表扬了他的学生用功的态度。

k.syn: commit, delegate, deliver, entrust, confide, consign, hand over
k.ant: hold, keep, retain 保留
k.en: to entrust for care or preservation

ex.en: I commend my fate into your hands.
ex.cn: 我的命运就拜托给你了。

k.en: to recommend as worthy of confidence or notice

ex.en: I commend this book to anyone interested in learning more about American history.
ex.cn: 我把这本书推荐给所有对美国历史感兴趣的人。

m: shares MEND with MEND/RECOMMEND — to COMMEND = to vouch for, MENDing one's reputation. — commend, to praise / entrust.

k.syn: equal, tantamount
k.ant: unequal, disparate, preponderant 不相同的，（重量，重要性，数量上）超过的
k.en: equal in measure or extent

ex.en: Five yards is commensurate with fifteen feet. 5
ex.cn: 码等于15英尺。

k.syn: commensurable, proportionate
k.ant: disproportionate 不相称的
k.en: corresponding in size or degree; proportionate

ex.en: a job commensurate with her abilities
ex.cn: 一份与她能力相称的工作

m: CO- (together) + MENSUR (measure) — measured together = of equal scale. — commensurate, proportionate.

k.en: a paid advertisement

m: CO + MERC + IAL — MERCantile content pushed at you. (Mercury, the god of commerce.) — commercial, advertisement.

k.syn: amalgamate, fuse, mix, immix, commix, compound, mingle, immingle, intermingle, intermix, merge
k.ant: break down, break up, separate, unmix 分开
k.en: to blend thoroughly into a harmonious whole

ex.en: Ground waters originating in different beds commingle.
ex.cn: 来源于不同层位的地下水相互混合。

ex.en: Fact and fiction commingle in the story.
ex.cn: 事实和虚构混合成了故事。

m: CO- + MINGLE — to mingle thoroughly together. — commingle, to mix together.

k.syn: dedication, devotedness, fealty, piety, steadfastness
k.en: the state or an instance of being obligated or emotionally impelled

ex.en: a commitment to a cause
ex.cn: 投身于某项事业

k.syn: conviction, belief, faith
k.en: a strong belief in something

k.ant: ambivalence, equivocation 矛盾，含糊其辞
k.en: the act of revealing one's view of

ex.en: He made a commitment to pay the rent on time.
ex.cn: 他承诺按时交房租。

m: from COMMIT — dedication / pledge. — commitment, dedication / pledge.

k.syn: loyal, faithful, allegiant
k.ant: confirmable 确定的
k.der: noncommittal adj. 不明确的：giving no clear indication of attitude or feeling
k.en: loyal to a belief, organization, or group, and willing to work hard for it

ex.en: remain committed to one's youthful ideal
ex.cn: 坚持不懈地追求自己年轻时的理想

ex.en: a noncommittal reply
ex.cn: 一个不明确的回答

m: from COMMITMENT — devoted, pledged. — committed, dedicated.

k.syn: spacious, roomy
k.ant: constricted, cramped, snug, constringed 狭窄的
k.en: comfortably or conveniently spacious: roomy

ex.en: a commodious closet
ex.cn: 宽敞的衣橱

m: rhymes with INCOMMODIOUS / shares root with ACCOMMODATE — both mean spacious / fitting. — commodious, spacious.

k.syn: justified, logical, rational, reasonable, reasoned, valid, well-founded, levelheaded
k.ant: groundless, illogical, invalid, irrational, nonrational, nonsensical, nonvalid, unfounded, uninformed, unjustified, unreasonable, unreasoned, unsound 无逻辑的，不合理的；preposterous 荒谬的
k.en: displaying common sense, based on sound reasoning or information

ex.en: The only commonsensical solution would be to divide the children into groups according to age.
ex.cn: 唯一合理的解决办法就是按照年龄把孩子分成组。

m: COMMON + SENSE + ICAL — based on common sense. — commonsensical, sensible.

k.syn: tumult, turmoil, pandemonium, hurry-scurry
k.ant: tranquility, calmness, quiet, serenity 安静；order 有序
k.en: an agitated disturbance

ex.en: The commotion was created when the nation's top rock band arrived in town.
ex.cn: 当全国顶级摇滚乐队来到小镇时，人们骚乱了。

m: from MOTION — much motion = uproar. — commotion, uproar.

k.syn: concise, brief, laconic, compendiary, succinct
k.der: compendious adj. 简洁而全面的：concise and comprehensive
k.en: a brief summary of a larger work or of a field of knowledge: abstract

ex.en: a compendium of information
ex.cn: 资料概要

ex.en: his compendious knowledge of this subject
ex.cn: 他关于这一学科的全面知识

k.syn: compilation, miscellany
k.en: 各种项目的列表或集合：a list or collection of various items

ex.en: Compendium of Materia Medica
ex.cn: 《本草纲目》

m: shares COMPEND- with COMPENDIOUS / EXPEND — a single bound "comprehensive expand". (Or: COM-pendium contains PEN — many books summed into one penned summary.) — compendium, a comprehensive summary.

k.syn: conceit, pomposity, pompousness, pride, self-admiration, self- assumption, smugness, vanity
k.ant: anxiety 忧虑；humbleness, humility, modesty 谦虚
k.en: a feeling of self-satisfaction, coupled with an unawareness of trouble

ex.en: Complacency is the enemy of study.
ex.cn: 自满乃学习之敌。

ex.en: A momentary complacency was quickly dispelled by the shock of cold reality.
ex.cn: 短暂的自满很快就被残酷的现实赶跑了。

m: rhymes with PLEASANT-cy — being so pleased with yourself you stop trying. — complacency, smug self-satisfaction.

k.syn: affability, amenability, amiability, good-naturedness
k.ant: obstinacy 固执；churlishness 粗野
k.cn: 愿意顺从，讨好，彬彬有礼
k.en: disposition to please or comply: affability

ex.en: She speaks with complaisance.
ex.cn: 她说话彬彬有礼。

ex.en: The complaisance of his girlfriend is such that she meekly goes along with everything he says.
ex.cn: 他的女朋友讨好他，对他言听计从。

m: COMPLY-like — eager to please. (Don't confuse with complacence.) — complaisance, eagerness to please.

k.syn: amenable, conformable, docile, submissive, tractable
k.ant: balky, contumacious, disobedient, incompliant, insubordinate, intractable, noncompliant, obstreperous, rebel, rebellious, recalcitrant, refractory, unamenable, ungovernable, unruly, willful, wayward 顽固的，难驾驭的 compliment n.［'kɑ:mplɪmənt］ v.［'kɑ:mplɪment］
k.en: ready or disposed to comply: submissive

ex.en: a corrupt regime aided by a compliant television station
ex.cn: 一个在顺从的电视台帮助下的腐败政府

k.syn: praise, commend, eulogize, extol, laud
k.ant: vituperative 责骂的
k.der: complimentary adj. 称赞的：expressing or containing a compliment
k.en: an expression of praise, admiration, or congratulation

ex.en: a man meriting the compliments and homage of his fellows
ex.cn: 一个值得他的伙伴们尊敬和称赞的人

k.en: formal and respectful recognition: honor

ex.en: How about a delicious dessert then, with our compliment?
ex.cn: 给您上点甜点怎么样，算是我们小小的敬意。

m: from COMPLY — willingly going along with rules. — compliant, obedient.

k.en: an expression of praise

m: COMPLIMENT contains COMPLI ≈ COMPLETE — completing someone with kind words. (Note: with E = praise; with I-MENT = praise.) — compliment, kind praise.

k.syn: conform, submit, observe
k.ant: defy, disobey, rebel against, violate, breach, transgress 违背
k.en: to conform, submit, or adapt（as to a regulation or to another's wishes）as required or requested

ex.en: The devices comply with industry standards.
ex.cn: 该设备符合工业标准。

ex.en: There will be penalties against individuals who fail to comply.
ex.cn: 谁不遵从谁就会受到惩罚。

m: COMPLY rhymes with APPLY — both involve following directions. — comply, to follow rules.

k.syn: contain, settle
k.ant: distraught, restless 发狂的，不平静的
k.der: composure n. 镇定：a calmness or repose especially of mind, bearing, or appearance
k.en: to free from agitation: calm

ex.en: She took a deep breath and composed herself.
ex.cn: 她做了一个深呼吸，使自己安定下来。

k.syn: constitute, comprise, make up Ordinary people merely think how they shall spend their time; a man of talent tries to use it. 普通人只想到如何度过时间，有才能的人设法利用时间。 ——德国哲学家　叔本华（Arthur Schopenhauer, German philosopher） “最初的梦想要靠坚持才能到达，一路上的无限风景见证着我的勇敢与执着。” （罗弥，2007年10月，Verbal 730，Quantitative 800，AW 6.0）
k.en: to form the substance of: constitute

ex.en: composed of many ingredients
ex.cn: 由很多配料组成

m: CO + POSE — POSE the parts together; or POSE oneself calmly. — compose, to put together / stay calm.

k.syn: admixture, alloy, amalgam, amalgamation, cocktail, combination, composite, fusion, intermixture, meld, mix, conflation, synthesis
k.en: something composed of or resulting from union of separate elements, ingredients, or parts

ex.en: mixed the chemicals together to form a new compound
ex.cn: 将化学试剂混合形成新的化合物

k.syn: amalgamated
k.ant: noncompound 非混合的
k.en: consisting of two or more substances, ingredients, elements, or parts

ex.en: a compound word
ex.cn: 复合词

ex.en: “Steamboat”is a compound noun.“
ex.cn: 汽船”是一个复合名词。

k.syn: chain, conjugate, couple, hook, interconnect, interlink, join, link, yoke
k.ant: disconnect, disjoin, disjoint, dissever, disunite, separate, unchain, uncouple, unhitch, unyoke 分开
k.en: to put or bring together so as to form a new and longer whole

ex.en: the German language's propensity for compounding words
ex.cn: 德语喜欢复合单词

k.syn: aggrandize, amplify, augment, boost, enlarge, escalate, expand, extend, raise, swell
k.ant: abate, decrease, diminish, dwindle, lessen, lower, minify, reduce 缩小，减少
k.en: to make greater in size, amount, or number

ex.en: We compounded our error by waiting too long to call for help.
ex.cn: 我们等着不寻求帮助增大了我们的错误。

k.en: to agree for a consideration not to prosecute（an offense）

ex.en: compound a felony
ex.cn: 私了案件

m: CO + POUND — POUND many ingredients together into one compound. — compound, mixture / to intensify.

k.syn: capsule, collapse, compact, condense, constrict, constringe, contract, shrink, telescope
k.ant: increase in volume, decompress, expand, balloon 体积增大； outspread, outstretch 伸展，扩张
k.en: to reduce in size or volume as if by squeezing

ex.en: compress a computer file
ex.cn: 压缩电脑文档

ex.en: The science textbook compresses a lot of information about human reproduction into a few short chapters.
ex.cn: 科学教科书将人类的繁育知识压缩成了几个短章节。

m: CO- (together) + PRESS — press together, squeeze. — compress, to squeeze.

k.syn: accommodation, negotiation
k.en: to adjust or settle by mutual concessions

ex.en: Eventually we reached a compromise on the number of hours per week that would be devoted to piano practice.
ex.cn: 最后我们在每周练琴的时间上妥协了。

k.syn: hazard, imperil, jeopardize, menace, peril, venture
k.en: to place in danger

ex.en: Officials were concerned that his statements would compromise national security.
ex.cn: 官员们认为他的言论会危害国家安全。

m: CO + PROMISE — both sides PROMISE to give a little. (Also: "compromised" = your security has been broken-promise.) — compromise, to settle / endanger.

k.syn: anxiety, misgiving, scruple
k.ant: absence of misgiving 不担忧
k.en: anxiety arising from awareness of guilt

ex.en: compunction of conscience
ex.cn: 良心不安

ex.en: a brutal murderer who killed without compunction
ex.cn: 一个野蛮没有良心的杀人犯

m: contains PUNCT (prick, as in "puncture") — a pricking of conscience. — compunction, twinge of guilt.

k.syn: catenate, chain, couple, hook, interconnect, interlink, join, link, yoke
k.ant: disconnect, disjoin, disjoint, dissever, disunite, separate, unchain, uncouple, unhitch, unyoke 分开
k.en: to put or bring together so as to form a new and longer whole

ex.en: The movie actually concatenates several episodes from various books into one extended narrative.
ex.cn: 这部电影把很多书中的片段混合成了一个故事。

m: CON- (together) + CATENATE (chain) — to chain together. — concatenate, to link in a chain.

k.syn: dented, depressed, dished, indented, recessed
k.ant: bulging, convex, protuberant 凸的
k.en: curved like the inner surface of a sphere

ex.en: a concave lens
ex.cn: 凹透镜

m: CON + CAVE — curving inward, like the inside of a cave. — concave, curving inward.

k.syn: cache, secrete, disguise, mask, occult, ensconce
k.ant: display, exhibit 展示；bare, disclose, expose, reveal, uncover, unmask 揭露
k.en: to prevent disclosure or recognition of

ex.en: Drunkenness reveal what soberness conceal.
ex.cn: 酒后吐真言。

m: CONCEAL contains CEAL ≈ "seal" — to seal away from view. — conceal, to hide.

k.syn: acknowledge, grant, confess
k.ant: aggression 侵犯
k.der: concession n. 让步：the act of yielding
k.en: to grant as a right or privilege

ex.en: She grudgingly conceded his point.
ex.cn: 她不情愿地承认了他的观点。

k.syn: capitulate, give in, quit, submit, succumb, surrender
k.ant: resist 抵抗
k.en: to cease resistance（as to another's arguments, demands, or control）

ex.en: He conceded as soon as it became clear that he could not win.
ex.cn: 当他明显赢不了的时候他就停止了抵抗。

m: from CEDE — to yield ground, admit defeat. — concede, to yield / admit.

k.syn: center, focus, rivet
k.ant: deploy, dispel, disperse, dissipate 散开，驱散
k.en: to bring or direct toward a common center or objective: focus

ex.en: concentrate one's efforts
ex.cn: 集中精力

k.syn: condense
k.ant: dilute, water down 稀释
k.en: to make less dilute

ex.en: Prolonged boiling is required to concentrate the sap when making maple syrup.
ex.cn: 制作枫糖浆时，要进行长时间的煮，使汁液充分浓缩。

k.syn: accumulate, amass, assemble, collect, congregate, garner
k.ant: dispel, disperse, dissipate, scatter, break up, disband 驱散，解散
k.en: to come together in one body or place

ex.en: Recent immigrants tend to concentrate in port cities.
ex.cn: 近来的移民倾向于聚集在海港城市。

m: CON- (together) + CENTER — bring to center. — concentrate, to focus / intensify.

k.syn: collaborative, combined, cooperative, united
k.ant: separate 分开的；individual, single, sole, solitary 单独的
k.en: planned or accomplished together

ex.en: The ITER project, commendable though it is, should be merely a component of a concerted effort.“
ex.cn: 国际热核聚变实验堆”计划虽然是值得称赞的，但是它仅仅是（人类）齐心协力的一部分。

ex.en: A victory results from the concerted effort of the entire team.
ex.cn: 一项成功来自整个团队的齐心协力。

m: a CONCERTED effort = made in concert (together). — concerted, jointly arranged.

k.syn: appease, pacify, assuage, mollify, placate, propitiate, calm（down）, ease, soothe, palliate, disarm
k.ant: annoy, enrage, vex, nettle, rile, incense, inflame, exasperate, infuriate, discompose, disturb, perturb, upset 烦扰，激怒，打扰
k.en: to lessen the anger or agitation of

ex.en: A principal tried to conciliate the parents who did not receive their tickets to the graduation ceremonies.
ex.cn: 校长试着平息没有收到他们学校毕业晚会门票的家长的气愤情绪。

k.syn: attune, reconcile, coordinate, accommodate
k.ant: contentious 好争吵的；belligerent 好战的
k.der: conciliatory adj. 调和的，安抚的
k.en: to make compatible: reconcile

ex.en: It will be hard to conciliate the views of labor and management regarding health benefits.
ex.cn: 调解劳资双方对于健康保险金的观点很难。

m: from RECONCILE — to bring together / soothe. — conciliate, to soothe / mediate.

k.syn: comity, compatibility, peace
k.ant: conflict, discord, dissension, variance 冲突，不和
k.en: harmony or agreement of interests or feelings; accord

ex.en: No discord, no concord.
ex.cn: 不打不成交。

m: CON- (together) + CORD — heartstrings tied together = harmony. — concord, agreement / harmony.

k.syn: agree, coincide
k.ant: differ, disagree 不同意
k.en: to express agreement

ex.en: concur with an excellent opinion
ex.cn: 同意一个好的想法

k.syn: coexist, synchronize, co-occur
k.en: to occur or exist at the same time

ex.en: The race to the moon, the Vietnam War, and the civil rights movement all concurred in the 1960s.
ex.cn: 登月、越战和人权运动都发生在二十世纪六十年代。

k.syn: band together, collaborate, concert, conjoin, league, team up, unite
k.en: to participate or assist in a joint effort to accomplish an end

ex.en: All people concurred to pass the reform legislation.
ex.cn: 所有人都联合起来使改革法案通过。

m: CON- (together) + CUR (run) — running together = agreeing or happening together. — concur, to agree / coincide.

k.syn: patronizing
k.der: condescension n. 屈尊俯就：patronizing attitude or behavior
k.cn: 摆出高人一等的姿态的
k.en: displaying a patronizingly superior attitude

ex.en: treat sb in a condescending manner
ex.cn: 以屈尊俯就的态度对待某人

m: to CONDESCEND = to come down from above; condescending = patronizing. — condescending, patronizing.

k.syn: due, deserved, merited, justified, warranted
k.ant: undeserved, undue, unmerited 不应得的
k.en: deserved, appropriate

ex.en: condign punishment
ex.cn: 应得的惩罚

ex.en: A suspension without pay is condign punishment for breaking the company's code of business ethics.
ex.cn: 停职是违反公司商业道德准则应得的惩罚。

m: rhymes with RESIGN — a condign punishment is fittingly deserved. — condign, well-deserved.

k.syn: compassionate, sympathize with, yearn over
k.der: condolence n. 同情：sympathy with another in sorrow
k.en: to express sympathetic sorrow

ex.en: We condole with him on the death of his father.
ex.cn: 我们对他父亲的死深表同情。

m: CONDOLE contains DOLE — to DOLE out condolences. (Latin DOLOR = pain.) — condole, to express sympathy.

k.syn: disregard, ignore, overlook, remit, shrug off, gloss over
k.ant: denounce 谴责；exact 强求
k.en: to overlook, forgive, or disregard（an offense）without protest or censure

ex.en: By his silence, he seemed to condone their behavior.
ex.cn: 他的沉默流露出了他对他们行为的宽恕。

ex.en: He is too quick to condone his friend's faults.
ex.cn: 他太快了，忽视了他朋友的过错。

m: CON + DONE — "con(sider) it DONE" — overlook it as if it never happened. — condone, to overlook / tacitly accept.

k.syn: facilitative, useful
k.ant: unhelpful, useless 无用的
k.en: tending to promote or assist

ex.en: be conducive to education
ex.cn: 于教育有利的

ex.en: The state's long-standing low tax is conducive to entrepreneurship.
ex.cn: 政府长期的低税率有利于创业者。

m: from CONDUCT — leading toward a result. — conducive, helpful / favorable.

k.en: the leader

m: CON + DUCT + OR — the conductor DUCTs the music together (DUCT = lead, as in "educate" / "introduce"). — conductor, orchestra leader / one who leads.

k.syn: assured, self-assured, self-confident
k.ant: diffident 不自信的
k.en: having or showing assurance and self-reliance

ex.en: Thicker and fuller hair would make a man more confident at work.
ex.cn: 越来越浓密的头发让男人在工作中更加自信。

m: from CONFIDENCE — sure of oneself. — confident, sure of oneself.

k.syn: imprison, commit, confine, immure, incarcerate, jail
k.ant: discharge, free, liberate, release 释放
k.en: to shut or keep in, especially to imprison

ex.en: The thief was confined in a prison.
ex.cn: 小偷被关在监狱里。

k.syn: limit, circumscribe, restrict
k.ant: exceed 超越，超过
k.en: to keep within limits

ex.en: Please confine yourself to the subject.
ex.cn: 请不要离题。

m: shares FIN- with FINITE / FINISH — set the end-bounds, hold within limits. — confine, to keep within limits.

k.syn: conjunction
k.ant: divergence 分叉
k.en: the coming together of two or more things to the same point

ex.en: a happy confluence of beautiful weather and spectacular scenery during our vacation
ex.cn: 旅途中美好的天气和壮丽的景色——令人欣喜的汇合

m: CON- (together) + FLU (flow) — flowing together (where rivers meet). — confluence, a flowing together.

k.syn: baffle, bewilder, confuse, muddle, perplex, puzzle
k.ant: clarify 使清醒
k.en: to throw into a state of mental uncertainty

ex.en: We are all confounded by his self-contradictory claims.
ex.cn: 我们都对他自相矛盾的言论感到困惑。

k.syn: misidentify, mistake, mix up
k.ant: discriminate, distinguish 区分
k.en: to fail to differentiate（a thing）from something similar or related

ex.en: I think you must have confounded astrology with astronomy.
ex.cn: 我想你一定是把占星术和天文学搞混了。

k.syn: belie, debunk, falsify, disprove, rebut, refute
k.ant: confirm, validate, verify 证实，证明为真
k.en: to prove to be false

ex.en: new discoveries that confounded much of what archaeologists thought they have known about the ancient Mayan civilization
ex.cn: 一些新的发现颠覆了考古学家们长期以来对于古代玛雅文明的认识

m: CON + FOUND — what you THOUGHT you'd FOUND turns out to be wrong, confounding you. — confound, to confuse / refute.

k.syn: affront, brazen, encounter, face, meet
k.ant: dodge, duck, parry, shirk, sidestep 躲避
k.en: to come face to face with, especially with defiance or hostility

ex.en: You must confront your fear in order to conquer it.
ex.cn: 要战胜你的恐惧，就必须首先敢于面对。

m: CON- (face) + FRONT — face to face. — confront, to face directly.

k.syn: coagulate, solidify, indurate, clot
k.ant: melt, liquefy 熔化，液化
k.en: to change from a fluid to a solid state by or as if by cold

ex.en: The blood had started to congeal.
ex.cn: 血液开始凝结了。

m: CON- (together) + GEAL (freeze) — to freeze/thicken together. — congeal, to thicken / solidify.

k.syn: agreeable, amicable, compatible, unanimous
k.ant: discordant, incompatible 不和睦的，不和谐的
k.en: having or marked by agreement in feeling or action

ex.en: a congenial host who invited us for a feast
ex.cn: 一位邀请我们美餐一顿的友好主人

k.syn: delightful, dulcet, felicitous, palatable
k.ant: unpleasant 令人不悦的
k.en: giving pleasure or contentment to the mind or senses

ex.en: a couple relaxing in the congenial atmosphere of a luxury health SPA
ex.cn: 一对沉浸在奢华水疗寓所中令人愉快气氛里的夫妻

m: CON- (with) + GENIAL — sharing a friendly nature. — congenial, friendly / agreeable.

k.syn: consonant, compatible, consistent, harmonious, accordant
k.ant: disagreeable 不一致的；conflicting, incompatible 冲突的，不可协调的
k.en: being in agreement, harmony, or correspondence; congruous

ex.en: a theory congruent with the known facts
ex.cn: 一个与已知事实相一致的理论

k.syn: identical, exact
k.ant: disparate 迥异的
k.en: coinciding exactly when superimposed

ex.en: These two triangles are congruent.
ex.cn: 这两个三角形是全等的。

m: CON- (together) + GRU (agreeing) — fitting together perfectly (geometry: congruent triangles). — congruent, perfectly matching.

k.syn: speculation, supposition, surmise, theory
k.ant: fact 事实；axiom 公理
k.en: a conclusion deduced by surmise or guesswork

ex.en: That was only a conjecture, not a fact.
ex.cn: 那只是猜测而非事实。

k.syn: calculate, estimate, guess, gauge
k.ant: prove 证明
k.en: to decide the size, amount, number, or distance of（something）without actual measurement

k.syn: assume, imagine, presume, speculate, suppose
k.en: to form an opinion from little or no evidence

ex.en: conjecture that this disease is caused by a defective gene
ex.cn: 揣测这种疾病是由基因缺陷导致的

m: CON- (together) + JECT (throw) — throw ideas together = guess. — conjecture, a guess.

k.syn: conspire, intrigue, plot, collude, machinate, put up
k.en: to cooperate secretly or have a secret understanding; collude

ex.en: They connived to take over the throne.
ex.cn: 他们密谋篡位。

k.syn: blink, disregard, overlook, wink
k.ant: disapprove 反对
k.en: 假装忽视或并未采取措施阻止错误：to pretend ignorance of or fail to take action against something one ought to oppose

ex.en: The guards were suspected of conniving at the prisoner's escape.
ex.cn: 警卫人员有纵容犯人越狱的嫌疑。

m: CONNIVE rhymes with CONTRIVE — both involve scheming. — connive, to conspire secretly.

k.syn: aesthete, cognoscente
k.en: a person who enjoys with discrimination and appreciation of subtleties and details especially in matters of culture or art

ex.en: a connoisseur of wine and cigarette
ex.cn: 烟酒鉴赏的行家

k.syn: adept, artist, authority, maestro, master, maven, proficient, virtuoso
k.ant: amateur, inexpert, nonexpert 业余者，非专业者；tyro, neophyte 新手
k.en: a person with a high level of knowledge or skill in a field

ex.en: works that are highly prized by connoisseurs of art glass
ex.cn: 被玻璃艺术制品专家高度赞誉的作品

m: CON + NOISSEUR ≈ "con + KNOW + sieur (sir)" — Sir Know-It-All about wine and art. — connoisseur, an expert with refined taste.

k.syn: careful, exact, heedful, meticulous, painstaking, scrupulous
k.ant: careless, remiss 粗心大意的
k.en: taking, showing, or involving great care and effort

ex.en: a conscientious researcher
ex.cn: 一丝不苟的研究者

k.syn: conscionable, ethical, honest, moral, upright, principled, scrupulous
k.ant: unscrupulous 不正直的
k.en: governed by or conforming to the dictates of conscience

ex.en: a conscientious police officer
ex.cn: 一名有良心的警察

m: from CONSCIENCE — guided by conscience, careful and thorough. — conscientious, careful / dutiful.

k.syn: accord, assent, agreement, harmony, unanimity, unison
k.ant: disagreement 不同意见
k.en: general agreement; unanimity

ex.en: The board has finally reached a consensus.
ex.cn: 董事会最终达成了一致。

m: CON- (together) + SENSUS (feeling) — common feeling. — consensus, general agreement.

k.syn: aftermath, effect, outcome, result
k.ant: source, origin, cause, antecedent 根源，原因
k.en: something produced by a cause or necessarily following from a set of conditions

ex.en: negative consequences of the war
ex.cn: 战争带来的负面后果

k.syn: moment, magnitude, weight
k.ant: triviality 琐碎的事
k.en: significance; importance

ex.en: a mistake of no consequence
ex.cn: 不重要的错误

m: CON + SEQUENCE — what follows in SEQUENCE; or "of consequence" = of importance. — consequence, result / importance.

k.syn: hidebound, reactionary, die-hard
k.ant: radical, aggressive 激进的
k.en: favoring traditional views and values; tending to oppose change

ex.en: a conservative political stance
ex.cn: 一个守旧的政治立场

k.syn: muted, repressed, low-key, understated, unpretentious
k.ant: flamboyant, ostentatious, splashy 浮夸的，张扬的
k.en: not excessively showy

ex.en: dressing in conservative outfits so as to make a good impression at job interviews
ex.cn: 身着低调得体的套装以期待在面试时留下一个好印象

k.syn: alert, heedful, cautious, circumspect, gingerly, guarded, wary, vigilant
k.ant: heedless 不谨慎的，莽撞的
k.en: having or showing a close attentiveness to avoid danger or trouble

ex.en: He made conservative investments, and so he wasn't ruined when the market went into a free fall.
ex.cn: 他只进行谨慎保守的投资，因此市场崩盘时他并没有受到严重损失。

m: from CONSERVE — wanting to keep things as they are. — conservative, cautious / traditional.

k.syn: greenhouse, hothouse
k.en: a greenhouse for growing or displaying plants

ex.en: The college's conservatory is entirely devoted to cultivating and displaying orchids.
ex.cn: 学校的温室几乎全部用于兰花的种植和观赏了。

k.en: a school specializing in one of the fine arts

ex.en: an opera conservatory
ex.cn: 歌剧学院

m: from CONSERVE — a glass house that conserves plants; or an arts school that conserves traditions. — conservatory, greenhouse / arts school.

k.syn: husband, preserve, save
k.ant: dissipate, lavish, waste, squander 浪费
k.en: to keep in a safe or sound state, especially to avoid wasteful or destructive use

ex.en: conserve natural resources
ex.cn: 保护自然资源

m: to CONSERVE energy = to save it. — conserve, to save.

k.syn: extensive, substantial, large-scale
k.en: sufficiently large in size, amount, or number to merit attention

ex.en: a considerable amount of fortune
ex.cn: 一笔可观的财富

k.syn: important, significant, consequential, momentous, weighty
k.ant: trivial 不值一提的；insubstantial, negligible, nominal, trifling 不重要的，可忽视的
k.en: worth consideration; significant

ex.en: a considerable artist
ex.cn: 一位举足轻重的艺术家

m: CONSIDER + ABLE — worth CONSIDERing because it's substantial. — considerable, sizable.

k.syn: comfort, solace, soothe, calm
k.ant: distress, torment, torture 使痛苦
k.en: to alleviate the grief, sense of loss, or trouble of; comfort

ex.en: Only her children could console her when her husband died.
ex.cn: 当她丈夫去世时，只有她的孩子们才能抚慰她的悲痛。

m: CONSOLE contains SOLE — when you're alone (SOLE), someone consoles you. — console, to comfort.

k.syn: unify, combine, amalgamate
k.ant: dissolve, sunder, fragment 解散
k.en: to join together into one whole; unite

ex.en: consolidate several small school districts
ex.cn: 合并了几个小的校区

k.syn: enhance, strengthen, fortify, reinforce
k.ant: abate, attenuate, undermine, weaken 削弱
k.en: to make firm or secure; strengthen

ex.en: consolidate the defense line
ex.cn: 加固防线

m: CON- (together) + SOLID — make solid together, unify. — consolidate, to unify / strengthen.

k.syn: harmonious, compatible, congenial, consistent, congruous, correspondent
k.ant: conflicting, dissonant, discrepant, inconsistent 不和谐的，不一致的
k.en: being in agreement or harmony; free from elements making for discord

ex.en: His performance was rarely consonant with his reputation.
ex.cn: 他的表现与名气大不相符。

m: CONSONANT — letters that SOUND with other letters; or CONSONANT WITH = sounding together with. — consonant, in agreement.

k.syn: apparent, clear, distinct, evident, manifest, plain, patent, noticeable
k.ant: hidden, concealed 隐藏的
k.en: obvious to the eye or mind; attracting attention

ex.en: a conspicuous change in her appearance
ex.cn: 她外貌的明显改变

m: from CONSPICIOUS — easily seen, standing out. — conspicuous, highly visible.

k.syn: plot, contrive, connive, cogitate, intrigue, put up
k.en: to plan together secretly to commit an illegal or wrongful act or accomplish a legal purpose through illegal action

ex.en: A group of POWs conspired to abscond.
ex.cn: 一批战俘密谋潜逃。

m: CON- (together) + SPIRE (breathe) — breathe together secretly = plot. — conspire, to plot together.

k.syn: nominate, designate, authorize
k.ant: discharge, dismiss 撤除职务；abdicate, resign 退位，辞职
k.en: to appoint to an office, function, or dignity

ex.en: He was constituted manager.
ex.cn: 他被任命为经理。

k.syn: compose, comprise, form
k.der: constitution n. 构成；宪法；体格
k.en: make up, form, compose

ex.en: Water constitutes the greater part of the human body.
ex.cn: 人体中包含着大量的水分。

m: CON- (together) + STITUTE (set up) — set up together, form. — constitute, to make up.

k.ant: release 释放
k.syn: bridle, check, confine, imprison, restrain, trammel
k.en: to force by imposed stricture, restriction, or limitation

ex.en: Low temperature constrains the chemical reactivity.
ex.cn: 低温降低了化学

k.syn: coerce, compel, oblige, sandbag
k.cn: 使人屈服于（压力、感情等）
k.en: to cause（a person）to give in to pressure

ex.en: constrained by his conscience to tell the truth
ex.cn: 受良心的驱使，他说了实话

m: CON- (together) + STRAIN — strain together, restrict. — constrain, to restrict.

k.syn: capsule, constrict, narrow, compress, squeeze, telescope
k.ant: broaden, expand, outstretch 扩展，扩宽
k.en: to make narrow or draw together

ex.en: A styptic pencil stopped the bleeding by constringing the small blood vessels at the site of cut.
ex.cn: 止血笔通过挤压伤口处的血管阻止了出血。

m: CON- (together) + STRINGE (string) — pull strings tight. — constringe, to make tight.

k.syn: assemble, build, fabricate, make, produce, rear, set up
k.ant: demolish, destroy, raze 摧毁
k.en: to form by assembling or combining parts

ex.en: construct a new library
ex.cn: 建一座新图书馆

k.syn: contrive, devise, excogitate, fabricate, vamp up
k.en: to create or think of by clever use of the imagination

ex.en: He managed to construct a theory that fits all the facts.
ex.cn: 他成功提出了一个与所有事实相吻合的理论。

m: CON- (together) + STRUCT (build) — build together. — construct, to build.

k.syn: accomplished, finished, virtuosic, versed, veteran
k.ant: amateur 业余的
k.en: extremely skilled and accomplished

ex.en: a consummate liar
ex.cn: 一个老练的骗子

k.syn: flawless, impeccable, perfect
k.ant: defective 有漏洞的
k.en: complete in every detail; perfect

ex.en: The difficult aria displayed her consummate skill.
ex.cn: 高难度的咏叹调展示了她完美的技巧。

k.syn: maximum, paramount, supreme, top, utmost
k.ant: minimal 最少量的
k.cn: 最高档次的，最大规模的
k.en: of the greatest or highest degree or quantity

m: CON + SUMMATE ≈ SUMMIT — reaching the SUMMIT of skill. — consummate, supremely skilled / complete.

k.syn: infectious, pestilent, transmissible, epidemic
k.ant: incommunicable 不传染的
k.en: communicable by contact; catching

ex.en: contagious diseases
ex.cn: 传染病

k.syn: catching, spreading
k.en: exciting a similar feeling or reaction in others

ex.en: The enthusiasm of the new club members was contagious.
ex.cn: 新成员的热情具有很强的感染力。

m: CON- (together) + TAG (touch) — touched together, spreading. — contagious, spreading by contact.

k.syn: pollute, defile, taint, infect
k.ant: purify, sanitize 净化
k.der: contamination n. 污染；contaminant n. 污染物
k.en: to soil, stain, corrupt, or infect by contact or association

ex.en: Bacteria contaminated the wound.
ex.cn: 细菌感染了伤口。

m: CON- (together) + TAMINATE (touch) — touch with impurity. — contaminate, to pollute.

k.syn: satisfy, gratify, rejoice
k.ant: discontent 不满足
k.en: to appease the desires of

ex.en: One glass of beer every day could content him.
ex.cn: 每天一杯啤酒就能满足他。

k.syn: motive, subject, theme, topic
k.en: a major object of interest or concern（as in a discussion or artistic composition）

ex.en: Although I appreciate the poem's lyrical qualities, I don't understand its content.
ex.cn: 虽然我很欣赏这首诗歌的节奏美，但我无法理解其主题。

k.syn: intention, purport, sense, significance
k.en: the idea that is conveyed or intended to be conveyed to the mind by language, symbol, or action

ex.en: The speech was filled with fancy words but devoid of any real content.
ex.cn: 这篇演讲辞藻华丽，但是没什么实质性内容。

m: CON + TENT — what's in your TENT (what's inside); or CON-TENT = with a tent over your head, you're satisfied. — content, what's inside / satisfied.

k.syn: controversial, disputatious, polemical
k.en: likely to cause contention; argumentative

ex.en: contentious contents in a movie
ex.cn: 电影中引发争论的内容

k.syn: belligerent, bellicose, combative, truculent, litigious, pugnacious, scrappy
k.ant: dovish, peace-loving 爱好和平的
k.en: exhibiting an perverse and wearisome tendency to quarrels and disputes

ex.en: The Tartars were a contentious people who terrorized much of Asia and eastern Europe during the Middle Ages.
ex.cn: 鞑靼是一个好斗的民族，他们在中世纪时期统治了亚洲和东欧的大片土地。

m: from CONTEND — likely to provoke a contest, quarrelsome. — contentious, quarrelsome / controversial.

k.syn: adjacent, abutting, neighboring, juxtaposed, verging
k.ant: apart, separate 分开的
k.en: sharing an edge or boundary; touching

ex.en: contiguous nations at war
ex.cn: 交战中的邻国

m: CON- (together) + TIG (touch) — touching together, adjacent. — contiguous, sharing a border.

k.syn: deform, distort, warp, misshape
k.en: to twist, wrench, or bend severely out of shape

ex.en: a contorted version of the truth
ex.cn: 被歪曲的真相

m: CON- + TORT (twist) — twist together, distort. — contort, to twist out of shape.

k.syn: agreement, compact, convention, treaty
k.en: a binding agreement between two or more persons or parties, especially one that is written and enforceable by law

ex.en: a billion-dollar contract from Department of Defense
ex.cn: 来自国防部的巨额合同

k.syn: compress, condense, concentrate
k.ant: expand 扩展；inflate 膨胀
k.en: to reduce in size by drawing together; shrink

ex.en: contract muscle
ex.cn: 收缩肌肉

k.syn: catch, get, sicken
k.ant: heal 痊愈
k.en: to become affected by a disease or disorder

ex.en: He contracted a severe cold that later turned into pneumonia.
ex.cn: 他染上了重感冒，最终发展成为肺炎。

m: a CONTRACT pulls parties together; to contract = to shrink or catch (a disease). — contract, an agreement / to shrink / to catch.

k.syn: breach, infringe, transgress, fracture, deny, contradict, gainsay, reject
k.ant: uphold, support, buttress 支持；comply, conform, observe 顺从
k.der: contravention n. 反对；违反，触犯
k.en: to violate, to oppose in argument: contradict

ex.en: contravene the proposal with no reservation
ex.cn: 毫无保留地反对这一提案

m: CONTRA- (against) + VENE (come) — come against, violate. — contravene, to violate.

k.syn: compunctious, regrettable, remorseful, apologetic, penitent, repentant, rueful
k.ant: impenitent, unrepentant 不知悔改的
k.cn: （因为有罪孽或过错而感到）后悔悲痛的
k.en: feeling or showing sorrow and remorse for a sin or shortcoming

ex.en: too late to feel contrite
ex.cn: 现在后悔为时已晚

m: CON + TRITE ≈ "thoroughly TRITE-en down" — ground down by guilt. (Also: CONTRITE rhymes with TRITE — but contrite is the opposite of trite, deep remorse.) — contrite, deeply remorseful.

k.syn: balky, contumacious, defiant, insubordinate, intractable, obstreperous, rebellious, recalcitrant, refractory, restive, ungovernable, unruly, untoward, wayward, willful
k.ant: obedient, docile, ruly 顺从的
k.en: stubbornly disobedient; rebellious

ex.en: Contumacious insurgents refuse to talk.
ex.cn: 不愿服从命令的叛军拒绝对话。

m: rhymes with TENACIOUS but rebellious — stubbornly disobedient. — contumacious, willfully disobedient.

k.syn: enigma, mystery, puzzlement, riddle, secret
k.en: a paradoxical, insoluble, or difficult problem; a dilemma

ex.en: a difficult conundrum even for the experts
ex.cn: 一道即便是专家也束手无策的难题

m: rhymes with HUMDRUM — but a CONUNDRUM puzzles you, unlike humdrum routine. — conundrum, a baffling problem.

k.syn: heal, recover, recuperate, recoup, snap back
k.ant: aggravate, deteriorate, intensify 恶化，加剧
k.en: to recover health and strength gradually after sickness or weakness

ex.en: the time needed to convalesce after an operation
ex.cn: 手术后需要用来康复的时间

m: CON- + VALESCE (grow strong, as in "valor") — to grow strong again, recover. — convalesce, to recover.

k.syn: accommodation, amenity, ease, facility
k.ant: burden, millstone 负担
k.der: convenient adj. 方便的，便捷的
k.en: fitness or suitability for performing an action or fulfilling a requirement

ex.en: the convenience of living in megacity
ex.cn: 住在大城市的便利

m: CONVENIENCE contains CON + VENIE (come) — what conveniently "comes together" for you. — convenience, ease.

k.syn: custom, ritual, manner
k.ant: deviation 背离（习俗）
k.der: conventional adj. 传统习俗上的
k.en: general agreement on or acceptance of certain practices or attitudes

ex.en: By convention, north is at the top of most maps.
ex.cn: 按照常规，北方在大多数地图的上部。

k.syn: treaty, agreement, compact, contract
k.en: a general agreement about basic principles or procedures

ex.en: Under this circumstance Geneva convention does not comply.
ex.cn: 此情形下《日内瓦公约》无效。

k.syn: assembly, congress, council, gathering
k.en: a coming together of a number of persons for a specified purpose

ex.en: attend a convention of mathematicians in California
ex.cn: 参加加州的一次数学家集会

m: CON + VENTION (come) — people who come together (a meeting); or what people have come to agree on (custom). — convention, custom / large meeting.

k.en: tending to move toward one point or to approach each other:

ex.en: convergent evolution
ex.cn: 趋同进化

m: from CONVERGE — coming together. — convergent, meeting at a point.

k.syn: acquaint, aware, familiar, informed, versed
k.ant: ignorant, unfamiliar 无知的，不熟悉的
k.en: having frequent or familiar association

ex.en: conversant with the accounting system
ex.cn: 熟知会计系统

m: well-versed, familiar with — like a conversation partner who knows the topic. — conversant, familiar with.

k.syn: proselyte
k.en: one that is converted

k.syn: persuade, proselytize, bring, lead
k.en: to bring over from one belief, view, or party to another

ex.en: European missionaries converted thousands to Christianity.
ex.cn: 欧洲传教士使成千上万的人改信了基督教。

k.syn: alter, change, transform, transfigure
k.der: converter n. 转换器；convertible adj. 可转变的 n. 敞篷跑车
k.en: to alter the physical or chemical nature or properties of especially in manufacturing

ex.en: convert water into ice
ex.cn: 将水转化成冰

m: CON- + VERT (turn) — turn around, change. — convert, to change / one who has changed.

k.syn: bulging
k.ant: dent, concave 凹陷的
k.en: having a surface or boundary that curves or bulges outward, as the exterior of a sphere

ex.en: A convex function has the property that a line joining any two points on its graph lies on or above the graph.
ex.cn: 凸函数具有如下的性质：其图像上任何两点之间的连线都在这两点间函数曲线的上方。

m: the opposite of CONCAVE — bulging outward (cave goes in, vex goes out). — convex, curving outward.

k.syn: carry, transfer, ferry, transmit
k.en: to take or carry from one place to another; transport

ex.en: goods conveyed by sea
ex.cn: 海路运输的货物

k.syn: conduct, communicate, impart, pass on
k.ant: withhold 隐瞒
k.en: to impart or communicate by statement, suggestion, gesture, or appearance

ex.en: He was struggling to convey his feelings.
ex.cn: 他正在挣扎着表达他的情感。

m: CON + VEY — "con-VEY" sounds like "convoy" — a convoy CONVEYS goods. — convey, to transmit.

k.syn: assurance, certainty, certitude
k.ant: dubiety, incredulity 怀疑；uncertainty, incertitude 不确定性
k.en: the state of being convinced

ex.en: a warrior of strong conviction
ex.cn: 一名信念坚定的战士

k.syn: sentence
k.ant: acquittal 宣告无罪
k.en: state of being found or proved guilty

ex.en: evidence that led to the suspect's conviction
ex.cn: 证明嫌疑犯有罪的证据

m: from CONVICT — strong belief, or a verdict of guilt. — conviction, firm belief / guilty verdict.

k.syn: assemble, convene, summon, muster
k.ant: adjourn 休会
k.en: to bring together by or as if by summons

ex.en: convoke Parliament
ex.cn: 召开国会

m: CON- (together) + VOKE (call) — call together. — convoke, to summon a meeting.

k.syn: complex, knotty, involved, sophisticated, twisted, tangled, labyrinthine
k.ant: straightforward 直截了当的
k.en: complicated; intricate

ex.en: a convoluted way of describing a simple mechanism
ex.cn: 用令人费解的方法解释一个简单的原理

m: CON- + VOLUTED (rolled, as in "voluminous") — twisted up, complicated. — convoluted, complicated.

k.cn: 痉挛（强烈的、无法控制的肌肉收缩）
k.en: an abnormal violent and involuntary contraction of the muscles

ex.en: Convulsions are usually accompanied by loss of consciousness.
ex.cn: 痉挛常常伴随着失去意识。

k.syn: commotion, ferment, tumult, upheaval, clamor, tempest, uproar
k.ant: serenity, tranquility 宁静
k.der: convulse v. 剧烈震动；痉挛
k.en: a violent disturbance

ex.en: a regime in convulsion
ex.cn: 动荡中的政权

m: from CONVULSE — violent shaking. — convulsion, violent fit.

k.syn: box, cage, corral, encage, enclose, envelop, fence, hedge, immure, include, pen, wall
k.ant: free, liberate, release 释放
k.en: to confine in a restricted and often crowded area

ex.en: Those restless kids were cooped up in the house on a rainy.
ex.cn: 雨天那些好动的孩子们被困在房子里。

m: a chicken COOP is a small enclosure — to coop someone up = confine. — coop, to confine.

k.syn: plentiful, abundant, ample, gushing
k.ant: sparse, dearth, scant 稀少的，缺乏的
k.en: large in quantity; abundant

ex.en: a copious harvest
ex.cn: 大丰收

m: CO + PIOUS ≈ "a COPY-ous amount" — many copies, abundant. — copious, abundant.

k.syn: vamp, flirt
k.en: a woman who makes teasing sexual or romantic overtures; a flirt

ex.en: A coquette though she might appear to be at first, Violetta from Verdi's La Traviata is actually yearning for true love.
ex.cn: 乍看之下，威尔第歌剧《茶花女》中的维奥莱塔是一个轻浮的女子，但实际上她渴望真爱。

k.syn: trifle, dally, flirt, mess around
k.der: coquet v. 不认真对待，调情；coquettish adj. 调情的
k.en: to flirt; to deal with something playfully rather than seriously

ex.en: He is interested only in coquetting with her, not marrying her.
ex.cn: 他只想与她保持暧昧关系，而不结婚。

m: a COQUETTE is a flirty woman (French for "little hen" — strutting). — coquette, a flirt.

k.en: a unit of wood

m: CORD of wood — a stack tied with a CORD (rope). — cord, a unit of stacked wood.

k.syn: perimeter
k.en: a line or ring of police, soldiers, or vehicles preventing people from entering an area

ex.en: a cordon of police
ex.cn: 警察围成的警戒线

k.syn: close, obstruct, block
k.en: to form a protective or restrictive cordon around

ex.en: cordon off the area around the explosion scene
ex.cn: 在爆炸现场周边设置警戒线

m: CORDON contains CORD — a CORD of police forms a line. — cordon, a guarding line.

k.syn: plentitude, plethora, wealth, profusion, affluence
k.ant: lack, pittance, deficiency 缺乏，少量
k.en: an overflowing store; an abundance

ex.en: a cornucopia of employment opportunities
ex.cn: 大量的就业机会

m: the CORNUCOPIA is the "horn of plenty" — overflowing abundance. — cornucopia, overflowing abundance.

k.en: a usually colored circle often seen around and close to a luminous body (as the sun or moon)

m: the sun's CORONA — the crown-like halo. — corona, halo / crown.

k.syn: enthronement, crowning
k.ant: abdication 退位
k.en: the act or ceremony of crowning a sovereign or the sovereign's consort

ex.en: Two different musical pieces by Mozart
ex.cn: （a piano concerto and a mass）are both titled“Coronation”. 莫扎特两部不同的音乐作品（一部钢琴协奏曲和一部弥撒）都被称作“加冕”。

m: from CORONA (crown) — the crowning ceremony. — coronation, crowning ceremony.

k.syn: bodily, carnal, corporal, somatic, physical, fleshly
k.ant: spiritual 精神上的
k.en: not spiritual

ex.en: corporeal suffering
ex.cn: 肉体上的折磨

k.syn: material, physical, substantial, sensible, tangible
k.ant: intangible, disembodied, immaterial 无形的，无实体的
k.en: not immaterial or intangible

m: from CORPORE (body) — bodily, physical. — corporeal, bodily / physical.

k.syn: confirm, authenticate, justify, substantiate, validate, verify
k.ant: controvert, contradict, deny 反驳，否认
k.en: to support with evidence or authority; make more certain

ex.en: new evidence to corroborate the defendant's story
ex.cn: 能证实被告的故事的新证据

k.syn: back, bolster, buttress, reinforce, support
k.en: to provide evidence or information for（as a claim or idea）

ex.en: My personal experience does not corroborate your faith in the essential goodness of people.
ex.cn: 我的经历可不赞同你关于“人性本善”的信念。

m: CO + ROBOR (strength, as in "robust") + ATE — strengthen with backup evidence. — corroborate, to confirm with evidence.

k.syn: erosive, caustic
k.ant: noncorrosive 无腐蚀性的
k.en: tending to destroy slowly by chemical action

ex.en: Concentrated sulfur acid is highly corrosive.
ex.cn: 浓硫酸有极强的腐蚀性。

k.syn: sarcastic, barbed, acerbic, satiric, acrid, mordant, tart
k.en: bitingly sarcastic

ex.en: corrosive satire
ex.cn: 讽刺性的作品

m: from CORRODE — eats away (acid, criticism). — corrosive, eating away / scathing.

k.syn: wrinkled, creased, folded
k.ant: smooth 光滑的
k.en: shaped into a series of regular folds that look like waves

ex.en: corrugated paper
ex.cn: 褶皱的纸

m: CORRUGATED cardboard has wavy ridges. — corrugated, ridged / wrinkled.

k.syn: universal, catholic, global, worldwide
k.ant: insular, provincial 狭隘的
k.der: cosmopolitism n. 世界主义，天下主义
k.cn: 有世界性眼光的，包容的
k.en: having worldwide rather than limited or provincial scope or bearing

ex.en: a cosmopolitan traveler
ex.cn: 云游四海的旅行者

m: COSMO (world) + POLITAN (citizen) — citizen of the world. — cosmopolitan, worldly / inclusive.

k.syn: caress, cuddle, dote, pet, mollycoddle
k.ant: slight 怠慢；abuse 虐待
k.en: to treat as a pet; pamper

m: to COSSET = to pamper (originally: a hand-raised pet lamb). — cosset, to pamper.

k.syn: abide, endure, stand, stomach, wear
k.en: to put up with（something painful or difficult）

ex.en: The college administration will not countenance cheating.
ex.cn: 该大学管理部门决不容忍作弊的存在。

k.syn: accept, favor, subscribe
k.ant: disapprove, frown 反对
k.en: to have a favorable opinion of

ex.en: I don't countenance such behavior in children of any age.
ex.cn: 对于任何年龄的孩子，我都不推崇那种行为。

k.ant: agitation, discomposure, perturbation 急躁，焦虑
k.syn: aplomb, calmness, composure, sangfroid, serenity
k.en: evenness of emotions or temper

ex.en: The doctor's purposeful countenance was in stark contrast to everyone else's hysteria.
ex.cn: 医生带有目的性的沉着与其他人的歇斯底里形成了巨大的

m: COUNT + ENANCE — your face that others COUNT on; to COUNTenance = to count something as acceptable. — countenance, facial expression / to tolerate.

k.syn: forged, bogus, spurious, pseudo, feigned, artificial, phony
k.ant: authentic, genuine 真实的
k.en: made in imitation of something else with intent to deceive

ex.en: counterfeit money
ex.cn: 假币

k.syn: copy, forge, imitate, simulate, mimic
k.en: to make a fraudulent replica of

ex.en: counterfeit the signature
ex.cn: 仿造签名

k.syn: affect, assume, fake, pretend
k.en: to present a false appearance of

ex.en: counterfeit a happy expression while visiting a sick friend
ex.cn: 在看望患病的朋友时装出一副高兴的样子

m: COUNTER- (against) + FEIT (made) — fake, made to imitate. — counterfeit, fake.

k.syn: annul, repeal, rescind, revoke, cancel
k.ant: approve, permit, sanction 批准
k.en: to revoke（a command）by a contrary order

ex.en: countermand an order
ex.cn: 撤销命令

m: COUNTER- (against) + MAND (command) — to override the previous command. — countermand, to revoke an order.

k.syn: woo, pursue, invite
k.ant: spurn, snub 摒弃，怠慢
k.der: courteous adj. 殷勤的；彬彬有礼的
k.en: to seek the affections of

ex.en: court the young lady by bring her flowers every day
ex.cn: 通过每天送花追求那个年轻女孩

m: to COURT someone = to woo them. (Also: a court of law / a tennis court.) — court, to woo / pursue.

k.en: an assembly or band of usually 13

m: COVEN contains COVE — witches gather in a hidden COVE-like nook. — coven, a witches' assembly.

k.syn: cloistered, hidden, secret, sheltered
k.ant: open, overt, aboveboard 公开的
k.en: not openly shown, engaged in, or avowed

ex.en: covert alliance
ex.cn: 秘密联盟

k.syn: concealment, hermitage, hideaway, nest, lair
k.en: a place where a person goes to hide or to avoid others

ex.en: set up a covert from which to watch wildlife without being detected
ex.cn: 设立了一个隐蔽的野生动物观测点

m: the opposite of OVERT — hidden, secret. — covert, hidden.

k.syn: acquisitive, avaricious, avid, rapacious, grasping
k.ant: easily-satiated 容易满足的；unenvious 不艳羡的
k.en: marked by inordinate desire for wealth or possessions or for another's possessions

ex.en: cast covetous eyes on their neighbors' fields
ex.cn: 对他邻居的土地垂涎

m: from COVET (one of the Ten Commandments: "Thou shalt not covet") — greedily wanting. — covetous, greedily desiring.

k.syn: intimidate, browbeat, bully, hector, awe
k.ant: embolden, encourage, inspirit 鼓励
k.en: to frighten with threats or a show of force

ex.en: cow sb. into doing sth
ex.cn: 威胁某人做某事

m: to COW someone — make them as docile as a COW. — cow, to intimidate.

k.syn: cravenness, dastardliness, gutlessness, poltroonery, pusillanimity, spinelessness
k.ant: courage, guts, pluck, intrepidness, nerve, stoutness, valiance, valor 勇气
k.en: lack of courage or resolution

ex.en: cowardice in the face of danger
ex.cn: 面对危险时的懦弱

m: from COWARD — lack of courage. — cowardice, lack of courage.

k.syn: fawn, flinch, grovel, quail, recoil, wince
k.en: to cringe in fear

ex.en: The dog cowered under the table.
ex.cn: 狗吓得蜷缩在桌子下面。

m: rhymes with POWER but the opposite — POWER stands tall, COWER crouches low. — cower, to crouch in fear.

k.syn: bamboozle, dupe, cheat, deceive, beguile, delude, take in
k.en: to mislead by means of a petty trick or fraud; deceive

ex.en: The salesman cozened the old lady into buying his goods.
ex.cn: 推销员诱使老太太买下了他的东西。

m: COZEN rhymes with COUSIN — your sketchy COUSIN tries to COZEN you out of money. — cozen, to trick.

k.syn: confined, limited, restrained
k.ant: commodious, spacious 宽敞的
k.en: uncomfortably small or restricted

ex.en: a cramped cubbyhole in an office
ex.cn: 办公室里狭小的隔间

m: CRAMPED contains CRAMP — so tight your muscles CRAMP. — cramped, confined.

k.syn: coarse, crude, rude, incult, uncouth, vulgar
k.ant: civilized, polished, refined, urbane, genteel 有教养的
k.en: so crude and unrefined as to be in discrimination and sensibility

ex.en: crass receptionist
ex.cn: 粗俗的招待员

m: CRASS rhymes with GRASS — but unlike fresh grass, CRASS is unpleasant and crude. — crass, crude / vulgar.

k.syn: need, yearn, desire, pine, thirst, itch, long, yen
k.ant: spurn 摒弃
k.en: to have an intense desire for

ex.en: crave alcohols and cigarettes
ex.cn: 渴望烟酒

m: CRAVE rhymes with GRAVE — your CRAVING is so deep it goes to the GRAVE. — crave, to desire intensely.

k.syn: cowardly, gutless, pusillanimous, spineless
k.ant: dauntless, fearless, gallant, gutsy, intrepid, stalwart, stout, stouthearted, valiant, valorous 勇敢的
k.cn: 非常懦弱的，因胆小而遭人鄙视的
k.en: lacking the least bit of courage; contemptibly fainthearted

ex.en: a craven deserter
ex.cn: 一个懦弱的逃兵

m: CRAVEN contains RAVEN — the cowardly RAVEN flees at the slightest threat. — craven, contemptibly cowardly.

k.syn: wrinkle, corrugation, fold, furrow
k.en: a line made by pressing, folding, or wrinkling

ex.en: flatten the creases of the map
ex.cn: 抚平地图上的折痕

m: CREASE rhymes with GREASE — but CREASE is a fold mark; GREASE makes it slippery. — crease, a fold mark.

k.syn: belief, credit, faith, trust, reliance
k.ant: doubt, skepticism 怀疑
k.en: firm belief in the integrity, ability, effectiveness, or genuineness of someone or something

ex.en: give credence to gossip
ex.cn: 相信绯闻

m: from CREDIT (believe) — belief, acceptance. — credence, belief.

k.syn: believing, unwary, gullible, naïve, unsuspecting
k.ant: disbelieving, skeptical 怀疑的
k.en: disposed to believe too readily; gullible

ex.en: accused of swindling credulous investors
ex.cn: 被控欺骗轻信的投资者

m: from CREDIT (believe) — too quick to believe. — credulous, gullible.

k.syn: stream, branch, brook, rivulet Every day I remind myself that my inner and outer life are based on the labors of other men, living and dead, and that I must exert myself in order to give in the same measure as I have received and am still receiving. 每天我都提醒着自己：我的精神生活和物质生活都是以别人的劳动为基础的，我必须尽力以同样的分量来报偿我所获得的和至今仍在接受着的东西。 ——美国科学家　爱因斯坦（Albert Einstein, American scientist） “Just do it. — It pays.” （武阳乐，2007年10月，Verbal 700，Quantitative 800，AW 5.0，录取院校：Princeton物理系）
k.en: a natural stream of water normally smaller than and often tributary to a river

ex.en: go wading in the creek
ex.cn: 在小溪中涉水而行

m: CREEK rhymes with WEAK — a small WEAK stream, not a river. — creek, a small stream.

k.syn: drag, loiter, tarry
k.ant: scurry, run 快步走，跑
k.en: to go very slowly

ex.en: creep through the crowd
ex.cn: 在人潮中缓慢前行

k.syn: crawl, grovel, slide, slither
k.ant: swagger 昂首阔步
k.en: to move slowly with the body close to the ground

ex.en: The kitten crept silently across the floor before suddenly pouncing on the mouse.
ex.cn: 小猫静静地匍匐穿过地板，然后突然向老鼠发起猛扑。

m: CREEP rhymes with SLEEP — moving stealthily so others SLEEP through it. — creep, to move stealthily.

k.ant: decrescendo（声音）渐弱
k.en: a gradual increase in volume of a musical passage

ex.en: The movement begins with a crescendo of a clarinet.
ex.cn: 乐章伴随着渐强的单簧管奏响。

k.syn: apex, acme, apogee, climax, crest, peak, pinnacle, summit, zenith
k.ant: nadir 最低点
k.en: the peak of a gradual increase

ex.en: Complaints about stifling smog conditions reach a crescendo.
ex.cn: 对让人窒息的烟雾的抱怨到达了顶峰。

m: CRESCEN + DO — shares CRESC- with CRESCENT (growing moon) — both mean growing larger. — crescendo, a rising to a climax.

k.syn: apex, acme, apogee, climax, crescendo, peak, pinnacle, summit, zenith
k.ant: bottom 底部
k.en: the top, as of a hill or wave

ex.en: on the crest of a wave
ex.cn: 如日中天，非常成功

m: CREST rhymes with BEST — at the CREST you're at your BEST height. — crest, peak / top.

k.syn: downcast, dispirited, low, gloomy, melancholic, sorrowful, woeful
k.ant: elated, buoyant, excited, exhilarated, exultant 激动的，欢悦的
k.en: dispirited and depressed; dejected

ex.en: be crestfallen at the failure
ex.cn: 因失败而垂头丧气

m: CREST + FALLEN — your crest (head-high pride) has fallen. — crestfallen, dejected.

k.syn: favoritism
k.cn: 任人唯亲，对好朋友的偏袒
k.en: favoritism shown to old friends without regard for their qualifications

ex.en: officials practicing cronyism
ex.cn: 任人唯亲的官员

m: from CRONY (longtime friend) — favoring your friends in hiring/politics. — cronyism, favoring friends.

k.syn: arch, bow, curve, hook, swerve
k.ant: straighten, unbend, uncurl 拉直，伸直
k.en: to cause to turn away from a straight line

ex.en: Crook your finger to pull the trigger.
ex.cn: 弯曲你的手指来扣动扳机。

m: CROOK rhymes with HOOK — both bend; the shepherd's crook is a hooked staff. — crook, to bend.

k.syn: whisper, grumble, mumble, murmur
k.ant: shout, yell 大声喊叫
k.en: to sing or speak in a gentle murmuring manner

ex.en: croon mellow tunes
ex.cn: 低声唱柔和的调子

m: CROON rhymes with MOON — singers CROON love songs to the MOON. — croon, to sing softly.

k.syn: huddle, squat
k.ant: arise, stand 站立
k.en: to lower the body stance especially by bending the legs

ex.en: He crouched behind a rock and watched vigilantly.
ex.cn: 他蹲伏在石头后面，警惕地观察着。

m: CROUCH rhymes with COUCH — bend low like climbing onto a low couch. — crouch, to bend low.

k.syn: critical, decisive, key, pivotal, vital
k.ant: inconsequential, insignificant, trivial 不重要的
k.en: extremely significant or important

ex.en: a crucial step in his professional career
ex.cn: 他职业生涯中具有决定性的一步

m: CRUCIAL contains CRUC (cross, as in CRUX) — at the cross-roads, the decisive moment. — crucial, vitally important.

k.syn: rumple, crimple, crease, fold, wrinkle
k.ant: smooth 使平滑
k.en: to press, bend, or crush out of shape

ex.en: crumple a piece of paper
ex.cn: 弄皱一张纸

k.syn: collapse, founder, implode, tumble, yield
k.en: to fall down or in as a result of physical pressure

ex.en: The box crumpled when I accidentally dropped a brick on it.
ex.cn: 我不小心扔了块石头在箱子上，它就倒塌了

m: CRUMPLE rhymes with RUMPLE — both crush into wrinkles. — crumple, to crush / collapse.

k.syn: brace, buttress, column, stay, support, underpinning, underpropping
k.en: something that supports or sustains

ex.en: a crutch for local economy
ex.cn: 当地的经济支柱

m: CRUTCH rhymes with CLUTCH — you CLUTCH a CRUTCH for support. — crutch, a support.

k.syn: core, substance, kernel, gist, pivot
k.ant: trifle, triviality 无价值之物
k.en: the basic, central, or critical point or feature

ex.en: the crux of the problem
ex.cn: 问题的核心所在

m: CRUX = Latin for cross — the critical CROSS-road, the central point. — crux, the central point.

k.syn: covert, furtive, secret, stealthy
k.ant: public, open 公开的
k.en: secret or occult

ex.en: cryptic message
ex.cn: 秘密的信息

k.syn: ambiguous, equivocal, nebulous, obscure, unclear, vague
k.ant: clear, explicit, obvious, plain 清晰的
k.en: having or seeming to have a hidden or ambiguous meaning

ex.en: The senator made some cryptic explanations about the military operations.
ex.cn: 参议员就军事行动给出了含糊其辞的解释。

k.syn: arcane, enigmatic, impenetrable, inscrutable, mystic, occult, uncanny
k.ant: comprehensible, understandable 可以理解的
k.en: being beyond one's powers to know, understand, or explain

ex.en: They were puzzled by the cryptic e-mail message left on his computer.
ex.cn: 他们被他电脑里让人费解的电子邮件所迷惑。

m: from CRYPT (hidden) — hidden meaning, mysterious. — cryptic, mysterious / coded.

k.en: manner of preparing food; style of cooking characteristic of a particular country or region

m: CUISINE rhymes with ROUTINE — a chef's culinary routine. (French "cuisine" = kitchen.) — cuisine, cooking style.

k.syn: blameworthy, censurable, guilty, reprehensible, sinful, reproachable
k.ant: inculpable, innocent 无罪的
k.en: deserving of blame or censure as being wrong, evil, improper, or injurious

ex.en: culpable behaviors
ex.cn: 该受谴责的行为

m: from CULPA (fault, as in "mea culpa") — at fault. — culpable, blameworthy.

k.syn: educate, instruct, illuminate, nurse
k.ant: degrade, deteriorate, impair 损坏，降低品质
k.en: to improve by labor, care, or study

ex.en: cultivate the mind
ex.cn: 开化心智

k.syn: grow, breed, produce, raise, develop, nurture
k.ant: balk, frustrate, thwart 阻碍
k.en: to promote the growth of（a biological culture）

ex.en: cultivate vegetables
ex.cn: 种植蔬菜

m: CULTIVATE contains CULT — a CULTivator nurtures crops or talents like a CULT(ure). — cultivate, to develop / nurture.

k.syn: unhandy, ponderous, heavy, unwieldy, onerous, thorny
k.ant: light, weightless 轻的；handy 容易处理的
k.en: difficult to handle because of weight or bulk

ex.en: a cumbersome piece of machinery
ex.cn: 一件笨重的机器

m: CUMBERSOME contains CUMBER (burden) — encumbered, weighed down. — cumbersome, bulky / awkward.

k.syn: artful, crafty, devious, foxy, sly, tricky, wily
k.ant: artless, naïve, unsophisticated 天真无邪的
k.en: marked by or given to artful subtlety and deceptiveness

ex.en: cunning tactics
ex.cn: 狡猾的战术

k.syn: clever, deft, handy
k.ant: heavy-handed, ham-handed 笨拙的
k.en: skillful with the hands

ex.en: Only the most cunning cabinetmaker could have crafted such a beautifully proportioned chest of drawers.
ex.cn: 只有手工超凡的家具工才能打造出如此精致协调的衣柜。

k.syn: artifice, deception, fraud
k.ant: ingenuousness, sincerity 真诚
k.en: the inclination or practice of misleading others through lies or trickery

ex.en: She used cunning and subterfuge to work her way up the corporate ladder.
ex.cn: 她通过欺骗在公司中步步攀升。

m: CUNNING contains CUN ≈ "con" — a CON artist is CUNNING. — cunning, sly / clever.

k.cn: （博物馆、收藏品展览、图书馆等的）管理员，馆长
k.en: someone who is in charge of the objects or works

m: CURATOR contains CURA (care, as in CURE) — one who CARES for the collection. — curator, keeper of a collection.

k.syn: crosspatch, fusser, griper, grouser, growler, grumbler, whiner
