k.ant: agreeable person 令人愉快的人
k.en: an irritable and complaining person

ex.en: a terrible old curmudgeon
ex.cn: 一个坏脾气老头

m: CURM + UDGEON — sounds like grumpy mumbling "crm-udgeon". (Also rhymes with BLUDGEON — grumpy enough to bludgeon.) — curmudgeon, an ill-tempered person.

k.en: a set of courses

m: CURR + ICULUM — Latin CURR = run; the curriculum is the course you RUN through. (Same root as "current.") — curriculum, course of study.

k.syn: headlong, overhasty, pell-mell, precipitate, precipitous, rash
k.ant: fastidious, thorough, deliberate, unhurried, unrushed 极关注细节的，考虑周到的，不匆忙的
k.en: acting or done with excessive or careless speed

ex.en: a cursory glance at the headline
ex.cn: 匆匆地瞥了一眼标题

m: from CURSOR (runner) — done in a hurry, surface-level. — cursory, hasty / superficial.

k.syn: abrupt, bluff, brusque, downright, unceremonious
k.ant: circuitous, mealymouthed 迂回的
k.cn: 言词简略的，直接（以至显得粗鲁）的
k.en: being or characterized by direct, brief, and potentially rude speech or manner

ex.en: his curt reply
ex.cn: 他简略而略显粗鲁的回答

k.syn: aphoristic, compendious, elliptical, laconic, pithy, sententious, succinct, terse
k.ant: diffuse, long-winded, prolix, verbose 冗长啰嗦的
k.en: marked by the use of few words to convey much information or meaning

ex.en: On a daily basis she e-mailed to her commanders curt reports on the situation.
ex.cn: 她每天向长官做简短汇报。

m: CURT rhymes with SHORT (in feel) — a CURT reply is cut SHORT. — curt, abruptly brief.

k.syn: abbreviate, abridge, syncopate, truncate, cut back
k.ant: elongate, protract, prolong, extend, lengthen 延长
k.en: to make less in extent or duration

ex.en: curtail your holiday
ex.cn: 缩短你的假期

m: shortens like cutting a TAIL — to curtail = to cut short. — curtail, to cut short.

k.syn: misanthrope, naysayer, pessimist
k.en: a person who believes all people are motivated by selfishness

ex.en: A cynic might think that the governor visited the hospital just to gain votes.
ex.cn: 一个愤世嫉俗者认为州长去医院探视仅仅是为了拉票。

m: CYNIC rhymes with CLINIC — a CLINICal, distrusting view of motives. — cynic, a distrusting skeptic.

k.ant: specialist 专家
k.der: dabbler n. 涉猎者，浅尝辄止者：one not deeply engaged in or concerned with something
k.en: to work or involve oneself superficially or intermittently especially in a secondary interest

ex.en: dabble in arts
ex.cn: 对艺术稍有涉猎

ex.en: He's not a dedicated musician, just a dabbler.
ex.cn: 他并不是专门的音乐家, 只不过是个业余爱好者

m: to DABBLE in painting = dip in lightly without serious commitment. — dabble, to engage lightly.

k.syn: demented, deranged, lunatic, psychotic, unbalanced, unsound
k.ant: balanced, sane 头脑正常的
k.en: having or showing a very abnormal or sick state of mind

ex.en: The king was clearly daft, talking to trees and rocks.
ex.cn: 国王俨然已经疯了，对着树和石头说话。

k.syn: fatuous, featherheaded, nonsensical, preposterous, senseless
k.ant: judicious, prudent, sagacious, sapient, wise 明智的
k.en: showing or marked by a lack of good sense or judgment

ex.en: This is a daft plan, doomed to wretched failure and merciless ridicule.
ex.cn: 这是一个不明智的决定，最终难逃悲催的结局和被无情嘲讽的下场。

m: DAFT rhymes with DRAFT — like a poorly drafted plan, daft is foolish. — daft, foolish.

k.en: an early photographic process

m: a DAGUERREOTYPE was the earliest photograph (Louis Daguerre's invention, 1839). — daguerreotype, an early photograph.

k.syn: podium, rostrum, tribune
k.en: a raised platform, as in a lecture hall, for speakers or honored guests

ex.en: The speaker took his place at the front of the dais.
ex.cn: 演讲者在主席台就坐。

m: DAIS rhymes with PRAISE — speakers stand on the DAIS receiving PRAISE. — dais, a raised platform.

k.syn: dawdle, loll, lounge, hang around
k.en: to spend time doing nothing

ex.en: I kept dallying at my desk until I couldn't put off doing my work any longer.
ex.cn: 我一直浪费时间直到作业不能再拖了。

k.syn: disport, frolic, recreate, rollick, sport
k.en: to engage in activity for amusement

ex.en: He spent his college years dallying, seemingly determined to acquire as little knowledge as possible.
ex.cn: 他在大学里面尽情玩乐，不学习。

k.syn: crawl, creep, dillydally, drag, lag, linger, loiter, tarry
k.ant: dash, hasten, scoot, scurry 匆忙
k.en: to move or act slowly

ex.en: Don't dally on the way to the interview.
ex.cn: 别在去面试的路上磨磨蹭蹭。

m: DILLY-DALLY is the everyday phrase for wasting time — DALLY alone means the same. — dally, to waste time.

k.ant: ameliorator 促进物
k.en: one that deadens, restrains, or depresses

ex.en: Rain put a damper on our picnic plans.
ex.cn: 下雨打破了我们的野餐计划。

m: DAMPER contains DAMP — a damper makes things DAMP / squashes the mood. — damper, a restraining influence.

k.syn: fop, gallant
k.en: a man who gives exaggerated attention to personal appearance

ex.en: That dandy was willing to spend thousands of dollars just to get the Hermes Birkin for his girlfriend.
ex.cn: 那个纨绔子弟愿意花数千美元，只为他的女朋友买Hermes Birkin的包包。

k.syn: awesome, fabulous, superb, sensational, splendid, unsurpassed
k.ant: atrocious, awful, execrable, lousy, pathetic, wretched 糟糕的，差劲的
k.en: of the very best kind

ex.en: That's a dandy new racing bike.
ex.cn: 真是辆最好的比赛用自行车。

m: Yankee Doodle DANDY — the song's hero stuck a feather in his cap and called it "macaroni" (= flashy dress). — dandy, an elegantly dressed man.

k.syn: damp, wettish
k.en: slightly or moderately wet

ex.en: Vegetables tended to go bad quickly in the dank cellar.
ex.cn: 蔬菜在潮湿的地下室很容易变坏。

m: DANK rhymes with STANK — a DANK basement DOES stink, damp and musty. — dank, unpleasantly damp.

k.syn: natty, sharp, snappy, spruce
k.ant: frowsy, unkempt, slovenly 不整洁的
k.en: being strikingly neat and trim in style or appearance

ex.en: The students all looked very dapper in their uniforms.
ex.cn: 学生们穿着校服显得非常整洁。

m: DAPPER rhymes with SNAPPER — a SNAPPY-dressed gentleman. — dapper, neatly stylish.

k.syn: blotchy, mottled, specked, piebald, splotched, stippled, spotted
k.ant: unspotted 没有斑点的
k.en: marked with small spots or contrasting with the background; mottled, spotted

ex.en: a dappled fawn
ex.cn: 一只小花鹿

k.syn: marbled, mottled, piebald, pinto, splotched
k.en: having blotches of two or more colors

ex.en: a forest that was vibrant with the dappled foliage of autumn
ex.cn: 充满秋天的生机、色彩斑斓的森林

m: DAPPLED contains DAPPLE ≈ "dab + apple" — apples DABbed with sun spots. — dappled, mottled / spotted.

k.syn: audacious, brash, madcap, overbold, reckless, temerarious
k.ant: circumspect, guarded, heedful, prudent, wary 小心谨慎的
k.en: foolishly adventurous or bold

ex.en: His daredevil stunts are sure to end in disaster someday.
ex.cn: 玩火者必自焚。

k.syn: foolhardy, irresponsible, harum-scarum
k.ant: responsible 负责任的
k.en: having or showing a lack of concern for the consequences of one's actions

ex.en: a daredevil driver who thinks that racing on city streets is a harmless game
ex.cn: 不计后果的司机认为在大街上飙车对人无害

m: DARE + DEVIL — one who dares the devil himself; reckless. — daredevil, recklessly bold.

k.syn: flitter, flutter
k.en: to move suddenly and rapidly

ex.en: The dog darted across the street.
ex.cn: 狗飞奔过马路。

k.syn: affront, barb, offense, sarcasm, slight, slur
k.en: an act or expression showing scorn and usually intended to hurt another's feelings

ex.en: The darts flew fast and furiously when the two former lovers bumped into each other at the party.
ex.cn: 旧情人在party上偶遇，立马开始激烈的人身攻击。

m: DART rhymes with HEART — like Cupid's DART that HEARTs you in an instant. — dart, to dash quickly.

k.syn: demoralize, dishearten, dismay, dispirit, frustrate, unnerve
k.ant: pusillanimous, trepid, craven, easily discouraged, meek, timorous 胆怯的
k.der: dauntless, undaunted adj. 无畏的，大胆的
k.en: to lessen the courage or confidence of

ex.en: She was not at all daunted by the size of the problem.
ex.cn: 她根本没有被问题的大小给吓倒。

m: DAUNT rhymes with HAUNT — being DAUNTed is being HAUNTed by fear. — daunt, to intimidate.

k.syn: bum, dally, loll, loaf, lounge, hang about, kick around
k.ant: hie 匆忙，抓紧
k.en: to spend time idly

ex.en: dawdle the day away
ex.cn: 闲荡一天

k.syn: crawl, creep, dally, dillydally, lag, linger, loiter, tarry
k.ant: bolt, hasten, course, dash, speed, scurry 匆忙，疾行
k.en: to move or act slowly

ex.en: If you continue to dawdle, we'll be late for sure.
ex.cn: 你要是再拖拉，我们铁定要迟到了。

m: DAWDLE rhymes with DOODLE — both fritter time away. — dawdle, to waste time.

k.syn: expressionless, impassive, inexpressive, stolid, vacant
k.ant: demonstrative, expressive 有表现力的
k.cn: 无趣的，无生气的，不活泼的
k.en: marked by impassively matter-of-fact, as in style, behavior, or expression

ex.en: a deadpan comedy
ex.cn: 一个没有笑点的喜剧

m: DEAD + PAN (face) — a face dead of expression — straight-faced. — deadpan, expressionless.

k.syn: want, deficit, insufficiency, paucity, pinch, scantiness, scarcity, undersupply
k.ant: plethora, spate, copiousness, abundance, adequacy, amplitude, opulence 过剩，丰富
k.en: an inadequate supply

ex.en: a dearth of evidence
ex.cn: 证据不足

m: rhymes with EARTH but the opposite of full — DEARTH = scarcity. — dearth, a shortage.

k.syn: bummer, calamity, catastrophe, cataclysm, fiasco, fizzle, flop, washout
k.ant: complete success, éclat, blockbuster 大成功
k.en: a complete failure; fiasco

ex.en: the debacle of the war
ex.cn: 战争的彻底失败

m: DEBACLE rhymes with TENTACLE (sort of) — chaos that has tentacles into everything. (Or: DE + BACLE ≈ "de-buckle" — the wheels come off.) — debacle, a disastrous failure.

k.syn: land
k.ant: embark 上船，装货
k.en: to unload, as from a ship or an airplane; disembark

ex.en: The seasick passengers debarked as soon as the ship dropped anchor.
ex.cn: 晕船的乘客等到轮船一靠岸抛锚就立即下船。

m: DE- (off) + BARK (boat) — get off the boat (also for planes/trains). — debark, to disembark.

k.syn: abase, debauch, degrade, demean, demoralize, deprave, deteriorate, profane, subvert, vitiate
k.ant: aggrandize, canonize, deify, elevate, exalt, ennoble 使神圣
k.en: to reduce to a lower standing in one's own eyes or in others' eyes

ex.en: Our failure to win a single game completely debased our image.
ex.cn: 我们一场未胜的战绩彻底让我们的形象一落千丈。

m: DE- (down) + BASE — push to the base level, lower. — debase, to degrade.

k.syn: devitalize, enervate, enfeeble, prostrate, sap
k.ant: invigorate, fortify, strengthen, beef up 使有活力，加强
k.en: to impair the strength of; enfeeble

ex.en: The virus debilitates the body's immune system.
ex.cn: 病毒削弱了免疫系统。

m: DE- + BILIT (strength, as in "ability") — drain of strength. — debilitate, to weaken.

k.syn: dross, dust, litter, offal, refuse, effluvium, junk
k.en: discarded or useless material

ex.en: the unsightly debris left after mining operations had ceased
ex.cn: 采煤工程中止后留下来的难看的废墟

m: DEBRIS rhymes with FREE-bee — left lying around for free after destruction. — debris, scattered wreckage.

k.syn: uncloak, uncover, undress, unmask, show up
k.ant: camouflage, cloak, disguise, mask 继续伪装，掩盖
k.en: to reveal the true nature of

ex.en: debunk a supposed miracle drug
ex.cn: 揭穿所谓的灵丹妙药的真面目

k.syn: belie, confound, confute, discredit, falsify, rebut, refute
k.ant: confirm, establish, validate, verify 证明…为真
k.en: to prove to be false

ex.en: a Web site that assiduously debunks urban legends
ex.cn: 一个不遗余力证明假传闻的网站

m: DE- (remove) + BUNK (nonsense) — remove the nonsense. — debunk, to expose as false.

k.ant: farewell performance 告别演出
k.en: a first public appearance

ex.en: made her single debut
ex.cn: 完成了她的处女秀

m: DEBUT contains DE + BUT — DE-BUT, taking the first step OUT. — debut, first appearance.

k.syn: degeneracy, degeneration, degradation, deterioration, downfall, eclipse
k.ant: ascent, rise, upswing 提高，提升
k.en: a process, condition, or period of deterioration or decline; a change to a lower state or level

ex.en: The book condemns the decadence of modern society.
ex.cn: 该书批判了现代社会的堕落。

m: from DECADE-LIKE decline — decay of moral or cultural standards. — decadence, decay / moral decline.

k.en: to pour off（wine, for example）without disturbing the sediment

ex.en: The bottles were uncorked and the wine was decanted an hour before the meal.
ex.cn: 瓶塞被打开，美酒在正餐前一小时倒出。

m: DECANT contains DE + CANT (tilt) — TILT the bottle DOWN to pour. — decant, to pour gently.
a DECIBEL — a unit of sound (named after Bell, hence the bel). — decibel, sound-volume unit.

k.syn: break, crack, decrypt, decode
k.ant: cipher, encipher, encode, encrypt 加密
k.en: to read or interpret（ambiguous, obscure, or illegible matter）

ex.en: We deciphered the hidden message to find out when we were supposed to meet.
ex.cn: 通过破解暗语，我们得知何时要碰头。

k.syn: apprehend, perceive, recognize, seize, sense, make out
k.cn: 对…有清晰想法，理解，解读
k.en: to have a clear idea of

ex.en: a convoluted thriller, the plot of which I was never able to actually decipher
ex.cn: 一部错综复杂的恐怖电影，情节我一直没有理解

m: DE- (un) + CIPHER (code) — un-code. — decipher, to decode / make out.

k.syn: break, crack, decipher, decrypt
k.en: to change（as a secret message）from code into ordinary language

ex.en: The agents worked into the night to decode the intercepted message from the enemy spy.
ex.cn: 情报人员夜以继日地破解从敌方间谍拦截的信息。

k.syn: apprehend, perceive, recognize, seize, sense, make out
k.cn: 对…有清晰想法，理解，解读
k.en: to have a clear idea of

ex.en: a complex literary text difficult for many people to decode
ex.cn: 一个深奥的文学文本，许多人都无法理解

m: DE- (un) + CODE — un-code. — decode, to translate from code.

k.syn: form, propriety
k.ant: proper 适当的
k.en: appropriateness of behavior or conduct; propriety

ex.en: High standards of decorum are usually required when attending the opera.
ex.cn: 去听歌剧的时候需要遵守高标准的礼节。

ex.en: decorous behavior
ex.cn: 得体的举止

m: from DECOR — proper appearance, dignified behavior. — decorum, proper conduct.

k.ant: vigorous, sturdy, sound, robust, hale 健壮的
k.en: weakened, worn out, impaired, or broken down by old age, illness

ex.en: a decrepit old man
ex.cn: 一个虚弱的老年人

m: DECREPIT contains CREP ≈ "creak" — old buildings CREAK and groan. — decrepit, broken-down with age.

k.syn: delinquency, dereliction, misprision, nonfeasance, oversight
k.en: the nonperformance of an assigned or expected action

ex.en: default on a loan
ex.cn: 拖欠贷款

m: to DEFAULT on a loan = fail to pay; the DEFAULT setting = the fallback. — default, failure to pay / standard fallback.

k.syn: delay, remit, shelve, hold off, hold over, lay over, put off
k.en: to put off; postpone; defer

ex.en: We agreed to defer a discussion of the issue.
ex.cn: 我们决定推迟问题的讨论。

k.en: to submit to another's wishes, opinion, or governance usually through deference or respect

ex.en: defer to her father's wishes
ex.cn: 遵从她父亲的意愿

m: DE- (away) + FER (carry) — to carry off to later, postpone; also "defer to" = yield to. — defer, to postpone / to yield.

k.syn: acquiescence, compliancy, docility, obedience, submissiveness
k.ant: imperious, impudent 专横无礼的
k.der: deferential adj. 表示尊重的，恭敬的：showing or expressing deference
k.en: a readiness or willingness to yield to the wishes of others

ex.en: He is shown much deference by his colleagues.
ex.cn: 他的同事很尊敬他。

m: from DEFER — respectful yielding. — deference, respectful submission.

k.syn: want, dearth, deficit, famine, paucity, scantiness, scarcity
k.ant: surfeit, abundance, adequacy, amplitude, opulence, sufficiency 过度，充足
k.en: the quality or state of being deficient: inadequate

ex.en: There is a deficiency of fresh food in the diet of many of the working poor.
ex.cn: 很多劳工阶层的饮食都缺乏新鲜食物。

m: from DEFICIT — a lack. — deficiency, a shortage.

k.en: a narrow passage or gorge

ex.en: They climbed up the mountain through a defile.
ex.cn: 他们通过一条小路爬到山上。

k.syn: profane, violate
k.en: to treat（a sacred place or object）shamefully or with great disrespect

ex.en: Art conservators were careful not to do anything that might defile the holy relic.
ex.cn: 公物管理员要防止任何玷污圣迹的行为。

k.syn: befoul, foul, pollute, taint
k.ant: decontaminate, purify 使纯净
k.en: to make unfit for use by the addition of something harmful or undesirable

ex.en: supplies of meat that had been defiled by maggots
ex.cn: 肉已经生蛆了，不能再食用了

m: DE- (down) + FILE — to make filthy, defile a sacred place. — defile, to corrupt / make filthy.

k.en: a chemical spray applied to plants to cause the leaves to drop off

m: DE- (off) + FOLIA (leaves, as in "foliage") — strips leaves off. — defoliant, leaf-killing chemical.

k.en: to cut down and clear away the trees

m: DE- (away) + FOREST — strip away the forest. — deforest, to clear forests.

k.syn: cunning, adroit, dexterous, expert, masterful, virtuoso
k.ant: awkward, maladroit, ham-handed, amateur, artless, unprofessional, unskillful 笨拙的，不精通的
k.en: characterized by facility and skill

ex.en: He finished off the painting with a few deft strokes of the brush.
ex.cn: 他简单熟练地用几笔便完成了画作。

m: DEFT rhymes with LEFT (and right) — a DEFT player uses both hands skillfully. — deft, skillfully nimble.

k.ant: foment 煽动
k.en: to make less dangerous, tense, or hostile

ex.en: defuse the crisis
ex.cn: 平息危机

m: DE- (un) + FUSE — disarm the bomb's fuse; calm a tense situation. — defuse, to calm / disarm.

k.syn: mock, rebel, oppose, confront
k.ant: acquiesce, obey, comply with, conform to 默默接受，遵守； capitulate to, submit to, succumb to, surrender to, yield to 屈服
k.en: to go against the commands, prohibitions, or rules of

ex.en: defy the court
ex.cn: 蔑视法庭

m: DEFY rhymes with DENY — DENY orders openly. — defy, to resist openly.

k.syn: dampen, desiccate, parch, scorch, sear
k.ant: hydrate, saturate with water, reconstitute 用水浸润
k.en: to remove water from; make anhydrous

ex.en: She bought a dehumidifier in order to dehydrate the damp basement.
ex.cn: 她买了一个除湿器来给地下室除湿。

k.syn: deaden, devitalize, enervate, petrify, sap
k.ant: brace, energize, enliven, invigorate, vitalize, vivify 使生机勃勃
k.en: to deprive of vitality or savor

ex.en: Years of being trapped in a loveless marriage had dehydrated his spirit.
ex.cn: 多年不幸的婚姻消磨了他的活力。

m: DE- (un) + HYDRATE (water) — remove water. — dehydrate, to dry out.

k.syn: glorify, revere, venerate
k.en: to offer honor or respect to（someone）as a divine power

ex.en: Some ancient pagans deified such objects of nature as trees and rivers.
ex.cn: 很多古代的异教徒将一些自然事物比如树木、河流当作崇拜对象。

k.syn: adore, adulate, canonize, dote
k.en: to love or admire too much

ex.en: materialistic people who deify money
ex.cn: 那些眼里只有钱的物质之人

k.syn: aggrandize, canonize, dignify, ennoble, glorify, magnify
k.ant: debasement 贬低
k.en: to assign a high status or value to

ex.en: Valentino was virtually deified by legions of female fans.
ex.cn: 瓦伦蒂诺被大批女粉丝崇拜。

m: DEI (god, as in "deity") + FY — to make into a god. — deify, to worship as a god.

k.syn: oppress, sadden, weigh down, bum out
k.ant: brighten, buoy, lighten, rejoice, cheer up 使高兴
k.en: to lower the spirits of; dishearten

ex.en: Nothing dejects a TV pundit more than the reality check that nobody cares what he thinks.
ex.cn: 没有什么比大家都漠不关心更让电视节目专家沮丧了。

m: DE- (down) + JECT (throw) — throw down spirits. — deject, to dishearten.

k.syn: adverse, baleful, baneful, detrimental, mischievous, nocuous, noxious, pernicious
k.ant: beneficial, salutary, salubrious, wholesome, benign, harmless, innocuous 有益的，安全无害的
k.en: harmful often in a subtle or unexpected way

ex.en: deleterious to health
ex.cn: 对健康有害的

m: DELETERIOUS contains DELETE — DELETE-erious substances delete your health. — deleterious, harmful.

k.syn: calculated, considered, reasoned, thoughtful
k.ant: impetuous, casual, unadvised, uncalculated, unconsidered, unstudied 随意的，未经过深思的
k.en: characterized by or resulting from careful and thorough consideration

ex.en: a deliberate decision
ex.cn: 一个慎重的决定

m: DELIBER + ATE — Latin LIBRA (balance, as in scale) — to weigh on the scales = think carefully. — deliberate, intentional / careful.

k.syn: daintiness
k.ant: crudity, coarseness, roughness 粗糙
k.en: fineness of appearance, construction, or execution; elegance

ex.en: lace of great delicacy
ex.cn: 精致的蕾丝

m: from DELICATE — fineness; or a special food treat. — delicacy, fineness / fine food.

k.syn: agitation, distraction, hysteria, rage, rampage, uproar
k.cn: 精神错乱；极度兴奋，发狂
k.en: an acute mental disturbance characterized by confused thinking and disrupted attention usually accompanied by disordered speech and hallucinations; frenzied exitement

ex.en: Shoppers were running around in a delirium the day before Christmas.
ex.cn: 购物者在圣诞节前夕疯狂扫货。

m: DELIRIUM contains DELIRI ≈ "delirious" — both wild and confused. — delirium, mental confusion.

k.ant: drizzle 毛毛雨
k.en: a drenching rain

k.syn: inundation, overflow, spate, torrent
k.en: a great flow of water or of something that overwhelms

ex.en: receive a deluge of offers
ex.cn: 收到一大堆offer

m: DELUGE rhymes with REFUGE — the DELUGE makes you seek REFUGE on higher ground. — deluge, a flood.

k.syn: hallucination, illusion, unreality, falsehood, misconception
k.ant: transparent 坦诚的
k.en: a false idea

ex.en: This was not optimism, it was delusion.
ex.cn: 那不是乐观主义，那是妄想。

m: from DELUDE — a false belief held against reality. — delusion, false belief.

k.syn: probe, investigate, inquire into, look into, dig into
k.en: to make a careful or detailed search for information

ex.en: The book delves into the latest research.
ex.cn: 这本书深入探究了最近的研究。

m: DELVE rhymes with TWELVE — DELVE into the TWELVE volumes thoroughly. (Or: DELVE contains DEL ≈ "dell, dig.") — delve, to investigate deeply.

k.syn: firebrand, fomenter, incendiary, instigator, kindler, provocateur
k.en: a leader who makes use of popular prejudices and false claims and promises in order to gain power

ex.en: That politician is just a demagogue who preys upon people's fears and prejudices.
ex.cn: 那就是一个蛊惑民心的政客，利用民众的害怕和偏见来欺骗他们。

m: DEMA (people) + GOGUE (leader) — a leader who exploits popular passions. — demagogue, a populist agitator.

k.syn: exacting, fastidious, finical, finicking, fussy, picky
k.en: not easily satisfied or pleased

ex.en: His mother could be demanding at times.
ex.cn: 他母亲有时候很难取悦。

k.syn: arduous, burdensome, challenging, exacting, grueling, killing, laborious, onerous, persnickety, taxing, toilsome
k.ant: light, unchallenging, undemanding 不费劲的，轻而易举的
k.en: requiring much time, effort, or careful attention

ex.en: The demanding assignment kept them working all night long.
ex.cn: 那项劳神的任务使得他们熬夜。

m: from DEMAND — hard to satisfy; requiring much. — demanding, hard to satisfy.

k.syn: annihilation, decimation, devastation, havoc, wreckage
k.ant: building, construction, erection, raising 建造
k.en: the act or process of wrecking or destroying, especially destruction by explosives

ex.en: the demolition of dangerous buildings
ex.cn: 危楼的拆毁

m: from DEMOLISH — destruction. — demolition, destruction.

k.syn: exemplify, instance
k.ant: unable to prove 不能证明
k.en: to show or make clear by using examples

ex.en: The paleontologist hopes to demonstrate that dinosaurs once existed in central Peru by unearthing the fossil evidence.
ex.cn: 通过发掘化石，古生物学家试图证明恐龙曾经在秘鲁中部生活过。

k.syn: clarify, construe, demystify, elucidate, explicate, illuminate
k.ant: obscure 使模糊
k.en: to make plain or understandable

ex.en: A few striking facts should demonstrate the complex nature of our topic.
ex.cn: 一些显著的事实可以使我们的复杂话题变得好懂。

k.syn: bespeak, betray, display, evince, manifest, reveal
k.en: to make known（something abstract） through outward signs

ex.en: The babysitter's actions during the emergency demonstrate beyond doubt her general dependability.
ex.cn: 保姆在紧急情况下的表现毋庸置疑地展示了她一贯的可靠性。

m: DEMONSTRATE contains DEMON + STRATE — show clearly enough that even a DEMON could see (the STRATa). — demonstrate, to show / prove.

k.syn: daunt, dishearten, dismay, dispirit, unnerve
k.ant: invigorate, cheer 鼓舞
k.en: to undermine the confidence or morale of; dishearten

ex.en: demoralize the staff
ex.cn: 使员工士气低落

k.syn: abase, corrupt, debauch, degrade, demean
k.ant: elevate, ennoble, uplift 使崇高，提升
k.en: to lower in character, dignity, or quality

ex.en: We refused to be demoralized by our humiliating defeat.
ex.cn: 我们拒绝由于这场屈辱的战败而被贬低。

m: DE- (un) + MORAL — strip away morale. — demoralize, to crush spirit.

k.ant: profound 深奥的
k.en: popular, common

ex.en: demotic entertainments
ex.cn: 大众化的娱乐

m: from DEMOS (people) — of the common people, vernacular. — demotic, popular / vernacular.

k.syn: challenge, exception, expostulation, fuss, kick, protest, remonstrance, stink
k.ant: accept, accede 接受
k.en: to voice opposition; object

ex.en: demur at the suggestion
ex.cn: 反对提议

k.en: hesitation（as in doing or accepting）usually based on doubt of the acceptability of something offered or proposed; qualm

ex.en: We accepted his offer to pay for our dinners without demur.
ex.cn: 我们毫不犹豫地同意了他买单。

m: DEMUR rhymes with MURMUR — politely MURMUR an objection. — demur, to object mildly.

k.syn: belittle, depreciate, derogate, dismiss, disparage
k.ant: honor, acclaim, applaud, exalt, extol, glorify, magnify 给以…荣誉，赞扬
k.en: to express scornfully one's low opinion of

ex.en: denigrate one's opponents
ex.cn: 诋毁某人的对手

m: DENI- + GRATE (wear down) — to wear down someone's reputation. — denigrate, to belittle / defame.

k.en: the final resolution or clarification of a dramatic or narrative plot

ex.en: a surprising/unexpected denouement
ex.cn: 令人惊奇的、出人意料的结局

m: from French — the DENOUEMENT is the unraveling/resolution at the end of a story. — denouement, the resolution.

k.syn: condemn, objurgate, rebuke, reprimand, reproach, reprove
k.ant: panegyric, accolade, eulogy 赞颂，颂词
k.der: denunciation n. 谴责：an act of denouncing especially: a public condemnation
k.en: to express public or formal disapproval of

ex.en: The governor has denounced the court's decision and vows to press for a constitutional amendment.
ex.cn: 州长公开指责法庭判决不公，准备投票进行修宪。

k.syn: anathematize, censure, execrate, reprehend, reprobate
k.en: to declare to be morally wrong or evil

ex.en: The church council denounced the bishop's teachings, officially declaring them to be heresy.
ex.cn: 该教理事会指责主教的布道可耻，声称它们是异端学说。

m: DE- + NOUNCE (announce) — publicly announce against. — denounce, to publicly condemn.

k.syn: cavity, depression, hollow, indenture, recess
k.ant: bulge, convexity, projection, protrusion, protuberance 凸起
k.en: a depression in a surface made by pressure or a blow

ex.en: a dent in the side of a car
ex.cn: 汽车侧面一处凹陷

k.syn: abate, downscale, downsize, dwindle
k.ant: aggrandize, amplify, augment, boost, enlarge, escalate 增大，增强
k.en: to make smaller in amount, volume, or extent

ex.en: Hopefully this vacation won't dent our bank account too much.
ex.cn: 但愿这次旅行不会让我们银行存款缩水太多。

m: DENT rhymes with BENT — a DENT is where metal got slightly BENT. — dent, a small depression.

k.ant: cover 遮盖
k.en: to divest of covering; make bare

ex.en: Drought has completely denuded the hills of grass.
ex.cn: 干旱“脱去”了山上的草使山变得光秃秃的。

m: DE- + NUDE — strip naked / bare. — denude, to strip bare.

k.syn: consume, devour, drain, exhaust, draw down, play out, use up
k.ant: enrich, renew, replace 使富有，更新
k.en: to decrease the fullness of; to make complete use of

ex.en: Miners depleted the vein of copper ore after only a few months.
ex.cn: 矿工们在短短几个月内就把一整片铜矿开采完了。

m: DE- + PLETE (full, as in "complete") — un-fill, exhaust. — deplete, to exhaust supplies.

k.syn: mourn, bewail, grieve for, wail for
k.ant: delight, exult in, glory in, rejoice in 高兴
k.en: to feel or express sorrow for

ex.en: a statement from the bishops deploring the loss of life in the war overseas
ex.cn: 一份来自主教们的声明，对海外战争中遇难的生命表示沉痛哀悼

m: DEPLORE contains DE + PLORE ≈ "plead" — strongly plead against. — deplore, to lament.

k.ant: concentrate 集中
k.en: to spread out, utilize, or arrange for a deliberate purpose

ex.en: deploy a sales force
ex.cn: 展开部署销售力量

m: DEPLOY contains PLOY — execute a PLOY by spreading out forces. — deploy, to position for action.

k.syn: banishment, displacement, expatriation, expulsion, relegation
k.en: the removal from a country of an alien whose presence is unlawful or prejudicial

ex.en: the deportation of all illegal immigrants
ex.cn: 驱逐所有非法移民

m: from DEPORT — being sent away from a country. — deportation, expulsion from a country.

k.syn: attest, swear
k.ant: perjure 作伪证
k.en: to testify to under oath or by affidavit

ex.en: He was nervous when the time to depose before the jury finally arrived.
ex.cn: 他很紧张，在陪审团面前作证的时刻终于到来了。

k.syn: defrock, deprive, oust, uncrown
k.ant: crown, enthrone 授予王权
k.en: to remove from a throne or other high position

ex.en: a military junta deposed the dictator after he had bankrupted the country
ex.cn: 军阀罢黜了使国家陷入赤贫的独裁者

k.syn: deposit, dispose, emplace, situate
k.en: to arrange something in a certain spot or position

ex.en: She deposed her fan and gloves on the dressing table.
ex.cn: 她把扇子和手套放在梳妆台上。

m: DE- (down) + POSE (place) — place someone down from power. — depose, to remove from office / give sworn testimony.

k.syn: dregs, precipitate, sediment, settlings, hoard, reserve
k.ant: process of eroding 腐蚀过程
k.en: a natural accumulation（as of iron ore, coal, or gas）

ex.en: rich deposits of oil and natural gas
ex.cn: 丰富的石油和天然气矿藏

k.ant: withdraw 取款
k.en: to put in an account

ex.en: We quickly deposited the check in a bank account.
ex.cn: 我们火速把支票存入银行账户。

m: DEPOSIT contains POSIT (place) — to DE-POSIT = place down. — deposit, to lay down / a layer.

k.syn: debauchery, iniquitousness, licentiousness, perversion, turpitude, dissoluteness
k.ant: morality, virtue 美德
k.en: immoral conduct or practices harmful or offensive to society

ex.en: He was sinking into a life of utter depravity.
ex.cn: 他自甘堕落。

m: from DEPRAVED — moral corruption. — depravity, moral corruption.

k.syn: deprecate, discountenance, disesteem, disfavor, frown（on or upon）
k.ant: approve, favor 喜欢
k.en: to hold an unfavorable opinion of

ex.en: They deprecates TV sitcoms as childish and simpleminded.
ex.cn: 他们批评电视情景喜剧既幼稚又脑残。

k.syn: belittle, denigrate, dismiss, disparage, cry down
k.ant: acclaim, applaud, exalt, extol, glorify 赞扬
k.en: to express scornfully one's low opinion of

ex.en: They boys deprecate the comedy as the stupidest movie of the year.
ex.cn: 男孩们认为这部喜剧是年度最傻逼电影。

k.ant: vaunt 夸张，吹嘘
k.en: play down, to make little of

ex.en: She deprecated her facility for languages.
ex.cn: 她不炫耀自己对于语言的天赋。

m: DE + PRECATE (pray) — "pray against," disapprove of. (Self-deprecating = praying against yourself.) — deprecate, to disapprove of.

k.syn: cheapen, depress, devalue, downgrade, mark down
k.ant: appreciate, enhance, upgrade, mark up 提价，升值
k.en: to lower the price or estimated value of

ex.en: New cars start to depreciate as soon as they are on the road.
ex.cn: 新车一上路便开始贬值。

k.syn: denigrate, disparage, play down, talk down
k.ant: acclaim, exalt, extol, glorify, magnify 赞扬
k.en: to lower in estimation or esteem

ex.en: He dared to depreciate Shakespeare, saying his works have no relevance for modern audiences.
ex.cn: 他胆敢贬低莎大人，说他的作品对于现代观众来说没有意义。

m: DE- (down) + PRECI (price) — drop in price/value. — depreciate, to lose value.

k.en: an agent, especially a drug, that decreases the rate of vital physiological activities

m: from DEPRESS — a substance that calms / slows the system. — depressant, a sedative.

k.syn: crestfallen, dejected, despondent, gloomy, low-spirited, wretched
k.ant: blissful, buoyant, gleeful, joyous, jubilant 情绪高的
k.cn: 不开心的，情绪不高的，消沉的
k.en: feeling unhappiness

ex.en: I was depressed and didn't feel much like going to the party.
ex.cn: 我感到很沮丧，不想去参加聚会。

k.syn: disadvantaged, underprivileged
k.ant: advantaged, privileged 具有优势的、特权的
k.en: kept from having the necessities of life or a healthful environment

ex.en: a depressed class of people whose living conditions are abominable even by third world standards
ex.cn: 境况悲惨的一群人，即使用第三世界标准来看他们的生存条件也是很糟的

m: from DEPRESS — pressed down, sad. — depressed, downcast.

k.ant: fecundity 丰饶
k.en: the condition of being deprived; privation

ex.en: serious sleep deprivation caused by long work hours
ex.cn: 由长时间工作导致的严重睡眠不足

m: from DEPRIVE — being deprived, lacking necessities. — deprivation, lack.

k.ant: plant 种植
k.en: to pull out by the roots; uproot

m: DE- + RACINE (root, French) — pull up by the roots. — deracinate, to uproot.

k.syn: disregardful, lax, neglectful, neglecting, remiss, slack
k.ant: extremely careful, attentive, conscientious, nonnegligent 特别仔细的
k.en: lacking a sense of duty; marked by a carelessly easy manner

ex.en: The guards were judged derelict in their duty.
ex.cn: 守卫们被判玩忽职守罪。

k.syn: deserted, desolate, disused, forgotten, forsaken, rejected
k.ant: pillar of society 社会的栋梁
k.en: a destitute homeless social misfit

ex.en: a section of the city that seemed to be frequented mostly by derelicts
ex.cn: 这座城市里流浪汉的集中区

m: DERELICT contains RELI ≈ "relinquish" — DE-RELI(nquished) = abandoned. — derelict, neglectful / abandoned.

k.syn: gibe, jeer, mock, scout, shoot down, laugh at
k.ant: veneration 尊敬
k.der: derision n. 嘲弄：contemptuous or jeering laughter; ridicule
k.en: to speak of or treat with contemptuous mirth

ex.en: My brothers derided our efforts, but were forced to eat their words when we won first place.
ex.cn: 我的兄弟嘲笑我们的努力，但是当我们得了第一名以后他们不得不收回那些话。

m: DE- + RIDE (ridicule) — to laugh down at. — deride, to mock.

k.syn: secondhand
k.ant: original, innovative, precursory 原创的，先驱的
k.en: lacking originality: banal

ex.en: their dull, derivative debut album
ex.cn: 他们毫无新意的首张专辑

m: from DERIVE — derived from something else, not original. — derivative, unoriginal.

k.syn: dip, fall, plunge, sink
k.ant: arise, ascend, climb, mount, upsweep, upturn上升，登上
k.en: to lead or extend downward

ex.en: The pathway descends to the river bank.
ex.cn: 通道一直向下延伸到河岸。

k.en: to originate or come from an ancestral stock or source, to pass by inheritance

ex.en: The house has descended through four generations.
ex.cn: 这座房子已经传了四代。

ex.en: a tradition descending from colonial days
ex.cn: 殖民时期传递下来的一项传统

m: DE- (down) + SCEND (climb) — climb down. — descend, to come down.

k.ant: forbears 祖先
k.en: one deriving directly from a precursor or prototype

ex.en: They are descendants of the original English and Scottish settlers.
ex.cn: 他们是最早一批英格兰和苏格兰定居者 的后裔。

m: from DESCEND — the lineage descending from an ancestor. — descendant, an offspring.

k.syn: defile, profane, violate
k.ant: sanctify, revere, hallow 尊敬，将…视为神圣
k.en: to treat（a sacred place or object）shamefully or with great disrespect

ex.en: desecrate the shrine
ex.cn: 亵渎圣地

m: DE- + SECRATE (sacred, as in "consecrate") — undo the sacred. — desecrate, to violate sacredness.

k.syn: dehydrate, parch, scorch, sear
k.ant: add water to, hydrate, drench 加水，浸透
k.der: desiccant n. 干燥剂：a drying agent（as calcium chloride）
k.cn: 使（食物）脱水以保存，使干燥
k.en: to preserve（a food） by drying

ex.en: add a cup of desiccated coconut to the mix
ex.cn: 加一杯椰蓉进行搅拌

k.syn: castrate, dampen, deaden, devitalize, enervate
k.ant: brace, energize, enliven, invigorate, stimulate, vitalize 使生机勃勃
k.en: to deprive of emotional or intellectual vitality

ex.en: That historian's dryasdust prose desiccates what is actually an exciting period in European history.
ex.cn: 那位历史学家枯燥无味的散文让欧洲一段扣人心弦的历史变得乏味单调。

m: DESICCATE — to dry out completely (think "desiccated coconut"). — desiccate, to dry out.

k.syn: assign, commission, constitute, nominate, place
k.ant: discharge, dismiss, expel, fire 解雇，开除
k.en: to pick（someone）by one's authority for a specific position or duty

ex.en: He has yet to designate his successor as head of the firm.
ex.cn: 他还没有给公司选定接班人。

k.syn: denominate, dub, entitle, label, style, term, title A man is not old as long as he is seeking something. A man is not old until regrets take the place of dreams. 只要一个人还有所追求，他就没有老。直到后悔取代了梦想，一个人才算老。 ——美国演员　巴里穆尔（J. Barrymore, American actor） “刷词，刷题，刷《再要你命3000》，为你刷出新世界” （童心，2007年10月，Verbal 700，Quantitative 800，AW 4.0 录取院校：Princeton University运筹与金融工程）
k.en: to give a name to

ex.en: He was designated Air Jordan by his fans.
ex.cn: 他被粉丝们称为“飞人乔丹”。

m: DE + SIGN + ATE — to SIGN someone for a role. — designate, to name / appoint.

k.syn: contemptible, detestable, dishonorable
k.ant: admirable, commendable, creditable, meritorious, praiseworthy 值得赞扬的
k.en: arousing or deserving of one's loathing and disgust

ex.en: Even within the prison population, pedophiles are regarded as particularly despicable.
ex.cn: 即使在囚犯内部，恋童癖者也是极其让人鄙视的。

k.syn: currish, dirty, execrable, ignominious, sordid, wretched
k.ant: honorable, lofty, noble, upright, venerable, virtuous 崇高的
k.en: not following or in accordance with standards of honor and decency

ex.en: the cad's despicable behavior toward women
ex.cn: 公车售票员对女性不得体的举动

m: from DESPISE — worthy of being despised. — despicable, contemptible.

k.syn: abhor, abominate, detest, execrate, loathe, disregard, flout
k.ant: love 喜欢
k.en: to dislike strongly

ex.en: I despise anchovies on pizza, and I refuse to eat them.
ex.cn: 我鄙视披萨上的凤尾鱼，所以从来不吃。

m: DESPISE contains SPISE ≈ SPITE — full of SPITE for. — despise, to scorn.

k.syn: authoritative, dictatorial, imperious, overbearing, peremptory, tyrannous
k.en: arbitrary, autocratic, monocratic, tyrannical

ex.en: a despotic tyrant
ex.cn: 专制的暴君

m: from DESPOT — tyrannical. — despotic, tyrannical.

k.en: a usually sweet course or dish (as of ice cream) usually served at the end of a meal

m: DESSERT — sweet course after the main meal (note: one S = desert, two = dessert sweet). — dessert, the after-dinner sweet.

k.syn: digressive, excursive, meandering, rambling, wandering
k.ant: carefully planned, strictly methodical, assiduous 精心计划的，有条理的，专心致志的
k.en: lacking a definite plan, purpose, or pattern

ex.en: a desultory search for something of interest on TV
ex.cn: 漫无目的地换台

k.en: disappointing in progress, performance, or quality

ex.en: a desultory fifth place finish
ex.cn: 让人失望的第五名

m: DESULT + ORY — Latin SALT (jump, as in "somersault") — jumping from thing to thing without aim. — desultory, lacking direction.

k.ant: tether 束缚
k.en: to separate or unfasten; disconnect

ex.en: Detach the white part of the application form and keep it.
ex.cn: 撕下申请表的白色部分并予以保留。

m: DE- (off) + TACH (attach) — un-attach. — detach, to separate.

k.ant: manumit 释放
k.en: to hold or keep in or as if in custody

ex.en: detained by the police for questioning
ex.cn: 被警察扣留下问讯

m: DE- + TAIN (hold) — hold back. — detain, to hold / delay.

k.syn: dissuade, inhibit
k.ant: inducement, incentive 引诱，刺激
k.der: deterrent adj./n. 威慑的；威慑力量
k.en: to prevent or discourage from acting, as by means of fear or doubt

ex.en: We wouldn't be deterred by threats.
ex.cn: 我们不会被威胁吓住。

ex.en: deterrent weapons
ex.cn: 威慑性武器

ex.en: a deterrent to theft
ex.cn: 对偷窃的威慑

m: DETER rhymes with TERROR — instill TERROR to discourage. — deter, to discourage.

k.syn: decadence, degeneration, deterioration, devolution, downfall, downgrade
k.ant: improvement, recovery, recuperation, rehabilitation, revitalization 改进，改善
k.en: a gradual sinking and wasting away of mind or body

ex.en: a continuing deterioration in relations between the two countries
ex.cn: 两国关系的持续恶化

m: from DETERIORATE — getting worse. — deterioration, worsening.

k.syn: deflection, departure, deviation, divergency
k.en: a turning away from a course or standard

ex.en: We'll regard this relapse as just a brief detour on your road to recovery from substance abuse.
ex.cn: 我们把这次旧病复发看成你戒瘾道路上的一个小偏离。

k.syn: deviate, diverge, sheer, swerve, veer
k.cn: 改变方向或者路线，绕道
k.en: to change one's course or direction

ex.en: We had to detour for a few miles around the section of highway under construction.
ex.cn: 为了避开正在维修的高速公路路段，我们不得不绕远几公里。

m: DE- (off) + TOUR — off the main route. — detour, an indirect route.

k.en: the process of detoxifying

m: DE- + TOXIFY — un-poison the body. — detoxification, removing toxins.

k.syn: belittle, depreciate, derogate, disparage, dispraise, write off
k.ant: extol, praise, eulogize, laud, panegyrize 赞美
k.en: to diminish the importance, value, or effectiveness of something

ex.en: It is wrong to detract from the achievements of other people in the same field.
ex.cn: 贬低同行是不对的。

k.syn: divert, call off
k.en: to draw the attention or mind to something else

ex.en: Numerous typos in the text detract the reader's attention from the novel's intricate plot.
ex.cn: 许多打印错误让读者分心，不关注复杂的情节了。

m: DE- (away) + TRACT (pull) — pull away (from value/attention). — detract, to lessen / distract.

k.syn: debris, residue, wreck
k.ant: valuable product 有用的物品
k.en: a product of disintegration, destruction, or wearing away: debris

ex.en: the detritus of war
ex.cn: 战争的残迹

m: DETRITUS contains DETRI ≈ "debris" — both mean leftover wreckage. — detritus, debris.

k.syn: constant, loyal, allegiant, dedicated, devout, loyal, pious, staunch, steadfast
k.ant: disloyal, faithless, perfidious, unfaithful 不忠诚的；recreant, traitorous, treacherous 背叛的
k.en: characterized by loyalty and devotion

ex.en: Good teachers are devoted to learning.
ex.cn: 好老师会专心学习。

k.syn: adoring, affectionate, fond, tender, tenderhearted
k.ant: unloving 无爱心的，冷漠的
k.en: feeling or showing love

ex.en: A devoted couple will enjoy sharing their lives with one another.
ex.cn: 一对恩爱的情侣喜欢和对方一起度过自己的人生。

m: from DEVOTE — committed loyally. — devoted, loyally committed.

k.syn: pious, religious, sainted
k.ant: antireligious, impious 不虔诚的
k.en: devoted to religion or to religious duties or exercises

ex.en: a devout Buddhist
ex.cn: 虔诚的佛教徒

k.syn: constant, loyal, allegiant, dedicated, pious, staunch, steadfast
k.ant: disloyal, faithless, perfidious, unfaithful 不忠诚的；recreant, traitorous, treacherous 背叛的
k.en: firm in one's allegiance to someone or something

ex.en: Devout Mavericks fans never lost faith in Nowitzki.
ex.cn: 忠诚的小牛队球迷从来没有对诺维斯基失去信心。

m: DEVOUT contains VOW — full of religious VOWs. — devout, deeply religious.

k.en: moisture condensed

m: DEW rhymes with NEW — fresh NEW droplets each morning. — dew, morning moisture.

k.syn: deft, handy
k.ant: ham-fisted, ham-handed, handless, heavy-handed, unhandy 手笨的
k.en: ready and skilled in physical movements

ex.en: a dexterous surgeon
ex.cn: 技术娴熟的外科医生

ex.en: The dexterous watchmaker was able to repair the antique watch's delicate gears and parts.
ex.cn: 手巧的手表匠能够修理古董手表精密的齿轮和部件。

k.syn: adroit, clever, cunning
k.ant: dull, foolish, silly, fatuous, unwise 傻的
k.en: mentally adroit and skillful: clever

m: from DEXTERITY — skillful with the hands or mind. — dexterous, skillfully nimble.

k.syn: demoniac, demonian, demonic, devilish, satanic
k.ant: angelic, seraphic 天使般的
k.en: of, relating to, or characteristic of the devil

ex.en: The police quickly mobilized to track down the diabolic serial killer.
ex.cn: 警察迅速行动起来追查这个恶魔般的连环杀人狂。

m: from DIABLO (devil) — devilish. — diabolic, devilish.

k.syn: vague, obscure, unexplicit, insubstantial, immaterial
k.ant: substantial 实在的；clear, distinct 清晰的
k.en: vague or insubstantial

ex.en: only a diaphanous hope of success
ex.cn: 只有一丝成功的希望

k.syn: transparent, translucent, transpicuous, gossamer
k.ant: opaque, impermeable of light 不透明的，不透光的
k.en: of such fine texture as to be transparent or translucent

ex.en: The bride wore a diaphanous veil.
ex.cn: 新娘戴着透明的面纱。

m: rhymes with TRANSPARENT-ous — DIAPHANOUS fabric is so light it's almost see-through. — diaphanous, gauzy / translucent.

k.syn: tirade, harangue, jeremiad, philippic, rant
k.en: a long angry speech or scolding

ex.en: He was forced to sit through a long diatribe.
ex.cn: 他被迫耐着性子听完一个长篇抨击性演讲。

k.syn: irony, satire
k.ant: encomium, eulogy, panegyric, tribute, laudatory piece of writing 颂词，赞赏作品
k.en: ironic or satirical criticism

ex.en: The movie reviewer wrote a diatribe of the movie describing it as having excessive sex and violence.
ex.cn: 那个影评人指责这个电影含有过多的色情和暴力。

m: DIA + TRIBE — speech DIRECTED at a TRIBE in bitter denunciation. (Or: DIA = through; TRIBE = rub — a tirade that rubs through you.) — diatribe, a bitter denunciation.

k.syn: homiletic, preachy, moralistic, sententious
k.ant: undidactic 不喜欢说教的
k.en: designed or intended to teach

ex.en: Parents' speech to their kids often seems to be painfully didactic.
ex.cn: 父母对他们孩子说的话似乎都是让人痛苦的说教。

m: DIDACTIC contains DID + ACT — "did the act of teaching" — preachily instructive. — didactic, instructive / preachy.

k.en: a perforated block through which metal or plastic is drawn or extruded for shaping; a stamp for coining

m: a DIE is a metal stamping tool — also one DICE. The dice/die is rolled or stamped. — die, a stamping mold.

k.syn: conservative, hidebound, old-fashioned, reactionary, ultraconservative
k.ant: liberal, nonconservative, open-minded, liberal 自由的，开放的
k.cn: 顽固的，保守的 n. 顽固的人，保守的人
k.en: strongly or fanatically determined or devoted/someone who opposes change and refuses to accept new ideas

ex.en: Some diehard smokers belied the doctors' suggestion.
ex.cn: 一些顽固的烟民违背了医生们的建议。

m: DIE + HARD — would rather die than change. — diehard, stubbornly resistant.

k.syn: unassured, bashful, retiring, self-effacing, withdrawn
k.ant: confident, assured, self-assured, self-confident 自信的
k.en: hesitant in acting or speaking through lack of self-confidence

ex.en: He is diffident to express his opinions in public.
ex.cn: 他在公共场合表达自己的观点时有点害羞。

k.syn: introverted
k.ant: extroverted, outgoing 外向的
k.en: reserved in manner; unassertive

ex.en: She was diffident about stating her opinion.
ex.cn: 她很内向，表达自己观点的时候有点害羞。

m: DIF- (away) + FIDENT (trust, as in "confident") — lacking trust in oneself. — diffident, shy / lacking confidence.

k.syn: circuitous, circumlocutory, long-winded, prolix, verbose, windy, rambling
k.ant: concise, pithy, succinct, terse, laconic 简洁的
k.en: being at once verbose and ill- organized

ex.en: a diffuse report
ex.cn: 冗长的报告

k.ant: concentrated 集中的
k.en: not concentrated or localized

ex.en: diffuse lighting
ex.cn: 散射光

k.syn: spread, extend, disperse, disseminate, scatter
k.ant: concentrate, center, centralize, focus 集中
k.en: to extend, scatter

ex.en: The photographer uses a screen to diffuse the light.
ex.cn: 摄影师用一个屏幕来分散光线。

m: DI- + FUSE — fuse outward, spread thinly. — diffuse, spread out / wordy.

k.en: a human finger

m: DIGIT contains DIG — your fingers DIG; or the DIGITs you press on a keypad. — digit, finger / numeral.

k.syn: aside
k.der: digression n. 题外话，跑题：the act or an instance of digressing in a discourse
k.en: to turn aside especially from the main subject of attention or course of argument

ex.en: digress from her prepared subject
ex.cn: 脱离她准备好的主题

ex.en: He digressed so often that it was hard to follow what he was saying.
ex.cn: 他经常跑题，我们很难跟上他说的内容。

m: DI- (apart) + GRESS (step) — step away from the topic. — digress, to wander off-topic.

k.syn: ruin, wreck
k.ant: be in use 还在使用；restore 恢复使用；mend, repair 修理； rejuvenate, renew, renovate 翻新
k.en: to bring into a condition of decay or partial ruin

ex.en: The house has been dilapidated by neglect.
ex.cn: 由于没人照看，这所房子已经要荒废了。

m: DILAPIDATE contains LAPID (stone, Latin) — stones falling away from a building (di-LAPID-ate). — dilapidate, to fall into ruin.

k.syn: expand, amplify, enlarge
k.ant: contract 收缩；narrow 使变窄
k.en: to enlarge or expand in bulk or extent; to become wide

ex.en: The cat dilated its eyes in the darkness.
ex.cn: 猫在黑暗中睁大眼睛。

ex.en: The drug dilates the blood vessels.
ex.cn: 药使血管膨胀。

k.syn: amplify, develop, elaborate on, flesh out, enlarge on
k.ant: abbreviate, abridge, condense, shorten 删减，缩短
k.en: to express more fully and in greater detail

ex.en: He refused to dilate upon his plan for improving the economy in the event that he won the election.
ex.cn: 他拒绝在他赢得选举的情况下透露更多关于振兴经济计划的细节。

m: DILATE contains DI + LATE — make wider / LATE-r in size. (Eyes DILATE in dark to let more LATE light in.) — dilate, to widen.

k.syn: delaying, procrastinating, dragging, lagging, tardy
k.ant: rapid, fast, fleet, precipitate, rocketing, swift, hasty, hurrying, scurrying 快速的
k.en: tending or intended to cause delay, characterized by procrastination

ex.en: dilatory tactics
ex.cn: 拖延战术

ex.en: The homeowner is claiming that local firefighters were dilatory in responding to the call.
ex.cn: 业主声称本地的消防队员对（火警）电话有拖延。

m: shares root with DELAY — DILATORY = causing delay. — dilatory, slow / procrastinating.

k.syn: dabbler, amateur, nonexpert, cognoscente; nonprofessional, amateur
k.ant: authority, expert, pro, professional, specialist 专家
k.cn: /adj. 业余爱好者（对艺术或知识领域涉猎浅薄者）/缺乏专业技术的
k.en: a person having a superficial interest in an art/lacking or showing a lack of expert skill

ex.en: Dilettante watch the scene of bustle, adept guard the entrance.
ex.cn: 外行看热闹，内行看门道。

ex.en: Many dilettante efforts could be seen at the sidewalk art show.
ex.cn: 街边艺术展上可以看到很多技术业余的作品。

m: DILETTANTE contains DELIGHT (sort of) + ANTE — one who DELIGHTs in dabbling without serious commitment. — dilettante, a dabbler.

k.syn: assiduous, industrious, sedulous, engaged, hopping, tied-up
k.ant: idle, inactive, unbusy, unemployed, unoccupied 空闲的
k.en: characterized by steady, earnest, and energetic effort: painstaking

ex.en: A new bride is diligent for three days.
ex.cn: 新娶媳妇三日勤。

ex.en: a student who has been unceasingly diligent in pursuit of a degree in mathematics
ex.cn: 一个不断勤奋追求数学学位的学生

m: DILIGENT contains DIL + GENT — "DIL(igent) GENTleman" hardworking and steady. (Also Latin DILIGERE = to single out, choose carefully.) — diligent, hardworking.

k.syn: thin
k.ant: condense, densify, concentrate, thicken 使浓缩
k.cn: 稀释/ adj. 经稀释的
k.en: to make thinner or less concentrated by adding a liquid such as water/of relatively low strength or concentration

ex.en: dilute a color
ex.cn: 稀释色彩

ex.en: a dilute acid that's safe to handle in the classroom
ex.cn: 稀释了的可以安全在教室处理的酸

k.syn: weaken
k.ant: fortify, strengthen 增强
k.en: to diminish the strength, flavor, or brilliance of by admixture

ex.en: The hiring of the new CEO diluted the power of the company's president.
ex.cn: 新的CEO削弱了公司董事长的权力。

m: DILUTE contains LUTE / LUEN ≈ "loose" — make less concentrated / LOOSEr. — dilute, to thin out.

k.en: a measure

m: DI + MENSION — Latin MENSURA = measurement. (Same root as MEASURE.) — dimension, measurement.

k.syn: abate, dwindle, lessen, lower, reduce, decline
k.ant: aggrandize, amplify, augment, boost, enlarge, escalate, expand, increase, raise, balloon 增加，增大
k.en: to（cause to）become smaller or less

ex.en: diminish an army's strength
ex.cn: 削弱军队力量

ex.en: The sound of the train diminished as our distance from it increased.
ex.cn: 当我们距离火车越远时，火车的声音就越小。

k.syn: belittle, deprecate, depreciate, disparage
k.ant: acclaim, applaud, exalt, extol, glorify, laud, praise 赞美
k.en: to lessen the authority, dignity, or reputation of: belittle

ex.en: diminish a rival's accomplishments
ex.cn: 贬低对手的成就

m: DI- (down) + MIN (small) — make smaller. — diminish, to reduce.

k.syn: blare, bluster, cacophony, chatter, clamor, discordance, racket, rattle, roar
k.ant: quiet, silence, still 安静
k.en: a loud continued noise

ex.en: The din of the engines was deafening.
ex.cn: 引擎的嘈杂声震耳欲聋。

ex.en: There's always a great din from the cafeteria during lunch.
ex.cn: 午餐时间经常从餐厅里传来很大的喧闹声。

k.syn: iterate, rehearse, reiterate
k.cn: 重复，使人厌烦地反复灌输
k.en: to say or state again

ex.en: Safety lessons dinned into us over and over.
ex.cn: 我们一遍又一遍地进行安全教育。

m: DIN rhymes with TIN — clattering TIN cans = LOUD din. — din, loud sustained noise.

k.syn: darkened, dirty, bedraggled, bemired, besmirched, dusty, mucky, muddy, nasty, smudged, soiled, sordid, stained, sullied
k.ant: clean, cleanly, immaculate, spotless, stainless, unsoiled, unstained, unsullied 干净的
k.en: darkened with smoke and grime; dirty or discolored

ex.en: A dingy room is always Dickensian image of the poor.
ex.cn: 一个肮脏而昏暗的房间经常作为狄更斯时代的穷人形象。

ex.en: The bed sheets were pretty dingy so we threw them in the laundry pile.
ex.cn: 床单太脏了，我们把它扔进了脏衣服堆。

m: DINGY rhymes with STINGY — both unpleasant; dingy = dim and dirty. — dingy, dirty / dim.

k.ant: ecumenical 世界范围的
k.en: of or relating to a diocese

ex.en: national or diocesan authority
ex.cn: 国家或教区权利

m: from DIOCESE — relating to a bishop's district. — diocesan, of a diocese.

k.syn: politic
k.ant: gauche, impolitic, tactless, undiplomatic, untactful 笨拙的，不机智的
k.en: employing tact and conciliation especially in situations of stress

ex.en: be very diplomatic with awkward clients
ex.cn: 总是有办法对付难缠的顾客

m: from DIPLOMACY — tactful in negotiation. — diplomatic, tactful.

k.syn: baleful, direful, foreboding, ill-boding, inauspicious, menacing, minatory, portentous, sinister, threatening
k.ant: propitious, unthreatening 吉利的，吉祥的
k.en: being or showing a sign of evil or calamity to come

ex.en: a dire forecast of a plunge in stock prices
ex.cn: 股市大跌不祥的预测

k.syn: alarming, direful, dread, dreadful, fearsome, forbidding, formidable, frightening, frightful, ghastly, hair-raising, horrendous, horrible, horrifying, intimidating, redoubtable, scary, shocking, terrible, terrifying
k.en: causing fear

ex.en: a series of dire tremors that hinted at a volcanic eruption
ex.cn: 一系列暗示火山爆发的恐怖的震动

k.syn: immediate, burning, compelling, critical, crying, emergent, exigent, imperative, imperious, importunate, instant, necessitous, pressing, urgent
k.ant: nonurgent, noncritical 不迫切的
k.en: needing immediate attention; urgent

ex.en: There is a dire need for food and medicine in the famine-stricken country.
ex.cn: 在闹饥荒的国家迫切需要食物和药品。

k.syn: cheerless, chill, depressing, depressive, desolate, disconsolate, dismal, drear, dreary, funereal, glum, lugubrious, miserable, morose, saturnine, somber, sullen, sunless, wretched
k.ant: bright, cheerful, cheering, cheery, festive, gay, sunshiny 愉快的
k.en: causing or marked by an atmosphere lacking in cheer

ex.en: With stock prices steadily falling, these are dire days on the trading floor.
ex.cn: 股价不断走低，交易大厅一片悲惨的景象。

m: DIRE rhymes with FIRE — a FIRE alarm signals dire emergency. — dire, dreadful / urgent.

k.syn: elegy, requiem
k.en: a slow, solemn, and mournful piece of music

ex.en: This funeral dirge is for the dead friend.
ex.cn: 这首葬礼挽歌是写给故去的朋友的。

m: DIRGE rhymes with URGE / MERGE — but somber: a DIRGE merges grief into music. — dirge, a funeral song.

k.syn: disenchant, undeceive
k.ant: lead into error 导致错误；mislead 误导
k.en: to free from error, fallacy, or misconception

ex.en: disabuse sb of the notion that...
ex.cn: 打消某人的…观点

ex.en: I must disabuse you of your feelings of grandeur.
ex.cn: 我必须消除你的傲气。

m: DIS- + ABUSE (the false belief) — to free someone from a misconception. — disabuse, to undeceive.

k.syn: dissatisfy, agitate, upset
k.ant: mollify 平息，抚慰
k.der: disaffect vt. 使不安，使不满：to fill with discontent and unrest; disaffection n. 不满，疏远：estrangement
k.en: discontented and resentful especially against authority; rebellious

ex.en: gangs of disaffected teenagers
ex.cn: 一群叛逆的年轻人

ex.en: The soldiers were disaffected toward the government.
ex.cn: 那些士兵对政府不满.

m: DIS- (un) + AFFECTED (attached) — detached, no longer loyal. — disaffected, dissatisfied / disloyal.

k.syn: demilitarize
k.ant: arm, militarize, put on guard 装备武器
k.en: to give up or reduce armed forces

ex.en: disarm the captured soldiers
ex.cn: 解除被捕士兵的武装

ex.en: The defeated nation was disarmed so that it would never again be a threat to international order.
ex.cn: 战败国被解除武装，这样一来它就不会再对国际秩序造成威胁。

k.syn: appease, assuage, conciliate, gentle, mollify, placate, propitiate
k.ant: anger, enrage, incense, inflame, enflame, infuriate, ire, madden, outrage 煽动，激起
k.en: to lessen the anger or agitation of

ex.en: Her future father-in-law was totally disarmed by her words.
ex.cn: 她未来的继父听了她的话彻底没脾气了。

k.en: to make harmless

ex.en: disarm a bomb
ex.cn: 拆除炸弹

m: DIS- (un) + ARM — remove weapons; or charm someone out of their defenses. — disarm, to remove weapons / win over.

k.syn: confusion, disarrangement, disorder, disorderliness, disorganization, havoc, mess, messiness, misorder, muddle, muss, shambles, jumble, welter
k.ant: order, orderliness 有序
k.en: a lack of order or sequence

ex.en: The room was in disarray.
ex.cn: 房间一片混乱。

k.syn: confuse, disarrange, discompose, dishevel, dislocate, disorganize, disrupt, disturb, hash, jumble, muddle, muss, rumple, scramble, upset, mess up, mix up
k.ant: arrange, array, dispose, order, organize, range, regulate, straighten up 使有序
k.en: to undo the proper order or arrangement of

ex.en: Changing offices disarrayed my papers completely.
ex.cn: 办公室搬迁使我的文件全部乱套了.

m: DIS- + ARRAY — array undone, disorder. — disarray, disorder.

k.syn: deny, repudiate, disaffirm, disclaim, disconfirm, gainsay, negate
k.ant: acknowledge, avow, concede 承认
k.en: to disclaim knowledge of, responsibility for, or association with；to declare not to be true

ex.en: disavow the rumor
ex.cn: 否认谣言

ex.en: She disavowed the testimony that she had given earlier in the trial.
ex.cn: 她在审判中否认了自己之前的证词。

m: DIS- (un) + AVOW (declare) — to declare you don't accept. — disavow, to deny / disclaim.

k.syn: perceptible, detectable, distinguishable
k.ant: indiscernible, imperceptible 不可辨识的
k.der: discernible/discernable adj. 可辨识的，可辨别的：perceptible
k.en: to perceive with the eyes or intellect; detect

ex.en: discern the motives
ex.cn: 辨识出动机

ex.en: too young to discern between right and wrong
ex.cn: 太年轻了而辨别不了是非

ex.en: a very discerning art critic
ex.cn: 眼光敏锐有洞察力的艺术评论家

ex.en: She has a discerning palate.
ex.cn: 她的味觉很敏锐。

ex.en: a discernible mark
ex.cn: 可辨别的痕迹

m: DIS- (apart) + CERN (separate, as in "discern") — to separate one thing from another mentally. — discern, to perceive clearly.

k.syn: fire, dismiss
k.ant: employ, engage, hire, take on, sign up or on 雇佣
k.en: to dismiss from employment

ex.en: discharge a worker/soldier
ex.cn: 开除员工/军籍

k.syn: disenthrall, emancipate, enfranchise, liberate, loose, manumit, release, spring, unbind, uncage, unchain, unfetter
k.ant: bind, confine, enchain, fetter 监禁，束缚
k.en: to set free（as from slavery or confinement）

ex.en: discharge the prisoners upon the signing of the peace treaty
ex.cn: 根据和平条约释放罪犯

k.syn: delivery, quietus, quittance
k.en: a freeing from an obligation or responsibility

ex.en: a full discharge from responsibility for the accident
ex.cn: 对事故完全不负有责任

m: DIS- (off) + CHARGE — release from duty / fire from a job / let off (a gun). — discharge, to release.

k.syn: acolyte, adherent, convert, epigone, partisan, votary
k.ant: leader, coryphaeus 领导者
k.en: one who accepts and assists in spreading the doctrines of another, follower

ex.en: A circle of dedicated disciples who conscientiously wrote down everything the prophet said.
ex.cn: 一圈忠实的信徒认真地记下了先知所说的话。

m: from DISCIPLINE — a follower who learns the discipline. — disciple, a follower.

k.syn: upset, addle, baffle, bamboozle, befog, befuddle, confound, fuddle, gravel, muddle, muddy, mystify, perplex, puzzle, vex
k.ant: compose, soothe, calm, pacify 使安定
k.en: upset, confuse

ex.en: invent cool new ways to discombobulate the old order
ex.cn: 发明新的好方法打乱了旧秩序

m: DISCOMBOBULATE contains BOB(UL) — the long silly word bobbles around in your mouth, throwing your sense of order into confusion. — discombobulate, to confuse.

k.syn: embarrass, abash, faze, fluster, mortify, nonplus, rattle
k.en: to put into a state of perplexity and embarrassment

ex.en: He was discomfited by the awkward situation of having his ex- girlfriend meet his current one.
ex.cn: 他的前女友看到他现任女友的情况让他很尴尬。

k.syn: balk, foil, thwart, baffle, checkmate
k.ant: advance, forward, foster, further, promote 推进，促进；encourage 鼓励
k.en: to prevent from achieving a goal

ex.en: Constant interruptions discomfited her in her attempt to finish the speech.
ex.cn: 经常打断她讲话导致她无法完成演讲。

m: DIS- + COMFIT (comfort) — make uncomfortable, embarrass. — discomfit, to embarrass / thwart.

k.syn: disturb, trouble, incommode, put out
k.ant: accommodate, assist, oblige, favor 帮忙
k.en: to cause inconvenience to: trouble

ex.en: The breakdown of her car didn't discommode her seriously.
ex.cn: 车的故障没有给她带来很大麻烦。

m: DIS- (un) + COMMODE (commodious) — make uncomfortable / inconvenience. — discommode, to inconvenience.

k.syn: agitate, bother, discomfort, disquiet, distemper, disconcert, disturb, perturb, upset, weird out
k.ant: calm, compose, quiet, settle, soothe, tranquilize 平息，抚慰
k.en: to disturb the composure or calm of; perturb

ex.en: GRE does not seem to discompose Jason; on the contrary, he looks rather relaxed. GRE
ex.cn: 没有使Jason感到慌乱，正相反，他看上去很轻松。

k.syn: disarrange, disarray, disorganize
k.ant: arrange, array, order, organize, range 使有序
k.en: to undo the proper order or arrangement of

ex.en: The wind ruffled her hair and discomposed her carefully arranged papers.
ex.cn: 风凌乱了她的头发，吹乱了她细心整理的纸张。

m: DIS- + COMPOSE — break composure, fluster. — discompose, to fluster.

k.syn: abash, discomfit, discountenance, mortify, rattle
k.ant: calm, compose, quiet, settle, soothe, tranquilize 平息，抚慰
k.en: to disturb the composure of

ex.en: We were disconcerted by the unexpected changes to the program.
ex.cn: 我们对项目未预料到的变化感到不安。

m: DIS- + CONCERT (harmony) — break harmony, unsettle. — disconcert, to unsettle.

k.syn: dissonant, cacophonous, disharmonic, disharmonious
k.ant: concordant, harmonious 和谐的
k.der: discordant adj. 不和谐的，刺耳的：disagreeable in sound; harsh or dissonant
k.en: lack of agreement or harmony（as between persons, things, or ideas）

ex.en: No discord, no concord.
ex.cn: 不打不成交。

m: DIS- + CORD — strings out of tune, disharmony. — discord, conflict / disharmony.

k.syn: abase, debase, degrade, demean, disgrace, dishonor, humiliate, shame, sink, smirch, take down
k.ant: aggrandize 提高名誉；canonize, deify 神化；elevate 提升；exalt 赞扬
k.en: to damage in reputation; disgrace

ex.en: discredit his opponents
ex.cn: 羞辱他的对手

k.syn: negate, doubt, distrust
k.ant: accept, believe, credit 接受，相信；confirm, prove, validate, verify 证实
k.en: to think not to be true or real

ex.en: I discredit the story that the old house is haunted.
ex.cn: 我怀疑那个房子闹鬼不是真的。

m: DIS- + CREDIT — remove credibility. — discredit, to undermine credibility.

k.syn: contrast, disagreement, disparity, dissimilarity, distance, distinction, diversity
k.ant: convergence, concord, consonance 一致，和谐；analogousness, community, resemblance, similarity 相似
k.cn: （在事实和宣称之间的）差异或矛盾
k.en: divergence or disagreement, as between facts or claims

ex.en: a large discrepancy between the ideal image and the reality
ex.cn: 理想与现实之间的巨大差距

m: DIS- + CREP (crackle, as in disagreement) — a gap / inconsistency. — discrepancy, an inconsistency.

k.syn: detached, disconnected, unattached, unconnected, separate
k.ant: continuous 连续的；attached, connected, joined, linked 连接的
k.en: constituting a separate entity

ex.en: a discrete variable
ex.cn: 离散变量

m: DISCRETE = separate, distinct (don't confuse with "discreet"). — discrete, separate / individually distinct.

k.syn: discreetness, prudence
k.ant: imprudence, indiscretion 轻率
k.der: indiscretion n. 不谨慎，不符合社会道德规范：an act at variance with the accepted morality of a society
k.en: the quality of being discreet; circumspection

ex.en: You must show discretion in choosing your teammates.
ex.cn: 你选择队友时必须慎重。

ex.en: resign because of financial indiscretions
ex.cn: 因为财政方面的不检点而辞职

k.syn: continence, discipline, inhibition, refrainment, restraint, self-control, self-restraint
k.ant: disinhibition, incontinence, unconstraint 不自制，不节制
k.en: the checking of one's true feelings and impulses when dealing with others

ex.en: In that job you'll be expected to show discretion and act like a professional at all times.
ex.cn: 那项工作要求你时刻保持自制，表现得像一个专业人士。

m: from DISCREET — quiet good judgment / freedom to decide. — discretion, prudence / authority to decide.

k.syn: elective, voluntary
k.ant: compulsory, mandatory, nonelective, nonvoluntary, obligatory, required 强制性的，非自愿的
k.en: left to discretion: exercised at one's own discretion

ex.en: In many restaurants, discretionary tipping is being replaced by a standard service charge.
ex.cn: 在很多饭店，标准的服务价格取代了随意支付的小费。

m: from DISCRETION — left to one's judgment. — discretionary, optional / left to judgment.

k.syn: differentiate, discern, distinguish
k.ant: confuse, mistake, mix up 混淆
k.en: to perceive the distinguishing features of; recognize as distinct

ex.en: discriminate different kinds of animals
ex.cn: 区分不同的动物

k.der: discriminatory adj. 差别对待的：marked by or showing prejudice; biased
k.syn: prejudiced, biased
k.ant: equitable, impartial, unbiased, impersonal, unprejudiced 公平的
k.en: to make a difference in treatment or favor on a basis other than individual merit

ex.en: The new law discriminated against lower-paid workers.
ex.cn: 这条新法律歧视低工资的工人。

m: DIS + CRIMIN + ATE — to detect the CRIMINAL difference between things; or to wrongly target a CRIMIN-ATE-d group. — discriminate, to distinguish / show bias.

k.syn: desultory, digressional, excursive, meandering, rambling, wandering
k.ant: keen on title, concentrated 关注主题的，集中的
k.en: moving from topic to topic without order

ex.en: a long, discursive article
ex.cn: 冗长杂乱的文章

ex.en: The speaker's discursive style made it difficult to understand his point.
ex.cn: 演讲者讲话杂乱，很难让人理解他的观点。

m: DIS + CURSIVE — like CURSIVE handwriting that wanders all over the page. — discursive, rambling.

k.syn: contemn, disrespect, slight, sniff, snub, look down
k.ant: honor, respect 尊敬；treat favorably 亲切地对待
k.en: to look on with scorn

ex.en: disdain that man for snobbishness
ex.cn: 鄙视那个势利小人

m: DIS- + DAIN (deign) — too proud to deign to acknowledge — scorn. — disdain, scornful pride.

k.syn: disengage, disentangle, untangle
k.ant: embroil, entangle 卷入；mesh 啮合
k.en: to set free from entanglement or difficulty

ex.en: Disengage the gears when you park the car.
ex.cn: 当你停车的时候要把（传动）齿轮分开。

ex.en: seek to disengage myself from the embarrassing situation
ex.cn: 试图把我自己从尴尬的气氛中解救出来

m: DIS- + ENGAGE — un-engage, separate. — disengage, to detach.

k.syn: belch, disgorge, eject, eruct, expel, jet, spew, spout
k.ant: swallow, ingest 吞下，咽下
k.en: to discharge by the throat and mouth; vomit

ex.en: He cannot disgorge a fish bone without the doctor's assistant.
ex.cn: 没有医生的帮助他吐不出鱼刺来。

ex.en: The volcano disgorged lava.
ex.cn: 火山喷出岩浆。

k.en: to give up on request or under pressure

ex.en: The corrupt officials refused to disgorge his ill-gotten gains.
ex.cn: 贪官们拒绝放弃赃款。

m: DIS- + GORGE (throat) — empty from the throat, vomit / spew out. — disgorge, to vomit / discharge.

k.syn: disaffect, displease, dissatisfy
k.ant: content, gratify, please, satisfy 使满意
k.en: to make ill-humored or discontented

ex.en: be disgruntled with sb
ex.cn: 对某人不满

k.syn: alien, alienate
k.ant: reconcile 调解，使和谐
k.en: to cause to change from friendly or loving to unfriendly or uncaring

m: DIS- + GRUNTLE (please, archaic) — leave displeased and grumbling. — disgruntle, to make sulkily discontented.

k.syn: camouflage, costume, mask
k.ant: unmask 使露出真相
k.en: to modify the manner or appearance of in order to prevent recognition

ex.en: The raiders disguised themselves as security guards.
ex.cn: 袭击者装扮成了保安人员。

k.syn: belie, blanket, conceal, cover, curtain, occult, veil
k.ant: bare, disclose, display, divulge, expose, reveal, show, uncover, unmask, unveil 暴露，展现
k.en: to keep secret or shut off from view

ex.en: That investigative reporter usually does a good job of disguising her true motives for interviewing a person.
ex.cn: 采访时，那个调查记者通常会很好地隐藏自己的真实目的。

ex.en: He always disguises his true feelings.
ex.cn: 他经常掩饰自己的真实感受。

m: DIS- (un) + GUISE (appearance) — alter appearance to hide. — disguise, to hide identity.

k.syn: aversion, disfavor, disliking, disrelish, mislike, unwillingness
k.ant: appetite, favor, fondness, liking, partiality, preference, relish, inclination 喜欢；willingness 情愿
k.en: a lack of willingness or desire to do or accept something; a lack of willingness or desire to do or accept something

ex.en: show a marked disinclination
ex.cn: 表现出明显的不情愿

m: DIS- + INCLINATION — leaning away from = reluctance. — disinclination, reluctance.

k.syn: sterilize
k.ant: infect, pollute, contaminate, taint 感染，污染
k.en: to free from infection especially by destroying harmful microorganisms

ex.en: disinfect with bleaching powder
ex.cn: 用漂白粉消毒

m: DIS- (un) + INFECT — kill the infection. — disinfect, to sterilize.

k.syn: unbury, unearth
k.ant: bury, inter, tomb, inhume 埋葬
k.en: to take out of the grave or tomb

ex.en: The Egyptian mummy was carefully disinterred in hopes that it would yield secrets about the Old Kingdom.
ex.cn: 那个埃及木乃伊被小心地挖掘出来，人们希望它可以告诉我们关于这个古老王国的一些秘密。

m: DIS- + INTER (bury) — un-bury, dig up. — disinter, to dig up.

k.syn: equal, evenhanded, impartial, just, nonpartisan, dispassionate, objective, square, unbiased, unprejudiced
k.ant: biased, inequitable, nonobjective, one-sided, partial, partisan, prejudiced, unjust 不公正的，有偏见的
k.en: free from selfish motive or interest: unbiased

ex.en: a disinterested decision
ex.cn: 公正的决定

k.syn: apathetic, incurious, insouciant, nonchalant, perfunctory, unconcerned
k.ant: concerned, interested 关心的，感兴趣的
k.en: having or showing a lack of interest or concern

ex.en: The city's philistines, naturally disinterested in art, voted to cut the museum's budget.
ex.cn: 城市里对艺术本来就不感兴趣的俗人们，投票表决要缩减博物馆的预算。

m: DIS- (without) + INTEREST (stake) — having no personal stake = impartial. — disinterested, impartial.

k.syn: chaotic, disorderly
k.ant: orderly, regular, systematic 有序的，规律的，系统的
k.en: being thrown out of orderly function

ex.en: a disjointed society
ex.cn: 混乱的社会

k.syn: disconnected, unconnected
k.ant: coherent, connected 连贯的
k.en: not clearly or logically connected

ex.en: a disjointed speech about a hodgepodge of things
ex.cn: 关于一大堆杂事的不连贯的演讲

m: DIS- + JOINTED — joints come apart, incoherent. — disjointed, incoherent.

k.syn: discrete, separate, disconnected, disunited
k.ant: combined, connected, jointed, mixed, united 连接的，混合的
k.en: marked by breaks or disunity

ex.en: a disjunctive narrative sequence
ex.cn: 分散的叙述顺序

m: DIS- + JUNCTIVE (joining) — opposite of joining, separating. — disjunctive, separating.

k.syn: break down, knock down
k.ant: assemble, construct 组装
k.en: to take to pieces also: to destroy the integrity or functioning of

ex.en: dismantle a machine
ex.cn: 分拆机器

m: DIS- + MANTLE (cover) — strip the cover, take apart. — dismantle, to take apart.

k.syn: chill, daunt, dishearten, dispirit, frustrate, unnerve
k.ant: embolden, encourage, hearten, nerve 鼓励，使勇敢
k.en: to cause to lose courage or resolution

ex.en: The excessive homework dismayed ourselves.
ex.cn: 过量的作业让我们失去了信心。

k.syn: agitate, bother, discomfort, discompose, disquiet, distemper, distress, perturb, unsettle, upset
k.ant: calm, compose, quiet, soothe, tranquilize 平息，抚慰
k.en: to trouble the mind of; to make uneasy

ex.en: Parents became increasingly dismayed by their son's GPA.
ex.cn: 父母对他们儿子的平均成绩非常焦虑。

m: DIS- + MAY (power) — sap power, fill with consternation. — dismay, to alarm.

k.syn: belittle, denigrate, deprecate, depreciate, derogate, degrade
k.ant: acclaim, applaud, exalt, extol, glorify, laud 赞扬
k.en: to lower in rank or reputation; to speak of in a slighting or disrespectful way; belittle

ex.en: use the past to disparage the present
ex.cn: 借古讽今

ex.en: Voters don't like political advertisements in which opponents disparage one another.
ex.cn: 选民不喜欢看到竞争对手互相贬低的政治广告。

m: DIS- (down) + PARAGE (rank) — knock down in rank, belittle. — disparage, to belittle.

k.syn: dissimilar, distinct, distinguishable, diverse, nonidentical, unalike
k.ant: alike, identical, indistinguishable, kindred, like, parallel, same, similar 相似的，相同的
k.en: fundamentally distinct or different in kind; entirely dissimilar

ex.en: This couple of seemingly disparate topics have something in common.
ex.cn: 这两个看似无关的主题其实有些相同之处。

m: DIS- (apart) + PAR (equal) — not equal, very different. — disparate, fundamentally different.

k.syn: disinterested, equal, equitable, evenhanded, impartial, just, nonpartisan, objective, unbiased, unprejudiced
k.ant: biased, inequitable, nonobjective, one-sided, partial, partisan, prejudiced, unjust 有偏见的，不公正的
k.cn: 客观公正的，不易被情绪或偏见影响的
k.en: devoid of or unaffected by passion, emotion, or bias

ex.en: Journalists should aim to be dispassionate observers.
ex.cn: 记者们应该朝着成为一个公正的观察者努力。

m: DIS- (without) + PASSION — without passion, coolly objective. — dispassionate, unbiased.

k.syn: alacrity, haste, swiftness, expedition, promptitude
k.ant: leisureliness, delay, procrastination 悠然；拖延
k.en: promptness and efficiency in performance or transmission

ex.en: do sth with dispatch
ex.cn: 迅速地做某事

k.syn: transfer, transmit, transport, pack off
k.ant: accept, receive 接收
k.en: to cause to go or be taken from one place to another

ex.en: to dispatch a messenger with urgent news
ex.cn: 发送一个紧急消息

k.ant: animate 使有生命
k.en: to deprive of life

ex.en: dispatch a criminal
ex.cn: 处决罪犯

ex.en: The man dispatched the termites with professional efficiency.
ex.cn: 那个男人技术纯熟地杀死了白蚁。

k.syn: conquer, defeat, subdue, triumph over, prevail over
k.ant: lose to 输给…
k.en: to achieve a victory over

ex.en: They dispatched the other team with breaking a sweat.
ex.cn: 他们险胜其他的队。

m: DIS + PATCH — patch a hole quickly; or send out (a messenger) to PATCH things up. — dispatch, to send swiftly / kill swiftly.

k.der: disposed adj. 喜欢的：willing or likely, inclined
k.syn: willing, inclined, predisposed, prone
k.ant: disinclined, indisposed 不喜欢的
k.en: to give a tendency to: incline

ex.en: faulty diet disposes one to sickness
ex.cn: 营养不全面导致疾病

ex.en: not feel disposed to argue with her
ex.cn: 不喜欢与她争执

k.der: disposal n. 处理，不再利用：the act of getting rid of something that is no longer wanted or needed
k.en: to get rid of; throw out

ex.en: the permanent disposal of radioactive wastes
ex.cn: 对放射性废物的永久处理

k.syn: array, arrange
k.ant: disarrange, disarray, disorder, upset, mess up, muss up 使混乱
k.en: to place, distribute, or arrange especially in an orderly way

ex.en: Dispose the surgical instruments in the exact order in which they would be needed.
ex.cn: 把手术仪器按他们被需要的顺序放置。

m: DIS + POSE — POSE things in place; OR dispose of = un-pose, throw away. — dispose, to arrange / discard.

k.syn: ignore, overlook, slight
k.ant: heed, mind, regard, attend to 关注
k.en: to pay no attention to

ex.en: disregard the advice of his executives
ex.cn: 漠视管理者的建议

k.syn: apathy, disinterestedness, incuriosity, nonchalance, torpor, unconcern
k.ant: concern, interest, regard 关心，感兴趣
k.en: lack of interest or concern

ex.en: Revelers fired guns in the air with complete disregard for the possible consequences.
ex.cn: 饮酒狂欢者朝空中乱开枪，一点也不关心可能产生的后果。

m: DIS- (un) + REGARD — pay no regard to. — disregard, to ignore.

k.syn: analyze
k.en: to examine, analyze, or criticize in minute detail

ex.en: dissect some basic problems in mathematics analysis
ex.cn: 仔细研究数学分析中的一些基本问题

ex.en: Let's dissect the plot of this thriller to see what makes it thrilling.
ex.cn: 让我们来仔细分析一下这个恐怖电影为什么恐怖。

k.syn: anatomize, assay, break down
k.cn: 切成片（尤其是为了解剖研究）
k.en: to cut apart or separate（tissue）, especially for anatomical study

ex.en: dissect the brain of Einstein
ex.cn: 解剖爱因斯坦的大脑

m: DIS- (apart) + SECT (cut) — cut apart for study. — dissect, to cut apart / analyze.

k.syn: dissimulate, affect, assume, bluff, counterfeit, fake, sham
k.ant: behave honestly 表现诚恳
k.en: to put on a false appearance

ex.en: dissemble fear with a smile
ex.cn: 用微笑来掩饰恐惧

ex.en: He dissembled happiness at the news that his ex-girlfriend was getting married to someone else.
ex.cn: 他听到前女友结婚的消息后假装高兴。

m: rhymes with ASSEMBLE but the opposite — DISSEMBLE = to put on a false face, hide your real one. — dissemble, to hide true feelings.

k.syn: broadcast, circulate, propagate
k.ant: gather, amass, garner 收集
k.en: to spread abroad; promulgate

ex.en: disseminate Marxism-Leninism
ex.cn: 传播马列主义

ex.en: The Internet allows us to disseminate information faster.
ex.cn: 互联网让我们更快地传播信息。

m: DIS- (apart) + SEMIN (seed) — scatter seeds widely. — disseminate, to spread widely.

k.syn: conflict, disaccord, discordance, discordancy, disharmony, dissent, dissidence, dissonance, disunion, disunity, division, friction, inharmony, schism, strife, row, divergence
k.ant: accord, agreement, concord, concordance, harmony, consensus, unanimity 和谐，一致
k.en: difference of opinion; disagreement

ex.en: Although we have dissension, we are friend all the same.
ex.cn: 虽然我们意见不合，但我们还是朋友。

ex.en: There is a continued dissension among historians on the exact spot of Columbus's first landing.
ex.cn: 对于哥伦布第一次的准确登陆地点这个问题，历史学家们总是存在分歧。

m: DIS- + SENSION (feeling, agreement) — disagreement among a group. — dissension, internal conflict.

k.syn: differ, disagree, nonconcur
k.ant: agree, assent, concur 同意
k.en: to differ in opinion

ex.en: I dissent from what you said.
ex.cn: 我不同意你说的话。

ex.en: Anyone who dissented was encouraged to speak out while they had the chance.
ex.cn: 如果谁有异议，有机会都可以发言。

k.syn: heterodoxy, nonconformity
k.ant: conformity 遵从；orthodoxy 正统
k.en: departure from a generally accepted theory, opinion, or practice

ex.en: The church reacted to any form of dissent by promptly excommunicating its proponents.
ex.cn: 教堂会立即开除任何反对正统教义的人。

k.syn: conflict, disaccord, discordance, discordancy, disharmony, dissesion, dissidence, dissonance, disunion, disunity, division, friction, inharmony, schism, strife, row, divergence
k.ant: accord, agreement, concord, concordance, harmony, consensus, unanimity 和谐，一致
k.en: a lack of agreement or harmony

m: DIS- + SENT (feel, agree) — feel differently, disagree. — dissent, to disagree.

k.syn: disband, dispel, disperse, scatter
k.ant: accumulate, gather, amass, cluster, assemble, concentrate, congregate 积聚
k.en: to drive away; disperse

ex.en: The wind finally dissipated the smoke.
ex.cn: 风终于吹散了雾气。

k.syn: waste, squander, lavish
k.ant: conserve 节省
k.en: to spend or expend intemperately or wastefully: squander

ex.en: dissipate too much time and effort
ex.cn: 挥霍了太多的时间与精力

k.syn: indulge
k.en: to indulge in the intemperate pursuit of pleasure, especially: to drink to excess

m: DIS + SIPATE — sounds like "dis-SIP-ate" — SIP by SIP it scatters away. — dissipate, to scatter / squander.

k.syn: dissipated, libertine, rakish, reprobate
k.ant: abstinent, abstemious, temperate, ascetic, stoic, spartan, self-denying 自我节制的；pure, uncorrupt, uncorrupted 纯洁的，未堕落的
k.en: lacking moral restraint; indulging in sensual pleasures or vices

ex.en: lead a dissolute life
ex.cn: 过着放荡无节制的生活

m: from DISSIPATE — morally loose, indulgent. — dissolute, morally unrestrained.

k.syn: liquefy, melt
k.ant: coagulate, solidify, freeze 凝结，凝固，结冰
k.en: to cause to pass into solution; to reduce（solid matter）to liquid form; melt

ex.en: Sugar dissolves in the water.
ex.cn: 糖在水中溶解。

k.syn: disintegrate, disband, break up
k.ant: consolidate, unify 使联合
k.en: to break into component parts; disintegrate

k.syn: dematerialize, vanish
k.ant: appear 出现；materialize 使实物化
k.en: to（cause to）cease to be visible; to（cause to）cease to exist

ex.en: The mist dissolved in the morning sun.
ex.cn: 雾消失在朝阳中。

k.syn: terminate, abrogate, annul, cancel, disannul, invalidate, negate, null, nullify, repeal, rescind
k.en: to put an end to by formal action

ex.en: The king dissolved parliament.
ex.cn: 国王废除了议会。

m: DIS- (apart) + SOLVE — break apart and disperse (in liquid or in organization). — dissolve, to break apart.

k.syn: conflict, disaccord, discordance, discordancy, disharmony, dissent, dissidence, disunion, disunity, division, friction, inharmony, schism, strife
k.ant: accord, agreement, concord, concordance, harmony, consensus, unanimity 和谐，一致
k.cn: （音调）不和谐，刺身；不一致，分歧
k.en: a harsh, disagreeable combination of sounds; discord; lack of agreement, consistency, or harmony; conflict

ex.en: cognitive dissonance
ex.cn: 认识上的分歧

ex.en: Dissonance among the three partners doomed the project.
ex.cn: 三个股东间的不调和注定了那计划的失败。

m: DIS- + SONANCE (sound) — clashing sound or disagreement. — dissonance, harsh disharmony.

k.syn: deter, inhibit
k.ant: encourage 鼓励；persuade 说服
k.en: to deter（a person）from a course of action or a purpose by persuasion or exhortation

ex.en: dissuade sb from
ex.cn: （doing）sth 劝阻/反对某人做某事

ex.en: Her parents tried to dissuade her from her intention to drop out of college.
ex.cn: 她的父母劝她不要退学。

m: DIS- (away) + SUADE (persuade) — persuade away from. — dissuade, to persuade against.

k.syn: dishonor, belittle, denigrate, deprecate, depreciate, degrade
k.ant: praise, acclaim, applaud, exalt, extol, glorify, laud 赞扬
k.en: to cause to lose honor, respect, or reputation

k.syn: bemire, besmirch, daub, muddy, smirch, smudge, soil, stain, sully
k.ant: clean 使干净
k.en: to make dirty

ex.en: hands distained with blood
ex.cn: 被血弄脏的手
