m: DIS- (un) + STAIN (color) — to discolor, dishonor. (Note: "distain" is rare; "disdain" is much more common.) — distain, to stain / dishonor.

k.ant: proximal 接近中心的
k.cn: 远离中心的，（神经）末梢的
k.en: situated away from the point of attachment or origin or a central point

ex.en: the distal end of nerve
ex.cn: 神经末梢

m: DISTAL contains DIST ≈ DISTANT — distal = the distant end. — distal, away from the center.

k.syn: expand, dilate, inflate, swell
k.ant: constrict, compress 收缩，压缩
k.en: to（cause to）swell out or expand from or as if from internal pressure

ex.en: The stomachs of starving people often distend.
ex.cn: 饥民的肚子经常涨起来。

m: DIS- + TEND (stretch) — stretch outward, swell. — distend, to swell.

k.syn: purify, filter, refine, fine
k.der: distillate n. 蒸馏物，纯化物，精华：a purified form; an essence
k.en: to increase the concentration of, separate, or purify by or as if by distillation

ex.en: Distill the water before pouring it in the steam iron.
ex.cn: 在把水倒到熨斗里前蒸馏一下

k.syn: dribble, drop, trickle
k.en: to fall or let fall in or as if in drops

ex.en: The basement walls distill water every time it rains heavily.
ex.cn: 每次下大雨时，地下室的墙壁都会渗出水来。

m: DISTILL contains STILL — a moonshine STILL distills liquid into pure spirits. — distill, to extract / purify.

k.syn: deform, misshape, torture, falsify, misinterpret, misrepresent, pervert, twist
k.en: to twist out of the true meaning or proportion

ex.en: A painter may exaggerate or distort shapes and forms.
ex.cn: 画家可能会夸张或扭曲形状。

ex.en: The coach's message was so distorted after passing through so many people that it was unintelligible.
ex.cn: 教练的信息在经过许多人传递后已经被歪曲得很难看懂了。

m: DIS- + TORT (twist) — twist out of shape. — distort, to twist out of shape.

k.syn: abstract, divert, detract, call off
k.ant: rapt 全心投入的
k.en: to draw or direct（as one's attention）to a different object

ex.en: be distracted by a sudden noise
ex.cn: 被突然的噪音分心

k.syn: agitate, bother, discomfort, discompose, dismay, disquiet, distress, perturb, upset
k.ant: calm, compose, quiet, soothe, tranquilize 平息，抚慰
k.en: to trouble the mind of; to make uneasy

ex.en: The students are easily distracted before the exam.
ex.cn: 学生们在考试前很容易焦虑不安。

k.syn: insane, delirious, hysterical
k.en: insane, mad

m: DIS- (away) + TRACT (pull) — pull attention away. — distract, to divert attention.

k.syn: agitated, delirious, distracted, frenzied, hysterical
k.ant: collected, composed, recollected, self-collected, self-composed, self- possessed 镇静的，平静的
k.en: deeply agitated, as from emotional conflict; insane

ex.en: Her distraught mother had spent all night waiting by the phone.
ex.cn: 她母亲忧心如焚，在电话旁守了一个晚上。

m: DISTRAUGHT contains TRAUGHT ≈ TAUT — emotionally pulled TAUT, frantic. — distraught, deeply upset.

k.en: a short or light-hearted song

m: DITTY rhymes with PRETTY — a short PRETTY little song. — ditty, a short simple song.

k.ant: nocturnal 夜晚的
k.en: occurring or active during the daytime rather than at night

ex.en: diurnal animals
ex.cn: 昼行性动物

k.syn: daily, day-to-day
k.en: occurring, done, produced, or appearing every day

ex.en: a love as constant and certain as the diurnal tides
ex.cn: 像每天潮汐一样始终如一的爱情

k.syn: bulletin, magazine, periodical, review, serial, journal
k.en: a publication that appears at regular intervals

ex.en: A microfilm contains a collection of diurnals.
ex.cn: 一个微缩胶片里包含很多期刊的合集。

m: DI- (day) + URNAL — daytime / daily (opposite of nocturnal). — diurnal, daytime / daily.

k.syn: detour, deviate, sheer, swerve, turn off
k.en: to change one's course or direction

ex.en: The deer abruptly diverged from its intended path the moment it spied the waiting lion.
ex.cn: 当那只鹿发现潜伏的狮子的时候就突然改变了自己原来的路线。

k.syn: branch out, divide, spread
k.ant: converge, join 会合
k.en: to（cause to）go or move in different directions from a central point

ex.en: At that point the road and the railroad tracks diverge.
ex.cn: 公路和铁路在那个点分叉了。

k.syn: differ, disagree
k.ant: conform 遵从
k.en: to become or be different in character or form: differ in opinion

k.syn: excurse, ramble, get off the subject
k.en: to depart from a set course or norm; deviate

m: DI- (apart) + VERGE — go in different directions. — diverge, to branch off.

k.syn: diverseness, multifariousness, multiplicity, variousness,
k.en: variety or multiformity

ex.en: the diversity of species
ex.cn: 物种的多样性

m: from DIVERSE — variety. — diversity, variety.

k.syn: deflect, redirect, swing, veer, wheel
k.ant: fix, set, settle 固定
k.en: to turn from one course or use to another: deflect

ex.en: divert traffic to a side street
ex.cn: 使交通转至辅路

k.syn: disport, entertain, regale, solace
k.en: to cause（someone）to pass the time agreeably occupied

ex.en: A light comedy divert the tired business executive.
ex.cn: 一个轻松的喜剧让疲惫的企业主管人员得到了放松。

k.syn: abstract, detract, call off
k.en: to draw the attention or mind to something else

ex.en: divert one's attention
ex.cn: 转移某人注意力

ex.en: The parents tried to divert the child with a toy while the doctor was giving her a shot.
ex.cn: 当医生要给那个孩子打针的时候，她的父母试图用玩具转移其注意力。

m: DI- (away) + VERT (turn) — turn aside. — divert, to redirect / amuse.

k.syn: deprivation, dispossession
k.ant: acquisition 获得；endowment 捐赠
k.en: the act of taking away from a person

ex.en: Melodramas were popular because they offered the audience a divestiture of neutrality.
ex.cn: 情节剧之所以受到欢迎是因为他们给观众一个缺乏中立的（世界）。

m: from DIVEST (un-vest, strip) — stripping someone of holdings. — divestiture, the act of stripping away.

k.syn: bare, disclose, discover, expose, uncover, unmask, unveil
k.ant: conceal, cover up, hide, mask, shroud, veil 保密，隐藏
k.en: to make known（as a confidence or secret）

ex.en: refuse to divulge details of the negotiations
ex.cn: 拒绝透露谈判的细节内容

ex.en: We tried to make him divulge the name of the winner, but he wouldn't budge.
ex.cn: 我们试图让他透露胜利者的名字，但是他没有那样做。

m: DI- + VULGE (common, as in "vulgar") — make common knowledge, reveal. — divulge, to reveal a secret.

k.syn: careen, lurch, teeter, totter, waddle
k.en: to progress feebly and unsteadily

ex.en: He could only dodder along after the operation.
ex.cn: 他手术后只能步履蹒跚地走。

m: DODDER rhymes with TODDLER — both walk shakily. — dodder, to walk shakily.

k.syn: avoid, escape, shirk, malinger, goldbrick, evade, parry, sidestep, circumvent, fence, hedge, avert, elude, shun, skirt, bilk, eschew, weasel “没有野心的人是给自己的懒惰找借口。” （卢璐，2006年10月，Verbal 750，Quantitative 790，AW 5.5，就职于Lazard，中国香港）
k.en: to avoid（a blow, for example）by moving or shifting quickly aside

ex.en: dodge a storm of bullets
ex.cn: 躲避枪林弹雨

ex.en: He dodged the first punch but was hit by the second.
ex.cn: 他躲过了第一次打击，但是没有躲过第二次。

m: DODGE rhymes with HODGEPODGE — quick zigzag movement. — dodge, to evade.

k.syn: put off, take off
k.ant: don, put on 穿上
k.en: to take off; remove

ex.en: The blazing sun soon had the men doffing their jackets.
ex.cn: 炙热的太阳很快就让男人们脱掉了夹克。

m: to DOFF a hat = take it off ("do off"). Opposite of don. — doff, to take off (clothing).

k.syn: insistent, persevering, pertinacious, tenacious, determined
k.ant: easily discouraged 容易气馁的；yielded 屈服的
k.en: continuing despite difficulties, opposition, or discouragement；showing no signs of slackening or yielding in one's purpose

ex.en: gain respect through sheer dogged determination
ex.cn: 凭借单纯的坚决赢得了尊重

ex.en: a madman who spent his life in dogged pursuit of power
ex.cn: 一个一生都坚持追求权力的疯子

k.syn: adamant, hardheaded, headstrong, inflexible, intransigent, mulish, obdurate opinionated, ossified, pertinacious, self-opinionated, self-willed, stubborn, unbending, uncompromising, unrelenting, unyielding, willful
k.ant: acquiescent, amenable, compliant, complying, flexible, pliable, pliant, relenting, yielding 顺从的
k.en: sticking to an opinion, purpose, or course of action in spite of reason, arguments, or persuasion

ex.en: Your dogged adherence to a really lame argument is embarrassing.
ex.cn: 你固执地坚持一个站不住脚的观点令人很尴尬。

m: rhymes with FOGGED but not — pronounced "dog-ged" (two syllables) — persistent like a dog with a bone. — dogged, persistently determined.

k.en: crudely or irregularly fashioned verse, often of a humorous or burlesque

m: DOGGEREL contains DOG — bad verse fit only for the DOG, not the printing press. — doggerel, awkward comic verse.

k.syn: dictatorial, dictative, authoritarian, authoritative, magisterial
k.ant: heresy, heterodoxy 异端邪说
k.der: dogmatic adj. 独断的，武断的：characterized by an authoritative, arrogant assertion of unproved principles
k.en: a doctrine or body of doctrines concerning faith or morals formally stated and authoritatively proclaimed by a church

ex.en: People are beginning to question the old dogmas.
ex.cn: 人们开始质疑旧的信条。

ex.en: The newspaper seeks to be independent of political dogma.
ex.cn: 这份报纸力求不受政治信条影响。

ex.en: a dogmatic critic
ex.cn: 独断的评论家

m: DOGMA contains DOG — held onto with DOG-like loyalty, can't be questioned. — dogma, accepted doctrine.

k.syn: abeyance, dormancy, latency, quiescence, moratorium
k.ant: continuation 持续
k.en: a state or period of inactivity, stagnation, or slump

ex.en: August is a time of doldrums for many enterprises.
ex.cn: 对于许多企业来说八月是一个低迷期。

ex.en: The economy is in the doldrums.
ex.cn: 经济低迷。

k.syn: dejection, depression, desolation, despondence, dolefulness, gloom, melancholy, unhappiness
k.ant: bliss, ecstasy, elation, exhilaration, exuberance, exultation, jubilation, rapture, felicity 极度欢喜
k.en: a state or spell of low spirits

ex.en: The team had been in the doldrums ever since losing the championship.
ex.cn: 整个队伍自从输了锦标赛以来一直情绪不佳。

m: the DOLDRUMS — the equatorial sea region with no wind, where ships stalled; figuratively, depression. — doldrums, low spirits / stagnation.

k.syn: agonized, bemoaning, bewailing, deplorable, grievous, miserable, plaintive, rueful, sorrowful, woeful
k.ant: happy, joyful, jovial, jubilant 高兴的
k.en: causing, marked by, or expressing misery or grief

ex.en: He lifted a pair of sapphire, dolorous eyes.
ex.cn: 他抬起了一双忧郁的、蓝宝石般的眼睛。

m: from Latin DOLOR (pain, sorrow) — mournful. — dolorous, sorrowful.

k.syn: idiot, fool, moron, simpleton, dullard
k.ant: illuminati, intellectual 智者；genius 天才
k.en: a stupid person

ex.en: What a dolt I have been!
ex.cn: 我是多么的愚蠢啊！

m: DOLT rhymes with JOLT and BOLT — a slow-witted person with no jolt of intelligence. — dolt, a stupid person.

k.syn: home, dwelling, habitation, abode, house, lodging
k.en: a residence; a home

ex.en: an alternate domicile in emergency
ex.cn: 紧急情况下的住所

k.syn: accommodate, bestow, lodge, harbor, put up
k.ant: banish, expel 驱逐
k.en: to establish in or provide with a domicile

ex.en: The university domiciles students in a variety of buildings in and around its urban campus.
ex.cn: 大学在城区校园周围为学生提供了多样化的宿舍。

m: DOMI- (home, as in "domestic") — your home. — domicile, a residence.

k.en: control

ex.en: escape from her mother's domination
ex.cn: 逃脱母亲的控制

m: from DOMINATE — total control. — domination, total control.

k.syn: assume, wear, put on
k.ant: doff 脱下
k.en: to put on（an article of clothing）

ex.en: He donned a raincoat for his trip.
ex.cn: 他为出行穿上雨衣。

m: to DON a coat = to put it on ("do on"). Opposite of doff. — don, to put on (clothing).

k.syn: donator, presenter, contributor, subscriber, patron, sugar daddy
k.ant: acceptor 受体
k.en: one that gives, donates, or presents something

ex.en: donors of funds to research foundations
ex.cn: 研究基金的捐赠人

m: from DONATE — one who gives. — donor, a giver.

k.syn: mess around
k.en: to scribble aimlessly, especially when preoccupied

ex.en: I often doodle when I'm on the phone.
ex.cn: 打电话时我经常乱写乱画。

k.syn: dawdle, trifle
k.en: to spend time in aimless activity

ex.en: I plan to spend the entire vacation just doodling.
ex.cn: 我打算整个假期就随便做点事打发时间。

m: DOODLE rhymes with NOODLE — squiggly NOODLE-like idle scribbles. — doodle, to scribble idly.

k.syn: dead, latent, lurking, abeyant, quiescent, inert
k.ant: active, busy, operating 活跃的
k.en: in a state of rest or inactivity; inoperative; in abeyance

ex.en: volcanoes which have been dormant for thousand years
ex.cn: 休眠了上千年的火山

k.syn: asleep, resting, napping, slumbering
k.ant: awake 清醒的
k.en: having biological activity suspended

ex.en: The bears lay dormant in their den during the winter.
ex.cn: 在冬季，熊在他们的穴里冬眠。

m: DORMANT volcano sleeps (Latin DORM = sleep, as in dormitory). — dormant, sleeping / inactive.

k.syn: morose, sulky, surly, moody
k.ant: gay 欢快的
k.cn: 闷闷不乐的，死气沉沉的
k.en: sullen, gloomy

ex.en: The captain's dour look depressed us all.
ex.cn: 船长闷闷不乐的脸色使我们所有人倍感低落。

k.syn: strict, sharp, austere, exacting, fierce, gruff, intimidating, rough, stark, stern
k.ant: benign, gentle, mild, tender 温和的
k.en: harsh and threatening in manner or appearance

ex.en: His dour criticism made us regret having undertaken the job.
ex.cn: 他严厉的批评让我们后悔接下这活儿了。

m: DOUR rhymes with SOUR — gloomy and severe. — dour, gloomy / severe.

k.syn: quench, extinguish, put out
k.ant: kindle, ignite, inflame 点燃
k.en: to put out（a light or fire）

ex.en: douse a fire with water
ex.cn: 用水熄灭火焰

k.syn: deluge, bathe, drench, soak, sodden, sop, wet
k.ant: dehydrate, desiccate, dry 脱水，干燥；parch, scorch, sear 烧焦，烤焦
k.en: to make wet

ex.en: The heavy rains thoroughly doused the tourists.
ex.cn: 大雨把游客淋得透湿。

m: to DOUSE the fire = drench it with water; douse the lights = extinguish. — douse, to drench / extinguish.

k.syn: de-emphasize, disregard, ignore, overlook, neglect, understate, play down
k.ant: address, emphasize, underscore 强调
k.en: to minimize the significance of, play down

ex.en: downplay the bad news
ex.cn: 对坏消息轻描淡写

m: DOWN + PLAY — play it down, minimize. — downplay, to minimize.

k.syn: deluge, downfall, cloudburst, rainstorm
k.ant: drizzle, sprinkle 细雨
k.en: a heavy fall of rain

ex.en: power failure due to the downpour
ex.cn: 暴雨导致的电力中断

m: DOWN + POUR — rain pouring down hard. — downpour, a heavy rain.

k.syn: authority, expert, master, maven, veteran, virtuoso
k.ant: amateur, layman 业余爱好者，门外汉；tyro, novice, neophyte, rookie, fledgling 新手
k.en: a person considered to be knowledgeable or uniquely skilled as a result of long experience in some field of endeavor

ex.en: a doyen in the industry
ex.cn: 业界的元老

k.syn: elder, senior
k.ant: junior 新人
k.en: the senior member of a group

ex.en: He's the doyen of the admission committee, and his opinion has considerable weight.
ex.cn: 他是招生委员会里的老人了，所以他的话格外有分量。

m: DOYEN rhymes with FOREMAN — both senior figures in a group. (French "doyen" = dean.) — doyen, a senior expert.

k.syn: drowse, nap, wink, slumber
k.ant: wake 醒来
k.en: to sleep lightly or briefly

ex.en: He dozed off during the lecture.
ex.cn: 他在讲座上睡着了。

m: DOZE rhymes with ROSE / DOZED off — a quick light nap. — doze, a light sleep.

k.syn: rigid, ironhanded, strict, stringent
k.ant: mild, genial, lenient 温和的
k.cn: 极其残酷的；十分严厉的
k.en: exceedingly harsh; very severe

ex.en: abolish a draconian legal code
ex.cn: 废除一部严酷的法典

m: from DRACO, the harsh Athenian lawgiver — DRACONIAN punishments are severe. — draconian, severely strict.

k.syn: extend, lengthen, prolong, protract, draw out
k.ant: drivel, gibber 快速（而模糊）地说
k.en: to speak slowly with vowels greatly prolonged

ex.en: The old woman was drawling on and on.
ex.cn: 老太太慢吞吞地说个没完。

m: DRAWL contains DRAW — to DRAW out the syllables (like a Texan). — drawl, to speak slowly.

k.syn: desolate, gloomy, sullen, dull, monotone, pedestrian, somber
k.ant: jocund 令人欢乐的
k.en: having nothing likely to provide cheer, comfort, or interest

ex.en: The day is cold, rainy, and dreary.
ex.cn: 天气寒冷，阴暗而沉闷。

k.syn: depressing, dismal, heartbreaking, mournful, pathetic, melancholy
k.ant: cheering 令人高兴的
k.en: causing unhappiness

ex.en: The couple decided to see a professional counselor in order to save their dreary marriage.
ex.cn: 这对夫妇决定咨询专业人士以拯救他们悲剧式的婚姻。

m: DREARY rhymes with WEARY — a DREARY day makes you WEARY. — dreary, dull and depressing.

k.syn: bathe, deluge, douse, soak, saturate, sodden, sop, wet
k.ant: dehydrate, desiccate, dry 脱水，干燥；parch, scorch, sear 烧焦，烤焦
k.en: to wet thoroughly

ex.en: The thunderstorm drenched us to the skin.
ex.cn: 雷雨把我们浇了个透。

m: DRENCH rhymes with QUENCH — water QUENCHes thirst by drenching the throat. — drench, to soak.

k.syn: abracadabra, nonsense, prattle, gabble
k.en: unintelligible or meaningless talk

ex.en: My roommate talks in her sleep, but it's just drivel.
ex.cn: 我的室友会说梦话，但都是些没有意义的呓语。

k.syn: babble, gabble, gibber, jabber
k.en: to talk stupidly and carelessly

ex.en: He always drivel on about his“distinguished”family.
ex.cn: 他总是痴痴地说着他所谓的“名门”出身。

m: DRIVEL rhymes with SHRIVEL — listening to drivel makes your brain SHRIVEL. — drivel, foolish talk.

k.syn: mist, sprinkle
k.ant: deluge, downpour 倾盆大雨
k.cn: /n.（下）细小、轻柔、似雾的雨，毛毛雨
k.en: a fine misty rain

ex.en: The intermittent drizzle was just heavy enough to spoil all of our outdoor activities.
ex.cn: 断断续续的小雨恰好大到让我们所有的户外活动都泡汤。

m: DRIZZLE rhymes with SIZZLE — but DRIZZLE is the opposite: gentle wet pattering. — drizzle, light fine rain.

k.syn: antic, comic, farcical, funny, hilarious, laughable, humorous, ridiculous, ludicrous, uproarious, whimsical
k.ant: lame, unamusing, uncomic, unfunny, unhumorous, unhysterical 不幽默的，无趣的
k.cn: 古怪有趣的，离奇可笑的
k.en: amusingly odd or whimsically comical

ex.en: a droll man with a strong dialect
ex.cn: 一个带着浓郁口音的有意思的人

m: DROLL rhymes with TROLL — quirkily amusing, oddly funny. — droll, amusingly odd.

k.syn: hum, buzz, bumble
k.cn: 低沉单调地说；嗡嗡地叫
k.en: to talk in a persistently dull or monotonous tone

ex.en: droning bees
ex.cn: 嗡嗡作响的蜜蜂

k.syn: burr, whir
k.cn: （昆虫振翅般）单调嗡嗡的声音
k.en: a monotonous sound like that of an insect in motion

ex.en: hear the drone of an helicopter overhead
ex.cn: 听到头顶直升机嗡嗡的声音

k.syn: dally, dawdle, hang, laze, loll, lounge
k.en: to spend time doing nothing

ex.en: Instead of getting a job, he preferred to drone and live off his parents.
ex.cn: 相对于找个工作来说，他更喜欢什么也不做，依靠他的父母过日子

m: a DRONE — a male bee that just buzzes around; or a monotonous voice. — drone, monotonous hum / male bee.

k.syn: labor, slavery, toil, travail, grind
k.ant: sinecure 闲职，美差；fun, play 玩耍，娱乐
k.cn: 苦工；单调、卑贱或无趣的工作
k.en: tedious, menial, or unpleasant work

ex.en: get away from the drudgery of their everyday lives
ex.cn: 摆脱每日单调无聊的工作

m: DRUDGERY contains DRUDGE — a DRUDGE does dull repetitive labor. — drudgery, dull menial work.

k.syn: debatable, doubtful, disputable, equivocal, problematic, questionable, shaky
k.ant: certain, incontestable, undeniable 确定的，毋庸置疑的；reliable 可靠的
k.cn: 充满不定性的，值得怀疑的
k.en: giving rise to uncertainty; questionable or suspect as to true nature or quality

ex.en: a dubious assertion
ex.cn: 一个值得怀疑的论调

k.syn: distrustful, skeptical, suspicious, unsure
k.ant: convinced, positive, sure 持肯定态度的，确信的
k.en: not feeling sure about the truth, wisdom, or trustworthiness of someone or something

ex.en: dubious about a diet that claims I can eat all I want and still lose weight
ex.cn: 对于一份声称可以一边随心所欲地吃而一边减肥的节食方案表示怀疑

k.syn: undecided, disinclined, loath, reluctant, reticent
k.ant: disposed, inclined 倾向于…的
k.cn: 迟疑不决的，不愿行动的
k.en: slow to begin or proceed with a course of action because of doubts or uncertainty

ex.en: I'm dubious about our plan to go hang gliding without having had any training.
ex.cn: 我对于不做任何训练就去玩滑翔感到很迟疑

m: from DOUBT — questionable. — dubious, doubtful.

k.syn: malleable, moldable
k.ant: inflexible 僵硬的
k.en: easily molded or shaped, malleable

ex.en: Gold is a kind of ductile metal.
ex.cn: 金是一种有延展性的金属。

k.syn: pliant, yielding, supple
k.ant: adamant, intractable, refractory, obdurate 倔强的，不易改变立场的
k.en: easily led or influenced

ex.en: a ductile personality
ex.cn: 易受他人影响的性格

m: DUCTILE metal can be drawn into wires (Latin DUCT = lead/draw); pliable. — ductile, easily shaped / pliable.

k.syn: sweet, agreeable, delightful, euphonic, mellifluous, tuneful, winsome
k.ant: cacophonous, grating 刺耳的
k.en: pleasing to the ear; melodious; generally pleasing or agreeable

ex.en: dulcet tones from harps and flutes
ex.cn: 竖琴和长笛发出的悦耳音调

m: DULCET tones — sweetly pleasant to the ear (Latin DULCIS = sweet). — dulcet, sweet-sounding.

k.syn: moron, simpleton, idiot, fool
k.ant: wit 机智的人；genius 天才
k.en: a stupid or unimaginative person

m: from DULL — a slow-witted person. — dullard, a stupid person.

k.en: a hill or ridge of sand piled up by the wind

m: DUNE rhymes with MOON — a moon-shaped sand mound shaped by wind. — dune, a sand hill.

k.syn: clone, duplicate, facsimile, imitation, mock, replica
k.ant: archetype, original, prototype 原型
k.en: something that is made to look exactly like something else

ex.en: He built a dupe of the original model which is locked in a vault.
ex.cn: 他仿造保险柜里的模型做了一个复制品。

k.syn: fool, victim, gull
k.ant: connoisseur 行家，鉴赏家
k.en: one that is easily deceived or cheated

ex.en: The swindler was able to escape with all of the dupe's money.
ex.cn: 骗子带着受骗人的财产成功逃跑了。

k.syn: bamboozle, beguile, cheat, cozen, delude, gull, hoax, hoodwink
k.en: to deceive（an unwary person）

ex.en: The public is easily duped by extravagant claims in advertising.
ex.cn: 大众极易被广告中夸大的介绍误导。

m: DUPE rhymes with DOOPed — a DUPE is one who got DUPED (fooled). — dupe, to deceive / a fool.

k.syn: artifice, deceit, craft, cunning, fraudulence, guile
k.ant: honesty, artlessness, forthrightness, guilelessness, ingenuousness, sincerity 诚实，坦诚
k.en: deliberate deceptiveness in behavior or speech

ex.en: accidentally reveal their duplicity
ex.cn: 不经意间暴露了他们的口是心非

m: from DUPLICATE (double) — being two-faced. — duplicity, deceitful double-dealing.

k.syn: abate, decrease, drop, diminish, reduce, shrink, taper, wane, knock down
k.ant: aggrandize, balloon, burgeon, increase 增加；accumulate 累积
k.en: to become gradually less until little remains

ex.en: His vast fortune is dwindling away.
ex.cn: 他的巨额财富正在慢慢缩水。

m: DWINDLE rhymes with SPINDLE — spun thinner and thinner on a spindle. — dwindle, to shrink.

k.cn: 阅读障碍，识别或理解书写文字的能力受到损伤
k.en: a learning disability involving difficulties in acquiring

m: from DYS- (bad) + LEXIA (reading) — reading disability. — dyslexia, a reading disorder.

k.syn: cantankerous, disagreeable, irritable, fretful, irascible, peevish, splenetic, surly
k.ant: amiable, easygoing, genial, good-humored, good-natured, good- tempered 随和的，和蔼的
k.en: bad-tempered

ex.en: The sultry day makes us dyspeptic.
ex.cn: 闷热潮湿的天气让我们变得脾气暴躁。

k.en: pertaining to, subject to, or suffering from dyspepsia

m: from DYS- (bad) + PEP (digestion) — bad digestion / sour temperament. — dyspeptic, irritable / digestion-troubled.

k.en: a young

m: EAGLE + LET (small) — a baby eagle. — eaglet, a young eagle.

k.syn: grave, serious, solemn, staid, sober
k.ant: facetious, frivolous, flip, flippant, playful 轻浮的
k.en: characterized by or proceeding from an intense and serious state of mind, grave

ex.en: an earnest machine operator
ex.cn: 一丝不苟的机器操作员

m: EARNEST contains EARN — only the truly serious EARN it. — earnest, sincerely serious.

k.en: a device for insertion into the outer opening of the ear (as to keep out water or deaden sound)

m: EAR + PLUG — plug for the ear. — earplug, sound-blocking ear insert.

k.syn: hail, hearing, sound
k.en: the range within which one may hear a person's unaided voice

ex.en: wait until he was out of earshot
ex.cn: 等到他听不见的时候

m: EAR + SHOT — within "shot" of the ear, hearable distance. — earshot, hearing range.

k.syn: blaring, blasting, deafening, loud, piercing, plangent, resounding, roaring, stentorian, thunderous
k.ant: gentle, soft（音乐）轻柔的
k.en: distressingly loud or shrill

ex.en: earsplitting noise of airplane engines
ex.cn: 飞机引擎的巨大噪音

m: EAR + SPLITTING — so loud it splits the ear. — earsplitting, deafeningly loud.

k.en: a frame for supporting something (as an artist's canvas)

m: EASEL rhymes with WEASEL — but it's the painter's stand. (Or: EASEL holds canvas with EASE.) — easel, a painter's stand.

k.syn: overhear, wiretap
k.en: to listen secretly to the private conversation of others

ex.en: eavesdrop on the conversation
ex.cn: 偷听谈话

m: to EAVESDROP — to listen secretly (originally: stand under the eaves of a house to hear inside). — eavesdrop, to listen secretly.

k.syn: boiling, exuberant, effervescent, vivacious
k.ant: tepid, torpid 无精打采的
k.en: zestfully enthusiastic

ex.en: The ebullient dancers left an enduring impression on us.
ex.cn: 热情洋溢的舞者给我们留下了难以磨灭的印象。

m: from EBULLIENT's BUBBLE-like sound — bubbling over with enthusiasm. — ebullient, exuberantly cheerful.

k.syn: strange, bizarre, erratic, idiosyncratic, odd, offbeat, outlandish, quaint, weird
k.ant: ordinary, regular 常规的
k.cn: 行为出格的，不循规蹈矩的
k.en: deviating from conventional or accepted usage or conduct

ex.en: an eccentric millionaire
ex.cn: 行为怪异的百万富翁

k.syn: fame, renown, brilliance, triumph
k.ant: debacle, fiasco 惨败
k.en: brilliant or conspicuous success

ex.en: The premier of Mozart's Le Nozze di Figaro enjoyed a great éclat in 1786.
ex.cn: 莫扎特歌剧《费加罗的婚礼》于1786年的首演获得了极大的成功。

m: EC- (out of) + CENTRIC (center) — off-center, unconventional. — eccentric, unconventional.

k.syn: decadence, degeneration, degradation, deterioration, ebb, fall
k.ant: ascent, rise, upswing 上升，崛起
k.en: a change to a lower state or level

ex.en: the eclipse of the town from a grand seaside resort to a tacky tourist trap
ex.cn: 该城镇从壮丽辉煌的海边旅游圣地退化成了俗气的坑人景点

k.syn: adumbrate, obscure, overshadow
k.cn: 使声望下降，使黯然失色
k.en: to obscure or diminish in importance, fame, or reputation

ex.en: Her score eclipsed the old record.
ex.cn: 她的分数使旧的纪录黯然失色。

k.syn: beat, exceed, excel, outshine, outstrip, surpass, transcend
k.ant: fall behind 落后
k.en: to be greater, better, or stronger than

ex.en: The brilliant young pianist now eclipsed even his own mentor in musical artistry.
ex.cn: 这位极富天赋的小钢琴家如今甚至已超越了他的音乐导师。

m: to ECLIPSE = to overshadow / outdo (the moon eclipses the sun). — eclipse, to overshadow / outshine.

k.syn: mania, rhapsody
k.ant: self-control 自控，自制；composure, sangfroid 镇定
k.en: a state of being beyond reason and self- control

ex.en: an ecstasy of rage
ex.cn: 无法自控的愤怒

k.syn: delight, elation, euphoria, exhilaration, rapture, transport
k.ant: depression, melancholy 沮丧
k.en: intense joy or delight

ex.en: be in ecstasy over the offer from Harvard University
ex.cn: 因被哈佛录取而欣喜若狂

m: ECSTASY contains EC + STASY (standing) — to be "out-of-standing" with joy, beside yourself. (EC- = out of, as in ECCENTRIC.) — ecstasy, intense joy.

k.syn: catholic
k.ant: diocesan 主教管辖特定小教区的
k.en: of, relating to, or representing the whole of a body of churches

k.syn: universal, cosmopolitan, global, planetary, worldwide
k.ant: provincial, insular 狭隘的
k.en: worldwide or general in extent, influence, or application

ex.en: an ecumenical scope
ex.cn: 国际视野

m: from ECUMENE (the inhabited world) — universal in scope, especially across Christian churches. — ecumenical, universal / inclusive.

k.en: a current moving contrary

m: EDDY rhymes with READY — water READY to swirl backward. — eddy, a swirling current.

k.syn: eatable, comestible, consumable, digestible, esculent
k.ant: inedible 不可食用的
k.en: fit to be eaten

ex.en: No chemicals in the laboratory is edible.
ex.cn: 实验室中的任何化学试剂都不能食用。

m: from EDIT-eat — fit to eat. — edible, fit to eat.

k.syn: building, erection, palace
k.ant: cottage 小棚舍
k.en: a large or massive structure

ex.en: The Capitol is one of the most impressive edifices in the United States.
ex.cn: 国会山是美国最令人印象深刻的建筑之一。

k.syn: architecture, configuration, framework, skeleton, structure
k.en: the arrangement of parts that gives something its basic form

ex.en: The edifice of the argument is quite simple, once you get past the fancy language.
ex.cn: 只要你看透了表面花哨的语言，你会发现这段论证的基本构架是非常简单的。

m: EDIFICE contains EDIFY (build up morally) — a grand built-up structure. — edifice, a large structure.

k.syn: eradicate, erase, expunge, exterminate, extirpate, liquidate, eclipse, obliterate, wipe, root out
k.ant: blazon, decorate 装饰；emboss 使凸起，使显现
k.der: self-effacing adj. 谦卑的，低调的
k.en: to eliminate or make indistinct by or as if by wearing away a surface

ex.en: efface those unpleasant memories
ex.cn: 抹去那些不快的记忆

m: EF- (out) + FACE — wipe out the face / surface. — efface, to erase.

k.syn: bubble, foam, froth
k.en: to bubble, hiss, and foam as gas escapes

ex.en: effervesce with bubbles
ex.cn: 冒气泡

k.syn: rejoice, exult, jubilate, triumph
k.ant: grieve 感到极度悲伤
k.der: effervescent adj. 热情洋溢的；effervescence n. 热情洋溢
k.en: to show high spirits or animation

ex.en: effervesce over the news of victory
ex.cn: 因胜利的消息而兴奋

m: from EFFERVESCENT (bubbling soda) — bubble up with enthusiasm. — effervesce, to bubble / be lively.

k.syn: consumed, debilitated, decadent, degenerate, depleted, drained, exhausted, feeble
k.ant: hale, sound, robust 强健的，充满活力的
k.en: depleted of vitality, force, or effectiveness

ex.en: an effete monarchy
ex.cn: 奄奄一息的君主制

k.syn: frail, invertebrate, nerveless, spineless
k.ant: backboned, firm, hard, strong, tough 刚毅的
k.en: lacking strength of will or character

ex.en: The government is too effete to take out the powerful special interests that really ruin this state.
ex.cn: 该国政府过于懦弱，不敢去摧毁那些祸害国家的特殊利益集团。

m: EFFETE rhymes with EFFETE/DEFEAT — exhausted, defeated of vitality. — effete, weak / decadent.

k.syn: malodor, stink, stench
k.ant: fragrance, aroma 芳香
k.en: an offensive exhalation or smell

ex.en: Repulsive effluvia made us vomit.
ex.cn: 这恶臭让我们作呕。

k.syn: dross, refuse, rubbish, trash, waste
k.en: a by-product especially in the form of waste

ex.en: With nothing but effluvia obtained, researchers decided to abandon this method.
ex.cn: 由于得不到目标产物，研究者们决定放弃这种方法。

m: EF- (out) + FLU (flow) — flowing out, bad smell or waste. — effluvium, foul outflow.

k.syn: audacity, brashness, brazenness, insolence, nerve, presumption, temerity
k.ant: decorum, propriety, courtesy, grace 得体；timidity 胆小
k.en: flagrant disregard of courtesy or propriety and an arrogant assumption of privilege

ex.en: The man had the effrontery to insult her father.
ex.cn: 这个男人胆敢辱骂她的父亲。

m: EF- (out) + FRONT — putting your face out boldly, shameless nerve. — effrontery, shameless boldness.

k.en: shining brilliantly;

m: EF- (out) + FULG (shine) — shining out brightly. — effulgence, radiant brightness.

k.syn: emotional, expansive, demonstrative, gushy, passionate
k.ant: inhibited, reserved, restrained 节制的；undemonstrative, unemotional 不易动情的；numb, torpid 麻木不仁的
k.cn: 感情泛滥的/溢于言表的；感情表达不节制的/过度的；过分多情的
k.en: unrestrained or excessive in emotional expression

ex.en: The principal delivered an effusive address at the commencement ceremony.
ex.cn: 校长在毕业典礼上做了充满感情的演讲。

m: EF- (out) + FUSIVE (pour) — pouring out emotion excessively. — effusive, gushingly emotional.

k.syn: disinterested, impartial, unprejudiced, unbiased, objective
k.ant: inequitable, discriminating, unfair 有偏见的，不公平的
k.en: affirming, promoting, or characterized by belief in equal political, economic, social, and civil rights for all people

ex.en: the egalitarian principle guiding his endeavor
ex.cn: 引导他奋斗的平等主义原则

m: from EQUAL — believing in equality. — egalitarian, advocating equality.

k.en: the ethical belief that self-interest

m: from EGO — self-centeredness. — egoism, selfishness.

k.syn: circumstantial, complex, complicated, intricate, knotty, minute, particular, particularized, sophisticated
k.ant: sketchy, brief, compendious, summary 概要性的
k.en: marked by complexity, fullness of detail, or ornament

ex.en: an elaborate manual
ex.cn: 内容详尽的使用手册

k.syn: develop, expand, amplify, explain
k.ant: abstract, abbreviate, condense, simplify 简化；downplay, ignore, neglect, overlook 忽视，忽略
k.en: to expand something in detail

ex.en: The defense committee asked the PhD candidate to elaborate one critical assumption in his thesis.
ex.cn: 答辩委员会要求答辩的博士进一步说明他毕业论文中的一个关键假设。

m: EL- + LABOR — worked out in detail with much labor. — elaborate, detailed / to develop in detail.

k.syn: bouncy, flexible, malleable, resilient, stretchable, supple
k.ant: rigid, stiff 僵化的
k.der: inelastic adj. 无弹性的，非弹性的
k.en: easily resuming original shape after being stretched or expanded

ex.en: elastic rubber band
ex.cn: 弹性橡胶圈

k.syn: adaptable, adjustable, buoyant, pliable, volatile
k.ant: established, fixed, immutable 不变的
k.cn: 能（迅速从伤痛中）恢复的
k.en: capable of recovering quickly especially from depression or disappointment

ex.en: owe her success to an elastic optimistic nature
ex.cn: 将她的成功归因于能迅速走出悲伤的乐观品质

m: from ELASTICITY — stretchy, resilient. — elastic, stretchy / resilient.

k.syn: cheer, excite, exhilarate, inspire, stimulate
k.ant: depress, dishearten, sadden 使沮丧
k.der: elated adj. 高兴的；elation n. 高兴
k.en: to fill with joy or pride

ex.en: The phenomenal sales record elated him.
ex.cn: 卓越的业绩让他高兴不已。

m: EL- (out) + LATE (lift, as in "relate") — lift the spirits. — elate, to make joyful.

k.en: the joint

m: ELBOW contains EL + BOW — your arm BOWs at the L-shaped joint. — elbow, the arm joint.

k.syn: benevolent, charitable, humane, humanitarian, philanthropic
k.ant: parsimonious 吝啬的
k.en: of, relating to, or supported by charity

ex.en: an eleemosynary foundation funded by the Bill Gates
ex.cn: 由比尔·盖茨夫妇资助的慈善基金

m: rare word — ELEEMOSYNARY relates to charity (same root as "alms"). — eleemosynary, charitable.

k.syn: dirge, lamentation, requiem
k.ant: ode 颂歌，颂词
k.en: a song or poem expressing sorrow or lamentation

ex.en: a moving elegy played at the funeral
ex.cn: 葬礼上演奏的催人泪下的挽歌

m: ELEGY rhymes with EULOGY — both honor the dead. — elegy, a mourning poem.

k.syn: huge, colossal, enormous, gargantuan, gigantic, massive, prodigious, tremendous
k.ant: microscopic, minute, tiny, infinitesimal 细微的，微小的
k.en: having enormous size or strength

ex.en: an elephantine meteor crate
ex.cn: 巨大的陨石坑

k.syn: awkward, graceless, maladroit
k.ant: graceful 优雅的
k.en: clumsy, ponderous

ex.en: elephantine movements
ex.cn: 笨拙的行动

m: ELEPHANT + INE — like an elephant: huge or clumsy. — elephantine, huge / clumsy.

k.syn: advance, boost, enhance, ennoble, raise, upgrade
k.ant: abase, debase, demote, degrade 贬低
k.cn: （在道德、智力、文化水平上）提升
k.en: to improve morally, intellectually, or culturally

ex.en: a novel that both entertains and elevates readers
ex.cn: 一本寓教于乐的小说

k.syn: elate, enrapture, exhilarate, intoxicate, transport
k.ant: depress 使沮丧
k.en: to raise the spirits of

ex.en: one of the most elevating moments in their lives
ex.cn: 生命中最让人兴奋的时刻之一

m: to ELEVATE = to raise up (an elevator). — elevate, to raise up.

k.syn: arouse, evoke, excite, inspire, provoke, raise
k.ant: appease, placate, mollify, pacify 平息
k.en: to draw forth or bring out

ex.en: The king's speech elicited lasting cheer and applause.
ex.cn: 国王的演讲激起了持续的欢呼和掌声。

m: ELICIT rhymes with ILLICIT but the opposite — ILLICIT is illegal, ELICIT is to draw out. (E- = out, LICIT = let.) — elicit, to draw out.

k.en: marks or a mark (as ...) indicating an omission (as of words) or a pause

m: ELLIPSIS — three dots showing words ELLIPSED away (omitted). — ellipsis, the "…" punctuation.

k.syn: ambiguous, arcane, cryptic, enigmatic, equivocal, inscrutable, murky, nebulous, occult, opaque, vague
k.ant: clear, explicit, unambiguous, unequivocal 清晰明确的
k.en: of or relating to deliberate obscurity（as of literary or conversational style）

ex.en: give an elliptical response to the inquiry
ex.cn: 就问题给出一个含糊其辞的回答

k.syn: oval, ovate
k.ant: circular, round 圆形的
k.en: of, relating to, or shaped like an ellipse

m: from ELLIPSIS — omitting words, hard to follow; or shaped like an ellipse. — elliptical, vague / oval-shaped.

k.syn: clarify, clear, construe, explain, explicate, expound, illustrate
k.ant: confuse, obfuscate, obscure 使困惑；garble 曲解
k.en: to make lucid especially by explanation or analysis

ex.en: elucidate an abstruse equation in quantum mechanics
ex.cn: 阐明一个深奥难懂的量子力学方程

m: EL- + LUCID (clear) — make clear. — elucidate, to clarify.

k.ant: fatten 变胖；invigorate 使充满活力
k.en: to cause to lose flesh so as to become very thin

ex.en: become emaciated by long illness
ex.cn: 因长年累月的病痛而消瘦

k.syn: droop, flag, sag, decay, enfeeble, enervate, languish, wane, wither
k.en: to make feeble

ex.en: His hesitation emaciated the force of his argument.
ex.cn: 他的迟疑削弱了他论证的力度。

m: EMACIATE contains MACI ≈ MEAGER — meager, thin, wasting. — emaciate, to make thin.

k.syn: discharge, enfranchise, free, liberate, loose, manumit, release
k.ant: bind, shackle 束缚；enslave, enthrall 奴役
k.der: emancipation n. 解放，获得自由
k.en: to free from bondage, oppression, or restraint

ex.en: emancipate students from excessive assignments
ex.cn: 将学生从题海中解放出来

m: shares -CIPATE with ANTICIPATE — to free from bondage (Lincoln's Emancipation Proclamation). — emancipate, to liberate.

k.syn: ban, interdiction, proscription, sanction, veto
k.ant: approval, license, permission, prescription 许可
k.en: a legal prohibition on commerce

ex.en: a trade embargo on luxuries
ex.cn: 对奢侈品的禁运令

m: EMBARGO contains BAR — an official BAR on trade. — embargo, a trade ban.

k.syn: board
k.ant: disembark 下车，下船
k.en: to go aboard a vessel or aircraft, as at the start of a journey

ex.en: With all cargos embarked, the ship weighed the anchor.
ex.cn: 所有货物都上船了，货船拔锚起航。

k.syn: begin, commence, launch, initiate, start
k.ant: conclude, end, finish, terminate 结束
k.en: to make a start

ex.en: embark on a world tour
ex.cn: 开始了环游世界的旅程

m: EM- (on) + BARK (boat) — get on the boat / start a journey. — embark, to begin a journey.

k.syn: abash, discomfit, disconcert, faze, fluster, mortify, nonplus
k.der: embarrassing adj. 令人窘迫的；embarrassment n. 尴尬
k.en: to cause to experience a state of self-conscious distress

ex.en: bawdy jokes that embarrassed her
ex.cn: 让她尴尬的荤段子

k.syn: encumber, handicap, hinder, impede, inhibit, obstruct, shackle, stymie, trammel
k.ant: aid, facilitate 促进
k.en: to create difficulty for the work or activity of

ex.en: A lot of this paperwork is unnecessary and just embarrasses the organization.
ex.cn: 这些文案工作很多都是多余的，只会妨碍组织的工作。

m: EMBARRASS contains BAR — BARriers raised, unable to act, red-faced. — embarrass, to make uncomfortable.

k.syn: entrench, fix, ingrain, root
k.ant: extract, dislodge, uproot 取出，移出
k.en: to enclose closely in or as if in a matrix

ex.en: The thorn was embedded in her thumb.
ex.cn: 刺扎入了她的拇指。

m: EM- (in) + BED — set firmly in. — embed, to set firmly in.

k.syn: appropriate, peculate
k.ant: confiscate 没收充公
k.en: to appropriate（as property entrusted to one's care） fraudulently to one's own use

ex.en: limitations on the right of the state to embezzle private property
ex.cn: 限制政府挪用私人财产的权利

m: EMBEZZLE rhymes with BEDAZZLE — but criminally; you BEDAZZLE accounts and pocket the funds. — embezzle, to steal funds.

k.syn: encourage, animate, cheer, hearten, strengthen
k.ant: daunt, discourage, dishearten, dispirit 使沮丧，使丧失信心
k.en: to instill with boldness or courage

ex.en: be emboldened by the wine
ex.cn: 借酒壮胆

m: EM- (make) + BOLD — make bold. — embolden, to encourage.

k.syn: adorn, beautify, bedeck, blazon, ornament, garnish
k.ant: flatten, efface 抹平，抹去
k.en: to raise the surface of into bosses; especially to ornament with raised work

ex.en: embossed with a design of Shanghai skyline
ex.cn: 用刻有上海城市轮廓的浮雕图案进行装饰

m: EMBOSS contains BOSS — push the design like a BOSS, raised proudly. — emboss, to stamp with a raised pattern.

k.syn: accept, adopt, espouse, welcome
k.ant: abjure, abrogate, renounce, spurn 废除，摒弃，放弃
k.en: to take up willingly or eagerly

ex.en: embrace the opportunity to study further
ex.cn: 乐于接受继续深造的机会

k.syn: circle, encompass, envelop, wrap
k.en: to surround or cover closely

ex.en: The stone walls that embrace the monastery serve to symbolize its function as a retreat from an unquiet world.
ex.cn: 包围着修道院的石墙是它远离喧嚣尘世的象征。

m: EMBRACE contains BRACE — to BRACE someone in your arms. — embrace, to hug / welcome.

k.syn: elaborate, embellish, exaggerate, magnify, overstate, hyperbolize
k.ant: downplay, de-emphasize 轻描淡写
k.cn: （时常伴有夸张和想象内容地）详细说明
k.en: to give an elaborate account of, often with florid language and fictitious details

ex.en: embroider the story of his adventures in the army
ex.cn: 细说他服役时惊险的经历

m: to EMBROIDER a story = to add fanciful details (literally: stitch decorations). — embroider, to elaborate fancifully.

k.syn: migrate
k.ant: immigrate 移入，入境；repatriate 遣返
k.der: emigrant n.（向外的）移民；emigration n.（向外）移居，迁徙
k.en: to leave one's place of residence or country to live elsewhere

ex.en: have to emigrate to the United States due to political persecution
ex.cn: 因政治迫害而不得不移民美国

m: E- (out) + MIGRATE — migrate out of one's country. — emigrate, to leave one's country.

k.en: a position of prominence or superiority; a lofty place

ex.en: a scientist of great eminence
ex.cn: 声名显赫的科学家

m: from EMINENT — high distinction. — eminence, high standing.

k.syn: appeasing, assuaging, mollifying, mitigating, relieving
k.ant: aggravating, intensifying 使（局势、情况）加剧恶化的
k.en: making less intense or harsh

ex.en: soothe us in our grieves with emollient words
ex.cn: 用安慰的话语抚平我们的悲伤

m: EMOLLIENT contains MOLL ≈ MOLLIFY — both soothe and soften. — emollient, softening / soothing.

k.syn: copy, imitate, mimic, mime
k.en: to strive to equal or excel, especially through imitation

ex.en: a role model worthy emulating
ex.cn: 值得效仿的榜样

m: EMULATE contains MUL ≈ "mimic" — try to MIMIC and surpass. (Latin AEMULUS = rival.) — emulate, to imitate enviously.

k.syn: constitute, establish, legislate, pass, ratify, ordain, lay down
k.ant: abolish, repeal, rescind, revoke 废除
k.en: to establish by legal and authoritative act

ex.en: Congress enacted the tax reform bill.
ex.cn: 国会颁布了税法改革案。

m: EN- + ACT — turn into law/action. — enact, to make into law.

k.syn: glaze, varnish
k.ant: efface 抹去
k.cn: 涂亮漆；使具有光滑或亮泽的表面
k.en: to give a glossy or brilliant surface to

k.syn: adorn, beautify, bedeck, decorate, embellish, garnish, ornament
k.ant: disfigure 破坏…的外形
k.en: to adorn with a brightly colored surface

m: ENAMEL contains NAME + L — what you put your NAME on the L(ovely) shiny tooth surface. (Or: contains NAMEL ≈ "name + el" — a glossy named coating.) — enamel, glossy hard coating.

k.syn: accolade, applause, compliment, eulogy, laud, panegyric, salutation, tribute
k.ant: criticism 批评；abuse, invective, vituperation 辱骂
k.en: glowing and warmly enthusiastic praise

ex.en: receive encomiums from literary critics
ex.cn: 受到文学评论家的好评

m: ENCOMIUM rhymes with PODIUM — what you receive from the podium = high praise. — encomium, lavish praise.

k.syn: contain, comprehend, embody, entail, involve, subsume
k.ant: exclude 排除
k.en: to constitute or include

ex.en: a plan that encompasses multiple aims
ex.cn: 一项包含了多个目标的计划

k.syn: circle, embrace, enclose, environ, surround
k.en: envelop

ex.en: Berlin had already been encompassed by the Red Army and all Soviet soldiers were patiently waiting for the order to put a final nail in Nazi Germany's coffin.
ex.cn: 柏林已被红军包围，苏联士兵们静静地等待着最后的命令，彻底消灭纳粹德国。

m: EN- (in) + COMPASS — include within scope. — encompass, to include / surround.

k.syn: hamper, hinder, impede, obstruct, retard, stymie
k.ant: promote, further 推动；aid, assist, facilitate, help 帮助
k.en: to impede or hamper the function or activity of

ex.en: Negotiation between the two parties were encumbered by a lack of trust.
ex.cn: 两党之间的谈判因缺乏信任而受到阻碍。

k.syn: burden, lade, lumber, saddle
k.ant: unload 卸下
k.en: to place a weight or burden on

ex.en: Don't encumber your pack animal so much that it can hardly move.
ex.cn: 别让你的牲畜驮这么多东西，它都走不动了。

m: EN- + CUMBER (burden, as in "cumbersome") — load down with burdens. — encumber, to burden.

k.syn: aboriginal, indigenous, native, domestic
k.ant: exotic, foreign, nonindigenous, nonnative 国外的，外来的
k.en: prevalent in or peculiar to a particular locality, region, or people

ex.en: an endemic disease
ex.cn: 地方性疾病

m: EN- (in) + DEMIC (people) — native to a specific population/place. — endemic, native to an area.

k.syn: advocate, back, approve, certify, champion, sanction, support, uphold
k.ant: deprecate, impugn, oppose 反对
k.en: to express support or approval of publicly and definitely

ex.en: endorse a presidential candidate
ex.cn: 公开支持一名总统候选人

m: to ENDORSE a candidate = publicly back them (originally: sign on the back of). — endorse, to publicly support.

k.syn: debilitate, disable, enfeeble, fatigue, sap
k.ant: fortify, strengthen 强化；energize, invigorate, vitalize 给…注入活力
k.en: to weaken or destroy the strength or vitality of

ex.en: His constitution was enervated by lustful lifestyle.
ex.cn: 他荒淫无度的生活习惯已使他身体虚弱。

k.syn: dampen, deaden, petrify
k.ant: brace, energize, enliven, invigorate, vitalize, vivify 使充满活力
k.en: to deprive of emotional or intellectual vitality

ex.en: A lifetime of working in dreary jobs had enervated his very soul.
ex.cn: 一生忙于无聊的工作让他变得无比麻木。

m: EN- + NERVE — sap of nerve / weaken. (Counterintuitive: enervate = weaken, not energize.) — enervate, to weaken.

k.ant: disenfranchise, disempower 剥夺权利
k.en: to endow with the rights of citizenship, especially the right to vote

ex.en: Slaves in US were emancipated in 1863 but were not enfranchised until the Fifteenth Amendment went into effect in 1870.
ex.cn: 尽管在美国奴隶于 1863年就获得了解放，但他们直到1870年《第五修正案》正式生效才获得选举权。

k.syn: discharge, emancipate, free, liberate, manumit, rescue, unfetter
k.ant: bind, confine, enfetter 束缚；subjugate, subdue; enthrall 使臣服；使成为奴隶
k.en: to set free（as from slavery）

ex.en: In a way, modern labor-saving appliances enfranchised people, giving them much more leisure time.
ex.cn: 从某种意义上来说，那些节省劳动力的现代设备将人们解放出来，给予人们更多的休闲时间。

m: EN- + FRANCHISE (right) — give the right (especially to vote). — enfranchise, to grant rights.

k.syn: beget, cause, catalyze, generate, induce, invoke, produce, spawn
k.en: to cause to exist or to develop

ex.en: Her latest book has engendered a lot of controversy.
ex.cn: 她的新书引发了很多争议。

k.syn: actualize, appear, arise, form, materialize, spring
k.ant: cease 停止；disappear, perish 消逝，死亡；eradicate根除；quash 平息；terminate 终止
k.en: to come into existence

ex.en: Feelings of confidence and independence were only just beginning to engender within her.
ex.cn: 她的自信和独立的感觉才刚刚产生

m: EN- + GENDER (kind, generate) — give rise to. — engender, to cause / produce.

k.syn: absorb, engage, enthrall, fascinate, grip, immerse
k.en: to occupy exclusively

ex.en: He was completely engrossed in his work.
ex.cn: 他全神贯注于他的工作之中。

m: EN- + GROSS (whole) — absorb wholly. — engross, to absorb fully.

k.syn: mystery, conundrum, puzzle, riddle
k.cn: 难以理解或解释的事物，谜
k.en: something hard to understand or explain

ex.en: The smile on Da Vinci's masterpiece Mona Lisa has been an enigma for hundred years.
ex.cn: 数百年来，达·芬奇名作《蒙娜丽莎》上的微笑一直是个不解之谜。

m: ENIGMA contains ENI + GMA — "any-G-M-A"? — a mystery; the "Enigma machine" was the WWII coding mystery. — enigma, a mystery.

k.syn: apprise, instruct
k.ant: bewilder, confuse, confound, perplex 使疑惑
k.en: to give information to; inform or instruct

ex.en: enlighten us about the thorny problem
ex.cn: 启发我们思考这个棘手的问题

k.syn: edify, educate, illuminate, nurture
k.der: enlightening adj. 具有启发性的
k.en: to provide（someone） with moral or spiritual understanding

ex.en: Many people around the world have been enlightened by the teachings of Gautama Buddha.
ex.cn: 释迦摩尼的说教启迪了世界上许许多多的人的灵魂。

m: EN- + LIGHTEN — bring light/insight. — enlighten, to illuminate / educate.

k.syn: animosity, animus, antagonism, antipathy, feud, gall, hostility, rancor
k.ant: amity, comity 友好；concord 和睦
k.en: positive, active, and typically mutual hatred or ill will

ex.en: an unspoken enmity between two factions
ex.cn: 两个派系间心照不宣的仇恨

m: from ENEMY — hatred. — enmity, hostility.

k.syn: aggrandize, canonize, deify, dignify, glorify, magnify
k.ant: debase, abase, degrade, demean, humble, humiliate 贬低
k.en: to make noble

ex.en: Her skill and talent ennoble her profession.
ex.cn: 技能和天赋使她成为业界领军人物。

m: EN- + NOBLE — make noble / dignified. — ennoble, to make noble.

k.syn: doldrums, listlessness, tedium, weariness
k.ant: keen interest, energy, enthusiasm, exuberance 强烈的兴趣，生气盎然
k.en: listlessness and dissatisfaction resulting from lack of interest

ex.en: The kind of ennui comes from having too much time on one's hands and too little will to find something productive to do.
ex.cn: 倦怠来自手上有大把时间却没有有益的事可做。

m: ENNUI rhymes with LOO-EE — bored even of saying "oui" to anything. (French for boredom.) — ennui, weary boredom.

k.syn: install, lodge, nestle, perch, roost, settle
k.ant: unsettle, dislodge, displace 使从安置好的地方移开，驱逐
k.en: to settle（oneself）securely or comfortably

ex.en: ensconce oneself in a new job
ex.cn: 在一个新职位安定下来

k.syn: bury, cache, conceal, secrete
k.ant: display, exhibit 展示
k.en: to put in a hiding place

ex.en: ensconce the spare house key in a place where no thief would think to look
ex.cn: 把备用钥匙藏在小偷根本不会注意的地方

m: ENSCONCE contains SCONCE (a wall bracket) — settled into a SCONCE-like nook. — ensconce, to settle securely.

k.ant: solo 独唱
k.en: a work for two or more vocalists or instrumentalists

ex.en: The actor performed an ensemble piece.
ex.cn: 该演员参与了合唱。

m: ENSEMBLE contains SEMBLE ≈ "resemble" — performers RESEMBLE each other in style. (French "together.") — ensemble, a group performing together.

k.syn: follow You have to believe in yourself. That's the secret of success. 人必须相信自己，这是成功的秘诀。 ——美国演员　卓别林（Charles Chaplin, American actor） “GRE，我愿意将它解释成Glorious and Rewarding Experience。或许正如真爱是用来怀念的，青春就是用来追忆的——希望以及为了希望所付出的醉生梦死般的努力，才是我们青春存在的意义。” （王彦妮，2008年6月，Verbal 710，Quantitative 800，AW 5.0，现任职于北京新东方学校北美项目部）
k.en: take place afterward or as a result

ex.en: A brief but embarrassing silence ensued.
ex.cn: 一阵短暂且令人尴尬的沉默随之而来。

m: ENSUE rhymes with PURSUE — but ENSUE = follows after; PURSUE = chases. — ensue, to follow.

k.syn: interlace, intertwist, knot, snarl, tangle
k.en: to twist together into a usually confused mass

ex.en: We managed to entangle the string of lights into a hopeless mess of wires.
ex.cn: 我们将小灯泡的线纠缠成了一堆。

k.syn: complexify, perplex, sophisticate
k.ant: simplify, streamline 简化
k.en: to make complex or difficult

ex.en: The history of Alexander the Great is entangled by variant accounts of his exploits.
ex.cn: 亚历山大大帝的生平历史被不同版本的记录搞得很纠结。

m: EN- + TANGLE — get caught in a tangle. — entangle, to ensnare.

k.syn: ardor, avidity, desirousness, impatience, keenness, lust, thirst
k.ant: halfhearted 心不在焉的
k.en: urgent desire or interest

ex.en: In my enthusiasm to get going, I forgot to pack any foul-weather clothing.
ex.cn: 在强烈的出游热情下，我忘记带上防备坏天气的衣服了。

m: ENTHUSIASM contains ENTH ≈ "in-the-USE" — a Greek "god within" possessing you with eagerness. (Etymological: en-theos.) — enthusiasm, eager interest.

k.syn: allure, bait, beguile, decoy, seduce, solicit, tempt, lead on
k.ant: formidable 可怕的
k.en: to attract artfully or adroitly or by arousing hope or desire: tempt; lure

ex.en: entice sb into doing sth
ex.cn: 诱使某人做某事

m: EN- + TICE (lure) — lure in. — entice, to tempt.

k.syn: access, admission, admittance, gateway, ingress, passport, ticket
k.en: the means or right of entering or participating in

ex.en: Entrance to the club is by invitation only.
ex.cn: 该俱乐部只接受受邀入会。

k.syn: enrapture, enthrall, ravish, transport, carry away
k.ant: unprepossessing 不讨人喜欢的
k.en: to fill with delight, wonder, or enchantment

ex.en: be entranced by the view
ex.cn: 陶醉于景色

m: to ENTRANCE = to put into a trance with delight (different from ENTRANCE = doorway). — entrance, to enchant.

k.syn: ensnare, ensnarl, entoil, mesh, net, snare, catch up
k.ant: disentangle, untangle 使解脱
k.en: to lure into a compromising statement or act

ex.en: A string of inconsistent statements finally entrapped the witness.
ex.cn: 一系列不一致的陈述最终蒙骗了目击者。

m: EN- + TRAP — lure into a trap. — entrap, to ensnare.

k.syn: beseech, besiege, conjure, implore, importune, solicit, supplicate, plead to, appeal to
k.en: to plead with especially in order to persuade, ask urgently

ex.en: He entreated his boss for another chance.
ex.cn: 他恳求老板再给他一次机会。

m: EN- + TREAT (ask, French "traiter") — earnestly ask. — entreat, to plead.

k.syn: articulate
k.en: to utter articulate sounds

ex.en: Enunciate your words, and then you won't have to repeat them so often.
ex.cn: 把话说清楚，你就不用经常重复了。

k.syn: annunciate, broadcast, declare, herald, proclaim, promulgate, publicize, release, give out
k.en: to make known openly or publicly

ex.en: Today the President enunciated a new foreign policy.
ex.cn: 总统在今天宣布了一项新的对外政策。

m: from ANNOUNCE — pronounce clearly / state publicly. — enunciate, to pronounce / declare.

k.en: something that ornaments or protects the shoulder; a shoulder ornament on a uniform

m: EPAULET contains EPAUL ≈ "shoulder pad" / "épaule" (French shoulder) — shoulder ornament. — epaulet, a shoulder ornament.

k.syn: evanescent, fleeting, impermanent, temporary, transient, transitory, fugacious, fugitive
k.ant: eternal, permanent, perpetual, enduring, everlasting, ceaseless, immortal, undying 永久的
k.en: lasting a very short time

ex.en: ephemeral pressures
ex.cn: 暂时的压力

m: EPHEMERAL contains EPI + HEMERA (day, Greek) — lasting only a day, like a mayfly. — ephemeral, short-lived.

k.syn: august, grandiose, imposing, magnificent, majestic, monumental
k.ant: humble, unheroic, unimpressive, modest 适度的，印象不深的
k.en: surpassing the usual or ordinary, particularly in scope or size

ex.en: His genius was epic.
ex.cn: 他聪明过人。

m: EPIC rhymes with RELIC — a grand RELIC of heroic tales (Iliad, Odyssey). — epic, grand / heroic.

k.syn: bon vivant, gastronomist, gourmand, gourmet
k.en: one with sensitive and discriminating tastes especially in food or wine

ex.en: Thomas Jefferson was one of America's first great epicures.
ex.cn: 托马斯· 杰斐逊算得上是美国早期美食家一枚。

m: EPICURE rhymes with MANICURE — but for food: refined attention to the fine dish. (From philosopher Epicurus.) — epicure, a gourmet.

k.en: the outer, protective, nonvascular layer of the skin of vertebrates, covering the dermis

m: EPI- (upon) + DERMIS (skin) — the outer skin layer. — epidermis, the outer skin.

k.syn: adage, aphorism, apothegm, byword, maxim, proverb
k.en: a short, witty poem expressing a single thought or observation

ex.en: Benjamin Franklin's most famous epigram,“Remember that time is money.”
ex.cn: 本杰明·富兰克林最著名的警句就是，时间就是金钱。

m: EPIGRAM contains GRAM (small written thing, as in TELEGRAM) — a small witty saying. — epigram, a witty saying.

k.ant: preface 序文
k.en: a concluding section that rounds out the design of a literary work

k.syn: coda
k.en: the final scene of a play that comments on or summarizes the main action

m: EPI- (after) + LOGUE (speech) — the closing remarks at the end of a story. — epilogue, a concluding section.

k.en: one of a series

m: EPISODE contains EPI (upon) + SODE (path) — one step on the unfolding path. — episode, an installment / event.

k.en: the branch of philosophy that studies the nature of knowledge

m: EPISTEME (knowledge) + LOGY (study) — the study of knowledge itself. — epistemology, the theory of knowledge.

k.en: an inscription on a tombstone

m: EPITAPH contains EPI (upon) + TAPH (tomb, Greek TAPHOS) — words upon the tomb. — epitaph, a tombstone inscription.

k.syn: alias, cognomen, sobriquet
k.en: a descriptive or familiar name given instead of or in addition to the one belonging to an individual; a disparaging or abusive word or phrase

ex.en: King Richard I of England was given the very laudatory epithet“the Lion-Hearted”.
ex.cn: 英王理查一世因英勇大胆被赞誉为“狮心理查”。

m: EPITHET contains EPI + THET (placed) — a name placed upon someone ("Vlad the Impaler"). — epithet, a descriptive nickname.

k.syn: abstract, digest, encapsulate, outline, recapitulate, synopsize, sum up
k.en: to make into a short statement of the main points（as of a report）

ex.en: His personal code of behavior on the playing field is epitomized by his favorite saying, Nice guys finish last.
ex.cn: 他在赛场上的座右铭是：好男坚持到底。

k.syn: body, express, externalize, incarnate, incorporate, instantiate, manifest, materialize, substantiate
k.cn: 代表，体现，是…的典型范例
k.en: to represent in visible form; to be a typical example of

ex.en: The Parthenon in Athens epitomizes the ancient Greek ideal of architectural beauty.
ex.cn: 雅典的巴特农神庙体现了古希腊对于建筑美感的理念。

ex.en: behavior that epitomizes selfishness
ex.cn: 典型的自私行为

m: EPITOMIZE contains EPITOME — to BE the epitome (perfect example). — epitomize, to be the perfect example.

k.syn: balmy, genial, gentle, moderate, temperate
k.ant: agitation, excitability 激动
k.en: not easily disturbed; serene

ex.en: equable temperament
ex.cn: 温和的性情

m: from EQUAL — even-tempered, steady. — equable, even-tempered.

k.syn: disinterestedness, evenhandedness, fairness, impartiality, neutrality, nonpartisanship
k.ant: biased, discriminatory 偏袒的，差别对待的
k.en: lack of favoritism toward one side or another

ex.en: The lower wages paid to women for equal work violated the notion of equity.
ex.cn: 同等工作付给女性更低的报酬违背了公平的理念。

m: from EQUAL — fairness. — equity, fairness.

k.syn: coequal, coordinate, counterpart, peer, rival, parallel
k.en: equal in force, amount, or value

ex.en: That huge mansion is the equivalent of five ordinary houses.
ex.cn: 那栋巨大的公馆有五套一般户型的房子那么大。

m: EQUI- (equal) + VALENT (value) — of equal value. — equivalent, equal in value.

k.syn: fudge, hedge, weasel, prevaricate, palter
k.ant: clarity 清楚
k.der: equivocal adj. 模棱两可的：open to two or more interpretations and often intended to mislead; ambiguous; equivocation n. 模棱两可的话
k.cn: （带有欺骗目的地）模棱两可地说，说谎话
k.en: to use equivocal language especially with intent to deceive

ex.en: When asked about his tax plan, the candidate didn't equivocate.
ex.cn: 当被问到税收方案时，候选人直言不讳。

m: EQUI- (equal) + VOCATE (voice) — speak both ways equally, be deliberately vague. — equivocate, to speak ambiguously to deceive.

k.syn: corrode, fret, gnaw, nibble, bite at
k.en: to consume or wear away gradually

ex.en: Flooding eroded the hillside.
ex.cn: 洪水冲刷着山坡。

ex.en: inflation eroding buying power
ex.cn: 通货膨胀导致购买力下降

m: ERODE rhymes with CORRODE — both wear away over time. — erode, to wear away.

k.syn: ambulant, fugitive, nomadic, perambulatory, roaming, roving, vagabond, vagrant, wandering
k.en: traveling from place to place

ex.en: the errant gunslinger as a standard character in western novels
ex.cn: 在西部题材小说中，居无定所的持枪歹徒是经典形象

k.syn: misbehaving, mischievous
k.ant: behaved, behaving, nice, orderly 行为规矩的
k.en: straying from the proper course or standards

ex.en: errant youngsters
ex.cn: 误入歧途的青少年

m: ERRANT rhymes with ARRANT but means wandering — Latin ERR (wander, as in ERROR/ERRATIC). — errant, wandering / straying.

k.syn: changing, fluctuating, irregular, unequal, unstable, unsteady, varying
k.ant: changeless, constant, stable, steady, unchanging, unvarying 不变的
k.en: not staying constant

ex.en: Business at the fast-food restaurant has been so erratic lately that the manager never knows how much staff to have on hand.
ex.cn: 快餐店的生意最近非常起伏以至于经理也不清楚需要多少人手。

k.syn: bizarre, eccentric, offbeat, outlandish, out-of-the-way, peculiar, quaint, queer, remarkable, screwy, spaced-out, way-out, weird
k.en: different from the ordinary in a way that causes curiosity or suspicion

ex.en: The key to the code was the erratic punctuation the killer used.
ex.cn: 解开密码的关键是杀手使用的不同寻常的标点符号。

m: from ERR (to wander/err) — unpredictable, inconsistent. — erratic, inconsistent.

k.syn: knowledgeable, learned, lettered, literate, scholarly, well-read
k.ant: ignorant, unlettered, benighted, illiterate, uneducated, unscholarly 无知的
k.en: characterized by erudition; learned

ex.en: an erudite scholar
ex.cn: 渊博的学者

m: ERUDITE contains RUDE — but the OPPOSITE: E- (out of) + RUDE — taught out of rudeness, well-educated. — erudite, scholarly.

k.syn: aggrandize, amplify, augment, boost, expand, pump up, build up
k.ant: wane, diminish 减弱
k.en: to increase in extent, volume, number, amount, intensity, or scope

ex.en: We don't want to escalate the war.
ex.cn: 我们不想使战争扩大。

m: to ESCALATE = to climb up step by step (think escalator) — intensify. — escalate, to intensify.

k.syn: dodge, elude, evade, shirk, shun, weasel out of
k.ant: embrace, greet, welcome, habitually indulge in, seek 乐于接受，寻觅
k.en: to avoid habitually especially on moral or practical grounds

ex.en: The minister eschews involvement in local politics, since he doesn't want to diminish his moral authority in the community.
ex.cn: 首相回避参与当地政治，因为他不想降低其道德权威。

m: ESCHEW rhymes with CHEW — but ESCHEW = refuse to chew, avoid. — eschew, to deliberately avoid.

k.syn: abstruse, arcane, hermetic, recondite
k.ant: shallow, superficial 肤浅的
k.en: difficult for one of ordinary knowledge or intelligence to understand

ex.en: esoteric terminology
ex.cn: 深奥难懂的专业术语

k.syn: confidential, inside, intimate, nonpublic, privy, secret
k.ant: commonly accepted, generally known, open, public 公开的，普遍接受的
k.en: not known or meant to be known by the general populace

ex.en: The actor must have had some esoteric motive for leaving stage.
ex.cn: 该演员淡出舞台肯定有不为人知的原因。

m: ESOTERIC contains ESO (inner, Greek) — only the inner circle knows it. — esoteric, known only to a few.

k.syn: embrace, take on, take up
k.ant: abjure, repudiate 誓绝
k.en: to take up and support as a cause

ex.en: espouse the revolutionary cause
ex.cn: 支持革命事业

k.en: to take in marriage; marry

m: to ESPOUSE a cause = to take it as your spouse, commit to it. — espouse, to support / commit to.

k.syn: appreciation, estimation, favor, regard, respect
k.ant: disfavor, odium 不喜欢，憎恶
k.en: to regard with respect; prize

ex.en: be held in high esteem
ex.cn: 被高度敬仰

m: ESTEEM rhymes with EXTREME — to hold in EXTREME regard. — esteem, to respect.

k.syn: prestigious, reputable, applaudable, commendable, creditable, meritorious, praiseworthy
k.ant: contemptible, infamous, censurable, discreditable, illaudable, reprehensible 令人鄙视的
k.en: deserving of esteem; admirable

ex.en: an estimable adversary
ex.cn: 一位值得尊敬的对手

m: from ESTEEM — worthy of esteem. — estimable, deserving respect.

k.syn: alienate, disaffect, disgruntle, sour
k.ant: reconcile 使和好
k.cn: 使疏远，离间，使感情失和
k.en: to arouse especially mutual enmity or indifference in where there had formerly been love, affection, or friendliness

ex.en: He estranged several of his coworkers.
ex.cn: 他和好多同事疏远了。

m: ES- + STRANGE — make strangers of, alienate. — estrange, to alienate.

k.syn: grave, incise, inscribe
k.en: to produce（as a pattern or design）on a hard material by eating into the material's surface（as by acid or laser beam）

ex.en: The artist etched his landscape on a copper plate.
ex.cn: 艺术家将风景画刻在铜盘上。

k.syn: impress, imprint, infix, ingrain
k.en: to produce a vivid impression of

ex.en: In just a few pages the writer etched an unforgettable portrait of one of the more remarkable First Ladies.
ex.cn: 仅用数页，作者刻画出了一位杰出的第一夫人令人难忘的形象。

m: ETCH rhymes with SKETCH — both leave permanent marks. — etch, to engrave / impress deeply.

k.syn: ageless, everlasting, immortal, imperishable, perennial, perpetual, undying
k.ant: ephemeral 短暂的
k.en: having infinite duration; everlasting; perpetual

ex.en: eternal love
ex.cn: 永恒的爱

m: ETERNAL contains ETER ≈ "ever" — lasting EVER and ever. — eternal, never-ending.

k.syn: fluffy, gossamer, light
k.ant: heavy, leaden, ponderous 沉重的
k.en: resembling air in lightness, highly refined; delicate

ex.en: The bakery's scrumptious pastries have a wonderfully ethereal consistency.
ex.cn: 面包师可口的糕点有着一种奇妙的蓬松轻巧的质感。

k.syn: elysian, empyreal, heavenly, supernal
k.ant: chthonic, hellish, infernal, Tartarean 地狱的
k.en: of, relating to, or suggesting heaven

ex.en: a land of ethereal beauty and tranquility
ex.cn: 具有缥缈的美感和宁静的一片土地

k.syn: bodiless, formless, incorporeal, insubstantial, nonmaterial, nonphysical, spiritual
k.ant: bodily, corporeal, material, physical, substantial 物质的，有实体的
k.en: not of this world; spiritual; not composed of matter

ex.en: that ethereal attribute that every performer should have—charisma
ex.cn: 每位演奏者都应该具有一种精神上的特质——个人魅力

m: from ETHER — light, airy, otherworldly. — ethereal, delicate / heavenly.

k.syn: ethos, morality, morals, norms, principles, standards
k.en: rules or standards governing the conduct of a person or the members of a profession

ex.en: an old-fashioned work ethics
ex.cn: 传统的工作行为规范

m: ETHICS rhymes with TACTICS — but moral tactics. (Greek ETHOS = character.) — ethics, moral standards.

k.ant: defame, pan 诬蔑，指责
k.en: to speak or write in high praise of

ex.en: He was eulogized at his funeral as a caring husband and a good father.
ex.cn: 他在悼词中被称赞为一个好丈夫、一个好爸爸。

m: to EULOGIZE = to deliver a EULOGY (high praise, especially for the deceased). — eulogize, to praise highly.

k.en: the substitution of an agreeable or inoffensive expression for one that may offend or suggest something unpleasant

ex.en: using“eliminate”as a euphemism for“kill”“
ex.cn: 杀死”的委婉说法是“灭掉”

m: EU- (good) + PHEM (speak) — speaking nicely instead of bluntly ("passed away" for died). — euphemism, a softened expression.

k.syn: mellifluous, melodious, canorous, harmonizing, symphonious, tuneful
k.ant: cacophonous, discordant, disharmonious, dissonant, tuneless, unmelodious 刺耳的
k.en: pleasing or agreeable to the ear

m: EU- (good) + PHON (sound) — pleasant-sounding. — euphonious, pleasant to the ear.

k.syn: elation, exhilaration, intoxication, transport, rapture, rhapsody
k.ant: depression 沮丧
k.en: a state of overwhelming usually pleasurable emotion

ex.en: The initial euphoria following her victory in the election has now subsided.
ex.cn: 选举成功最初带给她的欢愉感现在已经消失。

m: EU- (good) + PHORIA (feeling) — overwhelmingly good feeling. — euphoria, intense joy.

k.syn: clear, vacate, void
k.ant: fill up, occupy, load 填满
k.en: to empty or remove the contents of

ex.en: They were ordered to evacuate the building.
ex.cn: 他们被命令搬出大楼。

k.ant: conquer 占领
k.en: to remove especially from a military zone or dangerous area

ex.en: During World War Ⅱ, children were evacuated from London to the country.
ex.cn: 二战期间，伦敦市的儿童被撤离到郊区。

m: E- (out) + VACU (empty) — empty out / leave a place. — evacuate, to clear out.

k.syn: ephemeral, fleeting, impermanent, temporary, transient, transitory
k.ant: ceaseless, endless, enduring, eternal, everlasting, immortal, permanent, perpetual, timeless, undying, unending, abiding, lasting 持久的
k.en: tending to vanish like vapor

ex.en: Beauty is as evanescent as a rainbow.
ex.cn: 容颜易老，年华易逝。

m: rhymes with FLUORESCENT — but EVANESCENT light vanishes quickly. — evanescent, fleeting.

k.syn: fugitive, slippery
k.ant: unequivocal 明确的
k.cn: 难以发现、捕捉、分离的；含糊其辞的
k.en: hard to find, capture, or isolate; equivocal

ex.en: an evasive statement
ex.cn: 模棱两可的陈述

m: from EVADE — slippery, dodging. — evasive, dodging / non-committal.

k.syn: ageless, enduring, eternal, immortal, imperishable, perennial, perpetual, undying
k.ant: impermanent, mortal, temporary, transient, ephemeral 短暂的
k.en: lasting forever; eternal

ex.en: To his everlasting credit, he never once gave in to temptation.
ex.cn: 他人品非常好，从来没有屈服于诱惑。

m: EVER + LASTING — lasting forever. — everlasting, eternal.

k.ant: harbor 收容
k.en: to put out（a tenant, for example）by legal process; expel

ex.en: Her landlord has threatened to evict her if she doesn't pay the rent soon.
ex.cn: 她的房东威胁说如果再不交房租就要把她赶出去。

m: EVICT contains E (out) + VICT ≈ "VICTim" — make the tenant a VICT(im) by throwing them OUT. (Or: shares root with CONVICT but reversed: convict = imprisoned, evict = thrown out.) — evict, to expel by legal process.

k.syn: bespeak, betray, declare, demonstrate, expose, manifest, reveal, give away
k.ant: conceal, keep hidden 隐藏
k.en: to make known（something abstract）through outward signs

ex.en: evince a strong desire
ex.cn: 明显地表现出强烈的欲望

m: EVINCE rhymes with CONVINCE — both involve showing evidence. — evince, to display clearly.

k.syn: elicit, inspire, raise
k.en: to call forth or up

ex.en: evoke memories
ex.cn: 唤起回忆

m: E- (out) + VOKE (call) — call forth (memories, emotions). — evoke, to call forth.

k.en: a female sheep

m: EWE rhymes with YOU — a female sheep that goes "yoo". — ewe, a female sheep.

k.syn: aggravate, complicate, worsen
k.ant: allay, alleviate, assuage, mitigate, relieve, palliate 缓和
k.en: to make more violent, bitter, or severe

ex.en: a heavy rainfall that exacerbated the flood problems
ex.cn: 大雨恶化了洪水问题

m: EX- + ACERBATE (sharp, as in "acerbic") — sharpen, make worse. — exacerbate, to make worse.

k.syn: choosy, demanding, fastidious, finical, fussy, pernickety, persnickety, picky
k.der: exact vt. 强求，索取：to force the payment or yielding of
k.ant: condone, forgive 赦免，免除（债务）
k.en: making severe demands; rigorous

ex.en: an exacting instructor
ex.cn: 一位严苛的导师

ex.en: exact tribute from a conquered people
ex.cn: 从征服的人民手中强取贡品

k.syn: arduous, burdensome, challenging, grueling, killing, laborious, onerous, taxing, toilsome
k.ant: light, unchallenging, undemanding 要求不高的
k.en: requiring much time, effort, or careful attention

ex.en: Editing and proofreading will always be an exacting task.
ex.cn: 编辑和校对是花时间花心思的工作。

m: from EXACT — demanding exactness. — exacting, demanding.

k.syn: aggrandize, canonize, deify, dignify, elevate, ennoble, magnify
k.ant: abase, degrade, demean, humble, lower in status 贬抑，降低身份
k.en: to raise in rank, character, or status; elevate

ex.en: Popular support and media hype have exalted Super Bowl Sunday to the level of a national holiday.
ex.cn: 大众广泛的支持和媒体的大肆宣传已然将“超级碗美式足球冠军赛”变成了一个全民狂欢的大节日。

k.syn: emblazon, extol, glorify, laud, magnify
k.ant: condemn 谴责
k.en: to glorify, praise, or honor

m: EX- (up) + ALT (high) — raise up high, praise. — exalt, to elevate / praise highly.

k.syn: aggravate, gall, nettle, peeve, rile, ruffle, vex, burn up
k.ant: mitigate, mollify 平息
k.en: to excite the anger of

ex.en: I was exasperated by the flight delays.
ex.cn: 我被飞机延误激怒了。

m: EX- + ASPERATE (rough, as in "asperity") — make rough/irritated. — exasperate, to irritate intensely.

k.ant: fill in 填满
k.en: to dig out and remove

ex.en: excavate soil from one area
ex.cn: 从某处挖土出来

m: EX- (out) + CAV (cave, hollow) — dig out. — excavate, to dig out.

k.syn: aberrant, abnormal, anomalous, exceeding, extraordinary, peculiar, unwonted
k.ant: commonplace, prosaic, customary, normal, ordinary, typical, unextraordinary, usual 平凡常见的

ex.cn: 外的，特别的，非凡的：being an exception; uncommon; extraordinary

ex.en: exceptional bravery
ex.cn: 非凡的勇敢

m: from EXCEPTION — unusual / outstanding. — exceptional, unusual / outstanding.

k.en: a passage taken from a longer work, such as a literary or musical composition

m: EX- (out) + CERPT (pluck) — plucked out passage from a longer text. — excerpt, an extracted passage.

k.syn: abuse, assail, belabor, castigate, lambaste, vituperate
k.ant: accolade, extol, flatter, praise lavishly 赞扬
k.en: to criticize harshly and usually publicly

ex.en: She was excoriated as a racist.
ex.cn: 她被指责是一个种族歧视者。

m: EX- + CORIATE (hide, skin) — strip the skin off, savagely criticize. — excoriate, to severely criticize.

k.ant: absorb, ingest 吸收
k.en: to separate and discharge（waste matter）from the blood, tissues, or organs

ex.en: excrete sweat
ex.cn: 排汗

m: EXCRETE contains EX (out) + CRETE — waste "created out" of the body. — excrete, to discharge waste.

k.syn: agonize, anguish, plague, rack, torment, torture, harrow
k.ant: exult 使欢跃
k.en: to inflict severe pain on; torture

ex.en: She has long been excruciated by a persistent pain in her back.
ex.cn: 她长久以来被背痛折磨。

m: from CRUX (cross) — to torture (originally: crucify). — excruciate, to torment.

k.syn: absolve, acquit, clear, exonerate, vindicate
k.ant: attribute guilt, inculpate, indict, criminate, incriminate 归罪，控告
k.en: to clear from alleged fault or guilt

ex.en: I have gathered evidence that will exculpate my client.
ex.cn: 我已经搜集到能够证明委托人无罪的证据了。

m: EX- (out) + CULPA (fault) — out of fault, declared innocent. — exculpate, to clear from blame.

k.syn: desultory, digressive, meandering, rambling, wandering
k.en: passing from one topic to another

ex.en: an excursive story line that some readers of Melville's novel find very rewarding
ex.cn: 梅尔维尔小说的一些读者非常喜欢松散的故事主线

m: from EXCURSION — wandering off-topic. — excursive, digressive.

k.syn: anathematize, censure, decry, denounce, reprehend, reprobate
k.ant: bless 保佑
k.en: to declare to be morally wrong or evil

ex.en: Leaders from all over the world execrated the terrorists responsible for the bomb blast.
ex.cn: 世界各国首脑强烈谴责对爆炸袭击负责的恐怖分子。

k.syn: abhor, abominate, despise, detest, loathe
k.ant: love 爱
k.en: to dislike strongly

ex.en: execrate anyone who would physically abuse children or animals
ex.cn: 痛恨那些虐待儿童或动物的人

m: EXECRATE rhymes with DESECRATE — both involve cursing what's sacred. — execrate, to denounce / curse.

k.syn: archetypal, imitable, paradigmatic, quintessential
k.der: exemplify vt.（通过榜样）示范，显示：to show or illustrate by
k.en: constituting, serving as, or worthy of being a pattern to be imitated

ex.en: As a hospital volunteer he has given exemplary service to his community.
ex.cn: 他作为医疗志愿者为社区起到了模范的作用。

m: from EXAMPLE — serving as a model. — exemplary, model / outstanding.

k.en: to release or deliver from some liability or requirement to which others are subject

ex.en: a man exempted from military service
ex.cn: 免服军役的人

m: EX- (out) + EMPT (taken) — taken out (of obligation). — exempt, free from obligation.

k.syn: consume, devour, drain, expend, spend, use up
k.der: exhaustive adj. 彻底的，完整的，详尽的：testing all possibilities; thorough; complete
k.ant: incomplete, partial 不完全的
k.en: to consume entirely: to make complete use of

ex.en: We exhausted our funds in a week.
ex.cn: 我们在一周内就耗尽了经费。

ex.en: conduct an exhaustive investigation
ex.cn: 做详尽的调查

k.syn: fatigue, frazzle, harass, wear out, weary, knock out
k.en: to wear out completely

ex.en: exhausted by overwork
ex.cn: 加班使得筋疲力尽

m: EX- + HAUST (drain) — drain completely. — exhaust, to drain / use up.

k.syn: electrify, galvanize, intoxicate, pump up, turn on
k.ant: soporific 昏昏欲睡的
k.en: to make cheerful and excited

ex.en: be exhilarated by her success
ex.cn: 为她的成功感到兴奋

m: EX- + HILAR (cheerful, as in "hilarious") — make highly cheerful. — exhilarate, to make joyful.

k.syn: encourage, goad, nudge, prod, prompt, egg on
k.en: to urge by strong, often stirring argument, admonition, advice, or appeal

ex.en: The speaker exhorted the graduating students to go forth and try to make a difference in the world.
ex.cn: 演讲者鼓励毕业生们向广阔天地前进，有所作为。

m: EXHORT rhymes with ESCORT — but with urgency: forcefully escort someone toward action. — exhort, to urgently encourage.

k.syn: compelling, dire, emergent, imperative, importunate, necessitous, pressing, urgent
k.ant: deferrable, noncritical, nonurgent 可拖延的，不紧急的
k.en: requiring immediate aid or action

ex.en: exigent circumstances
ex.cn: 紧急情况

m: EXIGENT contains EXIG ≈ "exit-urgent" — urgent enough to need an EXIT now. — exigent, urgent.

k.syn: gush, outpour, outpouring
k.ant: influx, flux, inflow, inrush 涌入
k.en: a mass departure

ex.en: the mass exodus from the cities for the beaches on most summer weekends
ex.cn: 在多数夏季的周末，大批都市人奔向海滩

m: EXODUS — mass departure (the biblical Exodus from Egypt). — exodus, mass departure.

k.syn: absolve, acquit, clear, vindicate
k.ant: censure, incriminate, inculpate, prove guilty 责难，证明有罪
k.en: to free from blame

ex.en: An investigation exonerated the school from any blame.
ex.cn: 一项调查使该学校免受责备。

m: EX- (off) + ONER (burden, as in "onerous") — take the burden off, clear of blame. — exonerate, to clear from blame.

k.syn: extravagant, intolerable, lavish, overdue, overweening, unconscionable
k.ant: middling, moderate, modest, reasonable, temperate 适度的，合适的
k.en: exceeding the customary or appropriate limits in intensity, quality, amount, or size

ex.en: exorbitant prices
ex.cn: 过高的价格

m: EX- (out of) + ORBIT — outside the orbit, way beyond reasonable. — exorbitant, excessive.

k.syn: outlandish, strange
k.ant: indigenous, familiar, nonglamorous, plain-Jane, unexotic 本地的；平常的
k.en: excitingly or mysteriously unusual

ex.en: She's famous for her exotic tastes.
ex.cn: 她以有异域风情著称。

m: from EXO (outside) — from outside / unusual. — exotic, foreign / unusual.

k.syn: broad, extended, far-reaching, rangy
k.ant: limited, narrow 有限的
k.en: having a great expanse or extent

ex.en: expansive beach
ex.cn: 广阔的沙滩

k.syn: communicative, eloquent, garrulous, glib, loquacious, talkative, vocative, voluble
k.ant: laconic, reserved, reticent, taciturn 话少的，沉默寡言的
k.en: characterized by high spirits, generosity, or readiness to talk

ex.en: The first Moon explorers were rarely expansive in describing their surroundings; on the contrary, their reports tended to be laconic.
ex.cn: 初期的探月者们很少对所处的周遭环境进行大量描述；相反，他们的报告显得格外简洁。

m: from EXPAND — wide-ranging; or in mood, openly talkative. — expansive, wide-ranging / openly communicative.

k.ant: retard 减速，阻碍
k.en: to speed up the progress of; accelerate

ex.en: expedite your plans
ex.cn: 加快你的计划

m: EXPEDITE contains EXPED + ITE — Latin PED (foot) — to speed up by freeing the feet. — expedite, to speed up.

k.syn: haste, celerity, dispatch
k.ant: foot-dragging 拖拉
k.en: speed in performance; promptness

ex.en: deal with the order with the greatest possible expedition
ex.cn: 以可能的最快的速度处理订单

m: from EXPEDITE — quick efficient action; or a journey. — expedition, speed / a journey.

k.syn: mend, redeem, atone for
k.en: to extinguish the guilt incurred by

ex.en: expiate one's sin
ex.cn: 赎罪

m: EXPIATE contains EX + PIATE ≈ "pious" — make yourself pious again, atone for sin. — expiate, to atone.

k.syn: conclude, elapse, terminate, decease, leave off, let up
k.ant: come to life 出生
k.en: to breathe one's last breath; die

ex.en: The patient expired early this morning.
ex.cn: 病人今早逝世了。

k.syn: discontinue, elapse, end, finish, terminate, wind up, wink out
k.ant: continue, persist, hang on 持续，续期
k.en: to come to an end

ex.en: My membership in the club has expired.
ex.cn: 我的会员资格到期了。

m: EX- (out) + PIRE (breathe) — breathe out (last breath); also: to lapse. — expire, to die / lapse.

k.syn: clear-cut, definite, specific, unambiguous, unequivocal, univocal
k.ant: obscure, implicit, implied, inferred, ambiguous, circuitous, equivocal, indefinite, unspecific, vague 模糊的
k.en: fully revealed or expressed without vagueness, implication, or ambiguity

ex.en: explicit instructions
ex.cn: 表达清晰的指示说明

k.ant: inchoate 未完成的，未形成的
k.en: fully developed or formulated

ex.en: an explicit plan
ex.cn: 成熟的计划

m: EX- (out) + PLICIT (folded) — fully unfolded, clearly stated. — explicit, clearly stated.

k.en: a notable or heroic act

ex.en: his wartime exploits
ex.cn: 他在战争期间的英勇行为

k.syn: abuse, leverage, play on, capitalize on, impose on
k.en: to employ to the greatest possible advantage

ex.en: exploit your opponent's weakness
ex.cn: 利用你对手的弱点

m: EXPLOIT rhymes with PLOY/POINT — use a plan to your advantage. (Also: an EXPLOIT = a heroic feat.) — exploit, to make use of / a heroic act.

k.syn: advocator, backer, booster, champion, espouser, friend, promoter, proponent
k.ant: adversary, antagonist, opponent 反对者
k.en: one that speaks for, represents, or advocates

ex.en: Exponents of space exploration earnestly called for more missions to the outer reaches of the solar system.
ex.cn: 太空探险的支持者强烈要求对太阳系的外边缘进行探索。

k.syn: expounder, guru, interpreter, practitioner, high priest
k.en: one who brings an art or science to full realization

ex.en: She has long reigned as the nation's leading exponent of modern dance.
ex.cn: 她长期以来被认为是该国现代舞的集大成者。

m: from EXPOUND — one who advocates / exemplifies. — exponent, an advocate / typical example.

k.syn: bowdlerize, obliterate, launder, red-pencil, clean up
k.en: to remove erroneous, vulgar, obscene, or otherwise objectionable material from（a book, for example）before publication

ex.en: an expurgated edition of the letters
ex.cn: 信件的删减版

m: EX- (out) + PURG (purge) — purge out the offensive parts. — expurgate, to remove offensive content.

k.syn: acute, dreadful, excruciating, explosive, ferocious, fierce, furious, intensive, keen, profound, terrible, vehement, vicious
k.ant: light, moderate, soft 适当的
k.en: extreme in degree, power, or effect

ex.en: suffer exquisite pain
ex.cn: 遭受强烈的痛感

k.syn: dainty, delicate, elegant, recherché, select
k.en: having qualities that appeal to a refined taste

ex.en: exquisite pen-and-ink drawings of city scenes
ex.cn: 精巧的城市钢笔画

m: EXQUISITE contains QUISITE ≈ "acquired" (rare/sought) — finely sought-after. — exquisite, beautifully fine.
