k.syn: current, immediate, ongoing, present-day, existent
k.ant: destroyed, extinct, lost, missing, dead, nonextant 丢失的，不存在的
k.en: still in existence; not destroyed, lost, or extinct

ex.en: the most charming writer extant
ex.cn: 目前活着的作家中最有魅力的一位

ex.en: extant manuscripts
ex.cn: 未毁坏的手稿

m: EXTANT — still in existence (opposite of extinct). — extant, currently existing.

k.ant: planned 有计划的
k.en: to do or perform（something）without prior preparation or practice

ex.en: A good talk show host has to be able to extemporize the interviews when things don't go as planned.
ex.cn: 一个好的脱口秀主持人要能够在计划之外即兴表现。

m: EX- + TEMPORE (time) — speak on the spur of the moment. — extemporize, to improvise.

k.syn: deodorize, excuse, explain away, gloss over, gloze over
k.en: to lessen or to try to lessen the seriousness or extent of by making partial excuses

ex.en: Try to extenuate their vandalism with the old refrain of“Boys will be boys.”
ex.cn: 企图用“江山易改本性难移”的俗语为他们的暴行开脱。

m: EX- + TENU (thin) — make the offense thinner, lessen its severity. — extenuate, to lessen the seriousness.

k.syn: bygone, bypast, defunct, expired, nonextant, vanished
k.ant: perpetuation 永存
k.en: no longer existing or living

ex.en: extinct species
ex.cn: 灭绝的物种

m: EX- (out) + TINCT (extinguish) — gone out, no longer exists. — extinct, no longer existing.

k.syn: blanket, douse, quench, put out, snuff out
k.ant: ignite, rekindle 点燃
k.en: to put out（a fire, for example）; quench

ex.en: The fire in the skillet was quickly extinguished by slamming the lid on.
ex.cn: 盖上锅盖后，平底锅的火苗很快熄灭了。

k.syn: annihilate, decimate, demolish, desolate, devastate, pulverize, ruin, shatter, smash, tear down, pull down, rub out
k.ant: build, construct, erect, raise, rear, set up, put up 建立
k.en: to bring to a complete end the physical soundness, existence, or usefulness of

ex.en: a fatal blunder that extinguished all hope that the team would actually win the play-offs
ex.cn: 一个致命的失误使得队伍赢得季后赛的希望完全破灭

m: EXTINGUISH contains TINGUISH ≈ "distinguish" but reversed — make INDISTINGUISHable, snuff out. — extinguish, to put out.

k.syn: carol, exalt, glorify, laud, magnify, resound
k.ant: censure, impugn, malign, deprecate, detract, rail, vilify, excoriate 批评
k.en: to praise highly; glorify

ex.en: extol the virtues of...
ex.cn: 赞美……的优点

m: EXTOL contains EX + TOL ≈ "toll" — ring out the bells in praise. — extol, to praise enthusiastically.

k.syn: wrest, wring
k.en: to obtain from a person by force, intimidation, or undue or illegal power

ex.en: The criminals extorted large sums of money from their victims.
ex.cn: 绑匪向受害者勒索大笔钱财。

m: EX- + TORT (twist) — twist out (money) by threat. — extort, to obtain by threat.

k.syn: pull, uproot, wrest, wring, yank, root out, tear out
k.ant: embed 嵌入
k.en: to pull or take out forcibly

ex.en: extract a wisdom tooth
ex.cn: 拔出一颗智齿

m: EX- (out) + TRACT (pull) — pull out. — extract, to pull out.

k.syn: bloodline, breeding, descent, genealogy, lineage, origin, family tree
k.en: origin; lineage; ancestry

ex.en: a family of French extraction
ex.cn: 有法国血统的家族

m: from EXTRACT — your extraction = where your lineage was "pulled out" from. — extraction, lineage / origin.

k.syn: impertinent, inapplicable, irrelative, accidental, adventitious, external
k.ant: applicable, apposite, apropos, germane, pertinent, relative, relevant 相关的
k.en: having no relevance

ex.en: an extraneous digression
ex.cn: 无关的跑题

k.syn: accidental, adventitious, alien, external, foreign
k.ant: essential, inherent, innate, intrinsic 本质的，核心的
k.en: not forming an essential or vital part

ex.en: The architect's streamlined modern style shuns any sort of extraneous ornamentation.
ex.cn: 建筑师流线型的现代设计避免了一切多余的装饰。

m: EXTRA- (outside) — outside the scope, irrelevant. — extraneous, irrelevant.

k.syn: profligate, spendthrift, squandering, thriftless, unthrifty, wasteful
k.ant: frugal, conserving, economical, penny-pinching, scrimping, skimping, thrifty 节省的
k.en: given to spending money freely or foolishly

ex.en: She has always been extravagant with her money.
ex.cn: 她总是挥金如土。

k.syn: baroque, exorbitant, immoderate, inordinate, lavish, overdue, overmuch, overweening, plethoric, unconscionable, unmerciful
k.ant: middling, moderate, modest, reasonable, temperate 适当的，适度的
k.en: going beyond a normal or acceptable limit in degree or amount

ex.en: The book doesn't quite merit the extravagant praise that it has received.
ex.cn: 这本书被盛赞过度。

m: EXTRA- + VAG (wander) — wandering beyond limits, excessive. — extravagant, excessive.

k.syn: disengage, disentangle, free, liberate, release, untangle
k.ant: enmesh, entangle, embroil 使卷入
k.en: to free or remove from an entanglement or difficulty

ex.en: extricate himself from financial difficulties
ex.cn: 使他摆脱财政困境

m: EX- (out) + TRIC (tangle, as in "intricate") — get out of a tangle. — extricate, to free from difficulty.

k.syn: ebullient, effervescent, frolic, buoyant, bouncy, vivacious
k.ant: austere 克制的；sullen 闷闷不乐的
k.cn: 非常高兴的，热情洋溢的
k.en: joyously unrestrained and enthusiastic

ex.en: Exuberant crowds rushed to greet the returning national champions.
ex.cn: 激动的人群争先恐后地向凯旋的全国冠军表示问候。

k.syn: ample, lavish, lush, luxuriant, opulent, plentiful, prodigal, profuse, riotous
k.ant: meager, scant, sparse 匮乏的
k.en: produced in extreme abundance

ex.en: an exuberant imagination
ex.cn: 丰富的想象力

m: EX- + UBER (rich, abundant) — overflowingly enthusiastic. — exuberant, overflowingly cheerful.

k.syn: bleed, ooze, seep
k.ant: absorb 吸收
k.en: to flow forth slowly through small openings

ex.en: a sticky resin exuded from the bark
ex.cn: 从树皮中分泌出来的黏稠的树脂

m: EX- (out) + UDE (sweat) — ooze out (sweat, charm, confidence). — exude, to ooze / radiate.

k.syn: delight, glory, jubilate, joy, rejoice, triumph
k.ant: lament, mourn 哀悼；bemoan 叹息，悲伤
k.en: to rejoice especially with feelings or display of triumph or self-satisfaction

ex.en: exult in a triumph
ex.cn: 沉浸在胜利的欢喜当中

m: EXULT contains EX (out) + ULT — leap OUT in joy (cousin of "jubilant"). — exult, to rejoice openly.

k.syn: apologue, parable
k.der: fabulous adj. 寓言般的，难以置信的
k.en: a story intended to teach a basic truth or moral about life

ex.en: This classic Christmas film is essentially a fable showing how every person's life has meaning.
ex.cn: 这部经典的基督教电影本质上来说是一个寓言，它告诉我们每个人的生命都是有意义的。

k.syn: legend, mythos, tale
k.en: a legendary story of supernatural happenings

ex.en: According to an ancient fable, the waters of the mountain spring are the tears of a woman weeping for her lost children.
ex.cn: 根据一个古老的传说，山中的这眼泉水是一名失去孩子的母亲所流下的眼泪。

k.syn: fabrication, falsehood, lie, mendacity, prevarication, story
k.ant: fact 事实；truth 真相
k.en: a statement known by its maker to be untrue and made in order to deceive

ex.en: The stories of lost cities of gold may have been fables deliberately concocted by Native Americans to dupe the Spanish.
ex.cn: 所谓的“失落的黄金之都”的故事也许只是美洲土著们杜撰出来忽悠西班牙人的。

m: FABLE rhymes with TABLE — at the dinner TABLE, grandparents tell FABLES. — fable, a moral tale.

k.syn: concoct, coin, devise, forge, fake, feign, invent
k.en: to make up for the purpose of deception

ex.en: be accused of fabricating evidence
ex.cn: 被指控有捏造证据的行为

k.syn: assemble, construct, erect, frame, fashion, manufacture, produce, rear, set up
k.ant: demount, disassemble, dismantle, dismember 拆解，拆毁
k.der: fabrication n. 组建；虚构的事物
k.en: to bring into being by combining, shaping, or transforming materials

ex.en: The house was essentially fabricated at the factory and then shipped to the site for assembly.
ex.cn: 这间房屋的核心主体在工厂内建好之后，它被运往目的地直接进行组装。

ex.en: All the key parts are fabricated from high quality titanium alloy.
ex.cn: 所有的关键零件都是由上好的钛合金打造的。

m: FABRICATE contains FABRIC — sew/construct (cloth or a story) from FABRIC. — fabricate, to construct / invent.

k.syn: humorous, jocose, jocular, waggish, witty
k.ant: earnest, sincere 真挚的，真诚的；lugubrious 哀怨的
k.en: joking or jesting often inappropriately

ex.en: Stop being facetious! This is the life-and-death moment.
ex.cn: 别开玩笑了！这可是生死攸关的时刻。

m: FACETIOUS contains FACE + IOUS — a joker keeps a straight FACE while teasing. — facetious, jokingly inappropriate.

k.syn: cursory, shallow, simplistic, superficial
k.ant: deep, profound 深刻的；comprehensive, exhaustive 全面的，详尽的
k.en: having or showing a lack of depth of understanding or character

ex.en: a facile slogan devised by politicians
ex.cn: 政客们肤浅的宣传口号

k.syn: cheap, easy, effortless, painless
k.ant: arduous, demanding, difficult, formidable, hard, laborious, toilsome, tough 费力的，困难的
k.en: easily accomplished or attained

ex.en: a facile victory
ex.cn: 一场轻松易得的胜利

ex.en: propose a facile solution to a complex problem
ex.cn: 给一个复杂的问题提议一个容易的解决方案

m: FACILE rhymes with FACTUAL but easier — too FAC(t)-ile, glib. (Latin FACILIS = easy.) — facile, superficially easy.

k.syn: ease, expedite, forward, further, help
k.ant: check, hamper, handicap, hinder, impede, obstruct, thwart, retard 阻碍，阻止；complicate 使复杂化
k.der: facility n. 设施；容易，方便
k.en: to make easy or easier

ex.en: The new airport will facilitate the development of tourism.
ex.cn: 新建机场将促进旅游业的发展。

m: from FACILE — make easy. — facilitate, to make easier.

k.syn: bloc, body, clique, coalition, sect, wing
k.der: factional adj. 派系的，派别的
k.en: a party or group（as within a government）that is often contentious or self-seeking

ex.en: affiliated with one faction
ex.cn: 附属于某个党派

ex.en: North-Ocean Faction
ex.cn: 北洋派

m: FACTION rhymes with ACTION — a small splinter group taking ACTION. — faction, a splinter group.

k.en: a statement or an argument based on a false or invalid

m: FALLACY contains FALL — your reasoning FELL into a hole. — fallacy, faulty reasoning.

k.syn: untilled, uncultivated
k.en: left untilled or unsown after plowing

ex.en: The field was lying fallow.
ex.cn: 那田地正在休耕中。

k.syn: dead, dormant, inert, inoperative, latent, unused, vacant
k.ant: functioning, occupied, operative, running, working 使用中的；active 活跃的
k.en: not being in a state of use, activity, or employment

ex.en: The coal mine has been lying fallow since the drop in prices made it unprofitable.
ex.cn: 自从煤价下跌造成亏损，这个煤矿就一直处于停工状态。

m: FALLOW rhymes with HOLLOW — both empty of activity; a fallow field is hollow of crops. — fallow, idle / unproductive.

k.syn: deception, fable, lie, mendacity, prevarication, untruth
k.ant: truth, verity 真相，实话
k.en: a statement known by its maker to be untrue and made in order to deceive

ex.en: Truth always rise above falsehood, as oil rise above water.
ex.cn: 油脂水面浮，真理胜谎言。

m: FALSE + HOOD — a lie. — falsehood, a lie.

k.syn: lurch, stagger, stumble, teeter, totter, wobble
k.en: to walk unsteadily

k.syn: balance, halt, hesitate, vacillate, waver
k.en: to be unsteady in purpose or action, as from loss of courage or confidence

ex.en: Mr. Garrison never once faltered in his demand that slavery be unconditionally abolished.
ex.cn: 盖瑞森先生从不对他废除奴隶的要求存有任何的犹豫。

m: FALTER rhymes with HALTER — a HALTER pulls a horse to a halt = falter. — falter, to hesitate.

k.syn: bigot, devotee, enthusiast, fiend, freak, maniac, partisan, zealot
k.ant: nonfan 非爱好者
k.en: a person marked or motivated by an extreme, unreasoning enthusiasm, as for a cause

ex.en: a soccer fanatic
ex.cn: 一个足球迷

k.syn: extreme, rabid, radical, revolutionary, ultra
k.ant: conservative 保守的；rational 理性的
k.der: fanatical adj. 狂热的；盲信的
k.en: marked by excessive enthusiasm and often intense uncritical devotion

ex.en: They are fanatic about sadomasochism.
ex.cn: 他们对SM十分狂热。

m: from FAN + ATIC — extreme fan, zealot. — fanatic, an extreme enthusiast.

k.syn: chimera, conceit, daydream, delusion, dream, hallucination, illusion, vision
k.ant: actuality, fact, reality, truth 事实，现实
k.en: imaginative fiction featuring especially strange settings and grotesque characters

ex.en: indulge in a fantasy
ex.cn: 沉迷于幻想

k.syn: dream, envisage, envision, fantasize, imagine, picture
k.der: fantastic adj. 梦幻般的，极好的
k.en: to form a mental picture of

ex.en: He regularly fantasies romantic encounters that he knows will never happen.
ex.cn: 他会定期意淫一些永远不可能发生的浪漫邂逅。

m: FANTASY contains FAN — a FAN's wild imagination. (Greek PHANT- = appear, illusion.) — fantasy, imaginary creation.

k.syn: burlesque, caricature, parody, sham, travesty
k.ant: serious play 正剧
k.en: a light dramatic composition marked by broadly satirical comedy and improbable plot

k.syn: joke, mockery, nonsense
k.en: ridiculous or empty show

ex.en: The enforcement of this law became a farce.
ex.cn: 这条法律的执行过程成了一场闹剧。

m: FARCE rhymes with PARSE — but a FARCE doesn't parse — it's absurd nonsense. — farce, an absurd mockery.

k.syn: allure, arrest, attract, bewitch, captivate, charm, enchant, enthrall, grip
k.ant: disgust, repel 使厌恶，使反感
k.en: to hold an intense interest or attraction for

ex.en: toys that fascinate infants
ex.cn: 吸引婴儿们的玩具

m: FASCINATE contains FASCI ≈ "fascinator" hat — captivating attention. (Latin FASCIN = bewitch.) — fascinate, to captivate.

k.syn: hunger strike
k.en: an act of abstaining from food

ex.en: fast to death
ex.cn: 绝食而死

k.syn: brisk, expeditious, fleeting, hasty, quick, rapid, speedy, swift
k.ant: slow 慢的
k.en: characterized by quick motion, operation, or effect

ex.en: The fast pace of construction resulted in our new house being done ahead of schedule.
ex.cn: 极快的施工使得我们的新房提前竣工。

k.syn: constant, dedicated, devoted, devout, loyal, pious
k.ant: disloyal, perfidious 不忠诚的；treacherous 背叛的
k.en: firm in one's allegiance to someone or something

ex.en: The two girls soon became fast and inseparable friends.
ex.cn: 这两个女孩子很快成为了忠于彼此的形影不离的好伙伴。

k.syn: firm, frozen, lodged, secure, stable, staunch, strong, stuck
k.ant: loose, shaky 松动的；insecure 不稳定的
k.en: firmly positioned in place and difficult to dislodge

ex.en: The rusty, old screws are so fast in the fitting that there's no hope of getting them out.
ex.cn: 这颗生锈的老铁钉已经钉得太死了，根本不可能将其取出。

m: FAST contains the letters of "AST" + F — to FAST = to fix yourself FAST (firmly) without food. (Same word as FAST = quick / FAST = stuck firm.) — fast, to abstain / quick / firmly fixed.

k.syn: demanding, exacting, fussy, hypercritical, squeamish
k.ant: undemanding 不挑剔的；cursory 粗略的，敷衍的；indiscriminate, uncritical 不加区分的
k.cn: 挑剔的，极仔细的，追求完美的
k.en: possessing or displaying careful, meticulous attention to detail

ex.en: be fastidious about personal hygiene and appearance
ex.cn: 极其注意个人卫生和外表

m: FASTIDIOUS contains FAST + IDIOUS — fast (firm) about IDeas of perfection. — fastidious, fussy / hard to please.

k.syn: critical, crucial, important, momentous, significant
k.ant: inconsequential, insignificant, petty, paltry, trivial, unimportant 不重要的
k.en: involving momentous consequences

ex.en: make a fateful decision to declare war
ex.cn: 做出了宣战的重大决定

k.syn: calamitous, cataclysmic, catastrophic, deadly, destructive, disastrous, fatal, ruinous
k.en: bringing death or disaster

ex.en: a fateful journey
ex.cn: 一趟致命的旅行

m: from FATE — momentous / disastrous. — fateful, momentous.

k.syn: plumb
k.en: to measure the depth of（as a body of water）typically with a weighted line

ex.en: The pilot had to continually fathom the river, which drought conditions had lowered to unprecedented levels.
ex.cn: 舵手不得不时刻注意河水深度，因为干旱已经使水深下降到了前所未有的程度。

k.syn: apprehend, cognize, comprehend, grasp, know, understand
k.ant: misunderstand 误解
k.der: fathomable adj. 可测量的；可知晓的
k.en: to penetrate and come to understand

ex.en: unable to fathom what he was talking about
ex.cn: 无法弄懂他在说什么

m: to FATHOM the depths = to measure / comprehend (a fathom is a unit of depth). — fathom, to understand fully.

k.syn: collapse, exhaustion, lassitude, tiredness, weariness
k.ant: refreshment, rejuvenation, revitalization 恢复活力
k.en: weariness or exhaustion from labor, exertion, or stress

ex.en: The day-long battle against the blaze left firefighters in a state of utter fatigue.
ex.cn: 与大火一整天的斗争使得消防员们处于极度疲惫的状态。

k.syn: drudge, drudgery, grind, labor, sweat, toil, travail
k.ant: fun, play 玩耍；sinecure 美差，闲职
k.en: something, such as tiring effort or activity, that causes weariness

ex.en: Although he had joined the army for action and adventure, much of his day seemed to be devoted to mindless fatigues.
ex.cn: 尽管当初参军是为了上战场冒险的，但他生活的大部分时间似乎都耗费在了没意思的苦差上。

m: FATIGUE rhymes with INTRIGUE but the opposite mood — INTRIGUE energizes; FATIGUE drains. — fatigue, exhaustion.

k.syn: asinine, brainless, foolish, obtuse, silly, simple, stupid, unwitty, witless
k.ant: sagacious, sapient 聪明的，睿智的；judicious, prudent, sensible, wise 谨慎的，明智的
k.en: complacently or inanely foolish

ex.en: Emperor Yang in the Sui Dynasty is not only a rare fatuous tyrant but also an outstanding poet.
ex.cn: 隋炀帝虽是少有的昏暴之君，但在文学上却是一位杰出的诗人。

m: FATUOUS rhymes with VACUOUS — both mean empty-headed and silly. — fatuous, foolish / silly.

k.syn: carper, castigator, caviler, censurer, critic, disparager, hypercritic, nitpicker
k.en: one who is given to petty criticism and constant complaint

ex.en: No sooner had we finished decorating the church than the parish faultfinder decided that she didn't like it.
ex.cn: 我们一完成教堂的装修工作，教区里挑剔的人就说她不喜欢。

m: FAULT + FINDER — one who finds faults. — faultfinder, a habitual critic.

k.syn: admiring, applauding, commendatory, complimentary, positive
k.ant: adverse, disapproving, negative 否定的
k.en: expressing approval

ex.en: Favorable reviews for the movie were few.
ex.cn: 关于这部电影的正面评价极少。

k.syn: advantageous, benefic, beneficial, favoring, good, helpful, salutary
k.ant: unfavorable, disadvantageous, untoward, unpropitious 不利的
k.en: tending to promote or facilitate

ex.en: mild climate favorable to his health
ex.cn: 有利于他健康的温和气候

m: FAVOR + ABLE — leaning toward favor. — favorable, approving / advantageous.

k.syn: cower, cringe, flatter, grovel, toady, truckle
k.ant: domineer 统治；专横跋扈
k.der: fawning adj. 阿谀奉承的，讨好的
k.en: to seek favor or attention by flattery and obsequious behavior

ex.en: fawn on one's superior
ex.cn: 向上司谄媚

m: a FAWN (baby deer) clings adoringly to its mother — to FAWN over someone is to flatter clingingly. — fawn, to flatter.

k.syn: abash, annoy, bother, disconcert, dismay, disturb, discomfit, embarrass, fluster, rattle
k.ant: calm, lull 使平静，使镇定
k.en: to disturb the composure of: disconcert, dismay

ex.en: Nothing can faze her.
ex.cn: 没有什么可以使她气馁。

m: FAZE rhymes with PHASE — to FAZE someone = throw them out of phase. — faze, to disturb / disconcert.

k.syn: bootless, fruitless, futile, meaningless, purposeless, useless, worthless
k.ant: effective, effectual, efficacious 有成效的
k.en: having no real worth or purpose

ex.en: years of feckless negotiations
ex.cn: 长达数年的毫无成果的谈判

k.syn: careless, heedless, inadvertent, irresponsible, slipshod, sloppy
k.ant: careful, cautious, circumspect, discreet, wary 谨慎小心的
k.en: careless and irresponsible

ex.en: The young man was feckless and irresponsible.
ex.cn: 这个年轻人既粗心又没有责任感。

m: FECKLESS rhymes with RECKLESS — both convey careless ineffectiveness. — feckless, ineffective / irresponsible.

k.syn: cornucopian, fertile, fruitful, lush, luxuriant, productive, prolific, rich
k.ant: barren, infertile, sterile 贫瘠的
k.en: fruitful in offspring or vegetation

ex.en: fecund black soil
ex.cn: 肥沃的黑土地

m: FECUND rhymes with SECOND — produces a new offspring every SECOND. — fecund, very fertile.

k.syn: debilitated, effete, enervated, faint, fragile, frail, infirm, languid, sapped
k.ant: mighty, powerful, robust, stalwart, stout, strong 强壮的，强健的
k.en: markedly lacking in strength

ex.en: The heartbeat was feeble and irregular.
ex.cn: 心跳虚弱且不规则。

m: FEEBLE contains EBB — strength EBBing away. — feeble, weak.

k.syn: affect, assume, counterfeit, dissemble, pretend, profess, sham, simulate
k.der: unfeigned adj. 没有伪装的，真诚的
k.en: to give a false appearance of

ex.en: I would never feign illness just to postpone a test.
ex.cn: 我绝不会为了缓考而装病。

m: FEIGN rhymes with REIGN — pretend to REIGN over a kingdom you don't have. — feign, to pretend.

k.syn: buffer, bumper, cocoon, cushioning, pad
k.en: a cushioning device, such as a bundle of rope or a piece of timber, used on the side of a vessel or dock to absorb impact or friction

ex.en: a fender on the SUV
ex.cn: 运动越野车的一个防护板

m: FENDER contains FEND — what FENDs off impact on a car. — fender, a buffer / guard.

k.syn: undomesticated, untamed, wild
k.ant: cultivated 人工培养的；domestic, tame 家养的，被驯化的
k.en: not domesticated or cultivated

ex.en: Animal experts discourage homeowners from trying to adopt feral animals as pets.
ex.cn: 动物专家们不建议户主们将野生动物收养为宠物。

k.syn: animalistic, beastly, brutal
k.ant: rational 理性的
k.en: having or showing the nature and appetites of a lower animal

ex.en: The movie reveals just how thin the veneer of civilization is and how feral we are at bottom.
ex.cn: 这部电影展示了所谓文明只不过是薄薄的一层纱，而内心深处我们仍然是野兽。

m: FERAL contains FER- ≈ "fierce" (similar root) — wild, untamed beast. — feral, wild / untamed.

k.en: of, relating to, or containing iron

m: FERROUS contains FERR — Latin FERRUM = iron (chemical symbol Fe). — ferrous, containing iron.

k.syn: enrich, manure
k.ant: deplete, drain, exhaust 消耗，耗尽
k.en: to make fertile

ex.en: Reading will fertilize vocabulary.
ex.cn: 阅读丰富词汇。

m: FERTIL + IZE — make FERTILE. — fertilize, to make fertile.

k.syn: boiling, hot, scorching, searing, sultry, sweltering, torrid
k.ant: arctic, chilling, cold, freezing, frigid, frozen, glacial, icy 冰冷的，寒冷的
k.en: having a notably high temperature

ex.en: Set out when the fervid heat subsides.
ex.cn: 当酷热消散之后出发。

k.syn: ardent, fervent, impassioned, passionate, perfervid
k.ant: cold, cool, dispassionate, emotionless, impassive, unemotional 淡定的，不为所动的
k.en: marked by great passion or zeal

ex.en: a fervid patriot
ex.cn: 热情洋溢的爱国者

m: FERVID and FERVENT share FERV — both have feverish intensity. — fervid, burning hot / passionate.

k.syn: ardor, enthusiasm, passion, zeal, zealotry
k.ant: apathy, indifference, impassiveness, unconcern 冷漠
k.en: great warmth and intensity of emotion

ex.en: A blind patriotic fervor is called chauvinism.
ex.cn: 盲目的爱国热情就被称为沙文主义。

m: FERVOR contains FERV — feverish intensity. — fervor, passionate intensity.

k.syn: corrupt, decompose, putrefy, rot, spoil
k.ant: heal, cure 愈合
k.en: to infect, inflame, or corrupt

ex.en: A dirty wound will probably fester.
ex.cn: 伤口弄脏了有可能会感染化脓。

ex.en: festering dispute over sth
ex.cn: 关于某事的争论更加恶化

m: FESTER rhymes with FOSTER but the opposite — FOSTER nurtures growth, FESTER nurtures rot. — fester, to rot / become infected.

k.syn: foul, fusty, malodorous, noisome, reeking, smelly, stinky
k.ant: ambrosial, aromatic, balmy, fragrant, redolent, scented 芳香的
k.en: having a heavy offensive smell

ex.en: Ammonia has a fetid odor that sickened the people.
ex.cn: 氨气有着令人作呕的气味。

m: FETID rhymes with PUTRID — both reek. — fetid, stinking.

k.syn: circumscription, constraint, curb, limitation, restraint, stricture
k.en: something that limits one's freedom of action or choice

ex.en: a fetter that prevents us from trying something new
ex.cn: 阻碍我们尝试新事物的枷锁

k.syn: chain, clog, enfetter, hamper, manacle, restrain, shackle, trammel
k.ant: enfranchise, free, liberate, unbind, unfetter, unshackle 解放； facilitate 促进
k.en: to restrain from motion, action, or progress

ex.en: be fettered by family responsibilities
ex.cn: 被家庭责任所束缚

m: FETTER rhymes with FOOT — fetters were leg shackles binding the FOOT. — fetter, a binding restraint.

k.syn: catastrophe, debacle, disaster, failure
k.ant: blockbuster, éclat, success 成功
k.en: a complete failure

ex.en: The Hitler's plot ended in a fiasco.
ex.cn: 希特勒的阴谋最终以彻底失败告终。

m: FIASCO sounds like "FI-ASS-co" — your project ends with you on your face. (Italian, from a botched glass-blowing.) — fiasco, a complete failure.

k.syn: capricious, fluid, mercurial, mutable, temperamental, volatile
k.ant: constant, immutable, invariable, stable, steady 稳定的，不变的
k.en: likely to change frequently, suddenly, or unexpectedly

ex.en: a fickle lover
ex.cn: 感情善变的情人

k.syn: disloyal, perfidious, recreant, traitorous, treacherous, unfaithful
k.ant: faithful, loyal 忠诚的
k.en: not true in one's allegiance to someone or something

ex.en: When the family's fortune disappeared, so did their fickle friends.
ex.cn: 当这个家庭的财富消逝殆尽时，他们的那些虚伪的朋友也无影无踪了。

m: FICKLE rhymes with TICKLE — your moods get TICKLEd in different directions. — fickle, ever-changing.

k.syn: chimerical, fabricated, fabulous, fictional, imagined, invented
k.ant: actual, existing, real 事实的
k.en: not real and existing only in the imagination

ex.en: His wartime exploits turned out to be entirely fictitious, as he had never even been in the military.
ex.cn: 他（吹嘘的）战时英勇行为是彻底虚构的，因为他根本就没有参过军。

m: FICTITIOUS contains FICTION — made up like fiction. — fictitious, made up.

k.syn: adherence, allegiance, commitment, devotion, faith, loyalty, piety
k.ant: disloyalty, infidelity, perfidy 不忠；treachery 背叛
k.en: the quality or state of being faithful

ex.en: They have never wavered in their fidelity to the cause of liberation.
ex.cn: 他们从不动摇对于解放事业的忠诚。

m: FIDELITY contains FID (faith, as in AFFIDAVIT) — being faithful. — fidelity, faithfulness.

k.en: to behave or move nervously

m: FIDGET rhymes with WIDGET — your hands restlessly play with a small WIDGET. — fidget, to squirm restlessly.

k.syn: extended, metaphorical
k.ant: literal 字面上的，逐字逐句的
k.en: expressing one thing in terms normally denoting another with which it may be regarded as analogous

ex.en: The word here is used in its figurative sense.
ex.cn: 这个词在这里取它的比喻义。

m: FIGURATIVE contains FIGURE — using figures of speech (not literal). — figurative, metaphorical.

k.cn: 傀儡（名义上为领袖但无实权的人）
k.en: a person given a position of nominal

m: FIGUREHEAD = FIGURE + HEAD — the head FIGURE on a ship's bow, ornamental but powerless. — figurehead, a powerless leader.

k.syn: figure, statuette
k.ant: colossus 巨像
k.en: a small statue

ex.en: His collection of figurines includes toy soldiers from every war that America has fought.
ex.cn: 他收藏的小雕塑包括了美国参与过的所有战争中的士兵模型。

m: FIGURINE = small FIGURE (FIGURE + diminutive INE). — figurine, a small statue.

k.syn: delay, hindrance, impediment, obstruction, postponement, procrastination
k.en: the use of obstructionist tactics, especially prolonged speechmaking, for the purpose of delaying legislative action

ex.en: The Senator used a filibuster to stop the bill.
ex.cn: 参议员拖延了草案的实施。

m: FILIBUSTER rhymes with BUSTER — a senator BUSTs the schedule by talking endlessly. — filibuster, prolonged speech to delay legislation.

k.cn: 精致复杂的金银丝细工饰品
k.en: delicate and intricate ornamental work made from gold, silver, or other

m: FILIGREE contains FIL (thread, as in FILAMENT) — thread-like delicate metalwork. — filigree, ornamental fine wirework.

k.en: a fragment

ex.en: metal filing
ex.cn: 金属屑

m: FILING comes from FILE (the tool) — bits FILEd off. — filing, fine metal shavings.

k.en: a young female horse, especially one under four years old

m: FILLY rhymes with SILLY — a young female horse can be silly and frisky. — filly, a young female horse.

k.syn: screen
k.en: to pass through a filter

ex.en: Steep the tea and then filter it to get rid of the leaves.
ex.cn: 浸泡茶叶，再将其过滤以去除废渣。

k.syn: clear, distill, purify
k.ant: adulterate 掺杂；contaminate 污染
k.en: to remove（usually visible）impurities from

ex.en: After frying the chicken, we filtered the oil and kept it in the refrigerator to use again.
ex.cn: 在炸完鸡肉之后，我们将滤去了杂质的油保存起来以供日后使用。

m: FILTER contains FELT — old filters were made of FELT mats. — filter, to remove impurities.

k.syn: close, coda, conclusion, end
k.ant: prologue 开场白；overture 序曲
k.en: the closing part, scene, or number in a public performance

ex.en: Didier Drogba pulled one back for Chelsea in a finale.
ex.cn: 德罗巴在终场结束时为切尔西扳回一球。

m: FINALE contains FINAL — the FINAL grand moment. — finale, the closing portion.

k.en: any of numerous passerine

m: FINCH rhymes with PINCH — a small bird that PINCHes seeds. — finch, a small songbird.

k.syn: adeptness, adroitness, cleverness, dexterity, proficiency
k.ant: awkwardness, ineptitude, gaucherie, ungainliness 笨拙
k.en: mental skill or quickness

ex.en: The musician shows wonderful finesse.
ex.cn: 那位音乐家展现出了非凡的技艺。

k.syn: contrive, frame, machinate, maneuver, manipulate
k.en: to plan out usually with subtle skill or care

ex.en: finesse the schedule
ex.cn: 精心安排日程

k.syn: avoid, dodge, eschew, evade, shun
k.ant: confront 直面
k.en: to get or keep away from（as a responsibility）through cleverness or trickery

ex.en: He tried to finesse the blame for the foreign policy fiasco, even though he was Secretary of State at the time.
ex.cn: 他竭尽所能躲避因失败的对外政策而可能遭到的指责，尽管他时任国务卿。

m: FINESSE contains FINE — fine skill / fine handling. — finesse, refined skill.

k.syn: demanding, exacting, fastidious, fussy, nice, particular
k.ant: undemanding, unfussy 不挑剔的
k.en: extremely or excessively particular, exacting, or meticulous in taste or standards

ex.en: have a reputation for being finicky eater
ex.cn: 有着挑食的名声

m: FINICKY contains FINIC ≈ "fine-icky" — too fussy about every fine detail. — finicky, fussy.

k.syn: droopy, floppy, lank, loose, slack, yielding
k.ant: stiff, sturdy 结实的，坚硬的；resilient 有弹性的
k.en: not firm or stiff; lacking normal or youthful firmness

ex.en: flaccid muscles
ex.cn: 松弛的肌肉

m: FLACCID rhymes with FLAT-cid — flat and limp. — flaccid, limp / flabby.

k.syn: banner, ensign, pendant, pennant
k.en: a piece of fabric that is used as a symbol（as of a nation）or as a signaling device

ex.en: We respect the flag of our fathers.
ex.cn: 我们尊敬父辈们的旗帜。

k.syn: decay, decline, deteriorate, emaciate, fade, fail, languish, sag, waste, wilt, wither
k.ant: thrive 繁荣；revitalize, revive 恢复活力
k.en: to become unsteady, feeble, or spiritless

ex.en: We flagged as we neared the end of the long mountain trail.
ex.cn: 当我们终于快到达漫长山路的终点时，已经筋疲力尽了。

m: to FLAG (verb) = to droop like a tired FLAG hanging limp. — flag, to grow weak.

k.syn: flaring, florid, garish, ornate, ostentatious, showy
k.ant: natural 自然的；conservative, quiet, understated 低调的
k.cn: 艳丽夺目的，炫耀的，充满装饰的
k.en: marked by or given to strikingly elaborate or colorful display or behavior; ornate

ex.en: Las Vegas showgirls wearing flamboyant headdresses
ex.cn: 打扮花哨的拉斯维加斯舞女

m: FLAMBOYANT contains FLAM- (FLAME) — blazing colorful display. — flamboyant, showily colorful.

k.syn: adulate, blandish, compliment, court, massage, overpraise, stroke, wheedle
k.ant: abuse 辱骂；censure, criticize 批评
k.en: to praise excessively especially from motives of self-interest

ex.en: Friends who flatter you to your face are not true friends.
ex.cn: 当面拍你马屁的朋友都不是真正的朋友。

k.syn: pique, plume
k.en: to think highly of（oneself）

ex.en: Don't flatter yourself that no one has ever thought of that idea before.
ex.cn: 不要自欺欺人地认为别人没有想到过那个想法。

k.syn: become, enhance, suit
k.ant: mar, spoil 破坏
k.der: flattering adj. 令人满足的，令人迷恋的
k.en: to show off becomingly or advantageously

ex.en: Orange flatters those with golden skin tones.
ex.cn: 橙色让黄肤色的人显得更漂亮。

m: FLATTER contains FLAT — flatten ego with smooth praise. — flatter, to praise insincerely.

k.syn: blight, blotch, defect, fault, imperfection, mar, scar, spot
k.en: an imperfection, often concealed, that impairs soundness

ex.en: The absence of flaw in beauty is itself a flaw.
ex.cn: 完美无瑕本身就是瑕疵。

k.syn: blemish, break, compromise, cripple, harm, hurt, impair, injure, spoil, vitiate
k.ant: fix, mend, repair, revamp 修复，改造
k.der: flawed adj. 有瑕疵的；flawless adj. 完美无瑕的；flawlessness n. 完美
k.en: to reduce the soundness, effectiveness, or perfection of

ex.en: That crack has flawed the vase to the extent that its value in the antiques market is greatly reduced.
ex.cn: 那道裂缝让这个花瓶在古董市场上大大地掉价。

m: FLAW contains LAW — a FLAW is a place where the design's LAW broke. — flaw, an imperfection.

k.en: a slender erect annual with blue flowers commonly cultivated for its bast fiber and seed

m: FLAX rhymes with WAX — both ancient natural materials; FLAX makes linen. — flax, a fiber plant.

k.en: to grow the plumage

m: FLEDGE rhymes with EDGE — birds get their flight feathers at the EDGE of independence. — fledge, to grow flight feathers.

k.syn: armada, caravan, cavalcade, motorcade
k.en: a group of vehicles traveling together or under one management

ex.en: fleet of the Royal Navy
ex.cn: 皇家海军的舰队

k.syn: brisk, expeditious, fast, hasty, nimble, quick, rapid, rattling, speedy, swift
k.ant: slow 缓慢的
k.en: moving, proceeding, or acting with great speed

ex.en: He was fleet as a deer.
ex.cn: 他如小鹿般敏捷。

k.syn: dart, flit, hurry, hustle
k.ant: plod 沉重而缓慢地走
k.en: to move or pass swiftly

m: FLEET contains FLEE — a FLEET of ships FLEEs swiftly across the ocean. — fleet, a group of ships / swift.

k.syn: adaptable, adjustable, alterable, changeable, elastic, fluid, malleable, modifiable, pliable, variable
k.ant: fixed, immutable, inflexible, invariable 确定的，不可变的；stiff, rigid 僵化的
k.en: capable of being readily changed

ex.en: Our schedules are highly flexible.
ex.cn: 我们的计划是非常灵活的。

k.syn: docile, manageable, tractable
k.ant: adamant, headstrong, intractable, mulish, recalcitrant, refractory, stubborn, obstinate 倔强的
k.en: susceptible to influence or persuasion

ex.en: Their boss was flexible and lenient.
ex.cn: 他们的老板既好说话，又很仁慈。

m: FLEXIBLE contains FLEX — able to FLEX without breaking. — flexible, bendable.

k.syn: cringe, quail, recoil, shrink, wince
k.ant: face, meet 面对；confront 直面
k.en: to draw back in fear, pain, or disgust

ex.en: He met my gaze without flinching.
ex.cn: 他毫不畏缩地与我对视。

m: FLINCH rhymes with PINCH — pull back from a PINCH. — flinch, to recoil.

k.syn: facetiousness, flightiness, frivolousness, frothiness, levity, silliness
k.ant: earnestness, gravity, seriousness, soberness, solemnity, solemnness 严肃，认真
k.en: unbecoming levity or pertness especially in respect to grave or sacred matters

ex.en: The flippancy of your answer annoyed me.
ex.cn: 你轻率的回答让我很恼火。

m: FLIPPANCY contains FLIP — making FLIP (offhand) jokes about serious things. — flippancy, casual disrespect.

k.syn: coquet, dally, frivol, toy, trifle
k.en: to behave amorously without serious intent

ex.en: The waitress at that restaurant flirts with all single male customers.
ex.cn: 这家餐厅的女服务员对所有的男性单身顾客都暗送秋波。

k.syn: dance, dart, fleet, flick, flit, flutter, hurry, rush, zip
k.en: to make an irregular series of quick, sudden movements

ex.en: The beautiful lady lazily watched the butterflies flirting among the wildflowers.
ex.cn: 这名美丽的女子慵懒地看着蝴蝶在野花丛中乱舞。

m: FLIRT rhymes with SKIRT — a flirty SKIRT-swirl. — flirt, to playfully tease romantically.

k.syn: dance, dart, fleet, flick, flirt, flutter, hurry, rush, zip
k.ant: plod 沉重缓慢地走
k.en: to pass quickly or abruptly from one place or condition to another

ex.en: Memories of the evening flitted through her mind.
ex.cn: 关于那一晚的记忆在她脑海中一掠而过。

m: FLIT rhymes with QUIT — birds FLIT and QUIT a perch quickly. — flit, to dart quickly.

k.syn: army, drove, herd, legion
k.en: a great number of persons or creatures massed together

ex.en: a flock of ill-disposed reporters at the press conference
ex.cn: 新闻发布会上一群不怀好意的记者

k.syn: mob, swarm, throng
k.en: to congregate or travel in a flock or crowd

ex.en: Vacationers flocked to the towns along the shore in order to escape the August heat.
ex.cn: 度假的人们一起涌向海岸，以躲避八月里的热浪。

m: FLOCK contains LOCK — a flock of sheep LOCKed together moving as one. — flock, a group / to gather.

k.syn: bombastic, flowery, grandiloquent, magniloquent, rhetorical
k.en: full of fine words and fancy expressions

ex.en: gave a florid speech to attract attention
ex.cn: 做了个辞藻华丽的演说以吸引眼球

k.syn: baroque, bedizened, flamboyant, fussy, luscious, ornate
k.ant: austere, plain, severe, stark, unadorned 朴素的，简单的
k.en: elaborately and often excessively decorated

ex.en: a florid architectural style
ex.cn: 华丽的建筑风格

m: FLORID contains FLOR (flower, as in FLORIST) — flowery and ornate. — florid, ornately flowery.

k.syn: blunder, fumble, lumber, plod, struggle, trudge
k.ant: glide, slide 滑行；breeze, waltz 轻松地行动
k.en: to proceed or act clumsily or ineffectually

ex.en: flounder through the desert
ex.cn: 步履蹒跚地穿越沙漠

m: to FLOUNDER = to flop helplessly like a FLOUNDER fish on a boat deck. — flounder, to struggle clumsily.

k.syn: bloom, burgeon, prosper, thrive
k.ant: languish, wane 变得衰弱，衰败；fail 失败
k.en: to grow luxuriantly; to achieve success

ex.en: The program will flourish once it receives adequate funding.
ex.cn: 这个项目只要有足够的资金就能大获成功。

k.syn: adornment, decoration, embellishment, ornamentation
k.en: an embellishment or ornamentation

ex.en: a bedroom with cute little flourishes
ex.cn: 一间带有可爱小装饰的卧室

ex.en: Her writing style is simple and clear, without unnecessary flourishes.
ex.cn: 她的文风很简单清新，没有那些不必要的修饰。

m: FLOURISH contains FLOUR — like dough rising with FLOUR, growing strong. — flourish, to thrive.

k.syn: despise, disregard, gibe, sneer, taunt
k.ant: respect, revere, venerate 尊敬
k.en: to treat with contemptuous disregard

ex.en: flout the academic norm by plagiarizing
ex.cn: 通过抄袭来蔑视学术规范

m: FLOUT rhymes with SHOUT — to FLOUT a rule = to SHOUT defiance against it. — flout, to mockingly defy.

k.syn: mutate, shift, swing, oscillate, vacillate, vary, waver
k.ant: stabilize 保持稳定；plateau 到达稳定状态
k.en: to shift back and forth uncertainly

ex.en: Stock prices fluctuate wildly.
ex.cn: 股票价格大幅波动。

ex.en: Samples of ice cores collected from Antarctica suggested that the atmospheric concentration of carbon dioxide has been fluctuating periodically.
ex.cn: 南极冰芯样本表明，大气中的二氧化碳浓度存在周期性的涨落。

m: FLUCTUATE contains FLUCT (wave, Latin FLUCTUS) — moves up and down like waves. — fluctuate, to vary irregularly.

k.syn: eloquent, silver-tongued, well-spoken
k.ant: inarticulate, ineloquent, unvocal 说话不清楚的
k.en: able to express oneself clearly and well

ex.en: a very fluent speaker who always communicates his points well
ex.cn: 一个总能明确指出要点的流利演说家

k.syn: effortless, facile, fluid, painless, ready, simple, smooth
k.ant: arduous, demanding, exacting, formidable, grueling, hard, laborious, murderous, rough, toilsome, tough 费劲的，艰苦的
k.en: involving minimal difficulty or effort

ex.en: a fluent performance of one of the oldest magic tricks in the book
ex.cn: 一场毫不费力的魔术，表演的是书本上最老的魔术之一

m: FLUENT contains FLU (flow) — words FLOW out smoothly. — fluent, smoothly verbal.

k.en: a chance occurrence

m: FLUKE rhymes with DUKE — a lucky strike worthy of a DUKE. — fluke, a lucky chance.

k.syn: blooming, florid, glowing, red, rosy, rubicund, sanguine
k.ant: ashen, doughy, livid, pale, pallid, wan 苍白的，病态的
k.en: having a healthy reddish skin tone

k.syn: affluent, loaded, opulent, wealthy
k.ant: destitute, impoverished, indigent, needy, impecunious 贫穷的
k.en: having goods, property, or money in abundance

ex.en: She's very flush now that she has her inheritance.
ex.cn: 自从继承了那份遗产，她变得十分富有。

k.syn: abounding, abundant, awash, fraught, lousy, replete, swarming, teeming, thronging
k.ant: inadequate, insufficient, scant, scarce, short 匮乏的，不足的
k.en: marked by abundance

ex.en: a field flush with flowers
ex.cn: 长满了鲜花的原野

k.syn: irrigate, rinse, sluice, wash
k.en: to pour liquid over or through in order to cleanse

ex.en: flush a wound with iodine
ex.cn: 用碘酒冲洗伤口

m: FLUSH rhymes with BLUSH — your face FLUSHes red; or you're FLUSH (overflowing) with money. — flush, blushing / abundant.

k.syn: abash, confound, confuse, discomfit, disconcert, discountenance, faze, mortify, nonplus, rattle
k.ant: calm, quiet, settle, soothe, tranquilize 安抚，使情绪稳定
k.en: to put into a state of agitated confusion

ex.en: A GPA of 1.0 flusters him. 1.0
ex.cn: 的绩点让他很慌乱。

m: FLUSTER rhymes with BLUSTER — both convey agitation. — fluster, to confuse and agitate.

k.en: of, relating to, or living in a stream or river

m: FLUVIAL contains FLU (flow) — relating to flowing rivers. — fluvial, of a river.

k.syn: demerit, dereliction, failing, fault, frailty, shortcoming, sin, vice, want, weakness
k.ant: merit, virtue 优点，美德
k.en: a minor flaw or shortcoming in character or behavior

ex.en: They admired their teacher despite his foibles.
ex.cn: 尽管他们的老师有些小缺点，但他们还是很敬佩他。

m: FOIBLE contains FOIL — foil tears at its thinnest spot. — foible, a minor character flaw.

k.syn: baffle, balk, beat, checkmate, discomfit, frustrate, thwart
k.ant: forward, foster, further, promote 帮助，促进
k.en: to prevent from being successful; defeat

ex.en: foil her enemy by pulling some strings
ex.cn: 通过一些关系和手段挫败了她的对手

m: in fencing, your opponent's FOIL FOILs your attack. (Also: FOIL = thin metal that wraps and covers.) — foil, to thwart.

k.en: a cluster

m: FOLIAGE contains FOLI (leaf, as in FOLIO/PORTFOLIO) — collection of leaves. — foliage, leaves of plants.

k.syn: abet, brew, ferment, incite, instigate, provoke, raise, stir
k.ant: quash, quell, quench, squash 平息，镇压
k.en: to promote the growth or development of

ex.en: He was accused of fomenting violence.
ex.cn: 他被指控煽动暴力行为。

m: FOMENT rhymes with MOMENT — at the right MOMENT, agitators FOMENT unrest. — foment, to stir up.

k.syn: guaranteed, infallible, safe, unfailing, fail-safe
k.ant: fallible 容易出错的
k.cn: 十分简单以至于不会失败的
k.en: so simple, plain, or reliable as to leave no opportunity for error, misuse, or failure

ex.en: Anything foolproof is impossible and what truly counts is probability.
ex.cn: 没有万无一失的事情，真正的问题在于概率。

m: FOOLPROOF = even a FOOL can't break it; PROOF against fools. — foolproof, impossible to fail.

k.syn: loose, unbound, unconfined, unfettered, unrestrained, untrammeled
k.ant: bound, confined, restrained, tied 有束缚的
k.en: having no attachments or ties; free to do as one pleases

ex.en: After having been chained for so long, the suddenly footloose dog ran about the yard tirelessly.
ex.cn: 这只长期被锁链束缚的小狗因为突然之间获得了自由，在院子里不知疲倦地奔跑。

m: FOOTLOOSE = FOOT + LOOSE — feet loose, free to roam. — footloose, free to wander.

k.syn: patience, temperance, tolerance, toleration
k.ant: impatience 不耐烦
k.en: tolerance and restraint in the face of provocation; patience

ex.en: reach the end of my forbearance
ex.cn: 到了让我忍无可忍的地步

k.syn: charity, clemency, leniency, mercifulness, quarter
k.ant: vindictiveness 恶意
k.en: kind, gentle, or compassionate treatment especially towards someone who is undeserving of it

ex.en: The judge showed forbearance, and gave the teenaged first offender a suspended sentence.
ex.cn: 法官网开一面，给了初犯的少年犯一个缓刑。

m: FORBEARANCE contains BEAR — to BEAR with patiently. — forbearance, patient self-restraint.

k.en: a shallow place in a body of water, such as a river, where one can cross

m: a FORD is a shallow place where you can WADE (or drive a Ford!) across a river. — ford, a shallow river crossing.

k.syn: ancestor, antecedent, ascendant, father, grandfather, primogenitor, progenitor
k.ant: descendant, offspring 后代
k.en: a person from whom one is descended; an ancestor

ex.en: The son of Abraham was traditionally considered to be the forebear of the Arabs.
ex.cn: 亚伯拉罕之子传统上被认为是阿拉伯人的祖先。

m: FOREBEAR = FORE (before) + BEAR — ones who came BEFORE; ancestors. — forebear, an ancestor.

k.syn: avert, deter, obviate, preclude, prevent
k.ant: abet, assist, facilitate, further, precipitate, promote 帮助，促进
k.en: to delay, hinder, or prevent by taking precautionary measures beforehand

ex.en: You can often forestall skidding on the ice simply by driving more slowly.
ex.cn: 通常你只需慢点开车就可以防止车辆在冰面上失控打滑。

m: FORESTALL = FORE (ahead) + STALL — STALL the problem before it starts. — forestall, to prevent in advance.

k.syn: beginning, introduction, overture, preamble, preface, prelude, prologue
k.ant: epilogue, coda 尾声；finale 终场
k.en: a preface or an introductory note, as for a book, especially by a person other than the author

ex.en: The foreword of this book is co-written by Jason and Franklin.
ex.cn: 这本书的序由Jason和Franklin共同写成。

m: FOREWORD = FORE (before) + WORD — words BEFORE the main text. — foreword, an introduction.

k.en: something forged

m: FORGER works at the FORGE — either smith or counterfeiter. — forger, blacksmith or counterfeiter.

k.syn: direful, dreadful, fearsome, forbidding, ghastly, horrible, intimidating, redoubtable, terrifying
k.ant: comforting 给人安慰的
k.en: causing fear, dread, or apprehension

ex.en: a nation that possesses formidable nuclear deterrence
ex.cn: 一个拥有可怕核威慑力量的国家

k.syn: arduous, challenging, demanding, exacting, grueling, heavy, laborious, sweaty, toilsome, tough, strenuous
k.ant: easy, effortless, facile, mindless, simple 轻松的，简单的
k.en: requiring considerable physical or mental effort

ex.en: Running a marathon is a formidable undertaking.
ex.cn: 跑马拉松是个困难的任务。

ex.en: They have recently made a formidable decision.
ex.cn: 他们最近做出了一个艰难的决定。

m: FORMIDABLE contains FORM — a FORM so massive and intimidating you don't dare challenge it. — formidable, frighteningly impressive.

k.syn: candid, forthcoming, frank, honest, open, outspoken, straightforward
k.ant: dissembling 虚伪的
k.en: free in expressing one's true feelings and opinions

ex.en: her forthright way of dealing with people
ex.cn: 她率真的为人处事方式

k.syn: direct, plain, straight
k.ant: circuitous, indirect, roundabout 不直接的，绕弯子的
k.en: free from ambiguity or evasiveness: going straight to the point

ex.en: He was forthright in appraising the problem.
ex.cn: 他直截了当地评价这个问题。

m: FORTHRIGHT = FORTH (forward) + RIGHT — going forward straight. — forthright, straightforward.

k.syn: energize, harden, invigorate, reinforce, strengthen, toughen
k.ant: debilitate, enervate, enfeeble, vitiate, weaken 使衰弱，削弱
k.en: to give physical strength, courage, or endurance to

ex.en: This country will fortify the coastal areas.
ex.cn: 这个国家将加强沿海地区的防御。

k.syn: brace, forearm, gird, nerve, poise, ready, steel

ex.en: She fortified herself for the incoming tennis tournament with a series of confidence-boosting exercises.
ex.cn: 她通过一系列练习赛来建立自信，为即将到来的网球锦标赛做好心理准备。

m: FORTIFY = FORT + IFY — make like a FORT, strengthen. — fortify, to strengthen.

k.syn: advance, encourage, forward, further, incubate, promote
k.ant: discourage, frustrate, inhibit 阻碍，妨碍
k.en: to help the growth or development of

ex.en: detect and foster artistic talent
ex.cn: 发现并培养艺术天分

k.syn: breed, cultivate, nourish, nurse, nurture, raise, rear
k.en: to bring to maturity through care and education

ex.en: a greathearted couple fostering two adopted children as well as their own
ex.cn: 对两个抱养的孩子像自己亲生的孩子一样培养的好心夫妇

m: FOSTER rhymes with BOOSTER — to FOSTER = to BOOST / nurture growth. — foster, to encourage / nurture.

k.syn: architect, author, creator, father, generator, initiator, originator, sire
k.ant: terminator 终结者
k.en: one that founds or establishes

ex.en: founders of the nation
ex.cn: 国父们

k.syn: collapse, flunk, miss, strike out, wash out
k.ant: succeed, work out 成功
k.en: to fail utterly

ex.en: The theater company foundered after its corporate funding dried up.
ex.cn: 在其基金告罄之后，这个戏剧公司彻底失败了。

k.syn: sink, submerge, submerse, go down
k.ant: float 漂浮
k.en: to become submerged

ex.en: The ship struck a reef and foundered.
ex.cn: 船因触礁而沉没。

m: to FOUNDER = your ship's FOUND-ation gives way, you sink. — founder, to fail / sink.

k.syn: battle, clash, combat, conflict, contest, hassle, skirmish, struggle, tussle
k.en: a physical dispute between opposing individuals or groups; a rough and often noisy fight usually involving several people

ex.en: The police were called in to break up the fracas.
ex.cn: 警察奉命去制止骚乱。

m: FRACAS contains FRAC (break, as in FRACTURE) — a noisy fight that BREAKS the peace. — fracas, a noisy quarrel.

k.syn: breach, break, rupture, schism, split
k.ant: unity 统一
k.en: the act or process of breaking

ex.en: a sudden fracture of the established order
ex.cn: 已建立起的秩序被突然打破

k.syn: disrupt, fragment, rive
k.ant: cement 接合
k.en: to cause to separate into pieces usually suddenly or forcibly

ex.en: Their happiness was fractured by an unforeseen tragedy.
ex.cn: 他们的幸福生活被突如其来的悲剧打破了。

m: FRACTURE contains FRACT (break, Latin) — a break. — fracture, a break.

k.syn: breakable, feeble, frail, friable, frangible, infirm, unsound, weak
k.ant: tough, durable 抗打击的
k.en: easily broken or destroyed

ex.en: a fragile piece of glass
ex.cn: 一块易碎的玻璃

k.syn: delicate, sensitive
k.en: easily injured without careful handling

ex.en: Babies are extremely fragile, so remember to care for them gently.
ex.cn: 小孩子特别容易受伤，所以要记得温柔地对待他们。

m: FRAGILE contains FRAG (break) — easily breakable. — fragile, easily broken.

k.syn: weak, effete, enervated, feeble, fragile, infirm, languid, unsubstantial
k.ant: robust 充满活力的；hale, sturdy 健壮的
k.en: physically weak

ex.en: Working for three days without any sleep made him extremely frail.
ex.cn: 连续三天的彻夜工作使他极为虚弱。

k.syn: characterless, invertebrate, nerveless, spineless
k.ant: faithful, resolute 意志坚定的，有坚定信念的
k.der: frailty n. 虚弱；（意志）脆弱
k.en: easily led astray; morally weak

ex.en: frail and pathetic humanity
ex.cn: 容易误入歧途的可悲人性

k.syn: negligible, slight, small
k.ant: good 很有希望的
k.en: slight, unsubstantial

ex.en: a frail hope of success
ex.cn: 成功的机会渺茫

m: FRAIL rhymes with FAIL — frail bodies FAIL easily. — frail, weak.

k.syn: deceitful, deceptive, dishonest, duplicitous, guileful, underhanded
k.ant: reliable, trustworthy 可信的；authentic 真实的；honest, straight 诚实的
k.en: characterized by, based on, or done by fraud

ex.en: a fraudulent interpretation of experimental data
ex.cn: 对实验数据的欺骗性解释

m: FRAUDULENT contains FRAUD — full of fraud. — fraudulent, deceitful.

k.syn: abounding, abundant, awash, flush, replete, thronging
k.ant: scarce 稀缺的
k.en: full of or accompanied by something

ex.en: an experience fraught with peril
ex.cn: 充满危险的经历

k.syn: uneasy, agitating, anxious, distressful, disturbing, restless, tense, unsettling, nail-biting
k.ant: calming, relaxing 使人镇静的，使人放松的
k.en: causing or characterized by emotional distress or tension

ex.en: a fraught relationship between the two neighboring countries
ex.cn: 两个邻国间令人担忧的关系

m: FRAUGHT rhymes with WROUGHT — but FRAUGHT is loaded with risks. — fraught, full of (anxiety / danger).

k.en: a person who sells services to employers without a long-term

m: FREELANCER = FREE + LANCER — a knight whose LANCE is FREE for hire (medieval mercenary). — freelancer, an independent worker.

k.syn: delirium, fever, furor, fury, hysteria, insanity, rage, rampage, uproar
k.ant: sanity 理智
k.en: a violent mental or emotional agitation

ex.en: She is subject to these frenzies several times a year.
ex.cn: 她每年都要暴怒几次。

m: FRENZY rhymes with KIDNEY/CHIMNEY but feels frantic — contains FREN ≈ "FRANTIC" energy. — frenzy, wild excitement.

k.syn: haunt, resort, visit
k.ant: avoid, shun 躲避
k.en: to pay frequent visits to; be in or at often

ex.en: frequent opera houses
ex.cn: 时常造访歌剧院

k.syn: common, constant, everyday, habitual, periodical, repeated
k.ant: rare 稀有的，少见的
k.cn: 时常发生的，重复再现的
k.en: happening at short intervals; often repeated or occurring

ex.en: He used to make frequent trips to Los Angeles.
ex.cn: 他从前经常前往洛杉矶旅行。

m: FREQUENT contains FREQ ≈ "FREQUENCY" — high frequency = often. — frequent, often-occurring.

k.en: the art of painting on freshly spread moist lime plaster

m: FRESCO contains FRESH — Italian "fresh" plaster painted while still wet. — fresco, a wall painting on wet plaster.

k.syn: irritable, fractious, peevish, pettish, petulant
k.ant: easygoing 随和的
k.en: inclined to be vexed or troubled

ex.en: Adolescence is the most fretful stages of human development.
ex.cn: 青春期是人类发展过程中最焦躁的阶段。

m: FRETFUL contains FRET — full of FRETs (worries). — fretful, irritable / anxious.

k.syn: crumbly, delicate, feeble, fragile, frail, infirm, weak
k.ant: sturdy, substantial 坚固的
k.en: easily crumbled or pulverized

ex.en: friable mineral rock
ex.cn: 易碎的矿石

m: FRIABLE rhymes with PLIABLE but the opposite — pliable bends, FRIABLE crumbles. — friable, easily crumbled.

k.syn: abrasion
k.en: the rubbing of one object or surface against another

ex.en: lubricant that significantly reduces friction
ex.cn: 能显著减少摩擦的润滑剂

k.syn: disagreement, conflict, contention, dissidence, schism, war
k.ant: accord, harmony, peace, concord 和谐
k.en: the clashing between two persons or parties of opposed views

ex.en: There is a friction between the professor and students.
ex.cn: 教授和学生之间存在不和。

m: FRICTION contains FRIC ≈ FRICTION (rubbing, Latin) — rubbing causes friction. — friction, rubbing / conflict.

k.cn: 在墙顶与天花板间起装饰作用的横条饰带，石膏线
k.en: a sculptured or richly ornamented band (as on a building); a wide decorative band

m: FRIEZE rhymes with FREEZE — the FRIEZE band runs FROZEN-still atop the wall. — frieze, decorative band on a wall.

k.syn: cold, arctic, chilling, freezing, frosty, glacial
k.ant: roasting, scalding, scorching, searing, seething, sizzling, sultry, sweltering, torrid 炎热的
k.en: extremely cold

ex.en: an unusually frigid winter of Wuhan
ex.cn: 武汉一个反常的寒冬

k.syn: indifferent, cold, emotionless, passionless, unresponsive
k.ant: ardent, amorous, cordial 饱含感情的
k.en: lacking warmth or ardor

ex.en: a formal but frigid welcome
ex.cn: 一个正式但冷漠的招呼

m: FRIGID contains FRIG ≈ "frigerator" (REFRIGERATOR) — fridge-cold. — frigid, very cold / unfriendly.

k.syn: border, boundary, circumference, edge, margin, perimeter, periphery, skirt
k.ant: center, core 中心，核心
k.en: something that resembles such a border or edging

ex.en: “Don't act on the fringes of the law,”warns the customs officer.
ex.cn: 海关人员警告说：“不要打法律的擦边球”。

k.syn: abut, border, neighbor, skirt, verge
k.en: to be adjacent to

m: FRINGE rhymes with HINGE — a HINGE sits at the edge of a door, like a FRINGE at the edge. — fringe, an outer edge.

k.syn: giddy, shallow, superficial
k.ant: earnest, sober, serious 严肃的
k.en: marked by unbecoming levity

ex.en: a frivolous young woman
ex.cn: 一个举止轻浮的年轻女子

k.syn: trivial, flimsy, light, petty, trifling
k.ant: consequential, eventful, meaningful, momentous, weighty 重要的
k.en: of little weight or importance

ex.en: The frivolous comment was soon forgotten.
ex.cn: 那个不值一提的评论很快就被遗忘了。

m: FRIVOLOUS contains FRIV ≈ "FRIVO-lous" — sounds light and silly itself. (Or shares root with FRIVOLITY.) — frivolous, silly / unimportant.

k.en: a large leaf (especially of a palm or fern)

m: FROND rhymes with POND — fern FRONDS hang over the POND. — frond, a fern leaf.

k.syn: delicate, gossamer, light
k.ant: weighty 重的；sturdy 结实的
k.en: made of light thin material

ex.en: a frothy laptop made of carbon fiber and alloys
ex.cn: 碳纤维以及合金制造的轻薄型笔记本电脑

k.syn: flighty, frivolous, puerile
k.ant: earnest, serious, sober 严肃的
k.en: gaily frivolous or light in content or treatment

ex.en: a frothy movie
ex.cn: 一部有喜感的电影

m: FROTHY contains FROTH — like FROTH on beer: light and bubbly, no substance. — frothy, light / unsubstantial.

k.syn: slovenly, unkempt, untidy
k.ant: dapper, neat, tidy, spruce 整洁的
k.en: having a slovenly or uncared-for appearance

ex.en: frowsy hair
ex.cn: 邋遢的头发

k.syn: fetid, noisome, smelly, stinky
k.ant: ambrosial, aromatic, fragrant, redolent 芳香的
k.en: having an unpleasant smell

ex.en: The abandoned house was dank and frowsy and barely fit for human habitation.
ex.cn: 这座废房子光线又暗，气味又难闻，几乎无法供人居住。

m: FROWSY rhymes with DROWSY — like just-woke-up DROWSY: rumpled and unkempt. — frowsy, unkempt / musty.

k.syn: sparing, economical, provident, stingy
k.ant: extravagant, prodigal, sumptuous 奢侈浪费的
k.en: characterized by or reflecting economy in the use of resources

ex.en: be frugal in her expenditures
ex.cn: 节约她的开销

m: FRUGAL contains FRUG ≈ "frugal-FRUIT" — Latin FRUX = fruit; live off small fruits, spend little. — frugal, thrifty.

k.syn: ruin, baffle, balk, circumvent, foil, thwart
k.ant: abet 帮助，鼓舞，教唆；accomplish, fulfill 实现
k.en: to prevent from accomplishing a purpose or fulfilling a desire

ex.en: He frustrated their scheme in time.
ex.cn: 他及时挫败了他们的阴谋。

k.syn: chill, daunt, dishearten, dismay, dispirit
k.ant: embolden, encourage, nerve, steel 鼓舞
k.der: frustration n. 沮丧；阻挠；frustrated adj. 沮丧的
k.en: to lessen the courage or confidence of

ex.en: frustrated by his failure
ex.cn: 为他的失败感到沮丧

m: FRUSTRATE contains FRUST ≈ "FRUST(rated) gust" — your plan gets gust-ed away. — frustrate, to thwart.

k.syn: criticize, blame, censure, condemn, denounce, denunciate, lambaste, reprehend, reprobate
k.ant: applaud, compliment, praise 赞美
k.en: to issue a thunderous verbal attack or denunciation

ex.en: fulminate the so-called curricular reform
ex.cn: 痛斥所谓的课程改革

m: FULMINATE contains FULM ≈ "FULM-inate" / Latin FULMEN (lightning) — denounce like thunder/lightning. — fulminate, to loudly denounce.

k.syn: botch, flounder, mess, stumble
k.en: to make awkward attempts to do or find something

ex.en: fumbled in his pocket for a coin
ex.cn: 从口袋里笨拙地摸出一枚硬币

k.syn: blunder, fault, gaffe, lapse, misstep, mistake
k.en: an unintentional departure from truth or accuracy

ex.en: She played the entire piano piece without a single fumble.
ex.cn: 她完美地演绎了整首钢琴曲。

m: FUMBLE rhymes with TUMBLE — your hands TUMBLE the ball when you FUMBLE. — fumble, to handle clumsily.

k.en: a plural of fungus

m: FUNGI = plural of FUNGUS — mushrooms and molds. — fungi, fungus organisms (plural).

k.en: an enclosed structure

m: FURNACE rhymes with FURNISH but it's hot — Latin FORNAX (oven). — furnace, a heating chamber.

k.syn: delirium, frenzy, fever, fury, hysteria, outrage, rage, uproar, wrath
k.ant: serenity, tranquility 平静，宁静；delight, pleasure 愉悦
k.en: a general commotion; public disorder or uproar; a state of intense excitement or ecstasy

ex.en: The decision to raise tax has caused a great furor among the working class.
ex.cn: 提高税收的决定引起了工人阶级的强烈骚动。

m: FUROR contains FUR ≈ FURY — furious uproar. — furor, an uproar / frenzy.

k.syn: secret, clandestine, covert, stealthy, surreptitious
k.ant: aboveboard 光明正大的；candid, forthright 坦率的
k.en: done by stealth

ex.en: a furtive glance at her
ex.cn: 朝她投去的隐秘一瞥

m: FURTIVE rhymes with FUGITIVE — both move secretively. — furtive, secretive / sneaky.

k.syn: anger, indignation, ire, mad, rage, wrath
k.ant: forbearance 容忍；delight, pleasure 快乐，愉悦
k.der: furious adj. 狂暴的，暴怒的 If you would go up high, then use your own legs! Do not let yourselves carried aloft; do not seat yourselves on other people's backs and heads. 如果你想要走到高处，就要使用自己的两条腿！不要让别人把你抬到高处；不要坐在别人的背上和头上。 ——德国哲学家　尼采（F. W. Nietzsche, German philosopher） “其中可以将GRE这样看，它所考查的不仅是英文和逻辑，更是恒心和毅力。所以如果你相信自己的能力，不妨将GRE看作最好的证明方式！” （温韬，Verbal 780，Quantitative 800，AW 4.5）
k.en: intense, disordered, and often destructive rage

ex.en: The gods unleashed their fury on the offending mortal.
ex.cn: 诸神将怒火降在了那些对神明不敬的凡人身上。

m: FURY contains FUR — like a wild beast's FUR bristling in rage. — fury, intense rage.

k.syn: careful, exact, meticulous, punctilious
k.ant: careless 粗心大意的
k.en: taking, showing, or involving great care and effort

ex.en: a fussy actuarial problem
ex.cn: 需要谨慎处理的保险计算问题

k.syn: bedizened, florid
k.ant: austere, plain, stark 朴素的，朴实无华的
k.en: elaborately and often excessively decorated

ex.en: The room, with its rococo furniture and its overabundance of knickknacks, is just too fussy for my taste.
ex.cn: 这房间里洛可可式的家具和过量的小饰品，让我觉得装修得有点过分以至于无法接受。

k.syn: choosy, delicate, demanding, exacting, fastidious, nice, particular, persnickety, picky
k.ant: undemanding, unfussy 不挑剔的
k.en: hard to please

ex.en: It is widely known that cats are fussy eaters.
ex.cn: 众所周知猫对食物很挑剔。

m: FUSSY contains FUSS — making a FUSS over every detail. — fussy, picky.

k.syn: antiquated, archaic, bygone, moldy, outdated
k.en: rigidly old-fashioned or reactionary

ex.en: fusty old carpets
ex.cn: 过时的旧地毯

k.syn: malodorous, fetid, musty, noisome, smelly, stale
k.ant: ambrosial, aromatic, fragrant, perfumed, redolent, savory, scented, sweet 有香味的，芳香的
k.en: saturated with dust and stale odors

ex.en: the fusty odor of a damp cellar
ex.cn: 潮湿的地下室里的腐臭味道

m: FUSTY rhymes with MUSTY — both mean stale and old. — fusty, old-fashioned / stale-smelling.

k.syn: abortive, bootless, fruitless, ineffective, useless, vain
k.ant: effectual, efficacious 有效的
k.en: serving no useful purpose; completely ineffective

ex.en: It would be an undoubtedly futile effort to persuade him.
ex.cn: 想说服他毫无疑问是徒劳的。

k.syn: flighty, frivolous, frothy
k.ant: earnest, serious 严肃认真的
k.en: lacking in seriousness or maturity

ex.en: the futile chatter of gossip columnists about the comings and goings of Hollywood celebrities
ex.cn: 八卦的专栏作家们对于好莱坞名人来来往往的随意闲谈

m: FUTILE rhymes with USELESS in feel; contains FUT ≈ "FU-tile" — your tile won't fit, all your effort is wasted. (Or: rhymes with UTILE — but with FU-, the OPPOSITE of useful.) — futile, useless.

k.syn: goad, impetus, impulse, incentive, irritant, spur, stimulus
k.ant: balm 安抚，慰藉
k.en: one that acts as a provocative stimulus

k.syn: annoyer, bother, persecutor, teaser, pest
k.en: a person who stimulates or annoys especially by persistent criticism

ex.en: a tactless gadfly during post-game interviews with the losing team
ex.cn: 在赛后采访败北方时一个令人讨厌的不懂人情世故的人

m: GADFLY = a biting fly that GADs (wanders) bothering livestock — figuratively, a person who pesters with criticism. — gadfly, an irritating critic.

k.en: a small

m: GADGET rhymes with WIDGET — both small mechanical things. — gadget, a small device.

k.syn: impropriety, indecorum, indiscretion
k.ant: decency, decorum, propriety 举止得体
k.en: a social or diplomatic blunder

ex.en: A gaffe is when a politician tells the truth. —— Michael Kinsley
ex.cn: 所谓出丑，就是政治家说真话的时候。（迈克·金斯利）

k.syn: blunder, lapse, misstep, oversight
k.en: a blatant mistake or misjudgment

ex.en: so-called debates, which were mainly about seeing which candidate made the most gaffes
ex.cn: 所谓的辩论，其实主要就是看哪个选手犯了最多的错误

m: GAFFE rhymes with LAUGH — a social GAFFE makes everyone awkwardly LAUGH. — gaffe, a social blunder.

k.syn: deny, contradict, contravene, naysay
k.ant: acknowledge, admit, avow, concede 承认；affirm 证实
k.en: to declare false

k.syn: disagree, refute, reject, repudiate
k.ant: concur 同意
k.en: to oppose, especially by contradiction

ex.en: No one dare to gainsay him.
ex.cn: 没人敢反驳他。

m: GAINSAY = GAIN + SAY — to "say against" (old English "gain-" = against). — gainsay, to deny / contradict.

k.en: a manner of walking

m: GAIT rhymes with GATE — through the GATE, you can see his GAIT (way of walking). — gait, manner of walking.

k.en: any of the very large groups of stars

m: GALAXY contains GALA — a GALA spread of stars. — galaxy, a star system.

k.syn: aggravate, exasperate, grate, inflame, provoke, pique, roil
k.ant: appease, assuage, calm, lull, pacify, placate 使平静，使平息
k.en: irritate, vex

ex.en: The sarcastic applause from the audience galled her.
ex.cn: 观众反讽的掌声激怒了她。

k.syn: animosity, animus, antagonism, antipathy, hostility, rancor
k.ant: amity 友好
k.en: a deep-seated ill will

ex.en: Her kindly feelings turned to gall when she found out her nephew only wanted her money.
ex.cn: 当她发现她的侄子只想要钱时，她之前友好的感情变成了憎恨与厌恶。

k.syn: audacity, brass, nerve, presumptuousness, temerity
k.en: shameless boldness

ex.en: I can't believe he had the gall to ask me how much I weigh.
ex.cn: 我不敢相信他居然胆敢问我有多重。

m: GALL rhymes with WALL — to GALL someone is to hit them like a brick WALL of audacity. (Also GALL = bile, hence "galling" = bitter.) — gall, audacity / bitter resentment.

k.syn: bold, courageous, dauntless, heroic, stouthearted, valorous
k.ant: craven, pusillanimous 胆小的
k.en: brave, spirited; nobly chivalrous and often self- sacrificing

ex.en: Gallant paratroopers jumped out of the plane without hesitation.
ex.cn: 英勇的伞兵们毫不犹豫地跳出了飞机。

k.syn: chivalrous, elevated, loft, magnanimous, sublime
k.ant: base, debased, degenerate, ignoble 卑鄙的，堕落的
k.en: having, characterized by, or arising from a dignified and generous nature

ex.en: The members of that service club are known for their gallant service to the community.
ex.cn: 那个服务社的成员因为他们对社区的慷慨贡献而为人所知。

m: GALLANT contains GAL — a knight who treats every GAL with chivalry. — gallant, brave and chivalrous.

k.en: the kitchen of an airliner, ship, or camper

m: GALLEY rhymes with ALLEY — a narrow ALLEY-like ship's kitchen. — galley, a ship's kitchen.

k.syn: provoke, agitate, excite, intoxicate, motivate, stimulate, pump up
k.ant: allay, lull, pacify 使平静
k.en: to stimulate or excite as if by an electric shock

ex.en: an issue that would galvanize public opinion
ex.cn: 激起大众评论的问题

m: GALVANIZE comes from GALVANI (the scientist who shocked frog legs) — to shock into action. — galvanize, to spur into action.

k.syn: bet, adventure, chance, risk, stake, venture
k.en: to bet on an uncertain outcome, as of a contest

ex.en: gamble on the train being late
ex.cn: 赌火车晚点

k.syn: compromise, hazard, imperil, jeopardize, threaten
k.en: to place in danger

ex.en: You don't want to gamble with your life, so buckle up.
ex.cn: 你不会想拿命来开玩笑的，所以系好安全带。

m: GAMBLE rhymes with TUMBLE — dice TUMBLE when you GAMBLE. — gamble, to bet riskily.

k.syn: frolic, caper, cavort
k.ant: plod, trudge 沉重缓慢地走
k.en: to leap about playfully

ex.en: young lambs gamboling in the meadow
ex.cn: 小羊羔在原野里欢快地跳跃

m: GAMBOL contains GAMB ≈ "gambo" (leg) — leg-leaping playfully. — gambol, to frolic.

k.syn: gangling, lanky, rangy, spindling, spindly
k.ant: stalwart 壮实的
k.en: awkwardly tall or long-limbed

ex.en: The suspect is a gangly high school boy.
ex.cn: 嫌犯是一个瘦高的中学生。

m: GANGLY contains GANG + LY — long-limbed like a GANG of giraffes. — gangly, awkwardly tall and thin.

k.en: an opening in the bulwark of a ship through which passengers

m: GANGWAY = GANG + WAY — the WAY a GANG of sailors boards the ship. — gangway, a ship's boarding plank.

k.syn: misrepresent, belie, color, distort, falsify, twist, warp
k.ant: clarify, elucidate 阐明
k.cn: 曲解，篡改，混淆（以至使无法理解）
k.en: to mix up or distort to such an extent as to make misleading or incomprehensible

ex.en: The summary totally garbles the results of the investigation.
ex.cn: 这份摘要完全曲解了调查结果。

k.syn: clear, distill, filter, purify
k.ant: adulterate, contaminate 掺杂；污染
k.en: to remove usually visible impurities from

ex.en: Garbled spices are less likely to contaminate a recipe.
ex.cn: 经过筛选的香料应该就不会影响食谱了。

m: GARBLE rhymes with GARGLE — speech sounds like you're GARGLING when GARBLEd. — garble, to distort / confuse.

k.syn: huge, astronomical, colossal, elephantine, enormous, gigantic, immense, mammoth, monstrous, titanic
k.ant: infinitesimal, minuscule 微小的
k.en: tremendous in size, volume, or degree

ex.en: a gargantuan waterfall in the rainforest
ex.cn: 热带雨林中的巨大瀑布

m: GARGANTUA was Rabelais's giant — anything GARGANTUAN is giant-sized. — gargantuan, enormous.

k.syn: gaudy, blatant, brazen, flamboyant, glaring, ostentatious
k.ant: dim, gloomy, murky, somber 黯淡的；conservative, quiet 不张扬的
k.cn: 过于鲜艳的，过于张扬的
k.en: marked by strident color or excessive ornamentation

ex.en: With garish makeup on, she looks exceedingly frivolous.
ex.cn: 浓妆艳抹之下的她显得格外轻佻。

m: GARISH rhymes with PARISH but the opposite of subtle — too LOUD and gaudy. — garish, excessively bright / showy.

k.syn: apparel, attire, costume, dress, suit
k.en: an article of clothing

ex.en: pack all garments
ex.cn: 给所有衣服打包

m: GARMENT contains GARM ≈ "garment-warming" — clothing that warms you. (Old French GARNIR = to equip.) — garment, an article of clothing.

k.syn: talkative, chatty, loquacious, verbose, voluble
k.ant: laconic, reserved, reticent, taciturn 缄默的
k.cn: 啰嗦的，话多得令人厌烦的
k.en: given to excessive and often trivial or rambling talk; tiresomely talkative

ex.en: garrulous traveling companions
ex.cn: 多话的旅伴

m: GARRULOUS rhymes with QUERULOUS — but garrulous is talkative, not complaining. Mnemonic: "GARRULOUS guys" can't stop yapping. — garrulous, excessively talkative.

k.syn: incision, laceration, rent, rip, tear
k.en: a long deep cut

ex.en: He got a gash in his knee that required for stitches.
ex.cn: 他的膝盖伤得很深，需要缝针。

k.syn: cut, incise, pierce, slash, slice, slit
k.ant: sew 缝合
k.en: to make a gash in

ex.en: Her face had been gashed by the rocks as she tumbled down the embankment.
ex.cn: 当她从堤岸上摔下来时脸上被刮了一道口。

m: GASH rhymes with SLASH — a SLASH leaves a deep GASH. — gash, a deep cut.

k.syn: evaporation, sublimation
k.ant: solidification 固化；liquefaction 液化
k.en: conversion into gas

ex.en: gasification of coals
ex.cn: 煤的气化

m: GASIFICATION contains GAS — turning solids/liquids INTO GAS. — gasification, conversion into gas.

k.syn: awkward, clumsy, crude, inept, maladroit, rustic, tactless
k.ant: graceful 优雅的；polished, refined, urbane 有教养的
k.cn: 笨拙的，缺乏社交经验的
k.en: lacking social experience or grace

ex.en: It would be gauche to mention the subject.
ex.cn: 提这个话题是很无礼的。

m: GAUCHE = French for "left" (the left hand was considered awkward) — clumsy in social settings. — gauche, socially awkward.

k.syn: blatant, brazen, flashy, garish, glaring, meretricious, tawdry, flamboyant
k.ant: austere, homely, plain 朴素的；conservative, understated, unflamboyant, unflashy 不张扬的
k.en: ostentatiously or tastelessly ornamented, excessively showy

ex.en: gaudy movie posters
ex.cn: 俗丽的电影海报

m: GAUDY rhymes with BAWDY — both excessive: bawdy in talk, gaudy in display. — gaudy, ostentatiously showy.

k.syn: standard, benchmark, criterion, measure, touchstone, yardstick
k.en: a measurement（as of linear dimension） according to some standard or system

ex.en: polls as a gauge of voter satisfaction
ex.cn: 用以衡量选民满意度的投票选举

k.syn: assess, determine, evaluate, figure, measure, scale
k.en: to determine the capacity or contents of

ex.en: It is hard to gauge his mood.
ex.cn: 要判断他的情绪很困难。

m: GAUGE rhymes with PAGE — both flat surfaces for reading; a GAUGE is a measurement-PAGE. — gauge, to measure / a measuring device.

k.syn: adapt, adjust, fit, suit, tailor
k.en: to adjust or adapt so as to make suitable

ex.en: gear the speech towards a conservative audience
ex.cn: 调整演讲以适应保守的观众

m: to GEAR something = to put it in the right GEAR for the situation. — gear, to adjust / equip.

k.syn: affable, agreeable, gracious, mellow, nice, pleasant, sweet
k.ant: caustic, mordant 尖酸刻薄的；truculent 凶残的
k.en: having an easygoing and pleasing manner especially in social situations

ex.en: a genial host who makes a point of speaking personally to each and every guest
ex.cn: 一个重视与每个客人私下交流的亲切的主人

k.syn: amicable, companionable, cordial, warmhearted
k.ant: antagonistic, hostile 有敌意的
k.en: having or showing kindly feeling and sincere interest

k.syn: balmy, equable, gentle, mild, moderate, soft, temperate
k.ant: harsh, inclement, intemperate, severe 严寒的
k.en: marked by temperatures that are neither too high nor too low

ex.en: genial sunshine in winter
ex.cn: 冬日的暖阳

m: GENIAL contains GEN- (kind, as in GENEROUS) — kindly / warm-hearted. — genial, friendly / warm.

k.syn: courteous, decent, decorous, mannerly, polite, polished, respectable, urbane, couth, cultured, refined
k.ant: churlish 粗暴的；loutish 蠢笨的
k.en: free from vulgarity or rudeness

ex.en: Her genteel behaviors at the ball make others conjecture that she must come from a distinguished noble family.
ex.cn: 舞会上她极有教养的举止让他人纷纷猜测她一定来自名门望族。

m: GENTEEL contains GENTLE — gentle and refined manners. — genteel, refined / polite.

k.syn: applicable, apropos, apposite, pertinent, relevant
k.ant: extraneous, irrelevant 无关的；inappropriate 不适当的
k.en: being at once relevant and appropriate

ex.en: details not germane to the discussion
ex.cn: 与讨论无关的细节

m: GERMANE rhymes with GERMAN-e — but actually GERM + ANE: shares GERM- with "germ of an idea" — relevant, related to the core idea. — germane, relevant.

k.en: a natural hot spring that intermittently ejects a column of water

m: GEYSER rhymes with GUYS-ER — picture GUYS jumping back as a GEYSER erupts. — geyser, a hot-water spring eruption.

k.syn: deride, jeer, ridicule, mock, scoff, sneer, taunt
k.ant: respect, revere, venerate 尊敬
k.en: to deride or tease with taunting words

ex.en: gibe at the umpire
ex.cn: 嘲弄裁判员

m: GIBE rhymes with TRIBE — outsiders GIBE at the TRIBE's customs. — gibe, to mock.

k.syn: flighty, frivolous, frothy
k.ant: grave, serious 严肃的；earnest 认真的
k.en: lacking in seriousness or maturity

ex.en: teach a bunch of giddy Girl Scouts how to make a fire
ex.cn: 教一群漫不经心的女童子军如何生火

k.syn: elated, elevated, euphoric, exhilarated, exultant, intoxicated, rapturous
k.ant: depressed, melancholy 忧郁的
k.en: joyfully elated

ex.en: He's clearly giddy at the news that his ailing grandfather will be fine.
ex.cn: 听到他祖父的病将无大碍，他的喜悦之情溢于言表。

m: GIDDY rhymes with KIDDIE — like an excited KID, lightheaded with joy. — giddy, lightheaded / silly with excitement.

k.syn: polish, refine, smooth
k.en: to give an often deceptively attractive or improved appearance to

ex.en: Any further retouch would be gilding the lily.
ex.cn: 任何形式的润色都会是画蛇添足。

m: GILD rhymes with GUILD — but it means coat with thin gold. "To GILD the lily" = over-decorate. — gild, to coat with gold / over-embellish.

k.syn: core, essence, kernel, pivot, quintessence, substance
k.ant: divergence 偏离（主旨）
k.en: the main point or part

ex.en: the gist of the argument
ex.cn: 论证的要点

m: GIST rhymes with FIST — grab the GIST in a FIST of meaning. — gist, the main point.

k.en: a large body of ice

m: GLACIER contains GLAC (ice, Latin) — a slow river of ice. — glacier, a mass of moving ice.

k.en: an open space

m: GLADE rhymes with SHADE — a GLADE is a sunny opening in a SHADY forest. — glade, an open space in a forest.

k.en: a person engaged in a fight to the death as public entertainment for ancient Romans

ex.en: He comes to Rome as a gladiator to seek revenge.
ex.cn: 他化成角斗士来到罗马寻求复仇。

k.syn: boxer, fighter, belligerent, combatant
k.en: a person engaging in a public fight or controversy

m: GLADIATOR contains GLAD ≈ Latin GLADIUS (sword) — a swordsman who fights to the death. — gladiator, a fighter / debater.

k.syn: adorn, bedeck, decorate, embellish, garnish
k.ant: strip, uncover 剥去
k.en: to coat with or as if with a glaze

ex.en: The storm glazed trees with ice.
ex.cn: 暴风雪给树木镀上了一层冰装。

k.syn: polish, burnish, furbish, shine
k.ant: rumple 弄皱
k.en: to give a smooth glossy surface to

m: GLAZE rhymes with BLAZE — a glossy BLAZE of finish on pottery. — glaze, to coat with a glossy finish.

k.syn: nonchalant, oily
k.ant: taciturn 沉默寡言的；awkward 笨拙的
k.cn: 流利圆滑的（常含有不真诚或欺诈的成分），油腔滑调的
k.en: marked by ease and fluency in speaking or writing often to the point of being insincere or deceitful

ex.en: a glib politician
ex.cn: 油嘴滑舌的政客

k.syn: cursory, shallow, superficial
k.ant: abstruse, deep, profound 深奥的，深刻的
k.en: lacking depth and substance

ex.en: glib solutions to the knotty problem
ex.cn: 对复杂难解问题的肤浅的解决方案

m: GLIB rhymes with FIB — smooth talkers full of FIBs. — glib, smoothly insincere.

k.syn: flash, scintillate, sparkle, twinkle, winkle
k.ant: dim 变得黯淡
k.en: to shine by reflection with a sparkling luster

ex.en: The calm sea glistened in the sunlight.
ex.cn: 日光下平静的海面波光粼粼。

m: GLISTEN contains LISTEN — but with light: "watch and LISTEN to the surface SHIMMER." Better: GLI- (gleam-like sound). — glisten, to sparkle wetly.

k.syn: bug, defect, fault, lapse, imperfection, peccadillo
k.ant: fatal error 致命错误
k.en: a minor malfunction, mishap, or technical problem

ex.en: postponement due to a glitch in a spacecraft's fuel cell
ex.cn: 宇宙飞船燃料电池故障引起的推迟发射

m: GLITCH rhymes with HITCH — a small HITCH in the system. — glitch, a small malfunction.

k.syn: crow, relish, triumph
k.ant: mourn 哀悼
k.en: a feeling of great, often malicious, pleasure or self-satisfaction

ex.en: gloat over his enemy's misfortune
ex.cn: 为敌人的不幸而幸灾乐祸

m: GLOAT rhymes with BOAT — sail your BOAT past the loser, gloating. — gloat, to take smug pleasure.

k.syn: black, dark, dim, dimmed, murky, obscure, stygian, pitch-dark
k.ant: bright, luminous, lucent, lucid 明亮的
k.en: being without light or without much light

k.syn: sullen, dejected, dour, melancholy, morose, saturnine, surly
k.ant: frothy 欢乐轻挑的；buoyant, cheerful, jubilant 高兴的
k.en: low in spirits

ex.en: feel gloomy about future career
ex.cn: 对未来的职业生涯倍感忧心

m: GLOOMY contains GLOOM — full of GLOOM (darkness/sadness). — gloomy, dark / sad.

k.syn: abstract, annotation, brief, epitome, synopsis
k.ant: amplification 详解
k.en: a brief explanation of a difficult or obscure word or expression

k.syn: façade, mask, veneer
k.en: a deceptively attractive external appearance

ex.en: She used a computer to give her astrological predictions the gloss of real science.
ex.cn: 她用计算机来给她的占星预测结果套上科学的外衣。

k.syn: disregard, ignore, neglect, overlook
k.ant: scrutinize 仔细研究
k.cn: 敷衍潦草做事（甚至忽略）
k.en: to deal with（a subject or problem）too lightly or not at all

ex.en: gloss over the problems
ex.cn: 不怎么理会这些问题

k.syn: excuse, extenuate
k.en: to make（something）seem less bad by offering excuses

ex.en: I don't want to gloss over her misbehavior, but keep in mind that she's been under a lot of stress lately.
ex.cn: 我不想为她的行为辩护，但是别忘了她最

m: GLOSS over something = give it only a SMOOTH surface treatment, ignore the depth. (Same word: a glossy sheen.) — gloss, surface shine / superficial treatment.

k.en: a list of often difficult or specialized words with their definitions

m: GLOSSARY contains GLOSS — a GLOSS-ary gives brief glosses of terms. — glossary, a word-list with definitions.

k.syn: lustrous, burnished, glistening, polished, shining
k.ant: coarse 粗糙的；dull, dim 无光泽的
k.en: having a smooth, shiny, lustrous surface

ex.en: glossy surface of the floor
ex.cn: 平整光亮的地板

m: GLOSSY contains GLOSS — having a smooth shiny GLOSS. — glossy, smoothly shiny.

k.syn: cloy, cram, fill, satiate, surfeit, oversupply, sate
k.ant: lack 缺乏
k.en: to fill beyond capacity, especially with food

ex.en: glut himself with Sushi
ex.cn: 吃寿司吃撑了

m: GLUT rhymes with GUT — your GUT is overfilled when there's a GLUT. — glut, an oversupply.

k.syn: adherent, adhesive, cloggy, gluey, sticky, tenacious, viscid
k.ant: fluid 流体的
k.en: of the nature of or resembling glue

ex.en: glutinous liquid
ex.cn: 胶状液体

m: GLUTINOUS contains GLUE — glue-like and sticky. — glutinous, sticky.

k.syn: gorger, gourmand
k.en: a person who eats or consumes immoderate amounts of food and drink

ex.en: a glutton for work
ex.cn: 工作狂

m: GLUTTON rhymes with MUTTON — picture a GLUTTON devouring a whole MUTTON. — glutton, an excessive eater.

k.syn: urge, abet, exhort, instigate, prod, prompt, propel, spur, stimulate
k.ant: check, curb 阻止；lull 使安静
k.en: to incite or rouse as if with a goad

ex.en: goad someone to do something
ex.cn: 激励某人做某事

m: GOAD rhymes with PROD — both sharp pokes that drive someone to act. — goad, to provoke into action.

k.syn: devour, gorge, guzzle, quaff, raven, swill
k.ant: nibble 小口咬
k.en: to swallow or eat greedily

ex.en: Lions gobble their prey.
ex.cn: 狮子狼吞虎咽地啃食它们的猎物。

m: GOBBLE — onomatopoeic, the SOUND of a turkey GOBBLING food fast. — gobble, to eat fast.

k.en: protective

m: GOGGLES — what protect eyes that GOGGLE (stare) at danger. — goggles, eye protection.

k.syn: idle, parry, shirk, sidestep
k.ant: dedicate, devote 致力于
k.en: to shirk one's assigned duties or responsibilities

ex.en: goldbrick his duty as a citizen
ex.cn: 逃避他作为公民的义务

m: GOLDBRICK = a fake brick of gold (a swindle); to goldbrick = pretend to work but actually loaf. — goldbrick, to shirk work.

k.syn: gap, gulch, notch, ravine
k.en: a narrow steep-walled canyon or part of a canyon

ex.en: Wenchuan earthquake is said to have no detrimental effect on Three- Gorge dam.
ex.cn: 汶川地震据称对三峡大坝没有破坏性影响。

k.syn: cram, devour, gobble, guzzle, quaff, sate, swill
k.ant: nibble 小口咬
k.en: to eat greedily or to repletion

ex.en: He gorged himself at the party.
ex.cn: 他在聚会上狼吞虎咽。

m: GORGE rhymes with FORGE — a deep cut in the earth (a GORGE) made as if forged. (Also: to GORGE = stuff your GORGE = throat.) — gorge, a deep canyon / to stuff oneself.

k.syn: ether, delicacy
k.ant: substance, entity 实体
k.en: something light, delicate, or insubstantial

ex.en: the gossamer of youth's dreams
ex.cn: 年少时缥缈的梦想

k.syn: diaphanous, ethereal, filmy, light, insubstantial, tenuous
k.ant: heavy, leaden, ponderous 沉重的
k.en: extremely light, delicate, or tenuous

ex.en: a gossamer explanation
ex.cn: 站不住脚的解释

m: GOSSAMER rhymes with SUMMER — gossamer cobwebs floating in SUMMER air. — gossamer, light / delicate.

k.syn: gorger, glutton
k.en: one who is excessively fond of eating and drinking

ex.en: the kind of gourmand who swallows food without even pausing to taste it
ex.cn: 不尝味道就下咽的那种嗜食者

m: GOURMAND contains GOUR ≈ "gourmet" but bigger — eats LARGE quantities. — gourmand, a hearty eater / glutton.

k.syn: bon vivant, connoisseur, epicure
k.ant: layman 门外汉
k.en: a connoisseur of food and drink

ex.en: a gourmet of Chinese food
ex.cn: 中餐美食家
