m: GOURMET contains GOURM ≈ refined GOURM- — refined small portions of fine food. — gourmet, a refined connoisseur of food.

k.syn: magnificence, augustness, brilliance, glory, majesty, nobility, resplendence, splendor
k.ant: frivolousness 无关紧要
k.en: the quality or condition of being grand; magnificence

ex.en: the glory that was Greece and the grandeur that was Rome
ex.cn: 希腊的荣耀和罗马的辉煌

m: GRANDEUR contains GRAND — magnificent grandness. — grandeur, splendor.

k.syn: rhetorical, bombastic, inflated, magniloquent, pretentious
k.ant: secretive 低调的，隐秘的；simple 简单的
k.en: a lofty, extravagantly colorful, pompous, or bombastic style, manner, or quality especially in language

ex.en: feel disgusted with his grandiloquent speech
ex.cn: 对他浮夸的演讲表示厌恶

m: GRANDILOQUENT = GRAND + ELOQUENT — pompously grand speech. — grandiloquent, pompously elaborate.

k.syn: extravagant, flamboyant, pompous, pretentious
k.ant: humble 谦逊的
k.en: characterized by feigned or affected grandeur

ex.en: grandiose words
ex.cn: 自命不凡的话语

k.syn: august, glorious, grand, imposing, magnificent, monumental, splendid
k.ant: trivial 微不足道的；humble, unimposing, unimpressive 平凡的
k.en: characterized by greatness of scope or intent

ex.en: a grandiose hydroelectric project
ex.cn: 宏伟的水电工程

m: GRANDIOSE contains GRAND — pretentiously grand. — grandiose, ostentatiously grand.

k.syn: act, perform, pretend
k.cn: 为了给人留下印象而表演，哗众取宠
k.en: to play or act so as to impress onlookers

ex.en: grandstand on the stage
ex.cn: 在舞台上哗众取宠

m: GRANDSTAND = the GRAND STAND in a stadium — performing for the crowd. — grandstand, to show off.

k.syn: abrade, rasp, scratch, scrape
k.cn: 刮擦（以发出刺耳的声音）
k.en: to make a rasping sound

ex.en: The sled grated along the bare pavement.
ex.cn: 雪橇与裸露的路面刮擦发出刺耳声。

k.syn: irritate, aggravate, gall, nettle, peeve, plague, provoke, vex
k.ant: soothe 使平静，安抚
k.en: to irritate or annoy persistently

ex.en: a noise that grates on one's nerves
ex.cn: 使人烦躁的噪音

m: GRATE rhymes with HATE — a GRATING noise you HATE. (Also: a GRATE = a metal grid; to grate cheese.) — grate, to scrape harshly / annoy.

k.syn: appease, cater, content, satisfy
k.ant: displease 使不满；grieve 使悲痛
k.en: to give what is desired to, to please or satisfy

ex.en: gratify her curiosity
ex.cn: 满足她的好奇心

ex.en: Her praise will gratify all who worked so hard to earn it.
ex.cn: 她的表扬会使所有辛勤工作的人们高兴。

m: GRATIFY contains GRATI- (pleasing, as in GRATITUDE) — make pleased. — gratify, to please.

k.syn: unfounded, unjustified, unreasonable, baseless, groundless
k.ant: justified, warranted 有理有据的
k.en: unnecessary or unwarranted

ex.en: a dubious request based on a gratuitous assumption
ex.cn: 基于无根据假设的可疑要求

k.syn: free, complimentary, unearned, voluntary
k.ant: merited 应得的
k.en: given or granted without return or recompense

ex.en: a gratuitous ticket
ex.cn: 免费的门票

k.syn: dispensable, inessential, needless, redundant, superfluous, surplus
k.ant: critical, essential, pivotal, vital 关键的
k.en: not needed by the circumstances or to accomplish an end

m: GRATUITOUS contains GRATUI ≈ "gratuity" (free tip) — given unnecessarily, also unwarranted. — gratuitous, uncalled-for / free.

k.en: an unconsolidated mixture of rock fragments or pebbles

m: GRAVEL rhymes with TRAVEL — small stones you TRAVEL across. — gravel, small stones.

k.syn: inexperienced, callow, fresh, raw, unfledged, young
k.ant: experienced, versed 经验丰富的
k.en: deficient in training, knowledge, or experience

ex.en: lead a squad of green recruits
ex.cn: 带领一队没有经验的新兵

m: GREEN — like a GREEN/unripe fruit, not yet seasoned. — green, inexperienced.

k.syn: convivial, extroverted, genial, outgoing, sociable, outgoing
k.ant: aloof 疏远的；antisocial 不合群的；introverted, reclusive 内向的
k.en: likely to seek or enjoy the company of others

k.syn: social
k.ant: solitary 独自的
k.en: tending to group with others of the same kind

ex.en: ecologically define human as gregarious carnivore
ex.cn: 从生态学上将人类定义为群居类肉食动物

m: GREGARIOUS contains GREG- (Latin GREX = flock) — a flocker, sociable. — gregarious, sociable.

k.syn: agonize, anguish, suffer, bemoan, bewail, deplore
k.ant: delight, exult in, glory in, joy, rejoice in 感到高兴
k.en: to（cause to）feel deep sadness or mental pain

ex.en: We all grieved over the lost cat.
ex.cn: 我们都为那只丢失的猫感到伤心。

m: GRIEVE contains GRIEF — feeling GRIEF. — grieve, to feel deep sorrow.

k.syn: frown, moue, pout, scowl
k.cn: /n. 因痛苦而面部扭曲，（作）怪相，（作）鬼脸
k.en: a sharp contortion of the face expressive of pain, disgust or disapproval

ex.en: She made a grimace when she tasted the medicine.
ex.cn: 她尝了一口药，然后做了一个鬼脸。

m: GRIMACE contains GRIM + ACE — a GRIM-faced ACE of pain. — grimace, a pained facial twist.

k.syn: beam
k.ant: pout 噘嘴生气
k.en: to express an emotion（as amusement）by curving the lips upward

ex.en: The boss grinned his approval.
ex.cn: 老板咧嘴一笑表示赞许。

m: GRIN rhymes with WIN — winners GRIN. — grin, to smile broadly.

k.syn: carp, fuss, grouch, grouse, grumble, wail
k.ant: crow, delight, rejoice 欢快，喜悦
k.en: to express dissatisfaction, pain, or resentment usually tiresomely

ex.en: All workers were griping about the new regulations.
ex.cn: 所有的工人都在抱怨新规章。

k.syn: aggravate, annoy, bother, chafe, exasperate, nettle, peeve, pique, rile, ruffle, vex
k.cn: （通过连续让人讨厌的举动）激怒，惹怒
k.en: to disturb the peace of mind of（someone）especially by repeated disagreeable acts

ex.en: Constant complaints from the customers griped her to the point where she started snapping back.
ex.cn: 顾客持续的抱怨激怒了她，让她也到了爆点，想要反驳回去。

m: GRIPE rhymes with WIPE / TRIPE — both unpleasant. To GRIPE = wipe-complain endlessly. — gripe, to complain.

k.syn: appalling, dreadful, ghastly, gruesome, hideous, horrifying, macabre
k.cn: 令人反感的，令人恐惧的
k.en: inspiring repugnance; gruesome

ex.en: a series grisly murders
ex.cn: 一系列令人恐惧的谋杀

m: GRISLY rhymes with GRIZZLY — a GRIZZLY bear attack is GRISLY. — grisly, gruesome.

k.syn: adore, fancy, savor, relish, get off on, rejoice in, revel in, delight in
k.cn: 享受，极其满意，过得快活
k.en: to take pleasure in

ex.en: Thrill-seekers who groove on skiing will love snowboarding.
ex.cn: 那些乐在滑雪中的追求刺激的人们也会喜欢滑雪板的。

ex.en: just sitting around, grooving on the music
ex.cn: 随便坐坐，在音乐中享受一下

m: in the GROOVE — the smooth track that lets things run perfectly; "to be in the groove" = enjoying it. — groove, to enjoy / a fitting track.

k.syn: hideous, homely, ill-favored, monstrous, unappealing, unattractive, uncomely, unsightly
k.ant: aesthetic, attractive, beautiful, bonny, comely, fetching, gorgeous, knockout, ravishing, seemly, sightly, stunning, taking, well-favored 悦目的，吸引人的
k.en: unpleasant to look at

ex.en: That bloody Halloween mask is grotesque.
ex.cn: 那个血腥的万圣节面具丑死了。

m: GROTESQUE contains GROT ≈ GROTTO (cave) — paintings in old caves were strange and distorted. — grotesque, distorted / ugly.

k.en: a small cave or cavern; an artificial structure made to resemble a cave

m: GROTTO rhymes with MOTTO — a hidden small cave where one repeats their MOTTO. — grotto, a small cave.

k.en: a group of trees

m: GROVE rhymes with COVE — both small natural pockets; a grove is a small group of trees. — grove, a small wood.

k.syn: cringe, creep, slither, wriggle
k.der: groveler n. 卑躬屈膝者：the one who lies with the body prostrate in token of subservience or abasement
k.en: to draw back or crouch down in fearful submission

ex.en: He made a groveling apology to the girl.
ex.cn: 他放下身段，给那姑娘道歉。

m: GROVEL contains GROUND-vel — face on the GROUND, debasing yourself. — grovel, to debase oneself.

k.en: to undertake

m: GUARANTEE contains GUARD — to GUARD a promise. — guarantee, a formal assurance.

k.syn: artfulness, canniness, craft, cunningness, deviousness, slyness, subtleness, wiliness
k.ant: manipulative 操纵的
k.der: guileless adj. 朴实的：free of guile; artless
k.en: the inclination or practice of misleading others through lies or trickery

ex.en: A person so full of guile he can't even be trusted to give you the correct time of day.
ex.cn: 他这个人狡诈成性，说的话都不足为信。

m: GUILE rhymes with WILE — both mean cunning trickery. — guile, sly cunning.

k.syn: contrition, penitence, remorse, repentance, self-reproach, shame
k.ant: impenitence, remorselessness 无悔意
k.en: a feeling of responsibility for wrongdoing

ex.en: He was wracked with guilt after he accidentally broke his younger sister's antique grandfather clock.
ex.cn: 在不小心弄坏他妹妹的古董老爷钟后，他备受自责的折磨。

m: GUILT rhymes with GUILT-trip — that tripping feeling of remorse. — guilt, remorse for wrongdoing.

k.syn: dewy-eyed, exploitable, naive, susceptible, unwary, wide-eyed
k.en: easily duped or cheated, readily taken advantage of

ex.en: They sell overpriced souvenirs to gullible tourists.
ex.cn: 他们卖高价纪念品给易上当的旅客们。

m: GULLIBLE rhymes with SWALLOWABLE — they swallow any story. (Also: shares root with GULL = to deceive.) — gullible, easily fooled.

k.cn: （大水长期冲刷地面之后腐蚀形成的深的）沟壑
k.en: a deep ditch or channel cut

m: GULLY rhymes with FULLY — a GULLY is FULLY worn into the ground by water. — gully, a water-cut ravine.

k.en: a similar plant exudate

m: GUM rhymes with PLUM — both sticky-sap-like substances; tree GUM is sticky resin. — gum, tree sap / chewing gum.

k.syn: exodus, outpour, outpouring, spout
k.ant: dribble, drip, drop, trickle 涓涓细流，滴
k.en: to（cause to）flow forth suddenly in great volume

ex.en: water gushing from the hydrant
ex.cn: 水从消防栓中喷出

k.syn: drool, effuse, enthuse, fuss, rave, slobber
k.en: to make an exaggerated display of affection or enthusiasm

ex.en: an aunt gushing over the baby
ex.cn: 对着婴儿母爱泛滥的姑姑

m: GUSH rhymes with RUSH — water RUSHes out, gushing. — gush, to flow / express effusively.

k.syn: burst, ebullition, eruption, explosion, flush, gush
k.en: a sudden intense expression of strong feeling

ex.en: The stressed-out coworker cried out with a gust of emotion.
ex.cn: 压力巨大的同事开始嚎啕大哭。

m: a GUST of emotion — like a GUST of wind: sudden burst. — gust, a sudden burst.

k.syn: gulp, quaff, swig, booze, soak, tipple
k.en: to drink especially liquor greedily, continually, or habitually

ex.en: guzzle beer
ex.cn: 狂饮啤酒

m: GUZZLE rhymes with NUZZLE / GUTTER — chugging drink down the GUTter of your throat. — guzzle, to drink greedily.

k.en: suitable for living in; fit for habitation

m: HABITABLE contains HABIT + ABLE — able to be inHABIT-ed. — habitable, livable.

k.en: a writer who aims solely for commercial

m: a HACK writer HACKs out routine work like chopping wood — uninspired. (Also: HACK = chop / a taxi.) — hack, a routine writer-for-hire.

k.syn: banal, cliché, commonplace, hack, threadbare, trite, well-worn, stereotyped
k.ant: fresh, offbeat, original, novel 新鲜的
k.en: lacking in freshness or originality

ex.en: hackneyed slogans
ex.cn: 老掉牙的口号

m: HACKNEYED contains HACK — a HACKneyed phrase has been chopped and repeated to death. — hackneyed, overused / clichéd.

k.syn: hushed, peaceful, placid, serene, tranquil, untroubled
k.ant: tempestuous, stormy, agitated, inclement, restless, rough, turbulent, unquiet, unsettled 暴风雨的；暴躁的
k.en: free from storms or physical disturbance

k.syn: booming, flourishing, lush, palmy, prospering, roaring, thriving
k.ant: miserable, depressed, unprosperous 萧条的
k.en: prosperous, affluent, vigorous growth and well-being especially economically

ex.en: halcyon years
ex.cn: 丰年

m: HALCYON sounds like "hal-SEE-on" — picture a peaceful seabird sitting calm on calm seas (Greek myth: the halcyon bird calmed the waves). — halcyon, peaceful / prosperous.

k.syn: bouncing, fit, robust, sound, well-conditioned, wholesome
k.ant: effete, infirm, anemic, wan, decrepit, blighted 衰弱的，衰老的
k.en: free from infirmity or illness; sound

ex.en: a hale and hearty old lady
ex.cn: 硬朗矍铄的老奶奶

k.syn: drag, draw, haul, lug, tow, tug
k.ant: drive, propel, push 推
k.en: to cause to follow by applying steady force on

ex.en: The fishermen haled the huge net onto the deck of the ship.
ex.cn: 渔民们将巨大的渔网拖到渔船甲板上。

m: HALE rhymes with WHALE — strong as a WHALE. (Also "hale and hearty".) — hale, strong / healthy.

k.syn: ensign, impresa, logo, symbol, totem, trademark
k.ant: uncharacteristic feature 不典型的特征
k.en: a conspicuous feature or characteristic

ex.en: The sense of guilt is the hallmark of civilized humanity.
ex.cn: 内疚是人类文明的特征。

m: HALL + MARK — the official MARK stamped at silversmiths' HALLs to certify quality. — hallmark, a distinctive feature.

k.syn: consecrate, sacralize, sanctify
k.ant: desecrate, deconsecrate, desacralize, desanctify 亵渎
k.en: to respect or honor greatly; revere

m: HALLOW rhymes with HOLLOW but the opposite — to HALLOW = make HOLY (hallowed ground). (Halloween = All Hallows' Eve.) — hallow, to make holy.

k.syn: chimera, daydream, delusion, fancy, figment, illusion
k.ant: truth, verity
k.en: a false idea or belief

ex.en: Some people have the common hallucination that gluttony during the holiday season doesn't have consequences.
ex.cn: 一些人有种错觉，认为假期里暴饮暴食不会有什么结果。

m: HALLUCINATION contains HALLU ≈ "hallway-illusion" — seeing things that aren't there. — hallucination, a false perception.

k.en: to make repeated efforts especially: to reiterate an opinion or attitude

ex.en: The lectures all hammered away at the same points.
ex.cn: 整场讲座都是在阐述相同的论点。

ex.en: hammer the information into the students' heads
ex.cn: 反复向学生们强调这条信息

m: to HAMMER a point home — drive it in over and over with a HAMMER. — hammer, to repeat insistently.

k.syn: cramp, encumber, fetter, handicap, hinder, impede, stymie, trammel
k.ant: facilitate, aid, assist, help 促进
k.en: to restrict the movement of by bonds or obstacles: impede

ex.en: Construction is hampering traffic on the highway.
ex.cn: 高速路上的建设阻碍了正常交通。

m: HAMPER rhymes with TAMPER — both interfere; a HAMPER (basket) tangles and slows you. — hamper, to obstruct.

k.syn: cripple, immobilize, incapacitate, prostrate
k.en: to make ineffective or powerless

ex.en: The downtown development committee claims that it's hamstrung by city ordinances.
ex.cn: 市中心发展委员会声称它受到了城市法令的阻碍。

m: HAMSTRING — cutting the back-of-leg HAMSTRING tendon disables a runner. — hamstring, to cripple / disable.

k.syn: crestfallen, dejected, despondent, disconsolate, doleful, melancholy, sorrowful
k.ant: buoyant, elated, sprightly, blissful, delighted, joyous, jubilant, upbeat 热情高涨的
k.en: sad, dejected

ex.en: She came home with a hangdog expression on her face.
ex.cn: 她一脸沮丧地回到了家。

m: HANGDOG = HANG + DOG — head hung like a scolded DOG. — hangdog, dejected.

k.syn: appetite, craving, hunger, itch, longing, lust, passion, thirst, yearning
k.ant: odium, lack of desire 讨厌
k.en: to have a strong or persistent desire: yearn

ex.en: hanker for adventure
ex.cn: 渴望冒险

m: HANKER rhymes with ANCHOR but the opposite — your heart isn't ANCHORed, it HANKERS for something. — hanker, to long for.

k.syn: aimless, arbitrary, desultory, erratic, scattered, stray
k.ant: methodical, systematic, nonrandom, orderly, organized, regular 系统的，有序的
k.en: marked by lack of plan, order, or direction

ex.en: We were given a haphazard tour of the city.
ex.cn: 我们进行了一次城市漫游。

m: HAP + HAZARD — happening by HAZARD (chance). — haphazard, random / disorderly.

k.syn: diatribe, jeremiad, philippic, rant
k.ant: speak temperately 有节制地说话
k.en: a long pompous speech, especially one delivered before a gathering

ex.en: She harangued us for hours about the evils of popular culture.
ex.cn: 她向我们絮叨了好几小时，批判流行文化的堕落。

m: HARANGUE rhymes with MERINGUE but bitter — a long, beaten-up speech. — harangue, a long ranting speech.

k.en: to irritate or torment persistently

ex.en: The troops harrassed the defeated army throughout its retreat.
ex.cn: 战胜的队伍在撤退过程中还不忘突袭一下败北的队伍。

k.syn: drain, fag, fatigue, outwear, tire, tucker out, wear out, knock out, burn out
k.en: to use up all the physical energy of

ex.en: The president had been visibly harassed by the demands of the presidency.
ex.cn: 总统被事务缠身，筋疲力尽。

m: HARASS rhymes with EMBARRASS — both make you uncomfortable, but HARASS keeps coming. — harass, to pester repeatedly.

k.syn: foregoer, herald, outrider, precursor
k.en: one that presages or foreshadows what is to come

ex.en: The October air stung my cheeks, a harbinger of winter.
ex.cn: 十月的寒风凛冽地吹在脸上，这是冬天的预兆。

m: HARBINGER contains HARBOR — historically, a harbinger went ahead to find HARBOR/lodging for the king's party — i.e., a forerunner. — harbinger, an omen / forerunner.

k.syn: accommodate, bestow, board, camp, chamber, domicile, lodge, quarter, take in, put up
k.ant: evict 赶出
k.en: to provide a place, home, or habitat for

ex.en: harbor a fugitive
ex.cn: 隐匿逃亡者

k.syn: bear, cherish, entertain, hold, nurse
k.en: to keep in one's mind or heart

ex.en: He had long harbored a grudge against his old employer, who had fired him without cause.
ex.cn: 他对前老板一直耿耿于怀，因为他无缘无故炒了他。

m: HARBOR rhymes with ARBOR — both shelter; you HARBOR a fugitive (give shelter) or HARBOR a grudge (shelter it inside). — harbor, to shelter / hold inside.

k.syn: hardened, sturdy, tough, cast-iron, inured, rugged, stout, vigorous, hard-bitten
k.ant: delicate, nonhardy, soft, tender, weak 柔弱的
k.en: able to withstand hardship, strain, or exposure

ex.en: Chrysanthemums are hardy enough to survive a light frost.
ex.cn: 菊花能够经受霜打。

k.syn: adventurous, audacious, daring, dashing, emboldened, enterprising, gutsy, venturous
k.ant: unadventurous, unenterprising 没有冒险精神的
k.en: inclined or willing to take risks

ex.en: hardy souls who pioneered new paths into outer space
ex.cn: 那些愿意冒险探索外太空的先驱们

m: HARDY contains HARD — built HARD enough to endure. — hardy, durable.

k.syn: balanced, congruous, consonant, eurythmic, harmonic, accordant, coherent, compatible, concordant, conformable, congruent, consonant, correspondent, nonconflicting
k.ant: disharmonic, disharmonious, incongruous, unbalanced 不协调的； conflicting, incompatible, incongruous, inconsistent 冲突的
k.en: having the parts agreeably related

ex.en: a harmonious arrangement of archways and doorways in the palace courtyard
ex.cn: 宫殿的庭院、拱门和门廊排列有序协调

m: HARMONIOUS contains HARMONY — in harmony. — harmonious, in agreement.

k.syn: apply, employ, exercise, exploit, operate, utilize
k.ant: fail to utilize 没能够利用
k.en: utilize

ex.en: harness the sun's rays as a source of energy
ex.cn: 利用阳光作为一种能源

m: to HARNESS a horse = to PUT IT TO USE pulling the cart. — harness, to put to use.

k.en: a plucked stringed instrument

m: to HARP on something = pluck the same string of the HARP over and over. — harp, to dwell on tediously.

k.syn: agonize, beset, besiege, torment, torture, excruciate, plague
k.ant: assuage 减轻（苦恼）
k.en: to inflict great distress or torment on

ex.en: The villagers were gaunt and sickly, harrowed by years of disease and starvation.
ex.cn: 村民们枯瘦如柴，体弱多病，都是被多年的疾病和饥饿折磨的。

m: HARROW = a spiked farm implement that drags and tears soil; to HARROW someone = drag and tear at their feelings. — harrow, to torment.

k.ant: comfort, mollify 安慰 Every man's work, whether it be literature of music of pictures or architecture of anything else, is always a portrait of himself. 每个人的工作，不管是文学、音乐、美术、建筑还是其他工作，都是自己的一幅画像。 ——美国教育家　勃特勒（Samuel Brtler, American educator） “真正努力过才能发现自己的潜能有多大。不要给自己犹豫后退的借口，让小宇宙爆发吧！” （刘琮，2009年6月，Verbal 720，Quantitative 800，录取院校： University of Southern California, Annenberg School of journalism and Communication, Public Diplomacy）
k.en: to disturb or distress by or as if by repeated attacks; harass

m: HARRY rhymes with HARASS — like Prince HARRY chased relentlessly by paparazzi. — harry, to harass continuously.

k.syn: grating, grotesque, jarring, unaesthetic
k.ant: soft, aesthetic 柔和的，有美感的
k.en: unpleasantly coarse and rough to the touch, disagreeable to one's aesthetic or artistic sense

ex.en: The harsh lighting in the cafeteria makes the food look slightly off- color.
ex.cn: 咖啡厅劣质刺眼的灯光让食物看起来卖相不佳。

k.syn: afflicting, agonizing, cruel, excruciating, galling, grievous, harrowing
k.ant: clement, forbearing, gentle, indulgent, lax, lenient, tolerant 温和的，宽松的
k.en: unduly exacting, given to exacting standards of discipline and self-restraint

ex.en: a harsh judge when it comes to drug users and especially drug dealers
ex.cn: 对瘾君子特别是毒贩处罚格外严厉的法官

m: HARSH rhymes with MARSH — both rough; a HARSH critic is rough. — harsh, severe / rough.

k.syn: accelerate, bundle, fast-track, rush, speed up
k.ant: slow the progress of, check, retard, brake, decelerate, retard, slow down 阻碍，放慢
k.en: to speed up; accelerate

ex.en: The man's death was hastened by alcohol abuse.
ex.cn: 酗酒加速了这个人的死亡。

m: HASTEN contains HASTE — make haste, speed up. — hasten, to speed up.

k.syn: cursory, headlong, precipitate, rash, rushed, pell-mell
k.ant: deliberate, well-considered, unhurried, unrushed 深思熟虑的，不慌不忙的
k.en: fast and typically superficial; acting or done with excessive or careless speed

ex.en: a hasty decision
ex.cn: 轻率的决定

m: HASTY contains HASTE — done in HASTE without thought. — hasty, rushed.

k.en: an opening

m: a HATCH is a small DOOR/cover ("open the hatch"). — hatch, a small door / opening.

k.syn: affect, habituate, visit, hang at, resort to
k.ant: avoid, shun 避开
k.en: to visit often; frequent

ex.en: haunt the movie theater
ex.cn: 常去影院

k.en: to come to mind continually; obsess

ex.en: a riddle that haunted me all morning
ex.cn: 整个早上萦绕在我心头的谜

m: HAUNT rhymes with TAUNT — ghost memories TAUNT you, haunting your mind. — haunt, to revisit persistently.

k.syn: bumptiousness, imperiousness, peremptoriness, pomposity, presumptuousness, pretentiousness, superciliousness, superiority
k.ant: humility, humbleness, modesty, unassumingness, unpretentiousness 谦虚
k.en: haughtiness in bearing and attitude; arrogance

ex.en: He looked at her with the hauteur of someone who is accustomed to being instantly obeyed.
ex.cn: 他看着她，带着向来被顺从惯了的傲慢。

m: HAUTEUR contains HAUT (French "high") — looking down on others from a high position. — hauteur, haughty pride.

k.syn: asylum, harbor, refuge, retreat, sanctuary
k.ant: unsafe place, dangerous place 不安全的地方
k.en: a place of safety

ex.en: a haven for artists
ex.cn: 艺术家们的圣地

m: HAVEN rhymes with HEAVEN — a small HEAVEN of safety. — haven, a refuge.

k.syn: disarrangement, disarray, dishevelment, muddle, muss, tumble, welter
k.ant: order, orderliness 有序
k.en: a state in which everything is out of order

ex.en: The blackout caused havoc in the city.
ex.cn: 断电造成了城市一片混乱。

k.syn: annihilation, decimation, demolishment, desolation, devastation, extermination, extinction, obliteration, ruin, wreckage
k.ant: building, construction, erection, raising 建设
k.en: the state or fact of being rendered nonexistent, physically unsound, or useless

ex.en: The powerful hurricane wreaked havoc all along the coast.
ex.cn: 威力巨大的飓风摧毁着沿岸的一切。

m: HAVOC rhymes with HAVE-ALL-OFF — wreaks total destruction. (Old battle cry "cry havoc!" = unleash chaos.) — havoc, widespread destruction.

k.syn: cursory, overhasty, precipitate, precipitous, rash, pell-mell, helter- skelter
k.ant: deliberate, unhurried, unrushed 深思熟虑的
k.en: without deliberation

ex.en: terrified forest creatures in a headlong retreat from the rapidly spreading fire
ex.cn: 受惊的动物们惊慌失措地从不断蔓延的森林大火中逃离出来

m: HEAD + LONG — diving HEAD-first, the LONG body following. — headlong, recklessly headfirst.

k.syn: attend, harken, heed, mind
k.ant: ignore 不理睬
k.en: to give respectful attention

m: HEARKEN contains HEAR — "HEARken!" = pay HEAR(ing). — hearken, to listen attentively.

k.syn: embolden, inspire, inspirit, buck up, buoy up, cheer up
k.ant: daunt, dismay, discourage, dishearten, dispirit 使胆怯
k.en: to give strength, courage, or hope to; encourage

ex.en: Thinking we were hopelessly lost, we were heartened by the sight of a familiar farmhouse.
ex.cn: 悲催地以为我们完全迷路了时，前方一座熟悉的农场给了我们新的希望。

m: HEARTEN contains HEART — put HEART back into someone. — hearten, to encourage.

k.en: causing intense sorrow or distress

ex.en: a heartrending choice between saving his mother or his wife
ex.cn: 先救老妈还是先救老婆的纠结选择

m: HEART + RENDING — RENDING (tearing) the HEART. — heartrending, deeply moving / sad.

k.syn: carnality, debauchery, sybaritism, voluptuousness
k.ant: abstinence, asceticism, sobriety, temperance 禁欲，节制
k.en: the doctrine that pleasure or happiness is the sole or chief good in life

ex.en: Their spring break trip to Mexico became an exercise in heedless hedonism.
ex.cn: 他们春季的墨西哥之行成为了一次未加注意的享乐之旅。

m: HEDONISM contains HEDON (Greek HĒDONĒ = pleasure) — pleasure-seeking philosophy. — hedonism, pursuit of pleasure.

k.syn: ascendancy, dominance, dominion, predominance, preeminence
k.ant: lack of authority 缺少权利
k.en: preponderant influence or authority over others

ex.en: battle for hegemony in Asia
ex.cn: 争夺在亚洲地区的霸权地位

m: HEGEMONY contains HEGE ≈ "head" — head-rule of one country over others. — hegemony, dominant control.

k.ant: commendable 值得赞美的
k.en: hatefully or shockingly evil, abominable

ex.en: a heinous crime
ex.cn: 十恶不赦的罪行

m: HEINOUS rhymes with IGNORE-US (US ignored such crimes) — but pronounced "HAY-nous" — terrible. Better: HEIN ≈ "HATE-ein" — full of HATE-worthy evil. — heinous, monstrously evil.

k.en: a piece of property that descends to the heir as an part of an inheritance

m: HEIR + LOOM — passed from HEIR to HEIR; a LOOM-woven family treasure. — heirloom, a family treasure.

k.en: any of various protective head coverings usually made of a hard material to resist impact

m: HELMET contains HELM — a HELM that protects (also a ship's helm = wheel). — helmet, head protection.

k.en: an edge or border on a piece of cloth, especially a finished edge made by folding the fabric

m: HEM in / HEM around — the bottom-edge HEM of a skirt; to hem someone = surround at their edges. — hem, an edge / to surround.

k.syn: annunciate, broadcast, declare, enunciate, proclaim, promulgate
k.en: to make known openly or publicly

ex.en: herald the great tidings to all the world
ex.cn: 将重大消息告知全世界

k.syn: adumbrate, forerun, harbinger, prefigure
k.en: to give a slight indication of beforehand

ex.en: The reshuffle of the company's management heralded the sweeping changes to come.
ex.cn: 公司管理层的重新洗牌预示着即将到来的巨大变革。

m: HERALD rhymes with EMERALD — green-clad medieval HERALDs announcing the king's arrival. — herald, to announce / a forerunner.

k.en: an agent used to destroy or inhibit plant

m: HERB + ICIDE — kills HERBs/weeds (-cide = kill). — herbicide, weed-killer.

k.syn: dissent, dissidence, heterodoxy, nonconformity
k.ant: dogma, conformity, orthodoxy 正教，正统
k.cn: 异教，和普遍观点相悖的说法
k.en: a controversial or unorthodox opinion or doctrine, departure from a generally accepted theory, opinion, or practice

ex.en: the heresy of asserting that Shakespeare was not a great writer
ex.cn: 声称莎士比亚不是伟大作家的非主流观点

m: HERESY contains HERE — a HERE-tical view here on earth that defies official doctrine. — heresy, an unorthodox belief.

k.syn: heterodox, nonconformist, unconventional, unorthodox
k.ant: conforming, conformist, conventional, orthodox 正统的
k.en: departure from established beliefs or standards

ex.en: The belief that women should be allowed to have careers outside the home was once considered heretical.
ex.cn: 女人应该拥有自己的事业这一观点一度被认为是异端邪说。

m: from HERESY — holding heretical views. — heretical, unorthodox.

k.syn: abstruse, arcane, esoteric, recondite
k.ant: easily comprehended, shallow, superficial 容易理解的，肤浅的
k.en: relating to or characterized by occultism or abstruseness

ex.en: wrote hermetic poetry
ex.cn: 写晦涩难懂的诗歌

m: HERMETIC comes from HERMES (Greek god of secret knowledge) — sealed and esoteric. — hermetic, sealed / esoteric.

k.en: one that retires from society and lives in solitude

m: HERMIT contains HERM ≈ "alone-hermit-crab" — lives alone in its shell. — hermit, a solitary person.

k.en: dealing with reptiles

m: HERPET + OLOGIST — Greek HERPETON = creeping thing. A scientist of creeping animals (reptiles/amphibians). — herpetologist, reptile scientist.

k.syn: faltering, indecision, irresolution, vacillation, wavering, wobbling
k.ant: alacrity, impetuosity, inclination, willingness 冲动，乐意
k.en: the quality or state of being hesitant, reluctance

ex.en: Sales figures for the month were up, as consumers began to overcome their hesitance about purchasing big-ticket items.
ex.cn: 月销售额有所上涨，因为消费者对于大件商品的购买由观望转向消费。

m: HESITANCE contains HESIT (stop, Latin HAESITARE = stick fast) — stuck, slow to act. — hesitance, reluctance.

k.syn: dissenting, out-there, unconventional, unorthodox
k.ant: conforming, conventional, orthodox, regular, routine 正常的，正统的
k.en: holding unorthodox opinions or doctrines, not rigidly following established form, custom, or rules

ex.en: Her heterodox approach to teaching science initially met with some resistance from her peers.
ex.cn: 她对于教学的一些非正统方法最开始受到了同辈的抵触。

m: HETERO- (different) + DOX (opinion, as in ORTHODOX) — different from accepted opinion. — heterodox, unorthodox.

k.syn: adhere, cling, conform
k.en: confirm, adhere, to hold to something firmly as if by adhesion

ex.en: hew to tradition
ex.cn: 遵守传统

k.syn: cling to, keep to, stand by, stick to
k.ant: defect from 脱离，叛变
k.en: to give steadfast support to

ex.en: He was no longer able to hew to the party line, so he switched political parties.
ex.cn: 不再赞同该党的纲领，他投奔了另一个党。

m: to HEW closely to a rule = to chop your behavior to fit it. (Also: hew = chop wood.) — hew, to adhere / to chop.

k.syn: archconservative, brassbound, die-hard, old-fashioned, standpat, ultraconservative
k.ant: broad-minded, large-minded, liberal, nonconservative, nonconventional, nonorthodox, nontraditional, open-minded, progressive 进步的，开放包容的
k.en: tending to favor established ideas, conditions, or institutions

ex.en: The hidebound innkeeper refused to see the need for a Web site.
ex.cn: 死板的旅店老板坚决不肯装宽带。

m: HIDE + BOUND — bound up by your own old HIDE (animal skin), unable to grow. — hidebound, rigidly conservative.

k.syn: homely, ill-favored, monstrous, uncomely, unsightly
k.ant: affinity 吸引力
k.en: exceedingly ugly

ex.en: The old man wore a hideous Halloween mask that made the kids all jump with fright.
ex.cn: 老头戴着一个丑陋的万圣节面具，把小孩子全都吓跑了。

m: HIDEOUS contains HIDE — so ugly you want to HIDE from it. — hideous, extremely ugly.

k.syn: bustle, dash, hustle, scoot, scurry, scuttle, shoot, trot
k.ant: dawdle, crawl, creep, poke 闲荡，爬行
k.en: to go quickly, hasten

ex.en: We had best hie home before the rain gets worse.
ex.cn: 我们最好在雨下大之前赶快回家。

m: HIE rhymes with HIGH-tail — to HIE = to high-tail it, hurry. — hie, to hasten.

k.en: a character used in a system of hieroglyphic writing

m: HIERO (sacred) + GLYPH (carving) — sacred Egyptian carvings. — hieroglyph, an ancient pictograph.

k.syn: boost, elevate, heighten, hoist, upraise
k.ant: backset, drop, lower 使倒退，下降
k.en: to（cause to）rise up, to move from a lower to a higher place or position

ex.en: hike rents
ex.cn: 涨租金

m: to HIKE prices = to walk them upward like a hiker climbs. — hike, to raise.

k.syn: hysterical, ludicrous, ridiculous, screaming, sidesplitting, uproarious
k.ant: humorless, lame, unamusing, uncomic, unfunny 不好笑的
k.en: marked by or causing hilarity: extremely funny

ex.en: hilarious cartoons that the whole family can enjoy
ex.cn: 适合全家观看的搞笑动画片

m: HILARIOUS contains HILAR ≈ "hill of laughter" — Latin HILARIS = cheerful. — hilarious, very funny.

k.syn: melodramatic, operatic, stagy, theatrical
k.ant: undramatic 平淡无奇的
k.en: of or relating to actors, acting, or the theater

ex.en: a penchant for dish throwing, door slamming, and other histrionic displays of temper
ex.cn: 有倾向做出一些砸盘子、摔门和其他一些电视剧里的经典生气动作

m: HISTRIONIC contains HIST ≈ "history of stage" — Latin HISTRIO = actor. Theatrical. — histrionic, theatrical / overdramatic.

k.en: a place swarming with activity

ex.en: The house was a hive of activity as we prepared for the party.
ex.cn: 我们筹备派对时，房子里很是熙熙攘攘。

k.en: to store up; accumulate

m: a HIVE of activity — bees in a HIVE buzzing. — hive, a busy place / to store.

k.syn: cache, stash, stockpile, store, squirrel, stockpile
k.ant: lavish 挥霍
k.en: to keep hidden or private

ex.en: He's been hoarding empty yogurt containers all winter.
ex.cn: 他整个冬天都在攒空酸奶瓶。

m: HOARD rhymes with HORDE — a HORDE of treasure HOARDed away. — hoard, to stockpile secretly.

k.syn: aged, antique, dateless, immemorial, antediluvian
k.ant: modern, new, recent 新的
k.en: extremely old

ex.en: hoary legends
ex.cn: 上古的传说故事

m: HOARY rhymes with STORY — old white-haired men telling old STORIES. — hoary, very old / white-haired.

k.syn: agglomeration, assortment, collage, medley, mishmash, pastiche, potpourri
k.en: a mixture of dissimilar ingredients; a jumble

ex.en: a hodgepodge of styles
ex.cn: 混搭风格

ex.en: The exhibit was a hodgepodge of mediocre art, bad art, and really bad art.
ex.cn: 整场展览就是中庸、烂和非常烂的艺术作品的大杂烩。

m: HODGEPODGE rhymes with itself — sounds like a jumbled mess. — hodgepodge, a confused mixture.

k.syn: commendation, eulogy, hymn, paean, panegyric, salutation, tribute, dithyramb
k.ant: disrespect 不尊敬
k.en: expression of high regard: respect

ex.en: Homage to Catalonia
ex.cn: 向加泰罗尼亚致敬

m: HOMAGE contains HOM (man, Latin) — historically, a vassal MAN paid homage to his lord. — homage, respect / tribute.

k.syn: hideous, unappealing, unattractive, uncomely, unsightly
k.ant: pulchritudinous, aesthetic, attractive, beautiful, comely, gorgeous, handsome, knockout, lovely, pretty, ravishing, seemly, sightly, stunning, taking, well-favored 美丽的
k.cn: 其貌不扬的，朴素简单的
k.en: not attractive or good- looking

ex.en: homely truth
ex.cn: 朴素的真理

m: HOMELY rhymes with LONELY — but homely means plain (American sense) / homey (British). — homely, plain / unattractive.

k.syn: banality, bromide, chestnut, cliché, groaner, platitude, shibboleth
k.cn: 冗长乏味的道德讲演或训诫
k.en: a tedious moralizing lecture or admonition

ex.en: a TV movie filled with the usual hokey homilies about people triumphing over life's adversities
ex.cn: 一档说教性质的、充满了介绍逆境中的人们如何成就自我的电视节目

m: HOMILY rhymes with FAMILY — a family-style preachy lecture. — homily, a tedious moralizing speech.

k.syn: formalize, normalize, regularize
k.en: to make agree with a single established standard or model

ex.en: plans to homogenize the science curriculum in public high schools throughout the state
ex.cn: 计划将全州的公立高中的课程安排统一化

m: HOMO- (same) + GENIZE — make the same throughout (homogenized milk has uniform fat). — homogenize, to make uniform.

k.syn: edge, grind, strop, whet
k.ant: blunt, dull 弄钝
k.en: to sharpen or smooth with a whetstone

ex.en: hone the knife's blade to razor-like sharpness
ex.cn: 把小刀磨得像剃刀一样锋利

ex.en: He honed his crossword-puzzle skills by reading the dictionary.
ex.cn: 他通过背字典把填词游戏的水平练得炉火纯青。

m: HONE rhymes with STONE — sharpen on a STONE. — hone, to sharpen.

k.en: a payment for a service on which custom or propriety forbids a price to be set

m: HONORARIUM contains HONOR — a payment given as an HONOR (not a salary). — honorarium, an honorary payment.

k.syn: beguile, con, delude, dupe, fool, hoax, humbug
k.ant: disabuse 消除…的错误念头
k.en: to take in by deceptive means; deceive

ex.en: Don't let yourself be hoodwinked into buying things you don't need.
ex.cn: 别被忽悠着去买用不着的东西。

m: HOOD + WINK — pull a HOOD over their eyes, give a sly WINK. — hoodwink, to deceive.

k.en: giving exhortation

m: HORTATIVE shares HORT- with EXHORT — both urge action. — hortative, encouraging.

k.en: the science and art of growing fruits, vegetables, flowers

m: HORTI- (garden, Latin HORTUS) + CULTURE — cultivating gardens. — horticulture, gardening.

k.syn: cabin, camp, hooch, hut, hutch, hutment, shanty
k.en: a small, wretched, and often dirty house

m: HOVEL rhymes with SHOVEL — a tiny hut you'd SHOVEL out for shelter. — hovel, a small shabby dwelling.

k.en: the central part of a circular

m: HUB rhymes with NUB — both are central knobs. — hub, the central knob of a wheel.

k.ant: humility 谦逊
k.en: exaggerated pride or self-confidence

ex.en: His failure was brought on by his hubris.
ex.cn: 就是他的狂妄自大才导致了失败。

m: HUBRIS rhymes with NUMERIS — but it's the GREEK term: pride that the gods punish. (Or: HU-BRIS ≈ "HUge BRIStles of pride.") — hubris, excessive pride.

k.syn: modest, unassuming, unpretentious, demure, down-to-earth
k.ant: arrogant, bumptious, conceited, egotistic, haughty, high-and-mighty, imperious, lordly, overweening, peremptory, pompous, presuming, presumptuous, pretentious, self-assertive, supercilious, superior, toplofty, uppish, uppity 傲慢的
k.en: marked by meekness or modesty in behavior, attitude, or spirit; not arrogant or prideful

ex.en: a medical scientist who remained remarkably humble even after winning the Nobel Prize
ex.cn: 获得诺贝尔奖之后仍然保持谦逊的医学家

k.syn: base, humble, menial, servile, slavish
k.en: showing, expressing, or offered in a spirit of humility or unseemly submissiveness

ex.en: Please accept my humble thanks.
ex.cn: 请接受我的卑微的感谢。

m: HUMBLE contains HUM ≈ HUMUS (earth) — close to the earth, modest. — humble, modest.

k.syn: demureness, humbleness, lowliness, meekness, modesty
k.ant: arrogance, assumption, bumptiousness, hauteur, pomposity, presumption, superciliousness 傲慢，自大
k.en: the absence of any feelings of being better than others

ex.en: Haughtiness invites disaster; humility receives benefit.
ex.cn: 满招损，谦受益。

m: HUMILITY shares HUMIL- with HUMILIATE — but voluntary lowness: modesty. — humility, modesty.

k.syn: comedy, comic, drollery, drollness, funniness, hilariousness
k.ant: pathos 令人伤感的事物
k.en: something that is or is designed to be comical or amusing

ex.en: The speech is full of wit and humor.
ex.cn: 演讲妙趣横生。

k.syn: cater, gratify, indulge
k.en: to comply with the wishes or ideas of

ex.en: Parents need to know how to humor kids when they are upset.
ex.cn: 父母需要知道当孩子烦躁的时候怎么哄孩子。

m: to HUMOR someone = indulge their HUMOR (mood). — humor, to indulge / amuse.

k.syn: storm, typhoon
k.ant: calm 风平浪静
k.en: a violent rotating storm or system of winds

ex.en: The hurricane struck the coast early today.
ex.cn: 飓风今晨袭击了海岸。

k.syn: disturbance, furor, pandemonium, tumult, turmoil, uproar
k.cn: 飓风般的事物，引起动荡的事物
k.en: something resembling a hurricane especially in its turmoil

ex.en: economic news that unleashed a hurricane on the trading floor
ex.cn: 在交易大厅掀起轩然大波的经济新闻

m: HURRICANE contains HURRI ≈ "HURRY-cane" — a HURRYing destructive force. — hurricane, a destructive storm.

k.en: careful management or conservation of resources;

m: from HUSBAND (verb) — careful management of resources. — husbandry, thrifty management.

k.syn: bark, chaff, hull, shell
k.ant: core, kernel 核
k.en: a usually dry or membranous outer covering（as a pod or one composed of bracts）of various seeds and fruits（as barley and corn）

ex.en: a grey squirrel nibbling on a peanut husk
ex.cn: 一个啃着花生壳的灰松鼠

k.syn: bark, flay, hull, shell, shuck, skin
k.en: to remove the natural covering of

ex.en: the tedious task of husking coconuts
ex.cn: 无聊的任务——剥椰子

m: the HUSK is the HARSH outer shell of corn — to HUSK = strip that shell. — husk, an outer shell.

k.syn: coarse, grating, gravelly, harsh, rasping, rusty, scratchy, throaty
k.ant: mellifluous（声音）甜蜜的，甜美的
k.en: hoarse or rough in quality

ex.en: a voice husky with emotion
ex.cn: 富有感情的沙哑声音

k.syn: beefy, burly, hefty, muscular, powerful, strapping, rugged
k.ant: dwarf 矮小的
k.en: big and muscular

ex.en: a very husky young man, built like a football player
ex.cn: 一个体格像橄榄球运动员一样的高大的男孩

m: HUSKY rhymes with DUSKY — both gravelly; a husky voice is rough like dusty corn HUSKS. — husky, hoarse / burly.

k.syn: amalgam, bastard, compound, mixture, mule
k.en: something of mixed origin or composition

ex.en: a hybrid of medieval and Renaissance styles
ex.cn: 中世纪和文艺复兴的混搭风

k.syn: crossbred, mongrel
k.ant: purebred 纯种的
k.en: being offspring produced by parents of different races, breeds, species, or genera

ex.en: a hybrid rose called“American Beauty”
ex.cn: 一种被称为“美国丽人”的杂交玫瑰

m: HYBRID rhymes with MIDRIB — a mixed-breed creation. — hybrid, a mixed offspring.

k.syn: eulogy, homage, hymn, ode, paean, panegyric, psalm, salutation, tribute
k.ant: dirge, elegy 哀诗，挽歌
k.en: a song of praise or joy

ex.en: They sang a hymn of praise to God.
ex.cn: 他们唱赞歌赞美上帝。

k.syn: bless, carol, celebrate, emblazon, exalt, extol, glorify, laud, magnify, resound
k.ant: blame, censure, reprehend, reprobate 责难，斥责
k.en: to proclaim the glory of

ex.en: During the honeymoon following the inauguration, newspaper articles seemed to hymn the president's every move.
ex.cn: 在上任后的“蜜月期”之中，报纸新闻似乎在赞美总统的每一项措施。

m: HYMN rhymes with HIM — a song to praise HIM (a deity). — hymn, a religious song.

k.syn: coloring, embellishment, embroidering, exaggeration, magnification, overstatement
k.ant: understatement 有节制的陈述
k.en: a figure of speech in which exaggeration is used for emphasis or effect

ex.en: The debate was carried on with increasing rhetorical hyperbole.
ex.cn: 随着辩论的进行，双方修辞当中的夸张越来越多。

m: HYPER- (over) + BOLE (throw) — to throw beyond, exaggerate. — hyperbole, exaggeration.

k.syn: drowsy, narcotic, opiate, sleepy, slumberous, soporific
k.ant: stimulating 刺激性的；refreshing 使人精神焕发的
k.en: tending to cause sleep

ex.en: Her eyes soon grew heavy from the hypnotic rhythm of the train's wheels.
ex.cn: 伴随着催眠的铁轨声，她很快就有了睡意。

m: HYPNOTIC contains HYPNO (sleep, Greek) — sleep-inducing. — hypnotic, inducing sleep.

k.syn: artificial, backhanded, feigned, mealy-mouthed, phony, pretended, unctuous
k.ant: artless, candid, genuine, honest, sincere, undesigning, unfeigned 真诚的，诚实的
k.der: hypocrite n. 虚伪的人，伪君子
k.en: not being or expressing what one appears to be or express

ex.en: It's hypocritical to say mean things behind someone's back, and then to act nice when you want something from her.
ex.cn: 这种在背后说别人坏话、而有求于人时又装出另一副样子的人就是虚伪。

m: HYPOCRITICAL contains HYPO + CRITICAL — beneath the critic's outward judgment, contradicting it. (Greek HYPOKRITES = actor wearing a mask.) — hypocritical, two-faced.

k.en: a branch of zoology that deals with fishes

m: ICHTHYO- (fish, Greek) + LOGIST — fish scientist. (Greek ICHTHYS = fish, the early Christian symbol.) — ichthyologist, a fish scientist.

k.syn: bohemian, deviant, heretic, maverick, non-conformer
k.ant: conformer, conformist 循规蹈矩的人
k.en: a person who does not conform to generally accepted standards or customs

ex.en: He was an iconoclast who refused to be bound by tradition.
ex.cn: 他是个不愿被传统所束缚的叛逆者。

m: ICON + CLAST (break, Greek) — image-breaker; one who challenges revered traditions. — iconoclast, one who attacks established beliefs.

k.syn: adore, adulate, canonize, deify, dote, worship
k.ant: abhor, abominate, detest, dislike, hate, loathe 痛恨，憎恶
k.en: admires intensely and often blindly

ex.en: Some teenagers idolatrized Hitler more than their own parents.
ex.cn: 曾经有一些青少年崇拜希特勒超过自己的父母。

m: IDOLATRIZE contains IDOL — worship as if an IDOL. — idolatrize, to worship blindly.

k.syn: frisk, frolic, gambol, revel, lark
k.en: a carefree episode or experience

ex.en: a summer idyll on the coast of the Mediterranean
ex.cn: 地中海岸的悠闲夏日

m: IDYLL rhymes with IDLE — a peaceful IDLE rural scene (a poetic idyll). — idyll, a carefree pastoral scene.

k.syn: enkindle, fire, inflame, kindle, light, torch
k.ant: douse, extinguish, quench, put out 熄灭
k.en: to cause to burn; to set fire to

ex.en: The bombs ignited a fire which destroyed some 60 houses.
ex.cn: 炸弹引发的大火摧毁了大约60间房屋。

k.syn: arouse, incite, instigate, pique, spark, stimulate, stir
k.ant: appease, assuage, calm, conciliate, mollify, pacify, placate, propitiate, soothe 平息怒火，安抚
k.en: to arouse the passions of

ex.en: The insults ignited my anger.
ex.cn: 那些侮辱让我倍感愤怒。

m: IGNITE contains IGNI- (fire, Latin IGNIS) — set on fire. — ignite, to set fire to / spark.

k.syn: discredit, disesteem, dishonor, disrepute, infamy, obloquy, odium, opprobrium, reproach, shame
k.ant: glory, honor 荣耀；esteem, respect 敬意
k.en: the state of having lost the esteem of others

ex.en: He spent the remainder of his life in ignominy after being involved in a bribery scandal.
ex.cn: 因为卷入了受贿丑闻当中，他在耻辱中度过余生。

m: IG- (not) + NOMINY (name, NOMEN) — "no good name," disgrace. — ignominy, public disgrace.

k.syn: ignorance
k.ant: learning, literacy 受过教育
k.en: the condition of being unable to read and write

ex.en: a nationwide campaign against illiteracy
ex.cn: 全国扫盲运动

m: ILLITERACY contains LITER- (letter) — IL (not) + literacy. — illiteracy, inability to read.

k.syn: clerisy, intellectual, intelligentsia, literati
k.ant: fool, dolt, dullard, idiot, simpleton 傻子
k.en: persons who claim to be unusually enlightened

k.syn: aristocracy, elite, upper crust
k.en: individuals carefully selected as being the best of a class

ex.en: a book launching party to which only New York's cultural illuminati were invited
ex.cn: 一个只邀请了纽约上层文化精英的新书发布会

m: ILLUMINATI shares LUMIN with ILLUMINATE — "the enlightened ones." — illuminati, the elite / enlightened.

k.en: a lighting

m: from ILLUMINATE — light. — illumination, light / clarification.

k.syn: chimerical, fanciful, fantastic, fictional, fictitious, imaginary, suppositious, unreal
k.ant: factual 事实的
k.en: produced by, based on, or having the nature of an illusion

ex.en: the illusory definition of nationhood
ex.cn: 国家的虚幻定义

m: from ILLUSION — like an illusion. — illusory, illusory / not real.

k.syn: drink, guzzle, hoist, quaff, sip, sponge
k.ant: urinate 排尿
k.en: to take in（something liquid）through small openings

ex.en: Plants can imbibe water through their roots.
ex.cn: 植物可以通过它们的根摄取水分。

m: IMBIBE contains BIBE ≈ "to drink" (Latin BIBERE = drink, source of BEVERAGE). — imbibe, to drink in.

k.syn: complexity, complication, embarrassment, entanglement, involvement, misunderstanding, quandary
k.en: an intricate or complicated situation

ex.en: What investor would willingly become involved in this imbroglio?
ex.cn: 怎样的投资者才会像这样明知山有虎，偏向虎山行呢？

k.syn: altercation, controversy, disagreement, dispute, fight, quarrel, squabble, wrangle
k.ant: harmony 和谐
k.en: an often noisy or angry expression of differing opinions

ex.en: an imbroglio involving some big names in the entertainment industry
ex.cn: 一场涉及娱乐圈数位明星的纷争

m: IMBROGLIO contains BROIL — like being BROILed in a tangled mess. (Italian "imbroglio" = entanglement.) — imbroglio, a complicated mess.

k.syn: clone, copy, dupe, duplication, facsimile, mock, reduplication, replica, replication, reproduction
k.ant: archetype, original, prototype 原型
k.en: something that is made to look exactly like something else

ex.en: She usually wore imitations of her costly jewels.
ex.cn: 她通常只戴她那些贵重珠宝的仿制品。

m: IMITATION contains IMITAT- (Latin IMITARI = to copy) — a copy. — imitation, a copy.

k.syn: antiseptic, chaste, clean, decent, modest, pristine, pure, spotless, stainless, virgin
k.ant: coarse, indecent, obscene, vulgar 粗俗的,下流的；besmirched, dirty, filthy, foul, spotted, stained, sullied 有污点的，不干净的
k.en: free from dirt or stain; free from any trace of the coarse or indecent

ex.en: an immaculate soul
ex.cn: 一个纯净的灵魂

k.syn: absolute, faultless, flawless, impeccable, indefectible, irreproachable, perfect, seamless, unblemished
k.ant: amiss, defective, faulty, flawed, imperfect 有错误的，有误差的
k.cn: 完美的，没有任何错误的
k.en: being entirely without fault or flaw

ex.en: an immaculate rendering of the Queen of the Night 's aria
ex.cn: 《魔笛之夜后》咏叹调的完美演绎

m: IMMACULATE = IM (not) + MACULATE (spotted) — without spots, completely clean. — immaculate, perfectly clean.

k.syn: constitutional, essential, inborn, inbred, indigenous, ingrained, innate, integral, intrinsic, natural
k.ant: adventitious, extrinsic, extraneous 外在的，非本源的
k.en: being a part of the innermost nature of a person or thing

ex.en: Beauty is not something imposed but something immanent.
ex.cn: 美丽是内在的品格而非外加的东西。

m: IMMANENT contains MAN ≈ "remain" — staying within (don't confuse with imminent = about to happen). — immanent, inherent / dwelling within.

k.syn: ethereal, formless, incorporeal, insubstantial, nonmaterial, spiritual
k.ant: bodily, corporeal, material, physical, substantial 实体的
k.en: not composed of matter

ex.en: It is only possible to study immaterial forces like gravity by observing their effects on the physical world.
ex.cn: 要研究那些非实体的作用力，例如万有引力，只能观察它们对于现实世界的影响。

k.syn: extraneous, impertinent, inapplicable, inapposite, irrelevant
k.ant: applicable, apposite, apropos, germane, pertinent, relevant 相关的； crucial, important, significant 重要的
k.en: of no importance or relevance

ex.en: While undoubtedly upsetting, that story is immaterial to the question of why you are late.
ex.cn: 尽管那个故事的确很让人遗憾，但是和你为什么迟到没有半点关系。

m: IM (not) + MATERIAL — not made of matter; or not material to the case. — immaterial, irrelevant / non-physical.

k.syn: aged, age-old, antediluvian, antique, dateless, hoary, old, venerable
k.ant: contemporary, modern 当代的，现代的；recent 近来的
k.en: dating or surviving from the distant past

ex.en: a modern version of an immemorial myth
ex.cn: 一个远古传说的现代版本

m: IM (not) + MEMORIAL — beyond MEMORY, very old. — immemorial, ancient.

k.syn: resistant
k.ant: susceptible, vulnerable 易感染的，易受攻击的
k.cn: 不易被感染的，有免疫力的
k.en: of, relating to, or having resistance to infection

ex.en: The blood test will tell whether you are immune to the disease.
ex.cn: 验血可以判断你是否对这种疾病免疫。

k.syn: insusceptible, unaffected, unresponsive
k.ant: ductile, pliable, pliant, yielding 易受影响的
k.en: not affected by a given influence

ex.en: immune to persuasion
ex.cn: 不听劝

k.syn: exempt, free
k.ant: liable, responsible 负有责任的
k.en: not subject to an obligation imposed on others

ex.en: immune from taxation
ex.cn: 免税

m: IMMUNE contains IM (not) + MUNE (Latin MUNUS = duty/obligation) — not subject to obligation; safe from. — immune, protected / exempt.

k.syn: confine, constrain, incarcerate, intern, jail, imprison
k.ant: discharge, release 释放；enfranchise, free, liberate 解放
k.en: to confine within or as if within walls

ex.en: immure oneself for GRE
ex.cn: 闭门苦读GRE

m: IMMURE contains MURE ≈ MURAL (wall, Latin MURUS) — to wall someone in. — immure, to confine within walls.

k.syn: constant, fixed, inalterable, inflexible, invariable, unalterable, unchangeable
k.ant: alterable, changeable, elastic, flexible, mutable, variable 可变的
k.en: not capable of changing or being changed

ex.en: One of the immutable laws of television is that low ratings inevitably lead to cancellation.
ex.cn: 电视行业中一个不变的原则就是：低收视率的节目必然会被撤掉。

m: IM (not) + MUTABLE — not changeable. (MUTE = change, as in MUTATION.) — immutable, unchangeable.

k.en: a mischievous

m: IMP rhymes with LIMP / PIMP — a small mischievous demon. (Folklore figure.) — imp, a mischievous child / small demon.

k.syn: affectless, apathetic, cold-blooded, deadpan, emotionless, numb, phlegmatic, stoic, undemonstrative
k.ant: demonstrative, emotional, fervent, fervid, impassioned, passionate, vehement 充满感情的
k.en: giving no sign of feeling or emotion

ex.en: She remained impassive as the officers informed her of her son's death.
ex.cn: 她面不改色地听着警官宣读她儿子的死讯。

m: IM (not) + PASSIVE — not showing PASSION (PASS- = feeling). — impassive, showing no emotion.

k.syn: blameless, clear, guiltless, inculpable, pure, sinless
k.ant: guilty, sinful 有罪的
k.en: free from sin, guilt or blame

ex.en: the belief that there can be no such thing as an impeccable soul
ex.cn: 不相信存在无罪的灵魂

k.syn: absolute, faultless, flawless, immaculate, indefectible, irreproachable, perfect, seamless, unblemished
k.ant: amiss, defective, faulty, flawed, imperfect 有错误的，有误差的
k.en: free from fault or blame

ex.en: She had impeccable taste in clothes.
ex.cn: 她有着无可挑剔的穿衣品味。

m: IM (not) + PECCABLE (Latin PECCARE = sin) — without sin/fault. — impeccable, flawless.

k.syn: beggared, destitute, impoverished, indigent, necessitous, needy, penniless, penurious, threadbare
k.ant: affluent, flush, opulent, rich, wealthy 富有的
k.en: having very little or no money

ex.en: They were so impecunious that they couldn't afford to give one another even token Christmas gifts.
ex.cn: 他们穷到了连象征性的圣诞礼物都互送不起的地步。

m: IM (not) + PECUN (money, Latin PECUNIA) — without money. — impecunious, poor.

k.syn: clog, embarrass, encumber, fetter, hinder, inhibit, obstruct, retard, shackle, stymie, trammel
k.ant: aid, assist, facilitate, help 促进，帮助
k.en: to interfere with or slow the progress of

ex.en: Storms at sea impeded our expedition.
ex.cn: 海上的风暴阻碍了我们的远征。

m: IMPEDE contains PED (foot, Latin) — get in the way of someone's FEET. — impede, to obstruct.

k.en: to be about to occur; to be imminent or threatening

ex.en: Her retirement is impending.
ex.cn: 她即将退休

m: IMPEND contains PEND (hang, as in PENDANT) — hanging overhead, about to drop. — impend, to be about to happen.

k.syn: impassable, impermeable, impervious, impregnable
k.ant: passable, penetrable, permeable, pervious 可通过的
k.cn: 不可渗透的，不可穿透的
k.en: impossible to get through or into

ex.en: The ancient temple was surrounded by vast stretches of impenetrable jungle.
ex.cn: 包围着古老的神庙的是密不可穿的灌木丛。

k.syn: arcane, cryptic, enigmatic, incomprehensible, inscrutable, unfathomable, ungraspable, unintelligible
k.ant: fathomable, intelligible, understandable 可以理解的
k.en: incapable of being comprehended

ex.en: The textbook's language is completely impenetrable, at least to me.
ex.cn: 至少在我看来，这个教材就是天书。

m: IM (not) + PENETRABLE — can't be PENETRATED. — impenetrable, unable to be entered.

k.syn: remorseless, regretless, shameless, unashamed, unrepentant
k.ant: contrite, regretful, remorseful, rueful, penitent 感到悔恨的
k.en: not feeling or expressing humble or regretful pain or sorrow for sins or offenses

ex.en: remain impenitent about her criminal past
ex.cn: 对她过去所犯罪行执迷不悟

m: IM (not) + PENITENT — not penitent (sorry). — impenitent, unrepentant.

k.syn: command, decree, dictate, direction, directive, instruction, order,
k.en: a statement of what to do that must be obeyed by those concerned

ex.en: a secretary of defense who was fond of issuing harshly worded imperatives
ex.cn: 一个很喜欢发布措辞严厉的命令的国防部长

m: IMPERATIVE contains IMPER- (command, Latin IMPERARE) — a commanded order. — imperative, a command.

k.syn: authoritarian, autocratic, despotic, dictatorial, domineering, masterful,
k.en: fond of ordering people around

ex.en: an imperious little boy who liked to tell the other scouts what to do
ex.cn: 一个爱发号施令的小男孩，总是喜欢指挥别的童子军做事

m: from IMPERIAL — emperor-like, commanding. — imperious, domineering.

k.syn: extraneous, immaterial, inapplicable, inapposite, irrelevant
k.ant: germane, pertinent, relevant 相关的；crucial, important, significant 重要的
k.en: not having a clear decisive relevance to the matter in hand

ex.en: Your résumé needlessly lists extracurricular experiences that are impertinent to the PhD program for which you are applying.
ex.cn: 你的简历里列出的一些课外活动和你申请的博士项目是毫不相关的。

k.syn: audacious, bold, brash, brassbound, brassy, brazen, impudent, insolent
k.ant: meek, mousy, retiring, shy, timid 内敛的，胆小的
k.en: given to or characterized by insolent rudeness

ex.en: I don't like strangers who ask impertinent questions.
ex.cn: 我很讨厌那些问无礼问题的陌生人。

k.syn: discourteous, disrespectful, inconsiderate, rude, thoughtless
k.ant: civil, courteous, genteel, gracious, mannerly, polite 有礼貌的； considerate 为他人着想的
k.cn: 不守礼节的，不为他人着想的
k.en: showing a lack of manners or consideration for others

ex.en: impertinent salesmen who telephone people during the dinner hour
ex.cn: 在就餐时间打电话骚扰别人的无礼销售员

m: IM (not) + PERTINENT — not relevant; or rude (overstepping). — impertinent, irrelevant / rude.

k.syn: collected, composed, cool, disimpassioned, nonchalant, unflappable, unruffled
k.ant: choleric, touchy 暴躁的，易怒的
k.en: marked by extreme calm, impassivity, and steadiness

ex.en: The chef was absolutely imperturbable—even when the kitchen caught on fire.
ex.cn: 这个厨师可谓淡定到了极致——他连厨房着火的时候都依然沉着。

m: IM (not) + PERTURBABLE — can't be perturbed/disturbed. — imperturbable, unshakably calm.

k.syn: impassable, impenetrable, impermeable, impregnable
k.ant: passable, penetrable, permeable, pervious 可渗透的
k.cn: 不可渗透的，不可穿透的
k.en: not allowing entrance or passage

ex.en: The material for this coat is supposed to be impervious to rain.
ex.cn: 这件大衣的材料应该是能防雨的。

k.syn: bulletproof, imperishable, indestructible, inextinguishable, invulnerable
k.ant: delicate, sensitive, vulnerable 脆弱的，敏感的
k.en: not capable of being damaged or harmed

ex.en: a carpet impervious to rough treatment
ex.cn: 耐用的地毯

k.syn: immune, insusceptible, unaffected, unresponsive
k.ant: ductile, pliable, pliant, yielding 易受影响的
k.en: not capable of being affected or disturbed

ex.en: These sailors are impervious to fear.
ex.cn: 这些水手无所畏惧。

m: IM (not) + PERVIOUS (Latin PERVIUS = passable) — can't be passed through. — impervious, not penetrable.

k.syn: ardent, hasty, headlong, impassioned, impulsive, passionate, rash, vehement
k.ant: cautious, circumspect, wary 谨慎的，小心的
k.en: marked by impulsive vehemence or passion

ex.en: He is young and impetuous.
ex.cn: 他年轻易冲动。

k.syn: abrupt, hasty, hurried, precipitate, precipitous, rushing, sudden, violent
k.ant: calm, halcyon, placid, quiet 风平浪静的
k.en: marked by force and violence of movement or action

ex.en: an impetuous wind
ex.cn: 猛烈的风

m: IMPETUOUS contains IMPETUS (driving force) — driven by sudden force, headlong. — impetuous, impulsively reckless.

k.en: the quality or state of being impious

m: IM (not) + PIETY — without piety. — impiety, irreverence.

k.syn: adamant, dogged, headstrong, intransigent, mulish, obdurate, pertinacious, stubborn, unyielding
k.ant: acquiescent 默认的；compliant, flexible, pliable, pliant, yielding 易受影响的
k.en: sticking to an opinion, purpose, or course of action in spite of reason, arguments, or persuasion

k.syn: determined, grim, relentless, unappeasable
k.ant: placable 可平息的，可缓和的
k.en: not capable of being appeased, significantly changed, or mitigated

ex.en: an implacable enemy
ex.cn: 无法与之和解的敌人

m: IM (not) + PLACABLE (calmable, Latin PLACARE = appease) — can't be calmed. (PLACATE shares the root.) — implacable, unappeasable.

k.syn: apparatus, device, instrument, tool, utensil
k.en: a device used in the performance of a task

ex.en: gardening implements such as hoes, spades, and pruners
ex.cn: 诸如锄头、铲子和修枝剪一类的园艺工具

k.syn: administer, apply, effect, enforce, execute, invoke, perform
k.ant: cancel, repeal, rescind, revoke 撤销
k.en: to put into practical effect; carry out

ex.en: implement the new online application procedures
ex.cn: 实行新的网申程序

m: IMPLEMENT contains IM (in) + PLE (fill) + MENT — "fill in" with action; or a tool. — implement, to put into action / a tool.

k.syn: buckle, founder, tumble, yield
k.ant: explode 外爆，爆炸
k.en: to（cause to）collapse inward violently

ex.en: The flask imploded during the vacuum distilling.
ex.cn: 做低压蒸馏的时候烧瓶发生了内爆。

m: IM (in) + PLODE (burst, as in EXPLODE) — burst inward. — implode, to collapse inward.

k.syn: appeal, beseech, besiege, conjure, entreat, impetrate, implore, petition, plead, pray, solicit, supplicate
k.ant: demand 要求，强求
k.en: to make a request to（someone）in an earnest or urgent manner

ex.en: beggars importuning passers-by
ex.cn: 在恳求着过路人的乞丐

m: IM- (in) + PORTUNE (port, opportunity) — keep harping at the right opportunity. (Or: IMPORTUNE rhymes with FORTUNE — beg for fortune.) — importune, to beg insistently.

k.syn: august, epic, glorious, grand, imperial, magnificent, monumental, noble, splendid
k.ant: common, humble, inferior, low 低下的，平凡的
k.en: impressive in size, bearing, dignity, or grandeur

ex.en: The corporation's imposing headquarters were designed by one of the nation's cutting-edge architects.
ex.cn: 这家公司宏伟壮丽的总部是由该国最优秀的建筑师之一设计的。

m: IMPOSING contains IMPOSE — a presence that IMPOSES itself on the room. — imposing, impressively grand.

k.syn: charlatan, fake, fraud, hoaxer, mountebank, phony, pretender, quack, sham
k.en: one that assumes false identity or title for the purpose of deception

ex.en: The man who claimed to be a prince turned out to be an impostor.
ex.cn: 那个自称是王子的人原来是冒充的。

m: alternative spelling of IMPOSTER — one who imposes a fake identity. — impostor, an impersonator.

k.syn: barren, fruitless, infertile, unfruitful
k.ant: fecund, fertile, fruitful, productive 高产的，硕果累累的
k.en: unable to produce fruit or offspring

ex.en: Most mules are impotent.
ex.cn: 绝大多数的骡子都无法生育。

k.syn: hamstrung, handcuffed, helpless, impuissant, paralyzed, weak
k.ant: mighty, potent, powerful, puissant, strong 强大的，有能力的
k.en: lacking in power, strength, or vigor

ex.en: an impotent ruler who was just a figurehead
ex.cn: 一个无能的傀儡统治者

m: IM (not) + POTENT (powerful) — not powerful. — impotent, powerless.

k.syn: approximate, inaccurate, loose, squishy
k.ant: accurate, exact, precise, veracious 精确的，准确的
k.der: imprecision n. 不准确，不精确
k.en: not precise

ex.en: incomplete and imprecise satellite data
ex.cn: 既不完整又不精确的卫星数据

m: IM (not) + PRECISE — not precise. — imprecise, inexact.

k.en: something, such as a speech, that is made or done extemporaneously

m: IMPRESARIO contains IMPRESS — a showman who IMPRESSes audiences. (Italian for theater promoter.) — impresario, a theater producer.

k.syn: ad-lib, extemporize
k.ant: plan, premeditate 计划，预先考虑
k.en: to invent, compose, or perform with little or no preparation

ex.en: Since the award was a complete surprise, I improvised an acceptance speech.
ex.cn: 因为那个奖项完全是个惊喜，所以我即兴发表了一个获奖演说。

m: IM (not) + PROVISE (foresee, Latin PROVIDERE) — done without prior provision. — improvise, to make up on the spot.

k.syn: impolitic, inadvisable, indelicate, injudicious, tactless, undiplomatic, unwise
k.ant: advisable, politic, prudent, tactical, wise 明智的
k.en: lacking discretion, wisdom, or good judgment

ex.en: an imprudent investment he made many years ago
ex.cn: 他许多年前做的一笔不明智的投资

m: IM (not) + PRUDENT — not prudent. — imprudent, unwise.

k.syn: audacious, barefaced, bold, brash, brazen, impertinent, insolent, shameless
k.ant: courteous, genteel, mannerly, polite, proper 举止得体的
k.der: impudence n. 放肆无礼 “无论多久后的将来，无论当我遇到什么困难险阻，每当我回想起当年奋战GRE的日子，我的血液中仍然迸发着那种不安分的激情和冲劲，让我继续舔着伤口，在荆棘丛中勇往直前。” （周慧杰，2006年10月，Verbal 720，Quantitative 800，AW 5.5，现就职于凯雷投资集团，中国香港）
k.en: marked by contemptuous or cocky boldness or disregard of others

ex.en: Some children were well behaved, while others were impudent.
ex.cn: 一些孩子表现得很得体，另一些则很无礼。

m: IMPUDENT contains PUD ≈ "shameless pup" — bold and disrespectful. (Latin IM-PUDENS = shameless.) — impudent, brazenly rude.

k.syn: attack, assail, contradict, contravene, cross, disaffirm, deny, gainsay, negate, negative, traverse
k.ant: advocate, back, support, uphold 支持；authenticate 证实，证明
k.en: to attack as false or questionable; challenge in argument

ex.en: impugn a political opponent's character
ex.cn: 就政治对手的人品发难

m: IM (against) + PUGN (fight, as in PUGILIST) — fight against, attack. — impugn, to attack as false.

k.syn: impotence, powerlessness, weakness
k.ant: clout, potency, power, puissance 权势
k.der: impuissant adj. 无权无势的，无能的
k.en: lack of power or effectiveness

ex.en: In spite of their impuissance the group remains highly active.
ex.cn: 尽管手中无权，但这个组织仍然十分活跃。

m: IM (not) + PUISSANCE (power, French) — without power. — impuissance, powerlessness.

k.syn: casual, fluky, incidental, unintentional, unplanned, unpremeditated, unwitting
k.ant: calculated, deliberate, intended, intentional, planned, premeditated 有计划的，有预谋的
k.en: happening by chance

ex.en: an inadvertent encounter with a rattlesnake
ex.cn: 偶遇响尾蛇

k.syn: careless, feckless, heedless, irreflective, thoughtless, uncaring
k.ant: advertent, careful, heedful, mindful 留意的，小心的
k.en: marked by unintentional lack of care

ex.en: The military has said it was an inadvertent error.
ex.cn: 军方表示这是个疏忽大意的错误。

m: IN (not) + ADVERTENT (turning toward, attentive) — not attentive, accidental. — inadvertent, unintentional.

k.syn: untransferable
k.ant: alienable 可让与的
k.cn: 不可剥夺的，不能让与的
k.en: cannot be transferred to another or others

ex.en: inalienable rights of the citizen
ex.cn: 公民不可予夺的权利

m: IN (not) + ALIENABLE — cannot be alienated/taken away ("inalienable rights"). — inalienable, unable to be taken away.

k.syn: empty, insubstantial, pointless, senseless
k.ant: meaningful, significant 有意义的；deep, profound 深刻的
k.en: lacking significance, meaning, or point

ex.en: inane comments
ex.cn: 空洞的评论

m: INANE contains IN + ANE — "in vain" feel; empty (Latin INANIS = empty). — inane, silly / empty.

k.syn: dead, lifeless, insensible, insentient, senseless, unfeeling
k.ant: animate, living 有生命的
k.en: not having the qualities associated with active, living organisms

ex.en: He thinks that inanimate objects have a life of their own.
ex.cn: 他认为无生命的事物其实也是有生命的。

m: IN (not) + ANIMATE — not animated/alive. — inanimate, lifeless.

k.syn: induct, initiate, install, instate, invest, seat
k.ant: abdicate, resign 辞职，退位
k.en: to induct into an office by a formal ceremony

ex.en: A new leader will be inaugurated soon.
ex.cn: 新的领导即将就职。

k.syn: begin, commence, establish, institute, introduce, launch, open, plant, start
k.ant: cease, close, end, terminate 结束
k.en: to cause to begin, especially officially or formally

ex.en: inaugurate a new immigration policy
ex.cn: 实施新的移民政策

m: inaug ≈ 一诺. The new president raises his hand and makes 「一诺千金」—— a solemn pledge. — inaugurate, to formally install / launch.

k.syn: beaming, brilliant, dazzling, effulgent, glowing, lucent, luminous, lustrous, radiant, refulgent, shining
k.ant: dim, dull, lackluster 黯淡的
k.en: strikingly bright, radiant, or clear

ex.en: incandescent light bulbs
ex.cn: 明亮的灯泡

k.syn: ardent, demonstrative, emotional, fervid, impassioned, passionate, torrid, vehement
k.ant: cold, cool, dispassionate, emotionless, impassive, unemotional 无感情的，漠然的
k.cn: 热情饱满的，感情强烈的
k.en: characterized by glowing zeal

ex.en: It makes me incandescent with fury.
ex.cn: 它让我愤怒不已。

m: INCANDESCENT contains CAND (white-hot, Latin CANDERE) — glowing hot. (Same root as CANDLE.) — incandescent, brightly glowing / passionate.

k.syn: embodied, materialized, personified, typified
k.en: invested with bodily nature and form

ex.en: He referred to her as devil incarnate.
ex.cn: 他把她视作恶魔的化身。

k.syn: epitomize, incorporate, manifest, materialize, personalize, personify, substantiate, symbolize
k.ant: disembody 使（灵魂等）脱离躯体
k.en: to constitute an embodiment or type of

ex.en: the general view that Hitler incarnated extreme egotism
ex.cn: 通常认为希特勒代表了极端的以自我为中心

m: IN + CARN (flesh, as in CARNIVORE) + ATE — put into flesh, made physical. — incarnate, embodied in flesh.

k.syn: demagogue, exciter, firebrand, fomenter, inciter, instigator, kindler,
k.en: a person who stirs up public feelings especially of discontent

ex.en: behind-the-scenes incendiaries who were intending to overthrow the government
ex.cn: 企图推翻政府的幕后煽动者

m: INCENDIARY shares INCEND- with INCENSE / INCENSE-fire — fire-starting (literal or figurative). — incendiary, fire-starting / inflammatory.

k.syn: aroma, balm, bouquet, fragrancy, perfume, redolence, scent, spice
k.ant: fetor, malodor, reek, stench, stink 恶臭
k.en: a sweet or pleasant smell

ex.en: the heavenly incense of spring flowers
ex.cn: 春天花朵发出的美妙芳香

k.syn: aggravate, enrage, exasperate, inflame, infuriate, madden, outrage, rankle, rile, roil
k.ant: delight, gratify, please 取悦；appease, conciliate, mollify, pacify, placate, propitiate, soothe 缓和
k.en: to cause to be extremely angry

ex.en: This proposal will certainly incense female activists.
ex.cn: 这个提案肯定会激怒女权主义者。

m: INCENSE contains CENSE ≈ "to burn (church incense smoke); or to burn someone up = anger them." — incense, fragrant smoke / to anger.

k.syn: beginning, birth, commencement, dawn, genesis, kickoff, launch, nascence, onset, outset, start, threshold
k.ant: close, conclusion, end, termination, omega 结束，终止
k.der: inceptive adj. 开端的，初生的
k.en: an act, process, or instance of beginning

ex.en: This seemed like a good program at its inception, but it isn't working out as planned.
ex.cn: 一开始这像是个不错的项目，但是它没有按照我们的预期发展。

m: IN + CEPTION (take, as in RECEPTION/CONCEPT) — the taking-on of a beginning. — inception, the start.

k.syn: ceaseless, continual, nonstop, perpetual, running, unbroken, unceasing, uninterrupted, unremitting
k.ant: interrupted 中断的；discontinuous, intermittent 有间断的
k.en: continuing or following without interruption

ex.en: The incessant noise from an outside repair crew was a real distraction during the test.
ex.cn: 考试时，窗外维修工发出的持续不断的噪音着实让人分心。

m: IN (not) + CESSANT (ceasing) — not ceasing. — incessant, never-stopping.

k.syn: ace, hairbreadth, skip, neck, step
k.en: a very small distance or degree

ex.en: Give them an inch, and they'll take a mile
ex.cn: 得寸进尺。

ex.en: Inch by inch, we're making progress toward our fund-raising goal.
ex.cn: 我们在慢慢地接近筹款目标。

k.syn: crawl, creak, creep, limp, plod, slouch, snail
k.ant: dart, fleet, flit, scurry 飞奔，疾行
k.en: to move or cause to move slowly or by small degrees

ex.en: The car inched carefully across the snow-covered bridge.
ex.cn: 汽车慢慢地通过被大雪覆盖的桥梁。

m: INCH — the smallest measurement; to INCH = move an inch at a time. — inch, small distance / move slowly.

k.syn: aborning, beginning, inceptive, incipient, initial, nascent
k.ant: adult, full-blown, full-fledged, mature, ripe 成熟的，成型的； moribund 将死的
k.en: in an initial or early stage

ex.en: inchoate feelings of affection for a man
ex.cn: 刚刚产生的好感

k.syn: amorphous, formless, shapeless, undeveloped, unformed, unshaped
k.en: imperfectly formed or developed

ex.en: a vague, inchoate notion
ex.cn: 模糊而不成形的想法

m: INCHOATE rhymes with COW + TOAD weirdness — sounds half-formed itself. (Or: contains "in choate" ≈ "in CHO-(start)-ate.") — inchoate, just begun / not fully formed.

k.syn: burn, carbonize
k.ant: douse, extinguish, quench, put out 扑灭
k.en: to cause to burn to ashes

ex.en: The government is trying to stop farmers incinerating their own waste.
ex.cn: 政府开始阻止农民们焚烧垃圾的行为。

m: IN + CINER (ash, Latin CINIS) — turn to ASHES. (Cinerella → "of the ashes.") — incinerate, to burn to ashes.

k.syn: aborning, beginning, inceptive, inchoate, initial, nascent
k.ant: adult, full-blown, full-fledged, mature, ripe 成熟的，成型的； moribund 将死的
k.en: beginning to come into being or to become apparent

ex.en: an incipient economic recovery
ex.cn: 刚刚出现的经济复苏

m: shares CIPIENT- with INCEPTION — at the start. — incipient, just beginning.

k.syn: arouse, abet, foment, instigate, pick, provoke, raise, stir
k.ant: dampen, deter, discourage, dishearten, dissuade 劝阻
k.en: to provoke and urge on

ex.en: incite workers to strike
ex.cn: 煽动工人罢工

m: IN + CITE — "cite" them up to action; "cite" sounds like "sight" — set things in their sights. (Latin CITARE = stir up.) — incite, to stir up.

k.syn: bleak, harsh, severe, stormy, tempestuous
k.ant: bright, clear, cloudless, fair, sunny, sunshiny, unclouded 天气晴好的
k.en: lacking mildness

ex.en: inclement weather conditions
ex.cn: 恶劣的气象条件

k.syn: bitter, brutal, intemperate, rigorous
k.ant: charitable, clement, lenient, merciful 仁慈的
k.en: showing no clemency; unmerciful

m: IN (not) + CLEMENT (mild) — not mild, harsh. — inclement, harsh / merciless.

k.syn: discourteous, disrespectful, ill-mannered, impertinent, inconsiderate, rude, thoughtless, ungracious
k.ant: civil, considerate, courteous, genteel, gracious, thoughtful 体谅他人的，举止得体的
k.en: thoughtless; inconsiderate

ex.en: an incogitant litterbug
ex.cn: 不体谅人的乱扔垃圾者

m: IN (not) + COGITANT (thinking, as in COGITATE) — not thinking. — incogitant, thoughtless.

k.syn: conflicting, discordant, discrepant, dissonant, incompatible, incongruous, inconsonant
k.ant: congruent, congruous, consistent 一致的
k.en: not coinciding exactly when superimposed; not conforming to the circumstances or requirements of a situation

ex.en: Two triangles are incongruent.
ex.cn: 两个三角形不全等。

m: IN (not) + CONGRUENT — not matching up. — incongruent, mismatched.

k.syn: fallacious, illogical, invalid, irrational, unreasonable, unsound, weak
k.ant: logical, rational, reasonable, sound, valid, well-founded, well- grounded 有理的，论证有力的
k.en: not using or following good reasoning

ex.en: an inconsequential line of argument
ex.cn: 不合逻辑的论述

k.syn: fiddling, frivolous, inconsiderable, insignificant, minor, minute, negligible, nugatory, petty, slight, trivial
k.ant: consequential, eventful, important, meaningful, momentous, significant, substantial, weighty 重要的
k.en: of no significance

ex.en: That's an inconsequential problem compared to the other issues.
ex.cn: 和其他问题相比，这就是一个无关痛痒的小事。

m: IN (not) + CONSEQUENTIAL — having no consequence. — inconsequential, unimportant.

k.syn: certain, inarguable, incontestable, indisputable, indubitable, positive, sure, undeniable, unquestionable
k.ant: arguable, controversial, debatable, disputable, problematic, questionable, refutable 有争议的
k.en: not open to question

ex.en: incontrovertible facts
ex.cn: 无可非议的事实

m: IN (not) + CONTROVERTIBLE — cannot be controverted (argued against). (CONTRO = against, VERT = turn.) — incontrovertible, undeniable.

k.syn: incurable, irrecoverable, irredeemable, irremediable, irretrievable, unrecoverable, unredeemable
k.ant: curable, reclaimable, recoverable, redeemable, remediable, retrievable, savable 可以拯救的
k.cn: 不可救药的，积习难改的
k.en: incapable of being corrected or amended

ex.en: an incorrigible criminal deserving death penalty
ex.cn: 一个应该被判死刑的恶习难改的罪犯

k.syn: headstrong, intractable, obstinate, refractory, stubborn, unmanageable, unruly, willful
k.ant: compliant, docile, manageable, obedient, submissive, tractable 顺从的
k.en: difficult or impossible to control or manage

ex.en: an incorrigible, spoiled child
ex.cn: 被宠坏了的难管的孩子

m: IN (not) + CORRIG (correct, Latin CORRIGERE) — cannot be corrected. — incorrigible, unable to be reformed.

k.syn: charge, criminate, impeach, indict
k.ant: absolve, acquit, exculpate, exonerate, vindicate 免罪
k.en: to accuse of a crime or other wrongful act

ex.en: incriminate innocent people
ex.cn: 诬陷好人

m: IN + CRIMIN (crime) + ATE — pin a crime on. — incriminate, to charge with a crime.

k.en: an apparatus with a chamber used to provide controlled environmental conditions

m: from INCUBATE — the warm box where eggs or babies develop. — incubator, a warming chamber.

k.syn: charge, criminate, incubate, indict
k.ant: absolve, acquit, exculpate, exonerate, vindicate 免罪
k.en: incriminate

m: IN + CULPATE (fault, Latin CULPA) — pin fault onto. (Opposite of EXCULPATE.) — inculpate, to charge with blame.

k.syn: foray, inroad, invasion, irruption, raid
k.ant: retreat, withdrawal 撤退
k.en: a hostile entrance into a territory

ex.en: homes damaged by the incursion of floodwater
ex.cn: 被洪水入侵破坏的房屋

m: IN + CURSION (running, as in CURSOR) — running into enemy territory. — incursion, an invasion.

k.syn: ineffaceable, ineradicable, inerasable, ingrained
k.ant: eradicable, erasable, removable, washable 可抹去的
k.en: impossible to remove, erase, or wash away

ex.en: indelible ink
ex.cn: 难以擦拭的墨水

k.syn: impressive, memorable, unforgettable
k.ant: forgettable 容易被忘记的
k.en: not easily forgotten

ex.en: most indelible experiences
ex.cn: 最难以磨灭的经历

m: IN (not) + DELIBLE (erasable, Latin DELERE = erase, as in DELETE) — un-deletable. — indelible, unable to be erased.

k.syn: compensation, indemnification, quittance, recompense, redress, remuneration, reparation, requital
k.en: compensation for damage, loss, or injury suffered

ex.en: The widow now lives on a pension and an indemnity from her late husband's company.
ex.cn: 这个寡妇如今依靠社保和前夫公司的保险赔偿过日。

m: IN (not) + DEMN (damage, Latin DAMNUM) — protection against damage. — indemnity, compensation for damage.

k.syn: charge, criminate, impeach, incriminate
k.ant: absolve, acquit, exculpate, exonerate, vindicate 免罪
k.en: to accuse of wrongdoing; charge

ex.en: indict the mayor for fraud and embezzlement
ex.cn: 起诉市长受贿和挪用公款

m: INDICT contains DICT (say) — to formally SAY someone is charged (pronounced "in-DITE"). — indict, to formally accuse.

k.syn: disinterested, equal, impartial, just, nonpartisan, objective, unbiased, unprejudiced
k.ant: biased, one-sided, partial, prejudiced 有偏好的；unjust 不公平的
k.en: marked by impartiality

ex.en: They believed their art teacher could offer an indifferent judgment on their works' merits.
ex.cn: 他们相信艺术老师会对他们作品的价值做出公正的评价。

k.syn: average, common, fair, intermediate, mediocre, medium, moderate, ordinary, passable, so-so
k.ant: exceptional, extraordinary 极好的；inferior, poor 低档次的
k.cn: （质量、档次等）中规中矩的，一般的
k.en: of average or below average quality

ex.en: an indifferent but drinkable cup of coffee
ex.cn: 一杯尚可入口的咖啡

k.syn: aloof, apathetic, detached, incurious, nonchalant, numb, pococurante, remote, unconcerned, uninterested
k.ant: attentive, concerned, interested 关切的，有兴趣的
k.en: marked by a lack of interest, enthusiasm, or concern for something

ex.en: indifferent about the result of CET-4
ex.cn: 对四级成绩不感兴趣

m: IN (not) + DIFFERENT — "makes no difference to me." — indifferent, unconcerned.

k.syn: aboriginal, endemic, native
k.ant: exotic, extraneous, foreign 外来的；nonnative 非本地的
k.en: originating and living or occurring naturally in an area or environment

ex.en: the culture of the indigenous people of that country
ex.cn: 那个国家原住民的文化

k.syn: constitutional, essential, immanent, inborn, inbred, ingrained, innate, integral, intrinsic, natural
k.ant: adventitious, extrinsic 外在的；acquired, studied 后天习得的
k.en: being a part of the innermost nature of a person or thing

ex.en: The drive to create is indigenous to humanity.
ex.cn: 人类创造的动力是与生俱来的。

m: INDIGENOUS contains INDIGENE (native) — born of the place. (Latin INDU + GIGNERE = born within.) — indigenous, native.

k.syn: beggared, destitute, impecunious, impoverished, necessitous, needy, penniless, penurious, threadbare
k.ant: affluent, opulent, rich, wealthy 富裕的
k.en: lacking money or material possessions

ex.en: indigent people who require some outside assistance
ex.cn: 需要外来援助的贫苦人民

m: INDIGENT shares INDIG- with "in dig need" — Latin INDIGERE = lack. — indigent, poor / needy.

k.syn: critical, crucial, imperative, necessary, necessitous, needed, required, requisite, vital
k.ant: dispensable 可替代的；redundant, superfluous, surplus 多余的
k.cn: 必不可少的，不可或缺的
k.en: impossible to do without

ex.en: She was becoming indispensible to him.
ex.cn: 对他而言她逐渐变得不可替代。

m: IN (not) + DISPENSABLE — cannot be dispensed with. — indispensable, essential.

k.syn: educate, instruct, lesson, teach, train, tutor
k.ant: learn, study 学习
k.en: to instruct especially in fundamentals or rudiments

ex.en: indoctrinate students with the notion of egalitarianism
ex.cn: 向学生灌输平等主义的思想

m: IN + DOCTRINE — pour DOCTRINE in. — indoctrinate, to teach beliefs systematically.

k.syn: lazy, idle, shiftless, slothful, sluggish
k.ant: diligent, industrious 勤奋的
k.en: averse to activity, effort, or movement

ex.en: The sweltering afternoon made us indolent.
ex.cn: 炎热的午后使我们懒散不愿行动。

m: INDOLENT contains DOL ≈ "doll" or "DOLE-day" — taking it easy, lazy. (Latin IN-DOLERE = not feel pain, take it easy.) — indolent, lazy.

k.syn: goad, impulse, incentive, motive, spur, stimulus
k.ant: deterrent 抑制物
k.en: a motive or consideration that leads one to action

ex.en: offer an expensive watch as an inducement to ratify the proposal
ex.cn: 赠送名贵手表以求计划得到批准

k.syn: conversion, convincing, persuading, suasion
k.en: the act of reasoning or pleading with someone to accept a belief or course of action

ex.en: He gave up smoking only after a prolonged inducement by all the other family members.
ex.cn: 在所有家庭成员坚持不懈的劝说之下，他终于愿意戒烟了。

m: INDUCEMENT contains INDUCE — what INDUCEs you to act. — inducement, an incentive.

k.syn: decadent, forbearing, lenient, luxurious, tolerant, sybaritic
k.ant: ascetic 禁欲的；draconian 严酷的
k.en: showing, characterized by, or given to indulgence

ex.en: indulgent aristocrats
ex.cn: 生活放纵的贵族

m: INDULGENT contains INDULGE — letting yourself or others have too much. — indulgent, permissive.

k.syn: affectless, callous, heartless, inhumane, merciless, obdurate, pitiless, ruthless, unsparing, cold-blooded
k.ant: charitable, compassionate, humane, kindhearted, merciful, tender 仁慈的
k.cn: 铁石心肠的，冷酷无情的
k.en: having or showing a lack of sympathy or tender feelings

ex.en: an indurate heart that admits no love or mercy
ex.cn: 一颗不认可爱或仁慈的冷酷心灵

k.syn: concrete, congeal, firm, freeze, set, solidify
k.ant: liquefy 液化；soften 柔化
k.en: to become physically firm or solid

ex.en: Great heat indurates clay.
ex.cn: 高温使黏土变硬。

k.syn: fortify, inure, season, steel, strengthen, toughen
k.ant: enfeeble, weaken, undermine 弱化，使虚弱
k.cn: 使习惯于（如艰苦或尴尬之境况）
k.en: to make able to withstand physical hardship, strain, or exposure
