ex.en: Such a brutal upbringing could only callous his soul and indurate his heart to the suffering of others.
ex.cn: 这样一种残暴的培养方式只会让他的灵魂变得麻木，让他的心灵对于他人所受的折磨无动于衷。

m: INDURATE contains DURE (hard, as in DURABLE/ENDURE) — make hard. — indurate, to harden.

k.en: constantly, regularly, or habitually occupied: diligent

m: from INDUSTRIOUS — quality of being industrious. — industriousness, diligence.

k.syn: indefinable, indescribable, inexpressible, unspeakable, unutterable
k.ant: communicable 可传达的；expressible 可描述的
k.en: incapable of being expressed

ex.en: ineffable ecstasy
ex.cn: 无法用语言表达的狂喜

m: IN (not) + EFFABLE (sayable, Latin EFFARI = utter) — unsayable. — ineffable, indescribable.

k.syn: certain, inescapable, inevasible, inevitable, unavoidable
k.ant: avoidable, evadable 可避免的；uncertain, unsure（结果等）不确定的
k.en: not to be avoided, changed, or resisted

ex.en: Although death is an ineluctable fate for any and every individual organism, it lays the foundation of the metabolism that perpetuates the planetary ecosystem.
ex.cn: 尽管死亡对于任何单个的有机体而言是不可避免的宿命，但它奠定了能使整个行星生态系统永不衰竭的新陈代谢的基础。

m: IN (not) + E (out) + LUCT (struggle, Latin LUCTARI = wrestle) — can't struggle out of. — ineluctable, inescapable.

k.syn: absurd, foolish, fatuous, preposterous, stupid, witless, unwise
k.ant: judicious, prudent, sagacious, sapient, smart, wise 聪明的，明智的
k.en: displaying a lack of judgment, sense, or reason

ex.en: an inept and irresponsible remark on his paper
ex.cn: 对他文章的荒谬而不负责任的评论

k.syn: amiss, graceless, improper, inapposite, infelicitous, malapropos, perverse, unseemly, unsuitable
k.ant: appropriate, becoming, felicitous, fitting, genteel, proper, seemly, suitable 恰当的
k.en: not appropriate for a particular occasion or situation

k.syn: incapable, inexpert, unfitted, unskillful, unqualified
k.ant: capable, competent 有能力的；proficient, masterful 精通的，技艺精湛的
k.en: generally incompetent

ex.en: a hopelessly inept defense attorney
ex.cn: 严重不靠谱的辩护律师

m: IN (not) + EPT (apt) — not APT, not skilled. — inept, incompetent.

k.syn: dead, dormant, lethargic, idle, inactive, passive, torpid
k.ant: active, dynamic 充满活力的；passionate 饱含激情的
k.cn: （人）懒惰缺乏活力的；（物品）惰性的
k.en: sluggish in action or motion; deficient in active properties

ex.en: inert ingredients in drugs
ex.cn: 药品中的惰性成分

m: INERT shares INERT- with INERTIA — physically motionless / chemically unreactive. — inert, motionless / lacking vigor.

k.syn: certain, ineluctable, inescapable, inevasible, sure, unalterable, unavoidable
k.ant: evitable, avoidable 可避免的；uncertain, unsure 不确定的
k.en: incapable of being avoided or evaded

ex.en: The impact of the scandal on the election was inevitable.
ex.cn: 丑闻对于选举的冲击是不可避免的。

m: IN (not) + EVITABLE (avoidable, Latin EVITARE = avoid) — unavoidable. — inevitable, unavoidable.

k.syn: inflexible, adamant, obdurate, relentless, rigid, unyielding
k.ant: flexible 易受影响的；yielding 屈服的
k.cn: 无法劝阻的，不为所动的
k.en: not to be persuaded, moved, or stopped

ex.en: the seemingly inexorable rise in unemployment
ex.cn: 看起来无法阻止其高攀的失业率

m: IN (not) + EXORABLE (movable by prayer, Latin EXORARE = pray for) — won't be moved by pleas. — inexorable, relentless.

k.en: evil fame

m: from INFAMOUS — bad fame. — infamy, public disgrace.

k.syn: allure, captivate, bewitch, enchant, fascinate
k.ant: disgust 使反感
k.en: to inspire with unreasoning love or attachment

ex.en: a naïve girl infatuated by cajolery
ex.cn: 被甜言蜜语冲昏头脑的天真女孩

m: IN + FATU (foolish, as in FATUOUS) + ATE — make foolish (with love). — infatuate, to inspire foolish love.

k.syn: hapless, ill-fated, luckless, unfortunate, unhappy
k.ant: lucky, happy 幸运的，令人高兴的
k.en: not happy; unfortunate

ex.en: an infelicitous moment
ex.cn: 不幸的时刻

k.syn: inappropriate, indecorous, inept, malapropos, unfit, unseemly
k.ant: apt, appropriate, becoming, proper 合适的
k.en: not appropriate or well-timed

ex.en: make a very infelicitous remark
ex.cn: 做出极不恰当的评价

m: IN (not) + FELICITOUS (happy, Latin FELIX = happy) — not happy/well-chosen. — infelicitous, unfortunate / poorly worded.

k.syn: conflagration, holocaust
k.en: an intense fire

ex.en: A raging inferno posed a serious threat to the downwind villages.
ex.cn: 肆虐的大火给下风向的村庄带来了严重的威胁。

k.syn: hell, underworld
k.ant: paradise, heaven, nirvana 天堂，乐土
k.en: a place or a state that resembles or suggests hell

ex.en: the inferno of war
ex.cn: 地狱般的战场

m: INFERNO contains INFERN ≈ "infernal hell" — Dante's INFERNO. (Latin INFERNUS = lower regions, hell.) — inferno, a great fire / hell.

k.syn: creep, insinuate, penetrate, sneak, slip
k.ant: abscond 潜逃
k.en: to enter or take up positions in gradually or surreptitiously, as for purposes of espionage or takeover

ex.en: The intelligence staff had been infiltrated by spies.
ex.cn: 情报工作人员已经被间谍渗透了。

m: IN + FILTRATE — like water filtering through, sneak in. — infiltrate, to sneak in.

k.syn: endless, boundless, limitless, immeasurable, unfathomable
k.ant: finite 有限的；bounded, circumscribed, confined, definite, limited, restricted 有界限的，受限的
k.en: having no boundaries or limits

ex.en: the idea of an infinite universe
ex.cn: 无限宇宙的概念

m: IN (not) + FINITE — not finite, no end. — infinite, endless.

k.syn: debilitated, effete, enervated, feeble, frail, languid, sapped, unsubstantial
k.ant: hale, mighty, powerful, rugged, stalwart, stout, strong 强壮的，有力的
k.en: weak in body, especially from old age or disease

ex.en: her aging, infirm husband
ex.cn: 她上了年纪、身体虚弱的丈夫

k.syn: faltering, irresolute, vacillating, wavering
k.ant: resolute 果断的
k.en: lacking firmness of will, character, or purpose

ex.en: She has little patience with the“infirm of purpose”.
ex.cn: 她对那些优柔寡断的人没什么耐心。

m: IN (not) + FIRM — not firm; weak. — infirm, weak / sickly.

k.syn: enflame, enkindle, fire, ignite, kindle, light, torch
k.ant: douse, extinguish, quench, put out 熄灭，扑灭
k.en: to set on fire

ex.en: A carelessly tossed cigarette inflamed the papers in the trash can.
ex.cn: 一只被随意丢弃的香烟点燃了垃圾桶中的纸。

k.syn: aggravate, enrage, exacerbate, intensify, ire, irritate, provoke, rile, vex
k.ant: assuage, mitigate, mollify, pacify, placate, subdue 平息
k.en: to make more violent

ex.en: Retaliation served only to inflame the feud.
ex.cn: 冤冤相报何时了。

m: IN + FLAME — set FLAME inside. — inflame, to ignite / intensify.

k.syn: affluence, flux, income, inflow, inpouring, inrush
k.ant: exodus 大批离去；outflow, outpouring 流出
k.en: a coming in

ex.en: They anticipated an influx of tourists next month.
ex.cn: 他们预期下个月会有大批游客涌入。

m: IN + FLUX (flow) — flow IN. — influx, a flowing in.

k.syn: aggravate, enrage, exasperate, incense, ire, madden
k.ant: appease, assuage, pacify, placate, propitiate 平息，安抚；delight, gratify, please 取悦，讨好
k.en: to make furious

ex.en: be infuriated by the deliberate insults
ex.cn: 被蓄意的中伤激怒

m: IN + FURY + ATE — fill with FURY. — infuriate, to enrage.

k.syn: endue, imbue, implant, ingrain, instill, permeate, suffuse, steep
k.ant: extract 提取
k.en: to fill or cause to be filled with something

ex.en: New members infused enthusiasm into the club.
ex.cn: 新成员为俱乐部注入了激情。

k.syn: animate, exalt, motivate, stimulate
k.en: inspire, animate

ex.en: a sense of purpose that infuses scientific researchers
ex.cn: 给科学研究者打鸡血一般的使命感

m: IN + FUSE — pour IN, FUSE together. — infuse, to fill / steep.

k.syn: artful, clever, imaginative, innovative, inventive, original
k.ant: awkward 笨拙的；uncreative, unimaginative 无创造力或想象力的
k.en: marked by originality, resourcefulness, and cleverness in conception or execution

ex.en: Ingenious designers soon came up with a solution to the battery problem.
ex.cn: 聪明的设计者们很快便提出了电池问题的解决方案。

m: INGENIOUS contains GENI ≈ GENIUS — clever like a genius. — ingenious, cleverly inventive.

k.syn: creativeness, innovativeness, inventiveness, originality
k.ant: banality, cliché 陈词滥调
k.en: inventive skill or imagination

ex.en: There is little ingenuity in his articles.
ex.cn: 他的文章鲜有独创性。

m: from INGENIOUS — quality of being ingenious. — ingenuity, inventiveness.

k.syn: artless, innocent, guileless, naïve, simple, unaffected, unpretending, unsophisticated
k.ant: artful, cunning, sly 狡猾的；assuming, hypocritical 虚伪做作的； sophisticated, worldly 世故的，老练的
k.en: lacking in cunning, guile, or worldliness

ex.en: Photographs captured the ingenuous smiles of young children at play.
ex.cn: 摄影家捕捉到了演出中孩子们天真无邪的笑脸。

k.syn: candid, frank, open, plain, unconcealed
k.ant: ambiguous, equivocal, evasive 含糊其辞的
k.en: openly straightforward or frank

ex.en: her ingenuous thirst for knowledge
ex.cn: 她对知识毫不掩饰的渴望

m: INGENUOUS contains GENU ≈ "genuine" — innocently genuine. (Don't confuse with INGENIOUS = clever.) — ingenuous, innocent / candid.

k.syn: eat, consume, devour, intake
k.ant: evacuate, expel 排出；vomit 呕吐
k.en: to take into the body by the mouth for digestion or absorption

ex.en: An expert claims that the average person ingests considerably more calories than is necessary or desirable.
ex.cn: 一位专家宣称每个人平均摄取的卡路里大大超出了必要或者所需的范围。

m: IN + GEST (carry, as in DIGEST) — carry into the body. — ingest, to take in / swallow.

k.syn: constitutional, immanent, inborn, indigenous, inherent, innate, intrinsic
k.ant: adventitious 外来的，偶然的；extraneous, extrinsic 非本质的，外在的
k.der: ingrain vt. 灌输，使根深蒂固
k.en: forming a part of the essence or inmost being; firmly established

ex.en: ingrained prejudice against foreigners
ex.cn: 对外国人根深蒂固的偏见

m: IN + GRAINED — stained INTO the wood GRAIN, deeply embedded. — ingrained, deeply embedded.

k.en: an ungrateful person; one who does not appreciate kindness

m: IN (not) + GRATE (grateful) — not grateful. — ingrate, an ungrateful person.

k.syn: disarming, endearing, winsome
k.ant: disagreeable 令人厌恶的
k.en: capable of winning favor

ex.en: They adopted the orphan who had a most ingratiating smile.
ex.cn: 他们收养了那名有着最惹人喜欢的笑容的孤儿

k.syn: adulatory, deferential, fawning, flattering, toady, insinuating
k.en: intended or adopted in order to gain favor

ex.en: an repulsive ingratiating smile
ex.cn: 令人反感的阿谀奉承的微笑

m: INGRATIATING contains GRATI- (favor, Latin GRATIA = grace) — currying favor through flattery. — ingratiating, charmingly trying to please.

k.syn: constitutional, elemental, essential, inborn, ingrained, innate, intrinsic
k.ant: adventitious 外来的，偶然的；extraneous, extrinsic 外在的
k.en: involved in the constitution or essential character of something

ex.en: a disposition inherent in human nature
ex.cn: 人性内在的倾向

m: IN + HER (stick, Latin HAERERE) + ENT — sticking inside; built-in. (Same root as ADHERE, COHERE.) — inherent, built-in.

k.syn: antagonistic, hostile, opposing, unfriendly
k.ant: amiable, amicable, friendly, hospitable 友好的；amenable 服从的
k.en: reflecting or indicating hostility

ex.en: a cold, inimical voice
ex.cn: 冰冷而不友好的声音

k.syn: counter, disadvantageous, negative, prejudicial, unfavorable
k.ant: advantageous, favorable, positive, supportive, well-disposed 有利的，利好的
k.cn: 危及某人利益的，不利的
k.en: opposed to one's interests

ex.en: Laws were designed to enhance national security but some regard as inimical to cherished freedoms.
ex.cn: 法律旨在维护国家安全，但有人认为它危及了人们最为珍贵的权力——自由。

m: INIMICAL contains INIMI ≈ "enemy" (Latin INIMICUS = enemy). — inimical, hostile.

k.syn: incomparable, matchless, peerless, unique, unparalleled
k.ant: commonplace, ordinary 平凡的
k.en: not capable of being imitated

ex.en: her own inimitable style
ex.cn: 她特立独行的作风

m: IN (not) + IMITABLE — cannot be imitated. — inimitable, matchless.

k.syn: corruption, depravity, debauchery, evil, infamy, sin, unfairness, wickedness
k.ant: integrity, rectitude 正直；virtue 美德；disinterestedness 公正
k.en: gross immorality or injustice

ex.en: The use of illegal narcotics is not only a destroyer of personal health but also an iniquity that undermines our society.
ex.cn: 非法的毒品不仅仅摧残着个人健康，还是一颗危害社会的毒瘤。

m: IN (not) + IQUITY (equity, Latin AEQUUS = equal) — un-equal, unjust. — iniquity, gross injustice.

k.ant: terminate 终止
k.syn: begin, start, commence, inaugurate, introduce, launch
k.der: initiative n. 主动性，首创精神
k.en: to cause or facilitate the beginning of

ex.en: a chain reaction initiated by UV irradiation
ex.cn: 紫外线激发引起的连锁

k.syn: apprentice, beginner, novice, rookie, tyro
k.ant: veteran 身经百战的人
k.en: a person who is undergoing or has undergone an initiation

m: INITIATE contains INIT (begin, as in INITIAL) — begin / induct. — initiate, to start / induct.

k.syn: clue, cue, hint
k.en: a slight indication or suggestion

ex.en: They hadn't given us an inkling of what was going to happen.
ex.cn: 他们不给我们任何暗示将会发生什么。

k.syn: glimmer
k.ant: insight 深刻理解
k.en: a slight knowledge or vague notion

ex.en: We do not have even the faintest inkling of what the project was all about.
ex.cn: 我们对这个项目与什么有关毫不知情。

m: INKLING contains INK — just a tiny INK-spot of an idea, a hint. — inkling, a vague hint.

k.syn: anodyne, benign, harmless, inoffensive, nontoxic, safe
k.ant: damaging, detrimental, harmful, injurious, noxious, pernicious, invasive 有害的
k.en: producing no injury

ex.en: The government enacted a more strict regulation on innocuous preservatives.
ex.cn: 政府对无害防腐剂制定了更为严格的规定。

k.syn: bland, inoffensive, insipid, neutral, sapless
k.ant: provoking 刺激性的
k.cn: 乏味的，不会引起敌意的
k.en: not likely to give offense or to arouse strong feelings or hostility

ex.en: He made an innocuous remark to avoid conflict.
ex.cn: 为了避免冲突，他做了一个中庸的评价。

m: IN (not) + NOC (harm, Latin NOCERE) — harmless. (Same root as NOXIOUS.) — innocuous, harmless.

k.en: the introduction of something

m: INNOVATION contains NOV (new, Latin NOVUS) — making something new. — innovation, a new method.

k.syn: curious, inquiring, investigative, prying
k.ant: indifferent, unconcerned, uninterested 不感兴趣的；incurious 无好奇心的
k.en: inordinately or improperly curious about the affairs of others

ex.en: big sunglasses to frustrate inquisitive journalists
ex.cn: 让狗仔队的企图无法得逞的大墨镜

m: INQUISITIVE contains INQUIRE — full of inquiries. — inquisitive, curious.

k.syn: anesthetic, insensate, senseless, unfeeling, unconscious
k.ant: conscious 神志清醒的
k.en: having lost consciousness, especially temporarily

ex.en: The security guard was knocked insensible by a sudden blow.
ex.cn: 保安被突如其来的一击敲晕了。

k.syn: apathetic, bloodless, callous, dull, impassive, indifferent, nonchalant, phlegmatic
k.ant: concerned 关切的
k.en: not emotionally responsive

ex.en: insensible to workers' requests
ex.cn: 对工人的要求无动于衷

k.syn: crass, crude, incult, lowbred, tasteless, uncouth, uncultivated, uncultured, unpolished, unrefined, vulgar
k.ant: civilized, cultivated, cultured, genteel, polished, refined, smooth, tasteful 有品位的
k.en: lacking in refinement or good taste

ex.en: She married an insensible brute upon whom the niceties of life were completely lost.
ex.cn: 她嫁给了一个毫无品味的凡夫俗子，从此她的生活再无任何情趣。

m: IN (not) + SENSIBLE — unable to sense; or unconscious. — insensible, unaware / unconscious.

k.syn: gauche, impolite, insensible, tactless
k.ant: considerate 为他人着想的
k.cn: 缺乏机智圆滑的，缺乏社交技巧的
k.en: lacking tact

ex.en: so insensitive as to laugh at someone in pain
ex.cn: 如此缺乏社交技巧以至于嘲笑处于悲痛中的人

k.syn: anesthetized, dead, numb, senseless, unfeeling
k.ant: sensitive, tender 敏感的
k.en: not responsive or susceptible

ex.en: insensitive to either criticism or commendation
ex.cn: 对批评和表扬都不在乎

m: IN (not) + SENSITIVE — not sensitive. — insensitive, lacking awareness.

k.syn: discernment, perception, sagacity, sapience
k.ant: glimmer, inkling 略懂，略知一二
k.en: an instance of apprehending the true nature of a thing, especially through intuitive understanding

ex.en: an insight into the global-scale environmental problems
ex.cn: 对于全球性环境问题的深刻理解

m: IN + SIGHT — seeing INTO something deeply. — insight, deep understanding.

k.syn: flat, flavorless, mild, sapless, savorless, tasteless
k.ant: piquant 辛辣的
k.en: lacking flavor or zest; not tasty

ex.en: a rather insipid soup
ex.cn: 味道清淡的汤

k.syn: banal, bland, driveling, prosaic, tedious, uninteresting, vapid
k.ant: enchanting 引人入胜的
k.en: lacking in qualities that interest, stimulate, or challenge

ex.en: an insipid story of the prince and the princess
ex.cn: 一个关于王子和公主的无聊故事

m: INSIPID contains SIP — not even worth a SIP, tasteless. (Latin INSIPIDUS = without flavor.) — insipid, tasteless / dull.

k.syn: arrogant, audacious, bold, haughty, supercilious, impertinent, impudent
k.ant: courteous, polite 有礼貌的；respectful 恭敬的；meek, mousy, timid 胆小的
k.en: audaciously rude or disrespectful

ex.en: an insolent child with no respect or regard for anyone
ex.cn: 对他人不敬的无礼的小孩

m: IN (not) + SOLENT (custom, Latin SOLERE = be accustomed) — un-customary, rude. — insolent, rudely disrespectful.

k.syn: carefree, casual, indifferent, nonchalant, unconcerned
k.ant: anxious, careworn 焦虑的；concerned, worried 关注的，担心的
k.en: free from concern, worry, or anxiety

ex.en: an insouciant shrug
ex.cn: 无所谓地耸耸肩

m: IN (not) + SOUCI (worry, French) — without worry. — insouciant, carefree.

k.syn: appoint, designate, inaugurate, induct, install, nominate
k.ant: dismiss, oust 罢免
k.en: to set or establish in a rank or office

ex.en: The new Secretary of the Treasury was instated on Monday.
ex.cn: 新的财政部长于星期一被任命。

m: IN + STATE — put INTO STATE/position. — instate, to install in office.

k.syn: abet, arouse, excite, foment, goad, incite, inflame, provoke, stir
k.ant: assuage, allay, mitigate, mollify, pacify, soothe 平息，缓和
k.en: to goad or urge forward; to stir up

ex.en: This incident is instigated by a small group of people with ulterior motives.
ex.cn: 此次事件是被少数别有用心的人煽动起来的。

m: INSTIGATE contains STIG (prick, as in STIGMA) — prick someone into action. — instigate, to provoke.

k.syn: inject
k.ant: extract 抽取
k.en: to cause to enter drop by drop

ex.en: instill medication into the infected eye
ex.cn: 将药物滴入患者受感染的眼中

k.syn: breed, enroot, implant, inculcate, infix, infuse, ingrain, plant, sow
k.ant: remove 移除
k.en: to impart gradually

ex.en: instill a sense of responsibility to the young
ex.cn: 向年轻人灌输一种责任感

m: INSTILL contains STILL (drop, Latin STILLA) — pour in drop by drop. — instill, to gradually impart.

k.syn: begin, constitute, create, found, inaugurate, launch, start, set up
k.ant: abrogate, efface, rescind 废除；close, shut 关闭；phase out 淘汰
k.en: to establish, organize, and set in operation

ex.en: institute a new department
ex.cn: 创建一个新的部门

k.syn: association, institution, society
k.der: institution n. 机构；习俗，制度
k.en: an organization for the promotion of a cause

ex.en: a research institute
ex.cn: 研究机构

m: IN + STITUTE (set up, Latin STATUERE) — set up an organization. — institute, to establish / an organization.

k.en: a player

m: INSTRUMENTALIST contains INSTRUMENT — one who plays an instrument. — instrumentalist, a musician.

k.syn: balky, contumacious, intractable, mutinous, recalcitrant, rebellious, refractory
k.ant: amenable, docile, obedient, ruly, submissive, tractable 顺从的
k.en: not submissive to authority

ex.en: Insubordinate soldiers are court-martialed.
ex.cn: 抗命的士兵被送上了军事法庭。

m: IN (not) + SUBORDINATE — refusing to be subordinate. — insubordinate, disobedient.

k.syn: confined, local, narrow, parochial, provincial, regional, restricted
k.ant: cosmopolitan, ecumenical 有国际视野的；catholic（兴趣等）广泛的；receptive 善于接受的
k.en: being, having, or reflecting a narrow provincial viewpoint

ex.en: the insular thinking of peasant communities
ex.cn: 农民阶级的狭隘思想

m: INSULAR contains INSUL- (island, Latin INSULA) — isolated like an island. (Same root as ISOLATE.) — insular, narrowly isolated.

k.syn: block, isolate, quarantine, seclude, segregate, separate, sequester
k.ant: connect, link, unite 连结；integrate 使成一体
k.en: to place in a detached situation

ex.en: greenhouse gas that insulates ground infrared radiation
ex.cn: 能阻碍地表红外辐射的温室气体

m: INSULATE contains INSUL- (island) — make like an island, cut off. — insulate, to isolate / shield.

k.syn: rebel, anarchist, antagonist, malcontent, mutineer
k.en: one who breaks with or opposes constituted authority or the established order

ex.en: Insurgents armed with assault rifles and grenades ambushed a US convoy, resulting in heavy casualties.
ex.cn: 持有突击步枪和手榴弹的叛乱分子偷袭了美军车队，造成重大伤亡。

m: IN + SURGENT (rising, as in SURGE) — rising up against authority. — insurgent, a rebel.

k.syn: impalpable, imperceptible, imponderable, inappreciable, indiscernible, insensible, invisible
k.ant: corporeal 肉体的，有形的；palpable, tactile, tangible, touchable 可感知的
k.en: incapable of being perceived by the senses

ex.en: intangible value of a good reputation
ex.cn: 良好声誉的无形价值

m: IN (not) + TANGIBLE — can't be touched. (TANG- = touch.) — intangible, not graspable.

k.syn: critical, essential, indispensable, necessary, requisite, vital
k.ant: redundant, superfluous, surplus 多余的
k.en: essential to completeness

ex.en: an integral part of the undergraduate curriculum
ex.cn: 本科生课程的必修部分

k.syn: comprehensive, entire, full, grand, intact, perfect, plenary, total, whole
k.ant: imperfect, incomplete 不完美的，不完整的；partial 部分的
k.en: not lacking any part or member that properly belongs to it

ex.en: the belief that athletics are essential to an integral life
ex.cn: 坚信运动是完整的人生所不可或缺的一部分

m: INTEGRAL contains INTEG (whole, Latin INTEGER) — essential to the whole. — integral, essential.

k.syn: conscience, honesty, incorruptibility, rectitude, righteousness, scrupulousness
k.ant: baseness 卑鄙；deceit, deceitfulness, dishonesty, lying, mendacity, untruthfulness 欺骗
k.en: steadfast adherence to a strict moral or ethical code, devotion to telling the truth

ex.en: After a thorough investigation into“Climategate”, the panel concluded that the integrity of scientific community is still sound.
ex.cn: 经过对“气候门事件”的彻底调查，专家组认为学术界的信誉和道德仍然是值得信赖的。

k.syn: completeness, entireness, perfection, wholeness
k.en: the quality or condition of being whole or undivided

ex.en: try to maintain the integrity of the falling empire
ex.cn: 尽力维持日趋西山的帝国的统一

m: from INTEGER (whole) — wholeness of character. — integrity, moral wholeness.

k.syn: accessible, apprehensible, comprehensible, fathomable, lucid, understandable
k.ant: abstruse, recondite, inscrutable, insensible 难以理解的
k.en: capable of being understood

ex.en: military codenames intelligible only to those high-rank commanding officers
ex.cn: 只有高级指挥官才能理解的军事暗号

m: INTELLIGIBLE contains INTELLIG- (understand, Latin INTELLIGERE) — able to be understood. — intelligible, comprehensible.

k.syn: excessive, extreme, immoderate, inordinate, overindulgent, unrestrained
k.ant: equable 温和的；bridled, checked, constrained, controlled, curbed, governed, hampered, hindered, trammeled 受限制的，节制的
k.cn: 无节制的，极端的，不温和的
k.en: not temperate or moderate

ex.en: The tone of the article is very intemperate.
ex.cn: 文章的论调很极端。

m: IN (not) + TEMPERATE (moderate) — un-moderated. — intemperate, immoderate.

k.syn: accentuate, aggravate, amplify, deepen, enhance, magnify, redouble, strengthen
k.ant: abate, assuage, attenuate, mitigate, moderate 减缓，降低（程度）
k.en: to make intense or more intensive

ex.en: Both companies intensified their efforts to win the contract.
ex.cn: 为了获得这份合同，两家公司都加大了投入。

m: INTENSIFY contains INTENSE — make more intense. — intensify, to make more intense.

k.syn: bury, entomb, inhume
k.ant: dig, disinter, excavate, exhume, unearth 掘出
k.en: to place in a grave or tomb

ex.en: The infamous terrorist leader Bin Laden was interred at sea.
ex.cn: 臭名昭著的恐怖分子本·拉登被葬于海中。

m: INTER- means "into the earth" — to bury (Latin TERRA = earth). — inter, to bury.

k.syn: broker, buffer, conciliator, intermediate, mediator, peacemaker
k.ant: flame-fanner 煽风点火的人
k.en: one that mediates

ex.en: attend the meeting as the intercessor
ex.cn: 作为调停人出席会议

m: INTER (between) + CESSOR (one who goes, Latin CEDERE) — one who goes between. — intercessor, a mediator.

k.syn: ban, forbid, prohibit, proscribe
k.ant: authorize 授权；allow, permit, suffer 允许；approve, endorse, ratify, sanction 批准
k.en: to forbid in a usually formal or authoritative manner

ex.en: Though not interdicted by law, such an action is morally wrong.
ex.cn: 尽管这种行为不被法律所禁止，但是道德上来说是不对的。

k.syn: bar, block, check, hinder, intercept, impede, obstruct
k.ant: expedite 促进，加速
k.en: to stop, seize, or interrupt while in progress or on course

ex.en: Federal agents are able to interdict only a small percentage of the narcotic shipments into the country.
ex.cn: 联邦调查员们只能阻止一小部分流入国内的毒品。

m: INTER (between) + DICT (say) — say between (issue a prohibition between parties). — interdict, to forbid.

k.syn: breach, break, gap, interruption, interval, interlude, parenthesis
k.ant: continuation, continuity 持续
k.en: an interval of time between one event, process, or period and another

ex.en: Richard Wagner's operas usually require an interim of more than 30 minutes for performers to have a break.
ex.cn: 理查德·瓦格纳的歌剧通常需要长达 30多分钟的幕间休息时间以供演员调整。

k.syn: acting, provisional, temporary
k.ant: eternal, permanent 永恒的
k.en: serving in a position for the time being

ex.en: an interim government to maintain social stability
ex.cn: 维持社会稳定的临时政府

m: INTERIM contains INTER (between) — between two periods. — interim, in-between time.

k.syn: associate, connect, join, link, unite
k.ant: sunder 拆散
k.der: interlocking adj. 连锁的 “未曾想与雄鹰争锋，来赢得他人艳羡的目光，我却凭着志在四方的信念和风雨兼程的决心，成为站在金字塔尖的蜗牛，沐浴着清风，唱响青春无悔的乐章！” （汪洋，2009年10月，Verbal 710，Quantitative 800）
k.en: to become united or joined closely, as by hooking or dovetailing

ex.en: The branches of the trees interlock to form a natural archway.
ex.cn: 树枝相互缠绕在一起，形成了天然的拱门。

m: INTER + LOCK — LOCK things together. — interlock, to lock together.

k.syn: continual, ceaseless, endless, everlasting, perpetual
k.ant: completed 完成的
k.en: having or seeming to have no end

ex.en: A large audience fell asleep during the interminable sermon.
ex.cn: 许多观众都在冗长的布道演说中睡着了。

m: IN (not) + TERMINABLE (able to end, TERMIN- = end) — un-endable. — interminable, endless.

k.syn: episodic, erratic, occasional, periodical, recurrent, recurring
k.ant: constant, continuous, incessant, unceasing 持续不断的
k.en: coming and going at intervals, not continuous

ex.en: intermittent rain in June
ex.cn: 六月里断断续续的梅雨

k.syn: aperiodic, casual, catchy, discontinuous, irregular, occasional, spasmodic, sporadic, unsteady
k.ant: habitual 习惯的，惯常的；periodic, regular, repeated 定期的，有规律的，重复的
k.en: lacking in steadiness or regularity of occurrence

ex.en: The husband's intermittent employment put the family in a difficult position financially.
ex.cn: 丈夫不稳定的工作使得全家陷入了经济上的困境。

m: INTER + MITT (send) + ENT — sending in between, with gaps. — intermittent, occurring in bursts.

k.syn: breach, break, gap, interim, interruption, interval, interlude, parenthesis
k.ant: continuation, continuity 持续
k.en: break in continuity

ex.en: The democratic regime proved to be a short-lived interregnum between dictatorships.
ex.cn: 这个民主政权被证明只不过是两个独裁政权之间的短暂过渡罢了。

m: INTER (between) + REGN (rule, Latin REX/REGIS = king) — between reigns. — interregnum, a gap between rulers.

k.syn: ask, grill, inquire, milk, question
k.ant: answer, reply, respond 回答，回复
k.en: to question formally and systematically

ex.en: interrogate the prisoner of war for valuable intelligence
ex.cn: 审问战俘以获取有价值的情报

m: INTERROGATE contains ROGATE (ask, Latin ROGARE) — to ask between (formally question). — interrogate, to question formally.

k.syn: familiar, close, confidential
k.ant: distant, remote 疏远的
k.cn: 有紧密联系的，亲密无间的
k.en: marked by very close association, contact, or familiarity

ex.en: intimate friends since childhood
ex.cn: 孩童时期以来的密友

k.syn: confidential, esoteric, nonpublic, secret
k.ant: open, public 公开的
k.en: not known or meant to be known by the general populace

ex.en: They broke up after she shared intimate information with all 500 of her closest friends.
ex.cn: 在她把那些不为人知的消息告诉了她500个密友之后，他们分手了。

k.syn: acquaintance, amigo, comrade, confidant, friend, insider, mate
k.ant: stranger 陌生人；enemy, foe 敌人
k.en: a person who has a strong liking for and trust in another

ex.en: Usually quite aloof in public, he's actually quite relaxed with his intimates.
ex.cn: 通常他在大众面前显得比较孤傲，但和好朋友在一起时他却是个很放松的人。

k.syn: allude, connote, hint, imply, indicate, infer, suggest
k.ant: articulate 清晰明确地说
k.cn: 迂回地交流，间接地沟通
k.en: to communicate delicately and indirectly

ex.en: intimate a wish to leave
ex.cn: 暗示想离开

m: INTIMATE contains INTIM- (innermost, Latin INTIMUS) — known to one's innermost circle. (Verb form: hint at something innermost.) — intimate, closely familiar / to hint.

k.syn: browbeat, bully, coerce, cow, frighten, hector, terrify
k.ant: blandish, cajole, coax 用花言巧语讨好
k.en: to make timid or fearful, frighten

ex.en: He refused to be intimidated by the manager.
ex.cn: 他对经理的恐吓不为所动。

m: INTIMIDATE contains TIMID — make someone timid through fear. — intimidate, to frighten into submission.

k.syn: alcohol, liquor, stimulant
k.ant: refresher 使人清醒的东西
k.cn: 使人陶醉的东西（尤指酒精饮料）
k.en: an agent that intoxicates, especially an alcoholic beverage

m: INTOXICANT contains TOXIC — a substance that puts toxic-like effects in you. — intoxicant, an inebriating substance.

k.syn: adamant, headstrong, intractable, obstinate, pertinacious, stubborn, uncompromising, unyielding
k.ant: compliant, pliable 易受影响的；tractable, yielding 容易驾驭的
k.en: characterized by refusal to compromise or to abandon an extreme position

ex.en: an intransigent attitude
ex.cn: 不妥协的态度

m: IN (not) + TRANS (across) + IGENT — won't cross over to your side. (Latin INTRANSIGENT = not coming to terms.) — intransigent, refusing to compromise.

k.syn: audacious, brave, bold, courageous, dauntless, fearless, gallant, valiant, valorous
k.ant: apprehensive 恐惧的；cowardly, craven, gutless, pusillanimous, timorous 怯懦的，胆小的
k.en: characterized by resolute fearlessness, fortitude, and endurance

ex.en: an intrepid explorer
ex.cn: 无畏的探险家

m: IN (not) + TREPID (timid, Latin TREPIDARE = tremble) — not trembling, fearless. — intrepid, fearless.

k.syn: conspiracy, design, intrigue, machination, scheme
k.en: a secret plan for accomplishing evil or unlawful ends

ex.en: The intrigue was quickly discovered, and the would-be assassins were arrested.
ex.cn: 随着阴谋的迅速识破，未得手的刺客被逮捕了。

k.syn: appeal, attract, enthrall, entice, excite, fascinate, interest, occupy
k.ant: ennui, pall 使厌倦
k.en: to arouse the interest, desire, or curiosity of

ex.en: The children are apparently intrigued by the tale.
ex.cn: 孩子们很明显被这个传奇故事吸引了。

m: INTRIGUE contains TRIG ≈ "trick" — a TRICK-y plot. — intrigue, a secret plot / to fascinate.

k.syn: congenial, constitutional, immanent, inborn, inherent, innate, native, natural
k.ant: adventitious, extraneous, extrinsic 外在的
k.en: of or relating to the essential nature of a thing

ex.en: the intrinsic worth of a gem
ex.cn: 宝石的内在价值

m: INTRINSIC contains IN + TRINS ≈ "inwards" — belonging within, essential. — intrinsic, inherent.

k.syn: instinctive
k.ant: acquired 后天习得的
k.en: knowing or perceiving by intuition

ex.en: The twins have an intuitive awareness of each other's feelings.
ex.cn: 这对双胞胎天生就能感知彼此的感受。

m: INTUITIVE contains IN + TUITION (looking, Latin INTUERI) — perceived inwardly without proof. — intuitive, instinctively known.

k.syn: avalanche, deluge, drown, engulf, overflow, overwhelm, submerge
k.ant: drain 排空
k.en: to cover with or as if with flood

ex.en: inundated with trash e-mails
ex.cn: 被垃圾邮件所淹没

m: INUNDATE contains UND (wave, Latin UNDA) — wave over. (Same root as ABUNDANT.) — inundate, to flood.

k.syn: accustom, familiarize, habituate
k.cn: 使习惯接受不好的东西
k.en: to accustom to accept something undesirable

ex.en: children inured to violence
ex.cn: 习惯了暴力的孩子们

k.syn: fortify, indurate, season, steel, strengthen, toughen
k.ant: enfeeble, soften, weaken, undermine 弱化，使虚弱
k.en: to make able to withstand physical hardship, strain, or exposure

ex.en: The hardship of army training inured her to the rigors of desert warfare.
ex.cn: 军营里的艰苦训练把她打造成了能适应沙漠艰苦战事的女兵。

m: INURE rhymes with ENDURE — to inure = make able to endure hardship. — inure, to harden by habit.

k.syn: abusive, opprobrious, scurrile, scurrilous, truculent, vitriolic, vituperative
k.ant: adulatory, flattery 阿谀奉承的；complimentary 称赞的
k.en: of, relating to, or characterized by insult or abuse

ex.en: invective comments on female activists
ex.cn: 对女权主义者的侮辱性评论

m: IN + VECT (carry, Latin VEHERE, as in VEHICLE) + IVE — verbal attack carried at someone. — invective, abusive language.

k.syn: gripe, grouse, object, protest, remonstrate, repine
k.ant: support 支持；delight, rejoice 感到高兴
k.cn: 激烈抗议，表示强烈不满
k.en: to protest or complain bitterly or vehemently

ex.en: inveigh against the bank industry
ex.cn: 对银行业表示强烈不满

m: INVEIGH rhymes with WEIGH — to inveigh = throw your full WEIGHT against in protest. — inveigh, to denounce vehemently.

k.syn: allure, bait, decoy, entice, entrap, seduce, tempt
k.ant: demand 强求
k.en: to win over by coaxing, flattery, or artful talk

ex.en: inveigle consumers into buying the item
ex.cn: 诱骗顾客购买商品

m: INVEIGLE rhymes with EAGLE — but a sneaky EAGLE-eyed lure. (Old French AVEUGLE = blind — to blind someone with flattery.) — inveigle, to entice by flattery.

k.syn: budget, pool, repertoire, reservoir, stock
k.en: a detailed, itemized list, report, or record of things in one's possession, especially a periodic survey of all goods and materials in stock

ex.en: The dealership has an unusually large inventory of pre-owned vehicles.
ex.cn: 销售商手中有着一份巨大的二手车库存单。

k.syn: abstract, brief, epitome, outline, résumé, summarization, synopsis
k.en: a short statement of the main points

ex.en: They decided to offer the public an informative inventory of everything that is known about the virus at this time.
ex.cn: 他们决定向公众提供一份极富价值的报告，它涵盖了目前为止关于这种病毒的所有认识。

m: INVENTORY contains INVENT — what's been INVENT-oried (catalogued). (Latin INVENIRE = find, list.) — inventory, a stock list.

k.syn: chronic, entrenched, ingrained, rooted, settled, hard-cored
k.ant: adventitious 偶然的
k.en: firmly established by long persistence

ex.en: the inveterate tendency to overlook the obvious
ex.cn: 总是忽视显而易见事物的习惯

m: INVETERATE contains VETER- (old, as in VETERAN) — long-grown-old habit. — inveterate, deeply ingrained.

k.syn: abhorrent, detestable, obnoxious, odious, repugnant, repellent
k.ant: agreeable, gratifying, pleasant 令人高兴的
k.en: tending to cause discontent, animosity, or envy

ex.en: the invidious task of arbitration
ex.cn: 令人反感的仲裁任务

k.syn: covetous, envious, jaundiced, jealous, resentful, green-eyed
k.en: having or showing mean resentment of another's possessions or advantages

ex.en: Inevitably, his remarkable success attracted the invidious attention of the other sales representatives.
ex.cn: 不可避免地，他的巨大成功引来了其他销售代表羡慕嫉妒恨的眼光。

m: INVIDIOUS shares VID with ENVY (Latin INVIDIA = envy/spite) — likely to provoke envy or hatred. — invidious, causing resentment.

k.syn: animate, energize, fortify, reinforce, strengthen, vitalize
k.ant: dampen, deaden, debilitate, emaciate, sap 使衰弱；demoralize 使士气衰落
k.cn: 使精神，使强壮，鼓舞激励
k.en: to impart vigor, strength, or vitality to

ex.en: news that invigorates the public
ex.cn: 给大众打鸡血的新闻

m: IN + VIGOR + ATE — fill with VIGOR. — invigorate, to energize.

k.syn: bulletproof, impregnable, invulnerable, unbeatable, unconquerable
k.ant: conquerable 可被征服的；surmountable 可超越的；vulnerable 脆弱的，敏感的
k.cn: 不可战胜的，不可超越的
k.en: incapable of being conquered, overcome, or subdued

ex.en: The soccer team proved to be invincible.
ex.cn: 这支球队证明了自己是不可战胜的。

m: IN (not) + VINCIBLE (defeatable, Latin VINCERE = conquer) — cannot be conquered. — invincible, unbeatable.

k.syn: enforce, effect, execute, implement, perform
k.ant: suspend 暂停，搁置
k.en: to put into effect or operation

ex.en: New train timetable has been invoked.
ex.cn: 新的火车时刻表已经生效。

k.syn: beget, bring, catalyze, cause, create, engender, generate, induce, produce, result
k.en: to be the cause of（a situation, action, or state of mind）

ex.en: We should be prepared for the possibility that any solution may invoke another set of problems.
ex.cn: 我们必须要为这样一种可能做好心理准备：任何解决方案都可能带来一系列新的问题。

m: IN + VOKE (call, Latin VOCARE, as in VOICE) — call IN, summon. — invoke, to call upon / put into effect.

k.syn: choleric, irritable, peevish, petulant
k.ant: affable 和蔼的
k.en: marked by hot temper and easily provoked anger

ex.en: The new boss is so irascible that several employees have resigned.
ex.cn: 由于新老板太容易发飙，许多员工已经递交了辞呈。

m: IRASCIBLE contains IRA (anger, Latin) — quick to anger. (Same root as IRE, IRATE.) — irascible, easily angered.

k.syn: aggravated, apoplectic, choleric, enraged, exasperated, infuriated, ireful, mad, wrathful
k.ant: calm, halcyon 平静的；delighted, pleased 高兴的，满意的
k.en: extremely angry

ex.en: an irate taxpayer
ex.cn: 极为愤怒的纳税人

m: IRATE contains IRA (anger, Latin) — full of ire. — irate, intensely angry.

k.en: a lustrous rainbowlike play of color

m: IRIDESCENCE contains IRIS (the rainbow goddess) — rainbow-shimmering. — iridescence, rainbow-like color play.

k.syn: aggravation, bother, exasperation, frustration, headache, irritant, nuisance, vexation
k.en: something that is a source of irritation

ex.en: One of the prof's major irks is a cell phone that rings during a lecture.
ex.cn: 课堂上突然响起的手机铃声是最让教授火大的事情之一。

k.syn: annoy, bother, fret, gall, provoke, ruffle, vex
k.ant: appease, assuage, pacify, placate, propitiate, soothe 安抚，平息
k.en: to cause to be irritating, wearisome, or vexing to

ex.en: She irked her friends by chewing her gum loudly during the movie.
ex.cn: 她因为看电影时嚼口香糖弄出声响而惹怒了她的朋友们。

m: IRK rhymes with JERK — a JERK irks you. — irk, to annoy.

k.syn: firm, invulnerable, secure, sound, tenacious, tough
k.ant: fragile 脆弱的
k.cn: 非常坚固的，坚不可摧的
k.en: so firm or secure as to be unbreakable

ex.en: pride on their ironclad fleet
ex.cn: 对他们坚不可摧的舰队感到自豪

m: IRON + CLAD — clothed in IRON; unbreakable. — ironclad, indestructible.

k.syn: entrenched, ineradicable, ingrained, inveterate, rooted
k.ant: eradicable 可根除的
k.en: impossible to uproot or destroy

ex.en: Smoking has become an irradicable bad habit for him.
ex.cn: 吸烟已成了他不能根除的恶习。

m: IR (not) + RADIC (root, Latin RADIX, as in RADISH) + ABLE — cannot be uprooted. — irradicable, unable to be removed.

k.ant: factorable 可约分的
k.en: incapable of being factored into polynomials of lower degree with coefficients in some given field（as the rational numbers）or integral domain（as the integers）

ex.en: irreducible integrals
ex.cn: 质数

m: IR (not) + REDUCIBLE — cannot be reduced. — irreducible, cannot be made smaller.

k.syn: water
k.en: to supply（dry land）with water by means of ditches, pipes, or streams; water artificially

ex.en: irrigate crops periodically
ex.cn: 定期灌溉农作物

k.syn: flush, rinse, wash
k.en: to flush（a body part）with a stream of liquid（as in removing a foreign body or medicating）

ex.en: irrigate the wound
ex.cn: 冲洗伤口

m: IRRIGATE contains RIG ≈ "riGate" → rivers. (Latin RIGARE = water.) — irrigate, to water (land).

k.syn: aggravate, annoy, exasperate, gall, inflame, nettle, peeve, provoke, rile, roil
k.ant: appease, assuage, pacify, placate, propitiate, soothe 安抚，平息
k.en: to provoke impatience, anger, or displeasure in

ex.en: His rude interruptions really irritated her.
ex.cn: 他粗鲁的打断激怒了她。

m: IRRITATE contains RIT ≈ "grit" — like grit in your eye. — irritate, to annoy.

k.syn: nut, problem, question
k.en: a matter that is in dispute between two or more parties

ex.en: focused on economic and political issues
ex.cn: 重点关注经济和政治话题

k.syn: print
k.en: to produce and release for distribution in printed form

ex.en: plans to issue a monthly newsletter
ex.cn: 计划发布新闻月刊

k.syn: discharge, emit, exude, release, vent
k.ant: withdraw 撤回
k.en: to（cause to）go, come, or flow out

ex.en: strange sound issued from the abandoned house
ex.cn: 废弃房中传来的奇怪声音

m: ISSUE rhymes with TISSUE — a single TISSUE issues from the box; an ISSUE comes out into discussion. — issue, to come out / a topic.

k.en: a narrow strip of land

m: ISTHMUS contains ISTH ≈ "this thmus," the narrow neck of land. (Greek ISTHMOS = neck.) — isthmus, a narrow strip of land.

k.en: the route of a journey or the proposed outline

m: ITINERARY contains ITINER (journey, Latin ITER) — a journey plan. — itinerary, a travel schedule.

k.syn: babble, blabber, drivel, gabble, gibber, mumbo jumbo
k.ant: speak slowly 慢慢地说
k.en: to talk rapidly, indistinctly, or unintelligibly

ex.en: monkeys jabbering at each other in their cages
ex.cn: 在笼子里唧唧歪歪的猴子

m: JABBER rhymes with BLABBER — both fast unintelligible talk. — jabber, to talk rapidly.

k.syn: drained, exhausted, fatigued, prostrate, spent, wearied, worn-out
k.en: depleted in strength, energy, or freshness

ex.en: After that long bar exam, I'm too jaded for anything but a nap.
ex.cn: 漫长的律师资格考试结束后，我累得只想睡觉。

k.syn: bored, tired, wearied, fed up
k.ant: absorbed, engaged, engrossed, interested, intrigued, rapt 有兴趣的
k.cn: 厌倦的，没兴趣没热情的
k.en: having one's patience, interest, or pleasure exhausted

ex.en: Even jaded sci-fi fans are finding this new space adventure fresh and exciting.
ex.cn: 即使是见多识广、口味很挑的科幻小说忠粉也觉得这次的太空探险非常新鲜有趣。

m: JADED contains JADE — like an overworked old JADE (a worn-out horse). — jaded, weary / disillusioned.

k.syn: jest, quip, wisecrack, banter, chaff, gag, jive, jolly, josh
k.ant: revere 尊敬
k.en: to say or do something jokingly or mockingly

ex.en: The characters in Oscar Wilde's plays jape with a sophistication that is rarely encountered in real life.
ex.cn: 奥斯卡·王尔德戏剧作品中的角色用一种十分世故的态度进行嘲讽，而这种态度在真实生活当中是很少见的。

m: JAPE rhymes with GAPE — when you JAPE someone, they GAPE in shock. — jape, to mock.

k.syn: argot, cant, dialect, jive, lingo, patois
k.en: the specialized or technical language of a trade, profession, or similar group

ex.en: medical jargon that the layman cannot understand
ex.cn: 外行不理解的医学术语

m: JARGON rhymes with ARGON (an inert gas) — both confusing to outsiders, hidden language. — jargon, specialized vocabulary.

k.syn: grating, cacophonous, strident
k.ant: melodious 音调优美的
k.en: harsh or discordant

ex.en: The final chord of that song is too jarring for me.
ex.cn: 那首歌最后的合声部分太刺耳了。

k.syn: amazing, astonishing, astounding, blindsiding, dumbfounding, flabbergasting, jaw-dropping, jolting, shocking, startling, stunning
k.en: causing a strong emotional reaction because of unexpectedness

ex.en: The jarring news that major financial institutions were on the verge of collapse.
ex.cn: 有消息称主要的几个金融机构处于倒闭的边缘，真是让人震惊。

m: JARRING contains JAR — like banging a JAR loudly, startling. — jarring, shocking / discordant.

k.syn: animosity, animus, antagonism, antipathy, gall, hostility, rancor
k.ant: amity 和睦，好感
k.cn: （因嫉妒或厌世而产生的）偏见
k.en: to affect with the negativity or bitterness of jaundice; bias

ex.en: the jaundice in the eyes of the two feuding neighbors
ex.cn: 两个有积怨的邻居之间的偏见

m: JAUNDICE contains JAUN ≈ "yellow" (French JAUNE) — yellow with disease/envy. — jaundice, biased / envious view.

k.syn: animate, brisk, energetic, frisky, perky, racy, spirited, vivacious
k.ant: staid, dead, inactive, inanimate, lackadaisical, languid, languishing, leaden, limp, listless, spiritless, vapid 无生气的
k.en: sprightly in manner or appearance: lively

ex.en: a jaunty stroll
ex.cn: 轻快的散步

m: JAUNTY rhymes with HAUGHTY but happier — a JAUNTY hat tilted with cheer. — jaunty, lively / spirited.

k.syn: arid, drab, dreary, leaden, monotonous, ponderous, tedious, weary
k.ant: absorbing, engaging, engrossing, gripping, interesting, intriguing, involving, riveting, thought provoking 促人深思的，吸引人的
k.en: not interesting; dull

ex.en: jejune lectures
ex.cn: 无聊的讲座

k.syn: adolescent, immature, infantile, juvenile, kiddish, puerile
k.ant: adult, grown-up, mature 成熟的
k.en: having or showing the annoying qualities（as silliness）associated with children

ex.en: an essay filled with jejune, simplistic opinions about international politics
ex.cn: 充满着有关国际政治的幼稚、简单看法的文章

m: JEJUNE rhymes with JUNE but means dull — June flowers turning to dust. (Latin JEJUNUS = empty stomach.) — jejune, dull / immature.

k.syn: distress, endangerment, imperilment, peril
k.ant: safeness, safety, secureness, security 安全
k.en: risk of loss or injury; peril or danger

ex.en: The city's firefighters routinely put their lives in jeopardy.
ex.cn: 消防队员们早就把生命置之度外了。

m: JEOPARDY rhymes with LEOPARD — facing a LEOPARD is jeopardy. (From the TV show "Jeopardy!".) — jeopardy, danger.

k.syn: butt, derision, mockery
k.ant: solemnity, solemn utterance 严肃
k.en: a frivolous mood or manner

ex.en: spoken in jest
ex.cn: 戏谑地说

m: JEST rhymes with WEST — a court JESTER tells JESTs (jokes). — jest, a joke.

k.syn: abandon, abdicate, discard, junk, reject, scrap, shed, slough, throw away
k.ant: rescue, salvage 救援，拯救
k.cn: 放弃，拒绝接受（想法、计划等）
k.en: to get rid of as superfluous or encumbering; omit or forgo as part of a plan or as the result of some other decision

ex.en: The Government seems to have jettisoned the bail-out plan.
ex.cn: 政府似乎已经放弃了这个救市计划。

m: JETTISON contains JET — JET cargo overboard from a sinking ship. — jettison, to throw away.

k.syn: accord, cohere, conform, correspond, harmonize, tally
k.ant: conflict 冲突
k.en: to be in accord: agree

ex.en: Your figures jibe with mine.
ex.cn: 你的数据与我的一致。

m: JIBE rhymes with VIBE — when ideas JIBE, they have a matching VIBE. — jibe, to agree / be in tune.

k.syn: chauvinist, nationalist, superpatriot, war hawk
k.ant: dove, pacifist, peacenik 反战派人士
k.cn: 极端爱国激进分子（通常表现为好战的对外政策）
k.en: extreme chauvinism or nationalism marked especially by a belligerent foreign policy

ex.en: jingoists who cry for war
ex.cn: 叫嚣着鼓吹开战的激进分子

m: JINGOIST rhymes with EGOIST — a jingoist's chest-thumping nationalism. (From an old English song "By Jingo!") — jingoist, an extreme patriot.

k.en: to be nervous

m: JITTER rhymes with FLITTER — both rapid nervous movements. — jitter, to shake nervously.

k.syn: blithesome, festive, gleeful, jocund, jovial, mirthful
k.ant: lachrymose, saturnine 悲哀的；dour, dreary, morose, serious 阴郁的
k.en: characterized by joking, playful

ex.en: a jocular man who could make the most serious people laugh
ex.cn: 搞笑的人可以使最严肃的人都开怀大笑

m: JOCULAR contains JOC- (joke, Latin JOCUS) — joking. (Same root as JOKE, JOCUND.) — jocular, joking / humorous.

k.syn: blithesome, jocose, jocular, jolly, jovial, mirthful, sunny
k.ant: lachrymose, saturnine 悲哀的；dour, dreary, morose, serious 阴郁的
k.en: sprightly and lighthearted in disposition, character, or quality

ex.en: old friends engaged in jocund teasing
ex.cn: 老朋友相见，互相打趣调侃

m: JOCUND shares JOC- with JOCULAR — both relate to joke/joy. — jocund, cheerful.

k.en: a movement, pace, or instance of jogging（as for exercise）

k.syn: arouse, excite, incite, instigate, pique, remind, stimulate, stir
k.ant: allay, alleviate, assuage, ease, mitigate, mollify, palliate, relieve, soothe 缓和
k.en: to rouse or stimulate

ex.en: an old photo that might jog your memory
ex.cn: 一张也许会唤起你回忆的老照片

m: to JOG memory — give it a small physical bump like a slow JOG. — jog, to nudge / run slowly.

k.en: to move or dislodge with a sudden, hard blow

k.syn: appall, floor, shake up
k.en: to cause an unpleasant surprise for

ex.en: The sneak terrorist attack jolted the country out of its indolence and indifference.
ex.cn: 偷偷摸摸的恐怖袭击将该国从懒惰和冷漠无情中惊醒。

m: JOLT rhymes with BOLT — a sudden BOLT-like shock. — jolt, a sudden shock.

k.syn: log, mark, put down, register, report, set down, take down, write down
k.en: to write briefly or hurriedly

ex.en: jot down an address
ex.cn: 简要地记下地址

m: JOT contains the smallest letter shapes — to JOT a quick small note. (Greek IOTA = smallest letter.) — jot, to write briefly.

k.syn: blithesome, festive, gay, gleeful, jocular, jocund, jolly, mirthful
k.ant: lachrymose, saturnine 悲哀的；dour, dreary, morose, serious 阴郁的
k.en: markedly good-humored especially as evidenced by jollity and conviviality

ex.en: a jovial host
ex.cn: 快活的主人

m: JOVIAL comes from JOVE (Jupiter) — those born under Jove's planet are merry. — jovial, jolly.

k.syn: exulting, glorying, rejoicing, triumphant
k.ant: lachrymose, saturnine 悲哀的；dour, dreary, morose, serious 阴郁的
k.en: exultingly joyful

ex.en: The nominee delivered a jubilant speech before the cheering crowd.
ex.cn: 在欢呼的人群面前，被提名人发表了充满喜悦的演说。

m: JUBILANT contains JUBIL- (rejoice, Latin JUBILARE) — celebrating like at a JUBILEE. — jubilant, joyfully triumphant.

k.syn: intelligent, judgmatic, prudent, tactical, wise
k.ant: daft, imprudent, inadvisable, inexpedient, indiscreet, impolitic, unwise 愚蠢的，轻率的
k.en: having or exhibiting sound judgment; prudent

ex.en: a judicious choice
ex.cn: 明智的抉择

m: JUDICIOUS contains JUDI (judge) — wise as a JUDGE. — judicious, wisely judged.

k.syn: steamroller
k.cn: 无法阻挡的力量，摧毁一切的强大力量
k.en: an overwhelming, advancing force that crushes everything in its path

ex.en: the juggernaut of industrialization
ex.cn: 工业化无法阻挡的力量

m: JUGGERNAUT contains JUG + GERN ≈ "GIANT JUGgered force" — an unstoppable force. (From Hindu Lord Jagannath's massive temple cart.) — juggernaut, an unstoppable force.

k.en: any of various herbivorous marsupials

m: KANGAROO — Australian hopping marsupial. (Mnemonic anchor: contains KANG + ROO; sounds like "KANG, a ROO!".) — kangaroo, a hopping marsupial.

k.syn: sight
k.en: the range of vision

ex.en: abstract words that are beyond the ken of children
ex.cn: 孩子们不能理解的抽象单词

k.syn: appreciate, apprehend, cognize, comprehend, grasp, perceive, savvy, understand
k.ant: misapprehend, misconceive, misinterpret, misperceive, misunderstand 错误地理解
k.en: to know（a person or thing）

m: KEN rhymes with KEN-NING (knowing, Old English) — your range of knowledge or sight. — ken, range of knowledge.

k.syn: abduct
k.ant: release, set free 释放
k.en: to seize and detain by unlawful force or fraud and often with a demand for ransom

ex.en: The child was kidnapped and held for ransom.
ex.cn: 歹徒绑架了孩子，并且向家属勒索赎金。

m: KIDNAP contains KID + NAP — snatch a KID while NAP-ping. (Old slang "nap" = grab.) — kidnap, to abduct.

k.syn: enkindle, ignite, inflame, torch
k.ant: douse, extinguish, quench, put out, snuff out 熄灭
k.en: to build or fuel（a fire）; to set fire to; ignite

ex.en: kindle interest
ex.cn: 激发兴趣

m: KINDLE rhymes with CANDLE — light a CANDLE = kindle a flame. — kindle, to ignite.

k.syn: agreeable, amicable, compatible, congenial, frictionless, unanimous, united
k.ant: disagreeable, discordant, disharmonious, disunited, incompatible, inharmonious, uncongenial 不一致的，不和谐的
k.cn: 类似的；具有相似或相近的起源、本性或性质的
k.en: having a similar or related origin, nature, or character

ex.en: She finally found people who were kindred spirits when she joined the hiking club.
ex.cn: 她加入登山俱乐部的时候终于发现了志向相同的人。

m: KINDRED contains KIN — sharing the same KIN/family. — kindred, related / similar.

k.syn: artifice, device, gambit, ploy, scheme, sleight, stratagem
k.ant: foolishness 愚蠢
k.en: a clever trick or stratagem; a clever way of doing something

ex.en: She's tried every knack in Cupid's book to get her guy to marry her.
ex.cn: 她试过了所有爱情三十六计，希望那男人娶她。

m: KNACK rhymes with SNACK — small SNACK-sized skill that comes naturally. — knack, a clever skill.

k.en: a tricky deceitful

m: KNAVE rhymes with NAIVE but the opposite — KNAVES exploit naive folk. (In cards, the Jack = the knave.) — knave, a dishonest rogue.

k.syn: massage, manipulate, mould, squeeze, stroke
k.en: to make or shape by or as if by folding, pressing, and stretching with the hands

ex.en: knead dough
ex.cn: 揉面团

ex.en: knead a painful calf muscle
ex.cn: 给疼痛的小腿肌肉按摩

m: KNEAD rhymes with NEED — bakers NEED to KNEAD dough. — knead, to press dough.

k.syn: braid, plait, weave
k.ant: ravel 解开
k.en: to form by interlacing yarn or thread in a series of connected loops with needles

ex.en: She knitted him a sweater for Christmas.
ex.cn: 圣诞节她为他缝了一件毛衣。

k.syn: bind, bond, combine, connect, fasten, join, link, meld, merge, tie, secure, unite
k.ant: disassociate, disconnect, disjoin, divide, sever, split, sunder 分开
k.en: to join closely; unite securely

ex.en: Sport knits the whole family close together.
ex.cn: 体育将整个家庭紧紧地联系在一起。

m: to KNIT a community = weave it together like KNIT yarn. — knit, to weave together.

k.syn: baroque, byzantine, complicated, convoluted, intricate, involved, labyrinthine, sophisticated, tangled
k.ant: easy, effortless, plain, simple 容易的，明显的
k.en: marked by or full of knots especially: so full of difficulties and complications as to be likely to defy solution

ex.en: The candidates cautiously gave their views on an array of knotty issues.
ex.cn: 候选人就一系列困难问题谨慎地给出了自己的看法。

m: KNOTTY contains KNOT — full of KNOTS, complicated. — knotty, complicated / difficult.

k.syn: credit, distinction, homage, honor, laurels
k.ant: infamy, notoriety 不好的名声
k.en: fame and renown resulting from an act or achievement

ex.en: Employees enjoy the kudos that the job brings as much as the financial rewards.
ex.cn: 正如同喜欢工作带来的经济回报一样，雇员们也很享受工作所带来的名声和荣誉。

k.syn: acclaim, accolade, applause, credit, distinction, homage, honor, laud, laurels
k.ant: belittlement, denigration, deprecation, derogation, diminishment, disparagement 贬损
k.en: acclaim or praise for exceptional achievement

ex.en: The attorney did pro bono work because it was the right thing to do, and not for any future kudos that it might bring.
ex.cn: 这名律师之所以为慈善机构和穷人提供免费服务，是因为他觉得这是正确的事情，而非为了任何可能因此而产生的赞扬。

m: KUDOS rhymes with BUDDIES — both positive social currency. (Greek KYDOS = glory.) — kudos, praise.

k.syn: capricious, fluctuating, fluid, inconstant, mercurial, temperamental, unsettled, unsteady, variable, volatile
k.ant: constant, immutable, invariable, stable, stationary, steady 稳定的
k.en: continually undergoing chemical, physical, or biological change; unstable

ex.en: labile mineral
ex.cn: 不稳定的矿物质

ex.en: an emotionally labile person
ex.cn: 一个多愁善感的人

m: LABILE rhymes with FRAGILE — both unstable. (Latin LABI = slip.) — labile, unstable / changeable.

k.en: lacking ease

ex.en: a labored speech
ex.cn: 表达困难的演讲

m: LABORED contains LABOR — words that took heavy LABOR to produce. — labored, awkwardly forced.

k.en: something extremely complex or tortuous

ex.en: a labyrinth of rules and regulations
ex.cn: 复杂的规定和条例

m: LABYRINTH was the legendary Cretan maze where Theseus killed the Minotaur. — labyrinth, a maze.

k.syn: afflict, distress, harrow, hurt, rend, torment, torture, wound
k.ant: allay, alleviate, assuage, ease, mitigate, mollify, palliate, relieve, soothe 减轻（痛苦等）
k.en: to cause deep emotional pain to; distress

ex.en: He was born into a family already lacerated with tensions and divisions.
ex.cn: 他出生在一个已经被冲突和分裂折磨不堪的家庭当中。

m: LACERATE contains LACER ≈ "laceration" — to lacerate. (Latin LACER = torn.) — lacerate, to tear / wound deeply.

k.syn: doleful, lamentable, lugubrious, melancholy, mournful, tearful, teary, weepy, woeful
k.ant: cheerful, delighted, jocund, jovial 欢乐的，快乐的
k.en: tending to cause tears, mournful

ex.en: The lachrymose mourners at the funeral required a steady supply of tissues.
ex.cn: 葬礼上多愁善感的哀悼者对纸巾有稳定的需求。

m: LACHRYMOSE contains LACHRY ≈ "LACRIMA" (Latin tear) — full of tears. — lachrymose, tearful.

k.syn: dim, dull, flat, lusterless
k.ant: burnished, glistening, glossy, lustrous, polished, shiny, sleek 光亮的
k.en: lacking brightness, luster, or vitality

ex.en: lackluster hair
ex.cn: 黯淡无光的头发

m: LACK + LUSTER — lacking LUSTER (shine). — lackluster, dull.

k.syn: apothegmatic, brief, capsule, compact, compendious, curt, pithy, succinct, summary, telegraphic, terse
k.ant: circuitous, circumlocutory, diffuse, prolix, rambling, verbose, windy, wordy 冗长的
k.cn: 简洁（以至于显得粗鲁或难以理解）的
k.en: using or involving the use of a minimum of words: concise to the point of seeming rude or mysterious

ex.en: His mentor's comment tends to be laconic but very much to the point.
ex.cn: 他导师的点评很简短，但是却总能说到点子上。

m: LACONIC comes from LACONIA (Sparta) — Spartans were famously brief in speech. — laconic, terse.

k.syn: assail, baste, belabor, berate, castigate, excoriate, reprimand, reproach, scathe, slam, upbraid, vituperate
k.ant: carol, extol, glorify, hymn, laud, magnify, praise 表扬，赞美
k.en: to scold sharply; berate

ex.en: Critics lambasted his performance.
ex.cn: 评论家严厉斥责了他的表演。

m: LAMBASTE contains LAM (hit) + BASTE (beat with grease) — beat severely with criticism. — lambaste, to harshly criticize.

k.syn: dirge, elegy, requiem
k.en: a composition expressing one's grief over a loss

ex.en: her lament for her grandmother
ex.cn: 她为她祖母写的悼词

k.syn: carp, complaint, fuss, grievance, gripe, grouch, grouse, grumble, moan, murmur
k.en: an expression of dissatisfaction, pain, or resentment

ex.en: the career woman's lament that there aren't any good men left
ex.cn: 职场女性的抱怨：好男人一个没剩下

k.syn: bemoan, deplore, grieve, moan, mourn, wail
k.ant: delight, exult, joy, rejoice 感到高兴
k.cn: 为…哀悼，表达痛苦或遗憾
k.en: to express sorrow or regret; mourn

ex.en: lament an innocent death
ex.cn: 为无辜的死者而悲痛

m: LAMENT contains LAM ≈ wailing "la-la-MENT" of grief. (Latin LAMENTUM = wailing.) — lament, to mourn / express grief.

k.syn: deplorable, distressful, grievous, heartbreaking, lugubrious, plaintive, plangent, sorrowful, woeful
k.ant: cheerful, delighted, jocund, jovial 欢乐的，快乐的
k.en: inspiring or deserving of lament or regret; deplorable or pitiable; mournful

ex.en: The lamentable cries of the women for their lost sons were heard throughout the village.
ex.cn: 女人们由于丧子之痛的哀嚎响彻整个村庄。

m: from LAMENT — worth lamenting. — lamentable, regrettable.

k.en: to divide

m: LAMINATE contains LAMIN- (thin plate, Latin LAMINA) — make into thin layers. — laminate, to layer thinly.

k.syn: burlesque, caricature, farce, mockery, parody, ridicule, spoof, travesty
k.ant: eulogy, ode, paean 颂歌
k.en: a harsh satire usually directed against an individual

ex.en: a lampoon of the movie business at the time
ex.cn: 对当时电影产业的一种讽刺

m: LAMPOON rhymes with BUFFOON — making a public buffoon of the target. — lampoon, to mock satirically.

k.en: a method of solid waste disposal in which refuse

m: LAND + FILL — fill in LAND with garbage. — landfill, a garbage dump.

k.syn: debilitated, effete, enervated, feeble, frail, infirm, lackadaisical, spiritless, sapped, enfeebled
k.ant: animated, energetic, vehement, vivacious 有精力的；mighty, powerful, stalwart, stout, strong 强壮的
k.en: lacking energy or vitality; weak

ex.en: be languid for weeks after surgery
ex.cn: 术后的几周都无精打采的

m: LANGUID contains LANGU- (faint, Latin LANGUERE) — drooping with weariness. — languid, drooping / listless.

k.syn: decay, droop, emaciate, fade, fail, flag, sag, wither
k.ant: burgeon, flourish, thrive, prosper 旺盛生长
k.en: to be or become feeble, weak, or enervated

ex.en: languish during the prolonged heat wave
ex.cn: 在持续的热浪下变得虚弱

m: from LANGU- (same root as LANGUID) — to lose vitality. — languish, to grow weak.

k.syn: collapse, exhaustion, frazzle, lassitude, listlessness, stupor, torpor, prostration
k.ant: verve, vim, animation, vitality 有活力
k.en: physical or mental inertness

ex.en: He enjoyed the languor brought on by a hot summer afternoon.
ex.cn: 他很享受夏日午后的慵懒。

k.syn: debilitation, enervation, enfeeblement, fragility, infirmity
k.ant: robustness, strength, vivacity 强壮，有力
k.en: weakness or weariness of body or mind

ex.en: The tropical heat sapped our strength, leaving us in a state of unaccustomed languor.
ex.cn: 热带的炎热气候消磨着我们的精力，让我们感到一阵不适的虚弱。

m: from LANGU- — state of weakness. — languor, listlessness.

k.syn: emaciated, lean, slender, svelte, tenuous, thin
k.ant: fat, fleshy, gross, obese 肥胖的
k.en: long and lean

ex.en: lank cattle
ex.cn: 瘦牲口

k.syn: droopy, flaccid, floppy, yielding
k.ant: inflexible, rigid, stiff, sturdy, tense 僵硬的；resilient 有弹性的
k.en: long, straight, and limp; not stiff in structure

ex.en: a woman with long, lank hair
ex.cn: 有着长而稀疏头发的女子

ex.en: Right after a shower, her lank hair hung down to her shoulders.
ex.cn: 出浴后，柔软的头发搭在她的肩上。

m: LANK rhymes with PLANK — long, thin, and flat like a plank. — lank, lean / limp.

k.syn: blunder, fumble, gaffe, miscue, oversight, peccadillo
k.en: a slight error typically due to forgetfulness or inattention

ex.en: a lapse in table manner
ex.cn: 餐桌礼仪的小过错

k.syn: cease, conclude, die, end, expire, finish, stop, terminate
k.ant: continue, persist, hang on 持续
k.en: to come to an end

ex.en: The contract will lapse at the end of the year.
ex.cn: 合同将于年底到期。

m: LAPSE rhymes with COLLAPSE — both involve falling. (Latin LAPSUS = a slip/fall.) — lapse, a slip / a stop.

k.en: a place where food is stored; a pantry

m: LARDER contains LARD — where the LARD (and other food) is stored. — larder, a food pantry.

k.syn: bestowal, donation, giveaway, present
k.en: something given to someone without expectation of a return

ex.en: The alumna's huge bequest was an unexpected largess.
ex.cn: 校友们的巨额遗赠是一笔意料之外的财富。

k.syn: bountifulness, generosity, munificence, openhandedness, philanthropy
k.ant: miserliness, parsimony, penury, stinginess 小气
k.en: liberality in giving or willingness to give

ex.en: be noted for his largesse
ex.cn: 因慷慨而闻名

m: LARGESSE contains LARGE — giving in LARGE amounts. — largesse, generous giving.

k.en: a rope for picketing

m: LARIAT rhymes with CHARIOT — a cowboy wields a LARIAT to chase like a CHARIOT. (Spanish LA REATA = the rope.) — lariat, a lasso.

k.syn: bang, bash, bat, beat, clap, hit, knock, punch, slam, slap, smash, stinger, stroke, swat
k.en: a hard strike with a part of the body or an instrument

ex.en: She suddenly felt the lash of her drunken husband's hand on her cheek.
ex.cn: 她突然感觉到脸颊被醉酒的丈夫扇了一耳光。

k.syn: baste, hammer, lace, lambaste, punch
k.en: to strike against with force or violence

ex.en: All night long a barrage of rain lashed the windows.
ex.cn: 倾盆大雨整夜敲击着窗户。

k.ant: unbind 解开
k.en: to bind with or as if with a line

ex.en: Secure the anchor by lashing it to the rail.
ex.cn: 将锚绑在栏杆上使其稳固。

m: LASH rhymes with SLASH / CRASH — all violent strikes. — lash, to strike / bind tightly.

k.syn: collapse, exhaustion, frazzle, languor, listlessness, stupor, torpor, prostration
k.ant: verve, vim, animation, vitality 有活力
k.en: a state or feeling of weariness, diminished energy, or listlessness

ex.en: Symptoms of anaemia include general fatigue and lassitude.
ex.cn: 贫血的通常症状包括体虚和乏力。

m: LASSITUDE contains LASS ≈ LAST/lazy weariness. (Latin LASSUS = tired.) — lassitude, weariness.

k.syn: dormant, fallow, inert, inoperative
k.ant: apparent, evident, manifest, obvious, plain 明显的；active 活跃的
k.en: present or potential but not evident or active

ex.en: a latent infection
ex.cn: 潜伏性传染病

ex.en: He has a latent talent for acting that he hasn't had a chance to express yet.
ex.cn: 他有着表演的天赋，只不过他还没有机会来表现这一才能。

m: LATENT contains LAT ≈ "lay" — laying hidden, waiting. — latent, hidden / dormant.

k.en: a machine for shaping

m: LATHE rhymes with BATHE — but a LATHE shapes wood by spinning. — lathe, a wood-shaping machine.

k.syn: authorization, license, freedom, leeway, free hand
k.ant: limitation 限制；custody 监护，拘留
k.en: freedom from normal restraints, limitations, or regulations

ex.en: Students are allowed considerable latitude in choosing courses.
ex.cn: 学生在选课时被给予相当大的自由度。

m: LATITUDE contains LAT (wide, Latin LATUS) — wide freedom of action. (Same root as the geographic latitude lines.) — latitude, freedom of action.

k.syn: adulatory, commendatory, complimentary, extolling, eulogistic, panegyric
k.ant: derogatory, depreciatory, disparaging, pejorative 贬低的
k.en: of, relating to, or expressing praise

ex.en: a laudatory review of the new play
ex.cn: 对新剧目的赞扬性评论

m: LAUDATORY contains LAUD (praise, Latin LAUDARE) — full of praise. — laudatory, expressing praise.

k.syn: copious, exuberant, gushing, lush, luxuriant, opulent, profuse, riotous
k.ant: moderate, modest, reasonable, temperate 适度的，合理的
k.cn: 奢侈的，大量的，无节制的
k.en: characterized by or produced with extravagance and profusion

ex.en: lavish buffet
ex.cn: 奢侈的自助餐

k.syn: blow, dissipate, fritter, misspend, squander, waste
k.ant: conserve 节约
k.en: to give readily and in large quantities; to use up carelessly

ex.en: a great actor who lavished his talent in lousy movies
ex.cn: 将才华浪费在低劣电影上的影星

m: LAVISH contains LAV ≈ "lavatory water" lavishly poured. (Latin LAVARE = wash.) — lavish, generously abundant.

k.syn: imbue, infuse, ingrain, inoculate, inspire, permeate, steep, suffuse
k.ant: extract 抽取
k.cn: 在…中加入轻松、活泼或变更的因素
k.en: to mingle or permeate with some modifying, alleviating, or vivifying element

ex.en: He needs to leaven his speeches with more humor.
ex.cn: 他需要在演讲中再加入点幽默元素。

m: LEAVEN rhymes with HEAVEN but earthier — yeast LEAVENS bread, lifting it. — leaven, to lighten / enliven.

k.syn: squint
k.ant: gape, gaze, glare, goggle, stare 盯着看
k.en: to cast a sidelong glance

ex.en: He gave her a leering look.
ex.cn: 他瞥了她一眼。

m: LEER rhymes with PEER — but a LEER is a creepy sideways PEER. — leer, to glance unpleasantly.

k.syn: dubious, distrustful, skeptical, suspicious, wary
k.ant: credulous 轻信的
k.en: suspicious or distrustful; wary

ex.en: be leery of strangers
ex.cn: 对陌生人存有怀疑

m: from LEER — eyeing something with suspicion. — leery, suspicious / wary.

k.syn: bequest, heritage, patrimony
k.en: something handed down from an ancestor or a predecessor or from the past

ex.en: the legacy of the ancient philosophers
ex.cn: 古代哲学家们的思想遗产

m: LEGACY contains LEG ≈ LEGAL — what's LEGALly passed down. (Latin LEGARE = bequeath.) — legacy, an inheritance.

k.syn: fable, myth, mythos
k.der: legendary adj. 如传奇般闻名的
k.en: a popular myth of recent origin

ex.en: Some ancient civilizations had legends about spirits that inhabited trees and rocks.
ex.cn: 一些古文明有着关于寄居于树木、岩石之中的精灵的传说。

k.syn: cutline
k.en: an explanatory list of the symbols on a map or chart

ex.en: The legend in the science textbook indicated that the accompanying picture had been enlarged by 1000%.
ex.cn: 科学课本上彩图的图例告诉我们这幅图片被放大了十倍。

m: LEGEND contains LEG (read, Latin LEGERE) — a story to be READ aloud. (Same root as LEGIBLE.) — legend, a traditional story / map key.

k.syn: army, battalion, flock, herd, horde, mob, swarm, throng
k.en: a large body of men and women organized for land warfare

ex.en: join the French Foreign Legion
ex.cn: 加入法国外籍军团

k.syn: beaucoup, multifold, multitudinous, numerous
k.ant: few, lack in number 少量的
k.en: many, numerous

ex.en: The problems are legion.
ex.cn: 问题不计其数。

m: LEGION contains LEG ≈ a Roman LEGION (army of thousands) — vast in number. — legion, a large multitude.

k.syn: clement, gentle, merciful, mild, sparing, tender, tolerant
k.ant: harsh, merciless, severe, strict 残酷的，严厉的
k.en: inclined not to be harsh or strict; merciful, generous, or indulgent

ex.en: the lenient sentences
ex.cn: 仁慈的审判

m: LENIENT contains LEN ≈ Latin LENIS (gentle/soft) — gentle in punishment. — lenient, merciful.

k.syn: baleful, deadly, deathly, fatal, mortal, murderous, pestilent, terminal, vital
k.ant: innocuous 无害的；healthy, salubrious, wholesome 有益健康的
k.en: extremely harmful; devastating

ex.en: This dagger is lethal.
ex.cn: 这把匕首是致命的武器。

ex.en: launch a lethal attack
ex.cn: 发动致命的进攻

m: LETHAL contains LETH (death, Greek LETHE = forgetfulness/death-river) — deadly. — lethal, deadly.

k.syn: dull, inert, quiescent, sluggish, torpid
k.ant: dynamic, energetic, robust, vigorous 有精力的；active 活跃的
k.cn: 没精打采的，行动迟缓的
k.en: of, relating to, or characterized by lethargy, sluggish

ex.en: A big nice meal always makes me feel lethargic and sleepy.
ex.cn: 一顿大餐吃完总是让我既慵懒又想睡。

m: LETHARGIC shares LETH- with LETHE (mythological river of forgetfulness) — drowsy as if you've drunk from it. — lethargic, sluggish.

k.en: an embankment for preventing flooding; a formal reception held by a person of distinction

m: LEVEE rhymes with HEAVY — a HEAVY raised wall against floodwater. (French LEVER = raise.) — levee, a flood-control embankment.

k.syn: informed, justified, logical, rational, reasonable, sensible, sober, valid, well-founded
k.ant: foolish 愚蠢的；groundless, invalid, unfounded, unjustified, unsound 没有根据的，理由不充分的
k.en: characteristically self-composed and sensible

ex.en: a levelheaded assessment of the problem
ex.cn: 对于问题的一个明智的评估

m: LEVEL + HEADED — head stays LEVEL under pressure. — levelheaded, calmly sensible.

k.syn: facetiousness, flightiness, flippancy, frivolousness, frothiness, silliness
k.ant: earnestness, gravity, seriousness, soberness, solemnity 严肃
k.en: excessive or unseemly frivolity

ex.en: The teachers disapprove of any displays of levity during school assemblies.
ex.cn: 老师们不允许学生在学校聚会中表现出任何轻浮的态度。

m: LEVITY contains LEV (light, Latin LEVIS) — lightness of mood. (Same root as LEVITATE.) — levity, lighthearted humor.

k.en: an author or editor of a dictionary

m: LEXICO (word, Greek LEXIKON) + GRAPHER — one who writes about words. — lexicographer, a dictionary maker.

k.syn: accountability, answerability, responsibility
k.ant: immunity 豁免权
k.en: the quality or state of being liable

ex.en: The company is trying to reduce its liability in this case.
ex.cn: 在这个案件中，公司试图减少他们的责任。

k.syn: burden, debit, drawback, handicap, hurdle, incommodity, manacle, saddle, trammel
k.ant: advantage, asset, edge, plus 优点，优势 When an end is lawful and obligatory, the indispensable means to it are also lawful and obligatory. 如果一个目的是正当而必须做的，则达到这个目的的必要手段也是正当而必须采取的。 ——美国政治家　林肯（Abraham Lincoln, American statesman） “每当我们对未来充满了各种美好的期望与幻想时，就该反思一下自己现在的努力是否配得上这幻境中的将来。莫问收获，但问耕耘。” （刘宜君，2009年10月，Verbal 730，Quantitative 800，录取院校：斯坦福大学，计算机系）
k.en: a feature of someone or something that creates difficulty for achieving success

ex.en: Their chief asset has now become a considerable liability.
ex.cn: 他们最大的优点如今已成了不可小觑的负担。

m: LIABILITY contains LIABLE — what you're LIABLE for; or a thing that holds you back. — liability, an obligation / drawback.

k.syn: nonconventional, nonorthodox, nontraditional, open-minded, progressive, radical
k.ant: conservative, conventional, hidebound, old-fashioned, stodgy, traditional 守旧的，传统的
k.en: not bound by traditional ways or beliefs

ex.en: parents who take a very liberal attitude toward letting their children stay out late
ex.cn: 思想开放，同意让他们的小孩晚归的家长们

k.syn: charitable, munificent, unselfish, unsparing, unstinting
k.ant: closefisted, miserly, niggardly, parsimonious, stingy, tightfisted 吝啬的
k.en: marked by generosity

ex.en: a doctor who has been very liberal in dispensing low-cost care to patients who could not otherwise afford it
ex.cn: 一位慷慨的医生，愿意帮助无法支付低额药费的病人

m: LIBERAL contains LIBER (free, Latin) — free-minded. — liberal, generous / open-minded.

k.syn: backslider, debaucher, decadent, deviate, pervert, profligate
k.ant: ascetic 禁欲者
k.en: one who acts without moral restraint; a dissolute person

ex.en: The legend of Don Juan depicts him as a playboy and libertine.
ex.cn: 有关唐璜的传说把他描绘成一个放荡不羁的花花公子。

m: LIBERTINE contains LIBER (free) — taking too much freedom in morals. — libertine, a morally unrestrained person.

k.en: the text of a work (as an opera) for the musical theater

m: LIBRETTO contains LIBR (book, Latin LIBER) — "little book" of opera lyrics. (Italian diminutive.) — libretto, the text of an opera.

k.syn: concupiscent, horny, lascivious, lecherous, libidinous, lubricious, salacious, wanton
k.ant: frigid, undersexed 性冷淡的
k.en: lacking legal or moral restraints; having a strong sexua, desire

ex.en: a moralist who decried what she regarded as the licentious and corrupt culture of the entertainment industry
ex.cn: 一个公开谴责在她看来无比堕落和放荡的娱乐圈文化的道德家

m: LICENTIOUS shares LIC- with LICENSE — taking license too far, immoral. — licentious, morally unrestrained.

k.en: the right

ex.en: a lien on property
ex.cn: 对财产的留置权

m: LIEN rhymes with LEAN — a creditor LEANS on you with a claim against your property. — lien, a creditor's claim.

k.syn: analogize, bracket, equate, equalize
k.ant: contrast 对比，对照以产生反差
k.en: to see, mention, or show as similar; compare

ex.en: Life is often likened to a journey.
ex.cn: 生活经常被比作一次旅行。

m: LIKEN contains LIKE — show how things are LIKE each other. — liken, to compare.

k.syn: flexible, lissome, lithesome, pliable, pliant, supple
k.ant: inflexible, rigid, stiff, stiffened 僵硬的
k.en: capable of being shaped: flexible

ex.en: She shaped the basket out of limber branches.
ex.cn: 她用柔软的树枝编了一个筐。

m: LIMBER contains LIMB — flexible LIMBs. — limber, supple / flexible.

k.cn: 幽默色情的诗（基本相当于荤段子）
k.en: a light humorous, nonsensical, or bawdy verse of five anapestic

m: LIMERICK — named after the Irish town of Limerick, home of the bawdy 5-line verse form. — limerick, a humorous verse form.

k.en: a large luxurious automobile, especially one driven by a chauffeur

m: LIMOUSINE — long luxury car (named after the cloak worn in the French region of Limousin). — limousine, a luxury car.

k.syn: droopy, flaccid, floppy, lank, yielding
k.ant: firm, stiff, sturdy, tense 坚硬的；resilient 有弹性的
k.en: lacking firm texture, substance, or structure

ex.en: Her hair hung limp about her shoulders.
ex.cn: 她的头发松散地垂在肩上。

k.syn: enervated, lackadaisical, languid, languishing, languorous, spiritless
k.ant: ambitious, enterprising 有雄心壮志的；animated, energetic, motivated 精力充沛的，有积极性的
k.en: lacking strength or firmness; weak or spiritless

ex.en: The team's limp performance has many people calling for the head coach's resignation.
ex.cn: 队伍不给力的表现让许多人呼吁教练下课。

k.syn: blunder, bumble, lumber, plod, struggle, stumble, trudge
k.en: to move or proceed haltingly or unsteadily

ex.en: The project limped along with half its previous funding.
ex.cn: 项目靠着之前一半的资金艰难地进行着。

m: LIMP rhymes with WIMP — both weak and droopy. — limp, weakly drooping / to walk lamely.

k.syn: crystal, clear, lucent, pellucid, transparent
k.ant: cloudy, murky, opaque, unclear, turbid 模糊不清的
k.en: characterized by transparent clearness

ex.en: limpid streams
ex.cn: 清澈的小溪

k.syn: collected, composed, cool, level, peaceful, placid, possessed, sedate, serene, smooth, tranquil
k.ant: agitated, discomposed, disturbed, flustered, perturbed 焦躁的
k.en: free from emotional or mental agitation

ex.en: the limpid outlook of a man who is at peace with himself as he awaits death
ex.cn: 一个人在平静地等待死亡时所持的淡然的世界观

m: LIMPID rhymes with INSIPID but the opposite — INSIPID is dull, LIMPID is clear. — limpid, transparently clear.

k.en: thread made from fibers of the flax

m: LINEN comes from LINUM (Latin for flax) — fabric made from FLAX plant fibers. — linen, flax-based cloth.

k.syn: crawl, creep, dally, dawdle, lag, loiter
k.ant: hurry, run, rush 飞奔
k.en: to proceed slowly; saunter

ex.en: Fans lingered outside the door.
ex.cn: 粉丝们在门外徘徊。

m: LINGER rhymes with FINGER — like a FINGER tracing slowly, you LINGER. — linger, to stay slowly.

k.en: a floor covering which is made of cloth covered with a hard shiny

m: LINOLEUM contains LIN (linseed-oil) + OLEUM (oil) — flooring made from linseed oil. — linoleum, a smooth floor covering.

k.en: downy material obtained by scraping linen cloth and used for dressing wounds; loose fibers from fabric

m: LINT rhymes with HINT — barely a HINT of fluff in your dryer trap. — lint, fabric fluff.

k.syn: flexible, limber, lithe, pliable, pliant, supple
k.ant: solid 坚硬的；inflexible, rigid, stiff, stiffened 僵硬的
k.en: easily bent; supple

ex.en: Rattan is such a lissome material that it can be used for all manner of furniture and baskets.
ex.cn: 藤是一种非常柔软而有韧性的材料，可以被用于形形色色的家具和篮子之中。

k.syn: agile, featly, feline, gracile, lithesome, nimble
k.ant: awkward, clumsy, graceless, ungainly 笨拙的
k.en: having the ability to move with ease; limber

ex.en: a lissome ballerina
ex.cn: 身姿轻盈的女芭蕾舞演员

m: LISSOME rhymes with WINSOME — graceful and supple. (Variant of LITHESOME.) — lissome, supple / graceful.

k.syn: angle, cant, heel, incline, pitch, slant, slope, tilt, tip
k.ant: erect 竖立
k.en: to set or cause to be at an angle

ex.en: The sudden lift of the load on the deck listed the ship badly.
ex.cn: 甲板上货物被突然提起，使船陡然倾斜。

m: to LIST means to LEAN to one side (a ship LISTS in rough seas). — list, to tilt.

k.syn: agile, featly, feline, gracile, lightsome, nimble
k.ant: awkward, clumsy, graceless, ungainly 笨拙的
k.en: characterized by easy flexibility and grace

ex.en: lithe dancers
ex.cn: 轻盈优雅的舞者

k.syn: flexible, limber, pliable, pliant, supple
k.ant: solid 坚硬的；inflexible, rigid, stiff, stiffened 僵硬的
k.en: easily bent or flexed

ex.en: lithe branches
ex.cn: 柔软的枝条

m: LITHE rhymes with BLITHE — both light and easy. — lithe, supple / agile.

k.syn: disinclined, indisposed, reluctant, reticent
k.ant: eager 渴望的；disposed, inclined 有意向的
k.en: unwilling or reluctant; disinclined

ex.en: I was loath to accept the fact that he had been killed in a terrorist attack.
ex.cn: 我极不情愿地接受了他在一场恐怖袭击中丧生的事实。

m: LOATH rhymes with OATH — taking an OATH only LOATH-ly, reluctantly. — loath, reluctant.

k.syn: abhor, abominate, despise, detest, execrate
k.ant: adore, love 热爱
k.en: to dislike someone or something greatly; abhor

ex.en: I loathe having to do this.
ex.cn: 我鄙视不得不这样做。

m: LOATHE rhymes with CLOTHE but the opposite of warm — to LOATHE is to find disgusting. — loathe, to detest.

k.en: a self-propelled vehicle that runs on rails and is used for pulling trains

m: LOCO (place, Latin LOCUS) + MOTIVE (moving) — what moves from place to place; the engine. — locomotive, a train engine.

k.en: an ore deposit; a rich source or supply

m: LODE rhymes with LOAD — a LOAD of ore in a mineral vein. — lode, an ore deposit.

k.syn: chivalrous, elevated, greathearted, high-minded, magnanimous, sublime
k.ant: base, debased, ignominious, mean 可耻的
k.en: elevated in character and spirit, noble

ex.en: lofty ideals
ex.cn: 崇高的理想

k.syn: altitudinous, tall, towering
k.ant: low 低的
k.en: rising to a great height

ex.en: lofty mountains
ex.cn: 高耸的山峰

ex.en: the ever-increasing lofty heights of the world's skyscrapers
ex.cn: 不断攀升的摩天大楼的高度

k.syn: assumptive, bumptious, haughty, lordly, peremptory, pompous, presumptuous, supercilious, superior
k.ant: humble, lowly, modest 谦逊的，低调的
k.en: having a feeling of superiority that shows itself in an overbearing attitude

ex.en: She acts all lofty and superior just because she went to Stanford University.
ex.cn: 她之所以表现得这么自大和不可一世，只因为她考进了斯坦福。

m: LOFTY contains LOFT — high up in the LOFT. — lofty, very high / noble.

k.syn: slouch, lounge
k.en: to act or move in a lax, lazy, or indolent manner

ex.en: He lolled back in his comfortable chair.
ex.cn: 他懒洋洋地倚在他舒适的椅子上。

k.syn: dally, dawdle, drone, laze
k.en: to spend time doing nothing

ex.en: Some members of the decorating committee were hard at work, and others were just lolling about.
ex.cn: 装修队的一部分人在很努力地工作，另一些则在偷懒。

m: LOLL rhymes with DOLL — a DOLL flopping LOLLS limply. — loll, to lounge lazily.

k.syn: askew, aslant, crazy, listing, oblique, pitched, skewed, slanted, tipping, uneven
k.ant: even, level, straight 平的
k.en: leaning to one side

ex.en: The portrait in the foyer was lopsided.
ex.cn: 门厅里的画像挂歪了。

k.syn: asymmetric, disproportional, irregular, off-balance, unbalanced, unequal
k.ant: balanced 平衡的；symmetrical 对称的
k.en: lacking in balance, symmetry, or proportion

ex.en: The arrangement of the furniture was lopsided.
ex.cn: 家具的摆放太不协调了。

ex.en: a lopsided score of 4-0
ex.cn: 四比零的压倒性比分

m: LOP + SIDED — one SIDE LOPPED off (cut off), unbalanced. — lopsided, uneven.

k.syn: chatty, conservational, gabby, garrulous, talkative, voluble
k.ant: laconic, reserved, reticent, taciturn, uncommunicative 话少的
k.en: given to fluent or excessive talk

ex.en: Sometimes the loquacious talk show host barely lets her guests get a word in.
ex.cn: 有时候多话的脱口秀主持人让她的嘉宾一句话都插不进来。

m: LOQUACIOUS contains LOQU- (speak, Latin LOQUI) — full of talk. (Same root as ELOQUENT.) — loquacious, talkative.

k.en: a drawing of lots in which prizes are distributed to the winners among persons buying a chance

m: LOTTERY contains LOT — drawing of LOTs to pick a winner. — lottery, a drawing for prizes.

k.syn: boorish, churlish, clumsy, crude, discourteous, uncouth, uncivilized, uncultured, unrefined
k.ant: courteous, civilized, genteel, graceful, polished, refined, urbane 有教养的
k.der: lout n. 举止粗鲁的人
k.en: having the characteristics of a lout; awkward, stupid, and boorish

ex.en: a boy with a loutish air
ex.cn: 一个举止粗鲁的男孩

m: LOUTISH contains LOUT — behaving like a LOUT (oaf). — loutish, oafishly rude.

k.syn: grease, oil, slick, smooth, wax
k.en: to coat（something）with a slippery substance in order to reduce friction

ex.en: lubricate the gears
ex.cn: 给齿轮打润滑油

m: LUBRICATE contains LUBR ≈ "slippery" (Latin LUBRICUS = slippery). — lubricate, to make slippery.

k.syn: beaming, brilliant, dazzling, glowing, incandescent, lucent, luminous, lustrous, radiant, refulgent, splendid
k.ant: dim, dull, lackluster 黯淡无光的
k.en: suffused with light
