ex.en: Those lucid bands that spread across the arctic sky are known as aurora borealis, or the northern lights.
ex.cn: 那些蔓延在北极天空中的光带就是北极光。

k.syn: balanced, clearheaded, normal, right, sane, stable
k.ant: brainsick, crazy, insane, lunatic, mad, maniac 疯狂的
k.en: having full use of one's mind and control over one's actions

ex.en: She decided to make out her will while she was still lucid.
ex.cn: 她决定趁着自己神志还清醒，列出她的遗愿。

k.syn: apprehensible, clear, comprehensible, intelligible, palpable, patent, pellucid, plain, understandable
k.ant: ambiguous, enigmatic, equivocal, indistinct, obfuscated, obscure, unclear 模糊不明确的
k.cn: 表达清晰的，简单易懂的
k.en: easily understood

ex.en: The teaching assistant tried to make his instructions as lucid as possible so that everyone would understand what to do.
ex.cn: 助教努力使自己的指令容易理解，从而让所有人都知道应该要干什么。

m: LUCID contains LUC- (light, Latin LUX) — clear as light. (Same root as TRANSLUCENT.) — lucid, clear / sane.

k.en: laborious or intensive

m: LUCUBRATION contains LUC (light) — studying by lamplight late at night. (Latin LUCUBRARE = work by candlelight.) — lucubration, intense scholarly study.

k.syn: drag, draw, hale, pull, tow, tug
k.ant: drive, propel, push 推动
k.en: to cause to follow by applying steady force on

ex.en: lug the lawn mower out into the backyard
ex.cn: 把割草机拖进院子

k.syn: bear, cart, convey, ferry, haul, pack, tote, transport
k.en: to carry laboriously

ex.en: I don't understand why he's always lugging all of his books around when his locker is right over there.
ex.cn: 我真搞不懂为什么他总是明明在有锁柜的情况下还随身扛着所有的书。

m: LUG rhymes with TUG — both involve heavy pulling. — lug, to drag heavily.

k.syn: deploring, doleful, dolorous, lamentable, melancholy, morose, plaintive, rueful, saturnine, sullen, woeful
k.ant: cheerful, delighted, jocund, jovial 快乐的
k.en: mournful, dismal, or gloomy, especially to an exaggerated or ludicrous degree

ex.en: his lugubrious tear-stained face
ex.cn: 他忧郁而带着泪痕的脸庞

m: LUGUBRIOUS rhymes with LUG-A-BRIOUS — sounds like dragging gloomy weight. (Latin LUGERE = mourn.) — lugubrious, mournfully exaggerated.

k.syn: break, breath, interruption, recess
k.en: a momentary halt in an activity

ex.en: the lull before the storm
ex.cn: 暴风雨前的平静

k.syn: allay, balm, becalm, compose, lullaby, quiet, salve, settle, soothe, still, tranquilize
k.ant: agitate, discompose, disquiet, disturb, perturb, upset, vex 打扰，扰乱
k.en: to free from distress or disturbance

ex.en: The absence of attacks for such an extended period had lulled the nation into a false sense of security.
ex.cn: 长期以来没有遭受攻击让这个国家产生了一种错误的安全感。

m: LULL contains LU + LL — the soothing "loo-loo" sound that calms a baby. — lull, to calm / a calm pause.

k.syn: berceuse, cradlesong
k.en: a song to quiet children or lull them to sleep

ex.en: The mother sang a lullaby to the baby every night.
ex.cn: 这位母亲每晚都给孩子唱摇篮曲。

k.syn: allay, balm, becalm, compose, lull, quiet, salve, settle, soothe, still, tranquilize
k.ant: agitate, discompose, disquiet, disturb, perturb, upset, vex 打扰，扰乱
k.en: to free from distress or disturbance

ex.en: Reclining peacefully on the deck, he was lullabied by the gentle motion of the ship.
ex.cn: 他躺卧在甲板上，随着船轻轻的颠簸放松下来。

m: LULLABY contains LULL — a song that LULLs to sleep. — lullaby, a calming bedtime song.

k.syn: timber, wood
k.en: tree logs as prepared for human use

ex.en: A huge amount of lumber will be needed to build the house.
ex.cn: 建这栋房子需要大量的木材。

k.syn: flounder, plod, stumble, trudge
k.ant: glide, slide 轻松地滑动
k.en: to walk or move with heavy clumsiness

ex.en: The elephant lumbered through the jungle.
ex.cn: 大象缓缓地穿越丛林。

k.syn: burden, encumber, freight, lade, laden, saddle, weight
k.ant: disburden, discharge, disencumber, unlade, unload 卸下，解脱
k.en: to place a weight or burden on

ex.en: lumber the expedition with unnecessary equipment and supplies
ex.cn: 不必要的仪器和补给品给此次远征徒增了许多负担

m: to LUMBER along — heavy slow movement like carrying LUMBER (timber). — lumber, to move heavily / timber.

k.en: a unit of luminous flux equal to the light

m: LUMEN comes from LUX/LUMIN (Latin for light) — the unit of light brightness. — lumen, a unit of brightness.

k.syn: celebrity, eminence, figure, icon, notability, star, superstar
k.ant: nobody, nonentity 小人物
k.en: a person who has achieved eminence in a specific field

ex.en: Buddhist luminary
ex.cn: 佛学大师

ex.en: Luminaries from the worlds of sports, entertainment, and politics were at the gala.
ex.cn: 全球体育界、娱乐圈和政界的名人都出席了此次盛会。

m: LUMINARY contains LUMIN (light) — a leading light in their field. — luminary, an eminent person.

k.syn: careen, dodder, falter, reel, stagger, stumble, teeter, totter, waddle
k.ant: progress smoothly 平稳前进；march, stride, swagger 游行，大步走
k.en: to move forward while swaying from side to side

ex.en: The ship lurched in the storm.
ex.cn: 船在风暴中摇摆前行。

m: LURCH rhymes with CHURCH (in sound) — a sudden tilting motion (like a drunken stumble outside the church). — lurch, to stagger.

k.syn: ambush, snake, steal
k.ant: appear, come out 出来
k.en: to lie in wait in a place of concealment especially for an evil purpose

ex.en: Dangers lurk in the path of wilderness.
ex.cn: 在这条荒野的小路上隐伏着危险。

m: LURK rhymes with MURK — hide in MURKY shadows. — lurk, to hide stealthily.

k.syn: booming, exuberant, flourishing, lively, luxuriant, rampant, thriving, verdant, vivacious
k.ant: blighted, faded, sere, withered 干枯的，凋谢的
k.en: growing vigorously especially with luxuriant foliage

ex.en: lush grass
ex.cn: 茂盛的草

k.syn: cornucopian, fecund, fruitful, productive, prolific, rich
k.ant: barren, dead, infertile, sterile, unproductive 贫瘠的
k.en: producing abundantly

ex.en: His lush fields were the envy of neighboring farmers.
ex.cn: 他富饶多产的土地让邻居羡慕嫉妒恨。

k.syn: ambrosial, luscious, palatable, savory, sensuous, tasteful, tasty, voluptuous
k.ant: flat, flavorless, insipid, stale, tasteless 乏味的
k.cn: （声音、味道）优美的，令人愉悦的
k.en: appealing to the senses

ex.en: the lush sounds of the orchestra
ex.cn: 管弦乐队奏出的悦耳声音

m: LUSH rhymes with PLUSH — both soft and abundant. — lush, abundantly luxuriant.

k.syn: brilliant, burnished, gleaming, glistening, glossy, polished, refulgent, rubbed, shining, sleek, splendid
k.ant: dim, dull, lackluster, lusterless 昏暗的
k.en: having a shiny surface or finish

ex.en: lustrous black hair
ex.cn: 乌黑光亮的头发

m: LUSTROUS contains LUSTER — shining with luster. — lustrous, glossy.

k.en: a condition of abundance or great ease and comfort; something desirable but expensive

m: LUXURY contains LUX ≈ "luxe" (Latin LUX = light) — lavishness that catches the light. — luxury, indulgent comfort.

k.syn: ballad, ditty, jingle, vocal
k.en: a short musical composition for the human voice often with instrumental accompaniment

ex.en: The guitarist improvised and sang a gentle lyric while playing.
ex.cn: 吉他手在弹奏的过程中即兴创作并哼唱了一首小曲。

k.syn: euphonious, lyrical, mellifluous, mellow, melodious, musical, poetical
k.ant: prosaic, prose 无聊乏味的
k.en: having a pleasantly flowing quality suggestive of poetry or music

ex.en: The film's lyric photography really enhanced its romantic mood.
ex.cn: 电影中如诗歌般的图像效果着实增强了浪漫的氛围。

m: LYRIC contains LYR ≈ LYRE (Greek harp) — songs originally sung to the LYRE. — lyric, song-like words.

k.syn: appalling, atrocious, dreadful, ghastly, gruesome, hideous, horrific, nightmarish, terrific
k.ant: agreeable, delightful, enjoyable, pleasant 令人愉悦的
k.en: suggesting the horror of death and decay; gruesome

ex.en: Impressively, Plants vs. Zombies presented a supposedly macabre theme in such an enjoyable way.
ex.cn: 令人难忘的是，《植物大战僵尸》将一个本应该十分恐怖的主题用一种如此欢乐的方式呈现出来。

m: MACABRE rhymes with CADAVER (in feel) — death-themed, gruesome. (From medieval "Dance Macabre.") — macabre, gruesome / death-themed.

k.syn: drench, drown, impregnate, saturate, sodden, sop, souse, steep
k.ant: wring 拧干；dehydrate, desiccate, parch, sorch, sear 烤干，烤焦
k.en: to make soft by soaking or steeping in a liquid

ex.en: macerate the sample in ethanol
ex.cn: 用乙醇浸软试样

m: MACERATE rhymes with LACERATE — but soak instead of tear; soften by soaking. — macerate, to soak and soften.

k.syn: besmirch, dot, dirty, soil, spot, stain
k.ant: clean, cleanse, purify, wash 清洗，弄干净
k.cn: 使有斑点，弄脏；玷污，损坏
k.en: to spot; blemish

ex.en: Her reputation was maculated after the affair with a married man.
ex.cn: 自从被指与一个已婚男士有染之后，她的名声受到了影响。

m: MACULATE — to make spotted. (Latin MACULA = spot/stain. Compare IMMACULATE = without spots.) — maculate, to stain / spot.

k.syn: gulf, vortex, whirlpool
k.en: a powerful often violent whirlpool sucking in objects within a given radius

ex.en: Their raft got caught in a maelstrom.
ex.cn: 他们的筏被一个漩涡卷住了。

k.syn: chaos, disorder, pandemonium, tumult, tumoil, upheaval, uproar
k.ant: calm 风平浪静
k.en: a violent or turbulent situation

ex.en: the maelstrom of war
ex.cn: 战争带来的乱世

m: MAELSTROM rhymes with MALE-STORM — a powerful whirlpool / chaotic storm. (Originally a Norwegian whirlpool.) — maelstrom, a violent whirlpool / chaos.

k.syn: august, epic, glorious, grand, imperial, imposing, massive, monumental, noble, regal, splendid
k.ant: humble, unimpressive 平凡的
k.en: strikingly beautiful or impressive

ex.en: a magnificent cathedral
ex.cn: 宏伟壮观的大教堂

m: MAGNIFICENT contains MAGN- (great, Latin MAGNUS) — greatly impressive. — magnificent, splendidly great.

k.syn: awkward, bumbling, clumsy, fumbled, gauche, graceless, heavy- handed, inept, unhandy
k.ant: adroit, ambidexterous, deft, dexterous, handy 灵巧的
k.en: lacking or showing a lack of nimbleness in using one's hands

ex.en: a maladroit movement
ex.cn: 笨拙的动作

m: MAL- (bad) + ADROIT (skilled) — opposite of skilled, clumsy. — maladroit, clumsy.

k.syn: debility, decrepitude, disease, feebleness, infirmity, infirmness, sickliness, unhealthiness
k.en: a vague feeling of bodily discomfort, as at the beginning of an illness

ex.en: He complained of depression, headaches and malaise.
ex.cn: 他抱怨说感到沮丧、头痛和身体不适。

m: mal- (bad) + -aise (ease) — bad-ease, vague unwellness. — malaise, vague feeling of being unwell.

k.syn: complainer, faultfinder, grouch, rebel
k.en: one who is in active opposition to an established order or government

ex.en: The chaos was caused by a handful of malcontents.
ex.cn: 混乱是由一小撮不满分子引起的。

k.syn: discontented, discontent, disgruntled, displeased, dissatisfied, ungratified
k.ant: contented, fulfilled, gratified, pleased, satisfied 满意的
k.en: dissatisfied with the existing state of affairs

ex.en: The film follows three malcontent teenagers around Paris.
ex.cn: 电影围绕着三个不满现实的青少年在巴黎展开。

m: MAL- (bad) + CONTENT — discontent, dissatisfied. — malcontent, a discontented person.

k.syn: bad, cruel, despiteful, evil, malevolent, malign, mean, nasty, spiteful, vicious, virulent, wicked
k.ant: benevolent, benign, benignant, charitable, kind, kindly 善良的，仁慈的
k.en: given to, marked by, or arising from malice; deliberately harmful

ex.en: spread malicious gossips
ex.cn: 散播恶意的流言蜚语

m: MALICIOUS contains MAL- (bad) + ICIOUS — full of bad intent. — malicious, intending harm.

k.syn: bad, cruel, despiteful, evil, malevolent, malicious, mean, nasty, spiteful, vicious, virulent, wicked
k.ant: benevolent, benign, benignant, charitable, kind, kindly 善良的，仁慈的
k.en: having or showing a desire to cause someone pain or suffering for the sheer enjoyment of it

ex.en: Both parties to the divorce showed a malign desire to make each other's future life utterly miserable.
ex.cn: 离婚双方都表露出了恶毒的愿望，希望对方未来的生活陷入彻底的悲剧之中。

k.syn: asperse, blacken, calumniate, defame, libel, smear, traduce, vilify
k.ant: acclaim, applaud, eulogize, extol, laud, praise 赞美；defend, vindicate 辩护
k.en: to utter injuriously misleading or false reports about: speak evil of

ex.en: the belief that it is possible to win an election without maligning anyone
ex.cn: 一种信念，那就是不需要诽谤他人而赢得选举是可能的

m: MALIGN contains MAL- (bad) + IGN ≈ "sign" — to put a bad sign on someone. — malign, to slander / harmful.

k.syn: goldbrick, shirk
k.ant: confront, face, meet 面对
k.der: malingerer n. 装病以逃避工作的人
k.en: to pretend or exaggerate incapacity or illness（as to avoid duty or work）

ex.en: He claims he's ill, but I think he's just malingering.
ex.cn: 他声称他病了，但我觉得他是装的。

m: MALINGER contains MAL- (bad) — fake illness for bad reasons. ("-inger" sounds like LINGER — staying away from work.) — malinger, to feign illness.

k.syn: moldable, waxy
k.en: capable of being extended or shaped by beating with a hammer or by the pressure of rollers

ex.en: a malleable metal
ex.cn: 有延展性的金属

k.syn: ductile, elastic, fluid, modifiable, plastic, pliable, pliant, supple, variable
k.ant: adamant, intractable, recalcitrant, refractory, ungovernable, unmanageable, unruly 难管制的
k.en: capable of being altered or controlled by outside forces or influences

ex.en: The cult leader took advantage of the malleable, compliant personalities of his followers.
ex.cn: 这个头目利用了他的信徒们容易被控制和顺从的特征。

m: MALLEABLE contains MALL (hammer, Latin MALLEUS) — shapeable by a hammer. — malleable, easily shaped.

k.en: an offensive smell; a stench or bad odor

m: MAL- (bad) + ODOR — bad smell. — malodor, a bad smell.

k.syn: compulsory, forced, imperative, incumbent, involuntary, necessary, obligatory, peremptory, required
k.ant: elective, optional, voluntary 可自由选择的，选修的
k.en: forcing one's compliance or participation

ex.en: GRE test is mandatory for all students, regardless of their nationality, who wish to apply for graduate schools in the United States. GRE
ex.cn: 是每个申请美国研究生院的学生（无论国籍）所必须参加的考试。

m: MANDATORY contains MAND (order, Latin MANDARE) — required by order. (Same root as DEMAND, COMMAND.) — mandatory, required.

k.syn: batter, deform, disfigure, distort, lacerate, mutilate, rend, wreck
k.ant: cure, heal, rehabilitate 治愈，康复
k.en: to injure with deep disfiguring wounds by cutting, tearing, or crushing

ex.en: His body was mangled beyond recognition.
ex.cn: 他的尸体已经被损毁到无法辨认的程度了。

k.syn: blow, bumble, bungle, butcher, fumble, mar, mess, ruin, foul up, screw up
k.en: to ruin or spoil through ineptitude or ignorance

ex.en: The orchestra had completely mangled Bach's music.
ex.cn: 乐队彻底演砸了巴赫的音乐。

m: MANGLE rhymes with TANGLE — but worse: MANGLE damages beyond repair. — mangle, to badly damage.

k.syn: base, contemptible, debased, despicable, detestable, execrable, mean, sordid, squalid
k.ant: lofty, noble, upright, venerable, virtuous 正直的，有道德的
k.en: mean; contemptible

ex.en: a mangy trick
ex.cn: 卑鄙的手段

m: MANGY contains MANGE — afflicted with MANGE (skin disease in animals); shabby. — mangy, shabby / contemptible.

k.syn: ardor, craze, enthusiasm, fervor, obsession, passion, preoccupation, prepossession, zeal
k.ant: apathy, indifference, nonchalance, torpor 冷漠，麻木
k.en: an excessively intense enthusiasm, interest, or desire; a craze

ex.en: a mania for neatness
ex.cn: 强烈的洁癖

m: MANIA — Greek root for madness (also the suffix -MANIA = obsession). — mania, intense passion / madness.

k.syn: apparent, clear, distinct, evident, lucid, obvious, palpable, patent, perspicuous, plain, transparent
k.ant: cryptic, enigmatic, indistinct, mysterious, obfuscated, obscure, unclear 晦涩的，难懂的
k.en: clearly apparent to the sight or understanding; obvious

ex.en: He is a manifest poseur.
ex.cn: 他显然是个装模作样的人。

k.syn: bespeak, betray, demonstrate, display, evince, expose, reveal
k.ant: conceal, hide 隐藏
k.en: to make evident or certain by showing or displaying

ex.en: His frustration is often manifested by a minor facial tic.
ex.cn: 他的沮丧通常会从脸部的细微抽搐中表现出来。

m: MANIFEST contains MANI (hand) + FEST (struck) — caught hand-in-the-act, plain to see. — manifest, clearly evident.

k.syn: handle, finesse, manage
k.ant: bungle, fumble 笨拙地做
k.en: to move, arrange, operate, or control by the hands or by mechanical means, especially in a skillful manner

ex.en: She manipulated the lights to get just the effect she wanted.
ex.cn: 她巧妙操控灯光，达到了她想要的效果。

k.syn: machinate, maneuver
k.en: to influence or manage shrewdly or deviously

ex.en: He manipulated public opinion in his favor.
ex.cn: 他巧妙地将大众观点导向了有利于他的一边。

ex.en: manipulate a foreign language
ex.cn: 熟练地掌握外语

m: MANIPULATE contains MANI (hand) + PUL (pull) — handle / pull strings. — manipulate, to handle / control deviously.

k.syn: affected, artificial, assumed, factitious, fake, feigned, pretended, pseudo, sham, spurious
k.ant: natural 自然的；artless, genuine, unfeigned 真诚的
k.en: having an artificial or stilted character

ex.en: a mannered speech
ex.cn: 做作的演讲

m: MANNERED contains MANNER — having a put-on MANNER. — mannered, artificially formal.

k.syn: free, emancipate, enfranchise, liberate, loose, release, unbind, unchain, unshackle
k.ant: detain 拘留；enchain, enfetter, enslave 奴役
k.en: to release from slavery or bondage

ex.en: Though he was an outspoken defender of liberty, this son of Virginia did not manumit his own slaves until he was on his deathbed.
ex.cn: 尽管这位维吉尼亚之子公开地支持自由，但他直到临死前才释放自己的奴隶。

m: MANU (hand, Latin) + MIT (send) — let go from your hand, free a slave. — manumit, to free from slavery.

k.syn: calligraphy, penmanship, script
k.ant: print, type, typewriting 打字稿
k.en: a book, document, or other composition written by hand

ex.en: beautiful Latin manuscript on the school's diplomas
ex.cn: 学校毕业证书上美丽的手写拉丁文

m: MANU (hand) + SCRIPT (written) — handwritten document. — manuscript, a handwritten text.

k.syn: blight, blotch, defect, deformity, disfigurement, fault, flaw, imperfection, mark, pockmark, scar
k.en: something that spoils the appearance or completeness of a thing

ex.en: mars on the furniture
ex.cn: 家具上的污点

k.syn: blemish, compromise, cripple, damage, deface, disfigure, harm, hurt, impair, injure, spoil, vitiate
k.ant: doctor, fix, mend, patch, renovate, repair 修补；adorn, beautify, bedeck, embellish, garnish 装饰
k.en: to impair the soundness, perfection, or integrity of; spoil

ex.en: The once flatroad surface is now marred by numerous potholes.
ex.cn: 曾经平整的路面如今被坑洞所破坏。

ex.en: an election marred by sexual scandal
ex.cn: 被性丑闻蒙上阴影的选举

m: MAR rhymes with SCAR — both leave damage. — mar, to damage / spoil.

k.syn: bog, fen, marshland, mire, moor, morass, quagmire, slough, swamp, wetland
k.en: an area of soft, wet, low-lying land

ex.en: The marshes along the coast support a remarkable profusion of plants and animals.
ex.cn: 沿着海岸线一带的沼泽为大量的动植物提供了栖息所。

m: MARSH rhymes with HARSH — a HARSH wet land. — marsh, wetlands.

k.en: any of various nonplacental mammals of the order Marsupialia, including kangaroos

m: MARSUPIAL contains MARSU (pouch, Greek MARSUPION) — pouched mammal (kangaroo, koala). — marsupial, a pouched animal.

k.en: of, relating to, or suited for war

m: MARTIAL comes from MARS (Roman god of war) — warlike. — martial, warlike.

k.syn: disciplinarian, purist, stickler
k.ant: reprobate 放纵的人
k.en: a strict disciplinarian

ex.en: He's a retired lieutenant and a bit of a martinet.
ex.cn: 他是一个退役的中尉，并且是一个有点纪律严明的人。

m: MARTINET — named after Jean Martinet, a famously strict 17th-century French drill master. — martinet, a strict disciplinarian.

k.syn: flash, miracle, phenomenon, prodigy, splendor
k.en: one that evokes surprise, admiration, or wonder

ex.en: The robot is a marvel of modern engineering.
ex.cn: 机器人是现代工程领域的奇迹。

k.syn: gape, gaze, goggle, wonder
k.cn: （因为壮观、美丽等而）表示惊讶
k.en: to feel amazement or bewilderment at or about

ex.en: marvel at the tranquility of Chopin's nocturne
ex.cn: 惊叹于肖邦夜曲中体现出来的宁静祥和

m: MARVEL contains MARV ≈ "MARV-elous" — that wonderstruck pause. (Same root as MIRACLE.) — marvel, to wonder at.

k.syn: crush, grind, smash, squeeze
k.ant: agglomerate 聚集成团
k.en: to cause to become a pulpy mass

ex.en: mash potatoes before adding it to the mixture
ex.cn: 在混合之前捣碎土豆

m: MASH rhymes with SMASH / CRASH — all involve crushing. — mash, to crush into pulp.

k.syn: facade, guise, mask, pretense, semblance, show, veil
k.en: a display of emotion or behavior that is insincere or intended to deceive

ex.en: Although she was deeply bored, she maintained a masquerade of polite interest as her guest droned on.
ex.cn: 尽管她早已对客人滔滔不绝的话感到厌倦，但她还是装出了一副饶有兴致的样子以示礼貌。

k.syn: act, disguise, pose, pretend
k.ant: betray, disclose, reveal, unmask 揭露
k.en: to disguise oneself

ex.en: masquerade as a policeman
ex.cn: 化装成警察

m: MASQUERADE contains MASK — a MASK-erade ball where everyone hides identity. — masquerade, a disguise / pretense.

k.syn: deftness, dexterity, finesse, prowess, virtuosity
k.ant: amateurishness 业余
k.en: possession or display of great skill or technique

ex.en: She has mastery of several languages.
ex.cn: 她熟练掌握多种语言。

m: MASTERY contains MASTER — being a MASTER. — mastery, expert skill.

k.en: to grind or crush (food) with or as if with the teeth

ex.en: Don't gulp everything down without masticating.
ex.cn: 不要不经咀嚼就将食物吞入

m: MASTICATE rhymes with MASCOT-CATE — but it's chewing (Latin MASTICARE = chew). — masticate, to chew.

k.syn: admit, enroll, inscribe, recruit, register
k.ant: commence, graduate 毕业；delist 除名
k.en: to admit or be admitted into a group, especially a college or university

ex.en: matriculate at Princeton
ex.cn: 在普林斯顿入学

m: MATRICULATE contains MATR (mother, Latin MATER) — be admitted to alma MATER (the school). — matriculate, to be admitted to a school.

k.en: a usually rectangular pad of heavy cloth filled with soft material or an arrangement of coiled

m: MATTRESS contains MATT ≈ "mat" — a thicker MAT to sleep on. — mattress, a sleeping pad.

k.syn: lachrymose, mawkish, mushy, overemotional, sentimental, tearful
k.ant: blithe, cheerful, jocund, jovial, jubilant 高兴的；apathetic, impassive, indifferent 冷漠的
k.en: effusively or tearfully sentimental

ex.en: Some naive students display an almost maudlin concern for the welfare of animals while ignoring basic social inequity.
ex.cn: 一些幼稚的学生忽视根本的社会不公平问题，而对动物的权益表现出一种多愁善感的忧虑。

m: MAUDLIN comes from Mary MAGDALENE (often depicted weeping) — overly tearful. — maudlin, sentimentally tearful.

k.syn: ace, adept, connoisseur, expert, maestro, master, professional, proficient, virtuoso
k.ant: amateur, dabbler, dilettante 业余爱好者
k.en: one who is experienced or knowledgeable: expert

ex.en: As an investment maven, he was doing well even when the market was doing poorly.
ex.cn: 作为一名投资高手，他在市场不景气的时候也能赚钱。

m: MAVEN rhymes with HAVEN — go to a MAVEN as your HAVEN of expertise. (Yiddish for "expert.") — maven, an expert.

k.syn: bohemian, deviant, heretic, iconoclast, nonconformist
k.ant: conformer, conformist, conventionalist 遵从传统的人
k.en: a person who does not conform to generally accepted standards or customs

ex.en: Some mavericks believe that both gravity and light are electromagnetic forces.
ex.cn: 一些特立独行的人认为，重力和光都是电磁力。

k.syn: dissentient, dissenting, dissident, heterodox, iconoclastic, unorthodox
k.ant: conforming, conventional, orthodox 遵从传统的
k.cn: 标新立异的，不合常规的
k.en: deviating from commonly accepted beliefs or practices

ex.en: a maverick view on marriage
ex.cn: 关于婚姻的非传统看法

m: MAVERICK — named after Samuel Maverick, a Texas rancher who refused to brand his cattle, so unbranded cattle = mavericks = independents. — maverick, a non-conformist.

k.syn: lachrymose, maudlin, mushy, overemotional, sentimental, tearful
k.ant: blithe, cheerful, jocund, jovial, jubilant 高兴的；apathetic, impassive, indifferent 冷漠的
k.en: excessively and objectionably sentimental

ex.en: a mawkish love story
ex.cn: 令人伤感的爱情故事

m: MAWKISH contains MAW ≈ "sickening" — sickly sweet sentiment. (Old Norse "mathkr" = maggot.) — mawkish, sickeningly sentimental.

k.syn: exiguous, niggardly, poor, scanty, scarce, skimpy, slender, slim, sparing, sparse, stingy
k.ant: abundant, ample, bountiful, copious, generous, liberal, plenteous, plentiful 大量的，富足的
k.en: deficient in quantity, fullness, or extent; scanty

ex.en: meager cultural resources
ex.cn: 贫乏的文化资源

m: MEAGER rhymes with EAGER — but the opposite of plenty: scant rations. — meager, scanty.

k.syn: base, contemptible, despicable, detestable, dirty, dishonorable, execrable, ignominious, sordid
k.ant: lofty, noble, venerable, virtuous高尚的
k.en: ignoble; base

ex.en: a mean trick to play on a credulous person
ex.cn: 耍在一个轻信别人的人身上的卑劣诡计

k.syn: closefisted, mingy, miserly, niggardly, parsimonious, penurious, tightfisted, ungenerous
k.ant: generous, liberal, munificent 慷慨的；extravagant, lavish, prodigal, profligate, spendthrift 浪费的
k.en: giving or sharing as little as possible

ex.en: a mean child who hoarded all her toys
ex.cn: 一个把她的所有玩具都藏起来的小气孩子

k.syn: aim, aspire, calculate, contemplate, design, intend, meditate, plan
k.en: to have in mind as a purpose or goal

ex.en: I mean to win this race.
ex.cn: 我一定要赢这场比赛。

k.syn: connote, denote, express, import, signify, spell
k.en: to serve or intend to convey, show, or indicate

ex.en: The national anthem means various things to various people.
ex.cn: 对于不同的人来说，国歌的意义不尽相同。

m: MEAN rhymes with LEAN — both can suggest stinginess; a LEAN miser is MEAN with money. — mean, stingy / unkind.

k.syn: amble, cruise, drift, float, ramble, roam, saunter,stroll, wander
k.en: to move aimlessly and idly without fixed direction

ex.en: meander along the river
ex.cn: 沿着河闲逛

ex.en: vagabonds meandering through life
ex.cn: 漂泊一生的浪子

m: MEANDER comes from the Maeander river in Turkey, famous for its winding course. — meander, to wind / wander.

k.syn: inconsequential, inconsiderable, insignificant, minute, paltry, peanut, petty, slight, trifling, trivial
k.ant: big, consequential, considerable, significant 大量的，重要的
k.cn: 少得可怜的，微不足道的
k.en: so small or unimportant as to warrant little or no attention

ex.en: The stingy man gave the parking attendant a measly tip.
ex.cn: 那个吝啬男给了停车保安少得可怜的小费。

m: MEASLY rhymes with SLEAZY (in feel) — both describe paltry, low things. (Originally: afflicted with measles.) — measly, contemptibly small.

k.syn: advised, calculated, considered, knowing, reasoned, studied, thoughtful, weighed
k.ant: casual 随意的；uncalculated, unconsidered, unstudied 没仔细考虑过的
k.en: deliberated, calculated

ex.en: a measured response to the terrorist attack
ex.cn: 就恐怖袭击做出的深思熟虑的反应

m: MEASURED contains MEASURE — done with careful measurement and thought. — measured, deliberately careful.

k.syn: interfere, interlope, intermeddle, intrude, obtrude, poke, pry, snoop
k.ant: disregard, ignore, neglect, omit, overlook 忽略，不理会
k.en: to intrude into other people's affairs or business; interfere

ex.en: Please stop meddling in my marriage.
ex.cn: 请不要来干涉我的婚姻。

m: MEDDLE rhymes with MEDAL but the opposite — instead of earning a MEDAL, you MEDDLE in others' affairs. — meddle, to interfere.

k.en: a nutrient system for the artificial cultivation

m: MEDIA contains MEDI ≈ "middle" (Latin MEDIUS) — the medium between cell growth and the lab. — media, a growth medium / middle substance.

k.syn: conciliate, intercede, intermediate, interpose
k.ant: arouse, encourage, excite, foment, incite, inflame, instigate, pique, spark, stimulate, stir 煽动激起
k.en: to intervene between two or more disputants in order to bring about an agreement, a settlement, or a compromise

ex.en: mediate a labor-management dispute
ex.cn: 调解劳工纠纷

m: MEDIATE contains MEDI (middle) — stand in the middle. — mediate, to be in the middle / negotiate.

k.syn: average, common, commonplace, fair, indifferent, medium, ordinary
k.ant: outstanding, excellent, exceptional, extraordinary, impressive, superior 优秀的
k.cn: 平庸的；质量中等偏下的
k.en: moderate to inferior in quality; ordinary

ex.en: Without a desire for success, life is at best mediocre.
ex.cn: 没有对成功的强烈渴望，人生最多也就是碌碌无为。

m: MEDIOCRE contains MEDI (middle) + OCRE ≈ "OK-ish" — middling, average. — mediocre, of middling quality.

k.syn: agglomerate, collage, hodgepodge, jumble, montage, motley, muddle, salad, shuffle, variety, welter
k.en: an unorganized collection or mixture of various things

ex.en: a medley of snack foods available on the buffet table
ex.cn: 自助餐桌上供应着各式各样的小吃

m: MEDLEY rhymes with MEDDLE — both involve mixing in. A MEDLEY is a mixed-up song. — medley, a mixture / song mash-up.

k.syn: humble, modest, unassuming, unpretentious
k.ant: arrogant, bumptious, haughty, imperious, pompous, presumptuous, supercilious 自大的
k.en: showing patience and humility; gentle

ex.en: He was a meek, mild-mannered fellow.
ex.cn: 他是个谦逊、温文尔雅的人。

k.syn: compliant, docile, manageable, obedient, submissive, tractable
k.ant: adamant, headstrong, intractable, obstinate, refractory, stubborn, unruly, unyielding 倔强的，不顺从的
k.en: easily imposed on; submissive

ex.en: He may be self-effacing, but he certainly isn't meek.
ex.cn: 他可能是个很低调的人，但他绝非软骨头。

m: MEEK rhymes with WEAK / GEEK — quiet and unassertive. — meek, quietly submissive.

k.syn: applicable, appropriate, apt, becoming, befitting, felicitous, fitting, proper, right, suitable
k.ant: improper, inapplicable, inapposite, inappropriate, inapt, infelicitous, malapropos 不合适的
k.en: precisely adapted to a particular situation, need, or circumstance: very proper

ex.en: In this case, splitting the winnings of the contested lottery ticket seems like a meet solution.
ex.cn: 在这种情况下，平分有争议的彩票奖金或许是个合适的解决方案。

m: MEET — old usage "meet for the occasion" = fitting (compare "meet and right"). — meet, suitable / fitting.

k.en: the belief that improvement

m: MELIORISM contains MELIOR (better, Latin) — belief the world can get BETTER. (Same root as AMELIORATE.) — meliorism, belief in progress.

k.syn: dulcet, euphonious, mellifluent, mellow, melodious
k.ant: cacophonous, raspy 刺耳的
k.en: smooth and sweet

ex.en: a mellifluous voice
ex.cn: 甜美的嗓音

m: MELLI (honey, Latin MEL) + FLUOUS (flowing) — flowing like honey. — mellifluous, sweetly flowing in sound.

k.en: a work as a movie or play characterized by extravagant theatricality and by the predominance

m: MELO (song, Greek) + DRAMA — exaggeratedly dramatic, like an over-sung opera. — melodrama, exaggerated drama.

k.en: a sweet or agreeable succession or arrangement of musical sounds

m: MELODY contains MEL- (song, Greek MELOS) — a sweet song. — melody, a tune.

k.en: a thin soft pliable sheet or layer especially of animal or plant origin, cell

m: MEMBRANE contains MEMBR ≈ "member-skin" — Latin MEMBRANA = skin covering a body part. — membrane, a thin lining.

k.syn: compromise, hazard, imperil, jeopardize, peril, risk, threaten
k.ant: rescue, save 救出
k.der: menacing adj. 具有威胁性的
k.en: to make a show of intention to harm; to place in danger

ex.en: Stockpiles of nuclear weapons continue to menace the inhabitants of this planet.
ex.cn: 大量的核武器继续威胁着这个行星上的居民。

m: MENACE rhymes with MENA-tion — a stern WARNING that hangs in the air. (Latin MINARI = threaten.) — menace, a threat.

k.syn: dishonest, deceitful, fallacious, lying, spurious, untruthful
k.ant: authentic, honest, truthful, veracious 真实的，诚实的
k.en: telling or containing lies

ex.en: mendacious tales about his adventures
ex.cn: 关于他冒险的虚假传说

m: contains MEND — swears he'll MEND it but really he's MENDacious — full of lies. — mendacious, given to lying.

k.syn: advisor, coach, counselor, guide, instructor, teacher
k.ant: disciple, pupil 弟子；apprentice 学徒
k.en: a trusted counselor or guide

ex.en: not only an lecturer but also a spirit mentor
ex.cn: 不仅是授课者，还是精神导师

k.syn: coach, counsel, lead, pilot, shepherd, show, tutor
k.ant: comply, follow, observe 遵从
k.en: to give advice and instruction regarding the course or process to be followed

ex.en: We're looking for volunteers to mentor students in career planning.
ex.cn: 我们正在寻找能指导学生职业规划的志愿者。

m: MENTOR — Odysseus's wise friend MENTOR who guided his son. — mentor, a guide / teacher.

k.syn: hack
k.en: a professional soldier hired for service in a foreign army

ex.en: hire a mercenary army to protect the VIP
ex.cn: 雇佣了一支佣兵来保护重要人物

k.syn: acquisitive, avaricious, avid, covetous, grasping, greedy, moneygrubbing, rapacious
k.ant: benevolent, generous, liberal, philanthropic, munificent 慷慨的
k.en: motivated solely by a desire for monetary or material gain

ex.en: Virtue flies from the heart of a mercenary man.
ex.cn: 唯利是图的人没有美德可言。

m: MERCENARY contains MERC- (pay, Latin MERCES, as in COMMERCE) — paid soldier; or motivated by money. — mercenary, money-motivated.

k.syn: capricious, changeful, fluctuating, fluid, mutable, temperamental, uncertain, variable, volatile
k.ant: certain, constant, immutable, invariable, settled, stable, steady, unvarying 稳定的，不变的
k.en: characterized by rapid and unpredictable changeableness of mood

ex.en: his mercurial temperament
ex.cn: 他善变的脾气

m: MERCURIAL — like the planet Mercury / quicksilver: fast-changing. (Roman god Mercury was swift and unpredictable.) — mercurial, rapidly changing in mood.

k.syn: condign, deserved, due, fair, justified, right, warranted
k.ant: undeserved, undue 不应得的；gratuitous, unjustified, unwarranted 没有根据的，无正当理由的
k.en: being what is called for by accepted standards of right and wrong

ex.en: a merited bonus
ex.cn: 应得的奖金

ex.en: The punishment, although harsh, was entirely merited.
ex.cn: 虽然惩罚很严厉，但的确是应该的。

m: MERITED contains MERIT — having earned by merit. — merited, deserved.

k.syn: enmesh, ensnare, ensnarl, entoil, entrap, net, snare, tangle, trap
k.ant: disentangle, untangle 解开，使解脱
k.en: to catch or hold as if in a net

ex.en: Dolphins sometimes become meshed in fishnets.
ex.cn: 有时海豚也会被渔网缠住。

m: MESH rhymes with FLESH — but a MESH net catches FLESH/fish; to mesh = entangle. — mesh, to entangle / fit together.

k.en: a strong or spellbinding appeal;

m: from MESMER — hypnotic spell. — mesmerism, hypnotic influence.

k.syn: alchemize, transfigure, transform, transmute, transpose, transubstantiate
k.ant: remain 保持
k.en: to change into a different physical form especially by supernatural means

ex.en: a science fiction story in which radiation metamorphoses people into giant bugs
ex.cn: 一个描绘辐射将人变成了巨大虫子的科幻小说

m: META- (change) + MORPH (shape) — change shape (a caterpillar metamorphoses into a butterfly). — metamorphose, to transform.

k.en: a figure of speech in which a word or phrase that ordinarily designates one thing is used to designate another, making an implicit comparison

m: META- (across) + PHOR (carry, Greek PHEREIN) — carry meaning across from one thing to another. — metaphor, a comparison.

k.en: a division of philosophy that examines the fundamental nature of reality and existence

m: META- (beyond) + PHYSICS — the study of what is beyond physics. — metaphysics, philosophy of being.

k.syn: ephemeral, evanescent, fleeting, momentary, rapid, transient, transitory
k.ant: enduring, lasting, permanent, prolonged 持久的
k.en: similar to a meteor in speed, brilliance, or brevity

ex.en: a meteoric rise to fame
ex.cn: 一夜成名

m: METEORIC contains METEOR — a METEOR streaks through fast and bright. — meteoric, swiftly brilliant but brief.

k.en: a science that deals with the atmosphere and its phenomena and especially with weather

m: METEOR + OLOGY — Greek METEORA = things in the air; the study of weather. — meteorology, weather science.

k.syn: neat, orderly, organized, regular, systematic, systematized
k.ant: disorganized, haphazard, irregular, unsystematic 杂乱的，混乱无序的
k.en: arranged or proceeding in regular, systematic order

ex.en: a methodical summary that included lists of points to memorize
ex.cn: 一份包含了记忆重点的有条理的总结

m: METHODICAL contains METHOD — done by clear method. — methodical, systematic.

k.en: of, relating to, or composed in poetic meter

m: METRICAL contains METER — having measured METER (poetic rhythm). — metrical, having rhythm.

k.syn: bravery, courage, dauntlessness, fortitude, guts, nerve, pluck, spirit, spunk, valor
k.ant: cowardice, cravenness, gutlessness, pusillanimity, spinelessness 胆小，怯懦
k.en: vigor and strength of spirit or temperament

ex.en: troops who showed their mettle in combat
ex.cn: 在战场上表现出坚强勇气的部队

k.syn: durability, endurance, stamina, persistence, resolution
k.en: staying quality: stamina

ex.en: Those trucks had proved their mettle in army transport.
ex.cn: 那些卡车通过在军队运输中的表现证明了它们的耐用性。

m: METTLE rhymes with METAL — both strong; a person of "mettle" has metal-strong character. — mettle, courage / spirit.

k.syn: aggravate, annoy, enrage, exasperate, incense, infuriate, ire, madden, nettle, peeve, vex
k.ant: appease, assuage, mollify, placate, propitiate 平息，安抚；delight, gratify, please 取悦，讨好
k.en: to cause to become offended or annoyed

ex.en: be miffed by her son's disobedience
ex.cn: 被她儿子的叛逆惹恼

m: MIFF rhymes with WHIFF — a tiny WHIFF of irritation. — miff, to slightly offend.

k.syn: migrant, mobile, nomad, nomadic, traveling
k.ant: nonmigrant, resident, sedentary 常居一处的，不迁移的
k.en: having a way of life that involves moving from one region to another typically on a seasonal basis

ex.en: migratory birds heading south for the winter
ex.cn: 为了越冬而往南飞的候鸟

m: MIGRATORY contains MIGRATE — periodically moving. — migratory, regularly moving.

k.syn: drain, exploit
k.en: to draw or coerce profit or advantage from illicitly or to an extreme degree

ex.en: milk the workers
ex.cn: 从工人身上榨取好处

ex.en: The interrogators milked the arrested spy, but he was dry.
ex.cn: 审问者想尽办法套被捕间谍的话，但是无功而返。

m: to MILK information = squeeze it out gradually like MILK from a cow. — milk, to extract gradually.

k.syn: ape, copy, copycat, emulate, mimic, simulate
k.en: to use（someone or something）as the model for one's speech, mannerisms, or behavior

ex.en: mime a dog begging for food
ex.cn: 模仿小狗乞食

m: MIME rhymes with MIM ≈ MIMIC — silent imitation. (Greek MIMOS = imitator.) — mime, to imitate silently.

k.en: a superficial resemblance of one organism to another, serving as a form of camouflage or protection

m: from MIMIC — biological imitation (a butterfly mimicking a leaf). — mimicry, imitation for camouflage.

k.en: a tall slender tower attached to a mosque

m: MINARET — the slender mosque tower (Arabic MANARAH = lighthouse, place of fire). — minaret, a mosque tower.

k.syn: baleful, direful, doomy, foreboding, ill-boding, inauspicious, menacing, portentous, sinister, threatening
k.ant: unthreatening 没有威胁的；reassuring 令人安心的
k.en: being or showing a sign of evil or calamity to come

ex.en: The novel's protagonist is haunted by a minatory black specter.
ex.cn: 这部小说的主人公被不祥的黑色幽灵所骚扰。

m: MINATORY contains MIN ≈ "menace" (Latin MINARI = threaten). — minatory, threatening.

k.syn: cut, dice, grind, hash
k.en: to cut or chop into very small pieces

ex.en: I'll buy some lean meat and mince it myself.
ex.cn: 我会买一些瘦肉然后自己切好。

k.ant: stride 大步走 “所谓抱负就是对现状的永不满足，有变化的生活才精彩，永远不要停下追逐梦想的脚步。” （赵禾，2006年10月，Verbal 700，Quantitative 800，录取学校：Cornell University Ms. Financial Engineering）
k.en: to walk with very short steps or with exaggerated primness

ex.en: The bride minced through the cathedral.
ex.cn: 新娘踏着细小的步伐穿过教堂。

m: MINCE rhymes with PRINCE — but "don't mince words" = don't tiptoe like a delicate prince. (Also: mince meat = chop fine.) — mince, to cut fine / walk daintily.

k.syn: associate, fraternize, socialize
k.en: to take part in social activities

ex.en: mingle at a cocktail party
ex.cn: 在鸡尾酒会上与他人交往

k.syn: mix, amalgamate, blend, commingle, immingle, intermix, merge
k.ant: divide, separate, sunder 分开
k.en: to mix so that the components become united

ex.en: mingle the ingredients
ex.cn: 将调料混合

ex.en: mingle fact and fiction
ex.cn: 糅杂了现实与幻想

m: MINGLE rhymes with TINGLE / SINGLE — but mixed: SINGLE drinks MINGLE at a party. — mingle, to mix.

k.syn: deprecate, diminish, discount, reduce
k.ant: inflate, magnify 膨胀，扩大；maximize 最大化
k.en: to reduce to the smallest possible amount, extent, size, or degree

ex.en: All striking forces are told to minimize civilian casualties.
ex.cn: 所有攻击部队被要求尽可能减少平民伤亡。

k.syn: underestimate, underrate, undervalue
k.ant: overestimate 高估；exaggerate 夸张，夸大
k.en: to underestimate intentionally

ex.en: minimize losses in our own company while maximize those of the rival
ex.cn: 低估我方损失而高估对手损失

k.syn: belittle, denigrate, deprecate, depreciate, derogate, diminish, disparage, trash, vilipend
k.ant: acclaim, applaud, exalt, extol, glorify, laud, magnify, praise 欢呼，赞扬
k.en: to express scornfully one's low opinion of

ex.en: Sore losers minimized the other team's victory.
ex.cn: 愤愤不平的失败者对获胜方的胜利表现出鄙夷。

m: MINIMIZE contains MIN (small) + IMIZE — make as small as possible. — minimize, to reduce / downplay.

k.en: an obsequious follower or subordinate; a favored servant

m: MINION rhymes with ONION — a layer of underlings, like onion skins around the boss. (Or: cartoon MINIONS who follow their boss.) — minion, a servile follower.

k.syn: bomb, boodle, bundle, fortune, pile, wad
k.ant: mite, modicum 少量
k.en: an abundant amount, especially of money

ex.en: worth a mint
ex.cn: 价值连城

k.syn: intact, original, perfect, pristine, unmarred, virginal
k.ant: impaired, damaged 有损坏的；stale 陈腐的
k.en: unmarred as if fresh from a mint

ex.en: a second-handed laptop in mint condition
ex.cn: 一台几乎全新的二手笔记本电脑

m: MINT rhymes with PRINT — money is PRINTed at the MINT; a MINT condition coin is freshly minted. — mint, a huge sum / pristine.

k.syn: atomic, infinitesimal, microscopic, miniature, minute, tiny
k.ant: colossal, elephantine, enormous, gargantuan, gigantic, huge, immense, mammoth, prodigious 巨大的
k.en: very small

ex.en: a minuscule progress
ex.cn: 极小的进步

m: MINUSCULE contains MINUS — "minus-cule," extremely small. (Same root as MINUTE.) — minuscule, extremely small.

k.syn: circumstantial, elaborate, full, particular, particularized, thorough
k.ant: brief, compendious, concise, succinct, summary, terse 摘要性的，简述的
k.en: characterized by careful scrutiny and close examination

ex.en: a minute description of the setting of the story
ex.cn: 故事背景的详细介绍

k.syn: frivolous, incidental, inconsiderable, insignificant, little, minor, negligible, nugatory, slight, trifling, trivial
k.ant: consequential, critical, crucial, momentous, significant, vital, weighty 关键的，重要的
k.en: very small or of small importance

ex.en: It is not sagacious to invest excessively in minute details.
ex.cn: 过分投入到不重要的细枝末节上是不明智的。

m: the same word as MINUTE (60 seconds), but pronounced "my-NOOT" — paying attention to small intervals = MINUTE detail. — minute, very small / detailed.

k.en: a minute or minor detail

m: MINUTIA contains MINUT (small) — a tiny detail. — minutia, a trivial detail.

k.syn: chimera, delusion, hallucination, illusion, phantom, vision
k.ant: reality 现实
k.en: something illusory and unattainable like a mirage

ex.en: Reunion with her husband has become a mirage.
ex.cn: 与她丈夫重逢已是可望而不可及的幻想。

ex.en: A peaceful solution proved to be a mirage.
ex.cn: 和平解决问题是不可能的了。

m: MIRAGE contains MIRA (look at, Latin MIRARE) — a deceptive thing you LOOK at. (Same root as MIRROR, ADMIRE.) — mirage, an optical illusion.

k.syn: dilemma, hole, impasse, jam, pickle, quagmire, rattrap, swamp
k.en: a difficult, puzzling, or embarrassing situation from which there is no easy escape

ex.en: stuck in a mire of emotional dependency
ex.cn: 陷于感情依赖的困境中

k.syn: bog, broil, delay, detain, entangle, entrap, retard
k.ant: enfranchise, extricate, free, liberate, rescue 使解脱
k.en: to hamper or hold back as if by mire

ex.en: be mired in the past
ex.cn: 陷于过去的回忆不能自拔

m: MIRE rhymes with TIRE — sinking into the MIRE TIREs you out. — mire, mud / to bog down.

k.syn: cheer, festivity, gaiety, glee, happiness, hilarity, jocundity, joviality, lightheartedness
k.ant: dejection, depression, desolation, despondence, doldrums, melancholy, oppression 沮丧，忧伤
k.en: gladness or gaiety as shown by or accompanied with laughter

ex.en: a man of little mirth
ex.cn: 不苟言笑之人

m: MIRTH rhymes with BIRTH — joyous laughter at a BIRTH celebration. — mirth, gladness.

k.en: a person who hates or distrusts mankind

m: MIS- (hate, Greek MISO) + ANTHROPE (human) — human-hater. — misanthrope, one who hates humanity.

k.en: to behave

m: MIS- (badly) + BEHAVE — behave badly. — misbehave, to behave badly.

k.syn: annoying, devious, errant, impish, misbehaving, naughty, playful, wicked
k.ant: decorous, urbane 举止得体的
k.en: playful in a naughty or teasing way

ex.en: The mischievous child broke the vase.
ex.cn: 那个调皮的孩子打碎了花瓶。

k.syn: adverse, baleful, baneful, deleterious, detrimental, evil, hurtful, injurious, nocuous, noxious, pernicious
k.ant: beneficial 有益的；anodyne, benign, harmless, innocent, innocuous, inoffensive, safe 无害的
k.en: causing harm or trouble

ex.en: mischievous rumors that defame him
ex.cn: 恶意中伤他的谣言

m: MIS- (wrong) + CHIEF (Old French CHEF = end/result) — coming to a bad end; playfully naughty. — mischievous, playfully naughty.

k.syn: garble, misapprehend, misinterpret, misperceive, misread, misrepresent, mistake
k.ant: appreciate, apprehend, catch, comprehend, fathom, grasp, perceive, savvy, seize, understand 获知
k.en: to mistake the meaning of

ex.en: An outsider might misconstrue the nature of the phenomenon.
ex.cn: 局外者可能会曲解这个现象的本质。

m: MIS- (wrong) + CONSTRUE (interpret) — interpret wrongly. — misconstrue, to misinterpret.

k.syn: brute, culprit, devil, felon, fiend, offender, rascal, reprobate, villain
k.ant: cavalier, chevalier（尤指对女士）彬彬有礼的绅士
k.en: one who behaves criminally or viciously

ex.en: robbed by a bunch of miscreants
ex.cn: 遭到了一伙歹徒的抢劫

m: MIS- (wrong) + CREANT (believing, Latin CREDERE) — wrong-believer; villain. — miscreant, an evildoer.

k.en: a minor wrongdoing; a criminal offense less serious than a felony

m: MIS- (bad) + DEMEANOR (conduct) — bad conduct; minor crime. — misdemeanor, a minor offense.

k.en: one who lives very meagerly in order to hoard money; an extremely stingy person

m: MISER rhymes with WISER but the opposite — wise people share, MISERs hoard. (Latin MISER = wretched.) — miser, a stingy hoarder.

k.syn: adversity, calamity, cataclysm, catastrophe, disaster, ill, mischance, mishap, tragedy
k.ant: fortune, luck, serendipity 幸事
k.en: bad fortune or ill luck

ex.en: unable to grasp why he had been struck by such a misfortune
ex.cn: 无法理解他为何会遇上如此不幸之事

m: MIS- (bad) + FORTUNE — bad luck. — misfortune, bad luck.

k.syn: apprehension, distrust, doubt, dread, fear, foreboding, incertitude, skepticism, suspicion
k.ant: assurance, belief, certainty, certitude, confidence, conviction, sureness, surety, trust 信心
k.en: a feeling of doubt or suspicion especially concerning a future event

ex.en: No one can dispel his misgiving.
ex.cn: 没有人能打消他的疑虑。

m: MIS- (wrong) + GIVING — your gut GIVES the wrong feeling about it. — misgiving, doubt.

k.syn: adversity, calamity, cataclysm, catastrophe, disaster, ill, mischance, misfortune, tragedy
k.ant: fortune, luck, serendipity 幸事
k.en: an unfortunate accident

ex.en: Mishap followed wherever he went.
ex.cn: 不论他走到哪里，厄运总是形影相随。

m: MIS- (bad) + HAP (luck, as in HAPPEN) — bad luck. — mishap, an unlucky accident.

k.syn: belie, color, distort, falsify, garble, misinterpret, misrelate, misstate, pervert
k.ant: clarify, explain, illuminate, illustrate 澄清，阐明
k.en: to give an incorrect or misleading representation of

ex.en: misrepresent the facts
ex.cn: 篡改事实真相

m: MIS- (wrongly) + REPRESENT — represent wrongly. — misrepresent, to portray falsely.

k.syn: atom, bit, hint, iota, molecule, particle, pittance, trace
k.ant: boodle, bundle, fortune 大笔（金钱）；colossus 巨大的事物
k.en: a very small object, creature, or particle, a very small sum of money

ex.en: I have only a mite left to buy lunch for the rest of the week.
ex.cn: 我只剩下一点钱来买本周剩下几天的午饭了。

m: MITE rhymes with MIGHT but the opposite — MIGHT is great, a MITE is tiny (also: a tiny insect). — mite, a tiny amount.

k.syn: allay, alleviate, assuage, ease, mollify, palliate, relieve, soothe
k.ant: aggravate, exacerbate, intensify 加剧
k.en: to make less severe or painful

ex.en: powerful drugs that mitigate pains
ex.cn: 强力的镇痛剂

m: MITIGATE contains MIT (soft, Latin MITIS) + IGATE — make softer. — mitigate, to make less severe.

k.en: patterns or techniques used as memory aids

m: MNEMONICS comes from MNEMOSYNE (Greek goddess of memory). — mnemonics, memory aids.

k.en: capable of moving

m: from MOBILE — ability to move. — mobility, movability.

k.syn: derision, joke, mock, ridicule, scoffing
k.ant: respect, reverence, veneration 尊敬
k.en: scornfully contemptuous ridicule

ex.en: Her deliberate mockery triggered a fierce fight.
ex.cn: 她的蓄意嘲弄引起了一场激烈的打斗。

k.syn: burlesque, caricature, farce, parody, sham, travesty
k.en: a false, derisive, or impudent imitation

ex.en: arbitrary methods that make a mockery of justice
ex.cn: 专断的方式是对司法公正的践踏

m: MOCKERY contains MOCK — a mocking display. — mockery, derisive imitation.

k.syn: abate, diminish, dwindle, ease, lessen, lower, modulate, subside, taper, temper, wane
k.ant: escalate, enhance, expand, heighten, intensify 扩大，升级，增强
k.en: to lessen the intensity or extremeness of

ex.en: Sopranos and tenors moderates their voices to fit the size of the theater.
ex.cn: 女高音和男高音们根据剧场的大小调节他们的声音以创造合适的效果。

k.syn: average, conservative, fair, intermediate, mediocre, modest, reasonable, temperate
k.ant: exorbitant, excessive 过分的；extreme, radical 极端的
k.en: being within reasonable limits; not excessive or extreme

ex.en: The new proposals regarding defense budget were met with only moderate enthusiasm.
ex.cn: 关于国防预算的新提案只得到了平淡的回应。

m: MODERATE contains MOD- (measure, Latin MODUS) — kept within measure. — moderate, restrained / to soften.

k.syn: atom, iota, mite, molecule, particle
k.ant: abundance, affluence 大量
k.en: a small portion; a limited quantity

ex.en: a modicum of food quota
ex.cn: 极少量的食物配额

m: MODICUM contains MOD (measure) — a small measured portion. — modicum, a small amount.

k.en: a tooth

m: MOLAR contains MOL (mill, Latin MOLA) — the grinding/milling tooth. — molar, a grinding tooth.

k.en: an embellishment in strip form, made of wood, that is used to decorate or finish a surface

m: MOLDING — wood shaped in a MOLD as decorative trim. — molding, decorative trim.

k.syn: allay, alleviate, appease, assuage, conciliate, mitigate, placate, propitiate, soothe
k.ant: aggravate, enrage, exasperate, incense, inflame, infuriate, ire, rankle, vex 激怒；antagonize 使敌对
k.en: to calm in temper or feeling

ex.en: mollify the angry customer
ex.cn: 平息消费者的怒气

m: MOLLIFY contains MOLL- (soft, Latin MOLLIS) + IFY — make soft / soothe. (Same root as EMOLLIENT.) — mollify, to soothe.

k.syn: coddle, cosset, indulge, pamper, spoil
k.ant: abuse, ill-treat 虐待
k.en: to treat with an excessive or absurd degree of indulgence and attention

ex.en: mollycoddle his only grandson
ex.cn: 溺爱他唯一的孙子

m: MOLLY (a girl's name, soft) + CODDLE (gently boil) — gently fuss over. — mollycoddle, to overpamper.

k.syn: exfoliate, exuviate, shed
k.ant: fledge 长羽毛

ex.cn: 随着年龄的增长，蛇会脱去老皮，长出大一点的新皮。

m: MOLT rhymes with JOLT — a JOLT of seasonal change makes a snake MOLT. — molt, to shed skin/feathers.

k.syn: consequential, considerable, crucial, eventful, important, monumental, pivotal, significant, vital, weighty
k.ant: inconsequential, negligible, slight, trifling, trivial 无关紧要的
k.en: of utmost importance; of outstanding significance or consequence

ex.cn: 斯大林格勒之战是第二次世界大战中具有重大意义的一次战役。

m: MOMENTOUS contains MOMENT — a MOMENT of huge weight. (Latin MOMENTUM = importance/weight.) — momentous, of great importance.

k.syn: boost, encouragement, goad, impetus, incentive, incitation, instigation, motivation, spur, stimulus
k.ant: deterrent 阻碍物；obstruction, resistance 阻力

ex.cn: 他们的运气开始转旺。

m: MOMENTUM contains MOMENT — built-up motion. (Same Latin root as MOMENTOUS.) — momentum, force of motion.

k.syn: crossbred, hybrid
k.ant: purebred 纯种的
k.en: of mixed origin or character

ex.en: homeless mongrel dogs on the streets
ex.cn: 街上的流浪杂种狗

m: MONGREL contains MONG ≈ "a-MONG" — mixed AMONG breeds. — mongrel, a mixed-breed dog.

k.syn: colorless, monochrome
k.ant: chromatic, colorful, iridescent, motley, rainbow, variegated 五光十色的
k.en: having or consisting of one color or hue

ex.en: monochromatic filter
ex.cn: 单色滤光片

k.syn: arid, dreary, drudging, dull, jejune, monotonous, pedestrian, ponderous, stale, stodgy, tiresome
k.ant: absorbing, engaging, engrossing, gripping, interesting, intriguing, involving, riveting 吸引人的
k.en: lacking variety, creativity, or excitement

ex.en: a monochromatic article eulogizing a hero
ex.cn: 一篇无聊的赞颂英雄的文章

m: MONO (one) + CHROMA (color) — one color. — monochromatic, of one color.

k.en: an eyeglass for one eye

m: MONO (one) + OCLE (eye, Latin OCULUS) — one-eye lens. — monocle, a single eye-lens.

k.syn: soliloquy, solo, speech
k.ant: dialogue 对话
k.en: a dramatic sketch performed by one actor

ex.en: deliver a tedious monologue
ex.cn: 做了一个冗长的独白

m: MONO (one) + LOGUE (speech) — one person speaking. — monologue, a solo speech.

k.ant: vociferous 嘈杂的
k.en: uttered or sounded in one unvarying tone

ex.en: a monotonous apathetic voice
ex.cn: 单调而冷漠的声音

k.syn: arid, dreary, drudging, dull, jejune, monochromatic, pedestrian, ponderous, stale, stodgy, tiresome
k.ant: absorbing, engaging, engrossing, gripping, interesting, intriguing, involving, riveting 吸引人的
k.en: tediously uniform or unvarying

ex.en: a sparkle in the monotonous background
ex.cn: 单调背景中的一个亮点

m: MONO (one) + TONE — only one tone, dull. — monotonous, repetitively dull.

k.syn: agglomerate, collage, hodgepodge, medley, motley, salad, variety
k.en: an unorganized collection or mixture of various things

ex.en: My memories of the childhood trip are a montage of the sights of two rivers, smells of hotpots, and sounds of light railway of Chongqing.
ex.cn: 我孩提时代旅行的记忆就是由两江风光、火锅的香味以及重庆的轻轨声杂糅而成的。

m: MONTAGE contains MONT ≈ "mount" (French MONTER = to assemble) — assembled mix of images. — montage, a mixed assembly.

k.syn: abeyance, delay, doldrums, dormancy, latency, quiescence, postponement, suspension
k.ant: resumption（从中断处）继续进行
k.en: a suspension of activity

ex.en: a moratorium on nuclear tests
ex.cn: 暂缓核试验

m: MORATORIUM contains MORA (delay, Latin MORARI = delay) — official delay. — moratorium, an official postponement.

k.syn: diseased, pathological
k.ant: verdant 青翠的

ex.en: a morbid condition
ex.cn: 有疾病的状况

k.syn: brainsick, crazy, deranged, lunatic, psychotic
k.ant: hale, salubrious, wholesome 健康的

ex.en: read the account of the murder with a morbid interest
ex.cn: 怀着病态的心态读谋杀报告

ex.en: exhibit a morbid fascination
ex.cn: 展现出一种病态的迷恋

m: MORBID contains MORB- (disease, Latin MORBUS) — relating to disease/death. — morbid, gruesomely fixated.

k.syn: acerbic, acid, acrid, barbed, caustic, corrosive, pungent, sardonic, satiric, scalding, scathing, tart
k.ant: congenial, genial 和蔼的
k.en: biting and caustic in thought, manner, or style

ex.en: feel embarrassed about the mordant satire of the critics
ex.cn: 对评论家刻薄的讽刺感到尴尬

m: MORDANT contains MORD (bite, Latin MORDERE) — biting wit. — mordant, sharply biting.

k.syn: decadent, deteriorating, dying, expiring, fading
k.ant: beginning, nascent 初生的，刚开始的；lively, thriving, vigorous, vital 有活力的
k.en: approaching death; about to die

ex.cn: 这个国家的银行业危在旦夕。

k.syn: antiquated, archaic, dated, fossilized, moth-eaten, outdated, outworn, rusty
k.ant: fresh, new 崭新的；promising 充满希望的
k.en: on the verge of becoming obsolete

ex.en: Some social conventions have been rendered moribund in face of modernization.
ex.cn: 不少传统习俗在现代化的影响下变得岌岌可危。

m: MORIBUND contains MORI (death, Latin) — near death. (Same root as MORTAL.) — moribund, dying.

k.syn: bleak, chill, dark, depressed, dire, dour, gloomy, lugubrious, saturnine, solemn, sulky, sullen, surly
k.ant: bright, cheerful, festive, jovial, jocund, sanguine, lighthearted, rejoiced 高兴的，欢快的
k.en: having a sullen and gloomy disposition

ex.en: morose job seekers who are inured to rejection
ex.cn: 习惯了被拒的郁郁寡欢的求职者

m: MOROSE rhymes with COMATOSE — both gloomy, lifeless states. — morose, sullen.

k.en: a sturdy vessel in which material is pounded or rubbed with a pestle

m: MORTAR rhymes with PORTER — but a MORTAR is the heavy bowl that grinds (Latin MORTARIUM). — mortar, a grinding bowl.

k.syn: agglomerate, collage, hodgepodge, jumble, montage, motley, muddle, salad, shuffle, variety, welter
k.cn: 综合物，马赛克般的东西
k.en: an unorganized collection or mixture of various things

ex.en: a mosaic of testimony from various witnesses
ex.cn: 从不同证人中得来的综合证词

m: MOSAIC — small tile pieces forming a Greco-Roman picture. (Originally: of the Muses.) — mosaic, an image of small pieces.

k.syn: cathedral, chapel, church, temple
k.en: a building used for public worship by Muslim

ex.en: a deadly suicide attack at the mosque
ex.cn: 在清真寺发生的致命自杀性袭击

m: MOSQUE rhymes with KIOSK — both small structures, a MOSQUE is the Islamic place of worship. — mosque, an Islamic temple.

k.syn: mobile, movable, portable, transportable
k.ant: immobile 不可移动的
k.en: exhibiting or capable of movement

ex.en: Aircraft carriers are recognized as a motile combat platform.
ex.cn: 航空母舰是一种移动作战平台。

m: MOTILE contains MOT (move, Latin MOTUS) — capable of moving. — motile, capable of motion.

k.syn: excite, galvanize, impel, innervate, provoke, rouse, stimulate
k.ant: discourage, dishearten 使泄气
k.der: motivation n. 动机；motivated adj. 被激励的
k.en: to provide with an incentive; move to action

ex.en: questions that excite and motivate youth
ex.cn: 激发青年人的问题

m: MOTIVATE contains MOT (move) + IVE — what MOVES you to act. — motivate, to inspire to act.

k.syn: assorted, chromatic, kaleidoscopic, heterogeneous, indiscriminate, magpie, piebald, variegated
k.ant: homogeneous, monochromatic, monotonous, unvaried 单一的，同一性的
k.en: （especially of colors）having elements of great variety or incongruity

ex.en: an arrangement of motley flowers
ex.cn: 多种不同花束的组合

m: MOTLEY rhymes with MEDLEY — both mean a mixed assortment (originally a multicolored jester's costume). — motley, varied / mixed.

k.syn: blotch, dapple, dot, marble, splotch, spot, stain
k.ant: blanch 漂白
k.en: to mark with spots or blotches of different shades or colors

ex.en: a black horse mottled with white
ex.cn: 身上带有白色斑点的黑马

m: MOTTLE rhymes with BOTTLE — patches of color like spilled BOTTLE stains. — mottle, to mark with spots.

k.syn: catchword, doctrine, dogma, idiom, slogan
k.en: a short expression of a guiding principle

ex.en: “Semper fidelis”is the motto of US Marine Corps.“
ex.cn: 永远忠诚”是美国海军陆战队的格言。

m: MOTTO rhymes with LOTTO — both short phrases you keep close. (Italian for "a saying.") — motto, a guiding phrase.

k.syn: aching, agonized, anguished, doleful, dolorous, grievous, lamentable, lugubrious, melancholy, sad, woeful
k.ant: cheerful, delighted, jocund, jovial, jubilant 快活的
k.en: feeling or expressing sorrow or grief

ex.en: The mournful survivors of the disaster were faced with the grim task of burying the dead.
ex.cn: 悲伤的幸存者面临着掩埋灾难中死者尸体的可怕任务。

ex.en: Mrs. Murphy fainted at the mournful news of her son's death. Murphy
ex.cn: 太太听到她儿子的死讯后晕了过去。

m: MOURNFUL contains MOURN — full of mourning. — mournful, sorrowful.

k.syn: action, motion, operation, shifting, stir
k.ant: motionlessness, stasis 静止
k.en: the act or process of moving

ex.en: There appears to be some movement in the bush.
ex.cn: 树丛中似乎有动静。

m: MOVEMENT contains MOVE — the act of moving. — movement, motion.

k.syn: besmirched, dingy, draggled, dusty, filthy, foul, nasty, smudged, smutty, soiled, sordid, stained, sullied
k.ant: clean, immaculate, spotless, stainless, unsoiled, unstained, unsullied 干净的
k.en: not clean

ex.en: The children got muddy after playing outside.
ex.cn: 孩子们在外面玩弄得很脏。

k.syn: cloudy, foul, murky, obscure, turbid
k.ant: clear 清晰的
k.en: lacking in clarity or brightness

ex.en: a muddy recording
ex.cn: 嘈杂不清的纪录

k.syn: becloud, befog, blur, cloud, fog, obfuscate
k.ant: clarify, illuminate 阐明
k.en: to make（something）unclear to the understanding

ex.en: That point is irrelevant and will just muddy the issue we're trying to resolve.
ex.cn: 那个观点和我们讨论的问题毫不相干，它只会妨碍我们对于问题的理解。

m: MUDDY contains MUD — like MUD-water, unclear. — muddy, unclear / dirty.

k.syn: attenuate, dampen, deaden, mute, soften, stifle, suppress
k.ant: amplify, enhance, magnify 放大，增强
k.en: to wrap or pad in order to deaden the sound

ex.en: close the window to muffle the outside noises
ex.cn: 关上窗户以减少外界的噪音

m: MUFFLE contains MUFF — wrap in a thick MUFF to silence. — muffle, to deaden sound.

k.syn: adamant, headstrong, immovable, intractable, obstinate, pertinacious, perverse, refractory, stubborn
k.ant: flexible, pliable, pliant, yielding 易改变立场的，易受影响的
k.en: unreasonably and inflexibly obstinate

ex.en: a mulish determination to act on his own
ex.cn: 铁了心要单打独斗

m: MULISH contains MULE — stubborn as a MULE. — mulish, stubborn.

k.syn: grunt, murmur, mutter, whisper
k.ant: articulate, enunciate 清楚地表达
k.en: to utter words in a low confused indistinct manner

ex.en: He mumbled an apology reluctantly.
ex.cn: 他不情愿地咕哝了句抱歉。

m: MUMBLE rhymes with FUMBLE — both unclear in execution. — mumble, to speak unclearly.

k.syn: carnal, earthly, materialistic, secular, sensual, worldly
k.ant: spiritual 精神上的；ethereal 虚无缥缈的，非尘世的
k.en: of, relating to, or typical of this world

ex.en: As for opera, I prefer mundane to religious themes.
ex.cn: 与宗教主题相比，我更喜欢世俗主题的歌剧。

k.syn: common, ordinary, prosaic
k.ant: extraordinary 非凡的
k.en: relating to, characteristic of, or concerned with commonplaces

ex.en: mundane concerns of day-to-day life
ex.cn: 关于日常生活的一般顾虑

m: MUNDANE contains MUND (world, Latin MUNDUS) — of this world, ordinary. — mundane, ordinary / worldly.

k.syn: bountiful, charitable, generous, liberal, openhanded, unsparing
k.ant: closefisted, mean, miserly, niggardly, parsimonious, penurious, stingy, tightfisted 小气的
k.en: very liberal in giving

ex.en: The university received a munificent foundation grant.
ex.cn: 那所大学收到了一笔慷慨的基金资助。

m: MUNI (gift, Latin MUNUS) + FIC (make) — gift-making, generous. — munificent, generous.

k.en: a very large image, such as a painting or enlarged photograph, applied directly to a wall

m: MURAL contains MUR (wall, Latin MURUS) — wall painting. — mural, a wall painting.

k.syn: black, caliginous, darkened, dim, gloomy, stygian
k.ant: bright, brightened, brilliant, illuminated, illumined, lucent, lucid, luminous 明亮的
k.en: being without light or without much light

ex.en: I didn't like walking around the murky campground without a flashlight.
ex.cn: 我不喜欢在没有手电的情况下在营地里走动。

k.syn: ambiguous, arcane, cloudy, equivocal, muddy, nebulous, obscure, occult, vague
k.ant: clear, limpid, pellucid, plain 清晰的
k.en: lacking clarity or distinctness

ex.en: He felt lost in the murky bureaucratic rhetoric.
ex.cn: 他感觉自己被官僚主义的晦涩说辞弄晕了。

m: MURKY contains MURK ≈ "dark" — gloomy and unclear. — murky, dark / unclear.

k.syn: grunt, mumble, mutter, undertone, whisper
k.ant: roar 怒吼
k.en: a low, indistinct, continuous sound

ex.en: We could hear the murmur of the audience throughout the entire performance.
ex.cn: 我们在整个演出当中都能听到观众的窃窃私语。

k.syn: carp, fuss, gripe, grizzle, grouch, grouse, grumble, moan, repine, whine
k.ant: crow, delight, rejoice 欢呼，高兴
k.en: to complain in low mumbling tones; grumble

ex.en: No prisoner dared murmur out toud.
ex.cn: 没有囚犯敢大声报怨。

m: MURMUR — onomatopoeic, the soft "mur-mur" sound of low complaint. — murmur, low muttering.

k.en: a heavy large-caliber muzzle-loading smoothbore shoulder firearm used from the 16th to 18th centuries

m: MUSKET rhymes with BUCKET — early gun (Italian MOSCHETTO = small fly, the dart name). — musket, an old gun.

k.syn: alter, change, fluctuate, modify, shift, transfigure, transform, vary
k.ant: remain 保持不变；plateau, stabilize 使稳定
k.en: to undergo or cause to undergo mutation

ex.en: Some chromosomes started to mutate after exposure to X-Ray.
ex.cn: 经X光照射之后部分染色体开始发生变异。

m: MUTATE contains MUT (change, Latin MUTARE) — change form. — mutate, to change form.

k.syn: dumb, inarticulate, speechless, uncommunicative, voiceless, wordless
k.ant: communicative, expansive, loquacious, talkative 爱说话的，话多的
k.en: deliberately refraining from speech

ex.en: He always remained mute no matter how much we pleaded for an answer.
ex.cn: 不论我们怎么恳求回答，他总是保持沉默。

k.syn: attenuate, soften, subdue
k.ant: sharpen 锐化
k.en: to soften the tone, color, shade, or hue of

ex.en: mute a color
ex.cn: 对颜色进行柔化

k.syn: dampen, deaden, muffle, stifle
k.ant: amplify, magnify 增强，放大
k.der: muted adj. 消声的，静音的
k.en: to soften or muffle the sound of

m: MUTE rhymes with CUTE but silent — MUTE buttons silence the TV. — mute, silent / to soften.

k.syn: carp, fuss, gripe, grizzle, grouch, grouse, grumble, moan, murmur, repine, whine
k.ant: crow, delight, rejoice 欢呼，高兴
k.en: to murmur complainingly or angrily

ex.en: mutter about the difficult assignment
ex.cn: 抱怨作业太难

m: MUTTER rhymes with BUTTER but darker — quietly grumble. (Onomatopoeic.) — mutter, to grumble softly.

k.syn: shortsighted, narrow-minded
k.ant: far-sighted, foreseeing, provident 有远见的；discerning 有辨别能力的
k.der: myopia n. 近视；鼠目寸光
k.cn: 缺乏远见的，缺乏辨别能力的
k.en: a lack of foresight or discernment

ex.en: a myopic view on climate change
ex.cn: 关于气候变化缺乏远见的观点

m: MYOPIC contains MYOPIA — short-sighted (literally and figuratively). — myopic, short-sighted.

k.syn: innumerable, numerous, uncountable, untold
k.ant: few, scarce, scanty 少的，缺乏的；countable, enumerable 可数清的
k.en: constituting a very large, indefinite number

ex.en: the myriad stars of a summer night
ex.cn: 夏日夜空中的无尽繁星

m: MYRIAD comes from Greek MYRIAS = ten thousand — countlessly many. — myriad, countless.

k.syn: base, bottom, foot
k.ant: acme, apex, climax, meridian, peak, pinnacle, summit, top, zenith 最高点 naïve［naɪ'i:v］
k.en: the lowest point

ex.en: the nadir on the curve
ex.cn: 曲线上的最低点

k.syn: artless, guileless, ingenuous, innocent, natural, simple, unaffected, unsophisticated
k.ant: sophisticated, worldly 世故的；affected, artful, assuming, dishonest, dissembling, guileful 虚伪做作的
k.en: lacking worldly experience and understanding, simple and guileless

ex.en: a child with a naïve charm
ex.cn: 天真无邪的孩子

m: NADIR — Arabic for "opposite of the zenith" (the lowest point). — nadir, the lowest point.

k.en: lacking worldly experience and understanding; simple and credulous

ex.en: a child with a naïve charm
ex.cn: 天真的孩子

m: NAIVE comes from French NAIF (natural, untrained). — naive, innocently inexperienced.

k.syn: egocentricity, egotism, self-absorption
k.ant: self-hatred 自我憎恨
k.en: excessive love or admiration of oneself

ex.en: In his narcissism, he just assumed that everyone else wanted to hear the tiny details of his day.
ex.cn: 在他自恋的眼中，仿佛所有人都想要听他日常生活里的细枝末节。

m: NARCISSISM — from Greek NARCISSUS, who fell in love with his own reflection. — narcissism, self-love.

k.syn: beginning, emerging, inceptive, inchoate, incipient, initial, introductory
k.ant: full-blown, full-fledged, mature, ripe 成熟的；moribund 将死的
k.en: coming or having recently come into existence

ex.en: The rise of the nascent middle class catalyzed a new economic boom.
ex.cn: 新生中产阶级的崛起催生了一场新的经济繁荣。

m: NASCENT contains NASC (born, Latin NASCI) — newly born. (Same root as NATIVE, RENAISSANCE.) — nascent, just emerging.

k.syn: dapper, smart, spruce
k.ant: frowsy, sloppy, slovenly 邋遢的
k.en: trimly neat and tidy

ex.en: a natty young woman
ex.cn: 整洁漂亮的年轻女子

m: NATTY rhymes with CHATTY but about clothes — sharply dressed. (Variant of "neat.") — natty, smartly dressed.

k.syn: disgust, repel, repulse, revolt, sicken
k.ant: delight 使愉悦
k.en: to feel or cause to feel loathing or disgust

ex.en: The malodor of the rotten meat made us nauseate.
ex.cn: 腐肉的恶臭让我们恶心。

m: NAUSEATE contains NAUSE (Greek NAUS = ship — seasickness origin) — sicken. — nauseate, to make sick.

k.syn: marine, maritime, navigational
k.ant: aeronautic 航空的；astronautic 航天的
k.en: of, relating to, or characteristic of ships, shipping, sailors, or navigation on a body of water

ex.en: nautical mile
ex.cn: 海里

m: NAUTICAL contains NAU (Greek NAUS = ship) — relating to ships. — nautical, of ships / sailing.

k.syn: decline, deny, deject, disallow, gainsay, oppose, refuse, reject
k.ant: accede, agree, concur, consent 同意
k.en: to oppose, deny, or take a pessimistic or negative view of

m: NAY + SAY — to say "nay" (no). — naysay, to deny.

k.syn: ambiguous, arcane, equivocal, hazy, indistinct, muddy, murky, obscure, occult, vague
k.ant: distinct 明显的；clear, definite, unambiguous, unequivocal 明确的
k.en: indistinct, vague

ex.en: a nebulous description of the topic
ex.cn: 对于主题的模糊描述

m: NEBULOUS contains NEBULA (cloud, Latin) — cloudy / unclear. — nebulous, vague.

k.syn: destitute, impecunious, impoverished, indigent, penurious, poor, threadbare
k.ant: affluent, opulent, wealthy 富有的
k.en: being in need; impoverished, poor

ex.en: As a child, she was extremely needy and had no self-confidence.
ex.cn: 她是一个非常贫穷的小孩，因此缺乏自信。

m: NEEDY contains NEED — full of NEEDs. — needy, in need.

k.syn: contradiction, denegation, denial, gainsaying, rejection, repudiation
k.ant: acknowledgement, affirmation, avowal 肯定，同意
k.der: negative adj. 否定的；负面的
k.en: the opposite or absence of something regarded as actual, positive, or affirmative

ex.en: She issued specific negations of all of the charges against her.
ex.cn: 她发表了明确的声明，否定了所有对她的指控。

m: NEGATION contains NEG (deny, Latin NEGARE) — denial. — negation, denial.

k.en: The state or quality of being negligent

m: NEGLIGENCE contains NEGLECT — failure to attend. — negligence, carelessness.

k.syn: arrange, bargain, concert, settle
k.ant: break down（谈判等）失败，破产
k.der: negotiable adj. 可商量的；negotiation n. 商量，谈判
k.en: to arrange or settle by discussion and mutual agreement

ex.en: negotiate the term of truce
ex.cn: 共商停战事宜

k.syn: contrive, finesse, frame, machinate, maneuver, manipulate, mastermind, wangle
k.en: plan out usually with subtle skill or care

ex.en: The prisoners negotiated their escape by using Morse code to tap messages to each other through the walls.
ex.cn: 犯人们通过敲击墙壁来传达莫尔斯电码，从而密谋了他们的逃跑计划。

m: NEGOTIATE contains NEG (not) + OTIUM (leisure, Latin) — "no leisure," busy work of bargaining. — negotiate, to bargain.

k.en: a new word, usage, or expression; the coinage of new words

m: NEO (new, Greek) + LOGISM (word) — a new word. — neologism, a newly coined word.

k.syn: abecedarian, apprentice, fledgling, freshman, novice, recruit, rookie, tyro
k.ant: veteran 老兵，身经百战的人
k.en: a beginner or novice

ex.en: a novice in the theater who had never even had a walk-on role
ex.cn: 一个连龙套都没跑过的新演员

m: NEO (new) + PHYTE (plant, Greek PHYTON) — newly planted, beginner. — neophyte, a beginner.

k.en: favoritism based on kinship ; favoritism shown or patronage granted to relatives

m: NEPOTISM contains NEPOT (nephew, Latin NEPOS) — favoritism toward relatives (originally popes promoting their nephews). — nepotism, favoring relatives.

k.syn: bravery, fortitude, guts, intrepidity, resolution, stamina
k.ant: cowardice, pusillanimity 怯懦
k.en: power of endurance or control; strength of mind to carry on in spite of danger

ex.en: nerves of steel
ex.cn: 钢铁般的意志

k.syn: animate, brace, cheer, embolden, encourage, inspirit, steel, strengthen
k.ant: appall 使胆寒；discourage, dishearten 使沮丧
k.en: to give strength or courage to

ex.en: He needs to nerve himself for the big game tomorrow.
ex.cn: 他需要为明天的大赛给自己加油鼓劲。

m: to have NERVE = to have boldness; nerves give you courage to act. — nerve, courage.

k.syn: aggravate, annoy, exasperate, inflame, infuriate, irritate, peeve, pique, provoke, roil, ruffle, vex
k.ant: appease, assuage, conciliate, mollify, placate, propitiate 安抚
k.en: to arouse to sharp but transitory annoyance or anger

ex.en: His pompous attitude nettled several people.
ex.cn: 他自大的性格得罪了不少人。

m: NETTLE — the stinging plant; to NETTLE someone = sting them with annoyance. — nettle, to irritate.

k.ant: acidify 酸化
k.en: to cause（an acid or base）to undergo neutralization

ex.en: industrial exhaust neutralized by lime
ex.cn: 被石灰中和的工业废气

k.syn: annul, cancel, counteract, frustrate, negate, nullify
k.ant: activate, vitalize 激活
k.en: to make inoperative or ineffective usually by means of an opposite force, influence, or effect

ex.en: influenced by the kind of propaganda that is difficult to neutralize
ex.cn: 被一种难以消除的宣传攻势所影响

m: NEUTRAL + IZE — make neutral / nullify. — neutralize, to render harmless.

k.syn: bond, connection, link, tie
k.en: a means of connection

ex.en: Correlativity does not sufficiently lead to causal nexus.
ex.cn: 相关性不是因果关联的充分条件。

k.syn: base, capital, center, core, focus, kernel, heart, hub, nucleus
k.ant: margin, periphery 边缘
k.en: a thing or place that is of greatest importance to an activity or interest

ex.en: As the nexus for three great religions, Jerusalem has had a troubled as well as illustrious history.
ex.cn: 作为三大宗教的圣城，耶路撒冷有着动荡而辉煌的历史。

m: NEXUS contains NEX (connect, Latin NECTERE) — a connection point. — nexus, a connection / core.

k.syn: bite, nip, peck, sip, tipple
k.ant: gobble 狼吞虎咽
k.en: to eat with small, quick bites or in small morsels

ex.en: waves nibbling the shore
ex.cn: 缓慢侵蚀海岸的浪

m: NIBBLE rhymes with DRIBBLE — small tentative bites. — nibble, to take small bites.

k.syn: accuracy, delicacy, exactness, fineness, precision, veracity
k.ant: coarseness, imprecision, inaccuracy, roughness 不准确，粗糙
k.en: careful attention to details; delicate exactness

ex.en: There's a nicety of detail in his meticulously painted landscapes.
ex.cn: 在他精心绘制的景观图中，有着十分精准翔实的细节。

k.syn: detail, particular, nuance, subtlety
k.en: a fine point or distinction

ex.en: the niceties of table manner
ex.cn: 餐桌礼仪的细微之处

ex.en: niceties of diplomatic protocol
ex.cn: 外交礼节中的细节

m: NICETY contains NICE — fine NICE-ness, precision in detail. — nicety, fine detail.

k.syn: nothing, nonentity, nullity, zip
k.ant: existence 存在
k.en: nothing; zero

ex.en: reduced to nil
ex.cn: 消逝殆尽

m: NIL comes from Latin NIHIL = nothing. (Same root as NIHILISM, ANNIHILATE.) — nil, nothing.

k.syn: bit, hint, little, mite, ounce, snap, trace
k.ant: abundance, affluence, avalanche 大量
k.en: a very small amount

ex.en: I'll have just a nip of your sandwich.
ex.cn: 我只吃一点点你的三明治。

k.syn: nibble, sip, tipple
k.ant: guzzle, quaff, swill 大口吃喝
k.en: to sip（alcoholic liquor）in small amounts

m: NIP rhymes with SIP — both small intakes. — nip, a small bite/sip.

k.syn: carp, cavil, fuss, niggle, quibble
k.der: nitpicker n. 吹毛求疵的人
k.en: to criticize by nit-picking

ex.en: a peevish critic always ready to nitpick
ex.cn: 一个总是吹毛求疵的古怪评论家

m: NIT + PICK — picking out tiny NITs (lice eggs) in hair, obsessive trivial fault-finding. — nitpick, to fault-find pettily.

k.syn: nightly, nighttime
k.ant: daily, diurnal 白昼的
k.en: of, relating to, or occurring in the night

ex.en: a nocturnal raid
ex.cn: 夜间突袭

k.en: active at night

ex.en: a nocturnal predator
ex.cn: 夜间活动的捕食者

m: NOCTURNAL contains NOCT (night, Latin NOX) — active at night. — nocturnal, of the night.

k.syn: deleterious, detrimental, insalubrious, noxious, unhealthy, unwholesome
k.ant: beneficial 有益的；healthy, salubrious, wholesome 有益健康的
k.en: noxious, harmful

ex.en: noisome chemical fumes
ex.cn: 有害的化学烟雾

k.syn: fetid, foul, malodorous, smelly, stinky
k.ant: ambrosial, aromatic, fragrant, perfumed, redolent, savory, scented, sweet 有香味的
k.en: offensive to the senses and especially to the sense of smell

ex.en: noisome garbage
ex.cn: 散发着恶臭的垃圾

k.syn: abhorrent, appalling, disgusting, hideous, loathsome, nauseating, offensive, repugnant, repulsive
k.ant: appealing, captivating, enchanting, fascinating 有吸引力的； agreeable, pleasant 令人愉悦的
k.en: highly obnoxious or objectionable

ex.en: noisome habits of littering
ex.cn: 让人反感的随地乱扔垃圾的坏习惯

m: NOISOME contains NOIS ≈ "annoy" (Old French ANOISE) — annoying / harmful. (Not related to "noise".) — noisome, foul / harmful.

k.syn: ambulant, fugitive, gallivanting, perambulatory, peripatetic, ranging, roaming, vagabond, vagrant, wandering, wayfaring
k.ant: settled 定居的
k.en: a member of a people who have no fixed residence but move from place to place usually seasonally and within a well-defined territory

ex.en: After college she became quite the nomad, backpacking through Europe with no particular destination.
ex.cn: 大学毕业后她开始漂泊，漫无目的地游历欧洲。

m: NOMAD contains NOM (pasture, Greek NOMOS) — wandering shepherds. — nomad, a wanderer.

k.syn: inconsequential, inconsiderable, paltry, trifling, trivial
k.ant: big, consequential, considerable, important, material, significant 重要的
k.en: so small or unimportant as to warrant little or no attention

ex.en: His involvement was nominal.
ex.cn: 他的参与不太重要。

k.syn: formal, paper, titular
k.en: existing or being something in name or form only

ex.en: nominal head of the party
ex.cn: 党派的名义首领

m: NOMINAL contains NOMI (name, Latin NOMEN) — in name only. — nominal, in name only / trivial.

k.syn: apathetic, disinterested, insensible, insouciant, perfunctory, unconcerned
k.ant: concerned, interested 忧虑的，有兴趣的
k.en: having an air of easy unconcern or indifference

ex.en: She was surprisingly nonchalant about winning the award.
ex.cn: 她竟然对得奖很淡然。

m: NON + CHAL (warm, Latin CALERE) + ANT — not-warm-up, indifferent. (French "not warmed up.") — nonchalant, casually unconcerned.

k.syn: beige, characterless, featureless, indistinctive, vanilla
k.ant: conspicuous, remarkable, striking 明显的，惊人的
k.en: lacking distinctive or interesting qualities

ex.en: Their performance was disappointingly nondescript.
ex.cn: 他们的演出令人失望，味同嚼蜡。

m: NON + DESCRIPT(ive) — un-describable for being so plain. — nondescript, lacking distinction.

k.syn: cipher, half-pint, insignificancy, lightweight, nullity, snippersnapper, twerp, whippersnapper, zero, zilch
k.ant: mogul, big shot, big wheel, bigwig, eminence, figure, magnate, personage, somebody 显要人物
k.en: a person or thing of little consequence or significance

ex.en: She was so quiet she was almost a nonentity at the meeting.
ex.cn: 她非常沉默，在会议上是个边缘人物。

k.syn: chimera, conceit, daydream, delusion, fancy, figment, hallucination, illusion, phantasm, unreality, vision
k.en: a conception or image created by the imagination and having no objective reality

ex.en: The arctic circle is a nonentity—you won't see it on the way to the north pole.
ex.cn: 北极圈是虚构的概念，去北极的路上你不会见到那么一个圈。

m: NON + ENTITY — not an entity, a nobody. — nonentity, an unimportant person.

k.syn: confound, confuse, discomfit, disconcert, discountenance, mortify, abash, faze, fluster
k.en: use to be at a loss as to what to say, think, or do: perplex

ex.en: I was nonplussed by his openly expressed admiration of me.
ex.cn: 我被他公开表现的仰慕弄得很窘迫。

m: NON + PLUS (more, Latin) — "no more" — at a loss for what to do next. — nonplus, to perplex.

k.syn: babble, blabber, drivel, prattle, mumbo jumbo
k.en: words or language having no meaning or conveying no intelligible ideas

ex.en: Many of the words in the poem are nonsense.
ex.cn: 这首诗歌当中很多词都是废话。

m: NON + SENSE — not making sense. — nonsense, foolish talk.

k.en: a V-shaped cut used for keeping a record

k.syn: attain, bag, chalk up, clock up, gain, hit, log, make, rack up, ring up, score, win
k.en: to obtain（as a goal）through effort

ex.en: a stunning performance that notched up a second Academy Award for the actor
ex.cn: 出色的表演为该演员拿到了第二座小金人

m: NOTCH rhymes with WATCH — score a NOTCH on your belt as you WATCH wins accumulate. — notch, a score / step up.

k.syn: original, unaccustomed, unfamiliar, unheard-of, unknown, unprecedented
k.ant: banal, timeworn, familiar, hackneyed, time-honored, tired, warmed- over 陈腐的
k.en: strikingly new, unusual, or different

ex.en: a novel scheme to collect money
ex.cn: 一种新颖的筹钱手段

m: NOVEL contains NOV (new, Latin NOVUS) — new and original. — novel, new / original.

k.syn: unwholesome, baneful, deleterious, detrimental, nocuous, pernicious, wicked
k.ant: beneficial, salubrious, anodyne, benign, harmless, hurtless, innocent, innocuous, inoffensive, safe 有益的，无害的
k.en: harmful to living things; injurious to health

ex.en: noxious waste
ex.cn: 有害的废弃物

k.syn: abhorrent, abominable, appalling, awful, disgusting, distasteful, dreadful, foul, fulsome, gross, hideous, horrid, loathsome, nasty, nauseating, noisome, obnoxious, odious, repellent, repugnant, repulsive, revolting, scandalous Education is a progressive discovery of our ignorance. 教育是一个逐步发现自己无知的过程。 ——美国历史学家　杜兰特（Durant, American historian） “敢于不断挑战极限的你将发掘拥有无限潜力的我。” （徐碧慧，2010年2月，Verbal 720，Quantitative 790，AW 4.5 录取院校：University of Oxford）
k.en: causing intense displeasure, disgust, or resentment

ex.en: a noxious new breed of horror movie in which graphic depictions of torture are presented as entertainment
ex.cn: 在一部新的恐怖电影中，对于酷刑生动的描述被当作娱乐，这让人很厌恶

m: NOXIOUS contains NOX (harm, Latin NOCERE) — harmful. (Compare INNOCUOUS = not harmful.) — noxious, harmful.
