k.ant: patent difference, lack of subtlety, sharp distinction, patency 明显的差别
k.en: a subtle distinction or variation

ex.en: a poem of little depth and nuance
ex.cn: 一首没有什么深度、没有什么辨识度的诗

m: NUANCE comes from French NUE (cloud) — a subtle shade like a cloud's gradient. — nuance, a subtle distinction.

k.en: to bring together

m: NUCLEATE contains NUCLE(US) — form a NUCLEUS / core. — nucleate, to form a core.

k.en: to seek the attention of by a push of the elbow

ex.en: accidentally nudged me as he squeezed past
ex.cn: 他过去的时候不小心用肘碰到了我

k.syn: encourage, exhort, goad, press, prod, prompt
k.en: to try to persuade（someone）through earnest appeals to follow a course of action

ex.en: The car salesman nudged me into taking a test-drive.
ex.cn: 汽车销售员说服我进行试驾。

m: NUDGE rhymes with BUDGE — a small NUDGE makes someone BUDGE. — nudge, to push gently.

k.syn: incidental, inconsequential, inconsiderable, insignificant, negligible, trifling, trivial
k.ant: consequential, eventful, important, major, meaningful, momentous, significant, substantial, unfrivolous, weighty 重要的
k.en: of little or no consequence; inconsequential

ex.en: The book is entertaining, but its contributions to Shakespearean scholarship are nugatory.
ex.cn: 这本书娱乐性不错，但是对于莎士比亚研究的贡献微不足道。

m: NUGATORY contains NUGA (trifle, Latin NUGAE) — trivial. — nugatory, of no value.

k.ant: withhold sustenance from 停止提供营养物
k.en: to supply with nourishment

ex.en: She nurtured her children through the winters with home-cooked soup.
ex.cn: 她整个冬天都给孩子们喂自制的汤。

k.syn: edify, illuminate, inspire
k.ant: impede, stunt, disregard 阻碍，漠视
k.en: to provide（someone）with moral or spiritual understanding

ex.en: He feels that his lifelong practice of reading the Bible daily has nurtured him in ways he cannot describe.
ex.cn: 一生中每天都阅读圣经在潜移默化中影响了他。

m: NURTURE shares NUR- with NURSE — to NURSE into health/growth. — nurture, to nurse / cultivate.

k.en: a fertile spot in a desert; a pleasant place amid difficult surroundings

m: OASIS — the Egyptian desert refuge; figuratively, any refreshing spot. — oasis, a desert refuge.

k.syn: pledge, troth, vow, word
k.en: a solemn, formal declaration or promise to fulfill a pledge

ex.en: an oath to defend the nation
ex.cn: 保卫祖国的誓言

m: OATH rhymes with BOTH — sworn before BOTH God and witnesses. — oath, a solemn promise.

k.syn: adamant, hard-nosed, headstrong, inflexible, intransigent, pertinacious, stubborn, unbending, uncompromising, unrelenting, unyielding, willful
k.ant: acquiescent, agreeable, amenable, compliant, complying, flexible, pliable, pliant, relenting, yielding 温顺的
k.en: resistant to persuasion or softening influences

ex.en: She is known for her obdurate determination.
ex.cn: 她以坚定不移著称。

k.syn: affectless, callous, compassionless, hard-hearted, heartless, indurate, inhuman, insensate, ironhearted, merciless, remorseless, ruthless, unsparing
k.ant: charitable, compassionate, humane, kindhearted, kindly, merciful, softhearted, sympathetic, tender, warmhearted 同情的，有爱的
k.en: having or showing a lack of sympathy or tender feelings

ex.en: the obdurate refusal of the crotchety old man to let the neighborhood kids retrieve their ball from his backyard
ex.cn: 那个怪老头冷酷无情地拒绝了孩子们进他后院找球的请求

m: OBDURATE contains DUR (hard, Latin DURUS) — hardened against persuasion. (Same root as ENDURE, DURABLE.) — obdurate, stubbornly hard.

k.syn: amenable, compliant, submissive, tractable
k.ant: contumacious, imperial, balky, contrary, contumacious, defiant, disobedient, froward, incompliant, insubordinate, intractable, noncompliant, obstreperous, rebel, rebellious, recalcitrant, refractory, restive, unamenable, ungovernable, unruly, untoward, wayward, willful 顽固的，不服从的
k.en: submissive to the restraint or command of authority

ex.en: That girl is so obedient that she does everything the first time she is asked.
ex.cn: 这个女孩如此顺从，叫她做事有求必应。

m: OBEDIENT contains OBEDI- (Latin OBOEDIRE = listen to) — listening to / following orders. — obedient, willing to obey.

k.ant: impertinent, impudent, imperious 不敬的，无礼的
k.en: a movement of the body made in token of respect or submission

ex.en: make obeisance to her mentors
ex.cn: 向她的导师们致敬

m: OBEISANCE contains OBE- (same root as OBEY) — a bow showing obedience. — obeisance, deferential respect.

k.syn: becloud, befog, blur, cloud, fog, muddy
k.ant: lucid 清晰的
k.en: to make so confused or opaque as to be difficult to perceive or understand

ex.en: obfuscate the reader
ex.cn: 让读者困惑

m: OB + FUSC (dark, Latin FUSCUS) — make dark / unclear. — obfuscate, to make confusing.

k.en: a notice of a person's death

m: OBITUARY contains OBIT (death notice, Latin OBIRE = to go away) — death notice. — obituary, a death announcement.

k.syn: compulsory, imperative, involuntary, peremptory, required
k.ant: discretionary, selective, elective, optional, voluntary 自由选择的，志愿的
k.en: of the nature of an obligation; compulsory

ex.en: obligatory military service
ex.cn: 强制性的服军役

k.syn: banal, cliché, commonplace, hack, hackneyed, moth-eaten, musty, shopworn, stereotyped, threadbare, timeworn, trite, well-worn
k.ant: fresh, new, novel, original, unclichéd, unhackneyed 新奇的
k.en: used or heard so often as to be dull

ex.en: the obligatory cliff-hanger endings for season finales of TV shows
ex.cn: 电视剧总是以恶俗的惊心动魄的结局作为完季

m: from OBLIGATION — required as obligation. — obligatory, required.

k.syn: accommodative, friendly, indulgent
k.en: willing to do favors

ex.en: An obliging concierge used his pull to get us reservations at the town's hottest restaurant.
ex.cn: 一个好心的看门人利用他的影响力帮我们在镇上最火爆的餐厅订到了座。

m: OBLIGING contains OBLIGE — going out of your way to OBLIGE. — obliging, helpful.

k.syn: askew, cant, inclined, leaning, listing, slanted, sloping, tilted
k.ant: direct, even, level, straight 笔直的
k.en: inclined or twisted to one side

ex.en: The old woman gave the eavesdropper an oblique glance.
ex.cn: 老妇人给了偷听者一个白眼。

m: OBLIQUE contains OBL ≈ "slope" (Latin OBLIQUUS = slanting) — slanted, indirect. — oblique, slanting / indirect.

k.syn: efface, eradicate, expunge, expurgate, exterminate, extirpate, wipe out
k.en: to remove from existence

ex.en: The March snowstorm obliterated our hopes for an early spring.
ex.cn: 三月的暴风雪摧毁了我们对于早春的念想。

m: OB + LITER (letter, Latin LITTERA) — wipe out the letters. — obliterate, to destroy completely.

k.syn: incognizant, insensible, unaware, unconscious, uninformed, unwitting
k.ant: mindful, vigilant, acquainted, cognizant, conscious, conversant, grounded, informed, knowing, mindful, witting 留意的，机警的
k.en: lacking conscious awareness; not informed about or aware of something

ex.en: He seemed oblivious to the fact that he had hurt her.
ex.cn: 他似乎并不知道他伤害了她。

m: OBLIVIOUS contains OBLI ≈ "forget" (Latin OBLIVISCI) — forgetting / unaware. — oblivious, unaware.

k.syn: billingsgate, fulmination, invective, scurrility, vitriol, vituperation
k.ant: adulation 极度谄媚
k.en: abusively detractive language or utterance; calumny

ex.en: she unleashed a torrent of obloquy on her opponent.
ex.cn: 她滔滔不绝地辱骂了对手一顿。

k.syn: discredit, disesteem, dishonor, disrepute, ignominy, infamy, odium, opprobrium, reproach, shame
k.ant: esteem, honor, respect 敬重，好名声
k.en: the state of having lost the esteem of others

ex.en: The accused murderer was condemned to live out his days in perpetual obloquy.
ex.cn: 凶手终将在骂名中度过余生。

m: OB + LOQUY (speech, Latin LOQUI) — speaking against; abuse. (Same root as ELOQUENT.) — obloquy, public abuse.

k.syn: ambiguous, arcane, enigmatic, equivocal, fuliginous, inscrutable, opaque
k.ant: explicit, manifest, clear, certain, accessible, nonambiguous, obvious, plain, unambiguous, unequivocal 明晰的，没有歧义的
k.en: not clearly understood or expressed; having an often intentionally veiled or uncertain meaning

ex.en: a fantasy writer who likes to put lots of obscure references in her tales of wizards and warlocks
ex.cn: 一个喜欢在巫师故事中添加很多含义模糊的引文的魔幻小说作家

k.syn: noteless, uncelebrated, unfamous, unknown, unrecognized, unsung
k.ant: celebrity 名人
k.en: not prominent or famous

ex.en: an obscure poet
ex.cn: 无名的诗人

k.syn: blear, blur, darken, dim, overshadow, shroud
k.ant: elucidate, brighten, illuminate, light up, lighten 阐明
k.en: to make dark, dim, or indistinct

ex.en: When it isn't obscured by smog, the view of the city from the observatory can be spectacular.
ex.cn: 如果在晴朗的天气，从天文台看出去的城市全景非常赞。

m: OBSCURE contains OB + SCURE ≈ "obSCURity" — sharing root with OBSCURITY (cover, Latin OB + SCURUS = covered). — obscure, unclear / unknown.

k.ant: supercilious 目中无人的
k.en: marked by or exhibiting a fawning attentiveness

ex.en: She's constantly followed by obsequious assistants who will do anything.
ex.cn: 她总是有一群谄媚的跟班，愿意为她赴汤蹈火。

m: OB + SEQUI (follow, Latin SEQUI) — following too closely, fawning. (Same root as SEQUEL.) — obsequious, servilely flattering.

k.ant: intensively disgust 极度厌恶
k.en: to haunt or excessively preoccupy the mind of

ex.en: The war obsesses her—she talks about nothing else.
ex.cn: 那场战争总是困扰着她，她张口闭口都谈。

m: OBSESS contains OB + SESS (sit, Latin SEDERE) — to sit upon the mind. (Same root as SESSION.) — obsess, to fixate.

k.syn: adamant, headstrong, intransigent, stubborn, uncompromising, unyielding, willful
k.ant: acquiescent, agreeable, amenable, compliant, complying, flexible, pliable, pliant, relenting, yielding 顺从的，服从的
k.en: perversely adhering to an opinion, purpose, or course in spite of reason, arguments, or persuasion

ex.en: obstinate resistance to change
ex.cn: 坚决抵制改变

k.en: not easily subdued, remedied, or removed

ex.en: obstinate fever
ex.cn: 高烧不退

ex.en: an obstinate headache
ex.cn: 难以治愈的头痛

m: OBSTINATE contains OB + STIN (stand, Latin STARE) — standing firm against. — obstinate, stubborn.

k.syn: balky, defiant, incompliant, insubordinate, intractable, rebellious, recalcitrant, refractory
k.ant: disciplined, amenable, biddable, compliant, conformable, docile, obedient, ruly, submissive, tractable 守纪律的，顺从的
k.en: stubbornly resistant to control; unruly; given to resisting authority or another's control

ex.en: The club's president was at his wits' end with obstreperous members who refused to cooperate.
ex.cn: 俱乐部主席拿那些不合作的会员一点办法都没有。

k.syn: blatant, clamant, clamorous, squawking, vociferant
k.en: engaging in or marked by loud and insistent cries especially of protest

ex.en: an obstreperous crowd protesting the government's immigration policy
ex.cn: 一堆吵闹的民众游行抗议政府的移民政策

m: OB + STREPER (noise, Latin STREPERE) — noisily resistant. — obstreperous, noisily defiant.

k.syn: encumber, fetter, handicap, hinder, impede, inhibit, stymie, interfere with
k.ant: facilitate, abet, aid, assist 促进，帮助
k.en: to impede, retard, or interfere with; hinder; to create difficulty for the work or activity of

ex.en: He was charged with obstructing justice by lying to investigators.
ex.cn: 他被指控向检察官说谎，妨碍司法。

k.syn: occlude 堵塞
k.ant: clear, free, open up, unblock, unclog, unplug, unstop 清除（障碍）
k.en: to prevent passage through by filling with something

ex.en: an unobstructed view
ex.cn: 无阻碍的视野

m: OB + STRUCT (build, Latin STRUERE) — build up against, block. — obstruct, to block.

k.syn: dumb, fatuous, mindless, oafish, opaque, senseless, unintelligent, vacuous
k.ant: insightful, apt, brainy, bright, brilliant, clever, fast, intelligent, keen, nimble, quick, quick-witted, sharp, sharp-witted, smart 有洞察力的，智慧的
k.en: not having or showing an ability to absorb ideas readily

ex.en: Are you being deliberately obtuse?
ex.cn: 你是故意装笨吗？

m: OBTUSE contains OB + TUSE (blunt, Latin OBTUSUS) — blunt-witted. (An obtuse angle is wider/blunter.) — obtuse, slow-witted.

k.syn: avert, forestall, preclude, stave off, head off
k.en: to anticipate and prevent（as a situation）or make unnecessary（as an action）

ex.en: The new treatment obviates the need for surgery.
ex.cn: 新的疗法使手术不再是必须的。

m: OBVIATE contains OB + VIA (way, Latin) — anticipate the way ahead, remove an obstacle. — obviate, to make unnecessary.

k.syn: ambiguous, arcane, equivocal, inscrutable, opaque, impenetrable
k.ant: bare, manifest, patent, readily fathomable 暴露的，可理解的
k.en: not easily apprehended or understood: abstruse, mysterious

ex.en: an occult reference in the text that has puzzled scholars
ex.cn: 文章中一处晦涩的、难倒了学者们的引注

k.syn: belie, blanket, cloak, conceal, cover, curtain, disguise, enshroud, mask, obscure, screen, shroud, suppress, veil, blot out, paper over
k.ant: bare, disclose, display, divulge, expose, reveal, show, uncloak, uncover, unmask, unveil 暴露
k.en: to keep secret or shut off from view

ex.en: occult their house from prying eyes by planting large trees around it
ex.cn: 在住所周围种满大树防止别人偷窥

ex.en: The actor's private life had long been occulted by a contrived public persona.
ex.cn: 演员的私人生活一直以来被一个精心打造的公众形象所遮掩。

m: OCCULT contains OCCULT- (hidden, Latin OCCULERE) — hidden / supernatural. — occult, hidden / supernatural.

k.en: a lyric poem

m: ODE — Greek ODE meant "song." (Same root as MELODY, COMEDY.) — ode, a lyric poem of praise.

k.syn: ignominy, infamy, obloquy, opprobrium
k.ant: esteem, honor, respect, hankering, infatuation 尊敬，渴望，着迷
k.en: strong dislike, contempt, or aversion

ex.en: Time did nothing to diminish the odium in which the traitor lived out his days.
ex.cn: 时间也没有减少大家对这个叛徒的唾弃。

m: ODIUM contains ODI (hate, Latin) — hatred. (Same root as ODIOUS.) — odium, intense hatred.

k.syn: extraordinary, peculiar, queer, unaccustomed, uncommon, uncustomary, out-of-the-way
k.ant: conventional, bathetic, hackneyed, common, ordinary, plain, usual 传统陈腐的，平常普通的
k.en: noticeably different from what is generally found or experienced

ex.en: This writer has an enjoyably offbeat sense of humor.
ex.cn: 这个作者有一种奇特而有趣的幽默感。

m: OFF + BEAT — not on the BEAT, unconventional. — offbeat, unconventional.

k.syn: ad-lib, extemporary, impromptu, improvised, unplanned, unpremeditated, unrehearsed
k.ant: considered, planned, premeditated, prepared, rehearsed 事先谋划的
k.en: without premeditation or preparation

ex.en: couldn't give the figures offhand
ex.cn: 无法立即给出数字

m: OFF + HAND — done off the top of your hand, without preparation. — offhand, casual / unprepared.

k.syn: interfering, intruding, meddlesome, obtrusive, presuming, prying, snoopy
k.ant: unobtrusive 不引人注目的
k.en: thrusting oneself where one is not welcome or invited

ex.en: an officious little man who was always telling everyone else how to do their jobs
ex.cn: 一个多管闲事的人，总喜欢对他人的事情指手画脚

m: OFFICIOUS contains OFFICE — overstepping your OFFICE, meddling. — officious, intrusively helpful.

k.syn: aloof, detached, distant, remote, unbending, unsociable
k.ant: sociable, cordial, friendly, social, warm 热情的
k.en: inclined to be distant and reserved; aloof

ex.en: consistently surly and offish with the would-be suitors who came calling
ex.cn: 对于追求者总是阴沉并且冷淡

m: OFFISH = OFF + ISH — keeping OFF from people. — offish, aloof.

k.en: the sense of smell; the act or process of smelling

m: OLFACTION contains OLF (smell, Latin OLERE) — sense of smell. — olfaction, the sense of smell.

k.syn: baleful, foreboding, inauspicious, portentous, sinister, threatening, minatory
k.ant: auspicious, unthreatening 吉兆的，不具有威胁的
k.en: of or being an omen, especially an evil one

ex.en: an ominous threat of war
ex.cn: 战争来临的凶兆

m: OMINOUS contains OMEN — bearing a bad omen. — ominous, foreboding.

k.ant: vacuous 无知的
k.en: possessed of universal or complete knowledge

ex.en: an omniscient deity
ex.cn: 无所不知的神

m: OMNI (all) + SCI (know, Latin SCIRE) — all-knowing. — omniscient, all-knowing.

k.syn: arduous, burdensome, challenging, exacting, grueling, taxing, toilsome
k.ant: requiring little effort, light, nondemanding, unchallenging, undemanding 轻松的，不费劲的
k.en: requiring much time, effort, or careful attention

ex.en: Building the scale model of the frigate was an onerous task.
ex.cn: 建造军舰的小模型非常花时间。

m: ONEROUS contains ONER (burden, Latin ONUS) — burdensome. — onerous, burdensome.

k.syn: blurry, dim, misty, murky, obscure, shadowy, unclear
k.ant: diaphanous, transparent 精致透明的
k.en: impenetrable by light; neither transparent nor translucent

k.syn: ambiguous, arcane, enigmatic, equivocal, inscrutable, occult
k.ant: clear, accessible, obvious, plain, unambiguous, unequivocal 清晰易懂的
k.en: so obscure as to be unintelligible

ex.en: opaque prose
ex.cn: 晦涩的诗歌

m: OPAQUE rhymes with TAKE / CLOAK — light can't get through. (Latin OPACUS = shaded.) — opaque, not transparent / unclear.

k.syn: comment, editorialize, note, observe, reflect, weigh in
k.en: to express opinions; to sate as an opinion

ex.en: You can opine about any subject you like.
ex.cn: 你可以畅所欲言。

m: OPINE contains OPIN ≈ OPINION — to give your opinion. — opine, to express an opinion.

k.syn: seasonable, well-timed
k.ant: inconvenient, untimely, inappropriate, unseasonable 不合适的
k.en: suitable or convenient for a particular occurrence

ex.en: an opportune moment
ex.cn: 方便的时机

m: OPPORTUNE contains PORT (harbor, Latin PORTUS) — "OB + PORT" = arriving at the right harbor at the right time. ("Opportunity knocks.") — opportune, timely.

k.syn: scurrilous, vitriolic, vituperative, contumelious
k.en: expressing contemptuous reproach; scornful or abusive

ex.en: opprobrious language
ex.cn: 脏话

k.syn: discreditable, disgraceful, dishonorable, ignominious, infamous, notorious, shameful, unrespectable
k.ant: good repute 好名声
k.cn: 臭名昭著的，令人鄙视的
k.en: bringing disgrace; shameful or infamous

ex.en: opprobrious conduct
ex.cn: 令人鄙视的行为

m: OPPROBRIOUS contains OPPROBR ≈ "reproach" (Latin OPPROBRIUM) — abusively reproachful. — opprobrious, abusively scornful.

k.en: a person regarded as an infallible source of wisdom or prophecy

m: ORACLE contains ORA (speak, Latin) — the speaker of prophecies (Oracle of Delphi). — oracle, a prophet / source of wisdom.

k.syn: address, declamation, harangue, peroration
k.en: a speech delivered in a high-flown or pompous manner

m: ORATION contains ORA (speak, Latin) — a formal speech. — oration, a grand speech.

k.syn: accustom, familiarize, initiate, introduce, orientate
k.ant: confuse 使迷惑
k.cn: 使确定方向，使熟悉或适应
k.en: to set or arrange in any determinate position especially in relation to the points of the compass; to make familiar with or adjusted to facts, principles, or a situation

ex.en: orient students toward a career in medicine
ex.cn: 指导学生从事医学的职业道路

m: ORIENT comes from "east" — to find your bearings (the rising sun was the reference). — orient, to find one's bearings.

k.syn: ingenious, innovative, inventive
k.ant: commonplace, banal, trite, familiar, hackneyed, time-honored, tired, warmed-over 陈腐的
k.en: independent and creative in thought or action: inventive

ex.en: The car has a highly original design.
ex.cn: 这车的设计很新颖。

k.syn: earliest, foremost, headmost, inaugural, initial, leadoff, maiden, pioneer, premier, virgin
k.ant: final, last, latter, terminal, ultimate 最终的
k.en: coming before all others in time or order

ex.en: The original plan had to be discarded when the situation changed drastically.
ex.cn: 当环境发生剧烈变化时，最初的计划就该被放弃。

m: ORIGINAL contains ORIGIN — at the origin, first. — original, first / inventive.

k.en: a branch of zoology dealing with birds

m: ORNITHO (bird, Greek ORNIS) + LOGY (study) — the study of birds. — ornithology, bird science.

k.syn: ceremonial, conventional, regular, routine
k.ant: casual, freewheeling, informal, irregular, unceremonious, unconventional, unorthodox 不常见的
k.en: following or agreeing with established form, custom, or rules

ex.en: He believes in the benefits of both orthodox medicine and alternative medicine.
ex.cn: 他既相信传统药物又相信替代药物的效用。

m: ORTHO (correct, Greek) + DOX (opinion) — the correct/accepted opinion. (Compare HETERODOX = different opinion.) — orthodox, traditionally accepted.

k.ant: remain static 静止
k.en: to swing backward and forward like a pendulum
vary between opposing beliefs, feelings, or theories

m: OSCILLATE rhymes with VACILLATE — both swing back and forth. — oscillate, to swing back and forth.

k.ant: make pliant, transcend conventions, amenable to change 使柔软，超越传统
k.en: to（cause to）become hardened or conventional and opposed to change

ex.en: a disease that ossifies the joints
ex.cn: 一种会使关节僵化的疾病

m: OSSIFY contains OSS (bone, Latin OS, OSSIS) — to turn into bone, become rigid. — ossify, to harden.

k.syn: flamboyant, flaring, flashy, splashy
k.ant: modest, artless, austere, unpretentious, conservative, quiet, understated, unflamboyant, unflashy 谦让的，朴素的，保守的
k.en: marked by or fond of conspicuous or vainglorious and sometimes pretentious display

ex.en: She wears an ostentatious diamond ring on her finger.
ex.cn: 她手指上戴着一枚巨大的钻戒。

m: shares OSTEN- with OSTENSIBLE — showing off. — ostentatious, showy.

k.syn: banish, bounce, chase, dismiss, expel, extrude, oust, boot out, cast out, run off, drum out, kick out, throw out, turf out
k.ant: take in, include, embrace, welcome 接纳
k.en: to exclude from a group

ex.en: He was ostracized from the scientific community for many years because of his radical political beliefs.
ex.cn: 他因激进的政治理想被驱逐出了科学界很多年。

m: OSTRACIZE — in ancient Athens, citizens voted to exile someone by writing names on OSTRAKA (broken pottery). — ostracize, to exclude from society.

k.syn: deprive, displace, uncrown, unthrone
k.ant: instate, crown, enthrone, throne 任命
k.en: to remove from a position of prominence or power（as a throne）

ex.en: be ousted from power
ex.cn: 被赶下台

k.syn: banish, boot out, cast out, chase, dismiss, drum out, expel, extrude, kick out, throw out, turf out, turn out
k.en: to drive or force out

ex.en: She was ousted from her job after it was proven she'd been pilfering company supplies.
ex.cn: 她贪污公司财物这事被证实后，她被驱逐了。

m: OUST rhymes with OUT — push someone OUT of position. — oust, to remove from position.

k.en: a clothing

m: OUTFIT — a FITted-out set of clothes for a particular occasion. — outfit, a set of clothing.

k.syn: companionable, extroverted, gregarious, sociable
k.ant: antisocial, introverted, nongregarious, reclusive, unsociable, unsocial 不合群的，不善交际的
k.en: openly friendly and responsive: extroverted

ex.en: a salesman whose aggressively outgoing personality could sometimes be overbearing
ex.cn: 一个销售员如果过于热情会让人不能忍

m: OUTGOING = OUT + GOING — heading OUT to socialize. — outgoing, sociable.

k.syn: bizarre, cranky, eccentric, erratic, offbeat, peculiar, remarkable
k.ant: conventional, familiar, nonexotic, nonglamorous, plain-Jane, unglamorous, unromantic 常规的，平凡的
k.en: strikingly out of the ordinary; bizarre

ex.en: an outlandish costume
ex.cn: 奇装异服

m: OUT + LAND + ISH — from far OUT-LANDS, strange. — outlandish, bizarre.

k.syn: outfox, outslick, outsmart, outthink, outwit
k.ant: yield 屈服
k.en: to overcome（an opponent）by artful, clever maneuvering

ex.en: He outmaneuvered his congressional opponent.
ex.cn: 他以计谋战胜了国会的对手。

m: OUT + MANEUVER — out-maneuver someone, beat them with skill. — outmaneuver, to outsmart tactically.

k.syn: alpha, commencement, genesis, inception, incipience, nascence
k.ant: termination, close, conclusion, end, ending, omega 终止
k.en: beginning, start

ex.en: I wish you'd mentioned this problem at the outset.
ex.cn: 我希望你开门见山地提这个问题。

m: OUT + SET — when you first SET OUT. — outset, the beginning.

k.syn: outfox, outmaneuver, outslick, outsmart, outthink
k.en: to surpass in cleverness or cunning; outsmart

ex.en: a plan to outwit their opponents at their own game
ex.cn: 在擅长的领域智取对手的计划

m: OUT + WIT — beat them with more WIT. — outwit, to outsmart.

k.syn: authoritative, despotic, dictatorial, imperious, masterful, peremptory
k.ant: meek, unassuming 谦逊的
k.en: domineering in manner; arrogant

ex.en: Her husband's overbearing manner made her miserable.
ex.cn: 她的丈夫专横傲慢，让她痛不欲生。

k.syn: capital, cardinal, central, chief, dominant, key, leading, master, number one, overmastering, overriding, paramount, predominant, preeminent, premier, primary, prior, sovereign, supreme
k.cn: 压倒一切的，（在力量或重要性上）势不可挡的；支配的，显著的
k.en: overwhelming in power or significance; predominant, coming before all others in importance

ex.en: the overbearing problem in our nation's schools
ex.cn: 我国中小学教育最突出的问题

m: OVER + BEARING — bearing down OVER everyone. — overbearing, domineering.

k.syn: lap, overlay, overlie, overspread
k.en: to occupy the same area in part

ex.en: Baseball season overlaps football season in September.
ex.cn: 棒球赛季和橄榄球赛季在九月有重合时间。

m: OVER + LAP — one thing LAPS OVER another. — overlap, to partially coincide.

k.ant: shadowy 模糊的
k.en: open and observable; not hidden, concealed, or secret

ex.en: overt hostility
ex.cn: 公然的敌意

m: OVERT rhymes with COVERT but the opposite — open vs. hidden. — overt, openly visible.

k.syn: preamble, preliminary, prologue, warm-up, curtain-raiser
k.en: an introductory section or part, as of a poem; a prelude

k.ant: coda 结尾
k.en: an instrumental composition intended especially as an introduction to an extended work, such as an opera or oratorio

ex.en: The parade down Main Street served as the overture for a weekend of fun and festivities.
ex.cn: 主干道上的游行活动拉开了一周节日活动的序幕。

m: OVERTURE comes from French OUVERT (open) — the OPENING music of an opera; opening proposal. — overture, an opening / proposal.

k.en: a combination of contradictory or incongruous words

ex.en: The phrase“cruel kindness”is an oxymoron.“
ex.cn: 残酷的仁慈”就是矛盾修饰法的一个例子。

ex.en: The phrase“Broadway rock musical”is an oxymoron. Broadway doesn't have the nerve to let the really hard stuff in the house.“
ex.cn: 百老汇的摇滚音乐剧”是个反讽。百老汇才不会允许吵吵闹闹的东西在里面演出。

m: OXY (sharp, Greek) + MORON (dull) — a sharp-dull paradox ("jumbo shrimp"). — oxymoron, a contradictory phrase.

k.en: any of various large, thick-skinned, hoofed mammals

m: PACHY (thick, Greek) + DERM (skin) — thick-skinned animal (elephant, rhino). (Same DERM root as DERMATOLOGY.) — pachyderm, a thick-skinned animal.

k.syn: appease, assuage, conciliate, mollify, placate, propitiate
k.ant: incite, rankle, tantrum, vex, discompose, truculent 煽动，激起
k.en: to ease the anger or agitation of

ex.en: pacify a crying child
ex.cn: 安抚一个哭泣的孩子

m: PACIFY contains PAC (peace, Latin PAX) — make peaceful. (Same root as PACIFIC.) — pacify, to calm.

k.en: a group of animals, such as dogs or wolves, that run and hunt together

m: a PACK of wolves — a tightly grouped predator group. — pack, an animal group.

k.syn: caricature, coloring, elaboration, embellishment, embroidery, hyperbole, magnification, overstatement, stretching
k.ant: meiosis, understatement 轻描淡写
k.en: the representation of something in terms that go beyond the facts

ex.en: That feature writer is sometimes guilty of padding, but he keeps it from getting out of hand.
ex.cn: 这位专栏作者有时候夸夸其谈，但是保持了适度。

m: PADDING contains PAD — extra PADding stuffed in to fill space, often unnecessary. — padding, unnecessary filler.

k.syn: accolade, citation, dithyramb, eulogium, eulogy, hymn, panegyric, tribute
k.en: a joyous song or hymn of praise, tribute, thanksgiving, or triumph

ex.en: Her retirement party featured many paeans for her long years of service to the company.
ex.cn: 她的退休派对满是对她多年在公司辛勤工作的赞颂。

m: PAEAN — from Greek PAIAN, a hymn of triumph to Apollo. — paean, a song of praise.

k.syn: careful, conscientious, fussy, meticulous, scrupulous
k.ant: slipshod, cursory, careless 漫不经心的，草率的
k.en: taking pains: expending, showing, or involving diligent care and effort

ex.en: It took months of painstaking research to write the book.
ex.cn: 这本书的写作耗费了几个月的苦心研究。

m: PAIN + STAKING — taking pains to do it right. — painstaking, meticulous.

k.syn: agreeable, blessed, congenial, delectable, delightful, delightsome, dreamy, dulcet, enjoyable, felicitous, grateful, gratifying, heavenly, jolly, luscious, pleasurable, satisfying, savory
k.ant: disagreeable, pleasureless, unpalatable, unpleasant, unwelcome 不愉悦的
k.en: giving pleasure or contentment to the mind or senses

ex.en: I always associate the palatable aroma of roasting turkey with Thanksgiving.
ex.cn: 我总是把香喷喷的烤火鸡的味道和感恩节联系起来。

m: PALATABLE contains PALATE — agreeable to the PALATE (taste). — palatable, agreeable to taste.

k.syn: deluxe, lavish, luxuriant, opulent, sumptuous
k.ant: ascetic, austere, humble, no-frills, spartan 简朴的
k.en: of the nature of a palace, as in spaciousness or ornateness

ex.en: a palatial penthouse apartment
ex.cn: 一套豪华的顶层公寓

m: PALATIAL contains PALACE — like a PALACE. — palatial, lavishly grand.

k.en: a particular range, quality, or use of color; an artist's board for mixing colors

m: PALETTE — the painter's PAL-ette holds a range of colors. — palette, a range of colors.

k.ant: interest, intrigue 激发兴趣
k.en: to lose in interest or attraction

ex.en: His humor began to pall on us.
ex.cn: 他的幽默开始使我们生腻了。

m: to PALL = to grow tiresome, lose its appeal. (PALL also = a dark cloth over a coffin.) — pall, to lose appeal.

k.syn: allay, alleviate, assuage, mitigate, mollify, relieve, soothe
k.ant: aggravate, exacerbate, worsen, increase intensity 加剧，恶化
k.der: palliative adj. 缓和的，减轻的
k.en: to make less severe or intense; mitigate

ex.en: palliative drug
ex.cn: 缓解剂

m: PALLIATE contains PALL (cloak, Latin PALLIUM) — cloak/cover up the symptoms. — palliate, to ease without curing.

k.ant: piquant 兴奋的
k.en: lacking in radiance or vitality; dull

ex.en: The movie is a pallid version of the classic novel.
ex.cn: 这部经典小说改编的电影很苍白无力。

k.syn: ashen, ashy, blanched, pasty, wan
k.ant: rubicund, blooming, florid, flush, full-blooded, glowing, ruddy, sanguine 红润的
k.en: having an abnormally pale or wan complexion

ex.en: a pallid girl who looked as though she'd never seen the sun
ex.cn: 一个面色苍白的姑娘，仿佛从来没有晒过太阳

m: PALLID contains PALL (pale) — pale/lifeless. (Same root as PALE.) — pallid, pale / lifeless.

k.syn: flourishing, prosperous, thriving, booming, lush, roaring, halcyon
k.ant: failed, depressed, unprosperous, unsuccessful 不成功的，不繁荣的
k.en: marked by prosperity

ex.en: the palmy days of the British drama
ex.cn: 英国戏剧的繁荣时期

m: PALMY contains PALM — sailors waved PALM branches in triumph at peak prosperity. — palmy, prosperous.

k.syn: touchable
k.ant: impalpable, intangible 无形的，触摸不到的
k.en: capable of being touched or felt

ex.en: a small but palpable lump in my neck
ex.cn: 我脖子上有一块很小但是能摸得到的肿块

k.syn: apprehensible, detectable, discernible, distinguishable, sensible
k.ant: imperceptible, inappreciable, indistinguishable, insensible, undetectable 隐晦的，细微的
k.en: easily perceived; obvious

ex.en: a palpable difference
ex.cn: 易觉察的差别

m: PALPABLE contains PALP (touch, Latin PALPARE) — able to be touched / felt. (Same root as PALPITATE.) — palpable, tangible / obvious.

k.syn: chaffer, deal, dicker, haggle, horse-trade, negotiate, equivocate
k.ant: candor 坦率
k.en: to act insincerely or deceitfully; haggle, chaffer

ex.en: unwilling to palter over the price of the house
ex.cn: 拒绝对房子讨价还价

m: PALTER rhymes with FALTER — but in honesty: shifty bargaining. — palter, to deceive / haggle.

k.syn: inconsequential, inconsiderable, insignificant, niggling, piddling, piffling, trivial
k.ant: significant, important, big, consequential, considerable, material 意义重大的
k.en: lacking in importance or worth

ex.en: Venture capitalists appear to be confused by such paltry amounts.
ex.cn: 风险投资家对如此微不足道的数量感到疑惑。

k.syn: cheap, cruddy, deplorable, despicable, grubby, lame, lousy, mean, scummy, scurvy, sneaking, wretched
k.ant: admirable, commendable, creditable, laudable, meritorious, praiseworthy 令人尊敬的
k.cn: 让人厌恶的，让人鄙视的
k.en: arousing or deserving of one's loathing and disgust

ex.en: a paltry, underhanded scheme to get someone fired
ex.cn: 一个卑鄙的炒掉某人的计谋

m: PALTRY rhymes with WATERY (in feel) — both diluted to nothing. — paltry, contemptibly small.

k.syn: blame, censure, condemn, denounce, dispraise, reprehend
k.ant: eulogize, extol, rave, laud, praise 称赞
k.en: a harsh criticism

ex.en: Almost all the movie critics have panned this latest sequel in a tired series.
ex.cn: 几乎所有的影评家都批评这部最新续集，认为是狗尾续貂。

m: to PAN a film = to give it a critical hot-PAN-ning. — pan, to harshly criticize.

k.ant: humility, unremarkable behavior, dullness 谦逊，寻常举止，平庸乏味
k.en: dash or flamboyance in style and action

ex.en: Belanger dances with an undemonstrative panache that draws one's attention as if by seeking to deflect it; through finesse and understatement, he manages to seem at once intensely present and curiously detached.
ex.cn: 贝朗格的舞蹈低调而华丽，似乎想通过使人走神而吸引观众的注意；他通过精湛的技艺和压抑的演绎，使他的表演让人感到忽远忽近。

m: PANACHE — a feathered plume on a helmet, hence flamboyant style. (French.) — panache, flamboyant style.

k.ant: limited 有限的
k.en: widespread; general

ex.en: pandemic malaria
ex.cn: 大规模的疟疾

m: PAN (all) + DEMIC (people, Greek DEMOS) — affecting all people. — pandemic, widespread disease.

k.syn: bluster, bustle, disturbance, furor, fuss, hubbub, moil, pother, ruckus, ruction, tumult, turmoil, uproar
k.ant: serene 安静
k.en: wild uproar or noise

ex.en: Christmas morning at our house is always marked by pandemonium.
ex.cn: 我们家圣诞节的早上总是免不了一番吵吵闹闹。

m: PAN (all) + DEMON + IUM — Milton's "Paradise Lost" name for the capital of all demons. — pandemonium, wild uproar.

k.syn: accolade, citation, commendation, dithyramb, eulogium, eulogy, hymn, paean
k.ant: anathema, denunciation, condemnation 诅咒，谴责
k.en: an eulogistic oration or writing

ex.en: write a panegyric on the centennial of the Nobel laureate's birth
ex.cn: 为诺贝尔获得者百年诞辰纪念日写一篇颂文

m: PAN (all) + EGYR (gathering, Greek AGORA) — a speech at a public gathering, all-praising. — panegyric, formal high praise.

k.en: a separate or distinct

m: PANEL contains PAN ≈ "flat board" (Latin PANNUS = cloth). — panel, a flat board.

k.syn: compendious, complete, comprehensive, cyclopedic, embracive, exhaustive, thorough, in-depth
k.ant: narrow 狭窄的
k.der: panorama n. 全景；全貌，概述
k.en: of an unobstructed or complete view of an area in every direction

ex.en: a panorama of American history
ex.cn: 美国历史总览

m: PAN (all) + ORAMA (view, Greek HORAMA) — all-around view. — panoramic, sweeping wide view.

k.en: a small room or closet where food, tableware, and similar items are stored

m: PANTRY contains PANT ≈ Latin PANIS (bread) — originally a bread-room. — pantry, a food storeroom.

k.syn: apologue, fable
k.en: a story intended to teach a basic truth or moral about life

ex.en: the parable in which the repentant sinner is compared to the returning prodigal son
ex.cn: 这个寓言故事将不愿悔过的罪人和回头的浪子进行了比较

m: PARABLE contains PARA (alongside) + BLE — Greek PARABOLE = comparison set alongside. (Same root as PARABLE in geometry.) — parable, an instructive tale.

k.syn: archetype, example, exemplar, ideal, model, pattern, paragon
k.en: one that serves as a pattern or model

ex.en: He was the paradigm of the successful man.
ex.cn: 他是成功人士的典范。

m: PARADIGM contains PARA + DIGM (show, Greek DEIKNYNAI) — shown alongside as a model. — paradigm, a model / pattern.

k.syn: bliss, empyrean, heaven, nirvana, utopia
k.ant: hell, inferno 地狱
k.en: an often imaginary place or state of utter perfection and happiness

ex.en: an idealist who trotted the globe looking for paradise
ex.cn: 一个奔波于全世界以寻找一片乐土的理想主义者

k.syn: elation, euphoria, exhilaration, intoxication, rapture, rhapsody, transport
k.ant: dejection, depression, gloominess, melancholy 沮丧，哀伤
k.en: a state of overwhelming usually pleasurable emotion

ex.en: that early stage of a romance when lovers are in paradise
ex.cn: 恋爱初期，恋人们都处于快乐兴奋的状态

m: PARADISE — Greek PARADEISOS = walled garden (originally Persian). — paradise, a heavenly place.

k.syn: dichotomy, incongruity
k.cn: 表面矛盾实际可能正确的话，悖论
k.en: a statement that is seemingly contradictory or opposed to common sense and yet is perhaps true

ex.en: The paradox is that fishermen would catch more fish if they fished less.
ex.cn: 有一个悖论：如果渔民减少捕鱼量的话，他们将会捕到更多的鱼。

m: PARA (against) + DOX (opinion, as in ORTHODOX) — against expected opinion. — paradox, a self-contradictory truth.

k.syn: archetype, example, exemplar, ideal, model, pattern, paradigm
k.en: a model of excellence or perfection of a kind; a peerless example

ex.en: a paragon of good husband
ex.cn: 模范好丈夫

k.syn: assimilate, compare, equate, liken, match, parallel
k.ant: contrast 对比，对照以产生反差
k.en: to compare with; parallel

ex.en: paragon retreat with treachery
ex.cn: 把撤退比作是背叛

m: PARA (alongside) + GON (rub, sharpen) — a touchstone alongside which others are tested. — paragon, a model of excellence.

k.syn: cardinal, chief, key, leading, main, predominant, preeminent, primary, principal, supreme
k.ant: ancillary, secondary 次要的；paltry, petty, trifling, trivial 不重要的
k.en: of chief concern or importance

ex.en: The paramount goal is to restore the colonial-era house with complete historical accuracy.
ex.cn: 最重要的任务就是十分精确地依照历史重建殖民时期的建筑。

m: PARA + MOUNT — at the highest MOUNTain peak. — paramount, supremely important.

k.en: a tendency on the part of an individual or group toward excessive or irrational suspiciousness

m: PARA (beside) + NOIA (mind, Greek NOOS) — mind beside itself. — paranoia, irrational suspicion.

k.syn: rephrase, restate, reword, translate
k.ant: quote（不加以修改地）引用
k.en: to express something（as a text or statement）in different words

ex.en: Could you please paraphrase your diagnosis of my health condition, using simpler language?
ex.cn: 您能否用更通俗的话语转述一下您对我健康状况的诊断呢？

m: PARA (beside) + PHRASE — restate alongside, in other words. — paraphrase, to restate in other words.

k.en: an organism that grows, feeds, and is sheltered on or in a different organism while contributing

m: PARASITE contains PARA (beside) + SITE (food, Greek SITOS) — feeds beside another. — parasite, an organism living off another.

k.syn: dehydrate, desiccate, dry, scorch, sear
k.ant: douse, drench, hydrate, steep, wash, water, wet 弄湿
k.en: to make extremely dry, especially by exposure to heat

ex.en: parch a surface from exposure to sun
ex.cn: 通过太阳照射烤干一个面

m: PARCH rhymes with SCORCH — both dry by heat. — parch, to dry by heat.

k.syn: discontinuity, interim, interlude, intermission, interregnum, interruption, interstice, interval
k.ant: continuation, continuity 持续不断；resumption 继续
k.en: an interruption of continuity; an interval

ex.en: a parenthesis in an otherwise solid marriage
ex.cn: 在本该是完满婚姻中的一个插曲

m: PARENTHESIS contains PAREN- (beside) + THESIS (placed) — placed beside as an interruption. — parenthesis, an inserted interruption.

k.syn: castaway, castoff, leper, reject
k.ant: respectable person 值得尊敬的人
k.en: one that is despised or rejected, outcast

ex.en: I felt like a pariah when I wore the wrong outfit to the dinner party.
ex.cn: 当我穿着不恰当的礼服赴宴时，我感觉自己被别人狠狠地鄙视了。

m: PARIAH — from the Tamil word for the lowest caste; an outcast. — pariah, a social outcast.

k.syn: coequality, coordinateness, equality, equivalency, par, sameness
k.ant: disparity, imparity, inequality 不公平；incommensurateness 不相称
k.en: the quality or state of being equal or equivalent

ex.en: achieve parity with our commercial competitors
ex.cn: 取得与我们的商业竞争对手同等的地位

m: PARITY contains PAR (equal, Latin) — same root as PAR in golf — equality. — parity, equality.

k.en: manner or mode of speech; vocabulary used in a particular field or context

ex.en: legal parlance
ex.cn: 法律用语

m: PARLANCE contains PARL (speak, French PARLER) — manner of speaking. — parlance, a way of speaking.

k.syn: burlesque, caricature, spoof, travesty
k.cn: （以嘲笑原作作者的）模仿作品
k.en: a literary or musical work in which the style of an author or work is closely imitated for comic effect or in ridicule

ex.en: The Back Dormitory Boys specialize in parody of Backstreet Boys.
ex.cn: 后舍男生擅长恶搞后街男孩。

k.syn: imitate, mock, mimic
k.en: to copy or exaggerate（someone or something）in order to make fun of

ex.en: parody a public figure's mannerisms
ex.cn: 模仿嘲弄一个公共人物的举止

m: PARODY contains PARA + ODE — a song mocking another song. — parody, a mocking imitation.

k.syn: burst, ebullition, eruption, explosion, flare, flash, flush, gush, outburst,
k.en: a sudden outburst of emotion or action

ex.en: a paroxysm of coughing
ex.cn: 突然一阵咳嗽

m: PAROXYSM contains PAROXY ≈ "sharp + spasm" (Greek PAROXYSMOS) — sudden sharp burst. — paroxysm, a sudden outburst.

k.en: work in the form of usually geometrically patterned wood laid or inlaid especially for floors

m: PARQUETRY contains PARQUET — wooden floor patterns. (French "little park" / enclosed space.) — parquetry, inlaid wood flooring.

k.syn: copy, ditto, duplicate, echo, quote
k.ant: coin, create, devise, invent 创造
k.en: to repeat or imitate, especially without understanding

ex.en: parrot others blindly
ex.cn: 盲目地人云亦云

m: PARROT — the bird famous for repeating; to parrot = mindlessly repeat. — parrot, to mimic mindlessly.

k.syn: avoid, dodge, elude, eschew, evade, finesse, scape, shirk, shun
k.ant: confront, face, meet 面对；embrace 欣然接受
k.en: to evade especially by an adroit answer

ex.en: He parried the embarrassing question with a clever reply.
ex.cn: 他以巧妙的回答躲避了这个令人尴尬的问题。

m: PARRY rhymes with CARRY — but in fencing, you PARRY (block) your opponent's blow. — parry, to deflect.

k.en: to resolve (as a sentence) into component parts of speech and describe

m: PARSE rhymes with PART — to break a sentence into PARTS. — parse, to analyze grammatically.

k.en: unusual or excessive frugality; extreme economy or stinginess

m: PARSIMONY contains PARSI (sparing, Latin PARCERE) — sparing with money. — parsimony, extreme thrift.

k.syn: biased, one-sided, partisan, prejudiced
k.ant: disinterested, equitable, evenhanded, fair, impartial, neutral, objective, unbiased, unprejudiced 公平的
k.en: inclined to favor one party more than the other: biased

ex.en: He is partial to Maverick.
ex.cn: 他偏爱小牛队。

k.syn: deficient, fragmental, fragmentary, half, halfway
k.ant: complete, entire, full, intact, integral, perfect, whole 完整的
k.der: partially adv. 部分地；partiality n. 偏袒，偏爱
k.en: lacking some necessary part

ex.en: a partial answer to the problem
ex.cn: 一部分的解答

m: PARTIAL contains PART — taking only one PART's side; or only PART of the whole. — partial, biased / incomplete.

k.syn: detail, specificate, specify
k.ant: abbreviate, abridge, condense, shorten 删减（内容等）
k.en: to go into or give details or particulars

ex.en: particularize the rules you must observe
ex.cn: 详述你需要遵守的规定

m: PARTICULAR + IZE — make particular, specify in detail. — particularize, to give details.

k.syn: acolyte, adherent, disciple, pupil, votary
k.ant: bellwether, leader 领导者
k.en: one who follows the opinions or teachings of another

ex.en: Partisans of the charismatic leader refuse to tolerate any criticism of him at all.
ex.cn: 跟随者们不允许任何有关于他们富有魅力的领导的负面言论。

k.syn: crusader, fanatic, ideologue, zealot, true believer
k.ant: adversary, antagonist, opponent 反对者
k.en: one who is intensely or excessively devoted to a cause

ex.en: a partisan of the revolution who was even willing to give her life for it
ex.cn: 一个愿意为了革命献出自己生命的坚定拥护者

k.syn: biased, one-sided, partial, prejudiced
k.ant: disinterested, equitable, evenhanded, fair, impartial, neutral, objective, unbiased, unprejudiced 公平的
k.en: inclined to favor one side over another

ex.en: a shamelessly partisan news report
ex.cn: 一篇无耻的、有倾向性的新闻报道

m: PARTISAN contains PART — taking one PART's side. — partisan, a strong supporter.

k.syn: bifurcation, breakup, cleavage, disunion, division, fractionalization, schism, scission, split, sundering
k.ant: unification, union 联合
k.en: the act or process of dividing something into parts

ex.en: the partition of Czechoslovakia into the Czech Republic and Slovakia
ex.cn: 捷克斯洛伐克被分裂成捷克共和国和斯洛伐克

m: PARTITION contains PART — divide into PARTS. — partition, to divide.

k.syn: burlesque, caricature, imitation, parody, spoof, travesty
k.ant: original work 原作
k.en: a literary, artistic, musical, or architectural work that imitates the style of previous work, often with satirical intent

ex.en: a pastiche of Botticelli's Birth of Venus
ex.cn: 波提且利画作《维纳斯的诞生》的仿品

k.syn: agglomerate, collage, hodgepodge, jumble, jungle, medley, montage, motley, salad, variety, welter
k.en: a pasticcio of incongruous parts; a hodgepodge

ex.en: a pastiche of dishes from many countries
ex.cn: 来自众多国家的饮食杂烩

m: PASTICHE rhymes with QUICHE — French for a pastry mix. (Italian PASTICCIO = a pie of mixed ingredients.) — pastiche, a hodgepodge imitation.

k.syn: apparent, blatant, conspicuous, evident, flagrant, manifest, obvious, plain
k.ant: concealed, hidden, invisible 隐藏的，不可见的
k.en: readily visible or intelligible: obvious

ex.en: Unfortunately, the patent flaw of the proposal did not deter the city council from putting it up for a vote.
ex.cn: 不幸的是，尽管提案中有着显而易见的缺陷，市议会依然将它拿出来进行投票。

m: PATENT contains PAT ≈ "plain to see" (Latin PATERE = lie open). (Same root as a patented invention being publicly disclosed.) — patent, plainly evident.

k.en: the study of the essential nature of diseases

m: PATHO (suffering/disease) + LOGY (study) — study of disease. — pathology, study of disease.

k.syn: façade, hull, skin, veneer
k.ant: essential quality 本质；core, kernel 核心
k.en: a superficial covering or exterior

ex.en: a superficial patina of knowledge
ex.cn: 表面肤浅的知识

k.syn: air, ambience, aroma, atmosphere, climate, flavor, halo, odor, smell, temper, vibration “不要把背GRE单词当成一种负担，要把它当成记忆的游戏、扩展视角的平台。” （宋歆怡，2008年10月，Verbal 700，录取院校：Newcastle University口译系）
k.en: an appearance or aura that is derived from association, habit, or established character

ex.en: Although the winery is brand-new, it has been constructed and decorated to give it a patina of old-world quaintness.
ex.cn: 尽管酿酒厂是全新建造的，但它的构造和装饰都刻意营造出一种古色古香的奇异氛围。

m: PATINA — the green PATINA on aged copper (Italian for "shallow plate"). — patina, an aged surface coating.

k.syn: aristocrat, noble, blue blood
k.ant: plebeian 平民，庶民
k.en: a man or woman of high birth or social position

ex.en: the rank of a patrician
ex.cn: 贵族等级

k.syn: aristocratic, genteel, gentle, grand, great, highborn, highbred, upper- class, wellborn
k.ant: baseborn, common, humble, ignoble, low, lower-class, mean 地位低下的
k.en: of high birth, rank, or station

ex.en: come from a patrician family
ex.cn: 出身贵族世家

m: PATRICIAN contains PATRI (father, Latin PATER) — descended from the Roman "fathers" (founding nobles). — patrician, an aristocrat.

k.syn: loyalist, nationalist, partisan
k.ant: apostate, recreant, renegade, traitor, turncoat 叛徒；insurgent, mutineer, rebel 叛乱者
k.en: one who loves his or her country and supports its authority and interests

ex.en: The USA Patriot Act has generated a great deal of controversy since its enactment.
ex.cn: 自实施之日起，美国的《爱国者法案》就产生了大量的争议。

ex.en: a fanatical patriot
ex.cn: 狂热的爱国者

m: PATRIOT contains PATRI (fatherland) — one who loves their fatherland. — patriot, a loyal citizen.

k.syn: advocate, back, champion, endorse, support, uphold
k.ant: baffle, foil, frustrate, sabotage 阻挠，从中破坏
k.en: to provide aid or support for

ex.en: a company that loyally patronizes the arts
ex.cn: 一个一直以来坚持赞助艺术界的公司

k.syn: condescend
k.cn: 以高人一等的态度对待
k.en: to adopt an air of condescension toward: treat haughtily or coolly

ex.en: a director with an unpleasant habit of patronizing even his most gifted actors
ex.cn: 一个有着令人讨厌的习惯的导演，他即便是对手下最有才华的演员也总是摆出傲慢的姿态

m: PATRONIZE contains PATRON — act like a high-status PATRON. (Same root: support OR look down on.) — patronize, to support / talk down to.

k.syn: dearth, deficit, drought, inadequacy, insufficiency, lack, scantiness, scarcity, shortage, undersupply, want
k.ant: abundance, adequacy, amplitude, opulence, plenitude, sufficiency, wealth 大量，丰富
k.en: smallness of number; dearth

ex.en: an extreme paucity of natural resources
ex.cn: 极度缺乏自然资源

m: PAUCITY contains PAU (few, Latin PAUCUS) — fewness. — paucity, scarcity.

k.syn: corpulent, fat, potbellied
k.ant: slender, slim, svelte, thin 瘦的，苗条的
k.en: having a potbelly

ex.en: a paunchy middle-aged man
ex.cn: 一个大腹便便的中年男子

m: PAUNCHY contains PAUNCH (belly) — having a big belly. — paunchy, big-bellied.

k.en: a small stone

m: PEBBLE rhymes with REBEL — but tiny: a small stone. — pebble, a small stone.

k.en: a small sin

m: PECCADILLO contains PECCA (sin, Latin PECCARE) — a small sin. (Same root as IMPECCABLE.) — peccadillo, a minor fault.

k.syn: abundance, bunch, bundle, dozen, mass, mountain, much, multiplicity, myriad, pile, plenitude, profusion, ton, volume, wealth
k.ant: bit, glimmer, handful, hint, little, mite, nip, ounce, peanuts, pittance, spot, sprinkle, trace 少量
k.en: a considerable amount

ex.en: Now you're in a peck of trouble.
ex.cn: 现在你有一堆麻烦了。

k.syn: bite, nibble, sip, tipple
k.ant: devour, gobble, gorge, guzzle, quaff, swill 贪婪地吃喝，大口吃喝
k.en: to eat reluctantly and in small bites

ex.en: Fashion models never really eat: they just peck at small meals in expensive restaurants.
ex.cn: 时装模特们几乎不吃东西——他们只是随便咬一口昂贵餐厅里的食物。

m: to PECK at food = take small reluctant bites like a bird PECKing. — peck, a small amount / to bite reluctantly.

k.syn: educator, instructor, preceptor, teacher
k.ant: disciple, pupil, student 弟子，学生
k.en: a person whose occupation is to give formal instruction in a school

ex.en: a boring pedagogue who is called“PPT reader”
ex.cn: 一个被戏称为“幻灯片朗读机”的无聊老师

m: PEDA (child) + GOGUE (leader) — child-leader, teacher. — pedagogue, a teacher.

k.syn: doctrinaire, dogmatist
k.en: one who pays undue attention to book learning and formal rules

ex.en: He is a perfect type of pedant.
ex.cn: 他是个十足的书呆子。

m: PEDANT contains PEDA (child) — once meant a teacher; now: someone who shows off learning. — pedant, a know-it-all.

k.en: an architectural support or base, as for a column or statue; a position of high regard

m: PEDESTAL contains PED (foot, Latin PES) — a stand under your feet (a statue's base). — pedestal, a base.

k.syn: footer, walker
k.ant: rider 骑手
k.en: a person traveling on foot

ex.en: a lane reserved for pedestrians
ex.cn: 行人专用道

k.syn: arid, dreary, dull, flat, jading, jejune, monochromatic, monotonous, stale, stodgy, tedious, wearisome
k.ant: absorbing, engaging, engrossing, gripping, interesting, intriguing, involving, riveting 吸引人的
k.cn: 平庸无奇的，令人厌倦的
k.en: causing weariness, restlessness, or lack of interest

ex.en: His style is so pedestrian that the book becomes a real bore.
ex.cn: 他的风格是如此平庸，整本书显得非常无聊。

m: PEDESTRIAN contains PED (foot) — one who goes on foot; figuratively, common/dull. — pedestrian, foot-traveler / dull.

k.en: a branch of medicine dealing with the development, care, and diseases of children

m: PED (child, Greek PAIS) + IATRICS (medicine, Greek IATROS = doctor) — children's medicine. — pediatrics, child medicine.

k.syn: gander, glance, glimpse, peek
k.en: a brief and sometimes furtive look

ex.en: take a peep at the new neighbors
ex.cn: 偷偷瞄了新邻居一眼

k.syn: mumble, murmur, mutter, twitter, whisper
k.ant: clamor, noise, outcry, roar 喧哗，吵闹
k.en: a slight sound or utterance

ex.en: I haven't heard a peep out of the children for an hour.
ex.cn: 我已经一个小时没听到孩子们的声音了。

m: PEEP — small bird sound; also a small look ("sneak a peep"). — peep, a small glance / sound.

k.syn: coequal, compeer, coordinate, counterpart, equivalent, fellow, match, parallel
k.ant: inferior 地位更低的人；superior 地位更高的人
k.der: peerless adj. 无与伦比的，不可比拟的
k.en: a person who has equal standing with others

ex.en: stand out among peers
ex.cn: 在同辈当中很出众

k.syn: blink, gape, gawk, gaze, goggle, rubberneck, stare
k.ant: glance, glimpse 投去一瞥
k.en: to look narrowly or curiously

ex.en: peer at the variety of marine life in the aquarium's huge tank
ex.cn: 好奇地注视着巨大水族箱中丰富多样的海洋生物

m: PEER rhymes with NEAR — peers stand near you (equals); also to peer = look closely. — peer, an equal / to look closely.

k.syn: aggravate, annoy, bother, exasperate, frost, gall, irk, nettle, pique, rile, ruffle, vex
k.ant: becalm, compose, lull, lullaby, quiet, salve, settle, soothe, still, tranquilize 使镇定
k.der: peevish adj. 易怒的，脾气不好的
k.en: to disturb the peace of mind of（someone） especially by repeated disagreeable acts

ex.en: be constantly peeved by his affected flattery
ex.cn: 总是被他做作的谄媚所惹恼

m: PEEVE rhymes with PEEVED — what makes you PEEVED. — peeve, to annoy.

k.syn: belittling, contemptuous, degrading, deprecatory, depreciative, derogative, disdainful, disparaging, scornful
k.ant: commendatory, complimentary, laudatory 赞美的
k.en: disparaging; belittling

ex.en: I agree that I am ambitious, and I don't see it as a pejorative term.
ex.cn: 我承认我“雄心勃勃”，但我并不认为这是一个贬义词。

m: PEJORATIVE contains PEJOR (worse, Latin PEJOR) — making something worse / disparaging. — pejorative, derogatory.

k.en: wealth

m: PELF rhymes with SELF — wealth (often dirty money) for one's own SELF. (Old French PELFRE = booty.) — pelf, money / riches.

k.syn: crystal, crystalline, limpid, liquid, lucent, transparent
k.ant: opaque 不透明的
k.en: admitting the passage of light

ex.en: pellucid spring water
ex.cn: 清澈透明的泉水

k.syn: apparent, distinct, evident, lucid, manifest, obvious, palpable, patent, perspicuous, plain, straightforward
k.ant: clouded, cryptic, enigmatic, indistinct, mysterious, obfuscated, obscure 模糊难懂的
k.en: transparently clear in style or meaning

ex.en: Her poetry has a pellucid simplicity that betrays none of the sweat that went into writing it.
ex.cn: 她的诗歌风格十分清新简约，丝毫没有体现出她为此付出的汗水。

m: PELLUCID contains LUC (light, Latin LUX) — light shines through. (Same root as LUCID.) — pellucid, transparently clear.

k.syn: bastille, coop, jail, prison, stockade
k.en: a place of confinement for persons held in lawful custody

ex.en: He spent six years in a federal pen.
ex.cn: 他在联邦监狱中度过了六年。

k.syn: box, encage, encase, envelop, fence, hedge, immure, include, wall
k.ant: release 释放；enfranchise, free, liberate, set free 使自由
k.en: to close or shut in by or as if by barriers

ex.en: Remember to pen up the dogs when visitors come over.
ex.cn: 客人来的时候别忘了把狗给关起来。

m: to PEN someone up = put them in a PEN (enclosure). — pen, to confine.

k.syn: fine, forfeit, punishment, retribution
k.ant: honor, reward 奖励
k.en: a punishment established by law or authority for a crime or offense

ex.en: The maximum penalty is 7 years' imprisonment.
ex.cn: 最为严厉的惩罚是七年的有期徒刑。

m: PENALTY contains PEN (punishment, Latin POENA) — punishment. (Same root as PUNISH.) — penalty, a punishment.

k.syn: affection, bias, disposition, leaning, partiality, predilection, predisposition, proclivity, propensity, tendency
k.ant: aversion, disfavor, disinclination, dislike, distaste, loathing, repugnance, repulsion 反感
k.en: a strong and continued inclination

ex.en: a penchant for Champaign
ex.cn: 非常喜爱香槟

m: ≈ PENDANT — someone who always wears a specific pendant has a penchant (strong liking) for jewelry. — penchant, a strong inclination.

k.syn: apologetic, compunctious, regretful, remorseful, repentant, rueful, sorry
k.ant: impenitent, remorseless, unapologetic, unrepentant 不知悔改的
k.en: feeling or expressing humble or regretful pain or sorrow for sins or offenses

ex.en: a penitent businessman who had come to ask for forgiveness
ex.cn: 一个来寻求宽恕的决定悔过自新的商人

m: PENITENT contains PEN (punishment) — feeling self-punishing remorse. (A penitentiary is where penitents go.) — penitent, repentant.

k.syn: broody, cogitative, meditative, melancholy, musing, reflective, ruminant, ruminative, thoughtful
k.ant: mindless, thoughtless, unreflective 不加思考的
k.en: given to or marked by long, quiet and often musingly sad thinking

ex.en: Rainy days often put her in a pensive mood.
ex.cn: 雨天总是让她陷入深深的思考之中。

m: PENSIVE contains PENS (weigh/think, Latin PENSARE) — weighing thoughts. (Same root as PENSION = weighed-out money.) — pensive, deeply thoughtful.

k.syn: destitution, impecuniosity, impoverishment, indigence, neediness, poorness, poverty, want
k.ant: affluence, opulence, richness, wealth 富裕
k.en: extreme dearth; barrenness or insufficiency

ex.en: Some have pessimistically regarded the higher education in China as an invisible and irresistible path to penury.
ex.cn: 有人悲观地把中国的高等教育看作一条通往贫穷的通道——无形但不可抗拒。

k.syn: closeness, miserliness, niggardliness, parsimony, penuriousness, stinginess, tightfistedness, tightness
k.ant: generosity, largesse, munificence, openhandedness, philanthropy 慷慨的
k.der: penurious adj. 贫穷的；吝啬的
k.en: extreme and often niggardly frugality

ex.en: They bitterly complained about their father's penury.
ex.cn: 他们哀怨地抱怨父亲的节俭。

m: PENURY rhymes with TENURE but the opposite — instead of secure salary, extreme poverty. — penury, extreme poverty.

k.syn: amble, cross, go, navigate, pass, peregrinate, proceed, ramble, transit, travel, traverse
k.en: to travel over or through especially on foot for exercise or pleasure

ex.en: We decided to lazily perambulate the entire length of the esplanade and enjoy the fresh air.
ex.cn: 我们决定悠哉地徒步走过滨海大道，享受清新的空气。

m: PER (through) + AMBUL (walk, Latin) + ATE — walk through. (Same AMBUL- as AMBULANCE.) — perambulate, to walk around.

k.syn: appreciable, apprehensible, detectable, discernible, distinguishable, palpable, sensible
k.ant: impalpable, imperceptible, inappreciable, indistinguishable, insensible, undetectable 不能感知的
k.en: capable of being perceived especially by the senses

ex.en: You should be able to note a perceptible temperature change when you add the second reagents.
ex.cn: 当你加入第二种试剂的时候，你应该能够察觉到明显的温度变化。

m: PERCEPTIBLE contains PERCEIVE — able to be perceived. — perceptible, noticeable.

k.syn: compulsory, forced, imperative, incumbent, involuntary, obligatory, required
k.ant: elective, optional, voluntary 可选择的
k.en: not allowing contradiction or refusal; imperative

ex.en: a peremptory order from the general
ex.cn: 将军下达的不容反抗的命令

k.syn: authoritarian, despotic, dictatorial, domineering, imperious, masterful, overbearing, tyrannical, tyrannous
k.en: fond of ordering people around

ex.en: The peremptory secretary began telling the crowd of reporters and photographers exactly where they had to stand.
ex.cn: 爱发号施令的秘书开始部署记者和摄影师们应该站在哪里了。

k.syn: assumptive, bumptious, haughty, imperious, lofty, pompous, presumptuous, pretentious, supercilious
k.ant: humble, lowly, modest 谦逊的
k.en: having a feeling of superiority that shows itself in an overbearing attitude

ex.en: She had such a peremptory approach to running the club that people started to avoid her.
ex.cn: 她用一种如此傲慢自大的方式来管理俱乐部，令大家纷纷开始躲避她。

m: PEREMPTORY contains PER + EMPT (take, Latin EMERE) — taking control entirely, allowing no refusal. — peremptory, allowing no refusal.

k.syn: ardent, demonstrative, emotional, fervid, feverish, impassioned, passionate, torrid, vehement, warm
k.ant: cold, cool, detached, dispassionate, emotionless, impassive, unemotional 冷漠的
k.en: marked by overwrought or exaggerated emotion: excessively fervent

ex.en: perfervid love letter
ex.cn: 热情洋溢的情书

m: PER (intensifier) + FERVID — extremely fervid (passionate). — perfervid, extremely passionate.

k.syn: backstabbing, disloyalty, infidelity, sellout, treachery, unfaithfulness, double cross
k.ant: allegiance, devotion, faithfulness, fidelity, loyalty, staunchness, steadfastness 忠诚
k.en: an act or an instance of disloyalty

ex.en: As loyalty unites lovers, so perfidy estranges friends.
ex.cn: 忠诚是爱情的纽带，欺诈是友谊的敌人。

m: PERFIDY contains PER + FID (faith, Latin FIDES) — "through faith" → faithlessness. (Compare FIDELITY = faithfulness.) — perfidy, treachery.

k.syn: bore, drill, hole, pierce, punch, puncture, riddle
k.en: to make a hole through

ex.en: perforate with a pin
ex.cn: 用针穿孔

m: PERFORATE contains PER (through) + FORATE (bore, Latin FORARE) — bore through with holes. — perforate, to pierce with holes.

k.syn: automatic, cursory, mechanical, superficial
k.en: characterized by routine or superficiality and often done merely as a duty

ex.en: His colleague gave his usual perfunctory nod.
ex.cn: 他的同事例行公事地向他随便点了个头。

k.syn: apathetic, casual, disinterested, incurious, indifferent, insouciant, nonchalant, pococurante, unconcerned
k.ant: concerned, interested 感兴趣的
k.en: lacking in interest or enthusiasm

ex.en: She gave the list only a perfunctory glance.
ex.cn: 她无所谓地随便瞅了名单一眼。

m: PERFUNCTORY contains FUNCT (do, Latin FUNGI) — going through the motions to be done with it. — perfunctory, done routinely without care.

k.syn: borderline, bound, boundary, circumference, confines, edge, frame, fringe, margin, periphery, skirt, verge
k.ant: center, core, heart, kernel 核心
k.en: the line or relatively narrow space that marks the outer limit of something

ex.en: soldiers guarding the perimeter of the camp
ex.cn: 守卫着营地边界的士兵

m: PERI (around, Greek) + METER (measure) — measure around. — perimeter, the boundary.

k.syn: book, magazine, newspaper, paper, review, serial
k.en: a publication that appears at regular intervals

ex.en: subscribe to three new periodicals
ex.cn: 订阅三份新的期刊

k.syn: continual, periodic, recurrent, recurring
k.ant: constant, continuous, incessant, unceasing 持续不断的
k.cn: 周期性的，有固定间隔的
k.en: occurring or recurring at regular intervals

ex.en: periodical announcements from airline personnel concerning the delay
ex.cn: 航空公司工作人员关于延误所进行的一遍又一遍的广播

m: PERIODICAL contains PERIOD — at regular periods. — periodical, recurring at intervals.

k.syn: ambulant, errant, nomadic, perambulatory, peregrine, vagabond, wandering
k.ant: sedentary, settled 定居的
k.en: walking about or from place to place

ex.en: peripatetic preachers
ex.cn: 四处游历的传教士

m: PERI (around, Greek) + PATETIC (walking, Greek PATEIN) — walking around (Aristotle taught while walking). — peripatetic, traveling around.

k.syn: circumferential, marginal
k.ant: central 中心的；interior 内部的
k.en: related to, located in, or constituting an outer boundary or periphery

ex.en: development in the peripheral areas of megacities
ex.cn: 大城市边缘地区的发展

k.syn: accessorial, accessory, appurtenant, supplemental, supplementary
k.ant: chief, main, principal 主要的
k.en: available to supply something extra when needed

ex.en: The IT consultant suggested that we update the drivers for all of the computer's peripheral devices. IT
ex.cn: 咨询师建议我们更新电脑所有外围辅助设备的驱动程序。

m: PERIPHERAL contains PERI (around) — on the periphery, around the edge. — peripheral, on the outskirts.

k.syn: decease, demise, die, end, expire, succumb, pass away
k.ant: breathe, live, survive 活着，存活
k.en: to become destroyed or ruined: cease to exist

ex.en: Thrive in calamity and perish in soft living.
ex.cn: 生于忧患，死于安乐。

ex.en: adapt or perish
ex.cn: 适应或是消亡

m: PERISH contains PER + ISH ≈ "per-finish" (Latin PERIRE = pass away). — perish, to die.

k.en: the deliberate, willful giving of false, misleading, or incomplete testimony

m: PERJURY contains PER (against) + JUR (oath, Latin JURARE) — falsely against an oath. — perjury, lying under oath.

k.en: the quality or state of continuing or enduring without fundamental or marked

m: from PERMANENT — lasting state. — permanence, lasting stability.

k.syn: passable, penetrable, pervious, porous
k.ant: waterproof 防水的；impassable, impenetrable, impervious, impermeable 不能渗透的
k.en: capable of being permeated or penetrated, especially by liquids or gases

ex.en: a permeable fabric that allows your body heat to escape
ex.cn: 一种能使身体热量耗散的透气纤维

m: PER (through) + ME (go, Latin MEARE) — able to go through. — permeable, able to be passed through.

k.syn: adverse, baleful, damaging, deleterious, detrimental, hurtful, injurious, mischievous, nocuous, noxious
k.ant: anodyne, benign, harmless, innocent, innocuous, inoffensive, safe 温和的，无害的
k.en: highly injurious or destructive

ex.en: Business may be troublesome but idleness is pernicious.
ex.cn: 工作烦人，懒散害人。

m: PERNICIOUS contains PER + NIC (harm, Latin NEX = death) — thoroughly harmful. (Same root as NOXIOUS.) — pernicious, harmful.

k.syn: coda, conclusion, denouement, epilogue, finale
k.ant: foreword, prelude, preface, prologue, overture 前言，序曲
k.en: the concluding part of a discourse and especially an oration

ex.en: He summarized his main points in his peroration.
ex.cn: 他在结束语中总结了自己的主要观点。

k.syn: address, declamation, harangue, oration, speech, talk
k.en: a usually formal discourse delivered to an audience

ex.en: a peroration celebrating the nation's long tradition of religious tolerance and pluralism
ex.cn: 为庆祝该国长期以来的宗教包容力和宗教多元化所做的一个演讲

m: PER (intensifier) + ORATION — the conclusion of a long oration (final flourish). — peroration, a closing speech section.

k.syn: ceaseless, dateless, deathless, endless, eternal, immortal, undying, unending
k.ant: ephemeral, evanescent, transient, transitory 稍纵即逝的； provisional, temporary 暂时性的
k.en: continuing forever: everlasting

ex.en: perpetual motion machine
ex.cn: 永动机

m: PER (through) + PET (seek, Latin PETERE) — seeking through forever. — perpetual, never-ending.

k.syn: agonize, anguish, curse, excruciate, harrow, plague, rack, torment, torture
k.en: to cause persistent suffering to

ex.en: people who were persecuted simply for practicing their religious faith
ex.cn: 因为实践宗教信仰而惨遭迫害的人

m: PER (thoroughly) + SECUTE (follow, Latin SEQUI) — relentlessly hunt down. (Same root as PROSECUTE, SEQUEL.) — persecute, to oppress.

k.syn: carry on, endure, persist
k.ant: renounce, succumb, surrender, yield 放弃，屈服
k.en: to persist in or remain constant to a purpose, idea, or task in the face of obstacles or discouragement

ex.en: Although he was frustrated by the lack of financial resources and support, he persevered in his scientific research.
ex.cn: 尽管因为资金不足而受阻，但他仍坚持进行科学研究。

m: PERSEVERE contains PER + SEVERE — keep going through severity. — persevere, to persist through hardship.

k.syn: backchat, badinage, chaff, jesting, joshing, raillery, repartee
k.en: good-natured teasing or exchanging of clever remarks

ex.en: Their tongue-in-cheek persiflage is sometimes mistaken for an exchange of insults by people who don't know them.
ex.cn: 他们半开玩笑的打趣有时候被不认识他们的人误认为是在互相对骂。

m: PERSIFLAGE — French for light banter. (PER + SIFLER = whistle through.) — persiflage, light banter.

k.syn: ceaselessness, continuance, continuity, durability, endurance, subsistence
k.ant: cessation, close, end, expiration, finish, stoppage, surcease, termination 停止
k.en: uninterrupted or lasting existence

ex.en: The persistence of the fever for a week caused me great worry.
ex.cn: 持续一周的发烧让我很不安。

m: from PERSIST — continuous effort. — persistence, steady continuation.

k.syn: alluring, appealing, attractive, captivating, charismatic, charming, comely, enchanting, handsome, pretty
k.ant: grotesque, hideous, ugly 丑陋的；homely 相貌平庸的
k.en: pleasing in personality or appearance; attractive

ex.en: apparently be attracted by a personable young man
ex.cn: 明显被一位风度翩翩的少年迷住了

m: PERSONABLE contains PERSON — pleasant in person. — personable, charmingly pleasant.

k.syn: astute, savvy, sharp, sharp-witted, shrewd, smart
k.ant: ignorant, oblivious, unknowing 未察觉的，无知的
k.en: having or showing penetrating mental discernment; clear-sighted

ex.en: an impartial and perspicacious judge
ex.cn: 一个明镜高悬（公正而极具洞察力）的法官

m: PER (through) + SPIC (look, Latin SPECERE) — able to see through. (Same root as SPECTACLE.) — perspicacious, sharply insightful.

k.syn: clarity, clearness, explicitness, lucidity, lucidness, perspicuousness
k.ant: obscureness, obscurity, unclarity 模糊不清；ambiguity 模棱两可
k.en: the quality of being perspicuous; clearness and lucidity

ex.en: The key of modern enterprise system is the prespicuity of property right.
ex.cn: 现代企业制度的核心是产权的明晰。

m: PERSPICUITY shares SPIC- with PERSPICACIOUS — clarity that lets you see through. — perspicuity, clarity of expression.

k.syn: appertain, bear, concern, refer, relate
k.en: to have a relation or connection; relate

ex.en: new evidence that pertains to the accident
ex.cn: 与事故相关的新证据

m: PER (thoroughly) + TAIN (hold, Latin TENERE) — to hold a connection to. — pertain, to be connected.

k.syn: adamant, headstrong, implacable, inflexible, intransigent, mulish, obdurate, perverse, stubborn, willful
k.ant: compliant, flexible, pliable, pliant, yielding 容易受影响的，容易控制的
k.en: sticking to an opinion, purpose, or course of action in spite of reason, arguments, or persuasion

ex.en: The pertinacious boy won't stop crying unless his desire is satisfied.
ex.cn: 这个固执的男孩在愿望没有满足的情况下是不会停止哭泣的。

m: PERTINACIOUS contains PER + TIN (hold, Latin) — holding firmly through everything. — pertinacious, stubbornly persistent.

k.syn: applicable, apposite, apropos, germane, material, pointed, relative, relevant
k.ant: extraneous, immaterial, impertinent, inapplicable, inapposite, irrelevant, pointless 无关的
k.en: having a clear decisive relevance to the matter in hand

ex.en: He impressed the jury with his concise, pertinent answers to the attorney's questions.
ex.cn: 他对律师提出的问题给予了简洁而恰当的回答，这给陪审团留下了深刻的印象。

m: PERTINENT contains PERTAIN — related, on point. — pertinent, relevant.

k.syn: agitate, ail, bother, discomfort, discompose, distress, unsettle, upset
k.ant: calm, compose, quiet, settle, soothe, tranquilize使镇定
k.en: to trouble the mind of; to make uneasy

ex.en: The naughty boy perturbed me enough to keep me awake that night.
ex.cn: 那个捣蛋的男孩让我烦躁到当晚失眠。

m: PER + TURB (disturb, Latin TURBARE) — thoroughly stir up. — perturb, to disturb.

k.syn: examine, scrutinize
k.ant: glance, glimpse, scan, skim 一瞥，迅速浏览
k.en: to read or examine, typically with great care

ex.en: peruse the manuscript to check for grammatical errors
ex.cn: 细读手稿以检查语法错误

m: PERUSE contains PER (thoroughly) + USE — thoroughly examine. — peruse, to read carefully.

k.syn: interpenetrate, percolate, riddle, suffuse, transfuse
k.en: to be present throughout; permeate

ex.en: The mixed smell of sawdust and glue pervaded the whole factory.
ex.cn: 锯末与胶水的味道弥漫了整个工厂。the corruption that pervades every stratum of society 充斥在社会每个阶层中的腐败

m: PER (through) + VADE (go, Latin VADERE) — go through everything. (Same root as INVADE.) — pervade, to spread throughout.

k.en: an inclination to emphasize adverse aspects, conditions, and possibilities or to expect

m: PESSIMISM contains PESS (worst, Latin PESSIMUS) — expecting the worst. — pessimism, gloomy outlook.

k.en: a chemical used to kill pests, especially insects

m: PEST + ICIDE (kill, Latin -CIDA) — pest-killer. — pesticide, insect-killer.

k.en: a club-shaped, hand-held tool for grinding or mashing substances in a mortar

m: PESTLE rhymes with WRESTLE — wrestle the herbs in the mortar with a PESTLE. — pestle, a grinding stick.

k.syn: appeal, cry, entreaty, pleading, solicitation, supplication
k.cn: 请愿，正式的申请；申请书
k.en: a solemn supplication or request to a superior authority; an entreaty

ex.en: a petition for divorce
ex.cn: 离婚申请书

k.syn: adj.re, beseech, conjure, entreat, impetrate, implore, importune, plead, solicit, supplicate
k.der: petitioner n. 申请人，请愿者
k.en: to make a request, especially a formal written one

ex.en: She is petitioning to regain custody of the child.
ex.cn: 为了重新获得孩子的监护权，她提出了正式申请。

m: PETITION contains PET (seek, Latin PETERE) — seeking formally. (Same root as APPETITE = strong seek.) — petition, a formal request.

k.syn: damp, dampen, deaden, devitalize, enervate, lobotomize, ossify
k.ant: brace, energize, enliven, invigorate, quicken, stimulate, vitalize, vivify 使有活力
k.en: to cause to become stiff or stone- like; deaden

ex.en: Pressure from family has petrified his once innovative mind.
ex.cn: 家庭的压力使他的头脑失去了往日的创造力。

m: PETRI (rock, Greek PETRA) + FY — turn to stone. — petrify, to harden / shock immobile.

k.en: a carving or inscription on a rock, especially a prehistoric one

m: PETRO (rock) + GLYPH (carving) — rock carving. — petroglyph, a rock engraving.

k.syn: inconsequential, inconsiderable, insignificant, measly, minute, paltry, peanut, slight, trifling, trivial
k.ant: consequential, considerable, important, material, momentous, significant 重大的，重要的
k.en: of small importance; trivial

ex.en: play petty tricks
ex.cn: 耍小聪明

m: PETTY contains PET ≈ French PETIT (small). — petty, trivially small.

k.syn: especial, exceptional, extraordinary, peculiar, preternatural, rare, singular, uncommon, unusual
k.ant: common, normal, ordinary, typical 普通的，典型的
k.en: being out of the ordinary; extraordinary; outstanding

ex.en: the phenomenal growth that the suburb has experienced over the last decade
ex.cn: 过去十年间郊区所经历的令人称奇的快速发展

m: PHENOMENAL contains PHENOMENON — extraordinarily noteworthy. — phenomenal, extraordinary.

k.syn: altruistic, beneficent, benevolent, eleemosynary, good, humanitarian
k.ant: misanthropic 反人类的；selfish 自私的
k.en: having or showing a concern for the welfare of others

ex.en: the philanthropic aims of the organization
ex.cn: 这个机构的博爱、利他的目标

m: PHIL (love, Greek) + ANTHROP (human, Greek) — loving humans. — philanthropic, charitable.

k.en: one who collects or studies

m: PHIL (love) + ATEL (free of tax, Greek) — lover of stamps (which exempt the recipient from postage). — philatelist, a stamp collector.

k.syn: affectless, apathetic, cold-blooded, emotionless, impassible, numb, stoic, stolid
k.ant: demonstrative, emotional, fervent, fervid, impassioned, passionate, vehement 感情丰富的，热情的
k.cn: 冷静的，无感情的，淡漠的
k.en: having or suggesting a calm, sluggish temperament; unemotional

ex.en: a strangely phlegmatic response to what should have been happy news
ex.cn: 对本该是一个让人开心的新闻的冷漠回应

m: PHLEGMATIC contains PHLEGM — one of the four humors associated with calmness. — phlegmatic, calmly unemotional.

k.ant: consequential, considerable, important, material, momentous, significant 重大的，重要的
k.syn: inconsequential, inconsiderable, insignificant, measly, minute, paltry, peanut, slight, trifling, trivial
k.en: so trifling or trivial as to be beneath one's consideration

ex.en: raise a piddling objection to the plan
ex.cn: 对计划提出一个微不足道的

m: PIDDLING contains PIDDLE — trivial like a child's piddly play. — piddling, trivial.

k.syn: blotched, dappled, marbled, mottled, splotched, spotted
k.ant: monochromatic 单色的
k.en: of different colors

ex.en: a piebald horse
ex.cn: 一匹杂色马

k.syn: assorted, eclectic, heterogeneous, hybrid, magpie, mixed, motley, promiscuous, varied
k.ant: homogeneous 由相同类型事物组成的
k.en: consisting of many things of different sorts

ex.en: his piebald ethnic background
ex.cn: 他身为混血儿的背景

m: PIE + BALD — like a magPIE: black-and-white patches. — piebald, having patches of two colors.

k.syn: colorant, dye, stain
k.en: a substance that imparts black or white or a color to other materials

ex.en: natural red pigment
ex.cn: 天然红色染料

k.syn: paint, stain, tincture, tinge, tint
k.ant: blanch 漂白；decolorize 使脱色
k.en: to color with or as if with pigment

ex.en: pigmented silk
ex.cn: 染过色的丝绸

m: PIGMENT contains PIG ≈ "PAINT" (Latin PIGMENTUM = paint). — pigment, coloring matter.

k.syn: appropriate, filch, hook, lift, pinch, purloin, steal, thieve
k.en: to steal stealthily in small amounts

ex.en: pilfer the commercial document
ex.cn: 偷窃商业文件

m: PILFER rhymes with FILTER — sneaking small bits out as if filtered. — pilfer, to steal small amounts.

k.en: a firm upright support for a structure, or a person who is a mainstay of a community

m: PILLAR rhymes with KILLER — a tall column. (Same Latin root PILA = column.) — pillar, a vertical column.

k.syn: abase, debase, degrade, denounce, humiliate, ridicule
k.ant: carol, eulogize, exalt, extol, laud 赞美
k.en: to expose to public contempt, ridicule, or scorn

ex.en: The candidate mercilessly pilloried his opponent.
ex.cn: 候选人在大众面前无情地羞辱他的对手。

ex.en: He resigned after being pilloried by the press.
ex.cn: 在遭到媒体嘲弄之后他愤然辞职。

m: PILLORY — the wooden stocks where criminals were locked for public mockery. — pillory, to publicly mock.

k.syn: aviator, bellwether, captain, guide, helmsman, leader, navigator
k.en: one employed to steer a ship

ex.en: a jet pilot
ex.cn: 喷气式飞机飞行员

k.syn: developmental, experimental, model, test, trial
k.ant: eventual, final, ultimate 最终的
k.en: serving as a tentative model for future experiment or development

ex.en: They carried out a pilot study before the larger, more expensive project was started.
ex.cn: 在更大规模、更为昂贵的项目开始之前，他们进行了初步研究。

k.syn: conduct, direct, guide, lead, route, show, steer
k.ant: follow 跟随
k.en: lead or conduct over a usually difficult course

ex.en: The lobbyists piloted the bill through the Senate.
ex.cn: 游说集团使法案得到参议院的批准。

m: a PILOT episode is a small TEST flight before the real series. — pilot, trial / leading.

k.syn: ache, crave, dream, hunger, itch, long, lust, repine, thirst, yearn
k.ant: abhor, detest, loathe 厌恶，反感
k.en: to yearn intensely and persistently especially for something unattainable

ex.en: pine for a lost love
ex.cn: 渴望那段逝去的爱情

k.syn: decline, dwindle, fade, flag, languish, waste
k.en: to lose vigor, health, or flesh（as through grief）

ex.en: Separated by their families, the lovers pined away.
ex.cn: 这对恋人因为被家人拆散而日益憔悴。

m: PINE rhymes with WINE — but you PINE for someone, slowly wasting away (compare "pine away"). — pine, to long for.

k.syn: acme, apex, climax, culmination, meridian, peak, summit, top, zenith
k.ant: bottom, nadir 最低点
k.en: the highest point of development or achievement

ex.en: a singer who has reached the pinnacle of career
ex.cn: 已到达事业巅峰的歌手

m: PINNACLE contains PINN ≈ "point" (Latin PINNA = peak/wing) — the high point. — pinnacle, the peak.

k.syn: accurate, close, delicate, exact, fine, hairline, mathematical, precise, refined, rigorous
k.ant: coarse, cursory, rough 粗略的；imprecise, inaccurate, inexact 不准确的
k.en: located, fixed, or directed with extreme precision

ex.en: The commander demanded the pinpoint location of artillery fire.
ex.cn: 指挥官需要火炮攻击的精确位置。

k.syn: determine, distinguish, locate, identify, recognize, spot
k.ant: estimate 估计
k.en: to locate, fix, determine, or identify with precision

ex.en: pinpoint the target by tracking calls from his cellphone
ex.cn: 通过跟踪手机信号精确定位目标

ex.en: pinpoint the cause of failure
ex.cn: 准确找出失败的原因

m: PIN + POINT — at the exact POINT of a PIN, very precise. — pinpoint, to locate exactly.

k.syn: peppery, poignant, salty, savory, spicy, zesty
k.ant: insipid, vapid, zestless 索然无味的
k.en: pleasantly pungent or tart in taste

ex.en: a piquant sauce
ex.cn: 开胃的酱汁

k.syn: exciting, pungent, racy, stimulating
k.ant: flat, pallid 单调的，无聊的
k.en: appealingly provocative

ex.en: a piquant glance
ex.cn: 充满挑逗的一瞥

m: PIQUANT contains PIQU ≈ "prick" (French PIQUER = sting) — pleasantly sharp in flavor. — piquant, pleasantly spicy.

k.syn: aggravate, annoy, exasperate, infuriate, irritate, peeve, provoke, rile, roil, ruffle, vex
k.ant: delight, gratify, rejoice 使高兴
k.en: to arouse anger or resentment in

ex.en: She was greatly piqued when they refused her invitation.
ex.cn: 他们拒绝她的邀请让她很生气。

k.syn: arouse, encourage, excite, fire, impassion, incite, instigate, move, stimulate, stir
k.ant: allay, alleviate, assuage, ease, mitigate, mollify, palliate, relieve, soothe 平息，缓和
k.en: to excite or arouse especially by a provocation, challenge, or rebuff

ex.en: radical remarks that pique their curiosity
ex.cn: 引起他们兴趣的激进评论

m: PIQUE shares PIQU- with PIQUANT — a stinging irritation. (Same root.) — pique, to provoke / irritate.

k.syn: arrogate, commandeer, convert, expropriate, preempt, press, seize,
k.en: to take or make use of under a guise of authority but without actual right

ex.en: a pirated version of the software
ex.cn: 盗版软件

m: PIRATE — sea robbers; to pirate software = steal it. — pirate, to steal / copy illicitly.

k.en: the distinctive quality of a sound, dependent primarily on the frequency

m: PITCH — the high or low position of a tone. (Cousin of PIKE-piked-up sound.) — pitch, the highness of a sound.

k.syn: apothegmatic, brief, capsule, compact, compendious, concise, laconic, succinct, telegraphic, terse
k.ant: circuitous, circumlocutory, diffuse, rambling, prolix, verbose, windy, wordy 冗长累赘的
k.en: precisely meaningful; forceful and brief

ex.en: The professor gave a pithy introduction to this course.
ex.cn: 教授就这门课程做了简短的介绍。

m: PITHY contains PITH (the inner core of a stem) — concentrated to its core. — pithy, concise and meaningful.

k.syn: affectless, callous, coldhearted, cruel, harsh, indurate, merciless
k.ant: charitable, clement, compassionate, humane, merciful, sympathetic, tender 有同情心的，仁慈的
k.en: devoid of or unmoved by pity

ex.en: a pitiless humiliation
ex.cn: 毫不留情的羞辱

m: PITY + LESS — without pity. — pitiless, without mercy.

k.syn: bit, mite, modicum, peanuts, trace
k.ant: cornucopia, opulence 富饶；boodle, bundle, fortune 大笔财富
k.en: a small portion, amount, or allowance

ex.en: The internship offers only a pittance for a salary, but it is a great opportunity to gain experience.
ex.cn: 实习所能挣到的报酬是很少的，但是它提供了获得经验的绝佳机会。

m: PITTANCE contains PIT ≈ "piteous" — a pitiful small allowance. — pittance, a meager amount.
