k.cn: （显示器或电视机图像的）象素
k.en: the basic unit of the composition of an image

m: PIXEL = PIX (picture) + EL (element) — a picture element on a screen. — pixel, a picture-element dot.

k.syn: appease, assuage, conciliate, mollify, pacify, propitiate, tranquilize
k.ant: anger, enrage, foment, gall, incense, inflame, infuriate, peeve, rile 激怒，使气恼
k.en: to lessen the anger or agitation of

ex.en: The colonists implemented a new policy to placate local opposition.
ex.cn: 殖民者采取了一项新政策以缓和当地的反抗情绪。

m: PLACATE contains PLAC (please, Latin PLACARE) — please / soothe. (Same root as IMPLACABLE.) — placate, to soothe.

k.en: something of no intrinsic remedial value that is used to appease or reassure another

ex.en: Candies are often adopted as the placebo for the dying patients.
ex.cn: 糖果通常作为安慰剂给重症患者服用。

m: PLACEBO — Latin for "I shall please" — given to please rather than treat. — placebo, a sham comfort treatment.

k.syn: steal
k.en: to steal and pass off（the ideas or words of another）as one's own

ex.en: In scientific community, plagiarizing other's paper is a felony.
ex.cn: 在学术界，剽窃他人的论文是一项重罪。

m: PLAGIARIZE comes from Latin PLAGIARIUS = kidnapper (kidnap someone's words). — plagiarize, to copy others' work.

k.syn: blaring, blasting, clamorous, deafening, earsplitting, resonant, resounding, sonorous, thunderous, vibrant
k.ant: muffled, muted 声音被减弱的；gentle, low, soft 声音轻柔的
k.en: having a loud reverberating sound

ex.en: The camera man was stunned by the plangent roar of wild animals.
ex.cn: 摄影师被野生动物发出的怒号吓得不知所措。

k.syn: doleful, dolorous, funeral, grieving, lamentable, lugubrious, plaintive, rueful, sorrowful, woeful
k.ant: agreeable, cheerful, delightful, enjoyable, jolly, pleasing 令人愉快的
k.en: having an expressive and especially plaintive quality

ex.en: a plangent song about a long-ago love
ex.cn: 一首关于往日爱情的凄凉歌曲

m: PLANGENT contains PLANG ≈ "plaintive bell ring" (Latin PLANGERE = beat/lament). — plangent, resounding mournfully.

k.en: a place that is planted or under

m: PLANTATION contains PLANT — a place where crops are PLANTed at scale. — plantation, a large agricultural estate.

k.en: capacity for being molded

m: from PLASTIC — quality of being moldable. — plasticity, ability to be shaped.

k.syn: highland, mesa, tableland, upland
k.ant: lowland 低谷；basin 盆地
k.en: a usually extensive level land area raised sharply above adjacent land on at least one side

ex.en: Qinghai-Tibetan Plateau
ex.cn: 青藏高原

k.syn: equilibrium, stage, stasis
k.en: a relatively stable level, period, or state

ex.en: reach a plateau of development
ex.cn: 达到发展的稳定期

m: PLATEAU contains PLAT (flat, French) — flat tableland; figuratively, a flat period. — plateau, a level period / tableland.

k.syn: banality, bromide, cliché, commonplace, homily, shibboleth, truism
k.en: a trite or banal remark or statement, especially one expressed as if it were original or significant

ex.en: the platitude of most political oratory
ex.cn: 政治说辞里的陈词滥调

k.syn: triteness, staleness
k.ant: novelty 新颖性；originality 原创性
k.en: lack of originality

m: PLATITUDE contains PLAT (flat, French) — a flat, unoriginal saying. — platitude, a trite remark.

k.syn: believable, credible, likely, possible, presumable, probable
k.ant: paradoxical 看似矛盾的；implausible, improbable, incredible, unbelievable, unlikely 不太可能的
k.en: superficially fair, reasonable, or valuable but often specious

ex.en: a plausible argument
ex.cn: 看起来头头是道的说理

m: PLAUSIBLE contains PLAU (clap, Latin PLAUDERE) — worth applauding as believable. (Same root as APPLAUSE.) — plausible, believable.

k.syn: corrugate, crease, furrow, ruffle, wrinkle
k.ant: flatten, smooth 抹平
k.en: fold

m: PLEAT rhymes with PLAIT — both involve folding fabric. — pleat, to fold cloth.

k.syn: abundance, affluence, cornucopia, myriad, plenty, profusion, stack, wealth
k.ant: dearth, deficiency, inadequacy, insufficiency 缺乏，匮乏；mite, peanuts, pinch, pittance, trace 少量
k.en: an ample amount or quantity; an abundance

ex.en: a plentitude of lumber for the current housing market
ex.cn: 充足的家具木材供给

m: alternative spelling of PLENITUDE — fullness. — plentitude, abundance.

k.syn: abundance, cornucopia, feast, overabundance, overflow, plentitude, profusion, surfeit, surplus
k.ant: dearth, inadequacy, insufficiency, paucity, scarcity, undersupply 缺乏
k.en: excess, superfluity

ex.en: a plethora of advice and a paucity of assistance
ex.cn: 给出大量建议而鲜有实质性援助

m: PLETHORA contains PLET (full, Greek PLETHEIN) — overfullness. (Same root in PLENITUDE etymology.) — plethora, an excess.

k.syn: adaptable, ductile, flexible, limber, plastic, supple
k.ant: inflexible, rigid, stiff, unbending 不易弯曲的
k.en: supple enough to bend freely or repeatedly without breaking

ex.en: pliable optical fiber
ex.cn: 柔软的光纤

k.syn: compliant, docile, obedient, pliant, tractable, subdued
k.ant: contumacious, insubordinate, intractable, obstreperous, recalcitrant, refractory, unruly 难控制的
k.en: easily influenced, persuaded, or swayed

ex.en: He took advantage of the pliable mind of youth.
ex.cn: 他利用了年轻人容易受他人教唆的特点。

m: PLIABLE contains PLI (fold, Latin PLICARE) — able to be folded / bent. — pliable, flexible.

k.syn: adaptable, ductile, flexible, limber, plastic, supple
k.ant: inflexible, rigid, stiff 不易弯曲的；unbending 难弯曲的
k.en: easily bent or flexed

ex.en: a pliant young tree
ex.cn: 易弯曲的小树

k.syn: compliant, docile, obedient, pliant, tractable, subdued
k.ant: contumacious, insubordinate, intractable, obstreperous, recalcitrant, refractory, unruly 难控制的
k.en: yielding readily to influence or domination

ex.en: She's proud and stubborn, you know, under that pliant exterior.
ex.cn: 你知道，她外表温顺，内心却骄傲而固执。

m: PLIANT shares PLI- (fold) with PLIABLE — bendable, flexible. — pliant, flexible / yielding.

k.syn: dilemma, jam, predicament, quandary
k.en: a situation, especially a bad or unfortunate one

ex.en: He was in a plight, trying to decide whether or not to take the job.
ex.cn: 他处在不知道是否应该接受这份工作的困境之中。

m: PLIGHT rhymes with FLIGHT but the opposite — instead of escaping, you're stuck in a difficult situation. — plight, a difficult situation.

k.en: to walk heavily or laboriously; progressing slowly but steadily

m: from PLOD — moving slowly. — plodding, slow and laborious.

k.syn: backbone, courage, dauntlessness, grit, guts, resolution, spirit, spunk
k.ant: cowardice, coward, spinelessness 胆小，怯懦
k.en: resourceful courage and daring in the face of difficulties

ex.en: full of pluck
ex.cn: 充满了勇气

k.syn: play
k.en: to sound（the strings of an instrument）by pulling and releasing them with the fingers or a plectrum

ex.en: pluck the harp
ex.cn: 弹奏竖琴

m: to have PLUCK = to have the gut to pluck the danger by its tail. (Also: pluck a string.) — pluck, courage.

k.syn: fathom
k.en: to measure the depth of（as a body of water） typically with a weighted line

ex.en: We will plumb the bay to make sure it was deep enough for the huge vessel.
ex.cn: 为了保证水深足够容纳这艘巨轮，我们将会测量海湾的深度。

k.syn: perpendicular, upright, vertical
k.ant: horizontal 水平的
k.en: exactly vertical

k.der: Such a movie is plumb trash and further evidence of the deterioration of popular culture. 这样的电影就是彻头彻尾的垃圾，它进一步证明了大众文化的堕落和腐化。
k.syn: absolute, categorical, complete, consummate, definite, pure, sheer, thorough, utter, very
k.ant: doubtful, dubious, equivocal, questionable, uncertain 不确定的； qualified 有条件的
k.en: having no exceptions or restrictions

k.syn: explore, delve, inquire, investigate, probe
k.cn: 仔细深入地检查；探索，探究
k.en: to examine closely or deeply

ex.en: plumb the book's complexities
ex.cn: 审阅这本书的复杂度

ex.en: questions that plumb the depths of stupidity
ex.cn: 探究智商下限的问题

m: PLUMB contains PLUMB ≈ "PLUMB-line" (Latin PLUMBUM = lead) — a lead weight on a string used to measure depth or check vertical. — plumb, vertical / to probe deeply.

k.syn: crash, decline, descend, dip, dive, fall, plunge, sink, tumble
k.ant: skyrocket, soar 飙升
k.en: to decline suddenly and steeply

ex.en: Overall GPA plummeted 0.5 points.
ex.cn: 平均学分骤降0.5。

m: PLUMMET contains PLUMM ≈ "plumb" (lead weight) — a lead weight falls fast. — plummet, to fall sharply.

k.syn: chubby, fleshy, fat, gross, obese, rotund, round
k.ant: lean, slender, svelte, thin 纤瘦的，苗条的；angular 因消瘦而棱角分明的
k.en: well-rounded and full in form

ex.en: In Tang Dynasty, being plump was a sign of ultimate beauty.
ex.cn: 在唐代，身材丰满是最美丽的象征。

k.syn: advocate, back, champion, endorse, patronize
k.ant: baffle, foil, frustrate, sabotage 阻挠，从中破坏
k.en: to give full support or praise

ex.en: We will plump for any candidate who supports stem cell research.
ex.cn: 我们会鼎力支持那些拥护干细胞研究的候选人。

m: PLUMP rhymes with BUMP — both round and full. — plump, round / full.

k.syn: crash, decline, descend, dip, dive, fall, plummet, sink, tumble
k.ant: skyrocket, soar 飙升
k.en: to descend or dip suddenly

ex.en: Stock's value plunged.
ex.cn: 股票价值骤然下降。

k.syn: drive, stab, stick, thrust
k.ant: extract 拔出
k.en: to cause to penetrate or enter quickly and forcibly into something

ex.en: The enemy plunged the dagger into his chest.
ex.cn: 敌人把匕首刺入他的胸膛。

m: PLUNGE contains PLUNG ≈ "sudden splash" — sudden steep drop. — plunge, to drop sharply.

k.en: government by the wealthy; a wealthy class that controls a government

m: PLUTO (wealth, Greek PLOUTOS) + CRACY (rule) — rule by the wealthy. — plutocracy, rule by the rich.

k.en: the medical care and treatment of the human foot

m: POD (foot, Greek POUS) + IATRIST (doctor, Greek IATROS) — foot doctor. — podiatrist, a foot doctor.

k.syn: bifurcate, diverge, divide
k.ant: coalesce, unite 合并，联合
k.cn: 使分开对立，使两极分化
k.en: to break up into opposing factions or groupings

ex.en: The controversy has polarized voters into pro-abortion and anti- abortion groups.
ex.cn: 这一争议使得选民分化成支持堕胎和反对堕胎两个群体。

m: POLARIZE contains POLE — push to opposite POLES. — polarize, to split into opposites.

k.syn: contention, controversy, disagreement, disputation
k.ant: agreement 一致，协议
k.en: a controversial argument

ex.en: The polemic between science and religion has never ceased.
ex.cn: 科学和宗教之间的争论从未停息。

m: POLEMIC contains POLEM (war, Greek POLEMOS) — verbal war. — polemic, a strong argument.

k.syn: cultivated, cultured, genteel, refined, urbane
k.ant: gauche, rustic, philistine 粗俗的；uncivilized, untutored 未开化的
k.en: showing a high degree of refinement and the assurance that comes from wide social experience

ex.en: He maintained a very polished tone in his correspondences.
ex.cn: 他在通信中保持着一种极有教养的口吻。

k.syn: buffed, burnished, glistening, lustrous, rubbed, satiny, sleek
k.ant: dim, dull, flat, lusterless 黯淡无光的
k.en: having a shiny surface or finish

ex.en: She could see her face reflected in the polished hood of the car.
ex.cn: 她可以在引擎盖光亮的表面上看到自己的脸。

m: POLISHED — sanded smooth and shiny; figuratively, refined. — polished, refined.

k.syn: diplomatic, judicious, perspicacious, sagacious, tactful, wise
k.ant: tactless, unsophisticated 缺乏谋略的，头脑简单的
k.en: characterized by shrewdness in managing, contriving, or dealing

ex.en: a politic secretary
ex.cn: 能干的秘书

k.syn: advisable, desirable, prudent, tactical
k.ant: imprudent, inadvisable, injudicious, unwise 不明智的，不合时宜的
k.en: suitable for bringing about a desired result under the circumstances

ex.en: It probably would not be politic to tell your boss that his latest idea is the worst thing you've ever heard.
ex.cn: 不要现在去告诉你老板说他的想法是你听过的最糟的，这是一个不明智的做法。

m: POLITIC contains POLIT ≈ POLITICAL — politically savvy / prudent. — politic, shrewdly sensible.

k.en: one that conducts polls; a person who surveys public opinion

m: POLLSTER contains POLL — one who conducts opinion POLLs. — pollster, an opinion-survey expert.

k.syn: affected, bombastic, flowery, grandiloquent, magniloquent, rhetorical
k.ant: homely 朴素的
k.en: excessively elevated or ornate

ex.en: The candidate was given to windy and pompous rhetorical speeches.
ex.cn: 听众们听了一场冗长而浮夸的演讲。

k.syn: arrogant, egocentric, haughty, pontifical, presumptuous, supercilious
k.ant: humble, modest, unpretentious 谦逊的 “多看多背多做题，不烦不倦不放弃。” （赵墨非，Verbal 790，Quantitative 800，录取学校：UCLA经济学 PHD）
k.en: having or exhibiting self-importance

ex.en: a pompous politician who insisted on boarding the plane first
ex.cn: 一个坚持要求优先登机的傲慢政客

m: POMPOUS contains POMP — full of POMP and self-importance. — pompous, self-important.

k.syn: appreciable, perceptible, sensible
k.ant: inappreciable 微不足道的
k.en: considerable enough to be weighed or assessed

ex.en: Climate change has exerted a ponderable influence on world politics.
ex.cn: 气候变化对世界政局有着不容忽视的影响。

m: PONDERABLE contains PONDER — worth pondering. — ponderable, worth weighing / considering.

k.syn: cumbersome, heavy, hefty, massive, onerous, weighty
k.ant: ethereal, gossamer, light, weightless 轻飘飘的
k.en: of very great weight

ex.en: a ponderous machine
ex.cn: 沉重的机器

k.syn: dreary, dull, flat, elephantine, monotonous, insipid, vapid
k.ant: absorbing, engaging, engrossing, gripping, interesting, intriguing, involving, riveting 极吸引人的
k.en: oppressively or unpleasantly dull

ex.en: a ponderous sermon no one really cares
ex.cn: 没有人真正关心的沉闷无聊的布道

m: PONDEROUS contains POND (weight, Latin PONDUS) — heavy. (Same root as PONDER = weigh thoughts.) — ponderous, heavy / dull.

k.ant: condescend 屈尊，俯就
k.der: pontification n. 傲慢的言行；pontifical adj. 傲慢的
k.en: to speak or express opinions in a pompous or dogmatic way

ex.en: pontificate to show a sense of superiority
ex.cn: 傲慢地说以显示高人一等的优越感

m: PONTIFICATE contains PONTIF ≈ PONTIFF (the Pope) — speak with papal authority and pomp. — pontificate, to speak pompously.

k.en: a hard, white, translucent ceramic made by firing a pure clay and then glazing

m: PORCELAIN — fine ceramic. (From Italian PORCELLANA, a cowrie shell that resembled the glaze.) — porcelain, fine ceramic ware.

k.syn: cogitate, consider, contemplate, deliberate, meditate, perpend, ruminate, study, weigh, chew over
k.ant: flip, glance, leaf, riffle, skim 粗略地看，迅速浏览
k.en: to read or study attentively（usually used with over）

ex.en: The committee will probably pore over the results of the study for a long time before making their decision.
ex.cn: 在正式做决定之前，委员会可能需要花很长的时间来仔细研究调查结果。

m: to PORE over a book = peer intently into its tiny PORES of detail. — pore, to study closely.

k.syn: passable, penetrable, permeable, pervious
k.ant: impassable, impenetrable, impermeable, impervious 无法穿透的
k.en: admitting the passage of gas or liquid through pores or interstices

ex.en: Lava rock has a porous structure which makes the material lightweight and highly moisture-retentive.
ex.cn: 火山岩的多孔结构使得它密度很小，同时有很高的吸湿性。

m: POROUS contains PORE — full of PORES. — porous, full of holes.

k.syn: baleful, direful, doomy, foreboding, inauspicious, menacing, minatory, ominous, sinister, ill-omened
k.ant: auspicious, propitious 吉兆的
k.en: being or showing a sign of evil or calamity to come

ex.en: an eerie and portentous stillness
ex.cn: 阴森神秘、充满凶兆的寂静

k.syn: amazing, astonishing, astounding, fabulous, marvelous, miraculous, prodigious, wonderful
k.ant: common, commonplace, ordinary 普通的
k.cn: 勾起兴趣的，令人遐想的
k.en: eliciting amazement or wonder

ex.en: The way in which he could bring together opposing forces was truly portentous.
ex.cn: 他整合两股对抗势力的方法着实令人好奇。

m: PORTENTOUS contains PORTENT (an omen) — full of foreboding. — portentous, ominous / weighty.

k.en: a group of investments

m: PORTFOLIO contains PORT (carry) + FOLIO (leaves/pages) — a folder you carry your work in. — portfolio, a collection of work.

k.syn: grandstander, poser
k.en: an affected or insincere person

ex.en: She is such a poseur and you will never know if she is really crying or pretending.
ex.cn: 她就是个爱装的人，你从不知道她是真的在哭还是只是假装而已。

m: POSEUR — one who POSEs / strikes a pose. (French.) — poseur, an affected pretender.

k.syn: assume, postulate, presuppose, premise, presume
k.ant: falsify 证明为假
k.en: to assume or affirm the existence of

ex.en: The committee posited that he was qualified for the election.
ex.cn: 委员会假定他是够格参加选举的。

m: POSIT contains POS (place, Latin PONERE) — set forth as a premise. — posit, to assume / propose.

k.syn: premise, presumption, presupposition, supposition
k.ant: conclusion 结论
k.en: something taken as being true or factual and used as a starting point for a course of action or reasoning

ex.en: One of the postulates that the true agnostic rejects is the assumption that it is even possible for us to know whether God exists.
ex.cn: 我们甚至可以知道上帝是否存在——这是一个真正的不可知论者所反对的假设。

k.syn: assume, conjecture, hypothesize, posit, presuppose, premise, presume, suppose
k.ant: belie, disprove, falsify 证明为假
k.en: to assume or claim as true, existent, or necessary

ex.en: postulate a causal relationship
ex.cn: 假定存在因果关系

m: POSTULATE contains POST + ULATE — set forth (Latin POSTULARE = demand). — postulate, to assume as true.

k.syn: attitude, carriage, poise, stance, station
k.en: a general way of holding the body

ex.en: A good upright posture will prevent backaches.
ex.cn: 良好的站姿可以预防背痛。

k.syn: attitudinize, feign, grandstand, masquerade, pose, pretend
k.der: posturer n. 故作姿态之人
k.en: to assume an artificial or pretended attitude

ex.en: posture to impress
ex.cn: 故作姿态以给别人留下印象

m: POSTURE contains POS (place) — how you place your body; or strike a pose. — posture, body position / pose.

k.syn: beverage, drink, juice, liquor, spirits
k.ant: non-intoxicant, soft drink 软饮料
k.en: a beverage, especially an alcoholic beverage

ex.en: Potables are offered at bar counter for free.
ex.cn: 饮料可以在吧台免费领取。

k.syn: drinkable, edible
k.ant: undrinkable 不能饮用的
k.en: suitable for drinking

ex.en: Price of the potable water has soared.
ex.cn: 饮用水价格飙升。

m: POTABLE contains POT ≈ "potable potion" (Latin POTARE = drink). — potable, drinkable.

k.en: a literary or artistic work of poor quality

m: POT + BOILER — a writer's quick work to keep the POT BOILing (food on the table). — potboiler, hack writing for income.

k.syn: authority, autocrat, monarch, ruler, sovereign
k.ant: figurehead（有名无权的）傀儡
k.en: one who has the power and position to rule over others

ex.en: a son of a potentate
ex.cn: 官二代

m: POTENTATE contains POTENT — a powerful ruler. — potentate, a powerful ruler.

k.syn: activate, energize, enhance, intensify, stir, wake, vitalize
k.ant: deactivate 使无效；abate, attenuate, dwindle, lessen, moderate, reduce 减弱，削弱
k.en: to make effective or active, or more effective or more active

ex.en: additives to potentiate the drug
ex.cn: 增强药效的添加剂

m: POTENTIATE contains POTENT — make more potent. — potentiate, to strengthen.

k.syn: grump, mope, sulk
k.ant: grin 露齿而笑
k.cn: （尤指撅嘴或板着脸）表示不悦
k.en: to show displeasure, especially by thrusting out the lips or wearing a sullen expression

ex.en: She pouted and didn't say a word to anyone all morning.
ex.cn: 她整个早上都闷闷不乐，没和别人说一句话。

m: POUT rhymes with SHOUT but silent — sticking out the lips in displeasure. — pout, to sulk facially.

k.syn: expert, guru, professional, specialist, virtuoso
k.ant: fledgling 无经验的人；quack 冒充内行的人
k.en: one who practices a profession

ex.en: medical practitioner
ex.cn: 医护人员，行医者

m: PRACTITIONER contains PRACTICE — one who practices a profession. — practitioner, a working professional.

k.syn: grassland, meadow, plain, savanna
k.en: an extensive area of flat or rolling, predominantly treeless grassland

ex.en: Canadian Prairie
ex.cn: 加拿大大草原地区

m: PRAIRIE — French for meadow. (Same Latin PRAT = meadow.) — prairie, a vast grassland.

k.syn: babble, chat, chatter, converse, gabble, jabber, prattle, twitter
k.en: to talk long and idly

ex.en: They have been prating on the phone for hours.
ex.cn: 他们已经煲了几个小时的电话粥了。

m: PRATE rhymes with PRATTLE — both empty talk. — prate, to chatter foolishly.

k.syn: discourse, sermonize
k.en: to deliver a sermon

ex.en: a minister who loves to preach
ex.cn: 一个爱布道的牧师

m: PREACH contains PREACH ≈ "speech" — give moral speech. — preach, to deliver a sermon.

k.syn: delicate, fragile, sensitive, touchy, unstable
k.ant: firm, stable 稳定的；safe, secure 安全的
k.en: dangerously lacking in security or stability

ex.en: a precarious livelihood
ex.cn: 不稳定的生计

k.syn: ambiguous, doubtful, dubious, equivocal, uncertain, unfounded
k.ant: indubitable, unambiguous, unequivocal, unquestionable 明确的，毋庸置疑的
k.en: dependent on uncertain premises

ex.en: His entire argument relies on a precarious assumption.
ex.cn: 他的整篇论证都建立在一个可疑的假设之上。

m: PRECARIOUS contains PRECAR (prayer, Latin PRECARIUS) — held only by prayer; uncertain. — precarious, dangerously unstable.

k.syn: example, instance, model, paradigm, pattern, standard
k.en: an earlier occurrence of something similar

ex.en: There has not been any precedent so far.
ex.cn: 到目前为止还没有先例。

ex.en: a landmark decision that set a legal precedent
ex.cn: 在法律上首开先河的重大决议

k.syn: antecedent, anterior, former, preceding, previous, prior
k.ant: ensuing 继而发生的
k.en: prior in time, order, arrangement, or significance

ex.en: Her violent behaviors may be explained by some precedent events in her troubled life.
ex.cn: 她的暴力行径或许可以由她之前苦难生活中遭遇到的一些事件来解释。

m: PRECEDENT contains PRE (before) + CEDENT (going) — what came before. — precedent, an earlier example.

k.syn: bluff, cliff, crag, escarpment, palisade
k.ant: ridge 山脊
k.en: a very steep or overhanging place

ex.en: a precipice too steep to climb
ex.cn: 过于陡峭而无法攀爬的悬崖

m: PRECIPICE contains PRECIP ≈ "head-first fall" (Latin PRAECEPS = headlong). — precipice, a steep cliff.

k.syn: cursory, flying, headlong, hurried, overhasty, precipitous, rash, rushed
k.ant: deliberate 慎重考虑的；leisurely, unhurried, unrushed 从容不迫的，不慌不忙的
k.der: precipitation n. 急促；沉淀；降水
k.en: acting or done with excessive or careless speed

ex.en: the army's precipitate withdrawal from the field of battle
ex.cn: 匆忙将军队从战场上撤下

k.syn: accelerate, escalate, expedite, hasten, impel, speed, trigger
k.ant: check, encumber, enfetter, hamper, handicap, hinder, manacle, obstruct, retard, trammel 阻碍
k.en: to cause to happen, especially suddenly or prematurely

ex.en: precipitate an international crisis
ex.cn: 产生国际危机

m: PRECIPITATE shares PRECIP- with PRECIPICE — done headlong, hastily. — precipitate, to bring on suddenly.

k.syn: deposit, sediment
k.en: something precipitated as a deposit on the earth of hail, mist, rain, sleet, or snow

ex.en: The storm brought several inches of precipitation.
ex.cn: 风暴带来的降水量达数英寸。

k.syn: haste, hastiness, hustle, precipitousness, rush
k.ant: deliberation 深思熟虑
k.en: excited and often showy or disorderly speed

ex.en: I fear that I may have acted with some precipitation on this matter, so I would like to reconsider.
ex.cn: 我害怕自己在这个事情上考虑得太仓促了，因此我想再想想。

m: from PRECIPITATE — falling from above (rain) / hastiness. — precipitation, rainfall / haste.

k.syn: abrupt, arduous, sheer, steep
k.ant: flat, level 平坦的
k.en: very steep, perpendicular, or overhanging in rise or fall

ex.en: a precipitous gorge
ex.cn: 险峻的峡谷

k.syn: cursory, flying, headlong, hurried, overhasty, precipitate, rash, rushed
k.ant: deliberate 慎重考虑的；leisurely, unhurried, unrushed 从容不迫的，不慌不忙的
k.en: acting or done with excessive or careless speed

ex.en: They soon regretted their precipitous actions in international affairs.
ex.cn: 他们很快就为他们在国际事务中的匆忙举动感到后悔。

m: from PRECIPICE — steep / sudden. — precipitous, steeply abrupt.

k.syn: avert, deter, forestall, obviate, prevent, stave off
k.en: to make impossible, as by action taken in advance

ex.en: Age alone will not preclude him from standing as a candidate.
ex.cn: 年龄并没有阻止他成为候选人。

k.syn: bar, exclude, except
k.ant: enclose 圈入
k.der: preclusive adj. 预先阻止的；除外的
k.en: to exclude or prevent（someone）from a given condition or activity

ex.en: He refused to preclude the subject from discussion.
ex.cn: 他拒绝将这个话题排出讨论范围。

m: PRE (before) + CLUDE (close, Latin CLAUDERE) — close off in advance. (Same root as INCLUDE, EXCLUDE.) — preclude, to prevent.

k.syn: foregoer, forerunner, harbinger, herald, outrider
k.ant: sequela, successor 后继者；descendant 后代
k.der: precursory adj. 先驱性的，开创性的
k.en: one that precedes and indicates the approach of another

ex.en: 18th-century lyric poets like Robert Burns were precursors of the Romantics.
ex.cn: 十八世纪的抒情诗人（如罗伯特·彭斯）是浪漫主义的先驱。

m: PRE (before) + CURSOR (runner, Latin CURRERE) — one who runs before. — precursor, a forerunner.

k.syn: ancestor, antecedent, foregoer, forerunner, precursor
k.ant: successor 继任者；descendant 后代
k.en: a person who has previously occupied a position or office to which another has succeeded

ex.en: a political legacy left by his predecessor
ex.cn: 他的前任留下来的政治遗产

m: PRE (before) + DECESSOR (departing one) — one who came / went before. — predecessor, one who held a position before.

k.syn: doom, fate, foredoom, foreordain, ordain, predestine, predetermine, preordain
k.en: to determine the fate of in advance

ex.en: Our victory in the tournament was seemingly predestined.
ex.cn: 我们在锦标赛中的胜利似乎是注定了的。

m: PRE (before) + DESTINE — set the destiny in advance. — predestine, to determine beforehand.

k.syn: affection, affinity, bias, disposition, inclination, leaning, penchant, predisposition, propensity, tendency
k.ant: aversion, loathing, nausea, repugnance, repulsion, revulsion 反感
k.en: a partiality or disposition in favor of something

ex.en: a predilection for travel
ex.cn: 热爱旅行

m: PRE (before) + DILECT (love, Latin DILIGERE) — preferred love. — predilection, a preference.

k.syn: distinguished, illustrious, incomparable, notable, outstanding, peerless, superb, supreme, unmatchable
k.ant: negligible, trivial 不重要的
k.en: having paramount rank, dignity, or importance

ex.en: The writer's style is brilliant and his command of words, preeminent.
ex.cn: 作者的文风和用词都十分出色。

m: PRE (before) + EMINENT — eminent above others. — preeminent, surpassing.

k.syn: appropriate, arrogate, commandeer, convert, expropriate, seize, usurp
k.der: preemptive adj. 先发制人的；先买的
k.en: to appropriate, seize, or take for oneself before others

ex.en: The naughty children had preempted front-row seats that were reserved for the guests of honor.
ex.cn: 调皮的孩子们把前排留给贵宾的座位占了。

k.syn: displace, replace, supersede, supplant
k.en: to replace with something considered to be of greater value or priority: take precedence over

ex.en: The special newscast preempted the usual television program.
ex.cn: 特别新闻取代了常规电视节目。

m: PRE (before) + EMPT (take, Latin EMERE) — take before others can. — preempt, to take first.

k.syn: plume
k.ant: rumple 弄皱
k.en: to smooth or clean（feathers）with the beak or bill

k.syn: groom, plume, primp
k.en: to dress or groom（oneself）with elaborate care

ex.en: She always preens herself in an elaborate suit before going to the opera.
ex.cn: 她去听歌剧之前总要精心打扮一番，穿上最华丽的服装。

k.syn: gloat, plume, pride
k.ant: efface 隐没，使不被人注意
k.en: to take pride or satisfaction in（oneself）

ex.en: He always preen himself on his ancestry.
ex.cn: 他总是因为他的血统而洋洋得意

m: PREEN rhymes with GREEN — birds PREEN their feathers; vain people PREEN themselves. — preen, to groom / show vanity.

k.syn: exordium, foreword, introduction, overture, preamble, prelude, prologue
k.ant: epilogue 尾声
k.en: a preliminary statement or essay introducing a book that explains its scope, intention, or background and is usually written by the author

ex.en: An informal brunch served as a preface to the three-day conference.
ex.cn: 一顿非正式的早午餐作为为期三天的会议的序曲。

m: PRE (before) + FACE — what FACEs you BEFORE the main text. — preface, an introduction.

k.syn: eloquent, meaningful, momentous, profound, revelatory, significant, suggestive
k.ant: inane 空洞的
k.en: weighty or significant; full of meaning

ex.en: the pregnant phrases of the Bible
ex.cn: 《圣经》中充满哲理的话语

k.syn: enceinte, expectant, expecting, gravid
k.en: containing a developing embryo, fetus, or unborn offspring within the body

ex.en: Being pregnant represents great news, but it comes with a lot of responsibilities.
ex.cn: 怀孕是件大好事，但是它也带来了许多的责任。

m: PREGNANT contains PREGN ≈ "about to give birth" — heavy with significance about to come out. — pregnant, full of meaning.

k.syn: deliberate, prearrange, prepare, preplan
k.ant: disregard, ignore, neglect, omit, overlook 忽视
k.der: premeditation n. 谋略，事先考虑
k.en: to think, consider, or deliberate beforehand

ex.en: carefully premeditate each step of his plan
ex.cn: 细致谋略他计划的每一步

k.syn: forethink, precogitate, predetermine
k.ant: disregard, ignore, overlook, slight 忽略
k.en: to think about and revolve in the mind beforehand

ex.en: He's carefully premeditating each step of his plan of campaign.
ex.cn: 他正在仔细地计划竞选计划中的每一个步骤。

m: PRE (before) + MEDITATE — think out beforehand. — premeditate, to plan in advance.

k.syn: absorption, engagement, engrossment, immersion
k.ant: apathy, indifference, nonchalance, unconcern 漠不关心
k.en: extreme or excessive concern with something

ex.en: He kept sinking back into gloomy preoccupation.
ex.cn: 他陷入了深深的忧虑之中。

m: PRE + OCCUPATION — your mind is OCCUPIED first by this concern. — preoccupation, mental absorption.

k.syn: dominant, paramount, predominant, prevalent, overruling
k.ant: secondary, subsidiary 次要的
k.en: having superior weight, force, importance, or influence

ex.en: a preponderant misconception
ex.cn: 一个影响甚广的错误概念

m: PRE + PONDER (weigh, Latin PONDUS = weight) — having greater weight. — preponderant, dominant in weight.

k.syn: alluring, attractive, appealing, captivating, charming, enchanting, pleasing, riveting
k.ant: abhorrent, appalling, disgusting, hideous, loathsome, repellent, repulsive 令人反感的
k.en: serving to impress favorably

ex.en: He was fascinated by her prepossessing appearance at first sight.
ex.cn: 他第一眼就被她迷人的外表吸引了。

m: PRE + POSSESSING — POSSESSes you with a good impression upfront. — prepossessing, attractive.

k.syn: absurd, asinine, fallacious, fatuous, lunatic, ludicrous, insane, irrational, unreasonable
k.ant: commonsensical 常识性的；reasonable, sensible 有道理的
k.en: contrary to nature, reason, or common sense

ex.en: a preposterous conclusion of quantum mechanics
ex.cn: 量子力学里有悖于常理的结论

m: PRE (before) + POSTER (after, Latin POST) — "before-after" reversed; backwards-absurd. — preposterous, absurd.

k.syn: augury, auspice, boding, foreboding, foreshadowing, portent,
k.en: something believed to be a sign or warning of a future event

ex.en: The sight of the first robin is always a welcome presage of spring.
ex.cn: 第一只知更鸟的出现总是迎接春天到来的象征。

m: PRESAGE contains PRE (before) + SAGE (wise) — wisely predicted in advance. — presage, an omen.

k.syn: foresight, forethought, providence
k.ant: improvidence, myopia, shortsightedness 目光短浅
k.en: knowledge of actions or events before they occur

ex.en: Most believers would probably agree that complete prescience is one of God's attributes.
ex.cn: 所有的信徒大概都会同意上帝能够预见未来。

m: PRE (before) + SCIENCE (knowing) — knowing in advance. — prescience, foreknowledge.

k.syn: convention, custom, decree, law, regulation, rule
k.der: prescribe v. 开药方；设立规定
k.en: something prescribed as a rule; especially an inherited or established way of thinking, feeling, or doing

m: PRE (before) + SCRIPTION (written) — written down beforehand as a rule or formula. — prescription, an established rule.

k.en: an additive used to protect against decay, discoloration, or spoilage

ex.en: contain no chemical preservative
ex.cn: 不含化学防腐剂

m: PRESERVATIVE contains PRESERVE — keeps it preserved. — preservative, a substance that prevents decay.

k.syn: credit, fame, influence, reputation
k.ant: infamy 坏名声
k.en: the level of respect at which one is regarded by others

ex.en: The prestige of the university has been irrevocably impaired by the plagiarism scandal.
ex.cn: 这所大学的声望已经因剽窃丑闻而受到了不可挽回的损失。

m: PRESTIGE contains PRESTIG ≈ "PRESTIDigitator" magic — the dazzling glow of high reputation. — prestige, reputation / standing.

k.syn: audacious, bold, brash, impertinent, impudent, insolent, pompous, presuming
k.ant: courteous, decorous, genteel, gracious, urbane 举止得体的，有礼貌的
k.en: overstepping due bounds（as of propriety or courtesy）

ex.en: Under such a circumstance his demand for attention was utterly presumptuous.
ex.cn: 在那种情况下他要求被关注是极其冒昧的。

k.syn: assumptive, bumptious, cavalier, haughty, imperious, lofty, overweening, peremptory, supercilious
k.ant: humble, modest 谦卑的
k.en: having a feeling of superiority that shows itself in an overbearing attitude

ex.en: The presumptuous doctor didn't even bother to explain to me the treatment that I would be receiving.
ex.cn: 傲慢的医生连给我说明我会接受何种治疗的耐性都没有。

m: PRESUMPTUOUS contains PRESUME — taking too much for granted. — presumptuous, overstepping bounds.

k.syn: affectation, camouflage, deceit, disguise, imposture, mask, masquerade
k.ant: sincerity 真诚
k.en: the act of pretending; a false appearance or action intended to deceive

ex.en: There is too much pretense in his piety.
ex.cn: 他的虔诚大多都是伪装。

k.syn: assumption, hauteur, imperiousness, loftiness, lordliness, pomposity, superciliousness, superiority
k.ant: humility, modesty 谦逊
k.en: an exaggerated sense of one's importance that shows itself in the making of excessive or unjustified claims

m: PRETENSE contains PRET ≈ "pretend" — a pretended front. — pretense, a false display.

k.syn: aberrant, abnormal, anomalous, extraordinary, phenomenal, magical, miraculous, unearthly
k.ant: common, ordinary, prosaic 平凡的
k.en: surpassing the normal or usual

ex.en: They exhibited preternatural courage in face of danger.
ex.cn: 他们在危险面前显示出了超乎寻常的勇气。

m: PRETER (beyond, Latin) + NATURAL — beyond natural. — preternatural, beyond what is normal.

k.syn: conquer, dominate, overcome, reign, triumph
k.ant: yield 屈服；lose 失败
k.en: to be greater in strength or influence

ex.en: a custom that still prevails
ex.cn: 依然盛行的传统

m: PRE (very) + VAIL (worth, Latin VALERE) — worth more than the rest, win out. — prevail, to triumph / be widespread.

k.syn: conventional, dominant, common, popular, predominant, preponderant, prevailing, rife
k.ant: absent, rare 缺少的，稀少的；unusual 与众不同的
k.en: widely or commonly occurring, existing, accepted, or practiced

ex.en: The kinds of accidents are frequently seen in places where snowmobiles are prevalent.
ex.cn: 这类事故在摩托雪橇盛行的地区很常见。

m: from PREVAIL — winning out, widespread. — prevalent, widespread.

k.syn: equivocate, fabricate, falsify, lie, palter
k.en: to stray from or evade the truth

ex.en: During the hearings the witness did his best to prevaricate.
ex.cn: 听证会上证人在竭尽全力地支吾其词。

m: PRE + VARI (turn aside, Latin VARICARE) — straddle / dodge the truth. — prevaricate, to lie evasively.

k.syn: ancient, early, primal, primeval, primitive
k.ant: late, recent 最近的
k.en: being or happening first in sequence of time

ex.en: primordial forms of life
ex.cn: 最原始的生命形态

m: PRIM (first, Latin) + ORD (order, Latin ORDIRI = begin) — at the very first beginning. — primordial, ancient / original.

k.syn: preen, dress up
k.en: to dress, adorn, or arrange in a careful or finicky manner

ex.en: She primps for hours before a date.
ex.cn: 她出门约会前要花数小时打扮。

m: PRIMP rhymes with PRIM — being PRIM and prissy about your appearance. — primp, to fuss over one's appearance.

k.syn: capital, cardinal, chief, dominant, grand, main, major, paramount, predominant, preeminent, primary
k.ant: secondary, subordinate 次要的
k.en: first, highest, or foremost in importance, rank, worth, or degree

ex.en: The region's principal city is getting hammered by a series of terrorist attacks.
ex.cn: 该地区的中心城市正在被一系列恐怖袭击侵扰。

m: PRINCIPAL contains PRIN (first, Latin PRIMUS) — first / chief. — principal, main / chief.

k.syn: immaculate, spotless, stainless, unsoiled, unstained, unsullied
k.ant: tainted, squalid, contaminated, besmirched, corrupted by civilization 污染的，被文明腐蚀的
k.cn: 纯净的，质朴的，未被文明腐蚀的
k.en: remaining in a pure state; uncorrupted by civilization; remaining free from dirt or decay; clean

ex.en: a pristine forest
ex.cn: 一片未被文明影响的森林

m: PRISTINE contains PRISTI ≈ "first untouched state" (Latin PRISTINUS = original). — pristine, untouched / pure.

k.syn: deprivation, loss
k.ant: repletion 充满
k.en: lack of what is needed for existence

ex.en: The constant privation of sleep was starting to affect his work.
ex.cn: 长期以来的睡眠不足开始影响他的工作。

m: PRIVATION contains PRIV (deprive, Latin PRIVARE) — being deprived. — privation, lack of necessities.

k.syn: delve, explore, inquire; delving, disquisition, exploration, inquisition
k.en: a penetrating or critical investigation

ex.en: probe into his background
ex.cn: 深入调查他的背景

m: PROBE rhymes with ROBE but feels invasive — a PROBE digs into the truth. — probe, an investigation.

k.syn: honesty, integrity, rectitude, righteousness, uprightness
k.ant: unscrupulousness, shiftiness, baseness, dishonor, lowness 肆无忌惮，欺骗
k.en: faithfulness to high moral standards

ex.en: A person of indisputable probity should head the disciplinary panel.
ex.cn: 一个真正正直的人应该来领导纪律委员会。

m: PROBITY contains PROB (good, Latin PROBUS) — moral goodness. (Same root as APPROVE = test as good.) — probity, integrity.

k.syn: aptitude, disposition, leaning, partiality, penchant, predilection, propensity
k.ant: aversion, antipathy, disinclination 厌恶，反感
k.en: a natural propensity or inclination; predisposition

ex.en: show artistic proclivities at an early age
ex.cn: 在很小的时候就表现出了对艺术的喜好

m: PROCLIVITY contains PROCLIV ≈ "slope forward" (Latin PROCLIVIS = sloping). — proclivity, a natural inclination.

k.en: to put off doing something, especially out of habitual carelessness or laziness

ex.en: She procrastinated and missed the submission deadline.
ex.cn: 她一直拖拖拉拉，导致错过了截止日期。

m: PRO + CRAST (tomorrow, Latin CRAS) — keep pushing to tomorrow. — procrastinate, to delay.

k.syn: acquire, attain, garner, knock down, pull down, bring in
k.ant: relinquish, forfeit, lose 放弃
k.en: to get possession of

ex.en: procured the prisoner's release
ex.cn: 得到了释放囚犯的许可

m: PRO + CURE (care, Latin CURA) — take care to obtain. — procure, to obtain.

k.syn: encourage, exhort, goad, nudge, prompt, spur, egg on
k.ant: rein 抑制
k.en: to try to persuade（someone）through earnest appeals to follow a course of action

ex.en: The strike may prod the government into action.
ex.cn: 罢工也许会迫使政府采取行动。

m: PROD rhymes with ROD — both used to poke and urge. — prod, to urge / poke.

k.syn: extravagant, profligate, squandering, unthrifty, wasteful
k.ant: penury 贫困; husbandry 节约
k.en: recklessly spendthrift

ex.en: prodigal outlays for her clothes
ex.cn: 买衣服时挥金如土

k.syn: fritterer, high roller, profligate, spender, spendthrift, squanderer, waster, wastrel
k.ant: economizer, penny-pincher 吝啬的人
k.en: someone who spends money freely or foolishly

ex.en: The million-dollar lottery winner was such a prodigal that his windfall was exhausted after only a few years.
ex.cn: 靠彩票中了百万的赢家是个败家子，几年之内就把这笔意外之财全部花完了。

m: PRODIGAL — like the biblical Prodigal Son who squandered his inheritance. — prodigal, wastefully spending.

k.syn: colossal, elephantine, enormous, gigantic, titanic, tremendous
k.ant: slight 微小的
k.en: impressively great in size, force, or extent; enormous

ex.en: a prodigious supply of canned food kept in the basement
ex.cn: 地下室贮存着的大量罐头食品

k.syn: amazing, astonishing, astounding, awesome, fabulous, miraculous, portentous, staggering, stunning, stupendous, sublime, surprising
k.en: causing wonder or astonishment

ex.en: stage magicians performing prodigious feats for rapt audiences
ex.cn: 舞台魔术师在全神贯注的观众们面前表演惊人的技艺

m: PRODIGIOUS contains PRODIG ≈ PRODIGY — like a prodigy: extraordinarily great. — prodigious, enormously impressive.

k.syn: defile, violate
k.ant: unviolated, inviolable 未被亵渎的，不可亵渎的
k.en: to treat（something sacred）with abuse, irreverence, or contempt

ex.en: Invading troops profaned the altar by playing poker on it.
ex.cn: 侵略军亵渎了祭坛，竟然在上面打扑克。

k.syn: abuse, misemploy, misuse, pervert, prostitute
k.en: to put to a bad or improper use

ex.en: He profaned his considerable acting talents by appearing in some wretched movies.
ex.cn: 在一些烂俗的电影里面出现简直就是浪费他的表演才华。

m: PROFANE contains PRO (before/outside) + FANE (temple, Latin FANUM) — outside the temple, irreligious. — profane, irreverent.

k.syn: extend, give, tender, trot out
k.ant: retain, withhold 保留
k.en: to offer for acceptance; tender

ex.en: She proffered her assistance in helping the two sides reach a compromise.
ex.cn: 她在斡旋双方达成妥协过程中尽了力。

m: PRO (forth) + OFFER — offer forth. — proffer, to put forward.

k.syn: accomplished, complete, expert, skilled, versed, virtuoso
k.ant: inept, incompetent, amateur, inexperienced, inexpert, jackleg, unprofessional, unseasoned, unskilled 无能的，没有经验的
k.en: having or marked by an advanced degree of competence, as in an art, vocation, profession, or branch of learning

ex.en: proficient in translating foreign languages
ex.cn: 精通外语翻译

m: PROFICIENT contains PROFIC ≈ "makes progress" (Latin PROFICERE = make progress). — proficient, skilled.

k.syn: deepness, profoundness
k.ant: superficiality 肤浅
k.en: something profound or abstruse

ex.en: His books are a mixture of playfulness and profundity.
ex.cn: 他的作品都是搞笑和深刻的结合体。

m: PROFUNDITY contains PROFOUND — depth of thought. — profundity, deep insight.

k.syn: abundance, mass, plentitude, scads, volume, wealth
k.ant: scanty 缺乏的
k.en: the state of being profuse; abundance

ex.en: snow falling in profusion
ex.cn: 雪量很大

k.syn: extravagancy, lavishness, prodigality, wastefulness
k.ant: economy, frugality, penny-pinching 节俭
k.en: the quality or fact of being free or wasteful in the expenditure of money

ex.en: In giving gifts to his girlfriend, he was generous to the point of profusion.
ex.cn: 在给女朋友的礼物支出一项，他慷慨到了几乎奢侈的地步。

m: PRO (forth) + FUSION (pouring, Latin FUNDERE) — pouring forth abundantly. — profusion, abundant outpouring.

k.en: tending to prohibit or restrain
so high or burdensome as to discourage purchase or use

ex.en: prohibitive prices
ex.cn: 抑制购买的高价

m: PROHIBITIVE contains PROHIBIT — preventing. — prohibitive, preventing access (especially by cost).

k.syn: balloon, boom, build up, escalate, expand, mushroom, snowball
k.ant: decrease in amount, dwindle, contract, decrease, diminish, lessen, recede, wane 数量减少，缩小
k.en: to grow or multiply by rapidly producing new tissue, parts, cells, or offspring; to increase at a rapid rate

ex.en: Rumors about the accident proliferated on the Internet.
ex.cn: 关于事故的小道消息在网上迅速扩散。

m: PROLI (offspring, Latin PROLES) + FER (carry, bear) — bear offspring rapidly. — proliferate, to multiply rapidly.

k.syn: diffuse, garrulous, rambling, verbose, windy
k.ant: pithy, taciturn, terse, succinct, concise, extremely brief 精炼的，寡言的
k.en: tending to speak or write at excessive length

ex.en: He habitually transforms brief anecdotes into prolix sagas that exhaust his listeners.
ex.cn: 他总是将简短的小趣事变成冗长的传说故事，让听众很疲倦。

m: PROLIX rhymes with PROLIFIC but for words — extending too far in words. — prolix, wordy.

k.syn: exordium, foreword, preamble, preface, prelude, proem, prolusion
k.ant: epilogue 结尾
k.en: the preface or introduction to a literary work

ex.en: The burglary, which he committed while still a teen, was but a prologue to a wasted life of crime
ex.cn: 他青少年时犯过的入室抢劫拉开了他偷盗生涯的序幕。

m: PRO (before) + LOGUE (speech, Greek LOGOS) — speech before the main story. — prologue, an introduction.

k.syn: elongate, lengthen, outstretch, protract, stretch, drag（out）, draw out
k.ant: abbreviate, abridge, curtail, cut, cut back, shorten, truncate 截短
k.en: to lengthen in extent, scope, or range

ex.en: Additives are used to prolong the shelf life of packaged food.
ex.cn: 添加剂被用来延长包装食品的保存期限。

m: PRO + LONG — make LONGer. — prolong, to lengthen.

k.syn: annunciate, declare, enunciate, proclaim, publicize, herald
k.ant: keep secret 保密
k.en: to make known openly or publicly

ex.en: The law was promulgated in June 1988.
ex.cn: 法律在1988年6月出台。

m: PROMULGATE contains PROMULG ≈ "announce out" (Latin PROMULGARE) — proclaim publicly. — promulgate, to publicly announce.

k.en: to read（copy or proof）in order to find errors and mark corrections

ex.en: She proofread the paper carefully.
ex.cn: 她仔细把论文校对了。

m: PROOF + READ — READ to PROOF (verify) for errors. — proofread, to check for errors.

k.syn: breed, multiply, reproduce
k.ant: fail to multiply 繁殖失败
k.en: to（cause to）continue or increase by sexual or asexual reproduction

ex.en: The dams along the river are interfering with the salmon's ability to propagate.
ex.cn: 河上的大坝影响了大马哈鱼的繁殖。

k.syn: broadcast, circulate, disseminate
k.ant: check 阻止
k.en: to cause to spread out and affect a greater number or greater area; extend

ex.en: the various ways in which churches can propagate the faith
ex.cn: 不同的宣传教义的方法

m: PROPAGATE contains PROPA ≈ "propa-pagate" (Latin PROPAGARE = breed/extend). — propagate, to multiply / spread.

k.syn: affinity, aptitude, bent, partiality, penchant, predilection, predisposition, proclivity
k.ant: aversion 厌恶
k.en: an often intense natural inclination or preference

ex.en: a neighbor who has an unfortunate propensity for snooping
ex.cn: 好管闲事的邻居

m: PROPENSITY contains PROPEN ≈ "hanging forward" (Latin PROPENDERE = hang forward). — propensity, a natural inclination.

k.syn: predictive
k.en: foretelling events: predictive

ex.en: Those lower-than-expected sales numbers were a prophetic indicator of the financial trouble the company would soon be in.
ex.cn: 那些低于预期的销售数字就是公司即将陷入金融危机的先兆。

m: PROPHETIC contains PROPHET — speaking like a prophet. — prophetic, predictive.

k.syn: appease, assuage, conciliate, disarm, mollify, placate
k.ant: enrage, incense, inflame, infuriate, ire, madden, outrage, antagonize, arouse hostility 激怒
k.en: to conciliate（an offended power）; appease

ex.en: The temple was built to honor the gods in times of plenty and to propitiate them in times of trouble.
ex.cn: 该庙宇的作用是，丰收之时感谢神明，有难之时安抚神明。

m: PROPITIATE contains PROPIT (favorable, Latin PROPITIUS) — make favorable. — propitiate, to appease.

k.syn: auspicious, encouraging, fair, heartening, optimistic, promising, upbeat
k.ant: dim, discouraging, disheartening, futureless, hopeless, inauspicious, unfavorable, unpromising, unpropitious 不吉利的
k.en: favorably disposed: pointing toward a happy outcome

ex.en: propitious sign
ex.cn: 吉祥的征兆

m: shares PROPIT- with PROPITIATE — favorable. — propitious, favorable.

k.syn: advocate, apostle, champion, expounder, espouser, friend, promoter, supporter, protagonist
k.ant: adversary, antagonist, opponent, detractor 诋毁者，反对者
k.en: one who argues in support of something; an advocate

ex.en: a proponent of the use of electric-powered cars
ex.cn: 电动能源汽车的支持者

m: PROPONENT contains PRO (forward) + PONENT (placing, Latin PONERE) — one who puts forward an idea. — proponent, an advocate.

k.syn: decorum, form, etiquette
k.ant: impropriety, indecency, indecorum 不合礼节
k.en: conformity to what is socially acceptable in conduct or speech

ex.en: When attending a wedding, there are certain proprieties that must be followed.
ex.cn: 参加婚礼时，有一些礼节需要遵守。

k.syn: appositeness, aptness, felicity, fitness, properness, rightness, seemliness, suitability
k.ant: improperness, impropriety, inappositeness, inappropriateness, inaptness, infelicity, unfitness, unseemliness, unsuitability, wrongness 不得体
k.en: the quality or state of being especially suitable or fitting

ex.en: I'm not sure about the propriety of serving champagne in these glasses.
ex.cn: 我不确定在这些杯子里倒香槟是否得体。

m: from PROPRI- (one's own, proper) — what's proper. — propriety, proper conduct.

k.syn: commonplace, everyday, routine, unexceptional, unremarkable, workaday
k.ant: abnormal, exceptional, extraordinary, odd, out-of-the-way, strange, exciting, preternatural, ingenious, imaginary 不寻常的，有独创性的
k.en: being of the type that is encountered in the normal course of events

ex.en: prosaic advice
ex.cn: 老掉牙的建议

m: PROSAIC contains PROS ≈ PROSE — flat like prose, not poetic. — prosaic, dull / commonplace.

k.syn: ban, enjoin, interdict, outlaw, prohibit
k.ant: sanction, permit, allow, let, suffer 允许
k.en: to prohibit; forbid

ex.en: acts proscribed by law
ex.cn: 被法律禁止的行为

m: PRO (before) + SCRIBE (write, Latin SCRIBERE) — to publicly write off as forbidden. (Romans posted PROSCRIPTIONS — lists of outlaws.) — proscribe, to forbid.

k.syn: accomplishment, achievement, discharge, enactment, execution, fulfillment, implementation, performance, perpetration, pursuance
k.ant: nonfulfillment, nonperformance 不履行，不完成
k.en: the doing of an action

ex.en: oversee the prosecution of the president's foreign policy
ex.cn: 监督总统对外政策的执行情况

m: PROSECUTION contains PROSECUTE — carrying through (a legal action, a plan). — prosecution, the carrying out / legal pursuit.

k.syn: convert
k.en: to persuade to change to one's religious faith

ex.en: The efforts of early missionaries to proselytize the Native Americans of Minnesota were largely unproductive.
ex.cn: 早期传教士对明尼苏达土著人进行的改变信仰的努力大多无功而返。

m: PROSELYTE = a convert (Greek PROSELYTOS = newcomer); to PROSELYTIZE = make converts. — proselytize, to convert (someone to a belief).

k.syn: hunt, probe, search, skirr
k.en: to go into or range over for purposes of discovery

ex.en: People had arrived in the valley to prospect for gold.
ex.cn: 人们来到山谷淘金。

k.syn: anticipation, contemplation, expectancy
k.en: the act or state of looking forward to some occurrence

ex.en: The prospect of a quiet, restful Sunday ended when our basement flooded.
ex.cn: 对一个宁静休闲的周日的期待被地下室涨水打破了。

m: PRO (forward) + SPECT (look) — look forward to. — prospect, a possibility / to explore.

k.syn: booming, flourishing, halcyon, lush, palmy, roaring, thriving
k.ant: depressed 萧条的；impecunious 贫穷的
k.en: marked by vigorous growth and well- being especially economically

ex.en: The company had a prosperous year.
ex.cn: 公司今年业绩斐然。

m: PROSPEROUS contains PROSPER — thriving. — prosperous, thriving.

k.ant: erect, upright 直立的
k.en: lying flat or at full length

ex.en: He was lying prostrate on the bed.
ex.cn: 他平躺在床上。

k.syn: debilitated, effete, enervated, enfeebled, languid, sapped; debilitate, devitalize, enervate, enfeeble, etiolate, sap, tire
k.ant: fortify, strengthen, beef up 加强
k.en: to reduce to extreme weakness or incapacitation

ex.en: illness that prostrated an entire family
ex.cn: 将整个家庭拖垮的疾病

m: PROSTRATE contains PRO + STRATE (lay flat, Latin) — laid out flat. — prostrate, lying flat / utterly exhausted.

k.syn: adaptable, universal, all-around
k.ant: static 固定的
k.en: displaying great diversity or variety; versatile

ex.en: He loved to show off his protean talent.
ex.cn: 他喜欢炫耀自己多样的才华。

m: PROTEAN comes from PROTEUS, the Greek shape-shifting sea god. — protean, versatile / changeable.

k.en: a code of correct conduct

ex.en: a breach of protocol
ex.cn: 对社交礼仪的破坏

m: PROTO (first) + COL ≈ "glued page" (Greek PROTOKOLLON = first sheet) — the first official sheet of formal procedures. — protocol, formal etiquette / procedure.

k.syn: elongate, lengthen, prolong, stretch, drag out, draw out
k.ant: curtail, abridge, abbreviate, shorten, cut back缩减
k.en: to draw out or lengthen in time; prolong

m: PRO (forward) + TRACT (drag, Latin TRAHERE) — drag out. (Same TRACT- as ATTRACT, DETRACT.) — protract, to lengthen.

k.syn: bulge, overhang, poke, project, stand out, stick out
k.ant: concave 凹陷
k.en: to jut out; project; bulge

ex.en: a handkerchief protruding from his breast pocket
ex.cn: 手帕从他的上衣口袋里伸出来

m: PRO (forward) + TRUDE (push, Latin TRUDERE) — push forward. — protrude, to stick out.

k.ant: concavity 凹陷
k.en: thrusting out from a surrounding or adjacent surface often as a rounded mass

ex.en: protuberant eyes
ex.cn: 暴鱼眼

m: PRO + TUBER (lump, Latin) — like a TUBER (potato), bulging out. — protuberant, bulging.

k.syn: economical, sparing, thrifty, farsighted, provident, scrimpy
k.ant: profligate 挥霍的
k.en: frugal; economical

ex.en: It is possible to be provident without being miserly.
ex.cn: 人可以做到节俭但不吝啬。

k.syn: farseeing, farsighted, forehanded, foreseeing, forethoughtful, prescient, proactive, visionary
k.ant: half-baked, half-cocked, improvident, myopic, shortsighted 目光短浅的
k.en: having or showing awareness of and preparation for the future

ex.en: Her provident measures kept us safe while we waited out the hurricane.
ex.cn: 她之前有远见的准备措施让我们在等待飓风结束的期间安然无恙。

m: PRO (before) + VIDENT (seeing, Latin VIDERE) — seeing ahead, prudent. — provident, prepared for the future.

k.syn: lucky, fluky, fortuitous
k.ant: unfortunate, mishap, hapless, ill-fated, ill-starred, luckless, star- crossed 不幸的
k.en: happening as if through divine intervention

ex.en: a providential escape
ex.cn: 幸运的逃脱

m: from PROVIDENCE — guided by foresight / luck. — providential, opportunely lucky.

k.syn: illiberal, insular, parochial, sectarian, narrow-minded
k.ant: ecumenical, broad-minded, catholic, cosmopolitan, liberal, open, open-minded, receptive, tolerant 世界范围的，开放的，包容的
k.en: limited in perspective; narrow and self- centered

ex.en: an artist who has been criticized for being provincial
ex.cn: 一个被批评非常狭隘的艺术家

m: PROVINCIAL contains PROVINCE — like the country PROVINCEs (away from the cosmopolitan capital), narrow-minded. — provincial, narrow-minded.

k.syn: impermanent, interim, provisionary, short-term
k.ant: long-term, permanent 永久的
k.en: provided or serving only for the time being; temporary

ex.en: He was appointed provisional executor of the industrialist's vast estate.
ex.cn: 在遗嘱中，他被指定为那位实业家的庞大资产的临时保管人。

m: PROVISIONAL contains PROVISION — a temporary provision. — provisional, temporary.

k.syn: interim, provisional, provisionary, provisory
k.ant: unconditional, long-term, permanent 无条件的，永久的
k.en: depending on a proviso; conditional; serving in a position for the time being

ex.en: a provisory permit to block off the street while movie scenes were being shot
ex.cn: 一个同意在电影拍摄期间封锁道路的临时许可

m: from PROVISO — with conditions / temporary. — provisory, conditional / temporary.

k.syn: arouse, excite, incite, instigate, pique, stimulate, stir, fire up
k.en: to incite to anger or resentment

ex.en: His teasing finally provoked her.
ex.cn: 他的调戏最终把她激怒了。

k.syn: abet, ferment, foment, instigate, stir up, whip up If you put out your hands, you are a laborer; if you put out your hands and mind, you are a craftsperson; if you put out your hands, mind, heart and soul, you are an artist. 如果你用双手工作，你是一个劳力；如果你用双手和头脑工作，你是一个工匠；如果你用双手和头脑工作，并且全身心投入，你就是一个艺术家。 ——美国电影American Heart and Soul “生如夏花，在追求结果绚烂的同时享受过程中的美好。” （杨璐，2008年10月，Verbal 800, Quantitative 800, AW 5.5, 录取学校：Massachusetts Inst. of Technology）
k.en: to stir to action or feeling

ex.en: rankings that are sure to provoke an argument among film critics
ex.cn: 必将引发影评家们争论的排名

m: PRO + VOKE (call, Latin VOCARE) — call forth (anger / response). — provoke, to incite.

k.syn: bravery, courageousness, daring, gallantry, guts, intrepidity
k.ant: timid, cowardice, cravenness, dastardliness, poltroonery 胆小，懦弱
k.en: superior strength, courage, or daring, especially in battle

ex.en: his prowess on the football field
ex.cn: 他在球场上过人的勇气

m: PROWESS contains PROW (brave, Old French PROU = valiant) — bravery / skill. — prowess, exceptional skill.

k.cn: 潜行于，巡游以猎取食物；徘徊
k.en: to roam through stealthily, as in search of prey or plunder

ex.en: I prowled the shop looking for sales.
ex.cn: 我在商店里转悠，寻找打折商品。

m: PROWL rhymes with HOWL — predators PROWL by night. — prowl, to roam stealthily.

k.syn: bluenose, moralist, puritan
k.ant: immoralist 不道德的人
k.en: 一个过分关心自己是否得体、谦逊或正确的人：a person who is greatly concerned with seemly behavior and morality especially regarding sexual matters

ex.en: The racy sitcom frequently satirizes exactly the sort of prude who would like to see the show taken off the air.
ex.cn: 这部活泼生动的情景喜剧正是在讽刺那种过分正经的人，他们希望取消这种娱乐性节目。

m: PRUDE rhymes with RUDE but the opposite — overly proper. — prude, an overly proper person.

k.syn: advisable, desirable, judicious, politic, tactical, intelligent
k.ant: foolish 傻的
k.en: marked by wisdom or judiciousness; wise

ex.en: prudent advice
ex.cn: 明智的建议

k.en: marked by circumspection

m: PRUDENT shares PRU- with PROVIDENT — looking ahead carefully. — prudent, sensibly cautious.

k.syn: nice-nelly, prim, puritanical
k.en: marked by prudery

ex.en: By the prudish standards of the 19th century, any depiction of the nude was scandalous.
ex.cn: 根据19世纪的保守标准，任何对裸露的刻画描述都是下流无耻的。

m: from PRUDE — overly proper. — prudish, primly proper.

k.en: a plum dried or capable of drying without

m: to PRUNE = cut back (as you cut PRUNES from a tree). — prune, to trim.

k.syn: interlope, intermeddle, intrude, nose, obtrude, snoop
k.en: to look or inquire closely, curiously, or impertinently

ex.en: Don't go prying into other people's business.
ex.cn: 别去打探别人的事。

m: PRY rhymes with SPY — both nose into others' affairs. — pry, to investigate intrusively.

k.syn: alias, nom de guerre
k.en: a fictitious name

ex.en: Mark Twain is the pseudonym of the American writer Samuel L. Clemens.
ex.cn: 马克·吐温是美国作家塞缪尔·L·克莱门斯的笔名。

m: PSEUDO (false, Greek) + NYM (name) — a false name. — pseudonym, a fake name.

k.en: the science that deals with mental processes and behavior

ex.en: She studied psychology in college.
ex.cn: 她大学专业是心理学。

k.en: subtle tactical action or argument used to manipulate or influence another

ex.en: He used poor psychology on his employer when trying to make the point.
ex.cn: 他表达观点时，对老板用了拙劣的心理战术。

m: PSYCHE (mind, Greek) + LOGY (study) — study of the mind. — psychology, mind science / mental tactics.

k.syn: devilish, impish, prankish, waggish
k.ant: sober, grave, staid 严肃的
k.en: mischievous; impish

ex.en: She had a puckish smile on her face.
ex.cn: 她脸上挂着顽皮的笑容。

m: PUCKISH — like the mischievous fairy PUCK in "A Midsummer Night's Dream." — puckish, mischievously playful.

k.syn: adolescent, green, immature, unfledged, unformed, unripened
k.ant: adult, experienced, grown-up, mature, ripe, sagacious 睿智的，成熟的
k.en: immature; lacking in adult experience or maturity

ex.en: puerile remarks
ex.cn: 幼稚的评论

m: PUERILE contains PUER (boy, Latin) — boyishly immature. — puerile, childish.

k.syn: force, might, potency, strength, vigor, sinew
k.ant: powerlessness, impotence, weakness 无力
k.en: power; might

ex.en: The president pledged to put the full puissance of the nation into the war effort.
ex.cn: 总统下令全国进入战争状态。

m: PUISSANCE contains PUISS ≈ POWER (French PUISSANCE = power). — puissance, power.

k.ant: ugliness, homeliness, hideousness 丑陋，平庸
k.en: great physical beauty and appeal

m: PULCHRITUDE contains PULCHR (beautiful, Latin PULCHER) — beauty. — pulchritude, beauty.

k.ant: solidify, build, construct, erect, put up, raise, rear, set up 硬化，构建
k.cn: 将…研磨成粉；将…彻底摧毁
k.en: to pound, crush or grind to powder or dust; to bring to a complete end the physical soundness, existence, or usefulness of

ex.en: Bits of pulverized rock filled the air.
ex.cn: 被碾碎的岩石碎片充斥在空气里。

m: PULVERIZE contains PULVER (dust, Latin PULVIS) — turn to dust. — pulverize, to crush to powder.

k.en: the usually humorous use of a word in such a way as to suggest two or more of its meanings or the meaning of another word similar in sound

ex.en: He's a skillful pilot whose career has—no pun intended—really taken off.
ex.cn: 她是个技术娴熟的飞行员，飞行生涯——没有别的意思——真正腾飞了。

m: PUN rhymes with FUN — wordplay for FUN. — pun, a wordplay joke.

k.syn: decorous, proper, starchy, stilted
k.ant: remiss, slipshod, casual, easygoing, informal, laid-back, unceremonious 粗心的
k.cn: 注意细节的，一丝不苟的
k.en: strictly attentive to minute details of form in action or conduct

m: PUNCTILIOUS contains PUNCT (point, Latin) — attentive to every fine point. — punctilious, fussily detail-oriented.

k.syn: savant, scholar
k.en: a person who gives opinions in an authoritative manner usually through the mass media

ex.en: The new laptop has gotten a thumbs-up from industry pundits.
ex.cn: 新出的笔记本电脑受到了业界专家的一致好评。

m: PUNDIT — Hindi PANDIT = scholar / wise teacher. — pundit, an expert commentator.

k.syn: acerbic, acid, acrid, caustic, mordant, sardonic, scalding, scathing
k.en: marked by the use of wit that is intended to cause hurt feelings

ex.en: pungent language
ex.cn: 辛辣讽刺的语言

m: PUNGENT contains PUNG (prick, Latin PUNGERE) — sharp enough to prick. (Same root as PUNCTURE.) — pungent, sharply piercing.

k.syn: diminutive, dwarfish, slight, subnormal
k.ant: enormous, considerable, grand, husky, king-size, outsize, overscale, substantial, whacking, whopping 巨大的
k.en: of inferior size, strength, or significance; weak

ex.en: I wouldn't mess with him—he makes bodybuilders look puny in comparison.
ex.cn: 我不敢招惹他——其他健身的人和他比起来都像小孩。

m: PUNY rhymes with TINY — both small. — puny, weak / small.

k.syn: chasteness, immaculacy, innocence, modesty
k.ant: impurity, unchasteness, unchastity 堕落，没有节操
k.der: impurity n. 杂质；purist n. 纯粹主义者
k.en: the quality or state of being clean; the quality or state of being morally pure

ex.en: struggle to live a life of purity while surrounded by wickedness
ex.cn: 即使周围充满邪恶，也要使自己的生命纯粹、完美

m: PURITY contains PURE — being pure. — purity, the state of being pure.

k.syn: haunt, rendezvous, resort, stamping ground, stomping ground
k.en: a place for spending time or for socializing

ex.en: The restaurant, the preferred purlieu of the theatergoing crowd, is always packed an hour or two before showtime.
ex.cn: 观剧的人群经常去的那间餐厅总是在演出开始前一两个小时爆满。

k.syn: backyard, neighborhood, vicinage, vicinity
k.en: an adjoining region or space

ex.en: We stopped at one of the several pubs in the purlieus of the stadium.
ex.cn: 我们在体育馆附近的一间酒吧前停下来。

m: PURLIEU contains PUR + LIEU (place, French) — "on its borders" / one's haunts. — purlieu, a frequented area.

k.syn: appropriate, filch, pilfer, snitch, thieve
k.en: to steal, often in a violation of trust

ex.en: fearing that someone might attempt to purloin a copy of the script for the show's season finale
ex.cn: 害怕该剧的完季那集剧本被盗

m: PURLOIN contains PUR + LOIN ≈ "put far away" (Old French PORLOIGNIER) — set apart for yourself, steal. — purloin, to steal.

k.en: to supply（food, for example）; furnish

ex.en: a little shop purveying handmade merchandise
ex.cn: 出售手工商品的小店

m: PURVEY contains PUR + VEY (see, Latin VIDERE) — see ahead and provide. (Same root as PROVIDE.) — purvey, to supply.

k.syn: craven, dastardly, gutless, poltroon, recreant, spineless
k.ant: brave, courageous, daring, dauntless, doughty, gallant, gutsy, intrepid, lionhearted, stalwart, stout, stouthearted, valiant, valorous 勇敢的
k.cn: 懦弱的，胆小得令人鄙视的
k.en: lacking courage and resolution, marked by contemptible timidity

ex.en: pusillanimous politicians who vote according to whichever way the political wind is blowing
ex.cn: 胆小懦弱的政客，舆论风吹向谁就投票给谁

m: PUSILL (very small, Latin) + ANIM (mind/spirit, Latin) — having a tiny spirit. — pusillanimous, cowardly.

k.syn: charlatan, fake, fraud, hoaxer, mountebank, phony
k.ant: honest practitioner 诚实从业者
k.en: a pretender to medical skill

ex.en: Don't bother to see that guy, as I've heard he's a quack with no actual training.
ex.cn: 别再找他看病了，我听说他就是个没有真才实学的江湖郎中。

m: a QUACK doctor — quacks like a duck claiming false credentials. — quack, a fake doctor.

k.syn: gulp, swig, swill
k.en: to drink（a beverage）heartily

ex.en: He stopped at a bar and quaffed a few beers.
ex.cn: 他在酒吧停下来，痛饮了几杯啤酒。

m: QUAFF rhymes with LAUGH — drink with hearty laughter. — quaff, to drink heartily.

k.syn: blench, cringe, recoil, shrink, wince
k.ant: become resolute, give bold 变得坚决、大胆
k.en: to shrink back in fear; cower

ex.en: She quailed at the thought of seeing him again.
ex.cn: 她想到还要再见他就害怕。

m: to QUAIL = to shrink and tremble like a small QUAIL bird startled. — quail, to lose courage.

k.der: qualified adj. 有限制的，不完全的
k.ant: unreserved, absolute, categorical 无保留的，不受限制的
k.en: to reduce from a general to a particular or restricted form

ex.en: qualified support
ex.cn: 有限的支持

k.syn: equip, fit, prepare, ready, season, train
k.en: to make competent（as by training, skill, or ability）for a particular office or function

ex.en: Raising five children has qualified her to be an advice columnist on parenting.
ex.cn: 抚养五个孩子的经历让她成为了育儿板块的专栏作家。

m: QUALIFY contains QUAL ≈ "quality" (Latin QUALIS = of what kind) — confirm someone has the right quality. — qualify, to limit / make eligible.

k.syn: catch-22, double bind
k.ant: state of complete certainty 胸有成竹
k.en: a state of perplexity or doubt

ex.en: I've had two job offers, and I'm in a real quandary about/over which one to accept.
ex.cn: 我有两个工作机会,但实在是进退两难，不知道应该选哪一个。

m: QUANDARY rhymes with BOUNDARY — stuck at a confusing boundary, uncertain which way to turn. — quandary, a state of confusion.

k.en: enforced isolation or restriction of free movement imposed to prevent the spread of contagious disease

ex.en: The cows will be kept in quarantine for another two weeks.
ex.cn: 牛群还需要被隔离观察两周。

m: QUARANTINE contains QUAR (forty, Latin QUADRAGINTA) — original 40-day isolation for plague-ship sailors. — quarantine, isolation period.

k.en: an open excavation or pit from which stone is obtained by digging, cutting, or blasting

k.syn: prey
k.ant: predator 捕猎者
k.en: an object of pursuit

ex.en: a hunter relentlessly tracking his quarry
ex.cn: 无情捕杀猎物的猎人

m: a QUARRY is a stone-cutting pit; also "the QUARRY" = the prey hunted. (Two senses, same word.) — quarry, a stone pit / hunted target.

k.syn: repress, squelch, subdue, suppress, clamp down on, crack down on
k.ant: engender, foment 引起，煽动
k.en: to put a stop to（something）by the use of force

ex.en: quash a rebellion
ex.cn: 镇压一次叛变

m: QUASH rhymes with SQUASH — both crush flat. — quash, to forcefully suppress.

k.syn: tremble，trill
k.en: to speak in a quivering voice; utter a quivering sound

ex.en: His voice quavered during the speech.
ex.cn: 整个演讲过程中，他的声音都是颤抖的。

m: QUAVER rhymes with WAVER — both shake / tremble. — quaver, to tremble (especially in voice).

k.syn: quash, repress, squash, squelch, subdue, suppress, clamp down on, crack down on
k.en: to put down forcibly; suppress

ex.en: quell riot
ex.cn: 镇压骚乱

k.syn: dumb, extinguish, hush, mute, settle
k.ant: foment, instigate, rouse, incite 煽动
k.en: to pacify; quiet

ex.en: quell fears
ex.cn: 减轻害怕

m: QUELL rhymes with QUIET / WELL — push the unrest down, calm it. — quell, to suppress.

k.syn: blanket, douse, put out, snuff out
k.ant: fire, ignite, inflame, kindle, light 点燃
k.en: to put out（a fire, for example）; extinguish

ex.en: We thoroughly quenched the campfire before we headed to bed.
ex.cn: 我们在睡前把营火完全熄灭了。

k.syn: assuage, sate, satiate, slake
k.en: to put a complete end to（a physical need or desire）

ex.en: This lemonade really quenches my thirst.
ex.cn: 这杯柠檬汁真是解渴。

m: QUENCH rhymes with WRENCH — wrench/extinguish a fire or thirst. — quench, to extinguish / satisfy.

k.syn: crabby, cranky, grouchy, grumpy
k.ant: forbearing, long-suffering, patient, stoic, tolerant, uncomplaining 容忍的
k.en: habitually complaining

ex.en: a querulous voice
ex.cn: 抱怨的声音

m: QUERULOUS contains QUER ≈ "query" — but with constant complaint instead of question. (Latin QUERI = complain.) — querulous, complaining.

k.en: a line of waiting people

m: QUEUE — a long line (literally "tail" in French QUEUE). — queue, a line of people.

k.syn: carp, cavil, fuss, niggle, nitpick
k.en: to find fault or criticize for petty reasons; cavil

ex.en: She spent the entire evening quibbling about the historical inaccuracies in the television series on World War Ⅱ.
ex.cn: 她一整晚都在对那部关于二战的电视剧里的历史错误吹毛求疵。

k.en: 微不足道的差别或不切中要点的异议，小反对：a minor objection or criticism

ex.en: My only quibble about the trip was that it rained a lot.
ex.cn: 我对这次旅行唯一的小不满就是下雨有点多。

m: QUIBBLE rhymes with NIBBLE — both small picky bites. — quibble, to make petty objections.

k.syn: dull, inert, lethargic, sluggish, torpid
k.ant: rambunctious, tumultuous, active, restlessly active 骚乱的
k.en: being quiet, still, or at rest; inactive

ex.en: a group of quiescent loungers
ex.cn: 一群平静懒散的流浪汉

m: QUIESCENT contains QUI ≈ QUIET (Latin QUIES = rest) — at rest. — quiescent, dormant / still.

k.syn: impractical, ideal, romantic, starry, utopian, visionary
k.ant: clear-eyed, clear-sighted 聪明的
k.en: having or marked by a tendency to be guided more by ideals than by reality

ex.en: She had quixotic dreams about the future.
ex.cn: 她对未来有着幻想。

m: QUIXOTIC comes from Don QUIXOTE, who tilted at windmills — chasing impractical ideals. — quixotic, idealistically impractical.

k.syn: allowance, portion, proportion
k.ant: unlimited number 不限数量
k.en: a proportional part or share

ex.en: The department set new sales quotas in May.
ex.cn: 部门设定了五月的销售定额。

m: QUOTA contains QUOT (how much, Latin QUOT) — assigned share. (Same root as QUOTE.) — quota, an allotted share.

k.syn: commonplace, everyday, frequent, ordinary, routine, ubiquitous
k.ant: remarkable, striking, extraordinary, unusual, rare, infrequent, seldom 不平常的
k.en: everyday; commonplace

ex.en: plagued by a quotidian coughing
ex.cn: 每天被咳嗽困扰

m: QUOTIDIAN contains QUOTI (every, Latin) + DIAN (day, Latin DIES) — every-day, mundane. — quotidian, everyday / commonplace.

k.syn: ragtag and bobtail, riffraff, scum, rag, trash, unwashed
k.cn: 混乱的人群；暴民；下层民众
k.en: a disorganized or disorderly crowd of people; the lowest class of people

ex.en: The crown prince was reminded that even the rabble deserved his attention and compassion.
ex.cn: 太子应该知道即使是草民，也值得他的关注和同情。

m: RABBLE rhymes with BABBLE — a babbling unruly crowd. — rabble, a disorderly crowd.

k.syn: delirious, ferocious, feverish, fierce, frantic, frenetic, violent
k.ant: logical, detached 合常理的，客观的
k.en: extremely zealous or enthusiastic; fanatical

ex.en: soccer fans whose rabid enthusiasm makes them go berserk when their team wins
ex.cn: 不冷静的粉丝们在球队获胜以后变得很狂暴

m: RABID — Latin RABIDUS = raging (rabies disease causes raging behavior). — rabid, fanatic.

k.en: a person skilled at telling stories and anecdotes entertainingly

m: RACONTEUR — French for storyteller (Latin RACONTARE = recount). — raconteur, a skilled storyteller.

k.syn: animate, brisk, frisky, jaunty, perky, spirited, vivacious
k.ant: tame, dead, inactive, inanimate, lackadaisical, languid, languorous, leaden, lifeless, limp, listless, spiritless, vapid 乏味的，死气沉沉的
k.en: vigorous; lively

ex.en: vivid writing and a racy plot that keeps readers turning the pages
ex.cn: 栩栩如生的手法和生动新鲜的情节让读者手不释卷

m: RACY contains RACE — fast-paced and lively (or risqué). — racy, lively / risqué.

k.syn: crass, lowbred, uncouth, uncultivated, uncultured, unpolished, unrefined, vulgar
k.ant: civilized, cultivated, cultured, genteel, polished, refined, ultrarefined, well-bred 有教养的
k.en: marked by or suggestive of flashy vulgarity or crudeness

ex.en: The dowager cringed at the thought of raffish tourists tromping all over her Persian rugs.
ex.cn: 孀居贵妇一想到低俗的游客践踏她的波斯地毯就痛苦不已。

m: RAFFISH rhymes with RAFFLE — but disreputable: a charmingly rakish vibe. — raffish, disreputably stylish.

k.syn: chaff, deadwood, debris, dreck, dross, effluvium, litter, offal, offscouring, refuse, riffraff, rubbish, scrap, spilth
k.en: discarded or useless material

ex.en: The front lawn was littered with the raffle that the workers had left behind.
ex.cn: 前院的草坪上满是工人们丢的垃圾。

m: RAFFLE — Old French RAFLE = act of seizing/sweeping up; figuratively, swept-up trash. — raffle, junk / sweepings.

k.en: a beam forming part of the internal framework of a roof

m: RAFTER contains RAFT — like a RAFT but overhead, holding up the roof. — rafter, a roof beam.

k.syn: agitation, deliriousness, delirium, distraction, furor, hysteria, rampage
k.en: violent and uncontrolled anger

ex.en: Her rages rarely last more than a few minutes.
ex.cn: 她的暴躁来得快去得也快。

m: RAGE rhymes with CAGE — the caged person RAGEs furiously. — rage, to be furious.

k.syn: raggedy, ragtag, tattered, tatterdemalion, shabby
k.ant: kempt, neat, trim 整洁的
k.en: dressed in tattered or threadbare clothes

ex.en: A ragged coat may cover an honest man.
ex.cn: 不能以衣著取人。

k.syn: broken, bumpy, coarse, irregular, jagged, lumpy, pebbly, rough, roughened, rugged, scraggy
k.ant: even, flat, level, plane 平整的；smooth 光滑的
k.en: not having a level or smooth surface

ex.en: She cut herself on the ragged edge of the tin can's lid.
ex.cn: 她被罐头盖的锋利边缘割伤了。

m: RAGGED contains RAG — full of RAGs / tatters; also rough / uneven. — ragged, tattered / rough.

k.syn: baste, berate, castigate, chastise, hammer, lambaste, rebuke, reprimand, reproach, upbraid
k.ant: accredit, applaud, commend, eulogize, extol, laud, praise 表扬，赞扬
k.en: to revile or scold in harsh, insolent, or abusive language

ex.en: We could hear the cook in the kitchen railing against his assistant and wondered if we'd ever get our food.
ex.cn: 我们可以听到厨师在厨房怒斥他的助手，由此我们怀疑我们点的菜还能不能做好。

m: to RAIL against = to angrily attack with words (loud as a clattering RAIL-line). — rail, to fiercely complain.

k.en: a dried grape

m: RAISIN — a dried grape (Latin RACEMUS = cluster of grapes). — raisin, a dried grape.

k.syn: debased, debauched, demoralized, depraved, dissolute, libertine, perverse, perverted, reprobate
k.ant: good, moral, pure, righteous, virtuous 有美好品德的；uncorrupted 不放荡的
k.der: rake n. 行为不检点的人，放荡的人
k.en: having or showing lowered moral character or standards; dissolute

ex.en: He wore his hat at a rakish angle.
ex.cn: 他帽子戴得吊儿郎当的。

m: RAKISH contains RAKE — a RAKE was a debauched gentleman. — rakish, dissolute / dapperly stylish.

k.syn: amble, cruise, drift, float, meander, perambulate, roam, saunter, stroll, traipse, wander
k.en: to move aimlessly from place to place

ex.en: ramble under the starlight
ex.cn: 在星光下漫步

ex.en: ramble around Beijing for a week
ex.cn: 在北京四处漫游一周

k.syn: blather, chat, chatter, drivel, maunder, patter, prattle
k.cn: 漫谈；长篇大论（并经常离题）地说或写
k.en: to talk at length without sticking to a topic or getting to a point

ex.en: ramble on about dating, homework, movies, and the local football team
ex.cn: 扯一些关于约会、作业、电影以及本地橄榄球队的闲谈

m: RAMBLE rhymes with AMBLE — both leisurely walks. (Same root as AMBLE.) — ramble, to wander / talk aimlessly.

k.syn: boisterous, clamorous, raucous, riotous, rowdy, tumultuous, turbulent
k.ant: orderly 有秩序的；calm, noiseless, peaceful, placid, quiet, serene, silent, soundless, tranquil 安静的
k.en: being rough or noisy in a high-spirited way

ex.en: That beach is often taken over by packs of rambunctious young people, so don't go there expecting peace and quiet.
ex.cn: 那个海滩经常被一帮吵闹的年轻人占领，所以不要指望过去能享受到平静和安宁。

m: RAMBUNCTIOUS contains RAM — like a young RAM bouncing around. — rambunctious, boisterously energetic.

k.en: a broad defensive wall or embankment built to fortify a place

m: RAMPART contains RAM — fortification walls thick enough to RAM into. — rampart, a fortification wall.

k.syn: rickety, shaky, tottering
k.ant: concrete, firm, solid, sturdy 坚固的；stable稳定的
k.en: appearing ready to collapse; rickety

ex.en: a ramshackle cabin in the woods
ex.cn: 树林中摇摇欲坠的小木屋

m: RAMSHACKLE contains SHACK — a rickety SHACK that looks barely standing. — ramshackle, poorly built / rickety.

k.syn: animosity, animus, antagonism, antipathy, bitterness, gall, grudge, hostility, jaundice
k.ant: amity, harmony, goodwill, rapport, rapprochement 和睦，友好； friendship 友谊
k.en: a bitter deep-seated ill will

ex.en: A good man terminates a friendship without rancor.
ex.cn: 君子绝交不记仇。

m: RANCOR contains RANC (rancid, Latin RANCERE) — bitter, rotten ill will. — rancor, bitter resentment.

k.en: select, assign, or arrange in a random

m: from RANDOM — make random. — randomize, to make random.

k.en: a person employed to patrol and protect a forest or park

m: RANGER contains RANGE — one who patrols a wide RANGE of land. — ranger, a forest patroller.

k.syn: aggravate, enrage, exasperate, incense, inflame, infuriate, ire, madden, rile, roil
k.ant: delight, gratify, please 使愉悦；appease, assuage, conciliate, mollify, placate, propitiate 安抚
k.en: to cause anger, irritation, or deep bitterness

ex.en: It rankles me when some schools can't even afford paper and pencils for the students.
ex.cn: 当我获悉有些学校连纸张和铅笔都无法提供给学生时，我感到十分愤怒。

m: RANKLE contains RANK (festering wound, Old French RANCLE) — fester with anger. — rankle, to cause persistent annoyance.

k.syn: castigation, diatribe, harangue, lambasting, philippic, reprimand, reproach, vituperation
k.ant: encomium, eulogy, panegyric, rhapsody, tribute 赞美之词
k.en: a long angry speech or scolding

ex.en: After complaining about the hotel's lousy service, the woman went off on another rant about the condition of her room.
ex.cn: 在抱怨完旅店差劲的服务之后，她继续对房间的条件开骂。

k.syn: bluster, fulminate, huff, rave, roar, spout
k.ant: grumble, murmur, mutter 低声抱怨
k.en: to speak or write in a noisy, angry or violent manner

ex.en: The old expert ranted that nobody paid any attention to his opinion.
ex.cn: 老专家怒气冲冲地叫嚷说没人听取他的观点。

m: RANT rhymes with CHANT but angry — a chant of anger. — rant, to angrily declaim.

k.syn: edacious, esurient, gluttonous, greedy, ravenous, voracious
k.en: having a huge appetite

ex.en: The manager at the buffet restaurant was apparently astonished by a team of rapacious professional basketball players.
ex.cn: 自助餐厅的经理很明显被那一队胃口巨大的职业篮球队员们震惊了。

k.syn: acquisitive, avaricious, avid, coveting, covetous, grasping, mercenary, moneygrubbing
k.ant: content, sated, satiated, satisfied 心满意足的
k.en: excessively grasping or covetous

ex.en: Some companies are rapacious and hardly looking for the long-term value.
ex.cn: 有些公司十分贪婪，而且不考虑长期利益。

m: RAPACIOUS contains RAP (seize, Latin RAPERE) — grabbing-greedy. (Same root as RAVENOUS, RAPTOR.) — rapacious, voraciously greedy.

k.syn: amity, communion, concord, fellowship, harmony, rapprochement
k.ant: animosity, antagonism, antipathy, bitterness, enmity, hostility, jaundice, rancor 敌意
k.en: a friendly relationship marked by ready communication and mutual understanding

ex.en: His good rapport with his students was one of the reasons why the school board named him Teacher of the Year.
ex.cn: 他和学生的关系和睦是他被校董会提名为“年度教师”的原因之一。

m: RAPPORT — French for relationship / connection. (RE + PORTER = bring back.) — rapport, harmonious relationship.

k.syn: amity, communion, concord, fellowship, harmony, rapport
k.ant: animosity, antagonism, antipathy, bitterness, enmity, hostility, jaundice, rancor 敌意
k.en: establishment of or state of having cordial relations

ex.en: a new era of rapprochement between China and Russia
ex.cn: 中俄两国睦邻友好关系的新纪元

m: RAPPROCHEMENT — French for "bringing closer." (RE + APPROACH.) — rapprochement, restoration of harmony.

k.syn: brute, devil, evildoer, fiend, knave, miscreant, rascal, reprobate, rogue, savage, scamp, varlet, wretch
k.ant: saint 圣人；cavalier, chevalier（尤指对女士）彬彬有礼的绅士
k.en: a mean, evil, or unprincipled person

ex.en: an unsafe place frequented by drunkards and rapscallions
ex.cn: 一个酒鬼和流氓经常造访的不安全的地方

m: RAPSCALLION rhymes with STALLION — an unruly stallion-like rascal. — rapscallion, a mischievous rascal.

k.syn: elated, elevated, enraptured, entranced, euphoric, exhilarated, intoxicated, rapturous, rhapsodic
k.ant: crestfallen, dejected, depressed 沮丧的；doleful, gloomy, melancholy, mournful, woeful 哀伤的
k.en: experiencing or marked by overwhelming usually pleasurable emotion

ex.en: a rock band that still attracts rapt crowds
ex.cn: 一个仍然拥有着狂热粉丝的摇滚乐队

k.syn: absorbed, concentrated, deep, engrossed, enthralled, focused, immersed, intent, observant
k.ant: absent, abstracted, distracted, inattentive 不专心的；apathetic, disinterested, unconcerned 冷漠的，不关心的
k.en: deeply absorbed; engrossed

ex.en: With a mixture of delight and awe, the rapt children stared at the chick in the incubator breaking out of its shell.
ex.cn: 怀着愉快和敬畏的复杂心理，孩子们全神贯注地盯着孵化箱中的小鸡破壳而出。

m: RAPT rhymes with CAPT(URED) — your attention is captured / seized. (Latin RAPTUS = seized.) — rapt, deeply absorbed.

k.syn: attenuate, dilute, thin
k.ant: concentrate, condense 使稠密，浓缩
k.en: to make rare, thin, porous, or less dense: to expand without the addition of matter

ex.en: rarefy the air
ex.cn: 使空气变得稀薄

m: RARE + FY — make RAREr / thinner. — rarefy, to make thin / refined.

k.syn: cursory, gadarene, headlong, hurried, overhasty, precipitate, precipitous, rushed
k.ant: deliberate 慎重考虑的；leisurely, unhurried, unrushed 从容不迫的，不慌不忙的
k.der: rashly adv. 草率地，仓促地
k.en: marked by or proceeding from undue haste or lack of deliberation or caution

ex.en: That was too rash a move: for now you've lost your bishop and probably the whole chess game.
ex.cn: 那一招棋着实是太草率了，现在你丢掉了象，很有可能会输掉整场比赛。

m: a RASH decision = made hastily, like skin breaking out fast. (Same word, two senses.) — rash, hasty / impulsive.

k.syn: cacophonous, coarse, croaky, harsh, grating, gravelly, gruff, husky, rasping, rusty, scratchy, throaty
k.ant: mellifluous, sweet 声音甜美的；gentle, soft, tender 声音轻柔的
k.en: harsh and dry in sound
