ex.en: The dying man was speaking in a raspy and barely discernible voice.
ex.cn: 将死的人在用一种沙哑刺耳且难以听清的语调说话。

k.syn: choleric, fiery, grouchy, irascible, peevish, pettish, petulant, prickly, ratty, testy
k.ant: agreeable, amiable, good-natured, good-tempered, well-disposed 脾气性格好的
k.en: easily irritated or annoyed

ex.en: Overwork tends to make him raspy.
ex.cn: 过度的工作让他变得烦躁易怒。

m: RASPY contains RASP — sounding like a RASP (file scraping). — raspy, harsh-sounding.

k.syn: accredit, approbate, authorize, certify, endorse, finalize, formalize, pass, sanction, validate, warrant
k.ant: decline, deny, disallow, disapprove, negative, reject, veto 否决
k.en: to give official acceptance of as satisfactory

ex.en: Lincoln's home state of Illinois was the first to ratify the 13th Amendment to the U.S. Constitution, which provided for the abolition of slavery.
ex.cn: 林肯的老家——伊利诺伊是美国第一个通过《宪法第十三修正案》的州，该决案提供了废除奴隶制的法律基础。

m: RATIFY contains RAT ≈ "ratio / reason" (Latin RATUS = reckoned, fixed) — formally fix in agreement. — ratify, to formally approve.

k.syn: deduction, intellection, reason, reasoning, sense
k.en: the thought processes that have been established as leading to valid solutions to problems

ex.en: As an expert in ratiocination, the detective Sherlock Holmes has few rivals.
ex.cn: 作为推理专家，大侦探福尔摩斯可谓无人能及。

m: RATIO (reasoning, Latin) + CINATION — the process of reasoning. — ratiocination, logical reasoning.

k.syn: allotment, allowance, apportionment, portion, provision, quota, share
k.en: an amount allotted or made available especially from a limited supply

ex.en: The meat ration was down to one pound per person per week.
ex.cn: 肉的人均供给量降至了每周一磅。

k.syn: allocate, allot, allow, apportion, assign, distribute, lot
k.ant: keep, retain, withhold 扣留，拒绝给予
k.en: to give as a share or portion

ex.en: The region has had to ration water during times of drought.
ex.cn: 干旱时该地区不得不按比例分配水资源。

m: RATION contains RATIO — a measured ratio of supplies. — ration, a measured allotment.

k.en: rationalize the problem 使问题合理化

ex.en: rationalize the problem
ex.cn: 使问题合理化

m: from RATIONAL — make rational. — rationalize, to justify with reasons.

k.syn: drool, effuse, enthuse, fuss, rhapsodize, slobber
k.ant: blame, censure, condemn, criticize, denounce, pan, reprehend 批评，指责
k.en: to make an exaggerated display of affection or enthusiasm

ex.en: Critics raved about the new play.
ex.cn: 新剧得到了评论家们的热烈赞扬。

k.syn: bluster, fulminate, huff, rant, roar, spout
k.ant: grumble, murmur, mutter 小声抱怨
k.en: to talk irrationally and wildly in or as if in delirium

ex.en: a man standing outside the city hall, raving like a lunatic about his tax bill
ex.cn: 站在市政大厅外、像疯子一样叫嚷着自己税单的人

m: to RAVE about a movie = enthusiastic praise; or RAVE = rant wildly. — rave, to enthuse / rant.

k.syn: disentangle, extricate, unbraid, unsnarl, untwine, unweave
k.ant: braid, knit, plait, weave 编织
k.en: to separate the various strands of

ex.en: Since the sweater is too small, you could ravel the yarnout and make something else with it.
ex.cn: 既然这个毛衣太小，你可以把它拆了然后织些新的东西。

k.syn: clarify, clear, elucidate, untangle, unravel
k.ant: complex, complicate, entangle, perplex, sophisticate, snarl, tangle 使复杂化
k.en: to clarify by separating the aspects of

k.syn: complex, complicate, entangle, perplex, sophisticate, snarl, tangle
k.ant: clarify, clear, elucidate, untangle, unravel 阐明
k.en: to tangle or complicate

ex.en: a ravelled story
ex.cn: 一个复杂的故事

m: to RAVEL is tricky: it can mean both unravel (loose threads) and tangle. (Same word, two senses.) — ravel, to unravel / tangle.

k.syn: captivate, enchant, enrapture, enthrall, rapture, transport
k.ant: disenchant, sober 使清醒
k.en: to overcome with emotion（as wonder or delight）

ex.en: I was completely ravished by those marvelous pictures taken by HST.
ex.cn: 用哈勃太空望远镜拍摄的那些壮观图片让我彻底地陶醉了。

m: RAVISH shares RAV- with RAPT (seized) — to seize with delight. — ravish, to fill with delight.

k.syn: annihilate, decimate, demolish, devastate, level, nuke, pulverize, ruin, smash, vaporize, wreck, tear down
k.ant: build, construct, erect, raise, rear, set up 建造
k.en: to destroy completely by or as if by knocking down or breaking to pieces

ex.en: Excavators were began to raze old school building.
ex.cn: 挖土机开始夷平学校的老建筑。

ex.en: An entire city block was razed by a terrible fire.
ex.cn: 大火摧毁了整个街区。

m: RAZE rhymes with RAISE but the OPPOSITE — instead of raising buildings, RAZE knocks them flat. — raze, to demolish.

k.en: a substance that enters into and is altered in the course of a chemical

m: REACTANT contains REACT — a substance that REACTs. — reactant, a chemical that reacts.

k.ant: liberal 允许变革的；progressive 进步的；radical, aggressive 激进的
k.syn: brassbound, conservative, die-hard, hidebound, traditionalistic, ultraconservative

ex.en: reactionary rulers
ex.cn: 保守的统治者

m: REACTIONARY contains REACT — one who REACTs against progress, wanting old ways back. — reactionary, opposing change.

k.syn: baste, berate, castigate, chastise, hammer, lambaste, rail, rebuke, reprimand, reproach, upbraid
k.ant: accredit, applaud, commend, eulogize, extol, laud, praise 表扬，赞扬
k.en: to criticize（someone）severely or angrily especially for personal failings

ex.en: You are so going to get reamed out when the boss learns that you wrecked the company car.
ex.cn: 要是老板知道你把公司的车弄坏了，你肯定会挨骂的。

m: to REAM someone out = bore them out with criticism (a REAMER drills holes). — ream, to scold harshly.

k.syn: acquire, attain, capture, harvest, gain, garner, obtain, procure, secure
k.ant: plant, seed, sow 种植，播种；forfeit, lose 丧失，没收
k.en: to collect（a crop or natural resource）or to receive as return for effort

ex.en: As you sow, so shall you reap.
ex.cn: 唯有付出，才有回报。

ex.en: She reaped large profits from her patents.
ex.cn: 她的专利给她带来了丰厚的回报。

m: REAP rhymes with HEAP — gather a HEAP of harvest. — reap, to harvest.

k.syn: assure, cheer, console, solace, soothe
k.ant: distress, torment, torture, trouble 使忧虑，折磨
k.en: to restore to confidence

ex.en: I tried to reassure her that the dog would come back home by nightfall.
ex.cn: 我尽力让她安心，说夜幕降临之前小狗一定会回家的。

m: RE (again) + ASSURE — assure again, calm. — reassure, to comfort.

k.syn: decline, deny, refuse, reject, repudiate, repulse, snub, spurn
k.ant: accept 接受；approve 赞同；welcome 欢迎
k.en: to reject or criticize sharply

ex.en: She rebuffed an invitation from her colleagues.
ex.cn: 她回绝了同事们的邀请。

ex.en: She rebuffed him when he asked her for a date.
ex.cn: 她拒绝了他的约会邀请。

m: REBUFF contains RE + BUFF ≈ "BUFFet" (a hard slap) — a hard rejection. — rebuff, a sharp refusal.

k.syn: balky, contumacious, defiant, incompliant, insubordinate, intractable, obstreperous, rebellious, refractory, unruly
k.ant: amenable, compliant, conformable, docile, governable, obedient, ruly, submissive, tractable 服从的，顺从的
k.der: recalcitrance n. 固执，不服从命令
k.en: marked by stubborn resistance to and defiance of authority or guidance

ex.en: a recalcitrant teenager
ex.cn: 执拗的少年

ex.en: The manager worried that the recalcitrant employee would try to undermine his authority.
ex.cn: 经理担心那些不服管理的员工会削弱他的权威。

m: RECALCITRANT contains CALCITR (kick, Latin CALX = heel) — kicking back against authority. — recalcitrant, defiantly resistant.

k.syn: abjure, abnegate, forswear, renege, renounce, repeal, repudiate, retract, withdraw
k.ant: adhere, insist, maintain, stick 坚持
k.en: to withdraw or repudiate（a statement or belief）formally and publicly

ex.en: The man has refused after torture to recant his heresy.
ex.cn: 这个人在受折磨后依旧拒绝放弃他的异教信仰。

m: RE (back) + CANT (sing/say, Latin CANTARE) — "sing back" / take back what you said. — recant, to formally retract.

k.ant: dominant 显性的
k.cn: （基因等）隐性的，由隐性基因控制的
k.en: of, relating to, or designating an allele that does not produce a characteristic effect when present with a dominant allele

ex.en: a recessive disease
ex.cn: 阴性基因控制的疾病

k.syn: backward, bashful, coy, demure, diffident, introverted, modest, retiring, self-effacing, sheepish, withdrawn
k.ant: extroverted, immodest, outgoing 外向的；gregarious, sociable 好交际的
k.en: not comfortable around people

ex.en: For such a recessive genius, the most comfortable thing is working alone in his lab.
ex.cn: 对于这样一个内向的天才而言，最舒服的事情就是一个人在实验室里干活。

m: RECESSIVE contains RECESS — going back into a recess; subdued. — recessive, retreating / dominated.

k.en: the tendency of a convicted criminal to reoffend after serving punishment

m: from RECIDIVATE — falling back into crime. — recidivism, criminal relapse.

k.syn: fluctuate, oscillate, sway, swing, vacillate
k.en: to move forward and backward alternately

ex.en: According to Marx's economic principle, the average price of a certain product should be reciprocating over its value.
ex.cn: 根据马克思的经济学理论，某一商品的平均价格应该在它的价值周边波动。

k.syn: recompense, repay, requite, retaliate, return
k.ant: owe 欠（钱、人情等）
k.der: reciprocal adj. 相互之间的，互惠的
k.en: to return in kind or degree

ex.en: They reciprocated the favor by driving their neighbor to the airport.
ex.cn: 他们开车送邻居去机场以还人情。

m: RECIPROCATE contains RE (back) + PROC (forward) — back-and-forth motion / return a favor. — reciprocate, to return in kind.

k.syn: audacious, bold, brash, brassy, brazen, careless, daredevil, rash, madcap, temerarious
k.ant: careful, cautious, circumspect, guarded, heedful, prudent, wary 慎重的，小心的
k.cn: 不考虑后果的，大胆鲁莽的
k.en: careless of consequences; foolishly adventurous or bold

ex.en: His reckless driving accounted for the accident.
ex.cn: 他鲁莽的开车行为导致了这场事故。

m: RECK (heed, Old English) + LESS — without heed. — reckless, careless.

k.syn: anchorite, eremite, hermit, isolate, solitary
k.en: a person who lives away from others

ex.en: He was sick of cities and crowds, so he decided to go live by himself in the woods as a recluse.
ex.cn: 他厌倦了城市和拥挤的人群，因此他决定像隐士一样遁隐山林。

k.syn: cloistered, hermetic, secluded, secluse, seclusive, sequestered
k.ant: gregarious, social 好交际的
k.en: marked by withdrawal from society

ex.en: a recluse poet who left a large amount of literature legacies
ex.cn: 一个留下了大量文学遗产的隐居诗人

ex.en: My neighbor is so recluse that I only see him about once a year.
ex.cn: 我的邻居太神秘了，一般我一年只能见他一面。

m: RECLUSE contains RE + CLUSE (close, Latin CLAUDERE) — closed off. — recluse, a hermit.

k.syn: blench, cringe, flinch, quail, shrink, squinch, wince
k.ant: confront, meet 面对
k.en: to shrink back, as under pressure or in fear or repugnance

ex.en: recoil from the snake
ex.cn: 因为害怕蛇而畏缩不前

ex.en: She recoiled from his touch.
ex.cn: 她因害怕与他接触而退缩。

m: RE (back) + COIL — coil back / spring back. — recoil, to draw back.

k.syn: accommodate, attune, conciliate, conform, coordinate, harmonize, key
k.ant: disharmonize 使不和谐；estrange 离间
k.en: to restore to friendship or harmony

ex.en: Historians have never been able to reconcile the two eyewitness accounts of the battle.
ex.cn: 历史学家们一直以来都无法协调这场战役两种记载之间的矛盾。

m: RE + CONCILE (council, Latin CONCILIUM) — bring back to a council, reunite. — reconcile, to bring back to harmony.

k.syn: abstruse, arcane, deep, enigmatic, esoteric, hermetic, occult, profound
k.ant: facile, shallow, superficial 肤浅的；easy, simple 简单的
k.en: difficult or impossible for one of ordinary understanding or knowledge to comprehend

ex.en: I think Quantum Mechanics and Random Process are the two most recondite courses in our curriculum this semester.
ex.cn: 我觉得量子力学和随机过程是这学期课表中最难的两门课程。

m: RECONDITE contains RECOND ≈ "hidden away" (Latin RECONDERE = hide). — recondite, obscure / abstruse.

k.syn: probe, scout, survey
k.en: to make a preliminary inspection of, especially in order to gather military information

ex.en: The wide utilization of unmanned aerial vehicles makes it much less risky to reconnoiter a certain area.
ex.cn: 无人飞行器的广泛运用大大减少了军事侦察的风险。

m: RECONNOITER contains RECONN ≈ "recognize" — to scout out and recognize. — reconnoiter, to scout.

k.syn: reconstruct, reorder, reorganize, reshuffle, retool
k.ant: annihilate, decimate, demolish, devastate, level, nuke, pulverize, ruin, smash, vaporize, wreck 摧毁
k.cn: 重建，（尤其是通过加水）使复原
k.en: to constitute again or anew; to restore to a former condition, especially by adding water

ex.en: reconstitute dried coffee powder
ex.cn: 冲咖啡

ex.en: Slowly Jewish communities ravaged by Nazi Germany were reconstituted and life began anew.
ex.cn: 被纳粹德国摧残的犹太人社区慢慢得以重建，新的生活开始了。

m: RE + CONSTITUTE — set up again. — reconstitute, to restore to original form.

k.syn: reassemble
k.ant: adjourn, suspend 休会
k.en: to gather, call together, or summon again, especially for a formal meeting

ex.en: The leaders will reconvene tomorrow.
ex.cn: 明天领导们会再次召开会议。

m: RE + CONVENE — convene again. — reconvene, to assemble again.

k.syn: honesty, integrity, probity, righteousness, scrupulousness, uprightness, virtue, virtuousness
k.ant: badness, evil, immorality, iniquity, sin, villainy, wickedness 邪恶，罪恶
k.en: the quality or state of being straight; moral integrity

ex.en: The principal encouraged the graduates to go on to live lives of rectitude.
ex.cn: 校长鼓励毕业生们在今后的生活中做一个正直的人。

m: RECTITUDE contains RECT (right / straight, Latin RECTUS) — moral straightness. — rectitude, moral integrity.

k.syn: decumbent, procumbent, prone, prostrate, reclining, supine
k.ant: erect, upright 直立的
k.en: lying down, especially in a position of comfort or rest

ex.en: lying recumbent on the floor
ex.cn: 躺在地板上

m: RECUMBENT contains RECUMB ≈ "lie down" (Latin RECUMBERE) — lying back. (Same CUMB- as INCUMBENT.) — recumbent, lying down.

k.syn: convalesce, heal, rally, recoup, recover, rehabilitate
k.ant: deteriorate, flag, wane, weaken 衰退，变得衰弱；die, expire, perish, pass away 死亡
k.der: recuperative adj. 有助于康复的
k.en: to recover health or strength

ex.en: He is gradually recuperating from a serious back injury.
ex.cn: 他正渐渐地从严重的背部损伤中恢复过来。

m: RE + CUPERATE ≈ "recapture" (Latin RECUPERARE) — recapture / regain health. — recuperate, to recover.

k.en: the transfer of resources, wealth, or rights among individuals or groups in society

m: RE + DISTRIBUTION — distribute again. — redistribution, reallocation.

k.syn: ambrosial, aromatic, fragrant, perfumed, savory, scented, sweet
k.ant: unscented 没有香味的；fetid, foul, malodorous, noisome, rancid, reeky, stinky 恶臭的，腐臭的
k.en: having or emitting fragrance

ex.en: be redolent with the aroma of baking bread
ex.cn: 充满了烤面包的香味

m: REDOLENT contains REDOL ≈ "smell of" (Latin OLERE = smell, as in OLFACTORY). — redolent, fragrant / suggestive.

k.syn: bright, distinguished, illustrious, luminous, notable, noteworthy, outstanding, preeminent, prestigious
k.ant: average, inferior, mediocre 平庸的，中等偏下的
k.en: worthy of respect or honor

ex.en: a surprising discovery by one of the most redoubtable figures in Egyptian archaeology
ex.cn: 埃及考古学领域最值得敬重的人物之一所做出的一个惊人发现

k.syn: direful, dreadful, fearsome, formidable, frightening, ghastly, horrifying, intimidating, scary, terrifying
k.ant: calming, comforting, consoling, lulling, pacifying, quieting, reassuring, relaxing, soothing 令人放松的
k.en: arousing fear or awe

ex.en: His next opponent would be by far the most redoubtable adversary the young boxer had ever faced.
ex.cn: 年轻的拳击手的下一个对手将会是迄今为止他见过的最可怕的敌人。

m: REDOUBTABLE contains REDOUBT ≈ "to fear deeply" (Latin DUBITARE = doubt) — formidable / commanding respect. — redoubtable, formidably impressive.

k.syn: excess, extra, supererogatory, superfluous, supernumerary, surplus
k.ant: deficient, inadequate, insufficient, meager, niggardly, scant, scarce, short, sparse 缺乏，稀疏
k.en: exceeding what is necessary or normal

ex.en: This area is already chockablock with shopping malls; another one would be redundant.
ex.cn: 这个地区已经是商场扎堆了，再建一个完全是多此一举。

m: REDUNDANT contains RED + UND (wave, Latin UNDA) — waves of overflow, more than needed. — redundant, excessive / repetitive.

k.syn: spin, swirl, turn, whirl
k.ant: calm 保持镇定
k.en: to be in a confused state as if from being twirled around

ex.en: My head reeled with the facts and figures.
ex.cn: 我的头脑被这些事实和数据给弄晕了。

ex.en: His mind reeled upon hearing the news that his employer had been indicted for fraud.
ex.cn: 当他听到雇主因为欺诈而被起诉时，他顿觉天旋地转。

k.syn: careen, dodder, falter, lurch, stagger, stumble, teeter, totter, waddle “GRE考验的不是脑力，不是体力，而是心力，踏踏实实地做好每个细节，阳光真的就会出现在风雨后。” （吕秋莹，2009年10月，Verbal 750，Quantitative 800，AW 4.0 录取学校：Columbia University）
k.en: to move forward while swaying from side to side

ex.en: The drunkard reeled down the alley.
ex.cn: 醉汉蹒跚地沿着小巷走去。

m: to REEL = to wobble / spin like a film REEL. — reel, to stagger / spin.

k.syn: adjudicator, arbiter, arbitrator, judge, umpire
k.en: a person who impartially decides or resolves a dispute or controversy

ex.en: serve as the unofficial referee in disputes over the family business
ex.cn: 作为一个非官方协调员，解决家庭事务中的纠纷

k.syn: adjudge, adjudicate, arbitrate, decide, determine, settle
k.ant: consult 咨询
k.en: to give an opinion about（something at issue or in dispute）

ex.en: Their father usually ends up refereeing any disputes concerning use of the big TV.
ex.cn: 最后往往是他们的父亲决定该看什么电视频道，从而解决了相关争执。

m: REFEREE contains REFER — a third party you REFER disputes to. — referee, a judge / arbitrator.

k.syn: filter, garble, purify, winnow
k.ant: adulterate, contaminate, pollute 掺杂，污染
k.en: to free（as metal, sugar, or oil）from impurities or unwanted material

ex.en: Oil is refined so as to remove naturally occurring impurities.
ex.cn: 原油通过精炼可去除其中天然存在的杂质。

k.syn: ameliorate, amend, better, enhance, enrich, help, meliorate, perfect, polish, upgrade
k.ant: deteriorate, worsen 恶化，降低
k.der: refined adj.（物品）精雕细琢的，（人）彬彬有礼的；refinery n. 炼油厂
k.en: to improve or perfect by pruning or polishing

ex.en: You'd better refine your backhand before the big tennis match if you want to throne.
ex.cn: 如果你想获得冠军的话，你最好在网球大赛开赛前再提升一下反手的技术。

m: RE + FINE — make finer / purer. — refine, to purify.

k.ant: absorb 吸收
k.en: to prevent passage of and cause to change direction

ex.en: The color of an objective is largely determined by what light it reflects.
ex.cn: 物体的颜色很大程度上是由它能反射的光所决定的。

ex.en: Mirrors indiscriminately reflect all visible lights.
ex.cn: 镜子不加区分地反射所有的可见光。

k.syn: bespeak, betray, demonstrate, evince, manifest, reveal, show
k.ant: conceal, cover, hide, mask, obscure, occlude, veil 隐藏
k.en: to make manifest or apparent: show

ex.en: Her book clearly reflects her religious beliefs.
ex.cn: 她的书明确地反映了她的宗教信仰。

ex.en: Where you come from could be easily reflected in your accent.
ex.cn: 你的口音可以轻易地暴露你来自哪里。

k.syn: consider, cogitate, contemplate, deliberate, meditate, muse, ponder, ruminate, think
k.der: reflective adj. 思考的，深思熟虑的
k.en: to think seriously

ex.en: I reflected on my path as an undergraduate and my future as a PhD candidate.
ex.cn: 我思考了我作为一名本科生的成长足迹以及我进修博士的未来。

m: RE (back) + FLECT (bend, Latin FLECTERE) — bend back (light, thought). — reflect, to throw back / ponder.

k.syn: balky, contumacious, defiant, incompliant, insubordinate, intractable, obstreperous, rebellious, unruly
k.ant: amenable, compliant, conformable, docile, governable, obedient, ruly, submissive, tractable 服从的，顺从的
k.en: resisting control or authority

ex.en: Refractory players will be ejected from the game.
ex.cn: 不服从裁判命令的球员将会被驱逐出场。

m: REFRACTORY contains FRACT (break, Latin) — breaks rules / resists treatment. (Same root as FRACTURE.) — refractory, stubbornly resistant.

k.syn: beaming, brilliant, dazzling, incandescent, lucid, luminous, lustrous, radiant, resplendent, splendid
k.ant: dim, dull, lackluster 黯淡无光的
k.der: refulgence n. 灿烂，光芒四射
k.en: shining radiantly; resplendent

ex.en: Refulgent sunlight broke through the clouds, creating huge swaths of light in the valley below us.
ex.cn: 耀眼的阳光穿透了云层，在我们脚下的峡谷中投下了一块巨大的光斑。

m: REFULGENT contains FULG (shine, Latin FULGERE) — shining brightly. (Same root as EFFULGENT.) — refulgent, radiant.

k.syn: contradict, disaffirm, disavow, disclaim, disconfirm, deny, gainsay, negate, negative, reject, repudiate
k.ant: acknowledge, admit, avow, concede, confirm 承认
k.en: to declare not to be true

ex.en: While the spokeswoman was publicly refuting rumors of a merger, behind the scenes the CEO was working to effect that very outcome.
ex.cn: 尽管女发言人一再公开否认合并的谣言，首席执行官却正在幕后为这一目标而努力。

k.syn: belie, confute, debunk, discredit, falsify, rebut, shoot down
k.ant: confirm, prove, validate, verify 证明为真，证实
k.en: to prove wrong by argument or evidence: show to be false or erroneous

ex.en: The triumph of Chinese athlete Liu Xiang in the 2004 Olympics effectively refuted the views that Asians are physically inferior to others.
ex.cn: 中国运动员刘翔在2004年雅典奥运会上的胜利有效地驳斥了亚洲人在体格上不如其他人种的观点。

m: REFUTE contains REFUT ≈ "drive back" (Latin REFUTARE = drive back) — drive back arguments. — refute, to disprove.

k.syn: recharge, refresh, rejuvenate, rekindle, renew, restore, resurrect, resuscitate, revitalize, revivify
k.ant: degenerate, deteriorate, worsen 衰退，恶化
k.cn: 使重获新生，使焕然一新
k.en: to bring back to life, practice, activity or a former condition of vigor

ex.en: The lizard is able to regenerate its tail.
ex.cn: 蜥蜴能够重新长出断尾。

ex.en: The whole community was regenerated thanks to a government grant for repairing all the old buildings.
ex.cn: 多亏了政府用于维修老楼的专项拨款，整个社区焕然一新。

k.syn: habilitate, reclaim, redeem, rehabilitate
k.en: to make better in behavior or character

ex.en: Every time he made a mistake, he would promise to regenerate.
ex.cn: 每次犯错后他都会保证洗心革面，下次不再犯。

m: RE (again) + GENERATE — generate anew. — regenerate, to renew.

k.syn: administration, authority, governance, jurisdiction, regime
k.en: lawful control over the affairs of a political unit（as a nation）

ex.en: A new party will have regimen over the nation and, hopefully, bring some much-needed change.
ex.cn: 一个新的政党将要上台，希望他们能带来一些迫切需要的改革。

m: REGIMEN contains REGI (rule, Latin REGERE) — a strict rule of conduct (also: a government). — regimen, a strict regulated routine.

k.syn: retrogress, return, revert
k.ant: advance, develop, evolve, progress 前进，发展
k.en: to go back; move backward

ex.en: To stand still is to regress.
ex.cn: 不进则退。

k.syn: atrophy, crumble, decay, decline, degenerate, descend, deteriorate, ebb, retrograde, rot, sink, worsen
k.ant: ameliorate, improve, meliorate 改善
k.der: regressive adj. 后退的，退化的；regression n. 后退，退化
k.en: to become worse or of less value

ex.en: The annual celebration has regressed to the point where it's nothing more than an excuse to get drunk.
ex.cn: 年度的庆祝活动已经堕落成一个尽情喝醉的借口了。

m: RE (back) + GRESS (step, Latin GRADI) — step backward. — regress, to revert.

k.syn: cure, fix, mend, rejuvenate, resuscitate, revitalize, revive, set up
k.ant: debilitate, enervate, enfeeble, weaken 使虚弱；relapse 旧病复发
k.en: to restore to a former state（as of efficiency, good management, or solvency）or a healthy condition

ex.en: new policies in hopes of rehabilitating the national economy
ex.cn: 被指望能振兴国家经济的新政策

ex.en: She decided to undergo physical therapy to help rehabilitate her broken elbow.
ex.cn: 她决定采取理疗来治疗受伤的手肘。

k.syn: habilitate, reclaim, redeem, regenerate
k.en: to make better in behavior or character

ex.en: an organization that rehabilitates criminals so they can reenter society
ex.cn: 一个让罪犯洗心革面的机构，以便让他们能重返社会

m: RE + HABILITATE (make able, Latin HABILIS = able) — make able again. — rehabilitate, to restore.

k.syn: practice, trial, dry run
k.ant: extemporization, impromptu, improvisation（无准备的）即兴表演
k.en: the act of practicing in preparation for a public performance

ex.en: We made a few mistakes in rehearsal, but we were pretty sure that we'd be OK on opening night.
ex.cn: 彩排过程中我们犯了几个错误，但正式开幕式当晚我们有信心做得很好。

m: REHEARSAL contains RE + HEARSE — "plow over again" (Old French HERCIER = harrow). Practice over and over. — rehearsal, a practice run.

k.syn: arm, authority, clutch, command, control, dominion, grip, hold, mastery, sway
k.ant: impotence, impotency, powerlessness 无权，无能
k.en: the right or means to command or control others

ex.en: A healthy nation should be governed by the reign of law and not by the will of its chief executive.
ex.cn: 一个健康的国家应该是法制的，而非由最高领导人的意志统领。

ex.en: They accused him of carrying out a reign of terror.
ex.cn: 他们指责他搞独裁暴政。

k.syn: dominate, domineer, prevail, predominate, rule
k.en: to be predominant or prevalent

ex.en: Chaos reigned in the classroom.
ex.cn: 整个教室一片混乱。

ex.en: Panic reigned as the fire spread.
ex.cn: 随着大火的扩散，恐慌也迅速蔓延。

m: REIGN rhymes with REIN — but REIGN means to rule. — reign, to rule.

k.syn: circumscription, confinement, limitation, stint
k.en: the act or practice of keeping something（as an activity）within certain boundaries

ex.en: The oversight committee called on the corporation to keep a much tighter rein on the activities of its contractors.
ex.cn: 监督委员会要求这个公司对其承包商的行为采取更为严格的管控。

k.syn: bridle, check, constrain, contain, curb, govern, hold, inhibit, keep, measure, manacle, regulate, restrain, rule, tame
k.ant: liberate, loose, loosen, unleash 释放，不再加以管束
k.en: to keep from exceeding a desirable degree or level（as of expression）

ex.en: A good man knows how to rein in his temper.
ex.cn: 君子知道如何按捺住火气。

m: REIN rhymes with REIGN — but REIN is the strap; to rein in = restrain a horse. — rein, to restrain.

k.syn: chime, din, iterate, recapitulate, rehearse, repeat
k.en: to say or state again

ex.en: Let me reiterate our stance.
ex.cn: 让我来重述我们的立场。

ex.en: I want to reiterate that under no circumstances are you to leave the house.
ex.cn: 我想再次重申：无论如何你都不能离开这间房子。

m: RE (again) + ITER (again, Latin ITERUM) + ATE — say again and again. — reiterate, to repeat.

k.syn: crow, delight, exuberate, glory, jubilate, joy, kvell, triumph
k.ant: bemoan, bewail, deplore, grieve, lament, moan, mourn, regret, weep 感到伤感、痛苦或遗憾
k.en: to feel joy or great delight

ex.en: We rejoiced over our unexpected victory on the soccer field.
ex.cn: 我们为预料之外的足球胜利而感到欢喜。

m: RE + JOICE ≈ "joy" (Old French JOIR) — full of joy. — rejoice, to feel joy.

k.syn: recidivate, regress, retrogress, revert
k.ant: habilitate, reclaim, redeem, regenerate 变好，洗心革面； convalesce, rehabilitate 康复
k.en: to slip or fall back into a former worse state

ex.en: The patient wondered whether his illness would relapse.
ex.cn: 病人想知道他的病会不会复发。

m: RE (back) + LAPSE (slip, Latin) — slip back. — relapse, to fall back into.

k.syn: cast, exhale, expel, irradiate, issue, vent
k.ant: absorb 吸收
k.en: to throw or give off

ex.en: We bought an air freshener that releases a pleasing scent into the room.
ex.cn: 我们买了一个能向房间里释放怡人香味的空气净化机。

k.syn: discharge, disenthrall, emancipate, enfranchise, liberate, loose, loosen, manumit, unchain, unfetter
k.ant: bind, confine, enchain, fetter, restrain 限制自由
k.en: to set free from restraint, confinement, or servitude

ex.en: The government was asked to release the prisoners immediately.
ex.cn: 政府被要求立即释放囚犯。

k.syn: bounce, cashier, dismiss, fire, remove, retire, sack, terminate
k.ant: employ, engage, hire 雇用
k.en: to let go from office, service, or employment

ex.en: They released the workers who couldn't handle the new technology.
ex.cn: 他们解雇了所有不能掌握新技术的工人。

m: RE + LEASE ≈ "loose" — set loose. — release, to let go.

k.en: to become more lenient, compassionate, or forgiving; to let up or slacken

m: RE + LENT (soft, Latin LENTUS) — become softer / give in. — relent, to soften / yield.

k.syn: applicable, apposite, apropos, germane, material, pertinent, pointed, relative
k.ant: extraneous, immaterial, impertinent, inapplicable, inapposite, irrelevant, pointless 无关的
k.en: having a bearing on or connection with the matter at hand

ex.en: Make sure your answers during the interview are short and relevant.
ex.cn: 确保你在面试时的回答简短而恰当。You need to bring all the relevant certificates with you. 你需要携带所有的相关证明。

m: RELEVANT contains RELEV ≈ "raise up" (Latin RELEVARE = lift) — lifts importance to the case. — relevant, pertinent.

k.syn: credo, creed, cult, faith, persuasion
k.en: a body of beliefs and practices regarding the supernatural and the worship of one or more deities

ex.en: The Jewish religion has followers in many parts of the globe.
ex.cn: 犹太教在全球范围内有大量信徒。

k.syn: devotion, piety
k.ant: atheism, godlessness 无神论
k.der: religious adj. 宗教的；忠诚的
k.en: belief and trust in and loyalty to God

ex.en: Without his religion, he would not have been able to survive all the difficulties he has faced over the years.
ex.cn: 如果不是他的虔诚，他是不可能渡过这些年来遇到的所有难关的。

m: RELIGION contains RELIG ≈ "bind back" (Latin RELIGARE = bind fast) — that which binds people to the divine. — religion, a system of belief.

k.syn: abnegate, cede, renounce, resign, surrender
k.ant: inaugurate 就职；usurp 篡位
k.en: to give up（as a position of authority）formally

ex.en: She relinquished his position to the company's vice president with very mixed feelings.
ex.cn: 怀着十分复杂的情感，她放弃了公司副总裁的职位。

k.syn: deliver, render, yield, turn in, turn over
k.ant: keep, retain, withhold 保留，不给予
k.en: to give（something）over to the control or possession of another usually under duress

ex.en: The boy reluctantly relinquished the illegal fireworks to the police officer.
ex.cn: 小男孩不情愿地把非法的烟花爆竹交给了警察。

ex.en: The court ordered him to relinquish custody of his child.
ex.cn: 法庭要求他交出孩子的监护权。

m: RE + LINQUISH (leave, Latin LINQUERE) — leave behind. (Same root as RELIC.) — relinquish, to give up.

k.syn: appetite, fancy, favor, fondness, like, love, partiality, preference, taste
k.ant: aversion, disfavor, disgust, dislike, distaste, hatred, loathing 反感，厌恶感
k.en: an appetite for something; a strong appreciation or liking

ex.en: She has great relish for early morning walks, which she takes nearly every day.
ex.cn: 她对早晨散步有格外的喜好——几乎每天早上她都会坚持出去。

k.syn: content, delectation, delight, enjoyment, gladness, gratification, happiness, satisfaction
k.ant: discontent, displeasure, dissatisfaction, unhappiness 不满，不悦
k.en: the feeling experienced when one's wishes are met

ex.en: He ate the bowl of ice cream with relish.
ex.cn: 他高兴地吃着冰激凌。

k.syn: adore, enjoy, like, love, rejoice, revel, savor
k.ant: abhor, abominate, detest, dislike, hate, loathe 厌恶，讨厌
k.en: to take keen or zestful pleasure in

ex.en: He is so hungry that he will relish even plain food.
ex.cn: 他是如此的饥饿，即使普通的食物也能让他满意。

m: RELISH rhymes with FETISH but the savory cousin — savor with enjoyment (also: a savory condiment). — relish, to enjoy heartily.

k.syn: averse, disinclined, indisposed, loath, unwilling
k.ant: willing 乐意的；disposed, inclined 倾向于做某事的；eager 渴望的
k.der: reluctance n. 不愿意，不情愿
k.en: feeling or showing aversion, hesitation, or unwillingness

ex.en: I'm reluctant to let him borrow my CDs since he never gives back anything I lend him.
ex.cn: 我很不愿意把我的CD借给他，因为他借了我东西从来不还。

m: RELUCTANT contains LUCT (struggle, Latin LUCTARI) — struggling against. — reluctant, unwilling.

k.syn: careless, derelict, disregardful, heedless, lax, lazy, neglectful, neglecting, slack
k.ant: attentive, careful, conscientious, meticulous, painstaking, scrupulous 细致的，仔细的
k.en: exhibiting carelessness or slackness

ex.en: I would be remiss if I didn't tell you how much I appreciated the lovely gift.
ex.cn: 如果我不告诉你我是多么喜欢这个可爱的礼物的话，那我就太疏忽大意了。

m: REMISS contains RE + MISS — missed something out of carelessness. — remiss, careless.

k.syn: alter, modify, recast, redo, refashion, remake, revamp, revise, rework, vary
k.ant: fix, freeze, set, stabilize 固定
k.en: to alter the structure of

ex.en: We decided to remodel the warehouse into a museum displaying the products of the company.
ex.cn: 我们决定把仓库改造成展览我们公司产品的展馆。

m: RE + MODEL — model again, redesign. — remodel, to renovate.

k.syn: challenge, complaint, demur, difficulty, fuss, objection, protest, question, remonstration, stink
k.ant: acceptance, acquiescence, agreement, approval, assent, sanction 同意，接受
k.en: an expression of protest, complaint, or reproof, especially a formal statement of grievances

ex.en: She seems deaf to her son's remonstrances.
ex.cn: 她似乎对儿子的抗议充耳不闻。

ex.en: Aggravated by the noisy metro construction, many residents wrote letters of remonstrance to city officials.
ex.cn: 许多被地铁建设的噪音激怒的居民向市政府投去了抗议信。

m: REMONSTRANCE contains MONSTR (show, Latin MONSTRARE) — showing strong objection. — remonstrance, a protest.

k.syn: contriteness, contrition, penitence, regret, remorsefulness, repentance, rue, self-reproach, shame
k.ant: impenitence, remorselessness 不知悔改
k.der: remorseful adj. 懊悔的，悔恨的
k.en: moral anguish arising from repentance for past misdeeds; bitter regret

ex.en: He felt a deep remorse for having neglected his family over the years.
ex.cn: 他为多年来忽略家人的行为表示深深的懊悔。

m: REMORSE contains REMOR ≈ "bite back" (Latin REMORDERE = bite again) — gnawing guilt. — remorse, deep regret.

k.syn: compensate, indemnify, recompense, recoup, redress, remedy, repay, requite
k.en: to pay an equivalent to for a service, loss, or expense

ex.en: He promptly remunerated the repair company for fixing the satellite TV.
ex.cn: 他迅速地支付了维修公司修理卫星电视所应得的酬劳。

m: REMUNERATE contains MUNER (gift, Latin MUNUS) — give a gift back; pay. — remunerate, to pay.

k.syn: cleave, lacerate, ribbon, rip, rive, rupture, shred, split, tatter, tear
k.ant: associate, coalesce, combine, conjoin, conjugate, connect, fuse, interfuse, join, link, unify 联合
k.en: to tear or split apart or into pieces violently

ex.en: Wolves rend a game to pieces.
ex.cn: 狼群会把猎物撕成碎片。

ex.en: pain that rends the heart
ex.cn: 撕心裂肺般的痛

m: REND rhymes with BEND but more violent — tear apart. — rend, to tear.

k.syn: apostate, betrayer, defector, deserter, recreant, traitor, turncoat
k.ant: adherent, loyalist, partisan 忠诚者
k.en: one who rejects a religion, cause, allegiance, or group for another; a deserter

ex.en: A band of renegades who had deserted their infantry units were making their way to Mexico.
ex.cn: 一支背叛了他们所属的步兵编队的士兵朝着墨西哥前进。

m: RENEGADE contains RENEG ≈ "deny again" (Latin RENEGARE = deny) — one who denies their loyalty. — renegade, a traitor.

k.syn: abnegate, cede, relinquish, resign, surrender
k.ant: inaugurate 就职；usurp 篡位
k.en: to give up, refuse, or resign usually by formal declaration

ex.en: renounce his nationality
ex.cn: 退出国籍

k.syn: forswear, recant, renege, repeal, repudiate, retract, withdraw
k.ant: adhere, stick 坚持，忠于
k.en: to refuse to follow, obey, or recognize any further

ex.en: renounce the authority of the church
ex.cn: 宣布与教会彻底决裂

m: RE (back) + NOUNCE (announce) — formally announce that you give it up. — renounce, to formally give up.

k.syn: doctor, fix, patch, recondition, repair, revamp
k.ant: blemish, break, damage, deface, disfigure, harm, hurt, impair, injure, mar, ruin, vandalize, wreck 破坏
k.en: to restore to a former better state（as by cleaning, repairing, or rebuilding）

ex.en: We will have to renovate the house extensively before we can move in.
ex.cn: 在入住之前我们必须大规模翻修一下这栋房子。

k.syn: recharge, refresh, rejuvenate, rekindle, renew, restore, resurrect, resuscitate, revitalize, revivify
k.ant: degenerate, deteriorate, worsen 衰退，恶化
k.cn: 使重获新生，使焕然一新
k.en: to bring back to life, practice, activity or a former condition of vigor

ex.en: The church was renovated by a new ecumenical spirit.
ex.cn: 新的宗教领袖的到来让教堂焕发新生。

m: RE + NOV (new, Latin NOVUS) + ATE — make new again. — renovate, to restore to newness.

k.syn: comeback, riposte
k.en: a quick witty response

ex.en: That repartee to the reporter's question drew laughs from the bystanders.
ex.cn: 对记者问题的机智回答引来了旁观者的笑声。

k.syn: backchat, badinage, chaff, jesting, joshing, persiflage, raillery
k.en: good-natured teasing or exchanging of clever remarks

ex.en: Repartee is harder to do with text messaging.
ex.cn: 用短信开玩笑困难多了。

m: REPARTEE contains RE + PART ≈ "reply quickly" (French RÉPARTIE) — quick witty reply. — repartee, witty conversation.

k.ant: banish, deport, expatriate 放逐，驱逐
k.en: to restore or return to the country of origin, allegiance, or citizenship

ex.en: As soon as the war ends, the government will start to repatriate war refugees.
ex.cn: 只要战争一结束，政府就会开始遣返战争难民。

m: RE + PATRI (fatherland) + ATE — return to one's fatherland. — repatriate, to send back to one's country.

k.syn: abort, abrogate, annul, cancel, disannul, invalidate, negate, null, nullify, rescind, revoke, vacate, void
k.ant: continue, keep 继续；enact, establish, legislate 立法，制订
k.en: to rescind or annul by authoritative act

ex.en: The company called the furniture store to repeal the order for six new desks.
ex.cn: 公司要求家具店撤销六张新桌子的订单。

ex.en: In 1933, Congress passed the 21st Amendment which repealed the Prohibition Amendment of 1919, thus making the sale, distribution, and use of alcohol legal once again. 1933
ex.cn: 年，国会通过了《第二十一修正案》，该法案废除了于1919年制订的《禁酒法》，从而使得酒的买卖、配送和使用再次合法化。

k.syn: forswear, recant, renege, renounce, repudiate, retract, withdraw
k.ant: adhere, stick 坚持，忠于
k.en: to refuse to follow, obey, or recognize any further

ex.en: If I find that you have been lying about this, I'll instantly repeal every promise I made to you.
ex.cn: 只要我发现在这个问题上你对我撒了谎，那我们之间的承诺就彻底破裂了。

m: RE + PEAL ≈ "call back" (Old French RAPELER = recall) — recall a law. — repeal, to formally revoke.

k.syn: buck, defy, fight, oppose, resist, withstand
k.ant: capitulate, submit, succumb, surrender, yield, give in 屈服
k.en: to fight against; resist

ex.en: Self-discipline ensures that you can repel the things that induce you.
ex.cn: 自律可以保证你抵制那些诱惑你的事物。

ex.en: I repelled the temptation to stay out late and call in sick the next day.
ex.cn: 我抵住了晚上玩通宵然后第二天谎称生病的诱惑。

k.syn: disgust, nauseate, repulse, revolt, sicken
k.ant: allure, attract, bewitch, captivate, charm, enchant, entice, fascinate, lure, seduce, tempt 吸引，诱惑
k.en: to cause aversion in: disgust

ex.en: Evil odors always repel me.
ex.cn: 臭味总是让我恶心。

m: RE (back) + PEL (push, Latin PELLERE) — push back. — repel, to drive away.

k.syn: budget, fund, inventory, pool, reservoir, stock
k.en: the complete list or supply of skills, devices, or ingredients used in a particular field, occupation, or practice

ex.en: The chef's repertoire of specialties seems to be limited, with several of the dishes appearing over and over again in slightly varied guises.
ex.cn: 这个厨师的所有拿手好戏看来是很有限的，因为上来的几盘菜看起来只是稍微换了一下装饰而已。

m: REPERTOIRE contains REPERT ≈ "to find" (Latin REPERIRE) — the list of pieces an artist can produce on demand. — repertoire, a list of skills / works.

k.syn: carp, complain, fuss, gripe, grouch, grouse, growl, grumble, inveigh, moan, murmur, mutter, wail, whine
k.ant: crow, delight, rejoice 高兴
k.en: to feel or express discontent or dejection

ex.en: There is no use repining over a love that's been long lost.
ex.cn: 抱怨早已失去的爱情是没有用的。

k.syn: ache, covet, crave, desiderate, hunger, itch, long, lust, pine, salivate, thirst, want, wish, yearn
k.ant: abhor, abominate, detest, dislike, hate, loathe 厌恶，讨厌
k.en: to long for something

ex.en: During the deep cold of winter, I repine for warm tropical beaches.
ex.cn: 在寒冬，我渴望温暖的热带海滩。

m: RE + PINE — pine again, complain longingly. — repine, to fret.

k.syn: sated, satiate, satiated, stuffed, surfeited
k.ant: empty, famished, hungry, starved, starving 挨饿的
k.en: having one's appetite completely satisfied

ex.en: Everyone is completely replete after the huge meal.
ex.cn: 在大餐之后，每个人都吃撑了。

k.syn: abounding, abundant, awash, flush, fraught, lousy, swarming, teeming, thick, thronging
k.ant: depleted, drained, exhausted 耗竭的；deficient, incomplete, insufficient, short 缺乏的
k.en: possessing or covered with great numbers or amounts of something specified

ex.en: a scholar replete with knowledge
ex.cn: 博学的学者

m: REPLETE contains PLET (full, Latin) — completely full. — replete, fully stocked.

k.en: the reporting of news

m: REPORTAGE — French for journalism / reporting. — reportage, factual reporting.

k.syn: ease, leisure, relaxation
k.ant: exertion, labor, toil, work 劳动，工作
k.en: a state of resting after exertion or strain

ex.en: The doctor ordered a period of repose for the patient suffering from insomnia.
ex.cn: 医生要求正在饱受失眠折磨的病人好好休息一阵。

k.syn: calmness, hush, peace, placidity, quietness, restfulness, serenity, still, tranquility
k.ant: commotion, pandemonium, tumult, turmoil, unrest, uproar 骚乱，动乱
k.en: a state of freedom from storm or disturbance

ex.en: We enjoyed the repose of a summer evening on a remote island.
ex.cn: 我们在遥远的小岛上共享了夏日傍晚的片刻安宁。

k.syn: idle, loll, lounge, relax, rest
k.ant: drudge, hustle, moil, strive, struggle, sweat, travail 努力工作
k.en: to take a rest

ex.en: They have to repose on couches because all beds have been occupied.
ex.cn: 因为所有的床都有人占了，他们不得不在沙发上休息。

k.syn: consign, delegate, deliver, entrust, leave, pass, transfer, transmit, vest, hand over, turn over, give over
k.ant: hold, keep, retain, withhold 保留
k.en: to put（something）into the possession or safekeeping of another

ex.en: The Constitution reposes the power to declare war to Congress, and to that body alone.
ex.cn: 宪法将宣战的权力交给了国会单独保管。

m: REPOSE contains POS (place, Latin) — place oneself back at rest. — repose, restful calm.

k.syn: berate, blame, castigate, censure, condemn, denounce, dispraise, fault, knock, lambaste, pan, upbraid
k.ant: extol, laud, praise 表扬
k.en: to express one's unfavorable opinion of the worth or quality of

ex.en: Without exception, book reviewers reprehended the novel's trite plot.
ex.cn: 毫无例外的，小说缺乏新意的故事情节受到了评论家们的责难。

m: REPREHEND contains REPREHEND- ≈ "seize back" (Latin REPREHENDERE) — grab someone for blame. — reprehend, to rebuke.

k.syn: crush, quash, quell, silence, squash, squelch, subdue, suppress
k.ant: foment, incite, instigate, provoke, stir 煽动，激起
k.en: to put down by force, usually before total control has been lost; quell

ex.en: Military quickly repressed the rebellion in the city and restored order.
ex.cn: 军方迅速平息了市内的暴乱，恢复了秩序。

k.syn: bridle, check, curb, muffle, smother, stifle, strangle, swallow
k.ant: express, loose, release, take out, unleash, vent（情感等的）表达，宣泄
k.cn: 阻止（正常的表达、活动或发展等）
k.en: to prevent the natural or normal expression, activity, or development of

ex.en: You can't repress your feelings forever, so tell her that you love her.
ex.cn: 你不可能永远压抑自己的感受，所以大胆地向她表白吧。

m: RE + PRESS — press back / down. — repress, to suppress.

k.syn: absolve, acquit, amnesty, condone, excuse, forgive, pardon, remit, respite, spare
k.ant: penalize, punish 惩罚
k.en: to postpone or cancel the punishment of

ex.en: Fourteen people, waiting to be hanged for the murder of a former prime minister, have been reprieved.
ex.cn: 十四个因为谋杀前首相而等待绞刑的人获得了缓刑。

k.syn: deliver, rescue, save
k.en: to prevent（something）from being closed, destroyed, etc., for a period of time

ex.en: The library has been reprieved and will remain open for at least another year.
ex.cn: 图书馆的状况得到了缓解，还将继续开放一年。

m: REPRIEVE rhymes with RETRIEVE — but REPRIEVE means to delay/cancel a punishment. (Latin REPREHENDERE = take back.) — reprieve, a delay of punishment.

k.syn: dishonor, opprobrium, reflection, spot, scandal, stain, stigma, taint
k.ant: credit, honor 荣誉
k.en: one that causes shame, rebuke or blame

ex.en: a reproach to this entire school
ex.cn: 整个学校的耻辱

k.syn: admonish, berate, castigate, chide, condemn, denounce, lambaste, rebuke, reprimand, reprove, upbraid
k.ant: commend, eulogize, extol, laud, praise 表扬
k.en: to express disapproval, criticism, or disappointment in（someone）

ex.en: reproached by their mother for untidiness
ex.cn: 因为邋遢而被他们的母亲批评

m: RE + PROACH ≈ "approach" — but coming at someone with blame. — reproach, to scold / a disgrace.

k.syn: brute, devil, fiend, miscreant, rapscallion, rascal, villain
k.ant: saint 圣人
k.en: a morally unprincipled person

ex.en: The program rehabilitates reprobates and turns them into hard- working, law-abiding citizens.
ex.cn: 这个项目让那些堕落的人改过自新，并将他们变成勤劳守法的好公民。

k.syn: debased, debauched, degraded, depraved, dissipated, dissolute, libertine, perverse, perverted, rakish
k.ant: good, moral, righteous, virtuous 有道德的；pure, uncorrupted 纯净的，未受污染的
k.en: morally corrupt

ex.en: He is a reprobate judge who could be bribed, and often with astonishing ease.
ex.cn: 他是一个堕落的法官，极其容易就被贿赂了。

k.syn: blame, criticize, censure, condemn, denounce, denunciate, reprehend
k.ant: commend, eulogize, extol, laud, praise 表扬
k.en: to condemn strongly as unworthy, unacceptable, or evil

ex.en: He reprobated his son's unconventional lifestyle.
ex.cn: 他怒斥他儿子不遵循传统的生活习惯。

k.syn: decline, disallow, disapprove, negative, nix, refuse, reject, repudiate, withhold
k.ant: allow, concede, grant, let, permit, vouchsafe 允许，给予
k.en: to be unwilling to grant

ex.en: The government will most likely reprobate the request for parole.
ex.cn: 政府很有可能会拒绝假释的要求。

m: REPROBATE contains REPROB ≈ "reject as not approved" (Latin REPROBARE) — disapproved person. — reprobate, an immoral person.

k.syn: commination, condemnation, denunciation, excoriation, rebuke, reprimand, reproach, stricture
k.ant: commendation, eulogy, praise 赞扬；endorsement 同意
k.en: criticism for a fault, rebuke

ex.en: The head teacher speaks in tones of gentle reproof.
ex.cn: 校长的话语中带有轻微的批评。

m: REPROOF contains REPRO ≈ "PROOF" against something — an act of rebuke. — reproof, a criticism.

k.syn: admonish, blame, chide, criticize, reprimand, reproach
k.ant: lambaste 严厉地责备；commend, eulogize, extol, laud, praise 表扬
k.en: to scold or correct usually gently or with kindly intent

ex.en: My piano teacher often reproves me for slouching while playing, observing that good posture helps one play better.
ex.cn: 我的钢琴老师常常会因为懒散的坐姿而批评我，他认为良好的坐姿能让我弹得更好。

k.syn: deprecate, discountenance, disesteem, disfavor, dislike, frown
k.ant: adore, approve, endorse, enjoy, favor, like 支持，喜欢
k.en: to hold an unfavorable opinion of

ex.en: The older generation has always reproved the younger generation's taste in music.
ex.cn: 老一代的人总是不喜欢新一代的音乐品味。

m: RE + PROVE — disprove someone's behavior. — reprove, to mildly rebuke.

k.en: an animal that crawls or moves on its belly (as a snake) or on small short legs (as a lizard); a cold-blooded vertebrate

m: REPTILE contains REPT (creep, Latin REPTARE) — creeping creature. — reptile, a cold-blooded crawling animal.

k.syn: contradict, disaffirm, disavow, disclaim, disconfirm, disown, gainsay, negate, negative, refute, reject
k.ant: acknowledge, admit, allow, avow, concede, confirm 承认
k.en: to declare not to be true

ex.en: She repudiated the charge that she had lied on her résumé.
ex.cn: 她否认了她在简历中作假的指控。

k.syn: balk, deselect, disapprove, negative, nix, refuse, reprobate, spurn
k.ant: accept, agree, approve 接受，承认
k.en: to show unwillingness to accept, do, engage in, or agree to

ex.en: We didn't like the terms, so we repudiated the contract.
ex.cn: 我们没有接受这份合同，因为我们对其中的条款表示不满。

m: REPUDIATE contains REPUDI ≈ "reject the bond" (Latin REPUDIUM = divorce). — repudiate, to disown.

k.syn: abhorrent, abominable, awful, disgusting, distasteful, hideous, loathsome, noisome, obnoxious, repulsive
k.ant: innocuous, inoffensive 无害的；agreeable, delightful, enjoyable, gratifying, pleasing 令人愉悦的
k.en: arousing disgust or aversion

ex.en: The idea of moving again became repugnant to her.
ex.cn: 又要搬家的想法让她感到厌恶。

k.syn: conflicting, discordant, incompatible, incongruous, inconsonant, inharmonious, mutually exclusive
k.ant: accordant, agreeing, compatible, concordant, conformable, congruent, congruous, consistent, consonant, harmonious 和谐的
k.en: not being in agreement or harmony

ex.en: Technically speaking, it may not be a violation, but it is certainly repugnant to the spirit of the law.
ex.cn: 技术上来说这还算不上违法，但是这肯定违背了法律的精神。

m: REPUGNANT contains PUGN (fight, Latin PUGNARE) — fighting against your sensibilities. (Same root as PUGNACIOUS.) — repugnant, distasteful.

k.syn: disgust, nauseate, repel, revolt, sicken
k.ant: allure, attract, bewitch, captivate, charm, enchant, entice, fascinate, lure, seduce, tempt 吸引，诱惑
k.en: to rebuff or reject with rudeness, coldness, or denial

ex.en: The scenes of violence in the film may repulse some viewers.
ex.cn: 电影中某些暴力场景可能会使观众反感。

m: REPULSE contains PULSE — pulse / push back. (Same root as REPEL.) — repulse, to drive back / disgust.

k.syn: celebrity, fame, name, renown, reputation
k.ant: infamy, notoriety 坏名声
k.en: a good reputation

ex.en: Before his national bestseller, he was a writer of little repute.
ex.cn: 在他的全国畅销书面市之前他是个没什么名声的作家。

m: REPUTE contains REPUT ≈ "think over" (Latin REPUTARE) — what people think about you. — repute, reputation.

k.syn: condition, demand, necessity, need, requirement
k.en: something necessary, indispensable, or unavoidable

ex.en: Calculus is a requisite for modern physics.
ex.cn: 微积分是学习近代物理的必要知识。

k.syn: critical, compulsory, imperative, indispensable, mandatory, necessary, obligatory, required, vital
k.ant: needless, unnecessary 不必要的；dispensable, optional 可替换的，可选择的
k.en: essential, necessary

ex.en: Oxygen is requisite for human to survive.
ex.cn: 氧气是维持人类生存的必要条件。

m: REQUISITE contains REQUI ≈ "required" (Latin REQUIRERE = require). — requisite, required.

k.syn: indemnify, reciprocate, recompense, recoup, reimburse, remunerate,
k.en: to make repayment or return for

ex.en: requite her love with hatred
ex.cn: 恩将仇报

m: RE + QUITE ≈ "reply / repay" (Latin QUIETUS = at rest) — settle the score. — requite, to repay.

k.syn: abolish, abrogate, annul, cancel, invalidate, negate, null, nullify, recall, repeal, revoke, void
k.ant: establish, enact 建立；continue, keep 维持，保持
k.en: to make void

ex.en: The government refused to rescind the order of curfew.
ex.cn: 政府拒绝撤销宵禁的决定。

m: RESCIND contains SCIND (cut, Latin SCINDERE) — cut back / cancel. — rescind, to revoke.

k.syn: closemouthed, constrained, laconic, reticent, restrained, silent, taciturn
k.ant: communicative, expansive, talkative 健谈的；garrulous, loquacious 话多的
k.der: unreserved adj. 无保留的，外向的
k.en: restrained in words and actions

ex.en: too reserved to offer a spontaneous criticism
ex.cn: 过于缄默而不愿意发表真实的看法

m: RESERVED contains RESERVE — holding back; restrained. — reserved, reticent.

k.syn: debris, remainder, remnant
k.en: something that remains after a part is taken, separated, or designated

ex.en: In the race of nature, there is no residue left for the late.
ex.cn: 在大自然的竞争中，总是早起的鸟儿才有虫吃。

m: from RESIDUAL — what remains. — residue, leftover material.

k.syn: abnegate, cede, relinquish, renounce, surrender, step down
k.ant: assume, inaugurate 就职，承担（责任）；usurp 篡位
k.en: to give up one's job or office

ex.en: She resigned her position at the university.
ex.cn: 她自动放弃她在大学的职位。

m: RE (back) + SIGN — sign your way out / give back the position. — resign, to step down.

k.syn: elasticity, flexibility, malleability, pliability
k.ant: inelasticity 无弹性
k.en: the property of a material that enables it to resume its original shape or position after being bent, stretched, or compressed

k.syn: adaptability
k.der: resilient adj. 有弹性的；能迅速恢复的
k.en: the ability to recover quickly from illness, change, or misfortune

ex.en: Her mental resilience helped her promptly walk out of the shadow of her father's death.
ex.cn: 她的精神恢复能力让她很快地走出了父亲去世的阴影。

m: RESILIENCE contains RESIL ≈ "jump back" (Latin RESILIRE = leap back). — resilience, ability to bounce back.

k.en: A sticky substance secreted by trees and plants, or a synthetic polymer used in making plastics and adhesives.

m: RESIN — sticky tree sap. (Latin RESINA = pitch.) — resin, sticky plant secretion.

k.syn: bound, decided, faithful, resolved, unwavering
k.ant: faltering, hesitant, indecisive, irresolute, jittery, vacillating, wavering 犹豫的，踌躇的
k.en: marked by firm determination

ex.en: We are glad to have such a resolute ally.
ex.cn: 我们很高兴能拥有这样一个坚定的盟友。

m: RESOLUTE contains RESOL ≈ "resolved" (Latin RESOLUTUS) — firm in resolve. — resolute, firmly determined.

k.syn: consonant, orotund, plangent, resounding, reverberant, sonorous, vibrant
k.ant: faint, low, muffled, muted, smothered, soft, weak 声音模糊的，低声的
k.en: strong and deep in tone

ex.en: A deep resonant voice rang out.
ex.cn: 传来了洪亮的声音。

m: RESONANT contains RESON ≈ "sound back" (Latin RESONARE) — echoing richly. — resonant, deeply echoing.

k.syn: able, adroit, competent, talented
k.en: able to act effectively or imaginatively, especially in difficult situations

ex.en: a resourceful man capable of dealing with difficult situations
ex.cn: 一个能巧妙解决困局的能人

m: RESOURCE + FUL — full of resources. — resourceful, clever / inventive.

k.syn: breathe, inhale
k.ant: smother, stifle, suffocate 停止呼吸，窒息
k.en: to inhale and exhale air successively

ex.en: unable to respire due to heart attack
ex.cn: 因心脏病发作而无法呼吸

m: RE + SPIRE (breathe, Latin SPIRARE) — breathe in and out. (Same root as INSPIRE, EXPIRE.) — respire, to breathe.

k.syn: break, lull, intermission, recess, pause, rest
k.ant: resumption 继续进行；exertion, labor, toil, work 劳动，工作
k.en: an interval of rest or relief

ex.en: toil without respite
ex.cn: 埋头苦干

ex.en: In the middle of each semester there came a short respite.
ex.cn: 在每个学期的期中，会有一个短暂的休假。

m: RESPITE rhymes with DESPITE — but a RESPITE is a welcome break. (Latin RESPECTUS = looking back, then "delay".) — respite, a brief rest.

k.syn: brilliant, glorious, gorgeous, grand, magnificent, splendid, sublime, superb
k.ant: dim, dull, lackluster 黯淡无光的
k.en: shining brilliantly

ex.en: Geography teacher showed us a picture of the resplendent aurora borealis.
ex.cn: 地理老师给我们展示了一张壮丽的北极光的照片。

m: RE + SPLEND (shine, Latin SPLENDERE) — shining brightly. — resplendent, brilliantly shining.

k.syn: prompt, sensible, sensitive, susceptible
k.ant: dispassionate 无动于衷的；detached, indifferent 冷漠的
k.en: quick to respond or react appropriately or sympathetically

ex.en: Children are often the most responsive members of the audience.
ex.cn: 儿童通常是观众中最为敏感的群体。

m: RESPONSIVE contains RESPOND — quick to respond. — responsive, reactive.

k.syn: restless, nervy, uneasy, uptight
k.ant: imperturbable 沉着的；phlegmatic 冷静的
k.en: marked by impatience or uneasiness

ex.en: I spent a restive night worrying about the next day's exam.
ex.cn: 我度过了忧虑的一晚，担心明天的考试。

k.syn: balky, contumacious, defiant, insubordinate, intractable, obstreperous, obstinate, perverse, recalcitrant, refractory
k.ant: amenable, biddable, compliant, conformable, docile, obedient, ruly, submissive, tractable 服从的
k.en: stubbornly resisting control

ex.en: Tired soldiers grew restive.
ex.cn: 疲惫的士兵变得越来越难以管理。

m: RESTIVE rhymes with FESTIVE but the opposite — restless, hard to control. — restive, restlessly impatient.

k.syn: bridle, check, constrain, curb, inhibit, measure, prevent, refrain, withhold
k.ant: discharge, release 释放
k.en: to limit, restrict, or keep under control

ex.en: The old lady restrained the child from picking the flowers.
ex.cn: 老妇人不准孩子们采摘花朵。

m: RE (back) + STRAIN (pull, Latin STRINGERE) — pull back / hold back. — restrain, to hold back.

k.syn: reanimation, rebirth, regeneration, rejuvenation, re.ewal, resurrection, resuscitation, revitalization
k.ant: decay, degradation, deterioration, downfall, ebb, fall 衰落
k.en: a restoration to use, acceptance, activity, or vigor

ex.en: Let's witness the resurgence of classical school.
ex.cn: 让我们见证古典乐

m: RE + SURGE — surge again. — resurgence, a rising again.

k.syn: reanimate, recharge, refresh, rejuvenate, rekindle, renew, resurrect, revitalize, revive, revivify
k.ant: faint 昏迷
k.der: resuscitation n. 复活，复兴；resuscitated adj. 复活的
k.en: to restore consciousness, vigor, or life to

ex.en: resuscitated by the kiss of the prince
ex.cn: 因王子的吻而苏醒

m: RE + SUSCITATE (stir up, Latin SUSCITARE) — stir back to life. — resuscitate, to revive.

k.syn: menial, servant
k.ant: lord, master 主人
k.en: a person attached or owing service to a household

ex.en: Knights are dressed for battle by their retainers.
ex.cn: 骑士们在上战场之前是由他们的家仆帮助着装打扮的。

m: RETAINER contains RETAIN — one who's RETAINed in service. — retainer, a servant.

k.syn: avenge, redress, requite, revenge
k.ant: absolve, condone, forgive, pardon 饶恕，宽恕
k.en: to pay back（as an injury）in kind

ex.en: We swear to retaliate for our losses.
ex.cn: 我们发誓要以牙还牙。

m: RETALIATE contains RE + TALI (such, Latin TALIS) — give back the like. — retaliate, to strike back.

k.syn: bog, brake, decelerate, delay, detain, hamper, impede, mire, slacken
k.ant: accelerate, catalyze, expedite, hasten, precipitate 加速，促进
k.en: to cause to move or proceed slowly; delay or impede

ex.en: Language barriers retarded their negotiating progress.
ex.cn: 语言障碍让谈判进程受阻。

m: RE (back) + TARD (slow, Latin TARDUS) — slow down. (Same root as TARDY.) — retard, to slow.

k.syn: closemouthed, constrained, laconic, reserved, restrained, silent, taciturn
k.ant: communicative, expansive, talkative 健谈的；garrulous, loquacious 话多的
k.en: inclined to be silent or uncommunicative in speech

ex.en: He was reticent about his plans.
ex.cn: 他对他的计划三缄其口。

k.syn: cagey, disinclined, dubious, indisposed, loath, reluctant
k.ant: willing 乐意的；disposed, inclined 倾向于做某事的；eager 渴望的
k.en: slow to begin or proceed with a course of action because of doubts or uncertainty

ex.en: Understandably, she's reticent about becoming involved with another religious sect.
ex.cn: 不难理解，她不愿意被牵扯进另一个宗教派别之中。

m: RETICENT contains RET ≈ "hold back" (Latin RETICERE = keep silent). — reticent, reserved.

k.syn: associates, entourage, followers, posse, retainers
k.ant: leader 领导者
k.en: a group of retainers or attendants

ex.en: The premier inspected the factory with his retinue.
ex.cn: 总理与随同人员一起视察了工厂。

m: RETINUE contains RETIN ≈ "retain" — those retained as the lord's followers. — retinue, an entourage.

k.syn: comeback, rejoinder, repartee, reply, response, return, riposte
k.ant: inquiry, query, question 问题，问询
k.en: something spoken or written in reaction especially to a question, especially a quick, witty, or cutting reply

ex.en: She responded to the heckler with a scathing but hilarious retort that instantly won over the audience.
ex.cn: 面对故意起哄捣乱的人，她那严厉但不失幽默的回答立刻得到了观众的肯定。

m: RE + TORT (twist, Latin TORQUERE) — twist back, sharp reply. (Same root as DISTORT, CONTORT.) — retort, a sharp reply.

k.syn: ameliorate, enhance, furbish, meliorate, perfect, polish, refine, upgrade
k.ant: deteriorate, downgrade, worsen 恶化，降低
k.en: to improve or change（a photographic negative or print）

ex.en: She was retouching her painting before the deadline.
ex.cn: 在截止日期到来之前，她一直在润色她的画作。

m: RE + TOUCH — touch up again. — retouch, to refine.

k.syn: abjure, abnegate, disavow, forswear, recall, recant, renounce, repeal, repudiate, withdraw
k.ant: tender 提出（建议，想法）；adhere, stick 坚持
k.en: to take back

ex.en: The newspaper had to retract its allegations against the mayor.
ex.cn: 报纸不得不收回针对市长的谣言。

m: RE + TRACT (drag, Latin) — drag back. (Same root as ATTRACT, DETRACT.) — retract, to take back.

k.syn: economize, reduce, cut down
k.ant: enlarge, expand 扩大，扩张
k.en: to curtail expenses

ex.en: Declining business forced the company to retrench.
ex.cn: 衰减的业务迫使公司削减开支。

m: RE + TRENCH ≈ "trim" (Old French TRENCHIER = cut) — cut back expenses. — retrench, to cut back.

k.syn: avengement, payback, recompense, reparation, repayment, requital, retaliation, revenge, vengeance
k.en: the dispensing or receiving of reward or punishment especially in the hereafter

ex.en: The neighborhood is being torn apart by an endless cycle of gang violence and retribution.
ex.cn: 这个社区正在被帮派间斗争和仇恨的恶性循环割裂。

m: RE + TRIBUTION ≈ "give back" (Latin TRIBUERE = give) — giving back what's deserved (usually punishment). — retribution, deserved punishment.

k.syn: backward, receding, regressive, reversed, withdrawing
k.ant: progressive 前进的
k.en: moving or tending backward

ex.en: This is a retrograde step and you will regret it.
ex.cn: 这是后退的一步，你以后肯定会后悔的。

k.syn: atrophy, crumble, decay, decline, degenerate, descend, deteriorate, ebb, regress, rot, sink, worsen
k.ant: ameliorate, improve, meliorate 改善
k.en: to decline to a worse condition

ex.en: The Dark Ages is the period following the fall of the Roman Empire when Western civilization seriously retrograded.“
ex.cn: 黑暗时代”指的是罗马帝国崩溃之后，西方文明严重退步的一个历史时期。

m: RETRO (backward) + GRADE (step) — stepping backward. — retrograde, moving backward.

k.syn: backward
k.ant: anticipatory, prospective 预期的，未来的
k.en: looking back on, contemplating, or directed to the past

ex.en: a retrospective glance at my youth
ex.cn: 朝我少年时代投去的回顾性一瞥

m: RETRO (back) + SPECT (look) — looking back. — retrospective, looking backward.

k.syn: conviviality, festivity, gaiety, jollity, merriment, rejoicing, revel, whoopee
k.ant: gloom, grief, melancholy, misery, mournfulness, sorrow, woe 悲哀，哀痛
k.en: noisy partying or merrymaking

ex.en: They were exhausted after the night of revelry.
ex.cn: 一夜狂欢后大家都筋疲力尽。

m: REVELRY contains REVEL — wild celebrating. — revelry, noisy festivity.

k.syn: avengement, payback, reprisal, requital, retaliation, retribution, vengeance
k.ant: forgiveness, pardon 饶恕，原谅
k.en: an act or instance of retaliating in order to get even

ex.en: Both sides were determined to get revenge for losses and showed little interest in ending the feud.
ex.cn: 双方都下定决心要为失去的东西报仇，对于结束他们之间的世仇毫无兴趣。

ex.en: The bombing was in revenge for the assassination of their leader.
ex.cn: 此次轰炸是对暗杀他们领袖的报复行动。

k.syn: avenge, redress, requite, retaliate
k.ant: absolve, condone, forgive, pardon 原谅，宽恕
k.en: to avenge（as oneself）usually by retaliating in kind or degree

ex.en: He finally revenged the death of his brother.
ex.cn: 他终于为兄弟的死报了仇。

m: RE + VENGE ≈ "vindicate" (Latin VINDICARE) — claim back. — revenge, retaliation.

k.syn: adore, deify, glorify, esteem, regard, respect, venerate, worship
k.ant: blaspheme, desecrate, profane, violate 亵渎
k.der: reverence n. 尊重；reverent adj. 恭敬的，虔诚的
k.en: to offer honor or respect to（someone）as a divine power

ex.en: He is revered for his valor.
ex.cn: 他因为英勇而受到尊敬。

ex.en: In some cultures people revere their ancestors, even leaving food offerings for them.
ex.cn: 在一些文化中，人们非常敬重先人，甚至将一些食物留下来供他们享用。

m: REVERE contains REVER ≈ "stand in awe" (Latin REVERERI = stand in awe). — revere, to deeply respect.

k.syn: amend, correct, emend, polish, redraft, refine, rework, upgrade
k.ant: discard 丢弃，抛弃
k.en: to look over again in order to correct or improve

ex.en: There are many problems involved in revising a dictionary.
ex.cn: 修正字典会牵涉诸多问题。

ex.en: With the snow, we'll need to revise our travel plans.
ex.cn: 下雪了，我们不得不更改旅游计划。

m: RE + VISE (see, Latin VIDERE) — see again. (Same root as VISION.) — revise, to amend.

k.syn: come around
k.ant: faint, black out 昏迷，失去知觉
k.en: to return to consciousness or life; to become flourishing again

ex.en: His father successfully revived him with artificial respiration.
ex.cn: 他的父亲用人工呼吸使他成功恢复意识。

k.syn: reanimate, recharge, refresh, rejuvenate, rekindle, renew, resurrect, resuscitate, revitalize, revivify
k.ant: degenerate, deteriorate, worsen 衰退，恶化
k.en: to become active or flourishing again

ex.en: Around midnight, I usually need to revive myself with a cup of strong coffee.
ex.cn: 在午夜时分，我需要一杯浓咖啡来提神。

ex.en: The success of the movie has revived her career.
ex.cn: 这部电影的成功让她的事业焕发第二春。

m: RE + VIVE (live, Latin VIVERE) — live again. — revive, to restore to life.

k.syn: abolish, abrogate, annul, cancel, invalidate, negate, null, nullify, recall, repeal, void
k.ant: establish, enact 建立；continue, keep 维持，保持
k.en: to annul by recalling or taking back

ex.en: revoke a will
ex.cn: 废除遗嘱

m: RE (back) + VOKE (call, Latin VOCARE) — call back, cancel. — revoke, to cancel.

k.syn: disgust, nauseate, repel, repulse, sicken
k.ant: allure, attract, bewitch, captivate, charm, enchant, entice, fascinate, lure, seduce, tempt 吸引，诱惑
k.en: to fill with disgust or abhorrence

ex.en: The smell of seafood revolts him.
ex.cn: 他对海鲜的腥味十分反感。

k.syn: defy, insurrect, mutiny, oppose, rebel, resist
k.ant: obey, submit 遵从，服从
k.en: to renounce allegiance or subjection（as to a government）

ex.en: The revolted against the dictator by burning his palace.
ex.cn: 他们通过焚烧宫殿来对抗独裁者。

m: RE + VOLT (turn, Latin VOLVERE) — turn against. — revolt, to rebel / disgust.

k.syn: bawdy, blue, coarse, crude, dirty, filthy, gross, indecent, nasty, obscene, pornographic, profane, vulgar
k.ant: decent, proper, seemly 恰当的，得体的
k.en: characterized by or indulging in vulgar, lewd humor

ex.en: entertain the guests with ribald jokes
ex.cn: 用荤段子来取悦客人

m: RIBALD rhymes with PIEBALD — but RIBALD humor is crude / vulgar. — ribald, vulgar / crude.

k.syn: insecure, precarious, shaky, unsound, unsteady, weak
k.ant: firm, sound, stable, sturdy 稳定的，稳固的
k.en: lacking stability or firmness

ex.en: The rickety coalition may break at any moment.
ex.cn: 脆弱的联盟随时都可能破裂。

m: rick(摇晃)+ety——摇摇欲坠的——rickety，不稳的。

k.syn: horseman, knight
k.ant: pedestrian, walker 行人，步行者
k.en: one that rides

k.syn: addendum, appendix, attachment, supplement
k.en: a clause appended to a legislative bill to secure a usually distinct object

m: ride(附搭)+r——附搭在法案上的附加条款——rider，附件。

k.syn: deride, gibe, jape, jeer, lout, mock, taunt
k.ant: commend, praise 表扬
k.en: to make fun of

ex.en: They always ridiculed everything she said.
ex.cn: 他们总是嘲笑她说的话。

ex.en: The term“big bang theory”was originally coined to ridicule the belief that the universe was created by a giant explosion.“
ex.cn: 大爆炸理论”起初是为了嘲笑一种观点而创造的，这种观点认为宇宙源自一场巨大的爆炸。

m: ridicul(可笑)+e——可笑地嘲笑——ridicule，嘲笑。

k.syn: abounding, abundant, awash, flush, fraught, lousy, replete, swarming, teeming, thick, thronging
k.ant: deficient, incomplete, insufficient, short 缺乏的
k.en: possessing or covered with great numbers or amounts of something specified

ex.en: a video game rife with violence and abuse
ex.cn: 充满暴力与虐待的电子游戏

m: rife——像ripe(成熟)，充满蔓延的——rife，充满的。

k.syn: crack, fissure, fracture, schism
k.ant: reconciliation, rapprochement 调和
k.en: a break in friendly relations

ex.en: a rift between two once allied nations
ex.cn: 两个往昔盟国的决裂

k.syn: cleave, divide, rip, split, sunder, tear
k.ant: associate, coalesce, combine, conjugate, join, link 接合
k.en: to cause to split open or break

ex.en: Hills were rifted by the earthquake.
ex.cn: 地震使得山崩地裂。

m: rift听起来像「裂了「。两个好兄弟因为一笔钱产生裂缝，从此老死不相往来——rift，裂缝，分歧。

k.syn: aggravate, annoy, enrage, exasperate, grate, inflame, infuriate, irritate, nettle, peeve, pique, provoke, roil
k.ant: allay, alleviate, assuage, ease, mitigate, mollify, palliate, relieve, soothe 平息，缓和
k.en: to make agitated and angry

ex.en: The new work schedules riled the employees.
ex.cn: 新的工作时间表激怒了员工。

m: 「惹了我(rile)！」——rile，激怒。

k.syn: clamor, commotion, disorder, ferment, tumult, turmoil, upheaval, uproar
k.ant: serenity, tranquility 宁静；order 秩序
k.en: public violence, tumult, or disorder

ex.en: Special police units equipped with riot shields quickly arrived at the airport.
ex.cn: 装备有防暴盾牌的特警们迅速抵达了飞机场。

m: riot谐音「乱透了「。球赛结束后球迷冲上街头，警车鸣笛，玻璃碎声此起彼伏——riot，暴动骚乱。

k.syn: age, develop, grow, mature
k.ant: fade, flag, shrivel, wane, wilt, wither 使枯萎
k.en: to make or become ripe or riper

ex.en: Age ripens a good wine.
ex.cn: 时间酿出好酒。

m: ripen = ripe（成熟的）+ n。青涩的小苹果挂在枝头，经过夏天的阳光，慢慢变红变甜——ripen，（使）成熟。

k.syn: ceremony, form, observance, protocol, ritual, solemnity
k.en: a prescribed form or manner governing the words or actions for a ceremony

ex.en: the marriage rites
ex.cn: 婚礼仪式的章程

m: rite谐音「日特「——特定的日子必须照规矩办。婚礼上司仪一步步念程序，新人跪拜、交杯、敬茶，少一步都不行——rite，宗教仪式。

k.syn: cleave, lacerate, render, ribbon, rip, rupture, shred, split, tatter, tear
k.ant: associate, coalesce, combine, conjoin, conjugate, connect, fuse, interfuse, join, link, unify 联合
k.der: riven adj. 被撕开的 Jovons saw the kettle boil and cried out with the delighted voice of a child; Marshal too had seen the kettle boil and sat down silently to build an engine. 杰文斯看见壶开了，高兴得像孩子似地叫了起来；马歇尔也看见壶开了，却悄悄地坐下来造了一部蒸气机。 ——英国经济学家　凯恩斯（John Maynard Keynes, British economist） “每天比别人多放纵自己一点，日积月累就必然会落后。每天比别人多做一点，日积月累就成为竞争的优势。” （相舒，Verbal 700，AW 5.0，录取学校：Georgia Institute of Technology）
k.en: to wrench open or tear apart or to pieces

ex.en: Lightning rived the tree.
ex.cn: 闪电把树劈裂了。

m: rive谐音「撕啊「。铁匠用楔子猛地一劈，原木应声而裂——rive，撕裂劈开。

k.syn: absorbing, arresting, engaging, engrossing, enthralling, fascinating, immersing, intriguing, involving
k.ant: insipid, vapid 索然无味的；boring, dull, monotonous, tedious 单调的
k.en: wholly absorbing or engrossing one's attention

ex.en: The riveting novel has, as previously expected, become a national best-seller.
ex.cn: 正如先前所预料的，这本引人入胜的小说成为了畅销书。

m: rivet(铆钉/抓住注意)+ing——像铆钉钉住注意力——riveting，引人注目的。

k.syn: brook, brooklet, creek, streamlet
k.en: a small stream

ex.en: Dream Rivulet Diary
ex.cn: 《梦溪笔谈》

m: rivul(小河)+et——小小的河流——rivulet，小溪。

k.syn: bouncing, dynamic, energetic, gingerly, hale, potent, strong, sound, vigorous, vital, wholesome
k.ant: delicate, frail, weak 弱不禁风的；ailing, diseased, ill, sick, unfit, unhealthy, unsound 不健康的
k.der: robustness n. 健壮，活力四射
k.cn: 精力充沛的；强壮的，健康的
k.en: full of health and strength

ex.en: a robust older man who still bicycles 10 miles a day
ex.cn: 一个每天骑行 10英里的充满活力的老人

m: rob(强)+ust——强壮的——robust，强健的。

k.en: any of various mammals of the order Rodentia, such as a mouse, rat

m: rod(啃)+ent——啃东西的动物——rodent，啮齿动物。

k.syn: disarray, disorder, disrupt, disturb, mess, muddle, rile
k.ant: arrange, array, order, organize, regulate 整理，使有序
k.en: to stir up; disorder

ex.en: He roiled the brook with his wood stick.
ex.cn: 他用他的木棍将溪水搅浑。

ex.en: Financial markets have been roiled by the banking crisis.
ex.cn: 银行业的危机搅乱了金融市场的秩序。

k.syn: aggravate, enrage, exasperate, incense, inflame, infuriate, irritate, provoke, rile, vex
k.ant: delight, gratify, please 使愉快
k.en: to displease or disturb

ex.en: Some of his roommate's habits began to roil him.
ex.cn: 他室友的某些习惯开始让他恼火。

m: roil——roll(翻滚)+i，搅翻使烦躁——roil，使烦恼搅浑。

k.syn: apprentice, fledgling, freshman, greenhorn, neophyte, novice, recruit, tyro
k.ant: veteran 老兵
k.en: recruit; novice

ex.en: The rookie replaced the injured regular at first base.
ex.cn: 这个新手代替了受伤的一垒手。

m: rookie谐音「入课「——刚入学的人。新警察入警校第一天，衣服还没穿整齐，子弹上膛都不会——rookie，新兵新手。

k.syn: catalog, checklist, list, menu, roll, schedule
k.en: a roll or list of personnel

ex.en: the roster of subscribers to the journal
ex.cn: 杂志订阅者名册

m: rostr(转)+er——转来转去的排班表——roster，值班表。

k.syn: dais, lectern, podium, stage, stand, tribune
k.en: a stage for public speaking

ex.en: He finally stood on the winner's rostrum.
ex.cn: 他终于站在了胜利者的领奖台上。

m: rostr(鸟喙)+um——像鸟喙突出的讲台——rostrum，讲坛。

k.syn: agitate, excite, motivate, provoke, stimulate
k.ant: allay, alleviate, assuage, ease, mitigate, mollify, palliate, relieve, soothe 平息，缓和
k.en: to stir up

ex.en: He was roused to fury.
ex.cn: 他大为光火。

k.syn: arouse, awake, awaken, waken
k.ant: lull 使睡着
k.en: to cause to stop sleeping

ex.en: The piercing siren roused her from a deep sleep.
ex.cn: 刺耳的警笛声将她从沉睡中唤醒。

m: rouse = arouse 去掉前面的a。猎人吹响号角，猎犬从草丛中蹿出——rouse唤醒了沉睡的猎犬，猎人精神也被煽动——rouse，激起唤醒。

k.syn: aristocracy, nobility
k.en: regal character or bearing
a payment to an author or composer for each copy of a work sold or to an inventor for each item sold under a patent

ex.en: charge KTV owners royalty fee
ex.cn: 向KTV索取版权费

m: royal(皇家)+ty——皇家收取的版税——royalty，版税。

k.syn: blooming, florid, flush, rosy, ruddy, sanguine
k.ant: ashen, pale, pallid, wan 苍白的，虚弱的
k.en: inclined to a healthy rosiness

ex.en: A rubicund complexion indicates good health.
ex.cn: 红润的气色意味着身体健康。

m: rubi(红)+cund——脸色红润的——rubicund，红润的。

k.syn: incipient, nascent, primitive, primordial
k.ant: developed, full-blown, mature, ripe 成熟的，发展完全的
k.en: being in the earliest stages of development

ex.en: The equipment of these past empire-builders was rudimentary.
ex.cn: 过去这些帝国的建造者们使用的都是极其原始的工具。

k.syn: basic, elemental, essential, fundamental, rudimental, underlying
k.ant: advanced, higher 进阶的，高级的
k.en: consisting in first principles: fundamental

ex.en: The dropout had only a rudimentary knowledge of science.
ex.cn: 这个辍学的孩子对科学知识只有最基本的一些了解。

m: rudiment(初步)+ary——初步基础的——rudimentary，基础的。

k.syn: contriteness, contrition, penitence, regret, remorse, remorsefulness, repentance
k.ant: impenitence, remorselessness 不知悔改
k.en: the feeling of regret, remorse, or sorrow for

ex.en: With rue my heart is laden.
ex.cn: 我的内心充满了遗憾。

m: rue谐音「如「——如果当初……。老人坐在窗边，手里捏着那张没寄出的信——rue the day，悔恨那一天——rue，后悔遗憾。

k.syn: rumple, wrinkle
k.der: ruffled adj. 有褶裥边修饰的
k.en: a strip of fabric gathered or pleated on one edge

k.syn: abrade, chafe, erode, roughen, rub, wear
k.ant: glaze, smooth 使平滑
k.en: to destroy the smoothness or evenness of

ex.en: The acid ruffled the surface of the catalyst.
ex.cn: 酸使得催化剂的表面变得粗糙。

k.syn: annoy, bother, chafe, frost, gall, grate, itch, nettle, peeve, persecute, pique, rasp, rile, vex
k.ant: allay, alleviate, assuage, ease, mitigate, mollify, palliate, relieve, soothe 平息，缓和
k.en: to disturb the peace of mind of（someone） especially by repeated disagreeable acts

ex.en: The stream of minor complaints finally ruffled him into snapping,“If you don't like the way I'm doing it, do it yourself!”
ex.cn: 一系列斤斤计较的抱怨终于让他爆发：“如果你不喜欢我做事的方法，你就自己去做！”

m: ruffle——把羽毛弄乱——ruffle，皱褶扰乱。

k.syn: cogitate, contemplate, deliberate, meditate, perpend, ponder, weigh
k.ant: ignore, neglect, overlook 忽视
k.en: to go over in the mind repeatedly and often casually or slowly

ex.en: ruminate the reason of past failures
ex.cn: 沉思以往失败的原因

m: rumin(反刍)+ate——像牛反刍一样沉思——ruminate，沉思。

k.syn: crinkle, crumple, ruffle, scrunch, wrinkle
k.ant: flatten, smooth 使表面平整；preen 整理羽毛，打扮
k.en: to wrinkle or form into folds or creases

ex.en: The guest rumpled the antique bedspread by sitting on it.
ex.cn: 客人坐在古董床单上把它弄皱了。

k.syn: disarray, disorder, disorganize, disrupt, disturb, mess, muddle, upset
k.ant: arrange, array, order, organize, regulate 整理，使有序
k.en: to undo the proper order or arrangement of

ex.en: The aunt would invariably rumple the little boy's hair whenever she came to visit.
ex.cn: 不论小男孩的姨妈何时来拜访，她总要弄乱他的头发。

m: rumple——rump(屁股)+le，坐皱了——rumple，弄皱。

k.syn: continuance, duration, persistence
k.en: an unbroken course of performances or showings

ex.en: The play had a long run.
ex.cn: 此剧一口气演了很长时间。

m: run有个冷僻义：连续演出。这部音乐剧在百老汇run了整整十年，场场座无虚席——run，连续演出（段）。

k.syn: arcane, enigmatic, impenetrable, inscrutable, mysterious, mystic, occult, uncanny
k.ant: comprehensible, fathomable 可理解的
k.en: having some secret or mysterious meaning

ex.en: painstaking efforts to decipher the runic inscriptions
ex.cn: 为破解神秘的碑文付出的辛勤劳动

m: rune(神秘符文)+ic——神秘符文的——runic，神秘的。

k.syn: breach, burst, disjoin, dissect, fracture, separate, split, sunder, tear
k.ant: associate, coalesce, combine, conjoin, conjugate, connect, fuse, interfuse, join, link, unify 联合
k.en: to part by violence

ex.en: Jealousy ruptured our friendly relationship.
ex.cn: 嫉妒破坏了我们之间的友谊。

m: 谐音「日扑车」——大卡车日扑而来，把路面砸出一道裂口——rupture，破裂、断裂。想象「破」字：rupt = burst（破）。

k.syn: artifice, maneuver, stratagem, trick, wile
k.en: a wily subterfuge

ex.en: This is a ruse to divide them.
ex.cn: 这是一招反间计。

m: ruse——法语计谋，诡计——ruse，诡计。

k.syn: bumpkin, churl, countryman, provincial, rube, yokel
k.ant: cosmopolitan, cosmopolite, sophisticate 见多识广的人
k.en: an awkward or simple person especially from a small town or the country

ex.en: a rustic who was awed by the prices that city dwellers had to pay
ex.cn: 一个被大城市房价所吓到的乡下人

k.syn: bucolic, country, pastoral
k.ant: urban 都市的
k.en: of, relating to, associated with, or typical of open areas with few buildings or people

ex.en: We went to a rustic area that is devoid of skyscrapers and shopping malls.
ex.cn: 我们去了一个没有摩天大楼和购物中心的乡村原野。

k.syn: clumsy, discourteous, gauche, impertinent, impolite, inelegant, rude, stiff
k.ant: graceful, polished, urbane 文雅的
k.en: lacking in social graces or polish

m: rust(锈)+ic——锈迹斑斑的乡村——rustic，乡村的。

k.syn: bolt, breeze, careen, career, hasten, hustle, jump, run, rush, scurry
k.ant: crawl, creep 匍匐前行；poke 缓慢行动
k.en: to move or act energetically or with speed; to proceed or move quickly

ex.en: The little boy rustled around enthusiastically on the first morning of the trip.
ex.cn: 旅行的第一个早晨，小男孩充满热情地跑来跑去。

m: rustle——枯叶沙沙声——rustle，沙沙声盗窃。

k.cn: 安息日的，周期性休假的
k.en: Relating to a period of paid leave granted to a professional for rest or study, typically every seventh year.

m: Sabbath(安息日)+ical——安息日的休假的——sabbatical，安息日休假的。

k.syn: damage, impairment, subversion, undermining
k.ant: assistance 支援
k.en: treacherous action to defeat or hinder a cause or an endeavor; deliberate subversion

ex.en: sabotage of the project by government officials
ex.cn: 被政府官员阻止的项目

k.syn: disrupt, foil, frustrate, obstruct, undermine
k.ant: assist, support 支持；advance, cultivate, forward, foster, further, nurture, promote 促进
k.en: to practice sabotage on

ex.en: He sabotaged his opponent's campaign with rumors.
ex.cn: 他用谣言来破坏对手的竞选活动。

ex.en: My ex-wife deliberately sabotages my access to the children.
ex.cn: 我的前妻故意阻止我和我的孩子们见面。

m: sabot(木鞋)+age——工人把木鞋扔进机器破坏——sabotage，破坏。

k.syn: cloying, sugary
k.ant: bitter 苦涩的
k.en: of, relating to, or resembling that of sugar

ex.en: a powdery substance with a saccharine taste
ex.cn: 有甜味的粉状物质

k.syn: fruity, maudlin, mawkish, mushy, sentimental, sugarcoated
k.ant: unsentimental 不动感情的
k.en: appealing to the emotions in an obvious and tiresome way

ex.en: The movie was funny, but it had a saccharine ending in which everyone lives happily ever after.
ex.cn: 电影还是很有意思的，但是结局太做作了——所有人从此都幸福地生活在了一起。

m: sacchar(糖)+ine——糖一样过度甜腻的——saccharine，过分甜腻的。

k.syn: blasphemy, defilement, desecration, irreverence, impiety, profanation, violation
k.ant: adoration, glorification, respect, reverence 爱慕，尊敬
k.der: sacrilegious adj. 不敬的，亵渎神明的
k.en: desecration, profanation, misuse, or theft of something sacred

ex.en: To play Mozart's music on a kazoo is sacrilege.
ex.cn: 用小木笛演奏莫扎特的音乐是对它的一种亵渎。

m: sacri(神圣)+lege(偷取)——偷取神圣之物——sacrilege，亵渎。

k.syn: burden, encumber, freight, lade, lumber, impose, inflict, tax, weight
k.ant: disburden, discharge, disencumber, unburden, unlade, unload 卸下
k.en: to load or burden

ex.en: He has saddled himself with a houseful of impecunious relatives.
ex.cn: 一屋子的穷亲戚成了他的负担。

m: saddle——骑马的鞍，给人加负担——saddle，负担。

k.syn: aegis, caution, defense, fail-safe, guard, palladium, precaution, preventive, security
k.en: a technical contrivance to prevent accident

ex.en: legal safeguards against fraud
ex.cn: 防止欺诈的法律措施

k.syn: bulwark, cover, defend, fence, fend, guard, keep, protect, screen, secure, shield, ward
k.ant: assail, assault, attack 攻击
k.en: to make safe; protect

ex.en: Sheepdogs safeguard the flock from attacks by wolves.
ex.cn: 保护羊群免受狼群攻击的牧羊犬。

m: safe(安全)+guard(守卫)——守卫安全——safeguard，保护措施。

k.syn: expert, illuminati, master, mentor, savant, scholar
k.ant: dolt, fool, idiot, simpleton 傻子
k.der: sagacious adj. 睿智的；sagacity n. 聪慧
k.en: one（as a profound philosopher）distinguished for wisdom

ex.en: The young prince made a pilgrimage to the sage, hoping to learn the meaning of life.
ex.cn: 年轻的王子走上了向智者取经的道路，希望能领悟到生命的真谛。

m: sage谐音「赛哥「——赛过所有人的大哥。白胡子老人盘腿而坐，一句话点醒迷途者，众人拜服——sage，智者圣人。

k.syn: conspicuous, noticeable, outstanding, prominent, remarkable, striking
k.ant: inconspicuous, unnoticeable 不明显的
k.en: standing out conspicuously

ex.en: The most salient feature of the book is its papyrus cover.
ex.cn: 该书最明显的特点就是纸莎草纸的封面。

m: sali(跳)+ent——跳出来引人注意——salient，显著的。

k.en: A large edible fish that migrates from the sea to fresh water to spawn, valued both as food and in sport fishing.

m: salmon——逆流而上的鱼——salmon，大马哈鱼。

k.syn: good, healthy, restorative, salutary, tonic, wholesome
k.ant: debilitating, deleterious, noxious, virulent 有害的，有毒的
k.en: favorable to or promoting health or well- being

ex.en: Every year I go to Kunming to enjoy its cool and salubrious climate.
ex.cn: 每年我都会去昆明享受它那凉爽宜人的气候。

m: salubr(健康)+ious——有益健康的——salubrious，有益健康的。

k.syn: good, healthy, restorative, salubrious, tonic, wholesome
k.ant: debilitating, deleterious, noxious, virulent 有害的，有毒的
k.en: beneficial, promoting health

ex.en: salutary exercise
ex.cn: 有益健康的锻炼

k.syn: advantageous, benefic, beneficent, benignant, favorable, friendly, helpful, kindly, profitable
k.ant: bad, disadvantageous, unfavorable, unfriendly, unhelpful, unprofitable 不利的
k.en: promoting or contributing to personal or social well-being

ex.en: The low interest rates should have a salutary effect on business.
ex.cn: 低利率对于商业而言应该是有利的。a salutary warning 善意的警告

m: salut(健康)+ary——有益的——salutary，有益的。

k.syn: greeting, regards, salute
k.ant: farewell, bon voyage（离别时的）再见，一路顺风
k.cn: 致敬，打招呼（表示欢迎和礼貌）
k.en: a polite expression of greeting or goodwill

ex.en: The veteran stepped forward, raising his hand in salutation.
ex.cn: 老兵向前一步，举起手致敬。

k.syn: accolade, citation, commendation, eulogium, eulogy, homage, hymn, paean, panegyric, tribute
k.en: a formal expression of praise

ex.en: The speaker introduced the evening's honored guest with a lavish salutation.
ex.cn: 主持人用大量赞扬的话介绍了今晚的嘉宾。

m: salut(招呼)+ation——打招呼——salutation，问候。
