k.syn: rescue, retrieve, save
k.ant: abandon, desert, forsake 放弃，抛弃
k.en: to save from loss or destruction

ex.en: salvage the torpedoed vessel
ex.cn: 拯救被鱼雷击中的舰艇

m: salv(救)+age——从废墟中救出的东西——salvage，救援。

k.syn: allay, balm, becalm, compose, lull, lullaby, quiet, settle, soothe, still, tranquilize
k.ant: agitate, discompose, disquiet, disturb, perturb, upset, vex 打扰，扰乱
k.en: quiet, assuage

ex.en: The company give him a raise in salary to salve his feelings.
ex.cn: 公司决定给他加薪以安抚他的情绪。

m: salv(治愈)+e——涂上治愈药膏——salve，药膏安慰。

k.syn: consecrate, hallow
k.ant: desecrate, profane 亵渎
k.en: to make holy

ex.en: The Constitution sanctified the rights of the people.
ex.cn: 宪法认为人权是至高无上的。

m: sanct(神圣)+ify——使神圣——sanctify，使神圣化。

k.syn: canting, deceiving, hypocritical
k.ant: devout, religious, pious 虔诚的
k.en: hypocritically pious or devout

ex.en: a sickening sanctimonious smile
ex.cn: 令人厌恶的伪善的笑容

m: sanctim(神圣)+onious——假装神圣——sanctimonious，假装虔诚的。

k.syn: accredit, approbate, authorize, certify, confirm, finalize, formalize, license, ratify, warrant
k.ant: interdict, prohibit, proscribe 禁止；decline, deny, disallow, disapprove, negative, reject, veto 否决
k.en: to make valid or binding usually by a formal procedure（as ratification）

ex.en: The President sanctioned covert operations.
ex.cn: 总统批准了秘密行动。

ex.en: The administration will sanction almost any field trip with educational value.
ex.cn: 基本上任何有教育意义的实地考察都可以得到批准。

k.syn: penalty, punishment
k.en: an economic or military coercive measure adopted usually by several nations in concert for forcing a nation violating international law to desist or yield to adjudication

ex.en: Another trade sanction is in effect.
ex.cn: 又一项贸易制裁生效了。

m: sanction——既制裁也批准，sanction，制裁批准。

k.syn: asylum, cover, harbor, haven, port, refuge, shelter
k.en: a place of refuge and protection

ex.en: In earlier times a criminal could use a church as a sanctuary.
ex.cn: 在以前，犯罪分子可以将教堂当作避难所。

m: sanct(神圣)+uary——神圣的地方——sanctuary，避难所。

k.syn: buff, file, hone, rasp, rub
k.en: to make smooth by friction

ex.en: Be sure to sand before you paint the shelf.
ex.cn: 在上漆之前一定要先磨光。

m: sand的类比题考点：沙粒组成沙丘，就像词语组成句子。抓一把沙，每一粒都微不足道，但堆成沙丘就壮观了——sand，沙，沙粒。

k.syn: assured, confident, doubtless, implicit, positive
k.ant: doubtful, dubious, uncertain, unsure 怀疑的，不确信的
k.en: having or showing a mind free from doubt

ex.en: I'm reasonably sanguine about the adoption of the latest proposal.
ex.cn: 我非常看好最新的那项提议获得通过。

k.syn: blooming, florid, flush, glowing, rosy, rubicund
k.ant: ashy, doughy, livid, lurid, mealy, pale, pallid, pasty, peaky, sallow, wan 面色苍白的
k.en: having a healthy reddish skin tone

ex.en: A baby with a sanguine complexion is more likely to leave the hospital early than a sickly-looking one.
ex.cn: 面色红润的新生儿比病怏怏的能更早出院。

m: sangu(血)+ine——血色红润，乐观的——sanguine，乐观的。

k.en: Conditions and practices conducive to maintaining health and preventing disease, especially through cleanliness.

m: sanit(健康)+ation——维护健康——sanitation，卫生。

k.syn: debilitate, devitalize, enervate, enfeeble, etiolate, prostrate
k.ant: bolster, fortify, invigorate, beef up 增强，使有活力
k.en: to weaken or exhaust the energy or vitality of

ex.en: Weeks of hard work had sapped him and left him exhausted.
ex.cn: 连续几周辛苦工作让他筋疲力尽。

k.syn: dynamism, energy, esprit, get-up-and-go, gusto, verve, vim, vitality
k.ant: lethargy, listlessness, sluggishness, torpidity 无精打采
k.en: active strength of body or mind

ex.en: a child full of sap and vivacity
ex.cn: 活力满满的孩子

m: sap——树木汁液，削弱力量——sap，汁液削弱。

k.syn: discerning, insightful, perceptive, prudent, sagacious, sage
k.ant: foolish, unperceptive, unwise 愚蠢的，不明智的
k.en: having or showing deep understanding and intelligent application of knowledge

ex.en: a man who is always good for valuable insights and some sapient advice
ex.cn: 一个以见解深刻、建议明智著称的男人

m: sapi(知道)+ent——有智慧的——sapient，明智的。

k.syn: affront, barb, indignity, offense, slap, slight, slur
k.ant: praise 表扬
k.en: a sharp and often satirical or ironic utterance designed to cut or give pain

ex.en: a voice full of sarcasm
ex.cn: 充满轻蔑的言论

m: sarc(肉)+asm——撕肉般的辛辣讽刺——sarcasm，讽刺。

k.en: of or relating to a tailor or tailored clothes

ex.en: poor sartorial taste
ex.cn: 很烂的穿衣品位

m: sartor(裁缝)+ial——裁缝相关的——sartorial，裁缝的。

k.syn: cram, glut, stuff, surfeit, satiate
k.ant: starve 饿
k.en: to glut; to satisfy（an appetite）fully

ex.cn: 这个消息充分满足了她的好奇心。

m: sate——satiate(满足)的缩短——sate，使厌足。

k.syn: replete, sate, stuff, surfeit
k.ant: tantalize 挑逗，不去满足
k.en: to satisfy fully or to excess

ex.en: A long drink of water satiated my thirst.
ex.cn: 一顿痛饮之后我的口渴得到了缓解。

m: sati(满足)+ate——满足到饱腻——satiate，使饱腻。

k.syn: lampoon, pasquinade
k.en: a literary work holding up human vices and follies to ridicule or scorn

ex.en: a satire about the music industry in which a handsome but untalented youth is turned into a rock star
ex.cn: 一部关于一个长得帅却没才华的年轻人如何被打造成一个摇滚巨星的讽刺文学作品

m: satire——一锅杂烩(satura)讽刺——satire，讽刺。

k.en: to ridicule or attack by means of satire

ex.en: The movie satirizes contemporary life.
ex.cn: 这部电影讽刺当下生活。

m: satire(讽刺)+ize——用讽刺——satirize，讽刺。

k.syn: bathe, douse, drench, soak, souse, bedraggle
k.ant: wring out 拧出（水）来
k.en: to wet thoroughly with liquid

ex.en: Saturate the sponge with water.
ex.cn: 将海绵浸透。

m: satur(充满)+ate——使充满——saturate，使充满。

k.syn: bleak, depressive, dismal, dreary, miserable, solemn
k.ant: jovial, bright, cheerful, comforting, cordial, festive, heartwarming, sunshiny 欢乐的
k.en: causing or marked by an atmosphere lacking in cheer

ex.en: The men awaiting interrogation by the police shared a saturnine silence.
ex.cn: 等待警察审问的人们脸色阴沉，沉默不语。

k.ant: genial 友善的
k.en: having a sardonic aspect

ex.en: a saturnine smile
ex.cn: 讥讽的笑容

m: Saturn(土星/阴暗)——土星影响下忧郁阴沉——saturnine，忧郁的。

k.syn: amble, ramble, range, stroll, wander, perambulate
k.en: to travel by foot for exercise or pleasure

ex.en: saunter slowly down the street
ex.cn: 在街上闲逛

m: saunter——leisurely stroll，悠闲漫步——saunter，漫步。

k.en: a flat, grassy plain with few trees, especially in tropical regions

m: savanna——热带草原——savanna，热带草原。

k.syn: pundit, scholar
k.ant: unlearned person 没有学问的人
k.en: a person of learning

ex.en: a savant in the field of medical ethics
ex.cn: 医学伦理方面的专家

m: sav(知道)+ant——知道很多的人——savant，博学者。

k.syn: ambrosial, appetizing, dainty, palatable, scrumptious
k.ant: noisome, fetid, foul, malodorous, putrid, rancid, reeking, smelly, stenchy, stinky 难闻的
k.en: appetizing to the taste or smell

ex.en: Cedar is one of the most savory of all woods.
ex.cn: 雪松是所有树木中味道最好闻的。

k.syn: agreeable, blessed, congenial, delectable, delicious, delightful, dulcet, enjoyable, felicitous, grateful, gratifying, heavenly, jolly, luscious, palatable, pleasurable, satisfying, tasty, welcome
k.ant: disagreeable, pleasureless, unpalatable, unpleasant, unwelcome 讨厌的，不受欢迎的
k.en: giving pleasure or contentment to the mind or senses

ex.en: Having to fire someone was not a task that the manager found at all savory.
ex.cn: 经理发现不得不开除某人是件非常不讨好的事。

m: sav(口味)+ory——味道好的——savory，美味的。

k.syn: chops, expertise, proficiency, know-how
k.ant: simplicity, tactlessness, inexperience 单纯，缺乏经验
k.en: knowledge gained by actually doing or living through something

ex.en: political savvy
ex.cn: 政治上的老练精明

k.syn: appreciate, apprehend, assimilate, behold, catch on, cognize, compass, conceive, decipher, decode, discern, grasp, perceive, recognize, register, seize, tumble to
k.ant: miss 不理解
k.en: to have a clear idea of

ex.en: The man growled,“Don't ever date my daughter again—you savvy?”
ex.cn: 那男人咆哮道，“你丫以后不准再见我女儿，你的明白？”

k.syn: astute, canny, clear-eyed, clear-sighted, hard-boiled, hardheaded, heady, sharp, sharp-witted, smart
k.ant: unknowing 无知的
k.en: having or showing a practical cleverness or judgment

ex.en: A particularly savvy investor, he was among the first to see the potential in tech stocks.
ex.cn: 他是一个有卓识远见的投资者，是第一批看到科技类股票升值潜力的人。

m: savvy——法语知道(savoir)+y，精明懂行的——savvy，精明的。

k.en: fine particles (as of wood) made by a saw in cutting

m: saw(锯)+dust(尘)——锯木头留下的粉尘——sawdust，锯末。

k.en: a sheath for a sword, dagger

m: scabbard谐音「死把它「——把剑死死收进去。骑士战后把染血的长剑往剑鞘里一插，利刃入鞘——scabbard，剑鞘。

k.syn: abundance, plenitude, profusion, slew, spate, wealth
k.ant: paucity 极小量
k.en: a large number or quantity

ex.en: scads of people showed up for the party
ex.cn: 参加派对的人们蜂拥而至

m: scads谐音「死贱「——便宜到死。仓库里scads of货，多得数不清——scads，大量许多。

k.en: A device for weighing; an instrument used to measure mass or weight.

m: scale——天平称量，scale，天平刻度。

k.en: a small straight knife with a thin sharp blade used in surgery

m: scalp(头皮)+el——切割用的小刀——scalpel，手术刀。

k.en: To analyze verse into metrical feet; to read according to metrical structure.

m: scan——扫描，scan，扫描分析韵律。

k.syn: exiguous, niggardly, scarce, sparse, stingy
k.ant: considerable, copious, voluminous, profuse, myriad 大量的，相当多的
k.en: barely or scarcely sufficient

ex.cn: 那年暑假适合青少年们做的工作很少。

m: scant = can't的变体——can't afford enough。书架上的书scant得可怜，只有三本——scant，不足的缺乏的。

k.syn: acerbic, acid, acrid, pungent, scalding, snarky, tart
k.ant: polite, calm compliment 礼貌的，平静的称赞
k.en: marked by the use of wit that is intended to cause hurt feelings

ex.en: a scathing review of the book
ex.cn: 尖刻的书评

m: scath(伤害)+ing——造成伤害的批评——scathing，严厉批评的。

k.syn: conflict, discordance, disharmony, dissidence, dissonance, disunion, friction, strife
k.ant: accord, agreement, concord, harmony, peace 一致
k.en: a lack of agreement or harmony

ex.en: a schism between political parties
ex.cn: 党派间的冲突

m: schism——sc+hism，分裂分歧——schism，分裂。

k.en: a large group of fish swimming together

m: school有个冷僻义：鱼群。一群鱼像上学的孩子一样整齐游过——这群小鱼也在上school——school，鱼群。

k.syn: gleam, glimmer, glisten, glister, luster, sparkle, winkle
k.der: scintillating adj. 才华横溢的：brilliantly lively, stimulating, or witty
k.ant: dull, foolly 迟钝的
k.en: to emit sparks

ex.en: diamond ring scintillated in the sunlight
ex.cn: 钻戒在阳光下闪闪发光

ex.en: a scintillating conversation
ex.cn: 智慧火花四溅的交谈

m: scintill(闪光)+ate——像火花闪烁——scintillate，闪烁机智。

k.ant: ancestor 前辈
k.en: descendant, child

ex.en: scion of a railroad empire
ex.cn: 铁路大王的后代

m: scion——贵族的后代嫩枝——scion，后裔嫩枝。

k.syn: bifurcation, cleavage, dissolution, division, partition, schism, sundering
k.ant: unification, union 统一
k.en: a division or split in a group or union: schism

ex.en: The scission of the labor union will compromise the workers' bargaining power.
ex.cn: 工会内部的分裂会削弱工人讨价的力量。

m: 谐音「死剪掉」——剪刀「scissors」的发音就藏在scission里！scissors剪开，scission是剪开、分裂的动作——scission，切断、分裂。

k.en: cutting = pestle: grinding 剪刀用来剪切=杵用来磨碎（工具及其功能）

m: scissor谐音「死磋「——死命磋磨。剪刀两片刃交错，咔嚓咔嚓——scissor，剪刀，剪切。

k.en: to treat or address with derision: mock

ex.en: scoff at the idea
ex.cn: 对这个提议不屑一顾

k.syn: cram, devour, glut, gorge, gormandize, gulp, ingurgitate, inhale, raven
k.en: to swallow or eat greedily

ex.en: He scoffed dinner before running off to the basketball game.
ex.cn: 他风卷残云般把晚饭消灭了，就直奔向篮球场。

m: scoff谐音「死看夫「——用鄙视眼神死盯对方。专家看到网上流传的「地平论「，嘴角一撇，轻蔑一笑——scoff，嘲笑嗤之以鼻。

k.en: a child's foot-operated vehicle consisting of a narrow footboard mounted between two

m: scooter谐音「死酷特「——特别酷地滑着走。小孩踩着踏板车咻地一声冲下坡道——scooter，踏板滑行车。

k.syn: dehydrate, desiccate, parch, sear, singe
k.ant: dampen, hydrate, wash, water, wet 使潮湿
k.en: to burn on the surface; to make dry

ex.en: scorching sun
ex.cn: 火辣辣的太阳

m: scorch谐音「死焦「。铁板烧太烫，肉片一放上去就焦了，冒起黑烟——scorch，烧焦烘干。

k.en: the music composed for a film or play; a set of twenty

m: score——乐谱的分数线——score，乐谱。

k.syn: despise, disregard, flout, contemn, disdain, slight, look down（on or upon）
k.ant: adulate谄媚；venerate, honor, respect, revere 尊敬
k.en: reject or dismiss as contemptible or unworthy

ex.en: They were scorned as fanatics.
ex.cn: 他们被指责为狂热分子。

m: scorn——嗤之以鼻轻蔑——scorn，轻蔑。

k.syn: hinder, thwart
k.en: to put an abrupt end to

ex.en: The prime minister scotched the rumors of her illness.
ex.cn: 首相出面澄清了关于她生病的谣言。

m: scotch——cut(切)+ch，切断阻止——scotch，阻止。

k.en: to clean, polish, or wash by scrubbing

m: scour——大力擦洗彻底搜寻——scour，擦洗搜寻。

k.syn: frown, pout, glare, gloom, glower, lower
k.ant: beam, grin, smile 微笑（表示高兴）
k.en: to contract the brow in an expression of displeasure

ex.en: scowl down at the misbehaving child
ex.cn: 对淘气的孩子皱起眉

m: scowl——像乌云，眉头紧皱——scowl，皱眉。

k.syn: chaff, debris, dreck, dross, dust, effluvium, junk, litter, offal, offscouring, raffle, refuse, riffraff, rubbish, waste
k.en: discarded or useless material

ex.en: The rest of this stuff is just scrap, so sweep it up and throw it away.
ex.cn: 剩下的这堆东西都是没用的，把它们打扫一下就扔了吧。

k.syn: ditch, dump, jettison, junk, toss, throw away
k.ant: retrieve 找回
k.en: to get rid of as useless or unwanted

ex.en: We've decided to scrap the first car.
ex.cn: 我们决定丢弃第一辆车。

k.syn: abandon, abort, drop, recall, repeal, rescind, revoke, call off, cry off
k.ant: continue, keep 继续，持续
k.en: to put an end to（something planned or previously agreed to）

ex.en: We scrapped our plans to go to Paris, and set out the next day for Prague.
ex.cn: 我们取消了去巴黎的计划，第二天启程奔赴布拉格。

m: 两人在废铁堆旁大吵，最后把计划也一起扔进废料堆——scrap，废料/口角/废弃。

k.syn: aggressive, assaultive, combative, militant, pugnacious, truculent
k.ant: nonaggressive, nonbelligerent, pacific, peaceful, unbelligerent, uncombative, timorous 胆小的，爱好和平的
k.en: having an aggressive and determined spirit, quarrelsome

ex.en: She was a scrappy girl despite—or, perhaps, because of—her small size.
ex.cn: 这个妹子好打斗，但是身材娇小，不过这很可能是原因。

m: scrap(打架)+py——好打架的——scrappy，好斗的。

k.syn: scratch, squiggle, scribble
k.ant: write carefully 仔细地写
k.en: to write or draw awkwardly, hastily, or carelessly

ex.en: He scrawled a quick note, stuck it in their mailbox, and hurried off.
ex.cn: 他草草写了张便条，塞进他们的信箱然后匆匆跑了。

m: scrawl——crawl(爬)+scr，爬虫般的字迹——scrawl，潦草地写。

k.syn: scratch, scrawl, squiggle
k.en: to cover with scribbles, doodles, or meaningless marks

ex.en: Students scribbled furiously as the teacher lectured.
ex.cn: 老师一边讲，学生们一边狂抄笔记。

m: 谐音「死刻爸」——小孩拿笔乱刻乱画，在桌子上潦草乱写——scribble，潦草书写、乱涂乱画。scrib像「刻」，ble是反复动作。

k.en: the written text of a play, film, or broadcast

m: script——写(scrib)成的文字——script，剧本手迹。

k.syn: conscionable, ethical, honest, honorable, moral, principled
k.ant: cutthroat, dishonest, dishonorable, immoral, unconscionable, unethical, unjust, unprincipled 不道德的
k.en: guided by or in accordance with one's sense of right and wrong; principled

ex.en: Less scrupulous companies find ways to evade the law.
ex.cn: 道德程度略低的公司钻法律的空子。

k.syn: careful, conscientious, fussy, meticulous
k.en: taking, showing, or involving great care and effort

ex.en: The task requires scrupulous attention to detail.
ex.cn: 这项任务需要对细节一丝不苟。

m: scruple(顾虑)+ous——充满顾虑谨慎——scrupulous，谨慎的。

k.syn: accessible, apprehensible, comprehensible, fathomable, legible
k.ant: mysterious, incoherent, incomprehensible, inscrutable, insensible 神秘而不可知的
k.en: capable of being understood through study and observation; comprehensible

ex.en: Her machinations and motives are all too scrutable to us who know her.
ex.cn: 她的阴谋和动机对于了解她的我们来说简直昭然若揭。

m: scrut(研究)+able——能被研究理解的——scrutable，可理解的。

k.syn: examine, review, scan, survey, plumb, check（out）
k.ant: casually glance, gloss over 随意一瞥，敷衍处理
k.en: to examine or observe with great care

ex.en: I closely scrutinized my opponent's every move.
ex.cn: 我仔细注意着对手的每一个举动。

m: scrutin(仔细)+ize——仔细检查——scrutinize，仔细检查。

k.syn: abrade, graze, scratch
k.en: to become scratched, chipped, or roughened by wear

ex.en: a countertop that won't scuff
ex.cn: 一块不易磨损的厨房台面

k.syn: barge, clomp, clump, flog, flounder, lump, plod, pound, shamble, slog, slough, stamp, stomp, stumble, stump, tramp, tromp, trudge
k.ant: breeze, coast, glide, slide, whisk 滑行
k.en: to move heavily or clumsily

ex.en: The miners scuffed past in heavy boots.
ex.cn: 矿工们脚着厚重的靴子，步履蹒跚。

m: scuff——拖着脚走的声音——scuff，拖着脚走刮痕。

k.en: carve, sculpture

m: sculpt谐音「死扣特「。米开朗基罗对着大理石，一凿一凿死死抠出人体轮廓，三年才雕成大卫——sculpt，雕刻。

k.syn: contumelious, invective, opprobrious, truculent, vitriolic, vituperative
k.en: given to the use of vulgar, coarse, or abusive language

ex.en: scurrilous attacks on the senator
ex.cn: 对参议院粗俗的攻击

m: scurr(粗语)+ilous——充满粗语的——scurrilous，说粗话的。

k.syn: deplorable, despicable, grubby, lousy, scummy, wretched
k.ant: admirable, commendable, creditable, laudable, meritorious, praiseworthy 让人称赞的
k.en: mean; contemptible

ex.en: She was beset by a whole scurvy swarm of con artists.
ex.cn: 她被一群下流的骗子艺术家蜂拥围攻。

m: scurvy——吃不到蔬菜的水手得坏血病，可鄙的——scurvy，可鄙的坏血病。

k.en: an implement used for mowing or reaping

m: scythe谐音「死的「。死神手持长柄大镰刀，划过麦田，一切生命应声倒下——scythe，长柄大镰刀。

k.syn: sordid, base
k.ant: decent and respectable 适当而值得尊敬的
k.en: sordid; base

ex.en: the seamy side of urban life
ex.cn: 城市生活的肮脏面

m: seam(缝隙)+y——缝隙里的阴暗面——seamy，下流的。

k.en: A picture or view representing a scene at sea.

m: sea(海)+scape(景)——海的景色画——seascape，海景画。

k.syn: cloistered, covert, isolated, remote, retired, sheltered
k.en: screened or hidden from view

ex.en: secluded monks
ex.cn: 隐居的僧人

m: seclud(关闭)+ed——被关闭隔离——secluded，隐僻的。

k.syn: bury, cache, conceal, ensconce
k.ant: divulge, display, exhibit 泄密，展示
k.en: to conceal in a hiding place: cache

ex.en: The police found the weapon secreted under the driver's seat of the getaway car.
ex.cn: 警方在逃逸车辆驾驶座下发现了隐藏的武器。

k.ant: absorb 吸收
k.en: to generate and separate（a substance）from cells or bodily fluids

m: secret(秘密)+e——保持秘密隐藏——secrete，隐藏。

k.syn: collected, composed, self-possessed, serene, tranquil, undisturbed, unperturbed, unruffled
k.ant: agitated, discomposed, disturbed, flustered, perturbed, upset 不淡定的，狂乱的
k.en: free from emotional or mental agitation

ex.en: She remained sedate under pressure.
ex.cn: 她在压力下仍保持镇静。

m: sed(坐)+ate——坐下来镇静——sedate，使镇静。

k.ant: migratory, peripatetic 流动的，巡游的
k.en: not migratory: settled

ex.en: a sedentary lifestyle
ex.cn: 很宅的生活方式

m: sedent(坐)+ary——整天坐着的——sedentary，久坐的。

k.syn: allure, decoy, entice, solicit, tempt, lead on
k.ant: repulse, ward off 厌恶，不被吸引
k.cn: 劝说（使不忠，使不服从），诱…误入歧途
k.en: to attract or persuade to disobedience or disloyalty

ex.en: The other firm seduced him with a better offer.
ex.cn: 另一家公司用更高的待遇想挖他墙脚。

m: seduce = se（分离）+ duce（引导，如 produce）。坏人把小孩引开正路，用糖果一步步骗走——seduce，引诱劝诱。

k.syn: assiduous, diligent, industrious, laborious, occupied, tied-up
k.ant: idle, inactive, unbusy, unemployed, unoccupied 懒惰的
k.en: involving or accomplished with careful perseverance

ex.en: a sedulous student
ex.cn: 一个勤奋的学生

m: sedul(勤奋)+ous——充满勤奋——sedulous，勤勤恳恳的。

k.syn: befitting, decent, decorous, genteel, respectable
k.ant: uncouth, ribald, indecorous, improper, inappropriate, indecent, indelicate, unbecoming, ungenteel, unseemly 粗俗的，不礼貌的
k.en: following the established traditions of refined society and good taste

ex.en: a young lady of seemly appearance, robust health, and keen intelligence
ex.cn: 一个打扮得体、身体健康、头脑敏锐的姑娘

m: seem(看起来)+ly——看起来适当的——seemly，适当的。

k.syn: member, partition, portion, section
k.en: to separate into segments

ex.en: I think I lost one segment of this model kit.
ex.cn: 这套模型的一个零件我找不到了。

m: 谐音「塞个门特」——把一整条面包切开，每一片塞进不同的格——segment，分割、段落、片段。seg像「切」，ment像「片」。

k.en: A large fishing net that hangs vertically in the water to trap fish.

m: seine谐音「塞那「——把鱼塞进那网里。渔民撒下大网，把整条河的鱼都兜住——seine，大捕鱼网。

k.ant: hamper further development 阻碍进一步发展
k.en: containing or contributing the seeds of later development

k.ant: derivative 派生的
k.en: of, relating to, or having the power to originate; creative

ex.en: a seminal novel
ex.cn: 新颖的小说

m: semin(种子)+al——种子般开创的——seminal，开创性的。

k.en: a college that trains students to become priests, ministers, or rabbis

m: semin(播种)+ary——播种信仰的地方——seminary，神学院。

k.ant: anesthesia, numbness 麻木
k.en: a perception associated with stimulation of a sense organ or with a specific body condition

ex.en: We felt just the smallest sensation of warmth when we leaned against the radiator.
ex.cn: 当我们靠着取暖器的时候，我们也仅仅感到了一点点的温度。

k.syn: flash, marvel, miracle, phenomenon, portent, prodigy, splendor
k.ant: unnoticed event 不知名的小事
k.en: a state of intense public interest and excitement

ex.en: the rookie hitting sensation of the American League
ex.cn: 这个菜鸟的表现轰动了整个全美大联盟

m: sens(感觉)+ation——感觉的产生——sensation，感觉。

k.syn: susceptible, vulnerable, subject（to）
k.ant: numb 麻木的
k.en: susceptible to the attitudes, feelings, or circumstances of others

ex.en: He's very sensitive to the sun and will burn if he's outside for any amount of time.
ex.cn: 他的皮肤对日光非常敏感，在外时间长了就会烧灼。

m: sens(感觉)+itive——感觉丰富的——sensitive，敏感的。

k.syn: custodian, guardian, keeper, lookout, minder, sentry, warden, watchman
k.en: a person or group that watches over someone or something

ex.en: a lone sentinel kept watch over the fort
ex.cn: 一个哨兵看守着碉堡

m: sentin(看守)+el——看守的人——sentinel，哨兵。

k.ant: free of infection 未被感染的
k.en: of, relating to, or causing putrefaction

m: sept(腐烂)+ic——腐烂的——septic，腐败的。

k.ant: precursor 先兆
k.en: a secondary consequence or result

m: seque(跟随)+la——跟随疾病而来的——sequela，后遗症。

k.syn: insulate, seclude, segregate, separate, cut off
k.ant: permit to mingle, desegregate, integrate, reintegrate 混合，综合
k.en: to set apart: segregate

ex.en: sequester a jury
ex.cn: 让陪审团分开讨论

k.en: to cause to withdraw into seclusion

ex.en: She was sequestered in her room.
ex.cn: 她深居简出。

m: sequest(隔离)+er——隔离分离——sequester，使隔绝。

k.en: one of the 6-winged angels

m: seraph——六翼的天使——seraph，炽天使。

k.syn: arid, droughty, thirsty, waterless
k.ant: lush, damp, dank, humid, moist, wet 葱绿的，湿润的
k.en: being dried and withered

ex.en: a sere region that can't support agriculture
ex.cn: 一片贫瘠不能发展农业的地区

m: sere——sear(灼烧)变体——干枯的——sere，干枯的。

k.cn: 意外发现珍奇（或称心）事物的本领
k.en: the faculty of making fortunate discoveries by accident

ex.en: They found each other by pure serendipity.
ex.cn: 他俩的相识属于缘分天注定。

m: Serendip(斯里兰卡古名)——童话王子旅行发现惊喜——serendipity，意外好运。

k.syn: arcadian, calm, hushed, peaceful, placid, restful, still, tranquil
k.ant: boisterous, clamorous, clattery, deafening, raucous, roistering, romping, rowdy, tumultuous, unquiet, uproarious 喧闹的
k.en: free from disturbing noise or uproar

ex.en: a serene vacation spot
ex.cn: 一处宁谧的度假胜地

k.syn: collected, composed, possessed, recollected, undisturbed, unperturbed, unruffled
k.ant: havoc, pandemonium, tumult, bedlam, riot, furor 混乱，骚动 “行百里者半九十，不要等到发现GRE成为自己申请的短板时才追悔不已。” （蔺琳，Verbal 700，Quantitative 800，AW 5.0）
k.der: serenity n. 平静：the quality or state of being serene
k.en: unaffected by disturbance; calm and unruffled

ex.en: a serene man who was everyone's source of support
ex.cn: 一个沉着镇静的男人，他是所有人的精神支柱

m: serene谐音「赛那「——那里的宁静无可匹敌。高山湖泊，水面如镜，一丝风都没有——serene，宁静镇静的。

k.cn: 农奴身份, 农奴境遇
k.en: the condition of being bound to the land as a serf; feudal servitude

m: serf(农奴)+dom——农奴制度——serfdom，农奴制。

k.syn: homily
k.en: public speech usually by a member of the clergy for the purpose of giving moral guidance or uplift

ex.en: a sermon whose message was that we should love our neighbors as much as we love ourselves
ex.cn: 主题是教导我们要像爱自己一样爱邻居的一次布道

m: serm(说话)+on——说教的话语——sermon，布道。

k.syn: curved, curvy, devious, sinuous, tortuous, twisted, winding
k.ant: direct, straight, straightaway 直的
k.en: winding or turning one way and another

ex.en: The country inn lies at the end of a rather serpentine road.
ex.cn: 乡村小酒吧坐落在一条非常蜿蜒的道路的尽头。

m: serpent(蛇)+ine——像蛇一样弯曲——serpentine，蛇形的。

k.ant: without notches, smooth 无凹口的，平滑的
k.en: notched or toothed on the edge

ex.en: a serrate leaf
ex.cn: 锯齿状的叶片

m: serrat(锯齿)+e——像锯子的齿——serrate，锯齿状的。

k.syn: compact, dense
k.ant: widely separated, airy, loose, open, uncrowded 稀疏的
k.en: having little space between items or parts

ex.en: Flowers came up every spring in their serried ranks.
ex.cn: 大量的鲜花在春季盛开。

m: serried里有series(系列)——一系列士兵密密麻麻挤成方阵——serried，密集排列的。

k.syn: base, humble, menial, slavish
k.en: meanly or cravenly submissive: abject

ex.en: He had always maintained a servile attitude around people with money.
ex.cn: 他对有钱人总是卑躬屈膝。

m: 谐音「塞儿窝」——被人塞进奴隶窝，只能卑躬屈膝地服侍——servile，卑屈的、奴颜婢膝的。serv像「serve（服侍）」，大家认识这个词。

k.syn: austere, harsh, ramrod, rigid, rigorous, stern
k.ant: clement, forbearing, gentle, indulgent, lax, lenient, tolerant 温和的，随意的
k.en: given to exacting standards of discipline and self-restraint

ex.en: a severe, uncompromising teacher
ex.cn: 一位严厉、不妥协的老师

k.syn: brutal, excruciating, grievous, onerous, oppressive, tough, trying
k.ant: easy, light, soft 轻松的
k.en: difficult to endure

ex.en: a severe winter that was among the coldest on record
ex.cn: 有记录的最寒冷的冬天之一

m: severe谐音「死威儿「——严厉得像要杀人。老教授发下卷子，全班不及格，脸色铁青，严酷极了——severe，严厉严峻的。

k.syn: chain, enchain, enfetter, fetter, gyve, handcuff, manacle, pinion, trammel
k.ant: loose, emancipate, unbind, unfetter, unshackle 解放
k.en: to deprive of freedom especially of action by means of restrictions or handicaps

ex.en: unwilling to shackle the dogs to the wall of the house
ex.cn: 不愿意把狗拴在墙边

k.syn: clog, cramp, encumber, fetter, hinder, impede, inhibit, interfere with, manacle, obstruct, short-circuit, stymie, tie up, trammel
k.ant: aid, assist, facilitate, help 促进，帮助
k.en: to create difficulty for the work or activity of

ex.en: shackled by poverty and ignorance
ex.cn: 被贫穷和无知所阻碍

m: shackle——像锁链一样束缚——shackle，枷锁。

k.syn: chase, course, pursue, trace, track, trail
k.en: to follow especially secretly: trail

ex.en: shadow the suspect to see what he was up to
ex.cn: 尾随嫌犯行踪

k.der: shadowy adj. 模糊的：faintly perceptible: indistinct
k.en: to（cause to）become gloomy or dark

ex.en: He had only a shadowy idea of what they wanted him to do.
ex.cn: 他对要做的事只有一个模糊的想法。

m: shadow——跟在影子后面——shadow，尾随。

k.en: a finely stratified rock formed from clay or silt

m: shale——shell(贝壳)+ale，像贝壳层叠的岩石——shale，页岩。

k.syn: facile, one-dimensional, skin-deep
k.ant: profound 深刻的，有深度的
k.en: having or showing a lack of depth of understanding or character

ex.en: shallow generalizations
ex.cn: 一个浅显的概览

m: shallow——浅水的，肤浅的——shallow，浅的。

k.ant: debunk 揭穿真相
k.en: the quality of deceitfulness; empty pretense

ex.en: condemn the rigged election as a total sham
ex.cn: 指责这次有作弊的选举是彻头彻尾的骗局

k.syn: act, affect, bluff, counterfeit, cloak, dissemble, fake, simulate, pretend, profess, put on, pass for
k.en: to present a false appearance of

ex.en: sham a most unconvincing limp just to get sympathy
ex.cn: 非常拙劣地假装自己腿脚不便，以博得同情

k.syn: bogus, forged, phony, spurious, unauthentic
k.ant: genuine, authentic, bona fide, real, unfaked 真正的
k.en: not genuine; fake

ex.en: street vendors selling sham designer handbags to gullible tourists
ex.cn: 街头小贩向易上当的游客兜售山寨的设计师手包

m: sham——假的骗局——sham，骗局假装。

k.en: a piece of broken

m: shard谐音「碎啊「。花瓶从桌上滚落，摔成七八片锋利的碎片——shard，陶瓷碎片。

k.syn: idle, indolent, slothful
k.ant: industrious 勤奋的
k.en: lacking in ambition or incentive: lazy

ex.en: shiftless spongers who never thought to do anything for themselves
ex.cn: 无志气的弃民们，从来没有想过改变自己

m: shift(行动)+less——不想有任何行动的——shiftless，懒惰的。

k.syn: antiseptic, kempt, orderly, prim, uncluttered, well-groomed
k.ant: disheveled, disordered, disorderly, messy, sloven, unkempt, untidy 混乱的，不整洁的
k.en: marked by meticulous order and neatness

ex.en: I like to keep my car shipshape.
ex.cn: 我喜欢打理我的车。

m: ship(船)+shape(形状)——船上一切井然有序——shipshape，整齐的。

k.syn: avoid, dodge, elude, eschew, evade, shun, weasel out of
k.en: to get or keep away from（as a responsibility）through cleverness or trickery

ex.en: shirk one's duty
ex.cn: 逃避责任

m: shirk谐音「绕开「——shift（转移）+irk（烦恼），把烦恼绕开。员工看到老板布置任务就溜——shirk，逃避责任。

k.ant: deep 深的
k.en: having little depth: shallow

ex.en: shoal waters of the bay
ex.cn: 海湾附近的浅水区

m: shoal——shallow(浅)+al，浅水区——shoal，浅滩鱼群。

k.syn: banal, cliché, commonplace, hackneyed, trite, well-worn
k.ant: new, fresh, novel, original, unclichéd, unhackneyed 新的
k.en: worn-out, as from overuse: trite

ex.en: the shopworn suggestion to job applicants to“just be yourself”
ex.cn: 老掉牙的让应征者“做你自己”的提议

m: shop(商店)+worn(磨损)——在商店陈列久了磨损的——shopworn，陈旧的。

k.syn: crumb, driblet, mite, scintilla, spark, speck, sprinkling, tad
k.en: a small amount; a particle

ex.en: He struggled to retain a shred of his dignity.
ex.cn: 他勉强挽救了一点尊严。

m: shred = shred（碎片）。文件放进碎纸机，出来一条条细纸屑——shred，碎片，少量剩余，撕碎。

k.syn: crafty, cunning, devious, sly, subtle, wily, astute, savvy, sharp
k.ant: foolish, naïve, unknowing 愚蠢的，幼稚的
k.en: having or showing a practical cleverness or judgment

ex.en: He's shrewd about his investments.
ex.cn: 他是个精明的投资人。

k.syn: biting, bitter, keen, penetrating, piercing, raw, sharp, stinging
k.en: causing intense discomfort to one's skin

ex.en: She pulled her coat tighter against the shrewd breeze whipping down the alley.
ex.cn: 巷子里刮着刺骨的风，她拉紧了大衣。

m: shrewd——sharp(锐利)变体——精明锐利——shrewd，精明的。

k.en: shrine = climber: peak 朝圣者追求圣地=攀登者追求顶峰（追求关系）

m: shrine谐音「圣地那「。朝圣者跋山涉水，终于抵达那片圣地，跪地叩首——shrine，圣地神殿。

k.syn: bypass, disregard, gloss, ignore, neglect, minimize, overlook, overpass, slight, slur
k.ant: attend, heed, mind 留心，注意
k.en: to dismiss as of little importance

ex.en: The administration was willing to shrug off the problem.
ex.cn: 当局有意忽略这个问题。

k.syn: doff, douse, put off, take off
k.ant: don, put on 穿上
k.en: to rid oneself of（a garment）

ex.en: She shrugged off her coat and hung it up neatly.
ex.cn: 她脱下大衣，将其整齐地挂好。

m: shrug——耸肩表示无所谓——shrug，耸肩。

k.syn: avoid, dodge, duck, elude, eschew, evade, finesse, scape, shirk
k.ant: accept, embrace, welcome 接受，欢迎；pursue, seek 追寻
k.en: to avoid deliberately; keep away from

ex.en: After his divorce he found himself being shunned by many of his former friends.
ex.cn: 自从他离婚以来，他发现许多曾经的朋友都刻意躲着他。

m: shun——像躲瘟疫一样回避——shun，回避。

k.en: of, relating to, or expressed in relation to stars

m: sider(星)+eal——星相关的——sidereal，恒星的。

k.syn: beat, bypass, dodge, shortcut, skirt
k.ant: comply, follow, keep, obey, observe 遵守
k.en: to avoid having to comply with（something）especially through cleverness

ex.en: The eager enlistee sidestepped the regulations by lying about his age.
ex.cn: 急切的应募者通过谎报自己的年龄躲过了规定的限制。

m: side(侧面)+step(步)——侧身一步回避——sidestep，回避。

k.syn: distinguished, illustrious, luminous, notable, noteworthy, outstanding, preeminent, prestigious, redoubtable
k.ant: average, inferior, mediocre 一般的，低下的；insignificant, minor, unimportant 不重要的
k.cn: （在重要性、成就方面）非同寻常的
k.en: standing above others in rank, importance, or achievement

ex.en: The Louisiana Purchase is cited by many historians as one of the most signal events in American history.
ex.cn: 从法国手中购买路易斯安那被许多历史学家认为是美国历史上最重要的事件之一。

ex.en: a signal feat
ex.cn: 一项非同寻常的伟业

k.syn: beckon, flag, gesture, wave
k.en: to direct or notify by a movement or gesture

ex.en: They signaled at me to come over to their table.
ex.cn: 他们向我打招呼要我到他们那桌去。

ex.en: A lock on the suitcase might signal that there's something of value inside.
ex.cn: 手提箱上的锁可能意味着里面有贵重物品。

m: signal——发出信号，signal，重要的信号。

k.en: To smile in a silly, self-conscious, or affected manner.

m: simper——stupid smile，自以为是地傻笑——simper，傻笑。

k.syn: airhead, dolt, donkey, dullard, fool, idiot, moron
k.ant: intellectual, sage, wit 智者；genius, prodigy 天才
k.en: a person lacking in common sense; a stupid person

ex.en: His silly antics at office parties have earned him a reputation as a simpleton.
ex.cn: 他在办公室聚会上愚蠢古怪的行为让他被人认为是个蠢货。

m: simple(简单)+ton——头脑简单的人——simpleton，笨蛋。

k.en: an imitation of something real; a representation or model of a process

m: simulation谐音「思每拿雄」——科学家思考、模拟，每次拿一个雄性标本来仿制复制。或直接记：simulate就是「模拟/仿制」，simulation是其名词形式——simulation，模拟、仿制品。

k.syn: bad, evildoing, ill, immorality, iniquity, villainy, wrong
k.ant: good, morality, right, virtue 美德，美好的行为
k.der: sinful adj. 罪恶的，有罪的
k.en: an action that is or is felt to be highly reprehensible; morally unacceptable

ex.en: It's a sin to waste food when people are starving.
ex.cn: 当有人还在挨饿时浪费食物是一种罪过。

m: sin谐音「骂「（sin在英语里有「罪「的联想）。教堂里神父手指颤抖：「你犯了sin！「——sin，严重过失，罪。

k.syn: artless, genuine, heartfelt, honest, ingenuous, innocent, natural, real, true, unaffected, unfeigned
k.ant: affected, artful, artificial, assuming, dissembling, fake, guileful, insincere, phony, pretentious 做作的，假装的
k.en: being without hypocrisy or pretense; true

ex.en: She offered a sincere apology for her angry outburst.
ex.cn: 她为她先前的盛怒表示诚挚的歉意。

m: sincere——sine cera(不掺蜡)，不掺假的真诚——sincere，诚实的。

k.ant: drudge, drudgery, moil, sweat, toil, travail 艰苦的工作，苦活
k.cn: 拿钱不干事的职务；闲差事
k.en: an office or position that requires little or no work and that usually provides an income

ex.en: Brenda's job was by no means a sinecure: she actually had to work quite hard for her salary.
ex.cn: 布伦达的工作一点也算不上闲差事：实际上为了那点工资她必须没命地干活。

m: sine(没有)+cure(工作)——没有工作的职位——sinecure，闲职。

k.syn: energy, firepower, force, horsepower, might, muscle, potency, power, puissance, strength, vigor
k.ant: impotence, impotency, powerlessness, weakness 无力，虚弱
k.en: vigorous strength; muscular power

ex.en: Money is the sinew of love as well as war.
ex.cn: 爱情和战争都要用金钱来保证。

m: sinew——力量来源的肌腱——sinew，肌腱力量。

k.syn: char, scorch, sear
k.ant: incinerate 烧成灰烬
k.en: to burn superficially or lightly

ex.en: The iron is too hot, and you'll singe the nightdress.
ex.cn: 熨斗太烫了，你会把睡衣烫坏的。

ex.en: The marshmallows got a bit singed over the campfire, but we like them that way.
ex.cn: 棉花糖因为营火而有些许烧焦，但我们还是很喜欢它的味道。

m: singe——略微灼烧——singe，轻微烧焦。

k.syn: bending, curling, curved, curving, devious, serpentine, tortuous, twisted, winding, windy
k.ant: straight, straightaway 直的，直接的
k.en: marked by a long series of irregular curves; not direct

ex.en: The river flowed in a sinuous path through the lush valley.
ex.cn: 蜿蜒曲折的河流流过茂密的山谷。

ex.en: Thirty-year-old men are novel, rich in content and sinuous in plots.
ex.cn: 三十岁的男人是小说，内容丰富，情节曲折。

m: sin(弯曲)+uous——蜿蜒曲折——sinuous，蜿蜒的。

k.syn: gulp, guzzle, hoist, imbibe, quaff, swill
k.ant: vomit, throw up 呕吐
k.en: to swallow in liquid form, especially in small quantities

ex.en: slowly sipping the hot soup
ex.cn: 慢慢地喝着热汤

ex.en: She sipped her coffee while she watched the sun rise.
ex.cn: 她一边看着日出，一边喝着咖啡。

m: sip谐音「细品「——小口细品。老茶客端起龙井，小口抿一下，眯眼点头——sip，啜饮，小口喝。

k.en: A device that produces a loud warning sound, or something that is seductively alluring.

m: siren——引诱水手的海妖，或警笛——siren，警笛海妖。

k.syn: architecture, configuration, edifice, fabric, framework, framing, infrastructure, shell, structure
k.en: something forming a structural framework

ex.en: Only the charred skeleton of the house remained after the fire.
ex.cn: 大火过后整个房子只剩下被烧焦的骨架了。We saw a skeleton of the report before it was published. 在报告发表之前，我们看到了他的整体框架。

m: skeleton——骨骼框架——skeleton，骨架。

k.syn: disbeliever, doubter, questioner, unbeliever
k.ant: dupe, gull, pigeon 容易上当受骗的人
k.der: skeptical adj. 持怀疑态度的；skepticism n. 怀疑论
k.en: one who instinctively or habitually doubts, questions, or disagrees with assertions or generally accepted conclusions

ex.en: You can either be a skeptic or not, but I believe it anyway.
ex.cn: 至于你信不信，我反正信了。

m: skeptic = skep（疑）+ tic（人）。哲学课上总有一个人，什么都要问「凭什么「——skeptic，怀疑者。

k.syn: exiguous, meager, niggardly, poor, scant, scanty, scarce, skimpy, slender, slim, sparing, sparse, stingy
k.ant: abundant, ample, bountiful, copious, generous, liberal, plenteous, plentiful 大量的，丰富的
k.en: less plentiful than what is normal, necessary, or desirable

ex.en: The dieter complained about skimp meals that were served at the fat farm.
ex.cn: 这个节食者抱怨说减肥疗养地的伙食少得可怜。

k.syn: pinch, save, scrimp, spare, stint
k.ant: dissipate, lavish, prodigalize, squander, waste 浪费，挥霍
k.en: to give insufficient or barely sufficient attention or effort to or funds for

ex.en: We must skimp and save if we are going to afford a vacation this summer.
ex.cn: 要是我们这个夏天要出去旅行，那么就得开始节省出一些钱了。

m: skip(跳过)+imp——跳过不给——skimp，节省吝啬。

k.syn: altercation, controversy, disagreement, dispute, hassle, imbroglio, quarrel, spat, squabble, tiff, wrangle
k.ant: amity, concord, harmony, rapport, rapprochement 和睦，友好
k.en: a minor or preliminary conflict or dispute

ex.en: They had a skirmish over the rules before the debate began.
ex.cn: 在辩论开始之前他们因为规则而吵了起来。

m: skirmish——快速小规模冲突——skirmish，小冲突。

k.syn: borderline, boundary, circumference, compass, confines, edge, frame, fringe, margin, perimeter, periphery, verge
k.ant: center, core, kernel 核心
k.en: the line or relatively narrow space that marks the outer limit of something

ex.en: an old shack on the skirts of the town
ex.cn: 城镇边界上的一座老房子

k.syn: bypass, circumvent, detour, shortcut, sidestep
k.ant: pursue, seek 追寻
k.en: to go around or keep away from in order to avoid danger or discovery

ex.en: skirt the construction zone
ex.cn: 绕开建筑工地

ex.en: The new bill would make it harder for companies to skirt environmental regulations.
ex.cn: 新的规定会增加公司逃脱环境监管的难度。

m: skirt——围着走绕开——skirt，绕过裙子。

k.en: A short, comic theatrical piece that parodies or satirizes a subject.

m: skit——短小有趣的戏——skit，滑稽短剧。

k.en: a thick

m: slab——一大块石头或面包——slab，厚板。

k.syn: insecure, lax, loosened, relaxed, slackened, unsecured
k.ant: taut, tense, tight 拉紧的
k.en: not tightly fastened, tied, or stretched

ex.en: The rope is too slack.
ex.cn: 绳子太松了。

k.syn: careless, derelict, disregardful, lax, lazy, neglectful, neglecting, negligent, remiss
k.ant: attentive, careful, conscientious 专心的；alert, heedful, mindful, observant, regardful, vigilant, wary, watchful 警惕的
k.en: failing to give proper care and attention

ex.en: This building contractor is known mainly for his firm's slack workmanship and slipshod construction.
ex.cn: 这个建筑承包商因其公司不负责任的粗糙做工和马虎建造的建筑物而臭名远扬。

m: slack谐音「懈了「。弓弦松弛，箭射不出去；员工懈怠，工作停摆——slack，松弛的，懈怠的。

k.syn: assuage, quench, sate, satiate, satisfy
k.ant: tantalize, tease 诱惑，挑逗；arouse, excite, pique, stimulate 激起，唤起
k.en: to satisfy（a craving）; quench

ex.en: Mountain climbing has largely slaked my desire for adventure.
ex.cn: 登山很大程度上满足了我对探险的渴望。

m: slake——把渴望拍灭——slake，使满足消渴。

k.syn: angle, outlook, perspective, standpoint, viewpoint
k.en: a way of looking at or thinking about something

ex.en: an interesting slant on the problem of underage drinking
ex.cn: 在青少年饮酒这个问题上一个有趣的视角

k.syn: canted, graded, inclined, leaning, listing, oblique, pitched, raked, slanted, sloping, tilting
k.ant: perpendicular, plumb, vertical 垂直的；horizontal, level 水平的
k.en: running in a slanting direction

ex.en: The computer keyboard has a slightly slant surface so that typing is more comfortable for the wrists.
ex.cn: 电脑键盘有一个微小的倾斜角，因此打字不会使手腕感到不适。

k.syn: bend, color, distort, falsify, misinterpret, misrelate, misrepresent, misstate, pervert, twist, warp
k.ant: clarify, clear, elucidate, illuminate, illustrate 澄清，阐明
k.en: to change so much as to create a wrong impression or alter the meaning of

ex.en: Reporters slanted the truth in order to push a political agenda.
ex.cn: 为了推动政治进程，记者们歪曲了事实。

m: slant谐音「斜了「。记者写报道时把事实tilted，倾向某一边——slant，倾斜，表达偏见。

k.syn: catalog, enroll, enter, index, inscribe, record, register, schedule
k.ant: delete, erase 删除，除名
k.en: to put（someone or something）on a list

ex.en: You've been slated for a three o'clock interview.
ex.cn: 你的面试被安排在三点。

m: 黑板(slate)原是石板做的，上面写候选人名单，旁边批注被骂惨了——slate，石板/候选名单/严厉批评。

k.syn: abundance, bundle, dozen, multiplicity, myriad, plentitude, profusion, scad, shipload, volume, wealth
k.ant: ace, bit, driblet, glimmer, handful, hint, mite, mouthful, nip, ounce, pittance, sprinkle, trace 少量
k.en: a large amount or number

ex.en: donate a slew of books to the university library
ex.cn: 向大学图书馆捐赠了大量的书籍

m: slew谐音「死多了「。仓库里摆了slew of 货物，堆到天花板——slew，大量许多。

k.syn: fiddling, frivolous, inconsequential, inconsiderable, insignificant, little, minor, minute, nugatory, trifling, trivial
k.ant: consequential, eventful, important, meaningful, momentous, significant, substantial, weighty 重要的
k.en: deficient in weight, importance

ex.en: a slight comedy that did nothing to further her career
ex.cn: 一个对她事业发展没有任何帮助的小喜剧

k.syn: fragile, frail, negligible, outside, slim, small, tiny
k.ant: distinct 显著的
k.en: small in degree

ex.en: only a slight chance of success
ex.cn: 只有很小的成功概率

ex.en: If you have even the slightest doubt, then don't do it.
ex.cn: 只要你有一点点疑惑，那就不要去做了。

k.syn: contemn, disdain, disrespect, snub, look down
k.ant: esteem, honor, respect, revere, venerate 尊敬
k.en: to treat with disdain or indifference

ex.en: Those snobbish music critics slight any style of music that doesn't fit their personal taste.
ex.cn: 那帮自命不凡的音乐评论家看不起所有不符合他们个人口味的音乐。

k.syn: bypass, disregard, ignore, neglect, overlook, overpass, shrug, slur
k.ant: attend, heed, mind 留心，注意
k.en: to fail to give proper attention to

ex.en: She slighted several major authors in her survey of 20th-century fiction.
ex.cn: 她在调查20世纪小说时忽略了几个重要的作家。

m: slight——轻视某人——slight，轻视微小的。

k.syn: cast, chuck, dash, fire, fling, heave, hurl, hurtle, launch, lob, loft, pelt, pitch, toss
k.ant: catch 接住
k.en: to send through the air especially with a quick forward motion of the arm

ex.en: sling stones at the checkpoint
ex.cn: 朝着检查站扔石头

m: sling——用投石器甩出去——sling，投石器吊带。

k.syn: greased, greasy, lubricated, oiled, slicked, slithery
k.ant: coarse, rough 粗糙的
k.en: causing or tending to cause something to slide or fall

ex.en: The road is slippery after rain, so drive cautiously.
ex.cn: 雨后路滑，谨慎驾驶。

k.syn: furtive, shady, shifty, sly, sneaking, stealthy
k.ant: aboveboard 光明正大的
k.en: given to acting in secret and to concealing one's intentions

ex.en: a bar where a lot of slippery characters were known to hang out
ex.cn: 一个有许多秘密人物出没的酒吧

k.syn: ambiguous, elusive, equivocal, evasive, fugitive, obscure, unintelligible, vague
k.ant: apparent, clear, distinct, evident, manifest, obvious, palpable, patent, plain, unambiguous, unequivocal 明确的
k.en: not precise or fixed in meaning, elusive or tricky

ex.en: a slippery concept that we had trouble understanding
ex.cn: 一个我们难以理解的模糊概念

m: slippery——滑溜的——slippery，易滑脱的。

k.syn: botchy, careless, messy, slapdash, sloppy, slovenly, untidy
k.ant: fastidious, meticulous 极仔细的；accurate, exact, precise 准确的
k.en: marked by carelessness or indifference to exactness, precision, and accuracy

ex.en: a slipshod piece of research
ex.cn: 一项随意的研究

ex.en: The hotel had always been run in a slipshod way.
ex.cn: 酒店总是在以一种随意的状态运营。

m: slip(滑)+shod(穿鞋)——穿着拖鞋马马虎虎——slipshod，马虎的。

k.syn: blowsy, dowdy, frowsy, slovenly, unkempt, untidy
k.ant: dapper, dashing, sharp, smart, spruce 外表整洁的
k.en: lacking neatness in dress or person

ex.en: a sloppy child who always seems to have spilled something on his clothes
ex.cn: 一个外表邋遢的小孩子，他的衣服似乎总被沾上了什么东西

k.syn: chaotic, disarranged, disarrayed, disheveled, disorderly, jumbled, littered, rumpled, tousled, tumbled
k.ant: bandbox, crisp, kempt, neat, orderly, organized, shipshape, snug, tidy, trim 整洁有序的
k.en: lacking in order, neatness, and often cleanliness

ex.en: dump the papers in a sloppy pile on the desk
ex.cn: 把纸张随意地叠放在书桌上

m: slop(溅出)+py——溅得到处都是——sloppy，草率的。

k.syn: idleness, indolence, inertia, languor, laziness, lethargy, shiftlessness, sluggishness, torpor
k.ant: assiduity, diligence, industry 勤奋
k.en: disinclination to action or labor: indolence

ex.en: Sloth is the mother of poverty.
ex.cn: 懒惰是贫穷的原因。

m: sloth——像树懒一样懒——sloth，怠惰。

k.syn: deadbeat, drone, idler, loafer, slug, sluggard
k.ant: doer, hummer, hustler, rustler 活跃的人，精力充沛的人
k.en: a lazy person

ex.en: She is no slouch when it comes to cooking.
ex.cn: 她做起饭来可一点也不懒。

k.syn: creep, drag, inch, limp, nose, ooze, plod, poke, snail
k.ant: fly, race, speed, whiz, zip 快走
k.en: to go or move slowly or reluctantly

ex.en: He slouched towards the church as if going to his own funeral.
ex.cn: 他缓缓地向教堂走去，仿佛是要去参加自己的葬礼一般。

m: slouch——懒散地坐或走——slouch，懒散。

k.syn: blowsy, dowdy, frowsy, sloppy, unkempt, untidy
k.ant: dapper, dashing, neat, sharp, smart, spruce, trim 外表整洁的
k.en: lacking neatness in dress or person

ex.en: For the sake of their image, the band members transformed themselves from clean-cut lads to slovenly rockers.
ex.cn: 为了他们自身的形象，乐队成员从健康向上的青年变成了邋遢的摇滚乐手。

m: sloven(邋遢鬼)+ly——邋遢的样子——slovenly，邋遢的。

k.syn: deadbeat, drone, idler, loafer, slouch, slug
k.ant: doer, hummer, hustler, rustler 活跃的人，精力充沛的人
k.en: an habitually lazy person

ex.en: The teacher tried to wake up the sluggards who were still sleeping at that late hour.
ex.cn: 老师尝试去叫醒那些还在睡觉的懒鬼们。

m: slug(懒虫)+gard——懒虫一样的人——sluggard，懒鬼。

k.syn: crawling, creeping, dallying, dilatory, dragging, lagging, languid, leisurely, snaillike, tardy, unhurried
k.ant: bolting, brisk, fast, fleet, hasty, lightning, meteoric, quick, racing, rapid, rocketing, speedy, swift 快速的
k.en: markedly slow in movement, flow, or growth

ex.en: The sluggish pace of the project is worrisome.
ex.cn: 项目进展缓慢让人担忧。

k.syn: dull, indolent, inert, lethargic, quiescent, sleepy, torpid
k.ant: active, animated, bouncing, dynamic, energetic, kinetic, spirited, vigorous, vital, vivacious 有活力的
k.en: slow to respond（as to stimulation or treatment）

ex.en: Reptiles are naturally sluggish at low temperatures.
ex.cn: 在低温下爬行动物的生理机能自然会趋于迟缓。

m: slug(懒虫)+gish——像懒虫一样——sluggish，迟缓的。

k.syn: catnap, doze, nap, rest, sleep, snooze
k.ant: arouse, awake, waken 醒来
k.en: to be in a state of sleep

ex.en: She slumbered for hours while the train rolled on.
ex.cn: 她在火车行进过程中睡了几个小时。

m: slumber = slum（贫民窟）+ ber。贫民窟里，人们疲惫地沉沉睡去，连梦都是灰色的——slumber，睡眠，沉睡。

k.syn: blot, brand, onus, smirch, smudge, spot, stigma, taint
k.ant: award, credit, glory, honor 荣誉，荣耀
k.en: a mark of guilt or disgrace

ex.en: Your drunken behavior at the wedding has cast a slur on this family.
ex.cn: 你在婚礼上醉酒后的行为给整个家族都带来了耻辱。

k.syn: mumble, murmur, mutter
k.ant: articulate, enunciate 清楚地表达
k.en: to pronounce indistinctly

ex.en: If you slur your words, the visa officer will have a hard time following you.
ex.cn: 如果你说话含糊不清，签证官就很难听懂你的话。

k.syn: bypass, disregard, forget, ignore, overlook, overpass, slight
k.ant: attend, heed, mind 留心，注意
k.en: to slide or slip over without due mention, consideration, or emphasis

ex.en: This documentary slurs over certain important facts as it offers a very biased case for a conspiracy theory.
ex.cn: 这部纪录片忽略了许多重要的客观事实，而只给出了带有偏见的阴谋论解释。

m: slur——把音含糊模糊——slur，含糊地发音。

k.syn: beguiling, cagey, crafty, cunning, designing, devious, foxy, guileful, scheming, shrewd, subtle, tricky, wily
k.ant: artless, guileless, ingenuous, innocent, undesigning 天真纯朴的，无伪装的
k.en: clever or cunning, especially in the practice of deceit

ex.en: The movie pairs a sly, dissembling ex-con with an upstanding, straight-arrow cop.
ex.cn: 电影把一个狡猾、善于骗人的出狱犯和正直、坦率的警察搭配在了一起。

k.syn: furtive, shady, shifty, slippery, sneaking, stealthy
k.ant: aboveboard 光明正大的
k.en: given to acting in secret and to concealing one's intentions

ex.en: The maid took a sly look at the letter on the table.
ex.cn: 女仆偷偷地看了一眼桌上的信。

m: sly——像狐狸一样狡猾——sly，狡猾的。

k.syn: fulsome, oily, slick, soapy, unctuous
k.ant: artless, earnest, genuine, heartfelt, sincere, unaffected 真诚的，真挚的
k.cn: 虚情假意的，过分恭维的
k.en: hypocritically, complacently, or effusively earnest

ex.en: He is slightly smarmy and eager to impress.
ex.cn: 他有点虚伪，急于给人留下印象。

m: smarm(谄媚)+y——油腻谄媚的——smarmy，谄媚的。

k.syn: alert, brainy, bright, brilliant, clever, exceptional, fast, intelligent, keen, nimble, quick, sharp
k.ant: airheaded, brainless, doltish, dumb, fatuous, mindless, obtuse, senseless, simple, stupid, vacuous, witless 愚钝的
k.en: characterized by sharp quick thought

ex.en: a smart child who could do well in school
ex.cn: 一个能在学校取得良好成绩的聪明孩子

k.syn: dapper, natty, neat, sharp, snappy, spruce, trim
k.ant: disheveled, frowsy, sloppy, slovenly, unkempt 不整洁的
k.en: being strikingly neat and trim in style or appearance

ex.en: Dressed in their smart new uniforms, the cadets proudly paraded around the grounds of the military school.
ex.cn: 穿着整洁的制服，军校的学员们骄傲地在训练场上游行。

k.syn: ache, hurt, pain, suffer
k.ant: allay, alleviate, assuage, ease, mitigate, mollify, palliate, relieve, soothe 缓和
k.en: to suffer acutely, as from mental distress, wounded feelings, or remorse

ex.en: The injection only smarted for a moment.
ex.cn: 打针只会痛一小会儿。

ex.en: He is still smarting over criticism of his clumsy performance.
ex.cn: 那些对他笨拙表演的批评仍然让他感到痛苦。

m: smart——聪明的也使痛苦——smart，聪明使痛苦。

k.ant: erudition 博学；insight 深刻的见解
k.en: superficial piecemeal knowledge

ex.en: a smattering of Greek grammar
ex.cn: 略懂希腊语语法

k.ant: ace, bit, driblet, glimmer, handful, hint, mite, mouthful, nip, ounce, pittance, sprinkle, trace
k.syn: abundance, bundle, dozen, multiplicity, myriad, plentitude, profusion, scad, shipload, slew, volume, wealth 大量
k.en: a small scattered number or amount

ex.en: Only a smattering of spectators presented.
ex.cn: 只有少数几个观看者出席了。

m: smatter(少量)+ing——少少的知识——smattering，一知半解。

k.syn: grin, simper
k.ant: cry, weep 哭泣；groan, moan, sigh 叹息
k.en: to smile in an affected, often offensively self-satisfied manner

ex.en: She tried not to smirk when they announced the winner.
ex.cn: 当他们宣布她获得了胜利时，她尽可能地保持不露出笑容。

m: smug(自满)+irk——自满地微笑——smirk，自鸣得意地笑。

k.syn: asphyxiate, choke, stifle, strangle, suffocate
k.en: to be or cause to be killed by lack of breathable air

ex.en: Children should never be left in cars alone because they could become trapped and smothered.
ex.cn: 小孩子不应该被单独留在车中，因为他们很容易被困并且窒息死亡。

k.syn: bridle, check, choke, control, curb, muffle, quash, quell, repress, squelch, suppress
k.ant: unleash, vent（感情）爆发，宣泄；express 表达
k.en: to refrain from openly showing or uttering

ex.en: He quickly smothered his inappropriate laughter at the funeral ceremony.
ex.cn: 他很快就控制住了自己在葬礼上不适当的笑声。

ex.en: Management smothered the true facts of the case.
ex.cn: 管理层隐瞒了案件的真相。

m: smother——把嘴(mother)捂住——捂住窒息——smother，窒息。

k.syn: complacent, egoistic, overweening, pompous, proud, self-satisfied, vain
k.ant: egoless, humble, modest 谦逊的
k.en: having too high an opinion of oneself

ex.en: The winner was so smug that he lost the support of the crowd.
ex.cn: 胜利者过于自大，失去了群众的支持。

k.syn: dapper, kempt, orderly, shipshape, spruce, tidy, trim, uncluttered, well-groomed
k.ant: disheveled, disordered, messy, slovenly, unkempt, untidy 邋遢的，混乱的
k.en: being clean and in good order

ex.en: The suburb's smug lawns and tree-lined streets bespeak a comfortable affluence.
ex.cn: 郊区整齐有序的草坪和种满树木的街道体现出那里生活的舒适和丰富。

m: smug——自满得意——smug，自鸣得意的。

k.en: To import or export goods secretly and illegally, evading customs or laws.

m: smug(秘密)+gle——秘密运输——smuggle，走私。

k.syn: entanglement, mesh, morass, net, noose, quagmire, quicksand, toil,
k.en: something that catches and holds

ex.en: Someday you'll find that your lies are a snare from which you can't escape.
ex.cn: 总有一天你会发现，你所说过的谎话都会变成你无法逃脱的困境。

ex.en: caught in the snare of drug addiction
ex.cn: 处于吸毒上瘾的困境之中

m: snare谐音「死拿了「。猎人在兔子必经之路埋下套索，兔子一跳进去，死死被拿住——snare，诱捕，陷阱。

k.syn: entangle, ensnarl, interlace, intertwine, intertwist, interweave, knot, ravel, tangle
k.ant: disentangle, extricate, unravel, unsnarl, untwine, untwist 解开
k.en: to twist together into a usually confused mass

ex.en: You'll be awfully sorry if you snarl your fishing line.
ex.cn: 要是你把渔网给缠在一起，你肯定会非常后悔的。

ex.en: The new regulation has succeeded in nothing but snarling up rush-hour traffic throughout the city.
ex.cn: 新的政策将全城高峰时期的交通变得更为复杂难解。

k.syn: bark, fulminate, rant, roar, scream, shout, storm, vent
k.ant: calm 冷静下来
k.en: to give vent to anger in surly language

ex.en: She snarled at me after I kept badgering her with questions.
ex.cn: 在我不断用问题骚扰她之后，她终于忍不住爆发了。

m: snarl谐音「乱「。毛线球被猫越抓越乱，绕来绕去纠缠成一团——snarl，缠结纠缠。

k.syn: deride, gibe, laugh, jeer, jibe, mock, ridicule
k.ant: esteem, honor, respect, revere, venerate 尊敬
k.en: to speak in a scornful, contemptuous, or derisive manner

ex.en: They would invariably sneer every time they passed the hapless nerds.
ex.cn: 每次经过那个不幸的书呆子身边时，他们总是会报以不变的嘲笑。

m: sneer谐音「瞧你呀「。情敌从鼻孔里哼了一声，嘴角斜着扯起——sneer，轻蔑嘲笑。

k.syn: aristocratic, bumptious, elitist, haughty, imperious, persnickety, pompous, presumptuous, supercilious
k.ant: egoless, humble, modest 谦逊的，不自大的
k.en: being or characteristic of a person who has an offensive air of superiority and tends to ignore or disdain anyone regarded as inferior

ex.en: I expected her to be snobbish but she was warm and friendly.
ex.cn: 我本以为她会是个自大的人，结果她非常热心和友好。

m: 丝诺逼(snob)！端着红酒、鼻孔朝天的精英范儿——snobbish，附庸风雅。

k.syn: contemn, disdain, disrespect, slight, look down
k.ant: esteem, honor, respect, revere, venerate 尊敬
k.en: to treat with contempt or neglect

ex.en: The snob in town always snubbed anyone she thought was beneath her.
ex.cn: 镇上那个自命不凡的人总是对那些她认为不如她的人不屑一顾。

m: snub谐音「石鸟「——像石头一样对你视而不见。宴会上名流从头到尾无视新来的年轻人，当空气——snub，冷落不理睬。

k.syn: douse, drench, drown, impregnate, macerate, saturate, sodden, sop, souse, steep
k.ant: dehydrate, dry, desiccate 脱水；parch, sear, scorch 烤干，烧焦； wring 拧干
k.en: to make thoroughly wet or saturated by or as if by placing in liquid

ex.en: That downpour soaked my hair, and now I look like a sight.
ex.cn: 那场大雨把我的头发淋湿了，所以我现在看起来格外滑稽。

ex.en: We ran for home as soon as the rain started, but our clothes still ended up soaked.
ex.cn: 我们一发觉下雨就开始往家里跑，但我们的衣服还是湿透了。

m: soak谐音「梭可「。布料整块推进染缸，梭进去，彻底浸透，每根纤维都吃透了颜色——soak，浸泡渗透。

k.syn: earnest, grave, humorless, sedate, severe, sober, solemn, staid, weighty
k.ant: facetious, flip, flippant, humorous, jesting, jocular, joking, playful 轻佻的，幽默有趣的
k.en: marked by seriousness, gravity, or solemnity of conduct or character

ex.en: She made a sober reply to what was only a teasing comment.
ex.cn: 她很严肃地回复了那个恶搞的评论。

ex.en: Illness is a sober reminder of our mortality.
ex.cn: 疾病是死亡给我们发出的严肃警告。

k.syn: abstentious, abstinent, continent, self-denying, temperate
k.ant: hedonistic, licentious, self-indulgent, sensual, sybaritic, voluptuary 纵欲的，享乐主义的
k.en: given to or marked by restraint in the satisfaction of one's appetites

ex.en: The millionaire decided to live a sober life after bankruptcy.
ex.cn: 那个百万富翁破产以后决定过有节制的生活。

k.syn: firm, informed, justified, levelheaded, logical, rational, reasonable, sensible, solid, valid, well-founded
k.ant: groundless, illogical, invalid, unfounded, uninformed, unjustified, unreasonable, unsound 没有道理的
k.en: based on sound reasoning or information

ex.en: a sober assessment of the situation
ex.cn: 关于局势有理有据的分析

m: sober谐音「醒了「。酒鬼喝到一半突然清醒，神色严肃起来，放下了酒杯——sober，清醒的，严肃的。

k.syn: awash, bathed, doused, drenched, logged, saturated, soaked, watered, waterlogged
k.ant: arid, dry干燥的
k.en: containing, covered with, or thoroughly penetrated by water

ex.en: We stripped off our sodden clothes and wrung them dry.
ex.cn: 我们脱下湿透了的衣服并将它们拧干。

k.syn: douse, drench, drown, impregnate, macerate, saturate, soak, sop, souse, steep
k.ant: dehydrate, dry, desiccate 脱水；parch, sear, scorch 烤干，烧焦； wring 拧干
k.en: to wet thoroughly with liquid

ex.en: Soldiers' boots were soddened by endless hours in muddy trenches.
ex.cn: 长时间身处泥泞的战壕使得士兵的靴子湿透了。

m: sodden谐音「沉了「。跌入泥塘，全身湿透，衣服沉得像铅——sodden，湿透的，沉重的。

k.syn: consolation, relief
k.en: comfort in sorrow, misfortune, or distress

ex.en: The kind words brought a little solace to the grieving widow.
ex.cn: 这些同情的话语给痛苦的寡妇带来了些许安慰。

k.syn: cheer, comfort, console, soothe
k.ant: agonize, distress, harrow, torment, torture, trouble 使痛苦，折磨
k.en: to comfort, cheer, or console, as in trouble or sorrow

ex.en: I did my best to solace those bereaved children.
ex.cn: 我尽力去安抚那些失去双亲的孩子们。

m: solace谐音「扫了愁「。好友轻轻拍肩，一句「没事了「，心里的愁闷被扫走了——solace，安慰，慰藉。

k.syn: associate, coalesce, combine, conjoin, conjugate, connect, couple, fuse, interfuse, join, link, marry, unify
k.ant: disassociate, disconnect, disjoin, divide, sever, split, sunder 分开
k.en: to join or unite

ex.en: Wires are soldered onto the circuit board.
ex.cn: 电线被焊接在电路板上。

ex.en: The agreement soldered the factions into an alliance.
ex.cn: 两个派系因这份协议而结成同盟。

m: solder谐音「死焊了「。工人拿烙铁把两根铜线焊死在一起，滴锡封口——solder，焊接，焊合。

k.syn: earnest, graveness, gravity, intentness, seriousness, sobriety, solemnness, staidness
k.ant: facetiousness, flightiness, flippancy, frivolity, frivolousness, levity, lightheartedness, play 轻佻，欢快
k.en: the quality or condition of being solemn

ex.en: Solemnity is a trick of the body to hide the fault of the mind.
ex.cn: 一本正经的态度是肉体为了掩饰精神上的缺陷而耍的花招。

ex.en: The coronation ceremony requires absolute solemnity.
ex.cn: 加冕典礼需要绝对的庄严肃穆。

m: solemnity = solemn（庄严）+ ity。葬礼上所有人屏息凝神，连咳嗽都不敢——solemnity，庄严肃穆。

k.syn: attentive, considerate, kind
k.ant: heedless, inconsiderate, thoughtless, unthinking 不关心的
k.cn: 为他人操心的，体谅他人的
k.en: given to or made with heedful anticipation of the needs and happiness of others

ex.en: The solicitous husband had already cleaned the house and cooked dinner by the time his wife returned home from work.
ex.cn: 这个十分体谅妻子的好丈夫在他妻子回家之前就把家里清理干净，还把晚餐都做好了。

k.syn: agog, anxious, ardent, avid, desirous, eager, enthusiastic, greedy, hungry, keen, thirsty, voracious, wild
k.ant: apathetic, indifferent, uneager, unenthusiastic 无所谓的
k.en: showing urgent desire or interest

ex.en: The family is solicitous to put this whole unfortunate affair behind them and to move on with their lives.
ex.cn: 这个家庭迫切希望能忘记那些不幸的事情，开始崭新的生活。

m: solicitous谐音「求你了「。护士弓着腰问老人冷不冷、饿不饿，满脸关切——solicitous，关心挂念的。

k.en: to make solid, compact

m: solid固体+ify——熔岩冷却，哗地凝固成石头——solidify，凝固。

k.syn: monologue
k.ant: chorus, ensemble 合唱
k.en: a dramatic or literary form of discourse in which a character talks to himself or herself or reveals his or her thoughts without addressing a listener

ex.en: A soliloquy always reveals the character's thoughts and feelings.
ex.cn: 一段独白经常反映出人物的思想和感情。

m: solo独自+loquy说话——一个人独自说话——soliloquy，独白。

k.syn: aloneness, insulation, privacy, seclusion, segregation, separateness, sequestration, solitariness
k.ant: camaraderie, companionship, company, comradeship, fellowship, society 陪伴
k.en: the quality or state of being alone or remote from society

ex.en: He sought the kind of solitude where his thoughts would be his only companions.
ex.cn: 他在寻找一种孤独感，只有他的思想陪伴着他。

ex.en: She wished to work on her novel in solitude.
ex.cn: 她想一个人安静地完成小说。

m: solo独自+tude状态——一个人独处的孤独状态——solitude，孤独。

k.ant: bankrupt 破产的
k.en: able to pay all legal debts

ex.en: They're going to have to prove that the company is now solvent.
ex.cn: 接下来他们必须要证明公司的偿贷能力。

k.ant: solute 溶质
k.en: a substance in which another substance is dissolved, forming a solution

ex.en: Some organic solvents, such as benzene, pyridine, furan, are poisonous to human's reproductive system.
ex.cn: 一些有机溶剂（比如苯、吡啶、呋喃）对人体的生殖系统有毒性。

m: solve解决+ent——能把所有债务solve掉的人——solvent，有偿付能力的。

k.syn: animal, bodily, carnal, corporal, corporeal, fleshly, material, physical
k.ant: mental, spiritual 精神上的；nonmaterial, nonphysical 非肉体的
k.en: of the body, especially as distinguished from a body part, the mind, or the environment

ex.en: a somatic disorder that was once thought to be“all in the patient's head”
ex.cn: 曾被认为“全是病人头脑中的问题”的生理机能紊乱

m: soma身体——somatic身体的，躯体的——somatic，肉体的。

k.syn: black, caliginous, dark, dim, gloomy, lightless, murky, obscure, pitch- dark, rayless, stygian
k.ant: bright, brilliant, illuminated, lit, lightsome, lucent, lucid, luminous 光亮的
k.en: so shaded as to be dark and gloomy

ex.en: The prison's somber interrogation room has the desired effect of striking fear and despair into the prisoner.
ex.cn: 牢房中阴暗的问讯间有着能让囚犯感到恐惧和绝望的效果。

k.syn: depressing, dire, dismal, funereal, lugubrious, melancholy, miserable, morbid, morose, saturnine, sullen
k.ant: cheerful, delighted, festive, gay, jocund, jovial 快乐的
k.en: causing or marked by an atmosphere lacking in cheer

ex.en: Her death put us in a somber mood.
ex.cn: 她的死让我们陷入了悲伤沮丧的情绪之中。

m: somber谐音「扫门「——扫帚扫过门带来阴霾。葬礼之后，整个房间笼罩在灰暗的阴影中——somber，忧郁阴沉的。

k.syn: doziness, drowsiness
k.ant: insomnia, sleeplessness, wakefulness 失眠
k.en: the quality or state of desiring or needing sleep

ex.en: Somnolence is likely to be the most typical reaction to this novel.
ex.cn: 读这部小说最典型的感受就是想睡觉。

m: somno睡眠(同insomnia)+lence——困意袭来，somnolence了——somnolence，瞌睡。

k.en: A musical composition for one or two instruments, typically in three or four movements.

m: 扫哪啊(sonata)？用提琴弓扫出奏鸣曲——sonata，奏鸣曲。

k.ant: doggerel 幽默不正式的诗，打油诗
k.en: a 14-line verse form usually having one of several conventional rhyme schemes

ex.en: a sonnet that celebrates love
ex.cn: 一首歌颂爱情的十四行诗

m: son+net，网住十四行——十四行诗把情感网住——sonnet，十四行诗。

k.syn: cheer, comfort, console, solace
k.ant: agonize, distress, harrow, torment, torture, trouble 使痛苦，折磨
k.en: to bring comfort, solace, or reassurance to

ex.en: There seemed to be no words sufficient to soothe the widow.
ex.cn: 任何话语好像都不足以安慰这个寡妇。

k.syn: becalm, compose, lull, lullaby, quiet, quieten, salve, settle, still, tranquilize
k.ant: agitate, discompose, disquiet, disturb, perturb, upset, vex 打扰，使不安
k.en: to free from distress or disturbance

ex.en: He took her in his arms and soothed her.
ex.cn: 他把她抱在怀中以让她镇静下来。

k.syn: abate, allay, alleviate, assuage, ease, mitigate, mollify, palliate, relieve, soften
k.ant: aggravate, exacerbate, intensify 恶化
k.en: to make more bearable or less severe

ex.en: Hot tea with honey will soothe a sore throat.
ex.cn: 加了蜂蜜的热茶可以缓解喉咙的酸痛。

m: 搜斯(soothe)，轻柔地搜寻烦恼并抚平——soothe，安抚缓和。

k.syn: balm, emollient, placebo, salve, unguent
k.ant: irritant, stimulant 刺激物
k.en: something yielded to placate or soothe

ex.en: provide with the salary raise as a sop
ex.cn: 提高工资作为安慰

k.syn: backhander, boodle, cumshaw, fix
k.en: something given or promised in order to improperly influence a person's conduct or decision

ex.en: As a sop to the teachers' union for supporting his reelection campaign, the mayor promised to push for the abolition of the residency requirement.
ex.cn: 市长答应推动废除获得绿卡的限制条件，以此作为换取选举中教师联盟支持的条件。

k.syn: douse, drench, drown, impregnate, macerate, saturate, soak, sodden, souse, steep
k.ant: dehydrate, dry, desiccate 脱水；parch, sear, scorch 烤干，烧焦； wring 拧干
k.en: to wet thoroughly

ex.en: My book fell in the swimming pool and was thoroughly sopped before I could fish it out.
ex.cn: 我的书掉进了游泳池，在我把它捞出来之前已经彻底湿透了。

m: sop谐音「哄哄「。孩子哭着要玩具，妈妈随手递了块糖，暂时哄住——sop，安慰物，用来哄人的让步。

k.syn: sophistry
k.en: deceptive or fallacious argumentation

ex.en: Political selection is more dependent on sophism and less on economic literacy.
ex.cn: 政治选举更多的是依靠诡辩，而非简练的文笔。

m: sophist诡辩者+ism——古希腊诡辩学派说话绕圈——sophism，诡辩。

k.en: acquired worldly knowledge or refinement; lacking natural simplicity or naiveté

m: sophisticated老练+tion——见过世面的人处事圆滑老练——sophistication，精明世故。

k.syn: hypnotic
k.ant: stimulant 兴奋剂
k.en: a drug or other substance that induces sleep

ex.en: Alcohol is a poor soporific because it disturbs sleep patterns, and so can worsen sleep disorders.
ex.cn: 酒精是一种欠佳的安眠药，因为它会干扰睡眠的固定模式，从而恶化睡眠紊乱的情况。

k.syn: narcotic, opiate, slumberous, somniferous, somnolent
k.ant: arousing, waking, invigorating, stimulating 刺激性的
k.en: causing or tending to cause sleep

ex.en: This medication is soporific, so do not drive after taking it.
ex.cn: 这种药物是催眠的，服用之后切忌开车。

ex.en: the soporific heat of summer
ex.cn: 夏日里催人欲睡的炎热

k.syn: dozy, drowsy, sleepy
k.ant: awake, conscious, wakeful 清醒的；alert 警醒的；sleepless, insomniac 无法入睡的
k.en: of, relating to, or marked by sleepiness or lethargy

ex.en: A big lunch always makes me soporific in the afternoon.
ex.cn: 中午一顿大餐总是让我下午感到困倦。

m: sopor睡眠+ific产生——讲故事三分钟孩子秒睡，soporific——soporific，催眠的。

k.syn: besmirched, dirty, dusty, filthy, foul, grubby, muddy, nasty, smudged, soiled, stained, sullied, unclean
k.ant: clean, immaculate, spotless, stainless, unsoiled, unstained, unsullied 干净的
k.en: not clean

ex.en: I will not let my children grow up in such a sordid environment.
ex.cn: 我绝不会让我的孩子在这样一个肮脏的环境之中成长。

k.syn: base, contemptible, despicable, detestable, dishonorable, execrable, ignominious, mean, vile, wretched
k.ant: honorable, lofty, noble, straight, upright, venerable, virtuous高尚的，值得尊敬的
k.en: marked by baseness or grossness

m: sordid谐音「骚了底「——翻到最骚的底部。扫黑行动深挖，地下室腌臜肮脏，勾当卑鄙——sordid，肮脏的，卑鄙的。

k.syn: bombproof, fast, firm, stalwart, strong, sturdy
k.ant: ramshackle 摇摇欲坠的；rickety, unstable, unsteady 不稳定的
k.en: marked by the ability to withstand stress without structural damage or distortion

ex.en: The bridge is structurally sound.
ex.cn: 这座大桥在结构上是稳固的。

k.syn: bouncing, fit, hale, healthy, hearty, robust, well, well-conditioned, wholesome
k.ant: ailing, diseased, ill, sick 生病的；decrepit, enfeebled, feeble, infirm 虚弱的
k.en: free from injury or disease; exhibiting normal health

ex.en: The horse is getting old, but still perfectly sound.
ex.cn: 虽然在变老，但这匹马依然相当健康。

k.syn: analytic, coherent, consequent, rational, reasonable, sensible, valid, well-founded, well-grounded
k.ant: illegitimate, illogical, incoherent, inconsequent, invalid, irrational, unreasonable, unsound, weak（逻辑上）站不住脚的
k.en: based on valid reasoning

ex.en: Sound reasoning alone should tell you that the result is invalid.
ex.cn: 从纯粹严谨的逻辑演绎出发，这个结论是不正确的。

m: sound有个冷僻义：稳固的、健全的。桥梁工程师用锤子敲梁柱，听sound判断结构是否sound——sound，稳固的，健全的。

k.syn: exiguous, meager, niggardly, poor, scant, scanty, scarce, skimpy, slender, slim, sparing, stingy
k.ant: abundant, ample, bountiful, copious, generous, liberal, plenteous, plentiful大量的，丰富的
k.en: less plentiful than what is normal, necessary, or desirable

ex.en: Many slopes are rock fields with sparse vegetation.
ex.cn: 许多山坡都是不长植物的石坡。

m: sparse谐音「太稀了「。老人头发稀疏，梳子一过，头皮都露出来了——sparse，稀疏的，缺乏的。

k.syn: austere, plain, simple, stark
k.ant: deluxe, luxurious, plush 奢华的
k.en: marked by simplicity, frugality, or avoidance of luxury and comfort

ex.en: Accommodations on the windjammer are spartan but clean and comfortable nevertheless.
ex.cn: 帆船上的装饰十分朴素，但是干净、舒适。

m: 斯巴达(spartan)士兵粗粮布衣，简朴到极致——spartan，简朴克制。

k.syn: altercation, controversy, disagreement, dispute, imbroglio, quarrel, squabble, tiff, wrangle
k.ant: amity, concord, harmony, rapport, rapprochement 和睦，友好
k.en: a brief petty quarrel or angry outburst

ex.en: Like any other couple, they have their spats.
ex.cn: 像其他夫妻一样，他们也会有争执。

ex.en: They were typical sisters, spatting one minute, playing together the next.
ex.cn: 她们是典型的姐妹：上一分钟还在吵架，下一分钟就又玩在一起了。

m: spat谐音「拌嘴「。两口子为谁洗碗拌了个小嘴，没一会儿又和好如初——spat，小争吵，口角。

k.syn: bath, deluge, flood, inundation, overflow, torrent
k.ant: drought 干旱
k.en: sudden flood

k.syn: abundance, bundle, dozen, multiplicity, myriad, plentitude, profusion, scad, shipload, slew, volume, wealth
k.ant: ace, bit, driblet, glimmer, handful, hint, mite, mouthful, nip, ounce, pittance, sprinkle, trace 少量
k.en: a large number or amount

ex.en: a spate of books about GRE
ex.cn: 很多关于GRE的书

m: spate谐音「水拍他「。暴雨后山洪暴发，湍急水流猛拍堤坝，突如其来——spate，突发洪水，大量涌现。

k.syn: concrete, distinct, especial, peculiar, precise, special, unique
k.ant: general, generic, universal 普遍的
k.en: of a particular or exact sort

ex.en: We need a specific type of pen to sign the diplomas.
ex.cn: 我们需要一种特制的笔来签署学位证书。

k.syn: clear, definite, definitive, explicit, express, unambiguous, unequivocal, univocal
k.ant: ambiguous, equivocal, implicit, indefinite, inexplicit, unspecific, vague 模糊的
k.en: so clearly expressed as to leave no doubt about the meaning

ex.en: specific instructions regarding the interrogation of prisoners
ex.cn: 有关如何审问犯人的明确指示

m: species特有+ic——特定品种的specific特征——specific，特有的。

k.syn: beguiling, deceitful, deceiving, deluding, delusive, delusory, fallacious, false, misleading
k.ant: aboveboard, forthright, straightforward 明确的
k.en: having a false look of truth or genuineness

ex.en: a specious argument that really does not stand up under close examination
ex.cn: 经不起仔细推敲的论证

m: 外表像真物种species，实则是假的——specious，似是而非。

k.syn: ace, bit, driblet, glimmer, handful, hint, mite, mouthful, nip, ounce, pittance, sprinkle, trace
k.ant: abundance, bundle, dozen, multiplicity, myriad, plentitude, profusion, scad, shipload, slew, volume, wealth 大量
k.en: a very small amount: bit

ex.en: She writes without even a speck of humor.
ex.cn: 她字里行间一点幽默感也没有。

m: 白衬衫上一个小speck污点——speck，小点少量。

k.syn: bystander, observer, onlooker, viewer, watcher
k.ant: actor, performer, player 表演者
k.en: one who looks on or watches

ex.en: join the organization as a temporary spectator
ex.cn: 作为短期观察员加入该组织

ex.en: The accident attracted a large crowd of spectators.
ex.cn: 事故引来了大量的围观群众。

m: spect看+ator人——来观看的人——spectator，观众旁观者。

k.syn: diapason, gamut, scale, spread, stretch
k.en: a broad sequence or range of related qualities, ideas, or activities

ex.en: the whole spectrum of 20th-century thought 20
ex.cn: 世纪所有不同的思潮

ex.en: A political spectrum is a way of modeling different political positions by placing them upon one or more geometric axes symbolizing independent political dimensions.
ex.cn: 所谓的政治光谱是一种将不同的政治立场可视化的模型，它通过一条或者多条几何坐标轴来定位不同的政治态度。

m: spect看+rum——看彩虹所有颜色的光谱——spectrum，光谱。

k.syn: assume, conjecture, daresay, imagine, presume, speculate, suppose, surmise, suspect
k.ant: prove, substantiate, validate 证明
k.en: to take to be true on the basis of insufficient evidence

ex.en: I speculate that someone has been using this cabin as a trysting place.
ex.cn: 我猜测有情侣把这个小屋当做他们幽会的地方。

k.en: to buy or sell in expectation of profiting from market fluctuations

ex.en: speculate on the oil market
ex.cn: 在石油市场上投机倒卖

m: spec看但看不清，只能speculate猜——speculate，推测。

k.en: cavern =diver: ocean 洞穴探险者探索洞穴=潜水者探索海洋

m: spel洞穴+unker人——钻进洞穴探险的人——spelunker，洞穴探险者。

k.syn: fritterer, profligate, spender, squanderer, waster, wastrel
k.ant: economizer, penny-pincher 节约的人；hoarder, miser, niggard 吝啬鬼
k.en: a person who spends improvidently or wastefully

ex.en: The spendthrift managed to blow all of his inheritance in a single year.
ex.cn: 这个败家子只用了一年就把自己继承的遗产挥霍一空。

k.syn: extravagant, improvident, squandering, thriftless, unthrifty, wasteful
k.ant: conserving, economical, frugal, penny-pinching, provident, scrimping, skimping, thrifty 节俭的 The supreme happiness of life is the conviction that we are loved. 生活中最大的幸福是坚信有人爱我们。 ——法国小说家　雨果（Victor Hugo, French novelist） “Windener地上六层，地下四层；背好GRE单词再在这里读书便有通天入地的感觉。” （周晟茹，2007年10月，Verbal 720，录入院校：Harvard University Asia Center）
k.en: given to spending money freely or foolishly

ex.en: Spendthrift consumers amassed a mountain of debt on their credit cards.
ex.cn: 那些爱挥霍财富的消费者们在他们的信用卡上累积了一大笔债务。

m: spend花+thrift节俭——花掉所有不知节俭——spendthrift，挥霍者。

k.syn: beaten, bleary, done, drained, enervate, exhausted, fatigued, jaded, prostrate, tired, wearied, worn-out
k.ant: active, energetic, invigorated, peppy, strengthened, strong, tireless, vitalized, weariless 充满活力的
k.en: drained of energy or effectiveness

ex.en: After all that exertion, we were completely spent.
ex.cn: 在完成所有的锻炼之后我们已是筋疲力尽。

m: 体力全spend光了——spent，精疲力竭失去效用。

k.syn: gangling, gangly, lanky, spindling
k.ant: squat 矮胖的
k.en: frail or flimsy in appearance or structure

ex.en: I prefer having rather spindly legs.
ex.cn: 我更希望有一双细腿。

m: spindle纺锤形+ly——纺锤形的细长脆弱——spindly，细长纤弱。

k.syn: brambly, prickly, thorny
k.ant: even, flat, plane, smooth 平滑的
k.en: bearing spines, prickles, or thorns

ex.en: low spiny bushes of sage
ex.cn: 低矮多刺的长满鼠尾草的灌木丛

k.syn: catchy, delicate, difficult, knotty, problematic, sensitive, sticky, ticklish, thorny, tough, troublesome
k.ant: easy, effortless, manageable, painless, simple, straightforward, uncomplicated, undemanding 简单的
k.en: requiring exceptional skill or caution in performance or handling

ex.en: This promises to be a spiny problem to negotiate.
ex.cn: 这有可能成为谈判过程中棘手的问题。

m: spine脊刺——刺猬spine上多刺——spiny，多刺的。

k.en: a top part or point that tapers upward; a pinnacle

m: spire谐音「刺破「。哥特式大教堂的尖顶直刺天空，细如锥子——spire，教堂尖塔，尖顶。

k.syn: angriness, choler, furor, fury, indignation, ire, madness, outrage, rage, wrath
k.ant: delight, pleasure 高兴，愉悦
k.en: feelings of anger or ill will often suppressed

ex.en: She vented her spleen on her boyfriend and felt much better for having done so.
ex.cn: 在她把怒气一股脑地倾泻到她男朋友身上之后，她感觉好多了。

m: spleen = 脾脏，中文「脾气」的「脾」就来自这里——脾脏(spleen)积毒，怒火中烧——spleen，怒气/怨恨。

k.syn: associate, coalesce, combine, conjoin, conjugate, connect, couple, fuse, interfuse, join, link, marry, unify
k.ant: disassociate, disconnect, disjoin, divide, sever, split, sunder 分开
k.en: to unite（as two ropes）by interweaving the strands, or to join（as two pieces of film）at the ends

ex.en: He taught me to edit and splice film.
ex.cn: 他教我如何剪辑和叠接胶卷。

m: splice谐音「拼接「。老船员把两截绳子的末端细心编在一起，纹丝不动——splice，接合，捻绳。

k.en: material or a device used to protect and immobilize a body part such as a broken bone

m: split断了+int——骨头split，用splint夹板固定——splint，夹板。

k.en: one of the rods or braces connecting the hub

m: 车轮speak了(spoke)！轮子说话，每条辐条都像伸出的手指——spoke，辐条（speak的过去式同形）。

k.syn: automatic, impulsive, instinctive, involuntary, mechanical, natural
k.ant: calculated, deliberate, intentional, planned, predetermined, premeditated, studied 有计划的，故意的
k.en: acting or activated without apparent thought or deliberation

ex.en: Hugging a crying child is simply a spontaneous reaction.
ex.cn: 拥抱一个哭泣的小孩是很自然而然的反应。

m: spontaneous = spont + aneous。想到就做，完全自发，没人安排——spontaneous，自发的，即兴的。

k.syn: burlesque, caricature, mimicking, parody, travesty
k.cn: 轻松幽默的模仿，小恶搞
k.en: a work that imitates and exaggerates another work for comic effect

ex.en: Many viewers thought that the spoof of a television newscast was the real thing.
ex.cn: 许多观众认为这则新闻报道被恶搞之后的作品才是真实的东西。

k.syn: beguile, cozen, delude, dupe, fool, hoax, misguide, misinform, mislead, trick
k.ant: disabuse, disenchant, disillusion, undeceive 使醒悟
k.en: to cause to believe what is untrue

ex.en: The public was spoofed by a supposedly plausible report of a UFO encounter.
ex.cn: 一个听起来真实的UFO遭遇报告欺骗了大众。

m: spoof谐音「斯谱「——照着谱子开玩笑地演。模仿秀演员把总统模仿得活灵活现，台下笑翻——spoof，恶搞模仿，轻松幽默的仿作。

k.syn: few, infrequent, occasional, odd, rare, scarce, seldom, uncommon
k.ant: frequent, habitual, repeated 时常发生的
k.en: not often occurring or repeated

ex.en: On the whole, situation has significantly improved with only sporadic disturbances.
ex.cn: 总体而言，局势得到了明显的改善，现在只有偶尔发生的骚乱。

m: sporadic谐音「偶尔的「——sport（运动）+ adic。零星的枪声sporadic传来，时有时无——sporadic，零星的，偶发的。

k.en: To injure a joint by twisting it suddenly beyond its normal range of motion.

m: spring弹起来，结果扭伤了脚——sprain，扭伤。

k.syn: active, airy, animated, brisk, energetic, frisky, gay, jaunty, kinetic, mettlesome, racy, spirited, vital, vivacious
k.ant: dead, inactive, inanimate, lackadaisical, languid, languishing, leaden, lifeless, listless, spiritless, vapid没精神的
k.en: full of spirit and vitality

ex.en: He was deeply impressed by the sprightly Gypsy dance.
ex.cn: 活力四射的吉普赛舞蹈给他留下了深刻印象。

m: sprite小精灵+ly——小精灵一样活泼轻盈——sprightly，活泼愉快。

k.syn: boost, catalyst, impetus, incentive, incitement, instigation, momentum, motivation, provocation, stimulus
k.ant: deterrent, disincentive 阻碍物
k.en: something that arouses action or activity

ex.en: The threat of losing its only sports franchise was the spur the city council needed to finally do something about the rising crime rate.
ex.cn: 有可能失去唯一的体育赛事举办权，这成为了促使市政府决定就攀升的犯罪率采取相关措施的因素。

k.syn: brace, buttress, mount, prop, reinforcement, shore, underpinning
k.en: a structure that holds up or serves as a foundation for something else

ex.en: a weak wall that might need a spur
ex.cn: 一面或许需要支撑的墙

k.syn: arouse, excite, exhort, goad, instigate, prod, stir, urge
k.ant: bridle, check, constrain, curb, deter, fetter, hamper, inhibit, manacle, shackle 制止，阻碍，束缚
k.en: to incite or stimulate

ex.en: Appreciation spurred his ambition.
ex.cn: 赞扬激起了他的雄心壮志。

m: 马刺(spur)刺到马屁股，马立刻冲出去——spur，刺激激励。

k.syn: apocryphal, bogus, counterfeit, fake, forged, phony, sham
k.ant: authentic, bona fide, genuine, real 真实的
k.der: spuriously adv. 伪造地，虚假地
k.en: lacking authenticity or validity in essence or origin; not genuine

ex.en: In statistics, a spurious relationship is a mathematical relationship in which two events or variables have no direct causal connection, but it may be wrongly inferred that they do, due to either coincidence or the presence of a certain third, unseen factor.
ex.cn: 在统计学中，“伪相关关系”是两个不存在因果关联的事件或变量，因为巧合或是不可见的第三方因素，而被误判为存在因果联系。

m: spurious长得像curious——「让我curious一下……果然是spurious！」——spurious，伪造的。

k.syn: decline, disapprove, negative, refuse, reject, reprobate, repudiate
k.ant: accede, accept, agree, approve, assent, consent, embrace 同意，接受
k.der: spurned adj. 被摒弃的，被拒绝的
k.en: to reject with disdain or contempt

ex.en: They spurned his recommendation.
ex.cn: 他们拒绝了他的推荐。

ex.en: Fiercely independent, the elderly couple spurned all offers of financial help.
ex.cn: 这对老夫妻极其独立，回绝了所有经济援助。

m: spurn谐音「踢走「。求婚者单膝跪地，女方冷笑一声，直接起身离开——spurn，藐视，摈弃，断然拒绝。

k.syn: altercation, argument, controversy, disagreement, dispute, imbroglio, quarrel, spat, skirmish, wrangle
k.ant: harmony 和谐
k.en: a noisy quarrel, usually about a trivial matter

ex.en: a brief squabble over what to do next
ex.cn: 关于接下来该做什么发生了争吵

m: squabble谐音「吵吧了「。兄弟俩为了最后一块饼干大声争吵——squabble，口角，为琐事争吵。

k.syn: black, dirty, filthy, foul, impure, nasty, slipshod, slovenly, unclean
k.ant: clean, immaculate, spotless, unsoiled, unsullied 干净的
k.en: dirty and wretched

ex.en: The migrants have been living in squalid conditions.
ex.cn: 移民们一直生存在肮脏的条件下。

k.syn: base, despicable, ignoble, scurvy, sordid, vile, wretched
k.ant: moral, noble 品格高尚的
k.en: morally repulsive

ex.en: the squalid atmosphere of intrigue and betrayal
ex.cn: 充满了阴谋与背叛的不正之风

m: squalid谐音「死烂地「。流浪汉的窝棚，地板腐烂，满是污垢——squalid，污秽的，肮脏的。

k.syn: blizzard, hailstorm, snowstorm, tempest
k.cn: （通常伴随着雨或雪的）风暴，暴风雪，暴风雨
k.en: a brief, sudden, violent windstorm, often accompanied by rain or snow

ex.en: A snow squall is expected tonight.
ex.cn: 今晚预计有暴风雪。

k.syn: cry, howl, screech, shout, shriek, shrill, squeal, thunder, yelp, yell
k.ant: murmur, mutter, whisper 低声细语
k.en: to scream or cry loudly and harshly

ex.en: The baby squalled in pain.
ex.cn: 婴儿因为疼痛而大叫。

m: squall谐音「嚎了「。婴儿突然嚎啕大哭，打破一室宁静，短暂骚乱过后又归于平静——squall，短暂骚乱，突发暴风。

k.syn: disband, dispel, disperse
k.ant: assemble, cluster, collect, concentrate, congregate, gather, ingather 使聚集，使集中
k.en: to cause（members of a group）to move widely apart

ex.en: A single blast of the shotgun squandered the herd of deer.
ex.cn: 猎枪的一声枪响让鹿群四处逃窜。

k.syn: blow, dissipate, fritter, lavish, misspend, waste
k.ant: conserve, husband 节约，节俭
k.en: to spend wastefully or extravagantly

ex.en: He squandered his inheritance on women and gambling.
ex.cn: 他把他继承的遗产浪费在女人和赌博上。

m: squander谐音「散了「。纨绔子弟把父亲的家产撒手散了，花得精光——squander，浪费，挥霍。

k.syn: chunky, dumpy, heavyset, squatty, stocky, stout, stubby, stumpy, thickset
k.ant: gangling, lanky 瘦高的
k.en: being compact and broad in build and often short in stature

ex.en: a squat but adroit craftsman
ex.cn: 一个又胖又矮，但是手艺灵巧的工匠

m: 蹲下来(squat)的姿势，又矮又粗——squat，矮胖蹲下。

k.syn: crush, muffle, quell, repress, squash, subdue, suppress, crack down
k.ant: abet, aid, assist, back, help, support, prop up 支持，帮助
k.en: to put a stop to（something）by the use of force

ex.en: The authority tried to squelch the workers' protest.
ex.cn: 政府试图镇压工人的抗议游行。

k.syn: dumb, extinguish, mute, quiet, settle, still, shut up
k.ant: foment, incite, instigate, provoke, stir 激发，激起
k.en: to put down or silence, as with a crushing retort

ex.en: His irritated glare squelched any other potential objectors.
ex.cn: 他怒目而视，使得其他反对者都不敢说话。

m: squelch谐音「死压的「。起义刚冒头就被铁腕死死压下去，一点声音都没有——squelch，压制，镇压。

k.syn: leer
k.ant: gaze, goggle 直视
k.en: to look or glance sideways

ex.en: The driver squinted as the sun hit his windshield.
ex.cn: 阳光使得驾驶员不得不把眼睛偏向一边。

m: 眼睛斜斜地眯着看(squint)——squint，斜视眯眼。

k.syn: ballast, poise, steady
k.ant: destabilize 使不稳定；overthrow, overturn, subvert 推翻，颠覆
k.en: to make or become stable, steadfast, or firm

ex.en: a policy that intended to stabilize the situation in Iraq
ex.cn: 一项旨在维护伊拉克局势稳定的政策

ex.en: The country's population has stabilized at 3.2 million.
ex.cn: 这个国家的人口维持在三百二十万。

m: stable稳定+ize——让马stable安定下来——stabilize，使稳定。

k.syn: bold, courageous, dauntless, fearless, gallant, intrepid, manful, stout, stouthearted, valiant, valorous
k.ant: cowardly, craven, fearful, gutless, nerveless, pusillanimous, spineless, spiritless, timorous 怯懦的
k.en: feeling or displaying no fear by temperament

ex.en: The stalwart soldiers in the army of Alexander the Great, who willingly followed him to the ends of the known world.
ex.cn: 亚历山大大帝军队中无所畏惧的士兵们愿意跟随亚历山大奔赴世界的尽头。

k.syn: brawny, firm, muscular, rugged, sinewy, sound, stout, strong, sturdy
k.ant: delicate, feeble, frail, weak, wimpy 脆弱的
k.en: marked by outstanding strength and vigor of body, mind, or spirit

ex.en: a lithe yet stalwart athlete
ex.cn: 身体轻柔但结实的运动员

k.syn: adament, obstinate, unbending, willful
k.en: firm and resolute

ex.en: a stalwart supporter of the UN
ex.cn: 联合国的坚定支持者

m: stalwart谐音「死靠那「——死死靠在那里的人。两米高的护卫纹丝不动站在门口，健壮坚定——stalwart，健壮的，坚定的。

k.syn: endurance, tolerance
k.ant: frailty 脆弱
k.en: physical or moral strength to resist or withstand illness, fatigue, or hardship

ex.en: A marathon challenges a runner's stamina.
ex.cn: 马拉松挑战参赛者的耐力。

m: RPG游戏的stamina耐力条——stamina，耐力体力。

k.syn: falter, stutter
k.ant: articulate 清楚地说
k.en: to speak with involuntary pauses or repetitions

ex.en: The frightened child started to stammer.
ex.cn: 受到惊吓的小孩开始结巴地说话。

m: st-st-stammer结巴了，说话卡住——stammer，口吃结巴。
