k.en: one of the divisions of a poem, composed of two or more lines with a recurring pattern

m: stanza谐音「站那「。诗朗诵时，读完一节（stanza）就停顿一下，在那里站一下——stanza，诗节，段落。

k.syn: amaze, astonish, astound, dumbfound, rock, scare, shock, stun, stupefy
k.ant: reassure 消除（恐惧或疑虑）
k.en: to frighten or surprise suddenly and usually not seriously

ex.en: They were startled at the prohibitive price.
ex.cn: 他们被高得离谱的价格吓到了。

m: 大明星star突然跳出来，把人startle吓到——startle，使惊吓。

k.syn: inactive, fixed, immobile, immotile, immovable, inert, stable, stagnant, stationary, still
k.ant: active, dynamic, lively, vibrant 充满活力的；mobile, movable 可移动的
k.en: characterized by a lack of movement, animation, or progression

ex.en: Both your pictures are of static subjects.
ex.cn: 你的两张照片都是静物。

ex.en: a static economy
ex.cn: 停滞不前的经济

k.syn: inflexible, invariable, rigid, stiff, steadfast
k.ant: capricious, changeful, fluid, mercurial, temperamental, versatile, volatile 容易变化的
k.en: showing little change

ex.en: a static population
ex.cn: 没有变化的人口

m: static谐音「死了不动「。收音机突然一阵杂音，信号卡住，静止不动——static，静态的，停滞的，静电噪音。

k.syn: altitude, elevation, inch
k.en: natural height（as of a person）in an upright position

ex.en: a man of surprisingly great stature
ex.cn: 拥有惊人身高的人

k.syn: merit, quality, value, virtue, worth
k.en: quality or status gained by growth, development, or achievement

ex.en: This club has grown in stature over the last 20 years.
ex.cn: 过去二十年间里这家俱乐部的水平得到了提升。

m: statue雕像+re——雕像的高度就是stature身高——stature，身高地位。

k.syn: constant, dedicated, devoted, devout, loyal, pious, staunch, steady, true-blue
k.ant: disloyal, perfidious, recreant, traitorous, treacherous, unfaithful 不忠的
k.en: firm in belief, determination, or adherence

ex.en: Her comrades remained steadfast despite brutal tortures.
ex.cn: 尽管经受了残酷的折磨，她的同志们都依然坚定忠诚。

m: steady稳定+fast固定——稳固不动——steadfast，坚定忠诚。

k.en: the act or action of proceeding furtively, secretly, or imperceptibly

ex.en: Both sides advanced by stealth.
ex.cn: 双方都隐秘地行军。

k.syn: backstairs, clandestine, covert, furtive, private, sneak, stealthy, surreptitious, undercover, underground
k.ant: open, overt, public 公开的，光明正大的
k.der: stealthy adj. 秘密的，隐秘的
k.en: intended not to attract attention

ex.en: The SWAT team carried out a stealth raid on the house, which was believed to be harboring a terrorist cell.
ex.cn: 特警们对这间被认为是恐怖分子老巢的房屋进行了秘密的突袭。

m: steal偷+th——偷偷地行动——stealth，秘密潜行。

k.syn: drench, drown, impregnate, macerate, saturate, sodden, sop, souse
k.ant: desiccate, dry, parch, sear 烘烤，烤干；wring 拧干
k.en: to make thoroughly wet

ex.en: Steep the tea for five minutes.
ex.cn: 浸泡茶叶五分钟。

k.syn: abrupt, precipitate, sheer
k.ant: gradual 逐渐变化的；flat 平坦的
k.en: having an incline approaching the perpendicular

ex.en: a very steep rock face that is nearly impossible to climb
ex.cn: 一面非常陡峭、不可能攀爬的岩石

k.syn: exorbitant, extravagant, extreme, immoderate, inordinate, insane, lavish, overdue, overweening, plethoric, stiff
k.ant: moderate, modest, reasonable, temperate 适度的
k.en: going beyond a normal or acceptable limit in degree or amount

ex.en: We would like to hire him, but his salary demands are just too steep.
ex.cn: 我们很想雇用他，可是他对于薪水的要求实在是太高了。

m: steep有个冷僻义：浸泡。泡茶时把茶叶steep在热水里，慢慢出味——steep，浸泡，使充分渗透。

k.syn: fetor, funk, malodor, reek, stink
k.ant: aroma, fragrance, perfume, scent 香气
k.en: a strong, foul odor

ex.en: We finally discovered the dead rat that was causing the stench in the living room.
ex.cn: 我们终于发现是死老鼠带来了卧室里的恶臭。

m: 夏天地铁里，旁边的人举起手臂——stench扑面而来，瞬间窒息——stench，令人难以忍受的恶臭。

k.en: a thin sheet with cutout letters or designs used to reproduce a pattern on a surface beneath by applying ink or paint

m: Stentor是希腊神话大嗓门英雄——stentorian，声音宏亮。

k.syn: barren, effete, fruitless, impotent, infertile
k.ant: verdant（因长满植物而）青翠的；fat, fertile, fruitful 肥沃的
k.en: not productive or effective

ex.en: a sterile land which used to be a forest
ex.cn: 曾经是森林的一块贫瘠土地

k.syn: aseptic, germfree, hygienic
k.ant: insanitary, unhygienic 不卫生的；contaminated, polluted 受污染的
k.en: free from live bacteria or other microorganisms

ex.en: The hospitals in the war-torn city often lack necessary drugs and sterile surgical supplies.
ex.cn: 在这个被战争所蹂躏的城市中，医院里通常缺乏必需的药物和无菌手术用品。

m: sterile手术室，一个细菌都没有——sterile，无菌的。

k.syn: disciplinarian, pedant, purist, martinet
k.cn: 坚持细节的人，一丝不苟的人
k.en: one who insists on exactness or completeness in the observance of something

ex.en: a stickler for perfection
ex.cn: 完美主义者

ex.en: a stickler for neatness
ex.cn: 有洁癖的人

m: 把规则sticker到处贴的人——stickler，一丝不苟的细节控。

k.syn: immalleable, impliable, inflexible, petrified, rigid, unbending, unyielding
k.ant: flexible, floppy, pliable, pliant, supple, yielding 柔软的
k.en: lacking in suppleness or flexibility

ex.en: a stiff cardboard packing box
ex.cn: 不易弯折的硬纸板盒

ex.en: When I got out of bed this morning my back was stiff as a board.
ex.cn: 今早起床时我的背和木板一样僵硬。

k.syn: clumsy, gauche, graceless, inelegant, rustic, stilted, uncomfortable, uneasy, wooden
k.ant: graceful, suave, urbane 风度翩翩的
k.cn: （在社交场合）缺乏优雅的，不自在的
k.en: lacking social grace and assurance

ex.en: She always felt stiff and ill-at-ease whenever she was introduced to her father's friends.
ex.cn: 每当她被介绍给父亲的朋友时，她总会感到浑身不自在和不舒服。

k.syn: exorbitant, extravagant, extreme, immoderate, inordinate, insane, lavish, overdue, overweening, plethoric, steep
k.ant: moderate, modest, reasonable, temperate 适度的
k.en: going beyond a normal or acceptable limit in degree or amount

ex.en: Don't you think that's a pretty stiff fine for such a minor infraction?
ex.cn: 你难道不觉得对于这么小的一个错误，罚款实在太高了么？

k.syn: arduous, challenging, difficult, formidable, grueling, laborious, strenuous, sweaty, testing, toilsome, tough
k.ant: easy, effortless, facile, light, mindless, simple, soft, undemanding 容易的
k.en: requiring considerable physical or mental effort

ex.en: We'll have a stiff climb to actually reach the summit.
ex.cn: 我们还有一段艰苦的征途才能登顶。

m: stiff谐音「死翻不了「。冬天早晨，手指冻得僵硬，连书页都翻不了——stiff，僵硬的，不灵活的。

k.syn: choke, muffle, mute, repress, smother, strangle, suffocate, suppress
k.ant: foment, incite, instigate, provoke, stir 激发，激起
k.cn: 抑制（声音、呼吸等）；阻止，扼杀
k.en: to cut off（as the voice or breath）; to keep in or hold back

ex.en: stifle free expression
ex.cn: 限制言论自由

m: stifle谐音「死压了「。他把笑声死死压在喉咙里，但肩膀还是在抖——stifle，抑制，压制。

k.syn: blemish, brand, onus, slur, smirch, smudge, spot, stain, taint
k.ant: award, credit, honor 荣誉；fame, glory, renown, repute 好名声
k.en: a mark of shame or discredit

ex.en: the stigma of cowardice
ex.cn: 懦弱的污名

ex.en: There's a social stigma attached to receiving welfare.
ex.cn: 接受福利有一种社会耻辱感。

m: stigma烙印——被打上永久耻辱的印记——stigma，耻辱污名。

k.syn: closefisted, mean, mingy, miserly, niggardly, parsimonious, penurious, sparing, stinting, tightfisted
k.ant: bountiful, charitable, freehanded, generous, liberal, munificent, openhanded 慷慨的
k.en: being unwilling or showing unwillingness to share with others

ex.en: too stingy to tip the waiter
ex.cn: 小气到不肯给小费

k.syn: exiguous, poor, scant, scanty, scarce, skimpy, sparse
k.ant: abundant, ample, bounteous, copious, plenteous, plentiful 大量的
k.en: less plentiful than what is normal, necessary, or desirable

ex.en: Many people may consider this a rather stingy amount of food.
ex.cn: 很多人都会认为这是分量很少的食物。

m: stingy谐音「死紧「。守财奴捂着钱包死死拉紧，掏出一分钱都像割肉——stingy，吝啬的，小气的。

k.syn: scrimp, skimp
k.ant: blow, dissipate, fritter, lavish, misspend, squander, waste 浪费
k.en: to be sparing or frugal

ex.en: They never stint with their praise.
ex.cn: 他们从不吝啬他们的赞美之词。

m: stint谐音「省啊「——对自己极度节省。他在吃饭上stint，每天只吃一顿，把钱省下来买书——stint，吝惜，节省。

k.syn: blotch, dapple, dot, freckle, mottle, speckle, splotch, sprinkle, spot
k.en: to mark with small spots especially unevenly

ex.en: They crossed a field stippled with purple lavender.
ex.cn: 他们穿越了一片有紫色薰衣草点缀的田野。

m: stipple谐音「死点了「。版画家用针一点一点刻出星空，密密麻麻的小点——stipple，点刻，点画。

k.syn: claim, designate, detail, particularize, qualify
k.ant: relinquish, surrender, yield, waive 放弃（权利等）
k.en: to specify or arrange in an agreement

ex.en: Total disarmament was stipulated in the peace treaty.
ex.cn: 和平条约中要求彻底解除武装。

m: stipulation条款——合同里stipulate写死了要求——stipulate，规定要求。

k.syn: ache, pang, prick, shoot, smart, sting, throe, tingle, twinge
k.ant: comfort, ease, easiness 舒适的感觉
k.en: a sharp unpleasant sensation usually felt in some specific part of the body

ex.en: He had to drop out of the race when the stitch in his side became too painful.
ex.cn: 因为他突然感到剧痛，所以不得不放弃比赛。

m: stitch谐音「一针「。外婆在灯下一针一线缝补，每一stitch都是爱——stitch，一针，缝线。

k.syn: inventory, pool, repertoire, reserve, reservoir
k.en: the inventory of goods of a merchant or manufacturer

ex.en: This type of product is currently out of stock.
ex.cn: 这种商品已经脱销。

k.syn: common, conventional, general, ordinary, prosaic, routine, standard, universal
k.ant: unique 独特的
k.en: commonly used or brought forward

ex.en: the stock answers to sensitive questions
ex.cn: 对于敏感问题的常备回答

m: 仓库里stock库存充足——stock，库存储备。

k.syn: barricade, barrier, blockade, fence, wall
k.en: an enclosure or pen made with posts and stakes

ex.en: a heavy stockade that protected the troops
ex.cn: 保护驻扎部队的厚厚的栅栏

k.syn: bastille, coop, jail, pen, prison
k.en: a place of confinement for persons held in lawful custody

ex.en: All POWs were kept in a remote stockade.
ex.cn: 所有的战俘都被关在一个遥远的监狱里。

m: stock木桩+ade——用木桩围起来的围栏——stockade，栅栏围栏。

k.syn: arid, dreary, dull, flat, jading, jejune, monochromatic, monotonous, pedestrian, stale, tedious, wearisome
k.ant: absorbing, engaging, engrossing, gripping, interesting, intriguing, involving, riveting 吸引人的
k.en: dull, unimaginative, and commonplace

ex.en: The sitcom was offbeat and interesting in its first season, but has since become predictable and stodgy.
ex.cn: 这部情景喜剧的第一季是离奇而有趣的，但是后几季就变得老套和乏味。

k.syn: conservative, hidebound, intolerant
k.ant: avant-guard 先锋派的；radical 激进的
k.en: extremely old-fashioned

ex.en: Without notice she received a letter from his stodgy father.
ex.cn: 毫无征兆地，她收到了一封来自她保守父亲的信。

m: stodgy谐音「死呆了「。教授念稿子照本宣科，全班昏昏欲睡，讲了三十年的老笑话——stodgy，平庸的，乏味的。

k.syn: aloof, apathetic, detached, forbearing, indifferent, impassive, phlegmatic, stolid, tolerant, unemotional
k.ant: demonstrative, emotional, fervent, fervid, impassioned, passionate, vehement 充满热情的
k.en: seemingly indifferent to or unaffected by pleasure or pain

ex.en: stoic resignation in the face of hunger
ex.cn: 默默忍受着饥饿

m: 斯多葛(stoic)派哲学家啥都无动于衷——stoic，坚忍冷静。

k.syn: fuel, rekindle
k.en: supply with fuel

ex.en: She was stoking the furnace.
ex.cn: 她正给火炉添木头。

k.syn: aggrandize, amplify, augment, boost, enlarge, escalate, expand, extend, multiply, raise
k.ant: abate, decrease, diminish, downsize, dwindle, lessen, lower, minify, reduce, subtract 减少
k.en: to make greater in size, amount, or number

ex.en: stoke workers' commitment to the company by raising their salaries
ex.cn: 通过加薪来提升员工对公司的忠诚度

m: stoke谐音「死烧了「。炉前的工人不停往炉膛里加柴，火越烧越旺——stoke，添加燃料，煽动。

k.syn: apathetic, catatonic, deadpan, emotionless, impassive, indifferent, numb, phlegmatic, stoic
k.ant: demonstrative, emotional, fervent, fervid, impassioned, passionate, vehement 充满热情的
k.der: stolidity n. 麻木，无动于衷
k.cn: 无动于衷的，感情麻木的
k.en: having or revealing little emotion or sensibility

ex.en: Her face showed nothing but stolid indifference.
ex.cn: 她的脸上只露出了麻木和冷漠。

m: stolid谐音「石头「。无论好消息坏消息，他的表情从不变化，像一块石头——stolid，感情麻木的，无动于衷的。

k.syn: abide, brook, countenance, endure, handle, stand, tolerate
k.ant: decline, refuse, reject, repudiate, spurn 拒绝
k.en: to bear without overt reaction or resentment

ex.en: I can't stomach his bragging.
ex.cn: 我受不了他自吹自擂了。

m: stomach本义是「胃「，能消化一切。什么侮辱都能stomach下去——stomach，容忍，忍受。

k.syn: blockade, filibuster, hinder, impede, obstruct
k.ant: collaborate, cooperate 合作
k.en: to be uncooperative, obstructive, or evasive

ex.en: lobbying efforts to stonewall passage of the legislation
ex.cn: 意在阻止法律通过的游说行动

m: stonewall = stone（石头）+ wall（墙）。像石头砌成的墙，问什么都不答，不合作——stonewall，拒绝合作，阻挠。

k.syn: bold, brave, courageous, dauntless, fearless, gallant, intrepid, stalwart, stout, valiant, valorous
k.ant: cowardly, craven, fearful, gutless, nerveless, pusillanimous, spineless, spiritless, timorous 怯懦的
k.en: having a stout heart or spirit

ex.en: a stouthearted army officer who risked his life to save his men
ex.cn: 为了拯救部下宁愿牺牲自己的勇敢军官

m: stout强壮+heart心——心脏强壮，无所畏惧——stouthearted，大胆的。

k.en: A person who secretly boards a vehicle, ship, or aircraft without paying the fare.

m: stow藏+away走——偷偷藏进船舱逃票——stowaway，无票偷乘者。

k.syn: channel, narrow, neck
k.ant: isthmus 地峡
k.en: a narrow channel joining two larger bodies of water

ex.en: Thousands of vessels pass through the straits annually.
ex.cn: 每年数以千计的货轮要通过这个海峡。

k.syn: affliction, agony, anguish, excruciation, misery, pain, torment, torture, travail, tribulation, woe
k.en: a state of great suffering of body or mind

ex.en: She was in dire straits over the loss of her mother's cherished necklace.
ex.cn: 她因为丢失了母亲最珍贵的项链而陷入了极端的痛苦之中。

m: straight直通道(strait)！两边是陆地的狭窄水道——strait，海峡。

k.syn: thread
k.en: a single filament, such as a fiber or thread, of a woven or braided material

ex.en: a strand of DNA DNA
ex.cn: 中的一条链

k.syn: abandon, desert, forsake, maroon, quit
k.ant: evacuate, reclaim, rescue 救出
k.en: to leave in a strange or an unfavorable place especially without funds or means to depart

ex.en: The convoy was stranded in the desert.
ex.cn: 车队被困在了沙漠之中。

m: 丝绕了又绕(strand)，编成绳子的一股——strand，一股线绳。

k.syn: artifice, device, gambit, gimmick, intrigue, maneuver, ruse, scheme, trick, wile
k.en: an artifice or trick in war for deceiving and outwitting the enemy

ex.en: a stratagem to secure customer loyalty
ex.cn: 用来稳固客户忠诚度的计谋

m: strat战略+agem计——战场上的计谋策略——stratagem，谋略。

k.syn: assort, categorize, classify, differentiate, distinguish, separate
k.ant: homogenize 使均匀；commingle, mingle 混合
k.en: to divide into classes, castes, or social strata

ex.en: Income distribution often stratifies a society.
ex.cn: 收入分配往往将社会分层。

m: stratum地层+ify——像地层一样分成各层——stratify，使分层。

k.syn: aimless, arbitrary, desultory, erratic, haphazard, scattered, slapdash
k.ant: methodical, orderly, organized, regular 有条理的，有组织的； systematic, systematized 系统性的
k.en: lacking a definite plan, purpose, or pattern

ex.en: No stray sighting of UFO's has been rigorously analyzed by scientists.
ex.cn: 在无规律出现的UFO事件之中，尚未有被科学家严谨分析的案例。

k.syn: deviate, digress, err, wander
k.en: to move away from a group, deviate from the correct course, or go beyond established limits

ex.en: stray from the main road
ex.cn: 偏离了主干道

m: stray猫咪到处乱走，偏离了家——stray，迷途偏离。

k.syn: energy, force, might, potency, power, puissance, sinew, vigor
k.ant: impotence, impotency, powerlessness, weakness 无力
k.en: the ability to exert effort for the accomplishment of a task

ex.en: We are facing an army of great strength.
ex.cn: 我们面对的是一支强大的军队。

k.syn: firmness, soundness, sturdiness
k.ant: insecurity, instability, precariousness, shakiness, unstableness, unsteadiness 不稳固
k.en: the ability to withstand force or stress without being distorted, dislodged, or damaged

ex.en: This cheap shelve unit doesn't have the strength to hold all those books.
ex.cn: 这个廉价的书架不足以支撑所有的书本。

m: strength谐音「斯特狼「。狼群有令人生畏的strength，浓度和力量同义——strength，力量，强度，浓度。

k.syn: furrow, streak, stripe
k.en: to mark with striations or striae

ex.en: The inner surface of the bark is smooth, of a pale, yellowish brown and very finely striated.
ex.cn: 树皮的内部十分光滑，显着偏暗偏黄的棕色，并且有着十分精细的条纹。

m: stria条纹+te——给木头刻上条带——striate，加条纹。

k.syn: censure, condemnation, denunciation, excoriation, obloquy, rebuke, reprimand, reproach, reproof
k.ant: commendation, eulogy 表扬，赞扬
k.en: an adverse criticism

ex.en: The reviewer made several strictures upon the author's style.
ex.cn: 评论家就作者的文风发出了不少责难。

m: strict严厉+ure——严厉的责难批评——stricture，责难。

k.syn: march, pace, parade
k.ant: mince 碎步走
k.en: to move with or as if with long steps

ex.en: The man strode to the door and slammed it.
ex.cn: 男人大步走向门口，狠狠地把门摔上。

m: stride谐音「死催的「。教练在后面大喊，运动员迈开大步拼命冲——stride，大步走，迈步前进。

k.syn: grating, harsh, hoarse, jarring, rasping, raucous, squawky
k.ant: mellifluous（声音）甜美的；harmonious 和谐的
k.en: characterized by harsh, insistent, and discordant sound

ex.en: plagued by the strident noise
ex.cn: 被刺耳的噪声所折磨

m: 刺耳的strident声音让人皱眉——strident，刺耳高声喧哗。

k.syn: aggression, assault, charge, offense, onset, onslaught, raid, rush
k.en: the act or action of setting upon with force or violence

ex.en: The first strike was directed at a munitions warehouse.
ex.cn: 第一轮攻击的目标是军火仓库。

k.syn: build, fabricate, forge, form, make, mold, shape
k.en: to form by stamping, printing, or punching

k.syn: bat, beat, hammer, hit, knock, nail, punch, swat
k.en: to aim and usually deliver a blow, stroke, or thrust（as with the hand, a weapon, or a tool）

ex.en: The drunkard angrily stuck the security guard.
ex.cn: 酒鬼愤怒地殴打保安。

k.syn: assail, beset, storm, set on
k.en: to take sudden, violent action against

ex.en: A rattlesnake strikes its prey with lightning speed.
ex.cn: 响尾蛇以惊人的速度攻击了它的猎物。

k.syn: bang, bash, bump, collide, crash, impact, impinge, ram, slam, smash, swipe, thud
k.en: to come into usually forceful contact with something

ex.en: One bullet-train struck the other one which had been forced to stop on a viaduct due to lightning strike, resulting in huge casualties.
ex.cn: 一辆动车撞向了另一辆因雷击而停在高架桥上的动车，造成了巨大的人员伤亡。

m: 古代铸币就是strike(打击)金属——用铁锤猛敲金属模具，奖牌就诞生了——strike，铸造。

k.syn: inflexible, rigid, taut
k.ant: lax, loose, slack 松弛的
k.en: tight, constricted

k.syn: draconian, exacting, ironhanded, rigorous, severe, strict, uncompromising
k.ant: lenient 宽大的，仁慈的
k.en: marked by rigor, strictness, or severity especially with regard to rule or standard

ex.en: Its drug-testing procedures are the most stringent in the world.
ex.cn: 它的药检程序是世界上最严格的。

m: string弦绷紧+ent——弦绷得紧紧的——stringent，严格绷紧。

k.syn: denude, disrobe, doff, unclothe, undress
k.ant: dress, gown, robe 穿衣；bedeck 穿衣打扮
k.en: to remove clothing, covering, or surface matter from

ex.en: Guards stripped and started to search the prisoners.
ex.cn: 守卫剥光了犯人，开始搜身。

m: strip谐音「死撕破「。强盗死命把外衣撕破剥下来——strip，剥去，脱衣，剥离。

k.syn: parade, prance, stalk, swagger
k.en: to walk with a pompous and affected air

ex.en: A pompous general strutted off the parade ground.
ex.cn: 盛气凌人的将军从阅兵场上趾高气扬地走过。

k.syn: brace, buttress, column, girder, support, underpinning
k.en: a structural element used to brace or strengthen a framework by resisting longitudinal compression

m: strut里藏着rut(车辙/困境)——走出困境(rut)，就开始strut大摇大摆了——strut，昂首阔步。

k.syn: workshop
k.en: the working place of a painter, sculptor, or photographer

ex.en: move to a larger studio
ex.cn: 搬进更大的工作室

m: 艺术家在studio工作室里创作——studio，工作室画室。

k.syn: constipate, stagnate, stifle, trammel
k.ant: encourage, foster, nourish 鼓励，培养
k.en: to deprive of vitality and render futile especially by enfeebling or repressive influences

ex.en: The accident stultified his previous efforts.
ex.cn: 这场意外使他过去的努力都白费了。

m: stulti(愚蠢)+fy——让人变傻失去效用——stultify，使无效。

k.syn: check, curb, dwarf, suppress
k.ant: advance, boost, foster, nourish, nurture, promote 培养，促进
k.en: to hinder the normal growth, development, or progress of

ex.en: The inhospitable climate had stunted all vegetation.
ex.cn: 不佳的气候条件抑制了所有植物的生长。

m: 成长被stunt卡住了——stunt，阻碍成长。

k.syn: coma, dullness, languor, lethargy, lassitude, listlessness, torpidity, torpor
k.ant: alertness, vigilance 警惕，警戒；vigor, vim, vitality, vivacity 活力，精力
k.en: a condition of greatly dulled or completely suspended sense or sensibility

ex.en: The heartbreaking man lapsed into an alcoholic stupor.
ex.cn: 那个伤心的男人陷入了无知觉的酒醉状态。

m: stupor谐音「傻了「。中毒者眼神呆滞，叫他也不应，整个人陷入麻痹——stupor，迟钝，麻痹，无知觉。

k.syn: durable, firm, robust, rugged, stalwart, stout, strong, sound, tough, vigorous
k.ant: delicate, feeble, frail, weak, wimpy 脆弱的；rickety, unsound, unstable, unsteady 不稳固的
k.en: marked by or reflecting physical strength or vigor; substantially made or built

ex.en: sturdy young athletes
ex.cn: 年轻有力的运动员

ex.en: Remember to wear sturdy boots because we will be going over sharp rocks and uneven terrain.
ex.cn: 别忘了穿一双靠谱的靴子，因为我们要在锋利的岩石和崎岖的地面上行走。

m: 结实sturdy，身体像树桩——sturdy，强健的。

k.syn: black, caliginous, dark, dim, gloomy, pitch-black
k.ant: bright, brilliant, illuminated, illumined, light, lightsome, lucent, lucid, luminous 明亮的
k.en: extremely dark, gloomy, or forbidding

ex.en: the stygian blackness of the cave
ex.cn: 山洞里令人恐惧的黑暗

m: Styx冥界河，漆黑如地狱——stygian，漆黑的。

k.en: a sharp, pointed instrument used for writing, marking, or engraving

m: style刻写+us——古代用来刻字的尖锥——stylus，铁笔。

k.syn: encumber, fetter, handcuff, handicap, hinder, impede, inhibit, manacle, obstruct, shackle, trammel
k.ant: aid, assist, facilitate, help 帮助，促进
k.en: to present an obstacle to

ex.en: stymied by red tape
ex.cn: 被冗长的政策条款所阻碍

m: 前面有障碍，被stymie住了——stymie，阻碍。

k.syn: conquer, defeat, squelch, subjugate, vanquish
k.ant: capitulate, surrender 投降；lose 失败
k.der: subdued adj. 臣服的；柔和的
k.en: to conquer and bring into subjection; to bring under one's control by force of arms

ex.en: They subdued the native tribes after years of fighting.
ex.cn: 他们经过多年征战终于征服了当地的部落。

m: sub下面+due给——让你俯首称臣——subdue，征服压制。

k.syn: citizen, national
k.ant: alien 外来人；potentate 统治者
k.en: one that is placed under authority or control

ex.en: Because of the tense situation in that country, British subjects were advised to return home as soon as possible.
ex.cn: 因为该国紧张的局势，英国公民被建议尽快返回英国国内。

k.syn: affected, conditional, contingent, dependent, reliant, subordinate
k.ant: independent, unconditional 独立的，无条件的
k.en: contingent on or under the influence of some later action

ex.en: The price of seafood is subject to weather conditions.
ex.cn: 海鲜的价格受天气影响。

m: sub下面+ject臣——国王的subject臣民——subject，臣民。

k.syn: conquer, dominate, overpower, pacify, quash, squelch, subdue, vanquish
k.ant: capitulate, surrender 投降；lose 失败
k.en: to bring under control and governance as a subject

ex.en: I would rather die than be subjugated.
ex.cn: 我宁死也不愿臣服。

k.syn: enfetter, enslave, enthrall
k.ant: discharge, emancipate, enfranchise, liberate, manumit, release, unfetter 解放，释放
k.en: to make subservient

m: sub下面+jug轭+ate——套上牛轭征服——subjugate，征服镇压。

k.syn: chivalrous, elevated, gallant, greathearted, lofty, magnanimous, noble
k.ant: base, debased, degenerate, degraded, ignoble, low 可鄙的
k.en: of high spiritual, moral, or intellectual worth

ex.en: the sublime virtue of having given all one's worldly goods to the poor
ex.cn: 将所有财产都捐给穷人的崇高美德

k.syn: amazing, astonishing, astounding, awesome, fabulous, miraculous, portentous, prodigious, staggering, stunning, stupendous, wonderful
k.ant: common, ordinary 平常的，普通的
k.cn: （因为杰出、尊贵、美丽等而）令人惊叹的
k.en: tending to inspire awe usually because of elevated quality（as of beauty, nobility, or grandeur）or transcendent excellence

ex.en: the sublime beauty of the canyon
ex.cn: 峡谷令人惊叹的美丽

m: sub向上升华+lime崇高——崇高庄严——sublime，崇高的。

k.syn: concealed, hidden, subconscious
k.ant: apparent, clear, evident, manifest, obvious, patent 明显的
k.en: below the threshold of conscious perception

ex.en: subliminal advertising in movies
ex.cn: 电影中的潜意识广告

m: sub意识门槛以下+limen门槛+al——潜意识——subliminal，下意识的。

k.syn: dip, douse, drench, drown, immerse, inundate, sop, submerse
k.ant: emerge 显现
k.en: to put under water

ex.en: The river bursted its banks, submerging an entire village.
ex.cn: 河水冲上了堤岸，淹没了整个村庄。

m: sub下面+merge合并——没入水中——submerge，使淹没。

k.syn: amenable, biddable, compliant, conformable, docile, obeisant, obsequious, servile, subservient, tractable
k.ant: contumacious, defiant, intractable, obstreperous, rebellious, recalcitrant, refractory, unruly 不顺从的
k.en: submitting to others

ex.en: submissive employees
ex.cn: 顺服的员工

ex.en: It's not in her nature to be submissive.
ex.cn: 服从不是她的本性。

m: sub俯首+miss使出去——俯首称臣——submissive，顺从的。

k.syn: inferior, junior, lower, minor, secondary, subject, tributary
k.ant: greater, higher, major, primary, prime, senior, superior 更优等的，更高等的
k.en: belonging to a lower or inferior class or rank

ex.en: His contention is that environment plays a subordinate role to heredity in determining what we become.
ex.cn: 他的观点就是：在决定我们成长的因素 ——环境和遗传之中，前者处于更次要的地位。

k.syn: conquer, dominate, overpower, pacify, subdue, subject, subjugate, vanquish
k.ant: capitulate, surrender 投降；lose 失败
k.en: to bring under one's control by force of arms

ex.en: It is one of the lessons of history that more powerful civilizations often subordinate weaker ones.
ex.cn: 强大的文明往往征服弱势的文明，这是历史的教训之一。

m: sub下级+ordin+ate——下级次要的——subordinate，下级次要的。

k.en: a writ commanding a person

m: sub传唤+poena刑罚——法院传票，不来有刑罚——subpoena，传票。

k.syn: amenable, biddable, compliant, conformable, docile, obeisant, obsequious, servile, submissive, tractable
k.ant: contumacious, defiant, intractable, obstreperous, rebellious, recalcitrant, refractory, unruly 不顺从的
k.en: obsequiously submissive

m: sub下面拼命+serv服侍+ient——讨好上司，卑躬屈膝——subservient，奉承屈从的。

k.syn: abate, decline, decrease, diminish, drop, dwindle, ebb, fall, lessen, moderate, recede, wane
k.ant: balloon, burgeon, escalate, expand, grow, increase, intensify, rise, snowball, soar, wax 上升，增大
k.en: to tend downward

ex.en: Local officials say the flood waters have subsided.
ex.cn: 当地官员表示洪水已经退去。

ex.en: As the noise of the siren subsided, I was able to fall back to sleep.
ex.cn: 随着警笛声的减弱，我可以再次睡着了。

ex.en: The pain will subside in a couple of hours.
ex.cn: 几个小时之内疼痛感就会退去。

m: sub下沉+side——水退了下沉消退——subside，下陷消退。

k.syn: minor, secondary, subordinate, tributary
k.ant: capital, cardinal, central, chief, key, leading, main, paramount, predominant, primary, principal, supreme 主要的，重要的
k.en: of secondary importance

ex.en: The river has several subsidiary streams.
ex.cn: 这条河拥有数条支流。

k.syn: accessory, auxiliary, contributory, supplemental, supporting, upholding
k.en: furnishing aid or support

ex.en: Attached are subsidiary materials.
ex.cn: 附件中是辅助资料。

m: sub附属+sid+iary——次要附属的——subsidiary，次要附属的。

k.syn: allotment, allowance, appropriation, grant, subvention
k.en: monetary assistance granted by a government to a person or group in support of an enterprise regarded as being in the public interest

ex.en: a subsidy to manufacturers during the war
ex.cn: 战争期间对制造商的补贴金

m: sub下面支付+sidy——政府从下面给的补助——subsidy，补助金。

k.syn: corporeal, concrete, gross, material, objective, physical, tangible
k.ant: tenuous, vaporous 空洞的，空幻的；diaphanous, ethereal, immaterial 非物质的
k.en: of, relating to, or having substance

ex.en: a world of dreams, even less substantial than a rainbow
ex.cn: 一个梦想中的世界，甚至比彩虹还虚无缥缈

k.syn: consequential, considerable, earthshaking, important, meaningful, momentous, significant, weighty
k.ant: inconsequential, inconsiderable, insignificant, little, minor, negligible, slight, trifling, trivial 微不足道的
k.en: considerable in importance, value, degree, amount, or extent

ex.en: made a substantial progress
ex.cn: 取得了实质性的进展

m: substance物质+ial——有实质内容的——substantial，实质的。

k.syn: attest, authenticate, confirm, corroborate, justify, validate, verify
k.ant: controvert, disprove, rebut, refute 反驳
k.en: to support with proof or evidence

ex.en: There is little scientific evidence to substantiate the claims.
ex.cn: 现在还缺乏足够的科学证据来证实这种言论。

k.syn: embody, epitomize, externalize, incarnate, incorporate, manifest, materialize, personalize
k.ant: disembody 使脱离形体
k.en: to give material form to

ex.en: The artist's intense feelings are substantiated by his paintings' bold colors and broad brush strokes.
ex.cn: 画家强烈的情感通过他画作中鲜艳的色彩和很宽的笔画体现出来。

m: substance实质+iate——用实证来证实——substantiate，证实。

k.syn: artifice, cheat, chicanery, deception, fraud, trickery, wile
k.en: deception by artifice or stratagem in order to conceal, escape, or evade

ex.en: The spy obtained the documents by subterfuge.
ex.cn: 间谍通过狡猾的计谋获得了文件。

m: sub下面+ter+fuge逃——躲在下面逃脱的诡计——subterfuge，托辞狡计。

k.en: something subtle, especially a nicety of thought or a fine

m: subtle微妙+ty——细微的差别——subtlety，微妙。

k.syn: overthrow, overturn, topple
k.ant: reinforce 加强
k.en: to overturn or overthrow from the foundation

ex.en: an alleged plot to subvert the state
ex.cn: 一个旨在推翻政权的谣传中的阴谋

m: sub底部+vert转——把底部翻转颠覆——subvert，颠覆推翻。

k.syn: apothegmatic, brief, compact, compendious, concise, laconic, pithy, summary, telegraphic, terse
k.ant: circuitous, circumlocutory, diffuse, long, prolix, rambling, verbose, windy, wordy 冗长的
k.en: characterized by clear, precise expression in few words

ex.en: His speech is always succinct and perspicacious.
ex.cn: 他的发言总是简洁而一针见血。

m: succinct！用最少的话说清楚——succinct，简明简洁。

k.syn: aid, assist, help, relieve, support
k.en: to go to the aid of

ex.en: We see it as our duty to succor anyone in need.
ex.cn: 我们视帮助他人为自己的职责。

m: succor谐音「速救了「。海难信号发出，直升机迅速飞来援救——succor，救援，援助。

k.syn: asphyxiate, choke, smother, stifle, strangle
k.der: suffocating adj. 令人窒息的，压抑的
k.en: to deprive of oxygen

ex.en: The law requires the owner of a discarded refrigerator to remove its door so that a child won't get trapped inside and suffocate.
ex.cn: 法律要求废弃冰箱的所有者移除箱门，从而防止儿童被困其中而窒息致死。

m: suf堵住+foc嘴+ate——堵住嘴，窒息——suffocate，使窒息。

k.syn: flush, fill, imbue, infuse, interpenetrate, percolate, pervade, transfuse
k.en: to spread through or over, as with liquid, color, or light

ex.en: a room suffused with warm sunlight
ex.cn: 一个充满温暖阳光的房间

m: 颜色suf弥漫+fuse——颜色染遍各处——suffuse，弥漫充满。

k.syn: frown, grump, mope, pout
k.ant: crow, delight, exuberate, jubilate, rejoice, triumph 感到高兴，雀跃
k.der: sulky adj. 生气的，不悦的
k.en: to be sullenly aloof or withdrawn, as in silent resentment or protest

ex.en: He would sulk for hours whenever he didn't get what he wanted.
ex.cn: 每当他的愿望没被满足时，他都会生几个小时的闷气。

m: sulk谐音「生闷气「。失恋了缩在被子里，不说话，不接电话，闷声生气——sulk，生闷气，愠怒。

k.syn: bleak, cheerless, dark, depressing, dire, gloomy, glum, gray, lugubrious, morose, saturnine, sulky, surly
k.ant: bright, cheerful, cheering, cordial, festive, gay, lighthearted欢快的，高兴的
k.en: causing or marked by an atmosphere lacking in cheer

ex.en: She remained sullen amid festivities alone.
ex.cn: 她独自一人在喜庆的气氛中闷闷不乐。

ex.en: sullen skies that matched our mood on the day of the funeral
ex.cn: 葬礼当天与我们很搭调的沉闷天气

m: sullen谐音「阴沉「。他考试不及格，回家后一言不发，脸色阴沉得像乌云——sullen，闷闷不乐的，阴沉的。

k.syn: abstract, brief, epitome, outline, recapitulation, synopsis, summarization
k.ant: elaboration 详细解释
k.en: an abstract, abridgment, or compendium especially of a preceding discourse

ex.en: Many book reports choose to begin with a summary of the book.
ex.cn: 许多书评以该书的摘要开始。

k.ant: prolonged, protracted 拖延的
k.en: done or executed on the spot and without formality

ex.en: a summary trial and speedy execution
ex.cn: 就地判决和快速处决

m: summary摘要——总结最要紧的部分——summary，摘要。

k.syn: acme, apex, climax, crescendo, crest, crown, culmination, meridian, peak, pinnacle, top, zenith
k.ant: bottom, nadir 最低点
k.en: the highest point

ex.en: the summit of his ambition
ex.cn: 他雄心壮志的顶点

m: 爬到山顶summit！最高点——summit，顶点。

k.syn: assemble, convene, convoke, muster, rally
k.ant: dismiss 解散
k.en: to call together

ex.en: The general summoned all his troops before the operation.
ex.cn: 行动之前，将军把所有的部队召集到了一起。

k.syn: call, hail
k.en: to command by service of a summons, especially to appear in court

ex.en: He was summoned to answer charges.
ex.cn: 他被传唤出庭应对起诉。

m: sum召集+mon命令——发出命令把人召来——summon，传唤召集。

k.syn: deluxe, lavish, lush, luxurious, opulent, palatial, resplendent, splendid, superb
k.ant: ascetic, austere, humble, spartan简朴的 And gladly would learn, and gladly teach. 勤于学习的人才能乐于施教。 ——英国诗人　乔叟（Chaucer, British poet） “面对辞藻堆砌的高峰，我们不应因身处词汇匮乏的谷底而绝望，征服都是从脚下开始的。” （姚佳雄，Verbal 700，Quantitative 800，AW 4.5，录取院校：Johns Hopkins University）
k.en: extremely costly, rich, luxurious, or magnificent

ex.en: The hotel claims to offer sumptuous furnishings and exquisitely prepared cuisine.
ex.cn: 酒店宣称有奢华的装潢和精心准备的食物。

m: sum奢华+pt+uous——极尽奢侈豪华——sumptuous，豪华的。

k.syn: disassociate, disconnect, disjoin, disunite, divide, part, rend, rive, separate, sever, unyoke
k.ant: bond, connect, join, link, unify, unite, yoke 连接，结合
k.der: asunder adv. 分开地，分裂地
k.en: to break apart or in two

ex.en: a city sundered by racial conflict
ex.cn: 一个因种族冲突而分裂的城市

m: sunder谐音「散了「，记住asunder（分开），sunder是动词。斧头一劈，木头从中sunder一分为二——sunder，分裂，分离。

k.syn: arrogant, assumptive, bumptious, haughty, imperious, pompous, presumptuous, overbearing, superior
k.ant: humble, moderate, modest 谦逊的
k.en: feeling or showing haughty disdain

ex.en: The aristocrat reacted to their breach of etiquette with a supercilious smile.
ex.cn: 贵族对他们破坏礼节的行为报以傲慢的一笑。

m: super超级+cilium睫毛——眼睑往上抬，傲慢地看人——supercilious，高傲傲慢。

k.syn: cursory, facile, perfunctory, shallow, sketchy, skin-deep
k.ant: profound 深刻的；comprehensive, exhaustive 全面的
k.en: lacking in depth, solidity, and comprehensiveness

ex.en: The official's superficial report on the situation provoked wide blame on the Internet.
ex.cn: 官员对于情况的敷衍陈述激起了网络上的广泛谴责。

m: super浮于+fic表面——只看表面，不深入——superficial，表面的。

k.syn: excessive, extra, redundant, surplus
k.ant: deficient, inadequate, insufficient, scanty, scarce, short, skimpy, sparse 不足的
k.en: exceeding what is sufficient or necessary

ex.en: a complicated algorithm that could filter all superfluous information
ex.cn: 一个能滤除所有多余信息的复杂算法

m: super超过+flu流溢——水溢出来多余了——superfluous，多余的。

k.cn: 添（声）加（色）（使形象、某因素或性质更加清楚）
k.en: to add as a distinct feature, element, or quality

ex.en: She superimposed her own interpretation when she retold the story.
ex.cn: 她重复故事的时候，加入了自己的解释。

m: super上面+impose强加——叠加在上面——superimpose，重叠置于之上。

k.syn: displace, displant, substitute, supersede, cut out
k.en: to usurp the place of, especially through intrigue or underhanded tactics

ex.en: Old traditions were fading away and being supplanted by modern ways.
ex.cn: 老传统逐渐式微，被现代方式所取代。

m: super取代+plant种——把原来的人踢走取代——supplant，排挤篡夺。

k.syn: bendy, flexible, limber, lissome, lithe, pliable, pliant, lithesome
k.ant: inflexible, stiff, rigid 坚硬的
k.en: readily bent; pliant

ex.en: supple limbs
ex.cn: 柔软的四肢

ex.en: a supple mind
ex.cn: 灵活的头脑

m: supple谐音「软了「。瑜伽老师把腿搭上脑袋，身体supple得像橡皮——supple，柔软的，易弯曲的。

k.syn: addendum, addition, expansion, increment, proliferation, step-up
k.en: something that serves to complete or make up for a deficiency in something else

ex.en: recommends taking a vitamin C supplement to prevent colds
ex.cn: 建议补充维生素C来预防感冒

m: supplement补充——额外补充——supplement，增补补充。

k.syn: beseech, conjure, entreat, impetrate, implore, solicit, plead to, appeal to
k.ant: demand 强求
k.en: to make a request to（someone）in an earnest or urgent manner

ex.en: God is not just someone to be supplicated in times of trouble.
ex.cn: 我们不应该只在困难时候才向主祷告。

m: supplicate谐音「跪求你「。囚犯双膝跪地，双手合十，向国王supplicate求赦免——supplicate，恳求，乞求。

k.syn: hypothesis, proposition, surmise, premise, presumption
k.ant: certainty 确定
k.en: an opinion or judgment based on little or no evidence

ex.en: It's pure supposition on your part that there's something illegal going on next door.
ex.cn: 你说隔壁在进行非法勾当纯属猜测。

m: suppose猜测+ition——猜测出来的东西——supposition，猜想推测。

k.syn: repress, smother, stifle, choke back, hold back
k.ant: stimulate 激励
k.en: to inhibit the expression of（an impulse, for example）; check

ex.en: suppress a smile
ex.cn: 忍住笑容

k.syn: clamp down on, crack down on, put down, quash, repress, silence, slap down, snuff out, squash, squelch, subdue
k.en: to put a stop to（something）by the use of force

ex.en: Nothing could suppress the rising tide of protest.
ex.cn: 任何事物都无法控制越来越激烈的游行示威活动。

m: suppress = sup（喝）+ press（压）。把情绪死死压下去，连一声叹息都不敢出——suppress，抑制，禁止。

k.syn: gouge, soak, sting
k.en: to charge（someone）too much for goods or services

ex.en: The airline has added a $30 fuel surcharge on all international flights.
ex.cn: 该航空公司对所有国际航线上涨了30美元的燃油税。

m: sur超过+charge收费——额外收费——surcharge，超载附加费。

k.syn: overabundance, overage, overflow, plethora, redundancy, superfluity, surplus
k.ant: deficiency, deprivation, insufficient supply, famish, starve 短缺，匮乏
k.en: to feed or supply to excess

ex.en: He surfeited himself with chocolate.
ex.cn: 他巧克力吃多了。

m: 饱到surfeit(sore-fit)——吃得太多，撑到疼——surfeit，过量饮食过度。

k.syn: abnegate, cede, relinquish, renounce, resign
k.ant: appropriate 挪用，占为己有
k.en: to give（something）over to the control or possession of another usually under duress

ex.en: surrender a contractual right
ex.cn: 放弃契约上规定的一项权利

k.syn: deliver, give up, indulge, yield
k.en: to give（oneself）over to something especially unrestrainedly

ex.en: He surrendered himself to grief.
ex.cn: 他沉湎于悲伤之中。

m: surrender举手投降——交出放弃——surrender，投降放弃。

k.syn: backstairs, clandestine, covert, furtive, privy, underground, underhanded, behind-the-scenes
k.ant: barefaced, aboveboard, open, overt, public 光明正大的
k.en: undertaken or done so as to escape being observed or known by others

ex.en: He took a surreptitious glance at her knees.
ex.cn: 他偷偷瞟了一下她的膝盖。

m: surreptitious偷偷摸摸的——surr+repti+ious，悄悄地——surreptitious，偷偷摸摸。

k.syn: prorogue, recess
k.ant: invoke 实行
k.en: to bring to a formal close for a period of time

ex.en: suspend the trial
ex.cn: 暂停审判

k.ant: erect, let fall 使直立，落下
k.en: hang

ex.en: suspend a banner proclaiming the town's“Heritage Days”from the archway
ex.cn: 在拱门上方悬挂一条庆祝该镇“遗产日”的条幅

m: suspend谐音「悬那「——悬在那里。工程暂停，起重机把大梁悬在空中，动也不动——suspend，暂停，中止，悬挂。

k.ant: incision, avulse 切开，撕脱
k.en: the process of joining two surfaces or edges together along a line by or as if by sewing

ex.en: The doctor cleaned, sutured, and bandaged the wound.
ex.cn: 医生将伤口清洗、缝合、包扎。

m: 梭子(suture)穿线，一针一针缝合——suture，缝合。

k.syn: bony, lean, skinny, slender, slim
k.ant: plump, corpulent, paunchy and awkward 丰满的，大腹便便而笨拙的
k.en: slender or graceful in figure or outline; slim

ex.en: The svelte dancer seemed to float across the stage.
ex.cn: 苗条的舞者看起来就像轻盈地漂浮在舞台上。

m: svelte体态苗条——像丝绒velvet一样苗条优雅——svelte，苗条优雅。

k.syn: prance, sashay, stalk
k.cn: 大摇大摆地走，趾高气昂地走
k.en: to conduct oneself in an arrogant or superciliously pompous manner

ex.en: The young man confidently swaggered across the room.
ex.cn: 年轻人高傲地在房间里走来走去。

k.syn: brag, vapor, vaunt, gasconade
k.en: to boast, brag

ex.en: I would swagger if I'd won first place in the bowling tournament.
ex.cn: 如果我赢得保龄球锦标赛的冠军，我肯定会自夸。

m: swagger大摇大摆自吹——swagger，自夸吹嘘。

k.syn: blaspheme, curse, cuss
k.ant: accolade 赞美
k.en: to use profane or obscene language: curse

ex.en: No one is allowed to swear in this house.
ex.cn: 这个房间里禁止说脏话。

k.syn: attest, depose, covenant, pledge, vow
k.en: to promise or pledge with a solemn oath; vow

ex.en: He swore his oath of allegiance to the queen.
ex.cn: 他宣誓效忠女王。

m: swear谐音「死了啊「。气急败坏时脱口而出——swear words，脏话，骂人话——swear，咒骂，发誓。

k.syn: boiling, scorching, searing, sultry, torrid, fiery
k.ant: algid, arctic, bone-chilling, freezing, frigid, frozen, glacial, ice-cold, iced, icy 严寒的
k.en: oppressively hot

ex.en: The air conditioning was broken, and it was sweltering in the room.
ex.cn: 空调坏了，房间里酷热难耐。

m: sweltering！热到出汗——sweltering，酷热的。

k.syn: detour, deviate, diverge, sheer, swing, veer
k.ant: maintain direction, straighten 保持方向，直行
k.en: to turn aside abruptly from a straight line or course

ex.en: The car swerved sharply to avoid the squirrel in the road.
ex.cn: 为了避开路上的松鼠，汽车猛地转向。

m: swerve突然转弯——突然改变方向——swerve，突然转向。

k.syn: gulp, quaff, swig
k.ant: sip 啜饮
k.en: to drink greedily, to eat greedily or to excess

ex.en: Considering the way she swills carbonated drinks, she ought to own stock in Pepsi company.
ex.cn: 鉴于她对碳酸饮料消费的贡献，百事公司应该给她分点股份。

m: swill痛饮——大口喝下去——swill，痛饮。

k.syn: bilk, con, hustle, fiddle, gyp, bunco, flimflam
k.en: to cheat or defraud of money or property

ex.en: Hundreds of people were swindled out of their savings.
ex.cn: 几百人都被骗去了存款。

m: swindle欺骗——骗取钱财——swindle，欺骗骗取。

k.en: A seat suspended by ropes or chains on which one can swing back and forth.

m: swing既是秋千也是摇摆。想象边sing边swing——唱着歌荡秋千——swing，摆动，秋千。

k.syn: debauchee, decadent, hedonist, sensualist
k.ant: spartan, ascetic 禁欲者
k.cn: 沉溺于奢侈逸乐者，酒色之徒
k.en: a person devoted to pleasure and luxury; a voluptuary

ex.en: The prince was remembered as a self-indulgent sybarite.
ex.cn: 王子酒色之徒的名声远扬。

m: sybarite沉于逸乐——Sybaris是古希腊奢靡之城——sybarite，沉溺奢靡者。

k.syn: fawner, flunky, lickspittle, toady, apple-polisher, bootlicker, brownnoser
k.en: a servile self-seeking flatterer

ex.en: When her career was riding high, the self-deluded actress often mistook sycophants for true friends.
ex.cn: 当这位演员的事业如日中天之时，她常常把一些马屁精误以为真朋友。

m: sycophant马屁精——syco+phant，吹捧者——sycophant，马屁精。

k.cn: 提纲，摘要；课文、演讲或研究课题的概要或提纲
k.en: an outline or a summary of the main points of a text, lecture, or course of study

ex.en: Have you got next year's syllabus?
ex.cn: 你拿到明年的教学大纲了吗？

m: syllabus课程大纲——syl+labus，大纲列表——syllabus，课程大纲。

k.der: syllogize v. 用三段论论证
k.cn: 由一般到个别的推理，演绎
k.en: reasoning from the general to the specific; deduction

ex.en: The syllogism was at the core of traditional deductive reasoning, where facts are determined by combining existing statements, in contrast to inductive reasoning where facts are determined by repeated observations.
ex.cn: 从一般到个别的推理是传统演绎推理的核心，事实结论由综合现存知识所确定，而归纳推理中的事实结论则由重复观测来确定。

m: syllogism三段论——syl合起来+log逻辑——syllogism，演绎推理。

k.ant: unrelated growth 无关联的生长
k.en: the living together in more or less intimate association or close union of two dissimilar

ex.en: The bird lives in symbiosis with the hippopotamus.
ex.cn: 这种鸟和河马是共生关系。

m: sym共生+biosis生活——共同生活——symbiosis，共生关系。

k.syn: balance, coherence, consonance, proportion
k.ant: disproportion, asymmetry, discordance, disproportion, disunity, imbalance, incoherence, violence 不均衡，混乱
k.en: balanced proportions

ex.en: The building has perfect symmetry.
ex.cn: 这栋房子是完美的对称构造。

m: sym对称+metry度量——对称的度量——symmetry，对称。

k.en: An elaborate musical composition for full orchestra, typically in four movements.

m: sym协同+phony声音——协同声音，交响乐——symphony，交响乐。

k.syn: coetaneous, coeval, coexistent, concurrent, contemporaneous, simultaneous
k.ant: occurring at different times, noncontemporaneous, out-of-phase 发生在不同时代的
k.en: having identical period and phase

ex.en: The synchronous arrival of a baby sister and loss of a beloved grandmother strongly affected the girl.
ex.cn: 妹妹的出生和奶奶的去世同时发生，给这个女孩造成了巨大的影响。

m: syn同步+chron时间——时间同步——synchronous，同时期的。

k.ant: antagonistic 敌对的
k.en: working together: cooperating

ex.en: synergic muscles
ex.cn: 协同肌肉

m: syn合作+ergic做功——协力做功——synergic，合作的。

k.en: having the same or a similar meaning

ex.en: Paris has always been synonymous with elegance, luxury and style.
ex.cn: 巴黎在人们心中就是优雅、奢侈和时尚的同义词。

m: syn同义+onymous名字——名字相同，同义的——synonymous，同义的。

k.syn: abstract, brief, digest, recapitulation, roundup, summarization, epitome
k.en: a brief outline or general view; an abstract or a summary

ex.en: Just give me a synopsis of the movie.
ex.cn: 把电影的大致情节给我说说就行。

m: syn概括+opsis看——概括地看，摘要——synopsis，摘要概要。

k.syn: admixture, alloy, amalgamation, compound, conflation, fusion, meld
k.ant: analysis, taking apart 分解
k.en: the combination of parts or elements so as to form a whole

ex.en: a philosophy that is a kind of synthesis of several schools of Western and Eastern thought
ex.cn: 哲学是东西方很多思想流派的综合

m: syn合成+thesis命题——合成命题——synthesis，合成。

k.syn: implied, unexpressed, unspoken, unvoiced, wordless
k.ant: explicit, express, expressed, spoken, stated, voiced 明确表达的，直率的
k.en: implied or indicated（as by an act or by silence）but not actually expressed

ex.en: tacit consent
ex.cn: 默许

m: tacit暗示——沉默中达成的默契——tacit，暗示的默许的。

k.syn: laconic, reserved, reticent, tight-lipped, uncommunicative
k.ant: garrulous, loquacious, glib, expansive, prolix, voluble 多话的
k.en: temperamentally disinclined to talk

ex.en: A taciturn man never initiates a conversation.
ex.cn: 一个寡言的人从不主动和人说话。

m: taciturn沉默寡言——tacit沉默+urn——嘴巴像封了瓮——taciturn，沉默寡言。

k.syn: dive into, wade in
k.en: to start work on energetically

ex.en: Once I clean the kitchen, I think I'll tackle the bathroom.
ex.cn: 等我把厨房打扫干净了，就去收拾浴室。

m: tackle谐音「踏克了「——解决难题就像踏平障碍。足球运动员铲断对方，渔网滑轮也叫tackle——tackle，处理，传动装置，滑轮组。

k.syn: diplomacy
k.ant: clumsiness, insensitivity, tactlessness 笨拙
k.en: a keen sense of what to do or say in order to maintain good relations with others or avoid offense

ex.en: She talked to her neighbor with supreme tact.
ex.cn: 她和邻居说话十分圆滑。

m: tact机敏——把握时机的touch触感——tact，机敏圆滑。

k.en: perceptible by touch: tangible

ex.en: The thick brushstrokes give the painting a tactile quality.
ex.cn: 粘稠的笔触给了这幅画一种厚实的感觉。

m: tactile触觉——tact触+ile可以——可以触摸的——tactile，有触觉的。

k.syn: graceless, ill-advised, imprudent, indelicate, injudicious, undiplomatic
k.ant: advisable, discreet, judicious, prudent, tactful, wise 小心谨慎的，明智的
k.en: bluntly inconsiderate or indiscreet

ex.en: tactless comments
ex.cn: 不明智的评论

m: tactless不机智——没有tact机敏——tactless，不机智笨拙。

k.en: frog = caterpillar: butterfly 蝌蚪变态成青蛙=毛虫变态成蝴蝶（变态关系）

m: tadpole蝌蚪——tad小+pole头——小小圆头的蝌蚪——tadpole，蝌蚪。

k.syn: blemish, darken, mar, spoil, tarnish, vitiate
k.ant: pristine, unspoiled, wholesome, unadulterated 纯洁的，健全的
k.en: to affect slightly with something morally bad or undesirable

ex.en: A tendency toward conceitedness taints that athlete's status as a role model.
ex.cn: 该运动员自负的倾向玷污了其行为模范的地位。

m: taint污染——tain染+t——染上了污点——taint，污染染上。

k.syn: amulet, fetish, mascot, mojo, periapt, phylactery
k.en: something worn or kept to bring good luck or keep away evil

ex.en: A pendant of white nephrite jade is often worn by Indians as a talisman to ward off heart disease.
ex.cn: 印度人通常戴一串软玉挂件作为护身符，防止心脏病发作。

m: talisman护身符——来自阿拉伯语，神秘护身符——talisman，护身符。

k.en: the claw of a bird

m: talon猛禽的爪——talion同态复仇，爪子报仇——talon，猛禽利爪。

k.en: A small, shallow drum with metal jingles around its frame, played by shaking or striking.

m: 吉普赛女郎甩着铃鼓跳舞，「探步嗯(tambourine)！」——tambourine，铃鼓。

k.syn: diddle with, fiddle with, fool with, mess with
k.en: to handle thoughtlessly, ignorantly, or mischievously

ex.en: tamper with tradition
ex.cn: 坏了规矩

k.en: to tinker with rashly or foolishly

ex.en: Don't tamper with my feelings.
ex.cn: 不要玩弄我的感情。

m: tamper窜改——tam干扰+per——胡乱调整损害——tamper，窜改损害。

k.syn: excursive, digressive
k.ant: essential 重要的
k.en: diverging from an original purpose of course: irrelevant

ex.en: tangent remarks
ex.cn: 不相关的评论

m: tangent切线——切出去，离题了——tangent，离题的切线。

k.syn: palpable, touchable
k.ant: impalpable, intangible, unable to perceive 无法感知的
k.en: capable of being perceived
possible to be treated as fact; real or concrete

ex.en: tangible evidence
ex.cn: 确凿的证据

m: tangible可触及——tange触+ible可以——可以触摸的确凿的——tangible，确凿的。

k.syn: pungent, strong
k.ant: bland, mild, smooth不刺激的
k.en: having a powerfully stimulating odor or flavor

ex.en: a tangy sauce with a strong aftertaste
ex.cn: 味道刺激的酱料，回味无穷

m: tangy刺激味道——tang刺激+y——辛辣刺激的味道——tangy，刺激性的。

k.ant: comfort, console, solace, alleviate, assuage, relieve, satiate 安慰，缓和
k.en: to excite（another）by exposing something desirable while keeping it out of reach

ex.en: He was tantalized by the possibility of earning a lot of money quickly.
ex.cn: 有人用迅速致富引诱他。

m: tantalize挑逗——Tantalus被罚看到食物却吃不到——tantalize，激起欲望挑逗。

k.ant: incommensurate 不相称的
k.en: equivalent in value, significance, or effect

ex.en: a relationship tantamount to marriage
ex.cn: 到了谈婚论嫁地步的关系

m: tantamount等价——tant那么多+amount量——量相等，等价——tantamount，等价的。

k.syn: blowup, explosion, fit
k.ant: pacification 平静
k.en: a fit of bad temper

ex.en: have a tantrum
ex.cn: 大发脾气

m: tantrum谐音「探楼「——探出楼去大发脾气。超市里小孩躺地嚎哭，手脚乱踢——tantrum，勃然大怒，发脾气。

k.syn: abate, decline, diminish, dwindle, shrink, wane, drain away
k.en: to become gradually narrower or thinner toward one end

ex.en: taper to a point
ex.cn: 逐渐变细成为一点

k.syn: subside, die down, phase down, fall away, let up
k.en: to diminish or lessen gradually

ex.en: The storm finally tapered off.
ex.cn: 风暴逐渐平静下来。

m: taper——像蜡烛一样，越烧越细——taper，逐渐变细。

k.syn: crawling, creeping, dallying, dawdling, dilatory, dragging, lagging, languid, sluggish
k.ant: bolting, brisk, fleet, flying, meteoric, rocketing, scooting, scudding, scurrying, swift, whirling 迅速的
k.en: moving slowly: sluggish

ex.en: He was tardy to work.
ex.cn: 他做事拖拖拉拉。

k.syn: behind, belated, delinquent, overdue
k.ant: early, inopportune, precocious, premature, unseasonable, untimely 提早的
k.en: delayed beyond the expected or proper time

ex.en: a tardy arrival
ex.cn: 迟到

m: retarded(迟滞/迟缓)去掉re-和-ed，中间的核心正是tardy——tardy，迟到/迟缓。

k.syn: blemish, mar, stain, vitiate
k.en: to affect slightly with something morally bad or undesirable

ex.en: An arrest for shoplifting tarnished her reputation.
ex.cn: 因在商店行窃被捕玷污了她的名誉。

m: tarnish谐音「它脏了「。银制奖杯放了几年，表面发黑，光泽全无——tarnish，失去光泽，使暗淡。

k.en: material, such as waterproofed canvas, used to cover

m: tarpaul(焦油)+in——用焦油处理过的防水油布——tarpaulin，防水油布。

k.syn: agreeable, delectable, delightful, satisfying, savory
k.ant: uninteresting, disagreeable, unpalatable, unpleasant, unwelcome 无趣的，令人失望的
k.en: giving pleasure or contentment to the mind or senses

ex.en: the tasty prospect of getting his revenge
ex.cn: 他报仇成功的诱人前景

m: tasty谐音「太好了「。不只是食物好吃，还可以说设计非常tasty，十分吸引人——tasty，吸引人的，令人愉悦的。

k.syn: dilapidated, scruffy, seedy, sleazy, tatterdemalion, threadbare
k.ant: smart 整洁漂亮的
k.en: somewhat worn, shabby, or dilapidated

ex.en: a tatty shirt
ex.cn: 破旧的衬衣

m: tatty谐音「烂的「——tat本指破布。旧衣市场里挂满褪色破旧的衣物——tatty，破旧的，褴褛的。

k.syn: bait, hassle, haze, heckle, needle, ride
k.en: to reproach or challenge in a mocking or insulting manner: jeer at

ex.en: The boys continually taunted each other.
ex.cn: 男孩子们不断互相挑衅。

m: taunt谐音「逗你「。对手故意在脸书上嘲讽，专门戳痛点——taunt，嘲弄，挑衅。

k.ant: lax, loose, slack 松弛的
k.en: not loose or flabby

ex.en: taut muscles
ex.cn: 紧实的肌肉

k.syn: kempt, orderly, shipshape, trim
k.en: kept in proper order or condition, kept in trim shape; neat and tidy

ex.en: a taunt ship
ex.cn: 保养得不错的船

m: taut谐音「绷「。吉他手拨弦前把弦调到taut绷紧状态——taut，紧绷的，不松弛的。

k.syn: meretricious, gaudy, flashy, garish
k.ant: exquisite 高雅的
k.en: cheap and gaudy in appearance or quality; ignoble

ex.en: a tawdry attempt to smear his opponent
ex.cn: 企图污蔑对手的下三滥的手段

m: tawdry谐音「土不啦叽「。地摊货镶满廉价亮片，俗得刺眼——tawdry，俗丽的，廉价而花哨的。

k.syn: arduous, burdensome, exacting, grueling, laborious, onerous, toilsome
k.ant: light, easy 轻快的
k.en: requiring much time, effort, or careful attention

ex.en: The journey proved to be very taxing.
ex.cn: 旅途很艰辛。

m: 征税(taxing)就是负担——繁重费力——taxing，繁重费力的。

k.en: the study of the general principles of scientific classification

m: taxi(分类)——给所有生物分类打标签——taxonomy，分类学。

k.en: to move or act with violence, haste

m: tear除了泪水，还有「猛冲「义。运动员像风一样tear down the track，撕裂空气冲过终点——tear，猛冲，高速冲刺。

k.syn: drab, dreary, jading, monotonous, stale, stodgy, stuffy, weary, drudging
k.ant: entertaining, absorbing, stimulating, engaging, engrossing, gripping, interesting, intriguing, involving, riveting 令人愉快的，吸引人的
k.en: tiresome because of length or dullness: boring

ex.en: a tedious public ceremony
ex.cn: 冗长无聊的公开仪式

m: tedious谐音「死了去死「。老教授照着三十年前的讲义念，同学们昏昏欲睡——tedious，冗长乏味的。

k.syn: careen, dodder, lurch, reel
k.ant: stabilize 使…稳固
k.en: to move unsteadily: wobble

ex.en: She teetered down the street in her high heels.
ex.cn: 她穿着高跟鞋，摇摇晃晃地沿街走着。

k.syn: balance, dither, falter, hang back, scruple, shilly-shally, stagger, vacillate, waver, wobble
k.ant: dive in, plunge in 积极做
k.en: to show uncertainty about the right course of action

ex.en: He was teetering on the brink of making a decision about college.
ex.cn: 他正在为选择哪所大学而犹豫不决。

m: 跌跌(teeter)撞撞！踩高跷走钢丝——teeter，摇摇欲坠。

k.ant: intemperance 饮酒过度
k.en: the principle or practice of complete abstinence from alcoholic drinks

m: teetotalism = tea + total + ism。喝茶取代喝酒，total完全不碰酒精——teetotalism，禁酒主义。

k.syn: compelling, conclusive, convincing, forceful, persuasive, satisfying
k.ant: inconclusive, indecisive, ineffective, uncompelling, unconvincing, unpersuasive 无效的
k.en: effective, expressive

ex.en: the most telling evidence
ex.cn: 最有力的证据

m: telling谐音「它灵「。这招儿它灵！一个telling argument把对方辩驳得哑口无言——telling，有效的，显著的。

k.syn: audacity, brashness, presumption, presumptuousness
k.ant: circumspection, cautious approach, pusillanimity 谨慎
k.en: foolhardy disregard of danger; recklessness

ex.en: She was punished for her temerity.
ex.cn: 她因冒失吃到了苦头。

m: temerity谐音「踢你「。明知对方是虎，还伸手去摸，这种temerity简直要命——temerity，鲁莽，冒失。

k.ant: immoderate, extreme, frivolous 极端的
k.en: avoiding extremes in behavior or expression

ex.en: He is rather temperate in his appraisal of the movie, calling it good but not great.
ex.cn: 他对这部电影的赞赏很适度，说其“还好”而不是“很棒”。

k.syn: abstentious, abstinent, continent, self-abnegating, self-denying, sober
k.ant: self-indulgent 放纵的
k.en: given to or marked by restraint in the satisfaction of one's appetites

ex.en: a soft-spoken, serious-minded person of temperate habits
ex.cn: 一个说话温和、认真严肃的人，其兴趣爱好都很适度

m: temperate = temper（节制）+ ate。不冷不热，饮食有度，七分饱——temperate，适度的，有节制的，温和的。

k.syn: cataclysmal, tumultuous, turbulent
k.ant: serene 平静的
k.en: marked by sudden or violent disturbance

ex.en: In terms of social change, the 1960s are generally considered the most tempestuous decade in recent American history.
ex.cn: 就社会变革来说，20世纪60 年代是近现代美国历史上最动荡的十年。

m: tempestuous = tempest（风暴）+ uous。像暴风雨一样汹涌，感情tempestuous激烈动荡——tempestuous，暴烈的，狂暴的。

k.cn: 行动躲躲闪闪以争取时间、躲避争论等
k.en: to act evasively in order to gain time, avoid argument, or postpone a decision

ex.en: “Colonial officials ... ordered to enforce unpopular enactments, tended to temporize, to find excuses for evasion”
ex.cn: （J. H. Parry）“被命令来执行不受欢迎法案的殖民地官员们倾向于敷衍了事，寻找逃避的理由”（J.H. 帕里）

m: temporize谐音「拖拖拉拉「——用时间（tempo）拖延。谈判桌上一方故意拖延，反复妥协，不作决定——temporize，妥协，拖延周旋。

k.syn: defendable, justifiable, maintainable, supportable, sustainable
k.ant: unjustified, unsound, specious, indefensible, fallacious 不合理的
k.cn: 有据可依的，无懈可击的
k.en: capable of being held or defended; reasonable

ex.en: a tenable theory
ex.cn: 一个站得住脚的理论

ex.en: a tenable outpost
ex.cn: 一个守得住的前哨

m: tenable → 谐音「挺得住」。论文答辩时导师猛攻，你的论点站得住脚，能顶住质疑——tenable，有据可依、无懈可击的。想象一棵大树被风吹也不倒，根扎得稳稳的。

k.syn: dogged, insistent, persevering, pertinacious
k.ant: negotiable, vacillated 可商量的，踌躇的
k.en: persistent in maintaining something valued or habitual

ex.en: a tenacious advocate of civil rights
ex.cn: 公民权利的坚定捍卫者

k.syn: adherent, adhesive, clingy, glutinous, tacky, viscid
k.ant: nonadhesive 不具有粘性的
k.en: tending to adhere to objects upon contact

ex.en: You'll have enough time getting those tenacious gum off of your wool sweater.
ex.cn: 你有足够的时候去把羊毛毛衣上面那块粘着的泡泡糖弄下来。

m: 谐音「太能死撑」——运动员双腿已经快断了，还是死撑着不放弃，像用十条绳子死死绑住不松口——tenacious，顽强不屈的、坚持不懈的。

k.syn: partial, distorting, subjective, biased
k.ant: unbiased, unprejudiced 公平的
k.en: marked by a tendency in favor of a particular point of view: biased

ex.en: He made some extremely tendentious remarks.
ex.cn: 他的一些评论颇具偏向性。

m: tendentious谐音「踢哦丹迪俗」——记者写报道，总往一个方向倾斜，偏袒己方，像天平被人偷偷按住一边——tendentious，带倾向性的、有偏见的。

k.syn: extend, proffer, trot out
k.ant: withdraw 撤回
k.en: to offer formally

ex.en: tender a letter of resignation
ex.cn: 正式提交辞职信

k.syn: beneficent, benevolent, benignant, compassionate, softhearted
k.ant: atrocious, barbarous, bestial, brutal, callous, cruel, fiendish, inhuman, insensate, sadistic, savage, truculent, uncompassionate, vicious 尖酸刻薄的
k.cn: 考虑周到的，关心同情的
k.en: having or marked by sympathy and consideration for others

ex.en: an especially tender teacher who loves having kids with special educational needs in her class
ex.cn: 特别无微不至的老师，爱着那些需要特别教育的孩子们

m: tender → 谐音「递上来」。外交官在谈判桌上正式将文件递到对方手中，这个动作就是tender——tender作动词，正式提出、递交。比如「tender one's resignation」递交辞呈。

k.ant: substantial 实质的
k.cn: 没有实际内容的，空洞的
k.en: having little substance; flimsy

ex.en: a tenuous argument
ex.cn: 站不住脚的论点

m: 谐音「太虚了」——你的理由tenuous，像薄薄一层纸，稍微一戳就透，毫无实质内容——tenuous，脆弱的、没有实际内容的。

k.syn: lukewarm, halfhearted, uneager, unenthusiastic
k.ant: ardent, ebullient, feverish, keen, passionate, wholehearted 热心的
k.en: showing little or no interest or enthusiasm

ex.en: a tepid response
ex.cn: 冷漠的回应

ex.en: the tepid conservatism of the fifties
ex.cn: 五十年代那种温和的保守主义

m: tepid → 谐音「太婆的」。太婆泡的茶，端来已经不烫不凉，太婆对新科技的热情也就这样——tepid，温吞的、不热心的。想象摸一杯温水，不冷不热，tepid。

k.syn: argot, cant, dialect, jargon, jive, lingo, patois, patter, slang
k.en: the special terms or expressions of a particular group or field

ex.en: the terminology favored by sportscasters
ex.cn: 体育节目转播员喜欢用的那些术语

ex.en: medical terminology that can be hard for the patient to understand
ex.cn: 那些病人们难以理解的医学术语

m: term（术语）大家都认识，terminology就是「关于术语的那门学问」。医学术语、法律术语各成体系，让外行听得云里雾里——terminology，专业术语（的总称）。

k.ant: outset, sustain 开始，持续
k.en: the final point；the end

ex.en: Stockholm is the terminus for the southbound train.
ex.cn: 斯德哥尔摩是向南去的列车的终点站。

m: terminus → 谐音「终点，咱们到了」。公交车司机广播：「terminus！全线终点站，请乘客下车」——terminus，终点、终点站。拉丁语直接借来，想象公路尽头一块大石碑刻着「TERMINUS」。

k.syn: anxiety, dread, fearfulness, fright, horror, panic, trepidation
k.en: a state of intense fear

ex.en: Many civilians fled in terror.
ex.cn: 很多黎民百姓极度恐慌，逃难去了。

m: terror → 谐音「太热了！」——不对，这个大家都认识。真正的画面：terror=极度恐惧，比fear升级了好几档。想象恐怖袭击现场，人群四散奔逃，那种骨髓里的恐惧就是terror。比apprehension（担忧）重得多。

k.syn: compact, compendious, laconic, pithy, succinct
k.ant: circuitous, circumlocutory, diffuse, long-winded, prolix, rambling, verbose, windy, wordy 冗长啰嗦的
k.en: brief and to the point; effectively concise

ex.en: He dismissed me with a terse“no”.
ex.cn: 他用一个简单的“不”把我打发了。

m: terse → 谐音「太省事了」。老将军回复下属长篇汇报只写两个字：「知道。」——这就是terse，言简意赅到极致。记住：terse≈brief但带着冷淡、甚至粗鲁的意味。

k.en: a person who has made a will or given a legacy

m: 谐音「踢死踹他」——老头临死前把儿子踢一脚：「把我的遗嘱交给律师！」立遗嘱的人——testator，立遗嘱者。

k.syn: attestation, confirmation, documentation, substantiation, testament, validation
k.ant: disproof 反驳
k.en: firsthand authentication of a fact

ex.en: The jury heard 10 days of testimony.
ex.cn: 陪审团进行了10天的听证。

m: 谐音「踢死踹摩你」——证人在法庭上被律师踢来踢去，还是坚持口述自己亲眼见到的——testimony，证词、证言。test大家认识（测试），在法庭上「测试」你说的是否属实。

k.syn: choleric, irascible, peevish, perverse, pettish, petulant
k.ant: affable, good humor, unable to irritate, imperturbable, patient 和蔼可亲的，冷静的，有耐心的
k.en: easily annoyed: irritable

ex.en: That coworker would be easier to get along with if she weren't so testy all the time.
ex.cn: 如果那个同事不是一直那么易怒的话，她还是挺好相处的。

m: 谐音「踢死气」——一点小事就被踢到爆发，特别死气（暴躁）。同事拿笔碰了他一下，他就炸了——testy，易怒的、暴躁的。

k.ant: detach, tear loose 分开，撕裂
k.en: to fasten or restrain by or as if by a tether

ex.en: They tethered the horses in the shade.
ex.cn: 他们把马拴在棚子里。

k.en: the limit of one's strength or resources

ex.en: drought-stricken farmers at the end of their tether
ex.cn: 受干旱打击的农民已经忍无可忍

m: tether → 谐音「套它」。牧场主把马儿用绳套住拴在木桩上：「套它，别让它跑」——tether，用绳子拴系、束缚。引申义：at the end of one's tether（忍耐到了极限）。

k.syn: dramatic, histrionic, melodramatic, stagy
k.ant: natural, understated, reserved, restraint 自然的，保留的
k.cn: 做作的，夸张的，矫揉造作的
k.en: marked by exaggerated self-display and unnatural behavior

ex.en: assume a theatrical pose
ex.cn: 摆出夸张的姿势

m: theatrical → theater(剧场)+ical。演员在台上夸张地捂胸倒下，台下观众看出来了：他在现实中也这样，说话theatrical——theatrical，像在演戏一样矫揉造作的。剧场里的表演放到现实就是做作。

k.en: a government ruled by or subject to religious authority or divine guidance

m: theo-cracy，theo就是「神」（theology神学大家都听说过），cracy就是「统治」（democracy民主统治人人知道）。神说了算的政体——theocracy，神权政治国家。

k.en: the study of religious

m: theo神+logy学问——研究神的学问——theology，神学。

k.syn: curative, healing, officinal, remedial, restorative
k.en: of or relating to the treatment of disease or disorders by remedial agents or methods

ex.en: Gentle exercise can be therapeutic for hospital patients.
ex.cn: 适量的运动对病人恢复有好处。

m: therapeutic → therapy(治疗，大家都认识)+eutic。医院里挂满了therapeutic字眼，本质上就是「和治疗有关的」——therapeutic，治疗性的、有疗效的。就像school对应scholastic，hospital对应therapeutic。

k.ant: smooth 平滑的
k.en: full of thorns

ex.en: Stay out of the thorny brambles unless you want a ton of scratches.
ex.cn: 离那些长满刺的荆棘丛远一点，除非你想满身都是窟窿。

k.syn: catchy, delicate, knotty, prickly, ticklish
k.en: full of difficulties or controversial points: ticklish

ex.en: a thorny problem
ex.cn: 棘手的问题

m: thorny → thorn(刺，玫瑰上的刺)+y。玫瑰美丽但thorny，处理thorny问题就像徒手抓玫瑰茎——thorny，多刺的；引申为棘手的。这个词根大家都认识：thorn就是刺。

k.syn: banal, cliché, commonplace, hackney, shopworn, trite
k.en: overused to the point of being worn out; hackneyed

ex.en: a novel filled with nothing but threadbare clichés
ex.cn: 一部满是陈词滥调的小说

k.syn: beggared, destitute, down-and-out, famished, impecunious, impoverished, indigent, necessitous, needful, pauperized, penniless, penurious, poverty-stricken, skint
k.ant: affluent, deep-pocketed, flush, opulent, silk-stocking, wealthy, well- heeled, well-off, well-to-do 富裕的
k.en: lacking money or material possessions

ex.en: She never knew that she had so many threadbare relatives until she won the lottery.
ex.cn: 赢了彩票之后才知道她有这么多穷亲戚。

k.syn: frayed, raggedy, ratty, seedy, shabby, tattered, worn-out
k.en: worn or torn into or as if into rags

ex.en: I love that threadbare shirt, but after 10 years of wear, it is time to throw it away.
ex.cn: 我很喜欢那件旧旧的衬衣，但是已经穿了10年了，是该扔了。

m: threadbare → thread(线)+bare(裸露)。破到布线都磨断露出来的旧衣服，穿了二十年的借口也一样——threadbare，磨破的；陈腐不堪的。画面：一件衬衫破到线头都出来了。

k.syn: flock, mob, swarm
k.en: to crowd together in great numbers

ex.en: commuters thronging the MTR platform
ex.cn: 持月票的乘客拥向地铁站

m: throng → 谐音「潮涌」。演唱会散场，人潮如涌，挤得水泄不通——throng，大批人群聚集、拥挤。想象地铁早高峰，人群throng进来，动词名词都能用。

k.syn: baffle, balk, checkmate, discomfit
k.ant: advance, cultivate, encourage, forward, foster, further, nurture, promote, support, aid, bolster, abet, foment, facilitate 促进，支持
k.en: to oppose successfully

ex.en: She did all she could to thwart his plans.
ex.cn: 她竭尽所能阻挠他的计划。

m: thwart → 谐音「挡住了」。足球射门最后一刻被门将扑出，计划被thwart——thwart，阻挠、阻挡。想象一根横木「thwart」拦在路中间，what(什么)都过不去。

k.syn: huffy, tetchy, thin-skinned
k.ant: imperturbable 镇静的
k.en: easily offended or upset; touchy

ex.en: He is always ticklish whenever people refer to his baldness.
ex.cn: 大家一提起他的秃头，他就暴怒。

k.syn: catchy, delicate, difficult, dodgy, knotty, prickly, problematic, sensitive, spiny, sticky, thorny, touchy, tough, tricksy
k.cn: 棘手的，对技巧要求高的
k.en: requiring exceptional skill or caution in performance or handling

ex.en: Trying to tell him that his zipper is down without embarrassing him will be a ticklish task.
ex.cn: 如何体面得体地提醒他拉链没关好是一个技巧活儿。

m: ticklish → tickle(挠痒痒)+ish。挠痒痒一碰就炸的人，对批评也一样，稍微一说就跳起来——ticklish，易受激怒的、敏感的；也指需要小心处理的（情况）。

k.syn: altercate, bicker, brabble, hassle, quarrel, quibble, row, scrap, spat, squabble
k.en: a petty quarrel

ex.en: The couple got into a little tiff about what color sheets to buy for their bed.
ex.cn: 夫妇俩对于买什么颜色的床单争吵起来。

m: tiff → 谐音「踢飞」。两口子为谁忘买牛奶互相「踢」几句，小吵一架——tiff，小争吵、口角。注意是petty quarrel，不是大打出手。下次和朋友小拌嘴，就是一个tiff。

k.syn: mean, miserly, parsimonious, penurious, sparing, stinting
k.ant: bounteous, bountiful, charitable, freehanded, generous, liberal, munificent, openhanded, unsparing, unstinting 慷慨大方的
k.en: close-fisted; stingy

ex.en: The company is pretty tightfisted when it comes to bonuses.
ex.cn: 这个公司分红的时候很吝啬。

m: tightfisted → tight(紧)+fisted(握拳的)。钱包被手捏得死紧，掰都掰不开——tightfisted，吝啬的、一毛不拔的。画面：守财奴把钱抓在拳头里死握不放。

k.syn: banal, cliché, commonplace, hackneyed, stereotyped, threadbare, trite
k.ant: fresh, new, novel, original, unclichéd, unhackneyed 新奇的
k.en: hackneyed, stale

ex.en: timeworn jokes
ex.cn: 老掉牙的笑话

m: timeworn → time(时间)+worn(磨损的，wear的过去分词)。被时间磨旧的东西，用了几十年的表达方式——timeworn，陈腐的、用滥了的。和threadbare同义，但强调「被时间磨」这个过程。

k.syn: fainthearted, fearsome, scary, timorous, tremulous
k.ant: stalwart, adventurous, audacious, bold, daring, dashing, gutsy, venturous 坚定勇敢的
k.en: lacking in courage or self- confidence

ex.en: He gave her a timid smile.
ex.cn: 他给了她一个羞赧的微笑。

m: timid → 谐音「太咪的」（像小猫一样）。小猫咪看到陌生人马上躲起来，胆子极小——timid，胆怯的、不自信的。想象一只小猫「咪」地叫一声就缩回去了。

k.syn: timid, undaring, quailing, recoiling, scary, skittish
k.ant: stalwart, intrepid, adventurous, audacious, bold, daring, dashing, gutsy, venturous 大胆的
k.en: of a timid disposition: fearful

ex.en: be as timorous as a rabbit
ex.cn: 像兔子一样胆小

m: 谐音「踢磨了死」——胆小鬼被磨了又磨，踢了又踢，还是战战兢兢不敢动——timorous，胆小的、充满恐惧的。记住timid（胆怯）就更好记了，timorous是它的升级版。

k.syn: dye, pigment, stain, tincture, tint, suffuse
k.ant: decolorize 使脱色
k.en: to color with a slight shade or stain: tint

ex.en: Just slightly tinge the frosting with yellow food coloring to give it a lemony look.
ex.cn: 略微在霜糖表面加一点黄色色素，有一点柠檬的感觉就可以了。

m: tinge → 谐音「丁点儿」。画家在白色颜料里加了一丁点儿红，颜色微微带了点粉——tinge，给…染上少量色彩；名词：一丝、一点（带有某种气息）。

k.syn: diddle with, fiddle with, fool with, mess with, toy with, twiddle with
k.ant: adjust, repair 调整，修理
k.en: to handle thoughtlessly, ignorantly, or mischievously

ex.en: tinker with the engine
ex.cn: 胡乱调整发动机

m: tinker → tinkle(叮叮当当)+er。修锅匠叮叮当当乱敲一气，结果没修好——tinker，胡乱修补、拙劣地调整。「Don't tinker with it」别乱搞它。

k.syn: dye, pigment, stain, tincture, tinge, suffuse
k.ant: decolorize 使脱色
k.en: apply a usually slight or pale coloration to

ex.en: tint with only one color
ex.cn: 只用一种颜色染色

m: tint → 比tinge还浅，想象指甲上涂了一层半透明浅色——tint，涂浅色、微染；名词指浅淡的色调。和tinge区别：tint强调颜色本身的浅淡，tinge强调加了一丁点。

k.syn: harangue, diatribe, jeremiad, philippic, rant
k.ant: eulogy, encomium, panegyric, tribute 颂词
k.en: a long angry or violent speech, usually of a censorious or denunciatory nature; a diatribe

ex.en: a tirade of angry protest
ex.cn: 一个表达愤怒的抗议的长篇抨击性演讲

m: tirade → 谐音「踢人带」。被踢了一脚的人连珠炮地痛骂对方，一口气说了十分钟停不下来——tirade，长篇激烈的抨击演讲、痛骂。tire(累)+ade，骂到对方都累了。

k.syn: sycophant, groveler, flatterer
k.en: one who flatters in the hope of gaining favors

ex.en: He criticized that I was a toady.
ex.cn: 他批评说我是一个马屁精。

m: toady → 谐音「拖腚」。马屁精拖着屁股爬在地上，仰头向上司献媚，活像一只蛤蟆——toady，马屁精（名词）；拍马屁（动词）。toad本来就是蛤蟆，蛤蟆姿态=谄媚姿态。

k.en: A gripping tool with two hinged jaws used for holding or manipulating objects.

m: tong/tongs → 谐音「通夹」。夹糖用的糖夹子，通过夹的动作取东西——tongs，钳子、夹子。注意通常用复数tongs。厨房里夹冰块的工具就是tongs。

k.syn: medicinal, restorative, salubrious, salutary, salutiferous, sanative, wholesome
k.ant: insalubrious, noxious, unhealthful, unhealthy, unwholesome 不健康的 “若干年前，有人跟我说，没有GRE的人生是不完整的。现在我跟别人说，没有GRE的人生确实不是完整的。” （李丹，录取院校：Princeton University， Civil and Environmental Engineering）
k.en: producing or stimulating physical, mental, or emotional vigor; beneficial to the health of body or mind

ex.en: tonic medicine
ex.cn: 补药

m: tonic → 谐音「通克」（打通、克服疲劳）。喝一瓶运动饮料，精神通畅地克服困倦——tonic，滋补的；名词指补品、强壮剂。也指汤力水（tonic water）。

k.en: Of or relating to a barber or the practice of barbering.

m: tonsorial → tonsure(剃发，僧侣剃头)+orial。理发师拿着剃刀的技艺就是tonsorial art——tonsorial，理发的、与理发师有关的。类比：sartorial(服装的)对应裁缝，tonsorial对应理发师。

k.en: the upper part of the soil; the fertile surface layer of earth

m: topsoil → top(顶部)+soil(土壤)。最顶层的那一薄层土，含腐殖质最多最肥——topsoil，表层土。直白合成词，top+soil，顶部的土。

k.en: A violently rotating column of air that extends from a thunderstorm to the ground.

m: tornado → 谐音「台风那多」（比台风还多）——不对，更准确：tor(扭转)+nado旋转。龙卷风是剧烈旋转的气柱，tornado在西班牙语里就是「旋转的风暴」——tornado，龙卷风。

k.syn: asleep, benumbed, dead, insensitive, numbed, unfeeling
k.ant: feeling, sensible, sensitive 有同情心的，敏感的
k.en: lacking in sensation or feeling

ex.en: My tongue and throat remained torpid for a time following the endoscopy.
ex.cn: 胃镜检查之后，我的舌头和喉咙一直是麻木的。

k.syn: dull, inert, lethargic, quiescent, sleepy, sluggish, torpid
k.ant: active 活跃的
k.en: slow to move or act

ex.en: a torpid sloth that refused to budge off its tree branch
ex.cn: 趴在树枝上一动不动的树獭

m: torpid → tor(扭，同torque)+pid。像乌龟冬眠，身体「扭」不动了，整个人呆滞无力——torpid，倦怠的、迟钝的。torpedo(鱼雷)同源，鱼雷鱼（电鳐）能让人麻痹。

k.syn: apathy, casualness, complacence, disinterestedness, disregard, incuriosity, insouciance, nonchalance
k.ant: concern, interest, regard 有兴趣
k.en: lack of interest or concern

ex.en: After a lifetime of setbacks, defeats, and failures, he could only greet the latest bad news with a resigned fatalism and dull torpor.
ex.cn: 一生都充满了挫折、失败，他只能用宿命论和麻木不仁来迎接又一次的坏消息。

k.syn: languor, lassitude, listlessness, stupor
k.ant: vigor, vim, vitality, vivacity 活力
k.en: physical or mental inertness

ex.en: Following Thanksgiving dinner, we spent the rest of the day lounging about in a contented torpor.
ex.cn: 吃完感恩节晚餐后，我们慵懒地靠在沙发上度过剩下的时间。

m: torpor → 和torpid同源，名词形式。比torpid更极端，整个人陷入麻木冷漠的状态——torpor，冷漠、迟钝、麻木。冬眠动物的状态就是torpor。

k.en: a force that produces or tends to produce rotation

m: torque → 谐音「拖开」。拧螺丝时施加的扭力，「拖着」螺丝转动——torque，扭矩、转矩。物理课上讲：torque = 力 × 力臂。tor(扭转)这个概念贯穿tortuous/tornado/torque。

k.ant: trickling 涓涓细流的
k.en: caused by or resulting from action of rapidstreams

ex.en: a torrential rain
ex.cn: 倾盆大雨

m: torrential → torrent(急流，大家认识)+ial。洪水季节torrential rain倾盆而下——torrential，急流的、如洪流般的。torrential rain是固定搭配。

k.syn: hot, scalding, scorching, sweltering
k.ant: arctic, bone-chilling, freezing, frigid, frozen, glacial, icy 寒冷的
k.en: intensely hot

ex.en: the dry, torrid summers in southern Arizona
ex.cn: 南亚利桑那州干燥、炎热的夏天

k.syn: ardent, burning, fervid, flaming, glowing, impassioned, incandescent, intense, passionate, vehement
k.ant: dispassionate, emotionless, impassive, unemotional 冷漠的
k.en: having or expressing great depth of feeling

ex.en: torrid love affair
ex.cn: 炙热的爱

m: torrid → 谐音「太热的」。撒哈拉沙漠torrid zone，烈日烤得地面都开裂——torrid，酷热的；也指热情激烈的（torrid romance）。tor(烤)+rid，烤干的感觉。

k.syn: bending, curling, curvy, devious, serpentine, sinuous, twisted, windy
k.ant: direct, straightforward 直接的
k.en: marked by devious or indirect tactics: crooked, tricky

ex.en: a tortuous path
ex.cn: 一条蜿蜒的道路

m: tortuous → torture(折磨)+ous，但去了一个字母。山路蜿蜒曲折，像在折磨司机——tortuous，曲折的、转弯抹角的。不要和tortured混淆：tortuous是物理上或比喻上的弯曲，不直接。

k.en: device (as a band of rubber) that checks bleeding

m: tourniquet → tour(旅行)+niquet。旅途中受伤割破动脉，队友用腰带扎紧止血——tourniquet，止血带。发音「tur-ni-ket」，法语借词，医护人员的必备工具。

k.syn: acclaim, laud, praise, ballyhoo
k.ant: asperse, censure, denounce, berate, rate, revile, vituperate 指责
k.en: to promote or praise energetically; publicize

ex.en: tout victory in the war
ex.cn: 鼓吹战果

m: tout → 谐音「吐出来」。黄牛在门口大声吐出口号：「最便宜！最好！快来快来！」——tout，大力推销、吹捧宣传。英国俚语里ticket tout就是票贩子。

k.syn: diddle with, fiddle with, fool with, mess with, monkey with, tinker with, twiddle with
k.en: to handle thoughtlessly, ignorantly, or mischievously

ex.en: That microscope is a delicate instrument, not something to be toyed with.
ex.cn: 那台显微镜是精密仪器，不是随随便便的一个玩具。

m: toy → 小孩对玩具的态度：今天宝贝，明天扔一边。对事情toy with就是这种漫不经心的态度——toy (with)，草率对待、把…当玩具摆弄。「He was just toying with the idea」他只是随便想想而已。

k.syn: obedient, amenable, docile, flexible, manageable, compliant, submissive, tame
k.ant: balky, contumacious, defiant, disobedient, incompliant, insubordinate, obstreperous, rebellious, recalcitrant, refractory, restive, unamenable, ungovernable, unruly, untoward, wayward, willful 任性的，难以驯服的
k.en: readily giving in to the command or authority of another

ex.en: a tractable horse
ex.cn: 一匹温顺的马

m: tractor（拖拉机）大家都认识，tractable就是「能被拖拉机牵着走」——温顺得像一头被套了缰绳的牛，随便驾驭——tractable，温顺的、易管教的。

k.syn: calm, quiet, silence, serenity
k.ant: bustle, commotion, hubbub, pandemonium, tumult, turmoil, unquietness, unrest, uproar 喧闹，骚乱
k.en: a state of freedom from storm or disturbance

ex.en: enjoyed the tranquillity of the snow-covered field at dusk
ex.cn: 享受傍晚时分被雪覆盖的田野的静谧

m: tranquility → tranquil(平静，大家认识)+ity。湖面如镜，没有一丝风浪，那种宁静就是tranquility——tranquility，宁静、平静。tranquil形容词变名词加ity。

k.syn: break, outreach, outrun, overpass, overreach, overrun, overshoot, overstep, surpass
k.en: to rise above or go beyond the limits of

ex.en: a person who believes that any true understanding of God transcends human intelligence
ex.cn: 认为对上帝的真正理解是超越人类智商极限的人

m: 超越(transcend)！trans越过+scend爬，transcend，超越胜过。

k.syn: alchemize, metamorphose, transform, transmute, transpose, transubstantiate, make over
k.en: to alter the outward appearance of; transform

ex.en: transfigure the AutoCAD drawings with elegant symbols
ex.cn: 用优美的符号使AutoCAD图变得美观

m: transfigure → trans(变换)+figure(形象、外形)。耶稣在山上变容，外貌发光改变——transfigure，使改观、彻底改变外貌。不只是小修小改，而是形象上的根本转变。

k.syn: breach, break, contravene, fracture, infringe, offend, traduce
k.ant: follow, mind, obey, observe, comply with, conform to 遵守
k.en: to fail to keep; to commit an offense

ex.en: Don't even think about transgressing the drug laws of that Asian country, for punishments are severe and there's nothing that our government can do to intervene.
ex.cn: 别指望违背那个亚洲国家的药品相关法，因为惩罚非常严厉，我们政府完全无法干涉。

m: trans（越过，如transport）+ gress（走，如progress）——越过法律的界线往里走，就是犯罪——transgress，违犯法律、越界。progress是向前走，transgress是越界走。

k.syn: transitory, ephemeral, evanescent, fleeting, temporary
k.ant: ceaseless, dateless, deathless, endless, enduring, eternal, everlasting, immortal, lasting, long-lived, permanent, perpetual, timeless, undying, unending 永恒持久的
k.en: passing with time; transitory

ex.en: transient pleasure
ex.cn: 短暂的快乐

m: transient → trans(穿越)+ient。像候鸟穿越而过，不停留——transient，短暂的、转瞬即逝的。「transient visitors」过客。和permanent(永久的)相反。

k.syn: transient, ephemeral, evanescent, fleeting, temporary
k.ant: ceaseless, dateless, deathless, endless, enduring, eternal, everlasting, immortal, lasting, long-lived, permanent, perpetual, timeless, undying, unending 永恒持久的
k.en: existing or lasting only a short time; short-lived or temporary

ex.en: Most of life's joys are transitory.
ex.cn: 尘世间的欢乐都是过眼烟云，转瞬即逝。

m: transitory → transitor(过路者)+y。比transient更文学化，强调「注定要消逝」——transitory，短暂的、无常的。「All is transitory」万物皆无常。

k.syn: transparent, limpid, pellucid, lucent, lucid, transpicuous
k.ant: opaque 不透明的
k.en: permitting the passage of light; clear, transparent

ex.en: translucent dewdrop
ex.cn: 透明的露珠

m: translucent → trans(穿透)+lucent(发光，如luminous)。光能穿过但看不清里面，像磨砂玻璃——translucent，半透明的。透光但不透明，比transparent(完全透明)差一档。

k.syn: translucent, limpid, pellucid, lucent, lucid, transpicuous
k.ant: cloudy, opaque 不透明的
k.en: capable of transmitting light

k.syn: obvious, clear, apparent, patent, understandable, apprehensible, comprehensible, fathomable
k.ant: ambiguous, clouded, cryptic, enigmatic, equivocal, indistinct, mysterious, obfuscated, obscure, unapparent, unclarified, unclear, unclouded 难以理解的
k.cn: 没有歧义的，清晰易懂的
k.en: not subject to misinterpretation or more than one interpretation

ex.en: His meaning in leaving the conversation is transparent: he doesn't want to talk about his combat experiences.
ex.cn: 他结束这个话题的意图很明显：不想谈论当年参战的经历。

m: transparent → trans(穿透)+parent(显现)。玻璃完全透明，内心完全透明，谎言一眼看穿——transparent，透明的；容易理解的、显而易见的。parent里有「现形」之意。

k.syn: drudgery, fatigue, grind
k.en: work, especially when arduous or involving painful effort

ex.en: writer's literary travail
ex.cn: 作家辛勤的文学创作

ex.en: Labor Day is the day on which we recognize those men and women who daily travail with little appreciation or compensation.
ex.cn: 劳动节是我们向那些平日辛勤劳作但是很少受到大家关注并得到应有补偿的工人致敬的日子。

m: travail → 谐音「拖偎儿」（累得拖着腿蜷缩）。古时旅行(travel)极为艰辛，travail就是从travel同源来的艰辛劳动——travail，艰辛劳动、痛苦挣扎。in travail指在痛苦中挣扎。

k.syn: course, cover, cross, cut across, navigate, pass over, perambulate, peregrinate, proceed along, transit, travel
k.en: to travel or pass across, over, or through

ex.en: The spider traversed the wall from end to end.
ex.cn: 蜘蛛侠攀岩走壁。

m: 横穿(traverse)！trans+verse横过，traverse，横越。

k.syn: ape, copy, copycat, emulate, mimic, simulate
k.en: to use（someone or something）as the model for one's speech, mannerisms, or behavior

ex.en: She began to learn their language by mimicking the sounds they made.
ex.cn: 她开始通过模仿发音来学习他们的语言。

k.syn: caricature, burlesque, parody, spoof, mock
k.ant: paragon 完美的模范
k.en: an exaggerated or grotesque imitation, such as a parody of a literary work

ex.en: a travesty of his classmate's manner
ex.cn: 滑稽的模仿其他同学的举止

m: 谐音「踹哦闹死」——穿着奇装异服，踹门而入乱搞一通，对严肃的经典进行拙劣嘲弄——travesty，歪曲的模仿、对……的嘲弄。

k.syn: faithless, disloyal, perfidious, recreant, traitorous, unfaithful, unloyal, betraying
k.ant: constant, dedicated, devoted, staunch, down-the-line, faithful, fast, loyal, steadfast, steady, true 忠诚的
k.en: marked by betrayal of fidelity, confidence, or trust

ex.en: a treacherous ally
ex.cn: 不忠的盟友

m: treacherous → treachery(背叛，大家认识)+ous。背后捅刀子的行为——treacherous，背叛的；也指危险难测的（treacherous roads）。teach(教)没教好，变成了背信弃义者。

k.en: one who has charge of funds

m: treasurer → treasure(宝藏)+r。管宝藏的那个人，公司财务部——treasurer，财务主管、出纳员。treasure+er=管treasure的人，直白。

k.en: an agreement

m: treaty → treat(对待、谈判)+y。两国正式谈判（treat）后签下的协议——treaty，条约、协定。treat在老英语里有「谈判协商」之意，treaty就是谈判的产物。

k.syn: keen, sharp
k.ant: blunt, blunted, dull, obtuse 钝的
k.en: keen, sharp

ex.en: Even the most trenchant sword could not sever the bonds of loyalty between them.
ex.cn: 即使最锋利的刀剑也不能切断他们之间的忠诚。

k.syn: incisive
k.ant: vague 含糊的
k.en: vigorously effective and articulate

ex.en: trenchant criticisms
ex.cn: 一针见血的批评

m: trenchant → trench(战壕)+ant。战壕里的老兵评论犀利如刀，切中要害——trenchant，锐利的、尖锐有力的（言辞）。trench切割+ant，能切开的，比sharp更有文学味。

k.syn: fear, fright, horror, apprehension
k.ant: boldness, bravery, dauntlessness, intrepidity 大胆
k.en: the emotion experienced in the presence or threat of danger; apprehension

ex.en: trepidation about starting a new career
ex.cn: 对开创一项新事业感到恐惧

m: trepidation → tremble(颤抖)的变体+ation。上台前双腿发抖，心跳加速——trepidation，恐惧、战栗、忐忑不安。「with great trepidation」极度忐忑地。

k.syn: transgress, invade, intrude
k.ant: withdraw, retreat 撤退
k.en: to enter unlawfully upon the land of another; to commit an offense

ex.en: I consider him to be trespassing against all of us when he trespasses against any one of us.
ex.cn: 当他冒犯我们当中一个人的时候，他就冒犯了我们全部。

m: trespass → tres(三)+pass(通过)。「私人领地，不得擅自通过」，你偷偷pass进去就是trespass——trespass，非法侵入。「No trespassing」禁止擅入，路牌上常见。

k.syn: accolade, eulogy, encomium, panegyric, tribute 颂词
k.ant: denunciation, condemnation, criticism, censure, reproof, reprobation 批评，指责
k.en: a gift, payment, declaration, or other acknowledgment of gratitude, respect, or admiration

ex.en: pay a high tribute to
ex.cn: 赞颂

m: tribute → tribe(部落)+ute。古代部落向强者进贡，现在引申为颂词致敬——tribute，贡品；颂词；致敬。「pay tribute to」向…致敬，是固定搭配。

k.ant: pour, roll, stream, gush 喷涌
k.cn: 一滴一滴地流，缓缓地流
k.en: to issue or fall in drops

ex.en: Tears trickled down her cheeks.
ex.cn: 眼泪从她的脸上一滴一滴地流下。

m: trickle → trick(把戏)+le(小)。水一滴一滴地玩「消失戏法」，慢慢流走——trickle，一滴滴地流、细流。「information trickled out」消息缓缓泄露出来。

k.syn: bathetic, cliché, hackneyed, threadbare, timeworn, banal
k.ant: fresh, new, novel, original, unclichéd, unhackneyed新鲜的
k.en: hackneyed or boring from much use, not fresh or original

ex.en: “You win some, you lose some”is a trite expression.“
ex.cn: 有得必有失”是老生常谈了。

m: trite → 谐音「踢烂它」。这个说法已经被踢来踢去踢烂了，毫无新意——trite，陈腐的、陈词滥调的。「a trite expression」烂大街的说法。比hackneyed更常用。

k.syn: inconsiderable, insignificant, minor, petty, inconsequential, paltry, trifling
k.ant: consequential, material, momentous, weighty, indispensable, substantive, grandiose, massive 重要的
k.en: of little worth or importance

ex.en: Why spend so much time on trivial decisions, like whether the cola should be regular or diet?
ex.cn: 为什么花这么多时间在这种琐碎的决定上，比如可乐要喝一般的还是健怡的？

m: trivial → tri(三)+via(路，如via)+al。三叉路口的小事，街头小事，微不足道——trivial，微不足道的、无关紧要的。trivia就是「三叉路口的闲聊」，引申为琐事。

k.en: something gained or given in victory

m: trophy → 谐音「托高放」（把奖杯举高高）。赢了比赛把奖杯高高托起展示——trophy，奖品、战利品、奖杯。古希腊「tropaion」指战场上竖起的敌人武器作为胜利标志。

k.en: A group of performers, such as actors or dancers, who tour to different venues.

m: troupe → group(团体)的变体，t+r+oupe。一群人一起表演的剧团——troupe，（演员）剧团。和troop(军队)同源，都是「一群人」。

k.syn: shirk, malinger, goldbrick, avoid, escape, evade, parry, sidestep, circumvent, fence, hedge, avert, elude, shun, skirt, dodge, bilk, eschew
k.ant: dutiful 尽职尽责的
k.cn: /n./vi. 逃避责任（的）；逃避责任者
k.en: shirking responsibility; one who shirks duty

ex.en: play truant
ex.cn: 逃学

m: truant → true(真)+ant。「真的蚂蚁」去哪了？——实际上truant是那个「真的逃走了」的人，逃课的学生——truant，逃课者、逃避责任的（形容词）。play truant意为逃学。

k.syn: armistice, cease-fire, peace
k.en: a suspension of fighting especially of considerable duration by agreement of opposing forces

ex.en: truce agreement
ex.cn: 休战协议

m: truce → 谐音「撤」。双方喊「撤！」暂时停火——truce，休战、停战协定。「call a truce」宣布休战。不是永久和平，只是暂时停下来喘口气。

k.syn: contumelious, invective, opprobrious, scurrilous, vitriolic,
k.en: marked by harsh insulting language

ex.en: a theater critic who was notorious for his titanically truculent reviews
ex.cn: 一个以超大量尖酸刻薄言论著称的话剧评论家

m: truculent → truck(卡车)+ulent(充满)。一辆暴怒的大卡车横冲直撞，充满攻击性——truculent，凶猛的、好斗的、残暴的。truck的粗暴感帮助记忆。

k.syn: plod, lug, slog, flounder
k.ant: breeze, glide, slide, waltz, whisk, flit 快速通过
k.en: to move heavily or clumsily

ex.en: trudge over hill
ex.cn: 翻山越岭

m: trudge → 谐音「拖着走」。双脚沉如铅，背着大包，拖着脚步在泥地里艰难前行——trudge，拖着脚吃力地走、跋涉。比walk重多了，强调疲惫和费力。

k.syn: advertise, annunciate, blare, broadcast, declare, proclaim, promulgate, release, give out
k.en: to make known openly or publicly

ex.en: The losing party lost no time in trumpeting allegations of election fraud.
ex.cn: 落败的政党马不停蹄地开始公开宣称选举存在作假。

m: trumpet → trump(王牌)+et(小)。王牌乐器中的小号，声音洪亮穿透一切——trumpet，小号；也作动词「大声宣扬」。「trumpet one's achievements」大肆宣扬自己的成就。

k.syn: abbreviate, abridge, curtail, retrench
k.ant: elongate, extend, lengthen, prolong, protract 延长
k.en: to shorten by or as if by cutting off

ex.en: a truncated version of the 11 o'clock newscast following the awards show, which ran over its time slot
ex.cn: 颁奖典礼之后缩短版的11点新闻节目，已经超出了预定时长

m: truncate → trunk(树干)+ate。把树干截短——truncate，截断、缩短（文章、视频等）。trunk是树干/躯干，truncate就是把它cut掉一截。

k.syn: band, bind
k.ant: unbind, untie 解开
