k.en: to gather into a tight mass by means of a line or cord

ex.en: After stuffing the turkey, the chef quickly trussed it so the forcemeat wouldn't fall out during roasting.
ex.cn: 把填料塞进火鸡后，大厨迅速把它扎紧防止在烘烤过程中肉馅掉出来。

m: truss → trust(信任)去掉t。工程师trust这个承重框架能撑住大桥——truss，桁架、构架（建筑支撑结构）；也指捆绑（truss up a turkey）。

k.en: A small, powerful boat used to tow or push larger vessels.

m: tugboat → tug(用力拉)+boat(船)。港口里那艘矮胖的小船，用力拖着大轮船进港——tugboat，拖船。tug就是使劲拉的动作，tugboat专门干这活。

k.syn: disorder, disturbance, commotion, convulsion, ferment, turmoil, pandemonium, uproar
k.ant: quietude, quiescence, serenity, tranquility 安静
k.en: a disorderly commotion or disturbance, a riot

ex.en: His mind was in a tumult.
ex.cn: 他心烦意乱。

m: tumult → 谐音「土木炸」。工地爆破，尘土飞扬，人群骚动乱成一锅粥——tumult，喧嚣、骚动、暴乱。「the tumult of the crowd」人群的喧嚣。

k.syn: muddy, murky, obscure
k.ant: clear, limpid, lucid, pellucid, crystalline 清澈的
k.en: deficient in clarity or purity

ex.en: a turbid stream
ex.cn: 一条混浊的河

m: 谐音「踹悲的」——下雨后河水被泥沙踹搅一通，变得浑浊悲催——turbid，混浊的。disturb里的turb就是「搅动」，turbid是被搅浑的。

k.syn: cataclysmal, stormy, tempestuous, tumultuous
k.en: marked by sudden or violent disturbance

ex.en: a turbulent period in history
ex.cn: 历史上的动荡时期

k.syn: rough, rugged, stormy, tempestuous
k.en: marked by turmoil or disturbance especially of natural elements

ex.en: The turbulent rapids of the river were certainly daunting to those of us who were new to river rafting.
ex.cn: 湍急的流水必定让我们这些初玩漂流的人吓得不轻。

m: 谐音「踹不楞的」——海浪互相踹来踹去，楞是楞的翻腾汹涌——turbulent，汹涌的、动荡的。turbulent times指动乱年代，比turbid更广义。

k.syn: flatulent, bombastic, pompous
k.ant: simple, austere, unadorned, undecorated, unembellished 简朴的
k.en: excessively embellished in style or language

ex.en: turgid prose
ex.cn: 浮华的散文

m: turgid → 谐音「太鼓涨的」。文章写得鼓鼓胀胀，堆砌辞藻却空洞无物——turgid，浮夸的、过分华丽的（文风）；也指肿胀的（医学）。

k.syn: commotion, agitation, tumult, turbulence, disorder, ferment, clamor
k.ant: calm, ease, peace, peacefulness, quiet, tranquility, silence, serenity 安静
k.en: a state or condition of extreme confusion, agitation, or commotion

ex.en: My mind is in a turmoil.
ex.cn: 我心里很乱。

m: turmoil → turn(翻转)+oil(油)。把一锅油翻转搅动，乱成一团——turmoil，极度混乱、动荡。「in turmoil」处于混乱中，比tumult更强调内部的混乱状态。

k.syn: renegade, traitor, apostate, recreant, betrayer, quisling
k.ant: loyalist, partisan, adherent, supporter 支持者，追随者
k.en: one who switches to an opposing side or party; specifically: traitor

ex.en: He was labeled as a turncoat.
ex.cn: 他是一个叛徒。

m: turncoat → turn(翻转)+coat(外套)。翻转外套，露出里面敌方的颜色——turncoat，叛徒、变节者。画面极好：把外套翻过来穿，换了阵营。

k.en: A blue-green mineral used as a gemstone, valued for its distinctive color.

m: turquoise → Turkey(土耳其)+oise。这种蓝绿色宝石最早经土耳其传入欧洲——turquoise，绿松石；蓝绿色。土耳其蓝，想象爱琴海的颜色。

k.en: A small tower, typically one forming part of a castle or other large building.

m: turret → tower(塔)的变体+ret(小后缀)。城堡角落上的小尖塔，比主塔小——turret，小塔楼、炮塔。坦克上的那个旋转炮台也叫turret。

k.syn: appreciate, apprehend, cognize, compass, conceive, decipher, decode, dig, discern, perceive, recognize, savvy, seize, sense, tumble to, catch on to, make out
k.en: to have a clear idea of

ex.en: It took me a while to twig the true nature of the relationship between the two women.
ex.cn: 我花了很长时间才搞清楚这俩女人之间的关系。

m: twig → 谐音「细枝」，直接形象。折断树枝时「啪」一声，twig！——twig，小树枝、细枝。snap a twig折断细枝。也作俚语动词「突然明白」(英国用法)。

k.syn: misprint, bug
k.en: an error in typed or typeset material

ex.en: make one typo on the memo
ex.cn: 在样张上打错一个字

m: typo → type(打字)+o(错误的感叹)。打字员敲完一行，发现一个字母打错了：「哦！typo！」——typo，打字错误、印刷错误。日常最高频的词之一。

k.syn: novice, apprentice, neophyte, rookie, amateur, dabbler, dilettante, fledgling, recruit
k.ant: old hand, old-timer, vet, expert, professional, veteran 专家
k.en: a beginner in learning: novice

ex.en: a tyro in the art of piano
ex.cn: 钢琴界的新手

m: tyro → 谐音「太弱了」。新手入场，经验为零，被老手一眼看穿——tyro，新手、初学者。注意拼写：不是tire，是tyro。比novice更书面。

k.syn: omnipresent, universal, general, common
k.ant: extraordinary, infrequent, rare, seldom, uncommon, unfamiliar, unusual, unique, particular 独特的，不常见的
k.en: being everywhere at the same time; often observed or encountered

ex.en: The conflict between opposites is ubiquitous.
ex.cn: 对立的冲突无所不在。

m: ubiquitous → ubi(哪里，拉丁语，你见过「Uber」打车随叫随到)+quit+ous。Uber随叫随到、无处不在的感觉——ubiquitous，无处不在的。手机、Wi-Fi、便利店，都是ubiquitous的例子。

k.syn: undeniable, inviolable, sacrosanct, irrefutable, indisputable, unexceptionable, unimpeachable
k.ant: controversial 有争议的
k.cn: 无可争辩的，无法否认的，不可亵渎的
k.en: not to be violated, criticized, or tampered with

ex.en: an unassailable article
ex.cn: 无可争辩的文章

m: unassailable → un(不)+assail(攻击，如assault)+able(能)。连攻击都攻击不了的论点——unassailable，无懈可击的、无可争辩的。assail和assault同源，un+assail+able=攻不动。

k.syn: magical, miraculous, phenomenal, preternatural, superhuman, supernormal, transcendent, transcendental, unearthly
k.ant: ordinary, commonplace, normal 平凡的
k.en: being so extraordinary or abnormal as to suggest powers which violate the laws of nature

ex.en: The silence was uncanny.
ex.cn: 静得出奇。

m: uncanny → un(不)+canny(精明、正常)。超出正常精明范围的事，令人不寒而栗的离奇——uncanny，离奇的、令人毛骨悚然的。「uncanny resemblance」离奇的相似度。

k.syn: crass, rude, discourteous, disgracious, ungraceful, crude, ill-bred, ill- mannered, uncivil, unrefined
k.ant: genteel, cultivated, cultured, polished, urbane, seemly 得体的，有教养的
k.en: lacking in refinement or good taste

ex.en: The movie's uncouth humor seemed to be purposely offensive.
ex.cn: 电影里一些恶俗的幽默貌似是故意的。

m: uncouth → un(没有)+couth(教养，古英语)。没有教养的粗鄙之人，举止粗俗——uncouth，粗俗的、粗鲁的。couth本来是「知道、熟悉」，un+couth=不懂规矩。

k.syn: adulatory, gushing, hagiographic, oily, oleaginous, soapy
k.en: overly or insincerely flattering

ex.en: an unctuous appraisal of the musical talent shown by the boss's daughter
ex.cn: 拍老板马屁，说他女儿在音乐方面有着非常卓越的天赋

k.syn: artificial, backhanded, counterfeit, fake, feigned, hypocritical, phony, pretended
k.ant: artless, candid, genuine, heartfelt, honest, sincere, undesigning, unfeigned 真实的
k.en: characterized by affected, exaggerated, or insincere earnestness

ex.en: an unctuous effort to appear religious to the voters
ex.cn: 过分做作想在选民面前表现得虔诚

m: unctuous → unct(涂油，如unction涂油礼)+uous。神父涂油时的油腻感觉，引申为谄媚的油滑——unctuous，油腻的；谄媚奉承的（带讽刺意味）。「an unctuous smile」油腻的笑容。

k.syn: loser, victim
k.ant: bully 欺凌弱小者
k.en: a loser/victim or predicted loser in a struggle or contest

ex.en: She always supports for the underdog.
ex.cn: 她经常支持受害者。

m: underdog → under(下面)+dog(狗)。被压在下面的狗，永远处于劣势——underdog，输家、弱势一方。体育比赛里大家都喜欢支持underdog。

k.syn: strengthen, support, fortify, reinforce, bear, bolster, brace, buttress, carry, stay, sustain, underpin, uphold, prop up, shore up
k.ant: undermine, weaken, debilitate, enfeeble, enervate 削弱
k.en: to support or strengthen from beneath

ex.en: Facts and statistics undergird his commentary.
ex.cn: 事实和数据支持了他的评论。

m: undergird → under(下面)+gird(环绕加固，如girdle束腰带)。从底部环绕加固，给结构打好基础——undergird，加强、巩固底部支撑。

k.syn: weaken, debilitate, enfeeble, enervate
k.ant: strengthen, support, fortify, reinforce 加强
k.en: to weaken, injure, or impair, often by degrees or imperceptibly

ex.en: undermine a fortress
ex.cn: 挖地道破坏堡垒

m: undermine → under(地下)+mine(挖矿)。在地下挖空地基，使上面的建筑崩塌——undermine，暗中削弱、破坏。「undermine confidence」动摇信心，就是在底下偷偷挖。

k.syn: accentuate, italicize, stress, underline, bring out
k.ant: deemphasize, downplay 轻描淡写
k.en: to emphasize; stress

ex.en: underscore the value of education
ex.cn: 强调教育的重要性

m: underscore → under(下面)+score(分数线、刻划)。在文字下面划一条线来强调——underscore，下划线；强调。电脑键盘上「_」这个符号就是underscore。

k.syn: conservative, low-key, muted, repressed, restrained, sober, subdued, unflashy, unpretentious
k.ant: flamboyant, flaring, flashy, garish, gaudy, glitzy, ostentatious, splashy 夸张的；华丽的
k.der: understated adj. 不夸张的；朴素的：avoiding obvious emphasis or embellishment
k.en: to state or present with restraint especially for effect

ex.en: understate a problem
ex.cn: 低调处理问题

m: understate → under(低于)+state(陈述)。陈述得低于实际程度，轻描淡写——understate，轻描淡写、不充分陈述。反义是overstate夸大。「I'm a bit tired」说的是understatement，实际上累到快死。

k.en: avoiding obvious emphasis

m: understated → understate的形容词形式。低调的、不张扬的设计风格——understated，朴素的、含蓄的。「understated elegance」低调的优雅，是时尚界的高级词汇。

k.syn: deputy
k.en: one who is prepared to act another's part or take over another's duties

ex.en: be an understudy to sb
ex.cn: 作为某人的替补

m: understudy → under(在下面备用)+study(研究、学习角色)。替补演员在台下把所有角色都学会，随时准备替上——understudy，替补演员。好莱坞必备制度。

k.syn: unassailable, undeniable, irrefutable, indisputable, unimpeachable
k.ant: assailable, controversial 有争议的
k.en: not open to objection or criticism, beyond reproach

m: unexceptionable → un+exception(例外)+able。找不到任何例外来反驳，完美无缺——unexceptionable，无懈可击的、无可指责的。注意和unexceptional(普通的)不同！多了un才是「无懈可击」。

k.syn: collected, composed, disimpassioned, imperturbable
k.ant: perturbable, shakable, disturbed, nervous 紧张的
k.en: not easily upset or excited

ex.en: a unflappable general
ex.cn: 一个镇定的将军

m: unflappable → un+flap(扑腾、慌乱)+able。任何风浪都不让他扑腾——unflappable，处变不惊的、镇定自若的。flap想象鸟受惊扑腾翅膀，un+flappable=完全不扑腾。

k.syn: clumsy, awkward, blundering, maladroit
k.ant: coordinated, graceful, adroit, dexterous, lissome 协调的，灵巧的
k.en: having or showing an inability to move in a graceful manner

ex.en: ungainly movements
ex.cn: 笨拙的动作

m: ungainly → un+gain(获得优雅)+ly。获得不了优雅的状态——ungainly，笨拙的、不优雅的。「an ungainly gait」蹒跚笨拙的步态。

k.syn: unassailable, undeniable, irrefutable, indisputable, unexceptionable
k.ant: assailable, controversial, open to question 可争辩的
k.en: beyond doubt; unquestionable

ex.en: an unimpeachable evidence
ex.cn: 无可置疑的证据

m: unimpeachable → un+impeach(弹劾、质疑)+able。连弹劾都弹劾不了的，品格无可置疑——unimpeachable，无可置疑的、无懈可击的。比unexceptionable更强调道德上的不可指责。

k.syn: slovenly, slipshod, sloppy, unneat, untidy, topsy-turvy, jumbled
k.ant: bandbox, neat, orderly, organized, shipshape, tidy, trim, uncluttered, well-ordered 有序的，井井有条的
k.en: lacking in order, neatness, and often cleanliness

ex.en: unkempt hotel rooms
ex.cn: 凌乱的酒店房间

m: unkempt → un+kempt(梳理整齐，古英语comb的过去分词)。没有梳理过的状态——unkempt，凌乱的、不整洁的。「unkempt hair」乱糟糟的头发。kempt现在几乎只在un-形式里出现。

k.syn: ignorant, illiterate, uneducated, untutored
k.ant: educated, knowledgeable, erudite, literate, schooled, well-informed, well-read 博学的，受过教育的
k.en: not adept at reading and writing; deficient in the knowledge that can be acquired from books; illiterate

ex.en: unlettered sector of society
ex.cn: 未受教育的地区

m: unlettered → un+letter(字母、文字)+ed。没有文字知识的人——unlettered，文盲的、未受教育的。比illiterate更书面，强调「没有字母/文字教育」。

k.syn: demure, down-to-earth, lowly, meek, unassuming, humble, modest, unostentatious
k.ant: arrogant, bumptious, conceited, egotistic, fastuous, haughty, imperious, lordly, overweening, peremptory, pompous, presuming, presumptuous, self-asserting, supercilious, superior, toplofty, uppish, flamboyant, ostentatious 傲慢的，自负的
k.en: lacking pretension or affectation; modest

ex.en: an unpretentious virtuoso
ex.cn: 一位谦虚的大师

k.syn: artless, genuine, honest, ingenuous, innocent, naive, simple, sincere, true, unpretending
k.ant: affected, artful, artificial, assuming, dissembling, dissimulating, fake, guileful, insincere, phony 做作的，虚伪的
k.en: free from any intent to deceive or impress others

ex.en: a simple and unpretentious account about growing up in the rural South
ex.cn: 一段简单坦率的关于在南部乡村成长经历的叙述

m: unpretentious → un+pretentious(自命不凡的)。不装的、不摆架子的——unpretentious，谦逊的、朴实无华的。「an unpretentious restaurant」不摆架子的小馆子，往往才是真正好吃的地方。

k.syn: abortive, barren, fruitless, otiose, profitless, unavailing, unprofitable, unsuccessful, useless, vain
k.ant: deadly, effective, effectual, efficacious, efficient, potent, productive, profitable, successful, virtuous, telling 有效的
k.en: not productive; idle

ex.en: Her attempts to write a novel have been unproductive.
ex.cn: 她一直想写本小说的努力只是徒然。

m: unproductive → un+productive(有生产力的)。没有产出的努力，白忙一场——unproductive，徒劳的、无效的。「an unproductive meeting」毫无结果的会议，打工人最怕的。

k.syn: unreciprocated
k.ant: remunerative, gainful, lucrative, profitable 有报酬的
k.en: not reciprocated or returned in kind

ex.en: unrequited love
ex.cn: 无报答的爱

m: unrequited → un+requite(回报、报答)+ed。爱了但对方没有回应——unrequited，无回报的、单方面的。「unrequited love」单相思，文学中最常见的悲剧主题。

k.syn: indocile, indomitable, intractable, recalcitrant, uncontrollable, undisciplined, ungovernable, unmanageable, insubordinate
k.ant: amenable, biddable, compliant, conformable, docile, obedient, submissive, tractable, subdued, mild, manageable, disciplined 顺从的，守纪律的
k.en: difficult or impossible to discipline, control, or rule

ex.en: an unruly child
ex.cn: 一个不听话的孩子

m: unruly → un+rule(规则)+y。不接受任何规则约束的——unruly，难驾驭的、不守规矩的。「an unruly crowd」失控的人群。比disobedient更强调整体上的失控状态。

k.syn: assailable, controversial, open to question 可质疑的
k.ant: unassailable, undeniable, irrefutable, indisputable, unexceptionable, unimpeachable 无可指责的，无懈可击的
k.cn: 防守不住的，站不住脚的
k.en: not able to be defended

ex.en: an untenable position
ex.cn: 一个站不住脚的立场

m: untenable → un+tenable(站得住脚的)。站不住脚的，防守不住的——untenable，无法辩护的、站不住脚的。「an untenable position」无法坚守的立场，通常指论点或阵地。

k.syn: incalculable, innumerable, countless, innumerous, uncountable, uncounted
k.ant: countable, enumerable, numberable, quantifiable, calculable 可以计算的
k.en: too great or numerous to count

ex.en: untold wealth
ex.cn: 不知其数的财富

m: untold → un+told(被讲述的，tell的过去分词)。多到说不完的——untold，数不清的、无数的；也指「未曾诉说的」。「untold riches」数不尽的财富；「untold stories」未曾讲述的故事，一词两义很美。

k.syn: unpropitious, unlucky, misfortunate, unfortunate
k.ant: auspicious, propitious, favorable, fortunate 吉利的，幸运的
k.en: not favorable, unpropitious

ex.en: an untoward incident
ex.cn: 不幸的事故

k.syn: froward, headstrong, intractable, recalcitrant, refractory, unruly, wayward, willful
k.ant: controllable, governable, manageable, tractable 易管教的，顺从的
k.cn: 难以驯服的，难以驾驭的
k.en: given to resisting control or discipline by others

ex.en: a program for untoward teenagers that is designed to give them the kind of discipline that their parents were unable or unwilling to administer
ex.cn: 一个以任性、桀骜不驯青少年为对象的项目，旨在弥补家长管教不周之处

m: untoward → un+toward(朝向、顺利的方向)。不朝着好的方向走——untoward，不利的、倒霉的；不当的。「an untoward incident」不幸的事件。toward本来含「顺利」之意，un+toward=不顺。

k.syn: unaware, ignorant
k.ant: acquainted, aware, cognizant, conscious, conversant, grounded, informed, knowing, mindful 有意识的
k.en: not knowing, unaware

ex.en: Please forgive my unwitting interruption of your conversation.
ex.cn: 请原谅我无意间打断了你们的谈话。

k.syn: casual, chance, fluky, fortuitous, inadvertent, incidental, unintentional
k.ant: calculated, deliberate, intended, intentional, planned, premeditated, prepense 故意的，蓄意的
k.en: happening by chance; not intended

ex.en: an unwitting mistake made in copying the material
ex.cn: 在打印过程中一个无心的过失

m: unwitting → un+wit(知道、理解)+ting。完全不知道自己在做什么——unwitting，不知情的、无意识的。「an unwitting accomplice」不知情的帮凶。wit在英语里是「智慧、知道」，unwitting=没有意识到。

k.syn: uncommon, unordinary, unusual
k.ant: wonted, usual, routine, habitual, accustomed, customary 平常的
k.en: not habitual or ordinary; unusual

ex.en: He's honored for the unwonted courage he showed in battle.
ex.cn: 他因在战争中表现出的过人的勇气而受到嘉奖。

m: unwonted → un+wont(习惯，wont是古英语「习惯于」)+ed。不寻常的、不习惯的——unwonted，不寻常的、罕见的。「with unwonted enthusiasm」以异乎寻常的热情。wont现在日常少用，但unwonted是GRE高频词。

k.syn: baste, scold, revile, berate, vituperate
k.ant: laud, extol, glorify, hymn, magnify, panegyrize, flatter, fawn, cringe, adulterate 赞扬
k.en: to reproach severely

ex.en: upbraid sb with his ingratitude
ex.cn: 责备某人忘恩负义

m: upbraid → up(向上举)+braid(辫子/鞭子)。举起鞭子痛打，引申为严厉斥责——upbraid，严厉谴责、责骂。画面：被人拎起来骂。比criticize严厉得多，情绪激动。

k.syn: support, bolster, advocate, champion, endorse, espouse
k.en: to give support to

ex.en: He determined to uphold her views in the face of all challenges.
ex.cn: 他下定决心支持她的观点，尽管全世界都反对她。

m: uphold → up(向上)+hold(托住)。从下面托住、支撑——uphold，支持、维护、捍卫。「uphold the law」维护法律，「uphold a decision」维持裁决。

k.syn: debonair, civilized, genteel, cultivated, cultured, polished, refined, well-bred, courteous, gracious
k.ant: boorish, churlish, classless, clownish, loutish, uncouth 粗鲁的
k.en: notably polite or finished in manner

ex.en: a gentlemanly and urbane host of elegant dinner parties
ex.cn: 优雅晚宴的主人颇有绅士风度，彬彬有礼

m: urbane → urban(城市的)+e。城市里出来的人，举止优雅有礼——urbane，彬彬有礼的、温文尔雅的。urban是城市，城市人接受过熏陶，urbane是其优雅气质。

k.syn: corsair, freebooter, rover
k.en: a robber on the high seas

ex.en: little boys dreaming of sailing as pirates
ex.cn: 梦想成为海盗的小男孩

k.syn: arrogate, commandeer, convert, expropriate, pirate, preempt, press, seize, take over
k.ant: abdicate退位；crown 授予职位
k.en: to seize and hold（the power or rights of another, for example）by force and without legal authority

ex.en: usurp the throne
ex.cn: 篡夺王位

m: usurp → 谐音「用嗖抢」。皇帝还没死，野心家「嗖」一下抢过权杖——usurp，篡夺、强占（权位）。「usurp the throne」篡位，历史剧必备词汇。

k.en: interest =gouging: price 高利贷追求极高的利息=敲竹杠追求极高的价格 （追求关系）

m: usury → use(使用)+ury。借钱给你「用」，收取天价使用费——usury，高利贷、高利盘剥。「practice usury」放高利贷，历史上各文明都明令禁止的行为。

k.syn: articulate, bring out, enunciate, verbalize, vocalize
k.en: to articulate（words）; pronounce or speak

ex.en: She tried not to utter a sound as the doctor gave her a flu shot.
ex.cn: 她克制自己不在医生打针的时候喊出来。

k.syn: entire, total, stark, sheer, unqualified
k.ant: partial 部分的
k.en: complete; absolute; entire

ex.en: be at an utter loss what to do
ex.cn: 完全不知道怎样才好

m: utter → 双重含义助记：①utter作动词=发出声音，想象把话「吐」出来（out+ter→utter）；②utter作形容词=完全的、彻底的。「utter silence」完全的沉默，「utter nonsense」彻头彻尾的废话。记住两个用法，GRE都考。

k.syn: immunize
k.en: to inoculate with a vaccine in order to produce immunity to an infectious disease, such as diphtheria or typhus

ex.en: vaccinate sb for smallpox
ex.cn: 为某人接种牛痘疫苗以防天花

m: vaccinate → 发音近「喂死猫你」？不，近「哇可死内」。拆词更好：vaccine（疫苗）是核心，加上-ate（动词后缀）。想象医生拿着vaccine疫苗针筒对你说「张开手臂，我要vaccinate你！」小孩哇哇大哭但打完就有了immunity免疫力。

k.syn: hesitate, dither, waver, teeter, falter
k.ant: resolute, decide, dive in, plunge in 决定
k.en: to waver in mind, will, or feeling; hesitate in choice of opinions or courses

ex.en: Parents vacillate between saying no and yes, but actually neither response seems satisfactory to their children.
ex.cn: 父母总是犹豫不定，不知道是该同意还是该反对，但是事实上哪种答案对他们的孩子都不理想。

k.syn: oscillate, fluctuate
k.ant: equipoise平衡；静止
k.en: to sway from one side to the other; oscillate

m: vacillate → 发音「威西了特」，谐音「喂，洗了特」。想象一个人站在澡盆边犹豫：「喂，洗了特烫啊！」就这样反复摇摆、迟疑不决，迟迟不肯跳进去。vacillate = 犹豫不决、摇摆不定。

k.syn: airhead, brainless, dull, dumb, fatuous, foolish, inane, moronic, obtuse, simple, stupid, witless
k.ant: apt, bright, brilliant, clever, intelligent, keen, sharp, smart 聪明的
k.der: vacuity n. 愚蠢；空虚，空白
k.en: marked by lack of ideas or intelligence

ex.en: a movie that was derided for its vacuous dialogue
ex.cn: 一部因为对白十分愚蠢而被嘲讽的电影

m: vacuous → 拆词：vacuum（真空）去掉m加ous。脑袋里像真空吸尘器（vacuum cleaner）一样，一片空白、什么也没有。旁边的人问他问题，他只会用vacuous（空洞的）的眼神望着你，愚蠢得像个空罐子。

k.syn: caprice, fancy, freak, humor, vagrancy, whimsy
k.cn: 不可预测的思想或行为
k.en: an erratic, unpredictable, or extravagant manifestation, action, or notion

ex.en: Recently he had been prone to strange vagaries.
ex.cn: 最近他逐渐变得行为古怪起来。

m: vagary → 发音「威噶瑞」，谐音「喂，搁哪儿？」一个老太太把东西随便乱放，每次都找不到，被问到「喂，搁哪儿啦？」她自己也不知道——这种反复无常、捉摸不定的怪行为，就是vagary（奇思怪想、无法预测的行为）。

k.syn: ambiguous, enigmatic, equivocal, indefinite, inexplicit, unclear
k.ant: clear, definite, express, explicit, specific 清楚明确的
k.en: not clearly expressed

ex.en: The diplomat gave as vague a reply as he could at the press conference.
ex.cn: 外交官在记者招待会上尽其所能来含糊其辞。

k.syn: dim, faint, foggy, hazy, indistinct, indistinguishable, misty, murky, nebulous, obscure, opaque
k.ant: pellucid 清澈透明的
k.en: lacking definite shape, form, or character

ex.en: the vague outline of a building through the dense fog
ex.cn: 浓雾中建筑物模糊的轮廓

m: vague → 发音「威哥」，谐音「喂，哥！」你在大雾天里远远看见一个模糊身影，喊「喂，哥！」但看不清楚是不是你哥——一切都是vague（模糊的、不清楚的）。

k.syn: farewell, goodbye
k.ant: greeting 欢迎；debut 初次登台，首次亮相
k.en: an act of bidding farewell
an address or statement of farewell or leave- taking

ex.en: a valediction given by the college president upon his retirement
ex.cn: 大学校长退休时的告别演说

m: valediction → 拆词：vale（再见，许多语言通用词）+ diction（说话、措辞）。告别时的说辞 = valediction（告别辞）。毕业典礼上成绩最好的学生做valedictory speech（毕业告别演说），即此词的来源。

k.syn: audacious, bold, brave, courageous, dauntless, fearless, gallant, intrepid, stalwart, stout, valorous
k.ant: cowardly, craven, gutless, nerveless, pusillanimous, spineless, timorous 胆怯的
k.en: possessing or acting with bravery or boldness

ex.en: a valiant and moral knight
ex.cn: 一个勇敢和有道德感的骑士

ex.en: Despite their valiant efforts, they lost the game.
ex.cn: 尽管他们英勇奋战，但还是输掉了比赛。

m: valiant → 发音「威利恩特」，谐音「威利安特」。想象一个叫威利的人，安特（ant蚂蚁般渺小）却无比勇敢，独自冲入火场救人——小个子大勇气，valiant（勇敢的、英勇的）。

k.syn: analytic, coherent, consequent, rational, reasonable, sensible, sound, well-founded, well-grounded
k.ant: groundless, illegitimate, illogical, incoherent, inconsequent, invalid, irrational, unreasonable, unsound（逻辑上）站不住脚的
k.en: logically correct

ex.en: Only further experiments will show whether your theory is valid.
ex.cn: 只有进一步的实验才能验证你的理论是否正确。

ex.en: Your argument isn't valid because you're taking what should be the conclusion and using it as a premise.
ex.cn: 你的论述逻辑上是错误的，因为你把本该是结论的东西当成了前提条件。

m: valid → 拆词：val + id。val谐音「好」（粤语「掂」的感觉），id是身份证。拿出有效的id（身份证），这张证件是valid（有效的、有根据的）的，逻辑上站得住脚。

k.syn: conquer, dominate, overpower, pacify, subdue, subject, subjugate, subordinate
k.ant: capitulate, surrender, yield 投降，屈服
k.en: to defeat in a conflict or contest

ex.en: He vanquished his inner fear.
ex.cn: 他战胜了内心的恐惧。

m: vanquish → 发音「万克威序」，谐音「万军威势」。万军的威势压来，敌人被彻底vanquish（打败、征服）。记住：van（先锋）+ quish，先锋部队以绝对优势击溃对手。

k.syn: driveling, dull, flat, inane, insipid, jejune, sapless, tasteless
k.ant: absorbing, arresting, engaging, engrossing, enthralling, fascinating, immersing, intriguing, riveting 吸引人的
k.en: lacking liveliness, animation, or interest

ex.en: a song with vapid lyrics
ex.cn: 一首歌词乏味的歌曲

m: vapid → 发音「威屁得」，谐音「喂，屁的」。朋友讲了个笑话，你回应「喂，屁的！」——完全无趣、毫无意义，就是vapid（无聊的、乏味的）。像蒸汽（vapor）一样，只有气没有味道。

k.syn: gasify
k.ant: condense（气体）液化
k.en: to convert（as by the application of heat or by spraying）into vapor

k.syn: annihilate, decimate, demolish, devastate, extinguish, nuke, pulverize, raze, ruin, smash, wreck
k.ant: build, construct, erect, establish, raise, rear 建造
k.der: vaporous adj. 模糊的，空洞的
k.en: to destroy by or as if by converting into vapor

ex.en: The entire fleet was vaporized.
ex.cn: 整个舰队都被消灭了。

m: vaporize → vapor（蒸汽）大家都认识，加上-ize（使动后缀）。用强热把液体vaporize（使蒸发）成vapor。想象把水倒进滚烫的铁锅，「滋——」一声，瞬间vaporize，化为水蒸气消失无踪。

k.syn: conflict, disaccord, discordance, disharmony, dissent, division, friction, schism, variation, war
k.ant: accord, agreement, concord, concordance, harmony, peace 和谐，一致
k.der: variant adj. 有差异的，不同的
k.en: a lack of agreement or harmony

ex.en: Persistent variance within the band eventually caused it to break up.
ex.cn: 乐团中持续不断的分歧最终导致了它的解散。

m: variance → vary（变化）大家都认识，variance是其名词形式。两组数据之间有variance（差异、变量）。统计学中，数据离均值的偏差叫variance（方差）——数据「vary来vary去」产生的偏差值。
variegation → varied（多变的）大家认识，加-gation。像一块布料，颜色varied（多变）、斑斑驳驳，这种杂色效果叫variegation（杂色、斑驳）。热带鱼身上的彩色花纹就是natural variegation（天然杂色）。

k.syn: adorn, bedeck, bedizen, decorate, embellish, garnish, ornament
k.en: to give a smooth and glossy finish to

ex.en: Don't sit on that chair- I've just varnished it.
ex.cn: 不要坐在那张椅子上 ——我刚刚上过漆。

k.syn: blanch, extenuate, gloss, palliate, sugarcoat, veneer
k.ant: bare, disclose, discover, divulge, expose, reveal, uncloak, uncover, unmask, unveil 揭露
k.en: to cover or conceal（as something unpleasant）with something that gives an attractive appearance

ex.en: She tried to varnish her mistakes.
ex.cn: 她试图掩盖她的失误。

m: varnish → 发音「瓦尼序」，谐音「瓦你洗」。木头地板「把你（表面）洗干净」再刷上光亮的varnish（清漆），使表面光滑有光泽。varnish既是名词（清漆）也是动词（上清漆、使有光泽）。

k.syn: bound, hop, jump, leap, spring
k.en: to propel oneself upward or forward into the air

ex.en: The horse vaulted over the obstacle with ease.
ex.cn: 骏马轻松地跃过障碍物。

k.en: to accomplish something as if by leaping suddenly or vigorously

ex.en: She vaulted into a position of wealth.
ex.cn: 她一夜暴富。

m: vault → 发音「沃特」，谐音「我的」。银行保险库里全是「我的」宝贝，金库（vault）门厚重无比。vault另一个意思是「跳跃越过」——撑杆跳（pole vault），运动员跳过横杆就像跳进保险库一样需要爆发力。

k.syn: boast, brag, brandish, swagger
k.ant: belittle, deprecate, diminish, minimize, underrate, undervalue 轻视，瞧不起
k.en: to speak boastfully

ex.en: He always vaunted his country's military might.
ex.cn: 他总是吹嘘他的国家的军事实力。

m: vaunt → 发音「翁特」，近「旺特」。旺特（特别旺）的人喜欢vaunt（吹嘘）自己有多旺。更好记法：vaunt ≈ vain（虚荣）的行为，虚荣的人总要vaunt自己的成就，说个没完。

k.syn: avert, deviate, deflect, divert, redirect, swerve, swing, wheel
k.ant: straighten（使）直行
k.en: to change direction or course

ex.en: He veered the car abruptly to the right to avoid a collision.
ex.cn: 他向右猛打方向盘以避免撞击。

m: veer → 发音「维尔」，谐音「拐儿」（方言「拐弯」）。赛车手突然「拐儿」——急打方向盘veer（急转向）避开障碍物。车轮痕迹在地面划出一道弯，那就是veer（突然转向、改变方向）的轨迹。

k.syn: ardent, demonstrative, emotional, fervid, impassioned, intense, passionate, perfervid, torrid
k.ant: cold, cool, dispassionate, emotionless, impassive, unemotional 冷淡的
k.en: having or expressing great depth of feeling

ex.en: a vehement defender of the rights of minorities
ex.cn: 一个感情强烈的少数民族维权者

m: vehement → 发音「威哈门特」，谐音「威，喝门特！」想象一个人激动地拍桌子，威风凛凛地大喊「喝！」情绪极为强烈——vehement（强烈的、激烈的、热情洋溢的）。他的vehement protest（激烈抗议）震动全场。

k.syn: bribable, corruptible, dirty, purchasable
k.ant: incorruptible 不受贿赂的；scrupulous 正直的
k.en: open to corrupt influence and especially bribery

ex.en: a judge who is known for being venal and easily bought
ex.cn: 一名以贪污受贿、容易被收买闻名的法官

m: venal → 发音「维纳尔」，谐音「为哪儿」。贪官被抓后还在问「为哪儿出了问题？」——整个人venal（贪腐的、受贿的），为了钱什么都干。注意别混淆venal（贪腐）和venial（可宽恕）。

k.syn: broker, dealer, merchandiser, retailer, seller, trader
k.ant: buyer, consumer, purchaser 消费者
k.en: one that sells or vends

ex.en: We're thinking of making a deal with that other software vendor.
ex.cn: 我们在考虑和那个软件供应商做交易。

m: vendor → vend（出售）大家可能认识，加-or（人）= 卖东西的人。vendor（小贩、供应商）。街头小vendor摆摊卖东西，现代电商平台的seller也叫vendor。想象一排街头vendor摆摊吆喝的热闹场景。

k.syn: cover, façade, mask
k.ant: center, core, kernel 核心
k.cn: 薄木板，用以贴在家具外面的饰面板
k.en: a thin surface layer, as of finely grained wood, glued to a base of inferior material

k.syn: blanch, extenuate, gloss, palliate, sugarcoat, varnish
k.ant: bare, disclose, discover, divulge, expose, reveal, uncloak, uncover, unmask, unveil 揭露
k.en: to cover over with a veneer; especially to conceal（as a defect of character）under a superficial and deceptive attractiveness

m: veneer → 发音「维尼尔」，谐音「贴你额」。木匠把一层薄薄的优质木皮「贴你额」（贴上去）——veneer（镶饰面板、贴面）。引申义：用表面文明遮盖内在粗鄙，a veneer of politeness（礼貌的表面）。

k.syn: adore, deify, esteem, regard, respect, revere
k.ant: despise, disdain, disregard, flout, scorn 轻视，蔑视
k.en: to regard with reverential respect or with admiring deference

ex.en: She is venerated by the public as a saint.
ex.cn: 她被当作圣人，受万众景仰。

m: venerate → 发音「文呢瑞特」，谐音「文呢，锐！」。想象古代学者对文圣孔子「文！呢，锐！」般虔诚崇拜——venerate（尊敬、崇拜）长者和圣贤。ven + erate，以极高敬意对待，如供奉神像般尊崇。

k.syn: condonable, excusable, forgivable, pardonable, remissible, remittable
k.ant: heinous, indefensible, inexcusable, mortal, unforgivable, unpardonable 罪大恶极的，不可饶恕的
k.en: easily excused or forgiven

ex.en: Taking the restaurant's menu as a souvenir seems like a venial offense.
ex.cn: 把餐厅的菜单当成纪念品带走似乎是一个可以宽恕的罪行。

m: venial → 发音「维尼尔」，谐音「维尼熊」。小熊维尼偷蜂蜜，虽然不对但谁也不怪他——维尼熊的错误是venial（可宽恕的、轻微可原谅的）小过失。与venal（贪腐）不同：venal是大罪，venial是小错。

k.syn: toxic, toxin
k.ant: antidote 解药
k.en: poisonous matter normally secreted by some animals as snakes, scorpions, or bees

ex.en: an antidote to snake venom
ex.cn: 蛇毒的解药

k.syn: despite, malevolence, maliciousness, malignance, malignity, meanness, nastiness, spite, viciousness
k.ant: comity, friendship, goodwill 友善 “信仰、勇敢的心和自我控制是最强者的本能。” （刘栩志，录取院校：Harvard Kennedy School）
k.en: the desire to cause pain for the satisfaction of doing harm

ex.en: She spoke of him with venom in her voice.
ex.cn: 她对他的评论怀揣着恶意。

m: venom → 发音「维纳姆」，谐音「喂，难姆」（难你的母）。毒蛇喷出venom（毒液）时发出「嘶——」声，像在「喂，难姆！」地诅咒猎物。venom也指言语上的「毒」：his words were full of venom（他的话充满毒意）。

k.syn: honest, truthful
k.ant: dishonest, lying, mendacious, prevaricating, untruthful 说谎的，不诚实的
k.en: being in the habit of telling the truth

ex.en: a veracious witness
ex.cn: 诚实的证人

ex.en: He has a reputation for being veracious, so people generally take his word for things.
ex.cn: 他诚实的名声让其他人通常都相信他说的话。

k.syn: accurate, exact, precise, proper, right, true
k.ant: false, improper, inaccurate, incorrect, inexact, untrue, wrong 不准确的
k.en: precise, accurate

ex.en: a novel that presents a fairly veracious and unvarnished picture of the lives of affluent suburbanites
ex.cn: 一部准确而不加修饰地描述郊区富人生活的小说

m: veracious → vera + cious。vera谐音「维拉」，这个叫维拉的人非常veracious（诚实的、真实的），说的话句句属实。vera在拉丁语是「真实」，学习者可以联系vera（真）+ acious（充满）= 充满真实 = 诚实。

k.syn: ad verbum, directly, exactly, word for word
k.ant: inaccurately, inexactly 不准确地
k.en: in the exact words

ex.en: You can't just copy the encyclopedia article verbatim for your report - that's plagiarism.
ex.cn: 你不能一字不差地抄百科全书上的内容——这是剽窃。

m: verbatim → verb（动词）大家认识，atim谐音「阿提姆」。想象一台「阿提姆」录音机，把每一个verb（词）都一字不差地录下来——verbatim（逐字地、一字不差地）。法庭速记员要verbatim记录所有证词。

k.syn: circuitous, circumlocutory, diffuse, garrulous, long-winded, prolix, rambling, verbose, windy
k.ant: brief, compact, concise, pithy, succinct, terse 简洁的
k.en: containing more words than necessary

ex.en: a verbose orator
ex.cn: 啰嗦的演讲者

ex.en: She has a verbose writing style.
ex.cn: 她的文风很啰嗦。

m: verbose → verb（说话、语言）+ ose（充满）。充满words的演讲 = verbose（冗长的、啰嗦的）。想象一个人开口说话就像打开了水龙头，哗哗不停，废话连篇——verbose的人写报告永远超字数，说话永远不到点。

k.syn: green, grown, leafy, luxuriant, overgrown
k.ant: barren, impoverished, infertile, leafless, sterile 贫瘠的
k.cn: （因长满植物而）翠绿的，郁郁葱葱的
k.en: green with vegetation; covered with green growth

ex.en: verdant fields
ex.cn: 翠绿的田野

m: ver看起来像very——verdant = very green，非常绿——verdant，郁郁葱葱的。

k.syn: attest, authenticate, certify, corroborate, substantiate, support, validate, vindicate
k.ant: disprove, rebut, refute 反驳，驳斥
k.der: verified adj. 经证实的；verification n. 证实
k.en: to determine or test the truth or accuracy of, as by comparison, investigation, or reference

ex.en: We need to verify your passport.
ex.cn: 我们需要校验阁下的护照。

m: verify → very（非常）+ ify。「非常」确认一件事，才能verify（证实、核实）它的真实性。记者要verify所有消息来源，科学家要verify实验数据——「非常认真地确认」，就是verify。

k.syn: likely, plausible, probable
k.ant: implausible, increzdible, unbelievable 难以置信的
k.en: appearing to be true or real

ex.en: a verisimilar tale
ex.cn: 一个似乎为真的传奇

m: verisimilar → veri（真实）+ similar（相似，大家认识！）。「和真实的相似」= verisimilar（好像真实的、逼真的）。这部小说写得verisimilar，读起来像真实发生的事。similar是核心记忆锚点。

k.syn: authentic, credible, genuine, real, true, unquestionable, bona fide
k.ant: bogus, counterfeit, fake, false, mock, phony, pseudo, sham, spurious 伪造的，虚假的
k.en: being in fact the thing named and not false, unreal, or imaginary

ex.en: a veritable manuscript
ex.cn: 一份原稿

m: veritable → verify（证实）的形容词兄弟：veri（真）+ table（可以的）。「可以证实的真实」= veritable（真正的、确实的）。a veritable genius（真正的天才）。用于强调「这是货真价实、不假的」。

k.syn: argot, cant, dialect, jargon, lingo, slang
k.en: a nonstandard language or dialect of a place, region, or country

ex.en: phrases that occur in the common vernacular
ex.cn: 常见方言中的词组

k.syn: conversational, informal, nonliterary, vulgar
k.ant: bookish, formal, learned, literary 书面的，正式的
k.en: used in or suitable for speech and not formal writing

ex.en: write essays in a very easy-to-read, vernacular style
ex.cn: 用一种易懂的口头化风格写作

m: vernacular → 发音「沃纳克拉」，谐音「我哪块啦」（我是哪块的人啦）。各地人说话都有自己「我哪块的」口音——vernacular（方言、本地话）。每个地方的vernacular都不同，反映当地文化与习俗。

k.syn: adaptable, all-around, ambidextrous, protean, universal
k.ant: limited 受限制的，有限的
k.en: able to do many different kinds of things

ex.en: We have found a versatile baseball player who can play any position.
ex.cn: 我们发现了一个能打任何位置的全能型球员。

k.syn: capricious, changeable, fluid, inconstant, mercurial, skittish, temperamental, variable, volatile
k.ant: certain, changeless, constant, immutable, invariable, settled, stable, stationary, steady, unchangeable, unvarying 固定的，不变的
k.en: changing or fluctuating readily

ex.en: a versatile disposition
ex.cn: 多变的性格

m: versatile → verse（诗歌、转变）+ tile（瓷砖）。瓷砖可以用在地板、墙壁、厨房、浴室——versatile（多用途的、多才多艺的）。一个versatile演员能演喜剧也能演悲剧，能歌能舞，像多功能瓷砖一样百搭。

k.syn: acquaint, familiarize, inform
k.ant: misinform, mislead 误导
k.en: to familiarize by close association, study, or experience

ex.en: well versed in the theater
ex.cn: 精通剧院

ex.en: While in prison, he versed himself in the rights of the incarcerated.
ex.cn: 当他身处监狱时，他通过自学熟悉了囚犯所享有的权利。

k.syn: ballad, lyric, poetry, song
k.ant: prose 散文
k.en: a composition using rhythm and often rhyme to create a lyrical effect

ex.en: compose a short verse for his father's birthday
ex.cn: 为他父亲的生日创作了一首小诗

m: verse → 这里是「使精通」之意，不是「诗歌」。verse sb in sth = 让某人对某事精通。想象老师把学生「押进诗里」反复verse（吟诵），读多了自然就verse（精通）了。well-versed in（精通于）是常用搭配。

k.syn: dizziness, giddiness
k.ant: sobriety 清醒
k.en: a dizzy confused state of mind

ex.en: He had a dreadful attack of vertigo at the top of the tower.
ex.cn: 他站在塔顶，感到一阵令人窒息的眩晕。

m: vertigo → 发音「沃尔提勾」，谐音「我提勾」。站在高楼边缘，「我提着（勾着）」栏杆，头晕目眩——vertigo（眩晕症）。vert（转）+ igo，头不停地转，天旋地转，就是vertigo的感觉。高空、旋转都能引发vertigo。

k.syn: animation, dynamism, energy, exuberance, liveliness, robustness, vibrancy, vigorousness, vim, vitality
k.ant: lethargy, listlessness, sluggishness, torpidity 精神不振
k.en: vitality, liveliness

ex.en: The instrumentalists played with skill and verve.
ex.cn: 器乐表演者们有着高超的技巧与饱满的热情。

m: verve → 发音「沃夫」，谐音「活夫」（有活力的人）。一个「活夫」（充满活力的男人）跳上舞台，带着满满的verve（活力、热情、精神）表演，全场沸腾。短小精悍的词，满满的能量。

k.syn: artery, vein
k.en: a tube or canal（as an artery）in which a body fluid is contained and conveyed or circulated

ex.en: blood vessel
ex.cn: 血管

k.syn: boat, cruiser, destroyer, ferry, ship, watercraft
k.en: a watercraft bigger than a rowboat

ex.en: the largest military vessel afloat
ex.cn: 最大的军用舰艇

m: vessel → vessel既是「船」也是「脉管/血管」。联系：船在河道里运输货物，血管（vessel）在体内运输血液——都是「运输通道」。记住vessel = 船 = 血管，两个意思都是「中空的运输容器」。

k.syn: echo, ghost, relic, remain, remnant, residual, shadow, trace
k.der: vestigial adj. 残留的；退化的
k.en: the smallest quantity or trace

ex.en: A few strange words carved on a stone were the only vestige of the lost civilization.
ex.cn: 石碑上刻着的几个奇怪的字符是这个失落文明的唯一遗迹。

ex.en: the fossilized vestige of a dinosaur that traversed that muddy landscape millions of years ago
ex.cn: 数百万年横跨那块泥泞土壤的恐龙的化石碎片

m: vest(背心) + age → 一件穿了几十年的旧背心，只剩一点痕迹——vestige，残迹。

k.syn: incomplete, rudimentary, undeveloped
k.ant: adult, full-blown, full-fledged, matured, ripe, ripened 成熟的，发育完全的
k.en: （of certain organs or parts of organisms）having attained a simple structure and reduced size and function during the evolution of the species

ex.en: snake that has vestigial limbs
ex.cn: 拥有退化四肢的蛇

m: vestigial → vestige（遗迹）+ ial = vestigial（残留的、发育不全的）。人类的尾骨是vestigial structure（退化结构），远古时期有用，现在只剩vestigial的痕迹。阑尾也是vestigial organ（退化器官）。

k.syn: doyen, expert, master, maven, warhorse
k.ant: beginner, colt, fledgling, freshman, greenhorn, neophyte, novice, recruit, rookie, tenderfoot, tyro 新手
k.en: an old soldier; one having knowledge or ability gained through long experience

ex.en: As a veteran of overseas travel, she offered us solid advice about planning our trip.
ex.cn: 作为一名资深的海外旅行家，她给我们提供了许多关于旅行规划的中肯建议。

k.syn: accomplished, adept, consummate, experienced, masterful, professed, skilled, versed, virtuoso
k.ant: amateur, inexperienced, inexpert, unprofessional, unseasoned, unskilled, unskillful 业余的，经验不足的
k.en: having or showing exceptional knowledge, experience, or skill in a field of endeavor

ex.en: She is a veteran teacher who can mentor new teachers.
ex.cn: 她是一位资深教师，可以教新教师。

m: veteran → vet（老手、兽医）大家认识，加eran。vet + eran，老vet（退役军人、老手）经历过无数eran（era时代）的战争洗礼——veteran（老兵、老手、经验丰富的人）。a veteran journalist（资深记者）。

k.syn: blackball, decline, disallow, disapprove, kill, negative, refuse, reject
k.ant: accredit, approbate, authorize, clear, confirm, finalize, formalize, ratify, sanction, warrant 批准
k.en: to forbid or prohibit authoritatively

ex.en: The President vetoed the bill.
ex.cn: 总统否决了提案。

ex.en: We wanted to do a cross-country trip, but our parents vetoed it.
ex.cn: 我们想来一次跨国旅行，但是父母不肯批准。

m: veto → 发音「维托」，谐音「喂，托！」（喂，用力推开！）。在联合国安理会，一国代表拍桌子说「喂，托！我反对！」——行使veto（否决权）。veto是拉丁语「我禁止」，五大常任理事国各有一票veto权。

k.syn: aggravate, annoy, bother, exasperate, gall, grate, irk, nettle, peeve, rile
k.ant: appease, assuage, conciliate, mollify, placate, propitiate 平息
k.en: to bring trouble, distress, or agitation to

ex.en: She was vexed by her son's failure to clean his room.
ex.cn: 她因为儿子不清理房间而恼怒。

m: vex → 发音「威克斯」，谐音「喂，克死」（把我克死了）。这道数学题把我「克死」了，vex（使烦恼、使恼怒）得我抓狂。短小的词，记住：vex = 反复折磨你的小烦恼，像卡在喉咙里的鱼刺。

k.syn: achievable, attainable, doable, feasible, practicable, realizable, workable
k.ant: hopeless, impossible, impracticable, infeasible, unattainable, unviable, unworkable 不可行的
k.en: capable of being done or carried out

ex.en: a viable solution to the problem
ex.cn: 解决问题的一个可行方案

m: viable → via（通过、路）大家认识，加ble。「有路可走的」= viable（可行的、能存活的）。一个商业计划viable（可行）= 有可以走通的路。a viable solution（可行的解决方案）；a viable fetus（能存活的胎儿）。

k.syn: indirect, substitute, surrogate
k.ant: firsthand 直接的
k.en: performed or suffered by one person as a substitute for another or to the benefit or advantage of another

ex.en: use Internet as a vicarious form of social life
ex.cn: 把互联网当作一种虚拟的社交生活

m: vicarious → vicar（代理人、副牧师）+ ious。vicar是替主教代理教务的人——vicarious（代理的、替代性的）。vicarious pleasure（替代性的快乐）：自己不敢蹦极，看别人蹦极时感受到的那种兴奋，就是vicarious enjoyment。

k.syn: atrocious, barbaric, barbarous, brutal, butcherly, fiendish, heartless, inhumane, sadistic, savage, truculent
k.ant: benign, benignant, compassionate, humane, kind, kindhearted, merciful, sympathetic 仁慈的
k.en: having or showing the desire to inflict severe pain and suffering on others

ex.en: vicious slander
ex.cn: 恶意诽谤

k.syn: dreadful, excruciating, explosive, fearsome, ferocious, fierce, intensive, profound, vehement, violent
k.ant: light, moderate, soft 轻柔的
k.en: marked by violence or ferocity

ex.en: a vicious storm ripped through the region
ex.cn: 在境内肆虐的强烈风暴

m: vicious → vice（恶习、缺陷）大家认识，加ious。充满vice（恶）的人 = vicious（恶意的、凶残的）。a vicious dog（凶猛的狗）；a vicious cycle（恶性循环，注意：不是viscous！）。

k.syn: fluctuation, mutation, shift, variation
k.ant: uniformity 一致性，无差异
k.en: natural change or mutation visible in nature or in human affairs

ex.en: vicissitude of daily life
ex.cn: 日常生活的跌宕起伏

m: vicissitude → 发音「维西斯提尤得」。拆词联想：vice（恶）+ assist（帮助）+ ude，人生中好与坏相互交织助推——vicissitude（自然的变化、世事无常）。the vicissitudes of life（人生的起伏变迁），这是GRE高频词。

k.syn: cheat, cozen, deceive, defraud, dupe, fool, hoax, swindle
k.ant: disabuse, disenchant, disillusion, undeceive 使醒悟
k.en: to subject to deception or fraud

ex.en: victimized by a confidence man with a slick story
ex.cn: 被一个骗子用圆滑的故事欺骗

m: victimize → victim（受害者）大家认识，加-ize（使动）。把某人变成victim（受害者）= victimize（使受骗、欺骗、迫害）。骗子专门victimize老人，使他们成为诈骗的victims。动作主动、手段恶意是这词的关键。

k.syn: alert, attentive, awake, cautious, observant, sharp, watchful
k.ant: careless, heedless, inattentive, unmindful, unthinking, unwary 不谨慎的
k.en: alertly watchful especially to avoid danger

ex.en: Police warned the public to be vigilant and report anything suspicious.
ex.cn: 警方要求公众保持警惕，并上报任何有嫌疑的人或事。

ex.en: When traveling through the city, tourists should be extra vigilant.
ex.cn: 在这个城市旅行的游客应该保持格外的警惕。

m: vigilant → vigil（守夜、熬夜守候）+ ant。守夜的士兵（vigil + ant）保持高度警觉——vigilant（警惕的、警醒的）。ant（蚂蚁）也一直警觉地警戒，双重记忆。stay vigilant（保持警惕）是安全提醒常用语。

k.syn: definition, delineation, depiction, picture, portrait, portraiture, portrayal, rendering, sketch
k.en: a vivid representation in words of someone or something

ex.en: The general's memoirs are filled with revealing vignettes of some of the war's most compelling personalities.
ex.cn: 将军的回忆录里充满了短小的文章，它们揭露了战争中最引人注意的人物。

m: vignette → 发音「维涅特」，谐音「维尼特」。小熊维尼（Winnie）的故事里每一段小冒险，都是一个vignette（小插曲、小场景、简短的描述）。源自法语「小葡萄藤」，原指书页边的小装饰图案，后指简短的文字场景。

k.syn: brisk, dynamic, energetic, lively, robust, spirited, vital
k.ant: dull, lethargic, listless, sluggish, torpid 没有精神的
k.en: having active strength of body or mind

ex.en: He remains vigorous despite being over 80 years old.
ex.cn: 尽管他已年过 80，但仍活力不减。

k.syn: hard, hardened, inured, rugged, stout, strong, sturdy, tough, toughened
k.ant: delicate, soft, tender, weak 脆弱的
k.en: able to withstand hardship, strain, or exposure

ex.en: vigorous and sturdy little sheep bred to live in mountainous regions
ex.cn: 在山区喂养的健壮的小羊

m: vigorous → vigor（活力、精力）大家认识，加-ous。充满vigor = vigorous（精力旺盛的、强健的）。a vigorous exercise（剧烈运动）；vigorous debate（激烈辩论）。vigor是名词，vigorous是形容词，一对好兄弟。

k.syn: asperse, blacken, calumniate, defame, libel, malign, smear, traduce
k.ant: acclaim, applaud, commend, praise 表扬
k.en: to utter slanderous and abusive statements against

ex.en: be vilified by the press because of her radical views
ex.cn: 因为她激进的观点而被媒体抨击

m: vilify → vile（卑劣的、恶心的）大家可能认识，加-fy（使）。把人描述得vile（卑劣）= vilify（诽谤、辱骂）。政客互相vilify对手，媒体vilify罪犯。注意：vilify是用语言攻击，比slander更强调持续性的口头侮辱。

k.syn: animation, bounce, dynamism, energy, liveliness, robustness, verve, vibrancy, vigorousness, vitality
k.ant: lethargy, listlessness, sluggishness, torpidity 精神不振
k.en: robust energy and enthusiasm

ex.en: A little rest should give me back some of my vim.
ex.cn: 稍事休息我应该就能回复些许精力。

m: vim → 发音「威姆」，谐音「威猛」。一个人「威猛」无比，充满vim（活力、精力、干劲）。短词记短义：vim = 爆发性的活力和热情。full of vim and vigor（精力充沛）是固定搭配，两个词意思相近，叠加强调。

k.syn: absolve, acquit, exculpate, exonerate
k.ant: incriminate 使有罪
k.en: to free from allegation or blame

ex.en: The evidence would completely vindicate him.
ex.cn: 这个证据将彻底证明他的清白。

k.syn: attest, authenticate, certify, corroborate, substantiate, support, validate, verify
k.ant: disprove, rebut, refute 反驳，驳斥
k.en: to give evidence or testimony to the truth or factualness of

ex.en: Recent discoveries have generally vindicated the physicist's theories.

m: 为...平反(vindicate)！vind(复仇)+icate，让正义来复仇，洗清罪名——vindicate，平反。

k.syn: avenging, resentful, retaliatory, revengeful, vengeful
k.ant: forgiving, merciful, relenting 仁慈的
k.en: disposed to seek revenge

ex.en: vindictive hatred for his brother
ex.cn: 对他兄弟的仇恨

m: vindictive → 拆词：vindictive ≈ vindicate（辩护、洗清）的扭曲版。vindicate是洗清冤屈，vindictive是「要报复雪恨」的心态——复仇的（vindictive）。记忆：被冤枉后不走法律途径vindicate自己，而是心怀vindictive仇恨想报复。

k.syn: adept, connoisseur, maestro, master, maven, proficient, wizard
k.ant: amateur, inexpert 业余爱好者，非专业玩家
k.en: a person with masterly skill or technique in the arts

ex.en: a violin virtuoso
ex.cn: 资深小提琴艺术家

k.syn: accomplished, adept, consummate, experienced, masterful, professed, skilled, versed, veteran
k.ant: amateur, inexperienced, inexpert, unprofessional, unseasoned, unskilled, unskillful 业余的，经验不足的
k.cn: 经验丰富的，技艺精湛的
k.en: having or showing exceptional knowledge, experience, or skill in a field of endeavor

ex.en: The American virtuoso cellist Lynn Harrell joins the orchestra as soloist in Shostakovich's technically challenging Cello Concerto No 2.
ex.cn: 来自美国的技艺精湛的大提琴家林恩·哈勒尔作为独奏者加入了乐队，共同演绎肖斯塔科维奇充满挑战的《第二大提琴协奏曲》。

m: virtuoso → virtue（美德、才能）大家认识，加-oso（意大利语后缀，「充满」之意）。充满virtue（才能）的人 = virtuoso（艺术名家、演奏大师）。a piano virtuoso（钢琴大师）。意大利语风格的词，想象歌剧院里鼓掌的场面。

k.syn: decent, ethical, honest, honorable, moral, noble, righteous, straight, upright
k.ant: bad, evil, immoral, indecent, sinful, unethical, unrighteous, wicked, wrong 邪恶的
k.en: having or showing virtue, especially moral excellence

ex.en: Virtuous behavior is its own reward.
ex.cn: 美德本身就是一种回报。

ex.en: She felt that she had made a virtuous decision by donating the money to charity.
ex.cn: 她觉得把钱捐赠给慈善基金是一个高尚的决定。

m: virtuous → virtue（美德）+ ous。充满virtue = virtuous（品德高尚的、正直的）。a virtuous person（品德高尚的人）；virtuous cycle（良性循环，与vicious cycle相对）。virtue → virtuous，词形变化简单好记。

k.syn: poisonous, toxic, venomous
k.ant: innocuous 无害的；healthy, salubrious, wholesome 有益健康的
k.en: extremely poisonous or venomous

ex.en: virulent bacteria
ex.cn: 有毒的细菌

k.syn: cruel, malevolent, malicious, malignant, spiteful, vicious
k.ant: benevolent, benign, benignant, loving 仁慈的
k.en: marked by a rapid, severe, and destructive course

ex.en: a virulent look on her face
ex.cn: 面露凶色

m: virulent → virus（病毒）大家认识，virulent ≈ virus + ulent（充满）。充满virus般毒性的 = virulent（有毒的、剧毒的、恶毒的）。a virulent strain of flu（毒性极强的流感病毒）；virulent criticism（毒辣的批评）。

k.syn: adherent, adhesive, clingy, gluey, glutinous, tenacious, viscous
k.ant: slick 光滑的
k.en: having a glutinous consistency

ex.en: viscid tree resin
ex.cn: 粘性的树脂

m: viscid → 发音「威斯得」，谐音「喂，死得」。手碰到蜘蛛网上的viscid（粘滞的）丝，「喂，死得」粘住拔不下来！viscid比sticky更书面，强调「浓稠粘滞」的质感，比如蜂蜜、树脂都是viscid的。

k.syn: glutinous, syrup, viscid
k.ant: fluid, watery 流体的，易流动的
k.en: viscid; sticky

ex.en: viscous syrup that takes forever to pour from a narrow-neck bottle
ex.cn: 不知要花多久才能从窄口瓶中倒出来的粘稠糖浆

m: viscous → viscid的兄弟词，同样来自viscum（粘液）。viscous（粘稠的）比viscid更常用。想象浓稠的番茄酱从瓶口缓缓流下，那就是viscous（粘性的）液体。viscosity（粘度）是物理学术语。

k.syn: idealistic, imaginary, impractical, quixotic, romantic, utopian
k.ant: pragmatic 实事求是的，务实的
k.en: having or marked by a tendency to be guided more by ideals than by reality

ex.en: a visionary plan for a manned flight to Mars
ex.cn: 一个构想中的载人火星计划

k.syn: chimerical, dreamy, fabulous, fantastic, illusory, phantom, unreal
k.ant: actual, existent, existing, real 真实存在的
k.en: not real and existing only in the imagination

ex.en: claimed to have had visionary experiences of hell
ex.cn: 宣称有过到达地狱的幻觉

k.syn: farseeing, farsighted, forehanded, foreseeing, forethoughtful, prescient, proactive, provident
k.ant: improvident, myopic, shortsighted 目光短浅的
k.en: having or marked by foresight and imagination

ex.en: a visionary and legendary leader
ex.cn: 一个有远见的传奇领袖

m: visionary → vision（视野、幻象）大家认识，加-ary。有「超前vision」的人 = visionary（有远见的人；也指空想的）。史蒂夫·乔布斯是个visionary（有远见的人）；但visionary也可贬义，指「不切实际的空想家」，语境决定褒贬。

k.syn: animation, bounce, dynamism, energy, liveliness, robustness, verve, vibrancy, vigorousness, vim
k.ant: lethargy, listlessness, sluggishness, torpidity 精神不振
k.der: vitalize vt. 激发，使有活力
k.en: physical or mental vigor especially when highly developed

ex.en: Her vitality seemed to spread to everyone around her.
ex.cn: 她的活力似乎能影响身边的每一个人。

m: vitality → vita（生命，联系vitamin维他命）+ lity。维他命（vitamin）给你life（生命）和vitality（活力、生命力）。full of vitality（充满活力）；the vitality of a city（城市的活力）。

k.syn: blemish, cripple, deface, degrade, deteriorate, flaw, harm, impair, mar, undermine
k.ant: doctor, fix, mend, patch, rebuild, recondition, reconstruct, renovate, repair, revamp 修复，再生
k.en: to reduce the value or impair the quality of

ex.en: Too many grammatical errors can vitiate the soundness of your writing, so double-check is recommended before submission.
ex.cn: 太多的语法错误会削弱你论证的力度，所以建议交稿前检查几遍。

k.syn: abase, cheapen, corrupt, debauch, demean, demoralize, deprave, pervert, profane, prostitute, subvert
k.ant: elevate, ennoble, uplift 使高尚
k.der: vitiated adj. 受损害的；堕落的
k.en: to debase in moral or aesthetic status

ex.en: Penchant for coarse language vitiates what is otherwise a refined literary style.
ex.cn: 使用粗俗语言的倾向使得本该是一个高雅的文体变得不堪入目。

m: vitiate → 发音「维西特」，谐音「喂，洗掉」。好东西被「洗掉」了质量——vitiate（削弱、损害、使无效）。a flaw that vitiates the whole argument（一个缺陷使整个论证失效）。像清漆被稀释，光泽全无。

k.en: To convert or become converted into glass or a glassy substance through heat.

m: vitrify → vitreous（玻璃的）相关，vitri = glass（玻璃），加-fy（使）。使变成玻璃状 = vitrify。火山岩浆冷却可以vitrify成玻璃质岩石（黑曜石）。陶瓷在高温下也会vitrify，表面变成玻璃光泽。

k.syn: acerbic, acid, acrid, bitter, biting, caustic, corrosive, harsh, mordant, scalding, scathing, sharp, tart
k.ant: balmy, benign, bland, delicate, light, mellow, mild, nonabrasive, soft, soothing, tender 柔和的
k.en: bitterly scathing

ex.en: vitriolic criticism
ex.cn: 刻薄的批评

m: vitriolic → vitriol（硫酸、矾）+ ic。硫酸（vitriol）能腐蚀一切，vitriolic（刻薄的、犀利的）言辞像硫酸一样腐蚀人心。vitriolic criticism（腐蚀性的批评）让被评者颜面尽失、无地自容。

k.syn: abuse, assail, belabor, berate, castigate, excoriate, lambaste, reprimand, scold, rail, revile, upbraid
k.ant: admire, commend, laud, praise 赞赏
k.en: to abuse or censure severely or abusively

ex.en: He was vituperated for betraying his friends.
ex.cn: 他因为背叛朋友而被责骂。

m: vituperate → 发音「威图泊瑞特」，谐音「喂，图破瑞特」。想象古罗马角斗场，输家被人「喂，图破瑞特！」地大声咒骂——vituperate（谩骂、厉声责骂）。比criticize更激烈，比insult更持续，是一种怒气冲冲的谩骂。

k.syn: active, animated, bouncing, brisk, energetic, frisky, kinetic, mettlesome, spirited, sprightly, vital, zippy
k.ant: dead, inactive, inanimate, languid, languorous, leaden, lifeless, listless, spiritless, vapid 无活力的
k.en: lively in temper, conduct, or spirit

ex.en: a vivacious girl who became a successful sales rep
ex.cn: 一个成为成功销售代表的活泼女孩

m: vivacious → vivid（生动的）大家认识，viv = life（生命）+ acious（充满）。充满生命力的 = vivacious（活泼的、快活的）。a vivacious personality（活泼的个性）。vivacious通常形容人（尤其是女性）开朗活泼的特质。

k.syn: blatant, boisterous, clamant, clamorous, obstreperous, strident, yowling
k.ant: reticent, taciturn 沉默的；serene, tranquil 宁静的
k.en: making, given to, or marked by noisy and vehement outcry

ex.en: Vociferous opponents of the bill protested angrily outside the Congress.
ex.cn: 议案的反对者在国会外愤怒地进行声势浩大的抗议活动。

m: vociferous → vocal（声音的）大家认识，voci = voice + ferous（带来）+ ous。带来大量声音的 = vociferous（喧哗的、大声叫嚷的）。vociferous protesters（大声抗议的示威者）在街头高喊口号，声震四野。

k.syn: capricious, changeable, fluid, inconstant, mercurial, skittish, temperamental, variable, versatile
k.ant: certain, changeless, constant, immutable, invariable, settled, stable, stationary, steady, unchangeable, unvarying 固定的，不变的
k.der: volatility n. 易变性，易挥发性
k.en: characterized by or subject to rapid or unexpected change

ex.en: a boss of volatile moods
ex.cn: 一个性情多变的老板

ex.en: The stock market can be very volatile.
ex.cn: 股市瞬息万变。

m: volatile → 发音「沃拉太尔」。拆词：volat（飞）+ ile，像鸟一样飞来飞去，捉摸不定——volatile（反复无常的、易变的；化学上指易挥发的）。volatile market（动荡的市场）；volatile chemicals（易挥发的化学品）。

k.syn: choice, decision, discretion, will
k.ant: coercion, compulsion, constraint, duress, force, pressure 强迫，迫使
k.en: the act or power of making one's own choices or decisions

ex.en: beyond his volition or control
ex.cn: 超出了他的意志和控制范围

m: volition → vote（投票）大家认识，volition ≈ vol（意志）+ ition。用自己的意志「投票」选择 = volition（抉择、意志力）。of one's own volition（出于自己的意愿）= voluntarily。人区别于机器在于有volition（自由意志）。

k.syn: chatty, eloquent, garrulous, glib, loquacious, talkative, vocative
k.ant: reticent, taciturn 沉默的；laconic, reserved, succinct 惜字如金的
k.en: characterized by ready or rapid speech

ex.en: The voluble gadfly ruined the party.
ex.cn: 聚会被那个话又多又让人讨厌的人破坏了。

m: voluble → volume（音量、卷）大家认识，voluble ≈ vol（滚动）+ uble。话像音量（volume）滚滚而来、停不下来 = voluble（健谈的、滔滔不绝的）。a voluble speaker（滔滔不绝的演说者）口若悬河，让人插不上嘴。

k.syn: colossal, considerable, elephantine, enormous, gargantuan, gigantic, mammoth, numerous, oversize
k.ant: scanty, scarce 缺乏的；dwarf, little, small, undersized 数量少的
k.cn: 卷数多的，大量的，庞大的
k.en: having great volume, fullness, size, or number

ex.en: try to keep a track of voluminous academic database
ex.cn: 尝试追踪数目庞大的学术数据库

m: voluminous → volume（卷数、体积）大家认识，加-inous。体积（volume）巨大的 = voluminous（卷帙浩繁的、大量的、庞大的）。a voluminous wardrobe（衣服多得要命的衣橱）；voluminous notes（大量的笔记）。

k.syn: carnal, epicurean, luscious, lush, luxurious, indulgent, sensual, sensuous
k.ant: ascetic, spartan, self-denying 克己的，自制的
k.cn: 奢侈逸乐的，沉溺酒色的
k.en: given to or spent in enjoyments of luxury, pleasure, or sensual gratifications

ex.en: They spent a long and voluptuous holiday in Venice.
ex.cn: 他们在威尼斯度过了一个漫长而奢华的假期。

m: voluptuous → 发音「沃拉普丘斯」，谐音「我拿普丘斯」。想象古罗马贵族「我拿普丘斯（一堆享乐之物）」纵情声色——voluptuous（奢侈享乐的、沉溺酒色的；也指体态丰腴性感的）。voluptas在拉丁语是「快感、享乐」。

k.syn: edacious, esurient, gluttonous, swinish
k.ant: abstemious, abstentious 有节制的
k.en: having a huge appetite

ex.en: He has a voracious appetite.
ex.cn: 他的食量惊人。

k.syn: acquisitive, avid, covetous, grasping, greedy, hungry, rapacious, ravenous, thirsty
k.ant: apathetic, indifferent, uneager, unenthusiastic 无所谓的
k.en: having or marked by an insatiable appetite for an activity or pursuit

ex.en: a voracious reader who locked himself up in the study
ex.cn: 一个把自己锁在书房里如饥似渴读书的人

m: voracious → 发音「沃瑞秀斯」，谐音「我锐削斯」（我的牙锐利削食物）。牙齿锋利的食肉动物voracious（贪食的、狼吞虎咽的）地大口吃肉。引申：a voracious reader（如饥似渴的读者），voracious appetite for knowledge（对知识的贪婪渴望）。

k.syn: acolyte, adherent, devotee, disciple, fan, fanatic, partisan, pupil, zealot
k.ant: bellwether, leader 领导；apostate, defector, renegade, traitor, turncoat 叛徒
k.en: a person who is fervently devoted, as to a leader or ideal; a faithful follower

ex.en: votaries of the religious leader
ex.cn: 宗教领袖的追随者们

m: votary → vote（投票）大家认识，加-ary（人）。把全部的vote（选票/热情）都投给一件事的人 = votary（热心崇拜者、信徒、虔诚的信仰者）。a votary of classical music（古典音乐的狂热崇拜者），全心全意奉献。

k.syn: attest, avouch, guarantee, testify, warrant
k.en: to declare（something）to be true or genuine; to give a guarantee

ex.en: willing to vouch for her integrity
ex.cn: 愿意担保她的人格

m: vouch = vow(誓言)+ch——法庭上证人拍着胸脯，斩钉截铁地vow发誓：我为他担保！——vouch，担保，保证。

k.syn: bawdy, coarse, crass, crude, dirty, filthy, gross, lowbred, indecent, nasty, obscene, ribald, rude, uncouth
k.ant: civilized, cultivated, cultured, genteel, polished, refined, urbane, well-bred 有教养的
k.en: morally crude, undeveloped, or unregenerate

ex.en: He is a vulgar man but his music is quite divine.
ex.cn: 虽然他是一个粗俗的人，但他的音乐却十分神奇。

k.syn: conversational, informal, nonliterary, unliterary, vernacular
k.ant: bookish, formal, learned, literary 书面的，正式的
k.en: used in or suitable for speech and not formal writing

ex.en: Latin was once the language of scholars, and English the vulgar language used by common people.
ex.cn: 很久以前拉丁语才是学术界的官方语言，而英语是普通百姓所使用的。

m: vulgar → 发音「沃尔噶」，谐音「我哦嘎」（粗鄙的感叹声）。vulgar（粗俗的、低俗的、无教养的）的人说话「我哦嘎」，不堪入耳。vulgar joke（低俗笑话）；the vulgar masses（平民大众，中性用法）——注意语境决定褒贬。

k.syn: assailable, endangered, exposed, subject, susceptible
k.ant: guarded, protected, shielded 有保护的；invincible, invulnerable 无敌的
k.der: vulnerability n. 弱点，易受伤害
k.en: open to attack or damage

ex.en: The troops were deployed in a vulnerable position.
ex.cn: 部队被部署在了一个容易遭受攻击的位置。

ex.en: The fort was undefended and vulnerable.
ex.cn: 堡垒没有设防，十分脆弱。

m: vulnerable → vulner（伤口，联系wound）+ able（能够）。「能够被伤到」= vulnerable（易受攻击的、脆弱的）。vulnerable to criticism（容易受到批评）；vulnerable populations（弱势群体）。心理学中vulnerability（脆弱性）是热词。

k.syn: careen, dodder, reel, teeter, totter
k.en: to walk with short steps that tilt the body from side to side

ex.en: The duck waddled back into the water.
ex.cn: 鸭子一摇一摆地回到了水中。

m: waddle → wade（涉水）大家认识，加dle。鸭子涉水（wade）时左右摇摆，走路也waddle（摇摆着走、蹒跚而行）。企鹅和孕妇走路都是waddle——重心不稳，迈着小步左右摇晃，憨态可掬。

k.syn: babble, blather, drivel, gabble, prattle
k.ant: articulate 清楚地说
k.en: to talk or write foolishly

ex.en: This lecturer will waffle on for hours.
ex.cn: 这个老师会胡说八道地扯几个小时。

m: waffle → 华夫饼（waffle）大家都知道！华夫饼外表好看但里面是空洞的蜂窝格子——waffle（胡扯、废话连篇）的人说的话也是华丽的空洞格子，听起来有料实则无物。waffle on about sth（喋喋不休地胡扯某事）。

k.syn: buoy, drift, glide, hang, hover, raft
k.ant: flounder, sink 沉没；submerge 下沉
k.en: to float easily and gently, as on the air

ex.en: Heavenly aromas wafted from the kitchen.
ex.cn: 令人欣悦的香气从厨房飘出。

ex.en: A feather wafted past us and settled on the grass.
ex.cn: 一片羽毛飘过我们身边，落在草地上。

m: waft → 发音「沃夫特」，谐音「哇，飞特！」香气「哇，飞」过来了——waft（（使）飘浮、（使）飘散）。花香wafted through the window（花香从窗口飘进来）。轻盈、无声地飘动，是waft的核心意象。

k.syn: comedian, comic, droll, humorist, joker, wit
k.en: a humorous or droll person

ex.en: Some wag wrote a droll satire on the scandal.
ex.cn: 一些有才的人就丑闻写了一篇搞怪的讽刺文章。

k.syn: swish, switch, waggle
k.en: to move to and fro or up and down especially with quick jerky motions

ex.en: The dog wagged its tail.
ex.cn: 狗摆动着它的尾巴。

m: wag → 狗的尾巴wag（摇摆）大家都懂，引申为「幽默者」：能让人wag（摇摆/捧腹大笑）的人就是wag（诙谐的人、爱开玩笑的人）。the office wag（办公室里的开心果）总能在沉闷时刻抖出妙语，让大家摇头大笑。

k.syn: ashen, ashy, blanched, livid, lurid, sickly, pale, pallid
k.ant: blooming, florid, flush, full-blooded, glowing, red, rosy, rubicund, ruddy, sanguine 红润的，健康的
k.en: suggestive of poor health

ex.en: She looks a little wan after all that tiring work.
ex.cn: 在完成那些繁重的工作之后她看起来有些虚弱。

m: wan → 发音「旺」，但意思相反——wan（苍白的、病态的）是一点都不「旺」。生病的人脸色wan（苍白），毫无血色。a wan smile（惨淡的微笑）；she looked pale and wan（她面色苍白病恹恹的）。短词记：wan ≠ 旺，是「没旺」。

k.en: a very strong or irresistible impulse to travel

ex.en: His wanderlust would not allow him to stay long in one spot.
ex.cn: 他对旅行的渴望让他不可能在一处久待。

m: wanderlust → wander（漫游）和lust（强烈欲望）大家都认识！wander（到处游荡）的lust（强烈冲动）= wanderlust（旅行癖、对旅行的强烈渴望）。这是个德语来源的合成词，直接拆词秒懂，无需额外记忆技巧。

k.syn: abate, decline, diminish, dwindle, ease, ebb, fall, lessen, lower, moderate, recede, shrink, subside, taper
k.ant: accumulate, balloon, burgeon, enlarge, escalate, expand, grow, increase, intensify, mushroom, rise, snowball, soar, wax 增加，扩大，上升
k.der: waning adj. 衰退的，减少的
k.en: to decrease in size, extent, or degree

ex.en: In the evening the storm finally waned.
ex.cn: 傍晚时分风暴终于减弱了。

ex.en: The moon waxes and then wanes.
ex.cn: 月有阴晴圆缺。

m: wane → 月亮的盈亏：wax（增大、变圆）and wane（减小、变缺）是对义词对。月亮从满月开始wane（减退、衰退、亏缺），由圆变缺。兴趣的wane（减退）、权力的wane（衰落）都用这个词。记住wax（涨）vs wane（退）这对对子。

k.syn: absence, dearth, deficiency, drought, famine, inadequacy, insufficiency, lack, paucity, scarcity, shortage
k.ant: abundance, adequacy, amplitude, opulence, plenitude, plenty, sufficiency, wealth 丰富，大量
k.en: the condition or quality of lacking something usual or necessary

ex.en: There's a notable want of teachers in rural areas.
ex.cn: 农村地区存在着严重的师资短缺问题。

k.syn: ache, covet, crave, hunger, itch, long, lust, pine, repine, thirst, wish, yearn
k.ant: abhor, abominate, despise, detest, execrate, hate, loathe 厌恶，反感
k.en: to have a strong desire for

ex.en: I want a new car so badly!
ex.cn: 我太想要一辆新车了！

ex.en: She wanted more time to finish the test.
ex.cn: 她希望能有更多的时间来完成考试。

m: want → 这里是名词「短缺、贫乏」，不是动词「想要」。想象：因为want（缺少）食物，所以才want（想要）食物——两个意思其实同根同源：「想要」是因为「缺乏」。for want of（因缺乏……）：for want of better options（因缺乏更好的选择）。

k.en: clothespress

m: wardrobe → ward（守护、病房）+ robe（长袍）大家认识。守护长袍和衣物的地方 = wardrobe（衣橱、衣室）。电影《纳尼亚传奇》里，孩子们正是通过wardrobe（衣橱）进入魔幻世界——那个神奇的大衣橱。

k.syn: belligerent, hawk, jingoist, war hawk
k.ant: dove, pacifist 和平主义者
k.en: one who urges or attempts to stir up war

ex.en: Fortunately, the warmongers met with overwhelming opposition.
ex.cn: 幸运的是，好战者们碰到了巨大的阻力。

m: warmonger → war（战争）+ monger（商贩、贩卖者）大家都认识。贩卖战争（制造战争、煽动战争）的人 = warmonger（好战者、战争贩子）。类似构词：fishmonger（鱼贩）、scaremonger（散布恐慌者）、gossipmonger（散播八卦者）。

k.syn: avert, deflect, divert, veer
k.ant: straighten 使变直，使回归正轨
k.en: to turn from a correct or proper course

k.syn: deform, misshape, screw, torture
k.der: warped adj. 弯曲的，变形的
k.en: to twist（something）out of a natural or normal shape or condition

ex.en: The heat caused the wood to warp.
ex.cn: 高温使得木头变形了。

k.syn: distort, falsify, misinterpret, misrepresent, twist
k.ant: clarify, clear, explain, illuminate, illustrate 澄清
k.en: to change so much as to create a wrong impression or alter the meaning of

ex.en: The faulty English translation really warps the meaning of the original Chinese text.
ex.cn: 错误的英语翻译曲解了中文的原意。

m: warp → 发音「沃普」，谐音「歪噗」。木板受潮「歪噗」地变形——warp（使扭曲、使变形、使偏向）。木材、金属受热都会warp。引申：a warped sense of humor（扭曲的幽默感）；warp speed（曲速，星际迷航中超光速的说法）。

k.syn: assure, avouch, guarantee, vouch
k.en: to assume responsibility for the satisfactory quality or performance of

ex.en: The computer company unconditionally warrants all of its products for one full year.
ex.cn: 这个电脑公司对所有的产品提供为期一年的质量保证。

k.syn: approbate, authorize, clear, confirm, finalize, formalize, ratify, sanction
k.ant: decline, deny, disallow, disapprove, negative, reject, veto 禁止，否决
k.der: warranted adj. 担保的；warranty n. 保证，承诺
k.en: to give official acceptance of as satisfactory

ex.en: The law warrants these measures.
ex.cn: 法律允许这些措施。

m: warrant → war（战争/保护）+ rant（咆哮/命令）。将军咆哮着发出命令是有充分warrant（正当理由）的。warrant（使有正当理由；授权；逮捕令）。a search warrant（搜查令）；this warrants further investigation（这值得进一步调查）。

k.syn: alert, cautious, chary, circumspect, conservative, guarded, heedful, vigilant, watchful
k.ant: careless, heedless, incautious, unguarded, unmindful, unwary 不谨慎的
k.en: marked by keen caution, cunning, and watchfulness

ex.en: keep a wary eye out for signs of the enemy
ex.cn: 对敌人的形迹保持警惕

m: wary → war（战争）+ y。打仗时你得wary（小心谨慎、机警）——战场上一不小心就没命。be wary of strangers（对陌生人保持警惕）；a wary look（警惕的眼神）。比careful更强调「戒备、不信任感」。

k.syn: fritterer, profligate, spender, spendthrift, squanderer, waster
k.ant: economizer, penny-pincher 节约的人；hoarder, miser, niggard 吝啬鬼
k.en: one who expends resources foolishly and self-indulgently

ex.en: He ended up being a wastrel and a drunkard.
ex.cn: 他最后成了一个花天酒地的败家子，还是一个酒鬼。

m: wastrel → waste（浪费）大家认识，加-rel（人，有轻蔑意味）。浪费人生的人 = wastrel（游手好闲者、不负责任的人、败家子）。a wastrel who squandered his inheritance（挥霍遗产的败家子）。-rel后缀带贬义，如scoundrel（无赖）。

k.syn: climax, corner, event, landmark, milestone
k.en: a crucial dividing point: turning point

ex.en: a watershed moment in her life
ex.cn: 她命运的分水岭

m: watershed → water（水）+ shed（棚子/分流）。water在山脊两侧分流（shed）的地方 = watershed（分水岭、重要转折点）。a watershed moment（历史性转折点）；the treaty was a watershed in diplomatic history（条约是外交史的分水岭）。

k.ant: wane 月亮渐亏
k.en: to increase in phase or intensity, used chiefly of the moon, other satellites, and inferior planets

k.syn: grease, oil, slick
k.ant: coarsen, rough, roughen 使粗糙
k.en: to coat（something）with a slippery substance in order to reduce friction

ex.en: wax the floor
ex.cn: 给地板打蜡

k.syn: accelerate, accumulate, appreciate, balloon, boom, burgeon, enlarge, escalate, expand, proliferate, rise
k.ant: contract, decrease, diminish, dwindle, lessen, recede 减少，减弱
k.en: to increase in size, numbers, strength, prosperity, or intensity

ex.en: The commitment of the young volunteers to the cause seems to wax.
ex.cn: 青年志愿者们对于这项事业的投入似乎在增多。

m: wax → 月亮的盈亏：wax（增大）and wane（减退）是一对。月亮从新月开始wax（增大、变圆），由缺变圆。引申：enthusiasm that waxed and waned（时高时低的热情）。记住wax（涨）vs wane（退），与月相绑定，永不混淆。

k.syn: ambush, assault, lurk
k.en: to lie in wait for or attack from ambush

ex.en: Unsuspecting tourists are often waylaid by gangs.
ex.cn: 不警惕的旅客们常常被犯罪团伙偷袭。

ex.en: We were waylaid by a group of protestors with rocks.
ex.cn: 我们被一群示威者用石头偷袭了。

m: waylay → way（路）+ lay（躺，lay in wait = 埋伏等待）。在路（way）上埋伏（lay）等待 = waylay（拦截、伏击）。强盗waylay旅行者；记者waylay政客想要采访——后者是「半路拦截」的中性用法。

k.syn: disturbance, furor, hurricane, pandemonium, turmoil, uproar, whirl
k.ant: calm, peace, tranquility 平静，宁静；order 秩序
k.en: a state of wild disorder

ex.en: There was a welter of pushing and shoving.
ex.cn: 到处是推搡和拥挤的混乱局面。

ex.en: The troop withdrawal would plunge the country into a welter of anarchy and endless civil war.
ex.cn: 撤军会使得这个国家陷入无政府的动乱状态和无尽的内战中。

m: welter → 发音「威尔特」，谐音「乱唯特」（乱成一特）。拳击比赛中，welterweight（次中量级）选手互相殴打，welter（翻滚扑打）在一起，场面welter（混乱杂乱）一片。a welter of emotions（一团混乱的情绪）。

k.syn: adulate, blandish, cajole, coax
k.ant: coerce, compel, demand, force, oblige, require 迫使，强求
k.en: to persuade or attempt to persuade by flattery or guile

ex.en: wheedle him into working for them
ex.cn: 哄骗他为他们工作

ex.en: She pleaded and wheedled, but I wouldn't be swayed.
ex.cn: 她软磨硬泡地求我，但我仍不动摇。

m: wheedle → 发音「惠得」，谐音「喂，得！」小孩对妈妈撒娇「喂，得（给我吧）！」用甜言蜜语wheedle（哄骗、用花言巧语诱惑）妈妈买玩具。wheedle sb into doing sth（哄某人做某事），像蜜糖包裹的陷阱。

k.syn: edge, grind, hone, stone, strop
k.ant: blunt, dull 使变钝
k.en: to sharpen by rubbing on or with something（as a stone）

ex.en: whett the dagger with the grindstone
ex.cn: 用磨石把匕首磨锋利

m: whet → 发音「惠特」，谐音「喂，特！」（喂，特别饿！）。磨刀声「嚓嚓嚓」——whet（磨快、激起）食欲。whet your appetite（激起你的食欲/兴趣）是最常用搭配。这道开胃菜whet了我的appetite，让我想吃更多。

k.syn: breath, puff
k.ant: blast 强烈的气流
k.en: a quick puff or slight gust especially of air, odor, gas, smoke, or spray

ex.en: A whiff of fresh air reinvigorated him.
ex.cn: 一股清新的空气让他恢复了活力。

k.syn: flicker, glimmer, suggestion, touch, trace
k.en: an almost imperceptible sign of something

ex.en: Even a whiff of appreciation for everything I've done for her would have been nice.
ex.cn: 她哪怕是对我为她的付出流露出一丁点的感激之情也好啊。

ex.en: Humanity is unregenerable and hates the language of conformity, since conformity has a whiff of the inhuman about it.
ex.cn: （Anthony Burgess）人性是不能改造也无法统一的，因为一致性本身带有一点点不人道的意味。（安东尼·伯吉斯）

m: whiff → 发音「威夫」，谐音「一夫」（一夫当关）。「一」阵微风带来「一夫」的体味——whiff（一阵微风、一丝气味）。a whiff of perfume（一丝香水味）；a whiff of scandal（一点点丑闻的味道）。轻盈、短暂、一闪而过。

k.syn: capricious, fickle, freakish, mercurial, volatile
k.ant: resolute, unwavering 坚决的
k.en: prone to sudden illogical changes of mind, ideas, or actions

ex.en: It's hard to make plans with such a whimsical friend.
ex.cn: 和这样一个反复无常的朋友制定计划是很困难的。

m: whimsical → whim（一时兴起的念头）大家可能知道，加-sical。充满whim（奇思怪想）的 = whimsical（异想天开的、反复无常的）。a whimsical story（奇想趣味的故事）；a whimsical decision（凭一时兴致的决定）。whim是核心锚点。

k.syn: healthy, restorative, salubrious, recuperative, tonic
k.ant: insalubrious, noxious, unhealthy, unwholesome 不健康的，有害的
k.en: promoting mental, moral, or social health

ex.en: try to eat a more wholesome diet
ex.cn: 尽可能有更健康的饮食习惯

m: wholesome → whole（整体、完整）大家认识，加-some（-ful）。整体上对你好的 = wholesome（有益身心健康的）。wholesome food（健康食品）；wholesome entertainment（健康娱乐）。与harmful（有害的）相对，强调对身心全面的正面影响。

k.en: the quality of being evil, immoral, or sinful

m: wickedness → wicked（邪恶的）大家认识（「女巫」电影《Wicked》！），加-ness（名词后缀）。wicked → wickedness（邪恶、罪恶）。the wickedness of his plan（他计划的邪恶性）。wicked是形容词，wickedness是名词，词形变化直接。

k.syn: blench, cringe, quail, quiver, recoil, shrink, tremble
k.ant: confront, face, meet 直面；challenge 挑战
k.en: to shrink back involuntarily as from pain

ex.en: wince at the horrible corpses
ex.cn: 在令人恐惧的尸体面前畏缩

m: wince → 发音「温斯」，谐音「稳死」。打针时痛得「稳死」——wince（畏缩、因疼痛或尴尬而退缩）。he winced at the criticism（他听到批评时不由得退缩了）。wince是身体本能反应，皱眉、缩肩、倒吸凉气都是wince的表现。

k.syn: babbler, conversationalist, gabbler, gasbag, prattler
k.en: an exhaustively talkative person

ex.en: With a windbag like that, who needs a wind farm to meet our energy needs?
ex.cn: 有了他这样一个多话的“吹风机”，谁还要风力发电厂来满足电力需求呢？

m: windbag → wind（风）+ bag（袋子）大家都认识。一袋子风 = windbag（话多的人、废话精）。什么都说不到点、说了半天等于没说的人就是windbag。「一肚子风」= 说话没内容、不断吹嘘。比verbose更口语化、更有讽刺意味。

k.syn: circuitous, circumlocutory, diffuse, garrulous, prolix, rambling, verbose
k.ant: compact, concise, crisp, pithy, succinct, terse 简洁的
k.en: characterized by wearisome verbosity

ex.en: a windy saleswoman who told us a lot more than we wanted to know about vacuum cleaners
ex.cn: 一个啰嗦的女销售员，一个劲地说着我们不想知道的关于吸尘器的细节

m: windy → wind（风）大家认识，windy除了「多风的」还有「冗长罗嗦的」之义。说话「多风」= 说了很多但都是「风」（虚的）= windy（冗长的、吹牛的）。a windy speech（冗长乏味的演讲）刮了半天的风，什么实质内容也没有。

k.syn: adorable, charming, disarming, enchanting, endearing, sweet, winning
k.ant: abhorrent, abominable, detestable, hateful, loathsome, odious 令人厌恶的
k.en: generally pleasing and engaging often because of a childlike charm and innocence

ex.en: fascinated by her winsome smile
ex.cn: 为她动人一笑所倾倒

m: winsome → win（赢得）大家认识，加-some。能赢得人心的 = winsome（迷人的、讨人喜欢的）。a winsome smile（迷人的笑容）；her winsome personality（她讨人喜欢的个性）。win + some：赢得了一些人心，自然是winsome。

k.syn: astuteness, brilliance, foxiness, intelligence, keenness, perspicacity, sagacity, sharpness, shrewdness
k.ant: brainlessness, dullness, fatuity, lunacy, silliness 愚蠢
k.en: the natural ability to perceive and understand

ex.en: lack the wit to judge
ex.cn: 缺乏判断的智慧

k.syn: illuminati, pundit, sage, savant, scholar
k.ant: dolt, fool, idiot, simpleton 傻子
k.en: a person of exceptional intelligence

ex.en: a man who fancied himself as a great wit
ex.cn: 一个把自己想象成智慧的化身的男子

m: wit → 发音「威特」，谐音「惟它」（就靠它）。没有颜值没有财富，「就靠它」（wit机智）赢得了所有人的心——wit（机智、才思敏捷）。at one's wit's end（智穷计尽、束手无策）；witty（机智风趣的）是形容词。

k.syn: recede, retreat, fall back
k.ant: advance 前进；place, position, put 放置，安置
k.der: withdrawal n. 撤退；withdrawn adj. 性格内向的，离群的
k.en: to take back or away

ex.en: The army was forced to withdraw from the frontline.
ex.cn: 部队被迫从前线撤回。

m: withdraw → with（向后、相反）+ draw（拉）大家认识。往后拉 = withdraw（撤退、收回、隐遁）。withdraw money from bank（从银行取款）；withdraw from society（从社会中隐退）；withdrawn personality（内向孤僻的性格）。

k.syn: dry, fade, shrivel, wane, wilt, wizen
k.ant: revive 复活；bloom, flourish, prosper, thrive 繁茂
k.der: withering adj. 凋谢的，凋亡的
k.en: to become dry and sapless

ex.en: Amaranth is a legendary flower that never withers.
ex.cn: 传说中的 Amaranth是一种永不凋谢的花。

m: wither → weather（天气）大家认识，wither谐音相近。天气（weather）让植物wither（枯萎、凋谢）。a withering look（凌厉的一瞥，如枯萎般令人打蔫）；withering criticism（致命的批评，让人「枯萎」的评价）。

k.syn: decline, disallow, disapprove, detain, refuse, reject, reprobate, restrain, retain
k.ant: allow, concede, grant, permit 授予
k.en: to hold back from action; to refrain from granting, giving, or allowing

ex.en: withhold sensitive information
ex.cn: 限制敏感信息的传播

m: withhold → with（相反）+ hold（持有）大家认识。「反向持有」= 扣着不给 = withhold（扣押、拒绝给予）。withhold information（扣押信息）；withhold payment（扣押款项）。故意不释放、不提供，是withhold的核心。

k.syn: dry, mummify, shrivel, wither
k.ant: revive 复活；bloom, flourish, prosper, thrive 繁茂
k.en: to become dry, shrunken, and wrinkled often as a result of aging or of failing vitality

k.syn: faded, withered
k.ant: blooming 盛开的
k.en: shriveled or dried up

m: wizen → 发音「威曾」，谐音「喂，皱！」。老巫婆（wizard）年迈后wizen（凋谢、枯萎、干皱）——「喂，皱」成一团了。wizen/wizened（干瘪的、满是皱纹的）：a wizened old man（干瘪的老人）皮肤像晒干的葡萄干。

k.syn: rock, totter
k.ant: stabilize 使稳定
k.en: to move or proceed with an irregular rocking or staggering motion or unsteadily and clumsily from side to side

ex.en: The drunk stood up, wobbled for a moment, and fell forward.
ex.cn: 醉汉站了起来，晃动了一阵，然后向前倒了下去。

k.syn: falter, stagger, teeter, vacillate, waver
k.en: to show uncertainty about the right course of action

ex.en: We cannot tolerate the government wobbling at this critical time.
ex.cn: 我们不能容忍政府在这种关键时刻犹豫不决。

m: wobble → 发音「旺布」，谐音「旺卜」（像卜（卜卦）一样不稳定）。陀螺转到最后「旺卜旺卜」地wobble（摇晃、动摇）最终倒下。a wobbly table（摇晃的桌子）；her voice wobbled（她的声音颤抖了）。比shake更强调「不稳定的左右摇摆」。

k.syn: carnal, corporeal, material, mundane
k.ant: mental, spiritual 精神的；heavenly 天国的
k.en: of this world rather than spiritual or religion affairs

ex.en: preoccupied with worldly concerns
ex.cn: 纠缠于世俗的忧虑

ex.en: It is time you woke up and focused your thoughts on more worldly matters.
ex.cn: 现在你应该停止做梦，然后把自己的精力集中在更现实的问题上了。

k.syn: cosmopolitan, sophisticated
k.ant: ingenuous, innocent, naïve, unsophisticated 天真的
k.en: experienced in human affairs

ex.en: My little sister was worldly and sophisticated, quite unlike me.
ex.cn: 与我截然不同的是，我的妹妹在为人处世方面非常老练。

m: worldly → world（世界）大家认识，加-ly。只关心world（俗世）的 = worldly（世俗的、世间的、重视物质的）。worldly goods（世间财物）；worldly wise（老于世故的）。与spiritual（精神的）相对，偏向物质和现实层面。

k.syn: adulation, adoration, deification, idolatry, reverence, veneration
k.ant: dislike, dismissal, disregard, hatred, loathing, scorn 反感，厌恶
k.en: extravagant respect to an object of esteem

ex.en: the belief in swords and the worship of force
ex.cn: 刀剑信仰与武力崇拜

k.syn: adore, deify, glorify, revere, venerate
k.ant: blaspheme, desecrate, profane, violate 亵渎
k.en: to offer honor or respect to（someone）as a divine power

ex.en: The ancient Greeks worshipped many different gods.
ex.cn: 古希腊人崇拜不同的神明。

m: worship → worth（价值）大家认识，加-ship（表状态/关系）。对worth（价值）极高之物的关系 = worship（崇拜、膜拜）。hero worship（英雄崇拜）；worship the ground sb walks on（崇拜得五体投地）。worth + ship = 认定某物无上价值而崇拜。

k.en: A person who tends or herds horses, especially on a ranch.

m: wrangler → wrangle（激烈争论、争吵）+ r（人）。激烈争论（wrangle）的人 = wrangler。但wrangler也指「牧马者」——驯马需要和马「较劲争论」才能征服它。Wrangler也是牛仔裤品牌，正来源于此牛仔驯马的意象。

k.syn: bad, coarse, inferior, low-grade, mediocre, miserable, poor, rubbishy, terrible, trashy
k.ant: excellent, fine, first-class, first-rate, good, high-grade, superior 优质的
k.en: very poor in quality or ability

ex.en: the wretched conditions of the refugee camp
ex.cn: 难民营极恶劣的条件

k.syn: blue, crestfallen, dejected, doleful, dolorous, gloomy, melancholy, mournful, rueful, sorrowful, woeful
k.ant: blissful, buoyant, cheerful, delighted, glad, happy, joyful, jubilant 欢乐的
k.en: deeply afflicted, dejected, or distressed in body or mind

ex.en: She was wretched for weeks after breaking up with her boyfriend.
ex.cn: 她与男友分手后郁闷了几个星期。

m: wretched → wretch（可怜虫、不幸的人）+ ed。可怜（wretch）的状态 = wretched（沮丧的、悲惨的、极度可怜的）。a wretched life（悲惨的生活）；feel wretched（感到沮丧痛苦）；wretched weather（糟糕透顶的天气）。wretch是核心锚点。

k.syn: bending, crooked, curving, devious
k.ant: common, normal, usual 正常的
k.en: abnormally twisted or bent to one side

ex.en: a wry smile
ex.cn: 扭曲的笑容

k.syn: headstrong, obstinate, pertinacious, perverse, stubborn, wrongheaded
k.ant: amenable, compliant, complying, flexible, pliable, pliant, relenting, yielding 容易改变立场的
k.en: stubborn in adherence to wrong opinion or principles

m: wry → 发音「瑞」，谐音「歪」（许多方言「歪」读「瑞」）。「歪」着嘴、扭曲的表情 = wry（扭曲的；讽刺而幽默的）。a wry smile（苦涩而讽刺的微笑，嘴角「歪」着）；wry humor（扭曲、自嘲的幽默）。wry comment（带着讽刺意味的评论）。

k.ant: xenomania崇洋媚外的人
k.cn: 仇视（或畏惧）外国人（或外国事物）者
k.en: one unduly fearful of what is foreign and especially of people of foreign origin

ex.en: But from what I know, no way could this thoroughly US-educated woman
ex.cn: （from the age of 12）be a xenophobe. 但根据我的了解，这个从12 岁起就接受彻底的美国教育的女人不可能是抵触外国事物的人。

m: xenophobe → xeno（外国的，如xenon氙气——外来元素）+ phobe（恐惧者，如claustrophobe幽闭恐惧者）。恐惧外国人/异族的人 = xenophobe（排外者）。xeno + phobe两个词根都是GRE常见构词元素，认识了一劳永逸。

k.syn: chain, conjugate, hook, interconnect, join, link, unite
k.ant: disconnect, disjoin, dissever, disunite, separate, sunder, unchain, unlink, unyoke 分开
k.en: to become joined or linked

ex.en: yoke several ideas together to propose a novel theory
ex.cn: 将几个想法结合起来提出一个全新的理论

m: yoke → 发音「约克」，谐音「约客」（约束客人）。牛轭（yoke）把两头牛「约束」在一起，迫使它们一同耕作——yoke（连接、束缚；牛轭）。the yoke of oppression（压迫的枷锁）；yoked together（被束缚在一起）。约克（York）城？记成「被约束的城」。

k.syn: bucolic, bumpkin, churl, provincial, rustic
k.ant: cosmopolitan 云游四海的人
k.en: a naive or gullible inhabitant of a rural area or small town

ex.en: a lame comedy about the misadventures of yokels in the big city
ex.cn: 讲述乡下人在大都市中不幸遭遇的蹩脚喜剧

m: yokel → yoke（牛轭）+ l，用牛轭耕田的农民 = yokel（乡巴佬、天真淳朴的乡下人）。城里人眼中，扛着yoke（牛轭）的乡下人就是yokel——naive（天真）、gullible（容易上当）的农村人。带轻微贬义，指见识不广的朴实乡民。

k.syn: ardor, devotion, enthusiasm, fervidness, fervor, passion
k.ant: apathy, indifference, nonchalance, torpor, unconcern 麻木，冷漠
k.en: enthusiastic devotion to a cause, ideal, or goal and tireless diligence in its furtherance

ex.en: preaches with fanatical zeal
ex.cn: 带着狂热进行传教

m: zeal → 发音「捷尔」，谐音「结儿」（结实的意志）。满腔「结实」的热情 = zeal（热心、热诚、狂热）。zeal for justice（对公正的热忱）；with great zeal（充满热情地）。seal（印章）加z变zeal：把热情「盖章」认证，就是zeal！

k.syn: enthusiast, extremist, fanatic, fiend, freak, partisan, maniac, nut
k.ant: moderate 温和派
k.en: someone whose views and actions are very extreme, especially in following a particular political or religious belief

ex.en: In matters of taste, the art patron and collector Peggy Guggenheim was a zealot: she was for the strangest, the most surprising, the most satisfying, the best, the unique.
ex.cn: 就品位而言，艺术赞助人和收藏家Peggy Guggenheim是一个狂热者：她总是倾向于最新奇、最让人满意和最独特的佳品。

m: zealot → zeal（热诚）大家刚学过，加-ot（人）。热诚（zeal）过头的人 = zealot（狂热者、极端分子）。religious zealot（宗教狂热者）；political zealot（政治极端分子）。zeal是褒义的热情，zealot则有过度偏激的贬义色彩。

k.syn: acme, apex, apogee, climax, crescendo, crest, peak, pinnacle, summit, top
k.ant: bottom, nadir 最低点
k.en: culminating point

ex.en: at the zenith of his power
ex.cn: 他到达了权力巅峰

m: zenith → 发音「泽尼斯」，谐音「则你死」（到顶就死）。「爬到顶则你死」——权力到达zenith（顶点、最高点）后往往走向衰落。at the zenith of his career（在他事业的顶峰）；the zenith of the sun（太阳到达最高点/正午）。与nadir（最低点）相对。

k.syn: peppery, piquant, pungent, salty, savory, spicy, zingy
k.ant: bland, insipid, vapid, zestless 乏味的 List 28* “如果出国是一种信念，那GRE便是是否能坚守的第一道考验。” （陈曦，录取院校：University of Pennsylvania材料科学与工程系）
k.en: appealingly piquant or lively

ex.en: a zesty sauce
ex.cn: 味道刺激的酱汁

m: zesty → zest（橙皮、热情、刺激感）大家认识，加-y。充满zest的 = zesty（刺激的、充满活力的、美味的）。a zesty lemon flavor（刺激的柠檬风味）；a zesty personality（充满活力的个性）。zest原指柑橘皮的刺激香气，延伸为一切令人兴奋的刺激感。

k.syn: mount, swell, garner, snowball, build up, bulk（up）
k.ant: dissipate, diminish, dwindle, recede, wane 消散，减少
k.en: to increase gradually in quantity or number

ex.en: accumulate a fortune
ex.cn: 积攒了一笔财富

m: contains CUMUL(us) — clouds accumulate into big cumulus puffs. — accumulate, to gather over time.

k.syn: hardheaded, headstrong, intransigent, pertinacious, obdurate, uncompromising
k.ant: vacillatory, incline to yield, amenable, compliant, relenting, yielding 动摇的，易屈从的
k.en: unshakable or insistent especially in maintaining a position or opinion, unyielding; inflexible

ex.en: adamant about staying here
ex.cn: 执意留下来

m: contains DAM — an adamant person is like a DAM, won't yield to pressure. — adamant, unwavering.

k.syn: extemporary, impromptu, improvisational, offhanded
k.ant: considered, planned, premeditated, rehearsed 预先计划的
k.en: made or done without previous thought or preparation

ex.en: not bad for an ad-lib comedy routine
ex.cn: 对即兴喜剧表演来说已经不错了

m: short for Latin "ad libitum" (as desired) — when an actor has no script, they AD-LIB on the spot. — ad-lib, to improvise.

k.en: a usually short narrative of an interesting, amusing, or biographical incident

ex.en: He told us all sorts of humorous anecdotes about his childhood.
ex.cn: 他告诉了我们所有关于他童年的奇闻趣事。 He is a master raconteur with endless anecdotes. 他是讲故事的超级高手，总有讲不完的奇闻趣事。

m: ANE + DOTE — "a + n(ot) + EC + dote" — but cleaner: contains DOTE — at dinner you DOTE on a short story (anecdote) about your trip. — anecdote, a short personal story.

k.syn: sapless, infirm, feeble, decrepit, wan, pale, pallid, effete, lethargic
k.ant: vigorous, spirited 精力充沛的；forceful 有力量的
k.en: lacking force, vitality, or spirit

ex.en: an anemic economic recovery
ex.cn: 毫无活力的经济复苏

ex.en: Investors are worried about the stock's anemic performance.
ex.cn: 投资者担心股市低迷的表现。

m: contains ANEMIA — having anemia = weak, lacking iron. — anemic, weak / lacking vitality.

k.syn: analgesic, anodyne
k.en: something（as a drug）that relieves pain

ex.en: The dentist waited until the anesthetic took effect.
ex.cn: 牙医一直等到止痛药生效。

k.ant: sensate 有感觉的
k.en: lacking awareness or sensitivity

ex.en: be anesthetic to their feelings
ex.cn: 对他们的感受麻木不仁

m: AN- (no) + ESTHE- (feeling, as in "aesthetic") — no feeling. — anesthetic, drug that numbs feeling.

k.syn: breathing, live, living
k.ant: dead, expired, deceased, lifeless, nonliving 无生命的
k.en: having or showing life

ex.en: The lecture was about ancient worship of animate and inanimate objects.
ex.cn: 演讲是关于对生命体和非生命体的古代崇拜。

k.syn: bouncing, brisk, energetic, sprightly, vivacious
k.ant: inactive, lackadaisical, languid, listless, leaden 没有活力的，无精打采的
k.en: having much high-spirited energy and movement

ex.en: Animate dance will get the blood pumping.
ex.cn: 有活力的舞蹈会让人热血沸腾。

k.syn: brace, energize, enliven, invigorate, ginger（up）, pep up, vitalize 使有活力
k.ant: lassitude, lethargy 疲倦，疲乏
k.der: animation n. 生命力，活力：the quality or condition of being alive, active, spirited, or vigorous
k.en: to give spirit and support to

ex.en: The writer's humor animates the novel.
ex.cn: 作者的幽默使小说富有生命力。

k.syn: goad, prod, spur, galvanize, stimulate
k.en: to move to action

ex.en: a criminal animated by greed
ex.cn: 一个受贪婪驱使的罪犯

k.cn: 使活灵活现，使栩栩如生
k.en: to make or design in such a way as to create apparently spontaneous lifelike movement

ex.en: Kongfu Panda's very realistic panda was animated by CG.
ex.cn: 《功夫熊猫》这部电影通过CG技术使熊猫变得活灵活现。

m: contains ANIM (life, as in "animal") — to animate = to give life. Pixar animates cartoons. — animate, to give life or movement to.

k.syn: alluring, captivating, charismatic, charming, enchanting, engaging, entrancing, luring, seductive
k.ant: repellent, repelling, repugnant, repulsive, unalluring 令人厌恶的，不吸引人的
k.en: attractive, inviting

ex.en: The large salary made Goldman Sachs's offer more appealing to him.
ex.cn: 高薪使高盛的工作对他更有吸引力。

m: from APPEAL — having appeal = attractive. — appealing, attractive.

k.syn: amazing, astonishing, blindsiding, dumbfounding, shocking, startling, stunning, stupefying
k.ant: unsurprising 平常的
k.cn: 令人吃惊的，出乎意料的
k.en: causing astonishment or amazement

ex.en: The richness and variety of the undersea environment are astounding.
ex.cn: 丰富多样的海底环境是令人惊奇的。

m: contains ASTOUND — an astounding result stuns you. — astounding, astonishing.

k.syn: approval, authorization, concurrence, endorsement, license, permission, sanction, warrant
k.ant: interdiction, prohibition, proscription 禁止
k.en: an indication of approval carrying great prestige
prestige

ex.en: being rich...doesn't have the cachet it used to
ex.cn: 富甲一方，再也不像过去拥有那么高的声望了

k.syn: credit, distinction, esteem, homage, kudos, prestige
k.ant: infamy, notoriety 不好的名声
k.en: a mark or quality, as of distinction, individuality, or authenticity

ex.en: Federal courts have a certain cachet which state courts lack.
ex.cn: 联邦法院拥有州立法院所不具备的威信。

m: a CACHET is high prestige (the "cachet" of a luxury brand). — cachet, prestige / approval.

k.en: to decorate（metal）by engraving

m: to CHASE silver = to engrave decoratively — different sense from chase = pursue. The engraver CHASES patterns into the metal. — chase, to engrave decoratively.

k.syn: banish, dismiss, expel, extrude, kick out, cast out
k.en: to drive or force out

ex.en: chase the cat out of the garden
ex.cn: 把猫赶出园子

m: to drive cattle is to OB-EMBOSS them with shouts and prods, urging them on. — or embossing, to drive (cattle, etc.).

k.syn: angered, apoplectic, ballistic, enraged, furious, incensed, inflamed, enflamed, infuriated, irate, ireful, outraged, rankled, riled, wrathful
k.ant: angerless, delighted, pleased 不生气的；高兴的
k.en: feeling or showing anger: angry, irate
