ex.en: I absolutely get choleric when a salesman calls during the dinner hour.
ex.cn: 当一个推销员午休时间打来电话的时候，我彻底愤怒了。

m: rhymes with DANGER — A-NGER is a step from DANGER. — anger, intense displeasure.

k.syn: encumber, fetter, hinder, hold back, hold up, impede, inhibit, interfere with, obstruct, shackle, stymie, tie up, trammel
k.ant: aid, assist, help 帮助；facilitate 促进
k.en: to create difficulty for the work or activity of

ex.en: They always clog the courts.
ex.cn: 他们一直阻挠法庭工作。

k.syn: block, choke, clot, gum up, jam, obstruct, occlude, stop up, stuff
k.ant: clear, free, open up, unblock, unclog, unstop 清除，排除障碍
k.en: to prevent passage through by filling with something

ex.en: Within a few years the pipe began to clog up.
ex.cn: 没有几年，管子就开始堵塞了。

k.syn: clot, congeal, jelly
k.ant: melt, liquefy, fluidify, dissolve 融化，溶解；thin 使变稀薄
k.der: coagulant n. 凝结剂：an agent that causes a liquid to coagulate
k.en: to（cause to）become viscous or thickened into a coherent mass: curdle, clot

ex.en: The blood coagulates to stop wounds bleeding.
ex.cn: 血液会凝结以防止伤口流血。

m: milk COAGULATES into curds — thickens and clots. — coagulate, to thicken / clot.

k.syn: connive, conspire, contrive, intrigue, machinate, put up
k.ant: act independently 单独行动
k.en: to act together secretly to achieve a fraudulent, illegal, or deceitful purpose; conspire

ex.en: collude with competitors to control the price
ex.cn: 与竞争者合谋以控制价格

m: CO- (together) + LUDE (rhymes with allude) — secretly play together against someone. — collude, to conspire.

k.syn: aphoristic, compendious, curt, laconic, pithy, succinct, terse, elliptical
k.ant: circuitous, circumlocutory, diffuse, long-winded, prolix, verbose, windy, wordy 冗长的
k.en: marked by brevity of expression or statement

ex.en: a clear and concise account of the accident
ex.cn: 一份清楚简洁的事故描述

ex.en: Concise Design of Spacecraft Automatic System
ex.cn: 航天器自动控制系统简要设计

m: from CONCISION — brief and to the point. — concise, brief.

k.syn: meet, focus, concentrate
k.ant: diverge, deviate, digress 分离，分岔
k.der: convergent adj. 汇集的；convergence n. 汇集
k.en: tending to move toward one point or one another

ex.en: The main streets converge on a central square.
ex.cn: 众主干道在中央广场交汇。

m: CON- (together) + VERGE — come to a single point. — converge, to come together.

k.syn: contrast, disagreement, discrepancy, disparateness, disparity, dissimilarity, dissimilitude, distinction, otherness, unlikeness
k.ant: alikeness, analogy, likeness, resemblance, sameness, similarity 相同，相似
k.en: the quality or state of being different

ex.en: There is no fundamental diversity between the two ideologies.
ex.cn: 这两种思想意识之间并没有根本的分歧。

m: MISCELLANEOUS + NESS — the state of being mixed. (Latin MISCELLUS = mixed.) — miscellaneousness, a mixed assortment.

k.syn: ascendant, leading, outweighing, paramount, prevalent, principal, supreme
k.ant: subordinate（等级上）低的
k.der: dominate v. 占主导地位，统治；dominance n. 主导，主流
k.en: commanding, controlling, or prevailing over all others

ex.en: the dominant culture
ex.cn: 主流文化

k.ant: recessive 隐性的
k.en: of, relating to, or exerting ecological or genetic dominance

m: from DOMINATE — most powerful / controlling. — dominant, ruling / most influential.

k.syn: bright, beaming, glorious, luminous, radiant, splendid
k.ant: dim, murky, dull 模糊的，黯淡的
k.en: shining brilliantly; resplendent

ex.en: an effulgent sunset on the Atlantic
ex.cn: 大西洋上壮丽辉煌的日落

m: from EFFULGENCE — radiantly bright. — effulgent, radiantly shining.

k.syn: egocentric, self-centered, individualistic, navel-gazing
k.ant: altruistic, selfless 利他的，无私的
k.der: ego n. 自大，自我；egoism n. 利己主义；egoist n. 以自我为中心的人
k.cn: 利己的，以自我为中心的
k.en: being centered in or preoccupied with oneself and the gratification of one's own desires

ex.en: egoistic behaviors others detest
ex.cn: 令他人厌恶的利己行为

m: from EGO — focused on self. — egoistic, self-centered.

k.syn: famous, celebrated, distinguished, famed, notable, prominent, renowned
k.ant: mediocre, undistinguished 中庸的，平凡的
k.en: exhibiting eminence especially in standing above others in some quality or position

ex.en: an eminent young scientist
ex.cn: 一名杰出的青年科学家

m: EMI- (out) + NENT — standing out, prominent. — eminent, distinguished.

k.syn: admonishing, admonitory, monitory, premonitory, warning
k.en: serving as or offering a warning

ex.en: Armies have traditionally used public execution as an exemplary punishment for the crime of desertion.
ex.cn: 军队通常将公开处决作为玩忽职守罪的惩罚，以儆效尤。

m: EXAMPLE contains EX + AMPLE — sample EXposed, AMPLE proof. — example, a model.

k.syn: illogical, invalid, irrational, mad, reasonless, sophistic, unreasonable
k.ant: sound 论证有力的；valid 合乎逻辑的
k.en: containing or based on a fallacy

ex.en: The once-common fallacious claim that girls just weren't any good at math.
ex.cn: 曾经广为流传的一个谬论：女孩子无论如何就是学不好数学。

k.syn: beguiling, deceiving, deceptive, deluding, delusive, delusory, false, fraudulent, misleading
k.ant: authentic, veritable 真实的
k.en: tending to deceive or mislead

ex.en: fallacious testimony
ex.cn: 不真实的证词

m: from FALLACY — based on a false logic. — fallacious, based on faulty logic.

k.syn: caution, palladium, preventive, safeguard
k.en: a measure taken to preclude loss or injury

ex.en: There are so many fail-safes built into the system that a highly unlikely series of mistakes would have to be made before failure could occur.
ex.cn: 系统中嵌入了大量的保险措施，因此在系统崩溃之前，必须要发生一系列几乎不可能出现的错误。

k.syn: certain, foolproof, sure, unfailing
k.ant: fallible 容易犯错的 Trouble is only opportunity in work clothes. 困难只是穿上工作服的机遇。 ——美国实业家　凯泽（H.J. Kaiser, American businessman） “背GRE单词的过程，让我们铭记的不仅仅是单词，更是周围人的关爱。我们刷的不是单词，而是关爱。” （底骞，2009年10月，Verbal 730，Quantitative 800，录取院校：The Pennsylvania of State University, Department of Geography）
k.en: having no chance of failure

ex.en: a fail-safe device
ex.cn: 自动防故障装置

ex.en: Men have traditionally regarded flowers as the fail-safe gift for Valentine's Day.
ex.cn: 男士们长期以来认为鲜花是情人节永远不会错的礼物。

m: FAIL + SAFE — designed so that failure leaves things safe. — fail-safe, foolproof backup.

k.syn: apprentice, beginner, freshman, neophyte, novice, tyro, recruit, rookie
k.ant: veteran 老兵，身经百战的人
k.en: a person who is just starting out in a field of activity

ex.en: At hockey he's still a fledgling and needs to work on his basic skating skills.
ex.cn: 在冰球场上他还是一个菜鸟，还需要在基本的滑冰技术上加强练习。

m: FLEDGLING is a bird just FLEDGEd — figuratively, a newcomer just learning to fly. — fledgling, a beginner.

k.syn: fortuitous, lucky, providential
k.ant: hapless, unfortunate, unlucky, ill-fated 不幸的
k.en: coming or happening by good luck especially unexpectedly

ex.en: a fluky coincidence that kept me safely at home when the blizzard hit
ex.cn: 一个侥幸的巧合让我在暴风雪来袭时安全地待在家里

k.syn: casual, chance, inadvertent, incidental, unintended, unintentional
k.ant: calculated, deliberate, intended, intentional, planned, premeditated 蓄意的，有预谋的
k.en: happening by or depending on chance

m: FLUKY = like a FLUKE — lucky. — fluky, occurring by chance.

k.syn: coin, counterfeit, fabricate, fake
k.en: to make or imitate falsely especially with intent to defraud

ex.en: The boy forged his father's signature on his transcript.
ex.cn: 小男孩在他的成绩单上伪造了父亲的签名。

k.syn: build, construct, form, make, manufacture, mold, shape
k.en: to form（as metal）by or as if by heating and hammering

ex.en: a real man forged by adversity
ex.cn: 一个经磨难考验而成的纯爷们

ex.en: They agreed to forge closer economic and political ties.
ex.cn: 他们就打造双边的紧密政治经济联系达成了共识。

m: a blacksmith's FORGE both creates (forges metal) and counterfeits (forges signatures) — same word, two meanings. — forge, to create / counterfeit.

k.syn: important, consequential, momentous, significant, weighty
k.ant: trivial 不重要的
k.en: having importance, significance, or meaningfulness

ex.en: a full-bodied study of genetic engineering
ex.cn: 一项基因工程的重要研究

k.syn: strong, concentrated
k.ant: insipid 平淡无奇的
k.en: having richness and intensity of flavor or aroma

ex.en: full-bodied perfume in the cabin
ex.cn: 车厢内浓郁的香水味

m: FULL + BODIED — having full body (in wine: full flavor). — full-bodied, rich / substantial.

k.syn: blink, gawk, gaze, peer, stare
k.ant: glance, glimpse 瞥视
k.en: to look long and hard in wonder or surprise

ex.en: goggle at the characters on the stele
ex.cn: 凝视石碑上的文字

m: GOGGLE rhymes with BOGGLE — eyes BOGGLE wide open in a stare. — goggle, to stare wide-eyed.

k.syn: arduous, burdensome, laborious, onerous, taxing, toilsome, undemanding, facile 不费力气的
k.en: requiring much time, effort, or careful attention

ex.en: Cutting diamonds can be grueling work.
ex.cn: 切割钻石是项劳神的活儿。

m: GRUELING contains GRUEL — life is reduced to thin GRUEL after a grueling ordeal. — grueling, exhausting.

k.syn: bamboozle, beguile, cozen, delude, dupe, fake out, gaff, hoax, hoodwink, snooker, string along, take in
k.ant: undeceive 使醒悟
k.en: to cause to believe what is untrue

ex.en: We were gulled into believing that if we answered the e-mail, we'd somehow become millionaires, but instead we just got put on a list for junk mail.
ex.cn: 我们误以为只要回复了那封邮件，就能变身百万富翁，后来发现我们只不过是群发的垃圾邮件名单上的一员而已。

m: to GULL someone = make them swallow it like a gullible seabird (the GULL). — gull, to deceive.

k.en: to cut or chop with repeated and irregular blows

ex.en: hacking out new election districts
ex.cn: 开辟新的选区

k.syn: address, contend with, cope with, manage, maneuver, manipulate
k.en: to deal with（something）usually skillfully or efficiently

ex.en: just couldn't hack the new job
ex.cn: 无法搞定新工作

k.syn: abide, bide, brook, endure, handle, stand, stomach, sustain, tolerate
k.en: to put up with（something painful or difficult）

ex.en: She's not sure she can hack that miserable job much longer.
ex.cn: 她不知道还能忍受那项苦逼的工作多久。

k.syn: daffy, daft, dippy, harebrained, half-witted, preposterous
k.ant: judicious, prudent, sagacious, sapient, sensible, sound, wise 明智的
k.en: showing or marked by a lack of good sense or judgment

ex.en: a half-baked scheme
ex.cn: 不明智的计划

m: HALF + BAKED — not fully cooked, an undeveloped idea. — half-baked, poorly thought out.

k.syn: awkward, handless, heavy-handed, maladroit, unhandy
k.ant: deft, dexterous, handy, sure-handed, adroit 敏捷的
k.en: lacking dexterity or grace

ex.en: too ham-handed to use one of those tiny cell phones
ex.cn: 手指太不灵活了，用不了那种迷你型手机

m: HAM + HANDED — hands like big clumsy HAMS. — ham-handed, clumsy.

k.syn: hardened, sturdy, tough, cast-iron, inured, rugged, stout, vigorous, hardy
k.ant: delicate, nonhardy, soft, tender, weak 柔弱的
k.cn: 顽强的，经受得住困境、压力的
k.en: able to withstand hardship, strain, or exposure

ex.en: Hard-bitten Chinese people could endure both the scorching heat and the freezing cold.
ex.cn: 坚强勇敢的中国人民既能受得住酷暑，又能受得住严寒。

m: HARD + BITTEN — life has BITTEN you HARD; you've grown tough. — hard-bitten, toughened by experience.

k.syn: bamboozle, beguile, bluff, con, cozen, delude, dupe, fake out, gull, hoodwink, take in
k.en: to cause to believe what is untrue

ex.en: a skilled forger who hoaxed the art world into believing that the paintings were long-lost Vermeers
ex.cn: 一个老道的骗子成功让艺术界相信那些画作是遗失多年的维米尔的真迹

m: HOAX rhymes with COAX — to HOAX someone you must COAX them with a fake story. — hoax, a deception.

k.syn: budget, conserve, economize
k.ant: dissipate, lavish, prodigalize, squander, waste 挥霍，浪费
k.der: husbandly adj. 节俭的；husbandry n. 节俭
k.en: to use sparingly or economically

ex.en: Husbanding precious resources was part of rural life.
ex.cn: 节俭使用珍贵的资源是农村生活的一部分。

m: to HUSBAND resources = to manage them like a careful HUSBAND running a farm. — husband, to manage thriftily.

k.syn: bathe, beacon, emblaze, illume, illumine, irradiate, lighten
k.ant: blacken, darken, obfuscate 使黯淡，使昏暗
k.en: to make luminous or shining

ex.en: illuminate with a spotlight
ex.cn: 用聚光灯照亮

k.syn: clarify, clear, construe, demonstrate, demystify, elucidate, explicate, expound, illustrate, interpret
k.ant: obscure 使费解
k.en: to make plain or understandable

ex.en: Roosevelt's New Deal illuminates what the role of government is in Keynesian Economics.
ex.cn: 罗斯福新政阐明了政府在凯恩斯主义中的作用。

k.syn: edify, educate, enlighten, inspire, nurture
k.ant: confuse, perplex, puzzle 使困惑
k.en: to provide（someone）with moral or spiritual understanding

ex.en: how man is illuminated by a higher spirit
ex.cn: 人们是如何被更高境界的思想开化的

m: ILLUMINATE contains LUMIN (light, as in LUMINOUS) — bring light. — illuminate, to light up / clarify.

k.syn: adolescent, juvenile, youngish, youthful
k.cn: 未完全发展的，未发育成熟的
k.en: lacking complete growth, differentiation, or development

ex.en: Immature frogs are called“tadpoles”.
ex.cn: 未发育成熟的青蛙被称为“蝌蚪”。

k.syn: green, inexperienced, puerile, raw, unfledged, unripe
k.ant: adult, experienced, mature, ripe 成年的，成熟的
k.en: lacking in adult experience or maturity

ex.en: Many high school students are still too immature to foresee the consequences of their actions.
ex.cn: 很多高中生还是不够成熟，不能意识到他们的行为所带来的后果。

m: IM (not) + MATURE — not yet mature. — immature, undeveloped.

k.syn: approaching, coming, imminent, nearing, pending, proximate, upcoming, around the corner
k.ant: late, recent 最近发生的；distant, remote 遥远的
k.en: being soon to appear or take place

ex.en: an impending celebration of the 100th anniversary of the college's founding
ex.cn: 即将来临的百年校庆

m: from IMPEND — about to happen. — impending, imminent.

k.syn: compulsory, forced, involuntary, obligatory, peremptory, required
k.ant: optional 可选择的；voluntary 志愿性的
k.en: forcing one's compliance or participation by or as if by law

ex.en: requests that grew more and more imperative
ex.cn: 命令性越来越强的请求

k.syn: clamant, compelling, critical, crying, emergent, exigent, imperious, importunate, pressing, urgent
k.ant: noncritical, unimportant 不重要的
k.en: needing immediate attention

ex.en: an imperative need for medical supplies in the earthquake-ravaged country
ex.cn: 地震灾区国家对于医疗物资的迫切需求

k.syn: indispensable, necessary, necessitous, requisite, vital
k.ant: dispensable, inessential, needless, unnecessary 非必须的
k.en: impossible to do without

ex.en: Proper equipment is imperative for the success of this chemical experiment.
ex.cn: 合适的仪器是这个化学实验成功必不可少的条件。

k.syn: kingly, queenly, regal, royal
k.en: of, relating to, or suggestive of an empire or a sovereign, especially an emperor or empress

ex.en: Imperial College London
ex.cn: 帝国理工学院

k.syn: august, epic, glorious, grand, imposing, magnificent, monumental, noble, splendid
k.ant: common, humble, inferior, low 低下的，平凡的
k.en: large and impressive in size, grandeur, extent, or conception

ex.en: People envisioned an imperial city that would rival the capitals of Europe for beauty and magnificence.
ex.cn: 人们幻想着一个比欧洲各国首都更为动人和壮丽的巨大城市。

m: IMPERIAL contains IMPER- (rule, as in EMPEROR) — befitting an empire. — imperial, of an empire / majestic.

k.syn: arrogant, bumptious, haughty, lofty, lordly, peremptory, pompous, presumptuous, supercilious, superior
k.ant: humble, lowly, modest 谦逊的
k.en: arrogantly domineering or overbearing

ex.en: an imperious movie star who thinks she's some sort of goddess
ex.cn: 一个把自己当成某种女神的傲慢的影星

k.syn: clamant, compelling, critical, crying, dire, emergent, exigent, imperative, importunate, pressing, urgent
k.ant: noncritical, unimportant 不重要的
k.en: intensely compelling

ex.en: As war casualties mounted, the need for trained nurses became imperious.
ex.cn: 随着战争伤亡人数的攀升，对于受训护士的需求变得愈发迫切。

k.syn: blasphemous, irreverent, profane, sacrilegious
k.ant: pious 虔诚的；reverent 敬神的
k.en: lacking reverence for holy or sacred matters

ex.en: make impious remarks about the church
ex.cn: 对教会做出不敬的评论

m: IM (not) + PIOUS — not pious. — impious, irreverent.

k.syn: improvisation, extemporization
k.en: something, such as a speech, that is made or done extemporaneously

k.syn: ad-lib, extemporary, improvised, offhand, unplanned, unpremeditated, unprepared, unrehearsed
k.ant: considered, planned, premeditated, prepared, rehearsed 事先有所准备的
k.en: composed without previous preparation

ex.en: Our dinner guest thanked us with an impromptu song.
ex.cn: 客人们即兴高歌一首以表达谢意。

m: IM (in) + PROMPTU (readiness, Latin) — done at a moment's prompting. — impromptu, improvised.

k.syn: abracadabra, bewitchment, charm, conjuration, enchantment, glamour, hex, invocation
k.en: a spoken word or set of words believed to have magic power

ex.en: Hovering over the sick child, the witch doctor muttered mysterious incantations.
ex.cn: 巫医在生病的小孩子身边走来走去，嘴里念着神秘的咒语。

m: INCANTATION contains CANT (sing, as in CHANT) — magical sung words. — incantation, a magical chant.

k.syn: agitational, instigative, provocative, seditious
k.ant: conciliatory, pacific 安抚性的
k.en: tending to inflame

ex.en: an incendiary speech
ex.cn: 煽动性的演说

m: PROVOCATEUR — one who PROVOKEs (French agitator). — provocateur, an inciter.

k.syn: brood, hatch, sit
k.der: incubator n. 恒温箱；incubation n. 孵化，孵卵
k.en: to cover and warm eggs as the young inside develop

ex.en: The hen incubated her eggs for two weeks.
ex.cn: 母鸡孵蛋两周。

k.syn: advance, cultivate, encourage, forward, further, nourish, nurse, nurture, promote
k.ant: check, discourage, frustrate, hinder, impede, inhibit, obstruct, retard, shackle 妨碍，阻碍
k.en: to cause or aid the development of

ex.en: Hopefully, these youthful visits to the museum will incubate an enduring love of art.
ex.cn: 我们希望年轻时参观博物馆能够培养人们对于艺术的持久热爱。

m: INCUBATE contains CUBE ≈ "box" — eggs incubate in a warm box. (Latin INCUBARE = lie on.) — incubate, to develop / nurture.

k.syn: assiduous, busy, diligent, sedulous
k.ant: indolent, lazy, slothful 懒惰的
k.en: constantly, regularly, or habitually occupied, diligent

ex.en: The industrious PhD spends all his summer holiday in the laboratory.
ex.cn: 勤奋的博士整个暑假都在做实验。

m: INDUSTRIOUS contains INDUSTRY — full of industrious labor. — industrious, hardworking.

k.syn: notorious, opprobrious
k.ant: distinguished, esteemed, prestigious, reputable 声名显赫的，受尊敬的
k.en: having an extremely and deservedly bad reputation

ex.en: an infamous city for smuggling and prostitution
ex.cn: 因走私和卖淫而臭名昭著的城市

m: IN- (intensifier here) + FAMOUS — but with bad fame. — infamous, notoriously bad.

k.syn: creative, ingenious, inventive, original
k.ant: conservative, hidebound 保守的
k.en: characterized by, tending to, or introducing innovations

ex.en: an innovative macro-economic strategy
ex.cn: 创新性的宏观经济战略

m: from INNOVATION — new and inventive. — innovative, creatively new.

k.syn: impassive, insensate, insensible, senseless, unresponsive
k.ant: perceiving, sensible, sensitive 有知觉的
k.en: lacking perception, consciousness, or animation

ex.en: He refused to believe that the universe as we know it evolved from the random interactions of insentient particles of matter.
ex.cn: 他拒绝相信我们所知的宇宙是经由无生命的粒子间的相互作用演化而来的。

k.syn: impercipient, unwise
k.ant: discerning, insightful 深邃的，有洞察力的；sagacious, sage, sapient 睿智的
k.en: not having or showing a deep understanding of something

ex.en: an insentient therapist who failed to see what the teenager's real problem was
ex.cn: 一个对这名青少年的真正问题所在一知半解的医生

m: IN (not) + SENTIENT — not feeling. — insentient, without sensation.

k.syn: nacreous, opalescent, pearlescent
k.ant: monochromatic 单色的
k.en: displaying a play of lustrous colors like those of the rainbow

ex.en: an iridescent soap bubble
ex.cn: 一个五颜六色的肥皂泡

m: from IRIDESCENCE — shimmering rainbow colors. — iridescent, showing rainbow colors.

k.syn: butterflies, dither, jimjams, nerves, shakes, shivers, willies
k.ant: aplomb, calm, composure, equanimity, imperturbability, self- possession, tranquility 镇定，冷静
k.en: a sense of panic or extreme nervousness

ex.en: She suffered pre-wedding jitters.
ex.cn: 她有婚前恐惧。

m: from JITTER — nervous shakes. — jitters, anxious feeling.

k.syn: active, assiduous, bustling, diligent, engaged, industrious, occupied, sedulous
k.ant: idle, inactive, indolent, inert, slothful 懒散的
k.en: hard-working; industrious

ex.en: The volunteers have been commendably laborious in their cleanup of the beach.
ex.cn: 清理海滩的志愿者们的勤奋努力值得赞扬。

ex.en: He was gentle and kindly, living a laborious life in his Paris flat.
ex.cn: 他是一个温柔、好心的人，住在巴黎的公寓里，过着勤奋的生活。

k.syn: arduous, challenging, demanding, difficult, exacting, formidable, grueling, heavy, labored, rigorous, rough, rugged, severe, strenuous, sweaty, toilsome, tough
k.ant: easy, effortless, facile, light, mindless, simple, undemanding 轻松的，容易的
k.en: marked by or requiring long, hard work

ex.en: the laborious task of cleaning up the oil spill
ex.cn: 清除泄露石油的艰苦工作

m: LABORIOUS contains LABOR — full of labor. — laborious, requiring much labor.

k.syn: baroque, byzantine, complicate, complicated, convoluted, intricate, involved, knotty, sophisticated, tangled
k.ant: easy, effortless, plain, simple 容易的，明显的
k.en: of, relating to, resembling, or constituting a labyrinth; extremely complex or tortuous in structure

ex.en: The labyrinthine political situation of Middle East left us totally befuddled.
ex.cn: 中东地区复杂迷离的政治局势让我们彻底迷惑了。

m: from LABYRINTH — maze-like. — labyrinthine, maze-like.

k.syn: deluxe, lavish, luxuriant, luxury, opulent, palatial, plushy, silken, sumptuous
k.ant: ascetic, austere, spartan 简朴的
k.en: showing obvious signs of wealth and comfort

ex.en: The luxurious apartment was filled with the latest electronic gadgets and fine works of art.
ex.cn: 豪华的公寓里满是最新式的电子产品和精美的艺术作品。

k.syn: decadent, indulgent, overindulgent, self-indulgent, sybaritic
k.ant: abstemious, abstinent 克制的
k.der: luxury n. 豪华；奢侈，奢侈品
k.en: given to or marked by excessive gratification of one's desires

ex.en: He squandered his family fortune in the relentless satisfaction of his luxurious tastes.
ex.cn: 他为满足自己奢侈的品味而挥霍家族的财产。

m: LUXURIOUS contains LUXURY — full of luxury. — luxurious, lavishly comfortable.

k.syn: fetid, foul, frowsy, funky, fusty, musty, noisome, rank, reeky, smelly, stinking, stinky
k.ant: ambrosial, aromatic, fragrant, perfumed, redolent, savory, scented, sweet 芳香的，有香味的
k.en: having an unpleasant smell

ex.en: American musteline will eject amalodorous fluid when startled.
ex.cn: 美洲鼬科动物在受到惊吓时会喷出有恶臭味的液体。

m: from MALODOR — having a bad smell. — malodorous, foul-smelling.

k.syn: dramatic, hammy, histrionic, mannered, pretentious, stagy
k.ant: nondramatic, nontheatrical 平淡无奇的，非戏剧性的
k.en: exaggeratedly emotional or sentimental; histrionic

ex.en: Our office drama queen yet again made the melodramatic declaration that she was contemplating suicide.
ex.cn: 我们办公室的“影后”又一次在危言耸听了：这回她说她准备自杀。

m: from MELODRAMA — overly dramatic. — melodramatic, theatrically exaggerated.

k.syn: hypnotic
k.ant: awaking 觉醒的
k.en: of, relating to, or induced by mesmerism

k.syn: attractive, alluring, captivating, charming, drawing, enchanting, riveting
k.ant: disgusting, loathesome, repellent, repulsive 令人厌恶的； unappealing, unattractive 没有吸引力的
k.der: mesmerism n. 催眠术，难以抗拒的魅力；mesmerize vt. 催眠
k.cn: 令人着迷的，难以抗拒的
k.en: attracting and holding interest as if by a spell

ex.en: the mesmeric recital
ex.cn: 迷人的独奏

m: MESMERIC comes from Franz MESMER, the 18th-century hypnotist. — mesmeric, hypnotic.

k.syn: conceptual, ideal, ideational, notional, theoretical
k.ant: concrete 具体的，实实在在的
k.en: dealing with or expressing a quality or idea

ex.en: a work that deals with such metaphysical questions as the very nature of knowledge
ex.cn: 一部探讨诸如“知识的本质是什么”的理论问题的著作

k.syn: ethereal, heavenly, otherworldly, paranormal, preternatural, transcendental, unearthly, unworldly
k.ant: mundane 世俗的；natural 自然界的
k.en: of, relating to, or being part of a reality beyond the observable physical universe

ex.en: a metaphysical world beyond the one in which we live
ex.cn: 在我们居住的世界之外的“超然世界”

m: META- (beyond) + PHYSICAL — beyond the physical, philosophical. — metaphysical, philosophical / non-physical.

k.syn: careful, conscientious, exact, fussy, heedful, painstaking, punctilious, scrupulous
k.ant: careless, feckless, heedless, thoughtless 疏忽大意的
k.en: marked by extreme or excessive care in the consideration or treatment of details

ex.en: He was so meticulous about everything.
ex.cn: 他对所有事都一丝不苟。

m: METICULOUS contains TIC ≈ "tic-by-tic" attention. (Latin METUS = fear; literally "fearfully careful.") — meticulous, painstakingly careful.

k.syn: artificial, bogus, factitious, fake, false, imitative, mock, sham, simulated, substitute, synthetic
k.ant: genuine, natural, real 真实的
k.en: being such in appearance only and made with or manufactured from usually cheaper materia

ex.en: Police were concerned that the mimic gun, although intended only as a toy, might be confused with the real thing in certain situations.
ex.cn: 尽管仿真枪只是玩具，但警方担忧在某些情况下它会被误认为是真枪。

k.syn: burlesque, caricature, do, imitate, mock, parody, send up, spoof,
k.en: to copy or exaggerate（someone or something）in order to make fun of

ex.en: The comedian was famous for mimicking the President's distinctive lisp.
ex.cn: 这个喜剧演员因能滑稽地模仿那位总统特有的口齿不清而闻名。

m: MIMIC contains MIM (imitate, Greek) — copy mockingly. — mimic, to imitate.

k.syn: triviality
k.ant: gist 要点
k.en: a minute or minor detail

ex.en: plagued by minutiae
ex.cn: 被不重要的细节困扰

m: plural of MINUTIA — many small details. — minutiae, trivial details.

k.syn: antisocial, cynical, pessimistic
k.ant: philanthropic 博爱的；uncynical 不愤世嫉俗的
k.en: having or showing a deep distrust of human beings and their motives

ex.en: a solitary and misanthropic artist
ex.cn: 一个孤立的反人类艺术家

m: from MISANTHROPE — hating humanity. — misanthropic, hating people.

k.syn: annoying, devious, errant, impish, mischievous, naughty, playful, wicked
k.ant: decorous, urbane 举止得体的
k.en: engaging in or marked by childish misbehavior

ex.en: a new approach for disciplining a chronically misbehaving child
ex.cn: 一个教育调皮小孩的新方法

m: from MISBEHAVE — acting badly. — misbehaving, badly behaved.

k.syn: infraction, infringement, offense, peccadillo, violation
k.ant: felony 重罪
k.en: a crime less serious than a felony

ex.en: charged with several misdemeanours
ex.cn: 被指控几项轻罪

m: British spelling of MISDEMEANOR — same word. — misdemeanour, a minor offense.

k.syn: closefisted, mean, niggard, parsimonious, penurious, stingy, tightfisted
k.ant: lavish, prodigal, spendthrift, squandering 浪费的；generous, liberal, munificent, openhanded 慷慨的
k.en: marked by grasping meanness and penuriousness

ex.en: a miserly couple devoid of social conscience and responsibility
ex.cn: 一对没有良心和社会责任感的吝啬夫妻

m: from MISER — like a miser. — miserly, stingy.

k.syn: motile, movable, portable, transportable
k.ant: immobile, immovable 不可移动的
k.en: capable of moving or being moved

ex.en: a mobile missile launcher
ex.cn: 可移动导弹发射装置

k.syn: adaptable, fluid, inconstant, mercurial, mutable, protean, unstable, unsteady, variable, versatile
k.ant: fixed, steadfast, stable 稳定的，不变的
k.der: mobility n. 可移动性；mobilize v. 动员
k.en: changeable in appearance, mood, or purpose

ex.en: a highly mobile face
ex.cn: 善变的面孔

m: MOBILE contains MOB ≈ MOVE (Latin MOVERE) — able to move. — mobile, movable.

k.syn: antiquated, archaic, dated, fossilized, moribund, outdated, outworn, rusty
k.ant: fresh, new 崭新的；promising 充满希望的
k.en: having passed its time of use or usefulness

ex.en: dressed in a moth-eaten style
ex.cn: 过时的衣着

m: MOTH + EATEN — old clothes EATEN by MOTHS, outdated. — moth-eaten, antiquated.

k.syn: atrocious, degenerate, devious, evil, heinous, infamous, miscreant, vicious, villainous, wicked
k.ant: ethical, righteous, upright, virtuous 品德高尚的；beneficial 有益的；exemplary 模范的
k.en: flagrantly wicked or impious

ex.en: a nefarious scheme to assassinate the archbishop
ex.cn: 企图刺杀大主教的邪恶阴谋

m: NEFARIOUS contains NEF (not + FAS, Latin = right/lawful) — unlawful, wicked. — nefarious, wicked.

k.syn: careless, derelict, heedless, neglectful, remiss
k.ant: attentive, careful, cautious, heedful 专心的，谨慎的
k.en: failing to give proper attention or care

ex.en: negligent in taking care of the children
ex.cn: 粗心大意地照顾孩子

m: from NEGLIGENCE — careless. — negligent, neglectful.

k.en: being out of accord with what is considered normal or appropriate

ex.en: a high-flown, off-key speech by a newcomer
ex.cn: 来自新手的颇有野心、不寻常的一次演讲

m: OFF + KEY — not in the right musical KEY. — off-key, inappropriate.

k.syn: distrustful, nervous, suspicious, unbelieving, worried
k.ant: credulous 轻信的
k.cn: 多疑的，对他人极端恐惧和怀疑的
k.en: exhibiting or characterized by extreme and irrational fear or distrust of others

ex.en: a paranoid suspicion that the phone might be bugged
ex.cn: 疑神疑鬼地担心电话被窃听了

m: from PARANOIA — irrationally suspicious. — paranoid, distrustful.

k.syn: bouleversement, cataclysm, earthquake, hurricane, storm, tempest, tumult, upheaval, uproar
k.en: a violent disturbance（as of the political or social order）

ex.en: Darwin's introduction of the theory of evolution created paroxysms in both religion and science that are still being felt today.
ex.cn: 达尔文提出的进化论给宗教界和科学界都带来了巨大的冲击，时至今日我们仍然能感觉到。

k.syn: closefisted, mean, mingy, miserly, niggardly, penurious, stinting, tight, tightfisted, uncharitable, ungenerous
k.ant: generous, liberal, munificent 慷慨的；dissolute, extravagant, prodigal, wasteful 浪费的，挥霍的
k.en: frugal to the point of stinginess

ex.en: the stereotype of the dour and parsimonious Scotsman
ex.cn: 严肃而吝啬的苏格兰人的典型代表

m: from PARSIMONY — extremely thrifty. — parsimonious, stingy.

k.syn: abnormal, aberrant, anomalous, morbid
k.ant: normal 正常的；natural 自然的
k.en: being such to a degree that is extreme, excessive, or markedly abnormal

ex.en: She has a pathological fear of snakes.
ex.cn: 她对蛇有一种病态的恐惧。

m: PATHO (disease, Greek PATHOS) + LOGICAL — disease-related. — pathological, abnormally diseased.

k.syn: ceaseless, dateless, deathless, endless, eternal, immortal, perpetual, undying, unending
k.ant: ephemeral, evanescent, transient, transitory 稍瞬即逝的； provisional, temporary 暂时性的
k.en: continuing or enduring without fundamental or marked change; lasting forever

ex.en: A temporary compromise has been accepted until a more permanent solution can be agreed upon.
ex.cn: 在更为持久的决案被通过之前，一个暂时性的妥协方案被接受了。

m: PER (through) + MAN (stay, Latin MANERE) + ENT — stays through time. — permanent, lasting.

k.syn: bearish, defeatist, despairing, downbeat, hopeless
k.ant: optimistic 乐观的
k.en: tending to stress the negative or unfavorable or to take the gloomiest possible view

ex.en: With that pessimistic attitude, it's no wonder you're depressed.
ex.cn: 你的人生观如此悲观，难怪你会感到压抑。

m: from PESSIMISM — expecting the worst. — pessimistic, gloomy.

k.syn: lowbrow, materialist
k.ant: highbrow 文化修养高的人
k.cn: 市侩（注重物质而鄙视智慧或艺术的人）
k.en: a person who is guided by materialism and is usually disdainful of intellectual or artistic values

ex.en: The philistine's critics sometime limit artists' imagine.
ex.cn: 市侩之人的批评有时会限制艺术家的想象力。

m: PHILISTINE — historically the Philistines (biblical enemies of the Israelites); figuratively, uncultured boors. — philistine, an uncultured person.

k.syn: adaptable, ductile, flexible, malleable, pliable, pliant, resilient, supple
k.ant: inflexible, rigid, stiff 僵硬的
k.en: susceptible of being modified in form or nature

ex.en: the plastic quality of modeling clay
ex.cn: 模型泥的可塑性质

k.syn: affected, assumed, bogus, factitious, fake, false, feigned, mock, phony, pretended, pseudo, spurious
k.ant: artless, genuine, natural, spontaneous, unaffected, uncontrived, unfeigned 真诚的
k.en: lacking in natural or spontaneous quality

ex.en: There's usually a plastic cordiality at these corporate events.
ex.cn: 通常在这类公司活动上都有一种虚假的热情和友好。

m: PLASTIC contains PLAST (mold, Greek PLASSEIN) — moldable. — plastic, moldable / artificial.

k.syn: tramp, trudge
k.ant: flit 迅速飞过；gambol 雀跃
k.der: plodding adj. 走路沉重缓慢的
k.en: to walk heavily or slowly

ex.en: The soldiers slowly plodded across the marsh.
ex.cn: 士兵们慢慢走过沼泽地。

m: PLOD rhymes with TROD — heavy slow trudging. — plod, to trudge slowly.

k.syn: adumbrate, augur, forecast, foretell, portend, predict, prognosticate, prophesy
k.en: to foretell or predict

ex.en: The incident may presage war.
ex.cn: 这个事件可能是战争的征兆

m: PRE (before) + FIGUR (shape) — shaping ahead of time, hinting at what's to come. — prefiguring, foreshadowing.

k.syn: extravagant, high-rolling, spendthrift, squandering, thriftless, unthrifty, wasteful
k.ant: parsimonious, provident, thrift, economical, frugal, conserving 吝啬的，节俭的
k.en: recklessly wasteful; wildly extravagant

ex.en: leading a profligate life
ex.cn: 过着骄奢淫逸的生活

k.syn: fritterer, high roller, spender, spendthrift, squanderer, waster, wastrel
k.ant: economizer, penny-pincher 吝啬的人
k.en: someone who spends money freely or foolishly

ex.en: Profligate who could not really afford the grand style he maintained at Monticello, Jefferson died deeply in debt.
ex.cn: 再也支撑不了在蒙蒂塞洛之时的大手大脚的作风，败家子杰斐逊最终深陷债务危机。

k.syn: backslider, debauchee, decadent, deviate, libertine, pervert, profligate, rake, rakehell, rip
k.en: a person who has sunk below the normal moral standard

ex.en: A drunken profligate, he was given to wretched excess in every aspect of his life.
ex.cn: 他是一个堕落的醉鬼，在生活的方方面面都日趋堕落

m: PRO + FLIGATE (overthrown, Latin PROFLIGARE) — utterly thrown down (morally / financially). — profligate, recklessly extravagant.

k.syn: shave, shear, snip, trim
k.en: to cut off or remove dead or living parts or branches of（a plant, for example）to improve shape or growth

ex.en: The students were asked to prune their essays.
ex.cn: 学生们被要求修改文章。

m: FERMENTATION contains FERMENT — like yeast bubbling and reorganizing matter. — fermentation, breakdown / restructuring.

k.en: to（cause to）become gathered, contracted, and wrinkled

ex.en: pucker my lips
ex.cn: 撅起嘴

m: PUCKER rhymes with SUCKER — sucking puckers your lips. — pucker, to wrinkle / contract.

k.ant: methodical, orderly, organized, regular, systematic, systematized 井然有序的，系统化的
k.der: randomly adv. 随机地；randomize v. 随机挑选、排列
k.en: lacking a definite plan, purpose, or pattern

ex.en: We received several answers and picked one at random.
ex.cn: 我们收到了若干答案，并随机抽取了一个。

ex.en: aimless, arbitrary, desultory, erratic, haphazard, scattered, slapdash, stray

m: RANDOM contains RAND ≈ "running fast / wildly" (Old French RANDON = rapid pace). — random, without pattern.

k.syn: analytic, coherent, consequent, logical, reasonable, sensible, sound, valid, well-founded, well-grounded
k.ant: illogical, incoherent, inconsequent, invalid, irrational, unreasonable, unsound, weak 不合逻辑的
k.en: consistent with or based on reason; logical

ex.en: He insisted there was a rational explanation for the strange creaking noises and that there were no such things as ghosts.
ex.cn: 他坚持认为奇怪的咯吱声一定有一个合理的解释，而不是所谓的鬼在作怪。

k.syn: commonsensical, firm, informed, just, justified, levelheaded, sober, solid
k.ant: groundless, unfounded, unjustified 无理由的，无根据的
k.der: rationalize vt. 使合理化；找借口
k.en: based on sound reasoning or information

ex.en: Betting all of your savings on the lottery is not a rational move.
ex.cn: 把你所有的存款赌在彩票上可不是一个理智的决定。

m: RATIONAL contains RATIO — based on RATIO / reason. — rational, logical.

k.syn: answer, reply, respond
k.der: reaction n. 反应；reactant n.（化学反应当中的）反应物
k.en: to act or behave in response（as to a stimulus or influence）

ex.en: didn't know how to react to the cheers of the crowd
ex.cn: 不知如何对人群的欢呼声做出回应

m: RE (back) + ACT — act in response. — react, to respond.

k.syn: regress, relapse, retrogress, revert
k.ant: habilitate, reclaim, redeem, regenerate, rehabilitate 洗心革面，使改正
k.der: recidivism n. 再犯，重犯；recidivist n. 再次犯罪的人
k.cn: 回到原先的习惯，尤指重新犯罪
k.en: to return to a previous pattern of behavior, especially to return to criminal habits

ex.en: The suspect has recidivated for several times.
ex.cn: 这个嫌犯是个惯犯了。

m: RE (back) + CID (fall, Latin CADERE) — fall back into old habits. — recidivate, to relapse into crime.

k.syn: grim, inexorable, ironfisted, merciless, mortal, ruthless, unrelenting
k.ant: charitable, clement, merciful, lenient 仁慈的
k.en: showing or promising no abatement of severity, intensity, strength, or pace

ex.en: He was the most relentless teacher I have ever known.
ex.cn: 他是我所知的最狠的老师。

k.syn: adamant, dogged, headstrong, mulish, obdurate, pertinacious, rigid, uncompromising, unyielding
k.ant: acquiescent, amenable, compliant, complying, flexible, pliable, pliant, relenting, yielding 易屈服的
k.en: showing no signs of slackening or yielding in one's purpose

ex.en: The team's offense was relentless in trying to score.
ex.cn: 进攻球员毫不妥协地努力得分。

m: from RELENT + LESS — without softening, unyielding. — relentless, unyielding.

k.syn: avenge, redress, retaliate, revenge
k.ant: absolve, condone, forgive, pardon 饶恕，宽恕
k.en: to punish in kind the wrongdoer responsible for

ex.en: The future writer would later requite the abuse he suffered at the hands of his classmates by creating scathing portraits of them in his novels.
ex.cn: 未来的作家会把在同学那里受了委屈的帐算清的——他会在小说中把他们描绘成恶人。

m: RE + PAY — pay back. — repay, to compensate / take revenge.

k.syn: agitating, anxious, distressful, disturbing, fraught, restive, perturbed, uneasy, unquiet, unsettled
k.ant: halcyon, peaceful, serene, tranquil 平静的，安宁的
k.en: marked by or causing a lack of quiet, repose, or rest

ex.en: The patient felt restless from pain.
ex.cn: 病人因疼痛而无法平静。

m: REST + LESS — without rest. — restless, unable to be still.

k.syn: celebrator, merrymaker, partyer（also partier）, partygoer, reveler
k.ant: killjoy 扫兴的人
k.en: one who engages in merrymaking especially in honor of a special occasion

ex.en: the rowdy roisterers who fill the streets of New Orleans during Mardi Gras
ex.cn: 四旬斋前最后一天遍布新奥尔良各条街道的喧闹人群

k.syn: aseptic, germfree, hygienic, sterile
k.ant: noxious 有毒的
k.en: of or relating to health

ex.en: sanitary measures
ex.cn: 清洁措施

k.syn: comb, dig through, dredge, hunt through, rake, ransack, rifle, rummage, sort through, troll
k.en: to look through（as a place）carefully or thoroughly in an effort to find or discover something

ex.en: The police scoured the city for the criminal.
ex.cn: 警方全城搜索罪犯。

k.ant: self-asserting 自信
k.en: degradation or humiliation of oneself

k.syn: narcissistic, self-centered, self-infatuated, self-obsessed, self- oriented, self-preoccupied
k.ant: self-forgetting, selfless, unselfish 无私的
k.en: absorbed in one's own thoughts, activities, or interests

ex.en: a self-absorbed man who seems utterly oblivious to the social problems of his own urban neighborhood
ex.cn: 一个超级自私的人，对于街坊的事情完全视而不见

k.syn: depressing, desolate, dismal, dreary, morose, sullen, tenebrous
k.ant: merry, bright, cheery, comforting, cordial, festive, heartwarming, sunshiny 欢快的
k.en: causing or marked by an atmosphere lacking in cheer; funereal

ex.en: The decrepit mansion had a sepulchral tone that gave everybody a chill.
ex.cn: 破旧的公馆有着一种坟墓的气息，让人皆为之一颤。

k.syn: compress, condense, constrict
k.ant: balloon, expand, snowball, swell 增大，膨胀
k.cn: （在数量或价值方面）降低，减小
k.en: to become reduced in amount or value: dwindle

ex.en: His savings quickly shrank.
ex.cn: 他的存款急剧减少。

k.syn: blench, cringe, quail, recoil, squinch, wince
k.cn: （使）本能性地退却（如因遇到使人惊恐的事物），（使）退缩
k.en: to（cause to）draw back instinctively, as from something alarming: recoil

ex.en: shrink back from the approaching flames
ex.cn: 对着燃烧的火焰望而却步

k.en: to show reluctance: hesitate

ex.en: shrink from making such a sacrifice
ex.cn: 对做出这样的牺牲犹豫不决

k.cn: 隐蔽物，可起隐藏、保护或屏护作用的物体
k.en: something that conceals, protects, or screens

ex.en: under a shroud of fog
ex.cn: 在迷雾的掩护下

k.syn: belie, blanket, cloak, conceal, cover, curtain, disguise, mask, occult, screen, veil, blot out, paper over
k.ant: bare, disclose, display, divulge, expose, reveal, show 展示，暴露
k.en: to shut off from sight; screen

ex.en: She shrouded the fact that the child had been adopted.
ex.cn: 她将孩子是领养的这个事实隐藏起来。

k.syn: act, affect, assume, counterfeit, dissemble, fake, imitate, pretend, profess, sham
k.en: to have or take on the appearance, form, or sound of: imitate

ex.en: cosmetics that simulate a suntan
ex.cn: 用来模仿晒黑效果的化妆品

k.en: to create a representation or model of（a physical system or particular situation）

ex.en: carry out an experiment to simulate weightlessness
ex.cn: 进行了模拟失重状态的实验

k.syn: bootlegging, contraband
k.en: secret importation or exportation contrary to the law and especially without paying duties imposed by law

ex.en: He was arrested for smuggling drugs into the country.
ex.cn: 他因为向境内走私毒品而被逮捕。

k.syn: catch, enmesh, ensnare, ensnarl, entrap, tangle
k.ant: disentangle 解开；free, liberate 释放
k.en: to capture by or as if by use of a snare

ex.en: They snared a rabbit earlier in the day.
ex.cn: 今天的早些时候他们捕获了一只兔子。

ex.en: The car salesman successfully snared three potential customers.
ex.cn: 汽车销售员成功地让三个潜在客户上钩了。

k.syn: compact, hard, rigid, stiff, unyielding
k.ant: flabby, soft, spongy, squashy, squishy 柔软的，容易挤压的
k.en: having a consistency that does not easily yield to pressure

ex.en: The ice cream is too solid to scoop right now.
ex.cn: 冰淇淋现在太硬了，根本舀不动。

k.syn: firm, informed, justified, levelheaded, logical, rational, reasonable, sensible, sober, valid, well-founded
k.ant: groundless, illogical, invalid, unfounded, uninformed, unjustified, unreasonable, unsound 没有道理的
k.en: based on sound reasoning or information

ex.en: the only solid conclusion that the jury could have reached
ex.cn: 陪审团唯一可能做出的合理结论

k.syn: forceful, hearty, iron, lusty, robust, stout, strong, sturdy, vigorous
k.ant: uncertain, weak 迟疑的，软弱的
k.en: not showing weakness or uncertainty

ex.en: Some people see a solid handshake as a sign of strong character.
ex.cn: 有人认为握手毫不迟疑的人有着很强势的性格。

k.syn: cosmopolitan, debonair, knowing, smart, suave, urbane, worldly
k.ant: guileless, ingenuous, innocent, naïve 天真的
k.en: having acquired worldly knowledge or refinement; lacking natural simplicity or naiveté

ex.en: On the train I met a surprisingly sophisticated and widely traveled child.
ex.cn: 在火车上我见到了一个老练得让人惊讶的、见多识广的孩子。

k.syn: baroque, byzantine, complicated, convoluted, elaborate, intricate, involved, knotty, labyrinthine, tangled
k.ant: plain, simple 简单的
k.en: very complex or complicated

ex.en: a very sophisticated machine that is a marvel of modern design
ex.cn: 一个十分复杂的机器，是现代设计的最高成就

k.syn: ramble, scramble, straddle
k.der: sprawling adj. 蔓延的，不规则伸展的
k.cn: 杂乱无序地发展；蔓生，蔓延
k.en: to grow, develop, or spread irregularly and without apparent design or plan

ex.en: The city sprawls down the whole coast.
ex.cn: 城市顺着整条海岸线蔓延。

k.syn: blaring, blasting, clamorous, deafening, earsplitting, plangent, resounding, roaring, sonorous, thunderous
k.ant: gentle, low, soft 声音轻柔的
k.en: extremely loud

ex.en: an tenor with a stentorian voice
ex.cn: 拥有洪亮声音的男高音

ex.en: The professor's stentorian voice was enough to keep even the drowsiest student awake.
ex.cn: 教授洪亮的声音足以使最困的学生保持清醒。

k.syn: constitutional, elemental, essential, fundamental, substantial, vital
k.ant: inconsequential, inconsiderable, insignificant, insubstantial, negligible, nominal 不重要的
k.en: of or relating to the essence or substance

ex.en: The substantive information is their political stance.
ex.cn: 关键信息是他们的政治立场。

k.syn: delicate, elusive, evasive, faint, fine, impalpable, intangible, nice, slight
k.ant: blatant, conspicuous, obvious, patent, plain 明显的；open, public 公开的
k.der: subtlety n. 微妙；微妙的想法
k.en: （so slight as to be）difficult to understand or perceive

ex.en: subtle differences in meaning between the words
ex.cn: 单词词义之间的细微差别

k.syn: beguiling, crafty, cunning, cute, designing, devious, foxy, guileful, scheming, shrewd, sly, tricky, wily
k.ant: artless, guileless, ingenuous, innocent, undesigning 天真纯朴的，无伪装的
k.cn: 巧妙的，间接或带有欺骗性的
k.en: clever at attaining one's ends by indirect and often deceptive means

ex.en: used subtle methods of persuasion
ex.cn: 采用了十分巧妙的劝说方式

k.syn: inert, passive, dormant, torpid
k.ant: vigilant 警惕的
k.cn: 懒散的，倦怠的，消极的，漠不关心的
k.en: showing lethargy, passivity, or blameworthy indifference

ex.en: a supine legislature that is afraid to take action
ex.cn: 害怕采取行动的不作为的立法机关

k.syn: defenselessness, vulnerability, weakness
k.ant: immunity, invulnerability 免疫
k.cn: 易受感染的性质或状态
k.en: the quality or state of having little resistance to some outside agent

ex.en: A weak immune system causes increased susceptibility to disease.
ex.cn: 弱的免疫系统会增大患病的概率。

k.syn: disordered, chaotic, messy, anarchic, jumbled
k.ant: kempt, neat, orderly, organized, shipshape, tidy, trim, uncluttered, well-ordered 有序的
k.en: lacking in order, neatness, and often cleanliness

ex.en: He left his room topsy-turvy.
ex.cn: 他把他的房间弄得很乱。

k.syn: chaotic, confused, disarranged, disarrayed, disheveled, disorderly, jumbled, messed, rumpled, sloppy, unkempt, untidy
k.ant: bandbox, crisp, kempt, neat, orderly, organized, shipshape, tidy, trim, well-ordered 整洁的，有序的
k.en: lacking in order, neatness, and often cleanliness; totally disordered

ex.en: The office is still topsy-turvy even though we moved in months ago.
ex.cn: 尽管我们几个月前就搬了进来，办公室还是混乱不堪。

ex.en: turn our ordered life topsy-turvy
ex.cn: 把我们原本有序的生活彻底打乱

k.syn: belligerent, bellicose, combative, militant, aggressive, feisty
k.ant: nonaggressive, nonbelligerent, pacific, peaceable, peaceful, uncombative, uncontentious 好和平的
k.en: feeling or displaying eagerness to fight

ex.en: in an aggressively truculent manner
ex.cn: 以好斗的、进攻性的方式

k.syn: abjection, corruptness, debasement, debauchery, decadency, degeneration, degradation, demoralization, depravity, dissipatedness, dissoluteness, libertinism, perversion, rakishness
k.ant: probity 正直
k.en: inherent baseness: depravity

ex.en: moral turpitude
ex.cn: 道德上的卑鄙

k.syn: chromatic, kaleidoscopic, iridescent, motley, rainbow
k.ant: monochromatic 单色的；colorless 无色的
k.en: having discrete markings of different colors

ex.en: variegated costumes of the dancers in the nightclub
ex.cn: 夜总会舞者身上色彩斑驳的服装

k.syn: dark, evil, immoral, iniquitous, nefarious, sinful, vicious, villainous
k.ant: decent, ethical, good, honest, honorable, moral, righteous, sublime, upright, virtuous 高尚的
k.en: morally very bad

ex.en: a wicked urge to steal just for the sake of stealing
ex.cn: 纯粹为了偷盗而偷盗的邪恶冲动

k.syn: altercation, controversy, disagreement, dispute, fight, imbroglio, quarrel, squabble
k.ant: harmony 和谐
k.en: an often noisy or angry expression of differing opinions

ex.en: There was a bit of a wrangle over how much money to give the high school for its sports programs.
ex.cn: 这个高中应该获得多少体育项目的拨款引起了一阵纷争。

k.syn: altercate, argue, bicker, controvert, hassle, quibble, spat, tiff
k.en: to quarrel noisily or angrily

ex.en: Local residents wrangled for hours about property taxes.
ex.cn: 当地居民就房产税的问题争吵不休。

k.syn: alongside, side by side
k.ant: aimlessly, desultorily, erratically, haphazardly, irregularly, randomly, willy-nilly 混乱地
k.en: beside one another with bodies in line

ex.en: a group of youths riding four abreast
ex.cn: 四个一排骑着车的一群年轻人

k.syn: acquainted, conversant, informed, knowledgeable, versed, well- informed
k.ant: ignorant, unacquainted, unfamiliar, uninformed, unknowledgeable 无知的，不熟悉的
k.en: up to a particular standard or level especially of knowledge of recent developments

ex.en: She tried to keep abreast of the latest fashion trends.
ex.cn: 她总是尽可能地跟上时尚的潮流。

m: a + BREAST — walking abreast = side-by-side at chest height. Stay abreast of news = stay shoulder-to-shoulder with current events. — abreast, side-by-side / current.

k.syn: away, lacking, missing, nonexistent, wanting
k.ant: existent, present 存在的，出现的
k.en: not present, attending or existing

ex.en: The city's usual stir of activity was conspicuously absent due to the report of an escaped lion from the zoo.
ex.cn: 一则动物园狮子逃跑的消息让这座城市缺少了往日的繁华热闹。

k.syn: abstracted, distracted
k.ant: alert, vigilant, watchful, wary 警惕的；attentive, heedful, mindful, preoccupied 专注的
k.en: lost in thought

ex.en: He seemed absent because he nodded but didn't say anything.
ex.cn: 他只点了点头但没说话，看起来像是走神了。

m: ab- (away) + SENT — sent away = not here. — absent, missing / not present.

k.syn: accolade, applause, credit, distinction, homage, honor, kudos, laud,
k.en: public acknowledgment or admiration for an achievement

ex.en: Many people were involved in the search, but the person who actually found the missing girl got all the acclaim.
ex.cn: 许许多多的人都参与了搜救行动，但是只有真正找到那个迷失的小女孩的人才受到了称赞。

m: contains CLAIM — when many people CLAIM you're great = acclaim. — acclaim, public praise.

k.syn: accredit, applaud, cheer, commend, endorse, exalt, hail, praise, salute, tout
k.ant: castigate, excoriate, lambaste, pan, slam 猛烈抨击
k.en: to declare enthusiastic approval of

ex.en: He was acclaimed as the country's greatest modern painter.
ex.cn: 他被盛赞为国内最优秀的现代画家。

m: LAURELS = leaves of the laurel tree, awarded as crown to victors in ancient Greece — "resting on one's laurels". — laurels, honors / recognition.

k.syn: achieve, commit, execute, fulfill, make, perpetrate, prosecute, carry out
k.ant: fail 失败
k.der: accomplishment n. 成就；accomplished adj. 有造诣的
k.en: to carry through（as a process）to completion

ex.en: I don't think we can accomplish our goal unless we cooperate.
ex.cn: 我认为只有合作才能实现我们的目标。

m: "ac-COMPLI-sh" sounds like "complete-ish" — to accomplish = to complete. — accomplish, to complete / achieve.

k.syn: accordance, agreement, conformity, congruence, congruity, consensus, consonance, harmony, tune
k.ant: conflict, disagreement, incongruence, incongruity, incongruousness 不一致
k.en: a state of consistency

ex.en: This map doesn't seem to be in accord with the current layout of the streets.
ex.cn: 这幅地图似乎与当前的街道情况不尽一致。

k.syn: agree, chord, cohere, coincide, conform, correspond, fit, harmonize, jibe, sort, square, tally
k.ant: differ, disagree 不相同，不一致
k.en: to be consistent or in harmony

ex.en: a theory that accords with the known facts
ex.cn: 与已知事实相一致的理论

ex.en: He claims that the newspaper's quote does not accord with what he actually said.
ex.cn: 他声称报纸引用的与他真正所说的话并不相符。

k.syn: accord, award, grant, vest, vouchsafe
k.ant: withhold 保留，不给予；recant, retract, withdraw 撤回，收回
k.en: to grant or give especially as appropriate, due, or earned

ex.en: Women were finally accorded the right to vote in 1920.
ex.cn: 女性最终在 1920年获得了投票权。

m: contains CORD — a cord ties parties together = in accord. — accord, agreement.

k.syn: admit, agree, allow, concede, confess, grant, own
k.ant: deny 否认
k.en: to admit the existence, reality, or truth of

ex.en: He refused to acknowledge the fact that her daughter has gone.
ex.cn: 他拒绝承认女儿已经死去的事实。

m: contains KNOW + LEDGE — to acknowledge = to declare you KNOW it (and stand on solid ledge). — acknowledge, to recognize.

k.syn: attain, capture, draw, gain, garner, get, make, obtain, procure, realize, reap, secure, win, bring in
k.ant: forfeit, lose 丧失
k.en: to get as one's own

ex.en: bacteria that acquire tolerance to antibiotics
ex.cn: 产生了抗药性的细菌

ex.en: I have never acquired a taste for wine.
ex.cn: 我从来就不具备对葡萄酒的品味能力。

m: ac + QUIRE — sounds like "a CHOIR" — you acquire singers for the choir. — acquire, to obtain.

k.syn: avaricious, avid, coveting, covetous, grabby, grasping, mercenary, moneygrubbing, rapacious
k.ant: benevolent, generous, liberal, philanthropic, munificent 慷慨的
k.en: strongly desirous of acquiring and possessing

ex.en: Acquisitive developers are trying to tear down the ancient temple and build a shopping mall instead.
ex.cn: 贪婪的开发商打算铲平这座古老的神庙，取而代之建造一座购物中心。

m: built from ACQUIRE — the acquisitive person is always trying to acquire more. — acquisitive, eager to acquire.

k.syn: adjudge, arbitrate, decide, determine, judge, referee, settle, umpire
k.en: to hear and settle（a case, dispute or conflict）

ex.en: When my wife and I asked the salesclerk to adjudicate our disagreement, she agreed with me that the white shoes looked better.
ex.cn: 我和我妻子要求售货员来裁决我们之间的争论，她同意我的观点，认为白色的鞋子看起来更漂亮。

m: contains JUDGE (ad-JUDGE-icate) — the judge adjudicates. — adjudicate, to formally judge.

k.syn: ill, hardship, misadventure, mischance, mishap, tragedy
k.ant: fortune, fluke, luck, serendipity 好运气
k.en: a state, condition, or instance of serious or continued difficulty or adverse fortune

ex.en: The fire is the test of gold, adversity of strong man.
ex.cn: （Martha Graham）烈火试真金，逆境炼勇士。——玛莎·葛兰姆

ex.en: They finally overcame all the adversities of the Great Depression and rebuilt their fortunes.
ex.cn: 他们终于度过了大萧条的困境，开始重新创造他们的财富。

m: contains ADVERSE — adversity is adverse (bad) circumstances. — adversity, hardship.

k.syn: façade, guise, mannerism, pose, pretense, show
k.ant: artlessness, genuineness, innocence, naivety 天真，淳朴
k.en: the act of taking on or displaying an attitude or mode of behavior not natural to oneself or not genuinely felt

ex.en: His little affectations irritated her.
ex.cn: 他做作的小动作激怒了她。

m: contains AFFECT — an affectation is an artificial way of behaving you AFFECT. — affectation, artificial behavior.

k.syn: allege, assert, aver, avouch, avow, contend, declare, insist, maintain, profess, protest, purport, warrant
k.ant: deny, gainsay, disclaim, recant 否定，反对
k.en: to assert（as a judgment or decree）as valid or confirmed

ex.en: He was unwilling to affirm without further study that the painting is an original Rembrandt.
ex.cn: 在缺乏进一步研究的情况下，他不愿意声称这幅画作是出自伦勃朗的正品。

m: shares FIRM with FIRM/CONFIRM — to AFFIRM = to make FIRMly stated. — affirm, to declare positively.

k.syn: attach, bend, fix
k.ant: detach, undo, unfasten, unhook 松开，分开
k.en: to attach physically

ex.en: affix a stamp to a letter
ex.cn: 把邮票贴在信封上

m: contains FIX — to affix a stamp = to FIX it on. — affix, to attach.

k.syn: agony, anguish, dolor, excruciation, grief, hurt, misery, pain, rack, torment, torture, travail, tribulation, woe
k.ant: bliss, cheer, delight, ecstasy, elation, euphoria, exhilaration, exuberance, exultation, felicity, joy, jubilation, pleasure, rapture 喜悦，高兴
k.en: a state of great suffering of body or mind

ex.en: She listened with deep affliction as her daughter told her about the latest trouble she was in.
ex.cn: 听着女儿诉说自己目前艰难的处境，她感到深深的心痛。

m: contains FLICT (as in CONFLICT, INFLICT) — all involve striking blows; an affliction is a blow you suffer. — affliction, suffering.

k.syn: agog, anticipant, anticipatory
k.ant: apathetic, indifferent, unconcerned, uninterested 不感兴趣的，不为所动的
k.en: having or showing signs of eagerly awaiting something

ex.en: At the sound of the sleigh bells the children were all agape, waiting for Santa to appear.
ex.cn: 听到雪橇铃声，孩子们开始急切地等待圣诞老人的到来。

m: contains GAPE — mouth agape, jaw dropped. — agape, open-mouthed in surprise.

k.syn: deftness, dexterity, nimbleness, sleight, spryness
k.ant: awkwardness, clumsiness, gaucheness, gawkiness, gracelessness, ungainliness（行动）笨拙
k.en: quickness, ease and grace in physical activity

ex.en: His agility on the parallel bars has won him several medals.
ex.cn: 他在双杠上的矫捷身姿助他赢得了数枚奖牌。

m: from AGILE — the quality of being agile. — agility, nimbleness.

k.syn: congenial, delectable, delicious, delightful, dulcet, enjoyable, felicitous, gratifying, heavenly, palatable, pleasing, satisfying, savory
k.ant: abhorrent, abominable, disagreeable, disgusting, loathsome, nauseating, repellent, repugnant, repulsive, revolting 令人厌恶的
k.en: pleasing to the mind or senses especially as according well with one's tastes or needs

ex.en: Would you mind putting on some agreeable music for dinner?
ex.cn: 你介意晚饭时放一些令人愉快的音乐吗？the agreeable melancholy resulting from a sense of the transitoriness of natural beauty 因为感慨自然之美稍纵即逝而产生的令人愉悦的忧伤

k.syn: affable, genial, good-natured, good-tempered, gracious, mellow, nice, pleasant, sweet, well-disposed
k.ant: choleric, dyspeptic, fussy, grouchy, irascible, irritable, peevish, touchy 脾气不好的，易怒的
k.en: having an easygoing and pleasing manner especially in social situations

ex.en: I have an agreeable art teacher who lets me do pretty much whatever I want.
ex.cn: 我的艺术老师性格随和，允许我去做我想做的事情。

k.syn: amicable, compatible, congenial, consistent, consonant, frictionless, kindred, unanimous, united
k.ant: disagreeable, discordant, disunited, incompatible, inharmonious 不一致的
k.en: being in harmony

ex.en: These new security measures are not agreeable with our core concepts of personal freedom.
ex.cn: 这些新的安保措施和我们所信仰的个人自由的核心理念彼此抵触。

m: contains AGREE — easy to agree with, pleasant. — agreeable, pleasant.

k.syn: admixture, amalgam, cocktail, composite, compound, fusion, intermixture, mix, mixture, synthesis
k.ant: element（构成整体的）单个元素
k.en: a distinct entity formed by the combining of two or more different things

ex.en: Brass is an alloy, consisting of copper and zinc.
ex.cn: 黄铜是一种由铜和锌组成的合金。

ex.en: Television news has always been an alloy of journalism and show business.
ex.cn: 电视新闻一直以来都是新闻界和演艺圈的混合体。

k.syn: adulterate, contaminate, dilute, extend, lace, pollute, sophisticate, thin
k.ant: purify 纯化
k.der: unalloyed adj. 纯粹的，未掺杂的
k.en: to debase by the addition of an inferior element

ex.en: idealism that was alloyed with political skill
ex.cn: 因为掺入了政治技巧而被削弱的理想主义

m: ALL + OY — ALL the metals combined into ONE. — alloy, a metallic mixture.

k.syn: conflicting, contradictory, mixed
k.ant: certain, decided, definite, positive, resolute, sure, unquestioning 明确的，毫无疑问的
k.en: having a mixture of opposing feelings

ex.en: He maintained an ambivalent attitude to religion throughout his life.
ex.cn: 他一生都对宗教抱有矛盾的心理。

m: AMBI- (both) + VALENT (value/worth) — feeling pulled BOTH ways. — ambivalent, having mixed feelings.

k.syn: anachronic, anachronous
k.ant: accurate 准确的
k.en: chronologically misplaced

ex.en: Despite the occasional anachronistic word or concept, he has a good feel for the period.
ex.cn: 尽管有时会犯一些语言和概念上的时代性错误，但他对于那个时代的整体把握还是不错的。

m: ANA- (against) + CHRON (time, as in "chronological") — against the times, out of place in time. — anachronistic, out of its proper time.

k.syn: akin, cognate, comparable, connate, corresponding, matching, parallel, resembling, similar, suchlike
k.ant: different, disparate, dissimilar, diverse, unlike 不同的，不相似的
k.en: having qualities in common

ex.en: Bad-mouthing your sister is analogous to slapping her in the face - it's just as bad.
ex.cn: 说你姐姐的坏话和当面扇她耳光是类似的——它们都是不好的行为。

m: contains ANALOGY — analogous = forming an analogy = similar. — analogous, similar / comparable.

k.syn: adjoin, affix, append, attach, subjoin, tack
k.ant: abate, deduct, remove, subtract 削减
k.en: to join（something）to a mass, quantity, or number so as to bring about an overall increase

ex.en: plan to annex the supply room so as to make the classroom bigger
ex.cn: 计划将工具房并入教室，从而扩充其容量

k.syn: acquire, appropriate, arrogate, expropriate, seize, take over
k.ant: lose 失去
k.en: to incorporate（a country or other territory）within the domain of a state

ex.en: Rome annexed the Nabatean kingdom in 106 AD.
ex.cn: 公元前106年罗马帝国吞并了古城那巴顿。

m: contains "NEX(T)" — to annex a territory = to attach it next to your own. — annex, to add / append.

k.syn: commentate, footnote, gloss
k.en: to furnish（a literary work）with critical commentary or explanatory notes

ex.en: The advent of e-books enables user to freely annotate what they are reading without worrying about being fined by libraries.
ex.cn: 电子书的出现使得用户可以在不用担心被图书馆处以罚金的情况下自由地在读物上作注。

m: contains NOTE — to anno-NOTE = add notes to a text. — annotate, to add notes.

k.syn: antecede, forego, predate, preexist
k.ant: follow, postdate, succeed 晚于
k.en: to be of an earlier date than

ex.en: The church antedates the village itself.
ex.cn: 这座教堂甚至在村庄之前就存在了。

ex.en: Dinosaurs antedate cavemen by millions of years.
ex.cn: 恐龙比穴居人要早几百万年出现。

m: ANTE (before, as in "ante up" in poker) + DATE — to predate. — antedate, to come before in time.

k.syn: animosity, animus, antagonism, bitterness, enmity, gall, grudge, hostility, jaundice, rancor
k.ant: amity 友好，和睦；liking, partiality, predilection, prepossession 偏爱，喜爱
k.en: settled aversion or dislike

ex.en: I feel no antipathy towards any of my opponents in the tournament.
ex.cn: 我对锦标赛中碰到的任何对手都不存在反感。

k.syn: abhorrence, abomination, anathema, aversion, detestation, execration
k.ant: beloved, darling, dear, love 令人喜爱的人或事物
k.en: an object of aversion

ex.en: Cruelty to animals is one of my most deeply felt antipathies.
ex.cn: 虐待动物是最让我反感的行为之一。

m: ANTI- (against) + PATHY (feeling, as in "sympathy") — opposite feeling = dislike. — antipathy, strong dislike.

k.syn: antique, archaic, dated, fossilized, moribund, moth-eaten, obsolete, outdated, outmoded, outworn, prehistoric, rusty
k.ant: contemporary, current, modern, recent 当前的，现代的
k.en: outmoded or discredited by reason of age: being out of style or fashion

ex.en: We saw an antiquated hand-cranked rope-making machine at the textiles museum.
ex.cn: 我们在纺织博物馆里看到了一台手摇式古董制绳机。

ex.en: antiquated methods of farming
ex.cn: 过时的耕种技术

m: contains ANTIQUE — antique-d, very old. — antiquated, outdated.

k.syn: antipodal, antipodean, contradictory, contrary, diametric, opposite, polar
k.ant: equivalent, identical, same 完全一致的，相等的
k.en: being in direct and unequivocal opposition

ex.en: Spiritual ideals seem antithetical to the materialism embraced by modern society.
ex.cn: 精神上的理想境界往往同当代社会所推崇的唯物主义相抵触。

m: contains ANTITHESIS — antithetical = forming the antithesis (opposite). — antithetical, directly opposed.

k.syn: ghost, phantasm, phantom, shade, shadow, specter, spirit, spook, sprite, vision, visitant, wraith
k.ant: entity, substance 实体
k.en: a ghostly figure

ex.en: An eccentric claimed to have photographed an apparition in her very own house.
ex.cn: 一个行为怪异的人声称在她的住所里拍到了一张幽灵的照片。

m: contains APPEAR — an APPARITION is a sudden ghostly APPEARance. — apparition, a ghostly appearance.

k.syn: actionable, applicative, applied, functional, practicable, serviceable, useful, workable
k.ant: impractical, inapplicable, unusable, unworkable, useless 不可用的，不可行的
k.en: capable of being put to use or account

ex.en: Is that Act applicable in this case where suspect is a foreigner?
ex.cn: 那条法规适用于这个案件么，案件中的嫌犯不是本国公民？

k.syn: apposite, apropos, germane, material, pointed, relative, relevant
k.ant: extraneous, immaterial, impertinent, inapposite, irrelevant, pointless 无关的
k.en: having to do with the matter at hand

ex.en: He rarely makes comment that is applicable to our discussion.
ex.cn: 他的评论很少和我们讨论的题目搭边。

k.syn: appropriate, apt, becoming, befitting, felicitous, fitting, meet, pretty, proper, right, suitable
k.ant: improper, inappropriate, inapt, incongruous, malapropos, misbecoming, unbecoming, unfitting, unmeet, unseemly, unsuitable, wrong 不恰当的
k.en: meeting the requirements of a purpose or situation

ex.en: He has been busy in selecting the most applicable word for his PhD thesis.
ex.cn: 他一直忙于为自己的博士论文选择最为恰当的词汇。

m: contains APPLY — what you can apply. — applicable, relevant.

k.syn: alive, aware, cognizant, mindful, sensible, sentient, ware, witting
k.ant: ignorant, insensible, oblivious, unaware, unconscious, unmindful, unwitting 不知晓的，无知觉的
k.en: having specified facts or feelings actively impressed on the mind

ex.en: I am fully apprehensive of the opinions, I assure you.
ex.cn: 我向你保证这些观点我都理解了。

k.syn: afraid, aghast, fearful, frightened, horrified, hysterical, scared, shocked, terrified
k.ant: dauntless, fearless, intrepid, unafraid 无所畏惧的；confident 充满自信的
k.der: apprehension n. 理解；忧虑，恐惧
k.en: anxious or fearful about the future

ex.en: People apparently haven't recovered from the devastating terrorist attack and are still terribly apprehensive about the future.
ex.cn: 人们显然没有从那场严重的恐怖袭击中恢复过来，对于未来仍然充满了恐惧。

m: contains APPREHEND — apprehensive = full of apprehension, anxious. — apprehensive, anxious.

k.syn: cultivable, tillable
k.ant: barren, sterile, infertile, unfruitful, unproductive 贫瘠的
k.en: fit for or used for the growing of crops

ex.en: explore the west for arable land
ex.cn: 向西探索适合耕种的土地

m: AR + ABLE — "earth-ABLE," land that's ABLE to grow crops. — arable, suitable for farming.

k.syn: dictatorial, imperious, peremptory, willful
k.en: having or showing a tendency to force one's will on others without any regard to fairness or necessity

ex.en: an arbitrary piano teacher who makes all her students do the same exercises over and over again
ex.cn: 一个让学生反复练习相同曲子的专横的老师

k.syn: autocratic, czarist, despotic, monocratic, tyrannic, tyrannous
k.ant: limited（权力）受限的；democratic 民主的；republican 共和国的
k.cn: 独裁的，拥有无限权力的
k.en: exercising power or authority without interference by others

ex.en: a nation with no tradition of democracy, only a long history of arbitrary rulers
ex.cn: 一个从来没有民主传统，只有长期的独裁统治的国家

k.syn: aimless, desultory, erratic, haphazard, scattered, slapdash, stray, willy-nilly
k.ant: methodical, orderly, organized, regular, systematic, systematized 有序的，有组织的
k.en: lacking a definite plan, purpose, or pattern

ex.en: The order of the names of the 10 semifinalists is entirely arbitrary.
ex.cn: 半决赛上十名上场选手的顺序完全是随机的。

m: contains ARBITER — a judge who decides on whim. — arbitrary, random / based on personal whim.

k.syn: ambiguous, dark, elliptical, equivocal, murky, mysterious, mystic, nebulous, occult, opaque
k.ant: accessible, clear, obvious, plain, unambiguous, unequivocal 清晰明了的，明确的
k.en: having an often intentionally veiled or uncertain meaning

ex.en: The rebate form uses arcane language, the only purpose of which seems to be to disqualify buyers from actually getting a rebate.
ex.cn: 折扣单上的条文晦涩难懂，这样做的唯一目的也许就是让消费者不能真正地享受到折扣。

k.syn: abstruse, cryptic, deep, enigmatic, esoteric, hermetic, impenetrable, inscrutable, recondite, uncanny
k.ant: easy, facile, shallow, simple, superficial 浅显易懂的
k.en: difficult for one of ordinary knowledge or intelligence to understand

ex.en: Grammatical rules seem extremely arcane to generations of students who were never taught grammar in the first place.
ex.cn: 对那些从未学习过语法的几代学生来说，语法规则犹如天书。

m: rhymes with INSANE — ARCANE knowledge is so deep it sounds insane to outsiders. — arcane, mysterious / known only to few.

k.syn: altercation, controversy, disagreement, dispute, hassle, imbroglio, quarrel, squabble, tiff, wrangle
k.en: an often noisy or angry expression of differing opinions

ex.en: The couple's arguments were often loud enough to be heard all over the neighborhood.
ex.cn: 这对夫妻吵架的声音往往大到能被所有邻居听见。

k.syn: account, accounting, argumentation, case, explanation, rationale,
k.en: a coherent series of statements leading from a premise to a conclusion

ex.en: In his argument the author committed several logical fallacies which undermined its soundness.
ex.cn: 在该作者的论述中出现了若干逻辑谬误，从而降低了它的论证力度。

m: shares ARGU- with ARGUE — an ARGUMENT is what you ARGUE. — argument, dispute / line of reasoning.

k.syn: assertion, point, position, stand, thesis
k.en: an idea or opinion that is put forth in a discussion or debate

ex.en: It is and will always be my argument that we have too many problems here on earth to concern ourselves with manned trips to Mars.
ex.cn: 我现在和以后的观点都将会是：在我们解决地球上的问题之前，不应该花太多的心思考虑载人火星计划的事情。

m: REASONING contains REASON — the process of reasoning. — reasoning, logical argument.

k.syn: droughty, dry, sere, thirsty, waterless
k.ant: damp, dank, humid, moist, wet 湿润的，潮湿的
k.en: marked by little or no precipitation or humidity

ex.en: arid wastelands unfit for human habitation
ex.cn: 不适合人类居住的干燥荒原

k.syn: dreary, dull, flat, humdrum, jading, jejune, monochromatic, monotonous, pedestrian, stale, stodgy, tedious, wearisome
k.ant: absorbing, engaging, engrossing, gripping, interesting, intriguing, involving, riveting 有趣的，吸引人的
k.en: causing weariness, restlessness, or lack of interest

ex.en: a technically perfect but arid musical performance
ex.cn: 技术纯熟但音乐毫无感染力的表演

m: rhymes with ACID but with no moisture — ARID desert is bone-dry. — arid, dry / dull.

k.syn: arrangement, disposal, disposition, distribution, ordering, sequence, setup
k.ant: disorder, disorganization 混乱，无序
k.en: a regular and imposing grouping or arrangement

ex.en: a marching band's carefully choreographed array
ex.cn: 前进中的乐队所摆出的经过精心编排的队列阵形

k.syn: arrange, classify, codify, dispose, marshal, organize, range, systematize, draw up, lay out
k.ant: derange, disarrange, disarray, disorder, mess, rumple, upset 弄乱，打乱
k.en: to arrange or display in or as if in an array

ex.en: data arrayed in descending order
ex.cn: 按照降序排列的数据

ex.en: He arrayed his baseball cards in order of their rarity and consequent monetary value.
ex.cn: 他把他的棒球卡按照稀有程度以及随之附有的价值排列。

k.syn: adorn, beautify, bedeck, bedizen, blazon, embellish, emblaze, emboss, enrich, garnish, ornament, trim
k.ant: blemish, deface, disfigure, mar, scar, spoil 破坏，损毁
k.en: to dress or decorate especially in splendid or impressive attire

ex.en: He had already arrayed himself in his best clothes.
ex.cn: 他已经穿上了他最好的服装。

ex.en: a door arrayed for the holidays with a beautiful evergreen wreath
ex.cn: 在节日里被美丽的常青花环妆点的大门

m: rhymes with DISPLAY — an ARRAY is what's on dis-PLAY. — array, an ordered arrangement.

k.syn: abuse, bash, belabor, blast, castigate, excoriate, lambaste, savage, scathe, slam, trash, vituperate
k.ant: acclaim, commend, compliment, hail, laud, praise 表扬，称赞
k.en: to criticize harshly and usually publicly

ex.en: The union organizers assailed the chemical company for failing to provide a safe working environment.
ex.cn: 工会主席严厉批评这家化工企业不能为员工提供安全的生产环境。

k.syn: assault, attack, beset, charge, raid, rush, storm, strike, set on
k.ant: defend, guard, protect, shield 防御，守卫
k.en: to take sudden, violent action against

ex.en: He was assailed by a young man with a knife.
ex.cn: 他被一个持刀的年轻人袭击。

m: contains SAIL — an assailant SAILs in to attack. — assail, to attack.

k.syn: disproportion, imbalance
k.ant: symmetry 对称；balance, proportion 平衡
k.en: lack of balance or symmetry

ex.en: functional asymmetry of cerebral hemispheres
ex.cn: 大脑两半球功能的不对称性

m: A- (not) + SYMMETRY — lack of symmetry. — asymmetry, lack of balance.

k.syn: crumble, decay, decline, degenerate, descend, devolve, ebb, regress, retrograde, rot, sink, worsen
k.ant: ameliorate, improve, meliorate 改善，提升
k.en: to waste away; wither or deteriorate

ex.en: The years following the closing of the last textile mill, the town atrophied.
ex.cn: 随着最后一家纺织厂的倒闭，整个城镇开始衰退。

m: A- (without) + TROPHY (nourishment, as in Greek "trophe") — without nourishment, the muscle wastes away. — atrophy, to waste away.

k.syn: argue, authenticate, certify, corroborate, substantiate, support, testify, validate, verify, vindicate
k.ant: contradict, disprove, gainsay, rebut, refute 反驳，否定
k.en: to give evidence or testimony to the truth or factualness of

ex.en: Her fine work attests her ability.
ex.cn: 她的优秀作品能够证明她的能力。

ex.en: I can attest that she was at the party.
ex.cn: 我可以证明她当时在聚会上。

m: contains TEST — to attest = to testify, give witness. — attest, to give witness / confirm.

k.syn: accommodate, conciliate, conform, coordinate, key, reconcile
k.ant: disharmonize 使不和谐；disarray, disorder, disorganize, disrupt 弄乱，打乱
k.en: to bring into harmony

ex.en: After years spent in academia, he found it difficult to attune himself to the corporate culture.
ex.cn: 在学术圈摸爬滚打多年之后，他发现自己很难融入企业文化之中。

m: AT + TUNE — to bring into TUNE with each other. — attune, to bring into harmony.

k.syn: distinguished, imposing, portly, solemn, staid, stately
k.ant: flighty, frivolous, giddy, goofy, silly, undignified 轻浮的，不庄重的
k.en: having or showing a formal and serious or reserved manner

ex.en: Unsurprisingly, the head of the bank is an august white-haired gentleman.
ex.cn: 不出所料，银行的总裁是一名白发苍苍的、令人顿生敬意的绅士。

k.syn: baronial, epic, grandiose, imperial, magnificent, majestic, massive, monumental, noble, regal, splendid
k.ant: common, humble, mediocre, ordinary, unimposing, unimpressive 普通的，一般的
k.cn: 盛大的，令人印象深刻的
k.en: large and impressive in size, grandeur, extent, or conception

ex.en: an august golden anniversary celebration for the company
ex.cn: 该公司盛大的周年庆祝会

m: the month AUGUST is named after Caesar Augustus — majestic, dignified. — august, majestic / dignified.

k.syn: bright, encouraging, golden, heartening, hopeful, promising, propitious
k.ant: baleful, dark, direful, doomy, foreboding, gloomy, menacing, minatory, ominous, portentous, sinister, threatening 不祥的，凶兆的
k.en: pointing toward a happy outcome

ex.en: They began the season with an auspicious win against their strongest football rival.
ex.cn: 他们在赛季之初就战胜了最强的对手，为整个赛季赢得了好兆头。

m: AUSPICIOUS shares root with AUGUR — favorable signs from the birds. An auspicious start = good omen. — auspicious, favorable / promising.

k.syn: plain, spartan, stark, unadorned
k.ant: deluxe, lavish, luxurious, plush, sumptuous 奢华的；elaborate, fancy 花哨的
k.en: markedly simple or unadorned

ex.en: For the private office of the CEO of the large corporation, the room is unexpectedly austere.
ex.cn: 出人意料的是，这家大公司总裁的私人办公室居然十分朴素。

k.syn: dour, fierce, flinty, forbidding, formidable, gruff, intimidating, lowering, rough, rugged, severe, steely
k.ant: benign, benignant, gentle, mild, tender 和蔼的，温柔的
k.en: stern and cold in appearance or manner

ex.en: an austere fortress at the top of some formidable cliffs
ex.cn: 建立在悬崖峭壁之上的一座令人畏惧的堡垒

ex.en: In his memory his deceased grandfather is an austere, distant, cold person.
ex.cn: 在他的记忆之中，已过世的祖父是一个严肃、冷漠而难以接近的人。

m: rhymes with SEVERE — austere living is severe, stripped-down. — austere, severely plain / stern.

k.syn: automated, robotic, self-acting, self-operating, self-regulating
k.ant: manual 手动的
k.en: acting or operating in a manner essentially independent of external influence or control

ex.en: Will your next car be a manual or an automatic?
ex.cn: 你下辆车是手动挡还是自动挡？

ex.en: Modern subways are equipped with automatic screen doors.
ex.cn: 现代地铁都装备了自动屏蔽门。

k.syn: impulsive, instinctive, involuntary, mechanic, mechanical, natural, spontaneous
k.ant: calculated, deliberate, intentional, planned, predetermined, premeditated, studied 有计划的，蓄意的
k.en: acting or done spontaneously or unconsciously

ex.en: Carl's automatic use of the brakes narrowly averted a collision.
ex.cn: 卡尔不经意间踩了刹车勉强避免了一场车祸。

m: AUTO (self) + MATIC (acting) — self-acting. — automatic, self-operating / involuntary.

k.syn: affirmation, assertion, asseveration, avouchment, claim, declaration, insistence, profession
k.ant: disavowal 否认，否定
k.en: a solemn and often public declaration of the truth or existence of something

ex.en: With jingoism rampant, the peace candidate felt compelled to make an avowal of his patriotism.
ex.cn: 在沙文主义肆虐的情况下，这位爱好和平的候选人被迫公开承认自己是个爱国主义者。

m: contains VOW — an avowal is a solemn vow / public declaration. — avowal, an open declaration.

k.syn: boomerang
k.ant: succeed 成功
k.cn: 产生相反的结果，事与愿违
k.en: to have the reverse of the desired or expected effect

ex.en: My plan to throw her a surprise party backfired when she ended up sobbing that everyone had forgotten her birthday.
ex.cn: 我本想为她的生日开一个聚会作为惊喜，但没想到事与愿违，她啜泣着说所有人都忘记了她的生日。

m: BACK + FIRE — the gun fires backward at the shooter; the plan backfires. — backfire, to produce the opposite of the intended result.

k.syn: degenerate, lapse, recidivate, relapse, retrograde, retrogress
k.ant: advance, progress 进步，前进
k.en: to revert to a worse condition

ex.en: Keep these things in mind to help prevent you from backsliding.
ex.cn: 牢记这些东西，它们能防止你旧病复发。

m: BACK + SLIDE — sliding backward, regressing into bad habits. — backslide, to regress.

k.syn: balk, beat, check, discomfit, foil, frustrate, thwart
k.ant: advance, cultivate, encourage, forward, foster, further, nurture, promote 促进，鼓舞
k.en: to frustrate or check（a person）as by confusing or perplexing

ex.en: baffled by the language barrier
ex.cn: 因语言障碍而受阻

k.syn: befog, befuddle, bewilder, confound, discombobulate, disorient, maze, mystify, perplex, puzzle, vex
k.ant: clarify, elucidate 澄清，阐明
k.en: to throw into a state of mental uncertainty

ex.en: I was baffled by many of the scientific terms used in the article.
ex.cn: 这篇文章中的各种学术词汇让我很困惑。

m: to BAFFLE = to confuse like a riddle. — baffle, to confuse.

k.syn: befuddling, bewildering, confounding, confusing, enigmatic, puzzling, perplexing
k.ant: apparent, distinct, evident, lucid, manifest, obvious, patent, pellucid, perspicuous, plain, transparent 明确的，明显的
k.cn: 令人疑惑的，难以理解的
k.en: making great mental demands; hard to comprehend or solve

ex.en: I was constantly ill, with a baffling array of symptoms.
ex.cn: 我常常生病，伴随着一系列令人困惑的症状。

m: from BAFFLE — confusing. — baffling, perplexing.

k.syn: bang, bat, beat, crack, hit, knock, lash, poke, pound, punch, slam, slap, smash, spank, stroke, swat
k.en: a forceful blow

ex.en: He has not been the same ever since he received that bash on his head.
ex.cn: 自从他脑袋被别人打了之后，他就变得大不一样。

k.syn: baste, batter, buffet, hammer, lace, maul, nail, smash, strike
k.en: to strike violently and often repeatedly

ex.en: The angry child kept bashing her toy with a hammer until it broke.
ex.cn: 愤怒的小孩用锤子不断地敲击她的玩具，直到它彻底被破坏。

k.syn: abuse, assail, belabor, blast, castigate, excoriate, lambaste, savage, scathe, slam, trash, vituperate
k.ant: acclaim, commend, compliment, hail, laud, praise 表扬，称赞
k.en: to criticize harshly and usually publicly

ex.en: In all of talk radio no other host seems to enjoy bashing liberals as much as he does.
ex.cn: 在所有电台节目中再没有像他一样热衷于抨击自由主义者的主持人了。

m: BASH onomatopoeia — the SOUND of a hard hit. (Also "to bash" a politician = trash-talk.) — bash, to strike / criticize harshly.

k.syn: loll, lounge, relax, repose
k.ant: drudge, grind, hustle, labor, moil, plod, slave, sweat, toil, travail, work（辛苦地）干活，工作
k.en: to lie or relax in a pleasant warmth or atmosphere

ex.en: We blissfully basked at the seashore over the long holiday.
ex.cn: 我们在海滩上躺着晒太阳，享受着漫长的假期。

m: rhymes with TASK but the opposite mood — instead of working at a TASK, you BASK in the sun. — bask, to lounge enjoyably.

k.syn: indebted, obligated, obliged
k.en: owing something, such as gratitude, to another

ex.en: Not wanting to be beholden to anyone, he insisted on paying his own way.
ex.cn: 因为不想欠别人的人情，所以他坚持出自己的那一份钱。

m: BE + HOLDEN (held) — held in someone's debt, obliged to them. — beholden, indebted.

k.syn: befit, beseem, fit, serve, suite
k.en: to be necessary, proper or advantageous for

ex.en: It behooves you at least to try.
ex.cn: 你至少也应该尝试一下。

m: BE + HOOVE — sounds like "it BE-HOOVES you" = it's fitting / proper for you. — behoove, to be appropriate for.

k.syn: characterless, faceless, featureless, indistinctive, neutral, noncommital, vanilla
k.ant: diagnostic, discriminating, distinct, distinctive, distinguishing, identifying, peculiar, typical 有特征的
k.en: lacking distinction

ex.en: Some food critics have dismissed that chef's version of French cuisine as beige and boring.
ex.cn: 一些美食评论家认为那名厨师所做的法国菜平凡无奇，令人厌倦。

m: BEIGE rhymes with PRESTIGE but the opposite — the most boring color, no prestige. — beige, a featureless tan / dull.

k.syn: aggressiveness, assaultiveness, bellicosity, combativeness, contentiousness, disputatiousness, feistiness, militance, pugnacity, quarrelsomeness, scrappiness, truculence
k.ant: pacifism 爱好和平
k.en: an aggressive or truculent attitude, atmosphere, or disposition

ex.en: Among the Native American tribes of the colonial period, the Iroquois were known for their belligerence.
ex.cn: 在殖民时期的美洲土著部落中，易洛魁人因为他们的好战而著名。

m: contains BELLI (war, as in "rebellion") — warlike attitude. — belligerence, aggressive hostility.

k.syn: aggressive, agonistic, assaultive, bellicose, combative, contentious, disputatious, feisty, gladiatorial, militant, pugnacious, quarrelsome, truculent, warlike
k.ant: dove, pacific, peaceful 爱好和平的
k.en: inclined to or exhibiting assertiveness, hostility, or combativeness

ex.en: The coach became quite belligerent and spit at an umpire after being thrown out of the game.
ex.cn: 教练变得好斗起来，以至于在被罚出场外之后转身向裁判吐口水。

m: from BELLIGERENCE — warlike, eager to fight. — belligerent, hostile / warlike.

k.syn: agonize, anguish, bedevil, besiege, curse, excruciate, harrow, persecute, plague, rack, torment, torture
k.ant: comfort, console, solace, soothe 安抚，安慰
k.en: to cause persistent suffering to

ex.en: He has been beset by a lack of self-confidence virtually his entire life.
ex.cn: 事实上他的一生都被缺乏自信所困扰。

k.syn: assail, assault, attack, charge, raid, rush, storm, strike, set on
k.ant: defend, guard, protect, shield 防御，守卫
k.en: to set upon

ex.en: The settlers were beset by savages.
ex.cn: 这些移民遭到了野蛮人的袭击。

ex.en: The unsuspecting tourists were suddenly beset by robbers.
ex.cn: 丝毫没有准备的游客遭到了歹徒的突然袭击。

m: BE + SET — set upon by attackers / problems. — beset, to harass / attack.

k.syn: alluring, appealing, attractive, captivating, charismatic, enchanting, engaging, entrancing, glamorous, luring, magnetic, seductive
k.ant: repellent, repelling, repugnant, repulsive, revolting 令人反感的
k.en: having an often mysterious or magical power to attract
