m: from HYPOTHESIS — based on a hypothesis ("sub-thesis"). — hypothetical, supposed.

k.syn: crotchet, eccentricity, individualism, mannerism, oddity, peculiarity, quirk, singularity, trick, twist
k.ant: conformity, sameness 合乎（礼节等），千篇一律
k.en: an odd or peculiar habit

ex.en: His only idiosyncrasy is his inveterate wearing of sneakers, even with business suits.
ex.cn: 他唯一的怪癖就是无时无刻都穿着运动鞋——哪怕身上是西装革履。

m: IDIO- (one's own, Greek) + SYNCRASY — one's own peculiar mix. — idiosyncrasy, a personal quirk.

k.syn: dead, dormant, fallow, free, inert, inoperative, latent, unused, vacant
k.ant: active, alive, employed, functioning, operative, running, working 使用中的
k.en: not turned to normal or appropriate use

ex.en: The car was idle for two weeks while they went on vacation.
ex.cn: 因为他们出去旅行了，他们的车闲置了两周。

k.syn: indolent, lazy, shiftless, slothful
k.ant: diligent, industrious 勤奋的
k.en: shiftless, lazy

ex.en: an idle employee who always seems to be either on break or at lunch
ex.cn: 一个懒散的员工似乎不是在休息就是在吃饭

k.syn: dally, dillydally, drone, laze, loaf, loll, lounge
k.ant: drudge, grind, hustle, labor, moil, plod, slave, sweat, toil, travail, work（辛苦地）干活，工作
k.en: to pass（time）without working or while avoiding work

ex.en: idle the afternoon away
ex.cn: 打发下午的时光

m: IDLE rhymes with IDOL — but where IDOLs are revered, IDLE engines just sit doing nothing. — idle, inactive.

k.syn: discourteous, disrespectful, impertinent, impolite, inconsiderate, rude, uncivil, ungracious, unmannerly
k.ant: civil, considerate, courteous, genteel, gracious, mannerly, polite, urbane, well-bred 有礼貌的，得体的
k.cn: 没有教养的，没有礼貌的
k.en: badly brought up or showing bad upbringing: impolite

ex.en: Only an ill-bred, conceited person would demand that everyone cater to his whims.
ex.cn: 只有一个没有教养、狂妄自大的人才会要求所有人都去迎合他的想法。

m: ILL + BRED — badly raised, no manners. — ill-bred, having poor upbringing.

k.syn: distinguished, luminous, noble, notable, noteworthy, outstanding, preeminent, prestigious, redoubtable, signal
k.ant: average, inferior, mediocre 平凡的，较差的
k.en: well known and very distinguished

ex.en: the most illustrious scientists of the century
ex.cn: 本世纪最为杰出的科学家

m: ILLUSTRIOUS contains LUSTR (shine, Latin) — shining with fame. — illustrious, famous / distinguished.

k.syn: inextinguishable
k.ant: destructible, extinguishable, perishable 可被毁灭的
k.en: impossible to destroy; not subject to decay

ex.en: Energy is imperishable.
ex.cn: 能量本身是不能被毁灭的。

k.syn: ageless, continuing, eternal, everlasting, immortal, lasting, perennial, perpetual, timeless, undying
k.ant: emphemeral, evanescent, fleeting, transient, transitory 稍纵即逝的
k.en: enduring or occurring forever

ex.en: My memories are within me, imperishable.
ex.cn: 我的回忆已经和我融为一体，永恒不变。

m: IM (not) + PERISHABLE — not able to perish, lasting forever. — imperishable, indestructible.

k.syn: implied, unexpressed, unspoken, unvoiced, wordless
k.ant: explicit, expressed, spoken, stated, voiced 明确声明的
k.cn: 不言而喻的，心照不宣的
k.en: capable of being understood from something else though unexpressed

ex.en: The implicit agreement among members of the outing club is that everyone pays his or her own way on all trips.
ex.cn: 野外俱乐部成员之间一条心照不宣的约定就是：沿途所有开销都AA。

k.syn: assured, clear, confident, doubtless, positive, sanguine, sure
k.ant: doubtful, dubious, uncertain, unsure 不确定的，存有疑惑的
k.en: being without doubt or reserve

ex.en: Members of the expedition must have implicit trust in their leaders.
ex.cn: 远征队员必须百分之百信任他们的领队。

m: IMPLICIT contains PLICIT (folded) — folded inside, unspoken. (Compare EXPLICIT = unfolded.) — implicit, unstated / unquestioning.

k.syn: charlatan, fake, fraud, hoaxer, humbug, mountebank, phony, pretender, quack, quacksalver, ringer, sham
k.en: one that assumes false identity or title for the purpose of deception

ex.en: The man who claimed to be my townsman turned out to be an impostor.
ex.cn: 那个自称是我老乡的人结果是个骗子。

m: IMPOSTER contains IMPOST (impose) — one who imposes a false identity. — imposter, an impersonator.

k.syn: exemption, immunity
k.ant: liability 责任
k.en: exemption or freedom from punishment, harm, or loss

ex.en: She mistakenly believed that she could insult people with impunity.
ex.cn: 她错误地认为她可以肆无忌惮地羞辱他人，不受处罚。

m: IM (not) + PUNITY (punishment, as in PUNISH) — without punishment. — impunity, exemption from punishment.

k.syn: boost, goad, impetus, incitement, instigation, momentum, motivation, motive, provocation, spur, stimulant, stimulus, yeast
k.ant: deterrent, disincentive 抑制剂，阻碍物
k.en: something that incites or has a tendency to incite to determination or action

ex.en: The handsome reward for the missing wallet was an incentive for me to start looking.
ex.cn: 找到丢失钱包的可观报酬激励我展开搜寻。

ex.en: A little bonus will give employees an incentive to work harder.
ex.cn: 一点点奖金就可以刺激雇员更加努力地工作。

m: INCENTIVE contains CENT- ≈ "sing into action" (Latin INCANERE = to set the tune); a spur that gets you moving. — incentive, a motivator.

k.syn: compulsory, forced, imperative, involuntary, necessary, obligatory, peremptory, required
k.ant: elective, optional, voluntary 可选择的
k.en: imposed as an obligation or duty

ex.en: It is incumbent upon individuals to sacrifice for their country when it is in danger.
ex.cn: 当国家受难时，个人应该义不容辞地为国做出牺牲。

ex.en: It is incumbent upon the press to act not in its own best interests, but in society's best interests.
ex.cn: 对于媒体来说，为社会的利益（而非自身利益）服务是其应尽的义务。

m: INCUMBENT contains CUMB ≈ "cumbersome duty" — a duty resting on you (Latin INCUMBERE = lie upon). — incumbent, obligatory.

k.syn: amiss, graceless, improper, inapposite, infelicitous, malapropos, perverse, unbecoming, unfit, unseemly, unsuitable, wrong
k.ant: appropriate, becoming, befitting, decorous, felicitous, fit, genteel, meet, proper, seemly, suitable 得体的，合乎礼节的
k.cn: 不合乎礼节的，不得体的
k.en: conflicting with accepted standards of good conduct or good taste

ex.en: How can you make such an indecorous joke for a solemn moment in the marriage ceremony?
ex.cn: 你怎么能在庄严肃穆的结婚典礼上开那样一个不得体的玩笑呢？

m: IN (not) + DECOROUS (proper) — not proper. — indecorous, improper.

k.syn: breach, contravention, infringement, transgression, trespass, violation
k.ant: observance 遵守
k.en: a failure to uphold the requirements of law, duty, or obligation

ex.en: Speeding is only a minor infraction, but vehicular homicide is a serious felony.
ex.cn: 超速只是小的违章，但是用机动车故意杀人就是重罪了。

m: IN + FRACTION — a small FRACTURE of the rules. — infraction, a violation.

k.syn: excuse, rationalize, warrant
k.en: to prove or show to be just, right, or reasonable

ex.en: fail to justify the need for a new expressway at this time
ex.cn: 没能证明当前修建新的高速公路的合理性

ex.en: The storm warning justified his leaving early.
ex.cn: 他的早退因为风暴预警而显得理所当然。

m: JUSTIFY contains JUST + IFY — to make it appear JUST. — justify, to show as right.

k.syn: bottommost, closing, concluding, final, hindmost, last, latest, rearmost, terminal, terminating, ultimate
k.ant: beginning, earliest, first, foremost, inaugural, initial, maiden, opening, original, pioneer, primary 最初的
k.en: following all others of the same kind in order or time

ex.en: We're now in the lag end of the project.
ex.cn: 现在我们到了项目的尾声阶段。

k.syn: crawl, creep, dally, dawdle, dillydally, linger, loiter, poke, tarry
k.ant: blast, bolt, bustle, careen, career, dash, fly, hustle, rush, rustle, speed, whirl 疾行，快速行走
k.en: to proceed or develop with comparative slowness

ex.en: The tired puppy was lagging behind the rest of the pack.
ex.cn: 疲倦的小狗在队伍的后面缓缓地走着。

k.syn: decay, droop, emaciate, fade, fail, languish, sink, waste, wilt, wither, mope
k.ant: convalesce, rally, rebound, recover, recuperate 康复，复原
k.en: to lose bodily strength or vigor

ex.en: During the fourth quarter the whole team seemed to lag.
ex.cn: 到了第四节整支队伍似乎都已经精疲力竭了。

m: LAG rhymes with DRAG — both slow you down. — lag, to fall behind.

k.syn: javelin, pike, shaft, spear
k.der: lancer n.（19世纪法国的）枪骑兵
k.en: a weapon with a long straight handle and sharp head or blade

ex.en: The lance struck squarely on the knight's shield, knocking him from his horse.
ex.cn: 标枪正中骑士的盾牌，将他掀于马下。

k.syn: gore, harpoon, jab, pierce, puncture, spike, stab, stick
k.en: to penetrate or hold（something）with a pointed object

ex.en: Doctors used to lance infected sores, so that they could drain clean.
ex.cn: 以前医生会刺破受感染的疮，从而使脓液流出。

m: LANCE — a long pointed weapon (also a freelance / a lancer). — lance, a long spear.

k.syn: flabby, flaccid, insecure, loose, loosened, relaxed, slackened, unsecured
k.ant: taut, tense, tight 紧固的，牢固的；hard, harsh, rigid, rigorous, severe, stern, strict 严格的
k.cn: 松弛的，不紧的，不严格的
k.en: not tense, firm, or rigid

ex.en: exercises to improve lax muscles
ex.cn: 改善松弛的肌肉的锻炼

ex.en: The guidelines for the essay contest were fairly lax, permitting a wide variety of topics.
ex.cn: 本次作文大赛的要求相对比较宽松，允许写多种类型的主题。

k.syn: careless, derelict, disregardful, heedless, lazy, neglectful, neglecting, negligent, remiss, slack
k.ant: attentive, careful, cautious, conscientious, heedful, mindful, vigilant, wary 注意的，留心的
k.en: failing to give proper care and attention

ex.en: The university has been lax about enforcing these rules.
ex.cn: 学校对于这些规定的施行显得漫不经心。

m: LAX rhymes with WAX — both soften and slacken when warmed. — lax, slack / not strict.

k.syn: allowance, authorization, clearance, concurrence, consent, empowerment, granting, sanction, warrant
k.ant: interdiction, prohibition, proscription 禁止
k.en: the approval by someone in authority for the doing of something

ex.en: The company is seeking license to operate several more power plants in the state.
ex.cn: 公司正在寻求在国内多建造几个电厂的许可。

ex.en: A restaurant owner has to get a license to serve food and drink.
ex.cn: 餐厅营业者必须获得食物和饮品的销售许可。

k.syn: freedom, latitude, liberty
k.ant: confinement, custody, imprisonment 拘留，囚禁
k.en: the right to act or move freely

ex.en: Military commanders on the ground must be granted considerable license, as wars cannot be micromanaged by people back in Washington.
ex.cn: 战地指挥官应该被赋予相当大的自由，因为一场战争只靠华盛顿的人是不可能打赢的，他们无法进行具体指挥。

k.syn: debauchery, hedonism, libertinage, libertinism, licentiousness, profligacy, voluptuousness
k.ant: abstinence, asceticism, sobriety, temperance 节制，克制
k.en: disregard for standards of personal conduct

ex.en: a night of drinking and license at the nightclub
ex.cn: 在夜店里放纵饮酒取乐的一夜

k.syn: accredit, certify, commission, empower, enable, invest, qualify, vest, warrant
k.ant: disqualify 取消资格；forbid, interdict, prohibit, proscribe, veto 禁止
k.en: to permit or authorize especially by formal license

ex.en: A state statute licenses county sheriffs to choose their own deputies.
ex.cn: 州法律允许郡上的司法长官选择自己的副手。

ex.en: licensed to use deadly force
ex.cn: 获准使用致命火力

m: LICENSE contains LIC (allow, Latin LICET) — what's allowed. — license, permission / freedom.

k.syn: aggrandize, canonize, deify, dignify, elevate, ennoble, enshrine, ensky, enthrone, glorify, magnify
k.ant: abase, degrade, demean, humble, humiliate 贬低，瞧不起
k.en: to look on or treat（a person）as a celebrity

ex.en: She was lionized everywhere after her novel won the Pulitzer Prize.
ex.cn: 在她的小说获得普利策奖之后，她无论走到哪都受到追捧。

m: LIONIZE contains LION — treat as a LION (king of beasts), celebrity. — lionize, to treat as celebrity.

k.syn: absurd, comical, derisive, derisory, farcical, laughable, ridiculous, pathetic, preposterous, risible, silly
k.ant: logical, rational, reasonable, sensible 有道理的
k.en: meriting derisive laughter or scorn as absurdly inept, false, or foolish

ex.en: He made a ludicrous and easily detected attempt to forge his father's signature on a note to school.
ex.cn: 他试图在要上交学校的纸条上伪造父亲的签名，这实在是既可笑又容易被发现的花招。

m: LUDICROUS contains LUD ≈ "ludo" (play, Latin LUDUS = game) — playfully absurd. — ludicrous, ridiculously absurd.

k.syn: beaming, bright, candescent, dazzling, effulgent, glowing, incandescent, lambent, lucent, lucid, lustrous, radiant, refulgent, shiny, splendid
k.ant: dim, dull, lackluster 黯淡无光的
k.en: emitting or reflecting usually steady, suffused, or glowing light

ex.en: The luminous moon bathed the snow-covered fields with a pearly glow.
ex.cn: 在明亮的月光下，雪地仿佛被罩上了一层珍珠般的光晕。

k.syn: astral, brilliant, distinguished, illustrious, notable, noteworthy, preeminent, prestigious, redoubtable, signal
k.ant: average, inferior, mediocre 一般的，中庸的
k.en: standing above others in rank, importance, or achievement

ex.en: some of the most luminous writers in the nation's history
ex.cn: 该国历史上最杰出的一批作家

m: LUMINOUS contains LUMIN (light) — giving off light. — luminous, glowing.

k.syn: appalling, atrocious, dreadful, frightful, ghastly, gruesome, hideous, horrid, horrific, macabre, monstrous, nightmarish
k.ant: agreeable, appealing, delicious, delightful, enjoyable, enticing, inviting, pleasant, satisfying 令人愉悦的
k.en: causing horror or revulsion

ex.en: We quickly drove past the lurid scene of the crash.
ex.cn: 我们迅速驶过了可怕的车祸现场。

k.syn: ashen, ashy, blanched, cadaverous, doughy, livid, mealy, paled, pallid, pasty, peaked, wan
k.ant: blooming, florid, flush, full-blooded, glowing, rosy, rubicund, ruddy, sanguine 红润的，健康的
k.en: wan and ghastly pale in appearance

ex.en: The doctor was alarmed by the patient's lurid complexion.
ex.cn: 病人惨白的面色给医生敲响了警钟。

m: LURID contains LURI ≈ "lure-id" — sensational details that LURE attention. — lurid, sensational / shockingly vivid.

k.syn: conspiracy, design, intrigue, plot, scheme
k.en: a scheming or crafty action or artful design intended to accomplish some usually evil end

ex.en: The incredibly complicated machination to assassinate the president inevitably failed.
ex.cn: 刺杀总统的诡计极其复杂，最终不可避免地失败了。

m: MACHINATION contains MACHINE — engineering a clever scheme. — machination, a crafty plot.

k.syn: daredevil, madman
k.en: a person who seeks out very dangerous or foolhardy adventures with no apparent fear

ex.en: an incorrigible madcap who loves drag racing and white-water rafting
ex.cn: 一个热爱飙车和激流竹筏的无药可救的冒险狂

k.syn: audacious, bold, brash, daredevil, overbold, overconfident, reckless, temerarious
k.ant: careful, cautious, circumspect, guarded, heedful, prudent, wary 谨慎的，小心的
k.en: behaving or acting impulsively or rashly; foolishly adventurous or bold

ex.en: They switched from one madcap scheme to another.
ex.cn: 他们从一个鲁莽的方案换到了另一个。

m: MAD + CAP — wearing a CAP of MADness, recklessly daring. — madcap, recklessly impulsive.

k.syn: account, consequence, importance, moment, momentousness, significance, weight, weightiness
k.ant: insignificance, littleness, puniness, slightness, smallness, triviality 不重要
k.en: greatness in significance or influence

ex.en: The magnitude of the issue is severely underestimated.
ex.cn: 这个事件的重要性被严重低估了。

k.syn: enormousness, giantism, gigantism, hugeness, immenseness, massiveness, prodigiousness, vastness
k.ant: diminutiveness, minuteness, tininess 微小
k.en: greatness in size or extent

ex.en: The mountain's sheer magnitude usually leaves tourists speechless.
ex.cn: 巨大的山脉往往让旅游者哑口无言。

m: MAGNITUDE shares MAGN- with MAGNIFY — great in size or importance. — magnitude, great size / importance.

k.syn: cruel, despiteful, evil, malicious, malign, malignant, mean, nasty, spiteful, vicious, virulent
k.ant: benevolent, benign, benignant 善意的，和善的
k.en: having, showing, or arising from intense often vicious ill will, spite, or hatred

ex.en: The novel grossly oversimplified the conflict as a struggle between relentlessly malevolent villains on one side and faultless saints on the other.
ex.cn: 小说把这场冲突彻底简化成凶残恶毒的坏人和完美无瑕的圣人间的斗争。

m: MAL- (bad) + VOLENT (wishing, Latin VELLE) — wishing harm. — malevolent, ill-wishing.

k.syn: atrocious, brutal, cruel, despiteful, malevolent, malicious, malign, mean, nasty, spiteful, vicious, virulent
k.ant: benevolent, benign, benignant 慈善的，和蔼的；merciful 仁慈的
k.en: having or showing a desire to cause someone pain or suffering for the sheer enjoyment of it; disposed to do evil

ex.en: She has a malignant wish to poison everyone who was smarter, richer, or better-looking than she was.
ex.cn: 她有一个恶毒的愿望，那就是毒死所有比她聪明、有钱或者是比她更漂亮的人。

m: from MALIGN — actively harmful (medical: malignant tumor). — malignant, dangerously harmful.

k.syn: contrive, finagle, finesse, frame, machinate, manipulate, mastermind, negotiate, wangle
k.ant: botch, bungle, fumble, mishandle, muff, scamp, mess up 弄砸
k.en: to guide with adroitness and design or to bring about or secure as a result of skillful management

ex.en: They maneuvered him into signing the contract.
ex.cn: 他们巧妙地诱使他签下合同。

ex.en: The host maneuvered the conversation so as to avoid the touchy subject of her divorce.
ex.cn: 主人巧妙地控制着对话的内容，以避免触及有关她离婚的敏感话题。

m: MANEUVER contains MAN (hand, Latin MANUS) + EUVER (work, Latin OPERA) — "handwork," skillful manipulation. — maneuver, to skillfully manipulate.

k.syn: frontier
k.ant: interior 内部的
k.en: located at or near a border

ex.en: Marginal locations in the open-air market are a bit cheaper.
ex.cn: 露天市场边缘的地段通常都要便宜些。

k.syn: inconsequential, inconsiderable, insignificant, minor, minute, negligible, nugatory, slight, trifling, trivial
k.ant: consequential, eventful, important, meaningful, momentous, significant, substantial, weighty 重要的
k.en: not of central importance

ex.en: regard violence as a marginal rather than a central problem
ex.cn: 并不把暴力问题当作重要的核心问题来看

m: MARGINAL contains MARGIN — at the edges, not central. — marginal, of the edges / unimportant.

k.syn: cogitate, consider, contemplate, deliberate, mull, perpend, ponder, ruminate, study, weigh, wrestle
k.ant: disregard, ignore, overlook, slight 忽视
k.en: to focus one's thoughts on

ex.en: meditate a visit to her professor
ex.cn: 思索着去拜访她的导师

ex.en: I have been meditating a career change for months.
ex.cn: 我这几个月一直在考虑换一个工作。

m: MEDITATE contains MEDI ≈ "medical-thought" — Latin MEDITARI = think over. (Different root from MEDIA but feels related to deep middle thought.) — meditate, to think deeply.

k.syn: domestic, retainer, steward
k.ant: lord, master, mistress 主人
k.en: a servant, especially a domestic servant

ex.en: Immigrants to that country faced fierce prejudice and could expect to find work only as menials.
ex.cn: 前往那个国家的移民遭到了严重的歧视，他们也许只能找到仆人一类的工作。

k.syn: base, humble, lowly, servile, slavish, subservient
k.ant: arrogant, haughty, imperious, lordly, supercilious, superior 自大的，狂妄的
k.en: showing, expressing, or offered in a spirit of humility or unseemly submissiveness

ex.en: low-paid menial jobs such as cleaning the street
ex.cn: 一些低收入的卑贱工作，比如扫大街

m: MENIAL contains MEN ≈ "household servants" — Old French MEINIE = household. Lowly servant work. — menial, lowly / servile.

k.syn: flamboyant, flaring, flashy, garish, gaudy, glaring, loud, ostentatious, tawdry
k.ant: conservative, quiet, understated, unflamboyant 低调的，不张扬的
k.en: attracting attention in a vulgar manner

ex.en: The paradise they found was no more than a meretricious wasteland of casinos and bars.
ex.cn: 他们发现的所谓“天堂”只不过是一块有着赌场和酒吧的俗气不堪的荒漠罢了。

m: MERETRICIOUS contains MERE + TRICIOUS — superficial "mere tricks," tawdry. (Latin MERETRIX = prostitute, hence superficially attractive.) — meretricious, gaudily superficial.

k.syn: ambient, atmosphere, climate, context, environment, environs, medium, setting, surroundings, terrain
k.en: the physical or social setting in which something occurs or develops

ex.en: Young, innovative artists thrive in the freewheeling milieu that a big city offers.
ex.cn: 在大都市所提供的自由氛围中，年轻而富有创造力的艺术家们在事业上蓬勃发展。

ex.en: a historical milieu conducive to democracy
ex.cn: 有助于民主制度的历史背景

m: MILIEU contains MILI ≈ "middle" + EU — the middle environment around you. (French "in the middle.") — milieu, surroundings / environment.

k.syn: martial, service
k.ant: civilian 民用的
k.en: of or relating to soldiers, arms, or war

ex.en: NATO struggles with how to proceed military operation against Libya.
ex.cn: 北约就如何延续针对利比亚的军事行动出现了内部分歧。

ex.en: an encyclopedia of modern military aircraft
ex.cn: 现代军用飞行器百科全书

m: MILITARY contains MILIT (soldier, Latin MILES) — relating to soldiers. — military, of armed forces.

k.syn: incomprehension, misconstruction, misconstruing, misimpression, misinterpretation, misknowledge, misreading, misunderstanding, miscalculation, misjudging, misjudgment, misstep, slip, slipup
k.en: a failure to understand correctly; a wrong judgement

ex.en: tried to eliminate all misapprehensions about the planned riverfront development
ex.cn: 尽可能消除所有关于河流沿岸发展计划的误解

ex.en: a common misapprehension about how our language functions
ex.cn: 有关我们的语言如何运作的常见误判

m: MIS- (wrong) + APPREHENSION (understanding) — wrong understanding. — misapprehension, a misunderstanding.

k.syn: autocrat, lord, potentate, ruler, sovereign
k.en: one who rules over a people with a sole, supreme, and usually hereditary authority

ex.en: The ruling monarch of Britain at that time was Queen Elizabeth Ⅰ.
ex.cn: 伊丽莎白一世是当时英国的君主。

k.syn: baron, captain, king, lion, lord, magnate, mogul, prince, tycoon
k.ant: nobody, nothing, zero 不起眼的人物
k.en: a person of rank, power, or influence in a particular field

ex.en: the reigning monarchs of the recording industry
ex.cn: 唱片业的巨头们

m: MONO (one) + ARCH (rule, Greek) — rule of one. — monarch, a sole ruler.

k.syn: control, dominate, govern, reign, rule, sew up
k.en: to have complete control over

ex.en: They are virtually monopolizing the market.
ex.cn: 他们实际上已经垄断了整个市场。

ex.en: To their surprise it was the vice president who monopolized the conversation.
ex.cn: 令他们惊讶的是，主导整个谈话的居然是副总裁。

m: MONO (one) + POL (sell, Greek POLEIN) — one seller controls all. — monopolize, to dominate.

k.syn: forgetfulness, obliviousness
k.ant: alertness, awareness, cognizance, consciousness 警觉，知觉
k.en: a state of being disregardful or unconscious of one's surroundings, concerns, or obligations

ex.en: The spa experience was a week of pure nirvana.
ex.cn: 去做了一周的温泉水疗，这种经历真是彻底的放松。

k.syn: empyrean, fantasyland, heaven, lotusland, utopia
k.ant: hell, inferno 地狱
k.en: an often imaginary place or state of utter perfection and happiness

ex.en: They believe in a continuous cycle of births and deaths until the soul is perfected and achieves nirvana.
ex.cn: 他们相信生命会不断轮回，直到灵魂变得完美并升入天堂。

m: NIRVANA — Sanskrit/Buddhist term for liberation, blowing out the flame of desire. — nirvana, blissful release.

k.syn: homesickness
k.en: the state of being homesick

ex.en: Overwhelmed by nostalgia, she started to cry.
ex.cn: 被思乡之情淹没的她开始啜泣。

k.syn: reminiscence
k.ant: oblivion 遗忘
k.en: a wistful or excessively sentimental yearning for return to or of some past period or irrecoverable condition

ex.en: He felt a wave of nostalgia for the life he had left behind him.
ex.cn: 他开始怀念往昔的时光。

m: NOSTALGIA contains NOSTOS (homecoming, Greek) + ALGIA (pain) — homesickness. — nostalgia, sentimental longing.

k.syn: figure, icon, luminary, megastar, name, notable, personage, standout, star, superstar
k.ant: nobody, noncelebrity 不知名的人
k.cn: （尤指因为丑闻而出名的）名人
k.en: a person who is widely known and usually much talked about, especially for something bad; a notorious person

ex.en: a television show featuring notorieties from 20 years of scandals
ex.cn: 以二十年来因各色丑闻而出名的名人为卖点的电视节目

k.syn: infamy, obloquy, odium, opprobrium
k.ant: anonymity, oblivion, obscurity 不知名，默默无闻；celebrity, fame, renown, repute 好名声
k.en: the quality or condition of being notorious; ill fame

ex.en: She gained notoriety when nude photographs of her appeared in a magazine.
ex.cn: 她的裸照出现在杂志上让她“声名远播”。

ex.en: His comment about the President has given him a notoriety that he enjoys very much.
ex.cn: 他对于总统的评论让他获得了很让他满意的“名声”。

m: NOTORIETY contains NOTORI ≈ NOTORIOUS — bad fame. — notoriety, fame for bad reasons.

k.syn: breed, foster, nurse, nurture, raise, rear
k.en: to provide with food or other substances necessary for life and growth

ex.en: He willingly nourished a child that was not his own.
ex.cn: 他主动抚养了一名不是他亲生的孩子。

k.syn: advance, cultivate, encourage, forward, further, incubate, promote
k.ant: discourage, frustrate, hinder, inhibit 阻碍，妨碍
k.en: to help the growth or development of

ex.en: a friendship nourished by trust
ex.cn: 在信任的基础之上开出的友谊之花

m: NOURISH contains NOUR ≈ "NUR(se)" — feed and care for. (Latin NUTRIRE = feed, same root as NUTRITION.) — nourish, to feed / nurture.

k.syn: apprentice, colt, fledgling, freshman, greenhorn, neophyte, newcomer, recruit, rook, tenderfoot, tyro, virgin
k.ant: doyen, maven, veteran 有经验的人
k.en: a person new to a field or activity

ex.en: a novice chess player
ex.cn: 象棋初学者

m: NOVICE contains NOV (new) — newly arrived beginner. — novice, a beginner.

k.syn: abolishment, abrogation, annulment, cancellation, invalidation, negation, repeal, rescindment, voiding
k.ant: enactment, legislation 立法；establishment, founding, institution 建立
k.cn: （尤指法律条文等正式的）废除，废弃
k.en: the doing away with something by formal action

ex.en: the nullification of a treaty
ex.cn: 废除条约

m: NULLIFICATION contains NULL — making NULL. — nullification, formal cancellation.

k.syn: asleep, benumbed, dead, insensitive, numbed, torpid, unfeeling
k.ant: feeling, sensible, sensitive 有感觉的
k.en: devoid of sensation especially as a result of cold or anesthesia

ex.en: I have been sitting in the same position for too long and now my feet are numb.
ex.cn: 我一个姿势坐得太久，以至于双腿都发麻了。

k.syn: affectless, apathetic, catatonic, deadpan, emotionless, impassible, passionless, phlegmatic, stoic, stolid
k.ant: demonstrative, emotional, fervent, fervid, impassioned, passionate, vehement 充满感情的
k.en: devoid of emotion

ex.en: Apparently in shock, he answered the police officer's questions with a numb expression on his face.
ex.cn: 他显然是受到了惊吓，在回答警官的问题时一脸茫然。

ex.en: Years of physical and emotional abuse had rendered the woman numb and withdrawn.
ex.cn: 长年累月的肉体和精神上的折磨已让她麻木而寡言。

m: NUMB rhymes with DUMB — both unfeeling. — numb, without feeling.

k.syn: airhead, dolt, dope, dullard, dumbhead, fool, idiot, imbecile, moron, simpleton
k.ant: brain, intellectual, sage, wit 智者；genius, prodigy 天才
k.en: a stupid person

ex.en: Anyone who took him for an oaf and tried to cheat him would be in for a nasty surprise.
ex.cn: 任何把他当成傻子然后想借机欺骗他的人就等着大吃一惊吧。

m: OAF rhymes with LOAF — a clumsy person who LOAFs around. — oaf, a clumsy fool.

k.syn: alert, attentive, awake, cautious, sharp, vigilant, watchful
k.ant: careless, heedless, inattentive, unmindful, unthinking, unwary 不谨慎的
k.en: paying close attention usually for the purpose of anticipating approaching danger or opportunity

ex.en: Good reporters are keenly observant of everything around them.
ex.cn: 优秀的记者应该对身边所有的事情都保持高度的警惕。

ex.en: If you were more observant, you would perceive that something is troubling her deeply.
ex.cn: 如果你能再警觉一些的话，你就会发现她正被某些东西困扰。

k.syn: law-abiding
k.cn: 严格遵守（规章、制度或原则）的
k.en: diligent in observing a law, custom, duty, or principle

ex.en: observant of the speed limit
ex.cn: 严格遵守速度限制

ex.en: pious and religiously observant families
ex.cn: 虔诚而严守教规的家庭

m: OBSERVANT contains OBSERVE — watching closely / observing rules. — observant, attentive.

k.syn: antiquated, archaic, dated, fossilized, medieval, moribund, moth- eaten, outdated, outmoded, outworn, prehistoric, rusty
k.ant: contemporary, current, modern, recent 当前的，现代的
k.en: no longer in use or no longer useful

ex.en: I was told my old printer was obsolete and I couldn't get replacement parts.
ex.cn: 我被告知我的打印机已经被淘汰了，因此无法更换配件。

ex.en: an obsolete word
ex.cn: 一个已经废弃不用的单词

m: OBSOLETE contains OBSOL- (Latin OBSOLESCERE = grow old) — outgrown, no longer used. — obsolete, out of date.

k.syn: aggression, assault, charge, offense, onslaught, raid, rush, strike
k.ant: defense 防御
k.en: attack, assault

ex.en: withstand the onset of the army
ex.cn: 抵挡住了敌人军队的进攻

k.syn: alpha, baseline, commencement, dawn, genesis, inception, kickoff, launch, nascence, outset, threshold
k.ant: close, conclusion, end, ending, omega, termination 结束
k.en: the point at which something begins

ex.en: If you take enough vitamin C at the onset of a cold, you'll probably recover faster.
ex.cn: 如果你感冒刚一开始就服用足量的维生素C，你可能会康复得更快。

m: ON + SET — when something SETs ON. — onset, a beginning / attack.

k.syn: calvary, crucible, fire, gauntlet
k.ant: delight, enjoyment, pleasure 令人愉快的事物
k.en: a severe trial or experience

ex.en: The hikers were finally rescued after a three-day ordeal in the wilderness.
ex.cn: 在野外度过了三天的严峻考验之后，登山者们终于获救了。

m: ORDEAL rhymes with REAL DEAL but harsher — a severe test. — ordeal, a difficult trial.

k.syn: apparent, assumed, evident, ostensive, presumed, putative, reputed, seeming, supposed, prima facie
k.ant: actual, real 真实的
k.en: appearing to be true on the basis of evidence that may or may not be confirmed

ex.en: It must be the ostensible reason for their visit while their actual motives may be malignant.
ex.cn: 这肯定只是他们上门拜访的表面原因，他们背后的动机说不定恶劣至极。

m: OSTENSIBLE contains OSTEN (show, Latin OSTENDERE) — what's SHOWN on the surface (but not the truth). — ostensible, apparent on the surface.

k.syn: aftereffect, aftermath, consequence, fate, fruit, outcome, precipitate, product, result, sequel, sequence
k.ant: antecedent, cause, occasion, reason 原因；origin, root, source 源头
k.en: a condition or occurrence traceable to a cause

ex.en: Crime is often an outgrowth of poverty.
ex.cn: 犯罪往往可以归因于贫穷。

ex.en: A predictable outgrowth of the suburb's ever growing population will be the need for more schools.
ex.cn: 郊区人口攀升所导致的一个可以预见的后果就是需要更多的学校。

m: OUT + GROWTH — what grows OUT of something else. — outgrowth, a result.

k.syn: antiquated, archaic, demoded, fossilized, moribund, moth-eaten, obsolete, outworn, prehistoric, rusty
k.ant: contemporary, current, modern 现代的，当代的
k.en: no longer acceptable, current, or usable

ex.en: outmoded computers that can be recycled
ex.cn: 可回收的废旧电脑

m: OUT + MODED — out of MODE/fashion. — outmoded, outdated.

k.syn: disregard, ignore, neglect, overlook, overpass, pass over
k.ant: comply, observe 遵守
k.en: to set aside

ex.en: The Congress overrode the President's veto and passed the law.
ex.cn: 国会不顾总统的否决，强行通过了那项法令。

ex.en: He overrode all opposition to his plans.
ex.cn: 他对所有的反对意见不予理睬。

m: OVER + RIDE — RIDE OVER, ignore. — override, to ignore / set aside.

k.syn: becloud, befog, blacken, blear, blur, darken, dim, fog, haze, mist, obscure, overcast, overcloud, shroud
k.ant: brighten, illuminate, illumine, lighten 照明，照亮
k.en: to make dark, dim, or indistinct

ex.en: Large trees overshadow the yard and darken the house for much of the day.
ex.cn: 大树遮挡了射入院子里的阳光，使得屋子里一天之中的大多数时候都很昏暗。

ex.en: Her mother's illness overshadowed her childhood.
ex.cn: 她母亲的疾病让她的童年缺乏亮色。

k.syn: eclipse, outrank, outshine, outstrip, overbalance, overweigh
k.ant: fall behind 落后
k.en: to exceed in importance

ex.en: The forward's outstanding performance should not overshadow the achievements of the rest of the team.
ex.cn: 其他队员的贡献不应该因前锋的精彩表现而被忽略。

m: OVER + SHADOW — cast a SHADOW OVER, eclipsing it. — overshadow, to surpass / darken.

k.syn: deluge, drown, engulf, inundate, overflow, submerge, submerse, swamp
k.ant: drain 抽干
k.en: to cover with or as if with a flood

ex.en: That spring the massive runoff from melting snows overwhelmed the valley.
ex.cn: 那个春天，整个山谷被融雪形成的洪流吞没了。

k.syn: devastate, oppress, overcome, overmaster, overpower, prostrate, whelm
k.ant: relieve 使舒缓
k.der: overwhelming adj. 势不可挡的，无法抗拒的
k.cn: （感情上、精神上）使无所适从，使难以承受
k.en: to subject to incapacitating emotional or mental stress

ex.en: Just the thought of how much work there is to do overwhelms me.
ex.cn: 仅仅是想想还有多少工作没完成就几乎让我喘不过气了。

m: OVER + WHELM (cover/submerge, Old English) — completely covered over. — overwhelm, to flood / overpower.

k.syn: agitated, excited, frenzied, heated, hectic, hyperactive, overactive
k.ant: calm, collected, composed, placid, serene, tranquil 冷静的，沉着的
k.cn: 十分激动的，非常不安的
k.en: being in a state of increased activity or agitation

ex.en: She became overwrought when she heard that her child was missing.
ex.cn: 当她得知自己孩子失踪的消息后变得极其激动和不安。

k.syn: baroque, bedizened, flamboyant, florid, fussy, gingerbreaded, ornate, overdecorated
k.ant: austere, plain, severe, stark, unadorned 朴素的
k.en: elaborately and often excessively decorated

ex.en: The author's prose is overwrought with purple passages and florid metaphors.
ex.cn: 这个作者的散文里充满了词藻华丽的段落和比喻，显得有些过分。

m: OVER + WROUGHT (worked) — over-worked emotionally. — overwrought, agitated / overdone.

k.syn: argument, colloquy, conference, consult, council, counsel, debate, dialogue, parley
k.en: an exchange of views for the purpose of exploring a subject or deciding an issue

ex.en: seemingly endless palaver between the negotiating parties
ex.cn: 谈判双方之间看似没有尽头的对话

k.syn: babble, chatter, converse, gabble, jabber, prate, prattle, rattle, twitter
k.en: to talk profusely or idly

ex.en: Mothers were palavering and drinking coffee while watching their children play.
ex.cn: 母亲们一边看着她们的孩子玩耍，一边喝着咖啡闲谈。

m: PALAVER contains PAL ≈ "chat with a pal" — long pointless chitchat. (From Portuguese PALAVRA = word.) — palaver, idle chatter.

k.syn: beat, beating, pulse, throb
k.en: a rhythmic expanding and contracting

ex.en: a palpitation of the blood vessels
ex.cn: 血管有节奏的舒张收缩

m: PALPITATION contains PALP (touch/feel) — heart's felt beating. — palpitation, rhythmic throbbing.

k.syn: catholicon, cure-all, elixir, nostrum
k.en: a remedy for all ills or difficulties

ex.en: a woman who seems to believe that chicken soup is a panacea for nearly everything
ex.cn: 一个相信鸡汤是万能灵药的妇女

ex.en: Bicycles are not a panacea for the traffic problem.
ex.cn: 自行车并不是解决交通问题的万能药。

m: PAN (all, Greek) + ACEA (cure, Greek AKOS) — cure-all. — panacea, a universal remedy.

k.syn: attribute, character, criterion, diagnostic, differentia, feature, fingerprint, hallmark, mark, note, particularity, quality, specific, trait
k.en: a distinguishing characteristic

ex.en: It is a peculiarity of the house that there is no front door.
ex.cn: 没有大门是这座房子的一个特色。

m: PECULIARITY contains PECULIAR — distinctive trait. — peculiarity, a distinctive feature.

k.syn: preceptorial
k.ant: pupillary 学生的
k.en: of, relating to, or befitting a teacher or education

ex.en: I have no brow of such a pedagogical tone.
ex.cn: 我一点也不喜欢这种说教的口吻。

m: PEDA (child, Greek PAIS) + GOGUE (lead) — relating to teaching children. — pedagogical, of teaching.

k.syn: bark, flay, hull, husk, shell, shuck, skin, strip
k.en: to strip off an outer layer of

ex.en: She can peel apples with lightning speed.
ex.cn: 她能以飞快的速度削苹果。

k.syn: doff, douse, put off, shrug off, take off
k.ant: don, put on 穿上
k.en: to take off one's clothes

ex.en: peel off the wet clothes
ex.cn: 脱去湿衣服

m: PEEL — strip off the skin (apple PEEL, sunburned skin PEELing). — peel, to strip off skin.

k.syn: delicate, fine, keen, quick, sensitive, sharp
k.ant: dead, imperceptive, insensible, insensitive, numb 反应慢的，麻木的
k.en: able to sense slight impressions or differences

ex.en: Due to their ability to rotate their ears, cats are very perceptive when it comes to pinpointing the source of a sound.
ex.cn: 由于猫能够转动它们的耳朵，所以它们能十分敏锐地确定声源的位置。

k.syn: discerning, insightful, prudent, sagacious, sage, sapient
k.ant: brainless, dumb, foolish, idiotic, imbecile, moronic, silly, simple, slow, stupid, thoughtless, unintelligent, unwise, witless 愚钝的
k.en: having or showing deep understanding and intelligent application of knowledge

ex.en: A perceptive teacher was able to discover what was really troubling the youth.
ex.cn: 一个洞察力强的老师应该能察觉真正困扰青少年的是什么。

m: PERCEPTIVE contains PERCEIVE — quick to perceive. — perceptive, quickly aware.

k.syn: dangerous, grave, grievous, hazardous, jeopardizing, menacing, risky, serious, threatening, venturesome
k.ant: harmless, innocent, innocuous, safe, unthreatening 安全的，无害的
k.en: involving potential loss or injury

ex.en: perilous journey through hostile territory
ex.cn: 穿过敌方领土的危险行程

m: PERILOUS contains PERIL — full of peril. — perilous, dangerous.

k.syn: cumshaw, dividend, donative, extra, gratuity, gravy, lagniappe, perk, tip
k.en: something given in addition to what is ordinarily expected or owed

ex.en: Give the movers a perquisite if they do a good job.
ex.cn: 如果搬家工人做得好就给他们一些小费。

ex.en: The use of a company car is one perquisite of the job.
ex.cn: 能使用公司的汽车是这份工作的附加好处之一。

m: PER + QUIS (seek, Latin QUAERERE) — extra benefit sought along with the job. (Source of "perk".) — perquisite, an extra benefit.

k.syn: choleric, grouchy, grumpy, irascible, irritable, peevish, perverse, pettish, prickly, raspy, ratty, stuffy, testy
k.ant: forbearing, obliging, patient, stoic, tolerant 容忍的，忍让的
k.en: easily irritated or annoyed

ex.en: He is a petulant and fussy man who is always blaming everyone else for his problems.
ex.cn: 他是一个对细节非常在意而又容易生气的人，还总是把自己的问题怪罪到别人的头上。

m: PETULANT contains PET ≈ "pet (peeve)" — easily peeved. (Latin PETULANS = forward, impudent.) — petulant, sulky / irritable.

k.syn: colonist, colonizer, homesteader, settler
k.ant: follower 跟随者
k.en: one of the first to settle in a territory

ex.en: the hardships that the pioneers endured while taming the wilderness
ex.cn: 在驯服自然的过程中扩荒者所受到的种种磨难

ex.en: a pioneer in aviation
ex.cn: 航空业的先驱

k.syn: earliest, foremost, headmost, inaugural, initial, leadoff, maiden, original, premier, virgin
k.ant: final, last, latest, terminal, terminating, ultimate 最后的，终结的
k.en: coming before all others in time or order

ex.en: the nation's pioneer institutions for the education of African- Americans
ex.cn: 该国为了非裔美国人的教育而设立的第一批教育机构

k.syn: begin, constitute, establish, inaugurate, initiate, innovate, institute, introduce, launch, plant, set up
k.ant: close, end, shut, terminate, phase out 终止，淘汰
k.der: pioneering adj. 先驱性的，先导性的
k.en: to open up（an area）or prepare（a way）

ex.en: He single-handedly pioneered the university's institute for medical research.
ex.cn: 他单枪匹马创建了这所大学的医学研究所。

ex.en: rockets that pioneered outer space
ex.cn: 开创了外太空纪元的火箭

m: PIONEER comes from French PION (foot soldier) — original front-rank soldier; figuratively, a first explorer. — pioneer, a first explorer / innovator.

k.syn: devout, godly, religious, sainted, saintly
k.ant: antireligious, faithless, godless, impious, irreligious, ungodly, unholy 不虔诚的，无宗教信仰的
k.en: marked by or showing reverence for deity and devotion to divine worship

ex.en: a pious woman who decided to become a nun
ex.cn: 一个决心成为修女的虔诚女子

k.syn: constant, dedicated, devoted, loyal, staunch, steadfast, steady, true
k.ant: disloyal, fickle, inconstant, perfidious, recreant, traitorous, treacherous, unfaithful 不忠诚的，善变的
k.en: firm in one's allegiance to someone or something

ex.en: a pious supporter of his school's athletic teams, during winning and losing seasons alike
ex.cn: 学校体育队的忠诚支持者，不论他们是输还是赢

m: PIOUS contains PI ≈ Greek PIETAS (duty to gods) — religiously devoted. (Same root as PIETY.) — pious, religiously devout.

k.syn: catch, gimmick, gotcha, hitch, joker, snag, snare, trap
k.en: a pit flimsily covered or camouflaged and used to capture and hold animals or men

ex.en: caught in a pitfall
ex.cn: 掉下了陷阱

ex.en: Renting a house can be full of pitfalls for the unwary.
ex.cn: 对于那些不谨慎的人来说租房也是危机四伏的。

k.syn: hazard, imminence, menace, peril, risk, threat, trouble
k.en: a hidden or not easily recognized danger or difficulty

ex.en: One of the pitfalls of ignorance is that people will also assume you are stupid.
ex.cn: 无知的一个危害就是，人们总会假设你一无所知。

m: PIT + FALL — falling into a hidden PIT. — pitfall, a hidden danger.

k.syn: critical, crucial, decisive, key, vital
k.ant: inconsequential, inconsiderable, insignificant, marginal, minor, minute, negligible, nugatory, slight, trifling, trivial 不重要的
k.en: of the greatest possible importance

ex.en: The report was missing a pivotal piece of information.
ex.cn: 报告遗漏了最为重要的信息。

m: PIVOTAL contains PIVOT — the PIVOT point on which everything turns. — pivotal, of crucial importance.

k.syn: calm, halcyon, hushed, peaceful, quiet, serene, still, stilly, tranquil, untroubled
k.ant: boisterous, clamorous, noisy, raucous, roistering, rowdy, tumultuous, uproarious 喧闹的
k.en: free from storms or physical disturbance

ex.en: a vacation in the placid lake community
ex.cn: 在宁静的湖区度过的假日

k.syn: collected, composed, coolheaded, level, limpid, possessed, recollected, sedate, undisturbed, unperturbed
k.ant: agitated, discomposed, disturbed, flustered, perturbed, upset 不安的，焦虑的
k.en: free from emotional or mental agitation

ex.en: I know an exceptionally placid mother who was rarely upset by her six children.
ex.cn: 我认识一个异常淡定的母亲，她几乎从未因为自己的六个孩子而烦恼过。

m: PLACID shares PLAC- with PLACATE — pleasantly calm. (Same root, different sense.) — placid, calm.

k.syn: antic, coltish, elfish, fay, frisky, frolicsome, larky, rollicking, sportive
k.ant: earnest, grave, serious, sober, solemn, somber 严肃的
k.en: given to good-natured joking or teasing

ex.en: The little girl was lighthearted and playful.
ex.cn: 小女孩无忧无虑的，喜欢和别人打闹。

m: PLAY + FUL — full of play. — playful, full of fun.

k.syn: baseborn, common, humble, inferior, lowborn, lumpen, mean, prole, proletarian, unwashed, vulgar
k.ant: aristocratic, genteel, gentle, highborn, highbred, lofty, noble, patrician, wellborn 上流社会的
k.en: belonging to the class of people of low social or economic rank

ex.en: a man who rose to greatness but never forgot his plebeian past
ex.cn: 一个仕途通达、但从未忘却自己平民出身的人

m: PLEBEIAN contains PLEB (common people, Latin PLEBS) — of the common people. — plebeian, common / unrefined.

k.syn: abundance, cornucopia, feast, plentitude, plethora, wealth
k.ant: deficiency, inadequacy, insufficiency, undersupply 匮乏，不足
k.en: an amount or supply more than sufficient to meet one's needs

ex.en: a region blessed with a plenitude of natural resources
ex.cn: 充满着上帝赐予的丰富资源的地区

k.syn: bunch, bundle, dozen, multiplicity, myriad, plateful, plenty, profusion, ream, scads, spate, stack, volume
k.ant: ace, bit, glimmer, handful, hint, mite, nip, ounce, pittance, speck, spot, trace 少量
k.en: a considerable amount

ex.en: She has gathered a plenitude of information on the topic.
ex.cn: 有关这个话题她收集了大量的资料。

m: PLENITUDE contains PLENI (full, Latin PLENUS) — fullness. (Same root as PLENTY.) — plenitude, abundance.

k.syn: crawler, dallier, dawdler, dragger, laggard, lagger, lingerer, loiterer, snail, straggler
k.ant: speedster 速度快的人
k.en: someone who moves slowly or more slowly than others

ex.en: The guide halted the tour group so that the plodders who had fallen behind could catch up.
ex.cn: 导游让队伍停下来，以便那些落在后头的行动缓慢的人可以跟上。

m: from PLOD — one who plods. — plodder, a slow worker.

k.syn: concentrated, full, heady, lusty, muscular, potent, rich, robust, strong
k.ant: delicate, light, mild, thin, weak（味道等）淡的
k.en: having an abundance of some characteristic quality（as flavor）

ex.en: a plush, ripe wine
ex.cn: 味道浓郁醇美的红酒

k.syn: deluxe, lavish, luxuriant, luxurious, opulent, palatial, silken, sumptuous
k.ant: ascetic, austere, humble, spartan 朴素的，平凡的
k.en: notably luxurious

ex.en: a plush castle filled with priceless art and antiques
ex.cn: 一座充满了无价艺术品和古董的奢华城堡

m: PLUSH rhymes with LUSH — both rich and luxurious. — plush, luxurious.

k.syn: coddle, parboil, simmer, stew
k.en: to cook in a liquid heated to the point that it gives off steam

ex.en: He poached an egg for breakfast.
ex.cn: 他煮了一个荷包蛋做早饭。

m: to POACH an egg = simmer in water. (Distinct from "poach" = hunt illegally.) — poach, to simmer in liquid.

k.syn: hand, index, indicator, needle
k.en: a scale indicator on a watch, balance, or other measuring instrument

ex.en: The pointer on my bathroom scale must be stuck - I know I lost weight.
ex.cn: 浴室磅秤里的指针肯定坏了——我知道我体重应该有下降的。

k.syn: advice, hint, lead, recommendation, tip
k.en: a useful suggestion or hint usually from an expert

ex.en: Here are a few pointers to help you make a choice.
ex.cn: 这里有一些能帮助你作抉择的建议。

m: POINTER — what POINTs to something (instrument needle, helpful tip). — pointer, an indicator / hint.

k.syn: down-to-earth, earthy, hardheaded, matter-of-fact, practical
k.ant: fanciful, idealistic, impractical, unrealistic, utopian, visionary 幻想的，不务实的
k.der: pragmatism n. 实用主义，务实主义
k.en: a practical approach to problems and affairs

ex.en: Pragmatic men of power have had no time or inclination to deal with social morality.
ex.cn: （K. B. Clark）务实的当权者不会有时间或者意向去处理社会道德的问题。——K. B. 克拉克

ex.en: a pragmatic man, not given to grand, visionary schemes
ex.cn: 一个不迷恋花哨空想的计划，而更注重实际的人

m: PRAGMATIC contains PRAGMA (deed, Greek) — focused on what works in deeds, not theory. — pragmatic, practical.

k.syn: condition, essential, must, necessity, precondition, requirement, requisite, sine qua non
k.en: something that is necessary to an end or to the carrying out of a function

ex.en: Competence is prerequisite to promotion.
ex.cn: 能力是提升的必要条件。

ex.en: I don't think a large dwelling place is a prerequisite for a happy life.
ex.cn: 我并不认为大的住所就能带来幸福的生活。

m: PRE (before) + REQUISITE (required) — required before. — prerequisite, a precondition.

k.syn: appanage, birthright, entitlement, privilege, right
k.en: an exclusive or special right, power, or privilege

ex.en: It is your prerogative to refuse to attend religious services.
ex.cn: 你有权力不参加宗教活动。

m: PRE + ROGATIVE (asking, Latin ROGARE) — first to be asked, given priority. — prerogative, an exclusive right.

k.syn: advertise, announce, blare, blazon, broadcast, declare, enunciate, herald, promulgate, publicize, publish, release, trumpet
k.ant: conceal, hide 隐藏；silence, suppress 压制（观点的发表）
k.en: to declare publicly, typically insistently, proudly, or defiantly and in either speech or writing

ex.en: I was wearing a button that proclaimed my choice for President.
ex.cn: 我身上挂着一个纽扣，它能够彰显我支持谁当总统。

ex.en: He launched a coup and proclaimed himself President.
ex.cn: 他发动了一场政变，然后宣称自己已成为总统。

m: PRO (forth) + CLAIM — CLAIM forth publicly. — proclaim, to announce publicly.

k.syn: arrangement, blueprint, design, plan, program, scheme, strategy, system, road map
k.en: a specific plan or design

ex.en: an ambitious project to develop the city's underground transport
ex.cn: 一项开发城市地下交通系统的雄心勃勃的计划

k.syn: balloon, beetle, billow, bunch, jut, overhang, poke, pouch, pout, protrude, swell
k.en: to extend outward beyond a usual point

ex.en: Some boulders projected dangerously out above the trail.
ex.cn: 一些巨石非常危险地在道路上凸了出来。

k.syn: augur, estimate, extrapolate, forecast, predict, predetermine, presage
k.der: projected adj. 计划的；projection n. 凸起物；投影
k.en: to calculate, estimate, or predict（something in the future）, based on present data or trends

ex.en: project next year's expenses
ex.cn: 预测明年的开销

m: PROJECT contains PRO (forth) + JECT (throw, Latin) — thrown forth. (Same root as REJECT, EJECT.) — project, to throw forward / a planned undertaking.

k.syn: dominion, ownership, possession, property, proprietorship
k.en: ownership

k.syn: private
k.ant: public 政府提供的；state-owned 国有的
k.en: privately owned and managed and run as a profit-making organization

ex.en: a proprietary hospital
ex.cn: 私人医院

m: PROPRIETARY contains PROPRI (one's own, Latin PROPRIUS) — owned. — proprietary, of an owner / private.

k.syn: archetype
k.ant: copy, imitation, replica, reproduction 复制品
k.en: an original model on which something is patterned

ex.en: The manufacturer exhaustively tested the prototype of the vehicle before approving production.
ex.cn: 生产商在将该车型投入生产之前对样机进行了详细的测试。

m: PROTO (first) + TYPE — first type / model. — prototype, an original model.

k.syn: lush, luxuriant, prosperous, weedy
k.ant: sparse 稀疏的
k.en: growing thickly and vigorously

ex.en: a rampant growth of weeds in the neglected yard
ex.cn: 废弃的院子里杂草疯长

k.syn: abandoned, intemperate, runaway, unbounded, unbridled, unchecked, uncontrolled, unhampered, unhindered, unrestrained
k.ant: bridled, checked, constrained, controlled, curbed, governed, hampered, hindered, restrained, temperate 受制约的
k.en: occurring without restraint and frequently, widely, or menacingly

ex.en: Mayor promised to put a stop to the rampant crime that plagued the city.
ex.cn: 市长承诺要采取措施，终止那些扰乱城市治安的猖獗的犯罪活动。

ex.en: rampant corruption in city government
ex.cn: 市政府中猖獗的腐败

m: RAMPANT contains RAMP — running up a RAMP unchecked, like wild plants climbing. — rampant, unchecked / widespread.

k.syn: edacious, esurient, gluttonous, greedy, rapacious, voracious
k.ant: content, sated, satiated, satisfied 心满意足的
k.cn: 食量大的，贪食的；贪婪的
k.en: having a huge appetite; greedy for gratification

ex.en: He had moderated his ravenous appetite.
ex.cn: 他控制了自己的食欲。

ex.en: ravenous for power
ex.cn: 渴望权力

m: RAVENOUS rhymes with RAVE-NOUS — but it's hunger: hungry like a wolf RAVing for food. (Same root as RAPACIOUS.) — ravenous, extremely hungry.

k.syn: answer, complete, comply, fill, fulfill, keep, meet, satisfy
k.ant: breach, break, transgress, violate 违反，违背
k.en: to do what is required by the terms of

ex.en: The coach redeemed his promise to take the players out for ice cream if they improved their fielding over the season.
ex.cn: 教练履行了他的诺言：如果球员们能够在赛季中提升防守能力的话，他就请他们吃冰淇淋。

k.syn: habilitate, reclaim, regenerate, rehabilitate
k.der: redeemable adj. 可赎回的，可挽救的
k.en: to make better in behavior or character

ex.en: The unfaithful husband made a determined effort to redeem himself in the eyes of his wife and children.
ex.cn: 不忠的丈夫下定决心要在妻子和孩子的监督下改过自新。

m: RE (back) + DEEM (buy, Latin EMERE) — buy back / make good. — redeem, to fulfill / make amends.

k.syn: bracing, cordial, invigorating, rejuvenating, restorative, reviving, stimulating, stimulative, vitalizing
k.ant: deadening, debilitating, draining, enervating, enfeebling, exhausting, sapping, wearying 令人虚弱的
k.cn: 令人身心振奋的，提神的
k.en: having a renewing effect on the state of the body or mind

ex.en: A glass of cold water is very refreshing on such a hot day.
ex.cn: 大热天里的一杯冰水真是让人心旷神怡。

ex.en: It is refreshing to hear some good news about him.
ex.cn: 能听到他的好消息真是让人振奋。

m: from REFRESH — making fresh again. — refreshing, invigorating.

k.syn: debris, remainder, remains, residue, vestige The man who has made up his mind to win will never say“impossible”. 凡是决心取得胜利的人是从来不说“不可能的”。 ——法国皇帝　拿破仑（Bonaparte Napoleon, French emperor） List 31* “‘独上高楼，望尽天涯路。’GRE是登高的小小台阶，在脚踏实地的同时，目光应时刻凝聚在更远大的理想上。” （薄瓜瓜，录取院校：Harvard Kennedy School）
k.en: something left over

ex.en: a remnant of his past glory
ex.cn: 他辉煌过去的一丝残余

ex.en: The shop is selling remnants of cloth at half price.
ex.cn: 商店在半价抛售剩余的衣物。

m: REMNANT contains REM ≈ "remain" — what REMAINs. — remnant, a leftover.

k.syn: abnegate, forswear, recant, renounce, repeal, repudiate, retract, withdraw
k.ant: adhere 坚持
k.en: to solemnly or formally reject or go back on（as something formerly adhered to）

ex.en: She refused to renege the principles by which she had always lived her life, even if it resulted in losing her business.
ex.cn: 尽管有可能使她失去她的事业，但她仍然拒绝放弃生命中所坚持的那些原则。

m: from RENEGADE root — deny / go back on. — renege, to break a promise.

k.syn: refill, reload
k.ant: consume, drain, empty, exhaust 清空，耗尽
k.en: to fill or make complete again; add a new stock or supply to

ex.en: He went to replenish her glass.
ex.cn: 他去为她续杯了。

m: RE + PLEN (full, Latin PLENUS) — fill again. — replenish, to refill.

k.syn: clone, copy, copycat, duplicate, imitate, reduplicate, render, reproduce
k.ant: originate 原创
k.en: to make an exact likeness of

ex.en: We replicated the famous painting in our art class.
ex.cn: 我们在艺术课上临摹一幅名画。

k.syn: redo, reiterate, renew, repeat
k.der: replicable adj. 可复制的，能复现的
k.en: to make or do again

ex.en: I cannot replicate your results when I do the experiment myself.
ex.cn: 我自己的实验里无法重复你的结果。

m: REPLICATE contains REPLI ≈ "REPLY/REPLICATE-fold" (Latin REPLICARE = fold back) — make a folded-back copy. — replicate, to copy.

k.syn: leftover, remaining, vestigial
k.ant: comprehensive, entire, full, integral, total, whole 全部的，完整的
k.en: of, relating to, or characteristic of a residue

ex.en: residual radiation from nuclear tests
ex.cn: 核试验所遗留下来的残余辐射

m: RESIDUAL contains RESID ≈ "sit back" (Latin RESIDERE = remain seated) — what remains. — residual, remaining.

k.syn: damages, indemnity, quittance, recompense, recoupment, redress, remuneration, reparation, reprisal, requital
k.en: a making good of or giving an equivalent for some injury

ex.en: The victims are demanding full restitution.
ex.cn: 受害者要求全额补偿。

m: RESTITUTION contains RESTIT ≈ "set up again" (Latin RESTITUERE = restore) — restoring what was taken. — restitution, restoration / compensation.

k.syn: reanimate, recharge, regenerate, rejuvenate, rekindle, renew, resuscitate, revitalize, revive, revivify
k.ant: kill 杀死
k.en: to bring back to life, practice, or activity

ex.en: People believed that his body would be resurrected.
ex.cn: 人们相信他的身体将会复活。

ex.en: Attempts are being made by both parties to resurrect the stalled arms negotiations.
ex.cn: 双方正在努力重启受阻的武器谈判。

m: RE + SURRECT (rise, Latin SURGERE) — rise again. — resurrect, to bring back to life.

k.ant: absentminded, forgetful, oblivious 健忘的
k.en: having the ability or capacity to retain knowledge or information with ease

ex.en: Her retentive memory helped her sail through the history test.
ex.cn: 她的好记性帮助她顺利通过了历史考试。

m: RETENTIVE contains RETAIN — good at retaining (memory). — retentive, holding well.

k.syn: reacquire, recapture, reclaim, re-collect, recoup, recover, regain, repossess, retake
k.ant: lose, mislay, misplace 丢失
k.en: to get back again

ex.en: I need to retrieve the book from my friend so I could return it to the library.
ex.cn: 我需要先把书从朋友那里拿回来才能还给图书馆。

m: RE + TRIEVE (find, Old French TROUVER) — find again. — retrieve, to recover.

k.syn: echo, reecho, resonate, resound
k.en: to continue or be repeated in a series of reflected sound waves

ex.en: The sound of thunder reverberated from one end of the mountain to the other.
ex.cn: 打雷的声音在山间不断回荡。

m: REVERBERATE contains VERBER (whip, Latin VERBER) — sound bouncing back like a whip's crack. — reverberate, to echo.

k.syn: lexical, linguistic, vocabular, wordy
k.en: of or relating to words or language

ex.en: The next war that those two nations fight won't be rhetorical - it will be with bombs and bullets.
ex.cn: 两国之间的下一场战争可不会是斗嘴皮了—— 他们会来真格的。

k.syn: bombastic, flatulent, florid, fustian, gaseous, gassy, grandiloquent, oratorical, orotund, purple, windy
k.ant: prosaic 平凡无奇的；plain, simple 简单的
k.cn: 言辞华丽（却往往无内涵）的
k.en: full of fine words and fancy expressions but mostly meaningless words and phrases

ex.en: You can skip over the rhetorical passages and still get the gist of the essay.
ex.cn: 你可以跳过那些词藻华丽的段落，直接获取文本的主旨。

ex.en: The new governor delivered a long rhetorical speech about our state's bright future but laid out no specific programs for ensuring it.
ex.cn: 新的执政官发表了词藻华丽的长篇演说，畅谈国家的美好未来，但却没有提出实现这一目标的具体措施。

m: RHETORICAL contains RHETOR (orator, Greek) — relating to speech. — rhetorical, of language / oratorical.

k.syn: abracadabra, babble, drivel, gabble, gibber, jabber, nonsense, prattle
k.en: language marked by abstractions, jargon, euphemisms, and circumlocutions; confused or meaningless talk

ex.en: The security guard gave me some kind of rigmarole about passes and authorizations.
ex.cn: 保安不知所云地跟我说了一些关于通关和授权的话。

k.syn: cruel, heartless, inhumane, ironfisted, iron-hearted, pitiless, merciless, mortal, relentless, remorseless
k.ant: charitable, compassionate, humane, kindhearted, merciful, sympathetic, tender 有同情心的，善良的
k.en: having no pity

ex.en: an office supervisor with a ruthless disregard for others' feelings
ex.cn: 一个从不顾及他人感情的无情领导

k.syn: hallowed, holy, inviolable, sacred, unassailable, untouchable
k.ant: blasphemous, irreverent, profane, sacrilegious 亵渎的
k.cn: 极为神圣的，不可侵犯的
k.en: most sacred or holy

ex.en: The teacher's book of grades is sacrosanct, and someone could be expelled for changing anything in it.
ex.cn: 老师记录成绩的小册子是极为神圣的，任何篡改它的人都可能受到被退学的惩罚。

k.syn: affliction, bane, curse, nemesis
k.ant: benefit, blessing, boon, felicity, godsend, manna, windfall 恩赐，福音
k.en: a source of harm or misfortune

ex.en: The sheer ubiquity of food seems to be the scourge of humanity, as evidenced by the obesity epidemic.
ex.cn: 无所不在的食品似乎要成为人类的祸害了，这一点可以从肥胖病的泛滥得以佐证。

k.syn: aegis, ammunition, armor, cover, guard, protection, safeguard, security, shield, wall, ward
k.en: something that shelters, protects, or hides

ex.en: The target will be difficult to reach as it is behind a screen of anti- aircraft batteries.
ex.cn: 目标在防空炮火的掩护之下难以靠近。

ex.en: Please keep away from the screen door.
ex.cn: 请不要倚靠屏蔽门。

k.syn: defend, fence, fend, forfend, protect, secure
k.ant: assail, assault, attack 攻击
k.en: to drive danger or attack away from

ex.en: Gunships were called in to help screen the troops from further attacks.
ex.cn: 武装直升机被呼叫对步兵进行支援，使之免受进一步的攻击。

k.syn: belie, cloak, conceal, curtain, disguise, enshroud, mask, obscure, occult, shroud, veil
k.ant: disclose, expose, reveal, uncloak, uncover, unmask, unveil 揭露
k.en: to keep secret or shut off from view

ex.en: Bushes screened the swimming pool from passersby on the street.
ex.cn: 灌木丛将游泳池和街上的行人隔绝开来。

k.syn: conserve, economize, husband, pinch, save, skimp, spare
k.ant: dissipate, lavish, squander, waste 浪费
k.en: to avoid unnecessary waste or expense

ex.en: They had to scrimp and save for years in order to be able to afford a house.
ex.cn: 他们不得不节俭度日才能攒够钱买房。

k.syn: compunction, misgiving
k.ant: assurance, certainty, certitude, confidence, conviction, sureness 确信
k.en: an uneasy feeling about the rightness of what one is doing or going to do

ex.en: The survey showed that many students had few scruples about cheating on papers or exams.
ex.cn: 调查显示，很多学生对于在论文中或考试时作弊没有丝毫的愧疚。

k.syn: demit, disaffiliate, quit, withdraw
k.ant: enlist, enroll, join, sign up 加入
k.cn: 脱离，退出（组织、团体、联盟等）
k.en: to withdraw from an organization（as a religious communion or political party or federation）

ex.en: They threatened to secede from the coalition.
ex.cn: 他们以退出联盟相要挟。

k.syn: dogmatism, illiberalism, intolerance, narrow-mindedness, partisanship, small-mindedness
k.ant: broad-mindedness, liberalism, open-mindedness, tolerance 开明
k.en: stubborn or intolerant adherence to one's opinions or prejudices

ex.en: An ideological sectarianism prevented the political party from ever being anything more than a fringe group.
ex.cn: 意识形态上的顽固和保守使得这个政党只能成为一个边缘团体。

k.syn: carnal, earthborn, earthbound, fleshly, material, mundane, sublunary, temporal, terrene, terrestrial, worldly
k.ant: heavenly, unearthly, unworldly 非尘世间的；spiritual 精神上的； religious 宗教的；clerical 牧师的
k.en: of or relating to the worldly or temporal

ex.en: That's an issue for the secular authorities, not the church.
ex.cn: 这个问题和宗教无关，是一件世俗事务。

k.syn: aftermath, conclusion, consequence, fate, fruit, outcome, product, result, sequence
k.ant: antecedent, causation, cause, occasion, reason 起因，原因
k.en: a result or consequence

ex.en: Higher prices are a logical sequel to higher costs for manufacturers.
ex.cn: 从逻辑上来说，更高的价格是生产商付出更高的生产成本的结果。

k.syn: budge, dislocate, displace, disturb, relocate, remove, reposition, transfer, transpose
k.ant: anchor, fix, freeze, secure, set 使固定
k.en: to change the place or position of

ex.en: She shifted the vase closer to the wall so that it wouldn't get knocked over.
ex.cn: 她把花瓶靠墙挪动了一点，以防止它被碰倒。

k.syn: fluctuate, mutate, snap, vary
k.ant: plateau, stabilize 达到稳定点，不变
k.en: to pass from one form, state, or level to another

ex.en: She watched the aurora in fascination as its colors shifted from green to blue.
ex.cn: 她着迷地看着极光的色彩从绿变成蓝。

k.syn: commute, exchange, substitute, swap, switch, trade
k.en: to give up（something）and take something else in return

ex.en: My father and I shifted seats just before takeoff so that I could sit by the window.
ex.cn: 起飞前我和我父亲交换了座位，这样我就可以靠窗坐了。

k.syn: crooked, deceitful, deceptive, duplicitous, fraudulent, guileful, rogue, shady, sharp, underhanded
k.ant: aboveboard, honest, straight 正派的，光明正大的
k.en: having, displaying, or suggestive of deceitful character

ex.en: shifty practices such as turning back the odometers on used cars
ex.cn: 一些欺骗性的花招，比如把旧车的里程表调零

ex.en: He had a shifty face so I won't trust him.
ex.cn: 他看起来贼目鼠眼的，我不会相信他。

k.ant: metaphor 暗喻
k.en: a figure of speech comparing two unlike things that is often introduced by like or as

ex.en: Even though both similes and metaphors are forms of comparison, similes indirectly compare the two ideas and allow them to remain distinct in spite of their similarities, whereas metaphors compare two things directly. For instance, a simile that compares a person with a bullet would go as follows:“Chris was a record-setting runner as fast as a speeding bullet.”A metaphor might read something like,“When Chris ran, he was a speeding bullet racing along the track.”
ex.cn: 尽管明喻和暗喻都是比喻的手法，明喻会间接地对两个对象进行比较，同时允许差异的存在，而暗喻则直接对两者进行比较。比如说，用明喻来把一个人比作子弹会这样说：“克里斯是一个不断打破记录的选手，他跑得就和高速子弹一样快。”而暗喻可能会说：“当克里斯奔跑的时候，他就是一颗在赛场上高速飞行的子弹。”

k.syn: fainthearted, fearful, fearsome, mousy, scary, timid, timorous, tremulous
k.ant: adventuresome, adventurous, audacious, bold, daring, dashing, gutsy, venturesome, venturous 大胆的
k.cn: 容易受到惊吓的，胆小的
k.en: easily frightened

ex.en: The kitty is skittish around people she doesn't know.
ex.cn: 小猫看到不认识的人就会显得特别胆小害怕。

k.syn: flighty, fluttery, hyper, hyperactive, hyperkinetic, jittery, jumpy, nervous, skittery, spasmodic, spooky
k.ant: imperturbable, nerveless, unexcitable, unflappable, unshakable 不为所动的，沉着的
k.en: easily excited by nature

ex.en: The skittish colt leapt up when we approached.
ex.cn: 一看到我们靠近，小马驹就激动地猛跳起来。

k.syn: capricious, changeful, flickery, fluctuating, fluid, inconstant, mercurial, mutable, temperamental, uncertain, unstable, unsteady, volatile
k.ant: changeless, constant, immutable, invariable, settled, stable, stationary, steady, unvarying 不变的
k.en: likely to change frequently, suddenly, or unexpectedly

ex.en: The skittish housing market had both buyers and sellers on edge.
ex.cn: 变幻莫测的房市让买家和卖家都坐立不安。

k.syn: economical, economizing, provident, scrimping, thrifty
k.ant: prodigal, profligate, spendthrift, squandering, wasteful 浪费的
k.en: marked by or practicing careful restraint（as in the use of resources）

ex.en: The sparing couple are trying to save up enough for a house.
ex.cn: 节俭的夫妻希望能够攒钱买套房。

k.syn: exiguous, niggardly, poor, scant, scanty, scarce, skimp, skimpy, slender, slim, spare, sparse, stingy
k.ant: abundant, ample, bountiful, copious, generous, liberal, plenteous, plentiful 足量的
k.en: less plentiful than what is normal, necessary, or desirable

ex.en: Unfortunately, the explanation of the health insurance plan was somewhat sparing on details.
ex.cn: 不幸的是，健康保险计划上的说明似乎不够详细。

ex.en: The map is sparing of information.
ex.cn: 这张地图的信息量太少了。

k.syn: chip, flake, sliver, spall, splint
k.ant: chunk, slab 厚板
k.en: a sharp, slender piece, as of wood, bone, glass, or metal, split or broken off from a main body

ex.en: She got a splinter from the unfinished wall.
ex.cn: 她的手被还没有装修的墙扎了一根刺。

k.syn: slice
k.en: to split or break into sharp, slender pieces

ex.en: splinter the carrots into little sticks
ex.cn: 把胡萝卜切成丝

k.syn: babble, drivel, gabble, gibber, jabber, sputter, stammer, stutter
k.ant: articulate, enunciate, pronounce 清楚地说
k.en: to speak hastily and incoherently, as when confused or angry

ex.en: They begin to splutter and move restlessly about if they feel time is slipping away without some return.
ex.cn: 当他们感到时间在悄悄流逝却一无所得时，他们便开始激动地说话，不安地走动。

k.syn: ill, nauseated, qualmish, queasy, queer, queerish, sick, sickish
k.ant: healthy, well 状态良好的
k.en: affected with nausea

ex.en: The rolling of the ship made the young sailor squeamish.
ex.cn: 摇晃的船只让年轻的水手感到恶心。

k.syn: counterpoise, equilibration, equilibrium, equipoise, poise
k.ant: disequilibration, disequilibrium, imbalance, nonequilibrium, unbalance 不平衡
k.en: a condition of balance among various forces

ex.en: For the time being, the populations of the national park's predators and prey remain in stasis.
ex.cn: 目前国家公园中捕食者和猎物的数量保持着一定的平衡。

ex.en: Language is a primary element of culture, and stasis in the arts is tantamount to death.
ex.cn: （Charles Marsh）语言是文化当中的主要元素，艺术的停滞和平衡就等同于死亡。——查尔斯·马什

k.syn: constant, dedicated, devoted, devout, faithful, fast, loyal, pious, steadfast, steady, true, true-blue
k.ant: disloyal, inconstant, perfidious, recreant, traitorous, treacherous 不忠诚的；fickle 善变的
k.en: steadfast in loyalty or principle

ex.en: a staunch believer in the democratic system
ex.cn: 民主制度的坚定拥护者

ex.en: No matter what happens I will be your staunchest supporters.
ex.cn: 不论发生什么，我都会是你最忠诚的支持者。

k.syn: concept, conception, generality, notion
k.en: a conventional, formulaic, and oversimplified conception, opinion, or image

ex.en: The noble savage was a stereotype that appealed to 18th-century intellectuals, who viewed European civilization as decadent and corrupt.“
ex.cn: 高贵的野蛮人”这一概念是为了迎合十八世纪的文人而提出的，他们将欧洲文明视作堕落和腐化的文明。

k.syn: abase, debase, degrade, demean, discredit, disgrace, dishonor, foul, humiliate, shame, sink, smirch
k.ant: aggrandize, canonize, deify, elevate, exalt 提升（名誉、声望等）
k.en: to characterize or brand as disgraceful or ignominious

ex.en: Urban construction workers are often stigmatized by the rest of society as lazy and dirty.
ex.cn: 城市里的建筑工人往往被社会里的其他人污蔑为是懒惰而肮脏的。

k.syn: backup, cover, fill-in, relief, replacement, reserve, stand-in, surrogate
k.en: a person or thing that takes the place or function of another

ex.en: If you like, you can use nuts as a substitute for coconut in that recipe.
ex.cn: 如果您不反对，我们可以将菜单里的椰子换成坚果。

k.syn: displace, relieve, replace, supersede, supplant, cut out
k.en: to take the place of

ex.en: substitute moral power for physical force
ex.cn: 用道德的力量取代暴力

ex.en: BFR molecules have some atoms substituted by bromide atoms, which makes it fire-resistant.
ex.cn: 在溴化阻燃剂的分子中，一部分原子被溴原子取代，从而使它不易燃烧。

k.syn: bow, capitulate, concede, submit, surrender, give in
k.ant: resist 抵抗
k.en: to yield to superior strength or force or overpowering appeal or desire

ex.en: She refused to succumb to her fears and defiantly walked through the dark cemetery.
ex.cn: 她拒绝向自己的恐惧屈服，而是倔强地走过了幽暗的墓地。

k.syn: decease, demise, expire, perish, pass away
k.ant: endure, stand 忍受住；breathe, live, survive 存活
k.en: to be brought to an end（as death）by the effect of destructive or disruptive forces

ex.en: The patient lay so still and pale that everyone thought he had succumbed, and then he opened his eyes.
ex.cn: 面色惨白的病人静静地躺在床上，正当所有人都以为他已经过世时，他突然睁开了眼睛。

ex.en: The doctor worked tirelessly until finally he, too, succumbed to the plague.
ex.cn: 医生不知劳累地工作，直至最后，他也死于瘟疫。

k.syn: displace, relieve, replace, substitute, supplant, cut out
k.en: to displace in favor of another

ex.en: That edition of the dictionary that you have has been superseded by a more recent one.
ex.cn: 你买的那版字典已经被最近新出的版本取代了。

k.syn: acid, bearish, bilious, cantankerous, disagreeable, dyspeptic, ill- humored, ill-natured, ornery, splenetic
k.ant: amiable, good-humored, good-natured, good-tempered 性格好的
k.en: irritably sullen and churlish in mood or manner

ex.en: The surly receptionist told us we would have to wait outside in the rain.
ex.cn: 态度粗暴的接待员说我们必须在外面等——哪怕是在下雨。

k.syn: guess, hypothesis, shot, supposition, theory
k.ant: fact 事实
k.en: a thought or idea based on scanty evidence

ex.en: My surmise is that the couple's“good news”is the announcement that they are going to have a baby.
ex.cn: 我猜那对夫妻说的“好消息”就是他们要有小孩了。

k.syn: assume, conjecture, daresay, imagine, infer, presume, speculate, suppose, suspect
k.ant: demonstrate, prove, substantiate, validate 证明
k.en: to form a notion of from scanty evidence

ex.en: From his tone I surmised that he was unhappy.
ex.cn: 从他的语气中我猜测他并不高兴。

k.syn: conquer, defeat, overbear, overcome, overmatch, prevail, subdue, triumph, win
k.ant: lose 失败
k.en: to achieve a victory over

ex.en: an Olympic swimmer who surmounted endless obstacles to achieve her goals
ex.cn: 一个克服重重困难最终实现目标的奥运游泳运动员

k.syn: beat, better, eclipse, exceed, excel, outclass, outmatch, outshine, outstrip, overtop, transcend
k.ant: fall behind 落后
k.en: to become better, greater, or stronger than

ex.en: She always tried to surpass her older brother at anything he did, which results in his diffidence.
ex.cn: 她在任何一个方面都要比她哥哥优秀，由此导致了他自信心的缺乏。

k.syn: break, outreach, outrun, overpass, overreach, overrun, overstep
k.en: to go beyond the limit of

ex.en: The sales of the band's newest CD have surpassed the combined sales of its last two albums.
ex.cn: 乐队新专辑 的发售量甚至超过了前两张销售量的总和。

k.syn: backup, cover, fill-in, relief, replacement, reserve, stand-in, substitute
k.en: one that takes the place of another

ex.en: For some people, Google Earth is never a justified surrogate for actual travel.
ex.cn: 对于一部分人而言，谷歌地球不是实地旅行的合理替代品。

k.syn: oversight, supervision, watch
k.en: close observation of a person or group, especially one under suspicion

ex.en: government surveillance of suspected terrorists
ex.cn: 政府对于恐怖分子嫌疑人的监控

k.syn: domestic, domesticated, tamed
k.ant: feral, savage, undomesticated, untamed, wild 野生的
k.en: reduced from a state of native wildness especially so as to be tractable and useful to humans

ex.en: Every evening, a wild Canada goose is at the food trough with our tame geese.
ex.cn: 每天傍晚的食槽边总会出现一只野生加拿大鹅和我们饲养的家鹅一起进食。

k.syn: arid, drab, dreary, drudging, humdrum, insipid, jading, jejune, monochromatic, monotonous, pedestrian, ponderous, stale, stodgy, tedious, tiresome, wearisome
k.ant: absorbing, engaging, engrossing, gripping, interesting, intriguing, involving, riveting 令人感兴趣的
k.en: lacking spirit, zest, interest, or the capacity to excite

ex.en: That action movie was so tame I fell asleep about 20 minutes into it.
ex.cn: 那部动作片实在是太无聊了，我刚开场二十分钟就睡着了。

k.syn: bridle, check, constrain, contain, curb, govern, hold, inhibit, keep, measure, regulate, rein, restrain, rule
k.ant: unleash 宣泄（感情等）
k.en: to keep from exceeding a desirable degree or level（as of expression）

ex.en: Try to tame your language when you are in front of the kids.
ex.cn: 在孩子面前注意一下你的措辞。

k.syn: bafflement, befuddlement, bemusement, bewilderment, confusedness, distraction, muddle, mystification, perplexity, puzzlement, whirl
k.ant: assurance, certainty, certitude, confidence, conviction, sureness 确信
k.en: a state of perplexity or complete bewilderment

ex.en: My mind has been in a tangle ever since I learned some information about an old, deceased acquaintance.
ex.cn: 自从我获知一位已故的老熟人的一些消息之后我就陷入了困惑之中。

k.syn: enmesh, ensnare, ensnarl, entrap, mesh, net, snare, trap
k.ant: disentangle 解开
k.en: to seize and hold in or as if in a snare

ex.en: He was at last tangled in the web of lies that he had told to everyone.
ex.cn: 他最终被自己所编织的谎言缠住了。

k.syn: abundant, ample, awash, cornucopian, flush, fraught, lousy, replete, swarming, thick, thronging
k.ant: bare, barren, blank, devoid, empty, stark, vacant, void 贫瘠的，空无一物的
k.en: possessing or covered with great numbers or amounts of something specified

ex.en: oceans teeming with life
ex.cn: 孕育着无穷生命的海洋

k.syn: carnal, earthborn, earthbound, fleshly, material, mundane, secular, sublunary, terrene, terrestrial, worldly
k.ant: heavenly, unearthly, unworldly 非尘世间的；spiritual 精神上的； religious 宗教的
k.en: of or relating to earthly life

ex.en: The master told his disciples not to worry about temporal concerns, but instead focus on spiritual matters.
ex.cn: 大师要弟子们不要为尘世间的纷争而苦恼，而是将精神集中于灵魂上的思考。

k.syn: ad interim, impermanent, interim, provisional, provisionary, provisory, short-term, temporary, trial
k.ant: final 最终的；ceaseless, endless, eternal, immortal, permanent, perpetual, undying 永久性的
k.en: not fully worked out or developed

ex.en: Our plans are only tentative at this point and will depend on whether you can come.
ex.cn: 我们的计划只是暂时性的，具体还要看你能否前来。

k.syn: affectless, callous, cold-blooded, heartless, indurate, inhumane, insensitive, merciless, obdurate, ruthless
k.ant: charitable, compassionate, humane, kindhearted, merciful, sympathetic, tender, warmhearted 慈善的
k.cn: 冷漠无情的，不顾及他人感受的
k.en: largely unaffected by the needs and feelings of other people

ex.en: She was so thick-skinned that she was clueless about the fact that the joke had hurt her friend's feelings.
ex.cn: 她很少顾及他人的感受，以至于根本没有意识到那个玩笑伤害了她朋友的感情。

k.syn: bang, boot, exhilaration, frisson, jollies, kick, rush, titillation, wallop
k.en: a pleasurably intense stimulation of the feelings

ex.en: Everyone gets a real thrill out of the Independence Day fireworks.
ex.cn: 每个人都因独立日的焰火表演而感到无比的兴奋。

k.syn: arouse, charge, electrify, excite, exhilarate, galvanize, intoxicate, provoke, stimulate, titillate, pump up
k.ant: bore, jade, pall, tire, weary 使厌倦
k.der: thrilling adj. 刺激的；thriller n. 恐怖电影
k.en: to cause to experience a sudden sharp feeling of excitement

ex.en: I was thrilled to hear that you got the promotion that you'd been so desperately wanting.
ex.cn: 听到你终于获得了梦寐已久的升职，我感到非常激动。

k.syn: immediate, punctual, speedy
k.ant: belated, late, tardy 迟到的，延迟的
k.en: done, carried out, or given without delay

ex.en: When I order a pizza, I expect it to be delivered in a timely manner.
ex.cn: 当我在外面点了披萨饼，我就希望它能按时送到。

k.syn: opportune, seasonable, well-timed
k.ant: inopportune, unseasonable, untimely 不合时宜的
k.cn: 恰到好处的，合乎适宜的
k.en: appropriate or adapted to the times or the occasion

ex.en: Timely invitation to lunch came just as I was starting to feel hungry.
ex.cn: 正当我开始感到饥饿的时候，就有人适时地邀请我吃午饭了。

k.syn: bagatelle, frippery, triviality
k.en: something of little importance or value

ex.en: Let us not speak of trifles when our nation may be going to war.
ex.cn: 战事在即，我们还是先放下这些琐事吧。

k.syn: coquet, dally, flirt, frivol, toy
k.en: to behave amorously without serious intent

ex.en: Do not trifle with me unless you mean to ask me to marry you.
ex.cn: 如果你不是真心想和我结婚，就请不要来玩弄我的感情。

k.syn: frivolous, inconsequential, inconsiderable, insignificant, minor, minute, negligible, nugatory, slight, trivial
k.ant: consequential, eventful, important, meaningful, momentous, significant, substantial, weighty 重要的
k.en: lacking in significance or solid worth

ex.en: Deciding what you want to do for a living is no trifling matter.
ex.cn: 决定维持生计的行当可不是一件小事。

ex.en: trifling differences between the theatrical and DVD versions of the movie
ex.cn: 这部电影影院版和DVD版的细微区别

k.syn: baron, captain, king, lion, lord, magnate, mogul, monarch, prince
k.ant: nobody, nothing, zero 不起眼的人物
k.en: a person of rank, power, or influence in a particular field

ex.en: an oil tycoon who's widely considered the most powerful man in the county
ex.cn: 被认为是郡上最有势力的一个石油大亨

ex.en: The automobile tycoon is on the verge of bankruptcy.
ex.cn: 汽车巨头濒临破产的边缘。

k.syn: dictator, oppressor, pharaoh, strongman
k.en: a ruler who exercises absolute power oppressively or brutally

ex.en: The people universally feared the tyrant, who was notorious for his frequent use of torture.
ex.cn: 人们都非常恐惧这个暴君，因为他有着经常折磨人的臭名。

k.syn: dudgeon, huff, miff, offense, peeve, resentment
k.ant: contentment, delight, gratification, happiness, pleasure 满意，高兴
k.en: the feeling of being offended or resentful after a slight or indignity

ex.en: He would take umbrage at the slightest suggestion of disrespect.
ex.cn: 哪怕是一点点的不敬也会让他不悦。

k.syn: consentaneous, uncontested
k.en: having the agreement and consent of all

ex.en: a unanimous vote to upgrade the school's computer facilities
ex.cn: 一致同意对学校的计算机设备进行升级

k.syn: abortive, barren, bootless, fruitless, ineffectual, unproductive, unprofitable, unsuccessful, useless, vain
k.ant: effective, effectual, efficacious, fruitful, potent, productive 有效的； successful 成功的
k.en: producing no results

ex.en: an unavailing effort to avert a war
ex.cn: 试图避免战争的徒劳尝试

k.syn: ceaseless, continual, continuing, incessant, nonstop, perpetual, unbroken, uninterrupted
k.ant: discontinuous, intermittent 不连续的，有间断的
k.en: going on and on without any interruptions

ex.en: Unremitting rain lasted for six days.
ex.cn: 连续下了六天的雨。

k.syn: abreast, acquainted, conversant, informed, knowledgeable, well- informed
k.ant: ignorant, unacquainted, unfamiliar, uninformed, unknowledgeable 无知的
k.en: having information especially as a result of study or experience

ex.en: versed in the latest developments in aeronautics
ex.cn: 熟知航空业的最新发展情况

k.syn: accomplished, adept, complete, consummate, experienced, expert, master, practiced, professed, skilled, veteran, virtuoso
k.ant: amateur, inexperienced, inexpert, jackleg, unprofessional, unseasoned, unskilled 业余的，不精通的
k.en: having or showing exceptional knowledge, experience, or skill in a field of endeavor

ex.en: well versed in the techniques of laser surgery
ex.cn: 精通激光手术方面的技术

k.syn: grotesque, hideous, homely, monstrous
k.ant: aesthetic, attractive, beautiful, comely, cute, fair, gorgeous, handsome, ravishing, seemly 漂亮迷人的
k.en: unpleasant to look at

ex.en: a truly vile combination of colors
ex.cn: 着实丑陋的混搭色调

k.syn: base, contemptible, despicable, detestable, dishonorable, execrable, ignominious, mean, nasty, paltry, sordid, wretched
k.ant: honorable, lofty, noble, straight, upright, venerable, virtuous 有道德的，高尚的
k.en: morally despicable or abhorrent

ex.en: Nothing is so vile as intellectual dishonesty.
ex.cn: 没什么比智力上的欺骗更为可鄙的了。

k.syn: calling, employment, occupation, profession, trade, work
k.ant: hobby 爱好
k.der: vocational adj. 职业的，为职业做准备的
k.en: the work in which a person is regularly employed

ex.en: She finally made soccer her vocation instead of just a hobby.
ex.cn: 她终于将足球从爱好升级为谋生的职业。

k.syn: accord, award, grant, vest
k.ant: withhold 保留，不给予；recant, retract, withdraw 撤回，收回
k.en: to grant or furnish often in a gracious or condescending manner

ex.en: refuse to vouchsafe an explanation
ex.cn: 拒绝给出任何解释

k.syn: peeper
k.en: one obtaining sexual gratification from observing unsuspecting individuals who are partly undressed, naked, or engaged in sexual acts

ex.en: They accidentally found out that their ostensibly benign neighbor is actually a voyeur.
ex.cn: 他们偶然间发现那个表面和善的邻居居然是一个偷窥狂。

k.syn: absent, lacking, missing, nonexistent
k.ant: existent, present 存在的，出现的
k.en: not present or in evidence

ex.en: Grass is almost entirely wanting in that arid wasteland.
ex.cn: 那片荒漠几乎没有长一丝青草。

k.syn: deficient, dissatisfactory, ill, inferior, lousy, paltry, poor, unacceptable, unsatisfactory, wretched
k.ant: acceptable, adequate, passable, tolerable 可接受的；satisfactory 令人满意的
k.en: not being up to standards or expectations

ex.en: He examined her work and found it wanting.
ex.cn: 他检查了她的工作，发现不合要求。

ex.en: We tried her cooking and found it to be very wanting.
ex.cn: 我们试了试她的厨艺，结果发现很不尽人意。

k.syn: balky, contrary, contumacious, defiant, intractable, obstreperous, rebellious, recalcitrant, refractory, unruly
k.ant: amenable, biddable, compliant, conformable, docile, obedient, ruly, submissive, tractable 顺从的
k.cn: 刚愎自用的，不服管束的
k.en: following one's own capricious, wanton, or depraved inclinations

ex.en: He had always been the most wayward of their three children.
ex.cn: 他总是三个小孩里最不听话的一个。wayward children with a history of behavioral problems 过去行为上有些问题的调皮小孩

k.syn: survive
k.ant: decease, die, expire, perish, succumb, pass away 死亡，消亡
k.cn: 安全度过（危机等），经受住
k.en: to come through（something）safely

ex.en: We have weathered worse crises, and so we'll survive this one.
ex.cn: 更为严重的危机我们都闯过了，这个也不会有问题的。

ex.en: They weathered a terrible storm while at sea.
ex.cn: 他们在海上平安渡过了一场巨大的风暴。

k.syn: adamant, headstrong, immovable, implacable, inconvincible, inflexible, intransigent, mulish, obdurate, pertinacious, perverse, stubborn, unyielding, wrongheaded
k.ant: acquiescent, agreeable, amenable, compliant, flexible, pliable, pliant, relenting, yielding 易受影响的
k.en: obstinately and often perversely self- willed

ex.en: Finally the parents sought professional counseling for the willful child.
ex.cn: 最终家长不得不为倔强的小孩寻求专业咨询。

k.syn: conscious, deliberate, intended, intentional, knowing, purposeful, voluntary, willed, witting
k.ant: accidental, chance, haphazard, inadvertent, incidental, random 不经意的，偶然的
k.en: done deliberately

ex.en: a willful attempt to cheat her siblings out of their rightful inheritance
ex.cn: 想将继承权从她的兄弟姐妹手里骗过来的企图

k.syn: helplessly, inescapably, inevitably, perforce, unavoidably
k.en: whether desired or not

ex.en: After her boss fell sick, she willy-nilly found herself directing the project.
ex.cn: 在她老板生病后，她发现自己不论愿不愿意，都得接手这项工程。

k.syn: aimless, arbitrary, desultory, erratic, haphazard, scattered, slapdash, stray
k.ant: methodical, orderly, organized, regular, systematic 有序的，有组织的
k.en: without order or plan

ex.en: willy-nilly taxing laws
ex.cn: 杂乱无序的税收法

k.syn: flag, hang, loll, sag, swag
k.en: to become limp or flaccid

ex.en: The plants wilted after I forgot to water them for three whole days.
ex.cn: 在我整整三天忘记浇水之后，植物们全都萎蔫了。

k.syn: decay, droop, emaciate, fade, fail, lag, languish, sink, waste, wither
k.ant: convalesce, rally, rebound, recover, recuperate 康复，复原
k.en: to feel or exhibit the effects of fatigue or exhaustion

ex.en: She had wilted a bit after walking around the hot and humid city.
ex.cn: 在炎热潮湿的城市中行走使得她略显憔悴。His brain wilted from hitherto unprecedented weariness. 在前所未有的疲劳面前，他开始变得脑力衰弱。

k.syn: beguiling, cagey, crafty, cunning, devious, foxy, guileful, scheming, shrewd, slick, sly, subtle, tricky
k.ant: artless, guileless, ingenuous, innocent, undesigning 天真的，单纯的
k.en: clever at attaining one's ends by indirect and often deceptive means

ex.en: He is an experienced and wily old statesman.
ex.cn: 他是一个经验丰富而狡诈的老政治家。

ex.en: His wily plan only rebounded on himself.
ex.cn: 聪明反被聪明误。

k.syn: eavesdrop, overhear, tap
k.en: to tap a telephone or telegraph wire in order to get information

ex.en: This line is not clean as CIA might have wiretapped it.
ex.cn: 这条线路不安全，因为中情局可能已经在监听了。

k.syn: longing, nostalgic, reminiscent, yearning
k.ant: apathetic, indifferent, insouciant, nonchalant, perfunctory, unconcerned 无所谓的，不感兴趣的
k.en: full of yearning or desire tinged with melancholy

ex.en: There was a wistful look in his eyes when he spoke of his childhood.
ex.cn: 当他谈起他的童年时，眼眶中不禁流露出一种带有忧伤的怀念之情。

k.syn: twist, wrench, wring
k.en: to pull, force, or move by violent wringing or twisting movements

ex.en: wrest the lid off this pickle jar
ex.cn: 把泡菜罐的盖子拧下来

k.syn: scrape, scrounge, squeeze
k.en: to gain with difficulty by or as if by force, violence, or determined labor

ex.en: farmers who were used to wresting a living from the barren land
ex.cn: 一度依靠贫瘠的土地辛苦度日的农民

k.syn: adamant, headstrong, immovable, implacable, inconvincible, inflexible, intransigent, mulish, obdurate, pertinacious, perverse, stubborn, unyielding, willful
k.ant: acquiescent, agreeable, amenable, compliant, flexible, pliable, pliant, relenting, yielding 易受影响的 Word List 1　与说有关的词根构成的单词 1. 词根dic, dict 词根dic（t）表示say［说］. 常见词predict［预测］字面义 say［dict=say］before［pre-=before］，即预先说（结果）. 同大部分与说相关的词根一样，这个词根也有“words”的含义，dictionary［字典］就是a collection of words.
k.cn: 固执己见的，坚持（错误观点）的
k.en: stubborn in adherence to wrong opinion or principles

ex.en: In spite of other's objections, he is always wrongheaded in his opinions.
ex.cn: 他总是不管他人的反对而固执地坚持自己哪怕是错误的观点。
